Wow some of the other reviews on here completely SLAM this place-its really not as bad as they made it out to be (and i can be picky). 

The service was good, drinks were fairly priced for downtown. The Caesar salad they make at the table is PHENOMENAL, as was their calamari appetizer. The entrees however lacked flavor, but a little salt took care of this. I would definitely come here again for the ambiance, drinks and appetizers. The dining room is soo pretty and cozy. 
I will not rush back, but I definitely would go there again.

Insanely overpriced.  A society that accepts minuscule cupcakes for $3.50 is a society that is destined for collapse.

The cookie sandwiches were delicious though.

This place was just OK to me.  It came highly recommended and had a lot of history, so I wanted to try it out.  The atmosphere was definitely its best quality.  Very "homey" and Italian.  I had no complaints about the service or even the food but it wasn't nearly all that I'd heard it made out to be.  I ordered the spaghetti with tomato and mushroom sauce and it was fine - but I've had better.  I wouldn't coming rushing back here anytime soon but it also wasn't a bad dining experience.

Been here 2 times now and each time I love it more. The people who manage the establishment are really nice. I mean REALLY NICE. I even asked my friends: "These guys are NOT FROM PHILADELPHIA. They are too nice and too polite!" Well, besides the great customer service, the place is amazingly clean and FEELS LIKE HOME. I mean it has a country home flair in decoration. 

I love the menu, again it feels yummy and tasteful. Sandwiches and soups and entrees. I also love the beer selection and the fact that every cooler is organized by country and region. I have been here after work and also late at night and both times service was excellent. I will try it for brunch one of these days after my soccer game :-)

Also, since I live close to broad and Christian this is a great walking location for me. 

And prices for beers and the food is VERY REASONABLE and it is all available for take-out.

How nice are these people? Last Friday night, three of us are starving after a workout and we call Hawthorn for dinner hours. They only served dinner until 9 pm but with 5 minutes to go, they were willing to take our order over the phone. By the time we got there our food was ready and yummy to boot. I love that I can pick my beer from the fridge, I love their friendly attitude and I love that they make great food with enough variety for a vegetarian. Thank you folks.

Good food, great beer!!!

Just went here for the first time. It's really close to me, so I'm surprised I haven't been before. Anyway, we sat outside on a lovely 70-degree April evening, and the only problem the entire time was that it took forever for a waitress to get to us--probably 15+ minutes. I was literally walking inside to find someone when she walked out.

The waitress was super nice though, and after she showed up everything was great. We ordered the mediterranean platter, the burger, and the short ribs, all of which were very tasty. (They do tapas-style small plates,) The hummus, I realized later, was garlic-breath-inducing, so they might want to rein it in a little. Everything else was great though, so I don't really have any complaints.

Hawthorne's has a 1000+ bottle beer list, so you definitely won't be disappointed in that regard. You can have the beer charged to your table, or you can take it home.

All in all, it was a very nice experience, and I'd definitely recommend it if you like beer and you're nearby.

Good solid fresh food, buuuut it was my impression that they were trying too hard.  The menu had plenty of brie, exotic salamis, greens, and whatnot.  They must specialize or be famous for their quiches.  They had several types of tap...

I had the Reed's Ginger drink.  As someone who loves ginger I loved this drink, I uploaded a picture.  It was most definitely very ginger-y!!

We chose to be seated outside with Cavalier King Charles Spaniel Charlie for lunch.  They brought Charlie a doggie water bowl; I thought that was a nice touch.  On the other hand the seats were a wobbly, but nothing too life-changing.

Overall, although tasty I have trouble justifying paying $8 for a salami, bree, lettuce, and tomato sandwich for lunch.

No credit cards.

EVERY time I go there, I order Pepper Crusted Sirloin... YUMMY.  Love the BLT salad which is complimentary with the steak meal.  Oh, and the garlic mashed potatoes... yum yum.  Staff... always friendly every time I've been there.  I think they have great food.

One thing that I noticed right off the bat is this place is pricey even for lunch. 

Its a korean / japanese please which is an odd but inviting combo for me since I love Korean. 

We ordered all korean which prompted the waitress to try and get us to order sushi and even bringing us some free. I dunno, maybe they were trying to get rid of it. 

The Galbi was ok, but 18.99 ouch. The banchan was limited and it was real small portions of like 4 items. 

The soondubu was decent and I wish they had a combo with galbi, but they didnt. 

Even though the korean was very expensive, the sashimi seemed reasonably priced so perhaps i would come back to try some of that. 

The service was good and prompt as well.

This is the best Pho joint in Philly, in my opinion, of course. After trying many other places, the broth and meat at Pho Ha is the freshest and most tasty.

And inexpensive! A small bowl is $6.05, including tax. Can't beat that with a bat!

Super fast service as well.

Booo. This place sucks!

Service is TERRIBLE. 

Food is GROSS. The only items palatable were the hummus and eggplant salad. The chicken and steak "kabob" seemed like it was heated, over and over again.

This place is pure novelty. It's popularity based on the fact that you sit on a couch and eat with your hands. Big freaking deal.

This is one of my favorite restaurants in the city. I go here about once every two weeks and always order the same 2 things (-which I'll get to in a moment.) 

What spurred me to write this review is that I went to Shaio Lan Kung last night- alone. I've never gone to a restaurant by myself before but after a grueling weekend at work and with a pocket full of cash and a good book, I decided that a quiet evening gorging myself at my favorite hole-in-the-wall (and it is a hole-in-the-wall... no one goes there for the atmosphere) was just what the doctor ordered. However, I was afraid that I'd be hurried, or ignored, or have to deal with an annoyed waiter (less food= less tips) but was both relieved and surprised when my service was on-point as always (though a little forgetful, which I find charming. "Oh! Your tea! I'll get it right now!" *scurry in the back, run to my table tea in hand looking slightly but endearingly embarrassed*) I read my book for a long while and slowly munched my food while my water magically refilled itself every time I turned the page (Shiao Lan Kung- the enchanted eatery of China Town?) and the food was, as it always is, orgasmically delicious. 

As a vegan my options are somewhat limited in a place like SLK, but it doesn't matter because they have two of the most delicious vegan meals in the entire city (rendering other options superfluous.) This is what I get (and my friends get, and their friends get, and so on, and so on...) 

SALT BAKED TOFU ($7.95) 
EGGPLANT WITH GARLIC SAUCE IN A HOT POT ($8.95) 

This is food enough for two people (though I ate it all myself last night and waddled home with unbuttoned jeans.) The tofu is not baked at all, but rather fried (or enchanted to appear fried) and comes out sauceless, topped with chopped scallions and hot peppers, and outrageously delicious. I don't even like tofu, but this is undeniably amazing. (My meat eating friends even order it!) Salty, crisp, yummy. 

The eggplant hot pot is of another world. It comes out spitting and sputtering in a thick garlic sauce with wood ear mushrooms, scallions, and thick pieces of superbly cooked eggplant. When my Dad visited Philly we got this dish 3 times in 2 days. The portion is huge, the price is right, and thus far I've never got that slimy-coated-greasy-stomach that some Chinese joints leave you with. 

Also, as for the reviewer who said they never see Asian people at SLK, they must have gone on backwards day. It's usually filled with non-english speaking Asians when I am there (or non Asians like myself that know good food when they find it), in fact I was first introduced to this restaurant by a Chinese friend who ordered for me in Cantonese so I wouldn't know what I was getting (...so I couldn't go without him.) 

This is a great place for vegans and vegetarians to go with omnivores, or go with each other, or, as I found out last night, to go alone. I highly recommend you witness the magic for yourself.

Chicken maroosh & the Chicken Shish Tawook- LOVE IT!! Now that i'm rarely in the w philly area during the week, i really miss this place (too bad they're not opened on sundays). I had the maroosh today for lunch and it was so tasty. This is one of my favorite places in philly. I used to eat here on a weekly basis when i first moved to philly ten years ago. The owner is really nice and friendly (not the typical philly service you usually get elsewhere); he's great at remembering names. Maybe they can expand and open one up in jersey? (hint, hint) ;)

this is a great place for a quick meal.  the owner (or whoever is at the counter) is very nice and obviously proud of his food.  he is more than happy to explain what chaat is and the different varieties of chaat.  the lamb was cooked very nicely.  my food was very spicy, just as i requested.  they also have an interesting indian version of coca-cola in glass bottles.  neat place and would definitely stop by again.

When I heard that Sir Bobby Flay was opening up a burger joint in my town, I freaked out! Me & my friend wandered down to University City the day after the grand opening to get a taste of BBP. When we arrived the line was long, as we had expected but surprisingly moved pretty quick (almost half the time the staff had anticipated). The menu features many unique burgers including the LA (avocado, watercrest), the Napa Valley (goat cheese, lemon honey mustard), & of course... the Philadelphia (cheese whiz, onions, hot peppers). 

I ordered the Classic but forgot to get it crunchified! But the un-crunchified burger did not disappoint, along with the fries and onion rings that were equally delicious. They offer an array of sauce options as well: the standard ketchup & mustard, plus chipotle ketchup, jalapeno relish, & burger sauce. You're also suppose to get a side of "fry sauce" but for some reason they forgot to give us some. Service was pretty quick & the staff was consistently friendly (sometimes too friendly).

The decor of this place is awesome. It's not trying to hard to be fancy nor is it overly cheesy. Although it is a bit overwhelming with it's tall ceilings and excessive floor space. It almost looks more like a cool bar rather then a restaurant.

All in all I highly recommend BBP. Bring a friend & an appetite and you're good to go.

Very simple but excellent quality.  You don't have to spend a fortune (like Budakon) to prove quality.  Loved it...loved it...loved it.  Parking right next door or numerous parking lots around the area.  Close to Rittenhouse Square if you want to walk after dinner.
Also, Good Dog is right next door if you want to hang at the bar with friends after dinner...

The wait was over an hour on a late Saturday afternoon.  For some reason they only had one cashier working....as I got closer towards the register they finally had another person working the register!  (there are ONLY two registers).  I ordered a Dallas burger medium-well, sweet potatoe fries and a coconut milkshake.  My companion ordered a Napa Valley well done and a mango milkshake.

We sat down and were greeted by a young man who brought us water.  He was not our waiter...just a greeter of some sort?  We didn't even SIP our water and BAM our burgers were out!  WTF?!  THAT was super quick service!!!  No complaints here!

The greeters/servers were very nice coming by every few minutes to ask if everything was okay.  The burgers were delicious and definately worth the wait in line.  The sweet potatoe fries were okay the sauce on the side was a bit runny (should be a little thicker to STICK on the fry).  The milkshake....well...I would return JUST for that!  My companion didn't utter a word while he devoured his burger and his shake - that's a good sign :)

There were plenty of seats and at no time did I feel like we were being rushed out....on the other hand....I'm not a lingerer...I eat and I'm out.  I enjoyed the meal and the atmosphere and would definately go back!!

Waited in line on a Monday at 5:45 for forty-five minutes while only moving from outside to a few feet in and then bailed to another location because I had a Sixers game to make. Damn you Dallas burger, I will have you yet! It was especially bad for me because knowing that goodness was out there made me all the more disappointed in the impostor I got at the golden arches down the street. Think I'll hold off on my return until all those mouths trying to keep me from my destiny go on summer break, but I am certain I will be giving a much higher rating then. Suppose I could review McDonald's for now.....

patience is a virtue that i do not have.  
bobby flay (i do love throwdown) + burgers = what could go wrong?

so it's a gorgeous sunny day in west philadelphia, and it's 11am and i'm hungry.  off i trek in search of the perfect burger.

so the rate limiting step between me and my slab of meat turns out to be the line of hungry people...and one cashier.  i mean, really?  it's lunch time, there's a line out the door, and you have *one* cashier at the register??  

it's enough to get this now hypoglycemic girl in a little hissy fit.

once i sat down and had a bite of my burger (a little bit pinker than the medium i asked for and too many sesame seeds on my bun)... i calmed down a bit.  it was a good burger.  the sweet potato fries with a too watery honey mustard dipping sauce were also pretty good.

dear bobby, now that you've got your food down, can you work on efficiency?  thanks.

amc

I went here with a coworker two days after the opening for lunch. We knew we were in for a wait and gave ourselves an hour for a leisurely lunch.

I have to commend Bobby's team on excellent logistical planning. There is a BBP employee assigned to monitor the line. She stands outside and lets people know how long the wait will be, asks them about what they think they will order, and hands out menus. We were told it would be about an 40 minute wait until we could order and that suited us because the office is around the corner. The line was out the door, but it moved constantly. Also, it was sunny outside, so I enjoyed standing in the sunshine. As one other person mentioned, yes, there is a single cash register. It's likely that way on purpose, to make the line move at a speed which the cooks can follow.

Once we got to the head of the line, I ordered a LA turkey burger, crunchified, my coworker ordered a Napa Valley Burger, and we split an order of sweet potato fries. We both got the fountain sodas which come with free refills. We chose to sit at the bar and wait for our orders, which was nice because a waitress came by to refill one of our drinks for us, although it was self-serve. Our food arrived in record time: around 5 minutes after we ordered.

On to the burger; my burger was perfect. The turkey was moist, not overcooked, and the huge melty slice of cheese on touch was just the right amount. I barely noticed the tiny sprig of watercress, but it was nice for presentation. The bun was toasted on both sides which made the sesame seeds exceptionally delicious. The chips on top of the burger, AKA the crunchification, were a nice touch of texture.

The sweet potato fries weren't great, just okay. The side of sweet mustard that came for dipping was over-sweet. I don't think I'd order the fries again.

I'm going back for dinner with my husband soon. This is going to be a place I frequent.

One of my favorite restaurants in Chinatown!  My family has been coming here since I was 5, and we have never been disappointed.  This is still our go-to place whenever we have family or friends visiting.  I would definitely recommend the fish maw soup, sizzling stuffed tofu, eggplant and pepper, stir-fried shredded beef with peppers, seafood casserole/hot pot, fresh oysters (their specialty), salt-baked seafood, and clams in black bean sauce.  They are known for their cantonese dishes, and there's a reason for the Zagat awards and reviews.  The portions are very good, and the service is awesome; they will tell you what the freshest items, as far as seafood is concerned, of the day are and will go out and get you a fish from the seafood market if you want a kind that they don't have in their kitchen tank.  My family always orders way more food than can be consumed in even 2 sittings just so we can live off of the leftovers (which are still good!) for the week.  You have to stop by here if you're in Chinatown.  Just don't get Americanized dishes because you're coming here for authentic cantonese food!

I work only a few steps away from Smokin Betty's and had been meaning to try this place since it opened.  I was not put off by the annoying local union guys who picketed every day (apparently they were not happy that the owner tried to save money by not being held hostage to their ridiculous wages and used non union workers for some of the construction) , just never really got a chance to go.  Well I am pleased to say that the annoying union guys are gone.  So last week I decided that I could wait no longer, I needed to decide for myself if the place is good.  It was a nice day outside and they do have outside seating and tables were open but I just can't fathom eating outside on Sansom Street unless I was homeless. The smell from the numerous dumpsters that litter the street can sometimes be bad, plus you have tons of trucks zipping around spewing their smelly fumes, so outdoor seating was out.  Now lets get inside and get to the food and libations.  We were greated by a pleasant server who led us to a table downstairs, i had heard that everytime you went their to eat you were hustled upstairs but on this day we were seated downstairs and upstairs was closed.  I still have not seen the upstairs but the downstairs is ok as far as decor. One cool feature is that the windows open all the way from top of ceiling to bottom of floor.  On a hot day, get those suckers open and enjoy a quasi outdoor eating experience without actually having to be outside. I like.  So i am seated and given menu and list of beverages.  The draft beer list is most impressive.  I was a warm day and a beer was what was called for but what to choose.  I told server what my tastes were and what I was looking at and she said the one I had honed in on was really good with a crisp clean finish (forgot the name but that I why i am going back today - to get more of that beer and get the name).  I ordered the beer and enjoyed it trmendously.  Almost tempted me to blow off work the rest of the afternoon and enjoy a few more (maybe for today's visit - so boss please don't read this review and its not me).  Ok so the atmosphere is good (indoor), the libation is tasty, but how about the food.  Well I went simple and ordered a burger.  WOW (i may have used wow in other reviews but its rare, I try to save the WOW for when it really counts) is the only word I can use to describe this burger.  I am a big five guy's fan and enjoy their fast food burger and for fast food still consider them the best of the lower tier of burgers joints.  Well as for restaurant or Pub burgers, Ted's Montana had been my favorite (Bison or Beef) however I may be ready to proclaim Betty's burger as my new favorite after only one burger (that is saying something).  It was cooked perfectly, the fries were good (5 guys still owns the fry title though), the toppings were fresh, the bacon crispy and thik, unlike the sad sack bacon that is served on so many burgers.  The size of the burger and bun were just right and complimented one another nicely.  I am a burger nut and enjoy some A-1 on my burgers (if this is a crime, then GFY, you use ketchup, I use A-1) well gues what, i negelected to ask for A-1 but am glad that I did, this burger was so good, the sauce may have wound up taking away from it.  I will go again and order the burger again, I will do this several times before I try something else (just the way I am).  Eventually I will try some of the other menus selections but for now, since this is my new favorite burger, I will have to contineu to get them.  Go, Eat. Decide for yourself.  Beer Selection is nice, menu has plenty of good selections for people that are not as burger minded as myself, and the prices were very reasonable. I consider it a worthy addition to the area.

Great food and bread, great selection, awesome location.
Prices are quite high, wait service is fair-to-poor and the delay for the food preparation is confusingly looong.  I've been here when it was dead, and it still took them forever to make a sandwich!  I'd love to know what they are doing in the kitchen when it take 45 minutes to make eggs!
On top of that, the wait-staff is often more interested in their own private conversations than in making eye contact with you every 5-10 minutes to see if maybe you want to order a coffee to pass the interminable wait...
But then, Manyunk on the whole is a bit played out.

The o-toro was to die for, although I've had better uni.  The salmon was good.  I haven't tried the rolls, but the udon was flavorful and delicious.  The prices are a little steep so I wouldn't recommend coming here often (unless your wallet could use some lightening).  However, I will be back if only for the o-toro.  It just melts the moment it is placed in your mouth.  Okay, now this is making me hungry...

I've heard legacies of Koreana since I moved to West Philly. But due to its awkward location I had it mistaken for a takeout portion of the Indian  restaurant in the same location since it was the only spot that they had clear signage for (either that or some random extension of Atlantis Gentleman's Club). But Burger Assassin's helpful review assured me that it indeed the location I've been looking for, and since then its been pure bliss.

If you're looking for a no frills spot that gives you the chance to sink your taste buds into something different , then the Korean food here is perfect for you. one of the best dishes I ordered here was a variation of an omelet that was stuffed with rice and all kinds of fresh vegetables. but the next time I come I definitely plan on ordering some of that Bim Bim Bap! another thing I like is the fact that they have all kinds of Japanese market staples like different teas and Pocky. Toss in the friendly staff and fact that there's not too many dishes that hit past the 410 mark, and you have a solid winner.

I love coming here.  It's a quaint little cafe on the edge of Chinatown that serves you amazing siphon coffee in a mug - complete with a doily underneath and a little pastry on the side.  The dumpling sampler is a must-try, for sure.  They have quite a variety of lunch specials and noodle soups, although I always tend to stick with the dumplings and beef noodle soup because I'm a creature of habit (I know, I know...I will get around to trying everything eventually but my tried and true dishes never disappoint!).  But don't leave without having a cup of coffee!  Definitely better than Starbucks, Dunkin, or Peet's.  It is so good that I'm considering investing in my own siphon, although I'm not sure how I'll be able to use it in a dorm.  Oh well, in the meantime, I'll just be stopping by Ray's every time I'm home even though it's still an hour-long drive from where I live.

I did not want to like Sweet Lucy's as much as I did.

As a selfish, lazy man, I often imagine that the best restaurants in Philadelphia are those within a very reasonable walk or subway ride from my house.  When I take off in an automobile to get a meal, there's a subtle hope in the back of my head that I will not enjoy myself and thus, will not have to schlep any sort of distance to get the meal again.  The problem with eating at Sweet Lucy's is that it will force me to overcome my lethargy many times in the coming future so that I may consume some excellent BBQ.  

The sides are fantastic.  The cornbread tastes exactly how I want cornbread to taste like.  The baked beans taste exactly how I want baked beans to taste like.  The ribs are on par with Bebe's(my more geographically favorable dig).  They are smokey, low in fat chunks, and slide easily off the bone.  They offer their vinegar and hot sauces if you want to add some extra kick yourself and it is awesome.  

When I go to Sweet Lucy's, everyone wins except mother earth, as I will doubtlessly be increasing my carbon footprint while traveling back and forth from this location.  Sorry Gaia.  If movies have told me anything about the world, you will soon become angry with my blatant disregard for your health and send spirits/natural disasters/possessed animals/poisonous evil trees(oh god what) after me in order to wreak horrible vengeance against myself and all other humans.

Cafe Ole is the only place that I will leave my apartment for in the rain. Their iced tea is like a ray of sunshine on cloudy day...a shot of raspberry flavor, a sprig of mint, and a big slice of lemon? Yes please.

p.s. it's even better on sunny days.

I have been non-dairy for years, and just recently went gluten-free (except for the occasional cheat beer), so I was assuming my days of moist baked goods were over. Thankfully, I was wrong! 

The first time I went in to Sweet Freedom (after stalking them on Facebook for months before they opened), I tried their vanilla cupcake with blue icing. While I could definitely tell it was gluten-free -- that is, the cake was a little grainy -- the difference was that it also succeeded in being incredibly moist.   I heard the the chocolate cupcake is better, so I can't wait to try that one next time. 

Based on the cupcake, I am definitely going to look into getting a birthday cake there when the time comes! (They do special orders, I found out; check the Web site.)

The other standout for me was the magic bar, which tastes like it has peanut butter in it, yet doesn't! The brownie and the apple crumble were also good.

Amazing, cheap, delicious, quick, friendly, clean, etc etc etc good things!  This place I could live in.  It makes me wander why I didn't discover this sooner and had this at least three times a week as a meal.  I love the steak, the brisket, the flank steak, etc!!!!  I have tried a few of the locations nearby and noone is as great tasting as this one.  Highly recommend it!

This place is a cut above the rest. I am in no way, shape, or form a vegan nor am i allergic to any gluten. I swung by this place to pick up a Magi bar, which I had heard a lot of hype about. It's basically this chocolate bar with a graham crackery crust and coconut shavings sprinkled on top. Let me say that i am not really big on coconut but oh.. my.. god... these bars were AMAZING! It was one of those experiences where you're having a really bad day but once you bite into your treat, birds start singing and the sun gets brighter.

The Magic Bars are that good. Go to Sweet Freedom, you won't be disappointed! Possibly the best treats in the city.

El Guero Canelo is a wonderful, fun, well-run restaurant close to downtown. We ordered from the window and it was super fast - about 2 minutes - for our number to be called. Yes it's a bit hard to hear your number but they have electronic boards throughout the restaurant that make it easy. The Mexican hot dogs are very tasty and inexpensive. The best thing by far is the condiment table - with roasted onions and peppers, salsa and a variety of vegetables that are out of this world. Very unique! Everything we tried was excellent - and overall it was a great experience. El Guero Canelo is highly recommended - a new simple favorite in Tucson.

Part of a restaurant's appeal always resides in the ambiance.  Unfortunately, the one here is utterly devoid of any tact and displays only an uncouth degree of sheer ignorance.  This is a tourist attraction, for sure, and their steaks are fairly decent, but the offensive signs stating: "This is America, when ordering, speak English" are reason enough for me to stay away.  So abysmally rude, even to an American, like me.  

Go to Pat's instead.  They give you a bigger portion of steak, and it's more flavorful.

For the price and location, it is worth checking out. 

The burger meat was adequate and the toppings delightful.  The milkshakes are out of this world!  

However, the service is poor and they messed up our orders.  I went with ten friends -- most of whom think the food is just "ok."

I had worked in CC for 4 years or so before trying this guy.  There was a lot of hype around him, but I had never been.  I'm used to spending $5 for lunch plus I'm not really one for the gimmicky stuff (soup nazi-ishness, music, garlic, plants, etc) so I had my doubts. 

I was AMAZED.  The food is absolutely spectacular.  I'm not sure what half of the stuff is, but it's unbelievably good.

FYI, the last time I went a couple weeks ago, the guy said this was his first day back in like 4 months.  He supposedly also owns a restaurant (which I'd love to know about) and it was being remodeled.

EDIT: I ate there again 5/21/10 and found he had raised his prices to $13 per platter.  A $3 INCREASE!  How a 30% increase in price is reasonable, I have no idea.  I'm no longer in the city for work, but even if I was, I can't justify paying that much anymore.  $10 - only a few bucks more than a deli hoagie - is one thing, but 2+ times as much as a normal lunch?  Hearing his "QUALITY! QUALITY!" rants when his prices were justifiable was one thing, but hearing that sh*t now is like a rapist whispering sweet nothings into your ear.

While I always try to do my part to ensure that friends support Tucson originals, they need to uphold their end of the bargain. Char's has done business from the cozy 5th Street storefront for more than ten years. The floating palace painted onto a southwestern mural is kitsch central, but they've become complacent about their food. My friend and I like it hot; I remember when I was able to order Tom Kha Gha SPICY at Char's and my eyes would water. I didn't used to mind when the appetizer and entree were served at the same time because doggonit this was a fine Thai restaurant in mid-town.  We carefully explained that we wanted our pad thai HOT and it was sweet and bland. When we told our server, she handed us a lazy susan with a few spice pots. She pointed out the "hot" one, and although we doused both soup and entree, nothing produced even a minor burn. 
For those who simply crave decent Thai toast, green beans in chile sauce milder than Campbell's soup, and a rice and chicken stuffed pineapple (which will bring an "ooo" and "aaa" from a dinner group), Char's can still suffice. Sadly, though, P.F. Chang has a more diverse menu, more competitive prices, and much better service.

New location: 2 stars, Old location: 5 stars
 
Guadalajara Grill used to be located just down the street from its current location. The original location had an authentic vibe; sitting outside on the patio was a necessity, with the lush greenery, colorful decorations, and fireplace; it was perfect regardless if it was during the day grabbing drinks with friends or at night celebrating a birthday with a group. I used to frequent Guadalajara quite often, whenever in the mood for Mexican, and always left feeling blissful. 

Since I had moved out of Tucson a few years back, I was excited to revisit one of my favorites when last in the area... When i heard it was at a new location I was excited, of course this must be an improvement on the already perfect, although very crowded establishment. I walked in and was immediately disappointed. Guadalajara used to have an authentic and festive feel while the new location had no soul, it was just like every other Mexican restaurant in town. 

Regardless, I was excited to eat my favorite meal from Guadalajara.... chips with fresh, made at the table, hot hot hot salsa and tortilla soup (no chicken) with extra cheese and avocados. I was served tomato paste with my chips. Guess the salsa lady was not on duty yet. Not a problem, I've been there before when they served pre-made salsa. So I asked for a hot version... the reply, "this is all we have". Rude. How do you call yourself a Mexican restaurant with no hot sauce?! The tortilla soup still tasted the same so at least that made my experience decent. The service has always been hit or miss so I am not going to rate the place based on the staff, as that is not what made Guadalajara great back in the day. I am very disappointed to see what Guadalajara has turned into, all authenticity had been lost.

My friend treated me to an eyelash extension full set lat year, and Kim did an absolutely amazing job.  They looked so natural and the room was so comfortable that I fell asleep during the process.  The salon is a bit pricey (minus 1 star) but they do use Aveda products and sell the full line (so grateful because I use the tourmaline cream almost exclusively and can get it here).  If you want to treat yourself for a day, come here, and then get some shopping done, too.  :-)

We had never eaten chaat before, but I have to say that Indian street food is excellent (especially from Desi Chaat House).  It is the same family that own and operates the Desi Village near 46th & Baltimore.  Chaat is very different from standard Indian restaurant cuisine.  We had the lamb and vegetable samosa chaat. It was tasty and affordable (@ $5-6 per entree). I would definitely go back again, and recommend it to friends.

Great food.  I tried the pineapple fried rice with shrimp and my wife had the curry beef; both are excellent.  For appertizers, the chicken wings are excellent; the sampler doesn't really stand out.

Parking is a little hard to find around here.  The parking lots charges $12 on evenings; road side are usually full...

Love this place.

Their lettuce chicken wraps are great.  As are their avocado egg rolls.  I have not had a bad meal yet. 

Their service is a little less thrilling.  Love the fact that they have black linens.  No more white fuzz!

I'm not a concert venue type of a girl but when I see a band I like I wanna move around. Not only that but I'm short so buying a $40 ticket where I'm going to have a seat is sketchy because, who knows, I may get stuck with a big tall block head in my view. 

When I walk into the Electric Factory I feel like I'm in a movie where something crazy is gonna go down. You know what I mean, it's either the bathroom murder scene, the action sequence or the angsty teen film with a band. I love it. 

The walls have posters of bands I grew up listening to and not the over-commercialized kind. There is plenty of room in the sold out show to move around or to squish through people to the stage. I like the lower right side near the doors the best because you always can see the band, people don't shove you around and you can leave as soon as it's over. 

At the bar I can get away from the under-age kids but stay weary for the amateur drinkers. While an opening act was playing an ugly, self-tanner and gel infused couple were grinding and making out. BARF. BUT....I love the bartender who snapped when he saw how annoyed the rest of the world was with them.   

TIP: This is one venue you're more than likely to meet that band you love. Try the parking lot before they go on or they usually announce to meet them in the back. I'm not an autograph fanatic so this tip is based from word of mouth. 

Downside: $10-$20 parking and underage kids (although they've been friendly)

One of my mom's friends is the food supplier for this restaurant so I'm sure that we get the VIP treatment every time we go together for dinner.  Nevertheless, the food is very good, and you can see them making the roti, which is cool.  It's also not as dark as Penang, which means that I can see what I'm eating and not accidentally eat the fish eye from the Tom Yum Fish Head Casserole.  The mango fish is quite delicious, and the hainan chicken rice and actual chicken are well and subtly flavored.  The meat is quite tender and amazing with the chef's soy sauce.  The chow kweh teow (I'm killing the spelling on this one) was pretty mild but still appetizing and they were more than happy to put in extra squid when I asked.  The Tom Yum Fish Head Casserole is my mom's favorite dish here but is a bit too spicy for me (I've been able to recreate a milder version at home, thank goodness).  The salted fish fried rice is just okay - not bad but not amazing either.  As for appetizers, the chicken and beef satay were well-marinated and grilled so that the outside was slightly crispy and the inside nice and tender.  The roti was a little greasy but tasted amazing when dipped in the curry - pretty similar to Penang's.  They also had a sushi menu and I was able to get a Dragon Roll, which was nice and fresh.  The sunomono was also a hit.

The prices are pretty similar to Penang's, but they do have the sushi option and the service is slightly better here.  I would stop by if you're in the mood for Malaysian.
The best high-end pure vegetarian food in Philadelphia, and one of the best in the country.

I have a problem.

I have been thinking about the vegetable samosas from Desi Chaat House a lot.  It is causing a serious interruption in my daily functioning.

Sometimes I will be in the middle of a conversation and I will pretend to get a call so that I can go outside, hail a cab, and go to Desi Chaat house for the samosas.  I return, sometimes an hour later, and attempt to continue the conversation as if nothing had happened.  My friends think I'm on drugs and my girlfriend is accusing me of having an affair but they don't know how much I love these samosas.

But Brad, why not introduce these people to Desi Chaat house?  

The answer is simple, hypothetical question-asker...it is because I am a man who's mind is rife with delusional paranoia.  I fear that my friends and loved ones, knowing my weakness, will buy out the samosas before I am able to get to the restaurant and they will sell them to me at ridiculous markups.  Like a hopeless addict, I will be enslaved to the wills of those I once trusted. 

Illogical? yes.  Impossible?  Nope.

...ok back to reviewing.

When you order them, they will offer to pour chickpeas over them.  Let them do this.  

The best way I could describe the chaat is...wacky.  It is a peculiar mix of sweet, spicy, creamy, and aromatics.  I've had the vegetable samosa and mumbai chaat and both are good.

The chicken roll and lamb roll make an excellent lunch.  The meat is tender and seasoned extremely well.  I highly recommend these items.  For a very vegetarian-leaning culture, India sure makes some excellent meat dishes.

On top of it all, the staff is ridiculously friendly and always asking for feedback on how they can improve.  It is tasty, inexpensive, unique, and has the best veggie samosas in Philadelphia.

Note: I like veggie samosas a lot more than most people so bear that in mind when you consider this review.

I am an avid meateater but this restaurant is amazing.  The pan seared tofu was delicious - slightly crispy and bursting with flavor.  The fingerling potatoes are so much better than fries.  Definitely stop by if you're near University City.  This is a must-try.

You can't hate on this place. Mostly, if you love a burger, you're going to like this place guaranteed. But then again, a good cheeseburger warms my heart just as much as a perfectly timed mojito.

I had the Dallas Burger and honestly it's been a long time since I've ordered a burger "medium rare" and truly got it medium rare. That makes me smile right off the bat.

The jalapeno sauce they have has even made me want to go home and replicate. That's the when a restaurant goes from a 3 to a 4 star-- if it makes me want to go home and duplicate. Afterall, imitation is the highest form of flattery.

I could do without the ostentatious 45 minute line (and that was during a line that was just barely out the door). They have two cash registers. Use 'em, people.

**Rule number one: this is not the place to go to for restaurant week. Go during a special occasion and you're ready to spend some money ($20-$35) on an entree and you're happy to spend another $30 on appetizers and (1) drink. 

TIMING: The earliest reservation they had for me was 10:15 on a Saturday night, and that was when I called Saturday morning. So it takes some planning to eat at a normal time at this restaurant.

FEEL: The ambiance of this place is pretty on point. Nicely decorated without being cluttered and overstated-- the usual trendy Steven Starr way. 

COCKTAILS: Besides being expensive, the cocktails weren't very impressive. We had all the caipirinhas which tasted lovely. People say they are strong, but I am a light weight and I was still standing. I'm more of a fan of the big glass of alcohol than the small but expensive kind so don't take my word for it. I also had a dark and stormy which was very tasty. But if you don't like ginger beer stay away.

APPETIZERS: We ordered Rainbow ceviche which was SUPER FRESH tasting. The black and white sesame seeds with the lime juice and the fish was an amazing balance. But I now crave/daydream about the Smoked Marlin. They come nicely presented as mini tacos and you could easily wipe out the whole lot of them they are so freakin' scrumptious. Nice smoky taste that's not overwhelming and I don't have marlin often so that was also a treat. 

MAIN: We all ordered seafood. Thank God we did. I hesitated on the Chilean Sea Bass only to later thank my lucky stars that I ordered it. The quinoa was creamy fantastic although could have used more than the half cup they served. The sea bass was poached and then pan seared TO PERFECTION. Sea bass is a pretty light fish, and it literally MELTED in my mouth. It made me wonder why I haven't ordered that fish out more often!! It's definitely back on my radar thanks to this wonderful recipe. A friend ordered the Maine Diver Scallops-- also done to perfection with the pastel de chocolo being really really special. It was like cream corn wrapped in filo dough. But that description doesn't sound as savory as it really is. My friend usually doesn't finish her entree when we go out but this time she did. *I would stay away from the Sugarcane Tuna. Although cooked perfectly, it was on the boring/safe side in terms of taste. The shrimp served with the dish were small and did not look as fresh.

DESSERT: And truthfully, although our server (who was amazingly nice and very frank about the food which I find helpful in decision making) raved about the Banana Tres Leches-- it was kinda dry. But it was served with AMAZING coconut rum sorbet. AMAZING. But tiny scoop.

SUMMATION: It's rare for me to love BOTH appetizers and two out of three main dishes. I'd give this place a 91% on a test. Therefore definitely worthy of 5 stars.

I've been here twice for Sunday brunch.
Food is good - Service sucks.
Its like everyone's first day; everyday.

The first time, the waiter asked us if we wanted chips then never brought them.  HA!  Thanks.
The last time I was there, I waited over 30 minutes for an egg.  WTF.
I won't be back.  There's too many better places to go.

I got the roast duck noodle soup with a marinated egg and a side of the marinated roast beef.  Portions are pretty good, and the soup was amazing on a cold day.  I've had better marinated roast beef (from Ka Man in Edison) but it was decent.  I'm not going to complain because I came here for the noodle soup and that was quite satisfying.  The menu is small, but if you're in the mood for some very flavorful soup, then this the right place.  The egg gave the dish a finishing touch.  The service is FAST (as in less than 3 min. from ordering to dish sitting in front of me beckoning me to eat).  This is a come-for-the-food place but the food is definitely more than enough to draw anyone who appreciates good, savory noodle soup in.

I will start by saying that I have had Pho from all over the country.  Sorry but the Pho was not authentic tasting. Hardly any beef in it as well. Spring rolls were dissapointing too. We were really hoping it would be good so that we didn't have to drive downtown but it wasn't even close to being decent to try again. :-(

So much pressure, so I ordered like 4 sandwiches.  Appreciate the fact that the meat was thinly sliced.  Thick salty meat tends to give me a headache.  The sandwich bread wasn't soaked which is good for keeping one in the fridge.  I went during rush hour (lunch time) and I didn't have to wait long.  The only thing I wish is that they had lunchtime combos.

We picked this spot to celebrate our anniversary. It was an excellent choice! The seafood was super fresh and wonderfully prepared, the sunset waterfront view was amazing, and service was smooth and spot on. Highly recommend this place. The atmosphere is not trendy but definitely sophisticated and overall the dining experience was great. I am still remembering the conch fritters appetizer...

As good as the ones in PARIS.... 
Interestingly enough the chef speaks spanish.,.,, 
My favorite foolproof bring visitors to Ybor place to go to...
Cafe au lait is amazing.  

Actual Creme de marrons crepes... wow.

Come to this place for the experience - not the food. 

From the beginning to the end, the staff took care of me and my group. When you get there, you have to enter through a pretty ornate front door - only after knocking or ringing the bell.

You're then brought to be seated in a comfortable, culturally decorated section of the restaurant, which has sectional rooms that are spread out through the restaurant's 4 floors.

Our belly dancer was fun, and the staff was friendly and very accommodating throughout our 3-hour  experience there.

As for the food (the only thing that kept me from giving them a 5 rating), it left much to be desired - and I believe was the cause of a day of stomach problems ( I won't be graphic) the following day.

Every meat placed in front of us was dry. Which came as a surprise because things were heavily seasoned. Throughout the courses, we were served plates of meats, breads, and veggies, many of which were both spicy and sweet - but not in a good way. I'd recommend trying the vegetarian options as it is a little more difficult to dry out vegetables.
They changed chefs and the food is slightly better. If you order the Michoacan tacos (pork), they ALMOST pass for a decent taco.

Hawthorne's can kiss my ass.

My friend made a reservation for 12 of us after the Broad Street Run. They told her they don't normally take reservations, but they'd make an exception and accomodate us since it was a special day. They took her credit card number down and we made a reservation for noon.

FIrst, they told her that the reservation would only be for an hour. What restaurant tells a party - regardless of size - who want to come and spend a large amount of money that they will have a limited time to eat? Personally, I'd have made reservations elsewhere at this point.

As tends to happen with large events like this, we wound up about 30 minutes behind. At 11:30, my friend called and said we would be getting there at 12:30, and they simply told her that would be no problem.

Shortly after noon, the rest of our party starts to show up and are told they can't be seated until everyone arrives. They also neglected to say on the phone (as we learned later from the friends who arrived) that if we got there at 12:30, they would still push us out at 1.

Despite what was said on the phone, when we arrive at 12:20, we're told they gave the tables away at 12:15, and there will be a wait to be seated - and we probably won't be seated togher. To make up for the inconvenience and complete lack of communication among the staff, we were offered some glasses of water.

We walked out and walked up to Marathon Grill at 10th and Walnut where they were very happy to seat us and have our business.

tl;dr: Took our reservation, said no problem when we called to say we'd be late, gave our tables away, told us we'd have to wait and wouldn't be seated together, offered us water

Nobody makes espresso like they do here.  I buy their coffee blends by the pound (Anthony's Blend, and Verona) every time I'm home to bring to school because as much as I love Starbucks and Au Bon Pain, they just aren't Anthony's.  The baristas are great at helping you figure out which blend is right for your palate.  

The cafe itself smells like heaven, and their gelato and cannolis are  delectable, as well.  They also sell homemade pizelles, which are quite possibly one of my favorite baked goods.  They are light and crispy, with the perfect touch of anise.

The only downside is parking( minus 1 star), but I just usually park at Rite Aid and get myself a soda or People (hey, I need a tabloid every once in a while).  If you're a coffee connoisseur, this will be your Mecca.

I'm a byo-aholic, and went here to celebrate a friend's engagement. It is located on South Street, but don't let that scare you as South Street has a reality check on Broad Street, so you're safe from Flash mobs, and cheesy tshirt stores and Duck Tours.

Pumpkin is a big name in that neighborhood, with a Cafe and a to-go store all in Grad-ho.

The space is small, but this cute spot is great for a special occasion. To be honest, none of the food really stood out or was very memorable, but the cozy, candle lit atomsphere was very comfortable and would tempt me to come back.

Hmm, I'm not sure I understand their student discount policies...  I went here to pick up supplies I needed for a class I am taking at Ai of Tucson and apparently the framer guy did not apply a student discount to my purchase.  When I returned to ask why I didn't receive one, another cashier told me that you would have to have a card and spend at least a hundred bucks to get discount applied.  What a fucking crock of shit.  How is a student discount being advertised to lure STUDENTS from a school going to effectively make them return to this place to buy even more supplies just to get a discount?  This is bullshit.  I think I will return to Sarnoff Art Supply from here on out.  Thanks so much, Posner's for fucking me over, but YES... you gave me the difference back, BUT... I still don't understand your silly discount plan.  Go to Sarnoff, everyone.

Came here with two friends and we each ordered 3 different burgers and split them into thirds so we can sample the burgers we selected. Honestly, is ok but not one of the best in Philly. It was pricey for a burger and it did not come with fries.

Originally I came here last night, but unfortunately the place is closed on Mondays.  Today I tried again, and at about 9pm the place was still busy (they close at 10).  The service is very friendly, helpful, and when the food's ready they bring it out to your table.  The menu on their website and in the actual restaurant has a lot of pictures which is very helpful especially if you're not familiar with the standard dishes.  I got the vegetarian dolsot bimbop which is probably the most frequently ordered item.  It comes in a sizzling hot pot, so that's always special.  The reason I am giving this place 3/5 is because what I ordered seems to be a classic, but it was not flavorful.  This dish is served with a fried egg on top, and at other places the egg is runny so it blends in with the food underneath, so this is what I was expecting, but the egg on this bimbop was fully cooked and did not mix with the food as well.  In addition to the seating room inside, there are tables outside which add to the fun atmosphere, but cars kept pulling up and idling right next to the tables.  So, this place has great service and atmosphere, but judging on tonight's dish alone, I'd come to eat here again only if a couple of the other Korean places I frequent in the city were closed.

I've only been here for some drinks and socializing. The atmosphere and prices make it the "hang out place for the rich/cool kids on campus." The atmosphere and vibe is not so much my style, too modern and sterile feeling. 

Lots of college kids come here to see and be seen.

A regular lunch spot for me.  I am a big fan of the vegan sandwich, as well as the tomato/mozz/olive tapenade sandwich. The option of "chips or salad" comes with your sandwich selection--I always get salad; diced cucumber, tomato and some lemon juice. All sandwiches can be pressed/warmed up in the panini machine.  

I enjoyed an amazing spinach salad with goat cheese, walnuts, dried apricots, prunes, cranberries and pesto dressing.

The hummus plate is large enough for two to share and comes with two very large pitas.  

Iced Green Tea is my staple in the warmer months and their cappuccino is warm and smooth.  The place can get loud and hectic (those folks behind the counter work their butts off!), but Ole is totally worth it.

This offers cheap, fast (once you get to the front of the line) vegetarian options if you're at Penn.  While it's not as good as some of the Middle Eastern trucks on my campus, it's decent and a good for a quick lunch when you need something that you can eat while walking.  If you're into seitan, they have it here, along with the requisite falafel.

Alright, so I've been back here a couple of times. 

Each time has been about the same; try to find a table on a Friday or Saturday night after having passed the bathrooms at the front which smell weird. After having found a table, wait several minutes for a waitress to come by. Order drinks/food, get said drinks/food, wait for waitress to come by at the end of the night to collect payment. 

Two nights ago, I went here with my friend who can't get enough of their Scorpion drinks. The thing about those drinks is that I'm not sure if she likes the way they taste or the fact that they come in glasses as big as your head. They have a single serving one, but she just can't get enough of the big ones (har har). She ordered one and we pretended to share it since I can't drink anymore. This wasn't made known to me until she'd started drinking away and I asked why she kept pushing the glass in front of both of us when she took a drink. They only allow the big ones to be ordered for two people. 

She also found out after ordering two that there's a limit: TWO. Now, I agree with them that this is a ton of alcohol (these drinks are pretty strong) and there should be a limit, but after you've ordered (and presumably drank) the two, you can order single servings of other alcoholic drinks. She got a mai tai which seemed to have just as much liquor as the Scorpions. After drinking 1 and a half of the Scorpions-For-Two, she snuck drinks from our other friend's single person Scorpion, a few of the Mai Tai she then spilled everywhere. I hope not everyone drinks like this here or they have a big mess on their hands. 

I also noted that a couple had come in and were sitting near us. It's not a big deal, but as someone who wasn't interested in killing off my remaining braincells with rum, I took notice of them. They'd ordered drinks and got them, but they seemed to be waiting almost an hour or so before the waitress came by with the  food they'd ordered. I got the feeling the

I met up with some friends two days ago after I'd already eaten dinner a couple of hours beforehand. I wasn't hungry, but that doesn't mean that the smells of the food being eaten near me and the size of their menu didn't make me wonder if I couldn't just squeeze in a few more calories. So, we did.

My boyfriend and I shared a nachos appetizer because I let him pick and he's not adventurous. So, nachos it was. Our friend's had already started with drinks before we got there and raved about the place to us (we'd never been there). They ordered a chicken pasta and turkey sandwich which came with fries. This was an adventure of itself as the last time they were there, my friend's dad was there and ordered this specific sandwich. Out of laziness or really believing this was a rare delicacy only thought up and asked for by his father, my friend called his dad on behalf of his girlfriend (my friend) to find out what was on that sandwich. Turns out it was just a club. 

Anyways, the food took awhile (they were busy as it was 7pm on a Saturday), but the drinks stayed full by our waitress who kept stopping by to check on us. The food came and their's looked really good, the few fries I was forced to eat by my friend who couldn't help but share were really good. Our nachos? Eh. I've had better at an Applebees or even Taco Bell, but it did look fancy. I picked everything off of the few that I ate and just nibbled the overly salted chips. For nearly $10, I would have expected better. And with a menu as long as their's I'm sure that won't be a problem next time. 

When we were all done, the waitress stopped by and shoved what looked like a dessert menu in my hand. No one else, just me. I thought was not only strange, but rude as I was in the middle of telling a story when she stopped by and hadn't even realized what happened until I looked down and had to tell her No Thanks. Just a little creepy.

The person who said this place is loud was dead on. Our friend kept yelling everything he was saying which would have seemed a little rude, but this place is pretty noisy. 

Three stars.

Okay, I returned for a second visit.  The lady who was working there was super super super super friendly and attentive to my needs.  I asked her if I would receive a student discount since I'm attending an art school, she clarified that I would.  It just so happened that the first visit was a mistake on another employee's part.  Anyhow... I will definitely return to this store to buy more supplies if needed.  Again, the woman was super friendly!

I heard of this place from my friend and thought I had to go since I love Thai food. 
My boyfriend and I got here a little earlier so we did not have a touble finding a parking spot, however all the food stands were not ready yet.  So we decided to check out the temple and how they were praying.  The temple reminded me of the time I went to Bangkok.  Thai people love to decorate their temples with a lot of colors and shining gold.  

Now let's go get some food!!  Most of the meals they have are $5 and desserts are $3.  I think some of the sales go to the fund of this place.  It is definitely cash only.  We had a noodle soup and dumplings and fried bananas.  Second time I went here I think I ordered a green curry.  

Everything is alright.  But please remember they are all food stands so do not expect restaurant quality food from this place.   What's fun about this place is that you see a lot of people and food, and get to eat your food outside on a picnic table.  There are not too many Thai temples where they hold a Sunday morning market.  It is definitely a place you should check out once!
Love this place. Been coming here since it opened up as I am a ex t-loc. From just grabbing drinks with friends to eating dinner this is a fun and chic place to frequent.

Great pizza and a fantastic place for lunch.  The people here are really friendly and big soccer fans so props for that!  I probably stop by here at least once a week for lunch as I work in the area.  The decor is very simple, but is very clean and welcoming as opposed to some other pizza joints on South street.

The thin crust pizzas are heated up in the pizza oven and taste really good and fresh.  My favorite is the spinach, tomato, and ricotta pizza, which really hits the spot and makes my afternoon hum by. 

I've had delivery from here which I was a little disappointed by, but I think if you eat in or carry out, you'll be fine.  I think thin crust pizza just needs to be eaten fresh, or it's not quite the same.

You are kidding me! An hour wait, no opportunity to make a reservation for later in the day. AND no seating to wait.  
So what? You wait for an hour for the table, you wait for the wait staff to come to your table, you wait for your food, you wait for them to remember that you are at your table to bring you your check..
This is supposed to be a service industry, not a cattle herding industry.

AJ'S is heaven for those of us like me who could spend hours upon hours at the grocery store wandering in and out of aisles. Located in the La Encantada shopping center, this is a place where grocery store meets restaurant all in a chic setting with dark lighting provides a sophisticated ambiance. AJ's sets itself apart from any other grocery store I have ever been to. Pricey? Oh yes. Do not go here to do your typical shopping. Instead I love coming here to grab lunch (either from the deli, the outside grill, or the sushi bar) or to pick up imports which most grocers do not carry. The bakery selection is wonderful, it's hard to come here and not buy a pastry and some large juicy chocolate covered strawberries. I also enjoy the coffee selection AJ's has to offer; only time I've enjoyed home brewed coffee more than the coffee house drinks. if you can get over the high-end prices, AJ's will become a favorite of yours as well. Now if only there was one in San Diego...

Having been away from Philly for awhile, I don't know all the good local places, so my family picked Bistro Romano for my birthday. I'm glad they did. I can't imagine how there are any bad reviews of this place- to start, its old city charm and coziness perfectly match the charm of the surrounding neighborhood. We had outstanding service- not rushed but attentive. The artichoke appetizer was excellent, and we all enjoyed the tableside-prepared Caesar salad. The table had a mix of veal and pasta for entrees and everyone enjoyed their dinners. My veal saltimbocca wasn't the best veal i've ever tasted, but it was very good- simple but flavorful and well cooked. We also enjoyed our desserts, and it's always nice when things are made in house. I would give Bistro Romano an extra half star if I could. It was classically good fresh food in a nice atmosphere with good service. I'd definitely go back if I were in town again.

I stopped by for a quick lunch at Bobby's Burger Palace while paying a quick weekend visit to Philly with my girlfriend. All of the burgers are pretty reasonably priced (generally in the range of $6-8 or so), and there's plenty of variety in terms of the kind of burger you can get. I ended up getting the LA Burger, while my girlfriend went for the Bobby Blue Burger. We also ordered a side of sweet potato fries and a milkshake as well. It was pretty packed for a Saturday afternoon; we weren't able to get a seat at the bar, but we ended up scoring a seat at the end of a table.

Service was very quick despite the crowd - we got our burgers a little over 5 minutes after sitting down. The milkshake was great - not too heavy, but still tasty as the classic. The fries also surprised me as well...I've had sweet potato fries, and generally I'm put off by the 'sweetness' of the fries. These were more fried and tasted great with one of the many ketchup assortments that were available. As for the burger itself - it was delicious. I have been a hamburger guy my entire life (I detest cheeseburgers), but even with cheddar melted all over the pattie, I still enjoyed the burger immensely. The avocado relish added that unique avocado flavor I'm a fan of, and a mixture of the chipotle ketchup and the burger sauce really brought out the flavor of the entire sandwich. My girlfriend was a huge fan of the Bobby Blue; I thought there was a bit too much Blue Cheese on it, but it was still very delicious as well.

So why only 4 stars? Simply put, I honestly feel like I could create this burger if I took a regular pattie and added the accompanying condiments and toppings. Unlike Shake Shack, where you can really taste the flavor in the burger itself without adding ketchup or the Shack Sauce, I thought this was a rather ordinary burger that could be easily replicated. Maybe it's wrong to hold it to a higher standard, but the restaurant is run by a freakin' Iron Chef - I expect to be blown away, even if it's just a burger joint! The meal itself was fantastic, and I'd definitely come back once in a while for a sit-down lunch (it's a bit heavy, so don't plan on doing anything athletic after chowing down). But the real value-add here is the variety of toppings you can add to the burger...just not the burger itself.

Just a disclaimer first: I hate Bobby Flay. I find him obnoxious, he is indeed my most loathed TV personality and that's without exaggeration. When he comes on TV, I usually change the channel but when he opened up a new Burger Palace in my city, I made it my business to go check it out and see it the pomposity was warranted. I know, it's only a burger joint, not like an actual restaurant that he actually cooks in, but still...

It's understandable that a trendy burger joint opened by an American Iron Chef in University City will be mobbed. The line usually wrap around the building on  weekends and there's about half an hour wait. So me and a friend gave up once, but returned by myself on a week night when school's out and only had to wait about 10 minutes to purchase and got a table.

Since I'm not a big fan of Goat Cheese, I opted for a medium well Dallas Burger (Spice Crusted / Coleslaw / Monterey Jack / BBQ Sauce / Pickles), an order of fries and a pistachio milkshake. On every table, there is a condiment rack loaded with salt & pepper, ketchup, yellow mustard, Chipotle ketchup, Jalapeno hot sauce and a burger sauce. 

Since there wasn't many people, I got my food within 5 minutes which I was surprised. The burger is meaty, well-proportioned and it tasted good, certainly not the best burger I've ever had but it was pretty satisfying. The fries comes with a BBP fry sauce that tasted a little bit like an aioli, though both the fries and the sauce were quite salty. The star of the whole show was the milkshake and reading through the other comments, it seems like everybody liked the milkshakes. 

I do like the trendy decor, though I would have loved to have some utensils with me so I can scoop up the missed coleslaw on my plate. The price seems to be reasonable. I'm sure a lot of people would love a vegetarian option. At the end, I think it was decent, but I suffered from MSG attacks halfway home and I have tasted better burgers in half the other places. And no, my meal in general didn't give me any clues on why Bobby Flay is so full of himself on TV.

I have been here twice, the prices are inexpensive, but the decor downstairs reminds me more of a pizzeria than a sushi place.  The food itself is slightly above average.  Not my first choice for sushi.

Traditionally, when asked to sit at a restaurant, one should expect that a waiter or similar entity should approach within a few minutes in order to get drink orders or answer questions.  This did not occur.  We were seated and then ignored until we approached the register to place our order.  Honestly, this isn't a big deal but I figure it is worth mentioning as an addendum to the other service complaints.

Their mango juice is pretty darn good and their chicken sish taouk sandwich is large and satisfying.  It was a little light on the sauce but that is forgiveable.  Sahara Grill is a fine place to go for a relatively inexpensive, decent lunch in the area.  If you have someone visiting from out of town and they say, "I want a lunch that's just like other mediterranean places I'm used to.  I don't like when people get too zany with their cooking" then this is the place for them!

On the action-movie-parallel-satisfaction scale, Sahara Grill would be Arnold Schwarzenegger's Collateral Damage.

I've been here a couple of times, most recently with my girlfriend last night for dinner. She's a huge fan of the place - says it's the best Chinese food in Philly Chinatown, and having been there a couple of times I'm inclined to agree it's definitely one of the best. It's not the biggest restaurant in town, so you may have to share one of the bigger tables with other people or wait for a spot to open up if it's crowded. Luckily, I've gotten a seat right away, and the waiters are very good at bringing out the food promptly as it's ready and making sure you have tea and water handy. I'm a fan of that, because a good number of Chinese restaurants I go don't bother with the water unless requested.

The sauteed clams in black bean sauce are very, very delicious. The sauce adds a very nice marinade to the clams, and it works well to eat them with rice. Last night, for some reason, they didn't do it in black bean sauce and instead altered the dish, unrequested, by adding very ground-up pork. It was okay, but the sauce really brings out the flavor in the clams. The other problem was that some of the shells chipped, and I ended up getting chunks of the shell in with the pork. The fish maw soup is also really good; it's a light way to start off a meal without filling you up. The dumplings are decent, if not memorable, but their lobster is extremely delicious. It's the first time, surprisingly enough, that I've gotten lobster to eat as a standalone entree, and was it good. It was very fresh and succulent, and my only complaint is that we didn't get a bigger lobster!

All in all, this is a nice little spot to grab a meal, and as noted the service is pretty good compared to other Chinese restaurants I've been to in the past. The clams are a must-try, and they seem to do seafood in general quite well.

This is halfway between a cafeteria and a sit-down restaurant.  There is decent seating and your food is brought out to your table but you order and pay at the counter.  There should probably be a sign stipulating that all orders are taken at the register otherwise they might wonder why there are customers sitting at tables looking around with confused looks.

Foodwise, this was just okay.  I got the chicken taouk platter, which came with salad, rice, pita, grilled vegetables, and 1 kebab for ~$13, including tax.  The chicken was adequately spiced but could have used a bit more sauce.  The rice was topped with some almonds, and was pretty good.  Salad was very bland and tasted like it had merely been spritzed with a tiny amount of lemon juice and olive oil and a scattering of spices - definitely could have used more of actual dressing.  The pita was...well, a pita, although I think that it would have gone nicely with some hummus.  The portion size is pretty small, and probably wouldn't have filled anyone besides me up.  Maybe if they added a kebab?

The restaurant itself is very clean, with an open kitchen, and the overall experience was adequate for a quick, inexpensive lunch place.  It's good for those of you searching for a traditional, middle eastern lunch without any twists.

Cantaloupe Martini. Absolute love in a glass.

They also serve the famous Scorpion bowls... 

A dark, hole in the wall with some funky decorations. Not a trendy spot so it's a hit or miss crowd but that is why I like it. A good place to enjoy the company of those you came with. This is also a restaurant but I recommend only going for drinks.

I made my family to go there after reading reviews on Yelp. They were not very hungry in the first place and not impressed by the way the restaurant looked. We had one 5-year old picky eater, one whining a pre-teen, and one-year-old who had her snack right before so that (we were hoping-this trick usually works well in other places)she doesn't bother us much. Should have known better, because she kept eating everything she could reach! So much to say about the food that  kids cannot stop eating! I was the only one excited about the place at the beginning, but by the end of the meal EVERYONE was in great mood. Even pre-teen got nice and happy, and picky eater ate every single bit we put on her plate - 3 times.

 We ordered Salt and Pepper Tofu, Green and Black Beans Chicken, 2 kinds of soups ( Seaweed one was the best), XO Grouper, Wine Chicken and Orange chicken. They don't bring each plate to the person who ordered it, they rather put all of them in the middle of the table and provide empty plates in front of everyone, so that, using the large spoons provided, each can put any amount of anything on his or her own plate. So, pretty much everything is served the way appetizers are normally served in American restaurants. Note, that portions are very large.

Make sure you check the specials board located by the register - those offers are not even on the menu. We ordered our Green and Black Beans Chicken from it and my husband said that this is something he is going to order every time he comes here from now on.

The service was great, I don't know why so many people commented about how poor it is. I prepared for the worst in that department, but was surprised to realize that it;s quite the contrary. Everyone was very attentive, quick and friendly. They didn't even mind the baby who was loud and messy, not even  the gloomy pre-teen, they smiled to both of them and tried to entertain them (successfully). The waitress didn't even hesitate when the 5-year old was placing an order and told her that the only things she wanted were macaroni n' cheese or chicken nuggets. She replied with an offer of orange chicken nuggets and was granted a consent. Orange chicken turned out to be beautiful and delicious and our daughter said this is the only kind of chicken nuggets she wants to eat from now on.

No dessert on the menu, but if you ask, the will provide one. We, however, chose the boba tea in the tea room right  next to this restaurant. 

Our bill turned out to be little more then $50 and we felt like leaving a good tip. Will definitely be back

This is truly a wonderful example of real Italian food.  Growing up in New York, I believe I am a great critic of the way Italian food should taste.  Everything is fresh and tasty.  Service is great.  The only problem you might have is the noise level.  If you go with a large crowd you can hardly hear what is said.  Aside from that, it is a real treat.  We took our daughter's in-laws there from Texas and they flipped over the food.  Scordato did it again.

If you're into big hunks of meat this is really the spot for you.  Perfectly cooked steak and excellent wine.  It's not really great for a date spot just the level of music/noise.  I thought the strawberry cheesecake was the best I had.

I was in the area and chose Jack's above others based on several Yelp reviews.  What a mistake apparently.
I sat at the bar with a friend instead of taking a table.  We were presented with a dinner & bar menu by the bartender.  We were surprised by the dinner entree prices for this area, many of which were close to $30 each.  
Based on the Yelp reviews we chose to start off with the Mussels and I also chose the soup du jour Broccoli Cheese.  One Yelper described the Mussels as "best in city," obviously this person never had the mussels at Eulogy or Monk's(Eulogy get the nod).  They were decent but nowhere near that echelon of greatness.  The chorizo in them was very good though.  The cheese soup, because the broccoli was scarce, was very tasty.  It had good flavor.
Next course I ordered the BBQ Brisket sandwich and my friend ordered the Portabella sandwich.  
A Yelper prasied the Brisket sand as "amazing" and the Portabella as "very good tasting, but bready."  Well, one was right.  The Portabella was definitely bready.  The brisket sandwich bordered awful.  I couldn't even tell the quality or taste of the meat because of the super strong BBQ sauce flavor.  It was so overwhelming.  While the Portabella was very bready as warned it did taste ok with a strong cheesy, pesto flavoring.  They should use a thinner bread for this sandwich.
The fries for each sandwich I thought were pretty good but my friend didn't care for them much.  They are on the crispy, almost burnt side but with good flavor.  
Even the Wayerbacher and Flying Fish Summer Ale couldn't save this meal.
Although the atmosphere is nice in there, last night they had the whole front open on a chilly night.  I had to eat with my jacket on the whole time.
Also, I read about the Southern biscuits and jam served with dinner and got excited about it especially cause across the bar I could see someone eating them.  The nice, but a little strange bartender neglected to bring them to us. Bummer.
Overall, if it had not been for the terrific company I was with, this was a pretty disappointing experience for me.  Considering the amount of restaurants in this area I won't be heading back here anytime soon.
Now I wish I had taken a shot at the Urban Saloon right next door.  Shoulda did the Eeny, meeny, miny, mo thing...

Fogo De Chao is great, but if you don't really care about atmospher and only care about the MEAT....then this place is must go! For less than half of the price than Fogo, you can stuff yourself with delicious steak! oh yeah....never a good idea to take a girl to all you can eat meat! lol
Saw this place on food channel and decided to try out.  Their Veal and Mussels were SO GOOD!...down side was they didn't take credit card...CASH only...but they do have ATM inside...lol

Just crossed off another Philadelphia "must-do" - the food cart. 

After wandering around center city amidst the wafting smells of cheese steaks, falafel, and Chinese food, my roommate and I decided on the Mediterranean food cart on the corner of 20th and market which, at prime lunch time, was encircled by an army of hungry professionals.

And for good reason. This guy is not messing around. Juicy, tender chicken on a bed of fresh greens, macaroni salad, sliced apples and topped with a few pieces of crispy fried falafel. 
To.
Die.
For. 

Oh and the man behind it all--a riot.  From his fully adorned food cart lined with garlic cloves and hanging plants, the blaring traditional Greek music, and the constant reminder that "you eat this food, you'll never get sick" --what a character.

Food portions were huge too, definitely enough food to split with a friend which makes the semi-hefty $13 price tag worth it.  In any event, the meal was freaking awesome. Three hours later and I'm prettttty sure I'm still in a food coma.

Cynthia is the best! I am super lazy and my hair is generally uncooperative. After a brief consultation with Cynthia, she identified what I was generally looking for but kicked it up a notch or two. I let her work her magic and I now have a awesome low maintenance cut that not only frames my face beautifully but broke me out of the boring. She is genuinely excited about her profession and takes serious pride in her work. She definitely should since her skill and technique are flawless with the end results as proof. I have not gotten so many compliments on my hair in a long time. Why are you still reading this?! Make an appointment with her already! You won't regret it!
