It is fu-fu for sure! My parents would rather get a steak next door but when my wife and I dine alone its North for sure!

A new discovery in awesomeness for the Philadelphia summer nightlife scene; for just ten dollars a lawn ticket can be purchased to the Mann's Orchestral BYOB picnic jamboree. Apparently whenever the orchestra plays at the Mann you are allowed to bring in lawn chairs no bigger than 28", any food you feel inclined to eat, and any drink you might have a thirst for, alcohol included. We went to a performance of Tchaikovsky followed by a fireworks show last night and were quite pleased, all things considered. 

To begin I suppose I should describe the layout of the venue. The performance area is situated at the bottom of a bowl like hill, with an artfully constructed wooden building over and around three sides of the stage to funnel the sound outwards and up. Right against the dais is a large area of seats, and behind these seats is the main lawn seating area with the ideal concert real estate. This general admittance zone extends to the top of the hill where it is hemmed in by a tree line. If you stand on this main hill facing the stage, there is a large wing to the left with levels of extra lawn seating for bigger events, and then a fence to it's left . While this extra seating does not afford any real view of the concert, the view of the city skyline that can be seen from there is breathtakingly spectacular. 

If you were standing on this extra seating hill, at the top is a tented restaurant area. On the side of the tent farthest from the stage is another small patch of grass ringed on two sides by tent walls, and then on the third by a chain link fence with dumpsters beyond; This is where I was able to find open grass to sit, and the only view was of an abandoned factory. I know what you are thinking: "How can he give this place five stars when standing outside the property line would have let him hear more musical notes than where he sat?" (during the 1812 overture we listened to the dishwasher's clattering silverware in lieu of the cannons) The reason is because I arrived to an eight o'clock concert at 8:20 and understand I got what I deserve, so next time I will be much earlier. Plus, I can recognize greatness even when I prevent myself from experiencing it (the far corner was a big party anyway, so there was some consolation). 

P.S.- We may have been in the absolute worst possible seats on the grounds for the concert, but when the fireworks went off afterwords they were directly overhead. Suck on that, front row center. 

-Also, biting bugs were out in force.

After my first visit to the The Loft (an offbeat theatre with the best popcorn in Tucson), I bought an annual membership.  I love foreign films, and they show the best of them, as well as indies and other excellent films that you don't see elsewhere.

The Troc will always be one of my favorite concert venues in the city, and I came back recently for the first time in 5 years because one of my co-workers was performing with his band in a $10,000 showdown (which unfortunately they did not end up winning *tear*).  The acoustics were still as good as I remember them being; however, there was a whole lot of moshing going on, mainly by teenage kids there to see some of the other bands (who were nowhere near as good and absolutely nothing like the band I was there to see), and that shocked me at first.  Since it was so crowded when I saw Hanson perform there 5 years ago I barely noticed the ledge on which one could stand or sit during the performance, but a few of my other co-workers and I watched the performance from there.

The Lawn.
The music.
The picnic basket.
The Summer.

The traffic jam getting outta there.
The bugspray (bring some)

The Mann.

Pro: I loved the atmosphere of Rincon Market, and I enjoyed the reasonably priced sandwiches and baked goods.

Con: Other than what seemed to be the main guy in the deli (where they make the cold sandwiches) who was very personable and helpful, every other worker there was at best coolly polite, and at worst snotty.

This place is really nice if u want to get away without going far. Nature lovers will enjoy a stroll thru this serene "  forest in the hood ". I'm taken away every time I go.. And quickly brought back to reality when I leave - since the projects are right down the street. Don't worry though ( u scardy cats ) no one will mess with you.  Cool place tho- check it out.

I went there with my mum and the experience was horrible. 

We were the only one there and it took them 20 minutes to come to us and when they gave us our water, there was a booger on the side of the cup. 

Me, I'm a patient woman so I just set the cup to the side with a smile, not wanting to make any trouble for them but when they sat my quail out, I became enraged. It was poorly cooked with the blood still inside it.

I loathe this place.

I love it here! The staff isn't all up in your face but approach you with a smile on their face and ask if you need anything and when you tell them no, they don't bother you anymore. 

The location is beautiful, right down from my favorite ice cream parlour - Scoop de Ville! But any way, I need to stop talking about food and focus on Sephora - the way the store presents itself is eye catching and they have everything I need without me looking around.  

My wallet goes boom when ever I'm in here but its worth it.

I love Ocean Prime!  It's consistent.  Everytime you go there, you get a great meal.  I love to go for lunch because the meals are much more reasonable then.  Their iced tea of the day is fabulous.  I always get their lump crab meat salad for lunch.  Ther chilean sea bass for dinner is fabulous!  If you want to try it out, go between 4-6pm for their happy hour.  They have great drinks and appetizers for great prices.

Sunday brunch=fabulous.  Lunch=very good.  Dinner=not a fan!  Their Sunday brunch is amazing.  I normally hate buffets, but theirs is really about quality, not quantity.  Oysters, sushi, shrimp cocktail, salads, drinks, dessert room, everything is fabulous.  Yes, it's expensive at $45, but since they include unlimited drinks, it's really worth it if you're going to have a few bloody mary's or mimosa's.  Their fish sandwich and tacos are good for lunch.  The ambiance is also very nice since it's next to the water.  This place would really be five stars, but I did not have a good experience with their dinner.  We all got fish entrees, and they were all dry and overcooked.  This is pretty bad for a seafood restaurant and considering the price point of Oystercatchers.  I'll never go back for dinner, but I'll definitely be back for lunch and brunch.

The pho here is standard and they definitely give you your money's worth! For $6 bucks you'll get a huge bowl (tho "huge" is just "regular" in the Vietnamese dictionary) of pho packed with whatever cow parts your little heart desires. For an extra dollar you get probably the biggest bowl of pho you've ever seen in Philly! Each bow comes with a plate of raw herbs and veggies including: bean sprouts, jalapenos, Thai basil, lime and cilantro-ish leaf. The restaurant has a cafeteria feel in that it's loud and full of people. The turnover rate here is quick since it takes a maximum of 1 minute from the time you order your pho to the time it's actually on your table. I always feel bad for the one friend who orders a rice dish here. Don't get me wrong, the rice dishes are great; however, since they take longer than a minute to get to your table, you can bet everyone has devoured their Pho before you've seen your dish. The tables are stocked with chopsticks, spoons, napkins, condiment dishes, paste-y Saracha and grounded Saracha, Hosin sauce, fish sauce and chilli oil. I love to eat my pho plain but most people like to add Hosin sauce. On the weekends they serve a beef noodle soup called "Bun Bo Huey". It has large chunks of beef, with thicker noodles in a lemongrass soup. It might be an acquired taste but I really enjoyed it and it's definately worth a try.

My god, what a pain in the ass it is to get there and back (at least for We The Carless), especially when the Mann Center Loop bus is running hyper-late... after waiting 20+ minutes,  I ended up jumping in a cab so that I could make it there before Spoon got back on the tour bus... a tragic waste of two beers' worth of money.

On the other hand, the surroundings are beautiful, we had a great view of the stage, and the sound quality was much more solid than I expected for an open-air venue - especially for a demanding act like Arcade Fire.

I was also impressed by the staff, who dealt with typical concert chaos (people blocking the aisles, climbing on the barricades between sections, etc.) without losing their shit. One guy in the row in front of us was disabled, and since he couldn't stand up for the show, he had a totally blocked view. To help him out, one of the event staff cleared out some of the people sitting immediately in front of him by promoting them to open seats in the front pit section... definitely a pretty rockin' thing to do.

And I managed to keep my warm fuzzies even while I watched the minutes ticking away in a packed bus in the parking lot after the show, so all in all, a good experience.

Awesome pizza! My only complaint is that the bottom of the pizza be cooked a little bit longer. The bottom was a little soggy and not crunchy. I loved the sauce. It has tons of flavor. There also wasn't too much cheese on it which is what I like. Overall I would get this pizza again. $13.50 for a pizza is pricey but its worth it. Update: I put slices of pizza in a toaster oven for 5 to 7 mins and it tasted even better!
Okay, how can anyone not fall in love with whipped cream sandwiched between freshly baked sponge cake?  I love the classic vanilla.  Yes, it will clog my arteries but that's what Lipitor is for.

I have been there twice now.  The first time was very good - I had the  house fried rice, which was a large, tasty portion, and a pork dish that was mildly spicy and good.  The place is frequented by locals and many appear to be of Chinese descent.  

My second visit was not nearly as good.  The house fried rice was okay, but had a little bit of an odd flavor.  I'm not sure whether it was in the spicing or maybe the oil used was not quite right.  The orange chicken was not good.  It was pretty heavy on the breading and had an off taste.  It wasn't a good, fresh, orangy taste.  I may give them another try, hoping last time was just off, but I really don't live with restaurants having too many off days. So, my three star rating splits the difference of 4 for the first visit and 2 for the second.

This place is still descent but is not as good as when it first opened. That said it is good pho for the far NE it is owned and run by non-Vietnamese so of course it is not authentic. I will come back here when I am too lazy to drive up to Adams to Pho 75.

Just a note that they are CASH ONLY - but they have an ATM right inside the door.

If you're looking for an actual Chicago style deep dish pizza pie then this is the place. I didn't say a thick crusted pizza that was made in a deep pan (although they make those too), I'm talking about a thin crust in a deep pan filled with ingredients (a pizza pie). The pizza is flippin awesome. The service is a little lacking though but not enough for me to take any stars away. The waiter did not want to list the beers that they have and was not going to provide a list either. There are two small chalk boards with the beers listed on them if you're interested. If you're seated outside then you will have to go inside to read them. This is also kind of unfortunate because they have a decent selection of craft brews. Anyway, go for the food and just know what you want. Questions may just slow things down.

I live in San Francisco but am from Tucson.  When I'm home I go to Raging Sage almost every day.  Their coffee and pastries are very good--this place could definitely hold its own in San Francisco.  I recommend the iced coffee, the awesome homemade chai, and whatever jam scone just came out of the oven.
Great lunch. Burrito with chorizo and beans was very filling and quite tasty. Fast service got us in and out in 45 minutes.

After reading these Yelp reviews, my boyfriend and I couldn't wait to try this place (we anxiously waited a week until it reopened after renovations). The food lived up to the hype...we each got Pad Thai and it was fresh, flavorful and a HUGE portion. I got the vegetarian ($8) and he got the chicken ($9) and after trying both, I'd opt for the chicken next time. Shrimp rolls (aka summer rolls) are delicious! BBQ chicken vermicelli is awesome! Service is quick, prices are great and food is excellent quality.

This was officially the best meal I have ever consumed. I went with two other people and we started off with the ceviche sampler, which was quite tasty. We went on the recommendation of our server, who was excellent. The mojitos and caipirinhas were delicious as well. Then we had the appetizer sampler - mindblowing! The oysters were absolutely amazing - I salivate when thinking about them. We were fairly full after the appetizer sampler, so we opted to split two entrees among the three of us - the beef tenderloin and the tuna. The tuna was incredible...perfectly cooked and seasoned, huge portion. We weren't thrilled about the tenderloin...the seasoning was a bit strong/weird. We didn't finish it. But overall, an amazing meal. Be prepared for a big check (over $200 for us), but it is well worth it for a special occasion.

Best. Pancakes. Ever. I have been dreaming about them since I first had them a week and a half ago..if only they weren't closed Sunday-Tuesday. For about $4 you get 3 huge pancakes, which can easily be split between two people. Lightning fast service, piping hot food, super cheap. All-around winner!

Nice atmosphere (very 60's), good food (not spectacular...just good), however, very good service (would be a 4 for this alone.)
Had the scallops as an appetizer and my husband had the walnut salad.   He had the lamb...which was very good... I had the lobster risoto. It was good...  just not great.
The waiter, Vance, was really nice and very accommodating.  He knew just when to come over and didn't hover.  It was a relaxing dinner experience .... 
I made the reservation on "OpenTable" and placed a comment that this dinner was for our anniversary.  Our waiter made sure to acknowledge this and offered his congrats...
I had the "Creme Brulee" for dessert... the pudding was wonderful and the sugar glaze on top was really good.  They brewed a fresh pot of coffee for us ...it was the perfect ending to our meal.
Since this establishment is near all of Academy of Music and other venues, I would definitely consider this restaurant when attending a show...

This is my favorite place to go for sushi. Even my picky eater kids will eat here and they actually love the unagi (eel)! The specialty rolls are what makes this bistro my favorite.  "Lobster Lotus" is a large roll made with lobster and then fried in tempura batter.  It's my favorite and I highly recommend it to the timid sushi virgins, as it's not too exotic. It's a bit pricey, but specialty rolls are generous in size.

Doing cleaning on the new home until 11 last night, there wasn't much open within walking distance.  This place was open.  It's cash only, so we had to go to CVS first and get some cash back, annoying.  Then, we go in and there is no a/c.  It's hot and humid.  The guy that runs it isn't too friendly, though oddly he's nice to the bums that come in for change.  Weird spot.  Pizza is actually OK, but don't bother unless you're just out of options.

Restaurant was in a beautiful location, right on the water. We decided to sit outside, which was nice. The service was fantastic! Sometimes I like to get recommendations from the staff and he suggested the chicken and waffles and the tuna. When I'm trying out something new, I want their specialities... I want to see what they're made of. We both ordered the chicken and waffles. It was actually really good! It was a big hunk of fried chicken and two thin "waffles" drizzled in honey, and some spicy collard greens. It was definitely filling and a good meal. We will definitely be going back!
Reliable food and extensive menu.  Good place to go if you need an extensive menu to keep everyone happy.

This place was amazing! Yeah there is a little bit of a wait, but so what? The food was amazing! Tiramisu was delicious too! It's one of my favorite places here in Tucson and I don't mind the wait. The restaurant is just simply having a great business!

1st week in philly and on a wed. night this place was packed. It is loud, but I appreciate it when they keep serving until 2am. We also ended up going to smith's and continental too. 

1) Cheap beer and friendly people. I was told people were on the younger side here, but I think it was a little older crowd than normal.. And older means nothing over 33 still. Since I'm still in that "younger" crowd, i didn't really care.

So, I think I will go back with friends and meet new ones.  But if you want to catch up with friends and have a real in-depth conversation- go else where b/c you won't be able to hear anything.
They recently expanded and now have a full bar. Along with that bar they have killer "snow ice". You know what I'm talking about...they stuff that Taco Bell used to have. It totally makes the drink.

This place use to be good.  When the economy took a hit, so did the flavors of this restaurant.  The last time I was there, I did complain to the waiter that the food isn't as good as it suppose to be.  Judging by the clientele, the food doesn't taste authentic anymore.  There wasn't any fish sauce on the table!!!  What pho joint doesn't have fish sauce on the table?

Koreana is: quick, easy, cheap & delicious. 

My friend lived right across the street from Koreana and I'm pretty sure this was the only place we ever frequented whenever I was in University City. 

I'm sure it was love at first sight- i walked in and smelled the lovely aroma of korean food, then my eyes landed on the pictures of the food and then i may have almost fainted after seeing how ridiculously cheap the prices were!

There were many-a-times I sat at my place in North Philly contemplating the drive to University City just for some Koreana and then I realized I could go to 5th street...but hey, the most important part is, I contemplated long and hard about it!

I favor the jam bong because I love me some spicy seafood noodles but everything else is also great!

The interior of the place is OK and the seating can be kind of limited if it's busy but otherwise- awesome.

Amazing. I brought by bf and it might be an understatement to say he wigged the fuck out over the place. Loved it. I think he might break up with me to date Sweet Freedom if he could. We've done the Tour de Vegan Bakeries in a few cities before, and Sweet Freedom shines ... not to mention their vegan-nutfree-gluten-free-idon'tknow what stance.

The obvious conclusion is that their goods are made of magic. Get a cookie sandwich and call it a day, until you come back an hour later for more.

In the 15 years I've been ordering delivery from here I have never been disappointed. There is something for everyone. Vegan, spice addict, picky kid. All bases covered! They have super fast delivery and always throw in something free.( My favorite freebie is the cold sesame noodles.) If you like your food spicy, you need to tell them as it leans toward the mild. I ask for extra curry in my Mei Fun house special and it turns out fabulous.

This is as good as it gets. There is no pizza in comparison to Tacconelli's. I'm 30 years old and my parents have been taking me here since I was 8 years old. I remember driving there from South Jersey and my mom telling me this was the best. Well, she was right. 

I'm so happy I live a few miles away from here now because I crave this pie! My all time favorite is the pepperoni but the Signature Pie (spinach and tomato) is beyond amazing, too. 

You must try this place bu don't forget to call ahead and reserve your dough! Yes, thats right, reserve your dough....otherwise you are outta luck.

My experiences at Oggi just keep getting better & better.
I went to see Cynthia today - mostly because she made @Susan G. look HAWT and I wanted to look HAWT, too.

Well, I wish I had a picture - b/c she just colored my hair a GORGEOUS (and volatile) shade of red and cut it to perfection.

She is a sweet & talented lady - and has been with Oggi off & on for 14 years.

Cass who shampooed (and I mean SCRUBBED) my color out was a doll and I wish her the best of luck with her career.

Can't wait to come back.  I spent just under $200 though - Ouch.
(Includes tips and a bottle of $13 shampoo.)

Cynthia - you ROCK!

Johnny's is a fun stop with a great drive up feel.  Try the Egg and Cheese and Hots (sausage) with fryed onions, for breakfast, it's a eye opener! 
Their Coffee is pretty bad for a Truck Stop type place but the grub is good stuff !

This is the best of the rest for both Pizza Etc. This is by no means just a Pizzeria! It has so much more including an up- scale, updated classy atmosphere. Great salads, appetizers, desserts, staff, & much more. Just as recently as last evening, four of us had  two different pies & three different salads at one of the supposedly best  brick oven pizzerias plus in the Tucson area. It could not compare to Vivace  The owner has taken Pizza to another level. Just talking about the food, as the facilities cannot be compared, It is far superior to the famous Blanco in downtown Phoenix which has absolutely no atmosphere & is rated the best.  Mr.Beard of the famous Beard awards, check this place out you will be astounded.

The frozen pineapple margaritas and the guacamole were the standouts for me at El Rey. Both the Chicken and Zucchini Blossoms & Corn Quesadillas were a little dry.  The Queso Fundido was (as ridiculous as it sounds) too cheesy - next time, I would cut it with mushrooms.  I would pick Starr's El Vez over his El Rey.

I have been here 3 times with 3 different size groups and the experience is different each time.   The first time, it was a very large group of 20 people.  We stuffed ourselves into the "private" room and the service was good for the number of people that was there on a Saturday night.  The waitress ran around like chickens without heads but they got all of our orders in and it came out in a reasonable time.

The second time, it was HORRIBLE.  There was 10 of us and it took forever to get our food.  It was busy but not so busy that they should be taking that long.  When we got our food, they were missing things like forks, spoons, refills, etc.  We asked several times and we asked several different waitress. It was so bad that the owner came out to kiss our butts a little. 

The third time, it wasn't so bad.  It was again a Saturday.  We got in before the evening rush.  The waitress was pleasant and she did a good job with helping us get what we really wanted.  I think we got better service this time because it was slow and the group was alot smaller... just the 3 of us.

The sushi here is good; I would give it a 4 star.  The reason why I give it a 3 overall... they always give us better service when there is a Korean in the group. ALWAYS.

Today was my first time there.  I don't know why I didn't hear about it before today.  It has great sandwiches - the tripleta on cuban bread is wonderful and different from the standard Cuban.  The Cuban sandwich is also excellent.  Both were hot pressed and just right.   The cafe con leche was perfect.  They also have full meals, which I will have to try.  It has a bit of a dive feel, but the food is first rate.  This place is going onto the regular lunch rotation.

Im impressed with China Yuan. They have grown a lot like Yummy House and without all the hassle. I easily walked in last night with a group of 7 and was sat immediately. 

I've finally started venturing into their live food and im in love with hong kong style anything. The crispy roast pork is just crazy good.

Had the spicy beef soup.  Was very tasty.  Service very friendly.  You order at the counter then drop your plates off by the trash on your way out, so not exactly fine dining, but good food and some of the cheapest Korean around.
I had two appetizers for dinner. I had the crab claws in the most amazing broth!!! The peel and eat shrimp.. Well it was peel and eat shrimp LOL

I have absolutely no complaints about this place. I mean. What more do you want guys? No, it's not the best pizza joint, but it's far from being the worst. I see a lot of the other yelpers say they've gone here in a drunken stupor and probably don't know if they were eating pizza or dog food but I am happy to report that the two times I went here, I was far from drunk. In fact, I wasn't even drinking. I was just up late at nite coming from this place or that place and hungry as hell. Tower style pizza (whatever the hell tower style is, well, the jury's still out on that one...) certainly satisfied the craving. Just stick to getting a good ol' plain slice or maybe one topping and you'll be good as gravy. I mean, at 2am you're just tryin to get rid of the hunger pangs, not get full, right? A slice or two will do ya good.
This place is a true Tampa landmark.  Their signature food is their Devil Stuffed Crab and Their stuffed potatoes!  An absolute must try.  Uploaded pic of potato, meal in itself.

Noisy, overpriced, and uninspired mediocre food, Not even the cheescake is as good as many other places. Service was only so so. Took forever for our meeet and greet and the pace didn not get better from there. Nice decor, but did I say it was NOISY.

Chima was delicious! The meats were fresh and I always got the temperature I requested. The lamb was a bit dry, but every other meat was incredible. The mashed potatoes were amazing...I don't know what they put in them, but they are addicting! Pao de Queijo (cheese bread) is always incredible. My only complaints were that we had a 7 o'clock reservation and we had to wait until 7:15 to be seated (even though there were many open tables). And our server asked us at least 8 times if we wanted something to drink. Otherwise, the service was good until we asked for a check (it tooks over 20 minutes to get) and the food was awesome! Be sure to do some Googling to find coupons before you go!

Taco Bus looks just like you would expect it to. Originally there was a bus. It served tacos. Nowadays, it also has a building expansion added to it for seated dining. 

Came here on a Friday night for a bite before a night of drinking and general shenanigans. 

I had a burrito stuffed with conchinita pibil (seasoned pork), which would put any of the big burrito chains to shame. This thing was huge, flavorful, and a meal unto itself. Probably too much for a pre-drinking meal, but good god would it be amazing during the wee hours of the morning (open 24 hours!). Given, the part of town that the original Taco Bus is located isn't the ideal place you'd want to find yourself at 3-4am. Consider yourself warned. 

Also had a few sips of their hibiscus lemonade. Talk about pucker! Very sweet and very tart, it's enjoyable for those who want an instant sugar rush. Or need to be resuscitated. 

All in all, the Taco Bus is a great experience. If you're a Mexican food lover, give this place a spin. 

Enjoy!

LARGE selection of beer.  If you find a good one, you'll pay for it.  This place is pretty expensive.  The worst part is that it is not efficient.  You go to the coolers to try and find what beer you want, but you can't really look because the space is so small that everyone else is asking you to move so they can get in there, too.  Also, the line tends to wrap from the cashier directly in front of the coolers....so you've got a small space as it is, then a line, then all those still trying to browse.  I found it annoying overall.

I've been to Chabaa Thai Bistro several times and it is by far my favorite restaurant in Manayunk. Also, you can't beat that its a BYOB. I've had the appetizer sampler, the soft shell crab, the shrimp pad thai and the home-made pineapple ice cream for desert and all were delicious. I'm not sure how many BYOs there are in the Manayunk/Roxborough area, but this one is definitely the best.

My husband and I double dated with another couple and went to Ocean Prime during Tampa's Restaurant week. From first impressions, they do try to make you feel very VIP, from the red carpet at the front door and waterfall greeting and of course, the somewhat dismissive and too cool for you girls at the reception. The restaurant is beautiful, simply lovely to look at. It's divided into areas for bar area, lounge and main dining room. The bar is area is nice because the windows roll up in beautiful weather. The lounge features live piano music and attractive bartenders mixing cocktails with dry ice for drama. Wine list was a bit disappointing. In reviewing it, it seemed to echo quite a few bottles that are standard issue at Costco, just marked up alot more. The dining room is modern nautical, not unlike Mitchell's Fish Market which is Ocean Prime's younger, more inexpensive sister restaurant 2 miles down the street. We ordered 5 appetizers, 3 entrees and 2 desserts. The appetizers included a house salad, lobster bisque, calamari, surf and turf (beef shortribs and seared scallops) and wedge lettuce salad. I'm a big salad lover and loved my salad. My husband's lobster bisque was nice but could have had a more stronger lobster taste even though it was really delicate and smooth. Calamari and Surf/Turf appetizer were hands down the winners. If you love Asian inspired cooking you will adore the calamari (light texture, spicy, and plain yummy). If we weren't in such pretentious atmosphere the guys might have duked it out for licking privileges for the empty plate. Entrees were pistachio crusted salmon, filet mignon and grilled pork steak. Loved my salmon but feel I could have ordered the same thing and paid less at Mitchells Fish Market. Steak was great. Grilled pork chop basically set a new standard for how GOOD pork could be. They used some sort of black chili rub that was amazing. Pork is my favorite meat but I never knew it could be THAT good. Our table's main complaint was that at these prices, the sides were quite skimpy. I had 6 snap peas with my Salmon. At 30 dollars for the dish, a few more could have been doled out. Desserts were 2 orders of blueberry cheesecake. The feedback was that it was heavy cheesecake but still good. I can't say as I'm not a cheesecake person but will say I appreciated the beautiful presentation, creme fraiche and sugared blueberries. It's served as individual round cakes versus a wedge cut from a larger cake. It was among many of the little things they do in this restaurant to say, "Special just for you!" Although overpriced, I can say the food was wonderful. It may be a new favorite, with or without the pretensions. Service is definitely up there with some of Tampa's best restaurants. Fellow diners were dressed anywhere from tank top and jeans to business men in suits.

Once again the ladies of 13th Street have done a great job in maintaining their high quality of food and excellence.  This new addition to their already incredible businesses is FAB!!!  The food is delish - Marcie has done another outstanding job of bringing her flavors to the table.  We went opening night and were  so thrilled to see a full house.   Valerie  was there to greet us with her beautiful smile as usual.  I am so amazed how these two ladies continue to do what they do.  The newest project is always the last until the next surprise is around the corner.  

They are a wonderful part of the neighborhood and they continue to maintain a high standard for those of us who deserve it.

Keep up the good work ladies
Awesome food and great dessert. The food was hot and delicious down to the last bite. The dessert was great especially for the kids and I to share.
Very friendly, reasonably priced, and very good.  My first time trying scrapple!

I just came here with my boyfriend yesterday after trying a couple of times before. Once we came when it was closed on a sunday :( and once when breakfast was over. Other times it was way too crowded to wait. Yesterday we came when it opened, at 8am! There were people there, but a line didn't form until about 15 mins after we arrived. My bf got the breakfast special, 2 huge blueberry pancakes, 2 strips of turkey bacon, and coffee. His turkey bacon looked like slices of spam fried (of course the texture wasn't spammy). I got the Western omelette which came with toast. The omelette was a lot bigger than I thought. The ham in it was basically just shaved deli meat that they didn't chop up and just put in the middle. I didn't mind .. it also had sliced tomatoes, onions and green peppers. Definitely worth the money. I couldn't finish it so good thing my bf was there to help me! I also got chocolate milk which was really thick chocolate milk.

Anyway it was definitely a good hearty, breakfast. We're planning to try the apple dumpling next time because we were too full to get it this time.

the apple dumpling is really good with whipped cream. I had it once and it was amazing, the second time, no so much. probably because i got heavy cream instead..

Blueberry pancakes are always a win, and apple french toast may be my next favorite. So much more to try from this place!!!

OMG! I love this place!! Great hospitality by the owners Nephew and good fresh food!!! Now this is what it's ALL about! 
I go once a week.

All precooked ready to serve. It's ok if your into Applebee's and TGIFridays. 
Way over priced. You will pay for ambiance.
Names after a commie? I went once to meet friends but I won't go back again. I'm too much of a patriot.
Great atmosphere! So so food but I am a fine dining Hispanic with a great pallet. I won't go back unless it's for drinks and chips at the back bar.

Soho Sushi is in my top 3 in the Tampa Bay area.  The Sushi is 5 Stars, but the wait was a bit too long during my last visit.  And we had to stand all cramped by the front door.  Yes sometimes the wait is cool when you are waiting on a great meal.   I also felt as if the waiter didn't really care about his tables.  Young guy.  When someone serves my dinner, from driving thru a drive thru at Mc Donalds to a nice restaurant, I want my server to acknowledge me.  Some eye contact would be great.  Don't run off so fast before you check to insure we have everything and we are pleased.  Our server just wasn't working for us during the last visit.  But back to the Sushi.. Wonderful*****. Worth the wait.
I had a very uninspired chicken with pesto. It was quite tasteless. Just because it's French, people over-rate their crapes.

This was honestly the worst sandwich I have ever consumed. I got the lemongrass chicken and I couldn't finish it...it was that bad. I watched the woman heat up the chicken (pre-cooked) and it tasted exactly like canned chicken with mayo, and was still cold when she gave it to me. "Pickled vegetables" included a few carrots. AND I was completely overcharged, but the lady didn't speak enough English to understand. I was so disappointed...I had hoped this place would be like Baoguette in NYC, but no luck (and double the price). Never again will I step foot in here. Stay away

First time at Barbuzzo. Stopped in just for a cocktail, ended up....ordering the escarole (specials menu), margarita pizza, sardines and the bone marrow. Everything we ate was really good. The pizza crust wasgood, however got a little "soggy" toward the center, but not that bad. Sardines, were salty, a little crispy and pungent, excellent. The bone marrow was nice, I would have preferred the bone marrow just broiled with maybe some sea salt sprinkled ontop, Barbuzzo's version had perphaps a roasted pepper schmear ontop which was sweet but I think masked the savory beefy flavor of marrow. Don't get me wrong, it was very tasty, I just prefer bone marrow prepared plain and simple.  Bar tenders (John and Brady) were very friendly and poured some great drinks.  Will definitely be back soon!

Unbelievably disappointed. I had heard so much about this restaurant. We arrived as a group of 3 with a small child treating ourselves to a lunch here, as lunch is a great way to experience an expensive restaurant without paying too much. Like others, I will comment that service was unbelievably slow. We started out with an order of a dozen of their assorted oysters. After 30 minutes, they asked if we would prefer eating our entrees first or our oysters. Our entrees were ready but it would take another 10 minutes for the oysters. That was an odd thing to ask and I didn't understand why. It's an open kitchen and there were very few filled tables. 3 cooks should have been found the time to shuck at least one oyster. We opted for the entrees as we were all getting filled on our second order of bread. It took 40 minutes for them to bring a kids cup of water for my son. I ordered a salad to be doubled up to be large enough for an entree. OK and definitely not spectacular arugula salad. 98% arugula and the rest was slices of pear, walnut crumbles and cheese. For what was essentially 15 dollars, I could have gotten a great "wow I wish I knew how to make this at home" salad at many other similiarly priced restaurants in Tampa. The other two diners I was with received an overcooked Wagyu burger and an Ok grouper sandwich. Oysters came out last and although we ate it, it frightened one member in our group because they were so warm. They were only ok oysters even if they were from NewEngland and Canada. For their name they should be serving spectacular oysters. Each adult had one order of alcoholic beverage and the meal came to 100 dollars. I don't mind paying alot for a good meal but I have spent just as much having dinner at any of the other great restaurants in Tampa and left with better service and better prepared food: food that blows your mind. When you are charging these prices, you are also promising a commensurate level of food quality and trained staff. We found neither. While the service staff are always present, it feels disorganized and undertrained. On a side note, as a Mom who enjoys eating out with her two kids, I find that the Kids Menu at many restaurants is a reflection of the management and the chef's attitudes towards food. Rarely have I ever seen such JUNK on a kids Menu: Hot dog and chips, PB&J and fries, Mac N Cheese and chips, etc. for 8-10 dollars??? You may argue that the restaurants are not catering towards kids but I would argue that if you serve junk, then your belief is that people don't know any better.

im giving it 5 stars because this is the hood i grew up in. i do not drink beers, but would hang in here all the time in my early 20's never knowing it would turn out to be such a go-to spot now. it makes me proud that something good came out of the hood. 
my sister was in town and wanted to go there since we could walk from my moms house. she PROMISED me i would see someone i knew from our past. she was wrong! but , what i did see was couples date night, with outsiders who made this their destination spot. that makes me happy. my sister, her husband and her 2 friends tried MANY beers. and they were happy with all of their selections. the service was great. i stick to old faithful, vodka n club soda. too bad for me, they did not carry my drug of choice, kettle one. but i aint hatin. i hear they have food, but we got there at midnight. so food was a nogo. i want people from all over the philly area to continue to hang here. chickies is what it is, a big corp machine now. not the corner bar it was when we were growing up. this is just a small biz with big ideas that worked. support the lil man!

Went with a group of friends, the food was so so, my dish was served cool should have been hot, and I was last to be served after everyone else ate. Seems like a gringo place to go for out of towers. I really don't see the draw here for the crowds. There are much better Mexican restaurants in Tucson than this.

Staggering amounts of delicious Mediterranean food on super inexpensive price-fixe menu served in an intimate, romantic venue.  It's not a place you can go back to over and over again, because the menu is very limited.  But it is a place that you must go to if you live in Philadelphia for any amount of time.
The tomato pie from this establishment is pretty good, kinda like Jeff Daniels' acting.  Everything else I've tried there is not as good as the tomato pie.

Wow, talk about a workout!  This walk in intense and thankfully I had someone with me who let me stop multiple times.
I walked up it panting the whole time however got to the top in 38 minutes! I liked it quite a bit its definitely a great walk and could see myself being one of the ones who try to do this multiple times a week!
Today my legs feel great and I feel good!
So long story short its beautiful, scenic, and definitely worth going!
I'm taking my friends who are coming to town to visit! So I can't wait!

A fine mid-level Italian dinner once again.  I stopped by with the wife last night for dinner.  This was our second dinner there and I've also visited for lunch three or four times.  Free parking can really set the tone for a good experience.  It was a smart move by LaScala's to make a deal with the garage literally directly above them. 
 
It was busy but our Open Table reservation was honored right on time.  Our waiter (Dan) stopped by immediately to take our drink order.  He was a little slow once refilling our water but other than that he was great.
A quick note on seating; LaScala's has a busy bar and there are tables near it that can get quite noisy.  Go for the tables near the front window.  Much quieter.

Philadelphia is loaded with Italian restaurants.  At the top for most folks is Vetri and way way down at the bottom is the infamous Olive garden.  LaScala's is right in the middle both in quality and price.  I had a broccoli rabe and sausage appetizer that was just fine.  A ricotta tortellini entrée was also great.  Three varieties of warm bread accompanied the meal.  Other reviewers have commented on the portions sizes.  They truly are huge.  Expect to bring home a giant doggie bag.

To me rating any restaurant is about its ability to meet expectations.  I expect much more from a steak at Mortons than I do from one at Outback.  LaScala's gets 4 stars as a mid-level Italian eatery.

We are big supporters of the Mummers - and my little girl just loves the parade - especially the comics.

I am partial to the Fancy Brigades, myself.

Anyhow, this SMALL museum (kind of a glorified practice hall) is a once-must-see Philadelphia experience.  Neat.
Outstanding! Excellent salad bar, high quality meats, and a superb caipirinha ...Worth every penny.

I'm very sorry to give this place a bad review, but dinner last night was one thing after another... 

We didn't get a table until  over an hour past our reservation time, the bartender had short term memory loss and couldn't multi-task, waiter was absent, warm sides were served cold and they F'ed up desert.  It was the hubby's bday and when I made the reservation, the hostess said they would bring out a dessert and sing - that never happened.  

The salad bar at Fogo is better.  Can't personally say anything about the meats (I don't eat meat so I stuck to the salad bar) but the hubby enjoyed it.      

The servers spent a great deal of time on what looked like iPhones - I really hope it was a high-tech drink ordering system.  

Sorry to say but I doubt we will return.  For the price we paid we would have had a better dining experience elsewhere.

I stopped here to get some baked goods as a gift for friends that I was visiting  out of town.  I got a mixed dozen of chocolate chip and oatmeal cookies.  My friends really enjoyed the cookies, in fact they joked that they would have to pay me to come visit more often so I can bring them gluten-free baked goods.  I didn't actually try any of the cookies myself but my friend who can only eat gluten-free products told me many times that they were really good.

Erica is my colorist at Adolf Beicker and she's friendly, spunky and knows how to work with her clients to get a color they want and provides a relaxing experience.

I went from having blonde chunky highlights to an all-over dark brunette. The color coverage is even and beautiful. She listens to her clients, giving them what they want, while making sure they make choices that are flattering for them.

I recommend Erica any day!
went for lunch for the indian buffet the service is lacking but not bad for a buffet.

Wow. Wow. Wow. I am sorry, I can't formulate complete and thoughful sentences because I am still shocked by how awesome my food was last night. The hubs and I went to Barbuzzo for Restaurant Week and were incredibly pleased that the RW menu was very close to the actual one. The food was amazing. I started with the faza bean crostini (more of an amuse bouche), then the beet salad (unreal!), then the bronzino (starting to get full) and ended with the salted caramel bundino. Husband had the chicken liver crostini (not for me), the sheeps milk ricotta (unreal), the pizza (he was in heaven) and the tiramisu. I think the next time we go, we will just order the salted caramel bundino and 9 servings of it. I kid, but it is THAT good. Our server, Megan, was so nice and the patrons next to us (pretty close quarters) said they came particulary for the salted caramel bundino. Barbuzzo is cozy, reasonably priced and quite honestly, in contention for my new favorite place in the city. Also, I love that they take reservations. I mean, hello? What a fabulous idea!

Went here for dinner at 9:54

Interior: Small place but clean. Maybe 10 tables at max.

Food: Food wasn't too bad. We ordered the salt baked squid since most people said it was good. It wasn't. We also ordered Yeung Chow Fried Rice (their Cha-Siu is REALLY bad. tastes plasticky), Black bean clams (not bad), Ong choi with Ha Jeung (not bad), Dow Miu (not bad), and their fried tofu with shrimp on top (also not bad). Food was okay...

Service: Pretty good. Kept calling my dad "Pretty Boy". That was kinda awkward

Overall: 3.5 stars.

No parking so walk.

Lovely! Comfortable, casual atmosphere, great prices, and wonderful food. I have never been disappointed by Taqueria Juanitos. As someone else mentioned, there is a little more gristle on the carne asada than I prefer, but for $1.50 or so apiece, you are definitely getting what you pay for. Additionally, the restaurant is very clean, has a number of different hot sauces available on the tables as well as 2 different homemade ones, and plenty of seating and parking.

Unbelievable! Absolutely the best Chicago-style sandwich shop in town. I will drive past 2 Luke's locations to eat here, and when my sister and brother-in-law come back to town for a visit, they demand to be taken there at least once. 

Killer Italian beef sandwiches, great Chicago dogs, fried snacks galore, no-nonsense counter service, and the best fries in town. Plus, you can always catch a Blackhawks game there during the hockey season. This place is made of win. Did I mention the prices? CHEAP!
Great artwork, good liquor selection fabulous jukebox, and some of the best bartenders in town. $1 PBR and High Life, $2.50 well drinks, and always a good pour.
Super price, super great view, super weird waitstaff. Just go for drinks. Food was trying, but just not there yet. Just go for drinks. Nice and elegant atmosphere Just go for drinks.

Worth it for the ambiance alone - dark, private booths, wood and stained glass everywhere, and most importantly, good food. I have always gotten good service here, even when I've come on holidays. Note: they use a special holiday menu on busy nights. The to-go location next door is also a good option when they're busy if you live in the area. Great date place.

My wife and I didn't feel like cooking dinner at home on a Saturday night so we decided to go for dinner at Bollywood café before heading out for the evening. We arrived there at 8:30 pm.

 My wife ordered Tandoori Chicken (Indian Style) and I ordered Seekh Kebab Platter cooked Indian style. After waiting for approximately 40 minutes, the waiter bought us our order. He got us two orders of Chicken Seekh Kebab Platters. Completely different from what we had ordered. Chicken Seekh Kebab was very dried. Definitely not cooked to my expectations and absolutely not Indian Style. I told the waiter that I had ordered Seekh Kebobs made of either beef or goat meat (their menu never states that the Seekh Kebobs are chicken). At this time I was hungry and frustrated. At 10:00 pm I told the waiter that I want it wrapped to go, as I have to be at another place by 11 pm. I had no plans to spend rest of my Saturday night in Bollywood café watching Indian movie and I could have done it home at my leisure. Finally at 10:30 pm, I got my order and left the Bollywood Café. I did let the owner by the name of Raj know about it. This is my very first experience at this restaurant and hopefully the LAST. I was told by Raj that they don't make Beef Seekh Kebab and the chef had made a special 'Goat" Seekh Kebab for me. Have their complicated menu revised and specify what the item is made of. And, if they call Bollywood Café as a fast food restaurant than it is not because this café does not fit the category of a fast food restaurant. Honestly speaking if Chef Ramsey would have been there, he would have fired the chef. It is definitely not worth eating. I had to go to fast food restaurant before my wife and I head out for Saturday late night out. What a waste of time!!

I read the reviews of this restaurant, which were not good from various people who had eaten there, but I was not convinced. Now I am. I would rate this place not more than one star.

If I were to judge this place on it's food alone I'd give it a 4 but dining is more than just the food isnt it? On the plus side, fresh ingredients and a fun approach to making the bowl your bowl, just the way you like it. Nice variety on the menu but lose the photos.The server was really friendly but inept. My date ordered Pho with beef and was served beef and tripe. She's not a tripe fan. Not a cardnal sin but then again, it's not that hard to get orders right. On the minus side, total lack of any decor. It's stark and without any real personality. I was a bit put off in that the desserts are obviously bought in. The server gave me what appeared to be the dessert companies catalog with product numbers; and asked me to select one. Needless to say, I passed. Including 2 very nice appitizers, three of us dined for $39.00 which I felt was a fair price.

Rincon Market is kind of all over the place. It's a market, a salad bar, a coffee place, a restaurant, a deli, a bakery, and a few other things. They have a good beer selection (on bottles, you can't drink anything here) but have a bar adjacent to the place (Bob Dobb's). Like any neighborhood market, the selection of grocery items is limited and it's a little higher than, say, Safeway down the street, but that's not really what it's about.
When I lived closer and worked at UA, I would come here for dinner quite a bit, usually either for their dinner special or for a salad. The salad bar is reasonably priced when compared to other places, and it's usually pretty fresh, although the evening leaves a lot to be desired. I used to pay about $6, I think.
Weekends bring in the big crowds, mainly for the breakfast, which is really the best part of this place. $5 gets you a "farmers breakfast," which is scrambled eggs, 2 sausage links or bacon, potatoes with veggies (squash, zucchini, onions and red pepper), and a cinnamon roll or croissant. All of it is made in-house. Or you can go for the grill stuff, like eggs benedict, make your own omelet, or Belgian waffles. That runs around $6-7.
The coffee here is actually really, really good too. I used to stop by here in the mornings just to get some. They have a few different types that you can choose from, varying in roast and flavor, and actually have more than one decaf variety available (you don't see that much). Coffee is cheap too: $1.29 + tax.
The bakery is really the other section that, I think, shines the most. They have fantastic cupcakes, cakes, cookies, brownies, and other goodies. I would say they're one of the better bakeries in town.
The atmosphere is kind of that of a cafeteria or a food court. The tables are crammed together on one side of the center bar where the bakery and lunch counters are, while the other side, away from the windows, is a little more open, but also darker and less inviting.
They added free wifi fairly recently, which makes it not a bad choice to hang out at, although there are very, very few plugs available.
Parking is free. There is a private lot on the east side of the building, accessed from both 6th Street and Tucson Blvd., but you can also parallel park right out front.
I think this is a real gem and, if every neighborhood had a place like this, Tucson would be all the better of a town. Rincon Market, in that respect, makes Sam Hughes all the more desirable to live in.

Oh yeah, also they will wait a certain amount of time before bringing out the first pizza.  This amount of time varies depending on the night but can be easily defined by the following rule:

"Your first Tacconelli's pizza will be brought out at the exact moment in which you truly lose hope that your pizza will come out."

But seriously, it is the best pizza ever.  If you go with a party of 4, order 3 pies: 1 Margherita, 1 Peppers and onions, and one white pie with no other toppings.  This is the pizza trifecta and if you don't like it then you are wrong.

Ella's is Ella's, its not trying to be hip or packed it just does its own thing. Its finally gotten to where you can sit outside and the temperature is bearable. 

Ive ordered just about everything on the menu and its pretty consistently good. The Asian wings weren't my cup of tea and the hamburger is boring but they tell you that =)

Besides those 2 items everything ive had is above average. Its nice to finally get some places in Tampa with individuality that are trying different approaches with food and location.

If you havent tried Ella's on Sundays you need to.

Hinge is like a diamond in the rough.  Most people go to south philly or northern liberties for a good brunch, but the secret is that the best place to get brunch is in port richmond.  A close friend told me about hinge for months before I went the first time....I was in disbelief that anything good could really exist on this stretch of road...boy was I wrong.  Amazing coffee, food reasonable prices and NO WAIT.  Plus a great atmosphere and good service - and amazing specials.  

Seriously, if you haven't tried this place out you really should.  Totally worth it.
Getting a to go pepperoni pizza!! Can't wait to rush this home and eat it!

I've been eating here for years - this place has been consistently good. All three salsas are awesome, the queso suizo (basically, melted white cheese in a pan) is totally worth the heart attack it will surely induce if eaten regularly, and the bistek tacos are incredible. Also worth a try are the nopalitos campesinos (diced pork with cactus), pretty much anything served in a table grill and the fajitas. A+ margaritas, too! They serve everything on handmade tortillas, which are amazing. The only thing I haven't enjoyed here are the chimichangas and burritos, but you can get those anywhere. Unless you go on a Friday or Saturday night, there's usually not a wait, either - the place is huge!

Went to M restaurant on a dinner date with the bf.  The outdoor courtyard is adorable. It's easy to walk by and not notice, but if you take the time to go inside its got a great atmosphere. Looked like it was very friendly towards groups.  The corn polenta is one of the most delicious dishes I've had in the city. I was so pumped after having it, it set a high standard for my entree. Yum! Getting hungry thinking about it!

Met here for a lunch date with one of my friends.  I didn't want to come back but she insisted that the food has gotten better.  The food is still the same.  It is very Americanized and the flavors aren't as bold and balanced as it is suppose to be.

We did order the make your own spring rolls which was good.  The meat was seasoned well.  The portions was generous.  We shared the bun bo Hue, which wasn't pungent as it is suppose to be.

I am optimistic about Gavin's - an Argentine-owned Euro-style cafe, right near Taney Park and sorely needed.

The original Irish Bar (from years ago now serves as the coffee bar.

We had some cold drinks here yesterday and I got a home-made beef empanada that was so delicious.  The salad menu sounds great, too.

This is sorely needed in this area and I hope they will be successful.

Go, Gavin's!

I have just ordered food from Brushfire BBQ for the last time.
The brisket is ok, but often very fatty, yet dry at the same time. It seems that they cook it without any sauce, and then put whatever sauce you choose on it afterward - generally on an overcooked, dried out roll.
The "garlic toast" is a joke. Where is the garlic?
The mac & cheese is frightening.
The potatoes are tiny & dried out.
This place is NOT cheap - a 1/4 lb. sandwich is $7. So are the chili cheese potato and the chili cheese fries. You can get better quality at f'ing Eegees for WAY less.
7 out of 10 times we have ordered for delivery, they have forgotten an item, or screwed something up. At these prices, check the order before you put it in the bag.
I will not be talked into ordering from this place again.

Great bakery. Great.
Good salad bar - used to be great, not too sure what's going on with that - I was there on Friday at 5:30, and it was hardly stocked. At dinnertime. Get on it, people. Still, good prices and fresh ingredients, and at least 12 dressing options.
The deli is decent, but not like it was a few years ago. The cases look... empty. 
This place always had such nice employees - where did they go? They now seem bored and/or annoyed. Maybe this is why there were only two tables in there on a Friday night, when the place used to be full.

*Disclaimer* - we went here for drinks, not food, so this is not a review of the cuisine at North.
So, drinks at North. Suck. Are overpriced, served by morons, and poorly poured. 
On the night we were there the place wasn't busy, and the staff started closing up an hour early around us, while rolling their eyes and sighing, even though we were there for the tail end of an engagement party, during which our friends had racked up a huge tab and tipped well.
One bonus star for the one REALLY nice waitress and super clean dining area and bathrooms. Otherwise, a one-star experience.
Hire some smarter, more customer-service oriented people, North.

I guess I should have known that a place called The Cheesecake Factory would serve cheesecake that tasted like it came from a factory. My bad. But if your "thing" is cheesecake, you should probably not be serving crap out of a box for $8 a slice. Just sayin'.

Some of the best commercialized pizza that you're going to get in Center City!  Don't go here if you're in a rush.  Everything is made to order and takes a hot second.  But it is worth every second!  It costs more than other local pizza places...but again...it's worth every penny!  I can't recommend them highly enough!

A haiku to profess my love for barbuzzo:

Sheeps milk ricotta
Salted caramel budino
Return visit soon.

Seriously, this restaurant will be high in my must-oft-return list. 
the dishes that i had were good, the presentation is quite nice, the space is friendly, accessible and tasty wine and drink list, and i love the counter where i can watch the cooks at work.

they need to work a little on the temp of their wood burning pizza oven.  and please stop charging for bread.  

simple elegant food.  the ladies of 13th st have done it again.

Stopped in to grab a sandwich to go for lunch. 9:40 on a Tuesday morning - not too crowded and some people enjoying their coffee. Wanted a chicken salad sandwich but they either didn't have the deli salad ready or were out. Instead I selected the "Gobbler" sandwich - it was fantastic. The sandwich had a delicious split personality: part traditional deli turkey sandwich with tomato, crisp cucumber, just the right amount of spicy mustard; and part Thanksgiving leftovers with a layer of stuffing, served on this really good cranberry bread. The only thing I would do differently next time is ask them to toast the bread. The pastries looked really delicious, so must go back!

I really enjoy the food at this cafe.  Salads are delicious, never had their wraps, but the pizza is definitely my favorite.  It's a tight fit once inside as they only have seating for a handful of people.  However, whenever I visit Philly, I do love to bring food back home.

The bread for salads and soups is AMAZING!  It's my absolute favorite.

Every time I walk into the Foodery, I'm excited by the well stocked beer selection, which I am confident has been handled with care to avoid any possibility of spoiling.    

Every time I walk out of the Foodery, I feel like I've been robbed in broad daylight in front of a police station.

Why did two bottles of Stillwater Ale cost almost TEN DOLLARS more at Foodery than at a competitor (ahem, Food & Friends) across town?  I get the convenience factor of the location, and the fact that Foodery started the trend, but come on.  Maybe the price gouging works on the clueless but I'll mix my six elsewhere.

This review is from a meal a few months ago.

My boyfriend was in town, and we did all the touristy things around the city. He looked through the guide book I had, and was looking at the restaurants in the area, and we happen to be on South St.. I was new to Philly so didnt know any of the restaurants, we came here because of the great blurb in the book, we get to the restaurant and see all there zagat awards on the front, and are thinking this is great, we are going to have some great Italian food. 

NOT the case. We go in, the place was empty besides one other table that was finishing up.  We may have gone at an odd time, really late lunch most likely. The waiter was nice, I want to say a bit slow for us being the only table their. Boyfriend ordered spaghetti and meatballs, I tasted some, that had to be the worst pasta sauce ever, and the pasta was overcooked. I don't remember what I had exactly, it was a chicken dish, again not good at all, chicken had no flavor, the sauce was strange. What really bothered me was the pasta sauce, the stuff you get from the jars tastes a million times better than what we were served. 

Obviously we had not read the yelp reviews before eating here, but looking through them I see really high reviews and really low ones (which echo my experience at the restaurant). My boyfriend has a theory that the true cooks were locked away somewhere unable to cook any good food that is the restaurants claim to fame. I have no desire to return to this restaurant and find out otherwise, and i would not dream of suggesting this place to anyone, I would suggest they go to Olive Garden before coming here or making their own pasta and adding some jarred pasta sauce, either would be a better food experience.

Food was good and lots of it. Service was prompt and friendly. Food was $54 for 3 people, which wasn't that expensive considering all we ate. Not sure why the other two posters here had a bad experience, but this place is good and I recommend it.

Great tacos, but the place wasn't very clean. The bathroom was dirty and the general overall feel of the restaurant was not very high quality. Better go to the location on Anderson, they actually care about the atmosphere.

Great little place. I love the patio area. It's nice even on a hot summer night, as they have fans outdoors. The servers are very attentive, the food is great, and the prices are pretty reasonable. This is definitely my new go-to Mexican spot!

Also, you must, must try the Enchiladas Miguel. My favorite!

This place is great. The costumer service is wonderful and the food is amazing. We had to wait for a long time to get our food is my only complaint but the atmosphere and food when it arrived were great. You should definitely try it. It's a BYOB so make sure to bring your favorite bottle of wine. Also, if you are a vegetarian, they will make any meal vegetarian for you which is amazing!

I always go out for Italian food on my birthday. It's my favorite. My parents usually take me to my restaurant of choice back in Detroit and I go bananas. This year, however, was my first birthday actually spent in Philly as a resident of Philly, meaning Mike was on birthday duty. I couldn't really decide where to go, but a love for Ralph's runs through his family, as it came highly recommended by his parents and was also a destination on his brother's bachelor party tour of Philadelphia (I see you, brother), so I wanted to see what the dilly was. Plus I really wanted eggplant parmesan.

We rolled up and were sat without a reservation relatively quickly. The guys working all had on Phillies t-shirts, which was cute. We started with the complimentary crusty bread and a simple tossed salad. Shortly thereafter, our spaghetti came. We took a few bites from our respective bowls and Mike says to me, he says "Is there meat in this sauce? Yeah, there is." I entered a brief state of panic, but then I asked the server about it and he used CONTEXT CLUES gave me the vegetarian sauce to accompany my vegetarian entree and Mike the meat sauce to accompany his meaty entree. How thoughtful. He was a really nice guy, on top of it. Quick, but not short. You can tell they care about service.

The food was delicious and eeeexactly what I wanted. Everything was excellent, down to the diet soda. The eggplant parm consisted of a pool of sauce and a shitload of cheese and breaded eggplant medallions (which I prefer to the usual eggplant slabs that end up soggy and worthless). Mike enjoyed his veal parm so much he couldn't really talk much, either.

It was a perfect birthday dinner. Delicious food, great service, and reasonably priced. Five stars.

My wife loved the Ceviche. Normally your seafood and veggies are swimming in tomato juice like the way Mariscos Chihuahua makes it but not this one. It was fresh and crunchy and the crab and shrimp were seasoned just right. 
The bartender was amazing! All you have to do is tell them you want stiff drinks and they deliver! Good job! Fat tip! 
Plus it was packed and fun!

Went in this past Sunday night and finally made it down the menu to their tortas. They've been consistently busy for the past 6 months or so, with only a table or two empty each time we've gone in. So, obviously, the word is getting out on this place.
The torta was good but, in all honesty, it was not the best I've ever had. It was also a little small compared to others I've had. The meat was fantastic though, of course. I had the pastor, which I think is the best choice for a torta. I also had a carne asada taco which, as always, was fantastic.
Per the service, I've figured it out, I think. When the waiter comes up and takes your drink orders, he/she will be back and, when they come back, you need to order then. Otherwise, you'll be waiting a little while until they come back again. Yeah, that's not much time, but that's just how it is. You'll also likely be asked at least twice by different people about what you want to drink. Don't take offense, they're just trying to be efficient. Food often comes out super-quick because they have most of the meats made already. I don't blame them on that one though, of course. I mean, have you ever asada-d your own carne? Takes a while.
I've also determined that they are hell-bent on not giving me corn tortillas for my tacos. Third time I've been given flour after asking. Hey, that's okay though.
Another weird thing: on this last visit, I noticed on the ticket that the server's listed name was "Mercado." Eh? Maybe just what they give all tickets.
Another cool thing: you get free refills on aguas frescas, including horchata. Damn, that stuff is sugary though. Get your insulin ready.
Torta + taco + large caramelo (that my companion ordered) = $11.40 w/ tax. Not bad at all. Plus, we were in there less than 30 minutes even though they were busy.
They are now doing a promotion on Thursday nights with $0.99 domestic beer.

2010 Review - Theme: MyX Rocks

Suckers bash Howl-O-Scream for not being as "creative," "scary," or "realistic" as Halloween Horror Nights... I say shut up and eat your admission ticket.

We're talking VALUE VALUE VALUE!!! Seriously, what you pay is what you get at both parks... and for the price you pay at HOS, you get a lot!!!! I went during it's third weekend of running on a Saturday. $30 for passholders (it's seriously not hard to find someone who'll let you borror their pass for the discount) and visited EVERY house, EVERY scarezone, and watched 2/3 shows. And duh... rode thrill rides.

Even for value, HOS is not a cheap wussy. Depending on your luckiness (or unluckiness) in the conga line winding through the houses, the scareactors can really make you jump... or at least, really disturb you. True, some of the effects can look like cheesy props from a Halloween store, but the overall effect can still be disturbing. Sadly, out of seven houses... 6 were repeats from last year with minor (if any) changes. The only new house (MyX Backstage) was very short and not enough scareactors.

The scarezones this year seemed understaffed. Not lots of scares from there... and for some reason each of those scareactors seemed weak or tired. (Except the werewolf area, those guys REALLY knew how to hide and jump at you)

HOS is broadway on crack in its shows. Talk about SEXY, funny, and entertaining. The return show "Fiends" was sexy with its nurses as usual and did soem little changes this year that made me like the show more than in the past (COUGH! Hot nurse at the end) while the other musical show at the Desert Grill was hilarious-- eventhough I couldn't understand their talking at some points-- it was still hilarious.

The houses and rides all had less than a 10-minute wait. ALWAYS visit in the 2nd-4th weekends of operation.

PYRA SAYS: Did I mention value???? For that price HOS is definitely a YES!!! Why would you pay $30 more to visit HHN and get the same ideas but with hour-long (or more) lines????

I have no idea how the food is.  The restaurant had a few customers, and the waitress promptly took our order.... and then disappeared.  I started to get annoyed about 20 minutes after we had ordered our margherita pie.  After 35 minutes, I went up to the counter and asked when our pizza might be ready.  I have no idea what the waitress mumbled as she walked past me without looking me in the eye, but that was enough for me, so we walked out.

I hadn't been to raging sage since 2005, up until a month ago.  I decided it was time to revisit the place.  And boy I am so glad I did.  I really LOVE their outdoor seating, it's a little small, but it great on cool days.  

The coffee is good! I have had better, but I gave them 5 stars for their scones!  I love the fresh fruit scones.  Whenever I go the scones are still warm, they have the just out of the oven taste.

Today I asked the clerk where they get them, she told me the bakery is out back in another building, and most of the employees bake them, and they change daily.

SOO YUMMY!
If it is a nice day, go here for lunch.  Amazing outdoor seating area and good food.

Love this place! The food was delicious and unqiue. The drinks, artfully made the old fashioned way. Sure, they're a little stingy on who they let in and can quickly turn a reservation into a "who??" but I loved it. From the rules signing to the private booths, and wow the jelly fish tanks. Truly unique, wonderful atmosphere, and great music.

This gym reminds me of family.  Some of the instructors are simply amazing.  Everyone is friendly from the desk at the Sports Center to staff members. The classes are amazing especially love Zumba and Dance Party.  I am a runner so these classes compliment my running.

The View was AMAZING...but the service was lacking. We were there on a Thursday night and there weren't that many tables sitting down and it seemed like our waitress was taking care of all of them. We were left waiting more then we were actually eating. Food was just okay. Would go back but only to drink in the lounge area...the cocktail waitress was great, wish she was our dinner waitress. Worth going for the view...

The best thing about Bahama Breeze is the atmosphere- the deck is not to be missed! Their service and drinks are great, too. However, their food is only so-so. Every dish I've gotten seems a little 'off', except the Crab Claws St. Thomas, which they unfortunately discontinued.

UPDATE: Crab Claws St. Thomas are back! This affords them another star in and of itself. They also have a few new dishes, most notably the grilled chicken with cilantro crema. SO. FREAKING. YUM.

1. Sangria was mostly ice
2. Waited RIDICULOUSLY LONG to get served (both times I've been there)
3. Food was cold

Three strikes, I'm out.

We have been to NoRTH before, but never had anything from their Italian menu. BIG MISTAKE!

I ordered the cavatelli. Now, I consider myself knowledgeable when reading a menu and usually know the ingredients. I thought I was ordering cavatappi. So when they brought my plate and I did not see squiggly pasta in my bowl I thought they brought the wrong thing. OK so, regardless of the shape, I figure pasta is pasta and dug in anyway. Any foodie will understand this. I took the first bite and you ask yourself, "How do they get SOOO much flavor in their food?" And this was pasta and sauce only, before I tasted one of the numerous chunks of sausage scattered throughout. Halfway through I told myself I could not eat any more but I kept eating anyway. I just couldn't stop. I resisted the urge to pick up the empty bowl and lick it dry. Last time I did that, I got a wicked glare from the wife. Needless to say, at this point, the shape of the pasta was all but forgotten about, not that it mattered that much in the first place.

My wife had their version of chicken parmesan and declared it "delicious". My daughter had the spaghetti and meatballs and proclaimed, "Sorry Dad, it's better than yours". I HAVE BEEN CHALLENGED. I MUST MAKE SPAGHETTI AND MEATBALLS IMMEDIATELY!

Service was good and the ambience is great.... at least I think. I was transported to another place with that bowl of pasta. Somewhere east, west or south of NoRTH. If you are smarter than me, you already know the difference between cavatelli and cavatappi. If you don't, google away!

Big up's to Carlos for suggesting the place and for being great company. It was my first time at the West Tampa Sandwich Shop and it won't be my last. Place is definitely hard to find. It located on Armenia Just south of where Howard intersects and is located on the west side of the road. I looks kinda like a house if the parking is full around the shop then there is a small lot just south of it on Armenia. 

Had the Chorizo sandwich which which was excellently pressed & the mango milk shake both were awesome on my way I out I had the cafe con leche which also good. 

A hole in the wall with a lot of local flavor. If it wasn't recommended I would've missed out...

Looking forward to more UYE in Tampa.....

Met Anish Z. (who is an pretty cool guy) here for the Yelp Tampa UYE today after our initial plans were shifted. We talked about some future ideas for meetups and about the Yelp scene in Miami-a cool thing to learn about.

West Tampa Sandwich Shop is pretty awesome. The aroma when you come in is heavenly. I ordered a steak sandwich, which was nice and juicy. The vegetables on it were fresh and the bread was pressed just right. The french fries were golden and cooked enough to be crispy, but still soft. I also tried their plantain chips, which were decent. I ordered a Mango shake, and although the waitress initially gave me the wrong order, she was really quick and kind about changing it back to the right one. The food wasn't too heavy, but still filling.

I love the atmosphere in the restaurant though. It's like you're dining at home (the place looks like a house after all both on the inside and outside). It's a true Spanish diner in every sense of the name. You can tell who is a local, a regular, and just visiting...and that's part of the cool factor here.

One of the nest of Tampa's chinese places... a small place that probably would have gone unnoticed but given word of mouth an yelp has grown to be packed most days... and yes you can actually find asians who eat here.... salt and pepper calamari gets my vote too

Good NY deli transplanted to south tampa... good beer selection for my beer drinking friend appreciated the updated looks. Parking blows... the blue cheese chips with its sweet hint were excellent and I don't even like blue cheese. I've had brunch, lunch and tried the lunch box where you sit and the datz chef prepares a prix fix meal for you. I wasn't  a fan of the lunch box because I just didn't like what was made... too much mustard and vinegar with the pork chop dish.   But every thing I've ordered off the menu has been a success so I know the food is good if on the pricy side for a sandwich. Will be back.

Solid italian food from the Scordato family, a long time Tucson family. THE best made from scratch italian sausage I had ever had. (Penne and Sausage.) Salmon was very good  but it needed more than rice with little bits of vegetables in it. The menu seemed weak on the hot vegetable sides.  Our waiter Michael Gonzalez was witty, a great deal of fun and an excellent server. Weekday lunch was crowded, which say something in the economy.

Pad thai is ok. Service is sometimes ok, sometimes awkward and uncomfortable. I definitely will not be going back there anytime soon after the waitress was making fun of one of the other customers to us. Very unprofessional.

My dad has been bringing me here for as long as I could remember. They have the best hoagies hands down in south philly... This is really a south philadelphia hoagie. The lady in there that looks like Sandra Bullock is the best hoagie maker
BBQ chicken pizza is delish!! A bit light on the chicken, but still very good. Garlic knots weren't so great...I wouldn't get those again. But the pizza was good and the price was quite reasonable!

I was pretty disappointed in this place...I heard good things, but didn't experience them. The burger didn't even fill the bun...I got a few pieces of cooked mushrooms and the "cooked" onions were nearly raw. My boyfriend got the pulled pork sandwich and I thought it was extremely salty. My glass of wine was very small, and wasn't even cold (I had to put ice in my Pinot Grigiot). I know there's something fun about looking at menus on chalkboards, but it isn't as fun when you have to stand over another table of people just to see it.

OK, Brace yourself.  It's gonna be a long review.
