3 of us went to R2L for an amazing dining experience last night.
And even though there were some problems (big ones) with service, the exquisite food more than made up for it.

Danny & Zaga - you "make your mamas proud".
That semi-open kitchen was running very nicely.  Your garnish chef, poultry man and line crew were in a good groove.

Daniel Stern's newest creation is in a gorgeous, dimly lit (and on a hoppin' Saturday night - very loud but still, surprisingly, comfortable) space.  The light fixtures, sculpture, bathrooms, stainless steel salt & pepper shakers (unused and unneeded) even the jacket of the wine list - all set off the gorgeous floor to ceiling views of the city.

Our lovely female bartender mixed us excellent drinks (brandy soaked cherries that are simply divine - I could eat a handful easily) and explained the menu while we waiting for our friend to arrive.

After we were seated our new and VERY nervous waiter (I think he had been there 3-4 days) explained even more.  We forgave him for using his notecards.

What was not so forgivable was the service snafu.

2 of the 3 of us ordered apps which were brought in short order after a delicious house-made bread.  The apps - which I truly recommend (those here with low food reviews - man, I think you may have ordered the wrong things) were the steak tartare (served in a rough chopped with plenty of shallots.  I ordered mine "not too spicy" and it was delicious.  Accompanied by crispy toasts with sort sort of butter (I think she said "beet butter" but it must have been golden beets as the color was a pale yellow).

My friend was not yet even finished his appetizer - crabcakes (which I did not try but he liked them and they looked great.) and his entree and side were brought out.

Then we sat.

And sat.

And finally said - "go ahead and eat it while it's still hot".  So he started to eat his (signature dish) Veal 5-6 ways.  So fabulous.  YOU MUST TRY DAN STERNS famous veal plate.  Accompanied by crisp butter-tossed broccoli florets.

So while he ate and my husband and I sat there and stared around - I said "This is a screw up". 

And, indeed it was.

First our (now petrified) young server came to apologize.  Next the manager came and said that our entrees were "3 minutes away" and apologized and said that they would do something "special" to make it up to us.

When our entrees did arrive - they were so worth the wait.  The filet accomanied by prociutto (I always spell that wrong) wrapped leeks (divine) just melted in your mouth - and the shortribs (want a little beef with your beef?) were good, too.

I opted to get an app as an entree - and had the unqiue -and uber-rich - lobster mac 'n cheese.  It was more like mezza-canelloni each topped/stuffed with small, buttery lobster medalions.  Served with it was a copious amount of rich, creamy cheese sauce - cheddar, gouda and something else.

WOW.  Don't miss the lobster mac!

To make up for the weird dinner service (AWKWARD!) they comped our desserts, cappucinos (delish - La Colombe)  and 2 of the 4 glasses of wine.

The pineapple cucumber sorbet was simply perfect.  Refreshing and "sang" on the palate.  Served over diced honeydew and plums.  The blackberry verjus sorbet was also good - but nothing compared to that Pineapple Cucumber.  It was capogiro worthy and obviously house made - served in perfect quenelles. (Can't wait to try the Kalamansi icecream and of course - who doesn't love a good gianduja?) Oh, my thighs!

My friend ordered the chocolate cream and apple pie (with a decadent sour cream quenelle beside it.

Also to make things "special they gave us an amuse-bouche pre-dessert of cinnamon panna cotta with a lovely rose-water sugar crysal topping and two picture-perfect fresh raspberries.

After dinner they served us each a crisp (not rock-hard) house-made cinnamon biscotti and - the best yet - buttery blueberry financiers (OK I can't spell that either).

KUDOS to your pastry chef.  He or she is a keeper.

Oh and Zaga is really a master - wow.

Such fun to watch y'all work.  If you can fix a few kinks in your service and have a more welcoming hostess stand - the 5 stars will stick!

Good work!  We can't wait to try your place in University City.

Oh - and they have a small private party room that seats 12 or so - and I hear a big one that seats about 100.  This would be a great wedding or rehearsal dinner setting.

And I bet plenty of marriage proposals will go down here.

Swanky, urban, romantic, high quality American classics with a BIG twist.

I think that they have changed owners because somehow the food didn't taste the same and the portions are smaller.  The triple delight was okay, but the yellow chive frog legs were a bit bland.  I didn't see any of the old, familiar workers, but maybe, hopefully, it was just their day off.

El Sur is on my rotation for lunch.  I work in the area and eat at El Sur at least once a week.  The food is excellent--the chicken enchiladas with green sauce lunch special is my favorite.

Its proximity to Davis Monthan AFB attracts many airmen.  Please show them your appreciation for their service by picking up their check on occasion -- I do.

Moist delicious Red Velvet cupcakes from the Cheescake Factory at AJ's with a cup of Illy coffee, a beautiful thing. Also, the most consistently friendly and helpful staff of any food store its size in Tucson. The bakery staff, yes, definitely, but also  the AJ's staff at large. (I was there for two specific things from the butcher counter, then wandered over toward baked goods. If you've been there before, you know there's only one way that ends; You're leaving with some sweet delicious thing.)

Half of menu is breakfast and the other half lunch. Watching other people eat, the portions are very large. We were seated immediately and the service was very prompt. Went with the egg white omelet with rosemary potatoes, a side of turkey bacon, fresh fruit and coffee while the dining compadre had three eggs scrambled with bacon and a side of quinoa porridge and tea. Fruit was very fresh and ripe. Coffee was LaColombe. English breakfast tea was seved in fancy mesh bag, but not very tasty. Turkey bacon was more ham-like than bacon-y. Actual bacon was thickly sliced, more chewy than crunchy. First time we had quinoa, which is a protein-packed grain, served wicked HOT with fruit, agave nectar and cream. Potatoes are roasted and not at all greasy.

Two stories of films, videos galore, free popcorn - what more could you ask for?!

This place is fantastic, and I'm pretty sure they've had every movie I've ever wanted to see and even a few I probably don't.  There is such a wide selection here - horror, foreign, drama, and genres I'd never heard of.

My only complaint was the staff member I like to call "The Popcorn Nazi."  They were unpleasantly out of popcorn, but a fellow customer noticed a stack of bags sitting on top of the popper.  When he went to grab a few bags for my friends and me, she flipped out!  Seriously, I understand there are rules to follow, but we are still customers.  What's worse, she had no real reason to yell!  It's just popcorn in tiny bags!

The food is good here.  Pizzas and calzones are super fresh and tasty.  The only reason I'm giving 3 stars is that the restaurant itself is way too chaotic.  They technically have 2 lines... one for express pick-up and the other for ordering.  The only problem is they kind of ignore you a little when you're in the express pick-up line.  You might as well wait in the long "order" line because they won't get to you until that line has cleared.

Next time I'll stick with delivery or eating in...otherwise, the food is definitely legit.

Absolutely the longest wait for any delivery ever.
UPS would have been faster.
The food is good, so that helps but I would avoid their delivery service like the plague.

I'm writing this review on behalf of my parents (foodies who don't use computers much).  Last night they made plans to go to Bistro Romano and then go to see a play.  They loved Bistro Romano so much that they blew off the play and just stayed in the restaurant all night. They called me this morning gushing about how great it is.  I have never heard my dad so excited about a place in Philly.   It's now their favorite place to eat in the city.

We just left Hawthorne. I had ordered a pumpkin beer which both my husband and I found vile. I sent it back. At the end of our meal, i saw that the beer was still on the check. The man ( i think the owner) explained that he couldn't take it off because the beer was perfectly good and it wasn't their fault that I didn't care for it. I asked what would happen if i ordered food that wasn't to my liking. he said in that case, they would make something else. But i was not offered another beer.
so i paid the $7.00
the shame is that for the cost of the beer, wholesale, he could have created good will and kept a customer happy. Now- I wont go back again. There is simply no reason to be so chintzy. It's a pity. I live in the neighborhood and have eaten there many times.

Brought the in-laws here for a special dinner on Friday night. Did I mention how much sense it makes that Barbuzzo takes reservations? Well, it really does. I am so happy to know that the service and food is still as awesome as when it first opened!

Ive tried most of the menu, but the Gaeng Dang and the Koong Ob Woonsen are EXCELLENT. The appetizers and desserts are really delicious ! The atmosphere is very relaxing. Good service as well. Highly recommended!!

The food here is better than Pho Quyen because they are consistent with their flavors... and you know it is good because Viet people eat there.

The banh mi is good.  It is the best in town!  It is a bit of a wait for them so I call in ahead to order if I want the grill meat sandwich.  The boss man up front will ignore you so you have to just start spitting out your order at him.  He is a typical Asian person... they will ignore you until they hear the sound of money and they will perk right up.  I haven't had the noodle dishes yet but I always see Viet people ordering them more than the banh mi, so I will assume they are good.

I'm here weekly but more because of the AMC than the stores. Used to be the high end mall until international plaza. Has some staple stores like Banana/Gap/Old Navy/Macy but if you're going for the shopping you're more likely to be happy with international plaza. If you're going to the AMC park in the garage on the 3rd floor though outside probably the most convenient place

Both the food and service at Barbuzzo were outstanding. Our server was over-the-top friendly and helpful and extremely knowledgeable about the menu. The restaurant is very small and tables are very close. Low lights and wood decor lends itself to a hip, relaxing ambiance. 

We shared the sheep's milk ricotta appetizer (server's recommendation), and then I had the pacherri and my friend had the ravioli.  The ricotta was salty and creamy, and great on the toasted bread.   I was hoping for more burratta in the pacherri, but it was rich and filling anyways. Ravioli was excellent as well. Portions are small and meant to be shared, but the pasta was filling and the perfect size. We shared the caramel budino for dessert - it was delicious and I do not like pudding type desserts at all. Cocktail list was small, but very interesting - as in I want to go back to try one!

I took my Los Angeles guest there a week ago and she is still talking about how good the food was. The menu is a mix of the best of the Mediterranean with Spanish, Portuguese, Italian, and French influences rustled up next to each other comfortably. You can order as much or as little as you like. The dining room was comfortable and modern Center City, close without being too cramped. Our service was good.

Among the two of us, we shared the charcuterie platter, uovo pizza, chicken liver pate, and the Calabrian greens. Everything was solid, comfortable, perfect. No new culinary boundaries are being pushed here but it's good food done right in a modern American setting for reasonable prices. The wine list is amazing. I love that they serve quartinos of wine so you don't have to choose between ordering too much (a bottle) or shooting your wallet by buying glasses of wine. This is a godsend especially if you're dining with someone who doesn't drink wine. It's incredibly civilized and a custom I wish other restaurants would copy in this town. The markups on the wine doesn't seem to be so crippling either. 

For dessert, we split the budino and it's just as good as my fellow Yelpers have said.

This restaurant isn't original in any ground-breaking way but it does what it does so well and it's such a comforable place that I will be returning here, either for dinner or just for a drink and a snack at the counter. They have everything I love. Let's face it, I've dined in a lot of fine restaurants but my favorite places are never the most famous. Go to Barbuzzo. You'll love it.

I don't know how a four star place can receive so many recent bad reviews, I guess it's a sign that the place is deteriorating but at the same time this place had been there for years and only garnered 54 reviews?

I have patronized the place years ago and when I returned last night, the place seemed a lot brighter than I remembered. My boyfriend and I arrived to an early Friday night crowd which pretty much packed the place. The place looks bright, colorful and clean. I loved the middle eastern music in the background. What's questionable is the service.

There seemed to be a waitress who is handling most of the restaurant and a waiter/manager who was handling our table and a few nearby but choose to disappear for most of the night. After waiting for the waiter to come take our order, I asked for water. After going through the ordered appetizers, we were still waiting. And after asking the waitress for it, she returned 10 minutes later with one glass, and it's not until we asked for a second glass and waited for 10 more minutes that she returned with it. Asking for the check was also a hassle and a waiting game. I considered leaving without paying and she will probably not noticed or too busy to chase us down. My boyfriend gave me this line for the review: "The service left us DESERTed at Sahara Grill." Ba Dum Ching!

Horrible service aside, the food here wasn't bad. Between the two of us, we shared a hummus, one order of falafel and shared a mixed grill platter. Flavor-wise, I've had way better hummus and the pita bread that comes with it is rather stale and tough. I've also had better falafels but it's really not at all bad. I would prefer them to be slightly crispier but I didn't mind it much. With the salad that comes with an entree, we were having fun making pita pockets with hummus and falafel. The mixed grill came with flavored rice, a few pieces of chicken shish taouk, a few pieces of beef shish kabob and a long piece of kafta kabob. The meats are all excellent, I especially like the chicken and the beef shish kabob. Juicy and well seasoned, can't ask for more than that. 

We ordered a mango with yogurt drink as well as a yaron yogurt drink. The yaron is salty and strong in yogurt flavors, it's a bit of an acquired taste even after adding some sugar. The mango yogurt is rich and if there's a do over, I probably won't add the yogurt to it, since the mango flavor ended up too diluted for my taste.

One great thing about this place though, it's that it is very affordable and I can see myself returning even knowing that the service was subpar. Honestly the waitress will need someone else to help her at the end of the night her hair was already all disheveled serving 20 tables on her own while her counterpart is nowhere to be found but then I was reminded that it's not my problem, whatever the reason is, bad service is bad service.

Barbuzzo was amazing. Being from an Italian family, I'm generally loath to spend a lot of money on Italian food, as I could make it myself, or better, go to my parents' house. But I have to say, Baruzzo was well worth the moderate-to-high price tag ($100 for the two of us, including tip).

We had reservations (which can be tough to get given how small the space is and the restaurant's popularity) for 8pm on a Saturday. It was jammed, but we were seated within about 7 minutes of arriving. 

My only complaint about the whole experience is that we were seated near the door, which provided an...invigorating blast of cold air whenever someone came in or out. Try to avoid sitting near the door in the colder months, if possible. 

Oh, ok, maybe one more complaint -- the tables are uber close together, such that it feels like you're having dinner with the people next to you. Luckily, the people next to us were either mute, reticent, or hated each other, so we could dominate the limited audio space. 

Anyway, once the drinks and food arrived, it was all positives. I usually don't order mixed drinks, but I got a sage and concord-grape drink, made with fresh concord grape puree -- so good. 

The food is served tapas style -- order 2 or 3 dishes per person, and they come out of the kitchen in random order. I highly recommend the cheese board -- it's the biggest cheese board I've seen in a while, and I order the cheese board everywhere. The pizzas are excellent, and so were the caciocavallo-stuffed meatballs. 

Service was sluggish at first, but settled into a good groove after a while. 

All in all, this is a really cool place, and I would def go back.

I've had better- but the service was excellent on even a weekday.
And this place was hopping at 8:30pm, but I really had high hopes for a perfect meal.

Food:
1) House Salad (I can't stand it when a rest. thinks ice berg lettuce is okay for a salad
2) Standard spaghetti/meatballs/standard red sauce- is this even homemade?- but I really wanted bolognese sauce- who doesn't have bolognese sauce in a mom/pop Italian rest!!!!!!
3) bruschetta- okay.. but it really wasn't memorable
 
Thoughts/FYI
1) I enjoyed my company and service (he was excellent and attentive more than anything-maybe because it was later in the night/a weekday)
2) Cash Only- that doesn't really bother me much since they do have an ATM inside. Although when I think of cash only rest., you assume the prices would be much cheaper- it's not.. a little higher than standard.

Overrated and a little let down.

A breakfast favorite of mine! Really nice waitresses and really good, homestyle cooking. You get a huge amount of food for not much money (think grilled cheese & fries for $4.50) and EVERYTHING is good! I love the belgian waffles and french toast, and the hash browns... none better in Tucson! They *do* have kind of bad coffee, but I know I'm hard to please in that area, so don't take my word for it - go to the Hungry Fox and try it for yourself!

Love this incredible find.  Been going here for a couple of years.  I am definitely a health conscious eater.  Mostly healthy foods.  However, I do have some vices and this surely is one of them.  The hot dogs just melt in mouth.  Try the carmelos as well.  Fun place to take out of town guests.

For the last few years I thought it was worth paying the extra to go the gym at the J -- supporting a good cause, and all... But the center TV in the workout area is ALWAYS on FOX News. I asked to please change it to something non-political.  Or put MSNBC on another one of the side TV's to give some balance. Asked staff several times and was told that FOX News had to be on all the time because it's what "the people'' want.  "What people?", says I.  I'm a person too, aren't I?  "Sorry.  Can't change it." Who says so?  The staff? The management? The members? Who is it who insists that they need to have Glenn Beck screaming at me while I try to work out?  Do they think my blood pressure isn't high enough already?  After 2 e-mails went unanswered and staff refused to change the channel -- repeatedly -- I quit and found a happy new workout home at Bally's for half the price, and a wide range of TV stations to choose from.  So much for my fantasies of a progressive, a-political, welcoming Jewish environment. Yes, they might have good day care -- can't comment on that.  But be damn sure they aren't raising your toddler to be a little fascist. The management is apparently 3 steps to the right of Genghis Khan.
PS -- I'd be interested to know if FOX News is still the "must watch" TV in the gym. After quitting, I was assured by the management they would change, but don't know if that's really happened or not.

Conveniently located just a block from the Kimmel Center, we stopped by for a pre-theater dinner.  We were a little worried about only having 1.5 hours, but our waiter assured us that they're used to the theater crowd and we'd be out with time to spare.

We started with the Ahi Tuna Rolls (okay, but needed a higher fish:rice ratio) and the Homemade Gnocchi (so fresh and delicious!).  My entree was the Short Ribs special, which had been cooked for 24 hours - tender and great flavor and served with delicious Leek Mashed Potatoes.

Excellent selection of made-to-order salads, wraps, pizzas, calzones, and more. If you're craving gourmet pizza you're in luck. If you're headed to the gym and want to grab a healthy salad on the go, you'll find it here. Want hot soup or other comfort foods (nachos, quesadillas, desserts, baked potato, and more) you will find it and more at The Couch Tomato. I love the place because each time I go in, I can order something completely different and still leave satisfied and confident that I paid a good price for fresh, flavorful food, and great service too. The Couch Tomato has some excellent menu choices that you won't find at similar establishments which you should check out for yourself on their website (which you can order food directly from as well for delivery or in store pickup). I've never had a negative experience here but know that they take customer feedback very seriously and will make something right if there is a problem. I urge anyone who is voicing their concerns online to share them with the management/owners of the couch tomato - it is a breath of fresh air to have a local place that truly cares about its clientele and doesn't buy into feeding the masses regardless of service/outcome. This place is awesome. My friends and family agree because we get take out all of the time. I like eating there also - can't beat the attentive service, friendly, laid-back atmosphere and the free refills!!! I could go on for hours about the maddie bars and other desserts...Bottom line: if you're hungry - go there. They're busy for a reason - they have great food at competitive (IMO, great) prices and they're committed to complete customer satisfaction. This place is awesome! Just had the best pesto chicken pizza ever!!! My friends are here and they're fighting over the leftovers!!! Two thumbs up for the couch tomato cafe!!!

This restaurant is a real "ganga".  Food is outstanding and so amazingly inexpensive.  I had 2 huge Chicken Enchilladas with green sauce, rice and beans for $5.99. Huge plate.  My husband had the Chille Rellanos, for the same price.  Both were awesome.  The staff comes around with sauteed onions and peppers.  So good.  Staff is so warm and friendly.  I HIGHLY recommend this restaurant.

Been here many times, and the food is always good.  The pizzas are great, and with a salad they make a perfect meal.  Most of the main courses are very good too, including the Chicken Parmesan and the pork chop special.  Service is excellent, and the manager is always out on the floor overseeing the dining room.  The bartenders are helpful and congenial, and it's really hard not to make this place the default option when you've decided to go out.  They never disappoint.  (Frost is about ten steps away, and there's no better place to go for an ice cream desert.)

Carne asada burrito is excellent.  The large size in humongous, and the free condiments counter is the star here.   I'm not the biggest fan of their Sonoran hot dog - but you can slosh on the great condiments to your heart's content.  Haven't tried anything else.  4 1/2 stars rounded up to 5.

Had dinner at BK Tacos last week. Walked out full.

Pros: 
$3.99 for chorizo and eggs which were delicious.
Huge selection at the salsa bar.
Very tasty refrijoles.

Cons:
Loud, loud loud!!! Can you hear me yet?
Caramelo was just okay.
Carne asada taco was a tortilla with so-so meat. I don't understand all the fuss?
$2 for iced tea?

However, I would return just for the chorizo and salsa bar.
Friendly staff, great food from the delicious burger to the tempting salads!

The store is bright and makes you want to SHOP!

I love this Sephora even though I love MAC makeup. Sephora offers it all. They have so many brands.

Now they are on the expensive side I have found great items in here from makeup, skincare, to perfume.

The makeup brand I found to love is Makeup Forever, Benefit, and Sephora brand cosmetics.

The skincare that I loved from Peter Thomas Roth blemish mask it was $40 but I have had it for about 2 years now and it help even my esthetican even sells the product. I must get some more.

Then perfume they have it ALL. You can even go in and get whatever sample you would like. This is my vacation perfume hookup.. SHHH dont tell no body..

But SEPHORA overall is a great STORE!

This is a pretty hip new addition to Bay Street at International Plaza. The staff wear skirts (girls) or kilts (boys) with custom tees. If there's a wait, they take your number and text you when your table is ready. The text will give you the option to get marketing texts from them. Makes it convenient to walk around the mall if it's a long wait.
They have a serious selection of beers: tons on draft and many more bottled as well as wine and mixed drinks. They have tons of food options, starting with a seasonal menu. 
I ordered the burger sliders (medium) with Guinness onions and chips (steak fries). It comes with three sliders which is more than enough to fill you up. They were quite tasty as well. One friend ordered the lamb burger. He found it to be a bit "gamey," whatever that means. My other friend ordered a beef dip type sandwich served on a pretzel. She found it to be pretty rich but says it tasted very good.
We found the service to be very good. Our waitress was very patient with us as we perused the menu. Between the three of us, she probably answered 10 or more questions.
We didn't get to wander upstairs, but they have appetizers only and "pour your own beer" for which you are charged by the ounce. Downstairs they offer half pints and full pints for your beer, both very reasonably priced. 
Can't wait to return!

I'd say the Taco Bus lives up to the hype. I enjoyed my meal deal with a carne taco and chicken mole taco, both on flour tortillas. The guacamole is good too. I like how it comes with large chips and how're they're arranged. I found myself eating less chips and more guac that way. The refried beans were very good. I think they were topped with parmesan cheese. The rice was decent. It seemed more like fried rice to me...
My friend ordered the shrimpceviche. It looked kinda weird. Like shrimp in salsa. She said she wouldn't order that again.
The burritos looked huge. Come hungry if you want one of those.
The horchata is sweet and cinnamon-y, not thick but not watery-tasting. Very drinkable, but with the size of the cup, I think I'll pass next time and save the calories for my meal.

Love this place! We came here on a Friday night and it was busy even though we arrived at 10pm.

We ordered the cured meat board, uovo pizza, pacherri and fideua my way. The uovo pizza was crisp and rich. The paccherri was a ribbon pasta with a pork ragu. Fideua my way was a chorizo and seafood dish with short, broken noodles in a garlic/wine sauce. Everything was so good that my least favorite item was the cured meat board. And you really can't even go wrong with the cured meat board. For dessert we had the salted caramel budino. I don't know how to describe it other than it's a perfect balance of deep complex flavors delivered in a rich, creamy pudding like (almost custard like) dessert.

I also like the restaurant setup. It is pretty small and narrow but it has a completely open kitchen. It always feels more intense and alive when I can see the kitchen and chefs.

Whether I'm in Tampa, Orlando, or Ft. Lauderdale, I'm always a fan of Bahama Breeze. One absolute must have is the coconut breaded onion rings- huge, so crunchy, and not sopping with oil. I definitely cannot skip these! They come with two sauces, both of which are delicious. My other favorite is the chicken kabob. I swear the meat is sooooo tender. 
Also, they have tons of tropical, oh-so-full-of-calories, drinks. You'll definitely feel guilty when you leave here, but I'm sure you'll be back!

Bayshore 345 Condominiums is located off Bayshore offering Beautiful Views of Tampa Downtown, Bayshore One, Crescent Bayshore, TGH on Davis Island, and Bayshore. The Condominiums feature 24/7 Security, Sauna, Jacuzzi, Heated Pool, Library, Computer Room, Mail Room, Covered Parking, Gated Parking, and Pool Tables. 
 
Cons: Parking Spots are very difficult to get because a Real Estate broker took out the parking spots from properties before selling them. So most 2 and 3 bedroom places only have 1 spot instead of equal number of spots to how many bedrooms. Parking spots are too small. There is a ton of noise from Tampa General Hospital's Helicopter late at night for units that have a view of downtown, Window blinds supplied don't block out enough light at night.  Monthly HOA fees are a bit high. For a 2bd/2ba the HOA fees are $579 per month and $741 per month for a 3bd/2ba.

Firehouse provides good service, they're not hung up about the machines they work on, and they are right on top of a coffee shop. 

A few idiot reviewers claim they got bad service because they weren't dressed as hipsters. This seems impossible, once you check out how Firehouse's staff dresses. 

You could maybe quibble over them being closed Sunday and Monday, but it's not like they're an emergency ward or something!

The other nice element is that they save old bikes by bringing them back to life. This keeps them out of the waste stream and also lets youngsters see "how they used to make em".

  Also -- when a shop like Firehouse says a bike isn't worth fixing, BELIEVE THEM .... they work on bikes a lot of other shops wouldn't touch. I know.

Foodie Heaven. If you like food, if you really like food, this is THE place to buy it.  The best of everything and many foods you won't find anywhere else. From fresh Lychees and fresh figs, to exotic cheeses from around the world (such as Lancashire Cheese and many more), Himalayan red rice and the highest quality meats, poultry and seafood you'll find. Bar none. So much more it will boggle your mind and your palate. In my first trip I stayed 3 hours in order to see it all.  I even ate freshly made food at their inside eateries and took home cheeses and hand-made truffles. This is a one of a kind experience. But as with all good things, there is a trade off: it is expensive to shop here. The secret to this place is to choose wisely and not visit too often. We shop for special occasions and as a special treat.  Cook at home with the $30 per lb. filets and it is still less expensive then a night in a high end restaurant.

This place has some tasty dishes, but it is far too noisy and has some confusing menu choices.  Breakfast hours are too limited. Pluses: Low prices, a great salsa bar and some great options for families who can buy larger qualities and assemble the dish yourself. Be prepared for crowds.

I love this place. The chips are awesome, the salsas better, and the food is almost always very very satisfying. The only thing that chaps me is that they routinely try to seat us in this dismal back room where they put the loud families with noisy kids. We're not loud and we have one quiet adult kid. Otherwise it's in the neighborhood and always tasty.

I had passed by Golden Empress countless times en route to get my hair did by Candi at Salon Sugar (and had accumulated a collection of their take-out menus with the intention of eventually stopping in for a bite while in the area), but had never actually stopped in until a friend and I were scouring South Street for a reasonably priced place to have dinner before seeing a musical at Penn (which in itself is a whole other story; it ended up being pretty awful).  I suggested Golden Empress Garden since they had both a vegetarian and a regular menu so we went in and got seated right away.  I settled on the sweet and sour tofu and my friend settled on the General Tso's tofu, but to her dismay, it was a bit too spicy.  Other than that, I would definitely come back, most likely for lunch (since portions are smaller) since I spend a lot of time in the area.

I adore this local jewel. I love their Bada-Bing Shrimp as an appetizer to share. Their specials are always good but my all time go to is the Penne Iavarone....sundried tomato, creamy with goat cheese-OMG! The Sangria is usually very good, but inconsistent.

Awful.  The food ranged from bad to sub-standard.  The wait staff and kitchen staff maintained a constant, extremely loud stream of inanities (and we weren't anywhere near the kitchen, though many tables were positioned in that vicinity).  Our wait-person spoke extremely loudly to each table and was so disconnected from the concept of service that she returned five separate times at the end of our meal to utter very loudly "Thank you for choosing us tonight.  Please come and see us again."  Even with the "pasta night" discount, we spent far more than anything served was worth (even the artichoke appetizer, which was the one fairly well-prepared dish, was absurdly over-priced).  Perhaps the restaurant's business is suffering because of all this low-quality; I have never been treated so pushily by any wait staff.  When we said we couldn't eat any more and would like the check (really we were disgusted and wanted to get out of there ASAP), the waitress actually asked, "Are you sure you don't want a chocolate torte to take home?"  Five must be the magic number, because we counted five utensil drops, only one of which was by a customer (probably a function of the small table).  Combine all of the above with the dank, isolated-feeling interior and the cramped booths on the eastern side of the restaurant, in which they placed all two-person parties despite the slow traffic and ample availability of more spacious booths along the western edge, and finally with the fact that no one offered to take our bulky winter coats before leading us downstairs to such a tiny table) and you have one star.  Yuck!

I am a pizza maven especially with my N.Y. background.  My years in Queens has spoiled me rotten for the most authentic pies.  However, I lived in Calif. in the 70's and there was a place called Numero Uno which was a Chicago type pizza restaurant.  This was simply the best pizza I have ever eaten.  Not to place drop but I had pizza in Italy and was just thin crust and nothing to write home about.  So I have been searching all my pizza loving life for something similar to Numero Uno.  Thought Rocco's would help me find Nirvana.  Much to my chagrin it didn't quite reach my expectations.  It was good and a little different with the unique crust.  Well I guess I will just have to keep searching to replace the pizza I left behind in California.  Went back a few years ago and it was gone.

My friends were visiting from Boston for the weekend and were feeling like some margaritas and chips. As soon as we walked in, I asked the hostess if we could sit outside. She briefly glanced outside where there was an open but uncleared table and said "No. You can sit here." and sat us at a table near the door. OK... thanks.
My friends were not very impressed with their margaritas. My sangria was decent. I liked the sprinkle of cinnamon on top. The salsa was ok, but the chips definitely didn't stand out. The guacamole was tasty enough. We didn't like that there was a fluff of lettuce at the bottom of the cup to make it seem like there was more guacamole in it though. When they asked for more salsa, our waitress slapped the new cups of salsa on top of the old ones as if she couldn't be bothered to take the others away.
I was the only one to order an entree. (Maybe that's where the attitude came from.) I asked our waitress how the tamales tasted since it was a "Miguel's special." She said they were very good, but the expression on her face didn't seem very genuine. I should have taken that as a sign. They were horrible. Three tamales, not served in a corn husk, topped with a sour cheese. I could barely get through 1/3 of the first one! The yellow rice was mushy, the refried beans could very well have been canned. I'd had better (and cheaper) refried beans at Taco Bus on Hillsborough. Thank goodness she had the heart to take it off my bill. That's the only reason they squeezed the second star out of me.

I wandered down main street Manayunk on a nice fall Friday afternoon in search of a good meal and pleasant atmosphere for a lunch date.  Once I decided on Chabaa, I was not disappointed.  

They began by hitting on the little things, such as a friendly greeting and clean environment, continued to elevate things by taking the time to explain and answer questions, and locked up a solid rating with a great meal.

While I cannot say it was the best Thai I've ever had, it was well prepared, fairly priced, and did not leave me wanting more.  The coconut ice cream, served in a coconut, was a nice dessert to share.
Love it!  Portions are always so big.  I love their ahi tuna salad and all their yummy chessecakes.

We ate here for the first time last night. My husband had the veal parmesan and I had the butternut squash ravioli special. Since there was only 2 of us, we did not do family style but a la cartep.

My husband food arrived cold and the veal was mostly breading which is shameful considering the $25 price tag. The marinara sauce was uninspired and tasted like it came from a jar.

My butternut ravioli special was on the cool side and rather flavorless. The pasta did not taste fresh made and there was very little butternut filling in the ravioli pockets.

We would not go back again.

How thrilling, I thought, that a cute cafe that took credit cards was so conveniently close to work!! 

How charming, I mused, as I listened to cheesy music and gazed upon the homey diner-esque decor! 

How much less charming, I thought, after waiting 35 minutes for my lunch take-out....

How extremely less charming, I grumbled, as I ate Taiwanese Rice Noodles & Pork of a quality on par with a cheap Chinese take-out place. 

How abysmal, I reflected, as I ate dish that purportedly had a variety of vegetables that actually only contained a few shreds of carrot, a smattering of cabbage scraps, a moderate morsel of mushrooms and a few bland bean sprouts. 

How very unlikely, I muttered, would it be that I would ever return for more.

First off, this place is not a sushi bar. It's a BAR with sushi. We only went here because I had purchased a Groupon and we wanted to watch the U of A basketball game. The miso was good, but we had to ask if they had any. It wasn't listed on the menu. We ordered a spicy dragon roll, yellowtail, salmon, and another roll I can't remember. None of it was great.
The eel did not taste fresh and several pieces of the rolls fell apart as soon as I tried to pick them up (no wonder they don't have a sushi bar where you can watch them "craft" their sushi!). It was a very strange environment for a sushi place. Lots of older men with much younger women on dates. THe whole place is a bar atmoshpere - no restaurant type section. They were having a radio station party on the patio and a band was playing. The service was good and that's the only reason they are getting a 2nd star. If you are looking for good sushi, go elsewhere! It is however a good place for drinks and watching the game.

ETA: I guess we missed the restaurant part of the place with the teppanyaki tables. We had walked in the back door to the sports bar. We walked all throughout the patio area, wandering around and didn't see an entrance to that part. Wouldn't have mattered, we wanted to watch the game and the sushi would still have been bad.

A great selection of beers from around the world, including many Belgians on tap, does not make up for being overly crowded and overpriced.

After struggling to get a spot at the bar, a small group of friends and I (four in total) then struggled to get any attention from the bartenders.  Luckily, our neighbor passed over the list of beers on tap so we could get our evening started.  Once we got menus, we ordered a few items for picking, which took well too long to get out.  I should note the food review is merely apps, but again we found them nothing to write home about.  Pricing was fair, although some of the rare and very rare beers could get rather expensive (more so than I have seen at other places which carry these types of beers).

I will given them credit for a totally awesome beer selection and always rave about their "Beer Bible".  It is such a shame everything else fell short.  I hold out hope that is was just an off night, as I've heard too many wonderful things about this bar for it to have been this disappointing...

This is just great BBQ, even if it is a chain. Everything I've eated here, every dish, every sauce, every side is really great. Someone really did their homework in preparing the right balance of flavors. Eat her and you'll want to eat here again and again.

They have the best food ever! The Dutch have wonderful food and quality. On Saturdays it can be a little crowded however don't let that deter you it moves fast and their service is awesome.

They always have breakfast specials and their orange juice is the BEST! Their food is spectacular!
Amazing BBQ chicken pizza!! This is the best pizza in Philly....generous with the toppings, large size! Prompt delivery. I highly recommend this place...no complaints whatsoever!

This is the second time I've ordered from them and I'm still disappointed.
It's a real shame because I love their food.
Unfortunately their service is the pits.
It seems their people could care less about customer satisfaction.
It's almost like McDonald's Drive thru its so bad.
I need to stop, pull everything out of the bag and double check it all including taste testing the dressings to be sure they're correct.

It wasn't even busy, and they got it wrong.
I may give it one more shot, because like I said, I love their food, but I think one more mistake is about all I can handle from them.

Great local sandwich shop that has all the greats... beer, cheese, and good food twists.  I enjoyed the curried chicken and recommend highly.  I'm an out of towner but have gone when I am in town for the local feel.

Wow we really had a good time here and the food was pretty good as well.Big two story place with all the servers in kilts, reminds of us Ragland Road in Orlando, a fav place of ours.Friendly service from the minute we walked in. Took a look around and had to check out the our your own beer wall upstairs. They have quite the selection for sampling. You buy the card and choose your beer and pour size for ample tastings.We chose to skip this and sat downstairs with a couple black and tans. Tried the curry chips and both the curry sauces were very good. They certainly could be spicier though. Also had the jerk shrimp which were very good and flavorful, although a tad overcooked and dry, but good none the less.Hubby had the london broil, friends had the pub sliders, the lamb burger and I had the ahi sliders. I think mine was the best of the group but hubby firmly stands by his opinion that his was. All around a good meal for everyone.We didnt have room for dessert but that whiskey pudding sure sounded good. Defintiely a go back spot just for that!Nice to have a new place with decent food and service to go. not entirely authentic feeling, but it will certainly do tha trick!

A group of us decided to try this place for the 1st time on a Friday night. There wasn't many people there at the time (around 10pm?) Maybe there's another day when it's "jumpin". On this day in particular we found the place relaxed and inviting.

First impression walking through the doors was. OooO nice color scheme! As I continued to walk in and check out the area I was pleased to find wood floors, plush red booths and beautiful dark wood details. Some areas were tucked away from the main area so if on a date you could cozy up and chat comfortably. Overall appealing decor. 

Some random band was playing they had a decent sound real chill, and they seemed like they having a good time drinkin' and singin =) Nice background music I guess.

Had a beer I can't remember and the Blue Moon Burger. YUM-MAY! Loved it! The fries were served hot in those modern little metal stands with wax paper that looked like old news papers printed on it. The food my friends ordered was tasty also. I will totally come back to try some of the other foods offered and sample the beers they have.

Hells yah, I'm a fan. It's inspired me to open up my own business: I'm gonna be a gynecologist who also does brazillan waxes. Come, get your pap smear and then get waxed. Or maybe the other way around? Not sure what's more uncomfortable-- getting hair ripped out of your labia or getting your cervix scraped. They're remarkably similar. Or atleast the experience is: You're taken to a no-frills sterile-feeling room. You have to take off most of your clothes and spread yo'self fillet-style. The whole thing takes under ten minutes. And the cost of my co-pay is relatively the same as the cost as this Brazillan. The only difference is that my gyn attempts to make me feel at home at put my mind at ease as she palpates my uterus. Here, there's a "suck-it up, you ninny" air as they instruct you to pull your skin taut. And yet I happily tip them at the salon for services rendered.

P.S. They're not rude. They're Eastern European. And they asked me how I heard of them and I proudly said "Yelp!"

Why haven't I been here sooner? I love this concept and most cities have little markets that are like this and yet this is Tucson's only one! I love love love this little place...the coffee is okay but its not fabulous but for the price I honestly don't mind.
I love salad bars and am liking this place a lot.
I am all about it and think everyone else should be too!
We received a carrot cake at work as a present. It was absolutely delicious! I'd never heard of this place before, and I'm excited to have been introduced. Scrumptious to the very last bite!

I need to preface this review with a few notes: 1) It is based on multiple visits and not one specific visit and 2) The Bayou is probably my favorite bar in Manayunk.

That being said, you are probably wondering how I could rate my favorite bar only 3 stars?!?  While the Bayou has a lot to offer, which I will go into shortly, it is still just an average bar that is very consistent.  While I do not think it will ever blow me away, I know it will also never disappoint.

The Bayou also sponsors the Manayunk Sports and Social League, including the end of season banquets and a free drink after every game... So go sign up to play some fun coed sports where everyone wins (a free drink that is)!

So what does it offer... friendly service, two floors with three bars, decent food, good specials, clean yet with that hole-in-the-wall feel.  Also plenty of TVs to catch the game with friends or random strangers!

Wednesday $2 Roast Beef that actually tastes good and quizzo
Friday Happy Hour $1 You Call It (very nice way to start the weekend)

The food seemed healthier than most restaurants of this type to me. Very quick and attentive service, and food tasted excellent. The black beans were the best I've ever had. I will be going back for sure.

3.5 Stars.  I have heard alot of good things about Pumpkin and was excited to go there for dinner.  The atmosphere is laid back staff is very nice, pretty standard for most BYOBs.  The menu is small and everything sounded very good, so we saw a duroc pork terrine appetizer with a sweet and sour sauce...I now realize that I do not like terrine, this was of no fault to Pumpkin, I just wasn't prepared for it to be cold. As for the entree's, I had the scallops and my date had the chicken.  After tasting her chicken I realize I should have ordered that, it looked and tasted amazing, but the best part about it was the side of chicken stock with potato (they should just make that an entree).  I had about 3-4 delicious scallops for $26, the portions seemed a bit small for the prices.  In summary, the food was very good, but for the price, the portions could probably be a bit larger for my taste.  I'm on the fence as to whether I would return.

OD is such an awesome part of South Philly!!!  I have some of the best memories of going here while on vacation as a child.  And I went frequently when I moved to Philly as an adult.

I love the veal parm, which is my favorite dish.  I love the dessert case.  I love the waitresses who range from old, crabby goodness to kind-hearted plush lovies (one of which I witnessed save a woman's life with a FIERCE Heimlich one moment and then turn right around to finish another table's order the next!).  

The service is fast.  The price ain't too shabby.  It's relatively easy to get to by car or public transpo.  And where else can you see a 4'6" Asian woman chase a 6'4" black man around the car her car, which he had just rear-ended??!!  No where, that's where!!!

CLASSIC!  I heart The Oregon Diner!!!!

My best friend and I discovered Erawan Thai about six years ago during a summer rain storm when we were starving! We were so pleased with our discovery and loved every minute of it. It is a cozy place and they are so polite that she would often take a book there on a cold night and indulge in some "private time," as she calls it with coconut soup. It is a hidden gem! When my husband and I were renting on Chestnut Street, we would often stop in for a quick, affordable and satisfying meal. Now that we live in the Italian Market area, Erawan Thai will even deliver out there! What a relief! We typically get the pad thai (with chicken or shrimp), but I have tried a bunch of other dishes and have never been disappointed.

Earth cup has by far the best coffee in West Philly. This place is perfect for hanging out in the summertime because of their outside deck seating area. I consider it a take-out coffee shop in the winter because of the limited indoor seating. The staff has always been very friendly to me, especially the woman with the southern accent. One thing I don't like....they don't have a bathroom. Ive had to run to the RX and the gas station to relieve myself. I guess they are encouraging a short hangout time.

I took my mom here for her birthday and it was really fun! It has a Victorian feel and the tea and sandwiches were tasty. We had higher tea and had the honey tea and she crab soup. The owners were nice and helpful. I think this place would be great for a womens luncheon. I'm looking forward to going back. Have fun having tea!

Slightly above average pho in my book, very generous portions at ridiculously low prices, all being served to your table in the time it takes you to close your menu after you've order are all the key pluses.  

Just remember, while it might only be 60 cents to upgrade a small pho to a large pho (which is only $6.00 to begin with), the small will do a fine job of filling you up!  Enjoy!
Oh come on. Zabar's this ain't. And the bakery at Safeway is also pretty good.

I can understand some of the previous reviewers in their anger toward wait times here, but what needs to be understood is that this isn't our typical pizza place. Pizzas don't start out as plain with toppings thrown on and heated through in the oven, everything here is made to order and with fresh ingredients. 

I don't think that any one reviewer can argue that what they order is not fresh and slight to moderately above average on the taste scale. I do think that the wait staff could be beefed up, I don't know that I've ever seen more than two employees in the kitchen, one at the register/tending to tables, and the take-out guy on the bike, certainly with more staff a lot of these negative reviews might update positively just for better service. I don't know that I've ever seen this place packed, so I'm not sure that they feel justified to pay a second waitress though it most definitely is needed at times.

As for food, the menu is not your standard pizza and hoagie joint, everything on the menu is a bit more high scale. I personally never veer far from the portobello sandwich with mozzarella and roasted red peppers, or a greek pie with feta and olives. Portions are generous and because they are made to order sandwiches are always crisp, not terribly soggy, and pizzas are as Rachael Ray would say "yum-o".

I don't know what is happening to this Cheesecake Factory location at the International Mall, but my last few visits here have been plagued with bad service. One time I came in for lunch and the server took about an hour to come and take our order. He was nowhere to be found for half the time and we were seated in an area where all the customers were his. When he did appear he darted past our table faster than we could grab his attention. The food took forever to come out and I was left trying to explain to my friends who I had told Cheesecake Factory has excellent service what had happened. They are busy throughout the day, but it's no excuse for spotty service...The Westfield Brandon Mall location is just as busy and still manages to provide quality service on my visits there. A few other visits have had the same issue.

Today we came in at around 3pm and were seated near the bar area. They seated us right next to a birthday party of about eight people. The waitress came up to us to take our orders and we never saw her for the rest of our meal until the birthday party left. We never got our bread and we were starving. Just when I was about ready to just get up and tell them to cancel our order another waiter came out with our food and brought us some bread with our meal after we mentioned never receiving ours. It wasn't until this waiter saw the look on my face that our waitress decided to appear again.

The Monterrey Cheeseburger was pretty appetizing and made me feel a little better about the service. The Honey Mustard Mayo sauce and cheese were made well. The burger was cooked medium well as requested. There was a good amount of fries, but it looks like they served less than usual.  

The food was good, but the waitress simply wasn't making a good name for the Cheesecake Factory and honestly I'm sick of the bad service here. Probably won't be dining here much in the future. The Brandon one is closer anyway.

With a name like "Drinker's" I was a little skeptical about the food that we ordered to complement the (pitchers of) beer we were enjoying around happy hour. However, I was pleasantly surprised: the Nachos were light and crispy and generous smothered cheese and other toppings.  The Buffalo Fries were also smothered in cheesey goodness with a little spicy sauce to cut through it all.  Good bar food and great drink prices!

I came to Downtown for a job interview...it didn't go how I wanted it to. As I walked around Downtown I stumbled upon Eddie & Sam's Ny Pizza. I've heard about the place on Yelp and around the net so I stopped by to have a bite to eat. I got their cheese pizza combo (two slices of pizza for $5) and it was excellent! Perfect comfort food and made me feel completely better about my day. The crust is large and the cheese is oozing on your pizza.Their informative video on their Yelp page says they use water from New York and their pizza certainly does have a different taste then other places I have tried in Tampa. One of the best pizza places I have tried in Tampa.

It's nice to patronize a local shop instead of a big chain. The feel of the place and the friendly staff are great too. Cool selection.

Unfortunately that can not make up for the plethora of badly scratched DVD's on their shelves. Approximately 2/3 of the time I rent from there the movie stops playing partway through. I pull out the disk and sure enough it's badly scratched. In the future, if I rent from them again, I will inspect each disk I am renting before leaving the store and if need be have them polished.

It get's really old having to drive back to the store two hours after leaving just to have them say, "Yeah, It sure looks scratched." All those extra trips back to the store is just adding to global climate change people.

Oh, and yes, I have a very nice DVD player in excellent condition.

About the website: It's quite sad. It lists every copy of each movie even if there are 20 of them so when you 'browse' you have to start with movies starting with numbers and click next literally dozens of times just to get to the A's. They have no option to select movies beginning with, say, R. I'd hate to count how many times you'd have to click "next" to get there. Also the selection criteria defaults back to choose genre each time. I am quite comfortable navigating around websites and this one is a major waste of time.  It's easier to call first.

I like the place but they have some serious problems. I hope they can get their act together.
my favorite place to eat!   I'm a native person born and raised in Tampa

I'm from the Philly Area originally and I have to say that if I can't make it home for a cheesesteak, the only place I'll get one is at Frankie's. I love a pizza steak, especially because the first time I went in, I asked for one with sauce and got actual sauce. 

Add in the Tastykakes and this place is amazing. 

The only thing I'd ever change is to get something other than RC in the pop machine. 

The service is always awesome and prompt, accompanied by a big smile, even during hectic lunches where the line is out the door. When I've sprung for delivery, the delivery drivers are friendly and fast, too. The time quoted is always accurate.

Hmmm...cakes, chocolates, sandwhiches, coffee, salad, pizza, and lets not forget the sushi!
What a great concept of a place! I only had carrot cake and it was divine! I am excited to go back and hopefully try other things but I was all about the ambiance.
The only downside is the wait was long because the place is so popular! However, the cake was worth the wait!  I would have to say I enjoyed this place quite a bit! :)

Pros:
- Our server Manuel was friendly, courteous and efficient. I have nothing but good things to say about him.
- The food itself was very good, as were the margaritas and the freshly made salsa.

Cons:
- Even though my friend had made special wedding dinner arrangements with the restaurant weeks in advance, our entire party still had to wait 20 minutes for our table to be ready.
- The special menu for our party consisted of five main course choices. There were no prices, and at the bottom of the menu, I quote " Fresh Margarita or Cold Draft Beer Included." At the end of the meal...I received my bill for my entrée and two charges of $6.95 for my margaritas. Since when does "included" mean that they charge separately for what was "included?"
- My food arrived, and two minutes later another waiter said that my entrée for was for his section of the table, and literally took my plate and gave it to another guest. Thank goodness I had not touched the food yet.
- Their company party was that night, and I felt that they were in a rush to get us out of their establishment. When I asked three workers at the front desk where the bathrooms were located, they could barely tear themselves away from counting their tips to assist me.

I've been a server before, and I understand how hectic a restaurant atmosphere can be. That being said, there is no excuse for an inaccurate menu, playing trade-a-plate with my food, and workers that are more concerned about their tips than helping customers. Based on my experience, I will not be a customer of Guadalajara Grill in the future.

Service which was attentive but not annoying, a good date atmosphere, generous portions, and tasty dishes.  While the prices may be a bit high, they are in line with Main St Manayunk.  On the downside, the selection seemed limited.

Remember it is BYOB.

We came here for dinner on the Monday after Christmas.  The restaurant looked promising upon arrival because of the packed dining areas and the customers waiting on take out orders.  We waited for about 15/20 minutes, which was not too bad.  We were then seated at a table which had been available during our entire wait, which we attributed to the fact that they seemed understaffed.   We placed our order soon after our drinks arrived and were really looking forward to the meal.  We couldn't have been more disappointed.  First course, 3 of us had the salad.  Two of the salads were average, the third, a greek salad, did not come with Greek dressing. The server stated that they do not use Greek dressing and offered a balsamic vinegar, how strange.  The minestrone soup was absolutely awful.  It tasted like watered down marina sauce and had large chunks of barely cooked vegetables in it.  The main course didn't fare any better.  Three of us ordered veal dishes - veal parmigiana, combination eggplant and veal parmigiana, and veal fior di latte.  We all agreed that we couldn't taste any veal.  I think that the veal was so thin that it was absorbed into the breading, which was all we tasted.  The accompanying side of pasta was not edible.  Our 4th entree was the beef ravioli.  It had a funny taste, maybe it was of the frozen, store bought variety?  The server was nice enough, but the overall service was spotty.   We definitely will not be back.
Great sandwiches with delicious fresh rolls. The eggplant sandwich is my favorite and the absolute best of all the places we've tried in the area.

Stopped here for beer on the way home, and left with what I was looking for.  There is A LOT of different American beer here and the staff seems to know what they're talking about.  I really had to pee, and how perfect!  They have a public bathroom too!  Well, I don't know if it's advertised, but if you just ask, they will probably let you in.  I ended up getting a sixer of different beers, so you can mix and match.  They take credit credit cards.  If I remember correctly they guy working the register was pretty cute too.  Do not expect to find parking right away, or maybe even at all.

I enjoy going to Zona 78 for their happy hour!  They have great outdoor seating area, that when the night is cool it's a great place to have a nice bottle of wine.

The staff is great and wonderful, they are very knowledgable to what is ont he menu.

Great date place!
I'm bumping my previous rating up to 4 stars.  After having explored more of Main St and its evening entertainment options (bars), the Bayou offers a good time, great specials, and no cover!

We stopped in over the holiday after hearing good things from friends. It doesnt look like much from the outside but once youre in it feels like your in a very fancy dining room. 

We opted to go for a number of tapas instead of the entrees and most of them were fantastic. 

I definitely would recommend the calamari, the escargot and the lamb was awesome. I didnt care for the salsa rosa ceviche. 

The service was great and id definitely recommend checking it out. It can get a bit pricey though but I think its worth it.

The decor in this place is a little ehhh but that's really the only bad thing I have to say about it. The first time I came I was YOUNG and dragged along on parent's date night, but I remember their beef skewer being AMAZING (I've been a foodie since I was like, 10). I gave it another shot for a birthday dinner recently though and WOW. I started with fried green tomatoes that were on point (a star alone to this place just for having them!). My friend and I split this HUGE 20 oz filet mignon that might have been the best steak EVER. I mean it, EVER. Better then Burns, Ruths Chris, Mortons, Smith & Wolensky... THE BEST. It tasted like bacon! I swear, a bacon steak. DOES IT GET ANY BETTER THAN THAT?! It was tender and flavorful and fan freaking tastic. The peppercorn smashed potatoes were delicious as well. I would definitely recommend to someone, especially an out-of-towner as this is not a national chain...

This place is great!  Staff is super friendly and efficient. Patio is dog-friendly.  Guineas on draft. Bangers and mash are my wife's fav, but she also likes the ma & cheese. I like the french dip-piles high with meat and hot every time.  Also try the Irish nachos - a cheesy, potato delight.  

Highly recommend - our official neighborhood hangout.

First thing - we went on a fri night. It was packed, if its packed, wouldn't u think it would be good. Well... Wrong.
Atmosphere - loud like a chuckie cheese.
Salsa - plain, chips - no big deal
I orders a carne asada a la tampiquena steak w enchilada
Steak had quite of fat, rice was overcooked like it was probably the same rice from during the day, enchilada was just a hunk if cheese w the plain salsa. Hubby had carne asada - same thing - blah. Maybe there's something better but I wont stick around to find it.
Guacamole was also plain nothing in it except a bunch of spicy.

This review will cover the teppan yaki and the service. 

My heritage is  Japanese, I grew up eating Japanese food and I ate it while in Japan for two years and I cook some of it. Sakura's food is mediocre. We went tonight for our anniversary. I had the teppan yaki chicken and my wife had the shrimp. We feel both were slightly over-cooked. For teppan, it is best to cook the ingredients in the sauces. They didn't and poured the sauces on the meat & vegetables toward the middle end of cooking. As a result, there was hardly any flavor to the food. Rather bland. Even the fried rice was not so flavorful. And the chicken was just a hair too dry. 

Portions were enormous, however. We took our leftovers home. But we didn't eat any as the food really was not outstanding. You know how you usually look forward to eating your Asian food leftovers? Not in this case.

The soup they serve is just a salty stock soup. Cheap and not miso. It was a big whoop and I only had a few spoonfuls. The gyoza was deep fried, not pan fried then steamed like they are supposed to be. They tasted OK, but they were not true gyoza.

The chef gave the usual show that everyone expects. He was OK, nothing spectacular but OK. The waitress and hostess were very considerate and gave very good service.

The couple next to us ordered sushi (which he had to remind them to bring out). I was not impressed. It looked like whoever put it on the serving plate did not care. It was a bit sloppy and not very artistic in it's presentation. You may wonder why anyone should care about that, but in a true Japanese restaurant, presentation is everything. Food is an art to them and if a Japanese restaurant doesn't care about it's presentation, then it makes you wonder about the quality of the food. The couple didn't complain about the sushi, but they didn't say it was great, either. They didn't eat half of it and gave it to the family sitting next to them.

I forgot to mention that when the gyoza was initially served us by a serving assistant, it was cream cheese wontons. He brought it out and said here is your gyoza specifically. My waitress came out just after that and saw that it wasn't gyoza so she took it and replaced it with gyoza before I needed to bring it to her attention. Points to her for remembering we ordered gyoza and doing something about it. But I wonder why their staff doesn't know the difference between gyoza and stuffed wontons? They definitely look different. I would think the stuffed cream cheese would have been definitely a give away.

Prices were about what you would expect for a Benihana-type restaurant: My teppan yaki chicken was around $17 and my wife's shrimp and vegetables was $22. Oh, it was billed as "oriental" vegetables and had the usual suspects of Napa cabbage, carrots, sprouts, and onions but had only a couple of snow peas. 

If all you want is entertainment then I guess go. If you are looking for great food then don't. I do not know how this is supposed to be one of the top go to restaurants in town because I am not impressed.

Went last night with my family. We are new to Tucson and I am on my quest to find the right theater here.

We've been to five theaters now and I could tell immediately from the previews that the picture quality at this theater was definitely the best so far. A very crystal clear hi def experience. They use Texas Instruments DLP projection. Sound was excellent.

Seats are stadium seating with very comfortable high back rocker chairs. Ample space between the aisles.

I highly recommend this clean theater.

I used to love this place more than you could imagine, in fact my butt has graced their stools more times than I dare to count 
Having said that, my last few dinning experience there let me feeling unfulfilled. For one thing, the prices are through the ceiling, it cost me $10 for an ordinary breakfast that was just plain ole tasteless. 
I ordered Egg over medium, Sausage, Home Fries with onions and toast and coffee. well the eggs came out runny with the whites not firm, the Sausage was two 2 inch pieces that looked like a Tic Tac on a Whale next to the mountain of dried out Home Fries with my barely cooked Onions lazily thrown on the top of the Potato's. The Coffee was weak and the toast was barely kissed with butter. 
While I was trying to force my $9.89 breakfast, a Amish kid gave me a free small calender and I asked him "where's the calender with the sexy Amish girls on it". He recovered well and replied"we're out of those' and apparently I was out of a good Breakfast.

The perfect example of a restaurant that fails to live up to the hype. Super slow service mixed with below average value (food quality for cost). The atmosphere was decent, but hardly made up for the downfalls. As I frequent Main St, I'd recommend you pass on this place, as better alternatives are a short walk away.

This is probably my favorite "chain" restaurant...EVER however, the service we had the other day was crap. We got stuck with some bitter queen of a gay man who was less than amused to serve us. Other than that, the food is always yum & the Skinnylicious menu rocks!
This place used to be a lot better.  If you love sushi and teppan, go to Mr. An's instead.  Their restaurant is clean and beautifully decorated; the food is excellent and the servers are friendly.

Vastly over-rated.

My fries were hot, but raw... I sampled 5 from the batch to see if I could find any properly cooked.
The burger was just ok, nothing special at all.

In & Out is definitely better, which is where I think they compete. Our local Monkey Burger is a whole level or two above in quality. I'd rather pay the extra $2 to Monkey Burger and get drastically better food and support a local business.

I won't be going back on my own free-will...
Anyone who makes sticky buns this fine is a friend of mine.

It's a cute little place with a large bar downstairs, and dining and small bar upstairs. Their fries are apprently award-winning, but I didn't think they were anything special. I recommend sticking with the burgers, which are good. The mussles were not great, and the chicken cheese steak was awful.They have daily changing specials - last time we tried deep friend Tasty Kakes -- amazing!!

This is called a South Philadelphia hoagie....!!!!! If you want a grand experience with fireworks blasting come to Ricci's!! My dad brought me here as a young girl (he made me the Foodie I am today, lol) after school me still in my Catholic school uniform and we ordered the best hoagie ever....

The wait is quick and everyone just forms a line and they make your hoagie assembly line style. My favorite is the lady in there she looks like Sandra bullock she is very nice.... 

Hoagie roll is soft and toppings fresh.... 

RICCI never disappoints but get there early because they close like 3ish.

I came for lunch with a couple of friends. We weren't really expecting such an intimate atmosphere in an obsolete strip mall. It's a great spot for a date.
Everything we ordered was good. I had a purple corn soda which was right on the money. The ceviche mixta we shared was great. Fresh tasting and served up with some delicious toasted corn. 
I had a fish lunch special that was very good as well.
I'd definitely return for dinner so I can try out their lomo saltado (offered for lunch but seemed to heavy for me in addition to the ceviche).

Best view of the city -- hands down ... just remember, you are also paying a lot for that view!

This is the perfect first date place --- dimly lit, high in the sky, fancy drinks.  This is NYC meets Philly!

Love love loved it here! No wait on a Sunday morning! Seriously? If youre over the lines at Sabrinas and Honeys and want somewhere new try it here! Its Fabulous! Cute random lil artsy space but a lil crammed so strollers may be a lil tough if you have kiddos. My hubby gets the asparagus pesto plum tomato frittatas with perfect amount of potatoes which I eat and yummy grain bread! Also, you must get the homemade hot sauce! The fam splits the cannoli French toast! Challah French toast topped with cannoli cream bananas pistachios and chocolate chips. More like a dessert but def a MUST HAVE!! I usually get the spinach salad or a special as they change regularly. The waiter was super friendly and sweet. The service was prompt! We used to eat here all of the time, haven't been in awhile. I assume it's also a tad more crowded now on the weekends.

Food here is second to none!  Pad Thai and wonton soup are the best you will find in Tampa. The amazing chicken is just as described! Amazing.  Service is good and the staff is very friendly! One of my favorite places to go in Tampa.  Best Thai in Tampa hands down!

My ABSOLUTE favorite!!! If I had to choose a last meal it would be here. I have been eating here for years and years and it is always consistent. I feel it is way more laid back than most Steven Starr restaurants maybe because of it's location of Penn's campus. The futuristic decor, light up seats, sushi conveyor belt, subtle but funky music, and of course light up your own "pods" are just too fun! I used to get the spicy tempura rock shrimp with candied walnuts and pineapple, the crab spring rolls when I ate seafood for a moment, and I would ask for a side of wasabi mash which is the best! For vegetarians, the Pad Thai is also yummy as well. We usually get a sushi roll that is on special for the night although all of their rolls are yummy and  they even have some unique to Philly like a Penn roll. I  drink either a "pink" or a "yellow" as the martinis are named for a color depending on ingredients. There's always Sapporo or good old sake.  It's not totally over priced and really the $12 martinis are what adds up so if you're worried about price, go and just split meals even appetizers like we do because it's really enough food and we often have left overs. It's great for groups but remember to ask for a Pod as they reserve them for large parties! I also find it romantic as well; I always have a special night out here with the hubby. A Philly must eat!

Love, love, loved Carol and his entire team! They did my gorgeous wedding flowers.... All the bouquets, centerpieces, and ceremony pieces. They were exactly what I asked for and more! We went to a few high end florist in the city and loved his super no- stuffy way of working. 

What really sets Cal apart is the fact that I was able to actually make my own centerpieces and all of our bouquets which is something I don't know of any other florist doing!! Being super picky and an artist myself, it was fabulous and much appreciated! They were able to match my vision and bold colors EXACTLY! Each one of my flowers were hand opened, hand dyed, and hand sprayed!!!  I was even able to bring some touches myself like peacock feathers I wanted and vases.  

They even let me drop off a piece of lace from my Mom's wedding dress to wrap around my bouquet, how special. 

I gave them a budget, they worked with it and told me what flowers were in season to keep everything in budget. 

Cal is super sweet and funny and his team is so hardworking. For any girl planning a wedding with a particular vision, I highly recommend visiting Cal and his crew!!

Went there for lunch and the pricing was good.  Compared to other Capital Grilles I have dined in, this was at the bottom.  The location at a mall just seemed to bring it down a tad.  But the interior is nice, service is good, as was the food.  I had the filet salad and was cooked to order.  Just not the ambiance I thought it would be.  Oh and $9 for a piece of cake?  Dream on, it's not that good.

Pinky's is our neighborhood joint-- unique, locally owned, personable. Parking is a hassle but you can park across the street at Cappy's and walk over. There is almost always a short wait but it moves along.  Love the serve yourself coffee bar, and their lattes are good and generous. The wait staff is friendly and efficient. Food is solid, good eatin'.  They have great eggs Benedict, great frittatas, and I love their grilled cheese with prosciutto and basil. Only two things would make it better-- offering tomato juice and better quality fresh fruit.  Still its our favorite --So glad it's in the 'hood!

Love Pane Rustica-- great food and a groovy environment. This is a locally owned place with hands on owners. It is a little more expensive but well worth it.  Though PR doesn't do a traditional breakfast, it is a wonderful place to grab a quiet latte and a homemade tart.  Their bakery items are incredible-- great Rosemary focaccia.   If you love chocolate, try the espresso cookies--moist, chunky and crazy rich and chocolatey.  Lunch is always packed.  It can be loud.  Hunt for a table while your buddy orders.  The prosciutto, basil, mozzarella sandwich is wonderful, so is the roast beef -- rare, lean with good flavor.    Take out is easy-- order over the phone, pick it up and avoid the crush. Dinner is much more subdued and relaxed, with unusual, delicious entrees. Not really a family restaurant at night but they are very accommodating in creating options for kids. Twice while  dining there for dinner, I have been offered a loaf of bread to take home because they bake their bread fresh each morning and don't want their extra loaves to go to waste. Yah! Great place-- one of a kind!

We went here for wings. They were pretty bad. But at 35 cents a piece, how much can I complain?

The food is incredibly cheap, but make sure you have several of their inexpensive beers before eating anything, or you won't enjoy.

Good place for getting drunk and/or fulfilling drunk munchies on a budget.

Bad for anything that won't end in regretted decisions or hangovers.

We had a group of 4 of us to go here on Friday night.  We made a reservation for 8pm and got in right away.  The restaurant actually wasn't that busy.
