Served with yellow rice". The dish was still warm when it arrived on my table. The chicken was a little rough, but flavorful. The potatoes were great as were the vegetables. I was surprised that I actually did not like the chorizo on my plate (since I'm a fan of chorizo), perhaps it was just the way it was cooked. The yellow rice was moist, but a little plain. It might have been a little better with a side of black beans. I added some sour cream to give it a little more flavor. Overall, the dish was average but I probably would not order it again because it was just average. My Step-Dad had the 1905 salad which looked amazing. Maybe I will have that in the future.

One particular problem I identified during our visit was the portion of their children's menu. My sister ordered my nephew chicken tenders and chips. They only gave him 3 pieces of chicken strips. We asked the waiter about the order and he confirmed that this is the standard...I honestly don't know how they can even call this a meal. It was a good thing my six-year-old nephew was not hungry because it would have then taken two children's menu items to fill him. My seven-year-old cousin had their macaroni and cheese, which was a more appropriate portion for a child.

Overall, The Columbia Cafe is a nice spot in Downtown Tampa. The view outside is fantastic at night (and I'm sure it is also great on a beautiful sunny Florida day). The menu leaves a little to be desired, but has the classic Columbia choices. There is a full bar and live music on Friday and Saturday nights. If this restaurant was open a little later and promoted better it could be a popular spot for the night scene in the area and easily dominate the area with its signature Spanish dishes.

Oh man--I love the Bayou Bar. I've been coming here for years on Monday nights for $1.50 hard-shell crabs, .20 wings, and cheap beer. All of the food is delicious, and you can't go wrong with the specials. 

This place is zero frills, and pretty much as bro-y as it gets, but I love it nonetheless. It's often packed with participants from the Manayunk Sport & Social Club after after their games, so it can be tough to get a seat sometimes. They do have a large upstairs area for overflow, so there shouldn't be too much of a wait for a table, even if it is packed. 

We stopped in recently on a Sunday bar crawl, and the bartender made a killer mixed drink. It had whipped cream vodka, root beer vodka, and coke in it--tasted just like a root beer float. This is really the diametric opposite what I would usually order here, but I'm glad that I got it, as it was really good and creative. 

Anyway, make sure you add the Bayou to your list of places to visit when you're hanging out in Manayunk. And def try to make it out on a Monday night.
excellent food and the owners are the nicest people in the world.  their crust is crunchy and chewy and the cheesesteak is decent.

Saturday night at Pumpkin had all the trappings of a great dinner for two. The intimate space was lit by candlelight and the heavy curtains by the doorway kept out any chill. 

The seasonal menu had some variety, but there were only one or two selections from both the starters and main courses that appealed to me. I ultimately chose the celery root soup followed by pan roasted monkfish. 

Warm, crusty bread was delivered to our table alongside roasted garlic olive oil. It was a delicious start to the meal. Starters came out quickly, and my soup turned out to be heavenly. The bowl was presented with some sauteed shallots and small rounds of apple, and the broth was poured tableside. I couldn't get enough of the velvety broth and incredible flavors, but luckily the portion size was generous. My boyfriend very much enjoyed his appetizer, which had tender pieces of octopus and squid. 

After the soup, I couldn't wait to taste the entrees. They were delivered shortly after our apps were cleared and were nicely sized. Unfortunately, they didn't live up to the starters. My monkfish was slightly too salty and came served along a river of capers. Next to that was a gelatinous square of pork belly that lacked meatier bits. Across the table, a ridiculously large piece of beef cheek was tender like a short rib, only not as moist, and much fattier. Our favorite part of this course was the side, the name of which escapes me. It was grainy like brown rice and served in the style of risotto, with some minced vegetables throughout and a sprinkling of parmesan on top. 

Even though we didn't love our entrees, the things we did enjoy were truly special. Service was also attentive and very informed. I absolutely see the potential here and I already have plans to return for an upcoming special event.
I ate here yesterday and liked it. Tried this Hawaiian fish that was extremely interesting to look at and to eat. Maybe on the pricier side but the food is worth it.

I used to live a block away from Ralph's, which made it convenient to go to, although I only frequent it maybe once or twice a year. I've been there three times (once for my birthday, once with my roommate, and once with my family) since I've been in Philadelphia, and I often find myself craving their food.

It's your typical Italian food selection, but I recommend the BRUSCHETTA. This is the item I crave, and for only $6, you can't go wrong. Sometimes I want to get bruschetta for take-out as just my dinner. It's unlike any other bruschetta I've ever had, and actually makes me love tomatoes. 

The only thing that's a bummer, is that it's cash-only...so be prepared to hit up an ATM.  Other than that, it's a great environment and good for any type of event.

Lovely space and decor. I had a glorious pedicure, with an excellent massage, hot stones and all!! My opi axxium manicure in blackberry chutney still looks great(it's  been a week) except for my thumb, but I'm rough, and work wit my hands...they are also super sweet and efficient, my new Monday spot!!

I want to dislike this place because of the tacky ambiance, but alas, it is impossible.  Everything I have ever had there is delicious.  The milkshakes are the best around.  The food is reasonable and there is plenty to choose from...I am a fan of the chicken cheesesteak myself.  If you are planning a trip here, just make sure there are no high school dances in the area that night...seems to be a popular after-dance spot among the local children.

I went here for the first time tonight to have dinner with a friend. They have homemade goodies for sale to take home and the atmosphere was fun, but sophisticated. We were a little confused when we walked in because we weren't sure if we should seat ourselves or not. 

The menu has classics and more inventive things, as well. I ordered the butternut squash lasagna. The portion was huge and it was delicious. We also had the calamari as an appetizer. It was good and came with a dipping sauce that almost had a cinnamon-y taste. For two people who ordered 2 alcoholic drinks, an appetizer and two meals, the bill was $50. We were not at all disappointed.

Awesome beer selection. I hit up the Foodery whenever I want to try some new beers. A little pricey, but that's what one should expect when purchasing singled-out microbrews. Another big plus is that the Foodery has baller sandwiches. The Diablo (buffalo chicken, jack cheese, avocado, green peppers, and chipotle mayo) is awesome. I definitely recommend the Foodery if you're looking to try out some new brands/types of beer. Glad this place exists and is so close to my house!

CHEERS!

I love La Teresita. I've been going here since I was a little girl. Authentic Cuban food, fast service, and it taste  SO good! I recommend them for breakfast, Cuban Sandwiches, and they have the BEST papaya shake!! YUM!
Jay's Offers the best service! Their food is great! But especially their Carrot Cake dessert! BEST I've ever had! DELICIOUS!
The best coffee ever and the food is so fresh delicious. Comfy, cozy atmosphere.

The parking lot Thurs-Sun says it all. You'd think they were giving it away. We've been eating at Gavi's since he first opened at Broadway & Pantano (and Italian is rarely better than when Gavi himself was firing the food). I think most his authentic Italian bistros have closed, so the menu at Piazza now looks like the menu of old (which is a good thing). Lots to choose from and the food is always good. They have some decent breakfast offerings on the weekend as well. 

Having said that... Gavi's suffers from the same problem many restaurants by great chef's suffer from: Lousy front of house. Haven't had great service in a long time and often its mediocre at best. You just have to expect that. Unfortunately, the bad taste from the service affects the food experience. He fixes that, and he'll have one of the best restaurants in town.

I haven't found Italian food in Tucson worth driving further than Gavi's. Go & enjoy.

I am a South Philly native and of all the Pho restuarants available, I believe that Viet Huong may be one of the best. 
It is pretty inexpensive and there is never a wait for a table. 
I typically get on of two things when I go to a Vietnamese restaurant. I always order the charboiled chicken and vermicelli. I also get the wonton soup which is also pretty good. Both are very filling meals. 

There are only a few issues I see with this establishment. That is:
1. There are only male worker in FOH. 
2. The food tastes a little like it has too much salt and MSG. 

So if your not a feminist or afraid of getting high blood pressure and love Vietnamese then this is perfect.

What can I say, it's Sakura!  One of the only places in Tucson you can go that has an outdoor patio with live music on the weekend.  The atmosphere is what keeps me going back. Always friendly staff, they have the best bartender in town, Deb Ruiz and Charity is our fav waitress!  Best place to take a date, multicultural crowd, nice ambiance outside, good music with 360 Degree band as well as Jay Faircloth. There are other Sushi places in town but none that have all the attributes you can find at Sakura!

I've had two waxing appointments (leg and standard bikini) at Adolf Biecker (one with Suzanne and one with Amanda), and I've been more than satisifed each time. I've never been kept waiting longer than 5 minutes for an appointment, and each wax aesthetician that has performed my service has been very professional and good at what they do.

I am going to write this review in the context of what this bar represents.  It serves craft beer from the world over.  It is not a restaurant, it is not a "date" place, it is not a club, it's not a yuppy hang out for networking.  It makes no pretenses for anything other than to  to sell art in liquid form.  And it does this extremely well.  Yes,  you can buy food there, but the food, although quite excellent, is an afterthought.  The owner searches for great beer to put in his taps. The beers rotate - you rarely see the same one twice.   I don't believe a salesman hawking well-known mass produced beer is welcome in this establishment too often.  There are times that the beers on tap are not the ones that  trip my trigger, but you can be sure of one thing - they are all outstanding beers, and many people will like them.  I have had beers at times that were so good I thought the Almighty himself brewed them.  Some beers make repeat appearances, others are a one-time performer.  There are descriptions of each of the beers on the counter, so you can get a ball-park ideal of what you are ordering.  But you really won't know until you sip it.  It is always a special night when I can get in there and try the finest-tasting beer you can get your hands around.  The bartender Billy is simply great.  Scoats, the owner, is a genius, and I wish I had this bar a bit closer to home.  Atmosphere - cozy.  Somebody said the crowd was "rough around the edges", but this is just all wrong.  I could not disagree more.  It is not an unfriendly crowd at all.  Remember how 10 years ago the corporate world was clamoring and slobbering all over itself to come up with a slick and catchy "mission statement"?  Their mission statement would be to sell an impressive and eclectic collection of beers to beer lovers and reasonable prices.  Probably my most favorite business in the city.

I have eaten here at least ten times.  Being out of town for business can be lonely at dinner time.  Finding this friendly and comfortable place was great.  Generally sat at the bar for dinner.  Bartenders are as friendly and professional as they come.  Nice selection of quality red wines.  Food is very good although it is expensive.

Good authentic Chinese food. My wife and I have been searching for some tasty authentic Chinese food in the Florida area since she is asian and craving "her peoples food" as she calls it. The service was friendly and the plates were left empty. We had duck noodles and a beef dish which were both good but our favorite was by far the snow pea tips. We will go back.

Just got back from my 2nd visit to HUB.  A couple of Friends and Myself each got the Rotisserie Chicken:

 Chicken, Mac & Cheese, along with some Steamed Broccoli & Asparagus on a plate of loveliness.  The plate was really good and I really, really loved the Mac & Cheese.  

As for service, I have read quite a few complaints about issues with wait-times, and I have to say, our service was fantastic.  The place was full and we were done eating & walking out with the best Ice Cream ever (Oatmeal Cookie Dough) in about 30 minutes.

The owner seemed like a really nice guy,  happy and eager to please his customers.  The Ambiance, Vibe, & Music were even better than my 1st time at HUB and I am ready to return ASAP!

WOW! We totally loved our dining experience here. Everything was exceptional. The salt was heavy on the flank steak, but we were informed before hand, that if it was too salty, a special cut would be prepared for us. They really went over & above the norm to make certain we were happy. It is on the pricey side, but it was worth every penny! We will definitely be back!

This is the Cuban sandwich I have been waiting for!  For years I have been going to La Tropicana and Brocato's but this one takes the cake!  I LOVE the fact that the meats are really thinly sliced, but still stacked super high and deliciously pressed!

I got the combo with the cuban and garbanzo bean soup.  The soup was equally as satisfying and had the perfect combination of flavors.  I got a side tamale and dont recommend that really...... Stick to the Taco Bus for one of those.  

All in all, it was a great little cuban sandwich shop!  So great that I may even go back again today!

[insert standard I-never-eat-at-chains disclaimer/hedge here]

I'm not sure that my review will be representative of a normal experience at this Chipotle, as I visited during Spring Break. But when I visited, it was delightfully empty, and we strolled right up to the counter to order before taking our pick of seats (I'm sure this is aberrant). 

I ordered a veggie burrito, which, it turns out, is roughly the size of one of my wife's smaller purses. They pleasantly packed up this edible--and delicious--valise, and I went over to the drink area to pour some freshly brewed iced tea, which was also very good. 

The burrito was very good, and I thought the hot sauce was nicely spicy and delicious (too often are hot sauces either tasty and not spicy enough or face-charring and bland). And at $6 it was not a bad bargain. 

All in all a very good experience. Will be back next Spring Break.

I absolutely LOVE Charley's!  I really love the flavor of their steaks, probably becuase it's cooked over an open fire with oak and hickory.  They really do a nice job getting it to the proper doneness in comparison to some other steakhouses.  

If you like wine, they really have a nice wine list and if you like appetizers, there is an amazing stuffed mushroom appetizer that is just incredible!  

Overall, LOVE the steak at this place!

I picked up some hot and fresh Cuban bread earlier and it was soooo good!  I would also highly recommend the butter bread which is really Cuban bread with the butter already on the top.  

This is one of the last true Cuban bakeries so lets all do our due diligence to frequent it so it stays here in our wonderful city!
Good mall!  Too bad we cant combine IP and Westshore to have the best of all stores :-)

One friend got half truffle (tastes like a deep vanilla), half saffron.  I got half cream (tastes like tapioca pudding), half tiramisu.  Another friend got pineapple basil (very strong basil taste, but the citrus balances it out).  $4 for a small.  I definitely recommend this gelato place if you want to sample some potent flavors and having an indulgent experience without going crazy calorie wise.

I came by to thaw from the cold and wanted a Chai - unfortunately, they were out, so I opted for a Hot Chocolate instead. The cocoa was a little thinner than I typically like, but the generous helping of marshmallows (an option that you should opt for) added a creamy flavor.  The staff was very friendly and the cafe was cozy - I'll be back to check out the tempting array of baked goods.

Dang, I got BBQ sauce on my white shirt. Consider me initiated to Brushfire.  I got the smoky brisket dinner with corn muffins and corn-on-the-cob. It was very tasty and the juicy, chile-coated corn was outasite. My wife got the BBQ turkey dinner.  We liked that the portions were just right - we didn't feel overwhelmed with food.

I found it through Priority Club Dining, because I get hotel points if I use my Visa card here. Dinner for 2 for $22 was right in our budget. 

Brushfire doesn't claim to be fancy, and it's not. The decor is formica tables, a giant menu on the wall and flames painted on the walls. 

Everyone was very nice. The staff was friendly. My fellow diners included Christian bikers in black clothes and piercings, and a woman cop out on a date -- with her GUN! It's a great place for a stoner to get dinner because there is so much to look at.  And they have pecan pie in the bakery case.

The atmosphere and decor are wonderful. The first drink I wanted was unavailable but the second on was FABULOUS. The Ice Cream is to Die For but the food?  Not so good. I went with about 15 people and everyone felt the same. The burgers were dry, the soup too salty or weirdly smokey, not in a good way. The Pot Pies were good until the bottom, then they were salty. Also...I had the tomato soup and asked for crackers. They didn't have any. Who doesn't have crackers at a restaurant???? Did I mention the ice cream was to die for though?

I waited till I had visited HUB a second time to do my review. Why? Because I wanted to give it a second chance to win me over because my 1st impression was underwhelming...

First impression was all about the visual appeal. Industrial Chic with exposed walls, modern furnishing and overall cozy cool esthetic. I liked it very much.

After physical appeal...we get down to the nitty gritty. Food and Service.

Food ordered:
1st Visit-ordered the pastrami salad, salted caramel ice-cream, hard pear cider
2nd Visit- Alaskan cod au gratin, bourbon almond crunch ice-cream, "Don't take me for granate" cocktail

The salad
I see where they are going with this but IMO I think the pastrami cubes were just too tough and chewy it didn't compliment the delicate texture of the spinach, and there was missing an additional ingredient to make the salad stand out as something extraordinary. Also the size of the salad was on the small side for it to be an entree salad.

Alaskan Cod Au Gratin
Warning** This is a "one dish meal"  All the ingredients are baked together and served in a mini casserole dish...that being said it was tasty! Has comfort food appeal and was flavorful. It had dimension of flavors and textures.

Ice-cream:
Smooth rich ice-cream, it was hard to choose from all the flavors but the ones I selected with delish! HUB has it going on with the ice-cream! Would be AWESOME to offer sundaes??

Drinks:
Pear cider was bottled, so can't critique the bar for that one, and the "don't take me for granate" was "meh" flavors were too light didn't blow me away.

Service:
I had similar glitches with the service as previous reviewers commented, the servers were a bit aloof, and there wasn't graceful attentiveness or composure.

To sum it up:
After two visits I think I'll most likely be frequenting HUB for ice-cream and maybe drinks. I will be on lookout for any updates on the current menu. I understand the new trend of keeping it simple but there wasn't much on the menu that would inspire me to CRAVE it...it is very much cut and dry, and so was the presentation and plating. I'd like to see more energy, finesse and charm radiating from the atmosphere and food.

It's not my new favorite place but it's got visual appeal...nice place to take visitors from outta town to show off the swanky side of Tucson and is a nice place for a date like situation...

I took my wife here for her birthday and our party grew from 6 to 8 without my knowledge, so we sprung that on the restaurant at the last minute.  The space is fairly small in the colder months as the patio isn't open, so it understandably took a few minutes for the staff to accommodate us, but they did a good job.

The ambiance is lovely, and hopefully we weren't too loud as the space is fairly cozy, but you're not right on top of each other like La Viola.

Now for the food.  I didn't know this was a molecular gastronomy restaurant when I booked it, let alone a chef from Per Se!  My NYC based sister in law geeked out a little bit, and we knew we'd be in for an adventure.  The server very kindly helped guide us through the menu as a number of us had dietary restrictions and we were off to a selection of the small bites.  The candied pecans were a wonderful beginning, and the lamb bacon (!) and lomo as well as the hamachi and the leek soup were extremely tasty.  The soba noodles with oyster was a bit of a miss as I thought the flavor was a touch too delicate.  Our favorite small bite though, was the duck "prosciutto"  Wonderful flavor and a joy to look at and eat.  Considering a large portion of the table doesn't eat pork, it was especially fun watching them look at the dish and the looks on their faces when they ate it after confirming half a dozen times that it was indeed, not pork.

The main courses were quite tasty as well.  The fluke had a nice crusting and the grapefruit sauce and cauliflower puree was was a good accompaniment.  My favorite flavor of the evening though was actually the parnsip and vanilla puree that was a base for a duck dish.  It was unbelievably tasty and the next time I go, I'll be getting whatever dish that puree is on.

The service didn't rush us, and we ended up spending 2-3 hours here and really had a lovely evening.  It was a fitting end to a birthday for my better half, and we will be returning soon.
best bar in the northeast.  great beer selection and the food is very good.  best part is when the season changes and the seasonal beers come out.

I almost want to go back with my own bread for my pastrami sandwich, but that's because I like hearty ryes with my pastrami.

I was at Hub for an early dinner on the day that Tucson celebrated St. Patrick's day, March 12. The bar was full of people watching the UA game and having a a rousing good time. Good.

I liked the decor. My friend and I took a two-top on the banquette side. I liked the white upholstery on the banquettes, liked the hemp line above. Nice feel to the place. The hostess was on point and friendly. 

Maybe it was because of the parade, but there was friendly convo from nearby tables. My companion had settled on Corned Beef and Cabbage, but after the table on one side leaned over to recommend the World Famous Dog (hot dog wrapped in pastrami) she changed her mind and went with that. I was there for the pastrami.

My companion LOVED the World Famous Dog. She had the horseradish on the side and used it judiciously.

The flavor and texture of the pastrami was very good. It was tender and piled on in thick slices. I was surprised by a touch of sweetness, maybe brown sugar on the top, but it was a good flavor and I will go back for more. 

The sandwiches are served with fries, but other sides can be substituted. I ordered the cole slaw. 

The slaw had a mayo dressing with good caraway flavor, that had a bit less vinegar that I would have liked.

The only truly disappointing thing on that plate was the bread, which was a squishy, not particularly flavorful rye. The sandwich came dry AKA without mustard. That was fine for most of the first half, but at some point I wanted mustard. The waitress told me that they had yellow mustard and spicy. I asked to try both. The spicy tastes like straight-up powdered horseradish combined with dry mustard and liquid. (Think wasabi power.) You can't go wrong by ordering the mustard on the side and and then adding it to the sandwich according to your taste.

The part of the meal that was the biggest winner for our table was the Bourbon Almond Brittle ice cream. It was served at the perfect temperature, the base ice cream bourbon flavor was great and there was a good amount of almond brittle. A wonderful, rich ice cream for true ice cream lovers.

It was a nice outing.  I will return. I know many Americans prefer a very soft bread, but I consider Hub's pastrami worthy of a heartier bread. Maybe there will be a choice of breads at some point.

I didn't taste the World Famous Dog, but that got a full five stars from my friend.

Ordered Garlic bread ties and large (16") 1/2 cheese, 1/2 combo pizza with no onions. Cashier stated that she could not make half the pizza cheese. I told her I did not care what it cost, I just wanted half cheese. She stated "due to the way we make the pizza its impossible to make half cheese" ??? 

Garlic bread ties were not cooked all the way, would probably be good if it was cook all the way. Pizza came with 2 lbs of onions and was also not cooked all the way. Dining room was very hot and unpleasant. This store has only been open for 4 weeks so I think they are still learning how to cook  a pizza and run a business. I think they have potential, I may try it again in 3 months and see if anything changed.

Sakura's a tried and true happy hour stopover on restaurant row with a great half-price menu until 7:00 pm weeknights and a big hoppin' patio with live bands on the weekend. The happy hour menu is heavy on the deep fried side, but enormous, crisp veggies make up for the light batter and the appetizers are a great deal.The sushi has been improving steadily for the past year or so - fresher ingredients, decent-sized rolls, and rice with the right consistency. The special roll last Saturday was the Playboy - salmon and yellow roe, which didn't impress (bland) but was okay. The spider roll disappointed (no crunch) but the shrimp roll was big and tasty and not minced into oblivion. The patio ambience is laid back and sweet at sunset. Sakura is a venue that knows how to do sound properly before 10pm - sweet funky riffs as background create a mellow, SoCal vibe. Inside, the whole teppan yaki show goes on all night, and these are some of the best teppan chefs around. Fast, professional, and attentive to detail. This is NOT the case at the west location, but the Tanque Verde Sakura has it goin on. Aregato.

We live in the neighborhood and were really excited for Earth to open, since we knew these were the owners of the since-deceased Heavyweight brewing co.  We weren't disappointed. 

Their beers are usually very good, and sometimes very unique (beer brewed with oyster shells), appetizers are great, and the flatbreads are delicious. Their crust is definitely not typical, boring pizza crust. The dessert pizza with Nutella is also insanely good.
Apparently in this Sephora I do not exist. On numerous occasions, I have been ignored by the staff. When I asked a question,  the staff seemed like they wanted to help, but somehow disappeared.

I love, love, love eating here. In fact, I'm about to head over there in about 20 minutes.  The food is always superb!  There usually isn't a wait.  The atmosphere is clean and simple.  It can get a bit loud in there sometimes though when it's a full house.  The waitstaff is hit or miss also, but who cares as long as your getting consistently delicious food. 

Try the veggie eggs benedict.  Even if you aren't a veggie person this TASTES GREAT!!

Over years of coming here before Burdines was changed to Macy's, I've seen a lot of changes and up grades to this mall. I love coming to this mall. It's a drive getting out there for me, but well worth it. I love the stores here and the good up scale restaurants to eat at if you don't want the food court.

calling to reserve dough? and almost 20 bones for a pie ... in Kennsington? cuz the crust is thin enough my fat ass ate a whole pie by myself ? either way the margherita pizza is pretty tops. near perfect thin crust and a very nice sauce, I personally would take a bit more cheese but let's no quibble. I would stick to the margherita though, the other times I had pie's w/ toppings I have not been too impressed w/ the quality. the mushrooms tasted like they were from a can and the anchovies were pure shit. sausage and pepporoni were acceptable but the real star was the margherita...u sexy, saucy  little bitch !

My star rating is a little lenient because, after visiting, I realize that they still have some kinks and will require a second visit.
My SO had previously been here after receiving the various Facebook posts the restaurant had created. This should be a lesson in how to effectively use social networking (at least for this half of this decade) to launch a restaurant. They really did a good job of building up and marketing it.
Overall, the service here was fantastic. Very friendly bartender and server and no issue when we moved from the bar to the cute little window table they have by the bar. The food came out very quickly but we were there pretty early and they were not at all busy.
The big selling point here is the ambiance. Someone put a lot of thought into the layout of this place. There is seating everywhere. The walls are lined with these hip, cushy benches and tables in the main area can be moved around to allow for big groups. If you've ever been to On a Roll down the street, it's exactly like their setup. The bar room has the same sort of wall couch, but one is a plush gold and there are no tables except for right by the windows, which makes for a fantastic people watching experience. Long bar with three TVs in front of it so you can watch sports while drinking/eating. They have a great patio as well, which is rarity in Downtown but becoming more common. The restrooms round off the "this should be a nightclub" thoughts: everything is super hip and super modern.
I only had two food items: blue fries and mac n' cheese. These totaled $10 but I don't recall the distribution. I opted for the blue fries. I liked that they used "real" blue cheese, and not that wonky, pussy blue cheese that you find on burgers and stuff. And there was A LOT OF BLUE CHEESE. I mean, it was an absurd amount of cheese that I could not finish so, be aware that you must really like blue cheese to order this dish. The mac n' cheese was served in a small bowl full of macaroni shells. They prepare it by using a velveeta-like cheese sauce for the base, but they garnish the top with real cheddar and bake it. It was nice, and my SO liked it, but I don't really dig the oil-based cheese. Give me my full, brick crapping pound of blue cheese on top of a few fries and I'm happy.
My one, big ding to this chic little gastropub comes from the beer. They have two rotating taps and, when we came in, one was said to be a Ballast Point (San Diego) Double IPA which, because of its alcohol content (I was told 10%), had to be served in a smaller glass and was priced at $5 and did not go on happy hour. Fair enough but, when I drank it, it tasted precisely like Ballast Point's regular IPA, which I've had several times and quite like. It's a hell of an IPA, but it is not a double IPA. I ended up Googling it (they have do have wifi, btw) and found that the Ballast Point Sculpin that they were referring to as a double IPA was, in fact, the new name for Ballast Point's regular IPA. I felt a little shafted there. The bartender also tried to correct me with "Actually, I think the brewery is called Sculpin" but she was nice about it when I mentioned that the brewery was indeed Ballast Point and in San Diego and I had been there.
Otherwise, they have a nice, rounded tap selection. Some stuff that you won't find elsewhere, others that are good but you find everywhere. Most stuff is $3 at happy hour, which ends up as a very good deal.
I'll be back to try the regular food. The burgers looked interesting although pricey, but I've been told that they use mostly local ingredients, which you have to support.

One of my fav breakfast/lunch spots, especially during warm-weather days.  Perhaps the best chicken salad-salad around!  Iced coffee is a must as well.  

Snag a table outside if you can and be sure to bring cash (but you won't need too much of it - this place is super affordable!)

warning: you will not be able to kiss nor talk in close proximity to people ...for several hours after eating here. loaded with garlic/onion/etc. i think my BF almost broke up with me after i ate here once. i guess i'll just have to make him it here at simultaneously.

the falafel is what i order, because falafel is what i love. unfortunately, i do not love the falafel here. it is weirdly too firm, it is just not right, something about it. i can't put my finger on it. ive had much better falafel at other places, e.g. Rami's food truck (across from Penn dental school on 40th and locust). Actually Rami's is fantastic, I think I may go there tomorrow now that i'm salivating abut it.

i go here only if i get out voted on lunch. nothing wrong with the place, i've just had better falafel that I get so distracted reminiscing about it....

Husband and I made a trek across town to No. Libs to try Green Eggs Café,  last Sunday because the reviews were fairly optimistic!
The restaurant décor was a no frills spot with minimal accessories, a huge chalkboard boasts the daily specials and a small coffee counter in the back to grab take out or a cup o'java.  The hostess was very pleasant, but we had to wait 20 minutes for a seat- even though there were tables available against the wall, I soon figured out that they were reserved for parties of 4, which I understand, but the amount of people standing around waiting to eat could have been accommodated. I wouldn't have been so twisted if it wasn't drizzling outside and the back coffee area was a bit congested.
After the small wait- the waitress immediately rushed over to us and took our order.
Since I love all forms of Eggs Benny, I opted for the Veggie Benedict on the menu. I was eager to dig into it as soon as it arrived at the table, however, I can't say that I was wowed by the flavors. It was kind of bland and lacked seasoning. The sautéed spinach turned out to be some chopped pieces on top- which resembled more of garnish then a main ingredient.
My husband skipped the breakfast side of brunch and went for the Italian Panini sans the marinated artichokes and roasted red peppers. After the waitress, repeated the NO peppers or artichokes twice, the sandwich came out- fulled dressed! He disgruntly picked off the extras and bit into the sandwich, while the outside was toasted nicely-, the middle was cold. At this point, he was annoyed that I dragged him out of bed to come stand in the rain for cold and bland food and since we were seated in the middle of the restaurant, we felt the hostess hovering over us the entire time. 

All in all, not one of the best brunch experiences I had in a while, nor will we make a repeat appearance, but at least I can yelp about it.
Very good tzatziki sauce. Fantastic for the price when ordering a gyro sandwich. Great appetizers. Live music and dancing was great, perfectly entertaining while waiting for our meal.

Went here for dinner the other night. Was really suprised by how good the food was. It was definately slightly lighter than other indian restaurants I've been too before. The naan was thinner and lighter. The chicken tikki masala wasn't too rich, it had a little less cream. The flavors were delicious. We had vegetable pakoras that were fried to seeming perfection. I haven't tried their buffet here but their food was great.

The service was a little slow at times. It's a family owned, local place. It seems to take them a while to get everything going for the customer. Still, this is a minor deterrant given the food. I will be returning soon for a lunch buffet.

Bacon - now I have your attention.  Jalapenos - some of you are wavering but stay with me.  Perfectly medium - not too hot - jalapenos stuffed with a slightly spicy cream cheese filling, wrapped in bacon and topped off with just the right amount of a sweet-n-spicy sauce. A heavenly dish known as Pub Footballs.  I rarely order an appetizer, but this is one I won't pass up.  

This place is one of our regular haunts.  I like the traditional English-style pub atmosphere which feels a bit more upscale than what one usually finds in Tucson.  It's good for a group or a cozy dinner for two.  I've never been disappointed by the food or the service.  Can't really say that I have a favorite dish though.  Everything I've tried, and I like to try something different at each visit, has been excellent.  

Check it out.

Great place, great lunch values, the owner Massimo, and manager Davida, or David, are true to their Italian roots.
I love these guys, they are alway glad to see you, but best of all the food is great.  I hope I spelled their names correct, but I'm sure I didn't.
All I have to say is, class act without being snobs, gray place.  Look for their review on the latest issue of Arizona Highways.
JB

Ordered the lamb kabob platter. I think it was delicious but my inebriated state due to EE could have skewed my perception of things. So my apologies ahead of time if you end up going here and it's disgusting.

My pita was pretty good. My girl's salad was blah.

The food is good and our waitress was nice, but with the name like Pita Jungle, I expected a place that specialized in pitas. Chicken Pita, Pizza Pita, Brisket Pita, Desert Pita! This place was just a Greek Restaurant . If I am in the mood for Greek I will be going elsewhere.


Things to note:

I could not tell who worked there, There was no uniform what so ever. Maybe it was Halloween night, but there was a Cowboy with a Metallica shirt, a girl with a tank top that had her bra sticking out, a guy with his worn through shirt tucked in only under his gut. If there was a uniform, I would guess it was "Dress as if you just woke up"

The Baklava is made with something like condensed  Rose water and Orange blossom. It may sound like something cool, but its kind of gross and dry.
Yum...ordered steak crepe....its ok...maybe i should have tried the sweets.

Zios is the best! Their pizza, cheesesteaks, hoagies, and especially their pasta dishes are delicious! They also have great Italian specialties like arancini. They are a vital part of Midtown Village.

Feeling daring on a recent lunchtime stroll through downtown, I popped into the Hub.  Only open six weeks, I hadn't heard anything good or bad about the place but it's trendy exterior and sharp interior pulled me right in.  You have two choices, sit down at a table for a meal or walk up to the counter for just some ice cream.  I chose to do the latter, having already had lunch.  

The host was generous in her samples, and I decided to be adventurous... Bacon ice cream?  Why not?  It was not my favorite flavor, but I was glad I tried it.  The sample of the salted caramel was delicious, but I settled on the mint cookie ice cream which was wonderful.  The host informed me that all their ice cream is homemade, making the $2.75 pricetag for my single scoop seem reasonable.

I think I will be back again, and this time go for lunch as well!

UPDATE:  I have since been back to the HUB several times, and every time I am more and more impressed.  First off, the owner of the restaurant sent me an email to thank me for my review.  The fact that the owners take the time to read the reviews of the customers, and care about the feedback, says a lot about this place.  

One visit, I happened to make small talk with a woman standing near the ice cream counter who had a baby who looked to be about the age of my son (turns out they share the same birthday!).  Also turns out, she's one of the owners.  She recognized me from my Yelp picture and asked if I was Kate L., having just read the review.  Again, goes back to caring about their product and what folks having to say about their establishment.  

As a result of this, I'm changing my review from 4 stars to 5!  Thanks HUB for adding a great spot to Downtown Tucson.

I've heard great reviews about their food, a friend recently recommended their Chicken Pot Pie!  I'm going to be heading over there for lunch today... if you see me, say hi!

Happy hour at Blanco is pretty darn stellar.  I frequent Blanco quite a bit because my son is a huge fan of their guac and taquitos.  The service is always fantastic and tonight was no exception.   Nikki was our awesome server!  She was helpful and was always smiling. My friend Judith and I had to take advantage of the half off margaritas.  Judith had the blood orange and wished she had gotten a second.  I had the skinny margarita, which Nikki recommended and it was delicious! Organic tequila + agave nectar, +lime juice+ soda = one refreshing and super tasty margarita.  We enjoyed their guacamole for our starter.  I always squeeze some lime juice on my chips and salt them a bit. Nikki brought us a bowl of warm chips and the combo of the fresh chips and guacamole was perfection.  For dinner, Judith and I shared the machaca beef tacos. My son had his regular, beef taquitos. I loved our tacos.  Blanco is generous with a split plate and still gives a generous portion of rice and beans.  I must mention that Blanco has the best black beans, as they are full of flavor!  I think our bill was around $30.  Not too shabby!  We left absolutely satisfied.

Updated on April 2, 2016.

The food is better than ever and the value of a meal here is among the highest in the city.  The kitchen has a new tandoor oven, imported from the UK, and the owner is proud to show it off.  As frequent customers he gave us a "tour".  The kitchen continues to turn out superb cooking served by an attentive and friendly staff in comfortable surroundings.  The restaurant is a BYO and currently offers a discount for customers paying cash.  They do accept cards as well.

Superb food ranging across the entire menu.  Our party of four had chicken, lamb, shrimp, vegetarian, appetizers, nan, etc.  All the flavors were transparent and extremely satisfying.  In these early days (they opened a few weeks ago) there might be a temptation on the part of the owners to temper the spiciness a little and emphasize the sweetness, but as more people discover the restaurant there might be a slight shift.  Nonetheless, the sauces were absolutely wonderful and seemed not to spare the ghee!  Dessert, which was complimentary, was also delicious.  The prices are a bit higher than the usual West Philadelphia establishments but less than Tiffen (which ruins many dishes by over-salting), Bindi (too nouvelle for my taste) and other Center City restaurants.  The vegetable samosa was the best I've had in the city, filled with moist vegetables and not over-fried.

A second visit confirmed our first one.  The food is marvelous, the flavors wonderful.  The buija (onion fritters) and chana chat appetizers were superb.

Update:  Am downgrading the food.  I don't know if there has been a chef and/or management change but the quality of the food has declined.

I have really mixed feelings about this place.  Let me tell you why.

I come here to experience the trendy and hipster vibe of the local french cafe.  This is in place, check.

I come here for delicious french-inspired yum, check.

So while the two most important things were met, there was some lacking.  First of all, my french sandwich was really good, but really small.  It was also I think the 2nd most expensive item on the menu and not very filling.  If I come for brunch, I want to leave full.

Also, some details were lacking.  I ordered orange juice and I am not joking a carton and a straw are placed in front of me.  WTF?  You could at least pour it into a glass even if you aren't gonna squeeze it yourself.

I dunno.  If I'm gonna keep coming to a place I gotta leave satisfied.  The OJ carton and straw and still being hungry just didn't do it for me.  It's really a shame, too, because the sandwich was genuinely good.  I hate to give a low rating to a place with good food, but it is what was earned.

In fairness, the servers were nice and it's not like I had a horrible time.  There was just nothing that made me want to come back.

Tried this bakery by ordering one of each item in the display case...okay, that is a minor exaggeration ...but only minor. We went specifically so that my Celiac daughter, home on Spring Break, could relax and point to anything in the store and enjoy it.
We went on a Tuesday at lunch-time and they still had plenty of variety.  I recognized the woman who waited on us as a co-owner, from the photo on the bakery's website.  She was friendly and efficient.
We ate thumbprint cookies, cupcakes, sandwich cookies, a brownie sample and a couple of slices of tomato pie.  All were delicious. We ate the tomato pie at room-temperature but it would have been better warmed.  
We washed everything down with herb tea.  I love herb tea but coffee would have also have been welcome here.  I still gave this place 5 stars because the food is luscious.

I went to the Hub on a Saturday night and it was a hoppin' place!  It is very hip and I liked the layout!  The kitchen had already closed by the time we arrived (10:30pm), however they had drink specials that were nice to take advantage of.  My friend and I decided to have wine and I was set on having ice cream.  I loved being able to enjoy wine and delicious ice cream at same place.  My friend enjoyed the red blend ($5 glass) and I enjoyed the chardonnay ($5).  Moving on to the ice cream... sampled many flavors before I could make a decision.  I tried the toasted marshmallow (liked it), honey ginger (was not a fan), salted caramel (enjoyed it), etc.  I chose the oatmeal cookie dough and it was so yummy!  Looking forward to my next trip to the Hub.  I would like to have lunch there and would absolutely repeat a wine and ice cream night!

I was beyond excited when I heard the second King of Tandoor was opening in my neighborhood.  I ordered the Chicken Tikka Masala and my BF got the Motor Panir.  We ordered naan to accompany.  Everything was very flavorful.  I could have used more rice, we only got one portion to split. 

I would give a higher rating but the food caused stomach issues to my BF the next day.  I was fine the next day but the leftovers made my stomach hurt too.  I enjoyed the way everything tasted but something was perhaps not fresh enough, I am guessing the dairy since I ordered chicken and my BF got a vegetarian dish.  I hope they are just ironing out some wrinkles and they'll step it up a little.

Gotta love a place that knows what you want before you even ask. This is especially clutch when you're only half awake in the morning trying to get your day started with a tasty brew of Black Gold ASAP. It's even more impressive when you come down for your afternoon brace and they're already making your Americano.

Combine that great service and good coffee with some tasty desserts like their brownies, coffee cakes, carrot cakes, and cookies from 4th Street Deli, you've got a regular coffee spot you can rely on during a long week at work.

Thanks Passero's!

Date night, quiet night by the water, dinner after a long trip, then stop by Oystercatchers.  I felt as if I had driven to the Keys.  The moment I passed the hotel I felt that Tampa was miles away.  
Make reservations, then try to sit outside. Sit at the bar until a seat outside becomes available.  Try not to sit too close to the door, the constant open and close of the door can be distracting if your are sitting on the deck by the door.  However, a small price to pay for the view (bring your camera).  The calamari fries are truly something to have...amazing!  Crispy and fresh, seasoned lightly and accompanied by sauce enhancing the flavor with a little kick. Grilled grouper and grilled salmon.  The grouper was light and  flaky and not overly season the true grouper taste was able to come through. The salmon was a bit dry.  Order medium rare and I think it should be quite good.  Accompanied by a Chardonnay, Jordan, Russia Valley 2009.  The evening was amazing and thoroughly enjoyed my experience.  Oh, the airplanes are mildly annoying, but again the price to pay for sitting outside and being in a city.

Have only been here twice for takeout, but very good each time. Tandoori mixed grill expensive ($15) but generous. They threw in pudding and bread with my orders. 

Four stars b/c it's kind of pricey, but a welcome addition to the neighborhood.

God I love this place. 
I used to eat here a lot. At least once per week. I always get the exact same thing and it is always consistently fantastic. 
The servers here know how to make you feel special - I don't go in as often anymore (sometimes with a space of 2-3 months in between visits), but each time they do go in my sever (no matter who it is) always knows exactly what I want and how I want it. Even if it's someone who hasn't served me before! 
Little customer service things like that are what really makes the experience there special.

AMAZING! I love sandwiches, and I have been meaning to go ever since I saw my friend eat one for lunch. I got the chicken cutlet diablo. Hello spicy fried goodness! My bf got the beef diablo and it was fresh and amazing as well (i haven't had roast beef that good in a long time...sry jimmy johns, subways, etc). The $1 insanely huge pickle I bought on the side was great too.  Yes, the key word is: great. My other half of my sandwich is sitting in my fridge (the primo size you get a massive hoagie for a great price) ...it is fearfully awaiting to be eaten STAT once I am hungry again.

The place was packed when I went, but I got my sandwich super fast (about 3-5 minutes). I highly recommend. No place to sit, but we strolled to Rittenhouse to eat on the benches :)
This truly is the best NYC style pizza in Tampa. Crust, sauce, cheese, toppings - it's all good! I work downtown and I'm the kids' hero when I bring home an Eddie & Sams pizza.

Super Salads, Perfect Pizzas and the Stuffed Bell Pepper is just a little spicy with Daniel Scordato's perfect marinara.  The Antipasto is consistently fresh and delicious and the baked oysters are delectable.  Great wine list, nice ambiance and reasonable prices.  Not fancy, just plain good!  Now renamed Scordato's Pizzeria after Danny Scordato to avoid the confusion over two similarly named restaurants in the same plaza.

When it is 94 degrees in Tucson, Allegro starts to call my name.  Who am I kidding?  When it is cold and raining, Allegro is calling my name!  Their gelato is one of my absolute favorite sweet treats.  So this afternoon, I stopped at Allegro with my favorite kid to get my favorite gelato. I have been here numerous times between my first review and this review (my punch card is one away from being filled) however, I didn't feel a need to update as it continues to merit a 5-star review.  I also have been rather fixated on getting the pistachio and cream.  Mind you, I try lots of flavors and enjoy them but then always resort to ordering my two staple flavors.  My son has inherited the "I just want the same thing I always order" gene from me so he sticks with the cream.  Today I am pleased to say that I switched my order up!  I even gave the rose flavored gelato a sample but that was not my pick.  Today I had the strawberry and orange and vanilla.  The nice fella behind the counter is totally cool with people ordering three flavors in a small size.  I appreciate that because some other ice cream shops (which shall remain nameless) have this silly rule of a small only merits one flavor, but if you order a medium you are allowed two.  As always, we left Allegro in a stellar mood. 
 Warning- their gelato will add to your happiness!

What a unique experience here... The menu and the decor! So inventive and original! Who doesn't love a place with fuchsia uplighting, gymp covered chairs (yes like the kind you used to make things out of in camp), tons of Mexican wrestler masks, and a eating in car?  

We went around 8 pm on a Friday with no reservations and didn't even wait 5 minutes. We were seated and presented with a bowl of spicy chilli peanuts with cilantro... So yummy! We started with margaritas of course (I had a virgin pineapple because I'm preggers for only $3 and my husband had a honeydew which was $13. They were delish and only served on the rocks, no frozen ones here). 

I wasn't too hungry which was great because this is a small plates place. I had a fabulous soup, possibly one of the best soups I've ever had. It was sugared pumpkin and was offered with lump crab and roasted crushed walnuts on top! It was amazing!! Different and I would never think to put those ingredients together, but it really worked! Then I had the Mexican city style quesadilla filled with mushrooms and pure yumminess! Don't think big cheese quesadilla this was small and more like a tostada, but really enough food and really tasty. They do have some great vegetarian options!! My second choice would have been the grilled cactus, black bean, crispy quinoa and salsa tacos. 

My husband had a pot of refried beans and the skirt steak tacos which came 3 to a plate and were smaller, but he still wound up taking one home. 

Compared to those around us, we barely ate! The plates just kept coming for everyone! The salads, flatbreads and veggie nachos looked great too! Everything looked so interesting! Everything on the dessert menu looked good too! We were tempted by the flan with caramel, gala apples, toasted hazelnut with roasted apple sorbet, but we went with the classic churros to keep it traditional. They were so perfect soft, hot, and sugary! They came with two dipping sauces and were so moist I kept checking to see if there was cream inside. Yum! 

The waiters were very much in a rush and the hostesses weren't super friendly, but the food made up for it and the music was real good! 

Street parking was fabulously easy! Tons of spots, tons!  

It was a very mixed crowd... Older couples, college kids, even families, but mostly groups of young professionals. 

We intend on going back and will even bring our two year old to sit and eat in car which looked so fun! 

A really great time was had! We surely recommend it!

Nice to have a new dining option in the neighborhood - especially one where I can get healthy (ish) food.  Thumbs up on the food - I got the Mediterranean plate.  The baba ganoush was a standout, and the tabbouleh was good, too.  

This place is good - and it feels like it is 85% of its potential, and it would require some easy tweaks to get there.  It just feels like it is having a bit of an identity crisis, and here are my observations about why:  

1. The decor. I liked the decor - very nice job with a modern vibe - but the upholstery choice on the banquettes is hideous and does not fit the theme (very bright flowers, kinda retro-hippie).  

2. Staff attire.  Serving staff were all wearing street clothes - mostly cutoff shorts and tank tops.  The lack of uniform made it difficult to distinguish staff from guests who might be on their way to the restroom.  I get that they are going for a casual feel - but a nice PJ t-shirt and khaki shorts would accomplish that and maintain a consistent and professional look.  There was a manager lurking around with jeans and an un-tucked button-up shirt - he looked very sloppy.

3.  Service.  Service was friendly and on-time, and overall appropriate.  However, the server did flub up at the end of the meal.  In our party of 3, two of us finished first, and the plates were removed.  My friend was still eating her salad (literally had food in her mouth) when he came up to ask if she was done/wanted a box?  Then he hurried the check to the table without asking if we might be interested in desert (which we would have been).  A missed opportunity for a higher tab, but sort of mistake would be easily fixed with some more training.  Again, I will point out that the service was friendly and inviting.

I am sure that I will be back here often, it is a nice hangout.

Went here with co-workers for lunch.  And although service was a little slow, we were treated very well and given complimentary appetizers.  The food was excellent and worth the wait.  Not traditionally what one expects from an Indian restaurant but a lot healthier.  Well worth checking out.
The food was good and the setting was very nice.  Service, on the other hand, was spotty.  Given the price being charged, this rates 3 stars.

The service in the dining room is great.  On my last visit I had the opportunity to not only interact with our waiter but also listen to the other wait staff as the place was sparsely populated.  Everyone was friendly and helpful.  They know the menu and make suggestions.  The wait staff seem to enjoy working there. 

The menu is a little too much the same for my taste.  Variety is the spice of life so I wish it would change more often.  Most of the entrees  have been on the menu for too long.  As for the quality it is generally good.   Another review mentioned too much salt, and I've experienced the same problem with a couple of different dishes.  I do like the fact that you can put a whole wheat crust on your pizza though.

Good happy hour specials and half-price bottles of wine on Tuesday.   Overall it's a good place to go on occasion.

Stopped in here for dinner a few days ago and was not disappointed!! Wood fired, hot and sizzling is how our pizzas came out! Tried the Margharita and the Robiola,Fontina and Mozarella plus with salads and wine. Food and ambiance was fantastic! Perfect for our party of 4! Another little Tucson gem!

Pho Palace is well worth a visit.  There were 4 of us and we shared 2 appetizers (Spring & Summer rolls) - both were very good.  Three of the group tried Pho and all enjoyed it; none thinking that the broth was weak as some others have said in their reviews.  I had the Bun Thit Nuong - Grilled sliced pork and cucumber over rice vermicelli with fish sauce.  It was just as good as some that I've tried on Washington Ave. in Philly.  Before tax & tip, the bill per couple was around $18 

The staff was very friendly and we had a good time talking to our server who was originally from Vietnam.  It's a BYO - Cash Only.  We're looking forward to returning when we will try such items as Fried Quails and an interesting sounding Baked Calamari salad.

Service is very slow, whether crowded or sparse. This is usually an indicator that the food prep and distribution process is not too efficient. Food is meh. Have tried both chicken and beef dishes, too salty and dry. Having been in the middle east and Med regions many times, thanks to a butthead named Saddam Hussein, I think I know the cuisine; this place is to that food as Panda Express is to Chinese. Save your money for Ali Baba or Falafel King. Much better experience.

Just back from another great lunch at Frankie's.  I'm full, happy and feel like I can take on the world.  I have a little bit of Rocky in me after visiting the Philadelphia's Embassy on Campbell. 

The Amaroso's bread that comes in regularly is delicious. And whether you go cheesesteak with whiz or a turkey hoagie, you can't go wrong.  If you are lucky, you can spend a few minutes chatting up Frankie, himself.

The prices are reasonable, the food is good, and it's a place you will want to return to.  Also, check out the special deals at the Tucson originals website!

Bought a used bike here a few weeks ago and took it in today for a free tune-up.  They were great!  Super helpful and friendly.  I'll definitely be back for all my biking needs (which I assume will be many!)
Cute cafe with plenty of outdoor seating.  I love that they offer a 10 oz. size to-go cup, which is perfect for a mid-day latte that won't keep me up all night!

I eat Paleo, which means mostly meat and veggies, not much carb, so Mex can be a challenge. I asked for fajitas with no tortillas or rice or beans, and once the waitress clarified, she offered me a side salad. The fresh grilled onions and jalapenos were a treat, and the fajitas were good - not outstanding but very well executed. Huge drink cups (water!) and friendly service. It's a little loud, but I would be comfortable bringing kids here. Decent guac as a starter. Not a 5-star joint, but good, solid Mexican food with real people in a friendly atmosphere. Yum!

Best Bloody Mary's in Philly...no joke.  You can't go wrong with the brunch at JB's, especially the Gospel Brunch, which takes place every second Sunday of the month.  A chill atmosphere combined with great friends and awesome food...you seriously can't go wrong here!

i had forgotten about this place, until i saw a chris pronger interview where he mentioned green eggs and ham...  and voila, green eggs was back on my radar.  

i've done the sweet many-a-time (pancakes, peanut butter french toast...), but this time, i went for the kitchen sink.  it's a fat girl's dream come true.  biscuits and sausage gravy, eggs, onions and peppers smothered with cheese in a cast iron skillet.  reminds me of hash house a-go-go, but (only slightly) miniaturized.  i am so moving up to nolibs.  just so i can eat brunch here.

kudos to the sheer brilliancy of the place for offering a carafe of OJ with *complimentary* bottle of champagne for $30.  liquor laws be damned, right?

This is a great restaurant! It has a classic old style feel to it and the food is amazing. The bread they bring you is the best I have ever had and is served warm with garlic powder on top. Most entrees are served with a house or ceased salad, I like the house. The menu is varied and features steaks, seafood, traditional Italian dishes and several chicken dishes so there is something for everyone. Two of my favorites are the broiled salmon and a pasta dish with spicy tomato sauce and crab meat. They also have excellent sangria that is topped off with cinnamon.
Prices start at around $10 for a simple pasta to $20 plus for some of the steaks but most items are in the 13.99 to 16.99 which given the high quality bread and salad that is included is not bad. They have a large bar area and an outdoor bar on the back of the building upstairs I have not yet gone to. Service is great I highly recommend Iavarone's!

Please read this knowing that I am stingy with my five star ratings, plus I am reviewing three different experiences, with each one getting a different rating for an average of four stars. 

Experience #1 - this place had just opened up a stone's throw from our house. My wife and I are always looking for new places to try that won't break our budget. We stopped in on a Saturday and saw that they had a slice and drink special for, if memory serves, $3.50. We both order that. We get our slices, hers with pepperoni and mine with sausage. The first thing we notice is the pool of grease on top of the pizza. Once some of that is wiped away, we proceed. OK... I am not blown away, but it's a slice and I tell myself I will reserve judgment until I can taste a whole, fresh pizza. Rating for this trip: 3 stars.

Experience #2 - Delivery order... I call and ask if they are currently having any specials. I am told they have two large two toppings for $25. There are four of us and that's probably too much but one is not enough, so I go with that. I place my order and they tell me it will be 25-30 minutes. I am starving... perfect! ONE HOUR LATER and still no pizza. OK... I figure it's a fairly new place so I will cut them some slack. I am getting ready to call when the doorbell rings. The driver tells me he got lost. Our house is literally less than a mile from this place, so unless he is on foot wandering through our neighborhood, it's a little hard to swallow. On to the pizza... glad I gave them another chance after the slice visit. The pizza is great. It's still hot (this is why I had trouble buying the driver's story) and very cheesy, as pizza should be, IMO. The crust is crispy on the outside and soft on the inside. Their large pizzas are very large and one would have been enough for four of us. We have a whole pizza left. That's fine... it won't go to waste in my house. Rating for this trip: 4 stars

Experience #3  - Learning from the last experience, I allow plenty of time for delivery. I call and, again, ask about specials. I am told they have none. So I order one large pizza (since I know this will be enough food) with two toppings. My total: around $16. I am OK with this... BUT I am informed they now charge $2 for delivery. I grumble a little but I am too lazy to go out. Fine, I'll pay the two bucks. I am promised pizza in 20 minutes... yeah right. FIFTEEN MINUTES LATER... the doorbell. It's the pizza guy. Holy Pepperoni! How on earth could they make a pizza and get it to my doorstep in 15 minutes!? I pay the guy and we dig in to our pizza. It's piping hot (I look outside to see if there is an unfamiliar pizza oven in the front yard) and, again, delicious. Despite the two bucks I am happy: 5 stars. All three visits average 4 stars.

Lessons learned: 
1. Ordering a slice of pizza is kind of like that first kiss. It may be kind of a mess, but you are pretty sure going back and investigating the whole package will be worth your while.
2. Put a "pizza flag" in front of your house. You don't want your driver getting lost when your starving and stuck waiting for your pizza.

Had Troy for Thursday night Beginners Yoga and he rocked my sweat off. I really enjoyed his approach of not being competitive with others in the class but concentrating on your own practice and getting the most of your time there. He really emphasized that it was okay to make mistakes and to not worry about it because it would be adding stress when yoga should be doing the opposite of that. It really centered me. And I took 3 people who are new to yoga with me and they all loved the class as well. He was good to correct when needed and challenged individuals who were more advanced beginners. 

I find that newbies often comment on how it's hard to follow the moves because many times the names of poses are called out and you have to look around to figure out what's going on. I find that's true for 9/10 beginner classes I've gone to in all different studios. Unless you sign up for a 10 week beginners course, often the instructors are not doing all the moves with you so you have to rely on those attending to see what the moves are. I think this way of learning can be a little distressing, but once you realize that that's just how it is, it's okay to take a deep breath, look around to see what the move should look like, and take a stab at it. You're not cheating. :) And he'll come over and tweak you. 

This studio is great for those who like a very heated studio but not as hot as Bikram. 

I totally recommend this place and especially this class-- you spend a lot of time perfecting your stances instead of trying to cram many moves into one time period. If you're more advanced you may benefit from coming, but you won't be as challenged (we didn't do binding, or very many complicated variations of poses)-- an all levels class at this place would suit you better.

We love this place (for happy hour) and can usually get out of there with a couple of drinks, each, and an appetizer or two for around $25. That's why we love the place. It's our fallback place for happy hour. We don't dine there so much but have on occasion.

We have had most of the appetizers on the menu and what we have had has been fine. I only give it three stars because the actual food (from the menu) can be hit or miss. 

To elaborate: We went on Valentine's day. We got there early enough to have  our first round of drinks at happy hour prices so that's a plus. I ordered the short rib and my wife ordered the special, a surf and turf with a NY strip and grilled shrimp. My short rib was advertised with roasted shallots and Risotto Milanese. What could be better than slow roasted meat with delicious roasted shallots over a bed of creamy risotto? I was excited... only to be extremely let down. OK... the short rib was cooked properly, very tender. The accompanying sauce was way to sweet. I think it was a port wine reduction, which would explain the sweetness. This could have been counterbalanced with some salt, which seemed to be absent completely. I detest having to add salt to my food at the table. The risotto was warm, not hot, and pretty bland as well. My wife's steak and shrimp came with mashed potatoes. She had no complaints but did not rave either. Even with the first drinks at happy hour prices, we still spent over $100. Maybe it's just my opinion, but if you spend over $100 for dinner you should be blown away and I was not.

We have had a couple of pizzas here and those seemed to be very salty, but that is likely due to the pepperoni/salami combination. So... you see my point on the food being hit and miss.

I first discovered Dogfish Head 90 Minute IPA (it's now my favorite beer) at this place and that has kept me coming back. They have not had it for a while and say they may not have it "ever again".I think they are having a spat with the distributor or something.

We'll continue to do happy hour there. I will have to try every beer they have to see if I can find a new favorite. Darn... that's gonna be a tough job!

I've been here for lunch twice now and have thoroughly enjoyed it both times.  I bet dinner would be excellent as well - with a whole different room vibe too.  I hope to get here for that experience in the near future.  The room is interesting yet pretty simple and the seating is comfortable (I'm 6'2'' 230lbs), even if it is a bit curious.  The service is professional, friendly, and efficient - no qualms there. 

With respect to food, it's typical Janos Wilder, in my opinion.  The food uses very fresh ingredients that are combined in unique ways to create beautiful mingling of tastes and textures - all presented in an artful fashion.  Yes, some may say the portions are small, but they are satiating and quality costs - I'll take this over a pile of mediocre food any day.   The costs for the meal are at the higher end for lunch, but are well worth it and the place makes a great "special lunch" destination.  Items that we have really enjoyed are: Lily's Laotian Chicken + Pounded Green Papaya Salad, Seared Ahi on English muffins, the Spicy Minted Lamb Sausage and Pea Coulis on Paparadelle, and the Bread Pudding and coffee for dessert.  They may sound simple at first blush, but when you read the detailed description and taste the food you'll be amazed at the complexities and subtleties in each dish.  It's great to see this and other exciting restaurants appearing in downtown Tucson.

We had been looking forward to the opening of Talula's Garden for weeks and were incredibly excited to get an opening weekend reservation on Friday night.  Our excitement grew even more when the friends joining us for dinner got engaged on the way to dinner!  Needless to say, we were ready to celebrate; however, as a group, we were fully underwhelmed. 

The night started out promising with a welcoming hello by the hostess and beautiful ambiance, but went down from there.  The litany of shortcomings:  

Drinks- It took ten minutes to order a drink.  When we asked the server questions about the cocktails and beers, she was unknowledgeable and condescending, telling us to look at the menu (and reading off of it herself). When she seemed unsure, she said, "It's as local as you can get!"  

Cheese plate- Ordered it because of raves about the cheese monger.  The cheese was amazingly delicious. But again, both our server and the woman who delivered it were unsure of the selections and read off the menu to describe the cheeses.  Additionally, no vehicle was delivered to eat the cheese on.  We were given a cheese knife and a tiny jar of honey.  

Timing/Delivery- It took ten minutes to order drinks, ten minutes for our drinks to arrive, then another ten minutes to order an appetizer just because our server kept disappearing. We were having a leisurely time chatting, when our cheese plate arrived.  Less than five minutes later, our entire meal was delivered.  Brioche with ramp butter is promised with the meal; however, ours was delivered after our meal.  

Food- The food was tasty- perfect for a small plate, but too small for a meal. We ordered the lobster & pork belly (delicious!), the chicken (left something to be desired- dry chicken with disappointing risotto), and the beef & short rib (the short rib was to die for; the potato foam added nothing).  We were asked if "medium rare" was okay for the beef, to which we requested "medium" and "medium well".  However, when the meals were delivered, both came out medium rare.  Additionally, the local ingredients idea, while something we fully support, is incredibly forced and not so slyly tucked into every interaction and menu description.  

The atmosphere is gorgeous; the concept is lovely. The food was good, but not worth the build up. Hopefully Starr and Olexy tweak some details because they've got a great idea in Talula's Garden.  We can't wait for warmer weather and will definitely return to sit in the garden and enjoy a cocktail.
3.5 stars. Solid food, service and atmosphere. Enjoyed the steak salad and tortilla soup. Will return to try more. A little on the pricey side, though.

Astoundingly delicious.  Tried crema, cioccolato and zabaglione.  Over three subsequent visits, we tried biscotti,nocciola, cioccolato e menta,stracciatella, and limone sorbetto...all fantastic.Very intense flavors and just the right creaminess.  The guy had watermelon sorbet which was the best sorbet I have ever tasted.  The line moves very slowly as everyone is trying samples before they make their scoop decision, so try to figure out when the busiest times might be.  We were there at 9:45 on a Saturday night and the line was long.

Anytime I go to the south side of town I eat here. The tacos are phenomenal!! 

I've had breakfast here and wasn't impressed but lunch is amazing. I highly recommend the shredded beef tacos on flour tortillas with beans and rice. The salsa is so hot and my FAV!!

This place is one of my go-to lunch spots.  It's quick, tasty, and pretty darn cheap.  It also helps that it's so near my work, I can cut down on travel time during my limited lunch breaks.

I'm not really a huge fan of Mexican food (I know, I know... Why did I choose to live in Tucson, then?), but I love me some Pico de Gallo.

The fish tacos are rockin' and the sides are pretty great to boot.  I won't wax poetic on the amazing flour tortillas or the sure deliciousness of the seafood, because clearly I'm not alone in that opinion.

The portion sizes are also great for what you pay, and everything tastes so fresh.  Plus the salsa rocks my socks off.

Had a later reservation here on Saturday night...and it was really good! The decor was really cute, though I wish it was nicer out so I could have seen the outside area. We had a quick drink at the bar before sitting at our table. If your a margarita fan I recommend The Farmer, it was delicious! 

The brioche rolls they give you to start are AMAZING, I wish they were larger though they had no problem giving us multiple helpings! We got one of the cheese platters and it was really good, my only complaint was they didnt really give you anything to eat the cheese with....I thought that was weird. The pieces were on these tiny crispy cracker, but not really ideal when your sharing with a couple people. As starters we got the spring salad of herbs flowers and lettuces. Pretty good, great dressing and very fresh tasting. We also got the Ricotta Gnudi, which kind of tasted like little fresh mozzarella balls. They were this amazing sweet tasting onion broth type of thing. Highly recommend trying! 

For our entrees our table got 2 orders of the beef and braised rib, the chicken, and the halibut. All were excellent. I found the chicken to be a tad dry and a little boring, but my friend enjoyed it. The steak was out of this world. They halibut was very light and fresh. We also got the beets and new potatoes as sides. Both also good. 

For desert we had the doughnuts, a dark chocolate thing with homemade marshmellows and a banana napoleon sort of thing. Once again all delicious! But I think the chocolate one and the doughnuts were my favorite. 

All in all a very good meal, though our bill came to about $90 per person which was a little steep though we got a lot of food. And the food was so fresh and light that I felt full but not uncomfortable. Cant wait to go back for brunch!

Granted, I didn't try the pizza, but what I did have - the chicken caesar salad and the Italian hoagie - was absolutely delicious. The bread was incredibly fresh and soft & the caesar dressing just right. I usually pick up a few things while I'm downtown to hold me over for a few days, so I didn't taste the hoagie til the next day, but its roll was still as soft and fresh as the roll I'd had with the salad the previous day.

There was a bit of a fuss because I ordered online at 4:06 (my receipt was time stamped) for a 5:15 pickup, and realized around 4:10, that my Groupon/Eversave/LivingSocial voucher might not be valid on a pickup order. It said "valid for eat-in only after 5pm," and I had originally read it as for eat-in it would only be valid during certain hours. Whatever, I had JUST placed the order, so it should be easy to cancel, right... well, when I called at 4:12 (of course I was looking at the clock), the owner told me that they had already made the food and he didn't want to throw it out, so I would be allowed to use my voucher.

Kudos for allowing me to use the voucher, and he was really nice about it, but, like, aside from it being highly unlikely that my order was completed within 6 minutes of placing it, why the hell would you make a 5:15 order at 4:10??? The pointless lie annoys me even more than the fact that I didn't realize it made no sense until I hung up the phone.

"gluten free, dairy free, egg free, corn free, wheat free, peanut free, soy free, casein free." (also, no refined sugars)

I have no aversion to any of the things that Sweet Freedom manages to bake without, but we went in anyway (because I am a Food Network junkie). The 3 year old in my charge chose a pink cupcake ($3.50)... I tried to point her to the cupcake tops that were right next to them ($2), but she was insistent, so we got the cupcake. This child literally licked the icing, sat the cupcake down, and said "I'm done."
