I wanted to choke her and dropkick myself, for at that moment I realized I was just like everyone else, and had allowed a pre-schooler convince me to go against my better judgement. This may be my biggest shame to date.

It was a win, though, because my sandwich was absolutely and completely shockingly delicious, PLUS the chatter was lively, and I learned to put pipe-cleaners in my hair. 

P.S. - The Brit in the 3-pc suit at the next table was absolutely charming, and I realize I wasn't the only one who thought as much, as J (the very quiet little one) decided to wave goodbye during his exit.

Holy Guacamole!  I had  no idea that places like this still existed.   I remember going out to eat for under $20 back in the day, but these days you have to skimp to come in under $20 for 2 people.  The prices here are unbelievable.   We started with the guacamole to go with our chips and salsa.  Real hand made guac. I did find that I wished for a little spice, maybe some cilantro and garlic, but it was fresh with perfectly ripe avocados.  I sprinkled a little salt in it when no one was looking.   The main course was chicken enchiladas with green sauce for me and a carne asada plate for my husband.  Both tasty but again, a little more bland than I prefer.  Pretty sure I got out the salt shaker again.   

We did have 2 stellar experiences here.  One was the flan.  It was AH-MAY-ZING!  So creamy with a perfect consistency.   It literally melts in your mouth.  The other was the service.   The entire wait staff was attentive and on top of it.  We were greeted quickly, served quickly and our table was cleared quickly.  While we were looking over the menu my husband asks "can you see what beers they have" so I turn to look towards the kitchen area.  No tap so I'm looking for a cooler, but I can't see it.  Before I can even get turned around to report on my observations there is a server at my side asking me if I need something.  Totally taken back by a server that observes behavior and deduces there is a need I stammer something about beers and kinds and my husband, and I'm sure it made no sense.  No worries though as he was not deterred. He immediately began listing all beers and then even went into margaritas and other drinks.  Of course, I'm still reeling from shock so my brain isn't functioning yet.  I just sit there staring at him in amazement.  Husband to the rescue though - he orders a beer. 

So let's recount....chips and salsa, order of guacamole,  2 entrees complete with rice/beans, a beer (I never did really recover so I stuck with water) and a flan to share for twenty bucks and some change.  Impressive.  My only regret is that we decided to share the flan. Next time I order my own!

"Talula...Talula...I don't want to lose her
she must be worth losing if it is worth something"
I hummed the melody of this tune as I passed through the gate into Steven Starr's most recent venture.

Talula's Garden is a theme park. When you first enter the property, you will be impressed by the fantasy-like outdoor garden that they have set up. This is going to be a huge hotspot once the weather gets warmer. The decor is Alice in Wonderland meets humble farming home. The ambiance is honed to perfection...and your ass is going to pay for it. Talula's Garden is not a place that you go for dinner. It is a place that you go TO IMPRESS SOMEONE.

I dined here for a special occasion so we went all-out. As a result, I got to try a wide variety of dishes and here are my opinions on them:

The sturgeon rillette is basically a fish pate'. It was creamy, not too salty, pretty darn good.

The Rabbit Lasagnetta was kinda plain.

The Velouté is a scrumptious pea/squid soup with squid ink. It was creamy and squid-y and great. This was my favorite appetizer of the bunch.

The Caramelized Maine Sea Scallops are prepped well and have a light outside crisp from the searing that I really liked.

Beef and braised rib - OK this rib is the best piece of beef rib I've ever had. It was insanely tender. That being said, it was about the size of a fun-size Snickers bar. The beef was prepped to a satisfactory medium-rare but really couldn't hold a candle to the rib that it shared a plate with.

The marinated halibut with artichoke was fine.

Onto dessert...

Rhubarb Gateau - a rhubarb cake. I had never had rhubarb before and I liked it. It was kinda tangy with a citrus hint that was balanced out by the cake part of the dessert. It was fine.

Chocolate Creameaux - Basically dark chocolate with bacon powder and some other magic inside of it. Pretty good.

Anise Doughnuts - Served with a raspberry sauce(if memory serves me correctly...which it doesn't always do), this was the best dessert of the lot.

--

Look, there are other places in Philadelphia where you can get a better dinner for a lower price. That being said, the ambiance and service are so overwhelmingly good that I cannot dislike Talula's Garden at all. It is expensive and the portions are hilarious but it is what one would expect at a theme park.

On the action-movie-parallel-satisfaction-scale, this place is Pirates of the Caribbean.
I've eaten Indian food in 6 countries, including India, and our dinner here was perfectly delicious.

I loved this place
I hated this place
It is a local restaurant with a lot of character
The woman on the phone was "put out" with my questions
It is clean
The Sicilian pizza was more suited for California Kitchen
They cut their pizza in squares, not triangles
The restrooms were dirty
They used real sausage
$50.00 seemed a lot for 3 small pizzas and 4 slices of cheesecake
The pizza was good
The pizza was not great
And best of all
But at least
They had Eli's Cheesecake
They had Eli's Cheesecake

I've been driving by this place, wanting to check it out. On this rare Monday night out, I finally did. And It was so so so delicious! I've lived in India for a little while and I have had lots of Indian food. This one was one of the best I've had. We ordered Eggplant spread(not so Indian name but it comes w/ Nans and eggplant pureed made with Indian spices) and I got Chef's special Chicken dish... Just remembering how good the dish was making my mouth water. My friend got chicken tikka marsala and loved it! Friendly staff, amazing food and the special deal of 1 entree and 2nd entree 50% on mondays. On top of that, it's BYO! I think I found a new favorite place in Manayunk..

I've tried this place many times, and still don't care for it. I'd suggest going to the bar if anything; but as far as eating at a "table", I'd pass. While I respect the thought behind it, being forced to ring a bell to get in is a little ridiculous. The "tables" are very cramped if you have more than 2 people, and you're sealed off from other tables by tapestries and fabric. Every time after visiting here, I feel like I should expect to see a needle hanging out of my arm. Again, the bar is cool - but I'd pass on the tables...

Datz is (hopefully) going to be one of those South Tampa staples. Food is excellent and quality seems great. Dining room can seem a little cramped, especially if you're with a large party (be prepared to be bumped into a few times, usually). Prices are a little high at first glance, but for the amount of food you receive, its worth it. Also nice that they do a lot of charity events around town. Regardless, be sure to try the mac & cheese!

This is probably my favorite bar downtown. Pour House has only been around for about a year, but they've already cornered the market on good beers and reasonably-priced wines. They have 30-ish taps on the wall with a wide variety of beers - light/dark, local/import, etc. They also sell to-go packs, which is a nice touch.

They don't serve food, but have free popcorn and allow you to order in from other restaurants. They're also dog friendly (inside), and have a great patio out front. 

I'd definitely suggest this place for people in the area, it's got a great vibe and the service is fantastic.

Welcome to the neighborhood, OCF! My first trip was fairly solid. Four styles of coffee were offered and served in a French press, yielding multiple cups for my friend (who unfortunately didn't love the brew she selected). The barista asked if I wanted my latte flat, which I can honestly say I've never been asked before in my history of ordering lattes. Apparently it has to do with the thickness of the foam. 

The café has an Elixr meets Noble meets office building vibe. There is a lot of lime green, wood, funky chairs, and some more practical-looking chairs as well. I personally hope OCF will be a more mature alternative to La Va, where people will be able to have meetings or get some work done in a calmer setting. Time will tell. 

Cakes, croissants and other baked goods looked delicious, and come from The Sweet Life Bakeshop. I'll be returning soon for a slice of that lemon cake to go along with my coffee.

I ate here again last night and was very pleased as usual. The wait staff is attentive but not overbearing, the unique pizzas come out fast and are perfect with their fresh ingredients; and the antipasto is the best in town with EVOO drizzled over. Their white chocolate Panna Cotta is out of this world, one of my favorite desserts ever. And I almost forgot the wine list...the Prosecco and Super Tuscans are not to be missed. Reasonably priced, too.
Bonus: They are open on Mondays.

The Black Mussels and the Crispy Calamari are excellent starters! I would say this was the best calamari I've had and I used to live in New Orleans for 21 years, one of the better seafood destinations.

I'm glad we ate here since we did not know of them previously and the burger on the menu looks really interesting so we'll be back.
The food is exceptional. The service is excellent. Did I say that this is the best Italian in town? And the cost is reasonable. You obviously need a reservation. It is worth the wait.

Good family fun. Hang out by the river and get a smorgasbord of different dishes. All the dishes may not be the best food around but the place, people, and experience makes up for what is missing. Do order the bananas early and then check out the other items. And they do run out of the favorites first, so we likely missed a few of the best dishes. Will be back.

Pluses:

1. It's close to work and home
2. It's affordable if you order a special or visit during "happy hour".
3. Two words - salsa bar. Some of the best restaurant salsa around.
4. The food is fresh. You can see them grilling the meat and making tortillas when you order at the counter.

Minuses:

1. If you don't visit during "happy hour" or order one of their specials and just want, say, one or two tacos, you will get stripped down tacos for $3 each..
2. They charge extra for cheese on the tacos.
3. Two words - fish tacos. They are just not good here... avoid.
4. A baked potato with tacos? Maybe it's just me...

General Notes:

All the salsas in the salsa bar are spicy. If you don't like spicy, eat at the Green Burrito. The above mentioned "happy hour" is a great deal. Kind of weird to call it happy hour because there is no bar vibe to this place at all.  1/2 priced beer, nachos and "panchos" (loaded nachos). You can get a big pile of chips with cheese, sour cream, guacamole, jalapenos and meat for under $4.

All this being said... the food and specials are enough to keep me coming back.

Was here not to long ago and thought it was decent for gluten free, which I don't usually care for. The service was pleasant and helpful. I would go back if in the area again.

FYI, the recent series of nasty, negative reviews are probably all from one person who has something against the owners - since they all came on the same day and from brand new Yelpers, who only had one review - so take them w/ a grain of salt (or sugar).

Note to Yelp: The ability to give half star ratings would be nice. Three stars is too little for this place and four stars is too much.

My last two visits:

Visit #1 - the visit from hell. We had seen the sign from the street, $5 for burger, fries and a shake. I surprise my wife by calling her and inviting her for a weekday lunch. We meet there around 11:45. The lobby is packed with people trying to pay and those waiting to be seated. The poor hostess is trying to seat people and run the register while the phone is ringing... you get my point. We are seated about 10 minutes later. The server comes over and, since we know what we want, we don't really have to look at the menus. We place our order. At this point, it's close to noon. She brings our shakes  promptly. It's downhill from there. After about 15 minutes, we have not received our food. I also notice that no one else is getting their food either. Another fifteen minutes and still no food. All the servers are looking back into the kitchen in frustration. Our server keeps apologizing and promising our food will be right out. 12:45: still no food. My wife has to leave because she has a 1:00 meeting at work. I apologize to her, feeling bad because she got no lunch. I stay behind thinking I can eat mine quickly and pack hers up. Fifteen minutes later and STILL NO FOOD. I get up to leave. I go to the hostess stand to see if they at least want me to pay for the shakes, which of course, they don't. I had no cash with me so I ask the hostess if she can at least run my card for a tip for the server as, it was not her fault, and I think she should still get a tip. At this point, the server comes out carrying two burgers offering to pack them up. I am so frustrated. At this point I don't want the burgers. I ended up going to Jack-In-The-Box.

Visit #2 - a couple of weeks go by. This time, I tell my wife I will go early and order and she can meet me there. We've got to give them another chance for the $5 burger, fries and shake. Only the special has changed. It's now a burger, fries and a soft drink. Not a big deal. We order the special. This time we get our food in a reasonable time frame. The burger is so so... what do you expect for $5? When I go to pay, the hostess asks, "How was everything". I respond, "At least we got to eat this time". Her response indicated that she remembered me from the last visit. I don't know... is that a good thing?

We do go here for breakfast from time to time, which is what they do well. In all honesty, I think the Great Burger Fiasco was an anomaly... but it makes for a good story

I recently made my 2nd visit to Brushfire.  The first time was pre-Yelp, but I feel like this is a review update rather than an original review.  My first visit several months ago was a better experience.  The portions were huge and the food was fantastic.  This time around the portions were much smaller.  I will say that  either time it's more food than I could eat, but I still felt gypped in comparison.  Unfortunately I had way more bread than meat in my brisket sandwich.  The seasoned French fries were overly seasoned, and the creamy slaw was under-dressed and not nearly as good as I remember.

The meat though, is good.  The brisket was melt-in-your mouth tender.  The pulled pork incredible.  I do recommend ordering the sauce on the side unless you really, really like sauce.  I'm a little uneasy with making a committment to just one flavor.  Personally I like to try all the different flavors so "on the side" is perfect for me.  I also like the multiple iced tea choices.    

The staff is friendly, and they clear off the vacated tables quickly which is a plus in the small space.  I'll be back to see what a 3rd visit offers.

As I began writing this review it occurred to me that Firebirds is probably a chain. T-town is pretty tight knit when it comes to the restaurant community, and I'd never heard anything about the owners. I checked the website, and sure enough, a chain. Chain or not, good food is good food. The building makes me feel like I've walked into a lodge in the Rocky Mountains. It's as if the temps in my brain immediately drop, and I no longer remember the searing heat outside, even if only for a little while. 

I am certain that for the price and atmosphere this is absolutely the best steak in town. The meat is tender and flavorful and cooked just right. Even the lowly little sirloin is fantastic. I've tried many other menu items, but the steak is the best. At least that was my opinion until this visit. On this fateful night I discovered a new love. Steak who? I've met someone else who excited my taste buds even more - the herb crusted prime rib. So yummy! It was ordered medium, and it came out medium. The au jus was rich and the horseradish (which I don't typically like) was good. The herb crusting was a perfect compliment to the flavorful, tender meat. 

They have a large wine menu with some nice selections. I like that you can opt for an 8oz pour which is nice if you want to share a glass with someone. They also serve their wine at the right temperature - a huge plus in my opinion. 

The weakest link is the service. No one ever seems happy there. I'm beginning to wonder if it is a not-so-nice place to work. I guess the service is what I might expect from a chain. It lacks a sense of community.

AMAZING!!!  Went for Easter dinner...not a bad or mediocre thing to say!!!!!
FABulous Food!
Wonderful Service!
Outstanding Atmosphere!
Free Parking (Validated) PLUS
Kid-Friendly!?!?!? 
WE WILL DEFINITELY BE BACK!!! Can't believe we haven't discovered this gem sooner!
Great place to hit during happy hour, they have $3.50 red/white sangria's that  are pretty awesome get there at after 4pm and receive 1/2 off finger foods...

UPDATE: The Pho has gotten a little better. However, their "Seafood" Pho with shrimp, squid and IMITATION crab should be taken off the menu altogether. Another thing that puzzles me is their choice of music. Why do the owners think that Gipsy Kings and other flamenco guitar music works in a Pho joint? Weird...

I pass by this place everyday on my way home from school.  So Yesterday I decided it was time to go in and check them out.  I ordered my usual which is a beans rice and cheese burrito.  I don't like salsa or anything extra in my food, and I told the lady who took my order, but when I took a bite there was a mess of cilantro inside the burrito GROSS!  SO I had to dig out the green stuff, and re roll the burrito before the taste settled in.  

But after that the burrito tasted fine.  I am going to have to go back another day, and try it again without the mess.
Best pizza in Tucson hands down. Great service and great prices. Go for the lunch special or wings night! Love me some Rocco's!!!

Dollar drinks from 6-8 on Friday nights? a cheap drunk's dream.

The insane number of post-college guys stuck in frat boy mentality? enough to sober you up after your 5th dollar drink.

You MUST try the Carmelized Cauliflower with tahini...Fabulous! I work across the street from this place, and I go for special treats. The Falafel is very good. I prefer to eat outside because it's quieter. The staff is OK. Very friendly. I had one problem when I pre ordered my lunch to be ready by 11:30 to eat in. I only get 30 min. for lunch. I was there on time, my table was ready but the food had not been started, and the waitress knew nothing about it. 20 minutes later my lunch came but I had to get it to go because my 30 minutes for lunch was almost over.  I have heard the Philly Steak is very good. Can't wait to try it.

We ate at Rocco's yesterday, and it was delicious!  I was absolutely blown away by the hot wings.  We got the hot variety, and the sauce was unlike any I have ever had before.  It almost had the consistency of spaghetti sauce, but packed quite a kick.  If I had a spoon, I would have scraped up all the remaining sauce on the plate and ate it like soup.  The wings were also very crispy, which I love.  The deep dish pizza didn't disapoint either.  The vegetables were fresh and delicious, particularly the jalapenos, which were not from a jar.  My only complaint is that they didn't have many domestic beers.  Old Style seemed to be about it.  Their import selection was good, but with pizza and wings, I like a plain old Coors Light.

THE best burger I've EVER had. Tender, juicy, FULL of great taste.  I LOVE this happy hour. Great atmosphere, clientele, bar tenders and the food! YIKES! I mean who puts goose liver in burgers? Janos does!!!!

We have been here twice and both times asked ourselves "why don't we come here more?"   Both times the food was excellent.  We went first with my parents for dinner and my extremely picky Mom loved it.  She is all Italian, taught cooking/restaurant mgmt for 30+ yrs and an excellent cook.  We went for lunch the other day and again, excellent food.  The highlights for me were the fabulous pizza crust, bread, rabe that was perfectly green, yet tender.  I am not a big fan of polenta(sort of hate it), but here it was lovely.  The only cons: I prefer more garlic/chunk in my sauce, but theirs was very fresh tasting.  Also, we had 3 small children with us both times.  Super comfortable, low-key bar side and very attractive table side of the restaurant offer great options.  Beautifully done Italian.  And really can't believe the comments about the staff???  They were fantastic.  You won't be disappointed... unless you think Ragu is good Italian.

Hush puppies.  Nuff said.  (But I'll say a bit more since that wouldn't really count as a review).

Jimbos is one of those Tampa staples that as a local you come to know and love, like La Bamba, La Teresita, and Mons Venus (not that I've ever been to Mons).  If you are in Tampa and crave BBQ, Jimbos is the place to try.

Atmosphere is very casual and laid back with benches instead of the traditional restaurant look.  But don't let the looks deceive you. These guys know BBQ.  The pulled pork and chicken are my favorites. I hate messing with bones. 

And then there's the hush puppies.

Nice and warm, a bit on the greasy side which makes them perfect.  I could dine on hush puppies alone (and frequently did until I had to get serious about losing weight).   I can't have just two.  I think there is a law that says you have to have at least a dozen.  I am almost sure.  Almost. :)

I have had business lunches catered from Jimbos, especially around the holidays.  It is an office favorite.  I've been going for 15 years, and the place already looked old when I first went there.  They must be doing something right to have lasted this long. It gets packed at lunchtime.

If you are a BBQ fan, do yourself a favor and visit Jimbos. And get an extra order of hush puppies. You'll be glad you did.

A total Bollywood FLOP!
 
Dined at the Bollywood Friday evening. Although there was a formidable turnout of customers it wasn't overly busy, so we cannot use that as an excuse for the lethargic service we encountered., and also the awful food.

Our first impression was that it was a cute concept, the decor is trendy casual with a lounge vibe,, and a  nice outdoor dining patio. Large flat panel screens feature kitschy Bollywood music videos . 

When we arrived there was no one to greet us, so we took a table inside and waited 10 minutes, ignored by staff passing by frequently, I had to wave someone down to get a menu. The wine list is awful. Barefoot wines and Ecco Domani are the choices, and they didn't have three of the six imported beers listed on the menu. 

We waited almost 15 minutes to get our order taken and almost that long to have our first drinks brought to the table.

Since it looked like many of the items on the menu looked fried and greasy, we asked our server for recommendations. This turned out to be a bad idea because the two items she recommended were very disappointing.  First, she advised us that the kitchen was "backed up" and we would have to wait at least 30 minutes for our entrees and suggested an appetizer, Punjabi Chole-Batur that she highly recommended. After waiting another 20 minutes for the appetizer, I flagged her down and said that if the kitchen is "backed" up 30 minutes, then why bother getting an appetizer to hold us over? She then went back to the kitchen and brought us out this bland, soupy appetizer adorned with sesame hamburger buns! We were appalled! Burger Buns? When she saw our expressions she suggested that she could bring us naan, which we gladly accepted. After another 10 minutes, she brings the naan, which was really greasy pita bread!  Shortly thereafter the two entrees arrive. Kofta meatball curry (Highly recommended by our server)  and Chicken Tikka Masala, which was the only decent tasting item (although I have had much better.) The meatball curry was disappointing, very few meatballs, they must have been made with soy protein or something, had a weird texture.

This horrible dinner cost us a total of $70.00 with tax and tip, we could have spent that much somewhere else and gotten much better quality food, drinks and service. Live and learn.

talula is like catherine zeta-jones--sexy and refined, with just a little bit of crazy underneath.

i eagerly awaited the arrival of talula's garden, as i had never been to the original, and i was very curious to see what a pairing of starr and olexy would bring.

right from the moment you walk in, you see the juxtaposition.  non-matching outdoor seating, comfortable small tables, a buffet/console table among the tables, pretty country-esque pictures...  this is flanked by ridiculous 20 feet ceilings, a bar area that transmits sounds and voices like you're at your local watering hole.

hence, the touch of crazy underneath...  are you a upscale farmhouse, or happy hour meet-n-greet?

the server was super-knowledgeable, patient, and plain ol' sweet.  we got the curds & whey cheese platter, the gnudi were little cloud pillows bathed in a shallot jus.  and let's talk about the little happy brioche buns topped with a spring-colored ramp butter.  my scallops were good, my friend's beef was on the tougher side.

and...  get sides.  get cheeses.  get a dessert.  the portions are *tiny* for the price.  but i guess, if you are a refined lady like czj, you wouldnt eat til the point of full, huh?

classy, refined, right?  we had a highly attentive staff who constantly asked us for feedback.  i appreciate this, and i was more than honest with them.  i love that they want to continually improve, and i can't wait to go back.

Perfect in every way. The roll, meat, cheese and quick service. 

Awesome! I will be back every time I'm in Philly.

I rarely find myself stumped when reviewing a particular place, but TAPS is going to be one of them. 

The location and style of the restaurant is fantastic. Located in the lobby floor of Skypoint (one of the newer high-rises downtown), it offers a clean facility with great decor. It also has a nice outdoor seating area which looks over the museum and the riverfront park. 

The menu is inspiring and offers a lot of 'small-plate' items, which are fresh and tasty. Probably most notable is their wine selection - it's amazing. They also have a cool self-serve system that allows you to sample as many different kinds as you like, based on different pours and prices (sort of like a metro card).

The only qualm I have with this place is the service. Every time I've patronized the place, it's been a different experience. Sometimes the service is great, other times its wretched. Unfortunatly, I'd have to say that the service I've received here is worse than average based on every visit. Because of this, I couldn't force myself to give this the '3 stars' I would have.

If wine is your thing, I'd check it out. Don't rely on the staff for your drinks, it's better to buy a prepaid card and vend it for yourself...

Primo's makes a mighty good Italian. The flavors -- prosciutto, provolone, Genoa salami, and capicola -- are perfectly balanced. And the bread. My God, the bread. It's totally ruined me. Good service and fair prices, too.

One of my number one favorite brunch places. It's a cute, slick and clean place that has a feeling of morning freshness every time I walk in (even at 1pm in the afternoon). And the staff is so friendly.

Hot fragrant La Colombe coffee and fresh OJ squeezed by little Suzy? Yes, please.

Traditional eggs benedict with creamy rich hollandaise? Yes, please.

Wait, you can put that on a pretzel roll instead of an english muffin? Amazing.

You don't have a microwave and freezer on premise? This is magic.

I've also had cosmic experiences w/the breakfast burrito, veggie benedict and the steak + egg special. I can't wait to come back for more.

Actually liked it. I moved here from Chicago an it's impossible to find a good Mexican place around here. 
Service is friendly and fast
My order was a mango margarita. It's buy 1 get 1 free. Didn't know that but it was good and 5.95 for 2. Not bad.
Hubby had daiquiri yum too
We split the cadillac enchilada huge plate w fajita steak on top of two enchiladas w rice veggies and salad. Gd seasoning not just plain.
Chips and salsa were alright. 
Happy altogether considering last place by my house Was nasty and loud and full of rug rats lol
Happy seis de mayo to all!!

Just ate there last night with my wife and daughter. OH YEAH! It was goooood eatin'. I had a hankering for BBQ and always wanted to try Famous Dave's so we went.

We ordered the Feast for Two (I think that's what it was called.) billed as being able to feed 2-3 people. I'm telling you, if there are only 2 of you, you are going to be stuffed silly as this can feed 4. It came with a half rack of ribs, a half chicken, I think 6 slices of beef brisket (this was the optional choice--we could have chosen chopped pork or some sausage instead), BBQ beans and cole slaw (you can choose your two sides), and potato wedges. EVERYTHING WAS EXCELLENT!

The ribs were really tasty, the chicken was great, the brisket was tender. The BBQ beans were excellent, the cole slaw was as good or better than KFC's (my favorite until now). The cole slaw style was not fine cut like KFC's, but chopped, not huge pieces but around a little bigger than 1/4" pieces so it was very tasty. The potato wedges were the best we ever had, period. Perfectly lightly crisp on the outside and salted just right. They were not soggy potato wedges. Good enough to go back there for those alone, along with the beans and cole slaw.

I haven't tried the corn muffins yet as I was too busy chowing down on other things. The waiter gave us four to take home along with our left overs.

It was just fun being there. They serve this all on what looks like a fake plastic garbage can lid and it is put on a wooden pedestal. It was a fun presentation with all the food being on one platter. The decor is a car theme. Where we were in the restaurant it was pretty loud because there was a big party there enjoying themselves. Not complaining--just saying that people there were enjoying it as I.

The bill including tax was $40.38. We didn't have any drinks, just water as we are water people. Reasonable for three people. And like I said, this could feed four.

Highly recommended.

I posted this review under "Famous Dave's BBQ" as that is the official name of the restaurant.

Just ate there last night with my wife and daughter. OH YEAH! It was goooood eatin'. I had a hankering for BBQ and always wanted to try Famous Dave's so we went.

We ordered the Feast for Two (I think that's what it was called.) billed as being able to feed 2-3 people. I'm telling you, if there are only 2 of you, you are going to be stuffed silly as this can feed 4. It came with a half rack of ribs, a half chicken, I think 6 slices of beef brisket (this was the optional choice--we could have chosen chopped pork or some sausage instead), BBQ beans and cole slaw (you can choose your two sides), and potato wedges. EVERYTHING WAS EXCELLENT!

The ribs were really tasty, the chicken was great, the brisket was tender. The BBQ beans were excellent, the cole slaw was as good or better than KFC's (my favorite until now). The cole slaw style was not fine cut like KFC's, but chopped, not huge pieces but around a little bigger than 1/4" pieces so it was very tasty. The potato wedges were the best we ever had, period. Perfectly lightly crisp on the outside and salted just right. They were not soggy potato wedges. Good enough to go back there for those alone, along with the beans and cole slaw.

I haven't tried the corn muffins yet as I was too busy chowing down on other things. The waiter gave us four to take home along with our left overs.

It was just fun being there. They serve this all on what looks like a fake plastic garbage can lid and it is put on a wooden pedestal. It was a fun presentation with all the food being on one platter. The decor is a car theme. Where we were in the restaurant it was pretty loud because there was a big party there enjoying themselves. Not complaining--just saying that people there were enjoying it as I.

The bill including tax was $40.38. We didn't have any drinks, just water as we are water people. Reasonable for three people. And like I said, this could feed four.

Highly recommended.

By far the best cake anywhere in Tampa.  Their Alpine cake (yellow cake, chocolate froting, with white chocolate shavings) is so good I can only buy a piece at a time, or risk eating the entire thing in a day.  Their carrot cake is also to die for!

Very disappointing experience. Seated right away, but the Hostess looked like she had just got to work from the gym, nails cracked, hair a mess, kind of an attitude, but couldn't carry that off even. 

Seating was poorly placed with the chairs way to close to the wall, neither table in our grouping was level, also the table and chairs were kind of cheap on the patio. 

Server was a joke, slow and kind of "I got better things to be doing then waiting on people" attitude. Screwed up our order, but unable to come out and say anything, we ended up waiting for a good hour plus to get our main course. Bread took a half hour to get and replenishment was slow. 

The meals were maybe average, the appetizer was good not great,   the sorbet and the micro brew beer was pretty good. 

The price was a joke for the quality and experience. There are way more authentic and flavorful Italian restaurants in town, and in comparison to places in St. Louis, N.Y, S.F. D.C., L.A. and any number of cities, this place ranks above Olive Garden and maybe Carraba's. 

For the price, go to Roma's Imports, pickup your choice of appetizers, courses and deserts, throw a nice table cloth on the patio table, crack a bottle of wine and save a lot of money and have a better experience. If you have to go out to eat then Bazil's is way better as are several other places.

After many months of trying to find the time to visit M, we finally ventured over.  We had gotten a living social coupon and that made the trip even more exciting.  We went early in evening thinking outdoors would be wonderful - Well - LOL a sudden cold front moved through with some pretty gusty winds.  Luckily for us the tent from last weeks wedding was still up.  Enough of this  - the food = A+  I must say the descriptions on the menu were a bit over whelming but the taste and presentations made up for that real quick.  I also think we saw every server in the house.  At least 5 different staff members waited on us at different points in our meal. 

The quality and taste were wonderful.  Braised short rib for dinner and we never used our knives.  

I would suggest if you have not been to M, you put it on the "to do" list

just stopped on a last moment choice.  we had exceptions service and the food was certainly above expectation.  it will certainly be on our list of places to return to.

if you're looking for american made shirts etc, check the lables.  i couldn't find anything in the clothing line made in america.  to bad, there were several shirts i would like to have had.

The Loft is not only the best theatre in town. It's the only independently owned first run, AND cult, classic theatre in town. It's also a non-profit and and educational facility. They bring in filmmakers, and authors on a regular basis, have great film festivals, and is a huge contribution to the Tucson cultural scene. PLUS when you are a member (best membership in town) you get free monthly movies and free popcorn for every movie you go to. And where else can you see Rocky Horror???? The upstairs theatre is intimate and if you don't mind sitting close, you can sit on a couch. The downstairs theatre is huge (500 seats I think), and now that the air conditioning system has been fixed, very comfortable. Also, they serve alcohol. You can even get mimosas on Sunday. The people who work there are cool, the movies are a trip (Friday/Sat. night classics is my fav), and it's a wonderful opportunity for us to see films we'd normally never even know about. This is a good as movie going gets.

I'll tell you right now, I have an obsession with kitchenware.  Not an unhealthy obsession, but I admit I do love going into kitchen supply stores and just browsing around, fantasizing about what I'll buy in the hopefully not-too-distant future once I have my own place with a kitchen I can decorate and get cool stuff for.  I was particularly fascinated by all the baking supplies--a batter dispenser for perfect pancakes and cookies? I can't wait to get my hands on that!

I love this place. I've only had the Pho (with rare beef) and Bahn Mi (pork) but they're so good I cannot seem to want to try anything else. Service can be slow but the price is right and the food is excellent. A plus is to browse through the supermarket next door. Can't wait to go back again!

I worked my way through college in restaurants.  I love eating out.  I love patios.  I love anti-pasto.  I love Tucson nights.  First time at Giuseppe's.

OK, that being said, here goes:  Four of us at 7p on a Friday night, with a reservation.  One lonely, neglected-looking table on the patio - no silverware, etc.  We asked the hostess if we could sit there - sure!  No problem!  She took us to it right away - without wiping it down or setting it up.  That's cool, I'm happy we got a patio table.  A busboy shows up with silverware, but nothing to wipe the table off.  Off he goes.  Finally, we have the whole shebang - silverware, water, bread, etc.  Even menus!  So lucky!

Then...waiting....waiting...waiting....no waiter, no waiter, no waiter.  Workers rushing to and fro, looking over at us, but unable to penentrate the invisible force field, I guess.  Finally, we get the waiter who's serving the adjacent table to acknowledge us and says he, "I'm not your waiter, but I'll get him for you."  Gee!  Thanks, all-powerful keeper of the food.

Then...waiter fight!  No one wants us!  Do we smell?  Don't think so - can't say for sure....the last thing, I, a survivor of my restaurant days, want to see when dining out is the internal politics of the restaurant played out in front of me.  I tip!  Maybe I should've made that clear upfront!

Once we got a waiter, things went better, albeit slowly.  We ordered an antipasto and stuffed mushrooms to start, with a couple of glasses of wine, which came lickity-split.  The anti-pasto is divine - all kinds of goodies you might find on a plate in Italy - prosciutto, salami, asparagus, olives, two kinds of cheese - little bites so tasty.  I will come back and have this as a meal.  The stuffed mushrooms are seafood-less - yay, me!  I hate fishy stuff.  Four to an order, nice for us.

The main courses were slow to follow.  We ordered some bolognese dishes and the shrimp special pasta of the evening.  The bolognese was OK - not the best and I agree with a previous reviewer that the meat was oddly over-cooked or something.  A little rubbery.  My fave in town is at Tavolino's, but they are so much more pricey...this dish is a lovely 9.95 and the pasta is light and the portion is big - so, it's easy to recommend.  The pasta special was also good with generous amounts of shrimp over fetuccine.

No dessert - tooooooo stuffed.  

We had a nice time because we were all too mellow to really care about being sadly neglected for FIFTEEN minutes at the beginning, but if any one of us had been bugged by it, it would have been a different story.  

I highly recomment the anti-pasto, the mushrooms, the wine, the fresh pasta, but make sure to know who you waiter is supposed to be if you're sat at the lonely last table on the patio.

Beautiful restaurant with an open,airy feel. Easy parking adjacent to the restaurant, very nice and efficient staff. We went for lunch on a Tuesday.Started with perfect ice tea. 

we ordered: 
Open Faced Griddled Garlic Bread + thyme sautéed mushrooms, leek fondue, Madeira cream, grape tomatoes, Parmigiano reggiano..tasted as good as it looked. 

Papparadelle with Willcox Ham, Fresh Peas, garlic scapes, mushrooms + leeks moistened with chicken stock, olive oil, Parmigiano Reggiano, basil pesto ...told them to leave off the ham...a very rich dish...brought some home....yum. 

Seared Ahi on English Muffins with wasabi mayo, sesame slaw, tomato, avocado, unagi sauce + pickled ginger....nice combination of flavors..perfectly seared ahi...the fries were wonderful. 

Meze plate with falafel,humus,olives,salad and naan...very nicely done. 

Great addition to the downtown Tucson scene and I can get there on my bike!

Fun, Cultural, and most importantly yummilicious.

Make sure to bring the paper money. They don't except anything less. From reading the reviews, it says donations, but they surely expect how much they post for the cost.

Be careful too, they don't speak English, and they will try to rip you off of money. Just saying. It's like your in a foreign country for real.

I had spring rolls, and beef soup. It was so good. Got it just bc everyone else had the soup, yes definitely was scrumptious.

Hubby had ginger chicken mushroom with rice for $5. It was spicy though. The rest of the chicken options were gone.

The fave was definitely the fried bananas. Totally different than spanish fried bananas. It was definitely a heaven.

The setting is gorgeous by the hillsbourough river, and there are picnic tables through out the treelined area. 

The temple is very pretty, small but pretty and detailed.

Gotta check it out! Definitely a staycation destination!!

The place itself was nice enough, the bar looked active, and the service was fine.  I just didn't think the food tasted very good.  Between the group, we had fried oysters, duck, and the daily special-beef cheek.  Unfortunately, none of them were really very good.  We'll continue supporting downtown renovations and new restaurants.....just not this one.

Well the wife and I came here for our most recent anniversary.  I had the lamb shank and I don't remember what my wife got except that she ordered a soufflé for desert.  Our server was very engaging and overall we were well taken care of and really had a good time.  The food was great and we talk about going back some day.  It definitely caters to an older grey or mature set but I've realized we're one of them so I guess it's OK to be there.  They were very accommodating to my diet requests.  I had the crepes for dessert - yum!

Okay, so I've been here twice now - once for a regular dinner and once for a happy hour - and I have to say that this is just a gem all around. Food, drinks, atmosphere, service...
Don't get me wrong, I loved Barrio and was a little devastated when they announced they were closing. I also equate "Jano's" with "snobby" and will likely never patronize the La Paloma restaurant. But I really love this place.
For dinner, everything was just excellent. Very nice decor with a balance in atmosphere of not being too casual, but also not having the stuffiness that it could have. In fact, I'd even say it's actually a little less stuffy than Barrio was. The food was the real prize though. I got the duck (because how often do you get duck?) and it really eclipsed my expectations. The sauce, the tamale-like platter it sat on, the meat itself, everything was just very, very good. Same went for my companion's dish: fantastic. The portion size was also, I think, perfect, and the price, for what it was, was not that bad.
Then there's the happy hour. This is absolutely the best happy hour operating in the city at the moment. They only have four drafts, but all those drafts (Polestar, Barrio IPA, Dale's & Four Peaks' Downtown Brown) are $2.50.  Then all the food on the "bar menu" (which actually features elements of the main menu) is half off. HALF OFF! I got a "J Dawg" which featured black beans, napolitos and an avocado and lime sauce on an all-beef hot dog, served with fries for $3. That was enough food for me, but we didn't realize the portion sizes, and ended up getting a salad (which was fantastic), a Thai curry soup (wonderful and surprisingly spicy) and a carnitas papusa (like a fried tamale). Just really great stuff all around, but I was especially taken aback by the dawg. From the description, it's an obvious take on a Sonoran dog, but it's different and almost better, particularly with the napolitos addition, which adds a really interesting dynamic in the flavor of it.
But that's what Jano's is all about anyway: creating interesting flavors and textures so that they form a sort of culinary dance in every way. Hell, that's the art of it overall, right?
This is one of the many things happening in Downtown that are going to make that area a real draw, not only locally, but also for tourists, foodies, foodie tourists and even cynical Yelp-ers like myself. I'll be back, absolutely.

This is definitely a neighborhood hangout. When we walked in last night, and were seated, I peered around at the happy diners to see if I recognized any faces. This is the kind of place where you just run into people.

My honey and I met up with two friends of ours who live just 10 minutes, via walking from here, in Mt. Airy. We've noticed that this place always is doing brisk business; I think it's just the right kind of place that Mt. Airy needed when it opened a couple years back (good beers on tap, relatively inexpensive menu).

I started with the Kombucha ginger tea although I think my waiter may have given me the homemade ginger ale by mistake. It didn't taste at all like tea, but it was ginger-y and delish. My husband ordered a beer on tap which he enjoyed but was kind of surprised at its temperature. Apparently, beer on tap should be served room temp; his was ice cold.

We decided to split the two specials: an avocado salad and the pulled pork flatbread. Notice to the owner, if you're reading this review, please add the avocado salad to your regular menu; it was divine. The salad was the perfect mix of savory (ripe avocado slices, roasted red peppers, cheese, fresh olive oil), bitter (arugula) and sweet (a very tasty sweet balsalmic topping). We inhaled it and I considered ordering a second one. Next, came out our pulled pork flatbread. It was covered in a very yummy cheese, some "jalepeno" slaw, bbq sauce, and a miniscule amount of pulled pork. I mean, maybe 2 ounces total. Pulled pork isn't the most expensive ingredient on the planet, so this was surprising. The taste was great, but the amount of meat is why I'm docking them a star. If you're going to call it a "pulled pork" flat bread, make sure that you have enough of your main ingredient to warrant that. And the jalepeno slaw wasn't my favorite - it could have packed a more spicy punch and it was a little on the raw side (I like a crispy slaw, but this could have been a little more pickled). After we each had a drink, and one salad and one flatbread between the two of us, we ended up dropping $33 total. I am definitely a fan of the prices!

I don't love the menu, overall, I find it to be lacking. I'd like some more interesting flatbreads (goat cheese and fig, maybe?) But the specials made up for what the menu was lacking. Will I be back? Hmm...

Got there late for Friday the Firkinteenth, but still enjoyed the Grey Lodge Pub.  Very lively, with Firkin-ites spilling out onto the sidewalk.  Great food and beer, and above all, incredibly reasonable.  Amazing what a 15 minute ride out of the city can do for your wallet!.  They feature Polish Goodness pierogies, which were perfectly prepared with caramelized onions.  Very generous sandwiches and drinks (even the high octane brews were served in pints.)  Friendly service and patrons, and (mostly) good tunes playing.  I look forward to sneaking in there on a quieter night to enjoy more of their uncommon beer selection.

What a perfect setting in the City. If you dine here you will forget you are in the city. The food is A+ as well as service. Food artfully designed and presented.  We had a large party of 18 and were easily accomdated. I will be back to try the al fresco.

Given the other places in the area (Mikey's?), the proprietors have put together a decent (if unremarkable and overpriced).  

It's the kind of place where the waitresses are hired to look reasonably nice, while maintaining some dignity (it's a major place for Drexel staff to lunch).  

So, you don't come for the food, which is bland "American" fare. You come for the decor. 

Other pluses: the inside is relatively spacious, compares to the places up at 34th and samson.  (I'm talking about you, New Deck!).
I give 5 stars for their Sonoran Hot Dogs - the best ever!  The rest of the food is good, not outstanding.

This place is small, but man... great things in small packages. Awesome food.

The star is definitely the banh mi. Pork is the best seller. The pickled veggies inside is not a pickling overwhelming and doesn't overpower the meat. Hmm, i can't discern what's in the mixed meat banh mi, it felt fatty and soft... but still delicious. The bread stays crunchy... so you can buy several banh mi (banh us?) And eat it happily later- might as well, its only about $3 for one.

The boba is also awesome. You'll also see a small collection of other vietnamese meals as well as pre-prepared goodies.

Yes, the plaza parking lot can become like christmas eve shopping... especially during weekend meal times. Local vietnamese families haunt this place and it's easy to get forgotten if you're not familiar to the servers. This place serves deli style and has about seven tables. Don't expect a nicely decorated restaurant. This is straightout deli... and the fans love it

Went during the Italian Market festival, and got to the bakery at 3:15 (they are suppose to close at 3)...so there were little left to purchase. I got some tomato pie (with cheese)....hmmmm tasty. Fluffy, but not too fluffy, and thick. I ate it with pasta that night and it was a nice treat than the normal garlic bread.

Amazing!  A hair's breadth away from 5 stars - so I'mma gonna do it and give them all five - and especially Marina our kind, patient server who was so accommodating.

Firstly, my dining companion and I walked in 45 minutes EARLY for our 8 PM res.  This is a hostesses nightmare and we were prepared to be told to "sit at the bar" but we got a table (in the bar area) right away.

I had 2 of the tastiest Arnold Palmers you can imagine - made with the herb-infused house sun tea.

Everything we ate from the fluffy warm brioche rolls with ramp butter to the delicate "handkerchief" pasta (mine with umami rich rabbit, my friends was vegetarian) to the luscious side of garden beets (at least 2 varieties of beet - possibly 3 it was hard to tell) and candied orange peel and a touch of creamy fresh ricotta. - All was wonderful - but the real showpiece was the special of SEASON ONLY squash blossoms (which I love!!!) delicately fried - stuffed with goat cheese and herbs.

Calorie-city!  But so tasty - and we shared an order of 4 - so 2  seemed just about decadently perfect.

We also splurged and shared a Millefuille (more like quatrefuille - please tell me somebody gets my jokes...) of thinly sliced banana, toffee and chocolate served with a rich yet delicate quenelle (aka the Quenelle from hell) of dark chocolate sorbet. 

Wow.

Beets (pun intended) the heck outta Parc.
Similar price points but somehow we spent WAY less (no $60 seafood cocktail tonight, thank you very much).

Dinner for 2 with soft drinks and 1 dessert (no alcohol) was around $75 before tip.

I can't wait to go back.

GO TO TALULA'S. 
Go!
Go!
Go!

And ask for Marina - what a sweetheart.
Tatiana was a doll, too.

Great service, great food, precious decor (a little too cutesy for me, but it's Starry-eyed splendor in the grass.).

You got me!

One of my favorite restaurants to eat lunch. I always try something different and I like most of their dishes. I still give them an A for effort on the dishes I don't like because they're really original with their stuff. I wish they had more restaurants like this in Tampa!

I used my groupon today which was 55 for 115 worth of hair studios. Excited to find out this place is down the street from my house. 
Weird location bc the back faces the street.
Once I walked in, the salon looked very nice and put together.
No one was at the front desk at the time but I said hi to the first person I saw and she was very welcoming. 
Everyone was very nice and welcoming and they were able to give me hair mags and something to drink.
Lee was the woman who did my hair. She seemed quite knowledgable when I showed her bunches of pics that I liked. It was really nice that she knew what I liked and understood it very well.
For all that I wanted she explained how she could do it and how much it would be before hand so very considerate.
She did a great job and I am happy that I found a great hair place that I'll be happy to go back to.
Also there's one barber for men. He has his own spot and the men have their own flat screen to watch while they get cut.
Prices r pretty typical for highlights and hair cut but inthink it was worth it especially w my groupon.

Perfectly executed!

I went here for my birthday and all my expectations were met. There is a beautiful patio area that is actually NOT on the sidewalk, but in a little garden area. It annoys me to no end when a restaurant's "outside seating area" is on the sidewalk so that diners have the pleasure of eating with cars, fumes, and random pedestrians. No, thank you!

Inside, the decor retains the garden element which reminds of me a combination of Secret Garden/Alice in Wonderland/Anthropologie. The bar area is a perfect size for guests waiting for tables.

The food and service were both excellent. I ordered the lobster tail and slow cooked pork. My friends ordered the grass fed beef + ribs and the duck. I tasted all the dishes and they were all excellent. This was not a meal you could re-create at home.

We also ordered the squash blossoms, kid ravioli/totrellini, mushroom pate, and smoked sturgeon for apps and a cheese plate and the cookie sampler for dessert.

Everything exceeded expectations. Everything we ordered was inventive, beautifully plated, and most importantly delicious. Our waitress was attentive and extremely helpful. Her 2 wine suggestions were perfect complements to our meal.

I look forward to another visit here soon!

I ate here for the first time last night and I was disappointed.  For the price, $125 for three people, there was nothing memorable or exciting about the meals.  I'm in my 50s but even I was too old for the King's vibe.  We should have saved our money and pigged out on $125 worth of sushi someplace else.  My UofA kids called it overpriced and pretentious and they are right.  Sorry King we won't be back.

Baked goods at its finest, Dutch style.

As soon as you walk into the Reading Terminal Market, especially from the 12th Street, you immediately get a huge waft of this awesome aroma from the baked goods from Beiler's.  Apparently they are most proud of their sticky buns since they are making it constantly, however, I'm a huge of fan of their donuts, especially the apple fritters!

The fritters are huuge and for $1.55, definitely worth it.  Coated with sugar and just enough cinnamon, it's extremely fluffy and does not that oily taste at all.  It's surprisingly light and although not as much apple chunks as other fritters I've had, you can still taste it.  The iced donuts are cheaper and definitely bigger than your average.

Lots of baked bread, rolls, pies, and all sorts of goodies for your eyes to feast on.  Beiler's makes me wish I lived in Philly so I can sample more!

One of the better uniquely different restaurants in Ybor city!  Tomato basil soup was homemade and amazing.  Greek salad made the meal complete and satisfying.  Only criticism was the taped music playing outside -- please get an updated track and turn it down!

Little Italy delivered oodles of pies (Plain cheese, mushroom and veggie) for a large block party I attended.  Very good pizza.  "Class" neapolitan pies - not too thin (not pizza-on-a-cracker) not too thick.

Very good.

Dear Vero Amore, I'm afraid I'm going to need to downgrade you. Don't get me wrong, I'm still a big fan and will continue to visit you, but I've realized some disappointments in our relationship. You weren't very attentive the other night, but you weren't busy with others. I felt a bit neglected and hoped that you would offer to get me a drink or maybe tell me about your specials instead of just asking me if I was ready to order. I've also come to realize that $14 for so-so lasagna or $6 for 7 small meatballs is a bit much. Would it hurt you to give a little by putting some bread on the side? Relationships are about give and take, but I think you take more than you give. Do you really need to charge me $3 for bread too? I do still enjoy the time we spend together, but I wish our relationship could be more fulfilling.

I'm a fan for sure! Great salsa bar, awesome drink dispenser and great music! 
I had their sirloin tacos and really liked the flavor! 
Wife was a little sad they didnt have a steak salad but you can actually order the meat and grab some lettuce at the salsa bar:) 
Good stuff!

Great: The waiter (Derrick, i think it was) 
Their lemonade (fresh squeezed) 
Good: Their Burgers and Milkshakes 
Okay: Their Caesar Salad and Fries (you must ask for FF well done) 
Horrible: The Manager who kicks people out of tables because a line is at the door. He had the bus boy drop the check off without the waiters knowledge and then waited for us by staring at the table,-unacceptable.

I prefer this location to Locust Street because it is not as crowded. It tends to get crazy on weekend mornings, but it's usually to-go traffic rather than people camping out on their laptops. Because there's no WiFi, you can actually get work done here without being distracted by the internet. The coffee and espresso are good, and the baristas know what they're doing (Ish is the man). The croissants are decent, the muffins are not. The space itself is nice...well lit, good music, decent people watching. It's a solid neighborhood cafe.

If you're one of those Jersey kids who always whines about how no one gets Jersey here and the food is better in Jersey, and the people are better in Jersey, and Jersey's governor craps rainbows, etc. then this would be for you.
It's expensive, as stated, but it's also really, really good. They always put coupons in those books they hand out for free at UA at the beginning of the semester, and you can usually get a free order of fries or something out of them from that (pick up a few of them). And the fries are good, but they're expensive.
That broccoli rabe is one thing that brings me back here but, if you're looking for a real cheesesteak, this is your place. Is it going to be just like South Street? No, but you're in Arizona: get over it, douchebag.

This is a wonderful little spot tucked away from center city. In our neck of the woods, decent places to eat are few and far between (not counting you Memphis Tap). 

Ever since we moved into our house a few years ago this place was on my radar to try. I am totally not sure why it took us a while to get our bumms here but i am glad we did. 

We first did a family brunch (maybe for mom's birthday?). The brunch menu has something for everyone and we all love our meal. We started with a breakfast bruchetta to all share, which was plenty for the 6 of us. On the menu there were plenty of creative and plenty of "safe" offerings - both sweet and savory choices. But the menu was not overwhelming. The coffee was excellent and since we BYO'd it (champagne) they brought out a carafe of OJ for us - Maybe two (hey, we like to drink) 

The hubby and I returned a while later for dinner, which was also very enjoyable. And we have been back a few times since and have yet to be disappointed! 

A few tips: 
Breakfast is available for takeout too. 
Can we say fireplace (working!)\ 
Nice staff. 
Cozy, warm atmosphere. 
Parking CAN be tricky, but its not too bad. 
Local art is displayed proudly. 
I found the portions to be huge.

I had heard great things about the burgers here, so I had to check it out.  I got the Grace Burger, which is just your basic burger.  I ordered it medium well and it came out burnt!  The entire bottom was charred.  However, I didn't really notice it until mid-way through the burger; yes, it was still rather tasty.  I'll definitely be back to try another burger, but perhaps next time I'll order it medium.  Good beer selection, no frills type of place and the prices are affordable.

Honestly, I passed this nuevo Mex joint daily for years - years - on my frequent way into the gym, Xtreme, located across the street on South Howard Ave in south Tampa. But never once was I ever tempted to go in. It looks kind of inviting, with its clean green lighting. I'm just not attracted to sports bars, which this place also is. I believe it's operated by the same management that owns the nearby Lodge, Daily Eats and Ciccio & Tony's restaurant/bars.
The vibe is frathouse-meets-recent-college-grad. The wait staff are mainly very young women and the clientele is largely on the quite young side. Therefore, I expected the food to be iffy at best, since the customers at that stage in life are seldom too discriminating, having recently dined for years in college cafeterias.
There seems to be a dj because the thumping house dance music seems to get louder as the night goes on. So, between the many flatscreens showing tiresome sporting shows, the dj sounds, the high-heeled young things and the fairly untrained service, I didn't expect much. I was  surprised to see a board listjng quite a few craft beers, a positive relief from the ubiquitous Miller Lites that these places usually sell by the truckload. Unfortunately, our Cigar City Jai Lai's were flat or stale or end of the keg. The young bartender was apparently ill-equipped to judge this, when informed, and deferred to another youngster out back who sniffed and tested and told us the beer was actually okay. Well, no, it was not. However, to his eternal credit, he pulled out a 22 ounce bottle of Avery Maharasha, an exquisite craft beer and offered it to us free! For that simple act alone, I award The Lime 4 stars. This man understands customer service. We also ordered some queso dip which was fine. I didn't try any other food. The other beers on the menu are sufficient that I would return to try some more.
Great place to eat, light fair for summertime.  Lots to choose from for all tastes types.  I have been here twice I enjoyed both times.  Lots of veggie choices, but vegan is sparse.

Very good Thai food and very clean place. We came in without a reservation and they seated us anyway. The place was tiny but the servers made up for it with friendliness. I enjoyed the food but found the items a bit overpriced. I blame the pricing on location. Servings were perfect (Thai restaurants tend to overdo their portion sizes). Overall, I had a good experience but would look for cheaper Thai alternatives in less expensive neighborhoods.

By far my favorite breakfast and brunch joint in Northern Liberties!
The food is delicious, fast and filling.
I highly recommend the tofu scramble and their quinoa porridge - really good with fresh fruit on top!
We go there every Sunday for a reason.
The servers are also super cool and friendly.

Awesome place. Can't believe I never tried this place all of the years living in Tampa. 

Pretty quick and efficient service and the place was almost jam-packed on a Wednesday afternoon. Had a Cuban and it was delicious. Would definitely come back and would definitely come back to try other menu items.
chill setup, unique deconstructed dishes.  one problem- the taste of the dishes!
fun atmosphere, above average greek food.  good dessert selection

I think when meeting hungry friends after 10 pm in this part of town on a weekend, choices are limited. Pastiche can be relied on to sooth the soul with a jazzy, swanky vibe. The service, though, has taken minimalist aloofness to a new level. If the party isn't having wine, remove the napkin-stuffed balloon glasses pronto. Making eye contact through a sea of superfluous stemware is irksome. Please approach, even if we are talking. We'll stop long enough to order drinks, I promise. I hate to have to flag down a server who has walked by four times and hesitated to ask for our order because we were talking. No need to be phone kiosk aggressive, but please greet us at the very least.
This time we tried he fried avocado, a sparse, albeit interesting appetizer. Thick slices of creamy avocado are lightly breaded and flash-fried until they melt in your mouth. The sauces, a piquant little remoulade and a sweet tomato vinaigrette, are reasonable compliments. The steak salad covered in shoestring fries is an odd amalgam that combines crunchy and chewy, although mixing ketchup and greens was disturbing.
Sadly, empty dishes sit too long and the staff could seem happier to be here. I'm a veteran late night diner, and I hate to be made to feel that I am keeping the staff from a pressing engagement.

Yeah it's a chain but it's consistant good food with a wide selection. I ordered the Kobe burger medium came well. Told the waiter who promptly returned and gave me another comp'd it all together. Yeah I'd rather get it right at first but they did the right thing and I'm happy. Remember open seating at the bar if the wait is long be a vulture

Good, cheap, friendly. Supposed to be a daily "happy hour" that lasts all day - $2 beer and tequila shots, $2.50 sangria and margaritas. Didn't try these.

I brought home food for the three of us - I had the chicken mole special, which was OK, my fiancee had the carne asada, which was pretty good, and my roommate had the carnitas in a green salsa that was also pretty good. I also got a small birria soup, since I love birria. It was delicious.

All of this food, plus chips and salsa, was $25, which is ridiculously affordable.

We'll definitely be going again.

The menu scans I've posted are good as of 5/27/2011.

We weren't sure what to make of it when we walked by last night, but I'm absolutely glad we stopped in for a beer and dinner.

The Pub is a new restaurant/bar at International Plaza (it's housed in the old Bamboo Club spot), and it works out well as a British Pub.  There are about 20939784 different beers (ok, that's a little bit exaggerated) and other spirits.  As such, there's something for everyone!

One suggestion:  if you like a lighter beer (I'm talking color, not calories!) try out the Hobgoblin.  Don't be tricked by the dark brown color - it literally tastes like a light beer would!  It's really great!

The atmosphere is noisy but fun and full of visual distractions.  The menu is also chalk full of choices.  I had the shepard's pie while Andrew had the beef tenderloin.  Both of ours were really tasty and flavorful, but I'd have to give the win to the beef tenderloin.  Absolutely, fall-off-the-bone, mouth-watering yum!

What's that you say?  You love beer?  Ah, I do too.  Let me tell you the best part about this place:  Pour your own beer.  That's right.  Upstairs, they have a huge wall-o-beer that lets you swipe a card and dispense (by the ounce) the beer of your choice!  Amazing!

The service was attentive without being annoying, and part of what impressed me when we first arrived was that they use a system for checking you in that sends you a text message when your table is ready.  You don't have text messaging on your phone?  What decade are you still living in?  I'll be you have an 8-track or two lying around too, huh? :)

Anyway, one last tidbit:  I really expected a restaurant/bar to be more pricey at International Plaza, but it wasn't at all.  It was really right on par with most restaurants around Tampa that aren't in glamorous locations.

If you haven't checked this place out yet, you really should.

Went there to try out the first free class! What a pleasant experience! I not not a yogi but I do practice yoga often.  Thought this all level class was great, the instructor kept letting us know it's okay to do the modifications and kept helping us with our forms.

The instructor was great, he was walking around and verbally instructing. I usually like to have a visual, but that's okay. 

The studio was tiny! By the time everyone arrived at 9am, people have to make room for each other. I do think they need some sort of markers so they can make the best out of the space!

Could Green Line be slipping?  We took a trip back to the old neighborhood this weekend, and I insisted upon stopping at my old favorite coffee shop for an iced chai latte, which I remembered being very good.  This time, it wasn't.  It was watery, and the ice-to-beverage ratio was ridiculously high.

The treats are still tasty, thank goodness.  I had a $0.50 vegan mini-cupcake, and it was outstanding - much better than those served at most coffee shops.
Great combos for lunch! I tend to go soup and sandwich but the other options work as well. Reasonable pricing and food sizes for the area, however, the quality makes it all worth it.

nice place for gatherings and hanging out. Friendly staff. 

Food was OK. Not bad, not good, it just sat somewhere in the middle.

I cannot give enough compliments to Cynthia! 
Once again I am super lazy with my hair and this is one of the areas where I know little to nothing. Cynthia was patient and discussed what I was looking for until we both agree on color and how much. Literally, I came in just going "I want purple hair!" and after establishing whether or not it was going to be all or highlights we chatted about color tones and then she worked her magic mixing an awesome color that made me grin from ear to ear! It is purple but not so cartoon-y that I would have issues at work. I have gotten nothing but compliments even from random strangers on the street! She has a great sense of humor and will make you comfortable in seconds. I really cannot recommend her enough.

I do happy hour here pretty regularly. It's near work and there is outdoor seating, a key element for this time of the year. There's good people watching and most of the wait staff is pretty good. Shout out to Armando--you rock!! He was my server last night and was good enough that I'll be remembering to ask for him next time. That's a first. 

The happy hour prices are very reasonable, as long as you aren't too picky about what you're drinking. It's pretty much Coors Light, Yuengling, House Wine etc. but it's do-able. I prefer something a little more original.

SO, their White Sangria is fabulous if you are picking something off of the regular drink menu. Food-wise the Risotto Balls are a decent option off of the HH menu and I enjoy the Margherita Pizza as well.
Excellent service, great ambiance, okay food and wine. Meh.

Good beer, excellent prices.  Packed even early on in the night.  Definitely a good choice if you're looking for a place to drink with friends, but you have to be lucky to get a place to sit.  Oh, and no cover phew!

a great Tucson institution.  Do yourself a favor and keep an eye on what's playing and check out the trailers.  You'll find movies that are off the wall wonderful that no one has heard about.  The membership is a good deal even if you only go once a month.  Only minor issue is movie times can be a bit wonky.
A wonderful book shop to go to if you don't know what you want and a good place to go if you know what you want is a step left of main stream.

if more stars were an option, i would rate hinge higher.

picture it: 

port richmond, wednesday, late into the afternoon, and needing a cake for the girlfriend's birthday by friday evening.

entered "birthday cake" into yelp's "search for" section, then proceeded to call every available bakery for the next several hours. hinge is number 50.

after calling dozens of bakeries, i learned that some places were either closed by 5p (which is interesting to note) or had closed entirely (which is entirely sad to know), or, simply could not manage a cake with only two-days notice; fair enough on all accounts. by this point it was 8:30p, and it felt like my hand was going to fall off.

and then there was hinge. 

i spoke with a super friendly server who immediately put T (not sure if its an initial-only deal so we'll just go with that), the head dining and pastry chef, on the phone. we decided to meet in person, it honestly could not have been a more pleasant experience.

T was so receptive to what i wanted. she didn't talk over me, question the flavors i had chosen, or show fear in the face of a two day deadline (primary responses from the calls that afternoon to appr. 30 other bakeries). 

the result was.. amazing. remarkable. truly inspiring. she created exactly the flavor combination i was seeking: a summery blend of lemon, coconut and fresh strawberry. 

one layer of frosting consisted of their limoncello cream and another of delicious coconut. strewn atop were perfectly ripened strawberries and edible white chocolate leaves (some colored green, how cute!) for decoration. the cake itself was a delightfully dense, moist cake with loads of flavor, coupled with a pretty red/orange icing for the inscription. T, hinge, and the cake not only met my expectations, but sincerely exceeded them.

this cake was full of delectable, discernable flavors that felt light on the tongue and yet full in the mouth. i have now put hinge on retainer for all my celebratory cake needs. 

speaking of which, my mother's birthday is in a week and a half and i'm seriously putting my order in monday.

Pretty good BBQ. I got the 2 meat dinner and felt like the portions could have been a bit larger. The wifey got the pulled pork sammich with fries and the portions for her meal were more than plentiful.

We primarily grub down at Jazzy's BBQ due to location, cost and quantity, but what separates this place from Jazzy's is the option of a BBQ brisket sandwich. 

There are only a few joints where we eat BBQ in town, and Holy Hog has just been added to the list. I still haven't found that 5-star BBQ place in Tampa yet.

Good food for a good price.  The day to eat here is on Fridays, which is the only day they serve up CORNBREAD.  It's not the best cornbread, but because it's only served on Fridays and is usually gone by 7 PM, you're brainwashed into believing it's better than it actually is. :)

(It is their potato salad that is the best.  Almost as good as mom's!)

Jazzy's has a good product, especially their pulled pork.  This is the only real "mom-n-pop" BBQ joint in NW Hillsborough County, but Jazzy's is missing the boat in 2 areas.  For one, they only offer 2 sauces, which are portioned out by whoever prepares your plate.  Due to the complexity of individual tastes, having the sauces available on the table for each person to portion out to their own liking would be a HUGE plus for Jazzy's.  Second, there is no beef brisket.

All-in-all, I give a 4/5 rating.  I've been eating at this place since about 2000 and don't plan on stopping anytime soon.

Been eating here (once a month on average) since it opened in 1996. 
 
Best Italian food, PERIOD.  Don't let it's chain properties deter you.

You want some really unique guayabera shirts that you won't find in places like Macy's?  This is the place to be.  I only rate La France with a 4/5 because the down side is their size selection.  Everytime I go here, I find a shirt I really like, but the XL are missing in action.

4 of us went into this place to eat after a movie last night, and we were immediately seated.  Shortly after we arrived, two other tables were seated.  After roughly 10 minutes, the other two tablesa had their orders placed, and none of the staff had so much as glanced at us.  We got up, walked out and will never return.  I'd rate this place 0/5 if it were possible.

Where do I start?  First off, let me say that I'm not really sure what's happened over the years with the Pipo's-Pipo's Jr.-Pepo's family saga, so I'll just claim ignorance.  What I can tell you is that the Northdale location still has one of the best restaurant Cuban sandwiches in the Tampa Bay Area.  Once upon a time, they actually used real baked ham (not boiled lunch meat), but those days are long gone.  Regardless, this is still my preferred spot to grab a Cuban sandwich.  I recently saw Aguila's on MVF Nation, and that place has been on my hit list for a long time.  I'll say that I was disappointed to see they do not include salami on their Cuban and douse their pork in mojo, so I've removed them from the hit list.  Pipo's on Northdale cooks up a traditional roast pork made with garlic, olive oil and au jus and that's what separates their sandwich from the rest.  It is placed on the sandwich shredded, as if ordering a roast pork meal.  This makes the sandwich wet, juicy and freaking delicious!  Go get one of those bad boys today!

PS - I know the PIPO'S on Hillsborough is the original location where it all began, but I find that spot to be kind of dirty and the staff is less than friendly to anyone who is not Hispanic.  Northdale is clean and the staff is friendly to ALL.

CUBAN SANDWICH REPORT CARD:

Bread: Fresh, bakery unknown.
Ham: Lunch meat
Pork: Pernil Al Horno
Salami: Yes, but no peppercorns
Swiss: Yes
Mustard: Yes
Pickles: Yes
Pressed: Upon request
Lettuce/Tomato/Mayo: Upon request
Rating: 4.0 / 5.0

This is a restaurant that's consistently gotten better over its existence, and I think that's really admirable. There was a period where I kind of stopped coming, because they raised their prices but the quality of food stayed the same. Soon after though, the quality of food did go up to its current level with prices staying the same. They've cut some of the fat too, removing the awkward dessert case at the front that blocked traffic and tightening up the menu a little.
But it's still a small place, and that's the big concern. If you come at lunch on a Saturday, you need to either come early or expect to take it to go, because there's very little seating. Also, it's often best to not get a drink because of the mess that ends up being created by the narrow passage where the fountain is.
But the food is really good. Since this place originally opened and my first review appeared about three years ago, the bread has improved, the meat has improved and the sauces have improved. The result is that it ends up being pretty good.
The future for BrushFire is a second location in Midtown and a liquor license. I think the latter is a natural progression, but we'll have to wait and see how the expansion goes for them. I'll continue to support them just because I think they do a good job, and they're locally owned.
