Had my faithful Entertainment card and decided to go back to Guadalajara Grill once more.  Last time I went was for a happy hour for the office and it was plain awful.  Place was packed, noisy, food was yukky.  But I figured I'd give it another try.  Being I had the card was motivation for me to try it again.  We went early on Sat. night just before 5.00 PM.  It was quiet and I was quite overjoyed to enjoy a wonderful meal. Some newer different items on the menu such as Halibut Enchiladas with sauteed veggies and rice.  Rice was just so so.  But the Halibut was yummy as well as the veggies.  Hubby had a combo plate and enjoyed that as well.  Even had leftovers  which were just devoured the next day.  Of course, the salsa lady offers at the table service and it was made to order.  Excellent.  Being the cheapskate I am when it comes to food.  Not sure if I would run back here as they are a bit pricey.  La Sur on 22nd  for $4.99 has wonderful chicken enchiladas.   Much better value and just as good.

Bistec Rancho on Thursdays!  Wow - Too much good food to eat at a decent price.  However, do not... I repeat...  Do not go here for lunch on work days.  You will be worth NOTHING when you get back to the office.  I promise.

I've been to Mexico and the food really tastes MEXICAN.

Ironically, I've only been here a few times and have yet to eat a taco.  =/

I have to go 3/5 on this one.

My co-worker and I went here for lunch one day after their staff was handing out flyers in our office.  The chicken tiki masala was excellent, and I'd love to eat there again.  However, the food took 45 minutes to come out!  The guys sitting at the table next to us came in about 10 minutes later than we did, yet they received their food before we received ours.

If I go again, I'll make sure I've got a few hours to spare.

Best Cuban bread in town.

We like to go at 2 or 3 AM and get it while it's hot and fresh.  By the time we get it home, it is still warm enough to melt butter.  

Pan Cubano con mantequilla... It's the perfect early morning snack.

I live in Westchase and drive my cars 40 minutes south to Port Tampa to Prime Performance.  There is no one I trust more for vehicle repairs than Scott, and I've never had to take my car back for re-work.  This is the only place I've gotten work done since 2002!

The price is right and the level of trust is beyond words.

I've tried to like their pizza so many times over the years...  It always smells good when walking by and seems like a great idea...  But when you eat the pizza, it's just just missing something.    

I have also heard a lot of mamajahambo about them importing their tap water from NY and blah, blah, blah.  But I'm hear to tell you, as a previous NY-Style pizzeria owner, this would have absolutely no impact on the pizza crust.  It's flour, water and yeast, folks.  It doesn't matter whether the water comes from NY, Tampa or freaking Zimbabwe.  

I wouldn't recommend this place for the true NY-pizza enthusiast.  It's just "se deja comer."  De Losa's in Madeira, Paci's on Dale Mabry, New York New York or even Amore Pizza are better NY-style options.

Let's not even get started on how dirty the place looks.  Perhaps they import their grime from NY.

I love this place.  Very busy at lunch but they JAM their asses off to get you your food fast!  Food arrived in under 5 mins, and tasted great.  Staff was on the ball and friendly.  And you gotta love any food on a Pita!

A GREAT PLACE TO EAT!

Ive gone twice to the one using my...you guessed it GROUP ON! I have been to this one before when I lived in Chicago, and it is awesome and love it.

All of the brazzilian places have the same jist. Fancy salad soup bar, yummy appetizers like the cheesy bread balls, bananas, mashed potatoes or polenta.

You also have your red light green light which is fun to do, but the gauchos will come even though you don't have those signs.

My fave place to sit is outside with the nice fountain in the back. The waiters even say you can feed the turtles left over meet. I was all about that seeing I love animals. lol

The steak is wonderful amazing, tasty yummy. it's worth the $$$$$.

Absolutely spotlessly clean, very friendly service, and wonderful food.  Their garden salad with homemade croutons is beyond compare!  The prices are also very reasonable.  You can't go wrong getting food from NYPD!

Like Landmark, don't love it.  Food is good, but nothing to get super excited about.  Great spot if you're looking for some drinks and some appetizers to snack on.  The sandwiches are good, but they're missing something.  Maybe its just me, but nothing seems to taste extremely fresh here.  For dessert, I would recommend the banana xanga, closest thing to the old Applebee's Apple-Chimy-Cheesecake I've ever had.  They have awesome happy hour specials like any college bar should.  Plenty of TVs for sports fans.  Young crowd.  Young staff.  Would give Landmark a 3.5 stars if I could, but maybe I'll have some great experiences in the future that will call for an update.

The assorted cookie plate for dessert is always a good bet.  It may seem a bit odd to order cookies as a restaurant dessert, but these are not your ordinary cookies.  They have super hero powers which will rescue your taste buds from the evil grip of ordinary.  And they come with chocolate fudge dipping sauce - think of it as the super hero cape.   Both cookies and sauce are warm when they hit your table.  The other day the assortment included macaroons that were amazing.  Dip those in the chocolate sauce and, oh boy, I'm still thinking about that delicious decadence.  The flavors rotate on a daily basis, but with 4 different sets of 2, everyone at the table will find a flavor they like. 

My experience has been consistently good quality food and good service.  I'm typically there at lunch time so the bistro portions of their entrees are right up my alley.  As of late I enjoy the roasted pork with butternut squash risotto topped with figs and goat cheese.  This dish incorporates some of my favorite flavors in the world, and it is super yummy.   I actually like sitting in the bar for lunch.  The bartender is friendly and does a great job waiting the tables in there.

The Hungry Greek... Panos Kouzina... Best Greek for in their respective areas. Acropolis in WHY-BOAR... Best Greek food I've ever tasted anywhere. The stuffed mushrooms are literally the size of a baby's head, the gyro meat is so tender and juicy, but the pastitsio is incredible. If you haven't ever had this dish before, you're missing out. Let me try and explain it. It's sort of like a lasagna, but made with a penne-like pasta and a sweet tomatoes sauce with a slight nutmeg and cinnamon after taste. And yes, it's topped off with Gruyère and Parmesan cheeses. It's insane.

Side note, while you're eating, some guy will come from the kitchen and start breaking plates on the floor. Not sure what that's about, but it's fun to watch. The owner must get a hell of a deal on plates in bulk.

Do your stomach a favor and go get a fist full of this grub.

We used to go to Guissepe's on Tangue Verde the owners last restaurant.It was fantastic. I was very disappointed in the new place. Just another average Italian restaurant in Tucson. The prices are cheap though.
The Ravenous Roofer
I'll make it short - best coffee I've found in Tucson, cozy atmosphere, premises squeaky clean, courteous, friendly employees, and moderate prices.

From the smartest bartender, to the wonderful, creative food and presentation, M proved to me it is worthy of 5 stars.

Starting with the cheese plate, 6 choices from soft to hard, the selection was perfect.  Presented by the cheese sommelier with an introduction of each, added to the anticipation of which proved to be an extreme highlight.

The "cavatelli and fiori pasta" had both rich and fresh flavors, which made your palate yearn for the next dish.  And so it came, the short rib.  Tender, no fat, melt in your mouth, warm, delectable, comforting and probably the best short rib this side of Gramercy Tavern in NYC.  

The wine list was not overwhelming and not extensive...so be it.  The selection was adequate.

A varied crowd, from old to young, but certainly respectable and relaxed "up scale".  Perfect for a Saturday evening.

Oh my, I am going back for more.  I loved this place.

I normally go to my favorites, BUT this time, I wanted to try one of my Book marks. So my Brunch buddy & I came here Sat. noonish. Sat down, nice interior, chalk board with specials*, seats were very open, kitchen sat way in back. The servers seemed little off.Once we ordered the special-" Toasted Coconut creme brule french toast , with vanilla anglais creme sauce" it was HEAVEN, just enough -we split it. We also ordered side Turkey bacon(ham) PLEASE -Do not order this, unless you WASNT ham, thick & chewy like ham they sliced off at Thanksgiving. No, that 's not what i was going for !! Should have stuck to Swine-extra crispy!! Damn it!! Make a note of it!! BTW-the coffee here was excellent, robust & flavorful ,gave you cup raw sugar cubes-delightful!!!
All in all, good visit, lots locals sat outside, I'd go back, see how their service improves, little sluggish.

I am writing specifically about The Little Explorers Class on the first Wednesday of the month! Every month is a different theme! I took my 2 year old daughter for the first time on flowers and bees day and it got me hooked! It's $10 from 10am to about 11:30am for ages 2-4 which is great and just enough time to keep their interest, entertain and educate them and still get the home for lunch and nap time! It was great... First they started off in the building where the colored, read books on flowers, looked at different flowers through magnifying glasses,matched up flash cards with different flowers on them, and had someone showing beeswax and explaining about bees. Then we took a walk through the garden. Looked at, smelled and touched different flowers, visited the lily pond and learned a lot. It was insanely hot out and the instructors had spray bottles to mist the kids with. Then we went back into the air-conditioning for snack time which is based around the theme of the class! That day it was rosewater lemonade, and yogurt with granola and a edible flower in it! The kids then did a flower art project and while it was drying they moved under a tent outside for songs and a skit on bees which helped some kids with their fears! It was adorable! The instructors were attentive and friendly and I very much look forward to going back! Great activity for stay at home Moms! Look on their website for upcoming dates and themes. I believe there is a strawberry day coming up as well as a don't be afraid of bugs day too! It's a real short drive from the city, not even 10 minutes from South Philly! It's cheap, educational, and fun! 

UPDATE: We have been attending classes here for years. Now, with both of my children. It's truly lovely. We pack a picnic lunch for afterwards. It's always something I look forward to with my girls. They really do love it. Mr.Zack who now runs the classes and scheduling is so great. You can call him to reserve a spot or now you can do it on their website!! They do fill up quickly. Last I heard they were trying to add another class or may have. There's also a homeschooling day. I have a lot of good memories here with my kiddos. I cannot wait to head back there soon. We usually just do the yearly membership which winds up being cheaper than the individual classes. Highly recommend.

Went here this past Saturday and didn't have the same favorable experience.

We got there around 5 PM and were served some pulled pork and brisket sammiches that were warm and a tad bit on the dry side.  Also, the buns were hard and rubbery, which made chewing the sandwich arduous.  We also got some fries and they seemed to be recycled.  It was as if they were fired up earlier, unsold and saved for refrying later.

I am still going with a 3/5 rating though because I know that closer to lunchtime their food is on point.  So heed my words of advice fellow Yelpies, be careful going here in the evenings.  I won't go past 2 PM again.

A marked improvement over Barrio, its predecessor in the space, Downtown Kitchen + Cocktails is a more relaxed counterpart to Janos' La Paloma operation.  My wife and I came for lunch during the work week, and the restaurant was busy but not full.
We started with the calamari with mango and candied ginger, which proved an interesting take on the fried calamari staple, although I found the mango to be a little bit subdued.  I ordered the open face coffee BBQ beef brisket on griddled focaccia, which was rich and savory, without much of the sweetness common to barbecue in Arizona.  I liked the sandwich, although it didn't quite measure up to the (significantly sweeter) pulled pork available down the street at 47 Scott, and was a few dollars more expensive. My wife had the tuna nicoise salad, which was phenomenal, better even than the similar salad at the Cup Cafe.
The atmosphere at Downtown Kitchen + Cocktails is hip without being pretentious, and the staff were helpful and pleasant.  I'm looking forward to coming back for dinner and drinks.

We had the perfect meal here on a Sunday evening.  
This small BYOB is a place my friends and I had been wanting to try for ages but never seemed to get there.  As another birthday dinner popped up on our calendars, we made our decision based on geographic location since birthday boy lives kind of close to Pumpkin.  We went for the prefix Sunday 5 course meal for $36. and We booked a 6pm table of 6 since it was a work-night.  Timing was perfect.  The restaurant was relatively quiet as we arrived and we had such an attentive welcome and service.  

Rather than describe specifics, I get the pleasure of saying that everything we had was perfect, beautifully presented and prepared.  Servers kept our glasses full and introduced each of the complex components of every dish with the authority of a museum guide and built up anticipation one experiences from a magician.

Months later, a simple mention of this meal in conversation, we are all easily salivating at the idea of a return.  

Please, do not make me regret sharing this info with you.  If you read this, I'd appreciate you not attempt to reserve tables on nights I intend to go there.  Thanks.
Good lunch menu. Love the coffee setup. Very good service.

I mean this in the nicest way possible: Café Lutecia is the Doobie's of coffee shops. As cozy and lived-in as your grandparents' basement, this café is a far cry from some of the newer, trendier coffee shops. 

The food smelled heavenly when I stopped in yesterday for a quick iced coffee to go. Several people appeared to be regulars, and the tables outside are always occupied whenever I pass by.

I offer up only three stars because I think the coffee is average, but I know that I'll be returning soon for lunch based on the raves of other Yelp friends. The sight of a bubbling hot, cheese-topped French bread was all I needed to make a mental note to plan my next visit.

I won't be back due to the snooty, yet inattentive service. We went for the center city district sips promotion on a slow, but beautiful Wednesday afternoon lured by the beautiful outdoor seating area. There was no host, so we sat ourselves at one of the many empty tables.  The waiter came over and asked if we had a reservation. When we responded no, that we were here for Sips, he told us, "you can sit inside or over there." I understand that you may need tables for people not part of the promotion, but at least tell people in an polite tone or have a host to tell people where they should sit. Their promotional drinks were bottom of the barrel. They forgot to bring someone a drink after it was ordered twice. We got out as soon as we could after that.  
That is the purpose of a center city district sips promotion program, to get people to try something new and see if they will go back.  The answer for me is an emphatic no.  
It is a shame, too, because we all agreed that we would have liked to go back for brunch sometime. Our friend was also thinking it would be a great event space for his wedding. By treating the guests like second class citizens, they lost business.  So one needs to wonder why they are even taking part in the promotional program?
meh - nothing spectacular to report.  Everything we had was just okay.

Not as good today - but still a classic.
I still prefer Oregon over the Melrose and Penrose diners.

I am grateful to be able to find a few good "old school" diners.

DD had chicken fingers 'n fries (theirs are very fresh and crisp), DH and I had breakfast.

2.5.  Unfortunately, we were disappointed in our much anticipated trip to Laxmi's.  We bought a $30 Groupon for $15 and used it on our visit.  We ordered the chicken malabar for an app which was very good.  The chutney was excellent.  For entrees, we shared chicken tikka masala and lamb korma.

We had a lengthy discussion with our server about spice.  We said we wanted a little bit of spice ("3-4" as the waiter noted on a scale from 1-10).  We were hoping this would have a mild-moderate amount of spice given how hot Indian dishes can be, but we figured erroring on the side of caution would be OK.  I'd rate the amount of spice we ended up getting as a 0 on a scale from 1-10 which was disappointing.

Ignoring the lack of spice, the chicken tikka masala was OK and the lamb korma lacked basically any flavor at all other than the mild flavor of the meat itself.  This was, of course, quite disappointing.  I'm by no means a great chef and my fiancee remarked on our way home that the chicken tikka masala dish I make is better.  The garlic naan we ordered was really the only flavorful offering which is really a shame in the realm of Indian cuisine.

The serving sizes seemed small because both entrees were served in ~12 oz bowls, but once you got everything on the plate, it wasn't too bad albeit a little light on the chunks of meat.  Service was very good.

All in all, I don't think we'll be back.  It would have been way too expensive without the Groupon and even with it, the food just wasn't flavorful enough.  I've only been to Indian lunch buffets before this experience and I'd opt for the buffets simply based on flavor.  It's a shame because a good Indian place in Manayunk would be great.

I took my wife here for her birthday. Our reservation was for 6:00. We showed up about five minutes early and were seated immediately. We had a short chance to look at the wine list and menu. Soon we were greeted  by a server accompanied by a sommelier. We were asked what kind of wine we like to drink. I also interject that it was likely we would probably be ordering some sort of pasta dish or perhaps something with a red sauce. That seemed to be what caught our eye on first glance at the menu. So, the sommelier suggests an American wine, a cab from the Napa Valley. We take his suggestion.

Shortly before the wine arrives my cell phone rings. I get the dirtiest look from the people at the table next to us. I thought it strange that they were so offended by my phone playing the Imperial March from Star Wars. Anyway, the wine is served and we get a short lesson in wine from the sommelier. Having the sommelier was a nice touch.

I see lamb shank Ossobuco  with wild mushroom risotto on the menu. That's for me. My wife orders the Bolognese and we both order salads, hers the mista and mine the panzanella, a special that day.

Our salads arrive. A note: When the menu said panzanella I was expecting cubes of bread and not so much greens. What I got was a salad with  soggy crostinis and diced tomatoes on top. I expected more for the $12 price tag. Nonetheless, the vinaigrette it was dressed in was light and very good. My wife said the same about the vinaigrette on her salad.

Our salad plates cleared, the entrees arrive. When mine is set down in front of me I lose my breath. Mostly because of the sheer size of it. It looked more like a brontosaurus shank than a lamb shank. It was huge. I wish I had taken a picture of it, which my wife suggested but I was hesitant because of the previous reaction from our neighboring table. 

The Ossobuco is falling-apart-no-knife-needed tender and the sauce is very good , however, the risotto is overcooked almost to the point of being mushy but still has very good flavor. There was also this large piece of skin that was completely inedible. I know that the skin is vital in the cooking process, but I am not sure if it should be served. My wife is very happy with hers. 

I am not a food expert, in this life or any other, but I consider myself a competent cook, somewhat knowledgeable about food and, for sure, know what I like to eat, so I hope none of this comes across as snooty. Don't get me wrong. I am not unhappy with the food. These are just minor things and I feel like a snob because I bring them up. When you are paying this kind of money for a meal, I feel the need to be honest.

They gave my wife a free dessert for her birthday. We shared the tiramisu, which was the best we have had anywhere. The server even lit a candle and covered it with a wine glass while she carried it to the table. This was another photo opportunity missed when it was placed on the table.

It was little things like the candle by the server and the sommelier that made the overall experience amazing, I would not hesitate to recommend Tavolino to anyone.

Soul Food Sunday with a group of 5...

We got there a little past 11 AM and the place was already at 95% capacity, but we got right in and were seated immediately. As soon as we were seated, our waiter took our drink order. Immediately after bringing our drinks, we had our orders taken. The pace was pretty fast, which was a pleasant surprise. Some of the yelpies suggested service was a bit slow. 

Items that we ordered as a group were ribs, The Itis, fried chicken & waffles, a BBQ brisket sandwich and a funky monkey waffle. We also ordered the 3 side special for $9 and got the greens, cheese grits and jalapeño cornbread. WE ATE LIKE F&%CKIN' BOSSES.  My chicken & waffles was delicious. The chicken wasn't the best fried chicken I'd ever had, but the dish was good and filling. The waffle was light and fluffy and came with a maple bourbon gravy, which was good, but I like straight up syrup on my fried chicken & waffles, so I slathered them up proper and went to town. I traded a chicken leg for one of my bro's ribs, and I've got to say that was one of the better decisions I've made in life. The ribs might have been the most surprising item on the table. Never in my short life have I seen so much damn meat on a rib. It's like it was a hippo rib or something... I don't know. And the flavor was unyieldingly impressive to the taste buds. For a place that doesn't specialize slow-smoked BBQ full-time, I was utterly flummoxed. The cheese grits were good (and I don't like grits), the collard greens (with bacon) were perfect, and the jalapeño cornbread was ok (it was a little over-cooked).

We topped everything of with 3 shared deserts: The chocolate peanut butter chimichanga (pretty good--very peanut-buttery), the chocolate covered bacon (fantastic) and peach cobbler (good, but not better than Hungry Harry's BBQ in LOL).

Group opinions at the table: The BBQ beef brisket sandwich is good, but better at Holy Hog. The BBQ ribs might be the best on the planet. Better than Holy Hog and Jazzy's. 

I'm going with a 4/5 because the total experience was on point the first time, but a little lack-luster on my second trip.  The atmosphere was fun with Carl from Vinyl Fever fame spinnin' the Jazz wax and the cool art hanging on the walls.  The staff was friendly and very attentive, and the food was slammin'.  I will definitely be back, regardless of my taking away of a star!

OK, Here is my review. Frankie's South Philadelphia Cheesesteak was pretty good. It was a restaurant with a lot of character. it was clean, and I liked the yellow walls. I got the Cheesesteak and it was OK. I didn't think it was great but Frankies is worth trying just for the experience.

Retrospect has really adorable clothing and I usually want to buy everything in sight.  The downside is that most things are $10+, which is pretty pricey for a poor girl like me.  Overall it's a great place to look around in, and you just might find the old man pants of your dreams.
Awesome "Smashed Potatoes" and Prime Rib! Mojito's are great too! One of my favorite places to eat!

I'm going to jump right in to talk about my favorite dish on the menu. In fact, it is my favorite Italian dish. Wait, it may be my favorite restaurant dish in all of Tucson. Ravioli al Caprino. Tender, melt-in-your-mouth pasta. It's pink since it's made with beet juice but don't let that throw you. It is awesome. They stuff it with an herb and goat cheese filling and top it with not one but two sauces. A small amount of butter and sage sauce that enhances the richness and then a very light tomato sauce whose acidity balances the dish perfectly. I cannot put into words how wonderful this dish is. I've had it more than once and each time it has been fantastic. 

A couple of the other dishes I've tried have been good but not on the same par as the ravioli. Both were specials that aren't on the main menu. That extra kick of flavor was missing. Still tasty but not something I'm dying to eat again. The desserts are most excellent. 

Service is good though on my most recent visit, our waitress told us the specials before all of our party arrived and then didn't go through them again. She also didn't offer to get a drink for our last arrival. It wasn't that big a deal but not up to par with my expectations. 

I'll definitely continue to frequent this establishment. There are other menu items I want to try if I can ever tear myself away from the ravioli.

My go-to lunch spot!  You can get everything from sandwiches, salad, sushi, pizza, Chinese food, BBQ or burgers here.  Most days I am in a rush and just grab some sushi or make a salad, but when I have time I'll get the chicken quesadilla.  The 10 minute wait is completely worth it!  There is a small seating area near the salad bar if you prefer to dine in as well as some seating with shady umbrellas outside.  

Check out the bakery.  They make everything from eclairs to cookies and cupcakes... so many tempting desserts!  I actually ordered my wedding cake from Publix Greenwise (my guests loved it!).

So many great things I can say about this grocery store... good cheese and wine selection, fresh flowers, great sushi and pre-made meals.

I love Sephora, however I rather shop online. When you go into the store its either you have a every other person asking you do you need help or no one extending their help. Either way its annoying,Well I was just there on Saturday with my sister. Ok I know enough about make-up like Im no Bobbi Brown or Tarina Tarantino but I know my stuff. As I telling my sis about some products. This clerk comes out of nowhere with her damn input. We smile and walk away as I am talking again here comes Mrs.Knowitall. Like really where was you a week ago when I was here needing help?That really bothered me. My sister must have known I was getting irked because she suggested that we should go to Macy's. I love Sephora products and my little random free samples I get. But like I said in the beginning I rather shop online.

I love bread. It is one of the key measures by which I judge a restaurant. Vero Amore uses their fiery 800-degree wood fired oven working to bake delicious, chewy, tasty bread. When you visit, be sure to get the Wood Fired Bread with olive oil or butter. It's so hot, it steams when you cut into it. 

My wife and I also got the Prosciutto Caprese, a big round ball of homemade mozzarella, prosciutto and tomato slices with a pesto dressing. This was a salad that could be an entree and it hit the spot.

We were nearly full when our pizza with Andouille sausage (a la New Orleans!) arrived. Man oh man, you can't beat Andouille with cheese and a chewy crust. We couldn't finish it.

The extra-cute waitress who smiled every time she made eye contact told us about the chocolate chip cookie baked in a skillet and topped with two scoops of ice cream with chocolate sauce. We were too full but ordered it any way. You only live once, right? It was so good, I could come here and just have desserts for dinner.

My only complaint is the noise level. The floor in concrete, the walls are flat and there is no sound-dampening ceiling, so after a few drinks the diners (plus a really loud squealing baby) got really loud. The upside of the racket is that it forced people on cell phones to go outside to make their stupid mid-dinner calls.

I am coming back for more bread.

BEWARE of this place!  One visit and you'll be hooked.  Next thing you know you'll be contemplating 30 minute trips across town on the weekends and taking long lunches during the weekdays in order to get your fix.  

They have a lot of unique flavors, and everything is fresh, natural and made on site.  The guy behind the counter happily explained their ingredients and processes, and his passion for the product was obvious.  I tried several flavors which were all delish.  The salted caramel (they even make their own caramel sauce!) was awesome.  Since it was 105 outside I opted for the pineapple basil sorbet.  Not wanting to limit myself to just one flavor, I asked for a complimentary flavor suggestion.  Yogurt gelato (it tasted like original "tart' yogurt) was the suggestion, and it was perfect.  

The staff is great. Patiently doling out sample after sample.  They'll mix and match whatever you want.  They happily stuffed 5 different flavors into a cup for one patron.  

By far this is the best gelato I've had in Tucson.  If you haven't been yet, get there!

I've been coming here since I was a teenager. My family & I would often come here after church for brunch.

Now that I'm an adult, Oregon Diner has become the absolute go-to spot for brunch and late-night dining after the club. Out of a few places open 24 hours, this, by far, is the best place you get awesome eggs benedict, the best home fries and grits in the city, or an omelette at 3am.

Service is great, prices are even better. Oregon Diner is where it's at!

The line in the mornings is usually at least 5 people deep, but it moves pretty quickly.  The only thing I've had is their iced coffee and it's great - and cheaper than s*bucks.  The people behind the counter are all efficient and happy, and that's all you can ask for to start your day out right.

Something told me to give this place another try since it's so close to home, and the fam absolutely loves ice cream and frozen yogurt.  I'm going to stick with my original 2 stars.  The place was pretty busy when we got there, and people were darting around like chickens with there heads cut off.  It was chaotic as hell.  One woman kept cutting in front of me while I was waiting in line at the soft serve machines...  I suppose her order was front-burner to mine.  I was willing to overlook this because it wasn't really a reflection of the business (other than the layout being ineffective for more than 3 or 4 parties at a time), but just as I was reaching for the lever to fill my cup, AN EMPLOYEE jumped in front of me and got some yogurt for a drive-up order.  My wife and I stood there in utter disbelief.  It was crazy.

As for the yogurt itself, it was good.  I got the creme brulee and slapped some hot fudge on top of it.  All 4 of us thought the selections were tasty, but couldn't get past the frustration of gathering of the food.

Tonight will definitely be the last visit.

Compare this gelato to Frost (very good) and Café Italiano (mediocre) and you will realize that this gelato is among the finest on the planet. Seriously. I've had award-winning gelato in California, which was incredible, but Allegro's is truly in a class by itself.

Others have given details about some of the flavors that they have. Some flavor combination are just amazing. The creamy consistency and depth of flavor make it truly artisan gelato. EVERY flavor has something to offer and when you taste them, you get the "Oh wow!" moment. Allegro's will awaken taste sensories that you never knew you had.

Go and try Allegro's. Five ***** rating with a lot of ratings. You cannot go wrong

*********UPDATE

Just went there on Wednesday, 3/15/12. Of course "just" is a silly word as I go there quite often! The peanut butter gelato is--wow! I had the Spicy Chocolate, though. Just love that spicy kick it gives you. And I also had the Strawberry. SO GOOD. You see, we moved from Central Valley in CA where you get REAL strawberries. Not the gross, under-sweet, unripe, crunchy strawberries you get at the grocery stores. The strawberries that you get from the various stands around town where you buy 4 trays for $7 and then you eat them like candy because they are so sweet and juicy. And Allegro's Strawberry gelato tastes just like that! That's why his Strawberry gelato isn't always in the case. He only likes to use good strawberries. And there are really only two strawberry seasons.

This is just a testament to his commitment to freshness, quality, and taste. Seriously, there are not many delights where the pleasure sensation lasts LONG after it is over. Allegro Gelato is one of those rare ones.

************UPDATE NUMBER TWO

Went to a friend's wedding reception and--so cool--they had a Frost gelato cart there! I could tell just by the way it was scooping up that it was not the quality of Allegro's. Then I tasted it. I had the salted caramel and the strawberry. The salted caramel was passable, nothing great. The strawberry? Horrible! Tasted like medicine! It was weird. I could not finish it. My wife agreed.

Allegro, have you forever ruined me on quality of gelato? I can't stand anything less than top quality, which is you!
Great place to eat. Great appetizers, awesome happy hour tapas, food and the wait staff were great.

Don't get me wrong, you can get a decent meal here.  But I'm continually amazed at how bland the food is.  I've been here a few times with friends (who love it) and it just lacks all spice and flavor.  Maybe it's Mexican food for people who don't like Mexican food?  Maybe if you are starving, your legs are broken, and you can only manage to crawl to the restaurant, then you should eat here.  Otherwise, don't bother.

How I could have lived in Tucson all these years and not known about this place is beyond me. The salted caramel gelato is scintillating. Also tried saffron, pineapple basil, lemon and crema. All were awesome. As others have stated, the rotating freezers are the coolest, pun intended. Go there.
Yummm their jersey tomato pie is AMAZING! The sauce was so flavorful it was unreal! I can't wait to try the margherita..mmm fresh mozzarella and basil. Price is great too! Definitely recommended!

I used to love this place. 10  or 15 years ago (did Yelp even exist then?) I would have probably complained that there were not enough stars to award.

Alas, lately (I am sure it's just me getting old) I can only award three stars. The food is salty, inconsistent and service can be pretty bad.

Still, I have many fond memories of Micha's to fall back on. If only I could eat my memories.

This is easily one of my favorite places to relax, hang out with friends and enjoy good beer and food - the other two spots being New World Brewery and The Refinery.  Fun menu, ever-changing beers, blocks from home in Tampa Heights - what more can I ask for?

I think this place has a new name, I'm just not sure what it is.  I had dinner there last week and it was pretty decent!  You never know what to expect with these little dive-y places, but you hope you are finding a gem.

I got a tamale combo meal with one meat and one veggie tamale.  They didn't blow me away, but were very good with tasty sides.  I also got a small chicken taco that was great and I'll probably go that route next time.  My boyfriend got some kind of shredded beef chimi and this was the best of the night.  The tortilla was amazing - warm, full of flavor, and delicious!  The shredded beef was good too.  

The waitress was young and a bit flustered from their credit card machine not working.  Unfortunately they double charged our card and we're trying to work that out.  But overall it was a good, fairly inexpensive meal and I wouldn't mind stopping by again.

The new Yummy House location on Hillsborough Avenue was exciting to me as an enthusiast of the original location on Waters Avenue, but my experience there this past Friday night was bleak at best.

The service was horrific. The hostess told us there was a 20 minute wait and we waited much longer. When she did finally seat us, she incorrectly had us sit at the wrong table twice, both times not even bothering to take us to our table, but simply pointing. She told us they were waiting on two men to leave so they could clean a table for us and then sat us at another table. Once we finally sat at our real table we waited another 20 minutes for a server to appear.

The server arrived and did not ask us if we wanted anything to drink, but simply asked us if we were ready to order. We had to ask for drinks. We ordered our food and the food came out with long gaps between dishes, making it hard to combine the dishes. The recipes were similar to the original, with slight mishaps. We were served a Salt & Pepper Calamari that was actually Shrimp, but still announced as calamari. The waitress grew furious when she found out it was the wrong order. The two orders of calamari we did receive were not quite the same as the usual, with light seasoning and a different batter taste.

We were celebrating a birthday and wanted to check if there was any kind of special or promotion at the new restaurant, and the waitress almost misled us into paying an extra entree, which I canceled after confirming that they planned to charge for a "special".


The Duck and Dried Grape Fried Rice was tasty and on-par with what I normally enjoy. The Mongolian Beef was satisfactory. We hit a major problem when the waitress brought us "Mushroom Beef" when we had ordered Honey Pepper Beef. The waitress walked away when told it was the incorrect entree and spoke with her manager, proceeding to come back and argue that is was the right dish. I was sick of fighting with the service here, and was ready for this dining visit to end. 

The waitress did not come back for quite some time and when she finally did she asked us if we received all our food, clearly not having a clue what we did and didn't order. I asked for the check and the waitress had trouble calculating our order when most people paid in cash and with only one card paying the remainder. She decided to charge the number I gave her flat without bothering to actually use the register to calculate the difference. 

Once the check was closed we quickly made our leave from this disappointing visit to this new restaurant in Seminole Heights. Do yourself a favor and drive over to Waters Avenue until this new location gets its act together.

This place is the real deal. They can fix any color "mishap", and I mean ANY. The prices are reasonable, especially when you consider the skill level of the staff there. the cuts I get from Peggy are something I would expect from a top tier salon in a major metropolitan city, not necessarily a little place in a strip mall in South Tampa. The space is comfortable, unpretentious and friendly, but super professional. Barbara is a color genius who knows her stuff, and can just glance at your hair, and then come up with realistic recommendations.  I've tried a lot of places here in Tampa, but I'm done looking.

Went to dim sum with my family after hitting the Sanwa.  The place was packed and the parking lot was super full.  I hate to wait more than 10 minutes for a table if the place is packed because it could be another hour before you eat.  I walked in and there was  crowd at the door.  Oh-no!  I hear old Chinese women yapping and then they disappeared.  The hostess saw me and I was waiting for her to pull the stunt she did with Carlos in the first review.  I licked my lips and primed them to cuss her out. She hesitated a little when I told her how many's in the party.  I saw her think a little and walked me over to my table and handed us the menu.

The waitress came to take our drink order.  We order ice water and hot tea.  She only got it half right!  She brought out the hot tea for me and water for the baby and forgot about my husband.  We told her 3 waters and 2 teas!  I see the cart coming by and the waitress walked by us.  The ghetto came out: I stood and yelled for her to come back.  The waitress for our table called her by her name and that was when she came back.

The food is good but I didn't think it was extraordinary.  The white eggroll looking thing with the bright orange vegetable in it was the best thing on the menu.  It was something new and something I haven't seen before.  The sticky rice in lotus leaf is the best I have tasted in the whole country.  I think there was pumpkin in it.  The steam ribs was meaty and not all bone and cartilage like most places I have been to.  Everything else taste the same as other places around the country.

This place needs to work on their service.  This, lack of a better word, unprofessionalism would work in a hole in the wall place, but if you want to raise the bar and call yourself a bistro, you have to have a staff that speaks English clearly, the ability to make your clientele feel comfortable, feel at ease,  and that they are treated with individual attention.

Neat place, happened by it while out at International Plaza & while I've only been here once, I really enjoyed it. The atmosphere is open, the waitresses in kilts. 

The beer selection was extensive, the liquor selection to match. Had a pretty large menu consisting of the normal pub fare. I had their take on sheppard's pie with both ground beef & lamb, it was interesting & tasty, The GF had the stew which was hearty. If this place was closer to my home it would be my regular haunt.

We went there for dinner after searching yelp for a good Indian restaurant. We loved it. The interior is not really shiny, but the food is really good and so is the service. The bread is fresh and really yummy. When u go, try the mango lassi (mango juice w/ yogurt drink) it was the best I had in a long time. We'll definitely go back soon, and they have delivery too, which is great.

THIS REVIEW WAS FOR FORMER PLACE DIFFERENT OWNER AT SAME LOCATION. // The real deal, best gelato in town, and probably best ice-cream, period. Unlike a lot of Tucson food, this is as good as you'd find in places like Portland or Vancouver. I think the key is excellent quality ingredients and that it's made from scratch down to the caramel and fruit. He always has creative flavors too (like pineapple basil) that are interesting without being gimmicky. Pleasant service too.

Port Richmond and I have a long, funny relationship. We like each other, but we're also quite aware of the other's limitations. While you may be de rigeur for cheap warehouse space, freshly made pierogies, and swarms of rowdy polish teenagers, you are not quite a destination spot for fantastic food.

Hinge Cafe practically spits in the face of that last statement.

I have been many times - Father's Day brunch, last minute breakfast, weekday morning bagel & coffee - and every time, Hinge delivers.

The space: Cool, low-key, relaxed, lovely. You feel like you're eating at somebody's house. Somebody's really nice house. 
The noise level: Even when it's packed to the gills, I NEVER have to yell so my friends can hear me.
The food: Breakfast Bruschetta is PHENOMENAL. The Salmon Benny is revelatory, the roasted potatoes are seriously addictive, the coffee is delicious, the Monte Cristo, sinfully decadent with its ham and melted cheese, gooses you on the way down thanks to the addition of raspberry preserves. Yum. 
The service: Fantastic. Attentive, friendly, no ass-kissing, no pandering, no ignoring. Nobody is better than anybody at Hinge. Everybody's family.

The price is right, and If you have ever sat outside of another more-popular brunch spot in Fishtown or Northern Liberties for 20, 30 minutes before you were seated, you owe yourself a trip to the next neighborhood north for a fantastic meal with no wait. Not even on Sunday. At 11 AM. 

Come to think of it - I'm glad nobody really knows about Hinge. Stay away, bastards. It's mine.

Not a fancy sit down place where women make salsa table side, music and margaritas are flowing and food mediocre.....this is the real deal!!!! Best home made corn tortillas, best fish tacos, best beans, everything I have ever eaten here is amazing.....one of the BEST Mexican joints in this city. If you have not experienced this place, you are missing out big time!
Cheap drinks and comfy ambiance.  Great for day drinking and pretty crowded at night.  Awesome mojitos!

At the base of downtown Tampa's Skypoint building, this place give you a great view of the Glazer Children's Museum the adjoining park, the University of Tampa & Ashley Drive. 

The crowd here is mostly late 20's & above, many simply taking the elevator down from the Skypoint building itself to grab dinner & drinks. I've admittedly only ever had drinks here but every dish i've seen has look & smelled fantastic. 

It's a smaller venue (the bathrooms could be, you know, bigger), and until recently only served an admittedly extensive selection of beer & wine. They have started pouring some top shelf liquor, hence my presence. 

The best point of this place for me is being able to sit just off Ashley drive & have a drink on a gigantic sectional couch.

4 solid stars for happy hour.

Came here for Sips and got here right at 5. We desperately wanted some of the amazingly comfy couch seats outside, and luckily - I was the first one to arrive outside.

The servers were really nice and didn't judge me for drinking an entire beer and taking up a couch area big enough for 10 while waiting for my friends. Blue Moon and Sams Summer were $2 for Sips, which was great.

We had some yummy food - hoagie pizza, bbq wings, and shrimp tempura. 

Live music  and the guy was pretty good.

I have been trying to get some other friends to come here with me for happy hour ever since.

I might give a lower star rating if I cared too much about reception, because that is one snarky receptionist at the front (you'll know the one immediately if you've ever been there).  But the rest of the place is fabulous.  I've been there a couple of times and the staff is extremely attentive.

I'm a dude, too, and I care about my hair!  They take good care of it and you.
Stop in for some  lunch shopping and nothing changes in Sam's in or out of town they are all three same. And I'm not really big on the bulk stores (bj's, costco's ) they all look the same to me.
Casa Video is awesome!  Enough said.

Cool,hip, trendy delicious, consistently good with attentive servers.  Calamari is off the charts.  Great wine list, they pour a good drink and the city views are spectacular.  Noise level can be high but that's what happens in a very popular spot.  Braised short ribs are my favorite entree and the deserts are perhaps some of the best in town.

Had lunch here today. Chicken Philly pita was overflowing with all the right ingredients and then shrimp appetizer that my wife and  daughter shared was really well spiced and quite tasty.  Sat at the counter and watched the cooks working their magic which was really fun. The cook staff was clearly having a good time turning out great food and that can't be a bad thing.  Overall this is a much needed addition to the Tucson restaurant scene.

I really love the Curtis Hixon Waterfront Park in Downtown Tampa. It is located across the water from the beautiful University of Tampa and is a great place to take photographs. An ideal place to spend an afternoon at the park and watch the sunset. The monthly Rock the Park concert is held here for free the first Thursday of every month with local bands playing. Plenty of local restaurants are within a few minutes from here. The park is right next to the Tampa Museum of Art and Glazer Children's Museum. There is a dog park and a playground for kids. It is the most family friendly place I have seen on this side of town and it's nice to see people outdoors here and active instead of crammed inside of a mall.

This has become my go-to destination for craft beer in Tampa, which, while the biggest city in the area, seems to sport fewer craft beer options than its smaller cousins across the bay.

The Pour House is a very simple space carved out of the mostly huge and empty caverns that make up the ground floor level of Grand Central, the biggest of the newer condo projects in the Channelside area. At one time (prior to the recession), we were promised such amenities as a downtown Publix, as well as numerous restaurants, bars and retail shops at street level in these condo buildings. What we have, so far, is basically this craft beer bar. But that only means that it is very easy to park here for free.

There's a long granite bar and a long tiled wall of craft beer taps that change all the time. No food, but takeout is more than welcome, and they do offer free popcorn, a major plus. They seem to sell a lot of wine, as well, though why anyone would come here and order wine is beyond me. Maybe the wines are decent. I would not know. I am always focused on the craft beer and that is excellent, served properly, stored correctly and tapped with the proper equipment to ensure taste and freshness, something I cannot always say about some other places that are trying to cash in on the latest highly profitable craft beer craze. 

The beers here are $5.50 and up to $8, or so. The more costly drafts will likely sport a more than 7% alcohol content, i.e., you will catch a buzz on just one.

The Pour House is very dog friendly, which is another attraction for me. There are some leather couches and chairs, as well as lots of outdoor seating. Though they do offer live music, there is nowhere near enough. If they stepped up the entertainment here, this would be an ideal spot to drink a few IPAs.

Best italian cold cut sub, real meats, not just bolgna and mortadella, just the right amount of hots. Fries come out hot, great Philly cheese steak, both just like the east coast. Would be better with coke or pepsi products vs RC. Been in Tucson for 5 years, havent found a better italian sub.

I love this place!  The garden is amazingly serene despite being in the middle of the city.  I've been here 4 times in the last 2 months, for drinks and light dinner at the bar.

The drinks here are fantastic - I don't think I have had a bad cocktail yet.  The food is also excellent, although heavy on the salt (this seems to be a problem lately at many restaurants).

The rabbit is delicious - I have had it on 2 occasions.  The desserts are also incredible.  The Dark Chocolate Creameaux is a deconstructed s'mores with bacon crumbles.  I know it might sound weird, but it's to die for.   On my most recent visit, I tried the Millefeuille of Banana and Toffee, and that was also amazing.

I think this will be my favorite hang-out this summer.

By far, some of the best Chinese food in tampa bay. You have to get the salt and pepper calamari appetizer....it's fantastic. Fresh cuts of calamari lightly battered and topped with a garlicy, salty peppery concoction. The fried rice is superb (duck and grape). The xo scallops and shrimp was fresh and came with crisp snow peas. A lot of the other patrons got the walnut shrimp and they looked good too. 

I will definitely be back.

Delightful new location! Just reopene recently. Host Kim is extremely welcoming. She provides great suggestions of tea options. I had a pot of perfectly tasting mango black tea  Hubby had a great carafe of passion ice tea. They were both extraordinary tasting and put a smile on our face.
Our high tea consisted of chicken croissant sandwiches, pear and Brie toasted sandwiches, cucumber cream cheese sandwiches and turkey w jelly and curry. My fave were the first two mentioned. Scones were heavenly w Devonshire cream and strawberry jam. Lastly were fruit loaves strawberry cake and chocolate covered strawberries.
Everything was set up nice on the two layer serving platter. Very tiny looking like tapas but surprisingly very filling
I used a restaurant.con certificate and my bill was an even better price.
The ambiance atmosphere was captured perfectly w the decor music and the two dining room areas separated nicely by more formal w white linens to early flower garden room. 
U must check out the new place. It was a memorable experience!

Loved the location for a date night with hubby, we walked right up on a Saturday night and got prime seating outside. The food was good. Though the bread basket was not too good.  The cornbread was a bit stale and the other two pieces of pumpernickle seemed like an afterthought.  My salad was ok, pretty average and hubby said the salmon burger didn't taste like it was freshly made. read: pre-packaged and reheated. 

All in all, it was an ok night with ok food.  Nothing to write home about but we would go back.
Roccos is a local place with a ton of character and delicious pizza.

I really don't like writing bad reviews, but sorry guys, I had to on this one... I've been to the Independent in St. Pete many times, and think its "just alright". Being a Tampa resident, I figured it was time to try the one on my side of the bay...

Last night was the first (and probably will be the last) time I've visited this location. The woman working behind the bar was friendly enough, however the 'gentlemen', and I use the term loosely, working with her couldn't have been any more rude. Very short tempered and obviously wasn't interested in making any tips. I wasn't the only person in my party that griped about this; such a shame, because the bartenders and staff at the St. Pete location are stellar - especially compared to my experience last night...

The location itself is alright, the inside is a little dim but it's nice that they have more seating than their sister location. The outside patio was comfortable, especially with the fans. The fumes and noise from Florida Ave weren't terrible, but it would have been nice to have some music piped outside. Parking is decent, but could probably be more difficult during especially busy times.

Give it a shot, prices aren't too ridiculous - but make sure to avoid the balding guy, unless you like being treated like you don't matter... (Had I received better service, I probably would have rated them 4-stars, but it was THAT bad.)

This place is a nice addition (and change-of-pace) for DI. It's got a younger crowd than neighboring 'Yeoman's', but the service and their specials are fantastic. The outdoor seating area is nice as well, but inside is very comfortable and clean/sleek. Looking forward to trying the food sometime soon, it looked and smelled amazing. Thanks for the excellent service on many occasions!

Came here last night with a group of friends - first timer... As a recently-converted vegetarian, I wasn't too impressed by their "Sunday only" menu which featured mostly BBQ type cuisine, but I did glance through their normal menu - and would absolutely return on another night to sample that. 

While I only snacked off a friend's plate (a bite of a veggie burger and the fried pickles), my main course was their "Bloody Ella". Yum Yum Yum! If you like a bloody Mary, you have to try their take on it. The rim was sort of a spicy Lowry's type seasoning, but the vodka was tasty and the drink was prepared perfectly (besides having to add a few teaspoons of Tobasco for the extra kick that I love).

The building and their decor was fantastic, and our server couldn't have been any nicer. Don't be deterred by the location/neighborhood, it seemed safe and their parking was decent (because of the additional lot to their north).

Locked away from the busy streets is a secret alleyway that is cleverly designed as a garden with delicious fine dining. It is perfect for a glass of wine, cocktail or cheese plate. The prices are a splurge but try the 'beekeeper' with its honey lemonade, dark rum and ginger. It isn't exactly cutting edge but the 'beekeeper' went well with my meal and hey, if I didn't have to make it, even better.

On the other hand, my boyfriend thought the 'gardener' - a fragrant mint mojito was appealing but thought the rum was missing or it just tasted like the key ingredient was gone. On 2nd rounds, I stuck with my 'beekeeper' and the boyfriend tried plan B with the 'farmer' which was a step up in quenching the drink he wanted. Still, beekeeper was the winner in our little drink contest. As a side note--if you re-read that last paragraph I said it sounds like a naughty affair at the local farm. I dig it.

Talula's has a large selection of small cheese plates from $10 - 18. We decided on  the one called Not Your Granny's and it had 6 cow and goat cheeses made from local farms. Each had a smooth and creamy taste. We both had our favorites but they weren't the same cheeses. I've had better compliments with cheese boards at wine bars but I liked the toasted flatbread when compared to a fresh bread. We did have to ask for extra but there wasn't a problem when we requested a refill. Overall, Granny's was a 4 star cheese board.

 Our entrees were splurge #2 (unless you're bloody rich and do this all the time of course! Lucky!). The boyfriend had a duck breast, foie gras, confit leg parcel and beet greens with smoked beets for 28.00. I had the golden potato gnocchi, exotic mushrooms and egg in a raisin puree and mushroom jus for 25.00. Gnocchi is generally simple to make in most fine dining places. I'm torn with two thoughts: I think it was cooked the best at Talulas, but still more could have been done with the rest of it. The sauce was good and there isn't anything that I could complain about but fantastic food is fantastic food regardless of the price tag. I wasn't wowed and when you splurge you expect MORE. My boyfriend still had a dish that was also best described as generic in the world of fine dining. We liked our meals but we've had better.  

 Looking back I think the beekeeper and the cheese plate with the outdoor garden seating is what would bring me back to Talula's Garden.

Monday night at the newest Thai joint in town only proved that we are in desperate need of more good Thai food in this city. We scored a table for two around 6:45. By 7:15, every table was full. 

The small BYO makes use of its space by fitting as many tables as possible onto the dining floor. It's not too tight for diners once everyone is seated, but I noticed that it was difficult for the staff to get around at times. The walls really are as lime green as they look in the pictures, and the large windows have pretty green leaf patterns along the top panels, creating a tree effect. 

Throughout our dinner, our server seemed a bit swamped, but everything we ordered came out correctly. I did have to ask a couple times before I received a soda, and the same thing happened again when I needed a refill. In a few weeks, service should be smooth sailing.

We started with the dumplings, which were lightly fried and very flavorful. My boyfriend had a noodle dish that was phenomenal. The noodles were wide and flat and there were onions in the broth. I couldn't stop swiping bites. I had a chicken dish with bamboo shoots and bell peppers in garlic sauce. The dish was colorful and just what I wanted out of a Thai meal. 

The thing we liked most about our food was that the sauces and broths were very light and not at all syrupy. The flavors of the chicken and vegetables weren't cloaked in overly sweet or pungent sauces, so everything tasted bright and fresh. 

Lunch is a steal with three courses for under ten dollars. I looking forward to my next visit.

Love The Bricks!  Cool, low-key crowd, great beer selection, great wait staff, and awesome food.  My favorites are the kinky tuna and the greek salad, only it's not called a greek salad (can't remember the name).  Also, the Bird & Pig sandwich is delicious!
All these 5 star reviews are telling you to head over to the Loft and watch some of the best films you have never heard about. Tonight.

Went there this past Saturday (7/9) for dinner. Decided to sit outside, tables were dirty and three table were full of left over meals that were never cleared. Employees kept walking past and did nothing. The burgers were nothing special, gourmet?? Went to Burger Monger a few weeks ago, enjoyed much better.

I'm going to be a bit harsh here because my wife and I would really like to see a "real" pub in Tucson, or as real as it could be in the US anyway... This place could be it with a few changes.

We walked in and saw the "Please wait to be seated" sign. One of the bartenders had to come around and greet us, but she immediately said "sit anywhere you want". I noticed that sometimes she didn't come from behind the bar to say the same thing to others, and sometimes a server did it. It's slightly annoying to have to wait for a host/hostess, and then be told "sit wherever you want". Why wait then? This is not the way a real pub is supposed to work.

Management seemed to be a bit lacking. There were only 2 servers, there should have been 1 or 2 more probably. We had to wait a few minutes for a drink order, then a few minutes more to get them because the server got tied up. This is slightly annoying when it's 97 degrees outside at dinner time. And this is essentially a bar which serves food, drinks seem like they should be flowing freely. The above "please wait" situation probably wouldn't exist if there was a manager present, either.

We had Scotch eggs which were not authentic, but still OK. Salty was a word used before by others, that's probably a fair thing to say about these.

We both ordered bangers and mash. Canyon's Crown should be commended for trying to get the sausages right. This is as close as I've been to a banger in Tucson served for dinner. These had bread crumbs in them for sure. Nice. The mash were a bit sticky and salty. The beans should have just been (insert famous ketchup maker here) Baked Beans like many pubs would serve in the UK. Or at least better than those. These beans were nothing too special.

I don't know if it's fair to compare this to what a pub is supposed to be. Americans might not get it. But I still wish we had one. This is as close as we get in Tucson right now. It's still worth going to once in a while.

I live about six minutes away so I try to stop by the gardens on my days off to relax and sit on one of the benches with a book. It's a beautiful area kinda funny that it's next to what could be considered a "shady area" but I love it, it really is sorta like a rose in the concrete.

The breakfasts are the best. Try the oatmeal streudel and the fitgriddle. Both have yummy maple syrup.
The entrees are pretty good somehow despite being healthy but usually I could go with a little salt on top. The  different meats are usually tender and flavorful but the pasta is what could use some seasoning.
I've had several meals so far. I couldn't go full time with it, but most meals during the work week is do-able.

Tip: Save your containers. They have a recycling program. After 20 returned containers, you get a free small or medium entree!

Creative fillings for the savory crepes. Lots of variety for those guilty-pleasure dessert ones. My savory crepe was just ok. I had chicken and broccoli with bechamel sauce. I wasn't expecting the chicken to be shredded but it made for a less lumpy crepe.
For dessert, we split a strawberry nutella crepe. It was quite tasty and served with a few dollops of whipped cream.
It's a nice little place in Ybor to stop in for a sweet treat.
Service was kinda slow.

My family & I LOVE this place! Although I live across town, I'll gladly hop in the car at the mere mention of a deviled crab & Italian chef salad. Brocato's is always jammed-packed especially during the work week lunch hour. I call my order in so I don't have to wait as long. Finding a table to eat inside takes some patience, but there is plenty of additional seating outside. I have never been disappointed & the regulars don't the mind the wait. It's a Tampa dining treasure for sure!

I love the Mann - we sat on the lawn.   Unfortunately they removed some of the lawn and replaced it with PLASTIC CHAIRS.  Horrible.  More people sat in the grass than on those chairs.  I think that some chairs would have been a good idea but not so many.  Eliminate the center section of chairs and leave some on either side but BRING BACK THE GRASS.

Also, the "convenience fee" of ~$15 was outrageous and surely will be a deterrent from my attending a concert there.  I remember when the tickets were free.  Now we have to pay (no objection there) but $15 per ticket for "convenience"?

The Wolfgang Puck place looked cool with great views of the city.

I've had the opportunity to be here often enough to declare "yay! I'm a fan.". I'm not sure I'd drive specifically to come here, as I might as well go to the real thing in Ybor.  But as Channelside is one of my hangouts, the Columbia Cafe is hard to pass up.

The menu is not large but it's quality stuff. I've had the calamari, black bean soup, black bean cakes, AND black bean caviar (think black bean hummus - yum, I love hummus!). I've also had the empanadas, black bean soup, and Spanish bean soup.   Spanish bean soup was a bit salty.  

The bread - oooohhhh, the bread.  Nuf said.  :)

Their Cuban sandwich is pretty good, as is their fish.  Strangely enough they don't have "ropa vieja" in the menu.  I imagine they had to make certain compromises since they had to fit a restaurant into the lobby of the Tampa Museum.  Not to worry ropa vieja fans!  Walk 200 yards to the Tampa Marriott Waterside and ask for the "Havana Pot Roast".  It's ropa vieja!  And a good one too!

The white chocolate bread pudding is TO DIE for.  On second thought, I would drive over specifically to eat at the Columbia Cafe just so I could have this dessert.  It's topped with a rum sauce that will leave you wanting more.  My favorite is the the half Cuban sandwich and cup of black bean soup combo so I can save room for dessert!

The Columbia Cafe is a nice if somewhat informal place to have lunch or dinner.  If you have guests in town and are in the area, it's not a bad place to stop, especially after visiting the museum.

My only complaint isn't even a bad one... its so hard to get Phils tickets without a bit of planning.  Fantastic park - every seat is fantastic.  Great food.  Best fans ever.  

Yes the traffic sucks.  Yes the parking sucks, but deal with it.

I got a pork taco combo with beans and rice.  First, the lady was hand pressing the tortillas.  I could see the guy grilling the mean and onions.  It did take awhile to get my food, but with everything being made, I did not mind!  The soda machines were fun!  Who knew there were so many flavors of diet coke!

The food was flavorful and filling!  I didn't even get to the beans and rice.  I will go back when the next taco craving hits.

Here's a unique perspective: a person who doesn't eat beef goes to a burger joint!

"Whaaaaa????" You might say...

"Why?" You might ask...

Trust me, its not entirely as stupid a proposition as you might presume. Sure, 90% of their menu im not going to eat, and therefore not going to review. BUT vegetarians and french fry lovers everywhere will be kowtowing to the mere existence of this review.

Had been wanting to try this place ever since they showed up in AZ a while back. Ive been an occasional grilled cheese and fries orderer at In 'n Out for several years now and honestly really enjoy their take on it (the fries? not a huge fan, but i digress...)

5 guys offers for the veggies among us 2 options: Grilled cheese and a Veggie Sandwich. The website was unclear as to the difference, so I asked the lady behind the counter when we got there. The lady behind the counter was unclear as to the difference, so I ordered both. Ordered both 'all the way' (ketchup, mayo, mustard, lettuce, pickle, tomato, grilled  onion, grilled mushrooms)The difference? The bun on the grilled cheese is inside out.

Yup. Thats it.

Believe it or not, it makes a difference. Why? The grilled cheese's bass ackwards buns create good grill surface connection, and thereby create a nice crispy crust. Also, the grilled cheese somehow managed to stay together better.

For any fans of the In and Out version of this sandwich, I have two things to say: 1) 5 guys does it way better and with way more options than In and Out. and 2) for the price I paid for these two sandwiches, I could have eaten about  8 In n out grilled cheeses. You can figure it out for yourself which is more important to you.

Both I and my buddy grabbed some fries. I went cajun and he went original. The cajun was good, the original was better. The size of the fries, both in amount served and in girth of the individual fry is way bigger at 5 guys. The fries were good, but in my opinion a little undercooked, They were soft and well cooked all the way through, i just would have LOVED a crispy outside :(

On a side note, my buddy  and I both separately agreed that the cheese at 5 guys is way better. Its thicker, cheesier, and just tastes better. 

On another side note, unless youre a peanut fetishist, the salted peanuts is a gimmick, and theyre delicious, just take too much effort to actually get the peanuts out. 

Atmosphere was interchangeable with In n Out.

Staff were fun, friendly, inviting, and as helpful as possible. 

Ill probably go again sometime in the relative near future. When i get the craving. 

And yeah, people, its a burger place. Grease is involved. Get over it.

I've never been to Greece so I can't say I've tasted "authentic" Greek cuisine.  I have tasted just about every greek joint in Tampa Bay, and Acropolis is my favorite.  And of all their locations, the Ybor City location is by far the best of the best.

The hummus is absolutely delicious (did I mention I am a hummus nut?).  The falafel was a bit overcooked.  The pita bread is the Greek equivalent of the Columbia Cuban bread - YUM!  All their sandwiches and entrees are large, tasty, and delicious.  Flavor is there - not bland like other Greek restaurants.  

Atmosphere at the Ybor location is nice with live music and the napkin throwing ritual.  I would most definitely bring out of town guests here, and even come as a local.   Oohhh, almost forgot, they have great baklava...

Consistent, family-run and with family on-site and working. Nice wine list with reasonable prices. The menu is very good, Pastiche does not aspire to be an uppity fine-dining kind of place but rather to be friendly, contemporary and Tucsonan in personality. We bring our out-of-town friends here, and they haven't been disappointed.

I've really only ever been here for happy hour.  There was a point in time a few years ago where I was here every friday.  They have great drink specials during happy hour.  I think its dollar domestics and one liqour well drinks.  The drinks are drinkable but nothing special, then again they are only a dollar.  As far as food goes I have never had anything that was ineatable but its nothing super awesome.  It's simply bar food.  In my opinion at Bayou it's the people you're with who make or break the night.

Miguels is my favorite Mexican restaurant.  The atmosphere is relaxed and they have the best tasting carnitas in all of Tampa.  The obligatory chips and salsa are pretty decent too.

Algusto is just down the road with a little nicer atmosphere. They are both good although their food style is a little different.  Algusto is a little more "sophisticated" if you will, but by no means better. Just different. 

If you like Mexican try both out. Miguel's is sure to satisfy your carnitas craving.  Fajitas, burritos, and tacos are all nice, large portions and prices are very reasonable.  I'm a fan!

I'm tempted to give this a fifth star just to make up for all the childish whining in some of the other reviews. (Newsflash: Lots of places won't seat you until everyone is there. That's very common.)

I've been to Bellini a few times, the food is good, though not mind-blowing, the portions are standard, the menu diverse, the service attentive. And all at a very reasonable price. 

On our last visit we were greeted warmly by the manager (possibly the owner) he came over twice to check on us, which was totally unnecessary because our server was on top of things even though we were the only people seated outside.

It's not the type of place I'd go if I wanted to pull out all the stops or impress someone, but for a well-priced, enjoyable Italian dinner, it fits the bill exactly.

Want the best rolls ?Make sure its from sarcones.  Forget amoroso and liscios. Priced lower than they should for such a great product ! Hoagie,meatball sandwhiches ... Whatever you plan on having it'll taste better on a sarcone roll. worth the 15 miles drive from the NE

Yum, yum, yummy.  My mouth waters just thinking about the food here.  The vegetarian burrito, sans lettuce, is fantastic.  I swear they put an entire avocado in this thing.  Add some green salsa and eat it with jalapenos and my oh my.  The hubby loves the carne asada burrito, and you can't go wrong with a taco plate. The beans are the best I've had in town.  If you are looking for some down home mex you've got to check this place out.

This place was quite disappointing to say the least. The service was slow and unfriendly, the presentation of the fd was just basically just throw the food on the plate and the food was not even mediocre. $28 wasted on Texas hash which is roast beef w greasy au jus and a sunny side egg thrown on top. My blackstone  Benedict was no biggie either bacon spinach and tomato. Potatoes no biggie and they don't have fresh squeezed oj. Will not be back and I realize everyone raved this place and that's the reason I went there. Another oh well moment!

Mocha loved this store bc there were treats for him to try and lots of different high end dog food, leashes collars and toys but everything was quite pricey. Pricier than the petsmart and petco which r pretty pricey too but the stuff was definitely cute. If I want a once  a while higher end treat, We may get something. Check it out though! They also have a website shopdowntowndogs.com

Mmmmmm....Wright's Gourmet is a South Tampa icon. My favorites are the Beef Martini with a slice of Hummingbird cake on the side and fresh brewed iced tea with fresh mint leaves. Though you can't go wrong with any one of their many sandwiches, salads, & desserts.
Great local breakfast spot. Coffee self-served in mismatched mugs, bodacious breakfast sandwiches, & smiling servers make this a perfect Saturday morning stop.
Great for families. We always get the wings, chicken cesaer wrap or burgers. Lots of tvs to watch any and all sports all year round. Management is super supportive of local schools and little league.

When I really want a treat, I always know that Tanya will have a wonderful peanutbutter concoction available for me.  Whether it's the traditional peanutbutter chocolate milkshake, a peanutbutter banana smoothie, or just plain peanutbutter frozen yogurt; I can always get my PB fix here in the "creamiest" way.  I know I'm a bit of a nut for peanutbutter; but if there is anyone else out there like me, they'll love this place.  

And yes, for the more "normal" tastes - they always have a wonderful array of sweet and fruity flavors from which to choose.  If I was in a fruity mood, I would likely choose the Hawaiian Smoothie made with pineapple and mango.

As for my kids... they usually try all the flavors and then get a chocolate vanilla twist.  But, they keep it exciting by choosing lots of fun toppings.  You can make your own sundaes here.  But watch out... when you get to the register, you pay by the ounce and those tantalizingly artistic treats, piled high, will cost you plenty. Excellent marketing... no small cups available.

The owners take a lot of pride in this place and it's clear that your happiness is their happiness.

The fact that I have to give this place 4 stars makes me reconsider all my previous 4 star ratings because I feel like this place is significantly better than any other place I have given 4 stars to, and I can tell that I have just found my new favorite mexican restaurant in town. I sincerely hope that I can follow up this review on my next visit with a 5-star revision.

The phrases "no frills" and "attention to detail" almost never describe the same place, but here it absolutely does. Just about EVERYTHING made here is from-scratch, and the effort put into it is noticeable and delicious.

The menu is surprisingly extensive. I only saw the one side at first (obviously, right? I mean, not like I could see both sides at once), and thought they had a good selection. Then I saw the other side with just as many if not more options, and I was really conflicted for a minute as to what to get.

I got a chicken chimichanga, enchilada style with a side of rice. Service was expedient, the server was friendly, and the atmosphere was right up my alley - simple, laid back, and mellow. As with any restaurant of a particular ethnicity, its always a great sign to see people OF that ethnicity eating there. There were several hispanic families eating there, and also several men in full suits on their lunch break, and also several groups of guys out for a beer, etc...a real melting pot of customers. When that happens, you KNOW its got to be good food. 

They start you out with chips and salsa. The chips are made fresh, and they are fantastic. At first glance, they look like they will be too thick and heavy (as many homemade chips are), but theyre not. Perfectly textured and salted. The salsas come in condiment bottles yellow for mild and red for hot, and with a little cup that you can make your own spiciness level. I LOVE IT when mexican restaurants do this. Have it your way. The hot is not extremely hot, but i found a 50/50 mix was just the perfect heat and flavor for me.

The chimi comes out lookin all sexy-like. Enchilada sauce is fabulous! Also clearly homemade. Rice is delicious and clearly not from some box! I cut into my chimi, and I immediately notice my primary complaint about the whole meal: The chicken...its dry. Looks to be all white meat, which is a nice touch, but yeah, dry. I take a bite and yup, dry. Not as dry as Ive had at other places which I have given much lower reviews for, but pretty darn dry nonetheless. Flavor-wise, everything was fantastic and I DID really enjoy the meal.

The server checked up on me often and kept my glass full. I appreciate that. But the only other problem with my experience here was related to my particular server. A very nice gentleman, he had one very bad habit: mumbling. More than half the time he spoke to me, I had no idea what he was saying. 

"Hows your food?"
"Great, thanks."
"mmmhhbbeyuhhhem mehjfguha dhhhdhggmmm??"
"what?"
"mbuhhh ehuuyiamd thehhgnn goohoodamum??"
"uhm...im fine, thanks."
