Im not hard of hearing, and a similar conversation happened at least 4 times while I was there. By the second or third time, it gets annoying. 

Thanks to Corey D's recommendation, I also ordered the flan for dessert. GOOD CALL COREY! Some of the best flan I have ever had, and again, HOMEMADE! Maybe...JUST MAYBE...could have used a tiny minuscule touch of liqueur to really send it over the top.

Look, my reasons for not giving this place 5 stars are so minor that I really kinda feel guilty about it. I will be going back here...often. As often as possible in fact. So just pretend I gave the place 5 stars and go eat here already, ok?

Sushi is decent. There can be a really long and hectic wait on a weekend and I didn't really think that it was worth the hassel. Also, it took way too long to be greeted/put in our drink orders once seated and our sushi order got messed up. They did offer beer, wine, or sake while we waited but there are only three seats to wait and there were about 15 people crammed into the little area. They also way underestimated the wait time. I would rather an over-estimation with a nice surprise of being ready early than to constantly look at my watch as more and more people cram inside.

Of what we ordered, I most enjoyed the Mac n Cheese, which is a summer special including macadamia nuts and cream cheese. Since they messed up our order, we ended up with a Hawaiian roll. It involves soy paper. We did not enjoy that one at all. The roll with the rice paper was good. 

We did try the crab rangoon based on Yelpers suggestions. It does live up to the hype. It's not your typical was-it-or-wasn't-it-frozen variety. Filled with just enough cream cheese and crab, the scallion was a nice touch.

SoHo Sushi is owned by Yoko's son. Yoko's, which isn't too far away, has better sushi, is bigger and better equipped for a busy night (two sushi bars), and would be worth the wait if there was one. However, I'd probably return to SoHo Sushi to try their happy hour (daily 5-7pm). It's a dollar off their seasonal special rolls and hopefully less crowded.

This is one of my favorite lunch spots in Tampa. I was born in Buffalo and there is a special type of roast beef sandwich that is hard to find anywhere but there. Well these guys not only have it but they nailed it. The bread is a Kummelweck roll which is only found in upstate ny but somehow they have fresh soft salty ones here every time I have been. Outstanding. I also really enjoy the pineapple fritters if I'm in the mood for something fried and delicious.

Update: so now it's gonnna be even harder to choose what to eat, the Fried pork chop sandwich is phenomenal. It's is perfectly cooked, juicy yet crispy piece of pork with a very surprising amount of heat. And I like the heat! I was also able to have a side salad instead of fries to keep the fried factor down a bit. Only complaint is it's a large sandwich, that dared me to finish it all.. And I did happily!
My favorites are the picadillo (beef) and crabmeat. I don't care for the apple for dessert, but the guava and cheese is amazing!!!! Real big chunks of guava, not that pasty stuff.

Fantastic service. Absolutely, fantastic.

Pfct place for kids too.

Jerk chicken dinner was outstanding. Food is fresh and prepared to perfection.

Chef was helping servers. Great touch.

Coming from a land with no Five Guys and hearing all my out-of-town friends rave about them all the time, I figured it was time. 

Place was clean, considering how busy they were and the cashier was polite.  You get to pick what you want on your burger with no additional charge and they have malt vinegar for the fries.

The burger was alright.  I compare it to a Wendy's burger, just a little bit better.  It was a little more than I wanted to pay for a burger, fries, and a drink from a chain.  I would rather give that money to a local business.

I'm not a huge fan of Mexican because every dish seems to have the same flavors and basic ingredients. But every now and then I need to have the Chorizo Fundido and salsa. To me that is the essence of the Mexican flavors that I do like, not being a big fan of rice or beans. Their guacamole is OK too. Sometimes I would get a Quesadilla but usually it's too big to finish. Not a buffet fan, so I can't comment on that, but others seem to like it.

Been here twice for after dinner coffee - which was excellent by the way. Really need to get back to try the food and beer! Parking can be tough as they only have a few spaces in their lot but you can park across the side street or on the side street.

Taps is my favorite wine spot in Tampa! 

This spot offers an excellent selection of wines as well as beer and cocktails. If you are unsure which wine to order there are "wine dispensers" along the wall that you can purchase by the ounce. We usually purchase a few "wine samples" from the dispensers before purchasing a bottle. One of our favorite go-to bottles is the Duckhorn Merlot. The bottle is priced isn't marked up very high from the retail price so the bottle is a great deal. Some of my favorite appetizers are the Italian Sausage Sliders, Shrimp Tacos, and Tuna Tartare. If I had to pick my favorite appetizer I would say the sausage sliders since you receive such a generous portions, but they are closely tied with the tuna tartare. Free, live music usually occurs around 9pm on Fridays and Saturdays.

**Side note**: This spot gets very busy on Friday and Saturday nights so reservations are highly recommended if you wish to sit inside. Plenty of seating is available outside if you don't want to wait for a table inside.

Went on 7/10 with a group of 5 for dim sum at noon (had a rez). It was not too busy and we had plenty of yummy goodness for less than $20 each. Service was surprisingly good! I really enjoyed talking with some of the servers and the co-owner John was friendly and helpful. I'm so stoked as I love the original YH and was a bit worried the new one would have horrible service. I'm not doubting the other reviews at all - after all the original YH is known for spotty service. If you go at a non-peak time I'm sure you will have a great experience. If a peak time, enter at your own risk :)

Went to try out the pho and thought it was pretty good. next time I need to remember to have them not put the beef in the soup - I prefer to have it served raw so I can put it in just the right amount of time. I'm no expert on Vietnamese food but the service and pho were good. Looking forward going again sometime.

Kudos to Front Porch for a good Sunday brunch. If you like your Sunday brunch with a mimosa or bloody mary, this is the place to go. I love a good bloody mary and theirs is good and only like $4. They have a fun brunch drink menu if you're not a bloody mary fan. Oh and the food is good too!  My fave eggs bene plus omelettes, crepes, etc. Nice!  I've had the wings too and they were really good - nice and crispy and lightly sauced with a kick. Check it out!

I had the opportunity to visit the Landmark on Drexel's campus twice last week, Tuesday for lunch and Thursday for dinner. The wait staff is polite and friendly. The food was on par with the price. The portions were large and the drinks moderately priced. Tuesday I had the turkey club and on Thursday evening I had the crab cake sandwich; both meals satisfied my appetite but did nothing to stimulate my taste buds. It's worth a visit though for the atmosphere alone if you work near it.

Went for dinner Friday night with some friends.  It was our 2nd time eating there and we were not disappointed.  At 6:30ish there was already a bit of a wait for a table but the bar tenders and delicious drinks kept us occupied while we waited to be seated.  Our server was fantastic and left us wanting for nothing.  For the appetizer I ordered the Pastry Pillows.  They were very tasty and the gargonzola sauce that came with it complimented them very well.  I do have to say that I felt the pastry was a little under cooked but that didn't stop us from eating every bit of them.  For my entree I had the 20 oz Pork Chop with roasted asparagus spears and goat cheese smashed potatoes.  I knew when I ordered it that I would be taking the majority of the pork chop home but I couldn't resist.  It was absolutely delicious.  It was so good that I ended up eating the rest of the pork chop at 12 midnight the same night.  

I love this funky little restaurant and cannot wait to go back and try another dish.  I do believe our next visit will be to try out the Soul Food Sunday...mmmmmmmm my mouth is watering just thinking about it.

It is what it is. Iavarone's is a locally owned Italian steak house. It's not fabulous but it's not bad. I've had some things that were pretty good. The prices are much better than you'd see in a high-end steakhouse. Don't get me wrong, I love a good steak but hate paying $50 for one, with no sides. That's fine for special occasions but unless I win the lottery or receive a suprise inheritance, I can't afford to eat high end all the time! Recently discovered a decent wine at Iavarones - Bogle Phantom Proprietary Red. Not a great wine but it's not bad and won't set you back $40, $60 or $100 (depending on your tastes in wine :)

Let me preface this review by saying that I'm a bit of a health/nutrition nazi (except when it comes to alcohol), so I do NOT eat a lot of sweets/desserts/ice cream etc.  It drives my girl crazy.

that being said, Allegro makes the best frozen dessert i've ever had.  better than any ice cream, slushy, dairy queen, soft serve, or other gelato that i've ever tasted.  their salted caramel is amazing.  their herb-flavored gelatos are all really good, their spicy chocolate is excellent, their anise is very good...really, you can't go wrong.  and the owner guy is always really nice and accommodating when we want to try 19 flavors before deciding which one to buy, and once we decide, he really packs the cup.

this place is better is better than frost, in my opinion, and better than those stupid self-serve frozen yogurt and mix-in places that chicks go crazy for.  taste-wise, i think the closest i've had is the santa barbara ice cream place a few blocks north on campbell, but i still think allegro is better (and it's healthier).  the stuff isn't cheap (about $3 for a small, i think), but it's so good that it's actually a decent value.  besides, you shouldn't eat anything larger than a small without a friend helping you (fattie!).

I'm one of those people who sees the mall strictly as a place to shop, not so much to eat and socialize. I broke my rule last night and went to The Pub with some friends after some shopping, mostly to dodge the rain - but to also try it out since the word-of-mouth has been interesting.

The restaurant is very nicely decorated, classy almost. We sat outside on the 2nd floor balcony, which was nice and private. Their beer and cocktail list is impressive, and the prices seemed in line compared to neighboring establishments. 

The food was good, but very pricey for the size/quality. The goat cheese dip and fried pickles were scrumptious. Then our server suggested we order the calamari, and that's when things went south... 

Part of his spiel was saying that it was only the "good parts", not the legs, etc. That raised my concern a little; usually when you don't have the little legs and other pieces, it's not real squid. My suspicions were correct, I think it was either stingray or shark. Even asking the server after it came to the table, he said he wasn't sure. It was rough, flavorless, and a ripoff at ~$11. Sorry - but that took away a star; I'd rather have no calamari than imitation calamari.

All in all, the service was good, the drink prices weren't outrageous, but the food prices (and quality of the items) were borderline ridiculous. 

Whatever you do, stay away from the squid!

I've been in this place twice now - and like someone else said, the woman working there was not friendly at all.  In addition - this last time there was a German roach walking on one of the trays of buns.  She apologized profusely for us seeing that, and pulled the tray of buns out, but that's all I needed to see.  Everything there looks delicious - but I don't trust it to be clean.

Work that $10 lunch special, people. Get the Tom Yum Gai over the Tom Khai Gai soup. 

I thought I could be taking a risk by ordering the tilapia, but it was beautiful. There are 2 preparations of pan fried tilapia, I went with the Choo Chee Pla, more coconut milk plus 3 little pieces of baby bok choy. My piece of fish was crispy and tender. The sauce was delicate and totally different from the curry. To include this in the $10 prix fix is a total steal.

I went to Almas because my husband's watch's winder came off.  Also, the band of my watch was getting loose and would spontaneously pop open.  Right away, the owner could tell that there was rust in the watch, but he thought he could fix it.  He also tightened the pins on my watch clasp (for free) on the spot.

Communication about the progress of my husband's watch repair was good and initiated by the business rather than me.

They fixed his watch.  When I went to pick it up, the owner also checked the prongs of my engagement ring to make sure the diamond was secure, again at no charge.

The people at Almas were courteous and helpful.  I hope that I won't need anymore watch or jewelry repair, but if I do, I know this place is great.

(Oh, and if you were wondering, rhodium plating here is $80, a little high but probably a reflection of the high cost of precious metals right now.)

My husband had a hankering for fried shrimp last night, so we went to Shells.  Sadly, they no longer do the peanuts on the ground thing, but that's probably a good thing from a cleanliness standpoint.

What can I say?  The draft beer was icy cold, the food was very reasonably priced, and the service was attentive.

We started with the crab cakes, which were okay, but a little fishy, and the sauce used to dress the salad was very mayonnaise-tasting.  Yuck.

For our entrees, my husband got the fried shrimp, which he liked.  I got the Tuesday night lobster special.  1.5 lbs of Maine lobster for only $18.99.  And it wasn't overcooked, and I liked the broth.  Woot!  The parsley potatoes on the side were creamy, and the bread was good to sop up juices.  I appreciated the bib and the handi-wipes.  Only the side of vegetables was soggy, but for the price, I wasn't expecting magic.

We'll probably be back the next time my husband wants deep fried seafood.

About once a week my husband says two very provocotive words to me "Cherry Street?"  Now that I don't work in the city anymore, it's tough for me to meet him there, so we don't go as often as we would like to.

You see, my husband is crazy for Cherry Street's roast beef sandwiches.  And I have to admit, there are the best I have tasted. You can see the sandwich guy slicing the roast by hand at the end of the bar, which is kinda cool.

They also usually have a soup of the day, which I almost always order and have never been disappointed.  I distinctly remember a very flavorful Irish potato soup.  My husband enjoys their beer selection.

I guess the only negative, if I have to pick one, is they do have a very limited menu if you are not in the mood for a roast beef or a hoagie, and they don't serve fries.  :(

Eh.  I had heard so much about Culver's that maybe my expectations were too high.  Yes, the burger was good, but not great.  The fries and cheese curds were also good, but not great.  I had never tried frozen custard before, and I will admit that it has a smoother, creamier texture than regular soft serve.  If I go back to Culver's, it will be for the custard, not the food.

I like this mall for it's proximity to South Tampa. That said every time I go in here they have lost yet another store. I know the International mall is right up the road so maybe the area just can't support both. They still have a few good restaurants and a couple of good stores left so go while you can.

The Mann Center is where it's at, people.

Where else can you byo blanket, picnic & booze, sprawl out on a lawn and listen to "luminous scores" performed by renowned composers and orchestras from around the world?  

Precisely.

Last night, I had the pleasure of attending the Blue Planet Live performance by the Russian National Orchestra. As melodies filled the air, an accompanying film sequence displayed entrancing marine life such as extraordinary deep sea creatures, coral reefs, penguins, orca whales & dancing crabs. It was as though the creatures danced across the screen, their movements impeccably choreographed by the symphonies' crescendos and whirr of violins, flutes, trumpets and percussion instruments.

AND ALL THIS FOR $10?! (I feel like I'm the spokesperson for one of those lame infomercials - "but that's not all!"). But seriously, lawn seats are only 10 bucks (buy them there to avoid annoying processing fees). Plus you can take SEPTA's Mann Center bus loop which makes a bunch of stops in center city.

Love love love. Definitely one of my top philly summer activities.

Great stadium in a convenient area of town. What other stadium has a pirate ship? None. I've been here for Bucs, Bulls and US Soccer Matches and have always had a good time. The 5th star isn't there because the beer is about $2,000 a cup.

I'll put in a good word for this place if no one else will.  It's a jumbled big old Chinese market and it's not the prettiest and it's not in the ritziest mini-mall, but you can find things here that you can't find elsewhere, and some good bargains too.  G&L on 22nd is another jumbled old fashioned odd ball of an Asian market.
my dish the Choo Chee Pla was sooo good! I'll be back to try the tilapia or crab meat dish.  Keep up the great work
Yeah this could be better, but it is somewhat in it's infancy. Give it a little time Tampa. The location right now is the nest thing about it.

Although Beef O Brady's is a chain, this one feels more like just a regular neighborhood bar and restaurant. The food is good as are the drinks. The crowd is from all around Palma Ceia and kids are welcome here which makes it a good pick if you want to go to a restaurant and not have to worry if they're going to be to wild

I mean WOW......I wish we had the turnout not to mention the stadium down here in Tampa. I think in tim we will but in the mean time if you're up in Philly make sure you attend a game. Unmatched food, stadium, etc. This place is the real deal.

We have been to this small BYOB twice since it opened. We will definately be back. Service spotty but they did just open so I hope they get more efficient. I agree with reviewers that the food is not very spicy even if requested. We have tried several dishes off the menu and so far the tilapia was the best! Great welcome addition to our neighborhood. Can't wait to try more menu items. Probably would not travel to come here but since it is walking distance of CC will certainly be back.

So far I've eaten here twice. The first time I got the Georgia chopped pork with mac and cheese, green beans, corn on the cob and cornbread. OOOO yum!! The chopped pork is served over a piece of toast. It had a nice moist quality to it and it was just smokey flavored enough. I don't like too much smokey flavor but this was perfect. The mac and cheese!! OMG the mac and cheese!!! It's a jalapeno mac and cheese but if you're not so keen on heat, that's OK!! Its just spicy enough to make you pay attention. Best mac and cheese I've ever had. The green beans were long and nicely cooked. I tried one of the 6 bbq sauces they have on the table. It was called "Devil's Spit". Delicious!

Today I had the 2 meat dish. I got the half ribs and a sausage. This time for my sides I got the mac and cheese (of course) and the fries. The ribs were fantastic. The meat was tender and almost to the point of falling off the bone. Nicely sauced. Not too much, not too little. Fries were good. Everything came out STEAMING hot. 

Our server was "Famous Ron" and he was AWESOME!!!!  He noticed right away that my bf is a fast drinker so he started bringing 2 Pepsi's at a time. Big bonus points for that. He wasn't one of the waiters who hovers over you. He gave us our space but was also on top of our needs. He was probably the best server I've had in a LOOOOOONG time...so thank you Ron!!!

I am not a big fan of bbq, but Famous Dave's has converted me. I will definitely be making future trips and I am excited to try everything else they have to offer!

Beaune's Wine Bar  is a lesser known wine bar/restaurant outside of Ybor.  The restaurant has a romantic, cozy atmosphere without any of the pretentiousness.  The owner and staff are very friendly and knowledgable.  The adjoining wine store (West Palm Wines) has a great selection of European wines from $5 to $5,000.  For a $10 corking fee you can enjoy bottles of wine purchased at the store in the bar or restaurant.  I haven't been here for dinner (yet).  Usually we hang out on the cozy couches and order a charcuterie platter with different cheeses and meats and a bottle of wine.  

If you are a lover of good wine you must check out Beaune's Wine Bar!

Stopped by the Pour House for the first time last night. The bar was very laid-back and had a great selection of beers and wines. Most of the crowd seemed to be locals and several customers had brought their extremely cute dogs.  Highlights of the bar included the big leather couches and the old fashioned popcorn machine.  Will be back with friends.

Great food and atmosphere!!!  The mushroom risotto is to die for!  I don't even like mushrooms and I LOVE the mushroom risotto at Ciccio & Tony's!  The salads are delicious, healthy and made with the freshest ingredients. The sushi rolls were intricate and tasty.  I don't think there was a single dish that we didn't love.  Our waiter was super nice and guided us through the menu. All of his recommendations were spot on!  Cannot wait to return for more mushroom risotto!

Fastest sanwhiches in town!  My husband ordered our sandwiches on the iPhone app and ten minutes later dinner was at our apartment! Nothing fancy about the sandwiches.  They were just well-made sub sandwiches.  They have no-carb options on the menu with lettuce as the wrapping instead of bread.  Good meats, cheeses and breads.  Wish they would ditch the shredded iceberg lettuce.  Much healthier fast-food option than most.  Will order again.

We cam here for my wife's birthday this year that also just so happens to be Cinco De Mayo. We didn't want to go to a mexican joint so we chose the Bricks after reading some specials that they had going online. I have to say it was a good night and a lot of fun. Good drinks as well as food. Great ambiance, this is an older building in Ybor that they really did a great job of restoring. These guys are always having great food and happy hour specials. You won't be disappointed.

This is a hidden gem in Tampa. Nestled along the banks of the Hillsborough river, you will find a beautiful temple with Thai food stands along the perimeter. 

Definitely stop at the fried sweet potato stand and the coconut puffs-unbelievable. 

For $40 I got a wide array of food and a potted orchid. 

This is definitely an event worth experiencing.

Once again I have to rave about El Sur.  No, it is not fancy, but this "ganga" of a place reminds me that these people are not out to get you with high prices.  Just down home good food with an attitude to please every one who walks in the door.  The salsa is fairly plain but somehow it tastes great.  They serve 2 sauces, one hot and one mild in a plastic ketchup bottle.  I always order the Chicken Enchilladas with the green sauce.  Spectacular, soft chicken in a tortilla smothered with the tastiest green sauce along with a small salad, rice and refried beans.  The best part for me is the sauteed onions and jalapeno peppers that the wait staff brings around as much as you want them placed in your plate.  Heavenly stuff.  The surprise is this magnificent plate costs just $5.99.  Amazing!! Hubby had the Chile Rellanos and he just loved them.  It's a cute place that was probably a house at one time with colorful bright colors.  YUMMO!!!

This is a good place to sample wines you may not be familiar with before you commit to buying entire bottles. Sure, some of the pours are completely overpriced but I've found most decent vintages are always priced that way around town.

The red pepper & goat cheese bruschetta is delicious as is tuna crudo. The chicken satays were disappointingly dry and the roast beef sliders were very generic. 

The chipotle apricot barbque wings however were absolutely delicious especially with the chilly white we had to go with it. 

A little pricey, but wine drinkers should be used to that in this area. the beer selection is good, nothing unpredictable , but good selection and fair prices.

This is a good place to sample wines you may not be familiar with before you commit to buying entire bottles. Sure, some of the pours are completely overpriced but I've found most decent vintages are always priced that way around town.

The red pepper & goat cheese bruschetta is delicious as is tuna crudo. The chicken satays were disappointingly dry and the roast beef sliders were very generic. The chipotle apricot barbque wings however were absolutely delicious especially with the chilly white we had to go with it. 

A little pricey, but wine drinkers should be used to that in this area. the beer selection is good, nothing unpredictable , but good selection and fair prices.

Walking through the garden on a hot and humid Friday night, I was relieved to be sitting inside. But only because of the weather. The garden itself is a wonderland of flora, lights and the sounds of trickling water. Truly magical. 

Inside, the decor is a little more country farmhouse chic, and while the decorations can be a bit overdone in some areas, the overall feeling is very comfortable. We started with the seasonal sangria, served with fresh blueberries, green apple and basil. It was a refreshing choice for the day and I stuck with it on a second round. 

First up was "Not Your Granny's" cheese plate, consisting of six cheeses ranging from soft and slightly funky to a bold blue. Each came served along an appropriate accompaniment, and a ramekin with the most delicious flatbreads. 

Next we shared the peekytoe crab and lobster salad. I wasn't sure what to make of this, because every bite was different and I alternated between liking it a lot and not being so sure. I do have to admit, the substance covering the salad was a bit off-putting and I later noticed a neighboring diner picking around it as well. 

Fortunately, my main course of seared scallops with pine nuts and watercress picked things up again. The scallops were cooked perfectly. I was also so happy we ordered the delicious chard and braised leek gratinee at the last minute - it went perfectly with our dishes, and provided just enough food for a filling (but not too filling) dinner. Art had the wild striped bass with red quinoa, which he absolutely loved.

For dessert, French press coffee and the gooseberry almond crumble, served with black pepper meringue and heavenly lemon ice cream that I would personally like to order by the vat.

This place just opened a few days ago. It's always cool when you get a new place in town. It's even better when that place turns out to be good.

Burgers, sandwiches of all sorts and some dinner type items, like pot roast, make the menu pretty extensive. This means you will have choices to make. Burgers come single, double or triple - pretty self explanatory. You can order the "basket", which comes with a side (some are extra $$$) and a drink. Sides include fries, onion rings, mashed potaoes, fried cheese curds, among other things.

I had the Culver's deluxe burger with bacon. It  came with cheese, lettuce, tomato, onion and mayo. I got fries and my buddy got the cheese curds. We split them so we could try them both. As far as fast food burgers go, Culver's is somewhere near the top of my list.

Oh yeah, their signature item appears to be their custard. I did not try it, but it seems to be popular.

As I mentioned, this place is new. It's not without wrinkles. Once they get those wrinkles ironed out and/or some more locations pop up this will be added to my rotation of lunch spots.

Food is good but the portions are way too small compared to other Thai places. I think their green curry is probably the best in the city. The papaya salad was not that great, way too much dressing and small.

We happened to be in the area but were actually looking for breakfast. I remembered all the great Yelp reviews so I did the little kid thing with my wife, "Can we go, please. Everyone says it's good. Can we, can we, can we?" I think she agreed just so I would shut up.

The menu is on the wall and you order at the counter. I am into sampling the tacos. One fish, one cabeza and one birria. She is happy that they offer some breakfast options and orders the breakfast burrito with ham. eggs and potatoes. The bill was just over $10. So far so good.

The food is brought to our table and I don't see any salsa around. I go up to the counter and ask for it. I am handed a bottle and I ask if they have a mild option as my wife can't eat spicy stuff. He tells me this is the only one they have. He says it is spicy but it's "good stuff".

The fish taco was good, not great. The cabeza taco was OK, but I thought it had kind of a funky flavor. The birria was really good. It was the best of the three tacos. My wife said the the breakfast burrito was dry. There was no cheese or anything to moisten it up. The salsa was just OK. It had an odd orange color and seemed be be grainy.  I was not blown away as I thought I would be.

My expectations were high, so maybe it's just me and I need to go back and give them another chance. I usually wait until I have tried something at least twice before I review. There are certain exceptions, but in this case, I just don't see myself going back as it is very far off the beaten path for me and, frankly, not worth the trip.

The sandwiches here are so big you can barely bite them. The place is relatively small but has indoor and outdoor seating as well as two places to order from. I'd def try the deviled crab crabs. They are very good.

While a tad pricey, this place does have incredible ribs.  My husband's order were so tender they literally fell off the bone.  I ordered chicken which was just average.  It's a steak place though and I should have known better.  Next time steaks all around...they seem to do those well.

Service was great.  Our waiter was prompt and our glasses never made it to empty with out a refill or him checking to see if we wanted a new drink.

I don't make it up here too often (its a bit out of my way for average grocery shopping).  That said if I want to do something  special, their butcher block always has great ready to cook meats that will knock your socks off.  Last night we had the "Mexican -Style Stuffed Chicken"  After 35  minutes we were eating a wonderful blend of chicken, chile and cheese with just the perfect amount of spice.  While looking for a nice wine to accompany it, their wine stewards were able to get me set up with a great, not too expensive bottle.  Thanks guys!  Dinner was great!

A little bit of a tourist trap, but this Tampa landmark dates back to 1905 and is the oldest Spanish restaurant in Florida.

My favorite dish at Columbia is the Seafood Paella, but I also like the Ropa Vieja and Filet Steak Salteado. The 1905 salad and Cuban Black Bean soup are also fantastic. You will probably be too stuffed to order dessert, but if you want to bring home a Cuban treat try the white chocolate bread pudding or the churros.  

Live music is available every Friday and Saturday and you can make reservations in the Flamenco Room for live dance performances. Warning: it gets really LOUD in this room so I would not recommend booking this room for a romantic dinner out.

Unfortunately, the food is not always consistent at Columbia and they charge four times more than any other Cuban restaurant in Tampa. You would think that since you are paying more the food must be so much better than the little hole-in-the-walls, but this is not the case. Mostly you are paying for the atmosphere.

Maggiano's offers tasty American Italian food in family-style portions. My favorite dishes on the menu are Mom's Lasagna with meat sauce, Chicken Parmesan, and Cheese Canolinnis. Save room for dessert! The tiramisu, chocolate zuccoto cake and NY cheesecake are worth every sinful calorie!

There are private rooms availble for large groups and the main dining room is perfect for a casual, romantic night out. The curbside take-out is so convenient on nights that I don't have time to cook! Maggiano's has catered several work parties at our office. They deliver everything from the food to plates, utensils, and sterno warmers.  

Expect reasonably priced meals, huge portions, and friendly service!

I enjoyed the atmosphere of the restaurant as soon as I walked in and was surprised and happy to see that they served not only the homemade beer I knew about, but also homemade sodas and kombucha! I was impressed with their general mission statement on the back of the menus so I started out on a positive note. 

We had to wait a little while to order, but this was expected with such a large restaurant that was relatively crowded for a Tuesday night (which happened to be a great night to be there as they had Great Lakes IPA for only $2!) The pizza was delicious, the crust was to die for - incredibly flavorful and crispy. 

However, I was quite disappointed with the beers. I got the Bread+Circus APA, which I was very excited for after seeing it was brewed with the same house yeast they use in the breads. Though it seemed as if the yeast strain couldn't stand up to the alcohol as the beer was FAR too sweet and reminded more of a dubbel, trippel, or even slightly of an eisbock... perhaps there were hints of an APA somewhere under all that sweetness. My friend got the Rose Farmhouse Ale which was much more true to it's style, and though it wasn't fantastic, it was pretty good for a restaurant brewing their own beer - which I've come to be somewhat doubtful of.

The desserts were delicious, though seemed small even after I had read that they were quite small. Not too badly priced, though.

Overall, I would go here again to get some more delicious pizza, try their kombucha, and perhaps try another beer and hope for something a little more exciting.

If I had the choice, I would have given 3.5 stars, but gave 4 for the fact that they seem to care about sustainability and being eco-friendly.

Novel idea.  Very strong chef BUT management lacks.
Attitude has ruined three outings there.  Three times, we are out and so are our friends.  Pretentiousness only goes so far.  Pricey?  not too bad actually, but haughty and downright rude at times.  We do not enjoy being treated like imbeciles and that is what the braided house latch key dude did twice.   A call to manager was not so satisfactory, but we returned due to the Chef's talent and out of town friends.  Where do they get their staff?  
Suffer again?  I would rather head to Bella's or Timpano's....

So lets start at the title NYPD! I drove by one day and was thinking to myself "NYPD, what is that?" So I called in the troops, our local VW club is on the quest to find Tucson's best pizza, so naturally this place had to be tried.

When you first walk in you are greeted by a quaint dinning room with about 9-10 tables. The bar where you order is dead ahead and and has the stainless steel wrapped metal look. The decor is pretty cool, and makes you feel like you stepped in off the subway. 

After finding your spot to sit you take a look at the menu and instantly you find that your mouth starts watering. It wasn't long before our group decided on a 16" Philly cheesesteak pizza. that sounds ridiculous amazing I know! Oh and the best part.... It tasted better than it sounds. 

A few other things stuck out about this place as well. Unlike most pizza places in Tucson, this one had shakers for red peppers, black pepper, parmesan, and garlic! Their soda fountain is on the client side, so no annoying waits to get a refill especially when like me, your choking on a pepper that you breathed into the wrong tube, after trying to laugh out loud to a joke that a friend just told. Ahh the burn.  

This one ranked well in our clubs pizza tour. Its nice to find things other than cheese and pepperoni as options for pizza!

My only gripe: they hadn't gotten their liquor license at the time we went so no ice cold beer. =(

Been coming here since I was a kid.
Anything on the menu is a safe bet however my favorites are the spicy cheese fries,onion rings,royal burger and Texas Tommy ...oh yeah and the crab cake sandwich is off the chain.  
Don't forget to grab one of the best milkshakes in Philly.
Kingfisher is my favorite place for dinner in Tucson! The Macadamia Nut Crusted Hawaiian Fish is the most delectable thing I've ever eaten! Each time I go back I find a new favorite. Love it!

Blanco Tacos may not be authentic but I really like this place.  The food is fresh and well prepared.  The last visit, I started with chips, salsa and guacamole.  The salsa and guac were really fresh with lots of flavor.  The chips are nicely salted with sea salt.

For dinner, I had the avocado and tomato salad with goat cheese and pistachios.  The dressing was a honey lime dressing that added just the right touch of sweetness.

I also had a mixed berry margarita that rocked my world.  Had I not been driving, I could have polished off quite a few of those!

Lastly, upon the suggestion of the server, I had their chocolate cake.  It was a flourless cake with a salty chocolate dressing that was a great sweet and salty combination.

Service was prompt and attentive, the view was breathtaking.  I'll be spending a lot of time there.

This place just didn't do it for me. Tucson has a lot more to offer in terms of great Mexican food besides this place. The food wasn't memorable  and the prices were a bit high.

The atmosphere is great.... it's a good location for a restaurant and the views are pretty. It definitely appeals more to people who want to dress up and meet a date or group of  friends. 

There's definitely just better Mexican restaurants in Tucson where the food and prices are much better.

They had some great specialty drinks and the steaks were pretty nice.  Ambiance is a little more modern but really if you're looking for a great night out at a steak house in Tampa and you're willing to spend this kind of money you might as well go to Berns.

So I'm sitting here drafting this review as the spicy cream cheese and warmth of the jalapeños dances across my lips! Wow these lil guys are awesome. A lil pricey but hey life's to short to not enjoy good appetizers. So I just ordered the ccr. Chicken cheddar ranch. While I'm waiting I'll share the view. 

The bar is an old tavern style dark wood as is the ceiling complete with traditional wood rafters. The seating is a nice dark burgundy velour look. and the tables are a dark wood as well. The TV's are actually placed in a decent way that no matter where you're sitting you can enjoy a sport at the moment baseball is the flavor. My only gripe and purely my fault is the table that I choose is near the entrance and they didn't dress the windows to tame the Tucson sun. 

The beer menu is pretty impressive. They  actually have GOOD beers on tap such as gulden draak, bodingtons and more.

Thanks for reading.

Pastiche this past Saturday....


Saturday night with my hubby brought us to Pastiche. The bar had our favorites (house vodka for Mike, Jameson Gold for me) which we sipped while waiting for our table by the window. Tea lights flickering in red glass holders on each table lent a rosy ambience. We started with the cheese platter, (a favorite!) Mike's bistro portion of Rosemary Crusted Pork Tenderloin with butternut squash risotto, goat cheese, pine nuts and poached figs was delightful! I had the special: Certified Angus Beef Stroganoff. Melt-in-your-mouth beef with a silky, creamy sauce and perfectly cooked pasta was divine. We finished with sharing a generous slice of Banana Cake with sour cream frosting. Light, moist and flavorful, then visited their wonderful wine shop! One other note I MUST add. The always-watchful owner, Pat Connors, noticed that our order was taking a bit longer than usual. No problem, we realized that they were very busy. But he took the time to bring us extra slices of warm baguette to keep us fortified while we finished that delicious cheese plate. Personalized, caring service like that, keeps us returning again and again. When we left, it was raining, so we backtracked and visited their wine shop, where Amanda Lukas offered us a glass of currently-open wine. It was the perfect way to polish off our evening. By the time we finished our wine, the rain had stopped, so we headed home to savor our evening.

You can definitely stuff your face at Maggianos with the family style dinners that they provide. That said I went there today for lunch and since I didn't want to go into a pasta coma for the rest of the day, I had the chopped salad with crumbled bleu cheese. Really good.

So they have Brunswick Stew.....that is good enough for me so I could stop there. That said the BBBQ is really good. I'm a big fan of Carolina style BBQ and this is not that but it's still good. The baked beans are rad as well. Super nice staff and surprisingly good micro brew selection

I mean Greenwise is nice but it's not Whole Foods. When I walk through here I feel like I'm in a darker public with more wine and the prices just marked up on everything else. The cart escalator is cool though

I went here after a neighbor swore by this place. I mean it was good but I think I was just set up for disappointment. The way she made it sound, angels came from the heavens and served you and it was the best food in the world. It was good but that's about all.
This is a true Tampa original. It's just a little house, but the food is really good and the cafe con leche is the bomb. Get by here when you get the chance. The staff is very friendly.

I like the Press Box. Sure it's darker than hell in there but whatever. It's got random stuff all over the walls mostly sports memorabilia but the most interesting was a damn Bobcat that was in this fish tank looking thing, though the last time I went in here a sign was in it's place offering a reward for a stolen Bobcat....really??? Anyway the food is good bar food and there are a ton of TV's and they have the ticket so al is well.

I had a carne asada burrito and it was giant!  Reminded me of the burritos I got back home, a plus for sure.  

The service was below average, the only thing keeping it from five stars.  

Despite the lack luster service, I would visit again for a late night burrito craving for sure.

Icy draft beer, friendly staff.  The place was a little dead when we were there, but I can't complain - we definitely benefited with fast service. I do wish the buffalo sauce wasn't so sweet, but all other food was awesome. Maybe offer something not deep fried, and I'd give it 5 stars.

Great place to grab a drink w friends and support local businesses :)
They offer only popcorn and encourage you to BYOFood. What a different concept!

Pretty good Thai food, nice atmosphere, it wasn't crowded on a Wednesday evening but it wasn't empty either. Tables are kind of close together so if it was crowded it could be a little uncomfortable. Service could have been better but overall it was a good dinner and I will definitely be going back.

At least 3 days a week growing up, I would eat a Ham & Cheese Omelet at Michas.  The freaking BEST!  I am still here at least once a week for breakfast.
Micha's has got to be the best place in town for a Mexican Breakfast.  Omelets, Choriza, Machaca, Menudo...All fantastic!  The service is great and the kitchen is fast.  You can be in & out in 15/20 minutes.

Took a large dinner party and had a GREAT time!  The waiter did his best to talk us into the Sea Bass, so we all ordered it -- really, all six of us (he was a good salesman).  We were NOT disappointed.  While a bit expensive ($24), it was absolutely the best piece of fish I have ever had -- no kidding.  Melted in my mouth and the rice/veg mix that came with it set it off perfect.  I'll go back this weekend to have it again! I even tipped the waiter extra for the recommendation!  No need to "tip me" --- this is free!! :)

I don't really think you can call this a restaurant, but they do offer food. 95% of it is the fried variety. If you want a real meal, plan wayyy ahead and arrive very early to eat nearby if you're going to see a big act because the line is a headache and a half. It's general admission, but you have to be seated by a host so that they can maximize the tables. They only have one host stand so it's like a bottleneck effect. However, once your seated, every seat seems to be a good one. I very much enjoyed watching Anjelah Johnson with my sister, but next time I think I'd rather go for the earlier show since I think the traffic exiting may have made the entering part as hectic as it was.

Checked out The Lime on ladies night and was impressed with happy hour specials.  Went with two of my coworkers and got two rounds of drinks and two appetizers for around $50.  The guacamole was awesome, but the cheese and bean dip was on the bland side.  Nice crowd for a Wednesday night, but not too loud so you could actually have a conversation.  Super nice bartender and the owner stopped by our table to say hi and bought us a round of shots when he heard it was my co-worker's birthday.  Will be back!

Good service (but not too formal), well lit, and a very nice view.  I recommend coming here at sunset.

The seafood is seriously very good and the drinks are too... The fresh oysters were excellent. The price is as you would expect for food of this quality and a great view.

This is a nice place for a wonderful evening with your significant other or a serious dinner. The tables are nicely spaced leaving plenty of room for a private evening.

Valet parking.

5 stars for this little local coffee shop near the University.  Th e atmosphere is cute with out being a scene.  More importantly, it boasts great coffee (I love their cubano) and a wonderful assortment of pastries (perfect for breakfast, lunch or a late night snack).

Now that I'm back in the neighborhood, I plan to drop by for their yummies as often as possible!

North is cool. It's a good location and day or night, their patio is a great place to enjoy a meal with friends or a date. 

I've been here with a group of friends and it was a lot of fun. For larger groups, it definitely seems necessary to make reservations ahead of time, especially on weekends. 

North is also great because of their gluten-free menu. There was a gluten-free member of our group when I went and she was delighted that an Italian place actually had gluten-free options! 

Overall, a positive and enjoyable experience.

I have gone here several times and was pleased with it.  However, My husband and I went Saturday night for an early dinner (which means it was not crowded yet and there were plenty of empty tables).  We sat on the patio, which is nice even in the Tucson heat, and the misters were going full force.  It was unfortunate that they sat us in the middle of the patio where we were basically soaked by the misters.  Well, our bodies weren't soaked, but our table and everything on it kept getting very wet, and we had to continually wipe things off!  I know the value of having misters in the summer heat here in the desert, but this was ridiculous.  Since the service seemed to be very slow for some reason (did I say it was not crowded?) we decided to relocate to a table that was a little more sheltered.  They really should have removed the tables that were closest to the misting and only used those on the peripheral, even if it meant that later on a few parties may have to wait a little longer to be seated.  Our server was very friendly, perhaps a bit too much so.  He was a "cool" dude who had all the right comments and facial expressions as well as proper hand gestures that would put him into the "cool" category.  I dunno...I have become more annoyed recently with people in jobs that service the public.  They seem to think they need to become my new best friend, and it's cute at first but after a few minutes of it I do become quite annoyed and just wish they would do their job.  I like friendly and pleasant, but let's draw a line.  Anyway, the food was good...we split a burger plate and side salad, and it was great that we did otherwise we never would have been able to eat a full one each.  The waiter tried his best to talk us into a piece of cheesecake for dessert, but we really were too full and prefer to not take one "to go".  I promised the waiter I would return very soon and enjoy a piece of cheesecake and cup of coffee and he was happy with that.  LOL

Could have been a better experience, but the food here is usually very good and their menu has so many choices it is often hard to make a decision.  My recommendation?  Go there for lunch and just have piece of cheesecake and some coffee...you'll be glad you did!  :)

Frost who???? Seriously this place ROCKS!!!! I don't know why I waited so long to try this place!  As usual when I walk into a business it tends to be slow and quiet, but then within a minute of us walking in a line started to form.

SEA SALT CARMEL/MINT CHOCOLATE CHIP!!!! That's the fave so far!  The staff was very helpful in letting my husband and our friend sample everything, that they even held up the line without even knowing it.  

I would recommend this place to anyone! Great dessert place to go to after grimaldi's!

Best Hairstylist-Missi

Best Color-Barbara

I have been going to BFS for over 5 years now and I honestly get compliments weekly on my cut.  Missi is fantastic and listens to what your needs are.  She is not "scissor happy" and does not chop your hair off like a lot of stylists.  Missi is edgy and is up to date on the latest styles.  There have been many times that I have brought her a photo of a celebrity and she has been able to replicate the style for me.

Barbara is the owner and the color expert.  She is great at what she does.  She knows exactly what you need and is able to fix other colorists' mistakes.

I would highly recommend BFS to anyone in the Tampa Bay area.

Not a huge self serve Fro Yo place and not as many selections as others, but the quality is great and their selection of low cal fro yo is high.  

I especially like the fact that they have FRESH raspberries as a topping.  I haven't found anyone who offers this option.

BTW, the offer homemade ice cream too (which I tried once and it was awesome)

If I could bestow a sixth star to Max & Co., I would. If The Loft somehow folded, I would consider relocating. A culture of serious celluloid worship thrives here at the nexus between the Art Institute with all of its whacky young auteurs and indie cinema. The best event is the First Friday Shorts which is an edgy gong show event where local filmmakers enter films up to 15 minutes long which range from hilarious to downright frightening. The audience can scream "Gong it!" or "Let it play" and master of ceremonies Max Cannon adds cool commentary while roasting the budding directors. And where else can a film fan imbibe in canned Chilean wine while sitting next to a leather clad crew of transvestites, hmmm? Mondo Mondays upstairs are also worth a look, and the flop couches in front are a fine place to enjoy carefully selected 70s blaxploitation films with like minded couch taters. The Fox got the historic rehab $, but The Loft has the talent.

This place is great.  My husband loves junk/fast food, but I want him to eat more healthfully.  Evos is a great compromise.  He can get crispy fries that aren't deep fried and eat a burger made with hormone- and cruelty-free meat.  The wraps are a little small, but they are pretty tasty.  I had the crispy Buffalo chicken.  I wish there was blue cheese on it rather than ranch, but otherwise, it really was crispy and spicy.

The Pub at International Mall is a great addition to Bay Street.  The restaurant has a fun atmosphere and offers an awesome selection of beers. I hate going to a bar where my only option is mozzarella sticks or french fries. The Pub's menu offers everything from English Pub Fare, to sandwiches and salads. The drink menu is equally impressive with a wide selection of wines, martinis, scotches, and of course... beer! Check out the beers on tap upstairs!

The pretzle dip sandwich is my favorite item on the menu.  On other visits we have ordered the homemade chips, goat cheese and pita bread, and salads: all very good. The Lemon Drop and Blueberrry martinis are incredible. The outside on the patio is unbearable during the afternoon due to the Tampa humidity, but is very nice on a cool evening. Highly recommend checking out The Pub if you have not done so.

I remember when this was THE mall to go to back in the day...say 20 years ago....when Burdines still existed. Even though it was a hike for my parents to take me...this was the mall us girls wanted to go to. 
Since then, they have revamped the mall completely. They have higher end stores, which a few I can only afford to window shop as I keep walking, and then of course all of the other norm mall stores. 
I like 'The Limited' in this mall much better than anywhere else in the Tampa/Brandon area. Yes, size DOES matter...MORE clothes:- ) 
The food court has a pretty good selection of side eats if you want something quick and inexpensive. But if you want to sit down for table side service, they have plenty of restaurants to choose from such as: Maggiano's Little Italy (Great Italian food $$), PF Changs China Bistro $$, Mitchell's Fish Market (down right good seafood $$$), and in the same parking lot across from the mall is Seasons 52 (which is MY favorite place to dine!!$$) 
Now back to the mall...I'm so glad that they have a covered parking garage. One over by JCPenney and the other over by Macy's. They also have the option of valet if you really dont want to deal with trying to find a parking spot. It's so nice to go to a mall where all of the stores are still in business and there aren't any empty ones. Westshore has always been very well kept and clean. No riff raff like some other malls tend to get. But who knows, maybe I just go on good days.

We decided to check this place out.  I set my expectations low, so I'm happy to say that I was pleasantly surprised.  

We started with the crispy spring rolls.  I didn't love the filling or the dipping sauce, but they were served quickly and piping hot and super crispy.

For our main courses, we had the spicy noodles, red curry and a shrimp stir-fry.  I think they serve the food milder than I'm used to, but luckily they do have the different peppers/sauces that are traditionally available at Thai restaurants, so a little Thai chili in fish sauce fixed it up properly.

Did I mention that in addition to the expected Singha beer, they also have Chang?  It made me nostalgic for our beer comparison tests late night in Bangkok.

Service was lovely, and the bathroom was reasonably clean.

I ordered the chicken kabob sandwich on the whole wheat pita. The pita was super fresh and tasty. Everything tasted fresh, cucumbers, cabbage and onions were crisp. The only complaint I have is that some of the ingredients of the sandwich were not evenly distributed. For example, I didn't taste any of the hot sauce until the last 3-4 bites of the sandwich, and it seemed like all of the meat was on top and none at the bottom. However, I really enjoyed it overall and would stop by again. Their prices are slightly higher than I typically like to pay for lunch, but manageable for a treat once a month or so.

Modest price Chinese restaurant with an OPEN view kitchen; you can your food prepared with high standards.  Friendly, well spoken counter person.

Fresh & tasty although very mild seasoning.  Egg rolls crisp, house wonton soup packed, good flavor on shrimp, beef and pork entrees and no greasy pork fried rice.  I reheated leftovers and had no excess oil at all.

I'll order again for take out but will ask for extra spicy.

Food was okay, I guess. They give you a whole lot, but IDK I guess it just wasn't tasty to me. Service, however was fantastic! Very kind people and the place was subtle just they way I like it. Pricing depends on what you get obviously.

So this was my first time visiting Pita Jungle. I have heard good things about it and figured it was about time to take a trip down here. Not that I needed much of an excuse I was starving! 

So a friend came with me and we each ordered a different type of Pita and we had them split so we each could sample each others. I ordered the Mediterranean Roasted Chicken (Shawarma) which came packed to the gills with grilled marinated chicken breast in a pita, with mixed greens, tomatoes, onions, pickels, garlic sauce and tahini. This was rolled up in a bit of foil and was absolutely delicious!! I ordered this with a side salad that was about $3 bucks but looked like a $6 salad. It had the freshest juiciest red tomatoes and the freshest greens topped with feta and kalamata olives it made the perfect accent to my Mediterranean pita.

The other half of the pita I enjoyed was the Lavosh Shawarma Wrap with grilled marinated chicken breast, grilled tomatoes, grilled onions, and garlic sauce, wrapped in lavosh bread and wood-fired. This one was served with a side Greek salad just as good as the first. The Lavosh wrap was very interesting, the flavor was more spicy and had more of a BBQ feel. I like the Lavosh bread it was cooked until it was crispy so it flaked when you bit into it. 

The restaurant was really cute too, they had interesting tasteful artwork displayed on the walls that if you liked you could buy. I almost took one home. I hope its not there the next time I go or it may just have a new home! Anyhow back to decor. The jungle theme was handled very well in the center they have a long table that sits high like a bar, and the base is made from metal fabricated to look like roots and the base of a tree or huge vine. This accent was consistent throughout the bar. The whole place was nice and open feeling and well lit. The staff did a good job at keeping beverages pouring and even pointing out that Happy Hour was going to start and gave me a couple pointers for what a "good buy" is.

I gave this place a 5 star rating because as pitas go, I have only had one other rival and that place will get 5 stars too once I make it back.

This is going to be a 2 part review once now and a revisit review once this place is ridiculously crowded!

First off parking was horrid, the lines for drive through, were almost out to the street, and the line inside was even worse. It seemed there were people standing around everywhere confused about where to go and how to get there! It was utter chaos! Once I finally made it to the register the poor girl that took my order sounded as if she was discovered in the desert nearly dead from thirst! Her voice was almost nonexistent. Her and I joked as she told me it was because she lost it working the counter almost non stop since the place opened. Yikes. So anyhow I knew this place was churning and burning at full throttle so that is why I didn't give it the worst review I could. I mean its not everyday that Tucsonans and tourists alike can order Wisconsin based fast food! 

Which brings me to the order. I ordered the Shaved Prime Rib, and the onion rings. My friend ordered the Culvers Deluxe Bacon Burger and the cheese curds that we were soo excited to try. Once we got our food I was super bummed to bite into my shaved prime rib sandwich and find that it was cold! Seriously! That ruins a sandwich like this!. The burger on the other hand was amazingly good. Which is why I hold out hope that a second review and second visit might do this justice. But alas the cheese curds...... more like breaded poppers! I have never had cheese curds anywhere else and in my mind I imagined a sweet desert like cheese similar in consistency to cottage cheese. Boy was I wrong. The cheese curds tasted like they had been frozen and then flash fried, which is almost completely off of what the restaurant stands for. Better not come thirsty because to get a refill of a beverage took almost as long as it did to order, and since the lines were so long it was awkward standing next to a table in a line when someone is trying to enjoy their food. 

So Culvers.... we will see you again.

Don't think of this place as a Mexican restaurant. It is Mexican in the same way PF Chang's is Chinese. Influence and flavor. If you want good Mexican go to South Tucson and spend a lot less. 

Blanco is a nice place to go for a decent Margarita and some fun food. It has a nice setting and I have always found the service to top notch. You can argue about whether the food is authentic all you want, but the food is good and I would take non-authentic good food over authentic crap anytime. The last time I was there my daughter and I were waiting for her MacBook to be repaired so we walked upstairs for Margaritas and chips. A lot cheaper than shopping in the stores at La Encantata. The bartender on duty was entertaining and skillful.  It made us wish that the Apple Store repair guys weren't quite so quick.

Fox does seem to know how to run a decent shop!

AJ's is a beautiful grocery store.  The alcohol selection is amazing, so many unique choices.  I love how you are not bothered by annoying employees when shopping.  I wish there were more AJ's Fine Foods around Tucson.
One of my fav coffee places!  A full pot of french pressed coffee is seriously a deal.  I always leave this place feeling energized (or is it caffeinated?!)

Quick, no muss/no fuss wax.  If you've never had a wax "down there" then yes, it's going to hurt; suck it up, go back regularly and soon, it will be pain free.  The best times to go are evenings during the week where there is generally no wait.  If you want a cheap, effective wax, then this is your place.

Went here Saturday morning for breakfast, place had an average number of patrons. Ordered a Denver omelet, asked for hash browns, they don't serve them?  Really what restaurant doesn't server hash browns for breakfast???? Ok, ordered skillet potatoes.   Place seems like they wanted to be a southern style diner in Tucson, yeah just what I wanted, not.  When food came out the omelet looked like fried eggs that had the filling dumped on them, then folded over.  The potatoes where blackened, tasted burnt.  I have to say I hoped for more, but o well.  I will not be going back here.

I would have to rate Culver's so-so, the food honestly isn't anything special.  The employees are efficient on the dining floor always offering to  take your tray when finished.  The cheese curds remind me of bite size mozzarella sticks only with cheddar cheese replaced.  It's worth the try but  will soon blend in with all the other fast-food joints around town.   

Parking does need to expand. The best day's to go are on weekdays when everyone is working.  Heads up don't park next to the drive thru or you will be blocked in due to long lines.

"I want to live here." - Mike, a former NF regular in his youth. NIFTY FIFTY'S IS GREAT. 

Stopped in around midnight on our way home from a block party in the greater Northeast for my first FOURTHMEAL in like three years. I used to do a lot of eating after midnight, but once I became a real human with a real job it all kind of came to an end. Settled into a booth, among a couple of scattered groups our fellow young people, and one older couple with mullets that looked like they had been posted up for a few hours. The menu here is excellent. So many sodas! So many milkshakes! A big non-alcoholic beverage fan, I was close to getting a cherry vanilla soda, but was convinced by my partner in life to get a milkshake. I went for a marshmallow milkshake and it was basically the most delicious item of food or drink I've had in a long time. And, for reasons I cannot explain with human words or sounds, I went with an order of onion rings with cheese, with a side of ranch. Fat people food. Going to be farting the next day food. Damn the torpedoes. They were really delicious, but I couldn't finish all of them nor could I finish my milkshake. I like the vibe here. Like this is probably a shitty place to work, but everyone there is mad unofficial looking and young so it doesn't seem so bad and no one is in a bad mood, even though they're at work at midnight on a Saturday. I want to go back.

We went to Ciro's Speakeasy this Saturday without a reservation despite all the suggestions to call ahead. Had to wait 20-30 minutes in the parking lot before we were escorted in, but it was well worth the wait. Ciro's is a 1920's prohibition era themed bar and restaurant that serves handcrafted cocktails. Since we didn't have a reservation we were seated at the bar. Our bartender, Bob, was extremely friendly and guided us through the drink menu.  

My sister and I ordered the Kentucky Champagne, the Bramble, and two other fruity cocktails recommended by Bob. My husband chose a stronger potion and went with the "Penicillin", a scotch based cocktail with fresh candied ginger garnish. Did I mention all of the handcrafted cocktails were made with fresh herbs and juice? No flavored vodkas or red bull on this drink menu! 

We did glance at the dinner menu, but decided to come back with a reservation so that we could sit at a table. Bob mentioned the dining tables were small and at knee level so we may want to make the reservation at the bar. Will be back soon for the duck fries!

I know I can get a mani/pedi for $25 at lots of places in the city, but I'm willing to pay a bit more here, mainly for the massages!  Best time to go is early morning or during a weekday; do NOT go on Saturday afternoon; they tend to be super busy at this time and your experience can feel rushed.  Overall, they do a wonderful job and they even have Shellac manicures (if you feel the need to splurge!)

You know what really rocks about Mei Mei is, they offer a 4.99 lunch special 7 days a week. Most places only do it during the week. Not Mei Mei- they serve up hot and spicy soup or egg flower and a heaping plate of tasty food with not one, but TWO egg rolls. 

Mei Mei is a wonderful eat in/take out restaurant in a small shopping center. Two people can get out for around $13 including hot green tea or delicious ice tea (free refills). The food is fresh and delicious and you can even eat quite healthy as they have bean curd options, broccoli and shrimp. There is a shark fin soup on the menu, and varieties of wonderful noodles, fried or steamed rice, Kung Pao chicken, and other staples. The hostesses often struggle a little with English but are quick to help and the food comes out fast. It looks like take-out is popular here especially with those lunch prices.

The atmosphere is nothing to talk about, but it is relaxed and friendly. You get fortune cookies at the end, too.

Look up the definition of a sleazy auto dealer and you will find Tucson Auto Sales & Brokerage. When I purchased my Toyota truck I did a Carfax on it- sadly, whatever accident/repair that was done to the truck was not in the Carfax. When I took it to a mechanic the next day after buying it I discovered the truck had been wrecked so badly that it should not even be driven. Multiple damaged pieces that made it very dangerous. 

I took it back and, thankfully (I thought) was able to trade it for another vehicle. I found a Toyota 4Runner which cost more. I couldn't get them to talk down the price but I did have a mechanic look it over. They found little faults with it. So I took it off the lot. Found out soon enough it was a Canadian manufactured Toyota which meant getting a new key was near impossible (only in Canada), and had multiple problems: the starter was dying the moment I took it off the lot, it had a leaky moonroof. 

It felt dishonest and sneaky and I would not recommend getting your car there. It also has a bad write up in the Tucson Weekly from a while back when they screwed some foreign guy with a car he bought. Shady! AVOID!

This is the first QT to open in Tucson and it's my favorite. It's near my work so I pop in often for a hot blueberry tea, or cold filtered ice tea or soda pop from the fountains with filtered ice. QT has it together: clean, clean clean stores! Friendly and nice cashiers who often give you a break (the occasional coupon for a free sandwich, or a free refill). I get the 52 ounce mug and fill it up for just .86 cents. STEAL!

The 5 dollar lunch deal is the best. The fresh sandwiches are better than most places in town. A ham and cheddar is so good. They make pizzas and fresh pastries. I love coming to QT for everything, for the gasoline and snacks. It is clean, well lit and safe, unlike any other gas station in town. Even the bathroom is spotless!

For only being open a week the coffee is darn good!  We were walking by and saw that this place had finally opened.  Great location too. I had read the article in the AZ star a few weeks ago and was really excited to try the blue coffee.  I had ordered a iced mocha wow that's all I can say!  It tasted so smooth and rich in flavor just like I like em.

I will have to come and try the food, it looked pretty good from what I saw what other were eating.

I gotta upgrade this place. It's better than I previously gave them credit for.

It's probably blasphemous to say this - It's better than El Guero Canelo.

That being said, I have to say that previously I have been ignorant on how to eat this kind of food. I'll explain: I usually forgo most of the ingredients in the salsa bar and just go for the salsa. Both places have a litany of choices in the salsa bar: pickled onions, roasted jalapeños, cucumbers, limes, salsas (of course) and other goodies. I did not previously experiment with most of these items. Whether from fear or ignorance I cannot say. Let me just say that you have to pile on all the goodies from the salsa bar. Otherwise the food can seem very run-of-the-mill. El Taco Tote just flat out does the whole package better than Guero Canelo, from the tasty, freshly grilled meats to the freshly made tortillas.

Maybe it's unfair to compare the two places in this forum. If you are still reading after my blasphemous comment at the beginning, you are probably at least curious.

If I am on my death bed please serve me the Capital Grille Sesame Seared Ahi Tuna and a side of the Lobster Mac N' Cheese. Preferably with one of the restaurant's famous pineapple martinis.

Capital Grille has great atmosphere; the restaurant has a masculine "boys club feel" with the oversized chairs, dark wood paneling and private Scatch lockers.  The waiters are friendly and never pretentious and the food is to die for.  I usually order the ahi tuna and my husband will get a steak so I get to enjoy both of my favorites from the menu.  The desserts are also incredible, but I am normally stuffed at this point and have to take mine home.

The Capital Grille is a little pricier than my usual restaurants so I save this place for romantic date nights and special occassions.

TIP: Be sure to ask the hostess to validate your parking pass if you decide to valet!

This place is something else. Went on a weekend trip with the boys and told them we have to stop here bc my yelp friends believe this might be the wholly grail of sandwich places. It was amazing, BUT i will say i have a few negatives to say, just not enough to make it worth giving less than a 5 star.

The place is tiny, packed, not much seating, but it makes it a real old school experience. My friends knew we were in for something special once we saw the line and chaos.

The Cuban was HUGE.  But it didn't seem as much a cuban as a normal cold cut sandwich. it was a delicious cold cut samich, but i really wanted a cuban.  The deviled crab is good.  i'm not a crab cake kinda guy, so my opinion is not as a Crab cake conessier.  The real gem, and the saving grace for me to keep it a 5 and not knock it to a 4 is the potato ball. OMG. Its like sloppy joe, wrapped in creamy mash taters, then fried to perfection. It was one of the best things i've ever eatin.  I will be back, i will skip the sandwich bc a sandwich and a ball is just TOO much food. perfect to split for 2. 

TATTER BALL! TATTER BALL! I just want to chant it down the gym halls like a football team superfan.

The only reason I'm not giving brocato 5 stars is because my "pressed" Cuban was cold and the cheese wasn't even remotely melted. 

The servings are massive and the quality is superb. The Cuban was stuffed with fresh meats and Swiss cheese. 

I order the you pick 2 combo and got the Cuban and stuffed potato. I didnt know what to expect with the potato but it was awesome. It had a ground beef mixture in the middle. 

I brought half of it home to my boyfriend and he loved it. 

They do not have extensive hours and the place is packed all the time, so expect to wait a bit for your food but it's definitely well worth it.

The place is awsome !  Coming out from California, on a Friday eve this place is pure novetly.  I felt Miami Vice, I felt Hollywood, I felt Cuban, I felt beach ...  Just beautiful dress up people all around looks like celebs, gangerster, bankers.  Exotica cars outside, people & strangers hugging each other, mini-skirts, high heel.  Its all there.  I thought this would be more like Miami but it's Tampa !  In fact, someone please tell me where to go in that side of the coast that looks like this place :-)    Food ?!  I don't know, I was way busy drinking and checking out people, only swallow a few doezn of oysters and that's it.  I will be back shortly.

I have to drop this place down a notch. 

A weeknight and my cooking bones were tired. We'd had good pizza from here in the past so I thought I would just order some pizza. I was also hankering for some good wings - I hadn't had any in a while.

I make the call. I always ask if there are any specials before I order. There are none. I order one 12" pizza with two toppings and 10 wings. The total: ~$18. They charge $2 for delivery and $.35 because I paid with a credit card. Huh? Once the tip was added, the total bill came to over $24... kinda steep. I am told 40 minutes on the delivery.

30 minutes later... the doorbell. They are early. Yoo Hoo!

Jubilation over... disappointment sets in. Their fault: they got the toppings wrong. I ordered 1/2 and 1/2 and the got the halves mixed up. My fault: I pictured a 12" pizza being bigger. There are three of us to feed and I don't think this will be enough, so I whip out a salad to add to the feast. I was right... that and the pizza was cut into six incongruent slices. Some are huge and some are tiny. The wings were ordered mild. These had none of that wing bite at all. They tasted like they were coated with marinara sauce and baked... I am not a fan of these wings.

Botttom line: bad wings, not good value for the price and the pizza was not as good as I remember. I hear they have just picked up space next door to them and tripled the seating space. Sounds like a visit to their new digs is in order to see if this experience can be pardoned.

Tip: The $2 delivery charge can be avoided If your order is more than $20.

I have yet to have a better steak in this town than Charley's. High heat seared to perfection and incredible attentiveness in the service department. I was with a group of four and we were all treated over the top. Manager came over and spent time with us and checked on us a couple of times during our evening. All food was prepared perfectly and we probably over ordered on the sides as they are generous also. I defy you to say no when that dessert tray comes over at the end no matter how full you think you are. The others are good, but this is still my favorite for steak in Tampa.

OK - they did it!  They got the 5th Star (but I also do love Salumeria for my grating cheeses).

What a great place.

I have been to Brazilian Steakhouses before, but Texas de Brazil manages to stand apart from it's competitors. The restaurant is gorgeous; very modern with a touch of Latin flair. The dining room has cathedral ceilings and chandeliers.  There is also a very cool wine room and the outdoor patio has huge, comfty wicker chairs.  

The salad bar at Texas de Brazil was excellent and offered numerous choices from soup, salad, olives, antipasta selections and sushi.  If meat on a stick is not your thing there is an option to only eat from the salad bar.  The service was prompt and we were never without a friendly server offering us a different delectable selection of filet mignon, prime rib or bacon wrapped chicken.  The servers take the red and green cards seriously... so when you flip over the card to green be prepared to be overwhelmed with an awesome abundance of meat on a sword.

The food that was most memorable to me was the filet mignon, the lobster bisque, and the cheese stuffed rolls (minature balls of heavenly gooey cheese baked into a small roll).  

For all-you-can-eat meat I recommend trying Texas de Brazil!
4 stars for the fabulous sandwiches. Would be 5 stars if they were open longer! They're open 9-3 Monday thru Saturday, but get there before 2:45 because they start packing up early.

I'd read good reviews for this place, and we finally gave it a try yesterday.  The pizza was ok, but not all that.  The crust wasn't really thin enough, and the middle got soggy.  It tasted ok, but I can name 5 places that are better.  The wings were actually very good.  I recommend the extra hot.

Came back for dinner and the food was pretty good.  I would say the food was 4-stars.  The salt and pepper calamari was good.  I think it is better than Golden Phoenix's version.  The summer special of duck and honey bbq was so-so.  The duck wasn't very good.  It was very tough and I think the duck had a gamy smell to it.  The bbq pork was very good.  I ordered the house lo-mien for my toddler.  He loved it so much he was double-fisting the noodles into his mouth.

The service is a lot better then the last time I was there.  We were seated very promptly, waters came out fast.  The right amount of time was given to make a decision for the order.  The food came out quickly.  The portions was generous.  But it all went down hill from there.  No one came to refill our drinks until the end of the meal.  No one came to check on us to see if we needed anything.  When it came time for the check, they were fast to get you the check and fast to take payment.  For a more upscale place, I had to box and bag my own leftovers, which was a turnoff.

i ordered the Plantano Rellena. It literally tasted like vomit. I ALWAYS finish my meal but this time I couldn't. I paid the bill and slipped out. It is very cheap, i have to give it that and maybe I just ordered the wrong thing. It's very evident that they don't use fresh or quality ingredients.

The food from the cafeteria/buffet, whatever you want to call it, tasted really good and was really cheap.
