The only problem was it must have been cooked in unsanitary conditions because it made me shit my brains out for 2 days. I almost had to call in sick from work from the lack of sleep I got last night. I had to throw away the rest of the food, which I hate doing.

I will get right down to it.

The food: Frickin frabulous!

Me: Kobe burger  - It's true; pampered cows make better burgers. It was juicy, cooked to order and very flavorful. The bun held it together perfectly. A VERY good burger.

Wife: The Samburger - came with bacon and cheese. She doesn't usually say much but she could not stop saying how good her burger was. Zinburger scores here.

Ambiance: Sleek tables and chairs make this a hip, cool joint... if not a bit noisy. One note on the decor: There is a huge mural on the wall with happy cows grazing against a blue sky. Why are these cows happy? Because they are not the ones on your plate.

Price: A little steep. $52 for burgers and fries... and that was during happy hour, when wine, beer  and sides (including fries, which do not come with the burger) are half price. Yes, a bit much but I guess once in a while we deserve to spoil ourselves.

The verdict: This place just opened today and it's just up the street from us. Maybe it's a good thing we went this soon. When word gets out, there's gonna be a line... but it will be worth waiting in.

Very good burgers!  Not the best in town, but it's nice that it's an upscale bar that also serves hamburgers, fries and shakes.  

Now about the bar...they need a better variety of beers on tap.  There were only four (none of which I really liked), and even the bottled beer selection was limited.  It looked like they had a large selection of wine, but I don't drink wine.  They also need a few more televisions at the bar.  One is not enough!

And finally, the shakes.  We split a Bars of Zin shake, and it was delicious!  It was so good, I could even see myself stopping in and getting one to go.  It was $6, but very large.  That isn't much more than you will pay at DQ.

Overall, I enjoyed the food and the atmosphere.  We will be back.

Oggi gives off the vibe that it's trying too hard. The decor isn't anything I haven't seen before in my grandmother's apartment and the receptionists act like they're too good to help you. Upon entering the salon, I was not greeted by anyone and had no idea where to go. After walking up a flight of stairs and having a stylist tell me I was in the wrong place, I meandered back to what was apparently the reception area. One of the ladies behind the counter gave me a smock and said virtually nothing to me. 

The shampoo girl was amazing and after, I decided to be impulsive and fore go my trim and go for a short bob. My hair dresser was really nice and I thought he knew what I wanted when I said I wanted my bangs "just above my eyebrow, maybe a bit shorter." My bangs turned out A LOT shorter; however, my bob looks great, despite the fact that the short-short bangs make me look hippsterish. 

Bangs will grow, so although I'm not 100% happy now, in about 2 weeks I will be. With that being said, this place isn't worth the price or the hype.

A seven course meal for $25 a person?! Yeah, it's a lot of food and a lot of fun. Bring a group of friends and your appetites, enjoy the wine and if you're feeling saucy, attempt to belly dance with a professional.

Yelp you told me to come here. It was your fault!  I'm soooo...happy you told me to come.

This area is outside of our traveling box, well not really my husband and I drive everywhere, but don't usually come to this area, but we did last night!!!

We sat on the pet friendly porch very cute. Don't let the surroundings sway your decisions differently bc this place has been here since the 1800's so you know everything else came afterwards. 

Food was super memorable. Hubby had Pot roast with veggies all very tasty, and I had the Chicken Chevre. So flavorful...I can dream about it, and then come back later lol..

Glad Yelp told us too come on down!!!!:-)

4 stars bc we were bitten up by mosquitoes. bring your OFF so you don't get bitten up.

The burger was average.  The cheese curds were crispy, hot, and just the right amount of melty.  

The chocolate custard was thick and creamy.  Had it mixed with some Oreo cookies, very tasty.

The girls who served me were polite and fast.

A good treat once in awhile.

With so many positive reviews I feel like the party pooper, but I can't bring myself to give this place more than 2 stars.  I've had better.

I really wanted to like this place, and I've been here often enough.   The appetizers are good, and so are the desserts.  But the sushi not so much.  I am a fan of sushi rolls, like Mexican, Tampa, Sweet Potato, etc.   They all have cooked stuff inside.  And in most places I go, they cook the stuff when I order it so it's nice and fresh and crispy hot.  At Zen, the tempura fillings have apparently been fried some time before I order, because they are always cold and soggy.  And that's just not fun.

They have a good selection of rolls, and outside of the cold, soggy stuffing they were ok.   Presentation was also not one of their strong points.   For much, much, much better, nicer, fresher sushi, drive one mile down Sheldon road towards Citrus Park and visit Grille 54.   Now THOSE guys know tempura!  The Surf and Turf roll is out of this world.   And they cook it when you order it, not that morning.

Yoko's in South Tampa and Tokyo Bay in St. Pete round out my top three sushi joints I would recommend in the Tampa Bay area.  Zen, unfortunately, I cannot.  If I were stranded in Westchase and I craved sushi, I'd walk to Grille 54.   And if it was raining there's a Pitas Republic, Pizza Fusion, Bollywood, China Wok, Tijuana Flats, Burger 21, and on and on and on, all within a stone's throw of Zen.  There are too many better places too close by.  C'est la vie.
The family style meal is delicious. The chicken is tender and tasty, the corn tortillas are freshly made and the beams are delicious.

I went there for happy hour...not the best. Not even close. They have 3 things on their menu. They are at half price...I will give them that. The chicken comes wrapped in bacon. You MIGHT want to mention that since there are a lot of folks in FL who can't eat that stuff because religious reasons. I doubt I will ever be back.

Went to the Lime on a Saturday around 7:30. We were able to get a table. The space is at a premium here since its a pretty small restaurant. 

The food was really pretty good. I wasn't expecting much given the yelp reviews, but I was pleasantly surprised. 

I got a basic chicken bowl and my friend got the quesodillas. We shared some chips & salsa and to me that was the highlight of the meal. Fresh medium/hot salsa with very high quality chips. 

I did have a margarita on the rocks. The table next to me was upset because the frozen margarita machine was broken. I'm not sure how that happens on a Saturday, but...I know things happen. My margarita was very good. It wasn't a triple sec dump like most margaritas in Tampa have been. Bravo! 

In the tiny banquet room there was a plethora of douche bags and the ladies who love 'en drinking out of beer towers. Ehh I suppose that is to be expected on a Saturday night. 

All in all it was a pretty good trip and I will be back to try some more entrees. They do have a nice happy hour 4-7pm everyday I believe as well Not bad...for South Tampa.

I recently moved to Tampa and this is the first Chinese restaurant that I've been to that serves braised beef stew and that made me less homesick. 

I will definitely come back to try the dimsum.

Fried green tomato salad is a must. 

We went here on a pre fixe dinner and it was great. 

The filet was tender and was atop a potato and onion puree. 

The dessert was a panna cotta and was phenomenal. My bf wants to come back just for that dessert.

Food, service, atmosphere are all well above average if not totally excellent.  They pour a great drink, serve delicious appetizers, perfectly prepared entrees, delectable desert along with a friendly and attentive staff.

I was probably one of the first people to eat at Kona when they opened. Since they're right across the street from where I work, it was a regular haunt. 

I love their Happy Hour (and Reverse Happy Hour after 10pm) but recently the service has gone down hill. The last time I was in, they were less than attentive. I mean, they know my orders and what I drink and yet they never came around. It was really odd. Their Happy Hour is more of a deal for the food than it is for the drinks. The food prices are awesome...the drink prices are "ehhh..so so". 

I've eaten there several times for Dinner and it's been very good...not great, but very good. 

I will give them another chance and in fact I'm planning on doing it real soon. I guess I was just disappointed with the service last time. 

I should note that sitting at the bar outside is a delight in the fall and winter...it gets a lot of afternoon sun and the fans at the bar manage to keep you cool. I have a favorite place to sit, but I won't say where since I don't want you sitting there when I show up!!

This restaurant has a little something for even the pickiest eaters. 

We all know TCF is very consistent and their food is pretty high quality.

However, I wanted to write this review about their little known Happy Hour.

Talk about value. I order the Southern Chicken Sandwiches and Onion Rings. Well...for $5 you get 4...count 'em 4...sandwiches. They're a TCF version of the Chick-Fil-A original sandwich. The Onion rings come in a 1ft stack @ $5. They have a ton of other stuff on there for apps too. I really think that this might be the best deal going for HH in Tampa.

The drinks for HH are pretty good deal, but they don't price them so you can get hammered for $10. That could be dangerous anyways...being intoxicated at the mall could get VERY expensive.

I should add in closing that the HH is only offered in the bar. Like always, the seating in there if first come, first served. So, if you walk in...look to your right, if there's a free high-top table - it's yours.

I've eaten at The Capital Grille all around this awesome country. This Tampa one is very nice. It has a great Happy Hour and the wine list is longer than my arm. 

I did have one beef last time I was there. We were sat outside...not by choice but because our reservations were jacked up. Unlucky for us, we got sat next to a couple who were in what seemed to be a cigarette smoking competition. 

The waitress was less than attentive and when I didn't order the $75/bottle wine she suggested she wrote off our table and worked on others she was going to get a big tip at. She was a very attractive girl who seemed like she was used to getting by on her looks and not much more. I'm sure we would've been treated better if it were two guys at the table and not a guy & a girl. I'm sure she would've turned on more charm. 

I normally drop ~$200 here easily. I know it's not a $500 table, but never the less it's $200. I can't really justify spending that anymore if I'm going to be treated like a 2nd class citizen. 

Have you ever been to a restaurant and you see another waiter working their rear off for a table next to you...but you've got a different waiter and she's just going through the motions? Well...I had waiter envy that's for sure!

I'm sure I'll be back...but it won't be for some time. There are plenty of businesses who'd like my ~$200 and they'll work hard to make it a memorable meal.

Their business name states how much they charge per item to dry clean--ONLY $1.50! My wife and I have used them and they are just fine. There used to be a similar $1.50 dry cleaner where we used to live, so we were happy that we found another one just around the corner from us.

This is in the Safeway plaza at Prince and Campbell.

Not the biggest burger, not the most tastey burger, but definitely one of the most EXPENSIVE burgers I've ever had.  $12.75 for 1 shroom burger, fries and a soda.  Definitely not a place to take the whole fam for a burger at those prices.

I am on the fence as to whether I'll go back.  The budget is tight these days and if I'm going to spend $12+ per head on some food, I'd rather go to a casual restaurant.

Can't say enough about the JCC.  It really is "user friendly" and fills everyones needs from young to old.   I've been coming here for 5 years and really feels like home.  I've made many friends and relationships with the Staff and gym rats.  Me being one of the rats.  The instructors in the classes are the finest you can get anywhere.  Zumba, Power Sculpting, Yoga, Body Pumping all great.  Dance party  with Rhina is one of my favorites.  Not to mention, the workout room.  Newest equipment.  The JCC weaves a spark into my  life and adds so much positive vibes to my soul.  It starts my day on the right track.  Thanks JCC.

PROS: Mango Lassi, Naan, Veg Samosas
CONS: overcooked Chicken Tikka Masala (very dry) and Shrimp Tandoori Masala (pasty); steep price for Indian take-out

Oh, Frankie, how I love thee! Top notch ingredients DO make a difference, even when it comes to sandwiches. You can taste the difference, especially when I just hit another long-time Tucson institution this week and vowed I will never return.

Frankie has been doing it right for years. Hand trimmed rib-eye for his cheesesteaks, hand-sliced deli meats for his cold-cut sandwiches. Even the lettuce is not your run of the mill iceberg! Thinly sliced onions and really hot peppers that he roasts himself. Put all of these into an imported Amoroso roll, and it's pure heaven. This is why I've been returning to this place for years. No place like it even comes close, not even his former partner.

There is no better movie theater in Tucson for me. If you love moviemakers like David Lynch, Werner Herzog, Jim Jarmusch, Crispin Glover, this could be the place for you. If love movies like Withnail and I, grindhouse films, and old classics and cult films, Grey Gardens,  or enjoy Mystery Science Theater (go for Monday Mondays), the Loft is the place for you. 
Throw a stone and you'll find something to watch. There are old movies being shown from Orson Welles on up to Rocky Horror Picture Show sing-a-longs. There is audience participation nights. Old favorites from the 80s and 90s. You will meet filmmakers and actors there. You can drink beer in the theater or sip wine if you're sophisticated :) There is Fresco pizza. And become a member and get great discounts and help support indy filmmaking or catch an old Cusack move on Friday night. 

Look for freebie tickets at Bookmans to get you into some Loft films. It's an awesome deal. I love the upstairs theater with its couches and small intimate setting. I don't like big-run films very often so the Loft is tailor made for a person like me. I am not pretentious (I just try and pretend to be). And don't forget to see Spike and Mike's Cartoon film festival if you want some really racy good fun!

I admit I haven't rented a DVD or video in like a year. I hate saying this. But it's true, and times are a changing. That does not diminish the awesomeness of Casa Video though for finding the most rare, unique and out of print type films. I am shocked at what I will find at Casa. The documentaries are mesmerizing. The director selections are beyond belief, I once went through every Orson Welles and Kubrick film they owned in a frenzy. 
Casa is open late and has great 2 for 1 deals. New run releases, and every kind of film you need from getting something for the kids, to a new release, a rare and hard to find classic, or even a porno. Casa has popcorn waiting for you and also films for sale. It's a great place. I popped by a few months ago during a sidewalk sale, and I feel ashamed that I buy most of my films now or rent them at the library, but I know Casa is here when I am looking for something super hard to find. 

It's a heck of a lot better than the failed Blockbusters.

This super organic Publix is a bit of a concept store I suppose. It couldn't be in a more perfect location really. There's a lot of disposable income within a 3 mile radius. Don't go here expecting to find any deal busters. They do offer Buy 1 Get 1 every week which is nice...and the chances of you being behind any of those crazy extreme coupon people in line is VERY slim. Besides it'd be tragic to be seen using coupons at this Publix I get the feeling.

This place is more of an experience than a market. You certainly don't run in here to grab something and go. At least I don't. The parking is actually above the store. So...you ride an escalator down to the store. The cool part is when leave. You & and your cart ride up the escalator together - the cool factor here is very high.

I'm not sure, but I don't think that ugly people are allowed at this place. Seriously, I'm not complaining, but it's like walking on to a movie set. Let's just put it this way, there are a lot of people in their workout gear wearing makeup.

I guess the thing that keeps this from getting 5 stars is the fact I can't find everything I need here. I can find the best variety of fruit this side of a farmers market. Also, they've got a great deli where you can pick something up to eat.

Of course the Publix staff is super nice...and ready to help you out. This is one of the last grocery stores I know of that still will take your groceries out to your car.

This is like a toy store of tools and knic-nacks. I love it here! Big location, lots of things. I walked out with a magnetic tool tray great for putting bolts and keys in that sticks to the fridge, and some stuff to fix cracks in my windshield, an ultra cheap survival/hunting knife. And to top it off they threw in a freebie tool when I checked out.

This is like a fun Ace Hardware with security alarms and safes, massive amounts of hammers and gear, deck chairs, garden gnomes, 4X4 tow equipment, saws, and on and on. Prices seem fantastic. Lots of fun to browse for men and women and kids.

AJs products are incredible. Fresh, clean, super. Just look at the labels and realize what you are getting. You pay more, but you pay for what you get, and that's a lot. Want organics and quality? Incredible fruit and veggies section. Amazing beer and wine (my mouth watered at the $450 bottle of Dom Perignon!) There is amazing looking sushi prepared fresh. I bought a tiny baguette that is the freshest and tastiest bread I have ever eaten. The selection is amazing, you will find things from all over the world. They get rare and unusual items so it costs more. 

I don't think AJs is snobby. I did get some weird looks from the employees when I showed up in my ratty shirt and shoes, but I know what I want there and get great quality things that go into my body. And there are better and better deals the more you show up, just like any other grocery store. I'd rather get some things here than anywhere else in town. AJ's is a place to explore more. And there are no homeless people or ghetto customers in line , which I love!

I probably can't or won't shop here too often since, you know, I like to pay rent and bills and stuff. But for specialty items and rarer beers and things, this is the place. It's also fun to browse. Totally good times.

Funny that Food City is owned by Bashas and AJs. Because Food City seems to be the low end ghetto grocery store, but with prices so ridiculously high it's not funny. Shopping here is a sad thing because of that. People come in here expecting and needing low priced groceries but of a higher quality than a dollar store, and yet they get slapped with high costs. Food City is crummy everywhere, dirty floors, nasty stocking, questionable looking meats and veggies. Everything is suspect at Food City. I feel it is a safety issue going there too from the look of the place and the people. It's better to just go to another regular grocery store and pay the same price or less because Food City is just trashy.

Where is Grantstone Market located? Yeah.. Grant & Stone. Somebody asked me that once. Easy to find.
But don't bother. Grantstone is filthy. I have seen one robbery there and a couple of people stealing things. The parking lot is crawling with low life scum who prey upon you and your vehicle. Don't go after dark for sure. The vegetables and produce are often suspect and need to be examined. They get some cool stuff there and at good prices. But I have seen fruit flies and worse. Things seem old and unkempt and nasty. It's like Grantstone isn't even bothering to try and compete with Lee Lee Supermarket. 

So that makes it fairly easy to decide where to go, and Grantstone is not a good option.
one of the best independent theatres out there.  shoot for the upstairs theatre where you can watch an indie film on a couch!!!! love mondo mondays and cult classic nights

Great view, great food, tasty dessert. I would definitely recommend the calamari and the seafood carbonara on papardelle- rich but so worth it!


Minus one star for the hostess alone. If you care to hear my rant, read on. Otherwise, just skip the rest...

I made a rezzie on Open Table for 7:30pm  then called twice. Once to request a window table. Again to request that it face the sunset. I was polite and sweet and emphasized that it was a special occsaion, my big sister's birthday. When we arrived the weather was rainy. We expressed concern about not being able to see the sun set. The hostess piped in saying "The sun might poke out of the clouds. It doesn't usually set until about 8:10 lately." She then proceeded to take us to the opposite side of the restaurant facing the highway. ???????? She refused to acknowledge that I had called (and I know I spoke to her personally twice because I remembered her name). I understand if tables aren't available but to 1) not acknowledge not being able to meet a request and 2) to add the bit about possibly being able to see the view when you knew you were seating us facing the highway. It's like rubbing salt in a wound!

I'd return but probably only as a party of 2 since there seem to be more two tops next to the bay-facing window. I just can't justify the price if I'm facing the highway.

We ate here last night with two other couples.  We had reservations, but they had us wait 40 minutes for our table.  However, they brought us comped appetizers and a round of drinks and a bottle of wine to make up for it.  Other than the late seating, service was perfect.
  
Food was excellent.  We had the fried calamari and shrimp cocktail to start.  The shrimp was sweet and good.  The fried calamari was awesome -- perfectly crunchy and garlicky and spicy.

For our main courses, I had the kona-rubbed sirloin, and my husband had the lobster stuffed shrimp with an extra lobster tail on the side.  We split the mashed potatoes, lobster mac and cheese and creamed spinach with the table.  Everything was wonderful, although the appetizers and alcohol ruined my appetite, so I took about half of my steak home.  Oh, but that kona crust was so good!  The crunch and the flavor were perfect!

We were too stuffed for dessert.

I don't normally give 5 stars to chain type restaurants of this persuasion (especially not ones located in shopping malls), but the service and food were wonderful.  And the wine list had a nice combination of the big names and a couple smaller ones too.  There was a great variety that included very affordable bottles and some high end stuff.

Parking: there's valet or you can brave the shopping mall parking and fight with Cheesecake Factory patrons for the good spots.

Very pricey place but service is great here.  The waiter was very knowledge about the food and had great recommendations for my husband and I.  Recommend this place for a romantic night out with your signficant other.

This is one of my go-to lunch places for taking new employees. Both food and service are consistently good.   The dining room has a nice, relaxed feel even when its busy.  The patio is also really nice.  

I've not been for dinner so I can't comment on that, but sometimes I think the lunch menu is limited.  Then I realize that I always order 1 of 2 dishes anyway so I guess it works for me after all. The stuffed mushrooms or spinach and cheese souffle are my regulars. I always immensely enjoy the side of greens with the citrus-y vinaigrette - such a tasty way to get your veggies.   The bread with pesto dipping sauce is what carb dreams are made of.  Seriously, I could eat an entire basket myself. 

Seems they are always crazy busy at lunch so I suggest a reservation unless you are going early.

Not bad, but not the best indian in tucson by far. Unfortunately, it is the only indian place that I know of on the east side. I came here a few days ago for the lunch buffet and had I written the review immediately afterwards I might have given it 4 stars. Waiting a few days was a good call because I think I realize now that I was just excited to find an indian place sorta near where I live.

The buffet looks really good. Several good choices to choose from, but not as many as one would find at sheri punjab, for example. The buffet includes iced tea, but be forewarned its the lipton premade crap. I was impressed to find one item in particular on the buffet line--the mango ice cream. "Interesting!" I thought...but that will have to wait for later...

Got some palak, some mixed veggie something or other, some tandoori chicken, and some chicken curry with side of rice to get going. Despite being there only 15 minutes after opening, the rice was already dried out and not very good. It also had rather large pieces of bay leaf still in it, which is not very good eats if you ask me. Pass. The mixed veggies: pretty good. Ive had better, but not bad. The palak...i think some of the best ive had in tucson. Very tasty. Tandoori chicken: a bit dry, and though the flavor was good, it wasnt great. Nothing to write home about. The chicken curry I have to say looked so delicious...but WOW what a disappointment! There was literally almost NO flavor to this dish. I seriously dont know how they accomplished this feat of culinary disasters. I think if you were to boil chicken in water with no salt or anything, youd wind up with something more flavorful than this dish. The chicken in it was a mix of chunks of breast meat and some shredded chicken, it was overcooked and despite being submerged in sauce was actually quite dry. I don't recall any veggies in the dish, but it could have used some. Just odd...

The Naan was very good, and the server brought it to the table at the start of the meal. Some people in past reviews have complained about it, but I dont know what they were talking about. It was good, solid naan. Im not a huge fan of the method of serving it though. I much prefer sheri punjab's approach of putting it ON the buffet so you can have as much as you want without having to ask the server over and over and feeling like a fattie :P

The samosa's I think were the highlight of my meal. Much smaller than most buffets' versions, they were really quite delicious. The tamarind chutney for dipping was also very good. Definitely went back for seconds...and thirds...and I think fourths on these.

So finally dessert time; I went for the gulab jamun and the mango ice cream. The gulab jamun was very good...some of the better Ive had around. The mango ice cream was...not ice cream. It was more like pudding, and it was remarkably sour. Really a huge let down. 

At the end of it all, the bill was about 10 bucks, which is not unreasonable, but is significantly higher priced than all the other indian buffets in town...its even more expensive than the buffet at saffron. Thats just unacceptable. Ive now eaten at (to the best of my knowledge) all the indian buffets in tucson, and this one ranks the lowest for me and has the highest price. I just can't justify that in any way. Id rather spend the money on gas and go to a better buffet than do that again. I dont want to make it sound like the food was bad--it wasnt--just completely not worth the price. Take that for what its worth, and maybe consider heading to sheri punjab or saffron instead.

We eat here at least once a week, and it's great.  All Boars Head meats guarantee good quality food.  They give a good amount of meat on their sandwiches.  They could maybe use a little more cheese, but good anyhow.  The Italian bread is pretty good, and always soft and fresh.  The only real sandwich complaint is that the Rueben, including the bread, really needs to be toasted, not microwaved.

The side salads are all pretty good.  My favorites are the macaroni and potato salads.  The staff is always nice.  The atmosphere is very much like a NY deli, which is comforting and nice.  They also sell a good brand of bucatini pasta, which is hard to find in Tucson supermarkets, so that's a huge plus for this Italian that likes to cook at home sometimes.

I would definitely recommend Fortunato's to anyone and everyone!

We tried out this location a few days ago, and loved that it was bigger and more spacious than the original Tucson location.  We were seated fast, greeted by our server quickly, and had drinks quickly.  Our drinks were never empty.  The staff worked as a team to make sure the service was as good as possible, which is highly appreciated by us.  The truffle fries were good as usual, as were the burgers.  I love being able to personalize the burger exactly how I like it, thanks to their plentiful topping options. 

My only gripe is that the food comes out far too fast.  We like a more casual pace, especially when paying higher prices.  I want to enjoy my appetizer and have a few minutes after it before my meal comes out.  The only way that happens here is if you delay ordering your meal until you're ready.

It is nice to have a more upscale burger place on the eastside, finally!  We will keep going back for sure!

We were dying for NY style pizza and decided to try this place out.

I will say that I am disappointed that their delivery radius is 5 blocks.  What kind of delivery is that?

But... we drove the 2 miles to check out this place, and it was worth facing the Tampa rain.  My husband went with traditional pepperoni and gobbled down 3 slices.  I tried the eggplant parmesan pizza and the Buffalo chicken pizza.  Both were awesome, although I was stuffed after eating about half a slice of each (there are a LOT of toppings on the eggplant parmesan, but amazingly, the crust is still crispy).

We'll definitely come back.
LOVE the "not your average Mac & Cheese"; great wine lists as well

I would start by saying: please don't come here - but there's more than enough seating for all of us, so please, do, come here! The food on this menu is crazy. If you want a simple breakfast sandwich, you got it. But if you want some fancy french toast with peanut butter cups, you can have that too. You want a cup of black tea? OK - no problem. But if you want a fancy custom iced coffee, you can get that as well. The only problem I have with this place is it's method of payment. If you go with one other person you probably won't get to the $20.00 minimum needed for a credit/debit card since the prices are super reasonable. In the end, this is a definite go-to for breakfast/lunch, for sure.
Great selection of storefronts, restaurants along Bay Street are all amazing as well - can't wait for the H&M opening Fall 2011!
Great company to work for - always has amazing collection of items to choose from, everyone will be satisfied! Also great for accommodating large parties, unlike most local restaurants
One of the few seafood restaurants where you can regularly find Cobia - always has incredible selection of fresh seafood

First of all, I have to say I am very sad to see Montana Avenue go away - it was one of my favorite consistent place to eat. Seems like if the menu had changed now and then and more attention was paid to service there, it could have made it.

So, on to Zinburger:

This location was inserted in to the above mentioned space - and the changes put an accent on the flaws of this space. Montana Avenue was always noisy and the remodel has served to make the space even noisier. I actually had a hard time hearing my server - and the place was only half full. Also, there is a TV in the dining room. Major demerits from me on this. I will go to sports bar if I want to watch TV during dinner.

The huge cow mural is unfortunate - hey those are diary cows - hopefully we are not eating used-up milk cows? A little too close to the Chick-Fil-a marketing campaign.

The story here is burgers and wine.

The burgers are quite tasty, but the fact that the basic Zinburger is $10 before extras is a little dear for Tucson. $10 for a gourmet burger in LA seems too high....

We ordered the Chicken salad without chicken, a Zinburger with wild mushrooms, and the fried zucchini. All of the food was good, and the portions were generous.

The staff split the burger and salad on to two plates, which was really nice.

We (two of us) also split three glasses of wine - and we were happy with the options we had to choose from. The pours were on the stingy side....

Overall? I won't be back. There is just not enough here for me to get excited about.

We've been to Gavi quite a few times, and every time, I have an illusion that it will be better.  But, it never is.  I am done going here and hoping for the best.

The food is very far from being good Italian food, it is amateur at best.  The red sauce has spices in it not at all typical of traditional Italian red sauce.  The pizzas are soggy.  The salad greens are usually brown.  The bread is often stale and difficult to eat.  They cut out many items from their menu, which were some of the more edible items because they weren't all traditional Italian (yes, they actually CUT, with scissors, their menu apart).

The service here is possibly the worst service I've had in all of Tucson, consistently.  I don't remember a time when it has been good.  It is common to eat without a drink, or to not get things you asked for with your meal until you're mostly done or your food is cold.  It is surprising, because they seem to be staffed pretty well.

The atmosphere is weird, as well.  If you look one direction, you think you're in a little coffee shop cafe.  If you look in another, it looks like an old family style place with giant dining room tables.  The tables are packed in, and it's not too comfortable.

The prices are also a bit ridiculous for what they serve.  I'm not sure why they think their pasta dishes are worth $16+.

Overall, if you want good Italian food, flee from Tucson.  Gavi is just another let down in this town.  I would not recommend Gavi to anyone.

From someone who had lived for 25 years in the tri-state area, and has eaten at the famous Pat's King of Steaks in Philly, I can say that Frankie's is pretty good.  It is not identical to Pat's, but it doesn't have to be in order to be good.  Frankie's uses good ingredients, and they make sure they have the right ingredients to make a good sandwich.  The bun is so important, and Frankie knows that, and gets them directly from Philly.  He just flat out makes a good sandwich.

It is slightly expensive for what it is, but that won't stop us from going!  Would recommend to anyone.

There are several courtesy rules for hiking Tumamoc Hill:

1. Walk on the right side. When you are barreling down after reaching the top, it is only polite to let those of us who are huffing and puffing on up to let us have space to walk. More yourself/baby carriage/ etc. 

2. Stay on the path. This place belongs to UA research. Let's not have it closed it down! 

3. Hold your nose if you use the outhouse. 

And the rest is just this: take a big bottle of water. Be prepared to really huff it on the second half of the hike. And admire the view when you get to the top especially at sunset. It is amazing to look out at Tucson from here. No cars, no noise. This is an amazing place for exercise and for reflection and beauty. Tumamoc is tops for exercise.

Blanco is an enjoyable experience every time.  If you realize that it is not authentic Mexican food, but rather, it is more upscale, then you should enjoy it.  The food is fresh and good quality.  It is also very tasty.  The machaca quesadilla is very good, and a decent price at happy hour.  The seared ahi tacos are usually cooked perfectly, and very tasty.  We've also had the carnitas tacos, which are also flavorful.  The meals are slightly small, but you're paying for the upscale atmosphere.  It is not a grubby taco joint.

The service is usually pretty good, and always friendly.  The dining room can get a little bit cramped, but the views are so nice that it makes up for it.  

My only real gripe is that they charge $2.95 for a Coke.  Seriously?  

We would recommend Blanco to anyone.  We always have a nice time here!

We've eaten here quite a lot of times in the last few months, with friends and by ourselves.  There are goods and bads about Canyon's Crown.

The food is pretty decent.  It is not spectacular, but it is generally good and tasty.  We've had the Fish n Chips and Fishwich, which are fried nicely and usually tender.  The Pasta Alfredo dish is flavorful, and a nice consistency for an alfredo sauce.  The Grilled Cheese is simple but good, as is the Mac n Cheese.  They use multiple cheeses to help with the flavor.  Sometimes they need a little additional salt, however.  Their Potato Bacon soup is very good.  We've only tried one or two appetizers, and they've not been anything special, but definitely edible.  The portions are good, and the prices are not too bad.

The service is often more miss than hit.  It is often very hard to get a drink refill.  I don't think we've ever had a messed up order, but we've sat without drinks for a long time.  The bill can also take a little bit too long to get.  I really dislike complaining in restaurants, but on at least one visit, the drink refills were so bad that I had to flag down a different server for a drink.  That is not acceptable.

The atmosphere is ok.  It feels like a pub.  It can get a little bit too loud when they have the music going, but it's not so bad.

Overall, we enjoy eating here.  It is a good option on most days when we want to be low-key or want to eat somewhat quickly but yet sit down to a meal.  We would recommend Canyon's Crown to most!

A world class shopping experience.  Honestly, this place is one of the best things about Tucson.  Go for the fish and steaks when $ is not an issue.  I've actually made a special trip just for a large hunk of salmon that they smoke in house and the bear claw from the bakery....this is the world's best bear claw...not kidding. They offer a decent bagel as well and strangely the bakery prices are not at all high.  The bagel and bear claw are what we would pay at Safeway only the quality is 100 times better. Great employees up here..very friendly and helpful.

I wanted to love this place.  It's Cuban, it's cheap, I know that I like their papas frites and croquetas at MIA, but this place disappointed me.

Service is great, I'll give them that.  And prices are excellent.

But my husband's black beans were overcooked.  My moro rice was dry and bland.  My oxtail stew was a little too salty.

The place is fine, but it's not fantastic.

Giving them a neutral 3 stars for the time being. I have not tried this place yet, but I have to put my two cents in for my customer service experience with one of the managers.
I was headed over for lunch one day when I realized that my Groupon had already expired. I called the manager and mentioned that I had not realized this and was there any way they would still honor it? No was the reply. So I asked if they would honor at least the base price I paid for it. No was the reply again. He said they would actually lose money because of Groupon's cut. Ok, that's understandable. And it's not their fault that I failed to realize it was one of those Groupons with a shorter expiration date. I then asked if they would honor ANY part of it. Again, no, a very unapologetic one at that. Rally? Not even a dollar? You have my money so how can you be losing by throwing me a small bone to get me in the door at least once? Groupon is supposed to bring in more business but I guess he figures they already have enough.
I may still try this place due to the great Yelp reviews, but I'm not so sure they would value my patronage. :(

Bazil's is easily the best Italian food in Tucson, and this coming from a native New Yorker.  It isn't the best Italian I've ever had, but it is, again, the best in Tucson.

You get what you pay for at Bazil's.  Every entree comes with a delicious antipasto salad, pasta fagioli soup, endless bread, and the entree itself.  The salad greens are always green and fresh, the meats and cheeses are good quality and tasty, and the vinaigrette dressing is sweet yet a little tart and just absolutely wonderful.  It is an excellent salad, one of the best in town.  The pasta fagioli is also loaded with flavor, and is great to dunk your bread into so you get every last bit of the soup.  The bread is crunchy on the outside and soft on the inside, and a great compliment to a good Italian dinner.

We've only tried a few dishes here, personally when I find something I like I tend to stick with it, but what we've had has always been excellent.  The chicken in the Chicken Parmigiana is cut thinly and breaded perfectly, and baked perfectly with a lot of yummy cheese.  If you don't like cheese, don't order this!  It is then covered with their rich and flavorful red sauce, which is seasoned well and is a perfect consistency for Chicken Parmigiana and for their pasta dishes and side dishes.  We've also tried the Chicken Francaise, which also had well cooked chicken and a tasty lemon sauce.  My husband enjoys the baked potato on the side with butter and sour cream, but it is pretty basic as far as a baked potato goes.

We've also had their pizza to go, and it is also excellent.

The service here has always been friendly and pretty good, and the owners are kind and often present.  The atmosphere, while not the hip "trendy" style that everyone likes so much these days, is cozy and great to bring a date to.  You can really feel like you're in your own little world with your partner while eating here.

My one small gripe is that they charge per soda refill.  Soda should never not have free refills.  

We love Bazil's and go here for all of our special occasions.  We would recommend it to everyone!

We've been going to Biscuits for about a year and a half now, and we've had mostly good experiences.

The food is mostly average, but solidly average.  The prices are fair when you consider that this is basically just simple diner food.  We've had both breakfasts and lunches.  The egg omelets are generously portioned and have a good amount of ingredients stuffed in them.  It tastes fresh and not overly complicated.  The breakfast egg sandwich is a large sandwich, full of eggs, lots of cheese, and bacon.  The biscuits and gravy are decent and very fairly priced.  The burgers are nothing special and are probably just frozen patties, but they are edible.  The club sandwich was simple and good enough.  Overall, the food is consistent and simple American breakfast food.

The service is hit or miss sometimes.  At times it is good, and at other times, they are understaffed, so tables or people waiting to be seated get overlooked for a little bit too long.  When we get our "regular" waitress, the service is great!  They are all mostly nice and friendly, however.  The owner is usually around and is always very friendly and willing to have a chat.

The decor is not my cup of tea.  I don't personally enjoy the patriotic, Americana, red-white-and-blue, tchotckes, but it doesn't ruin my experience there at all.

I would recommend Biscuits to anyone looking for a decent breakfast or lunch on the eastside of town.

Enjoyed it. The food was really good and the drinks were perfect - A+ 

Service wasn't too bad, but there was only about 5 people in the restaurant on a Sunday afternoon - the staff did yell across the restaurant a couple times at each other, which wasn't the most professional. All in all, great drinks/food/atmosphere...

We've been going to El Potosino nearly once a week for lunch for the past month or two.  Each time was a good food experience, and we now prefer El Potosino to other burrito places in town.

The breakfast burritos are loaded up and always piping hot.  You can get any combination of breakfast ingredients that you like.  I usually get just eggs and cheese, and use their homemade hot sauce as a condiment.  They don't cheap out at all with either the amount of eggs or cheese, and they use a good combination of both to make the burrito taste very good.  My husband likes the carne asada burrito.  It is also loaded up with plenty of ingredients.  It has appropriately sized pieces of steak so you don't have to rip steak apart, making a mess to get a decent bite.  I'm not a fan of avocado or guacamole, but the guacamole they use tastes great, and I enjoy it.  It is seasoned well, and compliments the steak.  The tortillas for both burritos are always soft and they taste good.  The overall food quality seems decent.

The service is usually pretty ok.  Not overly friendly, but not bad either.  The food comes out in a reasonable amount of time for a burrito place.  The prices are right on -- I never feel ripped off here. 

We would recommend El Potosino over other burrito fast food places in town hands down!

My tummy loves Kon Tiki, my liver does not. My tummy always wins. I've been enjoying the Tiki since I was of legal age, and haven't stopped loving it. The atmosphere is just awesome, it's unlike anything else in Tucson. I'm not big on the college crowd that comes in to booze up, but at the right times of day, Kon TIki has such a wonderful aura about it, a mystique. 
I try different drinks when I come in, but always come back to the Navy Grog and Scorpion. My god, the alcohol content is so high! The drink prices are fair for the amount of liquor you get and for the tasty concoctions. This is a place a man can order a girly drink and not feel self-conscience about it. The Scorpion is great shared between two, with those 2 massive straws, but I like to hog it to myself.

When the booze pickles you, it's time to order off the menu. I wind up getting "monkey on a stick" which is meat on a kabob, on top of these wonderful sweet chips. The combo of those with the dipping sauce is superb. I've only ordered a meal here once, and wasn't overly impressed, so I just stick with drinks. Lots of them. A time or two I've even been cut off here because the booze really got to me. That is impressive.

We decided to go have some Micha's today for lunch after an appointment elsewhere.  In the past, we've had decent experiences, which is why I gave an extra star, but today's experience was more like a 2.5.

We both ordered carne asada burritos, enchilada style.  It was impossible to taste anything on the plate because of the extreme amount of salt in the food, mostly in the enchilada sauce.  It was unpleasant, and made me cringe a little bit from the intensity.  The meat was ok, but I did get a few fatty pieces.  The tortilla was good.  The salsa was good.

The portions are ok, but they should give rice and beans or some sort of side with their burritos if they are going to charge $8.50 ($10.50 with enchilada style and $13.00 if you want sour cream as well).  The service was fast, but not overly personable.  They just want to rush you out of the door and herd more people in as fast as possible.

Overall, I'm still inclined to say give Micha's a try.  It has been decent in the past, and I hope the heavy handed chef who salted the food today doesn't make it a habit.

I was torn between rating PF Changs 3 or 4 stars.  The food is good, but it's a far cry from real Chinese food. The service varies depending on the server... We've had great service, horrible service, and a lot in-between. The cocktails are horribly overpriced so my husband and I normally just order a pot of hot tea, which is incredible. Since we live close by we typically grab our food to-go in the curbside pick up area.

This P.F. Changs is located at the Westshore mall so it's always packed. I'd highly recommend making dinner reservations on Friday and Saturday nights because it can be at least a 30 to 45 minute wait after 7pm. The bathroom is poorly located next to the bar so you have to fight a crowd every time you want to go to the bathroom.

As I mentioned earlier, the food is tasty; just not authentic. The lettuce wraps and dumplings are two of my favorite appetizers on the menu. There are many mild and spicy dishes on the menu depending on your spice preferences. We enjoy a lot of spice so we usually order the spicy green beans and the Cho's Spicy Chicken. The honey glazed chicken and the wonton soup are also quite good.

I have never ordered dessert here, but I have tried a handful of the cocktails and they are usually too sweet and fruity for my taste. Be cautious of ordering cocktails that aren't listed on the menu. The bartenders have disappointed me on numerous occasions with poorly made Dirty Martinis and Cosmos.

All in all PF Changs is nothing special, but the food is consistent so you are guaranteed a decent meal.
Once again yummy house didn't disappoint. So freaking good.

Had a great lunch experience the other day.  The food was awesome as I would expect from a Zinburger.  The service was also great.  They were super busy, but our waiter took the time to discuss burger options and make suggestions.  Not that I don't already know the menu by heart, but my dining partner was there for the first time. Our order was taken promptly, our food arrived promptly and our iced tea glasses were always full. 

It's just as noisy as the one on the West side of town, but it has such a nice open feel.  In my opinion Zinburger is a better fit for this space.

You have not lived until you have had grass fed beef. NOT available here. Once you have had it, you will never go back to the ordinary and unhealthy grain fed beef.  Grass fed is loaded with healthy Omegas. Try Cafe Tremolo. Get healthy!

I'm on a quest once again to find the "best" in a particular category.  I've pretty much done it  with the Mexican thing as well as the Pizza thing.  I do consider myself a "foodie" and a bit of a food snob.  So therefore I believe my taste buds are very sensitive and right on the mark.   So here goes.  From what I remember about Mc Donald's and Burger King (haven't  eaten in them since the 70's since I am also a bit of a health nut) Culver's tastes just like them.  Pretty average. .  I had the one with bacon, wisconsin cheese, lettuce, etc.  The fries were OK.  Loaded with sodium.  Albeit, the custard was thick and creamy and quite good.  Not worth going again.  Sorry guys, but it  was not one of my winners.  I guess other folks like it because it was noisy and mobbed.  We went to the one on Thornydale.

I love the atmosphere of Acropolis on Friday and Saturday nights! Every night they have a gentlemen or two playing the bouzouki and singing traditional Greek songs, but on Friday and Saturday's they have a belly dancer that dances through the dining room. She is truly beautiful! Oh yeah...the food is awesome too :) They have a wonderful Greek salad!!! And my favorite is their Spanakopita! It is so yummy!

I like this place. I don't love it, but I like it. 

There isnt anything special about the set up here, it's the typical 2ish story bar in Ybor. What makes this place special is the people. 

You normal crowd here is anything but normal. This ain't MacDintion's. Eclectic is the best way to describe the atmosphere here. Make nice with the (normally hot) bartenders & you'll get taken care of.  There's usually live music & the crowd is full but not overwhelming. I'm never quite sure why Coyote Ugly has the crowd this places doesn't. Morons.

In terms of fast food, Culver's ranks amongst the best for us.  it is fresher, cleaner, and tastier that most.

The burgers aren't too thick or too thin, and they taste good.  I don't normally like fast food burgers, but these are edible for me.  The meat is better quality, and you can taste the difference from some of the other places.  The crispy chicken is fried very well, and is seasoned nicely.  The veggies they put on the sandwiches always look and taste crispy and fresh.  The cheese curds are unique and very good, and definitely worth trying.  They're the best side dish out of the ones we've tried.  The mashed potatoes were bland, and the cheese fries were just ordinary.  

The custard is thick and creamy and what will bring me back to Culver's over and over.  The texture is so much better than the "ice cream" (aka powder) that some places use for their desserts.  Culver's also isn't stingy with toppings, and they have plenty to choose from.  It is just really good.

It can take a little bit too long to get the food sometimes.  The last time I went through the drive-thru to bring the food home, once I got home, I found that both meals were wrong somehow.  However, everyone is friendly, and they walk around the dining room to assist you and clean up.  The prices are a little bit high, but when you consider you're getting much higher quality and fresher food, it doesn't matter so much.  I'd gladly pay more to get better food.

Overall, I would recommend Culver's to those who like fast food and/or custard.  it is worth a try!

We've now been here twice, once for drinks and once for dinner.  The drinks are incredible - the decor is 70's tiki which is way too fun.

We had a Kon Tiki combination platter appetizer.  The ribs were so tender the meat literally fell off the bone.  The crab puffs were tasty but seemed to be crab-less.  The shrimp was decent as well.

Hubby had the rib eye.  It was a little overcooked - more medium well than medium.  The quality of the meat itself isn't the highest but then neither is the price so it was fine.  My Mon Nui chicken had good flavor but was a little on the overcooked side.

Service was attentive and friendly - the atmosphere was fun.  The night we were there they had tricycle races out front.  

We'll definitely be making trips back here!

My family trio of husband Gary and mom and I (the dining trio) did a Labor Day treat at North. And it was a treat from beginning to end. I nabbed the receipt listing the goodies we ate, so here goes:

You can valet park or park in front of or behind the restaurant areas. There are a few restaurants all built in the same area so parking is a bit removed.

A yummy chewy bread started off things. I had to ask a serving assistant (the ones running around with iced tea/water pitchers, clearing plates, etc.) for some bread as it did not seem to be forthcoming otherwise.

It was very busy and people were bustling. The noise level at this restaurant is intentionally high, so if you like quiet ambiance, either eat outside or elsewhere. The view is lovely in both directions (north and south), and the southern half of the building is a gigantic glass-windowed curve. Really pretty, and contemporary.

Back to the goodies:
Mom had the Eggplant/onion pizza-on lavosh type crust, very thin and very tasty. The eggplant was nearly paper thin, cut long-ways, which takes great skill and a sharp knife! Served with a great cheese-not sure if Parmesan or Romano cheese. Yum!

Gary had the "market fish" of the day: a seafood medly of tomato-based angel hair pasty topped with mussels, a huge scallop and shrimp. It was delicious. He felt it was an ample serving, too.

I ate a chicken parmesan with a small side of spaghetti. (It was supposed to be angel hair, but I guess the chef or prep person goofed?) Very tasty as well. Two breasts, very tender, seasoned perfectly.

Our server was very friendly and cheerful, and apologetic about the wrong pasta being served to me. I did not make a big deal of it, but my mom told her it would be great if she added some of the correct angel hair to my leftover box. The waitress agreed, but never did follow through. That would've been a nice touch to do, in an upscale restaurant where the lunches cost 11-14 dollars average.

I also had a flavorful iced passion tea (no decaf available). It had so much ice that I had to refill it often. Get it with less ice so it lasts longer and is not so diluted.

We shared a killer good dessert-a warm, sour cherry bread putting, served in a bowl and topped with vanilla ice cream. Really good. The tart cherries really worked well with the sweet ice cream and bread pudding, that had a lovely spongy type texture. Kind of messy to share, but hey, we're family! We're the eating-out trio and we have fun wherever we go, by golly! And you should go to North and have fun, too. The food was impeccably great!

Been there twice now. First time was the better experience. the Deluxe Butterburgers were OK. Cheese curds are so-so. Frozen custard is good, but it's frozen custard. Nothing more special than Baskin Robbins, and just a little cheaper than Allegros. So I'd rather have BR and Allegros. The wait was about 7 minutes to order and 15 minutes to get our food when it was busy. Parking was crowded and only had two spots open when we came.

Second visit (today) was worse than the first. Wait time was about 7 minutes to order and 30 minutes to get our food. Had the Cheddar Burger. Looked like it was thrown together. Lettuce on all the burgers are shredded, so the pictures are false advertising as they depict whole leaf lettuce. The burgers were OK, nothing outstanding. We decided that we like In-N-Out burgers better. They are cheaper than Culver's, too. Quicker, that's for sure and parking is not an issue. Once again, there were only about two parking spots open when we came.

This time, in addition to the burgers, I tried the Atlantic Cod. Nothing spectacular. Rather bland tasting, actually. I've had better at cheapo Long John Silvers and at Jack In The Box. Batter was crispy, but a little on the thick side. The cod fillets themselves were real cod, not formed from parts, and good sized. But like I said, tasted bland. Cole slaw was good. Fries are OK. Root beer good.

I must note that the first time they brought out the cod dinner to me, it was almost burnt. I took two bites and decided that if I spent $9 on this dinner, I was not going to eat dried-out, chewy fish that was over-cooked. I had them take it back and give me a new one, which they did cheerfully. But tack on the additional 7-10 minutes of waiting time. Of course, I ate my burger while I was waiting, to be fair. But still...

My biggest complaint is that it took 30 minutes to get our order. We were not the only ones, either. How difficult is it to grill some burgers and throw a couple of cod in to the fryer? Will not be returning.

***UPDATE

I sent an email on the Culver's corporate contact webpage which allows you to state the specific restaurant you went to. No one has contacted me back so this is another "ignore you" instead of "contact us" corporation.

When I see a Colombian flag flying in the wind I get excited...lol, well that's my  husband's background so I have to go. 

The best part of our meal is the outside patio. It is nicely shaded with tons of fans. Very cool place. You can not even tell that it's right on the busy hillsborough.

The food, blah blah blah, and blah.  Is it frozen and heated up?  I don't know. Seemed like it. I don't pay for frozen and reheated. I can make that even better.

We had the paisa dish. I forget the name. It's steak, sausage, arepa, avocado rice and beans. It's big that's why we split it, but there was no flavor, seasoning, anything.

The food was not of a good quality nor taste.  Oh yeah, if you are looking for the bathroom. Go to the back of the building and that is where it will be.
Burgers are amazing. You won't get confused by this menu, nice and simple like In-N-Out but absolutely delicious burgers, salads, shakes, and pies.  Yummy!

WARNING: you'll get addicted to this hidden gem on DI...

Food was fantastic, prices were reasonable, and service was great. Best Thai food I've found in Tampa, thus far! 

Perfectly-cooked Pad See Ew with the fried tofu = my heaven

So this one is part of the Yelp has an app for that food fight. Ill be posting a review just for the app and then I'll update at a later time once I actually have a full blown meal here!.

This was stop #5 for us.

So this was my first time at Tavolino, and the place is gorgeous! the location the restaurant the surrounding area, it totally doesn't feel like the rest of the city here. I was on a mission this time around to meet up with some fellow Yelper's and run through a series of 5 restaurants for the food fight and this was my first stop.
 
For having a crowd of 12+ the staff did an amazing job with all the drinks and having all the appetizers come out at once! The appetizer was Vongole Al Timo it was a plate of little neck clams, cannelloni beans, tomatoes, thyme, and Pietro Rinaldi Aneis white wine. The plate came chock full of clams and a mixture of the beans in the white wine sauce and it was topped with some pretty good tasting bread that was toasted and worked well as an accent to the dish. The beans and clams were all cooked perfectly but what I kinda thought was the dish fell a bit flat. Even though I ate it all and thought it was good, it really didn't pop. I think the wine was almost wasted because there was nothing to catch it and bring it all together besides the bread. The execution was done in a way, however, that impressed me and none the less I will be back. You can catch a pic of the dish under the business profile here, and you too will probably agree that it looks stunning. The rest of the menu sounded so good and the folks were so nice here that I will definitely be back to enjoy a full meal here. But for now gotta run only 13 more restaurants to hit before the food fight can really begin!!

This one scored 3 of 5
When you have extremely poor customer service and poor quality food and also other conflicting news, all I could say, Good Reddens!!!

All I can say is that this pub has the best bar food I've had in a while.  With a great beer selection to compliment it.  I had ahi sliders with onion strings that were not only delish, but also unique. My friends had a shepherd's pie and some pretzel with slices of steak, onions and peppers that she said was exquisite.  Overall, I was impressed with there bar food and the unique twist on food.  Server was also very attentive.
Decent bar food and atmosphere, a great place to go if you're staying nearby on business.  Waiters are friendly and the spinach dip and sliders were pretty yum!

Wow, I can not believe how much I just paid for a horrible meal. The salad bar was not bad. The service was awful...had to ask 3 times for something to drink. I asked for some mashed potatoes (since it comes with the meal, why am I having to ask), they never did bring them until I was paying the check.  I declined them, as I was finished with my meal. I like my beef well done or med well, I requested this as soon as we sat down. The waiter assured me he would take care of it. I NEVER did see the beef.  Had to ask only 3 times for something to drink. Worst meal and service I have had in long time. All of our friends raved about this place....I will not be recommending it to anyone. Terrible!!!!

I have to admit that I was a bit shocked and plesantly surprised and the sushi and tempura feast that my friend and I partook in the other day. Iwas a little weary because word of mouth on this place is that it isn't so hot. Well I saw  a groupon for this place. It was a good deal and so I figured I'd check it out for myself.

I have to admit that I was happy I did. I'm usually not a big fan of raw fish on top of my sushi rolls, and I almost never eat raw nigiri. However, when our specialty rolls came out (my favorite was the cherry blossom btw) I saw it was covered in raw fish. Well, I took a bite and was very happy. It was extremely fresh, not overly chewy and just really tasty. Our tempura was also a treat.

So, for the price, the quality isn't bad. There is no all you can eat option here as far as I can tell. IF there was, I'd probably be eating at Sakura more oftne. The teppan steak looked good and I would definately go back if I were in the mood for either. 

The tikki bar and patio area is also pretty cool. There was decent music and some people dancing. If you're looking for a fun night out at a local Tucson establishment, this is worth checking out. It's not going to be some sort of upper class, my poop doesn't stink cuisine....but it is definately a step up from a lot of the local fare.

came here with my groupon~

it was alright, honestly i probably wouldn't come here without my coupon.  the things are kinda pricey... and the menu is really nothing special.  i did enjoy the dessert flight of shooters.  it's not alcohol, it's actually different flavor moouse in shot glasses.  we had the choco mint, smores, cappuccino, raspberry cheesecake and some other.. we enjoyed the choco mint a lot, it was a nice and cool finish after a big meal.  the other dish worth mentioning is their lobster mac and cheese.  A side that the waiters highly recommend.  It's a nice complement to the meal.  the top is baked to crisp and the mac and cheese is cheesy with noticeable flavor of lobster.  I enjoyed it.

their onion soup, however, was just too much for me.  it comes in a little pot and a THICK layer of swiss and provolone cheese on top.  the soup is decent on the first sip, but got too salty as you consume it... my boyfriend said it tasted like MSG water -_-;;;; so that's not good... 

probably wont be coming back.. tho my experience was fair.

My husband and I took advantage of the $35 Sunday tasting menu, and were very happy with the service and courses. I have a mustard allergy: Pumpkin is the first restaurant to which I've ever been that was so conscientious that the server inquired about the dangers of "cross contamination." I was extremely appreciative of the restaurant's solicitude and, not surprisingly, suffered no allergic symptoms. But of course it was the food that inspired my review. Each dish was beautifully prepared and presented, and featured seasonal ingredients that evinced the chef's creativity and inspiration. We started with an exquisitely composed chilled tomato soup with buttermilk that was garnished with crunchy pumpernickel crumbs. The second courses, which my husband and I shared, were also excellent: risotto with sweet grilled corn and a seafood foam (which I liked, despite my habit of rolling my eyes when restaurants serve the oh-so-trendoid foam), and a soft, coddled egg that was as rich as rich could be. A salad of various heirloom tomatoes followed, with "popcorn ice cream" on top that simply added flavor and heart. The pork entree featured tender medallions of pork and a delicate, runny quail egg. I ordered the skate; it was served (unexpectedly) rare with dollops of olive paste and fried cauliflower. I was planning to skip dessert - white chocolate cheesecake? chocolate cake?  - but the description belied how innovative and delicious were both items. The cheesecake turned out to be as rich and dark as caramel, having been made with goat's milk. As for the chocolate "cake," it was as gooey as a souffle. Our only quibble was with the noise: a large party sat in the middle of the restaurant and did some loud celebrating fueled by the bottles of whiskey and vodka on the table. This, however, was not Pumpkin's fault. I'd go back in a second.

On my way to Golden Phoenix when the traffic light on Busch Blvd goes berserk and I was a few feet away from Armenia Ave light.  I turned to my husband and told him I wanted to try China Yuan because Ricky gave it 4-stars and I trust a fellow Viet with similar taste buds.

Don't be scared of the outside.  The inside is clean; cleaner than a lot of Asian restaurants I have ever been to.  The waiter seated us quickly.  It was kinda weird that the the waiter walked away then came back later to take our drink order.  I guess he was in the middle of something when we walked in.  We order the double delight and they were more than happy to substitute the honey bbq pork for the crispy pork.  I got the kai-lan and the salt and pepper squid also.  The food came out very quickly and streaming hot... a big plus.

The roasted duck was very good.  It was meaty and the seasoning was very pleasant.  Most places I go to would have a sweet tasting or a very salty bird.  This duck was the perfect balance.  The crispy pork was good.  It isn't as good as the Oceanic market crispy pork but it was a great crunch and the pieces weren't too fatty.  I ordered another small order of crispy pork and was very disappointed with the second order.  They put it in the microwave to heat it up and caused the meat to stick together and it had a rubbery bite to it.  The reheating caused the crispy pork to release the oil/juice and it made it smell gamey.  The kai-lan was perfect.  I request that it be sliced into smaller manageable pieces since most places leave the stem whole.  They sauteed the kai-lan with garlic and salt.  It would taste a lot better if they add fish sauce to the kai-lan, but I am at a Chinese restaurant and not a Vietnamese one.  The salt and pepper squid was good.  Calamari, for you westerners, was very tender compared to the Yummy House Hillsborough location.

My complaint is that the bowl they brought the rice out in wasn't the cleanest.  The dishwasher missed the groove/lip where the lid sits on.  It didn't really bothered me too much because my rice didn't touch that area.  They don't use real chopsticks here and my hands were cramping from the disposable ones they offer.  When I asked for another pair, they thought I was Filipino and brought out a spoon and a fork.  I gave the guy a blank stare because I don't know how to use a spoon and a fork together.

Oh wow!!! This was my first experience eating here. I LOVED it! 
The service was outstanding and I love how they explain every detail about their restaurant and how the entire process works. 
They have a huge buffet that has all kinds of great side dishes, cheeses, California rolls, shrimp, salmon, other little eats, soup and salads along with breads. 
Then, once you sit back down at the table, they have waiters with all kinds of different meats on skewers, from prime rib, lamb chops, pork tenderloin, Parmesan crusted chicken, bacon wrapped chicken, to filet mignon, coming to your table to cut off fresh slices of any of the meats you are interested in eating. As part of their sides they bring to the table are garlic mashed potatoes and fried bananas that are covered with sugar and cinnamon as part of your "palette cleansing". Me...I ate them as a dessert. They were so delicious! 
I would suggest you come on an empty stomach for sure because this is a feast for kings! One of their signature drinks we had was the Caipirinha. A must try! 
I'm so looking forward to coming here again on a day when I feel like eating a lot...I had a great time here and a great place to come as a group too!

The food is wonderful, except for the weird avocado/guacamole which tastes like they put avocados in a blender and add water. 
If they only added a slice of avocado it would be a thousand percent better!!!

Chaotic-sports-lovers-food-happiness

The Pressbox website shows-off the place's ability to house Bucs players in their down time. No wonder-- it's minutes away from RayJay Stadium. The place is easy to pass-- built to look run down with wooden planks and scribbled murals, but inside it's typical sports bar strangeness-- sexy servers in tight umpire gear, full bar, laughing customers and loud TVs. It's a mecca for the local sports teams (and UF Gators)

This place seems to attract all sorts of people, so no fear for sports-idiots like me. My sexy server was very helpful, showed up at the right times-- and my marketing eye noticed-- also subtle in upselling. Nice.

Food-- Burgers DA BOMB! Cooked how I wanted, juicy, and the right amount of toppings. Bun's bottom half was soggy with juices at arrival-- I didn't like that part. Menu has about 14 types of "famous burgers." No boring BLT there-- all burgers scream butt loads of zing and flavor. 
And I detest steak fries, but these had flavor (tasty seasoning) The wings tasted like typical wings-- still yummy, but normal. The sandwiches are trampled by the burgers, but still delicious.

This was my third visit to Oystercatchers and the best so far because we went for dinner and enjoyed a spectacular sunset from our table. Upscale contemporary bar and dining room with out of this world views of the bay. While pricey the food and service has always been topnotch. We have had the brunch on Sunday, enjoyed a business lunch and caught up with old friends at dinner. The seafood mixed grill was plentiful and prepared perfectly, so good. Service at the bar was attentive and the cosmo was delish.
Be prepared to spend over$100 for two. 

The key lime pie was best  I have ever had, creamy and not overly tart.

I like getting delivery from the Golden Empress. I do agree that the in house atmosphere is lacking. As a vegetarian and occasional seafood eater, I am not really big on fake meats and have not liked several of the faux chicken, pork or beef veggie options I've tried. However; the bean curd, egg foo young and vegetable dishes are very good, as are their pickled cabbage salad and veggie spring rolls. It is a great place for me and my meat eating husband to get a meal. They provide really quick delivery, have very reasonable prices and depending on how hungry you are the average cost for two can be less than $20.
Ok at best with somewhat bland fare at a very inexpensive price.

I had the escargot app and duck entree. The escargot were very well seasoned and the duck was succulent and flavored with a hint of coffee.  A bit different but delicious all the same. The Pinot noir that we brought added a nice touch. Their wine pairing recs were right on target. The nutella pot du creme was just heavenly. Chocolate, coconut, and hazelnut creme all in one dish!  I will come back just for that alone. Service was okay but they did refill our bread basket, which was much appreciated because I love warm, fresh bread with olive oil.

I found this place while jogging in Tampa yesterday. I haven't had empanada in a long time, so I thought I'd try it. For those of you who have never had empanada. It is a stuffed pastry that was fried or baked in many countries of South America, South of Europe, and the Philippines. Not the healthiest if they are deep fried, and they usually have fish or pork filling. 
The kind that they have here had fruit, crab, pork and chicken, and spinach. 
I don't eat meat anymore, so I tried the guava and cheese. 
Not the healthiest either, but it was edible. 
I can not eat this stuff all the time, but I will definitely go back and try the Spinach and Artichoke.
The service here was quick and friendly. It is the perfect place to grab a party pack.

This has a pretty good atmosphere, kind of a hip feeling going on. You can sit upstairs or at a wooden table looking out onto Congress. Wonderful for people watching. Cozy chairs, and free wi-fi. The cafe au lait my friend and I had were nothing special. It was good, but not great. It wasn't hot enough for my tastes although she likes it at that temperature for sipping. It was served in glass mugs which is great to see the texture and foam. It was good, but also a bit pricey at $3.50. Not outrageous, just wasn't that big a mug.

It's trendy, and tries hard to be so; and I am not sure that's a good thing. The servers were cute and wore aprons. They do have beer in bottles and funky sandwiches at a decent price. This coffee is the famous "Blue Bottle." It had a strong kick and I felt the caffeine and some chicory in it. 

Would I go back? Possibly, if I'm around the area. Would I go out of my way to go there? Probably not.

We went there with a Groupon, otherwise haven't been in years.  The food was unremarkable at best.  Service was good.  Samosas were good, but I didn't find it as flavorful as my favorite Indian restaurant in town.  Probably won't make the trip across town again anytime soon.

I love the stadium because there are so many fun events that take place here. I have been to USF football games, Bucs football games, monster jam, and the Tampa heartwalk. At the Tampa heart walk, you can meet a lot of people and walk on the field and see the cheerleaders when you come to the finish line. They also have concerts here.  One negative experience, when the Bucs game was on Sunday afternoon, there were no towels or sunscreen for sale and it was a disaster. I ordered a ham and turkey duo and they served it to me on a hotdog bun! This was appalling because all they had to say was that they didn't have bread and I would have ordered something different before it was too late! I have volunteered in the concession stand before and it has never been a problem to let customers know what we have on the menu. Plus, the concession was offering 50% off on all food and beverages that day. This is good promotion however, isn't it embarrassing when you are making promotions after there have been so many complaints on the radio?  Remember, parking is a minimum of $8 everywhere you look including by HCC and Sweetbay. If you arrive early, you might be able to find parking at Chili's I've heard.

I really wish this place wasn't as terrible as it is. I went there today for lunch with some co-workers since it's literally across the street from my job. Our waitress was nice enough, but didn't really listen too well and seemed a little confused by the world. I felt that she'd stop by while we were in the middle of a bite or a conversation and ask how things were. Now, I'm all for concerned service, but if you ask me if everything is okay and I tell you that I could use more water, and then you completely ignore me, it starts to seem like you don't really understand the job.  
We started off with Hot Mess fries, with black beans, chipotle cheese sauce and jalapenos. The fries were pretty awful, but the cheese sauce was decent. The jalapenos weren't fresh, which made me sad. 
I got a crabcake sandwich, which wasn't to my taste. They went a little heavy on the old bay or something, but it just wasn't pleasing to me. 
My coworkers ordered the ahi tuna salad which was beautifully presented, but the word on the street was that while it was pretty to look at, it was also pretty terrible.
I'm almost tempted to give them another star just because they have a cute outside bar area, but I wasn't there so I can't say whether it's worth it. I'd probably go back to grab a beer, but with all the decent food in Philly, it seems stupid to ever eat here again.

If only this place was closer to where I live and/or work.  

I have been missing banh mi for a long time, so I found this place on yelp.  I had to do a lot of street driving to get here, but I was thrilled to find this place.  The bread was crispy, the veggies were crunchy, and the boba (try the avocado smoothy -- not to sweet and super creamy) was fluffy.
