English might be an issue here.  I ordered the combination meat banh mi and received a BBQ pork banh mi, but it was still tasty.  I'll have to go back another time to try out their pate and headcheese.

$5 for a 6" sub and boba drink.  Sure, the sandwich is smaller than one gets in southern California, but the boba is a great price.

You cannot go wrong at one of Danny Scordato's restaurants and this one will not disappoint.  Their lunch time bento boxes are a great way to sample various items on the menu.  This is one of my go-to places for lunch meetings.

This place is always packed and that's because the Scordatos know how to do italian.  Even though Joe no longer owns it, the food remains the same.  Reservations are a must if you do not like long waits.  The wine and good are out of this world!

Get the scotch eggs, amazing. I finally tried them and I am kicking myself for waiting so long. If you are hungover, get two orders!

Food has always been good here. Definitely get a plus from me for having Harp on tap. Cozy little place. Usually try to get something different everytime I go there. Sheperds pie was huge amount of food and delicious. Cant go wrong with Fish and Chips here. 

But seriously, GET THE SCOTCH EGGS!! They sound funny, but they are delicious.

Bellini has your everyday, average Italian-style food that is catered to a modern crowd for BYO celebrations or just a casual dinner. On my Friday night visit, we were refused our reservation until our entire party arrived---this is a tip to be on time and being stuck in traffic is not an excuse as I learned! 

The atmosphere: most of the seats are squished together so every once in awhile I had my chair jolted, shifted or shimmied. Luckily, I didn't stab myself with my fork or anyone else for that matter. Best seats may be outside or window seating. The inside is nicely painted but if you do visit Bellini check out the creepy-looking baby on the ceiling. It was a conversation piece for a hot second at my table.

While we were waiting, bread with plain oil was served. The food was nothing to rave or complain about. I had a chicken with mushrooms off of a common modern-Italian style menu. My gal pals had similar dishes and all seemed to be okay with their food. Service was pretty good considering it was a packed house and it did come out fast.

Great apple fritters, sticky buns, and other equally delicious artery-clogging baked goods. 

For a special treat, make sure to get their HOT donuts that are baked right before your eyes that are served only during the Pennsylvania Dutch Festival in August and at the Harvest Celebration in October at the Market. They may take time off your life but are entirely worth it!

Goat cheese-cheesecake...nuff said.  Went here for the first time for restaurant week.  Started with the chilled tomato soup, which was excellent, and I'm not really a fan of tomato soup either.  For the next course, I went with pork , which was just okay.  However, I tried some of my friend's skate, which had me wishing I would've ordered the fish for once.  I didn't even know that goat cheese could be used to make cheesecake, but it was pretty much the best thing that happened to me all week.  If they sold this stuff by the slice at their market location, I'd devote a good portion of my paycheck to it.  

Great date spot, so once I get one, I'll definitely be back.

Maybe I just wasn't in the right frame of mind or something, but I wasn't really blown away. Everything was decent (the tortillas were great), and fairly priced, and the building itself was really nicely put together, but nothing grabbed me to make me see why this is one of the higher rated Mexican places in town.
Maybe I just got the wrong things - I may give it another shot when I hit the area again. As it is, I'd rather hit birrieria guadalajara, even though the tortillas there aren't really as good.

just checked out retrospect today and i'm definitely a fan. 

it has that perfect vintage store feel -- eclectic with tons of stuff, but organized well for searching with ease. really great high and low mix of items too. and the staff was nice! the only downside was the shoes aren't arranged by size. other than that, another must-stop vintage shop in philly.

correction, NO stars!!! VERY GOOD REASON....Read on....


2 weeks ago my pregnant friend ordered jello via takeout. she ordered it in a normal voice...there was no kind of altercation or anything on the phone. She was eating her jello.....

AFTER SHE HAD EATEN ONE JELLO CUBE...THE NEXT CUBE ON HER SPOON HAD 2 ROACHES IN IT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

DEAD SERIOUS. NOT JOKING!!!!!!!!!!


BEWARE!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!


nothing more to say about this classic Philadelphia Institution. I'm sooooooo done!!

We went here for dinner last week with another couple.  They eat here regularly and love it.

I have to say that we were not as enamored.  The place is nice enough, staff is friendly (although the bar is sloooow).

But the menu was very basic, and honestly, I think that Maui steak should not be on a menu where steaks are in the $30 range.  I'd expect to see Maui steak at an Outback.

We started with the fried calamari, which was good.  Then I had the NY strip with the NYO mac and cheese.  My husband had the prime rib and smashed potatoes.  Later on, he confided in me that his was the worse prime rib he'd ever had.  My steak was cooked perfectly, but it was a little sweet.  And the mac and cheese was too rich.  I could only eat about a quarter of the dinner.

We had the chocolate cake for dessert which was fine but not mind blowing.

Wine list was not spectacular but not bad.

Very nice set up.  Booths in back, tables in front.  
Efficient servers, good bartenders...GREAT MARGARITAS.
I really hate to say anymore, because this place is around the corner from my house, and I don't want anyone to know how great it is.
I was hoping to keep it a secret, but as you can see by everyone's review, they hit it out of the park with this one.

I just left this fabulous place and it is pretty awesome!  It is a coffee shop that also sells beer and wine, with a loft, in Downtown Tucson.  It does not get any better.

The food is great also! I had a #6 pressed cheese sandwich (fresh mozzerella, tomato, fresh basil) and a ginger seltzer.  The sandwich was crispy on the outside, hot and fresh on the inside.  The melted cheese was so good!  The seltzer was tasty and refreshing.  I could definitely do that combo again.

I would recommend this place to anyone, as the service is also very good.  I only wish the table were a little bigger.

The best Italian restaurant in Tucson, hands down.  The fish of the day should never be overlooked but the Osso Bucco is among the best I have ever had.  Do not bypass the cheese souffle as an appetizer, it's rich, creamy and delicious.  The antipasto platter is fresh, delicious and filled with quality meats and cheeses.  Seafood soup is a sleeper and more like mild Cioppino with a silky smooth tomato sauce served atop pasta.  Service is always prompt and efficient and  dining outside when weather permits is always enjoyable.  I seldom give 5 stars but this is my top rated restaurant in Tucson.

The yoga at the this place is solid, but it's a little more of a "workout" and less of a "yoga practice." I tend to the like a yoga practice that happens to be a good workout and not the other way around. However, that depends on individual preference. Also, due to the style of yoga, every class is almost exactly the same. I only go once a week or so - so it's fine. But I couldn't do unlimited classes here, because I would get too bored.

That said - I was skeptical at first about the heat, but it turns out -  I love it! The yoga itself is intense and the heat makes it more intense. You might find your slipping around your mat unless you buy a nice non-slip mat or a yoga towel to go over top (I would highly suggest that if you are going regularly). 

Over the summer it was crazy crowded, due to all of the deals, but it seems to have been a little better lately. They upped the amount of people that can make a reservation for class online, I think assuming that some won't show - but if they all do, it's super duper crowded. 

Once I am out of my groupon classes, I'm not sure that I will buy more or not. Certainly unlimited, due to lack of variation. However, I like the heated style enough that I might get over the other factors and buy a class pass anyway.
Great food! Delicious Pho with lots of fresh toppings. A friend and I split summer rolls and each got Pho and it was $8 each - very inexpensive and a healthy, flavorful lunch. I highly reccomend!

TERRIBLE service - decent martinis- TERRIBLE service. Will never ever go back there. My wife and I went there for our anniversary. We thought we would pay the extra money to have a super nice dinner with some good drinks. We were there for a good hour before anyone acknowledged us. We had to wait another hour to pay our bill. I'm not sure what happened, if the waitress and staff were having a 'bad day' or what, but it was an awful experience!! Maybe your experience will be different. I hope so.

I normally snub my nose at chain restaurants, but I have a weakness for Carrabba's. The food is not authentic Italian, but it is very good.  Maybe it's because this is the restaurant where I went on my first date with my now husband.  Maybe it's because they offer curbside pick up and I am a lazy cook.  Either way I know I can count on a good meal when I eat at Carrabba's.

My menu favorites are the Chicken Parmesan, Lasagna, and the Chicken Bryan.  If you love goat cheese you will LOVE the Chicken Bryan.  I don't think I have ever had dessert at Carrabbas... the portions are just too big for me to save room!

I wish I could give 4.5 stars.

We went there for my husband's birthday.  I told the restaurant (via opentable.com) that it was his birthday, and they gave us a fantastic table, wrote him a sweet birthday card, and made sure that a candle was on his dessert.

Decor and service were perfect.  Our waiter was attentive, the servers were prompt, and everything just flowed.  It was nice to be at a restaurant where we didn't have to yell to be heard, where we could have a nice conversation.

We both had the tasting menu with the wine pairing.  We also shared an order of the serrano ham appetizer.  Since my husband is allergic to coconut, he was allowed to order dessert off the normal menu (and the waiter very nicely made sure that no coconut products were used in any of the rest of the food).

The amuse was a yellow pepper soup with tasso.  It was lovely.

They then brought out the serrano ham.  It was fine.  I did like that they brought out the first course wine (a 2008 rose) so we had something to drink with our first course.

The scheduled first course was a grilled shrimp salad.  The shrimp was huge and so tender.  The salad (spinach, olives, hazelnuts and goat cheese) was delightful.

The second course was tomato-crusted scallops with a smoked coriander/white truffle sauce.  I thought the citrus flavors overpowered the dish.  The scallops were huge and lovely, but I did have get some sand.  This was served with an interesting Chardonnay.

Our main course was pork tenderloin with a cherry sauce and bbq pork belly with lentils.  The tenderloin was fine, but my husband loved the pork belly with lentils.  I've never seen him eat lentils before.  The flavor was spot-on.  This course was served with a very drinkable but not especially remarkable 2005 Cab.

For dessert, I got the coconut macadamia napoleon, which was on the normal tasting menu.  I love the filo, which stayed crispy to the last bite.  I thought the flavors were good.  The ice wine served with dessert complemented it perfectly.  My husband had the cheesecake, and he said it was awesome.

All in all, it was a great experience, although I didn't love some of the wines, and I found that the dishes were a little inconsistent.  But the service and the setting were awesome.  And for the price, I cannot complain.  We will come here again, but we might stick to a la carte ordering (at least of the wine).

Mediocrity at its finest and honestly this is embarassing to be one of our best spots as a Tampa food spot.

This is what I like to call very americanized cuban food and flavors. The gimmick here is they overserve portions and offer most likely canned black beans with sandwiches.

No gracias pa mi !

I've eaten here a few times, most recently last night.  Excellent meal...and the BYOB factor helps.  We had a party of 5, 3 of whom showed up 15 minutes late.  The first 2 were sat immediatly (and the place was packed).  A server walked through the place with a large pot of pasta giving each table a small serving to taste (it was yummy!)  Yes, the place is small and gets crowded and noisy.  However, the food and lovely decor detract from that.

I had the salmon and it was great.  One of our very hungry male guests ordered the seafood ravioli.  When it came with only a few on the plate he mentioned to the waiter he was much hungrier than this would satisty, they brought out 2 more ravioli for free.  Overall portion sizes are decent.  No, you're not getting Spagetti house enormous size portions...but is that really necessary anyway!??

Perfect go to Italian place to beat high prices and get a great dinner.
SUPER good indian food! Great ambiance, BYOB, excellent service...what more can I say? Oh yeah...that we paid $50 for FOUR people, of which 3 are guys?? can't beat it!

Zona 78 is a pretty good place.  It's not super authentic pizza or Italian food, but what they do tastes good. 

We've had multiple appetizers.  The mozzarella garlic bread is a HUGE appetizer, with plenty of fresh garlic and lots of cheese.  Very, very tasty.  The prosciutto, brie, and apple app was also very tasty, but a little bit on the smaller side.  Still a good first few bites for two people to share.  The white pizza is another HUGE app, with tons of flavor, and worth trying.  The cilantro hummus wasn't my cup of tea.  Best of all, though, is that they are all discounted on the happy hour menu!

The pizza is enough for two people to share.  We usually build our own with pepperoni, fontina, and caramelized onions.  The pepperoni is cut thin, and has just the right amount of spicy flavor.  It is higher quality than most pepperonis I've had on pizza or sandwiches.  The caramelized onions are just slightly sweet, and cooked til they are nice and soft and tender.  

The atmosphere is nice and a little upscale feeling.  The prices are reasonable.  The service can be hit or miss, but lately it's been pretty decent.  The parking lot can be a huge pain in the butt because it is pretty small.  Overall, a good restaurant and good dining experience.

Well, what a disappointment. I am huge fan of the River Rd. location. We decided to check out the new location last night......this is how is goes down: 
Atmosphere - top notch. Beautiful inside and the patio is nice, the bar opens to the deck, love it! Another bonus is that it is not near as loud inside. 

Food - shockingly enough - bad. The care and attention that is lavished on the food at the River location is not here. The burger toppings were scattered on the burger, not artistically placed, the condiments came with a skin on them as though they had been sitting there for days and my Buffalo Chicken sandwich was ddddrrrrryyyyyyyyyy and tasteless. 

Service - not near as rushed as the River location (which is nice) but the fellow we had was either new to table waiting or just slow...... 

The restaurant is hardly a new recipe for Fox restaurant success so as far as I am concerned, my experience was inexcusable. I must admit I am confused as to why they switched it over to Zin as they did the previous restaurant much better. My overpriced burger cravings will take me to the River location next time.....

DECENT.  I love when a new place opens within blocks of my house, I love it even more then the food is good and the drinks are cheap. 

The tacos are teeny tiny, but good.  I had the carnita and pez taco, I couldn't decide which I liked more.  
Lots of mexican beers that you can't find in the hood... CASH ONLY - which could be a bummer, but there are plenty of atms around and one in the bar.  

There is a pinball machine, and sexy half naked lady mexican comics plastered on the bathroom walls.

I dig it. Decent.

The decor is unexpected for that area of town, but I guess that's how they could afford to spend more on their decorations budget.
The food was good. The crab corn soup was a bit thick. The Hong Kong pan fried noodles were very good. The seafood tofu pot was also quite tasty. It comes with roasted garlic so that's always a plus.
The service was poor. She was nice and friendly, but she just wasn't attentive. She completely forgot our order of fried rice even though she asked specifically what type of meat. I'll be back but not late at night when they're more interested in the football game on tv and setting up for the next day's dim sum rush.

Well, I gave Kona Grill another chance for my birthday. I tried the Big Kahuna Burger and it was legendary. I ordered it medium-well and they nailed it. They fresh cut fries were excellent as always.

I did use my Konavore coupon that they sent out for my birthday. They give you $15 of an entree. This is a very good deal + it's free to sign up = totally worth it. No, you don't have to eat there ON your birthday. I think they give you a week before and a week after if I recall correctly.

I didn't hit the bar up this time, but I'm sure I'll be back in there sometime soon. The weather is getting better and the patio at Kona is awesome.

Love the new stadium! Well, it's not really "new" anymore. We love to come here to watch the Bucs play. The club level is great for football games. There's plenty of really good food stations and draft beers to choose from. That's what I love about the club level. There's plenty of indoor seating before the game to grab a good hearty lunch...not to mention NOT having to deal with all the riff raff in general. 
It's worth it to spend a little extra for your tickets!!

Another good meal here. The owner makes sure to say hi to us, and our waitress is always friendly (we usually get the same waitress).  The egg sandwich was cheesy and tasty on a fresh bun.  The omelet was good as well.  The biscuit was a standard biscuit, and the gravy was good.  All in all, I'm a fan.  I'm a bigger fan when they're not super busy, cause the wait staff and service suffers a little when they are.  This visit was pretty quiet.

I am very happy to live near Harkins.  The theater is very comfortable with Stadium seating and plenty of restrooms.  The visuals and sound are excellent on all screens.

Annually I purchase Loyalty T-Shirt for $25 and 2 Loyalty cups at $5/each for my family.  My wife and I purchase tickets separately, taking turns with the t-shirt (ethical?).  After tickets, we pay $2 ($1/each soda, 2 free popcorns) for goodies.  The value cannot be beat.

Note: The White Castle burgers are small, but delicious.  I try to keep it cheap, but many times I just cannot resist those damn little burgers.

2nd Note:  The sushi restaurant next door, Hana Tokyo, has a Dinner and a Movie For Two deal right now.  It includes an appetizer, 2 entrees, and a dessert with two movie passes to Harkins Theatre for only $48.99.

I've been to this spot for 2 concerts and I have to say that I like it quite a bit. The acoustics are pretty good and the surroundings are beautiful. They have good beer available but watch because the Yards runs out QUICK. For $11 you can only afford so many anyway so I guess that's alright. :)

I've done seats and the lawn and there isn't a terrible spot in the house -- the music reverberates throughout.

It is tiny so I would suggest getting your food to go.  I had the lamb platter, which came with fries, pita, hummus, and potato salad. The meat was nicely seasoned but pretty tough and the garlicky hummus was great on the pita. I personally liked the mustard flavored potato salad but you have to like mustard. Fries were pretty good. everything is prepared in front of you and made to order, which is rare and very much appreciated. Portions are pretty good and it came to $25 for 2 platters. Not bad!  Meze is still my go-to place for lamb but the prices here are maybe $2 cheaper  it was also pretty busy today and all tables inside and out were taken. A good, inexpensive option if you're on campus and want to grab a quick bite.

It's 1:53 AM, and my group of friends are craving pizza at this point. I go to my Yelp app, and Tower comes up within walking distance. Despite all the bad reviews on service and the quality of pizza, we were able to pay and eat several slices of pizza within thirty minutes of when we arrived. Not bad at all! Perhaps it helped that it wasn't as crowded, and we were able to grab a table. 

By the way, it's cash only, and they had several pizza-by-the-slice options available: Cheese, mushroom, sausage, broccoli, and cheesesteak.

One of the (many) things I love about Che's is the talented the bartenders. Sometimes my budget is a bit tight and it's fiscally responsible for me to drink the $1 Miller High Life draughts or PBR cans, but when there's a little extra cash in the kitty, I love that the bartenders are not put off (and many times are actually excited) when i want a delicious cocktail but are not sure exactly what.  They always have fresh fruit on hand (Donovan's summer watermelon vodka drinks made with agave nectar are to die for!) and I have never had a mediocre bloody mary (Sunday afternoon into night is my favorite time to visit Che's.)

Top that off with arguably the best jukebox in town, rolling office chairs to sit on, top notch people watching both inside and out the window, friendly conversation with anyone in the place (employed or not employed by Che's), a woman owner (I love that this town is full of female entrepreneurs!) and free snacks mix for the asking and you've got the best neighborhood watering hole!

Pretty crummy surroundings and store, which I overlooked for a long while for an amazing selection of Asian foods. Many choices. Staff not frendly at all but maybe they don't speak English. 

I've had reasonably priced canned Lychees and fresh ones too that were good. Their other produce is dubious or not fresh at all. Bought some mushrooms there recently that were packaged and on sale. My good deal stunk up my kitchen and were so bad I tossed them out. 

I'm done with this place. Lee Lee for me.

Beer and sports....great place to come watch! The assortment of draft beers is a plenty. I like this location because it didnt seem too busy even on game day, and I like that when I walk in, that I can still get a seat at one of the tables by the bar. I guess you would still need to come somewhat early though too. 
My girlfriends and I only had their Ultimate Bloody Mary's (spicy and GOOD) and appetizers. Wings and Hummus dip...both were really good. One of these days I will have to make it a point to come here to watch football and eat a regular meal. I "yelp" its good!

Fun vibe, yummy and cheap eats.

Bar service is quick and friendly, table service sometimes misses the mark if the dining room is crowded.

Still a little musty or fume-y smelling from the renovation.

Overall, a cool place to have on the block.

It took me way too long to go here considering it is ONE block from my house, and I walk by it every day. Finally made it, and I'm glad this spot is one block from home. We ate the nachos de kenzo, and it was a challenge to finish (but we did, of course). Beer seems slightly overpriced for this area (we're spoiled in Fishtown believing that Kenzinger should only be $3 a pint), but it's ONE block from my house. Perhaps not the first choice when going out, but the easy choice so close to home.

One word to describe the whole experience: awkward.

Why?

When we first met with Carl he tried to crack a bunch of jokes, none of which were funny, and it was weird because he was pressuring us to laugh. Also, when we explained that we only needed a few floral items for our wedding he made us feel badly that we were using artificial flowers as well and tried to convince us to go all-natural. (Note: all of our artificial arrangements/centerpieces/etc. turned out AWESOME. We were able to go into our venue and set everything up the night before - which really reduced our stress level.)

Anyway, when we were taken to design the bouquets we bounced from employee to another and we didn't have ONE point person helping us out. I found that to be stressful and like I didn't have the chance to develop a good working relationship with one person.

I do have to say that the final product/prototype was quite beautiful. However, the quote we were given for two bouquets and two boutonniers was over $600.00, which I found to be outrageous.

Needless to say, we went elsewhere.

I haven't had legit Vietnamese food like this since I left Atlanta! Ordered vermicelli with pork, shrimp, and egg rolls. DELICIOUS. I used to eat a lot of Vietnamese food when I was living in Atlanta, but since I don't know many authentic places here in Tampa, I haven't been able to. But now! :)

Update: This weekend I got the pumpkin French toast with pecan butter and it literally left me speechless. I was having a hard time deciding between that, the apple crumb French toast, and the Snickers pancakes, but I am confident I made the best choice. It is, afterall, the season of the pumpkin.

My friend got the garden omelette and I think it weighed around 7 pounds. It was *gigantic.*

I'm already thinking about what I'm going to get next time........

Laid back atmosphere offering up a unique twist on sandwiches makes this newer establishment a great option. 

Def try the bird and pig sandwich.

The place is clean and nicely decorated.  The food is authentic Hong-Kong style Chinese food.  It's as good as it gets for Florida.  To find better Hong Kong style Chinese food you'd probably have to go to Toronto or Vancouver or San Francisco.  I highly recommend it if you like this kind of food.

first time to maggiano's... i know what? hah. anyways. went with my partner.. had the lunch for 2 special $39.95 with salad.entree.dessert and also extra pasta to take home...and trust me there was lots of pasta i took home...

was told maggiano's salad was good but to me, it was just another salad.. unfortunately didnt put the dressing on the side.. b/c mine was drenched to the core.. was very sweet and salty at the same time. 

lunch portions were huge. only ate 1/4 of my entree.. rigatoni.. what turned me off was the quantity but otherwise the dish was good.. best part was had a $10 off $30 or more meal coupon...cost was $42.00.. with coupon.. 32.00.. they say leave a 15% but i usu give 20%.. not much service to give but im a nice guy..left a $10 tip.. prob made the servers day.hopefully...

my eating partner was considering holding a reception there but we'll see

Soho Sushi is one of my favorite sushi restaurants in Tampa!  The seafood is super fresh and the menu has plenty of creative sushi rolls on the menu.  If you are not a fan of sushi there are other cooked options (tempura, teriyaki, fried rice, etc.)

The parking lot is usually packed during the lunch and dinner rush so arrive early to find a parking spot.  I usually order my sushi to-go so I don't have to worry about finding a parking spot.

Some of my favorites on the menu are the Tuna Nachos, Koto Roll, Red Dragon Roll, Green Menace Roll, and the Lion King Roll.  The specialty rolls are a little expensive so I usually limit myself to one or two specialty rolls and then add a cheaper roll like the spicy tuna. 

Check out Soho Sushi's Happy Hour specials: 2 for 1 drinks and discounted sushi rolls!

I stop in every few weeks to stock up on pasta they cut to order and the amazing frozen raviolis and olives.  This tiny store is like a cattle chute where you can only go forward, and you can't go back to grab stuff so make sure you get everything you need from the start.

Mmm, the Bella Donna with cheese and extra hot sauce is super good, yo. I don't know how they get their magic meatballs to taste so... meaty ("magical," since it's actually tofu), but it certainly makes vegetarianism a teensy bit more appealing!

Eats! American Grill has a friendly neighborhood bar vibe.  Great black and white early American photos cover the walls as well as big screen tv's broadcasting sporting events.  The menu is American Classic dishes... perfect for the picky eater.  I usually go for an edgier menu, but really enjoyed the traditional dishes.

Our waitress was super; friendly and never left us waiting for refills or the check.  We went on a Monday and got the $5 burgers - great deal!  I got the Black and Blue Burger (blackened seasoning with blue cheese) and added bacon to the burger...yummy!  The seasoned fries that came with the burger were great, but I would have swapped them for a side salad had I known we would get complimentary homemade kettle chips.  

Will be back to try the Waldorf Salad!

I don't eat fast food burgers often as my waistline just can't afford it; however, every now and then I find myself craving these burgers. I don't have that reaction to any other fast food burger in town. That earns them Yay! I'm a fan. One of my favorite things about Culver's is that I can choose my cheese. I absolutely despise American Cheese. Of all the possible contributions to the cheese world, this one had to be tagged "American". No wonder the French dislike us. 

I have 2 go-to burgers: the Wisconsin Swiss Melt and the French Onion Butterburger. Both are great. For obvious reasons you want to eat the melt on site. I'll give you a quick tip - as soon as it hits your table, flip it over. That helps to keep the "down" side from getting too soft. Since the "up" side has already cooled it can handle becoming the down side. The French Onion is stacked with awesome grilled onions and Swiss cheese. The sides are okay. Cheese curds, while novel, do nothing for me. The fries are average. The onion rings are okay. 

The interior is a step above the typical fast-food restaurant interior which I appreciate. I do love to sit within view of the drink counter so I can watch people struggle with the lid dispenser. Hilarious. Some people just never figure it out.

My wife and I have eaten at Zinburger twice in the last few weeks and both times it has been outstanding.  The staff is friendly and the manager went out of his way to talk to us about the restaurant and their products.

The burgers are great and the Chinese Chicken Salad is the best one my wife has ever had.

Having grown up in Key West I know a thing or two about authentic Cuban fare and this place get's it right on every little detail.  The friendliest bilingual staff, the amazing hot and fresh menu items, and the bare bones pricing that lets get out the door for under 10 bucks every time.

This is a great little off-the-beaten-path neighborhood bar to get away from the madness of SoHo and Ybor.  The rotating beer selection has something for even the most fickle beer sommelier to enjoy. The adjacent cafe serves coffee and features the best grilled cheese panini you'll ever have. Also, outside of James Joyce in Ybor, this is one of the few places to still have steel tipped dart boards!

This is embarrassing...I've lived in the Tampa bay area my entire life and have never been here. 

It's fantastically unbelievable. The garbonzo bean soup and honey Cuban are well worth the trip.
Super cool, crowd is eclectic, a bit young, a bit punk, I liked it. I didn't get to try the milkshakes, but the food was great.

Had the hot donuts this morning. Appollonia and Vanilla Lavender. Hot and yummy. The flavors were perfect. Loved them both equally. Can't wait to go back and try more. This place is cute, the staff was friendly, service fast. I agree, tables outside at some point would be nice.

Thank god it is just far enough away from my home that I don't go there twice a day and spend all my time eating donuts. Hopefully I can try the chicken soon.

Philadelphia needs more places like this. Really good casual food that isn't pizza, hoagies or cheese steaks.

Get the nutella-tehina-pomegranate and coconut pineapple. Between those two, most of my favorite flavors were included. The nutella was definitely an interesting combo with the pomegranate but it almost tasted like a trail mix flavored donut: nutty yet sweet and refreshing. And the coconut?  Well you all know my obsession with all things piña colada.  If you like coconut cake, you will like this donut. It was very crowded but the donuts were worth the wait and trek. Will return to try the fried chicken.

Gotta just give it a meh. . . 

I SO wanted to love this place - it had so much potential!  Mexican food, close to home and a decent place for a drink in the 'hood.  But sadly we were a touch disappointed.  Its cozy with decent decor, but nothing to get super excited over.  

The menu is sparse, but the food we did have was good.  The menu is essentiallychips/salsa,  2 kinds of nachos (which needed a description on the menu - would be nice to know the difference between the two without having to ask), burgers, tacos, burritos and enchiladas, plus assorted sides.  

We had the chips and salsa, with a side of guac, to chomp on to start.  It not really salsa-salsa, its really pico de gio.  Which I actually like better but was not what we were expecting.  Hubby had the meat tacos and I had one each of the fish and the shrimp.  Mine were delish!  I like that you can order the tacos individually.  

They  have a decent drink menu with a good selection of beers, however I think for the location the beers could be priced a dollar cheaper.  Our drinks were ok.  Hubby had a plain margarita and I had a blood orange margarita.  I enjoyed mine, but my husband thought his was too heavy on the sour mix.  He ordered a shot of tequila to dump into his drink, and the shot cost as much as the drink itself.  

Our server was sweet - there was some confusion at the beginning of our meal - but she seemed a little "in the weeds".  However, she was nice and was really moving so I wont hold anything against her.  

And hit to the owner - you really should put it on the menu that you are CASH ONLY!  Long story, but I was planning on paying with our credit card and didn't think I had the debit card on me (there is an ATM in the place).  If I hadn't realized I did have it with us and we had eaten our dinner only to find out you were cash only when the bill came it would have sucked!  Oh and I didn't get a draft beer, but did I see that they are served in plastic glasses?  Please get glass-glasses, plastic just ruins the beer and I am no longer in college.

This was my 2nd time eating at EATS! They do American classics and they do them VERY well. I was there on a Sunday during prime football watching time. They have Sunday Ticket, so you can grab your out of market games here.

This little place on South Dale Mabry is a great place to grab a bite. No, it's not a chain and yes it's very good at what it does. The staff here is very in tune to what's going on too. They saw I needed some extra napkins since I walked up to the bar and grabbed a cocktail napkin - they rushed over some "real" napkins right away once they saw that. 

I ordered the Northwest Walnut Marinated Salmon. It came with a very generous house salad (which I ordered "rabbit style" - no dressing) and on side. I got their sweet potato fries and they were done perfectly. The salmon was on the small side, but very good and super tasty given the marinate. Also...the dozen walnuts on top were a nice bonus.

I'm looking forward to exploring more of their menu next time I'm here. I did see they have a 2lb burger challenge - for $24.99 you get a 2lb burger & 1 lb of fries. You finish it in less than 30 mins and you get that wiped off you bill and an EATS! T-shirt to wipe you chin with.

If you're in the neighborhood stop by and check them out. There's plenty of parking in the rear. One word of warning...one of the TVs in there has no BLUE color. It's really trippy watching TV that way. I thought the Detroit Lions were the Eagles in throwbacks for the first 10 mins I was there. Obviously that's no big deal...the food was excellent.

Landmark is a decent lunch/dinner spot...definitely the Drexel Law hangout. Quizzo night is a lot of fun and the drink specials are good. Food is higher quality than Applebees/Chilis, but it's a similar atmosphere in my mind. The veggie burger is tasty, and the quesadillas were pretty good too! I love the outdoor seating when the weather is nice. Good go-to place, especially if it's convenient! Oh, and props to them for donating food last year for a Drexel Law student organization to serve dinner to 100 people at the Ronald McDonald House!

This place is not a drive by. The Fried Oreo Martini was a great starter. Tried the Buffalo Wings and the Beef on Weck. Both were excellent and the next best thing to going home to Buffalo. I'll be bringing my wife back for the Beef on Weck as this is a favorite of hers. I would go out of my way to hit this place, it's worth it.

I bought a GrouponNow deal for this place and stupidly forgot to pay with it when I checked out!!! Ugh! Anyway, this deli was OK, but nothing fantastic. I got a blackened chicken sandwich (I believe it was freshly cooked chicken sliced fairly thin for sandwich meat) and the flavor was good. She cut it completely unevenly, which normally wouldn't matter, except for the fact that I was sharing with someone else. I didn't really want a side, so I wish they had the option to purchase a sandwich alone. The prices are OK, but I think there are better places out there. But I will definitely be back to try their flavored oatmeal!!

I can only speak for going to Sips at Thirteen, but I was always a huge fan. Their cosmos were delicious - plenty of alcohol, especially for $4 or $5 (can't remember what it was)!! My friend got the mussels one time when we went and she said they were good. My only complaint is one time I was leaving and realized I needed bread/baguette to go with dinner. I asked the server if they had any bread I could purchase. She told me all they had was a hoagie roll and that it'd be $5. Wtf!

Ok, I need help here. I've been here ~4 times now, and I've been completely unable to order anything other than what I ordered the first time, not through any fault of theirs, but because what I got was so damn good.

Let me break it down.

You order a carne seca burrito. You order horchata.

Consume your order.

You simultaneously lament and relish the fact that you've made that meal choice, because you're too full to eat anything else, but everything was so tasty.

The rest of the menu could be amazing (from yelp reviews it looks like the chili beef in particular), or it could be mediocre, but I don't know if I'll ever know!

Yum! Chabaa was delish! Started with the fresh spring rolls, which were mediocre at best - it was obvious they had been made before and stuck in the fridge (the rice noodles were hard and dry). But the meal only got better from there! The panang curry with tofu was amazing - great flavor and very creamy sauce. The chicken pad thai was delicious too - fresh flavors and the meat was cooked very well. I thought the food was very good, but I'm not sure it's worth the higher price ($17 for chicken pad thai is a bit steep). But the ambiance is nice and the food was very good!

This place is hit or miss for me. Sometimes I've gone and the food is killer, other times not so much. On one particularly disappointing visit, I had to send my dish back because it was so bland! That said, service is consistent, lots of menu options, and it's reasonably priced.

Update: they get another star for their lobster carbonara. Holy cow it's good!

If you come here, you must leave with a piece of cheesecake for later.  My absolute favorite is the keylime cheesecake.  Second favorite has to be the caramel turtle.  I recently tried the pumpkin pecan which was very good.  Note: the pecan pie portion is ice cold though.

In regards to the food, there is so much to choose from.  I like the orange chicken dish and most of the pasta dishes.  My husbands favorite is the four cheese pasta.  Service is very good.  I have never been disappointed.  There is always a wait for a table unless you are an early bird and eat at 5.  

I recommend NOT to eat outside unless you are a smoker.  I came here for dessert one night and we decided to sit outside because the weather was nice... But I was coughing and covering my mouth the entire time so if you do not smoke, don't seat outside.

It's creepy when stores or restaurants open that are exactly what you've been hoping for.  

Had 4 kinds, all were so good here's a poem:

Philly was in quite a rut
it lacked a place with good donuts
with with federal involved
this problem was solved
but now we'll have double-wide butts

Tonight was our first time to try Culver's out, and we will not be going back.  My partner picked up 2 double bacon deluxes, 2 fries and 2 of the chocolate custard/ice cream cups with crushed cookies in them.  For one, the burgers were average, kinda like the burgers at a Chili's.  The fries were crinkle cut and cold.  The ice cream was also just okay... The cookies in them were not oreos...they tasted like cheap cookies you'd buy at a dollar store.  I do not understand all the hype about this place.  In and Out is and always will be better.

There was a lot of hype surrounding Federal Donuts; the newspaper article, the twits every day stating how fast everything sold out. Finally give in to my curiosity and drove all the way from KOP to give it a try.

The store is at a residential neighborhood,  so street parking only.  I got there at 8am on a Saturday, found the parking couple blocks away.  There were already 15 or so people waiting in line.   The store is not big, most people are ordering take out, there are 3-4 seats inside, nothing fancy.  The ordering is pretty efficient; a young lady on the register, one other boxing the donuts; there are couple more working in the back. 

Hot donuts are prepared as you order; fancy ones are ready made. $1.25 for hot ones; $2 for fancy ($20 for a dozen).  They also have their own coffee.  I ordered Va n i l l a - L a v e n d e r(hot donuts); C o c o n u t - P i n e a p p l e;  N u t e l l a - Te h i n a - P o m e g r a n at e; O r a n ge   B l o s s o m - P i s t a c h i o and H o n ey - A l m o n d.  The donuts size are pretty small. about 2/3 the size of the Dunkin Donuts ones.

Definitely better than grocery store donuts or Dunkin donuts; I like the pomegranate ones the best.

Overall, it is a nice donuts shop. probably not worth driving an hour to go just for the donuts; but if you are in the area, definitely worth a try.

Best park in the city. Urban dwellers and big-city transplants alike will appreciate this space surrounded by Tampa's tallest buildings and a bustling riverfront. 

The grassy area is huge, the playground is full of smart playthings, and there's a delish cafe (with wine!) and dog park nearby.

At a donut shop where the focus is on innovative ingredients, it's surprising that my favorite was the simple honey almond. I sampled all nine donuts on the menu. After one bite of each, I had a sugar high for hours. The honey drenched donut was by far the best tasting of the bunch. A distant second was the hot and fresh Indian spiced donut. As far as I know, Philly doesn't have any real donut shops that serve them fresh out of the fryer. 

The demand for this place has made for long lines and early sell outs. If you manage to get there in time, I'd suggest ordering a couple of the honey almond donuts and a hot coffee, which is also delightfully rich and not at all acidic.

I keep looking at my previous reviews for the Bayou and think I need to say more.  The place is a solid bar, especially for Main Street.  No, you aren't going to stay here all night on a Friday or Saturday night, but it is more than happy to help get your evening started or to serve as a place to hang on a low key weeknight.

Monday and Tuesdays it has wing specials.  Wednesdays a great deal on a Roast Beef sandwich, and Thursdays a good deal on a Meatball sandwich.

Oh, and the $1 you call it Happy Hours on Fridays... I did say it was a great way to kick off the evening, didn't I?

Terrrrrible service.  Sat at the bar for 15 minutes while multiple bar tenders ignored us. One female bar tender sat in front of us counting tips while four of us sat there.  They were not slammed by any means. Finally my girlfriend sincerely asked if they were closing and the tip counting bar tender was a total bitch.  Too bad, the place looked cool and had good beers on tap.  We'll stay at Yeoman's Pub next time.

I really enjoyed the classes I took with Steve. He's a great instructor and gave a lot of options for people of all levels. He was very friendly and eager to help me improve. It's obvious that he really cares about his clients and his job. I love that you can sign up online for classes in advance, especially because they fill up. My only complaint is that they do pack people into the studio during peak times. However, I would highly recommend this place!

I love the Mexican food here. It has to be the best in Tucson.

My only concern is the prices are a tad high, but the amount of food you get is worth it!

Pho Quyen needs one of those crazy inflatable guys outside the restaurant, because the prices are incredible. Two entrees, four egg rolls, a Vietnamese coffee, and a slice of pie came out to less than $30. I could not finish my Ph tái Bò Viên (sliced beef and beef meatballs in noodle soup), because it was so filling. AND it looked like there was still enough left to feed all of Brad and Angelina's children (for those of you who don't keep up with such vital information, that's like 5 kids). It's getting three stars, because the food was just good. Nothing spectacular. However, if you're looking to dine out on a budget, this is your place.

It's Saturday afternoon. My wife and I have spent the morning doing chores and part of the afternoon doing errands. Time for happy hour! It's always assumed we are going to Zona 78.

In the previous review, I mention that the food has been hit or miss. The last few times we have been there it's been more of a hit.

We visited during restaurant week a while back. Their special was: A salad for each, a pizza to share, a dessert to share and a bottle of wine. The price for all this: $30... a great deal. We each had a Caesar salad, which was just OK. We split the Tuscany pizza, which I really enjoyed. The fennel on the pizza was a nice complement to the other salty, earthy ingredients. The wife did not care so much for the portobellos on it. She does not like mushrooms. I snagged the ones she extracted from her pizza. Dessert was obviously forgettable, because I can't remember what it was. As I mentioned, all of this was washed down with a bottle of wine.

They have beer (all their beers are high-end stuff), wine and  well drink specials to accompany 1/2 price appetizers during happy hour. Some samplings:

Beer: Squatters Hoprising Double IPA - high alcohol content, hoppy and slightly sweet... A beer-drinker's beer.

Garlic Bread with mozzarella cheese, served with a side of rustic sauce - this is our go-to item. For the $4.75 price tag during happy hour, you can make a meal out of it.

Cilantro Hummus - a recent discovery.  A generous portion, served with warm flatbread.. very nice. Happy hour price: $3.25

Calamari - not your typical calamari. Served in a creamy, spicy broth that is just drinkable. Unfortunately, the  hot broth almost assures it will be overcooked. Slivers of fennel, again, are a nice addition. It will set you back $4.25 during happy hour. 

Heather M. mentions that the menu does not change very often. This is true, although the beer selection seems to rotate. I do wish for more variety as in the food. We go here quite often. Changes in the menu would be nice, but if they ever remove the garlic bread from their appetizer menu or change their recipe for the rustic sauce, I may question my loyalty.

The hype is here but I don't get it. The honey almond is mediocre at best.
Over oily tasting and not sweet enough. Highly overpriced and small as well. Since when did donuts price out at $2 for a small one? WTF. 
Didn't taste the quality and just glad I didn't fall for the hype of the chicken. I'll grab a Frangelli's instead.

It is currently 11am. We just scarfed down half a dozen donuts.

Got in line to find out that we may have been too late, but we were the lucky two to get the last hot sugared donuts today. Vanilla Lavender for him and Apollonia Spice for me. Thumbs up!

We also picked up some fancy donuts while they were still available: Coconut Pineapple, Pomegranate Nutella Tehina, Orange Blossom Pistachio, and Chocolate Raspberry. They were all pretty good in their own right, but I think the hot sugared are much better (maybe because they are hot). The "fancy" donuts are definitely "fancy."

Coffee is okay, staff are very friendly. Quick service but beware of the line! The earlier you get there, the better your chances of getting donuts (sells out around 10-11am) and fried chicken (sells out before 1pm)!

We came, we saw the "sold out" sign, we came back, we saw the "sold out" sign again.  Why must the rest of Philadelphia come between me and my donut/fried chicken obsession?

The only good thing to result from this boomerang trip into Pennsport was a tasty sample my boyfriend and I scored when he ignored the "sold out" sign and went in anyway.  The staff gave us advice on planning future visits and offered a sample from some odd-and-ends pieces that couldn't be sold.  It was a crunchy-friend, dusted in Za'atar spice, and juicy.  It was even better drizzled with honey.  I can't wait to try the Korean friend chicken glazed varieties.

I will return for more chicken and a shot at those donuts.  

FYI, they serve donuts from 7:30-11:30 AM.  At noon, they turn over the oil to fry up the chicken (no more donuts after that).

Great location, lots of parking.... and this is where the positive notes end.

AMC Westshore prices are a total rip-off!  Evening prices are $10 per ticket and the matinee options are almost non-existant.  The concession snacks will run you about $10 for a small popcorn and soda and chances are that the popcorn is stale from mixing the old and new popcorn together...yuck.  They use to have a free movie watcher's program, but now you have to pay an annual fee to be in their frequent watchers' program.  For these prices I'd rather go to Cinebistro.

The bathrooms are normally dirty: clogged toilets, empty soap dispensors, and dirty sinks.  Broken seats and sticky floors are expected in the theaters.  At least they have stadium seating.

Not my first choice of movie theaters, but I do go occasionally if I am shopping at Westshore Mall.

I have been to Kona Grill's happy hour on multiple occasions and enjoyed the sushi, hamburger sliders, and cocktails. The sushi rolls are on the skimpy side, but my expectations were low since the rolls were only $3 on the happy hour menu.  The food isn't anything special at Kona, but since they have daily happy hours and the restaurant is stumbling distance from our apartment the restaurant is a convenient place to grab drinks. 

I had no complaints on the restaurant until we had dinner at Kona Grill.  Our waitress was super friendly and attentive, but our meal was a huge disappointment.  I have never had such a terrible tasting and overpriced dinner! The steaks were poor cuts and overcooked and the seafood was mediocre at best. We ordered the lobster macaroni... $12 for a side dish that was bland and boring and had hunks of flavorless lobster thrown on top of the dish. Dessert was ok, but forgettable and very expensive.

My recommendation: Check out the happy hour specials in the bar, but don't waste your money eating dinner here.

I went back to Saigon Deli because I really wanted a combination meat banh mi.  This time, they got my order right, and I am delighted to report that it was excellent.  They used the right amount of pate, and the headcheese was very good.  The veggies were crispy and fresh, and the bread was perfectly crusty.

The avocado smoothie boba this time was not as delectable (the tapioca balls were not as perfectly cooked), but that still made it excellent as opposed to sublime (my past visit).

I'm returning here the next time I want banh mi and boba and have the time/energy to make the drive out.

After a long time coming, we went to  sample the Hog for dinner. Ribs were okay but a bit dry but helped to have sauce. Temp of meats were cool-warmish under the heat lamps and could've benefited from a quick shot on the grill. Sauces was ok and wanted more smoke, tang, and less sweet. Sausage was my favorite but didn't try the brisket. Sides varied with the corn fritters being a hit. Fresh cut fries were good. Mac was good and apple cobbler was too sweet for me but everyone else liked it. Good but not great, but I can see how folks would want to come by for a quick meal and even a beer. The setup with counters, wood benches, and sauces is comfortable but can imaging it getting busy and cramped, too. Friendly staff. If the food moves up a notch, I'd consider coming back for more.

I stopped in after the last Hyde Park Music Series event. The place was packed and they had great live music going on out on the patio.

The burger I had was great and yes I would've ordered some fish & chips, but those aren't on the menu. The burger was great and the chips that came with it were excellent. They could've used some seasoning, but hey no problem.

Another thing that blew my mind...was a bar with IRISH in the name, only had one cider...and that cider - Woodchuck. Not Magner's...Not Strongbow...but Woodchuck. LAME. I mean it's a IRISH bar folks. Ok, at least they have one cider, but it kind of showed me where the owners are coming from.

The bathroom at this place is pretty nice. It was even kept nice on the super busy night I was there. The sink is actually really cool looking, but you do have to touch it to turn it on and off...probably not the best idea at a bar LOL.

If you want to watch a game...this place has more TV's per sq foot than anyplace I've seen in Tampa. Nice work there.

Will I come back...? I'm sure I'll be back. I'd give them another chance, plus it's a much needed place in Hyde Park.

Had the complete joy of tasting a sample of their food during Bloktoberfest! I adore Thai food, and was so excited to keep coming back for more. The chicken pad thai and spring rolls were delicious!!! Will have to check out their full menu.

I'm huge fan, and you'll see me coming back!

$50 for 2 burgers, sweet potato fries and truffle fries....worth it? Meh...not so much. Although the burgers WERE good (nice clean taste to them) I didn't think they were so amazing that I'd pay that much money on a consistent basis to have them.
I eat here a couple times a year and am never disappointed. The servers are friendly, the place is clean and the food is good.

We went this past Thursday and I had a blast!  We did have a front-of-the-line pass, which I would highly recommend.  The lines themselves for the houses looked very long and there is no way you can do them all if you have to wait.

The overall theming for the haunted houses was amazing!  You would think that you walked into a real morgue and/or haunted casino.  I have also done Universal Halloween Horror Nights, but Halloscrea wins out on best overall haunted houses.

It was also nice to be able to ride a few of the rollercoasters.  We didn't have much interest in seeing the shows, but from walking by, they didn't look half bad.  I would highly recommend a trip to Halloscream!

The brie-apple-walnut-fig sandwich was delicious!  They have a nice selection of beer and wine.  Our book club recently had a meeting there, everyone left happy and full.  Also, go for the kale chips- super delicious side dish.  Can't wait to go back.

When a place gets as much hype as this one, rarely does a plan to get exactly what you want workout so well.  The plan was to time things so that we could show up at 1130, get a donut, wait to order chicken and then stuff our faces sharing two half chickens.

We got the chocolate raspberry fancy donuts because they were the only ones left.  Definitely the best jelly donut I've ever had, but it was not the donut I would have ordered if I had my choice.  We then waited around 15 minutes, took our numbers and ordered.  Another 15 minutes later we each had a box of fried chicken; harissa and chile garlic.

The fried chicken is fantasticly crispy and not greasy at all.  The harissa was exactly as promised, spicy but not hot  and cooked perfectly.  The chile garlic was saucy and intense.  I was glad we opted to share because a full half chicken of chile garlic could overwork the palate.

I cannot wait to come back.

I'd heard stories about the douche-bag saturated parties with the fist pumping beefheads and the slutty college gals, but I came here with a group of guys just to see if I could disprove the nightlife myth.

There was no cover for our group, although they did offer open bar ($35 for two hours) or a pay as you go option. I'm not a huge drinker so the open bar option would have been a waste for me. One of the guys kept me plied with drinks on his open bar tab for the 3 hours I wasted there anyway.

The music here sucks; as in, if you have any sense of hearing whatsoever, you probably do not want to be here unless you're trashed and could care less. I think only 3 songs were danceable the entire 3 hour time span I was there.

The crowd here, for the Halloween party at least, was at least 90% college students with constant shoutouts to Alumni and other groups about every 10 minutes.

Needless to say, the stories are true. If it looks like a duck and sounds like a duck, it's a duck. And Mad River is definitely one underage trashed college party of a duck.

Meh, mostly bland food. Some of the portions are small too. I got a shrimp dish recently and it was a handful of shrimp in a tub of sauce, definitely not enough food to eat for dinner. Also, the last time I got food delivered they brought the wrong order. I was really rooting for this place but they fall short.

Many Italian restaurants in Tucson. This one is up near the top. Quiet, intimate place. Family run. Ample portions and reasonable prices. We split the stuffed mushrooms. Delish. Entrees were penne & sausage and grilled pork. The sausage was home made and delicious. Just a bit spicy. Excellent. The pork was done to a turn. This place will definately get a return visit.

I was craving wonton soup and some other favorites. My delivery arrived in 15 minutes! I was amazed and pleasantly surprised by the quality of food. Been struggling to find a good Chinese restaurant on this side of town, so I could not be happier I found this place.

This place smells! No really, it actually smells kinda bad. I guess I forgot it was a heated studio which means you get all kinds of nasty going on (thank god it wasn't flu season).

I went to try out a class, only to find that mat rental was $3. Dang. What happened to $1 mat rental? They don't even clean them themselves! Plus ... this is gross ... but the first couple mats I pulled out were wet.

At the first class of the day.

Dude, that's just gross.

This is the spot to come to if your looking to enjoy some bomb ass, Mexican food. I must say there is so much hype about El Guero Canelo, and this place totally blows it off the map, not only are the prices WAY better but the food is hands down GOOD! If you didn't catch my quick tip than you must, I repeat, you MUST have a cabeza taco. Now all you out there that just squirm on the inside at the thought of what exactly the cabeza is or which animal it comes from don't, let your taste buds do the thinking. The sonoran dogs at this place are...eh just another boring hotdog wrapped in bacon caked with crap that hides how fatty the bacon they chose really is. Whats important is the fact that this place has the best  caramels in town. They are huge if you get the large and for the 3.99 they charge for it.... WAY worth it!! The salsa bar they have there offers a special type of cottage cheese salsa that I totally think should be a staple at many Mexican places. 

The decor is pretty cool, they have plenty of big screens to enjoy the games on, and they play some pretty good tunes too! its not all that vintage mariachi either! They worked well with our 15 person party as well. 

My only gripe is they don't have chips that are a reasonable price. 3 bux for one lousy non refillable basket!!! The waiter's reason to but = "But they are good!"  Wow OK buddy whatever you say!

None the less I give this place a 5 star rating because its so far from my place that I don't get it enough and when I do.... I hurt when I leave! literally like side pains! lol

Times are tough and I hardly eat out much these days, but when I do I pick very wisely, and Vivace's is a very wise choice!
Their salads and pizza are to me what crack is to a dope fiend!! 
Since my last visit, there are bigger crowds. Looks like Tucson is finding this hidden gem quickly:) 
Good job Daniel!
I am in love. If you've never been to a 'burger boutique', you are missing out.  Sweet potato fries, marshmallow ketchup, malt ball shakes... good.times.

Came here for lunch, food was decent, the service was pretty good too.  I got a few different rolls, nothing really extraordinary.  The miso soup hit the spot.  I have a suspicion their drinks are a little watered down, though.  They take Discover, and don't seem to mind splitting checks and stuff.  Lots of seating options.

Royal Palace Thai is nearby my office so my coworkers and I order take-out from here frequently. I usually order the Green Curry with chicken. This dish is so fragrant and flavorful and contains green curry paste sautéed with coconut milk, bell peppers, vegetables and bamboo shoots. I also like the Red Curry, Pad Thai, and Basil Chicken. All of these dishes are so good that I keep ordering them over and over again! I most recently tried the Yum Beef Salad: very yummy!  The spicy chili dressing is amazing and makes the dish feel so much more exotic than salad.

I like that so many of the dishes at Royal Palace Thai are on the healthier side, but the meals are still flavorful from all of the herbs and spices the chef cooks with. The restaurant uses fresh herbs and produce from local farmers markets when possible. The house soup is made from scratch every morning and it shows; this is some of the best soup! 

The mother-daughter duo that run the restaurant are always really nice and friendly.  They must keep track of orders because they always know what I ordered last time.  The parking lot is very small so you may have to park across the street during the lunch or dinner rush.

Checked out this place waiting for guavaween!  What I liked the most. I love sitting outside in the fresh air. It was a pretty decent location.

Food was just okay. I try to rate the food/place based on if I could have made it at home.  

Hubby got chicken santorini  - chicken with lemon white wine sauce with mushrooms, red green peppers and pasta. Alright. Just not that much of an extraordinary flavor. He liked it. He's pretty easy to please:-) You may definitely like it too. It's pretty big so you can even split it. I feel like there's another place that does a comparable dish and I think it's to die for so you have had better.

I had mahi plaki - tomato basil sauce with feta, roasted veggies and orzo.
What made it not so great was I was kind of freaking out that the fish was not cooked as long but I seemed to be okay after the fact. The tomato sauce seemed to just come right out of the diced tomato can from the grocery store. The veggies were okay, orzo seemed boxed. The best part is the feta...so therefore, okee do kee, i'll move on from there.

The prices seemed higher than what it was worth too.

The coolest part was the waitresses singing and dancing and doing all those greek traditions. i think even dish breaking lol. 

The inside was pretty, the bathrooms were clean.  That's the story and I am sticking to it :)
Great customer service. Awesome coffee and granola bars. Thanks Megan for being so fair with my Living Social :)

The BBQ is pretty good at Jimbo's. The chopped pork sandwich platter with hush puppies is my favorite meal to order at Jimbo's. I can never decide on which sides I want because the coleslaw, baked beans, and french fries are all so good.  Their mac & cheese is not my favorite.  I have ordered it several times and it is always on the bland side.  I think if they used less milk in the cheese sauce it would come out cheesier.  I forgive Jimbo's because their hush puppies are so amazing!  

I recommend checking out their breakfast: you get a huge meal for $6!  Expect a laid-back atmosphere, tasty BBQ, and cheap prices at Jimbo's.

Laxmi's was good. The flavor of the dishes were good, but nothing to rave about. There is definitely better Indian, especially for the price. But it's a convenient location and the food was tasty! I will probably go back.
This the original Yummy House in a classy new restaurant. Still the best authentic Chinese meals in Tampa Bay.
With out a doubt this is the best steak house any where n USA!

Great location for students in University City (no need to take a cab into South Philly anymore - yay!), but I was really disappointed by their current French Toast special. Everything about it was just unappealing - the spiced ricotta and the apple, pear, raisin compote sounded good on paper but in reality, I would go as far to say it tasted kind of gross. It was just too sweet and the compote had a really odd texture. I've had their French Toast specials before and they're usually straight-up amazing.

Since my family and I lived in KC for 6 years we have been craving BBQ for a while now.  My husband, kids, and I came her over the weekend and we had decent service and the food was good.  However I wouldn't say this is 5 star experience but definitely good and we will most likely go back again.

We went to Ocean Prime with a party of about 12-14; we had the semi-private room next to the bar and I must say I forgot that we were in Tampa...it felt like we were in a chic bar in NYC or even Miami....but as one yelper put it...the parking lot is shared with Crate and Barrell. 
That being said, the drinks list was unique, the Cosmos are fun-they put small pieces of dry ice in so they "smoke"...this was very festive as we went sometime before Halloween.  
The lobster bisque (I think everyone ordered this) was quite interesting in delivery, it looked like a lobster "meatball" alone on the plate, then the creamy soup was poured over; luckily after all of that delivery it tasted great.  We also tried the ahi tuna as an appetizer, that was VERY Yummy and I think I will get that over the heavy bisque next time! 
For my meal I ordered the Chilean Sea Bass, it was quite exquisite and I enjoyed every bite! My date ordered the surf n turf special, his steak was cooked well and I ate his lobster, which melted in your mouth! 
For desert we ordered the peanut butter pie- it was WONDERFUL-very tasty and a perfect way to end the meal.
Afterwards, everyone went outside on the patio, we had cigars with us so the men were able to enjoy the cigars-there was great service also at the bar on the back patio.  I would def enjoy coming here again with a big group!

Let's just say I totally outdid myself at Texas de Brazil.  Upon arrival you are first tempted with a lobster bisque that is "buffet style"...a trick to get you to the table of many treats! Once there, I found that they had a cured ham selection, some mozzarella, some sundried tomatoes, pepercini, and other items that was hard to say to no to; we are there for the meat right??! 
Anyway, a plate later I realize my date has been sampling the tasty steak while I was having fun with all of the other non-steak options! 
The meat was nice and salty, tender, and I must admit fun to have people coming to your table with spears and knives...I only had the steak, I was not interested in the pork or chicken.  
We also had the flan for dessert, this was the consistency of a NY style cheesecake...but was AWESOME! We will definitely be going back!

Two stars because they have nifty stuff, but the rest of the place is a loss. The employees can be a little snarky, and it's one of the most overpriced stores in Center City. 

For most kitcheny things, walk a few more blocks to Macy's for better service and much cheaper prices.

I agree with the store employees moving in packs.  Worse, moving in packs and blocking the aisles with merchandise trolley(s).  Some of the staff (legal to drink) were very nice.

My big complaint is that there coupon policy is the pits.  I wanted to use 3 coupons to buy 5 items.  Two coupons were the same (i.e buy 2 get $1 off).  The registers, I was told, would not allow me to use more than one coupon that was the same at a single transaction.  That is so wrong. It even says, purchase on the coupon.  You purchase $1/2 that is one purchase.  If I buy them and start another order, that is another transaction, not a purchase.  Confusing, but they really need to be better informed.  

There deal was, you buy 5 of the same product, you get a $5 gift card.  If I buy things separately, then I do not get the $5.   The same thing happened when I wanted to buy 2 lotions.  I said thank you and walked out empty-handed.

I called corporate and they said the policy is at the behest of the management.   The management needs to re-think the 1 same coupon per transaction policy.  I did inform them that I would be going to another location/store where my business is appreciated.  

I am not looking to buy 50 bottles of soda or 100 boxes of pasta. Ridiculous.

We went to this cozy, neighborhood spot with limited seating for brunch. I was drawn to the Alesia Sandwich which is served on a lightly toasted baguette - I loved the flavors and freshness. Less exciting was fresh-squeezed orange juice, which was watered down in addition to being both smaller and more expensive than the fresh squeezed lemonade. I'll be back for another sandwich, but will skip the OJ.

I didn't think it'd happen, but I've finally found ANOTHER Mexican joint in my 'hood. While I still love Que Chula Es Puebla for my delicious taco shell tacos, Loco Pez wins in almost every other category. 

Location: .02 miles from my house? Awesome!

Alcohol: Full bar with happy hour specials? Count me in!

Ambiance: Bar, tables, booths, and overall awesome, clean, decor? Perfect!

Sauces: I love me some sauces. When a waitress brings you over a bucket of sauces and explains what each one is (sweet, smokey, spicy, etc.) you know you're in for a treat.

The only downside to this place is that they're cash only and they don't have hard shell tacos or a more expansive menu. That's why I gave them a 4-star rating...but really, it's more like 4.5 stars.

This is what I ate and drank on my recent visit:

-The "coupe de ville" margarita - basically a blood orange margarita. It was *delicious.* I could have had more than 1...and more than 3...

-A friend and I split the $4. happy hour nachos, which were HUGE! Sour cream, cheese, red onion, jalepenos, and refried beans...can't get better than that.

-I then got three tacos: pollo (chicken), al pastor (shredded pork with pineapple), and chorizo and potato. My favorite, believe it or not, was the chicken! It tasted like it was infused with sweet and savory juices. I just wanted to let the taco sit in my mouth, but I did end up chewing and swallowing. My second favorite was the chorizo and potato taco. While I don't think it tasted like a "taco" it did remind me of a meaty pierogie, which is not a bad thing. While I'm rating the al pastor last, it wasn't a bad taco. I just didn't think the meat was as flavorful as it could have been.

Also - our waitress was nice, and the bartender seemed nice as well from the greeting we got when we first walked in the joint.

I'll definitely be returning. Do me a favor and don't go, I don't want to have to fight for a seat!

Oh, and P.S. - yes, the tacos are smaller than what you're used to seeing, but seriously, half the nachos and three tacos filled me up. They're not THAT small.

Wow - love this place!

My hubby and I were looking for a pizza place in Tucson.  Being new to the area, we pulled up Yelp and started looking.  Even though there were great reviews, my expectations weren't very high.

We arrived to a very nice location.  Seating took about 15 minutes (not bad for a Saturday night) and we were brought drinks pretty quickly.

For an appetizer, we split the fresh mozzarella and tomatoes that had white balsamic reduction and garlic toast.  This was delicious.  While I suspect the mozzarella isn't locally made, it was very good.  The star of this dish was the garlic toast.  The bread was very light and toasted to perfection.  It was dressed with olive oil and had just the right amount of garlic.

While we waited quite a while for our pizza, it was worth the wait.  The mortadella, salami and pepperoni pizza was in a word, amazing!  It was cooked perfectly, the sauce, cheese and toppings were in perfect proportion.  Very, very good.  

We intend to make our way through the pizza menu since it all sounds so good!

I have been here several times with an organization I belong to. We have our meetings here. 

Awesome little dive. This is an olllllddddd neighborhood-y bar. The atmosphere is nothing to scream about, but I could totally see people getting down and dancing in this place, if given the right circumstances. They have a great selection of beer on tap (Hop Devil!). I only had the potato soup, which was delicious, but everyone I go with always indulges further and raves about the food, roast beef sandwiches are apparently #1. 

The staff is super friendly and they really make you feel at home. 

Cheers!

What helps this Chicago transplant get over the lack of snow in the Southwest and missing Lake Shore Drive?  I will say that Rocco's helps ease the homesickness more than a little.

Deep dish pies, some crappy Chicago beer, and a few sentimental posters make it feel a little more like the Windy City.  I'll admit that my picture of Chicago is a little tainted, I left pretty young and I'll always have an overly rosy picture of the place, but Rocco's does a pretty good job of recreating the pizza I remember.

Also, can I just comment that I love their billboard phrases?  They are adorably funny on top of being delicious.

I hate that the prices are as high as they are (I'm broke as a joke), but the lunch specials are a little easier on the wallet while still filling your belly with all the carbs you could possibly desire in one sitting.
Pros: Great hike with a great view and a great workout.  One of Tucson's treasures. Cons: The guys who *run* up it and make me feel like  a slow slug.

We've been twice and enjoyed it both times. The unique burger selections have been consistently tasty. Throw in some crispy sweet potato fries and life is definitely good! 

This is not a must-eat-here-at-all-costs destination but considering the recent burger craze, if you feel the need to have a burger, it's worth a try.

If you enjoy McDonalds, please stay away. Cheap, mass produced burgers are down the street. Creative tasty burgers are here.

We popped by for lunch with some close friends on Sunday and sat outside on a smaller balcony. The bearded male waiters spoke with authentic-sounding strong accents and wore kilts. Thankfully, nobody dropped anything so we didn't learn more kilt-wise.

I started with a perfect martini as we puzzled over an enormous selection of beer. Our waiter kindly brought numerous samples. My wife made the choice, Belhaven stout, and a fine choice it was! Smooth, chocolaty, rich, well-balanced, and refreshing!

The special brunch menu was available but we decided to stick with the traditional Irish classics, fish and chips, shepard's pie, etc. All hit the spot!

For lunch at a mall, this was satisfying.

Let me start by saying I'm a meat eater but in my book that is not the star of the show even though that's what this place is all about.  The buffet bar is probably one of the best and I didn't even get to try everything I wanted to because I knew I would spoil my "meats" course.  Now I wish I had just gotten the buffet bar only.  I started with the gorgonzola grape salad which was so delicious and loaded with lovely creamy gorgonzola, the smoked salmon with mustard sauce was so good I had to stop at Publix on the way home and tried to duplicate it for a party i had the next day.  The shrimp were good but not outstanding but one slurp of the lobster bisque and I was heaven sent.  There was also the standard salad bar fare but I avoided that and went for a slab of cheese with some sort of Brazilian hard sausage.. yummy.  Also on the buffet bar was black beans and rice and potatoes au gratin.  I did not try the black beans and rice because i tend to make that at home a lot but did try the potatoes au gratin which weren't your typical Betty Crocker variety and very tasty.   Once the meats started coming around the servers (there are multiple) came around with galic mashed potatoes (not outstanding) and bananas sauteed in butter, sugar and cinnamon.  No need for dessert after that.  The meats themselves were good, the pork loin however was a little tough but the top sirloin was delicious.  I had to stop taking meats very quickly though as I had filled up from the buffet bar.  This is definitely a "do-over" for me.

I haven't been to Shiroi in a very long time - their other restaurant Doma is closer to home and is now our go-to place - but Shiroi never disappoints me.

We are even planning a big party here in April.

Love the spicy tuna hand roll here, house-made tamago, ohitashi.

Sublime!  Fresh and yummy.

I'm gonna call Char's my Thai base line...meaning this is about the bare minimum I am willing to accept as far as the quality is concerned for my Thai Food.

I'd been once before for lunch, and I remember 3 of us each ordering a different curry...all of us ordering them spicy, and none of them having any spice whatsoever. The lunch combo plate was large and seemed like a decent deal.

Coming down from a powerful Thai High produced in the depths of Vail at Sa-Ing about a week ago, I was in need of a fix. Char's is the closest Thai restaurant to my house, and I decided I would try them again...this time for dinner take-out. I ordered Kang Karee and Pad Thai, both chicken, and picked it up without incident a short while later.

The Kang Karee had good flavor, and was actually spicy this time, but the consistency was way off. This was more of a Kang Karee soup than a curry.
