The texture was watery and there was oil floating on top. The flavor was great, but being so watery, the curry just washed off of the chicken and potatoes when you tried to eat them. This needs to be fixed to be called a proper curry.

The pad thai looked fantastic, but seriously lacked flavor. Pad thai tends to either be more sweet or more savory, and I suppose this errs to the latter, but was so bland it didnt really warrant putting much thought into what the flavor actually was. This time, not enough sauce...the noodles were dry, and the raw bean sprouts which should add an earthy crunchy quality to good Pad Thai just made this one taste like someone put dirt in the recipe. Earthy, I suppose, but not in the right way.

I guess I need to either bite the bullet and head down to Vail every time I want some Thai or hit up Karuna's more often or something...but I think its going to be a while before I give Char's another go around.
I love Halloween.  Busch Gardens doesn't decorate like Universal Studios does but it was fun.  I'd go again.
Best beer and atmosphere in tampa. This is where real people hang out.
Chicken special is the best bang for your buck meal in Tampa bay...I gotta go back there for a Visit!

1.  Super glad they have vegan options.  The mushroom taco was really good, but the soy chorizo tasted kind of weird.  I liked the waffle fries, and the guac was tasty.
2.  The vibe is nice.  Music isn't too loud, comfy decor, and a good blend of lots of different types of patrons.  
3.  The thing I loved the most was the chipotle infused tequila with hisbiscus water drink thingy.  Once finals are over, I'm celebrating by drinking 3 of those I think.

There are so many things about this place that just don't work for me. If you've ever been to Italy, you will certainly feel the same way. Where to start?

They do serve enormous quantities of average food, with the intent of stuffing you to the gills, since it's basically all-you-can-eat. Apparently, the idea is to go there, order ridiculous quantities, get the leftovers to go, and you are set for the week! Quantity over quality. 

The dishes are American Italian, or Maggiano's little Italy Italian. They are certainly not Italy Italian. Do not, and I repeat not, take an Italian here. They will lose all respect for you, forever. As will I.

No. Just no, no, no.

Ok so I am sad to say that after years of knowing about this restaurant and passing it on several occasions, this was my first visit. I was starved and had a coupon which I had received in the email. The addition of the wi-fi availability made it worth the shot.

I must say that I was not disappointed. The menu is small but varied. I will advise you that the dinner prices are a bit higher than a casual dining establishment which I consider this restaurant.( Entrees avg. $23-26). They have an extensive drink and wine menu for those seeking that. And lastly for the green folks out there this restaurant touts is support of local proprietors  and  use of sustainable ingredients. 

After much contemplation I ordered the scallops with pappardelle in a mushroom, tomato, goat cheese sauce.  It was really good but the portion was not suitable for an entree to me. After eating every bite, I was still hungry! I thought it seemed a bit small for a $26 entree but my stomach agreed. So I learned a powerful lesson about dining at this place, DO NOT COME HUNGRY! (Or order an appetizer first!)

But overall I am very likely to try this restaurant again .... on a pay day when I have eaten both a large breakfast and lunch.

Nice healthy vegetarian fare in a trendy but friendly ambience. The sandwiches are mostly just fancy grilled cheese, but the bread and the cheese are high quality. The kale and quinoa salad was delicious. I thought the "blue bottle" coffee was overpriced and overrated compared to Cartel or Aqui, but I'm a coffee snob.
The crab cakes are delicious!  Great service.  Good location too. Give this place a try!

The old Chico's gang is back!  These are the same owners that opened the original Chico's that was a local favorite in the Westchase area.  This is a place where service is friendly, the vibe is fun and the food is great!
   And about the food....
   Starters: FREE, that's right FREE chips and homemade salsa.  This is not your grocery store in a jar salsa!  You will know it is fresh as soon as you take a bite.  This is the salsa that made Chico's so popular.  Oh yeah, and don't be surprised when you come back that it will taste a bit different as it is made fresh and depending on who makes it.  The core taste will remain constant, but you may notice a surprising and welcomed difference!  
  Appetizer:  An expanded, diverse and interesting array of appetizers.  We got the Buffalo Chicken Egg Rolls.  These are you big size egg rolls, not the little spring rolls you might expect.  They were stuffed with more chicken than I you would think and you will be surprised at how tasty they are.  A bit spicy, but their signature Cilantro Ranch dressing used as a dipping sauce really cools the spice down.  You get 2 egg rolls cut in half - plenty enough to share!
  I got the Sunburned Steak Burrito and it was very big, more than enough to eat and very flavorful.  The Chimchurri sauce really makes the burrito special.  The steak inside was very large and probably could have been more bit sized.  I got the burrito "Shack Style" which meant they covered it in Salsa and then melted cheese over that.  My wife loved it like that.  I wasn't as thrilled, glad I tried it that way as was suggested by the server, but doubt I will get it "shack style" again.  The only real downside to the plate was the side dish.  It was Shack Slaw and it was very small, maybe 2 forks full and had too much vinegar or lime, it was very overpowering and didn't compliment the burrito very well.  Next time I will add a different side dish for an extra .99 cent.
  My wife got the Mongolian Beef Bowl.  It was very flavorful. loaded with red peppers and bites of broccoli.  Surprisingly, it was over noodles and not rice.  My wife loved it.  I tried it and found it unique in taste and not what I expected.  It was flavorful and very filling.
   We will definitely be back on a weekly basis for sure.  Everything we saw going by to other tables looked so good, cant wait to try everything!

I guess being the only Chinese bakery in the area gives this place a four-star. Coming  from NY, I do not understand how this place is surviving.  Oh wait I do!  Like I said...being the only Chinese bakery in tampa.    

They do not carry too much varieties and often time they do not carry their limited full menu.  The taste is mediocre too.  You might think it is cheap, however Chinese bakeries are always cheap.  Even the ones in NYC sell their better tasting bread for around $1.

I wish I could rate this place higher. My quibble is simple; overpriced, average food. No sandwich on the menu is under $9 and most are over $10. A little bit much for pub food, especially at lunch. 

I am sure there are some winning choices on the menu, but what I have had has not blown me away. On different visits I have had:

The Reuben - standard pub fare but nothing that makes it stand out.
The Fishwich - a bland piece of fish on a bun with so- so tartar sauce.
Irish Nachos - Waffle fries with cheese and bacon, served with ranch dressing. These were what I enjoyed the most and, actually, tried to (semi-sucessfully) recreate at home.

All that said, they have a great selection of beers and lots of TV's for your sports viewing enjoyment. Maybe I will have to take a cue from Heather and try the Pub Footballs or just keep trying more of the menu looking for something to change my mind.

A new favorite lunch spot of mine. Other Yelpers and the venue's own web site touted the Awesomeness of the grilled cheese. But nowadays, so many places make this claim. Is this grilled cheese all it's cracked up to be? The answer is yes. Anjou pears are sauteed with honey and Chinese five spice poowder, then pressed bewteen two slices of rye, with -- wait for it...gooey Gruyere cheese. (Be still, my beating heart.) It's the ultimate salty/sweet combo and it's served with a side salad topped with tomatoes and crumbled bleu.  We also shared the hummus appetizer, which was seasoned nicely, served with grilled pita, and certainly plentiful.  The homemade veggie burger also looks a-ma-zing... I will be back -- as the "cafe" name implies, this place also serves coffee; next time I visit, I want to grab an espresso to go on the way back to the office.

I really freaking love this place. It is seriously one of my favorite places to get mexican food, namely carne asada (top sirloin) in Tucson. They have home made tortillas which are wonderful. You can buy meals for 2, 4 or buy the meat by the pound. Their quesadillas are authentic, filled with meat and cheese...I get tired of the typical tons of cheese and little meat quesadillas found around town. They have an incredible salsa and veggie bar. Their guacamole salsa is incredibly tasty. They also have some great tortilla chips sitting out for your pleasure. Their charro beans are great. I don't like rice or refried beans much so I can't really speak to theirs.

They also have this crazy, modern soda machine. It's touch screen with a potential 150 combinations of water and soda. There are so many different flavors that there is definately something for everyone. 

They are moderately priced, a little bit more expensive than similar places in town. However, for the quality and freshness, they are well worth the price.
super service, high end food.  a little pricey- but excellent for a special occasion.

Just went there for lunch as I was at the library next door. This was my first pho, so I was unsure as to what I would get.

I had the pho with "rare beef," no tendons or tripe for me. The beef was not rare, but cooked and that was OK with me. It was sliced thin and was tender and tasty. I really enjoyed the noodles. There was some kind of greens in the soup and I think it may have been some sort of mint or lemon grass or something along with sliced onions. The broth was very tasty. The only thing I found that was not quite good for me was the oil in the soup from frying the beef. But that was not a big deal. The noodles were the flat, thin kind. Really good. It comes with a plate of bean sprouts, mint, and a couple of slices of jalapeno.

The restaurant itself is clean, pleasant, and modern. Very nice.

I will go again sometime. They also have Chinese food on their menu.

I walked out paying $10 with a tip. The pho itself with tax was $7.40-ish. So this is not a cheap lunch. But I feel you get what you pay for.

We often ride our bikes down here with kids in tow to play in the water fountain or at the playground. Great spot to bring a picnic or even being back some food from downtown eateries. And the Realm mobile sport equipment rental is awesome! I'm surprised not more people come downtown.

It always helps to have reservations since it gets busy. We always start at the bar before getting seated at our table with an old fashion PineFin without the simple syrup and pineapple juice. 

We love the mussels and need bread to sop up the broth. Yum. 

The lobster bisque was good but Capital Grille has it better with more lobster and a nice sherry finish. 

The oysters were fresh but whoever was shucking did a poor job last night, since a few looked pretty mangled. I usually like Blue Point but they didn't have much flavor. 

The seared tuna was fresh but the crostini tasted stale without a crunch. 

The spinach salad worked better with Granny Smith apples to cut the overly citrus sweet vinaigrette. I usually like bacon but it didn't work for me with the dressing, so it was disappointing. 

Service seemed rushed and though attentive, it wasn't like the many times before. We have had some of the best experiences there but have had some hit and miss dining experiences, too. 

One of my favorite dish is the Shanghai sea bass. 

Finishing off with a creme brullee and key lime pie made it complete. Key lime had the right balance of sweet and tart, and the crust added the crunch.

Exquisite supper. Saw some negative reviews and was not expecting the quality we received. Everything was excellent, and I found a new wine for my list. The staff was great, including the cheese person (fromageilier?). 

We had the Rabbit Terrine and Butternut squash tortellini for appetizers, and the duck and the filet for entrees. All were superb. The cheese was cheddar, but from Scotland where the cows are fed whiskey mash as part of their diet. 

A little on the pricey side, but well worth it!
Excellent and interesting Thai food in a comfortable space and easy location.
Food is bland, place is too small and food is too expensive.
Too small, bad food and very overpriced, won't go back.

One of my favorite places in Tucson.  I get food to go from here at least once a week.  Wonderful sesame chicken, tasty fried rice, and AWESOME lo mein.  I highly reccommend the food to anyone and everyone.  

The place is a little dingy and rundown inside so I rarely eat in the restraunt.

Price is right and they have generous portions.

We decided to give Gavi another go, and what a mistake that was.  I'm going to keep this brief, because I'm still annoyed at wasting time with Gavi again.

The food stinks.  Basic tomato sauce with crunchy onions in it.  No depth of flavor.   They consistently serve brown lettuce in their salads.  It's actually quite disgusting.  Mushy pasta.  No flavor to the chicken in the chicken parm.  The list goes on and on.

The service is sub par.  I had to warm up my ice to get water.  My dish came out wrong (because their new menu stinks, see below), and it took 10 minutes or more to see our waitress.  Unacceptable.  This is consistently a problem.  The waitress we had on our last visit was actually slightly pissy about the food coming out wrong.  She could have been nice about explaining the menu changes.  Instead, she made sure to say, multiple times,  "You need to make sure to remember that the menu is changed."  Um, ok.  Got it, thanks.

The atmosphere is cramped and stuffy.

They raised their prices.  $18 for just some pasta and mozzarella?  Seriously?  They also changed their menu, but left some of the same menu items/descriptions on the menu, so you would never know you're getting a different dish.  Uh, ok.  It also looks like a diner menu now.  Why would I pay $30 for some dishes when I'm eating on construction paper and choosing my food off of a diner menu?

I guess I like their parking lot.  Is that a plus for any place though?

Anyway, sorry, Gavi.  We used to go to the Broadway location 2-4 times a month.  Then to the Kolb/Sunrise location 2 times a month.  Now, we're done.  No more.

The food was terrific, but only one wait staff was there on a Sunday afternoon at 3:30-4:00 ish. The pork tacos of destiny were very tasty, but were served to me cold (due to not enough wait staff) and they were very tiny, more an appetizer portion than "dinner" or "lunch." I would definitely go again if in the area, but wouldn't drive across town for it. Nice atmosphere!

I'm not really a fan of Olive Garden, but not bad for Olive Garden at this location.  Food was tasty, service fast, and reasonable prices. Not exactly authentic Italian food, but tasted good and nice atmosphere.

I have been eating here for many years and the food is consistently excellent. It is a favorite restaurant of my parents', too. Reasonable prices. Amazingly delicious food. Good service. Highly recommend.

Finally got to Frankie's with the family. Kids had a cheeseburger and chicken fingers with fries. I personally sampled the Chicken - really good chicken. Turns out to be Red Bird chicken, organic. Both kids gave Frankie's 5-stars. Nancy had a Phili Cheese steak and loved it, hard for me to love this style as I much prefer the Chicago version.
I had the Alaskan Cod fish and chips. When I got mine I seriously though of vandekamp's fish sticks. I was surprised though, actual fish steak properly breaded and really fresh tasting.
We met Frankie, super nice guy. I asked him about the fish and he went into a nice story about how many he had to go through to get the quality he as looking for, and I appreciate that. They were fantastic, I just wish there were twice as many.

I don't know where to begin! I ordered my filet well done. It came so tender and juicy, you Wouldent think it was well done. The waiter had a great sense of humor (matt) was his name. He was on top of his service. Here the second my coke got low and was very educated on all of the menu items, desserts, drinks and entrees. The atmosphere was kind of loud only because there were 4 different birthdays going on tonight. I'm actually sitting at the table as we speak. Digesting the best meal I have ever had. The steak had the perfect flavor. And for a side dish, get the scalloped potatoes. They are to die for.
Great. Better coffee than most coffee. And better candor than most busy thoroughfare cafes. Hooray for that.

Let me say first that I am a super-fan of Zona 78.  I love the River location and have eaten there many times a month for several years.  When I was pregnant, I craved their hummus!  I've always thought it was a little over-priced, but their food is great and I've always had decent to excellent service.

So when moving to the east side recently, I was thrilled there was another location close to me.  So far I've eaten there three times, and all three times have been just ok.  

The first time our dinner took forever to come out (we have a 1 year old, and keeping him still long enough for food to come out is always a challenge).  Our waiter apologized and explained the kitchen was running slow that night.  But the food was still good.

The second time it once again took our food forever to come out.  That time, the waitress gave us a coupon for a free appetizer as an apology.  Again the food was good, but I was starting to see a trend...

Just yesterday I went there for a third time.  It was just me and my son around lunchtime, however the restaurant was very slow.  I ordered the hummus appetizer for my lunch, and a kid's meal for my son... both should have been a quick prep, especially since there were so few people there.

After about 10-15 minutes (with no drink refills and no waitress in sight), I flagged a hostess down.  I asked her about how much longer it would be, and she apologized and brought us some (very tasty) bread with oil and balsamic.  Finally, a few minutes later our waitress popped up--she apologized and said she had thrown away the paper with our food order on it (meaning, it hadn't even been put into the system yet!).  She wasn't really apologetic, just kind of sped off.

From time of (first) order to time served, it was 35-40 minutes.  Thank goodness I had the day off of work, because if I was looking for something on my business hour I would have been screwed!  My $3 iced tea was only refilled once, and that was just before I left.  

Zona, I do love your food... but you've got some work to do in the service and timeliness departments!

The food at Charley's was great with the exception of our steaks. 

My sister and I ordered the Filet Mignon. We were disappointed to find that there was a large amount of far on our steaks. This much fat would be expected on a ribeye, but not filet mignon. I ordered my steak medium rare, but the steak I received was burnt on the outside and practically well done in the middle with just a hint of pink in the very center of the steak. My sister's steak was overcooked as well.

The table next to us returned their steaks and we probably should have done the same. Life is too short to eat overcooked steak!

I enjoyed the tuna tar-tar (not on the menu, but they made it for us per my husband's request). I also enjoyed my salad, my Washington Apple martini and my side items, but I unfortunately the steak was terrible. 

Besides my burnt steak the service was disappointingly slow. We went on a Monday night and it took almost two and a half hours from the time we arrived to the time we left. Our waitress disappeared with our credit cards for 20 minutes, only to return to our table and ask us again how we were splitting the check.

Maybe I came on an off night, but Charley's was a huge let down. There are too many great steak restaurants in Tampa to suffer through another burnt steak at Charley's.

Soft Opening and still nearly a top score.  Dan Dan noodles, um.

Apparently, there is a grand opening in the works (2 to 3 months after some build out) for this BYOB on Main Street.  I can't wait to check back in!

Another place that J (my 12 year old grandson - that's him in the picture) will agree to go to. I usually get the hot wings. Not bad. Service is usually pretty good. This location doesn't have a full bar but that's OK for us. It's convenient to home and doesn't feel as crowded or dark as the one on North Dale Mabry.

American, Chinese, Swedish, I really don't care, this is the kind of taste that I'm looking for in Chinese food. I have not been to China nor have I lived among the Chinese, so for what I know it could be totally American. Nevertheless, today this was good food. The kung pow chicken was not swimming in the brown sauce, the orange chicken was not battered beyond recognition. The lunch special gave the option of rice, fried rice or lo mien.

The service was fast, the food came out fast. It was pleasant.

I may never adjust to a restaurant being in a strip mall next to a nail place, but that's for me to work out. The decor was not something from an Elizabeth Taylor movie, but I've seen worse. The floor and the tables were clean, everything that I saw was clean--didn't visit the bathrooms.

We will return. You can bet your sweet bippy that I will be reporting back if there is a difference.

Oh, and the egg rolls are more like lumpia, because of the wrappers, I like that.

This place has potential.  But it's not being actualized. 

I was excited to see a Mexican joint open up in my neighborhood (I hadn't yet eaten at the truck)-- being from Southern California, I miss good Mexican.  This is OK Mexican.  And far overpriced!  Eight dollars for a burrito is outrageous.  Is it entirely locally, organically sourced?  If no, don't charge eight dollars.  If it is, at least mention it, it will cause less resentment.  Six bucks for a pair of tacos?!  Not worth it.  I tried the pork recently and it was ok, but no mention of what style-- (adobado would have been nice); there are no carntias on the menu either.  Not cool.  The breakfast tacos were decent, but nothing you can't make at home rather easily.   The pico de gallo is decent, the guac aint bad, but overall-- OVERPRICED (and really, what are you doing to that meat?  There are traditional seasonings! Marinades!)

Tips to owners:  for those prices, at least provide a salsa bar and free chips.  It's pretty common, though not in Philly apparently.  Some house made salsa verde, a hot one, and the pico would be fine; have some pickled jalapenos and carrots too, maybe some radishes and some lime wedges available-- that's a real salsa bar.  

And get Tapatio.  Valentina is a cop out.

Maybe start marinading your meat too.   

Also, provide a cheaper taco sans cheese and guac-- all we really need are some diced onions and cilantro on top, a squirt of lime juice maybe too.  Sell the stripped down tacos at a cheaper price.

Just tried the new location for the first time and it was definitely as delicious as the others! This time I got the veggie burger with Parmesan fries. The veggie burger was large, filling, and tasty, but they definitely skimped on my sauteed mushrooms and onions. The fries were decent, but nothing to write home about. I thought I remember the Parmesan fries being something other than regular fries with some cheese sprinkled on top. My dining partner got a short stack of banana pancakes, which she loved. The service was very good - our server was prompt in refilling coffee and water and checked on us a few times without being intrusive. The location is great and the building is beautiful. I will definitely be back!

Great atmosphere but the food is nothing special and the service stunk last time we went here. By request my buddy wanted to sit by water so reluctantly I gave it another try. FAIL! For Sunday brunch we were one of two or three tables there. We waited 20 mins for our mimosas to arrive after waiting 10 mins or so for a server to come out to our table. He actually went inside to get his own drink. Ok so the food is never bad here and the place is nice and perfect location but get your act together.
How can you go wrong.  The food is outstanding with generous portions.  If you are not sure what it is on the menu just ask.  Friendly staff works hard to make sure you have a great meal.

My "dining trio" group (that's husband Gary, mom and I) hit Firebirds on a busy night. I had exchanged a gift I got at Tiffany's, so I was feeling all pampered and such, ha ha!

We lucked out and didn't have to wait, getting a table near the cooking area. The restaurant has a nice ambiance, very open, with two levels, and this big ol' stuffed deerhead staring down at the diners. The ambiance reminds me of a northern hunting area feeling, something with snow outside. It was quite noisy on this night, but we still were able to chit chat just fine.

We had a friendly and very capable waiter (Eddy-or Edmund on the receipt), who accommodated my mom's concerns for too much spice by bringing her a generous sample of the lobster spinach queso (dip served with chips), $10.50.  It passed the "spice" test for her gringa tastebuds, & it was yummy. (I think they have a discount on appetizers in certain locations during happy hour.)

The food took a bit to come but we were happy enough, since we filled up a bit on the appetizer. 

Mom settled on the Colorado Chicken Salad. She enjoyed it. I got the Grilled Shrimp and Strawberry salad, with mixed greens, grilled shrimp, goat cheese, jicama, sugar roasted pecans, strawberries and balsamic vinaigrette ($14.50). It was surprisingly superb, a generous portion and I made lunch out of it the next day too. Gary dined on the Sesame Encrusted Salmon ($14.50), which came with a side or two. He got a huge, coarse-salted, baked potato. I tasted the fish, which had a fabulous texture and taste. Really nicely prepared, and a good sized portion, also (hey, those things matter!). He also had leftovers for lunch the next day.

The meal came with a freshly baked mini bread loaf, very soft and tasty. We didn't "do" dessert, so no idea about 'em. But I did see the choices, from creme brulee cheesecake to carrot cake and chocolate brownie with caramel sauce and other naughty delights. We really had a positive experience there!

The bright colors of Honest Tom's Taco Shop pop from the grays, browns, bricks and stones that make up the rest of the landscape of 44th Street in West Philly. Inside, a lone table and some counter seating are available, but I wouldn't bet on free seats given the modest size of what will undoubtedly become a popular breakfast, lunch and dinner destination. 

Since I had only tried Tom's breakfast tacos on one other occasion (and loved them), I decided to check out a chicken burrito on my first of what will probably be many visits. We beat a small rush by mere seconds and waited patiently at the counter for our food, which was up pretty quickly. My burrito was basic and yet really satisfying. There was more than enough chicken, plus rice, black beans, cheddar and guacamole. I would have dug a little sour cream but honestly, it wasn't needed. Instead, I doused the thing in that same delicious hot sauce that I had used for my breakfast tacos. I need a bottle of that for my house to add to the growing hot sauce collection. 

The menu is small but that's okay for a couple reasons. One, they just opened. Two, this isn't fine dining Mexican or a place that you are going to enjoy a long and leisurely meal. It's a quick, satisfying option. And six or eight bucks for lunch is definitely okay with me. I'm looking forward to my next visit and seeing the business settle in.

We started with the House Salad and loved the size (easily shared by 2 people as an appetizer), as well as the tasty peanut dressing.  I created a Beef Green Curry from the Build-Your-Own portion of the menu (don't get too excited...nothing too exotic on the list of choices) - I was happy with the amount of vegetables included in the curry.  One advantage of this place is that it's on the edge of town to the point where you can find street parking (with a little luck and some patience).

Certainly this is not a New York City Museum, but it has much to offer on it's own.  Sono Cafe is good for a quick bite with a lovely view of the park and river.  The building itself is a work of art with a piece of light art (Sky by Leo Villareal) at nighttime.  I would even vote the bathroom one of the trendiest in town!  And the artwork - yes, it's not a huge collection.  But you also don't have to spend an overwhelming amount of time here.  A one-hour visit gives you a good dose of art that is uplifting and mentally challenging.  The visiting exhibits have been remarkable in the new space, and I like how the ancient works are situated so near to the contemporary works - it helps me see art in a new way. I've especially loved the risks the museum took with the most current exhibit (video artist Janet Biggs), and I hope to see more risks like this in the future!

Well done, Tampa and Mayor Iorio! This park has brought life to downtown. I especially like the great views, Rock the Park concerts on the first Thursday of the month, and the art museum at nighttime. Also, my nephews loved playing in the fountains.

Great brunch place! I love the Best Damn Benedict with turkey sausage. It is small and crowded, but I've never had to wait an unreasonable amount of time. Always worth it. My alcoholic friends USED TO be disappointed that they don't have mimosas or bloody marys, but now they do. The food is still my vice of choice. Also love the Mexican frittata.

Overpriced considering the amount and quality of food you get for that price.  Portrays itself a 'high-end' authentic Indian restaurant, which i don't feel it is, being a foodie and an Indian myself.  Lacks flavor in most items served.  Not thrilled with Laxmi's.

We ordered a vegetarian platter as one of the starters, among others, because there were six of us there, so we figured we'd be able to try the different items on the plate.  The plate arrived with one samosa cut into two, one aloo tikki cut into two and two pakoras, for $9, totally not worth it!  We ordered chicken malabar ($8), a tiny breast of flattened chicken, battered with coconut and deep fried, enough for one person.

For main course: mixed non-veg tandoori grille ($23), which looked and sizzled like it would be the best tasting dish on earth, but after tasting it, it was quite bland, enough to the point that my husband had to send it back to have some moreflavor added.  They brought it back with some tikka masala sauce thrown over it, which now made it a curry instead of the tandoori grilled dish!  I ordered Chicken Tikka Masala, which i must say was tasty, but, in the entire karai (serving bowl), there were barely 5-6 tiny little pieces of chicken and the rest of it was the curry which was gravy.  At $14, again this item is not worth it.  We also ordered Lamb Rogan Josh ($17) which was ok, Daal Makhani and Mutter Paneer ($12 which is high for small portions of veg dishes) tasted good.  We also ordered a side of Raita.  It came in a little saucer like container, which again was only enough for one person ($3).

Since this place is BYO, we brought our own wine, which they did not help serve.  But they charged a $5 corkage fee for opening and pouring it ourselves!

Although the servers are polite, service is ok. They constantly forget to bring things and mistakes. Complimentary chutney & papadam these days is not a big deal.  Most indian restaurants do that obligingly.  We got one plate of a couple pieces of papadum, but since there were six of us, it was not enough for all of us, so we asked for more. We never got it.

We used our Restaurant.com $25 voucher, but I think i would rather take me and my foodie guests somewhere else worth our while, our dollars and our palates.

Very clean.  Friendly, always greeted.  Fast credit card terminals and always ask if I want a receipt (which I don't - so I appreciate this).  Did I mention CLEAN?

A bonanza of beverage choices (I personally like all of the fresh brewed teas they just added).  Awesome cups (I especially appreciate the stout lids on the larger cups).  Great selection of food and other convenience items.

I'm grateful to have this chain in our city.

Cafe Lutecia is one of the best kept secrets in Philly! I have been a loyal customer since 2002 and keep going back for their delicious sandwiches, fresh bread, creative quiche and fantastic soups. Only complaint is that my favorite sandwich ("The Lutecia") was discontinued about a year ago, but there are plenty of other options to keep me going back.

On a Friday afternoon, right before a week-long winter break, seventeen of my coworkers and I descended upon Sabrina's new West Philly location. We had just been given notice of an early dismissal from work, and we were looking to celebrate. We took over a large room in the restaurant for lunch, after calling ahead. 

I ordered the fish tacos at first, but was told that they were out of tilapia, so I had to go back to the menu. This was a simple task because there were about 10 different menu items over which I had wavered. So, I ordered the veggie burger instead and asked if they could add avocado. They could, for $1 extra which was no big deal. I had a choice between regular fries, sweet potato fries, or I could pay a little extra to get a side salad. I knew I should order the salad, but I can't resist a place that makes sweet potato fries. 

Serving eighteen people at once is no easy task, and the restaurant did a pretty good job. Only one order was missed, one of my coworkers had to wait an extra 5 minutes for her egg burrito while we dug into our dishes, lest they get cold. 

My veggie burger was high quality; it was very similar to one I've had at Whole Foods hot grill. The avocado on top was nice and fresh but a little skimpy for the extra dollar. The sweet potato fries were divine. With a little sprinkle of powdered sugar on top, they were similar to funnel cake. The little side of cabbage slaw made me feel a bit healthier about my meal choice. 

I'm definitely coming back to Sabrina's for lunch, the next time I'm looking for a filling meal in West Philly. I'm especially excited to try out their vegetarian cheesesteak, now that I've given up meat.
Doesn't allow dogs.  Nuff said!

I pretty much love all Indian food, seriously the spices and the cream sauces so delicious.  We came here the other night I was craving Indian food like no other. So we ended up here.   I ordered an appetizer mixer which included veggie samosa, veggie pakora, and some other things.  The samosa was okay but not fabulous and the veggie pakora was soggy. Which is always icky.  So then I ordered the entrees for all of us to share chicken korma, and chicken tikka masala.  The korma was much better spiced and the chicken tikka masala was okay. 
You do have to pay extra for rice.

Anyways it was awful but it wasn't fabulous.  Just kind of in-between.
Probably won't go back again the prices were high if we are going to spend money I would rather go to Saffron.

J. Alexander's is my least favorite steak restaurant in Tampa.  The restaurant attempts to be an upscale restaurant but fails with it's outdated decor, stained carpeting and worn out seating.  The food is nearly always a miss and it is hard for me to look past bad food.  My husband claims this restaurant used to be really good so maybe this is just a bad restaurant or the food has really gone downhill.

I have been to J. Alexander's twice and I can confirm: the steaks are tough and poorly seasoned.  I am not a fan of the shoestring french fries on the steaks.  My favorite side dish (maccaroni and cheese) was terribly bland and mushy.  The calamari appetizer was ok, but how can you mess up a frozen pre-made appetizer?

I think the reasonable prices lure in customers.  You can tell by the older crowd that their are a lot of loyal diners who have been eating here for many years.  My suggestion?  Pay $5 per plate more and go to a better steak restaurant or if that is out of your price range pick up some nice steaks at the grocery store and cook your own meal!

Decided to check this place out for lunch thanks to a restaurant.com coupon. The stipulation was two entrees must be ordered but luckily I brought my max-midday appetite. This place is huge and was pretty empty on this day but who knows when conventions are in town. We had a personal TV in our booth which was pretty cool, reminded me of the old ESPN zone in Baltimore's inner harbor.  

I ordered a rib-eye steak medium rare which came with fries and asparagus. The steak was grossly overcooked but I had to get back to work and service was already on the slow side so I took it down anyway. The fries and asparagus were consistent, both boring and not seasoned. The one thing 13 did have going were the delicious warm rolls with this maple walnut butter that was out of this world. I should also mention my lunch dates entree of eggplant Parmesan was a tasty plate.

I usually tend to steer clear of hotel restaurants and this just reaffirmed my intentional avoidance.
Incredible cocktails, fries & fondu. Different & fun atmosphere. Love taking friends here. Will be back again and again. Pricy but worth it.
Amazing calamari, lobster Mac n cheese & bread basket. I don't eat beef but have had great fish here. Service has always been excellent. It's a treat to eat here and I look forward to it every time.
Go to their Sushi bar and ask for their sushi chefs to make you one of their specialties...you will not regret it.   Really like their sashimi as well as their tempura sushi rolls

I like this place because it's just hot enough for me to break a sweat and actually feel like I'm getting a workout, but not so hot that I'm slipping and sliding all over my yoga mat.  They offer beginners classes, which are great, as they are slower-paced and the instructors often help you achieve proper alignment in the poses.  However, that will happen in any class, not just the beginner ones.  

My only beef about this place is that sometimes they can pack people in so tightly that it can become difficult to do some of the poses.  Not only that, but if the person next to you is having gastronomical issues, then the class won't be enjoyable.  

To avoid the crowded classes, try to go during off-peak times and you'll get more attention and have a pleasant experience.

Yup, I am a fan. Gary (Husband) and I enjoyed the 'Que for 2 yesterday. We've had it many times before and it's a great value instead of ordering separately. The price did go up a few dollars from the recent past, but that's the facts of life with all food costs rising these days.

The ambiance there is friendly and fun, with a picnic-table like setting, lots of wood and lots of separate little rooms with fun kitschy decorations on the wall. Our server brought big seasoned (tasted freshly made) potato chips with 'que. Nice touch.

The 'que platter boasted 4 meaty ribs (very tasty-we really, really like their rib flavor and preparation), 2 corn muffins, BBQ chicken (you can also get roasted chicken instead), brisket (very good; you have 2 other meat choices instead) and 2 sides. We chose potato salad and coleslaw, both very tasty and well prepared. We ate the ribs and some brisket, along with the salads, and took the rest home. They have about 5-6 BBQ sauce choices. We especially liked the Sweet and Zesty, Rich and Sassy...I think that's the name-it's the biggest bottle one, and the Devil's something, which had a cumin-type flavor. We didn't love the Texas one, which had more of a Worcester sauce flavor to us.

I've talked to the real Famous Dave (yes, he's that lively guy on the TV ads!) too. He has a neat life story and it's fun to support his restaurants. Keep an eye out for occasional promotions for this restaurant, too. OH, and the receipt has a survey that offers a free dessert next time with a validation code. I always like those survey incentives! I'll be baaaaack, Famous Dave! (not just for the dessert!)

I do not like this location as much as the original on Waters Avenue.  The new location attempts to mimic the vibe of a PF Changs, and that is something that I am not interested in when searching for Chinese food.  The dishes here taste almost the same as the first location, but they _do not_ come with rice.  What kind of Chinese food does not have rice?  I am not talking about crappy fried rice, but steamed white rice... You know, the staple of many cultures.  That really blew my mind, and quickly reminded me what this location is all about.

The Salt and Pepper Squid at any Yummy House location is my favorite, only because of the sweet, salty, spicy topping of fried garlic, cilantro, dried hot pepper, and green onion.  You could put that on anything and I would devour it. 

The Shrimp and Scallop in XO is always good, but we tried something new this time- Shanghai Pork Chops.  This variation is one of my guilty pleasures from living in Brooklyn.  The dish is normally called Peking Pork Chops.  The chops are sliced super thin with bone in, then lightly battered and covered in a sweet and sticky red sauce.  No the sauce is not sweet and sour sauce, it is more of a ketchup, soy, sugar, and Worcestershire sauce.  However, at Yummy House this dish is tough, thick cut, not crispy, and overly sweet.  It was beyond tragic...

Now that Halong Bay has almost perfected their version of the Salt and Pepper Squid dish with dry topping and the Shrimp/Scallop XO dish is so easy to make (thank you Lee Kum Kee XO sauce), I really have no reason to visit Yummy House anymore.

I can't believe I've never written a review for Charley's seeing as I've been here many times over the years.  It's probably not fair to say this, but they're docked a star immediately for the price.  I get that it's an upscale steakhouse, but it can get extremely outrageous quickly here.

That being said, we've found the best tasting, most reasonable thing on the menu.  The steak tartar here is by far the best I've ever had and it is easily enough for one person for a meal.  They use the very best cuts of meat, and it is only $15.95.  I've had their steaks and seafood here, but I always come back to the tartar.  

I actually wouldn't order a cooked steak here.  I've seen and heard that they're not worth the price.  The seasonal fish is excellent though.  Always cooked beautifully, and a massive serving size.  The bread is always amazing, creamed spinach is killer, and the potatoes au gratin are delicious.  I would also recommend the stone crab (if you can afford them) and the tuna tartar.  

I have also had inconsistent service here.  For a $$$$ type of establishment, they should really have the very best servers.  They tend to be a little slow sometimes, and worst of all, they can be curt.

Great stuff. Used to go to Cafe Japon @ International Plaza all the time, but we arrived @ 9 pm Saturday night to find it closed ( guess its been awhile since we went!) anyway, searched Yelp for best sushi Tampa & the 1st one that came up was Soho Sushi. Thought we'd have to wait forever for a seat (based on the Yelp reviews) but we walked in to find 2 seats @ the sushi bar right away (yes, it was packed, but we lucked out I guess). Super fresh, creative, really great. I think much better than Cafe Japon, and if that were still open & I had to choose, I'd go to Soho. We. Has sashimi ( the yellowtail kicked serious ass!), 3 rolls( wow!), & the geso ( calamari), everything was so fresh & tasty. Looking forward to going back soon!
Outstanding! Delightfully different! An exploration into unusual tastes. Attentive service. Quiet atmosphere. Enjoyed by all our party!

Han Dynasty has been on my list to try for months. On a trip to Manayunk, when I spotted the newly soft-opened location on Main Street, I was stoked. I convinced my husband to try it with me with the phrase "It's so hot on the Yelp boards." That's all it took.

The waiter explained that the restaurant was family style which meant food came out when it was hot. Being a fairly hungry girl, I was thrilled that our food would come out more quickly with this system.

First out was the eggplant in garlic sauce which was rated at a "3" for heat, out of 10. The chinese eggplant was slathered in a spicy red sauce and served piping hot. The brown rice I requested was a bit on the crusty side. Perhaps it would have been better to go with the empty carbs and order the white. Next out was the bok choy with black mushrooms. I've never had mushrooms with such an amazingly chewy texture. The bok choy was cooked perfectly and the sauce was fairly mellow for both heat and heaviness. I would recommend both dishes.

A man who I'm pretty certain was the owner, Han, greeted us warmly and taught us some Chinese words on our way out of the restaurant. Because we were eating at such an odd hour, 3PM which is neither lunch nor dinner, we got to see the cooks come out and eat their meal as we were leaving. There was a lot of soup on their table, so I'm determined to try that out next time we go.

I am amazed at the criticism I've seen on Yelp about this place, because I've been coming here since they opened (2003?), and I've never been disappointed.  The food is excellent, the ambiance is delightful, and the service was great.

My parents were in town, and I brought them here for their 53rd wedding anniversary.  It was a Wednesday night, and the place was about 2/3 full.  We were seated in a booth against the wall with no wait.  Those other times that I've had to wait, I've spent the time on a very comfortable couch with a cocktail.  

I always order the same thing, and it's not on the menu!  I describe it - angel hair pasta, salmon and shrimp, cream sauce, mushrooms and spinach - and for at least four years, the servers have nodded and said, "oh, that's such-and-such.  It's not on the menu, but we can make it."  And they do.  And it's always amazing.  How's THAT for customer service?

I love the decor and ambiance of the building.  The patio is a lovely space (open windows and hanging plants), and we would have eaten out there if it had been warmer.  The inside has dark wood beams, cream-colored walls and nice accent colors and art.  Everything has an Italian garden feel, and it's very cozy.  They sometimes have an excellent live jazz pianist.

In general, this is a fine restaurant, and one that I've returned to again and again.  It's a delightful space to spend a few hours, and the food is great.  It's consistently one of my favorites in Tucson.

Why 5 stars?  Because if you look on my profile, this is listed as my least meal on Earth.  It's hard to top that praise.

   ---   ---   ---

Samurai Blue...my favorite restaurant on the planet, and my first review on Yelp after being a prominent lurker for 2 years.

Here is what you need to know:

1) The greatest thing I have ever tasted in my life:

Tempura Lobster Roll - Order, and everything you thought you knew is changed.

2) The second greatest thing I have ever tasted in my life:

Blue Crab Rangoon - The best appetizer ever created.  The only bad thing about it is that there are only four little rangoons to go around.

3) The thing that is almost as good as the best two things in the world:

Blue Moon Roll - Usually the Tempura Lobster and Blue Moon rolls are ranked #1-2 amongst my friends on the all-time list.  It's probably a 50/50 ratio for which one is preferred, but this is my personal ranking.

4)  Live, breathe...and EAT the back side of the sushi menu:

Samurai Blue incorporates a lot of crazy sauces on the more expensive (but well worth it) back-side of the sushi menu.  The regular sushi, while tasty, is nothing out of the ordinary.


Aside from the food, the building itself is amazing.  I believe it is an old bank building.  From your table, the roof towers about 4 stories high above you.  It's very industrial, yet can be a romantic setting or just a place to take a friend for the best sushi of his/her life.

This is a default stop for anyone coming to town to visit- when my Mom comes to town, she wants to eat here every meal.  And my Mom's opinion is pretty important.

Why 5 stars?  Because of a very special menu item.

   ---   ---   ---

There is really only a reason for one entree to be on the menu:

Prime Rib Sandwich

This is one of those meals that's so good, I will never be able to order anything else at Jays.  I have been probably 7 or 8 times and I will continue to get the Prime Rib Sandwich every single time.  I'm a lifer.  I like for them to bring out plenty of horse radish because it tends to dissolve in the au jus.

As many others have noted, get the Mac-n-Cheese on the side, as it is incredible.

There's really nothing else you need to know.  It's a nice establishment- good for a date or good to take someone who wants to eat an amazing steak sandwich.

Why 5 stars?  Because holy selection!

   ---   ---   ---

The hardest part of going to Datz is deciding what to order.  It was much easier when they catered to my old job and I could sample several different kinds of mini-sandwiches.

Datz is about variety with the 3 main aspects of their menu - the sandwiches, the beer and the dessert.  They have all kinds of crazy beers- I even tried a Banana Bread last time I was there.  But the desserts...

***WARNING***

Do not come to Datz if your New Years Resolution is to lose weight and it's not your cheat day.  They have Blue Bell Ice Cream, which rings fondly in this Texan's heart.  They have a gelato bar, cupcakes, regular non-cup Cakes, and all kinds of random goodies.  It's good for your mouth, bad for your belly.

Why 4 stars?  Because it's not just a novelty, it's REALLY good Mexican food.

   ---   ---   ---

Taco Bus was cool before eating off of a vehicle was trendy.  Now it's playing the Godfather role.

My personal recommendation is to go with no onions or easy onions on whatever you get, because they pile them on and they are intense.  Getting rid of the onion after taste is a lot like getting rid of a hangover - you can lessen the pain throughout the day, but it truly never goes away until after you sleep.

You can't go wrong with Tacos, Burritos or Quesadillas.  I'm a steak guy, so I don't know about the other kinds of meat, but the steak is solid.  The rice and beans are very good, something us Texans are picky about.  The Horchata is a really interesting and tasty drink as well.

You aren't going to eat here and say it's the best mexican food you've ever had in your life...but it's open 24 hours and they cook the food on an actual bus.  It is definitely something every Tampa area resident should try once.

Why 5 stars?  Because it reminds of Mexican food from Houston.

   ---   ---   ---

My favorite Mexican restaurant in Tampa.

I'm from Houston, where restaurants like Miguel's are plentiful.  But while Tampa specializes in Cuban cuisine, it's Mexican seems to be lacking somewhat.

An order of Steak Quesadillas with rice and beans from Miguel's makes me feel right at home.  Legitimate Mexican food.....and now I'm hungry just thinking about it.  

My only picky issue is that it always smells like cleaning supplies in there, but you quickly forget about that when the food comes.

Why 4 stars?  Because they have a wide selection of great crepes.

   ---   ---   ---

Definitely the most crepes I have ever seen on a menu.  I believe they have over 100 between breakfast, sweet, and regular entree crepes.

It's a cool atmosphere buried in the heart of Ybor city.  The interior decor screams NYC, which adds an interesting element.  The wait staff is always very attentive and friendly.  

The crepes themselves are good, not great.  It is definitely a place you should try once, but your life will not be changed after trying one.  My favorite is the Texan crepe, but I haven't tried a sweet crepe yet.  The french onion soup is REALLY good...that I will highly recommend.  It's absolutely covered in cheese, which is never a bad thing.

Why 5 stars?  Because it's the staple of Tampa restaurants.

   ---   ---   ---

If you want to go to Bern's for the first time, here's the best advice I can give you:

1) On Friday, make a reservation for Bern's on Saturday at 5:00.

2) Sleep in as late you as you can Saturday.  Waking up at about noon would be ideal.

3) Eat a VERY light breakfast- think along the lines of a nutrigrain bar and maybe a piece of fruit.

4) Be very hungry by 5:00.

5) Order the Del Monico, and do not be tempted by anything else.

6) Maybe go a little light on the Baked Potato.  Everything else that comes out with your steak is amazing, but if you had to pick one thing out that isn't anything special, it would be the potato.  And you are going to need to conserve stomach space.

7) Pay your hefty (but well worth it bill)

8) Do the restaurant tour to let your stomach have a break

9) Order the Macademia Nut Sundae, or as they call it, "The Best Sundae in the World."

9b) If you're a chocolate fan, the "Chocolate-Chocolate-Chocolate" caused my non-foodie girlfriend to act in a way I've never seen before.  She completely loses her mind over that CCC.

10) Go for a post-game walk.  You'll need it.

Why 4 stars?  Because it's one of my favorite spots in Ybor.

   ---   ---   --- 

My favorite thing to order is a Peanut Butter Bar.  This is a very simple item to prepare, but it always seems to satisfy that craving you just can't put your finger on.

It's a pressed Hawaiian Sweet Roll with peanut butter and all kinds of fruit and toppings you can choose from to put in the middle.  I like to order fluff, creamy PB, grapes, strawberries and banana.  I thoroughly enjoy this.

Their regular entrees are solid, but not phenomenal in my opinion.  I am sure if I were vegan/vegetarian, I could possibly bump them up to 5 stars...but I love cow too much.  There are some things with meat on the menu, but they are in the minority.

The crowd is very hipster, it's a cool place to hang out if that's your scene.  They also have a decent selection of beer.

Why 3 stars?  Because I just don't understand the hype for their sandwiches.

   ---   ---   ---

Both times I have been, my sandwich resembled something my grandmother would pack for me to take to a picnic.  I was in a group of 5  while there last week and no one at my table was very impressed.

Their desserts however, are (no pun intended) their bread and butter.  We ordered Red Velvet Cake and Peanut Butter Chocolate Cake - we all walked away in awe.  That was probably the best RVC I have ever had...and I'm not a chocolate fan, but that one was awesome.

As a bonus, we saw Rays GM Andrew Friedman coming in as we were leaving - he's a pretty smart guy, so he probably came just for dessert.

Why 3 stars?  Because it's quantity, not quality.

   ---   ---   --- 

Obviously you need to get the Cuban Sandwich and the Deviled Crab or the Stuffed Potato.  That is basically why this restaurant was built.

You will get a TON of food with this combination.  If you don't take home leftovers, plan on having someone drive you home because you will fall into a food coma.

The deviled crab is tasty, but the cuban is nothing special, IMO.  It's just really big.

If you go at lunch, it is very packed in there.  The restaurant is as crowded with people as their sandwich is with meat.  Both instances result in general discomfort.

Why 5 stars?  Because it's a local champion.

   ---   ---   ---

The best Cuban Sandwich I have had in Tampa.  Hands down.

I know it's a pretty simple sandwich, but I've been on the hunt for my clear cut favorite for years.  The target has now been discovered, I will be a returning customer for a long time.

Why 4 stars?  Because I feel like I'm in NYC when eating here.

   ---   ---   ---

Best pizza in Tampa.

Tastes great, but is definitely at maximum grease levels.  Don't eat too much or you will probably hold a grudge.  It's tempting though, because Eddie & Sams will make you feel like you're in NYC!

Why 5 stars?  Because it's easily, my favorite breakfast place in Tampa.

   ---   ---   ---

It can get really crowded, and that is where the negatives end.

Right off the bat, you know you are in a good place when you venture over to the self-serve coffee bar.  They have about five kinds of coffee to choose from, which is awesome for someone like me who enjoys coffee but is no connoisseur.

But the BEST part of the coffee bar is the collection of 1980's mugs to choose from.  They have to constantly raid garage sales every weekend in search of the most random mugs available on Earth.

My first visit, I went in a group of four, and all four of us walked out knowing we found a new treasure.  I had the ultimate fried egg sandwich myself...every bite more miraculous than the next.

What really separates Pinky's from the rest of the pack is that EVERY-THING on their menu looks like it will change your life.  Stuffed French Toast?  Breakfast Quesadilla? Oatmeal Waffles?  All kinds of Frittatas? PANCAKE SANDWICH??

I hate typing reviews when I haven't eaten in a while.  I want to live at Pinky's.  I would do dishes and clean the parking lot for a permanent corner table.

The food is mediocre and the restaurant is dirty, but the worst thing about this place is the owner. Frankie alternates between being creepily over-friendly to the clientele and loudly berating his staff. 

If you are in the area, you will find better food at Brushfire, the Blue Willow, Beyond Bread, Yoshimatsu, or Lucky Wishbone. 

You can find a much better cheese steak at Daglio's on the East side. Do yourself a favor and skip this spot.

Meh...
Simple decor, limited meal choices, small portions and mildly soft flavors make this a sub par choice. Not a bad addition for the neighborhood, but I definitely won't be a regular
Adequate. Not my favorite crepe place in phila, but it gets a solid B

I went to Sparkroot on a Sunday night and I had the 4. fontina, portabellas, arugula with a side of kale chips. The sandwich was great, it was a bit of a wait but the cashier did warn us about that before placing our order, which I appreciated. The kale chips were also crispy and delicious so if you like veggies go ahead and get them.

Also the wine list, although brief, was great because all glasses were $5, simplifies and lets you pick whatever sounds the best to you. I had the Velvet Devil and it was quite tasty. All in all I'm going to have to try this place again for coffee in the near future.

If you're wanting something 'upscale Italian' on the east side.....this is the place.  Consistently great food and amazing happy hour deals.  Be sure you try the thin and flavorful Bianco pizza, it's not the sausage stuffed mess that some people are looking for!  Don't make yourself sick from too many 1/2 priced drinks and appetizers! Great staff and management and worth going to on a regular basis.

This place just...makes sense. Awesome unique drinks, a 'no BS' atmosphere, authentic tacos & burritos by the order, and amazing guacamole. For the most part, it gets really packed on pretty much any day BUT I've found seats become available fairly fast.

Sparkroot is yet another excellent establishment that has added to Tucson's downtown! I really wasn't sure what to expect, hadn't had a chance to survey the menu and simply went in not having a clue. Generally, not how I operate.  Tracy S invited me to dinner and since I was running late and starving, she went ahead and ordered for us.  We had the bruschetta, artichoke hummus and a pressed cheese sammie. 
I will start with my favorites... the artichoke hummus was so flavorful and perfect to pair with a nice glass of wine.  It is on my must order again list! Next item I thought was perfection- our pressed cheese sandwich with brie, apple slices and walnuts. I want more!!  I want to thank Ari Shapiro for having the concept of pressed cheese sandwiches! I'm a fan! So many excellent combinations to choose from!  Ari nailed the concept of smoothies with Xoom Juice and now has delighted me with these sammies!!  My non-favorite of the night was the bruschetta, which was adequate.  It was fresh but I didn't love the bread.  I suppose I'm old school with bruschetta and like the french bread version.  

What I loved...
Sparkroot's ambiance is hip and has this groovy vibe. The music that I couldn't seem to stop Shazam- ing.  They were playing such super cool music, i.e. Horse Feathers, White Hinterland, Youth Lagoon, etc.  So in a nutshell, I loved the food, the ambiance, the view of downtown from where we sat, the music, my glass of wine, and Tracy S for the suggestion and ordering us such a yummy meal! Sparkroot was a pleasant surprise find!

When you order a veggie burger under the sandwich/burger category you basically assume you'd be getting a veggie patty on top of some sort of bun/bread w/ whatever fixings you'd want on top, right?

At thirteen, that's wrong. When you order a veggie burger, you get the patty and three slices of tomato and a squiggle of mayo as plate decor. 

I don't get it. Where is the bun? Why is it under the sandwich section? Because according to the dictionary, they define sandwich as a food item, typically consisting of two or more slices of bread with one or more fillings between them. OR 
Verb: Insert or squeeze (someone or something) between two other people or things, typically in a restricted space or so as to be uncomfortable.

No bread so that means my patty was not restricted in space therefore it cannot be defined as sandwich

Correct your menu and stop confusing me.

Side note - other items I've tried here have been quite mediocre.

I like Rigos food a lot. Awesome chips and salsa. Great big cups of ice tea. Delicious topopo salads and tortilla soups. Wonderful tacos. And the buffet is killer- some of the best buffet food! I like when they have guacamole- all you can eat guac? Heck yeah!

The food is prepared very fast and very fresh and delicious and brought out to your table quickly. The chips and salsa become addictive. The wait staff is busy especially during the lunch hour. I don't like how noisy it gets inside Rigos, and how crowded I often feel in there. It's a tight fit in such a large space. The acoustics don't make it pleasant for me to have a good conversation with people in. But the food is top notch and I'm always pleased by the bill. Sometimes I just go light and eat a tasty bean and rice and lettuce burrito or a Calabacitas topopo (mmmmm) for about six bucks.

Rigos will divide up the bill so you don't need split checks. That's cool.

The buffet is to be experienced if it's your first time. Since I overeat, I usually stick with the menu. This is a very family run business and I love coming back to Rigos over and over! I even got my picture taken with Janet Napolitano here once!

The Pub is a great place for a bite to eat and drinks with a friend. The place is always buzzing with life and stands out perfectly at the International Plaza (IP). I love the European feel of the restaurant and the menu items are unique and tasty to boot. The servers are always friendly and although it certainly isn't the cheapest place to drink - it does have a certain style that makes the entire experience worth the extra cost. After a long day of shopping around IP, drinks with some buds at The Pub is a nice way to end the day.

I am consistently impressed with The Capital Grille. Their dry-aged steak is one of my favorites in Tampa Bay and their calmari is excellent! I am usually not a fan of cooked fish...something about it always make me feel sick (though I love raw fish!), but their salmon gets my blessing. Although The Capital Grille may be above the price-range of many, however their new Winter Plates lunch menu is affordable and during a tasting I really enjoyed their clam chowder and lobster roll. Dessert is also excellent - the cheesecake with seasonal fruits is superb and servers are very polite and attentive (as is the case at most restaurants in this price range). The food quality really makes the place valid for special occasion splurging as well - a great place for an anniversary or special birthday in my opinion. 

The atmosphere feels upscale and doesn't seem as much of a socialite scene that nearby Ocean Prime presents, but more a place to either have an intimate meal or a place to have a drink at the bar with some food (you'll often see professionals lounging there). Watch for their specials because they are often a steal.
This food city is kinda dirty and an eyesore in the neighborhood. They do however have the cheapest quality produce in town! everything else is more expensive than other stores.

Why 4 stars?  Because it takes a lot for me to like beer this much.

   ---   ---   ---

Seminole Heights scores another gem on their way to prominence in Tampa night life.

They have an enormous beer selection, which is where I first discovered Sitegl.  I'm not a huge beer drinker, but I really liked this Austrian treat and many others that I've tried at The Independent.

I like the indoor/outdoor setup, and of course, I love the dart board.  I don't know why there is a bar in America without darts.  It's probably because I don't mind doing the math, but for some reason I think it's kind of a novelty to keep score manually to keep score with chalk on the wall.

This bar is dog friendly, in case your pup wants to enjoy a cold Stiegl as well.  Just make sure you take take turns being each others DD.  Aren't I funny?  I insinuated that your dog could drive.

I used to love Olive Garden up in New York....well now I'm in FL I guess not anymore.
I loved Olive Garden because of their great food and good service.  After seeing how the Olive Garden in Pinellas Park was I did not expect much from this place either.  

Well at  this location, the server was good, and other staffs were welcoming too. The food itself was not as bad as the ones I got in PInellas Park  and sure it was not aweful.  But certainly it cannot even be compared with what I used to get up in NY. 

Don't get me wrong I love FL!  However after moving from NY to FL and seeing how I do not get what I used to get up in NY from the chain restaurants (like Olive Garden and Chllis) tells me something...Is it that they are too laid back and don't care?  or is it just bad managements??  I do not know but I certainly hope that they will get improved.

Bar none, the best Chinese food I've had, anywhere, ever.

I'm not even kidding. If I wanted to start this review with a joke, It'd go something like this:

Me: Knock knock.
You: Who's there?
Me: Gimme.
You: Gimme, who?
Me: Gimme all your money because this is a robbery and I needs it for my dry fried beef!
You: Ow, my liver!
Me: *flees the scene a la Cops*

You see that? This grub is almost good enough to induce murderous food lust. I say almost, because I took out the poison from my jacket (I always carry a little with me) and strongly considered applying it to each of my dinner companions to maximize the amount of food I was getting. I didn't... THIS time...

We did a whole ton of courses as a tasting menu organized by Dave H. Here's what I remember:

1) Cucumbers in chili oil - the name does not do these little guys justice. You have to have these. They're an odd mix of cold and hot, but it works.

2) Tripe in chili oil - I hate tripe. I LOVE Han's take on it. I was actually able to bite through it.

3) Cold sesame soy noodles - Semi sweet and vaguely nutty thanks to the sesame seed oil. A good contrast for the spicier things that we had.

4) Dumplings in chili oil - This was the hottest thing I remember that night. Not the spiciest thing I've ever tasted anywhere, but it packed a punch.

5) Dan dan noodles - With such a ridiculous name, it's easy to see how I was initially skeptical of them. I mean, I could practically hear the little oriental flute from Carl Douglas' "Kung Fu Fighting" going on in the background as I ate it. Still, this was probably one of the best things I had all night. Believe the hype, because these guys straddle that line between junky comfort food and quality cuisine that won't require you to spend the next 24 hours on a toilet seat.

6) Dry fried beef - Da bomb. Get this. NOW!

7) Cumin pork - my least favorite, but still pretty good.

8) Fish soup - standard hot/slightly sour soup with some kinda light-meat fish in it. I'm not so good at identifying fish meat. It was tasty.

9) Mapo tofu - I hate tofu. I liked mapo tofu. Spicy and pungent.

10) Salt-battered shrimp - very very salty. Not my taste, but maybe it was because I'd been eating a bunch of really hot things before that. You might like it, if you like salt battered squid.

11) Shredded chicken with crispy rice - This was surprisingly sweet and light. Very good as a contrast to the spicier dishes.

12) Some other stuff I'm sure I'm forgetting. Try it all!

This is the kind of food they write songs about. One of these days, Chef Han will not be remembered as he should - for saving us all from the combined robot/zombie apocalypse (Robozompocalypse 2020!) - but rather for his spicy dan dan noodles.

The Manayunk space is very well laid out - the dining room is more square than rectangle, so that means no fumbling your way around other diners trying to get to the rest room. Also, it's great for big groups, and is, at least since I last visited, still a BYO. 

On a final note, I met Han, and he was a really cool guy. I told him I'd be back with Dave sometime to sample his face-meltingest, sperm-killingist, spicy dish. I'm excited.

Pro-tip:
Bring friends to share with - everyone order a different thing, and have them all split family style. TOTALLY worth it.

Why 3 stars?  It used to be one of the best, but it's time for renovations.

   ---   ---   ---

Years ago when I lived in Texas, Raymond James was the #1 NFL stadium that I wanted to visit.  After attending several Bulls/Bucs games over the last few years, I think that it's still a decent venue but some money needs to go into a few updates.

The video boards are in desperate need of replacing.  They are very awkwardly shaped and small by today's standards.  There are no LED boards despite very ample room on the large facings between decks.

Outside of the pirate ship, the stadium is just boring in general.  Nothing stands out to make your trip to the football game memorable.  There's just a certain flare that's missing.

It's not a bad place to watch a game, but I think some creativity/pro-activity could be borrowed from the Lightning to improve the fan experience.

Why 4 stars?  Every October, it keeps me coming back!

   ---   ---   ---

First of all, if you are going to Howl-O-Scream, especially on a weekend in late October, there is one VERY important bit of information to know:

*****BUY A FRONT OF THE LINE PASS*****

If you don't, plan on waiting in lines of approximately two hours for each haunted house.  The houses are fun, but they are not worth two hour waits.

Despite knowing that no one can touch you, there is definitely an adrenaline rush that comes from these haunted houses.  Sometimes the characters hidden in the bushes throughout the park will get a good jump out of you.

The only negative from Howl-O-Scream is the weird dreams that come that night.  The images you'll see and the mindset you are in throughout the night can definitely take your mind to some strange places.

I had a blast the first time I went to Howl-O-Scream, but Busch Gardens reuses the same costumes and haunted houses so I wouldn't recommend going every year.  The tickets are cheap if you use coupons or go with a large group.  (Coupons are normally available at the local grocery stores or fast food chains.)

The park gets mobbed with tons of students and families since tickets are more affordable than the regular day prices.  I don't think the parents and chaperones do a very good job of supervising the children.  Kids were cutting in the ride lines and misbehaving.  

TIP: Buy a front of a line pass or expect to wait in line FOREVER for the rides.  One year we didn't buy a front of the line pass and only got to ride three roller coasters and go into two haunted houses.  We spent the majority of our time waiting in line which really took out the fun for me.

I'm a big fan of Coldstone Creamery, but I'd have to give this place the nod as the best.  Don't let the froyo in the name fool you. Sure, they have all manner of frozen yogurt, but they have real ice cream too, and its the best in Tampa Bay. And if that doesn't satisfy your sweet tooth, they have the most amazing self service toppings bar where you pay by weight. This gives extra chocolate chips a whole new meaning!

The quality of the ingredients is outstanding, service is good and the place is clean and well organized. It is worth it to come here after a good meal even if you have to drive a bit. You won't be disappointed.  And if you are, an extra scoop of chocolate chips and marshmallows ought to fix you right up!

Why 4 stars?  It's the best grocery store for everything but groceries.

   ---   ---   ---

It'd be like if the iPhone 5 was able to teleport matter over FaceTime, drive your car and go to the gym for you....but it struggled to make calls.

Publix Greenwise has everything under the sun; a hot food buffet, salad bar, gelato counter, full sandwich bar, an extensive bakery, pasta bar, an in-house event planner (!), a sushi chef, 4x the normal amount of steak/chicken/beef (including Kobe Beef) and more cheese than you could fit into the entire city of Seminole.

What holds Publix Greenwise back is that it's not a practical place for doing your actual grocery shopping.  Because most products in here are organic/green, it's impossible to find all of your normal supplies that you'd get from a regular Publix.

I had a Garlic Steak Sandwich with Mac-n-Cheese during my last visit.  It was surprisingly not great.  That being said, I would absolutely go back for lunch or dinner again because there is a vast selection of choices.

What's not to like about riding down an escalator to get into a grocery store?  I think that, and the part of the escalator that's just for shopping carts, is worth 3 stars alone.

We were so looking forward to having dinner there since they are so close to home. Food was really subpar almost on verge of terrible. Service was super slow. Garlic bread appetizer was soggy/spongy like it was microwaved. Tried pizza and pasta with sausage.  Not great at all.  Overpriced. We were the youngest people in there. I see why old people go there, They say your taste buds diminish when you age.

Why 4 stars?  Because it's another gem in Seminole Heights.

   ---   ---   ---

Mermaid Tavern is probably no more than half a mile from my house, so I was long overdue to stop by.  Last night, I stopped in after work and was very pleasantly surprised.  I fully expected a scary and run down dive bar, not a cool/hipster kind of dive bar.  I'm not a hipster myself, but they certainly know their restaurants/bars, so I like to live my life as an auxiliary hipster.

I had a milk stout and it was definitely the best stout I've ever had.  "It was a great finish," is as technical I get with beer-speak, but IT HAD A GREAT FINISH!!

There is an indoor and outdoor area.  Smoking is not allowed indoors, which is a huge positive for me.  Inside there's just a bar, a lot of beer, some stools, couches and chairs.  Mermaid Tavern is definitely in its infancy, but I expect big things to come.  I hope they add a kitchen, because I'm confident it will rock.  They've done everything right to this point.

Not much inventory. The usual poor service and I am sure cash only.
Walked in and walked out.

In am Asian Strip mall, kinda depressing. Not worth the trip.

Han: Everything okay? Do you need more water?
Me: No, thank you, I'm alright.
H: Are you sure? You really look like you need more water (as he fills the glass anyway)
M: I guess I could take a splash...
Alexandra: Why, because he's sweating?
H: Yes. (looks at my empty plate and the almost empty dry fry pork dish) Did you eat those peppers? You don't eat the peppers, they're just for flavor.
M: Oh. Oops...
No need to type up their extensive laughter or ensuing ribbing at my expense.

The food is just as good here as the Chestnut street location, though I think I enjoyed it much more because it's approximately 3 to 7 minutes from my house, which meant I was able to lay on my floor with pants undone to digest my overeating all the more quickly.

Obviously the meal contained Dan Dan, but I should have ordered two because there's never any left over of that for bringing home. Entrees included the dry fry pork I mentioned earlier, which tastes like crispy bacon in hot wing sauce (the goodly spicy and oily kind, not the overly buttery ish), and some very good and mildly sweet crispy rice chicken, which I liked, but also helped my figure out that I tend to only want heat when I eat (it has none by design); came very sauced up, and the bamboo and shrooms were my favorite non-chicken part in order of appreciation, but the rice cakes soaking up the gooey goodness were a novel treat as well. 

With only two of us dining, the three dishes we got had plenty remaing for a repeat at lunch the next day (if I hadn't inhaled the rest at about 2AM, that is), so don't go overboard on th ordering unless you, like us, want that. Also, this is a bit of a smaller venue than the Old City local, so RESERVATIONS ARE A MUST, but the large open air dinning room of this huge windowed BYOB gave it an intimate yet not crowded feel that I very much enjoyed.

Final conclusion- I never have to go to 2nd street for Sichuan again. Also, Han's awesome, but now I know Mama Han is just as rockin'.

*Update*
Tried the Pork fried rice from the kid/baby adult menu to see if Han rocked the generic Chinese food as well as he does his own stuff, but, since I actually like spice, I asked to have it heated up to Dan Dan levels. The meal was uneventful, though I suppose that is intentional, but it was still better than most places dish out. Heat was negligible, so on the slim chance I order it again I will ask them to double the Dan Dan spice. For those who find this pertinent (I certainly do), the Dynasty includes peas and carrots in their Pork Fried Rice, the latter coming al dente.

If you're into comedy, you need to pay the Improv a visit.  They feature nationally recognized talent as well as local up and comers who sometimes are very good.  Oddly enough it was here were I was first introduced to the delicacy of sweet potato fries many years ago.  I found the food decent, although I usually prefer one of the many local eateries.  Some sweet potato fries and pretzel sticks with cheese sauce keep me entertained, plus they have a full bar.  Service is decent.
