I hosted an office function once but found a lot of the comedy material NSFW.  If you are offended by foul language and prefer the Bill Cosby type of comedy, this place may not be for you. I don't mind the ocassional foul word or crude sexual reference, so for me it's a hoot.  Combine a visit to the Improv with dinner at the Acropolis or El Puerto for a magnificent Tampa night out!

The appetizers were great and the service is excellent.  I tried the Shrimp Pad Thai and it was good but was served in a fairly small portion.  This place has a great location but the parking lot is tiny and getting in and out of often causes traffic backups on Howard Ave too...

This place is a TOTAL RIP OFF FOR ANY SPECIAL EVENTS THEY HOST.  We went to a "jazz" night last night.  $20.00 to get in.  They said the wine list was "anything you buy off the shelf".  What they didn't tell you is that there was a $10.00 corking fee for each bottle (We assumed our twenty dollar cover would have taken care of the corking fee)  They charged $38 dollars for a cheese tray that should have been served for one. We couldn't find the cheese!!! Not kidding!  We saw another table's tray, and it was AMAZING! (They were friends with the owner)  They charged $36 for 6 sliders, that you could not find the meat, because it was the size of a quarter. We were a party of 5, our bill was 350.00 (our bottles cost $37, $15, and $10) and we had to go eat after since we were starving. TOTAL RIP OFF!   And THEY DIDN'T CARE...They have their clique and that is all they care about. If you're not friends with the owner you're treated like crap. Service was horrible also, with NO apology.

I had never been to an Ace Hardare store as I am mostly a Lowes/Home Depot man.  But I was looking for a very specific metric stainless steel screw, and the folks at West Marine suggested I visit Ace Hardare.  According to them, Ace had the best assortment of metric stainless steel screws in Tampa.  Based on their advice, I came here.

Wow!  I was impressed!  It's amazing how they fit so much stuff into such a small building.  Everything seemed very well organized, and they seemed to have everything!  The stainless steel screws are located in the back, and an Ace employee not only walked me back there, but he searched for and located the specific screw I was looking for.  That is customer service!  The sale that day was only a couple of bucks, but they earned a new customer.  I'm coming back because their friendliness, customer service, and broad selection of stuff has made me a fan!

I reviewed the University Square location, so I won't repeat that here just to say diddo.  I have visited four locations and the service and food quality is consistent in all four.  If you like deep fried turnovers filled with chicken, beef,  sausage, or even guava goodness, stop by any one of the Mr. Empanadas.  You won't be disappointed!

I'll also put in another good word for their mini-empanadas tray.  25 mini empanadas make for excellent socializing munchies or for movie night.  Beats popcorn and pizza rolls any day of the week, and weekends too!

I had been to Oggi for hair (with the aforementioned and sublime Cynthia - she rocks!) but never for spa treatments.

I actually did not realize they offer so many great things.
So when I heard about the mid-week (Tues, Wed or Thurs) 3 treatments for $99 special, I just had to indulge.

I had a "relaxation massage" (30 minutes of swedish) and 30 minutes of hot stone with Jenna.  She is a very nice gal and a talented therapist.

After that I had a marvelous paraffin manicure with (can't rember her name - Yelena??) a very excellent nail tech.  (Russian lady who's been there for years.)
I loved it!  Seriously - the mani lasted about 6 days chip free.  Great products and flawless technique.

If I were able to - I'd come here all the time.

Why 4 stars?  Because it was a solid experience.

   ---   ---   ---

I knew that the fried pork sandwich was the main event, but I was too tempted by the Philly Cheesesteak with the picture of Geno's in Philadelphia on the menu next to it.  Luckily, my friend got the fried pork sandwich, so I was able to try both.

Both items were really great.  The cheesesteak did not taste like Geno's, but it was really good.  I expected it to be closer to the original cheeseteak king because the menu mentioned that included cheese whiz, but it seemed to be much greasier than Geno's. 

The fried pork sandwich was really good and definitely unique.  It came with a spicy sauce, that apparently had an even spicier version as an option.  We were told by our server that the fried pork sandwich was going to be on some kind of food network show for spicy foods.  My friend got the more mild version though, and it was delicious.

I will definitely go back to EATS!

Stopped by Irish 31 on a Friday night around 7pm.  Great live music was playing and the patio was PACKED!  We managed to grab a half booth inside... a little bit of a squeeze for three girls, but we made it work.

The location of Irish 31 is great.  The restaurant/bar is in Hyde Park, across the street from the parking garage and next door to my favorite movie theater.  The decor inside is nice and modern with flat screen TVs and a touch of an Irish pub feel.  

Great drink selection.  I ordered a dirty martini with Kettle One Vodka. I wish that our waitress mentioned that the XXX was the same drink, but with blue cheese stuffed olives. I love blue cheese stuffed olives and would have preferred that cocktail had I seen it on the menu.  My sister got the Hyde Park Cosmo and loved it, although she said it didn't taste like a Cosmo.  I tried it and it almost tasted like a whiskey sour, but it was good.  Most of the bottled beers are domestic.  There are a few interesting bottled beers such as Cigar City Jai Alai, Left Hand Brewery Milk Stout, and Yuengling.  The draft selection is a little more impressive. 

Because we were overcrowded at our table we didn't order any food, but I will on my next visit.  The menu looked good and offered typical Irish and bar food such as potato skins, burgers, and shepherds pie.  The onion rings at the table next to us looked and smelled awesome.

My one gripe was that our waitress ditched us for 25 minutes.  We were trying to get our check and leave, but instead we were held hostage at our table.  She did have a big party, but she could have had one of the other waitresses check on us.  Finally I asked another waitress for our check so we could pay and leave.

Overall, great experience and will be back. Will come back on the weekend to try the brunch menu.

Tavolino is an excellent choice if you're looking for an upscale Italian restaurant in the foothills area. Tucked away in a combination office/retail complex at the southeast corner of Campbell and Skyline, Tavolino reminds me of a San Francisco Italian restaurant. I believe the owner is from Italy, and you can feel the Northern Italian influence in both the food and the decor. 

I usually go here for lunch, as the dinner menu is more pricey. The meat lasagna is the best I've had in Tucson (with the exception of Cafe' Torino in Oro Valley). I've also had the vegetarian lasagna, which they often have as a special, and it is also delicious. The sandwiches come on focaccia bread with a side salad, and the one with prosciutto is my favorite. 

They have a nice wine list, which is geared towards Italian wines, but I've had a few excellent whites here. Crisp, light, and satisfying. That's a good way to describe Tavolino.

Vivace is, without question, one of the best restaurants in Tucson. Whether you're looking for a good place for a business lunch or an intimate location for a romantic celebratory dinner, you will not be disappointed at Vivace. Run by the Scordato family, (long term restauranteurs in Tucson), the food and the service are consistently top notch. This is one of the few restaurants in Tucson that truly deserves its 5-star rating. 

I usually get the fish special, which is always delicious, and perfectly prepared. Normally it's served with vegetables and a bed of their famous rice (which has an unbelievably savory buttery taste). The veggies are always cooked perfectly, and I honestly don't know what they are putting in the rice, but it's incredibly delicious! 

The meat lovers in my family love the lamb chops and the osso bucco. My son swears that their filet is the best he's had in Tucson. Their wine list is extensive and excellent- many of the bottle prices are actually reasonable (under $40). They also have superb pasta dishes, with both red and white sauces, and outstanding desserts. My favorite thing to get at lunch is the spinach soufflé, which (if you like spinach and cheese) is heavenly. 

I always make a reservation on open table. You can get in at lunch without a reservation, but it's better if you have one, especially for dinner.

Why 5 stars?  Because this has become my favorite bar in Tampa.

   ---   ---   ---

Beers that I have tried since the Hand Milk Stout:

Bells Winter White

Victory Moon Glow

Saxo Belgian Blonde

Dunedin Red Head


All of the above have been really good beers.  I also tried the Dunedin Apricot Peach, which I would grade slightly below the others, but still a good choice.

I am not a beer connoisseur by any means.  I generally do not like the taste of beer.  Mermaid Tavern has probably the most tolerable variety of any place I have ever been to.

Why 4 stars?  Because it's my 2nd favorite sushi spot in Tampa.

   ---   ---   ---

Nothing can beat Samurai Blue, but Soho Sushi is definitely the next best thing.  My friend and I shared the following:

Crab Rangoon:  4/5 - Once again, the best I've had outside of Samurai Blue.

Mexican Roll:  3/5 - Typical mexican roll.

Green Menace Roll:  4/5 - Very good roll with scallops.  My friend was not a big fan, but I really liked it.

Red Dragon Roll:  5/5 - This was the main event.  This roll was "Samurai Blue" quality.  I know this review should not be about another restaurant, but Samurai Blue will continue to be the sushi standard in mind until it's topped.

Aside from the food, the parking lot was a mess.  Luckily two cars left as we were pulling in, otherwise I have no idea where we would have parked.  The restaurant was only at about half capacity, so I wonder where people park when the place is full.

Our server was very nice, but the sushi took a very long time to come out.  

Lastly, and most importantly, they had a bowl of red and green peppermints by the door as you walked out.  I will return to Soho for the Dragon Roll, but mostly for the green mints.

Once a week At least!!!

The food is great staff is the owner and his sons, Never had a bad meal here. 
try the super sub italian style.

This place has the best veggie burger in Tampa.  Period.

I first came to J. Alexander's many years ago, and I'm sure I had something other than the veggie burger, but honestly I don't remember.  At some point I tried the veggie burger, and from that point on I've had nothing else ever since. I Googled the recipe and found something in the Orlando Sentinel but I am not positive about its accuracy. It mentioned brown rice as one of the main ingredients, and that seems reasonable to me given the texture.

The burger comes with a ginger glaze and Monterey Jack cheese, both of which i always skip.  Yes, its that good that it needs none of that.  Maybe a little ketchup and mustard for tradition's sake.  It also comes with string potato fries but I skip those as well.

I never go alone, whether coworkers or family, and they usually get something else despite my pleas for the best veggie burger in Tampa.  They get salads, fish, and other entrees which look amazing but I can't speak to their taste as I usually only get a little bite.  What I have tasted is good, but it has been years since I've ordered anything but the veggie burger.

I'm not sure rating a restaurant only on its veggie burger is smart, but there you have it.  If you want the most awesome veggie burger experience in Tampa, go to J. Alexander's.  No, angels will not come down from the heavens and sing when you bite into it, but if you've eaten the traditional Boca burgers, this one will be like a filet mignon compared to quality dog food.  Seriously.

P.S. The carrot cake is served warm and is absolutely sinful!
Every time I go here I find exactly what I need.  Very helpful personnel who are so happy to assist with your search and answer your questions.

My wife and I eat at Gavi semi-regularly when we are in town for the winter. When we want big and bold Italian food full of flavor and complimented by an extensive wine list, Gavi is always our first choice. They can be a little pricy compared to the big chains, but for the money you get large portions of delicious food. The portions are large enough that you can easily get two more meals out of it if you don't over eat. Their house creamy Italian dressing is superb, the spicy creama calamari is out of this world, and the ambiance is pleasant.

We are mostly vegetarian so my wife generally orders the baked mostaccioli or the pasta pomodoro and I order either the stuffed eggplant or the eggplant salad (ask for the eggplant to be served hot atop the salad). Every time we go the food is delicious and prepared consistently. We've had our share of inconsistent staff (recently a newer waitress brought a meat-based mostaccioli after we specifically requested the standard baked mostaccioli), but the food has always been among the best Tucson has to offer.  If you disagree, please post the names of restaurants that you honestly feel are better and we'll give them a try.

The last two times we've visited we have had Sara as our waitress. She is delightful, attentive, etc. She knows the menu and has been consistently great, so I'd recommend asking for her.

Now I know what all the accolades, long lines in Clark Park is all about! Yup, I FINALLY got to try Honest Tom's. We would be walking in Clark Park and see the long line as we strolled through the Farmer's Market. I also recall reading a nice article a year or so ago in the City paper. So, when i read another article in City Paper that they opened up a store front, I definitely made it a goal on my new restaurant "to do" list! So, on this beautiful, spring evening, my friend suggested we try Honest Tom's.....

I ordered the Chicken tacos. my friend ordered beef Burrito.
1. Taco - the chicken was tender , moist and flavorful. I loved getting Guacamole on a meat taco. Yum! Tacos were very good and a good deal for $6. They use chicken breast and the pieces were big pieces not shredded, which is my preference. 

2.  My friend liked her burrito, although she said the meat was a little bit greasy. The burrito was HUGE and was $8.00

The service was quick and friendly. They even sent my receipt to my email address.

FYI -  Tom said the truck will be be in action late March. Saturdays in Clark Park and M - F in the usual spot near Drexel.

We will be back again!!
Good enough, not exceptional.  I ordered an omelette crepe which consisted of: egg.  Maybe salt & pepper. I managed to request tomato but forgot to request cheese so it was rather boring...

went for a surprise dinner.  had a party of around 20.  the 'host' or whatever he was wouldn't sit us until everyone was there, despite the fact that it was supposed to be a surprise.  he also was very rude and raised his voice with me because 'i didn't know how a BYOB works...people like to sit and drink after dinner.'  then what's the point of a reservation?  finally got our table, but then there were little fruit flies all over the wall by our table.  flying around our heads and open wine bottles.  kinda gross.  despite this, the food was half decent.

I've been using this Pharmacy for almost 4 years now.  The staff is friendly and I guess Target pays and treats them well cause I've seen pretty much seen the same workers all this time. What makes the pharamacy a stand out than other pharamcies is the automatic refill feature.  Not only will they automatically refill your bottle when you run out, but if you run out of refills, no need to worry, they will call or fax your doctor telling them to write a new prescription. Therefore, going forward, you never have to worry about if you have enough medication. The pharmacy does all the work for you at cost of ZERO!!!  The only thing you have to pay is the medicine.

I am a raw fish fanatic, and am always willing to drop the extra money for quality sushi, but this restaurant is definitely NOT the place to do it.

My boyfriend and I ordered edamame for starters, and three specialty rolls. The edamame was dried out from being over-microwaved, and the rolls were the smallest/dullest $15 rolls I have ever seen in my life. 

The ambiance is really nice for a sushi restaurant, which is why I gave it two stars instead of one, but it's definitely not worth the low-grade fish, stingy portions, and lack of plate presentation.

We were so dissatisfied with this place that my boyfriend and I went to another sushi joint the next day. We spent half the amount, and got twice the amount of food WITH better quality. Sure, I had to sacrifice the nice lighting and table cloth at the other place, but my stomach and I left a hell of a lot happier.

Outstanding authentic Chinese food. The food is first rate. It's also a fun location if you want to explore because there is a banh mi stand in th parlor next door, a good Asian Market in the same plaza and a Chinese bun store across the parking lot. This restaurant is foodie heaven!

When I first came to Florida many years ago I remember Cracker Barrels being a interstate highway thing, and we'd drive over an hour just to eat in one.  It was a treat!  When they started building them amongst "the people" and they started building this one, I was high fiving myself.

I'm a huge fan of the meat loaf and the dumplings. My wife has a pancake addiction. This Cracker Barrel is as good an experience as the ones along the highways. Service is always friendly and quick even when busy, and I love the blatant merchandising as you enter and exit.   Their peanut butter brittle is divine. I'm a fan!

The red curry with chicken will make you smile.  The dish itself is a bit soupy, which makes it perfect to have with rice.  It is The seating is a bit cramped but the service is fantastic and extremely fast.  Lunch specials are very inexpensive and pretty generously portioned.  I still like Thai Orchid's red curry the best but this place definitely gives my beloved standby a run for its money.
Been here about three times and they have some of the freshest sushi in tucson, a little pricey and a little too night clubbish after 7:30 8:00 at night great lunch specials and service

I am happy to report since my last review I have done the 'bathroom dance'! Yup, if you haven't been to Earth Cup in a few weeks, come and check it out. looks lovely. Earth Cup has a bathroom and a new, beautiful floor. Added some cool tables and has an AC and heating system, so, you can come and hang out with the very nice employees and go shopping afterwards (which I did today). Earth Cup has had some physical changes, but still has the same coffee, wonderful, coffee. Same pastries, wonderful pastries. Same owner, nice employees. It's a neighborhood coffeehouse! And I am glad I live only a few blocks away!! Yay! OH PS - the soups and grilled cheese have returned as of today 2/3/2012!!!

Had their bun bo hue for lunch! Broth is pretty good but of course it won't compare to home made bun bo. Wish they gave more huyet though. It came w/ the usually bean sprouts, shredded cabbage, wedge of lime, and mam tom. They didn't have the herbs that usually come w/ bun bo, which was a downer. 

Their complimentary banh thieu was yummy!!! I was so full from their bun bo hue that I took the banh thieu to-go...it was still crispy 3 hours later :)
Excellent service for an exquisite dining experience. This place is not for the hungry but the dishes are absolutely crafted uniquely and delicious to the palate.

On Friday night, all I could think about was pad Thai. So I knew that with an afternoon to myself planned for Saturday, I was going to treat myself to a movie and some takeout on my couch. Smile was one of the only places on Grub Hub with free delivery, although there was a $15 minimum. I ordered my pad Thai and threw in some lemongrass soup with shrimp. 

The meal arrived hot within thirty minutes. The soup was just right - the perfect amount of spicy, sour taste, with fresh vegetables (still crunchy!) and three large shrimp. The pad Thai was enough for two meals. It yielded four cups (I measured). It was a bit drier than I would have liked but I did enjoy the fresh bean sprouts. Next time, I'll ask them to make it a little bit spicy, since there wasn't any heat. Next time will probably come very soon.

Soooo good! The fajitas  as are amazing along with the salsa. This place treats the steak for the fajitas like steak so good.  I love the #17 with the red chilaquiles.

Enjoy it's soooo good, and I do disagree my food is always flavorful and always great.
Strangely interesting.  Not your normal Tucson scene.  I guess its nice to change it up every once in a while.  Be aware scorpion = hangover.
I've been a fan for years. I went in early Jan '12 with a group. Food was fantastic!

Yelp to the rescue!

Always on the lookout for Friday Fish Frys I did a search on Yelp for just such and this place came up. It was close to work. Say no more. 

For $7.99 you can get three pieces of fish, fries and coleslaw. For $2 more you can make it an all-you-can-eat. One comment about this: if you want more fries and not just more fish they will charge you $.50. This doesn't seem to make much sense as my buddy commented, "Fries are probably cheaper than fish and wouldn't you rather have someone filling up on fries." Just sayin'...

I have been here long, long ago for breakfast and the look of the place has changed completely. It used to have that total greasy-spoon feel to it. The decor is all country themed, but it's clean and I like what they have done with it.

As I mentioned, this visit was all about the fish fry and, I gotta say, it was really good. If you order the all-you-can-eat, they start you out with the above-mentioned three pieces and you can, obviously, get more if you want.  The fish and fries were hot and crispy and the coleslaw was crunchy and not drowning  in dressing. The tartar sauce seemed to be homemade.

Service was pretty good. It took a while for our server to make it to our table, but once she did serve us she was on top of everything. Halfway through our first serving, she asked us if she should put in an order for more. Yes, please...

They do have a Yelp check-in offer that is currently living in my phone. Yup, that means I gotta go back.
Great Cali-Mex on best west coast!

Love coming here for lunch! The mexican buffet for $9.95 is AWESOME.  But if you dont want to gorge yourself, order the Carne Asada , I think it's number #7 Combo.  I also order the Cosido.  Lately it's been cold in Tucson (60's) and their cosido just warms me up.  I usually order the large bowl because I know, I wont finish it, but I get to take the rest home for dinner...it is delishhhh!

Pros: Get your food fast when you eat early.  
           
Cons: You might feel rushed, but that's due to the volume of people that come in because they like to serve as many customers as possible.

I come to this store against my will. My wife makes me do it.  But, since I'm here, I browse.  There's cool stuff for guys, like really nice knife sets, fans, and other gadgets.  The framed art section is nice but pricey compared to Joann's (yes, they make me go there too).  The staff is very friendly and there's 20% off coupons in the paper all the time.  I can't give it 4 stars because I'm not personally a fan, but this is a very nice store if you need kitchen, bath, or bedroom accessories.

I am not sure why I haven't been here before. This southern girl is always looking for BBQ. I was driving down Kennedy and was starving.... out of the blue I say "I've never seen that BBQ place before".. mind you.. I have driven this way to work for the past 8 months. Anyway, we stop...and I was in heaven. Pulled pork, beans, coleslaw, hush puppies (which are damn good!) and sweet tea. What else can you ask for? I was really impressed.. this was some good stuff. No frills.. just good food.

Best pub atmosphere I've ever been to!   My boyfriend and I went during happy hour which is between 4-7pm every day! You get 1 dollar off all draft beers and snacks.  We used a coupon from the entertainment books which saved us 12 bucks on a meal, hence buy one get one free.  

As we walked in we were greeted  by a waiter whom said we can sit where ever we liked.  The space is cozy, with booths and tables that have a soft red colouring to accent the dark wooden floors and chairs.   Our waiter asked us if we wanted to try any of their 21 drafts they had on selection before deciding on a beer. *awesome! tasting free beer??!*
 I went with a Sam Adam's newest brew the Alpine Spring, it was a light tasting amber coloured beer.  
My boyfriend went with Strongbow cider. (beer)

For a starter we went with the southwest bourbon wings. Delish! The wings were crispy and the sauce was very mild and sweet, you didn't even need to use the ranch to take the heat away!

For meals I went with a challenge, the bangers & mash.  Which is 3 Irish prepared sausages layed upon baked beans and mashed potatoes and on top of your "bangers" are cooked onions which are transparent and cooked all the way thru.   I can honestly say I didn't eat the beans or onions because I don't like either so I can't give my 2 cents on  those but the bangers were savory with a slight crisp when you bit into them.  They were not spicy in anyway, a mild sausage.  The potatoes were mashed of course, plain but tasty.  

I'd def come here again because there are so many choices and options.  The waiter was very nice and knowledgeable on recommendations with your beverage and meal.

This was only my second time at Gavi's but I have been impressed both times.  I was in a button up shirt and black jeans which I think would have been appropriate for lunch, but I felt a little underdressed at dinner time.  Slacks and a dress shirt would have been more appropriate, maybe even a sport coat.  

The service was a little slow to start.  After taking turns to wash up, it was several minutes before we received any attention.  Once we were able to place our drink and appetizer order things got much better.  All of the food from the appetizer to the dessert was fantastic.  Much better than the chain Italian food restaurants that are closer to our house.  Not only was the food great, there was plenty of it.  Not one of us was able to finish our meal, so lunch tomorrow is going to be nice.  It is a little pricier than restaurants we frequent but it will be great for special occasions.   And we did get more than one meal out of it.
The food is so-so, but the manager is so incredibly rude that it's not worth going here even if your dinner is free. It's unfortunate for the servers because they are very nice.

TJ's is as autentico as it gets on Grant Road, and while the long picnic tables are often dirtier than necessary, the tacos and burritos are overstuffed and someone in the kitchen cares. The carne asada plate is delicioso, and is actually tender and is sweet and citrus-y like the classic South Side grilled version. It's best to take out at TJ's as the noise and open dining area are a bit much on a chilly night.

I live in the neighborhood and stop by from time to time.  Great food and atmosphere!  I don't mind the cash only policy, I just wish they'd serve their margaritas in pint glasses!  Bartenders are very friendly which is always a plus.

One of the best places to get Chinese food in the Tampa area.  The Dim Sum here is similar to places in NYC.  They offer a little more variety than other Dim Sum places in Tampa and are a little more creative with their dishes.   The decor is semi-upscale and makes for a nice dinner out with the family.

The best place in tampa for a nice casual meal in an upscale dining room. The service is the best, the food is the best. I always order the filet with bernaise sauce. It comes with a giant baked potato. For the appetizer, get the Mexico city spinach con queso dip. It's excellent. They refill your drinks before you completely drain them. You never have to wait for service here. A+!!!

Whether you're looking for the perfect caffeine fix to keep your day going, a delicious panini to lunch on, or plenty of chocolate and gelato options to satisfy your sweet tooth; Anthony's is a place that dishes it out with a classic Italian gusto!

As far as providing a coffee fix Anthony's delivers some of the best. And its espresso has gotten better since Vinny's informative review. But when I normally go for my caffeine fix here I normally go for their many varieties of Lattes, ESPECIALLY in Winter when they serve so many varieties that fit the season. For Lunch Anthony's features at least 8 sandwich varieties that are worth experiencing, and many of them are of the veggie variety!

But where Anthony's really shines is when it comes to satisfying your sweet tooth.  Next to Rim Cafe they might just offer some of the best hot chocolate options in the city. And they do have a decadent mix of gelatos that could give Capogiro a run for its money if they chose to run its flavors and market further. They do also have plenty of chocolates, especially of the covered fruit variety, as well as some decent cannolis.

Add on a website that features plenty of chocolate related gift packages, And you find an experienced South Philly Coffeehouse that can be VERY multifaceted.

This is the only place I go to in Tampa for authentic Cuban food and cafe can leche. 

However, do not expect anything fancy. 

DO expect a crappy, hole in the wall type joint, not so clean, with employees who could be in a good mood, or a bad mood.

Try to go here at off-peak times so that you have a better shot at getting decent service.

You go here for the food, not the ambiance or the service, period.

Best breakfast in Tampa but restaurant is too small and impossible to get a table at unless you arrive at 7 a.m. Then, if you do get a table, the place is so mobbed and small and the staff are so overwhelmed and annoyed that you get treated like CRAP.

They need to move to another location. Period.

Atmosphere is pleasant, as is the staff. The food, however, is just okay. I ordered a mixed green salad and veal medallions last night. The salad was lackluster, at best. The tomatoes had no taste, the dressing was a bit bitter. The medallions were okay, not memorable or yummy. The polenta looked and tasted like dull grits. The spinach was overly salty and garlicky. 
This is not Italian cuisine at its best!

Fantastic Vietnamese joint with a friendly owner who takes the time to explain the menu.  We ordered a combo platter that had vermicelli noodles, shredded cabbage, grilled pork and a sliced egg roll. It was served with a sweet chili like sauce. Phenomenal. Apparently the beef is even more popular but I thought the pork was pretty good. 

We both got a small pho ($5.85 each). The tables are equipped with all the necessary sauces and spiced needed for good pho. This places broth is the best. A little cloudy (where you know it's true stock and not just broth out of a can). 

Next time we go we are going to try the crepe and grilled pork chop dish.

I've reviewed the Columbia Cafe before, and I'm a big fan.  Today I came to revisit their Cuban sandwich as part of my quest to find the best Cuban sandwich in Tampa Bay. Any excuse is a good excuse to have a Cuban, incidentally my second one of the day.

The black bean caviar is outstanding.  Think hummus but with black beans. It's served chilled topped with onions and surrounded by traditional Cuban crackers. I love it.

They make a fine Cuban here. The bread is outstanding and the pork is delicious.  However, I am partial to juicier pork. I took pictures of the pork, and it resembles processed sliced pork, but it isn't.   Having tried the Caribbean Sandwich Shop earlier today, the difference is noticeable.  However, this Cuban is worthy to make it onto my list. 

The black bean soup is about the best I've had anywhere, and the Spanish sausage appetizer is delightfully full of oil and grease.  Pour that over white rice and you are in hog heaven. 

I'm a big fan of this place.  Highly recommended.

Wow, the food is great, but what's up with the music??? It's like being in a dance club with a booming drum beat going nonstop. Food deserves 5 stars, music -1, for a total of 4 stars. Please, we love your food, but the thumping loud music has got to go!

You first enter this establishment and the first thing you smell is dirt.  The bar has a foul odor that permeates the air, that combined with filthy carpeting greets you at the door.  

The food is good.

Service leaves much to be desired.

I was very unhappy that I chose this restaurant to bring friends from out of town, they really must think I'm craZy for suggesting such a place.

It used to be HOT, now its NOT!  Not for me anyway.  I hope they read the reviews and make some much needed improvements.

Duck spring rolls in a Frankford ave bar... show me another like this. Nice selection of beers, friendly service, nice patrons... what do you want besides this in  bar?
Try the fried pickles, no really try them... seriously.

I just finally decided to come here, and I'm not a coffee drinker.  But I do drink hot chocolate and I've ordered this a few times.  I like it better than the Dunkin Donuts version.

The pretzel cocoa is really good and even better if the owner makes it for you.  It has caramel syrup, whip cream, and a shot of salt which surprisingly fits in well!

I'll have to make this a once-in-a-while splurge because $3 for a small can quickly add up over the course of time.

My first visit to Antigone completely surprised me.  I don't like 4th Ave.  It's fairly dingy, dirty, and crawling with people, some of which are highly questionable, particularly when the sun goes down.  Therefore, I thought Antigone would be more of the same.  But not so!  The store is large, clean, and inviting.  Their book selection is solid.  They have tons of quirky and whimsical tchotchke type items.  They have lots of novelty items like bumper stickers and such.  It's a very interesting store to walk through.

Their prices are generally what's marked on the cover of the book per the manufacturer, so I wouldn't say they're great in that regard, just run of the mill.  They do have decent used and sale sections, though, so don't forget to look through those books!  

The service was cold.  Very cold.  I expect a LOT more from a local bookstore if you want me to come back to that store.  Particularly a store that's in a frustrating area, with little to no available/convenient parking.

Overall, Antigone is a decent independent bookstore.  I'll definitely go in and maybe buy something when I have the displeasure of being on 4th Ave.

Five star service, good sangria and large selection on the menu from paella to fish to veal dishes. We opted for the Tapas. All were good but some we liked more than others. To my surprise the wild rabbit my husband ordered was fabulous as was the pimento stuffed with veal. Meatballs also great and my husband really enjoyed the littleneck clams in a different sauce from what he usually gets. Chorizo, which I love, was somewhat disappointing in the chorizo gratin. The cold shrimp and crab with remoulade was good and would have been better if the sauce was not so sweet and a bit more the new Orleans style. We will definitely  go back to try new things and revisit the things we loved. Yelp directions totally off. Location is a block south of Hudson so no need to go to Hudson and make a U turn. We went on Sunday evening and were so happy that we had reservations. Highly recommend making reservations.

Yes these places are hit or miss.  Always have been.  You might have had a bad experience at a diner like this.  Problem is, diners open 24/7 with a lot of choices on the menu are going to screw some things up.  All I can tell you is how my experience was.  

In my case, thumbs up.  Anytime I can get a nice plate of eggs with some turkey sausage that doesn't suck, I'm good.  Anytime they don't charge me extra for a bagel instead of white toast, I'm even better.  Getting a good plate of eggs, turkey sausage, a bagel and butter, and hash browns for 8 bucks total, and they win my good graces.  

I might not drive past the Penrose Diner to get here instead, but it's worthy.
Deliciously simple and straightforward Italian fare with attentive service and very reasonable prices.  Reminds me of a few of my favorite haunts in Jersey!
I met a nice punk girl here one Saturday, back before the name change or whatever.
Average sushi-not as good as I remember it being.

The hunt for the best Cuban sandwich in Tampa Bay continues at Brocato's.  Judging by the crowd at 11 am (see posted pic) this place must be awesome!  The line to order and pickup stretched outside and around the building.  

"Let's try another place" my partner in crime suggested. 

"No!  Let's do this!  The hunt for the perfect Cuban cannot stop!" I replied.  It was that or " nahhh, we're already here". I can't quite remember. 

About 40 minutes after  we got there we finally got our Cubans.  The place is a mad house!  In a good way. :)

Aside from their sandwiches they seem to be famous for their devil crab cakes and fried stuffed potatoes.   This Cuban quest is starting to pack the pounds, so I opted not to try either of them, but made a mental note to come back.

The Cubans were huge (we ordered regular size and they were still huge!). The bread was yummy and the pork was delicious and juicy.  However, the inside of the sandwich was cold.  I don't like that.  If I order it pressed, I expect it to be hot.  It wasn't.

That small quibble aside, this was a very good Cuban.  It had plenty of pork even though ham dominated, as any good Cuban recipe requires.  But the pork was very juicy and definitely not processed.  That makes a great Cuban in my book.  They added lettuce and tomato, which is a minus, but that can be easily remedied when you order.

Despite being cold inside, I will add this to my list of best Cubans because it was a very good sandwich.  I have to admit I am getting cubaned out.  If I eat another Cuban I think I am going to scream.  Tomorrow I am trying Datz and am not sure I will be able to order another Cuban.  And there is about 92 other places in Tampa that claim to have the best Cuban.

Oh boy....

Burger Culture Food Truck offers gourmet burgers, french fries and milkshakes.  I ordered the Greek burger and my sister ordered the black and blue burger.  I assumed because our burgers were $8 a piece that they came with french fries or chips... this was unfortunately not the case.  That said, the burgers were huge and tasty!  The Greek burger was loaded with feta cheese, cucumbers, tomatoes and tzatziki sauce.  The black and blue burger had BBQ, apple slices, bacon and blue cheese.  Yum!!!

Some of the "fancier burgers" are a little more expensive, but well worth it for these innovative masterpieces! Would definitely recommend trying!

Alright I'm a white boy from Maine.  I moved here last summer and had yet to have a Cuban.  And from the reviews, this place sounded like the place to lose my virginity since it's less than a mile from my place.  Lots of buildup in the reviews left me saying "really?" when I opened the white wrapping.  It's a ham/salami/turkey/swiss sandwich heat pressed.  That's a Cuban?  Not saying it was bad... hey I'm giving the place 4 stars - it was good.  Just was underwhelmed by how American a Cuban is! (Or Italian arguably.)  The place was packed at 2pm so you know they have a lot of regulars - meaning this is a good spot.  Prices are cheap.  The strip mall this place is in is a little shady looking, but go for it. It's good stuff.

I tried this place because it's literally a block from my apartment and I'm always looking for new places to eat on my day off. I had the beef tacos, which were very tasty but incredibly overpriced. You get two tiny tacos for $6 and after eating them I was just as hungry as before I ate them. Also, I was the only person in the place and when I made my to go order the guy at the counter seemed annoyed that I interrupted his coffee drinking time. 

Conclusion: Overpriced hipster taco joint, won't be getting my money again probably.

Healthy fast food? Yes please. This is my go to when I want to eat everything from McDonald's. I can go here, have a really tasty healthier option, air fries and a shake and not feel guilty. What's not to love? Plus they have gourmet ketchup... I mean, really.

Awesome park that makes me miss Mayor Pam.  First class and a pleasure to visit with kids and adults too. It's clean, well maintained and feels safe despite being in the heart of downtown. Ice skating was a cool addition. Proximity to the Glazer Museum is a plus too!

like the cheesesteak joints
qdoba's always nearby
but a little worse
Avoid lunchtime at all costs unless giant crowds of babbling Penn kids are your thing. Avoid move-in week all together.

The restaurant feels very family orientated and a place where regulars go.  I love that type of establishment where the customer and waiter, waitress gets to know each other and look forward to seeing each other every week.  I saw ladies from the red hat society arrive for a lovely breakfast with each other, some war vets inside.  The biscuit and gravy was amazing, the gravy was so smooth and not salty which tends to happen when white gravy is made.  The biscuit was so light and fluffy, the bacon was crispy and not fatty.  The eggs were cooked to perfection and the potatoes had a slight seasoning to them.    It was a little pricey for a small place but I thought it was worth it because we need to remember the economy isn't it's greatest.  
Support local businesses!

Soooo sad!! This has been my go to place for the most amazing fettuccine alfredo in Tucson - they used to have beautiful, tender noodles bathed in a rich smooth alfredo. My mouth waters as i think of it. We chose to dine there last night and i ordered my standard with grilled chicken--What I got was a gluey hunk of al dente noodles with a dry, grainy alfredo - I almost cried! Talked to the waitress and she said they've recently made some changes --noooooo! Bring back the old recipe! Why fix it if it wasn't broke!!! Gonna give it one more try before moving on.

Simple. I like strong coffee and when in Suburban station I would PREFER Passero's coffee over the chains - Dunkin Donuts and Starbucks. This morning I got a cup of dark roast - "Black Gold". Yum! It is strong, without the bitter, burnt taste. Nice. Always good service and nice, cheerful employees. I have had espresso machiato's with extra foam and my americano. Not sure where they get there coffeee from but is very good.

Very worthy farm to table little cafe somewhat tucked away in downtown Tampa.

As a native Tampa Bay'er gone west coast, I'm always somewhat apprehensive about returning to the area and finding quality eats so I was very pleasantly surprised to find this sorta hidden gem on the waterfront in Harbor Island.

My friend who turned me onto this place actually knows Chef Ty who stopped by our table to tout the virtues of his latest creations....a brand new menu had just been introduced.

Ty is a fireball of energy and clearly passionate about his craft...so much so he started his own version of 'Edible' called 'The Local Dirt' focused on the sustainable movement around the area.

And most of the ingredients at Cafe Dufrain are sourced within 100 mile radius! 

Personally I had the Hot Brown which is a seasonable tribute to the KY Derby that I highly recommend. My friend gave his sloppy joe a glowing review and next time I'm keen to try the fish tacos featuring red snapper & scallops.

Worth the little bit extra effort to cross the bridge and plant yourself outside with a killer view to match the killer food.

***WARNING*** This company is a SCAM company.

We called them out to have the kitchen drain unstopped. He couldn't do it. Said there was more of a problem in that the pipe came to a "T" and after working on it for two hours he couldn't navigate his snake to clear it out. He said there was a more serious problem and pulled out his book of services and charges, called his superior at the office, and was advised to tell us that my landlord tear out the cabinets, sink, and wall to get to this "problem"--all for $2200!!! Right. We got another plumber out and he had it unstopped within 20 minutes total time. That includes the time it took for him to take his snake out, set it up, and run it. He said the actual time it took to get it unstopped was about 11 minutes.

They would not refund their charge of $165 or return my landlord's check. The plumber wanted to meet my landlord somewhere and "swap checks." Shady. She instead called the owner and said she would be down to get her check. When she arrived, he already deposited the check. They gave her a refund of $65. I can understand a service call fee, but they should refund her $100. They didn't unstop the drain after all.

A very nice little find on davis island. surprisingly good. had lunch there this week and was pleasantly surprised. friend staff. Try the corn fritter as an appatizer as I read in a long forgotten review-  imagine the consistency of a funnel cake with light and airy with corn blended in. Will be back, surprised it took so long to get there in the first place

Why 4 stars?  Because it's another Seminole Heights gem.

   ---   ---   ---

Another visit to a restaurant in the SHeights, and another moment of self-reflection where I ask myself, "Why the hell didn't you come here sooner?!"

I enjoyed my lunch Hans Solo, only in the company of some yelper reviews on my iPhone and some Front Porch goodness.

My order:

Duvel Golden Ale - 4 stars - Beer.

Chimay Blue Grande Res - 4 stars - Beer.

(I already feel the glaring judgment.)

(It is not normal for me to have two different 9% beers by myself at noon on a Saturday, but my new obsession with Belgium beer won out over my normal routine.)

Porch Bouillabaisse - 3 stars - In the defense of Front Porch, I didn't know what this dish was, nor do I have any clue how to pronounce it, even now.  I took the advice of a yelper or two and took a chance by ordering something I didn't know much about.  It wasn't bad, but I wouldn't order it again.  However, then we had a different story...

Banana Heaven - 72 stars.  Yes.  Si.  Oui.  Ja.  Sim.  Ken.  Sea.  Jes.  Hai.  

In this case, taking the advice of the yelpers more than made up for the average Bouillabaisse.  This was one of the best desserts I have ever had and should be a requirement to order, if ya know what's good for ya.

It makes me kind of sad that Tampa's goto Latin places are the Taco Bus & Brocattos. Sometimes I wish people would look around and realize hey there are no Hispanics eating here?

Vallarta's and Guadalajara are a couple of the many places I consider authentic Mexican cuisine. They aren't the greatest but they're authentic. You don't walk into a bar in Matamoros for taco's and get incredible food thats from the best cuts with all of the sides. What you get is a taste of people who have made the best ouf of what was given to them. The cheap cuts, leftovers, and things no one wanted is what they transform into magic and THATS authenticity.

Iavarone's is tucked away off of Dale Mabry and is really worth the trip if you live in Tampa. When Malio's closed I was so sad that I couldnt goto my favorite happy hour spot and some of my favorite meals. Thankfully his twin Carmine is around and serving up that same high quality food in a great environment.

Iavarone's thank you very much for keeping your picatta and food exactly the way your brother and you have been serving it my whole life!

Great selection of beers and great pub atmosphere. The food is consistent and there is a wide variety to choose from. I like the rotating drafts. The staff are all nice and the atmosphere is good for a night out with friends or with your family. I recommend a burger and waffle fries. Absolutely save room for the sticky toffee pudding. It's to die for.

We popped by for dinner and had a reasonable but not particularly memorable time.

This strip mall location is decorated with surf boards and they play a surfing movie. Honestly, it's hard to get in the surf mood in a strip mall. Perhaps if the floor was made of sand and we were getting sun burned...

The food was good, not great, and the prices were a bit higher than I expected for what we got. The solution had to be alcohol but even the $28 pitcher of sangria was weak.

Not being a major football or sports fan, other than the virtual fantasy type, I have to say I was impressed. The box seat accommodations included access to a large air conditioned area where you can relax, surf the web on your iPad, buy drinks, and enjoy life while others sweat it out to watch a often repeated sporting event.

Considering the tickets were free, this was definitely worth the price of admission. I really thought it was a nice stadium, and I'll return, the next time free tickets are offered.

Oh, I think the buccaneers won but, it was preseason so nobody cared.

5 stars is for the espresso... They really know how to pull a great espresso ristretto here. (I used to be in the coffee business, so I know a good one when I taste one). It's oh so rare to find espresso THIS GOOD in the Old Pueblo. For this reason, I'll come back to Sparkroot every time I'm downtown for one of the best espressos I've ever had in Tucson- or anywhere for that matter.

Sparkroot is a contemporary coffee house. It's a great gathering place, and I also love that they have free wi-fi. It's wonderful to come here for people watching or just hanging out. Trust me, you'll feel transported to another place. (This doesn't feel like Tucson). Try Sparkroot if you're looking for an escape from the ordinary Tucson grind. 

I always feel like I'm in San Francisco when I come here, and it makes me want to book a flight to the city by the bay! Sparkroot- you could become an EXPENSIVE habit if you bring out my wanderlust like this... I've yet to try the food, but I will someday soon... It's on my list!

While walking around Reading Terminal with a friend who was visiting from the U.K. she mentioned that she has never had a Cheesesteak. Well to our apparent luck Carmen's was just in sight.

After ordering we sat at the counter and were constantly being interrupted by some young kid who worked here. He was trying to flirt with my friend unsuccessfully...he just couldn't get the clue that it wasn't even going to happen. Our steaks arrived and after one bite we looked at each other with utter disappointment. Tasteless except for the flavor from the off tasting mushrooms. I apologized to my friend...after all, I felt bad that I took her here for her first taste of a true Philly classic. I can't wait for her to visit again so I can make up for my lack of judgment.

So for years now I've eaten sonoran style hot dogs, it all started on the south side of Tucson late at night. The whole BK and Guero Canelo thing.. I literally didn't think there was much of a difference years ago and would have been content with either of them. I moved away to nor cal surprised that sonoran style hot dogs were a tucson thing, lol... duh on my part, in retrospect.

Anyway I came back to visit once and a while and of course I had to get a sonoran dog and eegees everytime I visited. About a year ago I noticed that Guero canelo sonoran dogs started not being as good. I was perplexed... was my taste changing? Did I have false memories? I didn't get it at first. 

So I finally moved back to town and had another Guero Canelo sonoran dog with more time to reflect and I figured it out.... Guero went totally mainstream lol. He made the hot dog smaller and the bun way way bigger and the result was something that I didn't even really recognize. Once I figured it out I was like well damn I hope BK's didn't sell out too and try to give me a "sonoran bun-dog" and needless to say they didn't. 

After eating my BK sonoran dog I had flashes of memories when I traveled to the south side of tucson, afraid to maybe get shot all in the name of a freaking good sonoran dog. BK is now the king of the Sonoran dog in my book and I won't bother going back to Gueros for their sold out version. I was pulling for you Cinnamon Blonde but you let me down. Bring back the real version or I shall continue to tell everyone BKs is the real deal lol.
Excellent food and atmosphere. Definitely one of the best restaurants in Tampa Bay.

I know this place gets awesome reviews so that has brought me in a few times, but I really just dont get it. The decor is really nice but other than that the food is just ok.. The price isnt all that great either.. $12 for so so plain french toast is on the  borderline of rip off.

also doesnt anyone find it slightly unsanitary that they leave an open can of jam on the table all day, what if the table before seated someone with a cold? maybe its just me but i wouldnt touch it.

This place has changed alot over the years and not for the better. Owners are not friendly. Prices have drastically increased and depending on who services you, your salad may be a healthy portion or a few fork fulls. no one ever knows the prices of the cakes, sometimes it takes 15 minutes for someone to finally get a price. also seems like the prices change for different people. It's kind of disheartening considering I have gone to this place for years mainly because it's convenient for lunch at work.

Went for birthday - outstanding! Staff was very friendly, and quite helpful pointing out items that could be prepared gluten free. Atmosphere was old school east coast. The food was absolutely delicious! Highly recommended.

Why 4 stars?  Because the key to my morning heart is a good omelet.

   ---   ---   ---

2+ years living in Seminole Heights

Several hundred occasions driving right past Three Coins Restaurant

Zero trips inside.  

I just wasn't sure what to expect in there.  It recently dawned on my curiosity to check out Three Coins on Yelp, and the reviews guided me to finally stop in.

What I got was a better version of Denny's, that's closer to my house...and most importantly...locally owned!  Mmmm....local....(Homer drool sounds)

The girlfriend needed to catch a 6:55am flight, which meant I was up at 5:30am on a Sunday.  This typically would cause me borderline schizophrenia, but I suffered through waking up when it was still pitch black outside, knowing that a new Seminole Heights gem was on the docket for the morning.

I braved this venture alone, as not only were none of my friends awake at that ungodly hour, but I believe only 11 people in the city of Tampa were conscious during this time.

My omelet hit the spot, big time!  The fact that I was sitting across from a prostitute that was just getting off "work" for the night, was a bonus.  I got to listen to her haggle with the waitress over how much extra jelly cups would cost and why she couldn't do substitutions for free on certain menu items for take-out orders.  I couldn't tell if these silly restrictions were legitimate, or if she was a regular trouble maker that they were trying to eliminate from coming back.  Either way, I got dinner and a show.

So I met this dude who told me that Han Dynasty wasn't awesome. I heard his arguments about it being "too spicy" and how he wanted something more "like regular Chinese." He also told me to "stop hurting him" and that he had a "wife and child" later on in the conversation. I'm not sure. I couldn't hear him with the pounding of my own heartbeat in my ears. I also couldn't see him, as my vision had gone completely crimson.

Oh. 

Well.
Ahem.

Han Dynasty is fucking awesome. If you're a fan of the Old City location, you'll be a fan of this location. There are subtle changes in the food compared to the old city location(for example, the spicy cucumbers in chili oil are a little bit more garlicky here(better) and the three cup chicken is cut into larger pieces(worse) but all of this is nitpicking. This is still Han Dynasty and it still rules.

Get the Dan Dan noodles for the love of all that is holy.

After my first time at this location, I wrote a song about it - 
http://bradpodraymusic.com/handynasty.cfm

Based on reviews, we made a special trip to buy two packages of their medium tortillas. ($3.50 per bag). I've never really liked tortillas before, but I'm eating these as snacks, they are so good. And they're "just" tortillas!

Will have to go back and try their menu items.

Great Specials, Friendly Staff.  Place is great for Happy Hours and non Peak visits.  Way too busy during those friday and saturday nights.  Cannot / Have not ordered food their because its mostly fried in the same grease as Shellfish, which i am allergic to.  Great place to watch sports (especially if you're trying to find a non philly team!)

Why 3 stars?  Because there's no 3.5 option.

   ---   ---   ---

This could easily be chalked up to one of those "expectations were set too high" experiences.  Because this was a good meal and I don't have any real complaints about the food, aside from the fact that I was expecting something more amazing than they could deliver.  I read some mouth watering reviews and pictures, which led to this internal hype machine.

My steak was good, but not the best I have ever had.  Same can be said for the rice.  The plantains were good.  The fries were fries.  The service was slow, as I have come to expect from all Cuban/South American restaurants.

I did have my first experience with a Chicha Morada drink.  The server's english was broken, the best way he could describe it is that it was a, "non-alcoholic, corn base."

That doesn't sound so great, but I was in an adventurous mood.  It was tasty.  Like wine with no alcohol and more sugar.

Will I go back?  Yes.  I absolutely will.

Close to southern country breakfast as you can.  May I recommend the cinnamon roll! It is so light, fluffy and sweet and for only 2.99 you might as well share it because its big.   

When you first walk up to the Hungry Fox it appears small and doesn't look that busy.  As you open the front door on a Saturday morning their little knick 'knack  section is filled with people waiting to be seated in the dining area.  My wait was about 15 minutes for 2 people.  Seems like an average wait.  As you are standing around because chairs are limited in the area, there are several handmade items for sale.  

As we were seated to our table the dining area was small and had the New York attitude, take your order, small chat, serve your food then leave.   I had no complaints with this establishment good fast food breakfast.

I've been here several times and the food has been pretty good for the most part. I just get the beef burrito and it's usually done very well. On Sundays, they only have breakfast burritos consisting of potatoes, eggs, and bacon which is really good, but really heavy.

Unfortunately, the last time I was there, the service and food was surprisingly bad. I don't know what was going on (it wasn't even particularly busy), but the food - 2 burritos - took forever to come out, had the wrong ingredients in it, and the beef was overcooked so much it was nearly inedible.

Still, it's worth a go. Hopefully that last meal was a major fluke and our next order won't be as awful.

I was hungry yesterday, and my friends went to party city, and I went here instead.  I asked the waitress what was in the veggie sandwich, and she told me it was all their veggies, but nothing really stood out so I ordered a cheeseburger. Asked her for a small cup of fries, she said, "So you don't want the topper?"  apparently thats where they top the bag off with a million fries.  "No" I said.  I was pretty happy with my small fries and small cheese burger.

As an avid Vietnamese cuisine eater, I have to say this is one of the best places to get Pho, and Com Chien (Fried Rice).  I used to go to the one in Pinellas Park, but since I live in Tampa now, I go to this one.  You can't go wrong with the Pho soup!  The rainbow drink is awesome too, but they don't give you enough.

As a frequent visitor, good food at a great price. Tropichop bowls are great for lunch. Much better than fast food. I usually hit the Waters Ave location as it seems cleaner and better run than the Hillsborough Ave store. Food is comparable at both store, which is an accomplishment in any restaurant. 

Salsas are nice and fresh tasting, these locations push out a ton of food, and it's always really good. 

See ya at lunch time! Tropichop and a drink for $5? Love it...

C'mon guys, clean this place up so i can raise my stars.

Hands down, my new favorite craft beer joint in Seminole Heights. I had heard or read some vague rumors about this newer place, so I had to try it out. The new owners have taken what was evidently an older dive bar and totally cleaned it up, without destroying its 1950s-70s ambiance. They have pared down the original nautical theme, lit it properly in a darkly moody way, and have come up with a real winner. The only night I went, it was packed inside and outside on the deck, where smokers are confined. Although it has a strong wine selection, I was only interested in the craft beers. They have an excellent selection, not the usual 50 taps we are now seeing pop up everywhere, but a more curated selection that I am sure will be rotated regularly. The music was DJ on the night I went. I would like to see some live music in there, and perhaps that may come in due time.
The clientele was the usual Seminole Heights riff-raff, so different from the post-college brats who habitate Soho and Hyde Park, in a refreshing way. I will continue to return here, as often as possible. I recommend this place highly, if you are looking for above average wine and craft beer selections. The bartenders really seem to know their products very well.

3 STARS! 

Fresh sashimi, but the sushi rice could use some work. Delicious rolls but very typical like the "candy cane" roll with both red and white tuna sashimi on top of a california roll. Volcano roll is yummy as always. Many of the sushi chefs are not Japanese though. Not sure if that makes a difference but overall, I had a good experience. Servers are friendly and helpful. Jammed pack on a Monday evening, which means business is good for them.

I have never had what I would consider to be an excellent meal at The Front Porch.  I have been there multiple times and the food was always just OK.

Anyway, it's not that expensive and the food is not bad (but not great).  So, I guess you could do a lot worse.  But for the money, I would rather spend a hair more and go to Ella's for some tasty goodness then hang out at The Front Porch.

This place is really cool.  I took an older work friend to lunch last week and I picked Couch Tomato Cafe as Manayunk is pretty quiet at that time of day on a Tuesday and I knew I could park really close for him to walk.  We ate in the Bistro section on the second floor at a table with a great view towards Main Street.  The pizza was perfect - I had like a duck and gorgonzola one that I def would order again, the homemade soup was great, and the waiter from Brazil gets thumbs up for his friendly service.  Best of all, my older friend who is not much into going out to eat or food except for the standard stuff he eats at home loved the place. I like this cafe/bistro a lot at night but lunch at the Couch Tomato has its own positive vibes.

First the good - the tasty, authentic food. My husband was raised in the Basque region and he was pleased with everything we ordered (went tapas-style with no entrees). The pate, charcuterie, and mollejas were fantastic. Despite the food, there were several issues with the restaurant... There are far too many tables crammed in the small space. Our 'reservation' was meaningless - they were packed and we had to wait at least 30 minutes to be seated. And in general the ambiance was poor. They need to at least dim the lighting to make the atmosphere a bit more high end.
it has great Vietnamese and thai food but below average chinese, however, the thai iced tea and Vietnamese iced coffee is amazing!!!

Desert Toyota was very nice and helpful to me.  They were not pushy when buying a car and were willing to tell me what I needed to do.  Although I didn't buy a car from here I thought they were helpful and nice.

This restaurant is mostly sushi, but also a Korean restaurant.  Since the menu is stuck in between the two cuisines, you will not get the best of either.  While the sushi is decent, you can have better food at a strictly Japanese style sushi place in Tampa.  (Unless you are someone that just eats California Rolls, which is tragic.)  

While the Korean food is decent; it is an overpriced, limited menu, with very few panchan served.  You can get better Korean food at other Korean restaurants in the area for a better price and a more authentic taste.  (One Family, Rice, Soul of Korea). Matoi does not even serve lettuce wraps.  It is depressing when a Korean restaurant needs to serve sushi just to make the mainstream population happy.

I am only assuming that this location is some relation to the Sushi Sam Oh Jung located in Bradenton, FL that I often frequent.  They suffer from the same issues, but luckily for them, there are no other options for either Korean or decent sushi in the area--- which is why you will see me there when I cannot make the drive to Tampa.

LOVE! We stopped in last night,the place is comforting.
the d.j.'s not to loud,you can still talk.great stuff on the projector and amazing drinks! I was actually in shock over the prices,i felt i needed to pay more it was so reasonable!
its a really cute place,the staff is amazing! :D
I ill be back.I just found my new watering hole.
the porch outside under the big tree is nice also.
I love this place!

One of the better Viet restaurants I've visited in Tampa Bay. And we have tried many!

The make your own roll looks real appealing. We enjoyed the Pho but Restaurant BT has the off-the-chart best. Broth needed some more love. Rice dishes and noodles were good. Service was pleasant and efficient. 

It's nice having good food that's priced well and you are not worrying about cleanliness.

Had this for lunch and had high hopes but all I can say is SO SO for SoHO. Maybe it's the lunchtime "rush" but wasn't impressed. Small collection of rolls... lots of imitation crab rolls... I'm willing to try again but best in tampa... not so much

My wife and I went here for dinner on a night where all of the proceeds went to Northlight Community Center.  While I enjoy supporting Northlight, the food at Le Bus was just not good.  We ordered the nachos, a grilled chicken sandwich, and meatloaf.  The nachos were OK I suppose.  My grilled chicken sandwich was extremely dry and my wife's meatloaf was something I'd expect from a hospital.  The timing of the food's arrival was not ideal (our nachos took forever and then our entrees came when we were still working on the nachos) and the server hardly seemed to know what they had to offer.  Hopefully I don't ever have to go back to this place.

It's a little tiny hole in the wall place.  They have a small variety of Chinese pastries which seemed odd because I swore the local stores have a larger variety (which they supply.)  The prices are good: I selected 7 pastries and the total was under $7.  They only accept cash.  Fortune Star is an average place.  Comparing this place to the Chinatown in NYC, bread is a little harder and it isn't as flavorful.  It isn't bad but it isn't the greatest.  Compared to the place in Orlando, they are neck and neck.

They do offer the Asian cake with fruit.  I asked the lady what varieties they make and she seemed almost as excited as a lump on a log.  So I ended it at that.

I've been enjoying this lunch buffet for years, but it seems that it's had the same spread forever.  I'd like to see a bit of variety.  The lamb meatballs are delicious, and the garlic naan is amazing (brought to my table, scalding hot).  The Tandoori chicken is usually dry and over-cooked, though.  The veggie dishes are somewhat bland.  The space is comfy and well-lit, and the Indian music is a pleasant touch.  I like the painted mountain scenes on the wall.

The service is fine, but what do you expect for a lunch buffet?  Serve yourself, and thank-you-for-the-water.

The tab:  about $9 (before tip) for an all-you-can-eat lunch buffet.

I have been to sevIeral Denny's locations in Florida and everytime I give 3 stars but this one is a little bit of a disspointment.  

The waitress service was good although food was not the right order and did not came out at the same time.
The quality of the food was lesser than what I'm used to at other Dennys, which is a little sad for me because I usually like their food.  

I do hope that the chef was new and the food was just off that night, since this location is the closest from my work and my friends and I often have late night munches after work!

Good sausage, fresh rolls, pretty good prices how could you go wrong? This is a drive up, grab a sandy and roll type of place. I like the hot sausage with kraut and fried onions (specify fried onions otherwise you get a bunch of thickly chopped raw white onions in your kraut). This place is a contractor favorite so at lunch time be prepared to muscle in for a sausage. Their breakfast sandwiches seems pretty popular as well. If you're down there at the casino or the rock gym and you lost all your rent money or scaled a 5.10 bouldering problem and want a spicy pick me up or a reward for all your hard work head over you won't be disappointed(at least until the heartburn sets in...) It's worth it.

I have been there two years in a row with my 7 yr old son who requested it as part of our NY Yankees Tampa spring training weekend. The food is GREAT and the staff is very accommodating. The four cheese whole wheat crepes were the best I've had. Definitely make your way to Ybor city and try this place!
Good food, but not the best Indian I've had. Their garlic naan is very good, but I wasn't wowed by their chicken tikka masala. The lunch special was a good serving size and great price.

Been coming here for years.  Had the meatloaf which I enjoyed.  My friend has peach sangria which she enjoyed so much, she went back for seconds.

My only complaint was that when we asked to have things boxed, we were handed boxes instead of having it boxed in the kitchen.  Its just a nicety that I tend to look for in a restaurant.  Bread basket is nice, especially the cornbread.

They need to fix their parking lot... it's always a nightmare.  I totally agree with Thao N "This target is a lot like its parking lot - people are all over the place, customers are lost, confused, don't know how to walk/drive/park, and not really courteous."

This is, by far, my favorite spa in the city.  My friends took me here for a bridal spa party many years ago and I've been hooked ever since.  Never had a bad massage or a facial.  The facillites are also great:  They have a steam and sauna room (they both work!) and a nice-sized pool.  The spa lunch now lacks selection, so it's not worth paying $$$ for, but it's a nice option for people wanting to spend several hours there.  
It's a bit pricey, considering that there are a ton of discount offers from other spas in the city, but why waste money on so-so service??

I am a lover of Indian food and this place gives me good special with curries that  I can eat for a reasonable price. One thing, though, the curries are very spicy and I am a person who usually like spicy stuff more than the average person.  However, this stuff is so spicy, I find myself eating mild. I can't keep up with even the medium stuff.
In addition to the curry and naan, they often given you plenty of free appetizers and spices.

The atmosphere is small, cozy and very cute. The servers are very friendly and attentive to your needs.  Can't comment how it is for dinner, but its a cute little place for lunch.

Look out Sandwich lovers! Another Jimmy Johns store recently opened in the Old Pueblo. This was actually my first time eating after passing JJ's countless times day after day. Its new Campbell location is much more accessible than the others, which is  why I gave it a try. Personally, I didn't find much of anything gourmet in my Sub, but they did live up to their slogan of "SUBS SO FAST YOU'LL FREAK." In true American style, I went through the drive-thru, and was definitely "freaked" to receive my sub upon paying! Okay, now down to business. Sub was ok. I ordered the #16 CLUB LULU (JJs original Turkey bacon club with lettuce, tomato and mayo). It's your standard club sub on white hoagie bread. I found the bread was dry and chewy. However, my beloved BF raved about his #12 BEACH CLUB (turkey breast, provolone, avocado spread, cucumber, sprouts, lettuce, tomato mayo), insisting that everything about his sub was fresh, delicious and crisp. I have ultra picky taste buds I guess. I'll give JJ's another shot and next time I'll order something less ordinary.

Can I just say...HOLY HEAVENS! It's Halibut season! I try to buy my fresh fish based on the appropriate season, so with that said, I of course went to see Uri at RIncon first to check out his fresh fish selection. Uri as usual had the freshest fish available and is up to date with fish season. At $28.00 per pound, the Halibut was worth EVERY PENNY! Uri is so confident in the quality that he makes you smell the fish and asks you "what do you smell?" (FYI. correct answer is: "I don't smell anything at all!") If you're in with Uri he'll give you a recipe to try out. Sadly, Uri never remembers me and every time I've gone in for Halibut he's given me the same recipe. Regardless, I give him 5 stars for his knowledge, outstanding quality and customer service, but I have to give RINCON 4 starts, simply because their produce section is depressing and the over all selection is limited and over priced.

For those who don't know Uri, I suggest you stop by Rincon Market for your next fish purchase or at least stop in to check out the fish selection if you're not convinced. His display is simple and clean; no fluff here. Uri will school you whether you buy the fish or not.



 Bottom line: URI carries the weight of this 4-star rating.

I have been going to Sage for years now, it is without a doubt my favorite coffee shop in town. It is family owned and operated and they make their own baked goods. The ambiance is quaint and a little hippy. After literally years of going, there are things that I have come to love about R+S:
- first and foremost (as others have mentioned) is their baked goods. To beat a dead horse, the scones are amazing, especially when right out of the oven (the only time I order one, or else I find them a little dry) YUM! If you're waiting in a long line, expect to feel the antsy energy from the rest of the scone addicts...as people try to make their way to the display in hopes that there will still be some scones left for them. 
- The Coffee. It is very strong and bold (I drink their espresso drinks/americano). Their espresso is done right, it has thin layer of crema on it and it is pulled just right.The caffeine keeps me going all day. To be completely honest at this point I am not even sure it is good (it's smoky and almost burnt), but I can't drink anything else, I have become addicted.
- The staff is always friendly and they have very little turnover. This place really cares about it's customers. The manager Kay has been there for 6 years and is always willing to please. 
- I like the fact that they DO NOT allow cell phones and do not have wi-fi. The place is not by any means quiet, but it allows people to have conversations with other people, which is nice for a change. People often complain about no wifi, but I can only imagine how busy this place would be if it did in fact have an internet connection. 
All in all, I really like this place and go on an almost daily basis for my daily caffeine fix. At times it can be a little hectic in there and the coffee isn't always consistent, but it's a great neighborhood coffee joint!

In Summary you should be there BECAUSE:

1. No one in Tucson makes a scone like these guys. Savory Scones come out at 10:30 (Olive and goat cheese, or Green Chile and Cheese)
2. Did I mention their Super-Natural Brownie???? TRY IT
3. The coffee is roasted locally. 
4. Coffee is like rocket fuel: you will be set for the day: small coffee has 2 shots, medium 3 shots!

Although my profile might make me out to be an Italian.....I'm actually MEXICAN. Yes, it true buey (dude in Spanish)...I from di border town known as Nogaaaales. 

So, with that said, I prefer to eat my Mexican meals in the comfort of my mothers fabulous kitchen, where fresh salsa is made weekly, Machaca and Eggs for Sunday Brunch with Fresh Avocado and chilaquiles....

But anyway...this review is about Taqueria Juanitos!

I give this joint a solid 5 stars...and there are so many reasons I'll just list them:

-Kitchen is so clean you lick the floor
-Fresh Fresh Fresh Carne Asada is always rotated on the grill top
-Consistently ripe and delicious Avocados in Stock
-BEST QUESADILLA IN TOWN: (besides my own or my moms): These guys use the legit Sonoran Flour Tortilla that gets Crsipy and Flaky, Not Chewy and Flavorless like the Gringo types served at Guadalajara Grill. I prefer my quesadilla with Cheese only, but if my stomach is feeling strong I'll go for a Quesadilla with Asada in it. TASTE BUD ALTERING.***
-The CEVICHE: Simple, basic, but solid in flavor and freshness. Order it on a Tostada. Its citrusy flavors really hit the spot when you're craving something light and crisp.
-did I mention the rice and beans are always delish? I'm personally not a huge fan of Sonoran style rice and beans (the lard doesn't agree with me), but these sides always compliment the rest of the meal.
-Red house-made salsa is a 4 of ouf 5. Flavor profile is good, but doesn't have the WOW factor.
-Service is fast
-ALWAYS a great place to catch a diverse group of people dining together. I've eaten many lunches among business men, young mexican families, gangster types, men in Tiger Print Spandex. EVERYONE COMES HERE BECAUSE THE FOOD IS GOOD, CLEAN, FRESH.

VIVA JUANITO!
Cool place!  Great selection of beer & wine!  Had a salad which was just okay.  Very cool atmosphere!  Parking not so great, but can't expect much in downtown!

Big slices, yes, but let's not pretend they don't recoup that in their prices.  Certainly not the best pizza I have ever had, nor the worst.  Typical selection of toppings and supporting menu items.  Therefore, I am very confused reading the five star reviews.  Zio's is your average pizza place in a city that only has average pizza - I can say that being born and raised here in Philadelphia.  Three star, maybe four star on a good day, fits the bill.
