I live in Manayunk and after eating at Winnie's several times I've come to the conclusion that it's OVER-RATED.  Definitely one of the better places to dine on Main St, but there's more to Manayunk than Main St!  Food & drinks are good, a bit pricey.  The free bread is a nice treat.  Dine here if you must, it's a good time once in a blue moon.  My advice: EXPLORE other areas of Manayunk & Roxborough, you will find better!

Why 4 stars?  Because that was quite an awesome meal.

   ---   ---   ---

After a long day out on the softball fields and a subsequent nap, I was in no mood to get dressed up.  On top of that, my girlfriend was in South Dakota for work and that's too far away for her to meet me for dinner.  I mean she COULD have done it, but I guess it would have been inconvenient.  And not that I couldn't have taken a friend, but it seemed like a date spot, more than a bro spot from the pictures/website/reviews.

I was only inside long enough to pick my food up at the bar, but it was definitely a classy joint.  I felt very under dressed even just to be standing at the bar for 5 minutes.  Then again, I was only wearing a t-shirt, flip flops and a hat.  Oh, also shorts and underwear.  Almost forgot that.

I had the ribeye sandwich and the lobster mac and cheese.  It was just a dandy way to end my Saturday.  It wasn't so dandy on my wallet, but my taste buds enjoyed it.  Afterward, my stomach was a little pissed at me for eating so much...but whatever, it's such a hard fella to please sometimes.

I hate to name other restaurants in a review, but I have to just go ahead and say it - J. Alexander's Prime Rib Sandwich was supreme over this.  As much I love J's, the Mac-n-Cheese at Capital Grille was a bit better.

I would like to take my girlfriend to Capital Grille the next time we are both in Florida.  That doesn't happen a lot lately, though.

I have red pinstripes in my blood.  Let's rule the NL East again Phils ... and maybe this year the dice will roll in our favor all the way to a parade on Broad Street.  And to my favorite Phillie - Cliff Lee .... I wouldn't mind another seven shutouts this year as well.

Last night, I checked out a highly rated place by Yelpers only to find I did not agree with them.  Today, I am happy to agee with the majority of Yelpers as I came here for lunch today.

Han Dynasty occupies a place that has gone through several Chinese tenants (I reviewed the previous tenant, Beijing Homestyle) and so, the place carries a curse of doomed Chinese restaurants to even this fellow spiritual skeptic. So, the Yelp reviews were not enough to persuade me.

Once I ordered and ate, I am happy to say I agree with them.  This is not the place to go if you want your typical take-out Chinese stuff. (Although, there is an section in the menu called Baby Adults for more typical stuff as fried rice).   So, go to your local take out in you want traditional Chinese.  But for those who are adventurous, the cuisines are unorthodox but made with a lot of detail to ingredients and spices.  In other words, this is not food just thrown in the stir-fry, they take their time to make an authentic but mouth-watering experience. Stuff can be very spicy, but they do warn you about spices as they have a scale from 1 to 10 with 1 least spiciest and 10 most spiciest. The scale is very accurate. I had a dumplings appetizer which rated a 4 and did require an extra class of water to wash down.

Given the good reviews and the many people that are coming here, this may be the place that finally breaks the curse of failed Chinese restaurants.

J'adore Le Rendezvous!!  How fortunate we are to have found this gem in the desert.  The dining rooms are charming, the service is without parallel and the cuisine surpasses all of the above.  Dinner is a luxurious event here, so please plan to spend at least a couple of hours indulging the senses.  (Of course, if you have more pressing matters, the staff will see that you're in and out in good time.)  My husband had the Foie de Vignon (calves liver) last night and it was succulent perfection.  I tend to have a bunch of hors d'oeuvres (OMG, do they do gooood hors d'oeuvres!!!) and a salad.  Last night it was escargots, the Tres Champignons en Croute (three mushrooms in puff paste), and their special of the evening, a crab souffle ... I'm still dreaming of the crab souffle.  The salad I love was no longer on the menu, but our server, Monsieur Raymond, created one specially for me and it was superb.  Last, but not least, was their exquisite Grand Marnier souffle for dessert.  (The Four Seasons in NYC should take lessons from the chef at LRV for this delicacy.)  We consider ourselves "foodies" and I cook for us 9 nights out of 10 and enjoy it.  Le Rendezvous is an absolute pleasure which we thoroughly enjoy.  For a milestone family event in early February 2012, we brought our daughters, sons-in-law, and grandchildren for a family celebration at LRV on a Friday night -- 10 people, including 2 highchairs, and we were there for hours.  We were treated like royalty.  As to practical matters, they have a good wine list, a full bar and  plentiful parking.  Don't miss this wonderful restaurant with its gracious hosts and talented chef.  Messieurs Jean Claude, Gordon, Daniel et Raymond, je vous remercie tous du fond de mon coeur.

Hey have you ever ducked into a place for the first time and there's no other customer in front of you at the counter where you order and the lady working there expects you to know what you want right away and looks a little annoyed when you need to look at the menu and check out the cases containing the food.  That was Gavin's.  When I asked a couple of questions about a couple of Argentinian items I saw on the menu and didn't know what they were, she seemed annoyed to have to answer.  I have no idea why - I just walked in on a nice sunny afternoon with almost no one else in the entire cafe.  Anyway, she said they were out of the Argentinian pastries that I wanted to try so I got an empanada - it was decent but not earth-shattering - and a soup which was totally ordinary and not worth it.  But food aside what I'll most def remember is walking into a place for the first time...eager to try it out and being met within seconds by a bad attitude.

I love wandering down 4th Ave to this bookstore on a weekend morning stroll.  Their quirky variety of books (my favourites that I came across were Filthy Shakespeare and Goodnight, Keith Moon; I also once found Toilets of the World) always perks up my day. They are also great about promoting local authors, new writers, and books featuring our beloved Tucson.
My favorite meal in Tucson.  The sonoran hot dog was outstanding.  I was unhappy with the food in Tucson until I are here.  You must try it, yum!

I have been going to Adolf Biecker for years & since I've just recently started writing reviews on Yelp, it seems overdue for me to write one about my hair care.

I get highlights, low lights, single processing (so all color) and Keratin done by Jonathan, who is amazing!  He takes the time to get to know your desires but exams your hair to give realistic recommendations.  He even asks about your lifestyle & work schedule to gage expectations. He has made my hair healthier and prettier than anyone else.

I know and have seen him also do Japanese straightening and other types of relaxers.  And, I have known other people to come to him after colorists from other salons have ruined their color if not the condition of their hair.  He's worked miracles!!

He even gets tough with me when I'm being stubborn and not listening to his best advice.  More often than not, he gets the opportunity to say, "I TOLD YOU SO"!!!!

Love Love LOVE HIM!!!  Go to him and no one else!

Do you have a movie in mind?  Chances are Casa has it.  No offense to Casa, but there used to be a terrible smell that you would have to bear in order to rent.  The bad smell is gone! I suspect the smell was from stale popcorn oil. The employees at Casa are very nice and knowledgeable.

When I first moved to Tucson, I was told that St. Mary's was the place to go for best Mexican food in Tucson. I finally made the trek to the West side of 10 to see what the best Mexican food in Tucson tastes like.

After looking over the menu on the back wall, I decided I would get the carne seca burrito, side of chips and salsa, and a small coke. The food was good. The burrito was a good size, not "as big as your head", but big enough.

What I really liked about the place was it's local neighborhood feel. There were a lot of people comming and going at lunch time and most of them seemed to be regulars. If I lived in the neighborhood, this would be a place I go to a lot. If you have never been here and are not looking for something fancy, I would definitely give it a try.

What is so famous about these hoagies and cheesesteaks???  Is it that they are the skimpiest in size and fillings that I've ever eaten in Philadelphia?  Is it that they're priced higher than most other places?  Or is it that they taste like something a restaurant in California would call a Philadelphia hoagie or cheesesteak and make for their calorie conscious clientele??

I'm def on a hoagie binge of late.  Stopped by Ricci's this afternoon to grab one to eat while taking in some innings of the Phillies opener.  I went with the basic Italian.  Tasted fine, decent quality of ingredients ... but nothing extra worth anyone driving far out of their way to eat one of these.  It was not nearly as filling as I expected but not small either.  Are people downsizing the portions in hoagies of late?  I also had no wait and could order as soon as I walked in.  The biggest plus about this hoagie was the roll.  Liscio hoagie rolls are fresh and crisp which I find awesome.  And they don't get soggy even when you get delayed actually consuming your take-out.

Yes yes yess!!!! This place is great.. Spring rolls large and tasty but most important the Pho!! This broth is amazing and tasty big chunks of beef... And the ice coffee was strong and sweet the perfect finish!!! I'll be back for sure
Typical mall enough store to stop into and has most of the ones I like, cool food court and it has a nice movie theater were me and my family usually come.

I dont know why but I love Sams club its something about it. I am not sure if it is because it has cool things to buy like for your home with sweet deals or because its like a one stop shop. 

I use to work at a Sams club in Coral Springs in the wireless kiosk which was not only good money but a cool time. Nevertheless like I said its a one stop shop. I actually came here this time because of my whole horrible time of getting my stuff stolen from my cousins car the night at Iguanas I had to get new glasses. I had bought my first set in the Sams in miami so I did not think it would be that much to get a new set..... WRONG it was about almost the same price. lucky thing I had got a discount so it went from $170 to $117 not bad huh?! 

The guy helping me was a pretty nice swift guy. He helped me find the same glasses but in brown and got them in about a week. I came to pick them up in that week and got a nice new case and my spiffy glasses. I even got great service from the ladies at the membership desk by getting a new FREE plus membership card to enter Sams. So all around like I great service wish the price would have been cheaper but the discount was cool.

Really wanted to like this place, and maybe we just caught it in an off day.  We encountered a noisy room, rude hostess, dirty tea warmer on the table, and cards expressing the owner's desire to pray for us.  Won't be back.

The service here couldn't have been better. No complaints there. No complaints about the all you can drink limocello at the end of our meal. It was the best part. And that's not hard to do considering how bad the food was. Over cooked pasta, bland sauces, dry as a bone fish.
The decor could have been overlooked if the food was better, but it just added to the misery.  Nothing would have been batter than that junk.
Mama, focus on marketing your limoncello. You'll do much better.

WestShore Plaza is a decent mall, but the store selection has gone downhill a bit.  

In my opinon the mall is lacking a nice selection of department stores.  The current department stores are JCPenney, Sears, Macy's and Saks 5th Avenue.  JCPenney and Sears are a little too low end for my taste and Saks is too pricey and too snooty.  Macy's would be the reasonable choice, but the department store is in desperate need of a renovation.  The dressing rooms and the bathrooms are dingy and musty and the clothing racks are very disorganized.  I miss Burdines; it used to be the best department store at WestShore Plaza!

The food court is not impressive, but outside the food court there are dining options such as Pink Berry, PF Changs, Mitchell's Fish House, Maggiano's, and Seasons 52.

The highlights of the WestShore Mall for me include Lens Crafters, Ann Taylor, White House Black Market, The Limited, and Maggiano's.  Other than that I usually go to International Plaza because the shopping and dining selection is better.

Don't bother, so many other much better pizza places in town. 

The portions are huge and lots of choices but pretty greasy.

The service is terrible. On my first visit at lunch time they were fairy disinterested in helping us and I had to ask a second time for my salad. On my second visit I stood at the counter for several minutes and was  never acknowledged by the teen ager standing two feet away behind the counter on the computer.  I  won't go back.

This is a nice little restaurant that is hidden away on Harbor Island. There is a lot southern-inspired food like the corn pudding, collards, and pork cheeks but all with an interesting twist. The atmosphere and service, however, was less than ideal and it is a bit out of the way. For these reasons we are unlikely to return, but I would certainly say it's worth a visit.
Best sushi in Tampa. They do interesting, creative rolls and have quality, melt-in-your-mouth sashimi. The drinks are excellent too. Definitely the best choice in the area.

Mitchell's Fish Market is a seafood restaurant located at the Westshore Mall.  Not only does the restaurant use the freshest fish in all of their dishes, but they also offer a fresh seafood retail counter where patrons can purchase the catch of the day.

As you enter the restaurant you'll immediately notice the raw sushi bar, catch of the day seafood showcase and the open kitchen concept.  I love the comfy private booths in the dining room. These booths are the perfect spot to reserve for a romantic dinner if you don't want to sit in the regular dining room area. The bar has plenty of high top tables and booths as well seating around the bar. This area of the restaurant is a fun place to mingle and grab apps and cocktails.  The cocktails are all handcrafted by the bartenders; no premixed mixers. One of my favorite cocktails on the drink menu is the pineapple martini. 

The dishes that have really stood out on the menu for me was the ahi tuna, calamari, seared scallops, and the chocolate lava cake. 

Because this restaurant is at the WestShore Plaza Mall it can get busy so I would advise making dinner reservations!  Avoid searching for a parking spot in front of the mall and park in the parking garage behind the restaurant instead. There is also the option to pay for valet parking.

Alright Yelpers! 4-Stars, 4-Reasons


1. For me it's like home walking into Tavolino. The hostesses greet you with huge smiles and Davide (manager) is always there to greet you with a kiss and a hug.  Head Chef, Eduardo always makes sure to step out of the kitchen and say hello. Friendships are made here while enjoying delectable Italian goodies

2. Tavolino is great for a special night out, a family gathering or a nice meal when you just don't feel like cooking. So Basically I'm  saying it's a versatile.

3. Tavolino is probably the only place in town that sells Burrata( fresh mozzarella with a creamy center)...just typing the word made me hungry! It's always fresh and served with thinly sliced imported prosciutto, basil and tomatoes.   

4. Tavolino definitely has one of the Best if not THE BEST happy hour offerings in town. $5 pizza....Whaaat!? And it's thin crust, totally Italian style...you can taste the freshness of the tomato sauce in every bite. Perfectly fried Calamari, Farinata (garbanzo bean fries), and fresh oven baked bread are among the other menu items. Plus,  $5 prosecco. $5 Aperol Spritz ( my all-time favorite) $5 wine.....need I say more?

Tavolino is clearly one my Tucson favs.....so why the 4-stars? Well....although the the Italian flavors and ingredients are present and they are like family, the recently super sized pasta portions and entrees don't take me back to the hillside towns with cobblestone streets where Spaghetti Carbonara is not served with chicken or shrimp.  Rather, I'm just reminded that we're in America, and even Italian food can be super sized. (I think it's safe to say I left my heart in Italia)
  
BUT, Even with that said, Tavolino is as close as you'll get to Italia in Tucson and for that I am profoundly thankful!

Grazie!

Popped in the Manayunk branch of Old City's Han Dynasty for a couple of apps.  Well this is one place the hype proves true.  Had the Dan Dan Noodles - awesome!  The crispy cucumbers - def thumbs up  as well.  Taiwanese sausage - make it 3 for 3 in the awesome category.  But I have to return to talking about the Dan Dan noodles .... best noodle dish from any restaurant I've ever eaten in - and I'm talking Chinese, Italian, American, Thai, etc. etc.

I'm one lucky cat that AJs is literally a 5 minute walk from my house. AJs is so fabulous that I'm pretty sure people just cruise in there for an "escape" into all that is gourmet, specialty, delicious and downright picture perfect. It's hard to go to AJs with out making some unnecessary purchases. The flower displays alone bring out the "Martha Stewart" in me. If you make it passed the flower shop make sure to check out the fresh fish and phenomenal beef counters. Here you can definitely buy high quality  choice and prime beef that will never disappoint. They even have grass-fed beef! 
Don't be discouraged by the high prices, because AJs has crazy good sales on the reg. For example, you can buy PRIME New York Steak for like $12.00/lb on sale versus the normal $28.00. 
TIP: Sales usually fall on holiday weekends.

Beyond the beef counter is their delectable cheese section (my personal heaven), which has more than just your average triple cream bries and Manchego Cheeses. I've been lucky enough to score a truffled Pecorino! 

Hungry while shopping? No Prob! You can munch on all kinds of house-made goodies like grilled veggies, grilled fish, rotisserie chicken,  potato salad, pasta salad, Orzo salad and more.

or

Stop in an eat some fresh Sushi at the Sushi Bar. You know you're getting #1 sushi grade fish, with out a doubt!

Like Pizza? check out the Pizza and Deli counter where you can order a custom sandwich or grab a slice. Then cruise over for some Illy coffee and sweets. 

AJs even has an outdoor grill, open daily until 3pm. They make a GOOD hot dog (with Brats) YUM!

It's best to go to AJs when you are not starving or without a game plan, because it can do a number on your wallet!

Love you AJs!

Why 4 stars?  Because it was delicious but it was filling.

   ---   ---   ---

Jack Black was talking about the filet mignon from Iavarone's in Anchorman right?  A perfect description of a great meal. All of it was great, I cannot find room to complain.

I guess the only couple of things keeping my experience shy of 5 stars were the price and the fact that it was not THE best filet I've ever had.  It was very, very good, but I think Acropolis still wears that crown.

I ordered to-go during my visit, and look forward to dining in with the girlfriend, or with a friend looking for a romantic dinner with me.

I went there a couple months ago for a late lunch.  Been there before, and had forgotten it was there.  Ordered the eggplant parmigiana sandwich.

When they served it, it looked delicious.  I grabbed onto the sandwich to cut it in half, and...

A COCKROACH CAME CRAWLING OUT OF THE SANDWICH!!

OMG!!  As I was sitting at the bar, 3 or 4 other people saw it happen...  IT WAS DISGUSTING!!!

I walked out.  

Since then, I've told about 50 people.  And 3 or 4 have told me about similar instances.  Apparently they've had a problem with cock roaches for years.

I will NEVER go back.

Wanting a true country breakfast? Hungry Fox is just the spot for this craving.

Decor: Country cottage meets grandma's house. 

There is a little store attached the the restaurant... Homemade food items, trinkets, and the sorts.. It won't satisfy your shopping desires yet still fun to look around while waiting for your table. 

Food: standard American breakfast fare... you know, eggs made up a million  and a half ways and the sweet breakfast eats too. Nothing fancy or too special however delicious when craving a typical breakfast. I recommend trying the home fries vs the hash browns, getting an order of toast (it's homemade!), and getting some eggs made to your liking (over-easy for me please). Or satisfy your sweet tooth with a belgian waffle or some pancakes. 

Whatever you choose, say goodbye to more than half your calorie allowance all while it's still morning.

Lunch also served, mostly a variety of sandwiches. I always say.. don't go to a breakfast place for lunch therefore I have not tried any of these. 



useful info: pay upfront, not at the table.

As soon as I heard that there was a taco shop in the neighborhood I was super pumped to try it out.  I was not disappointed!  The menu is super simple--tacos or burritos for dinner--but all that they do they do well.  I got a bean burrito for $7 and it was huge!  All the different elements (the rice, beans, guacamole, salsa, etc) had a great flavor so that every bite was delicious.  The place itself is small but cute and welcoming.  Everything is made fresh with authentic ingredients.

I would definitely recommend Honest Tom's for anyone who loves a good taco or burrito!

My favorite Tucson restaurant, one of the few I think would have no-trouble competing in a larger "food city" like San Francisco or New York.  It's conventional Italian--but done right.  That means: Home-made pasta that actually has bite. That means good quality olive oil and bread.  That means topnotch canned tomatoes.  That means: Fresh simple ingredients in season.  No gimmicks like "cutting edge" combinations. Nothing here is "up and coming".  At the same time, there are subtleties to the combos that make it a bit different than your typical neighborhood Italian joint.

The chef does not use many spices or herbs, and the food is noticeably under-salted. That takes courage because the dish makes-or-breaks based on its raw flavors. If you love simple fresh understated combinations, you'll love Vivace.

To top it off, the service is impeccable (they refill without you noticing, they stay out of your way but are there when you need them).  

And--for the quality--the food is an extremely good value!   We had dinner for two (two dishes plus a soup), plus two coffees, plus desert for $60.

I've been coming here for years, and it's just as good now as it was then.  I've never had a bad dish here.  It is very rare in a meal in Tucson I think to myself "nothing could be improved." 

Reading the reviews, I laugh at some 20 yo's who say "I was youngest one here" or criticizing Vivace for not being trendy.  It's classy but not trendy. But it's not stodgy.  

Ok enough...

an FYI before I dive into my review! Pizzeria Vivace has changed it's name to Scordato's Pizzeria: Artisan Pizzas. (apparently using the word Vivace for both restaurants confused people)

The pizza here scrumptious and has been given the stamp of approval by my very own Italian friends from MIlan. It  tastes just like it came out of a wood burning oven....but actually they use a ceramic oven (which is equally as good and super hardcore)! And folks, that Charred layer on your pizza is totally NORMAL and traditional. It gives the pizza a deeper smoky flavor. But this Pie heaven isn't reached by using this oven alone..... Danny buys the premium Italian 00 pizza flour and you can taste it in every bite. The tomato sauce also shines through in every slice. 

My personal favorites are: The Shrimp and Pesto, and the Eggplant & Zucchini ( I add Sausage it to it, yum!)- But there is a pizza everyone can enjoy.

Aside from delivering pizza perfection every time, this joint without question has the best Eggplant Parmigiana (besides the one my boyfriends mother makes). The eggplant is thinly sliced and fried lightly to perfection. Layered with fresh mozzarella, basil and Pomodoro sauce. They do it RIGHT. This ain't no East coast Italian parmigiana that's basically breaded deep fried eggplant rounds topped with Marana Sauce....

The antipasto platter is always a fabulous starter. That garlic bread compliments all the cheeses and salumi, and is perfectly buttery ( trust me you won't go home with dragon breath :)

If I'm trying to lay-off the carbs I order the Chicken with Fresh Mozzarella & Tomato Salad. It is served on Bibb lettuce and it's Massive.

Drinks are half off after 8pm! 

at Lunch you can order a Bento Box for $10.95 that includes a choice of Salad, Appetizer, Entree and Dessert! Or go for a 6-inch pizza under $10.00 served with a very generous salad.

I frequent this place several times a month for many other reasons besides the obvious quality of the food.
-the staff is friendly 
-Danny's nephew Justin is the manager and he's delightful to chat with in English, Italian or Spanish!
-and Danny of course! He loves what he does and loves his customers.
-it's casual and totally laid back

Grazie Pizzeria VIvace! /Scordato's Pizzeria!
I've been going here for over 15 years. The sandwiches are the best I've tasted in Tucson. I just wish they had longer hours.

Please note that this review is for the tortillas only, as I haven't had a chance to try any of their other food.  However, if the rest is anything like the tortillas, than count on another 5 star review.  I have lived in Arizona my entire life, and have tried many, many different tortillas, but none are quite like this.  They are super thin, and kind of chewy.  They are so large that they have to be folded into squares to fit into a bag.  They are so good that they are one of the few foods I will drive all the way across town to buy.  They are a little bit of heaven, and they are on sale by the dozen at St. Mary's Mexican Food and Tortilla Factory.  Go buy some now!

Xoom, Xoom, Xoom! Love this place. There's a Juice for every type of taste, all the boosts you can imagine, and it's healthy! My fav is the Maxwell Smart( mango, pineapple, brain boost, energy boost) and I add Whey protein.  

And when I'm in a rush I call my order in and it's always ready for me when I pick up!

I also like that they use biodegrable cups that are made from sugar cane and bamboo. Just remember not to leave a cup in your car during the summer months because it will melt away!

Sparkroot is an excellent addition to the downtown community, no doubt!

Excellent coffee, ( although not roasted locally), nice service, Fabulous interior ( this is my shout out to Repp Design + Construction), and super tasty snacks!

Even though I make a pretty killer quinoa at home, I always order the quinoa here. Its simple but that avocado dressing is packed with flava baby!

If I lived closed to D-Town this would be my hub.

This is a big Target near the Crowne Plaza hotel (separate parking lot) - so very convenience to pick up anything that you forgot to pack in your suit-case.   This store is convenient to get to but busy .    

For some reasons, I don't feel this location is as well managed as the other Targets - in terms of appearance, inventory on the shelf, and the organization.

My friend and I came on a crowded weekend morning and waited about 20-30 minutes for a table (completely fine, we expected it)...

The food though, while good, was not super memorable (hence the downgrade from 4 to 3 stars). I only remembered I had been to the Sabrina's by Drexel when a friend mentioned going to the other one...

I had the stuffed french toast, and while it was good, it honestly wasn't above the level of diners back home in New York...My friend didn't really love her dish either. The coffee, I remember, was pretty strong and they were quick with refills. I like Sabrina's, I just doubt I will come back

Great new local place for breakfast / lunch -  HUGE portions and delicious!  Might want to scale back a bit since I have seen so many plates go back  half eaten and then get thrown away.  Seems like a waste -  The prices are still right on even if scaled back -  Understanding that it is a new place in town BUT the owners / managers are lurking around  and seem to be mostly in the way -  Let the staff do their jobs !!!!

CASH ONLY -  Get a CC machine !!!!!

Fantastic little place!

If we rated businesses on decor alone, this place would be a freaking 10.  Surf boards on the ceiling, liquid walls and massive glass garage doors?  Really?  Well-played, Surf Shack owners... Wellllllll-played.

Okay, we weren't starvin' Marvin' so we didn't really thrown down Golden Corral style on the menu, but the two ordered items were fan-freaking-tastic.  Item one, LONGBOARD FISH TACOS.  Oh you like fish tacos, playa?  And you think the ones they serve up at Bonefish are righteous?  GTFO then because Surf Shack has officially locked down the fish taco market.  Each taco has what amounts to about half a damn COD fillet, lightly breaded and fried to tender perfection.  Topped with pico de gallo?  Hell yes.  Don't think about it, just go order some--TODAY.  Item two, AHI POKE.  WTF is that, you ask?  It's Ahi tuna, red peppers and cucumbers tossed in poke sauce and served with won ton chips.  I don't even get down with Ahi tuna too much, but this was some delicious grub.  The tuna was ultra tender and very tasty.

As for the beers, they had a pretty wide selection including KONA Longboard Lager, which I had never heard of, but it was really good.

The only possible negative about this place could be the overwhelming amount of children.  It's a fun atmosphere sandwiched somewhere between adult and family dining, so an intimate date might be tough to pull off on the wrong night.  With that being said, we had no issues, complaints or problems with anything and plan on returning!  I'd give a 5, but I have to throw down here a few more times to check for consistency.

UPDATE------------------------------------------------------

I have been back and seen the light!  Everything pans out, so I've updated this 4 to a 5.  

Last Thursday, we made a cameo for "Taco Thursday," where all the tacos are $2 each.  This gives you the flexibility to sample the tacos on the menu without committing to an order of two for $6 or $7.  I've got to admit that this was one of the better dinning decisions of 2012 thus far!  Surf 'n Turf taco--amazing.  Filet mignon taco--tender and fan-freaking-tastic!  Bangin' shrimp taco--an instant favorite for me and I don't even like shrimp.  

Go buy you some!

I hate to give such a low star rating to one of Tucson's classic taquerias, but I have no choice. PICO how can I give you more stars when you serve up a taco with chicken that looked and tasted old; it had the crumbly texture of chicken you would find in a chicken fried rice from a dumpy Chinese restaurant. The Carne Asada was all fat.  Your Salsa squeeze bottles were basically empty, and when I asked for another one at the counter I was handed another almost empty! And the bottles were marked with salsa handprints, most likely from several days of being reused and not washed. 

For several years you had my heart and it breaks my Corazon to say this, but Adios PICO, I don't think i'll be back for a while.

I really can't say much about Casa that hasn't already been said, but I still feel the need to give a review. We have patronized Casa for many years. For long time fans, remember when they had the second, smaller store on Grant? We tried Netflix and it quite frankly sucked. Any other video outlet pales in comparison. Yes, there is popcorn everywhere but that's what we love about it. We are able to get new release Blu-Rays every time we go. No wait like Netflix.The staff will happily empty the outside box for you if you ask for a title. Something you want to see that no one else has ever heard of? Chances are, they'll have it. My daughter had to give written reports about plays, ballets, operas, etc. for her drama class. My husband and I looked at each other and said, "Casa". We absolutely love this place and hope that it will be around for a long, long time.

We went there last Monday for lunch. Best salsa I ever had. The salsa had roasted peppers In it. Went there yesterday just for that. The dumb ass waiter didn't have a clue about the salsa. He offered to bring a bottle of hot sauce. HIGHLY DISAPPOINTED.

Loco Pez is a cool Mexican pub hangout.  It seems to be crowded every night but if you can get there a little earlier, you not only get a place at the bar or a table, you get great Happy Hour prices.  The Nachos de Kenzo are a mountain of taste .... def don't order them for one but share .... and maybe you'll have some room for some of the items on the menu.  My fav is called a street dog.  This is how a hot dog should taste
.
The location in a great neighborhood at an unique intersection where there seems to be streets radiating out in every direction is worth finding.  Loco Pez is a thumbs up plus addition to the Philly scene.
Best Sushi on the west coast of Florida!! Always fresh and always friendly.

I love this place.  The first time I tasted a Chicken Maroush, I knew I'd be coming back for more.   Again and again.  The maroush is full of tasty grilled chicken and pickles and a creamy garlicky sauce that I just can't get enough of.  I've also tried other maroush:  Lamb Maroush is not bad, but some lamb pieces are not as tender -- I'm pretty picky about texture/succulence of meat so the Lamb just doesn't compare to the Chicken for me.  The Kofta is tasty also, though a bit on the dry side -- extra maroush sauce helps with that.  The Chicken Maroush is the best by far.

Saad's also has tubs of rice pudding in the fridge, which are yummy and very reasonably priced, if you feel like something sweet after.

Cute, comfortable BYOB with okay food.  Ordered the Drunken Noodles with Pork -- flavours were okay, not particularly authentic, and pork was tough.  Also tasted my companions' Pad Thai and Basil Chicken.  Pad Thai was very sweet.  Basil chicken dish didn't have much basil taste, which was disappointing.  Everything is very sweet, which is nice if you have a sweet tooth or are in the mood for sweet, but not that interesting in terms of flavours.   

Just okay. 

Smile Cafe is better.

2.5 stars

Very cute small place.  We ordered Spicy noodles and Fried Tilapia with pineapples, tomatoes, in a curry sauce, which was pretty good.  Noodles were yummy, with a nice spicy depth to it (not sure how to describe it -- almost like roasted hot pepper, if that makes sense).

I'd say this is about 3.5+ stars, not quite 4 stars.  Will be back if I feel like Thai & don't want to go too far or can't find a better place for it in Philly.

Fast, convenient place to grab solid food -- it was better than expected.  Tried platters with Chicken Kabob and Lamb Kabob.  Chicken Kabob was very flavourful and tender.  I'd definitely order that again.  Lamb was all right but not quite as good -- it was on the dry/overcooked side for me (but I'm picky about meat texture).  

Hummus and pita were fine.  For sides, I enjoyed the beet salad & roast eggplant.  Potato salad was blah.  Babaganoush was good too, if you like it with lots of tahini flavour.  It's nice that they have a station for you to get as much sauce as you need.  Beware: the green spicy sauce has a lot of heat.

Had Chaat for the first time here: Samosa Chaat.  Ingredients were fresh and everything was just really nicely put together.  Yum!

I'll be back.

Koreana is solid enough.  Convenient location.  So, the food is not great, but I'll keep going back as there don't seem to be too many Korean options around.  It's not bad for the area.  Beggars can't be choosers.

Have tried a noodle dish with squid (spicy red sauce), which was all right -- sweet and spicy.  Beef Kalbi is okay (could be marinated longer so that it's more flavourful & tender, but not too bad).  I think the pork was better than the beef.  Tofu stew was all right but too salty. 

In an area with more Korean options, I wouldn't bother going back, but in central Philly, this is about 3.5 stars.

I miss eating Bun Cha in Hanoi when they grill the meat by the side of the road & give you a huge plate of herbs to pick from.

The Bun here is pretty good.  Everything tastes good and fresh.   Very satisfying.

Not a huge pile of herbs like I'd hoped for, but I wasn't necessarily expecting it.

Meat Loaf here was great.  Served with roast brussels sprouts which were undercooked.  But meat loaf itself was yummy.

Also tried some hamburger that was stuffed with cheese, which was pretty good.  Burger was cooked more than I'd like, but I like it rare.

Grace Tavern makes good burgers -- they have that chargrilled taste, and they're juicy.  The sauce that comes with the fries is addictive.

Jambalaya was not so great.  Stick with the burgers.

Came here on Skewer Night and tried a bunch of different skewers.  Some are better than others, but it's a lot of fun to try different things. 

Not sure it'd be worth it at full price, but not bad.
3.5 stars.

Came here for brunch on a first date and had the Stuffed Challah French Toast, which was recommended.  Date and Sabrina's both scored points on that.  Toast was delicious and decadent!  Thankfully the French Toast was so huge that I brought the leftovers home and had the other half for brunch the next day.

Beautiful place to sit and talk for brunch.  Nice atmosphere.  Lots of windows.

Service was a bit odd.  I'd had about 2 bites of my Stuffed French Toast and got brought the bill.  Wasn't ready to leave, though, and we weren't explicitly asked to leave.  Just not sure if/why the waiter was trying to get us to leave before we'd eaten anything.  ah well.  odd.

Not sure how other food is here, but that toast is 5 stars.

For our anniversary this year, my husband took me to a fabulous dinner at Armani's.  Pricey.  WOW.  But so awesome.  Definitely a "special occasion" kind of place.  On the top floor of the Grand Hyatt overlooking the bay.  From the Chef's choice or pick your own antipasti to the lobster bisque - seriously the best I've ever had.

The view is phenomenal.
Our dinners were perfect.  And I mean perfect.
Dessert and coffee were wonderful as well.
The staff (and we mean staff) were all so professional and made you feel like the royal family dining there.
Will return.....when we save more money!  ;)

I should preface by saying that I know the GM and me and my 3 other guests were hooked up pretty well as far as the check goes.

With that said, the place has beautiful decor, nothing over the top, and it doesn't feel stuffy and old like other fancy restaurants in center city.

The bartender knew what he was doing (my sweet tea lemonade cocktail was delicious) and the appetizers were pretty good from what I can remember.  Nothing spectacular there.  If you're gonna eat here, skip the appetizers and opt for entree and dessert, which I'll get to in a bit.

For the entrees, I got red snapper, served with the skin still on and some kind of seafood paste-stuffed empanadas on the side which were peppery but really interesting in taste.  The snapper was cooked perfectly and it was so tender and delicious.  My boyfriend got a seafood hot pot and I was jealous I didn't because it smelled and looked amazing.

Now for the best part.  Dessert.  I expect most restaurants to serve the basics like cheesecakes and chocolate lava cakes.  So when the waiter pointed out the dessert that was on special for the night, we had to try it.  It was a chocolate gnocchi, which sounds crazy but it was honest to god the best dessert I've ever had. It had the consistency of a gnocchi but it was really a velvety, almost mousse-like chocolate.  And instead of pasta sauce was this creamy and light vanilla sauce.  Amazing.

Overall, I give it 4 stars for the superb service, perfectly cooked fish and amazing dessert.  I knock it down a star simply because I know that the bill would have been a bit out of my price range had we not been discounted.
Decor and ambiance are great highlighting a minimalist, contemporary/modern cafe with chill music. Coffee could be better. Starbucks' grande is considered their large.

Great new place in the neighborhood for breakfast. The inside is new, clean, airy, and green. Most of the food portions are enormous. My wife had the stuffed French toast. Which is 4 slices of French toast paired up with ricotta cheese between them. Add some fresh blueberries, strawberries, fruit compote, and fresh whipped cream and you have yourself a feast. I had a chorizo omelette which was more normal size, but was very good nonetheless. The price for 2 people for breakfast will cost you about $5 bucks more than at the Midtown Diner, but given the  quality and portion size here, it's worth it.

The only knock is it's cash only. Since just about every legal business takes debit cards nowadays, makes you wonder why they don't. The ATM machine by the cash register detracts from the otherwise nice decor.

Alright, I admit, since I've had really really good sushi, I'm probably more particular about this type of food.  The only good thing about this place was the company :P

Pros: Really nice wait staff, they smile, they're friendly, and very attentive.  I admit the Love Roll was a cool idea and not that bad, it was afterall, in the shape of a heart.  Maybe their best rolls are cooked or include shellfish - both of which I haven't had here.  Oh yes, and they offer "To-Yo" dollars everytime you dine here, so you can use them on return trips.

Cons: Soggy rolls.  The first time I was here, we ordered 4 rolls and I don't know if it was because they were all on the same plate, but anytime we ordered any roll with the mayo sauce, it was wet and soggy.  Tasted alright, sure, but the soggy-ness ruined it for me.  

Maybe I'll get cooked food here next time so they can get at least 3 stars out of me.

The Improv is a great spot to see a comedy show. The theater is small and intimate so almost all of the seats have a great view.

Our group of four arrived at The Improv about half an hour before the show started.  We were seated on the balcony so arrive earlier if you want a table on the floor. While the view was still good from the balcony; the bar tables are placed too close to each other. There was a larger woman sitting at the table next to ours so I was forced to pull my seat away from the table so that I could sit. Throughout the night I was uncomfortably squeezed next to my friends.  

The bar tables on the balcony are very tiny so our four beverages and two appetizers barely fit on the table. The sweet potato fries and chips were pretty good. I also heard the burger is pretty good from one of the nearby tables. As others have mentioned - this is a comedy club, not a restaurant, so come for the entertainment and get dinner elsewhere.

Tip: Register to win free tickets on Tampa Improv's website. The free tickets are typically for less known comedians and are usually for the weekday shows.

This is bar none the best wine bar in town.  The have many to choose from and with your pre-paid Taps card you can enjoy 1 oz, 3 oz and 5 oz pours of whatever you choose on the walls.

They also have a great selection of beers.

This is a great spot for my husband and I because it's the best of both worlds.

We enjoy the atmosphere, although there are mostly residents of Skypoint there, we've never had a bad time, with groups or just the 2 of us.

We like the convenience of it downtown and we usually have no problem finding parking on the street.

Best of all, they are open late so if we want to stop by after a party or after a game at the Forum or just for a drink, they always seem to be open for us.

We enjoy this spot and don't get there often enough.

Just had Sunday brunch .
There were four people in our group. We all ordered omlets. Three of the four were dry and overcooked. Plenty of ingredients, just left on way too long.
Not impressed'!!! Vegetarian omelet overcooked.  Eggs were brown on the outside but cheese was still not melted on the inside. Sausage was pink inside and undercooked.  If you do not know how grits should be cooked, please do not offer them.  Mine were thick enough to stand a fork up in.  What happen to smooth and creamy. Also cheese grits does not mean just melting cheese on top!
Great mimosas!

Dao Pan's will always have a soft place in my heart since it is here I discovered the deliciousness that is Pho. 

Several years have passed and travels and different pho restaurants have come into my life and unfortunately Dao Pan's isn't as amazing as some of the west side restaurants that serve amazing broths.

I will give it the third star though because the service here is always pretty amazing. They are incredibly fast (usually around 5-7 minute from when an order is placed) and continually come around filling water.

Their iced Vietnamese coffee is yum and they have a wide variety of Boba drinks (although they are prepackaged and not made fresh). They also carry Chinese food menu as well as Vietnamese.

My only real complaint is the broth is very light and doesn't compete with other westside pho shops. Also their cuts of meat leave some to be desired.

Since it is only five minutes from my house I definitely will still frequent Dao's thankful for the birthplace of my love of pho.

This is where you want to go if you want rad Tiki atmosphere and want to punish your liver. Seriously punish your liver. Kon Tiki's drinks ruin lives.I don't know how may times I've driven by here. I'm really glad my old friends invited me. The food is average, teriyaki, pineappley, chicken, steaks, fish, shrimp and the like. What's coolest though, is the divey Tiki inside! Service was great, but the drinks were outta control...did I mention the drinks? If you're looking for gourmet cuisine look elsewhere. If you just wanna hang out, have some drinkies with your friends and pour a bad day down your gullet, you're in luck! Definitely a fun place!

Went here for the first time last week. Espresso was great. Love the layout and the ambiance. Quiet, not too packed, fast wifi. Had a good time. Will be coming back. This place is sort of an undiscovered treasure - especially since other coffee shops in the same area are totally packed.

Echoing many of the prior reviews, Cherry St is definitely a local bar that seems to attract both older, blue collar, heartier folk exchanging war stories, as well as young hipsterish kids analyzing the biblical history LOST is based on.  Nonetheless, a "cheers" like atmosphere greets you where when you ask for a mixed drink a waitress as handsome as Carla will ask you if you want a double.

Decent beer selection with the standard big guys and handful of the usual suspect micros, served cold and at a decent price.

Had the hot pork sandwich, the waitress said they're known for it.  It was very good, esp the bread that was dipped in au jus.  Friend had the nachos, they were chips with shredded cheese on top...you're not coming here for the food unless its the roast beef or hot pork.  Problem was the food took FOREVER.  But...this really isn't the place to come for a quick dinner, a quick beer? Sure, but you'll probably end up staying longer than you thought.

Large signs warn that pizza here is "well done," meaning the thin white flour crust is burnt in the coal-fired oven. If you don't like the taste of carbon in your food, you cannot say you were not told in advance. We sat at the outdoor bar, which is a great spot along busy S. Dale Mabry Highway. The two Jai Alai IPA beers we had were $12, a bit excessive. But that's the high end of what many places charge now for craft beer. Our mini-meatball pizza was loaded with tiny, tasty "baby" meatballs, red sauce and ricotta cheese, plus the burnt crust. I liked it. I found it juicy and quite tasty. But $16.25 for a 12 inch pizza is getting up there, isn't it? I could have bought 6 pounds of hamburg at Publix for that price and made about 1000 baby meatballs at home.
Still, I liked the atmosphere. The servers seemed to be competent and pleasant, a nice switch from what usually happens at new eateries in Tampa, featuring mostly ill-trained, ill-tempered kids.
I would return for the rabe and sausage plate. True, no subs and only a couple of sandwiches, including, weirdly, a tuna sandwich. I would stay away at busier hours because the place is sort of tiny and I'm sure the waits are very long.

I LOVE this place. Maybe I'm partial because that's where my husband proposed, but still, we loved it way before then. This is a top-notch date night place. Get a little fancy and plan to spend some $ - not a cheap place. But honestly, at Ciro's, I don't care. The drinks are wonderful and unique, the food (esp the chicken&waffles) is nothing short of amazing. We take friends here for a special night out when they come into town. You're also getting a really great, one-of-a-kind experience. I've never been to, let alone heard of, another 1930's style speakeasy and supper club. They have a wonderful atmosphere and the private booths really set a lovely tone, although they can also handle reasonable sized groups. If you're looking for a special occasion place, or want something a little out of the ordinary - GO HERE!! You won't be disappointed if you have any taste for things classy and romantic.

Update: Giving them another star. I think they put time-released crack in their food, because although I wasn't 100% sold at first, now I find myself craving Jimbo's!

Good, but not amazing. I ordered the chopped pork sandwich, with baked beans & coleslaw. The beans were tasty, but pretty average. The coleslaw had an oddly bitter flavor and was quite soupy when it should have been creamy. The sandwich was amazing, but it's pretty hard to screw up smoked pulled pork. Their house BBQ sauce was tasty, but nothing to drool about- nice flavor, but a little too fluid. Points for serving it warm though. Nice touch. I also partook in their blueberry cobbler a la mode. Best part of the meal. Sweet, but not cloying- the cobbler was studded with pecans & served with a ridiculous mountain of soft serve. Delicious! Would I give this place another shot? Probably, but i wouldn't say it's a must have.

Opening up downtown's waterfront was genius judging by the crowds that gravitate to Curtis Hixon Waterfront Park whenever there is a big event or just a beautiful day. Some of the best ways to make the most of its serene setting, scenic views and undulating design includes taking what is said to be Tampa's largest outdoor yoga class Sunday evenings. People of all ages - and ranges of flexibility - gather outside the entrance to the Tampa Museum around 6 p.m. for an hour of yoga lead by trained professionals from the nearby Yoga Downtown Tampa studio on Cass Street. The class can be basic or a bit more advanced but the point is simply to try and that seems to be encouragement enough for folks to attempt all sorts of poses. There can be children racing about, Frisbees flying nearby and even trains chugging through the downtown core but somehow the overall result is quite relaxing. A $5 donation is requested and participants are responsible for dressing so they can move with ease and bringing a yoga mat or large towel to use during their practice.
This place is a gem.  The bartenders are amazing and the beer selection is superb.

During the Yelp Elite event some users brought up my past review of Samurai Blue, which I admit is dated. I have been back numerous times since 2009 and since the inception of my food blog (in fact I reviewed it on Urbanspoon in July of 2010) and have had some great experiences. The Hawaiian Roll is great and the California Roll/Salmon Rolls have both been a plus as well. The Samurai Roll and just about any roll they have crunchy or tempura battered has been excellent and the Endless Summer Roll  (tempura bananas, grilled eel, inside out with avocados on top) has been a winner as has the Tiger Eye Roll. Specials are reasonably priced and under $10.

After the Yelp Elite event I am adding their Tempura Lobster Roll to the list of one of my favorite rolls at Samurai Blue and will certainly order it many times in the future. The nigiri and sashimi as well as the Blue Crab Rangoons will certainly be on my list for future visits. What really makes this place five stars to me though is my opinion after meeting and talking with the owner/head chef Yung Kim and his wife Rosa Kim. They were both very personable and I could connect with them thanks to my past travels in South Korea. It's great to see happy and sociable entrepreneurs who have survived through a tough time in our area's economic history. Many Ybor favorites of mine have shut their doors since the recession in 2008.

Also their new drink: Yuzu Saketini - excellent.

I really believe in supporting restaurants that make an effort to supporting local initiatives and to me growing the food community in Tampa is a personally important mission, it is why I write about food and Samurai Blue is certainly on my list of approved sushi restaurants after the hospitality at the Yelp Elite event and after watching the great increase in flavor since my last review back in 2009.

What a great sushi restaurant !!!!  I was amazed at the quality of the food that they have here....I am not a huge connoisseur of sushi, but I will now compare every bit of sushi I consume in the future to Samurai Blue.  Wow....just wow.  The plates of fresh, and recently swimming delicacy come out to your table looking like a work of art, and the flavors are fantastic.  The seared sesame crusted tuna was so great...I will definitely enjoy that again.

With a full bar and a great selection of premium exotic drinks and sakes, how could anyone go wrong here?  This place is now on a short list of places I must visit whenever going to Ybor City....such a great job by the staff, and sushi chefs.

IF anyone is fearing the scenario of going to Ybor just to visit this place because they think its a hassle, fear not.  A parking garage is two blocks south of the restaurant (which is on the 2nd floor in Centro Ybor)....a one minute walk from the garage and your there...very convenient.  No excuses....visit Samurai Blue !!!

YELP !!!!!

Some of the freshest tuna and best designed rolls I've ever had. For cooked food, the quality of the tempura is a great gauge of a Japanese restaurant. The tempura is wonderful as its perfectly battered and not overly fried and soggy like so many restaurants fail to prepare properly...cough cough To-Kai.  Highly recommended. Granted it can be tough to spot as it's not accessible as one might hope, but its worth the expense. Can't beat it in the Northeast.

For me what used to be five stars is now three at best.  On three successive occasions I've been here, rib tips so over cooked and dry I couldn't swallow them.  Service typically outstanding, not so much.  Anybody can have a bad night, but three?  Management has obviously gone to the dogs.  Not even a response when I complained on their website?   Go to Shane's, it's not even a block away.

The best iced coffee I've ever had.  Seriously, it's the highlight of my day.  I work around the corner, and when that 2-3 pm crash rolls in, I head over for a treat.  I've never had iced coffee make me so happy.

I'm a sucker for a good Indian buffet, especially when I can stuff myself with rice, sauce, and naan, yum! Unfortunately, my trip to the buffet today was pretty disappointing.
The selection was about average: vegetable curry (mostly potatoes), a mushroom dish, lentils, chicken curry (on the bone), tandoori chicken, and lamb meatballs, plus rice, bread, salad, samosa, and dessert. I tried a little bit of everything, and wasn't impressed enough to try seconds of anything.
I like my Indian food very spicy, and I know that buffets usually tend to go "mild" or "medium" at best, but the sauces were all quite bland and hard to distinguish from one another. The meatballs had an odd texture, and even after pulling meat off the bones in the chicken curry, I kept ending up with pieces of gristle. The bread was on the thin side, not nearly as thick and soft as I've had elsewhere.
On the plus side, the samosas were excellent, and the service was very good; we were checked on often to see if we needed more bread or another pitcher of water. Their drink and dessert menu looked very good; if I end up coming back for dinner, I will definitely have to try something.
Overall, I wasn't impressed with their lunch buffet, and I'm not sure if I'll be back to try them for dinner; if, as another reviewer noted, they charge for rice in addition to entrees, I'll probably just stick to making my own curry at home unless I'm off in another part of town.
The best of evenings.Great wine and food and excellent service given by a young server named Tommy. See you again soon.

Review based on 5 night stay on 4th floor.

In town for a conference at the Convention Center.  Usually stay at the Embassy, but it was booked.

Decent hotel, though the distance to the convention center is just a bit too far to walk in the local weather (depending on your attire).

PROS:
Free Internet
Nice view of river

CONS:
Noisy (Lots of noise bleeds through the walls and window)
Small TV
No fridge (Spoiled by the Embassy)
Room/bathroom on the small side (Bathroom seems really cramped)
Free shuttle service up to 3 miles

For the most part, the staff was friendly and pleasant to deal with.  The thing that soured my experience the  most was the shuttle service.  Really hit/miss.

They have one complimentary shuttle that will take you anywhere within a 3 mile radius.  Normally, I think it probably works out great for them.  However, if your customer base is mostly from Convention Center traffic...when there is a convention/conference in town, one shuttle just doesn't cut it.  Have waited up to 30 minutes for the shuttle to come for a pick up (after calling and being told it would be about 5-10 minutes) at the convention center when I've seen shuttles for other hotel chains in the area that are located further away come back twice in the same time span.  I know its not a personal taxi service, but seriously, provide a good service or don't provide one at all.

I do have to caveat that with two instances of employees going the extra mile.  Chuck had punched out for the day and saw a few of us waiting on another driver to come back (Shuttle was in the waiting area, driver was MIA).  So he hopped in and drove us to our destination.  Austin was on the morning shift as far as I could tell and had been shuttling folks to/from the convention center.  On one occasion, he pulled back to the Sheraton and parked the shuttle against a wall (look like he was going to take a lunch break) and there were only two of us waiting.  As he walked by he asked if we were waiting on the shuttle and took us to our destination.  5 stars to both these gentlemen for taking care of us.

The main problem seems to be the surge of traffic for the shuttle and valet services when there is a big conference going on.  Would recommend Sheraton surge the personnel during these times and possibly get a second shuttle or figure something else out.  Because of the poor shuttle service, it gave me (and a few other guests that I had the opportunity to chat with) a lot of time to reflect on my experiences with the hotel while I waited for the shuttle to either come back to the hotel or for pick up in one of the locations within the 3 mile radius.

I would not recommend this hotel if you are in town for an event at the TCC and expect the shuttle to be your main mode of transportation.  I would still go for either the Marriott/Embassy due to their proximity to the TCC and walking distance to Channelside.

A Yelp friend once told me that she occasionally gets her nails done at this place so I decided to give it a shot. I was looking for something a little more low rent than my usual highfalutin places. At $45 for a mani/pedi, Rittenhouse Manicure & Spa isn't cheap, but that ten extra dollars gets you better services and a nicer environment than most of the quick and cheap spots. 

The pedi chairs were mostly full when I arrived around noon on a Thursday, but there were still two that were open on the other side of a half wall. I actually preferred this area to the larger room; it was more relaxing. I grabbed a rag mag and tried to relax while also bracing myself for what was sure to be a painful pedicure. I have very sensitive skin which is one of the reasons I normally shell out mad dough for nice salons. Fortunately, Lee was very gentle and the pedicure was actually enjoyable. 

Next up was the manicure. I normally ask to skip the cuticle cutters but my nails were in such bad shape that I just let it happen. And since Lee was so careful during my pedicure, I trusted that my hands would make it through without any bleeding. I think I had maybe one small nick when all was said and done but my nails look great so I'm not complaining. I only wish that she would have painted a little closer to the cuticle, since there is a small gap between the polish and cuticles. Why does this always seem to happen at certain salons?

As my nails were drying, I received a shoulder massage which was practically worth the extra money on its own. My nails look great and I'll likely return whenever I don't have the extra cash to plunk down on my fancier spots.

Surf shack is a great fast cas restaurant located in westchase. It's pretty similar to its sister restaurant, zim zari in trinity. 

The $2 tacos on Thursday is a great deal. Even though they are dirt cheap, they don't skimp on the toppings or portion size. 2 tacos and you're full. 

Each table receives complimentary chips and salsa and I must say, their chips and salsa are mighty tasty. 

Get here early on Thursday, the secret's out.

Referred by a friend....finally went to see what their Buffet was like. Mmmmmmm, Mmmmmm, Mmmmmm.....cheerful service, tasty chips, salsa and all you can eat Guacamole. Buffet well presented! Place was hustlin' & bustlin'. Lot's of peps in & out. Arrived just before breakfast buffet ended, fast to refresh buffet and get ready for the lunch rush. Glad we got there early, b'cuz this place was hoppin. Worth a trip back to try dinner from the menu!

They separated the grocer from the deli and it feels like a coffin when you walk in.  There is stuff everywhere but that is common for a place who's has definitely outgrown the space but who isn't comfortable enough to move to a more spacious location.  They have hired a lot more employees and I believe yelp help this mom and pop place take off.  Before, it took so long to get a sandwich because it was 2 women in the back doing the work but now I see the kitchen has at least 6 helpers and the front another 3-4 people.

I order the xu mai banh mi and damn it has gotten better.  I had a grilled pork to go but the bread go too crumbly after 6hrs.  I guess the limit is 4hrs if the bread is toasted but longer if not.  Publix must have came up with a different recipe for the bread because it doesn't taste like what I can get in the store.  The banh bo nuong is very soft and has a very chewy texture.  I believe there is a light glaze on it.

The boba drinks are ok.  It is better than the Thuy's Cafe version.  It is sweet but not overly sweet.  The boba is slightly under cooked .  It does taste a little watered down.

The only asian bakery in town I guess?

Pastries are so-so compared to 85 Degrees C worthy. I've tried a lot of their pastries but their bread texture and fillings are very similar to each other. I've had better back in the west coast. Bread/pastry texture should be lightly chewy, fluffy, and soft. Theirs is lacking.

I've only tried their honey dew melon roll with butter cream frosting...Cake was of perfect light texture but their filling was too salty. I'd suggest using fresh whip cream for the filling instead of butter cream. 

Never tried their specialty cakes, but that's because they only make cakes for pre-orders. Their slices are from cakes on the display the day before that they cannot sell. The place is a little run down...kinda like a hole-in-the wall places. 

2 stars for their lack of "love" they put into making their pastries and their lack of friendly service. 

Cash only if you're buying less than $5

Wow! Hands down, my favorite new discovery of the year.

First, I'll confirm that direct mail and coupons apparently still do work. I walked in with a $10 coupon toward any meal and walked out with three days' worth of breakfasts and lunches. 

The highlights:

 - Exceptionally clean store!
 - The organization of the meals in the refrigerators was amazing and made it very easy to shop
 - The food selection well exceeded my expectations
 - I've tried two things thus far -- Fitgriddle and Lemon Pepper Tilapia -- and I'd give the taste about a 3.75 out of 5. A lot of flavor overall, and some elements were good; others, OK. Nothing bad. When you factor in the convenience, price, selection and ease of heating, though, I'm more than OK with this taste score
 - Service was tight and efficient. More smiles, energy and interest always welcome (referring mostly to the ladies behind the counter)
 - The Small, Medium and Large concept is bloody brilliant, and it was (dare I say) fun to add up my daily caloric intake in advance
 - Love that they have a recycle program (20 empty containers = 1 free meal)
 - Prices!!!! I had the perception this idea would be ridiculously spendy. Wrong. Lunches cost about what a Chipotle or Panera Bread meal would cost ($7-9 for Medium-sized meals) but with a far more thoughtful approach to total nutrition
 - The store was active, and I liked this. There was a steady, busy flow of loyalists stocking up for the week ahead (I went on a Sunday), and I got some diner feedback on a couple of meal options
 - The hours are fantastic. I agree with the other reviewer who mentioned the hours are designed for working people

Wish List*:
 - Most of the customers there seemed to know exactly what to do and how to do it. I walked in blind (hadn't even been to the website). There was a great gentleman there to walk me through everything, but it would have been nice to have some large signage up in the store than explained the concept in plain language
 - Signage that covered the codes on the packages would have also been helpful. I was explained the S, M, L pretty quickly, but I still don't know what O means (One size?)
 - More signage that covers daily caloric intake stuff for height/weight. I can't imagine everyone who comes in is familiar with these basics
 - Strange one, but I like the idea of a scale in the store. Could be a showpiece, since one of the primary benefits is the weight-loss potential
 - Showcase a "story of the week" or something in the store, so customers are reminded of the life-changing benefits of the concept

*Disclaimer: I may have missed some of these during my first store visit. I was a little overwhelmed at my new gold mine. ;-)


All in all, I'll end with how I began: Fitlife has been my favorite recent local discovery! I feel a little late to the party, though ... based on the Sunday traffic, seems like this place might be Tampa's best-kept secret.

I visited at a 5 p.m. seating on Mother's Day with my son and parents. I have mixed reviews on my experience. I really wish it had been better -- it's a place I would have loved to recommend.

The Good Stuff:
 - We had a great window seat
 - Interior decor was very cute. Larger space but still remained quite cozy
 - The high tea food was lovely to look at and mostly good
 - Good tea selection that day
 - We had one item dropped off by another service person, a gentleman with dark hair, beard/goatee and light eyes -- he greeted us with a "Happy Mother's Day" and it was literally the ONLY point during the meal where we got that "special" feeling

The Not-So-Good Stuff:
 - Initial table greeting took about 10-15 minutes. We saw servers on the other side of the restaurant, but no one looked my way. There was a table of two near us who was obviously done and waiting for their check for 15+ minutes. I finally took out my cell phone and called the front desk to request that the hostess send over a server. That worked :)
 - Took 35 minutes to get the first serving of tea (imagine trying to calm a 6-year-old during a wait like that)
 - Tablecloth was filthy with something strange, white and caked into the cloth
 - I later found out that the owner's family had chipped in to help out on the holiday. That's fine, but watching a basketball-outfit-clad boy (replete with shiny silver shorts and a graphic Nike JUST DO IT T-shirt) clean tables is NOT appropriate. I later saw that he'd swapped the shorts for black pants, but the shirt remained. Also, our server had her bright pink cell phone poking out of her back pocket. Based on the intermittent service we received (there for 90 minutes and the server stopped by just three times, and two of the visits were to drop off food/tea), I can't help but wonder if she spent more time with the phone than with us
 - No mention of complimentary champagne and mimosas. Coincidentally, I saw another table drinking champagne and decided to order a glass (I assumed I'd have to pay). This treat would have been really nice to know before mid-meal
 - We were served tea but had no silverware/spoons. There was no one nearby to ask for some time so we all just shared the sugar spoon. It wasn't until after our food arrived that we got silverware from the basketball player
 - Our high tea arrived for three adults, and one sandwich was missing. Not a biggie; they realized it right away and the sandwich soon arrived on a separate platter. Just one problem: The sandwich came topped with a piece of white plastic that didn't belong there. We gave up on trying to flag down the server, and just removed it
 - Food was OK. Some of the sandwich bread had hardened, and the scones were VERY hard. You could tell it was the last seating of the day
 - We waited another 15 minutes at the end of the meal for the server to check in on us, bring the check, etc. No one arrived. We finally just got up and went to the front


Overall, I was disappointed. There was more negative than positive, and it was primarily service-based (which means correctable). This particular seating was busy but not crazy, and there was no excuse for some of the misses mentioned above. 

I love tea rooms and want this one to survive; aesthetically, it's on its way. But when it comes to service, no thanks. That's one of the main components of the high tea experience, and without it being solid, there's no reason to do this.

Why 5 stars?  Because it was one of the best and unique dining experiences that I've ever been a part of.

   ---   ---   ---

Ciro's completely knocked it out of the park!!!  

The service was about 150 stars.

The food was absolutely insane.

The cocktails were perfection.

The atmosphere was like nothing else I've ever experienced. 

Ciro's is on my very short list for the best of the Bay Area and definitely a place I would recommend for tourists and residents alike.  

As for my recommendations, I have to say that the gourmet popcorn is an absolute requirement when visiting Ciro's.  There is nothing like it on the planet.  It will make you think of popcorn differently for the rest of your life. 

We had the chicken & waffles - if you like spicy, this is the order for you.  It is definitely a couple of notches higher on the hot scale than your typical chicken and waffles.  The cheese platter is super as well.

As for cocktails, you will get a 10 minute explanation and run through about what is best for you.  I will let the professionals guide you on this train.

Ciro's = Stupid Awesome.

Magical Bangbang Shrimp with Savignon Blanc.
Simple but effective.

Bonefish is fantastic & highly recommended.

We have been going to terra sur for a year and we love it my husband is from peru and we love the traditional dishes as well as their different types of ceviches its a great change of pace from the usual peruvian food we eat all the time. great food great service great sangria by far one of our favorite date spots great atmosphere not loud you can talk and have a great time !!!!!

What a fantastic and beautiful restaurant! The food is delicious and you get so much for your money. It's fun to sit at the bar, in the main dining room or outside for people watching. The parking garage is so close, which is nice for going out to dinner in Ybor City. The staff was nice, helpful & knowledgeable. I will definitely go back again and again.
AMAZING! The chocolate brownie was soooo good! A little pricey, but very tasty. Definitely will be going back in the near future! Also the staff is very nice and friendly which is always a plus!

Just bulldoze this dump down already!  I first went to University Mall when I first moved to Tampa in 2000.  It was bad then and it has only gotten worse over the years.

There are no department stores in the mall unless you count the Dillards Outlet Store (I don't.).  The shopping options are terrible; the only store I would consider shopping at is Victoria's Secret.  The food court options are terrible with the exception of Chick-Fil-a. 

So there you go...  University Mall is the worst mall in Tampa. Only shop at University Mall if you need lingerie or chicken tenders.

My daughter and I took my bestie for her birthday and I have to say based on the reviews we thought it was going to be fab....meh not so much the service was great but the food was SALTY we might as well been licking a salt rock....mine was THE saltiest of all my daughter's was second and my bestie's who ordered fish was the least salty but still TOO much sodium if we didnt have hypertension before we did after we couldnt finish our meals......yeech

Mama Han did it right!  First off, its a BYOB, which already gives it an additional star, so if I could make it 6 stars, I would.

Staff was extremely friendly, and attentive.  They are very good with the water, and that is a huge deal!  The last thing you want when stuffing your face with very hot and spicy food is an empty glass of water.

So did someone mention that the food here is hot?  The dan dan noodles are as advertised, i may have to get 2 orders next time.  The entrees are loaded with delicious flavor, and although my mouth felt like I was chewing on a fireball, it was like an Asian flaming fireball gusher bursting with flavor, that I couldn't get enough of.

Just to give you a sense of the spiciness... When you order from a corner  Chinese restaurant, they usually put that pepper symbol next to the kung pao chicken to let you know it is hot.  Well at Han, the Kung Pao chicken is rated a 3 (out of 10) on their spicy scale, which is about equivalent to the water they serve in your goblet.

I will definitely be back multiple times, if you haven't tried it, you must.

WOW, I had a reservation "obviously" but when my party and I arrive I had the password but they said that they did not have my information down even though they called to give me the password. WTF yes its true that the food and drinks are amazing, but apparently thats only if they let you in. The Owner was really RUDE, then about 30 minutes later they called to see if I was able to come because they found my reservation under someone elses name. NO THANK YOU.

I read the review in the TBTimes and had to try this place out.

It's located about a block away from the central Ybor parking lot.  The restaurant has great high ceilings but isn't as loud as many other buildings of this sort.  I could actually hold a conversation with my husband without losing my voice.  Some of the staff was quite wonderful, although our actual waiter was a little indifferent to us and to our service.

Since the steak meals come with salad, we did not try out any of the appetizers.  I started with the wedge salad and my husband tried the Caesar salad.  I found the bleu cheese dressing to be a tiny bit sweet, but I found it otherwise delightful.  My husband thought there was too much anchovy in his dressing.

For our entrees, my husband had the prime rib, and I had the chopped steak.  The prime rib was a little rarer than my husband had ordered, and he felt the need to add a little salt to it.  Our waiter did not automatically bring au jus or horseradish to him, nor did he offer it.  My husband loved the mashed potatoes he had on the side.  I found the meat very tender.  My chopped steak was perfect.  The fried egg on top oozed a wonderful yolk to enrich the pan drippings into a wonderful sauce.  The meat was perfectly seasoned and cooked.  And I could have eaten those mushrooms forever.  I tried the green beans as my side, and they were very buttery but pretty darned good.

The wine list seems like a better deal if you can get a bottle.  Since my husband wanted tequila, I sampled two of the wines they serve by the glass.  The merlot was great, but the Australian red blend I tried wasn't quite to my taste.

For dessert, we shared the cheesecake.  I really wanted to try the strawberry shortcake or the flan, but my husband was insistent.  The cheesecake was dense and lovely, and we finished every last bite.

The whole food fest for two was under $100 including drinks and tip.  It's quite a deal.

The only deviled crab roll I've ever tried was from the Airport Variety store in Clearwater, supposedly famous for their cubans and crab rolls, and it was the most disgusting, soggy off tasting piece of food ever to have ventured into my mouth. 

Until a coworker brought me one from here. WOW, what have I been missing? All this time, who would have known, a delicious crispy HUGE crab filled yummy roll that was a meal to itself.

Coworker states it's about $6 or $7 for one of these babies but I can see why. He also stated, as most reviewers have, that you really should call in for pick up because it gets crazy packed and service can take forever.

I'll have to try this one in person!

What?! I could have sworn I wrote a review for BK's.  I love this place!  My family loves the place!  Their tortas are amazing, and they have the best sonoran dogs in town!  In a showdown of sonoran dogs from various restaurants in Tucson, this place wins hands down.

The decor here is pretty cool too.  I love that they use La Lotería cards underneath the glass on their tables.  The chandeliers are cool too, though they are a little incongruous with the rest of the place.  Check out the ceiling when you're here too.  I don't know what to make of the art pieces/posters, but it sure is interesting.  Really though, come for the food!
