I stopped in after being remiss in not visiting this place for a while. Still an exceptional little Viet deli. The BBQ pork banh mi is absolute heaven. The pho ga is magical. The place is now really mobbed at the key dining times, so watch out for that. But you must go and try either of these dishes. Forget frenetic Dale Mabry and take Himes Ave all the way to Waters. Yum.

Terra Sur is an upscale Peruvian restaurant located on the side of a dead strip mall.  This is not a place you just drive by, but it's a place you need to make a point to visit.  The food is outstanding. I had the paella sans mussels, a mixture of seasoned rice, tenderloin, chicken strips, shrimp and chorizo.  OMG. Bring your big boy clothes, the servings are huge!

My wife had the Anticuchos de Carne, 8 grilled pieces of skirt steak served with yellow rice and an amazing chimichurri sauce.  My wife ate that and half the paella, and we rolled out of this place fat and happy. 

The service was outstanding.  The ambiance is nice, classy, and upscale.  I'd definitely bring a client here.  

Nice looking AND tasting Latin American restaurants are few and far between.  Terra Sur is a gem of a restaurant for those looking for a delicious meal in a classy environment with great service.  This is as good as it gets!

The atmosphere is awesome. Boats going by, the food at SONO is over priced and too small of portions but I am not rating the park on that. You can play on the lawn, sit in the amphitheater or in the shade.

     Honestly the best thing to do is to grab a drink, sit and watch the kids fall off all the various contraptions they have at the playground.  There is this wheel that spins on its side and all the kids try to run on it. I have seen some awesome wipeouts! It's even more awesome because the parents are so negligent!

We had my bachelorette party dinner at Council Oak Steaks and Seafood in the Hard Rock Casino.  This restaurant gets packed so make sure you make dinner reservations!  Even though we had dinner reservations we had to wait thirty minutes for a table.  The wait wouldn't have been that bad, but we were in heels and there was no seating area to wait at.  

The restaurant is GORGEOUS.  From the moment you walk up to the hostess stand you see the brick wall with beads of water trickling down it.  The restaurant is very classy and modern and has a large open kitchen.  I love that diners are able to look into the kitchen because you never know what kind of environment your food could be cooking in... everything looks clean and sterile in the Council Oaks kitchen!

The prices aren't cheap, so save this spot for a night that you want to splurge on a good steak.  There wasn't a single item we ordered that wasn't cooked to perfection.  The USDA Prime dry aged filet mignon melted in your mouth.  We got a side of perfectly steamed asparagus to accompany our steaks.  Our table also got a macarroni dish for the table to split and it was to die for!  I finished the meal off with a creme brulee... so good!  It was a huge portion so I split it with my sister.

Sounds like whore house. The atmosphere is great. They have a lot of great beers. It is a nice and dark atmosphere to get away from the sun and relax. This would be my favorite bar to go to if I lived in or around Tampa.
Probably one of the best happy hours in Tampa bay. On the weekends, sit out on the patio and enjoy half off apps and beer/wine/martini specials.

So after reading the reviews of this place I found it to be exactly what I felt as I thought back on all the times I have been here with different girls that just have to have that boba drink. I have always had a bite here and there of the food here and really I always thought the portions were quite skimpy for the price tag. Today a couple girlfriends mentioned this place, and I said " ahh what the hell" and we went. We arrived around 7:45 ish pm and placed our order, we told the server we were going to sit outside as it was just an amazing summer evening with perfect breeze. Well she looked back at us like well you pay and we give it to you, kinda awkward. Anyhow we paid. We ordered, Tai curry chicken, teriyaki chicken, pho xe lu something. My soup was pretty good, so the reviews of the pho are on hit. THIS PLACE HAS GOOD PHO. Sorry added that for the skimmers. Anyhow we ordered 2 egg rolls, and the other girl ordered 2. Unbeknownst to us this place CLOSES at 8PM on FRIDAY!! WTF.. Its Tucson a sleepy ass a town!! Anyhow, they are sorta shooing customers out, and we are out on the patio with their nice bowls and spoons, and they literally shut the lights off and come up and flip the sign to closed just as we got our food. We noticed that they only brought one egg roll out and when we asked them about this they tried to act like we only ordered one. They don't offer an itemized receipt so it was yet again another awkward moment. They finally realized it after we talked to the person that took the order. Then the worst part was, being it was such a nice night we weren't really trying to just gulp down our meal and get the hell outta there. So we try to ignore the pressure that we are already there too long... a whopping 10 minutes, including the wait to order. The staff literally just stared at us, eventually I just asked for a to go container. This is pretty bad! I would at least let a customer know that we close in 15 minutes and we recommend a to go order. 

Food:
Pho = Really good!!! I like the tripe and the tendons, I never had this and it was pretty good, the texture was pretty spot on for a new food to try,

The chicken plates were pretty inconsistent, one was proportioned right, and the other was seriously a sad excuse for a 8 or 9 dollar price tag. 

What to take away from this:
Where the hell is this Miss Saigon's Im on my way!!

We came upon this place when we decided to go for a walk around Little Italy. My daughter and I fell in love with the place as soon as we realized that fresh pasta was made on the premises. We saw this girl cutting pasta through this machine by the window. That got us to go in! We bought pasta and sauce and loved it. We are now spoiled nd refused to buy boxed pasta! Will definitely return!

As soon as you take a seat at this place you feel like you're on the beach in california. All their tacos were great but the korean bbq and shrimp were definitely my favorites. Price is right and they even have specials on the tacos all the time. 

This is probably one of my favorite restaurants in Tampa, what a find and a must visit any time i'm back in Tampa.

I think everyone in Tampa has lived in the Cove at one point or another. This is a great starter apartment for young couples or recent college grads.  I lived in a studio apartment my first year out of college because I needed a safe, but affordable apartment.

The apartment might not be the most modern or have the most amenities, but it is a gated community, has three swimming pools, tennis courts and a small gym.  The gorgeous sunsets are the highlight of the apartment. Just grab a few beers and find a lawn chair by the pool for one of the prettiest views.

The location is great, minutes from the Gandy Bridge, Westshore Mall, and I 275.  The Green Iguana is stumbling distance from the apartment if your looking for a nearby burger and cocktail.

Downfalls of the apartment: waiting to make a lefthand turn onto Westshore Boulevard during in heavy traffic, the younger and noisy residents, and the paper thin walls. I could hear my next-door neighbor peeing in his bathroom through my adjoining wall!

My biggest gripe was that the apartment does not enforce the dog rules. There were constantly dogs running loose without leashes and some neighbors did not pick up after their dogs... Seriously, if you won't pick up after your dog then don't own a dog!!!

The apartment is not perfect, but it served it's purpose when I was young and broke.

We only stopped in for drinks before going somewhere else for dinner.  Love the building & location itself, but the decor could use some help!  This location deserves & needs more than what they've done with it!  Hope they do more to the place over time!  The drinks were good & the staff was friendly!

The man who took my order was borderline competent, at best. He got my name wrong, kept me on hold, and took an eternity to take down my very simple order.

I was told that the food would be delivered in 45 minutes. An hour later I called to see where my food might be, and was told that the driver was "just leaving." 

When the food finally arrived, it was cold. The delivery driver apologized for the wait, but did not explain it. 

The Buffalo wings were too sweet, and lacked the tanginess that is the defining feature of such fare. As a young pie, the pizza might have had some vigor, but in its old age the grease had congealed and the toppings lay limp and lifeless. The cheesecake was cheesecake, only hard to eat because they did not bring plastic utensils.

Yes! Movies are expensive if you look at it that way.
No! you cant bring in outside food.thank god.
And I personally adore this place
Its always clean and everyone has been nice there.
more then I can say for most places and I go to the movies a lot.
Its one of my only chances to find peace and relax.
and they have a photo booth me and the girl use for fun fridge pictures.YAY!

Our first time here. It was a very pleasant surprise. The service was good. The martinis well shaken icey perfect. The shrimp scampi was perfect. The shrimp huge and cooked perfect not tough but juicy. My boyfriend had the pizza oven roasted ribeye and it was incredible. Cooked perfect almost 2inches thick what a surprise. We will be back often.

UPDATE!

After years of saying NO to Wi-Fi, the Sage has given in. EVENINGS ONLY

When I moved to the area, the first think I looked for was karaoke. I tried this place during the week. I was greeted with a moderate crowd, perfect for weekday partying, GREAT bartenders, excellent karaoke selection and GERMAN BEER!!!!!! I returned a few weeks later but on the weekend  for my "karaoke fix". The party was hoppin', the place was almost filled to capacity. The singers were better than I expected and I got more GERMEN BEER!!!! The bartenders wasted no time flirting and playfully harassing we and the other women at the bar (We didn't mind). 

There was also an outdoor BEIRGARTEN in the back I hadn't noticed during my first visit. They had Dub Step, Techno and karaoke.

Great drink specials on Jager Bombs and Irish Car Bombs, even on the WEEKEND!

 I will be faithful customer for a long time to come :) 


HINT: arrive early for cheaper parking, like the "suntrust" lot behind the bar.

I am from Texas and I can honestly say this place is hands down the best place so far in tampa. The brisket was out of this world juicy tender awesome. The ribs and chicken were tasty too and the smoked sausage even better. 

Service was awesome people were kind and thoughtful deserved all their tip and more! 

absolutely will go there again 

Pricing was very fair

WERE DOING THE LOUIE LOUIE!!! Thanks Brett for this CYME. First off I had never been to one let a lone a free one it was cool. I like the gifts Brett had set up for us and the atmosphere was nice. The servers and the hospitality was over the top great. They were very busy with these thirsty yelpers but they held there own. Also, thanks to the manager that gave us complementary nachos(tasty) and pretzels. This was a good night with good convos and a nice spot that I could continue to go to. Thanks to Brett I had a good night. 


Oh and the guy singing in the front was jammin out....

This place is legit. I got a Shack Burger Double, some Fries, and a Black & White Shake.

The burger lived up to the expectations I had from all the hype surrounding this place. The fries are also very very good. Crinkle-Cut Fries are my favorite and they were not greasy, oily, or soggy here. It was crispy and not overly salty. The shake was also a good way to end the meal.

The wait was not too long for Opening Day at 1:30 (Maybe 20 minutes total). Service was great and the staff was friendly and insightful. They talked to everyone and gave out samples of their custard while people waited in line. They cleaned the tables once people left and I noticed they also responded to any order errors immediately.

Comparing this place to 500 Degrees and Five Guys (in terms of Burgers and Fries), I would rank them in this order:

1) Shake Shack
2) 500 Degrees
3) Five Guys

I have been a customer of Shake Shack for a long time at the NYC locations. When I heard they were coming to Philly I was super excited. Customer service top notch! The burgers are a nice thick size too not those flat like burgers. The concretes are delish! I love the crinkle fries.

I'm a huge fan of their California bowls and wraps. Even just the basic wrap or bowl are delicious. I'm not sure why another reviewer thought it was bland. Perhaps one of the cooks had an off night. I've loved everything I've tried here. Just go when you want something simple but surprisingly flavorful.

This is a 3 star restaurant. Inventive menu, but the food is just OK. Nice space, but the service is just OK. I usually have a California bowl and sometimes it's great, other times it's just OK. It's always kind of hot in there, also. They do have good sauces. Overall I would say this place is kind of gimmicky with OK food.

Oh. My. Word. The hour+ time I spent on line waiting for my chance at the famed New York City Shake Shack burger was, without a doubt, worth every hunger-pang inducing second. And I'd do it again. The Shack Burger was perfection.  The shake, dreamy.  The employees, friendly. The ambiance, sleek. Loved it from top to bottom and back again.  Thank you, Shake Shack, for bestowing the best damn burger I've ever eaten upon the City of Brotherly Love.

yes.  yes.  and...well....YES.

i hit up shake shack around 5:30 p.m. on the day of its grand-opening.  i'm familiar with  the D.C. location in DuPont Circle.  when i heard it was coming to Philly....i cried tears of joy.

yessssssss!

after debating for 10 minutes whether or not we should stand in line, we were sucked in by an uber-friendly hostess who provided us with a laminated menu.  

we ordered the shackburger with bacon (his was a double), cheese fries, and two fountain sodas.  a bit on the expensive side....but well worth it for a good burger, great fries, and really great service.

perfect crinkle cut fries...golden and fried to perfection.  my fiancee siad the salt was fried into the potatoes...whatever that lmeans...

we took our food, sat in Rittenhouse Square, enjoyed the scenery, and were pleasantly suprised by how GREAT everything was.

i've been to PYT....the burgers are for show.  dress them up with fancy names and things that don't belong on burgers....i just want meat, a good bun, some fresh toppings, and a decent sauce.

if i could give more than five stars, i would.  

i WILL be going again....really soon.

I got in line quarter of 11 AM on opening day and was pleasantly surprised at the size of the line. Every minute the line got longer and longer. I felt like all of the staff had taken ecstacy before beginning their shifts based on their wide-eyed enthusiasm and excitement. 

Doors opened and the line moved swiftly. I ordered the ShakeMeister Ale brewed specially for Shake Shack by Brooklyn Brewery. It was a refreshing pale ale that was perfect for washing down the delicious cheeseburger that I devoured within 60 seconds. The meat was juicy and flavorful and the bun was soft and buttery. I also had a side of perfectly crisp crinkle fries. Typing this review makes me want to get back in line and this time double the meat :). 

Next time I go back I will definitely try their shake of the day, the blackberry buttermilk sounded amazing.

The buzz for this place has been fed by hype from "bridge and bridge" people as well as transplants who really really wish they could have lived in "NYC" but had to settle for Philly instead.   Going some place to be seen is sad; it's especially sad when that place serves burgers and shakes.  Being impressed with a long line is a sign you're lacking something in your life.

I went, because I wanted to confirm that it was all hype, plus I love me some burgers and shakes. I just wasn't any more impressed by this place than I am by any decent diner that serves milkshakes.  Sorry, kids, you're buying into a slick product again.  But it's OK, because you look cool while doing it.

PS:  Doobies has had bowling alley tables since before they were cool, and don't make a big fucking deal about it.

Whoever thought of combining cheese-stuffed fried mushrooms with a juicy burger patty is absolutely genius.  Shake shack takes that one step further by adding Blizzard-like custard blends to the menu, which takes care of both dinner and dessert in one delicious fell swoop.  The shack stack is the perfect combination burger with the fried stuffed portabella and a juicy beef patty topped off with a mystery sauce that seems to be a yummy combination of garlic, dijon, mayo, and ketchup.  Whatever it is, it's delicious.  Unlike the patties at other semi-gourmet chain burger places, the thick patty here isn't overcooked or too dry (Five Guys, I'm looking at you here).  The buttered bun is pretty good too.  Just be warned that the combination of huge fried cheesy mushroom and a thick burger make for a very filling meal.  It doesn't look huge but appearances can be deceiving, as we all know from Macbeth.  I also got a Rittenhouse concrete (chocolate soft serve with coffe beans, marshmallow sauce, and a rich chocolate cookie dough) so I decided to save half of my burger so that I could attack the custard.  The concrete was deliciously creamy but very very rich and quite suitable for coffee-chocolate addicts, like me. I didn't get a chance to try the fries but  they looked very fresh and my friend enjoyed his immensely; they are the crinkle-thick-cut type and a nice golden color.  Next time, for sure.  Yes, the wait is long (30+ min) and the prices are a bit high ($9 for a shack stack and $4.25 for a half concrete) but the food is great and the atmosphere inside busy and crowded but fun.  I should also note that the other half of my burger is still delicious an hour and a half later (I'm eating it as I type; I did toast it in the oven for 3 min. though).  I really do need to find the shack sauce recipe...

Jimmy John's "Gourmet" sandwiches are not gourmet... it says that right on the menu.  I don't need gourmet; I just need a good, tasty, consistent sandwich.  Initially I rated the sandwiches 3 stars.  I wasn't crazy about the monsterous pile of iceberg lettuce, but overall the sandwiches were a-ok.

The food and the service have dramatically gone downhill at Jimmy John's.  50% of my orders have been delivered incorrectly.  The driver has forgotten my chips or cookie or one of my sandwiches.  I usually don't make the driver redeliver the missing food unless they bring me the wrong sandwich.  It's easier to just call and get the missing food refunded to my credit card.  The deliveries aren't even fast anymore.  I have waited up to 45 minutes for my order to deliver.  What's the point?  I may as well pick it up myself.  The poor drivers get overwhelmed with too many orders and I think that's why so many mistakes are made.

The quality of the sandwiches have also gone downhill.  I usually order the Vito Italian sub or the J.J. Gargantuan sandwich because they have the most meat and cheese.  The other sandwiches really skimp on the protein.  Lately the meat has not been the freshest.  I had to throw away my last Gargantuan sandwich because it tasted spoiled and I couldn't manage to stomach more than two bites.

Would not recommend Jimmy Johns even though they are one of the few restaurants that deliver.

We were SO excited to try Han after everything awesome we have heard. We ordered take-out: vegetarian dan dan noodles and tofu with garlic sauce. Overall, the food was pretty good, but it was very oily. It was nothing mind-blowing in our opinion. The texture of the noodles was different and good--they were almost doughy, but not undercooked. The tofu was a weird texture. The one positive is that neither of us ended up with "upset stomachs" the following morning, which usually happens after spicy Chinese. It's obvious that they use good ingredients!

I went today to Shake Shack after having been a couple of times in NYC and not feeling the hype. After this visiting this location, I can see the love, though.

I had a SmokeShack (basically a bacon cheeseburger with red pepper instead of tomato) and a rootbeer. The burger was cooked to diner-y perfection with grease, etc, etc. My only gripe is that the drink prices seem pretty inflated (almost $4 for my rootbeer).

What will probably keep me coming back is the service. As there was a long wait, I was hurriedly trying to put my debit card back in and be as quick as possible when I was ordering. The cashier went out of his way to tell me to take my time and make sure I had everything. In addition, the girl handling the "pickup order" section could not have been more pleasant and friendly. 

As for the restaurant itself, there are some tables but I wouldn't try to go here (at least until the hype dies down) with a large group as it's kind of a crazy atmosphere.

Sometimes it seems all I ever talk about is cupcakes. As Philly's unofficial "King of Desserts," I have to ensure that no cupcake goes untasted in our busy Philly market.  Someone this weekend actually walked by me, pointed and said, "Hey, there's the cupcake guy!"  While I do love (L.O.V.E.) my cupcakes, I have a lesser known but life-time long sweet tooth for all things ice cream.  (In fact, in between cupcake feedings, I can often be found lurking at PYT, Franklin Square's Square Burger and Bobby's Burger Place slurping down alcoholic and non-alcoholic shake concoctions filled with Tastykakes, birthday cake, candy bars, real pistachios and graham cracker. Needless to say, when I therefore heard that this Shake Shack place from NYC was moving to Philly I was excited!  I heard only good things about them - and I couldn't wait to get my hands on one of their cool treats.

Finally, it's opening day and a new shack is in town! With loud buzz and much fan fair, Shake Shack opened its doors today with it's delicious menu of burgers, hot dogs, fries, shakes, custard and "Concretes."   They even have specially made dog treats.  I was fortunate enough to attend two previews - and here's my full report!

When I first entered Shake Shack I was first power greeted by the super friendly hostess right at the door. She was joined by a team that would then welcome me, find out if I had any questions, provide recommendations and move me in and out of the line in a timely manner.  (Sometimes that is no small feat as I can dilly dally!)  The crew's high spirits, hand shaking and enthusiastic voice set the tone for what would be a fun experience.  As I worked my way towards the menu I was greeted by delicious but much smaller menu than I expected.  Apparently, the menu is not enormous but what they do, they do well! After great inspection, I  placed my order with the super friendly and helpful cashier.  She provided me with a buzzer and directed me to the back of the restaurant.   Then I waited, for what seemed like an eternity.  It wasn't in fact more than 10 minutes, but I was so hungry and so excited I nearly burst from my chair and ran track-style down the corridor to pick up my first tray of treats.  (No, seriously!)

From first glance, they didn't look like anything out of the ordinary. Then I tasted then and realized why Shake Shack had such a magic hold on New York City.   The stand-outs in the bunch are the Liberty Shell Concrete, the Shack-cago Dog and the Caramel Shake.  The Liberty Shell Concrete was a blend of vanilla custard with strawberry pure,  lemon ricotta and Termini Bros. cannoli shells.  The shells added a nice crispness to the blend -- and the lemon ricotta mingled with the custard was something dreams are made of.   The Shack-cago far exceeded my expectations!  In fact, it actually took my prize for best item on the menu -- which is a lot coming from someone who doesn't often order hot dogs.  What made it so special?  It was topped with everything I love:  Rick's Picks Shack relish, short pepper, celery salt, mustard, onion and pickle all literally.  It even had a cucumber on it, which provided some interesting texture against the dog itself.

Additionally, I enjoyed the fries, cheese sauce, Black and White milkshake and the Fair Shake.  The ShackBurger was delicious, but the overly pink center ruined the tasty toppings for me.  (Crew reported that all burgers are cooked medium, so make sure to ask for it medium well or well if you don't want the pink.) I definitely would order it again with special instructions next time.   The Center City Pretzel was probably my least favorite item.  It was super sweet but none of the flavors stood out.  This could have been due to all the Marshmallow sauce.  While it wasn't on my list to order again, I could see how someone who loved caramel and banana would be a fan!

While I ate my meal and took photos, the staff surprised me yet again with a small touch that went a long way!  My cashier came out from behind the counter and addressed me by name.  She wanted to know about my favorite item and how my experience was.  I later learned that the cashiers are actually trained to remember customers names and connect with them again during their visit.  Due to the high volume in the Shack, it was amazing to think they can juggle this on top of providing great food in a timely manner. Bravo to her and the whole team!

Overall, my experience was a good one!  I definitely will be dreaming of Liberty Shell Custards and Shack-Cago dogs this week.  I might even need to stop for another on my lunch break.

My favorite brunch place in all of Philadelphia. I can even tolerate the 1-2 hour wait for parties of 3+ on Saturdays and Sundays. Everything is amazing, the service is friendly and helpful (even when there are a zillion college students asking when their table will be ready), and the coffee is great. The food is DELICIOUS. My favorites are the French Toast (which by the way needs its own zip code) and the Eggs Benedict. I highly recommend both this location and the one at 9th and Christian.

Busy brunch/lunch on a Monday? I guess GEC is more popular than I thought it would be. Regardless, I found myself sitting at one of the neat wooden tables with a little plant as a center piece. The place is kept very neat and didn't make me feel like a loser by being a solo diner. Yay. 

The menu offered really yummy and intriguing options. From eggs to sweets to salads to sandwiches... And vegan dishes, too. They were all so awesome sounding. Creme brûlée French toast(what!), fig and prosciutto salad(huh?!), red velvet pancakes(for realz!!!)... I have at least three reasons right there to return for.

Anyways. Being the eggs benedict lover that I am, I was extra thrilled to see four different kinds of eggs benny options. The only sad part was choosing one since they all sounded great. I went with smoked lox eggs benedict with Rosemary potatoes. I think I hit a jack pot. I mean, the eggs(you get two) were perfectly poached then placed on top of soft brioche bread dressed with hollandaise sauce and fried capers. Then, there were rolls of salty, savory smoked salmon with veggie cream cheese. No dried salmon bs here. It was just delicious. The potatoes were a little dry but still very delicious nonetheless. Just enough rosemary aroma to accompany all the other amazing flavors on my plate. I was very happy. 

Like everyone else, I also think they serve great coffee. But that's really the last thing on my mind when I have a dish full of yummy food in front of me to consume. All. To. Myself. Yes, you can be jealous. 

Although I was happy that I made such a good choice on the first meal of my day, on the first day of this week, I wish that I had saved the luck for some other important matters of my life. Like choosing a  Mr. Right. Le sigh. Tis the life.

So yum... everyone raving about the burger but the Shakes are the reason to come...  Strawberry shortcake- my go to-, Banana's foster - not fake banana ish , PB and Choc, Choc Malt. All good. I'll hold my opinion on the burger till I try out burger monger too but I've been back to this place 5 or 6 times despite it being a little bit of a drive from S. Tampa and I'm sure I'll be back

Thanks Kenney D for making me trust Yelp again.  I have gone to few places yelp had good reviews of and was dissapointed.  Kenney D's did not!  Great Italian Beef - Great Fries.  We went back the next day it was so good.

Need to try more on the menu but to be honest - I can just have the beef and be just fine.

My only negative with this Publix is that I feel that I have to put on make-up with my yoga pants. Not fair. 

In all seriousness, I adore this place... as Collin said, it is a little piece of heaven in South Tampa. The ready to eat selections are always good, very fresh and for the most part healthy. At least you feel healthy because you are not eating out of a box!

It is pricy, I wouldn't do all your basic shopping here because you can for sure get the essentials much cheaper elsewhere but you do pay for quality... and when it comes to all the good stuff... the quality is there for sure.

Wonderful stay and what a welcome after checking out of a different Sheraton earlier (see Sheraton Gateway review). Before we even entered the hotel, I could tell the stay was gonna be pleasant and I wish we could have stayed longer than one night. Everyone and I mean, EVERYONE whom we encountered as far as staff, we're pleasant, accommodating, and professional...from the valet, to the housekeeping staff and everyone in between. Our room was quite large, very cozy, clean, and quiet. The pool area was beautiful and it overlooked the sparkling bay. There was a park right beside the hotel where you could walk the dog and the park provided the little bags to clean up after your dog. I have absolutely no complaints about this hotel whatsoever! I just wish all Sheratons were on the same level! I will be back and this time I will get a room facing the bay!

Found another work lunch spot! Very cute place! All of us liked how quaint the restaurant seemed because of its decor. We happened to all order Pad Thai, an obvious default pick for any Thai restaurant. The lunch came with a spring roll and a tapioca dessert. From the spring roll to the Pad Thai to the tapioca dessert, I was a happy foodie. And my wallet was pretty happy too. Reasonable prices for lunch. So who can complain?!

Good coffee and pastries but not much room inside to sit. The outdoor seating is nice though.  One of the baristas (maybe owner) is living in the old world though-- every time I go in there with a woman he has some chauvinist comment to make ("ohho ladies first..." "...and for the pretty lady", etc., etc...--  stfu pal, it's annoying).

It's pretty standard as far as malls go. Your typical middle of the road stores with a movie theatre and a few decent restaurants. It's a Westfield mall, so it's not nearly as well kept as a Simon mall (think International Plaza), but it gets the job done.

We had an appointment with Ellen today for our wedding cake tasting. She was very organized and listened to what my needs and wants are in a cake. 

She had me email her pictures of cakes I liked and was able to work off the emails. 

I really, really liked her and can't wait to get my quote.
The best place to find foreign movies including old vhs and dvd movies!!! Love this place!

I really like this place and should go more often.  If only it were a bit closer to home ...  A really great, eclectic, and inexpensive beer menu.  I hear the wine selection is great and the sangria is amazing (not a wine guy, but it's what I hear).  I genuinely dig the vibe of this place.  The owner is around often with his greyhound, and seems to keep to himself. Bartenders are all friendly.  This has a locals vibe to it.  The neighborhood is not great, but it's also not that bad.  Loads of parking.  Sometimes they have a BBQ truck outside, if not Taco Bus, Ella's, The Refinery are all close by.

Why 3 stars? Because it was average.

   ---   ---   ---

3.5 stars rounded down here. I do enjoy Kona Grill, but didn't find anything that wowed me enough to make a return trip. I heavily debated between Pad Thai or sushi, but with everyone else getting rolls, I didn't want to be the odd-man out.

I must have tried half the menu between all of the rolls on our table...and again...nothing was horrible or tremendous. I hate giving what's even considered a "neutral" review, but I didn't notice much of a difference between this sushi and what could be found at Publix. 

I would like to go back and try a regular entree, but until then...I apologize for the lack of stand-taking in this review.

This place was fantastic.

We went because my husband was craving a burger, and I wanted to try a local, non-chain restaurant.  What a wonderful surprise!

The best part of the place was the amazing service we received.  Our waiter was constantly checking on us (in a non-obnoxious way) and refilling drinks.  More impressively, the owner came to our table multiple times, remembered our names, and even comped us dessert.

I had the fried pork chop sandwich, and it was wonderful.  I went with the super spicy sauce, which had the right amount of kick for me.  Apparently most people find it too spicy, so even after the waiter insisted that I order the sauce on the side, the owner came out to make sure that I didn't want to switch to the normal spicy sauce.  Whatever.  Oh, the fries were perfectly crisp, and I loved the spices.

My husband had his cheeseburger, and he enjoyed it.  He had a cup of chili instead of fries, and he said that he'd happily order it again.

As I mentioned above, dessert was on the house.  The owner insisted that we try the orangesicle cheesecake, which tasted very orange-y and rich.  It was great (if you love oranges -- and I do), but next time, I want to try the neapolitan cheesecake.

The homemade chips and ranch dip were phenomenally simple yet tasty.  I'd go back to EATS again just for the chips and a beer.  But, of course, then I'd want the cheesecake.  And a fried pork sandwich.  

Oh, be forewarned: on Tuesdays, kids eat free, so Tuesdays are crowded with kids.  That might or might not be a positive in your book.

Food here was better than I expected, given that the waiter kept referring to me as 'dude' and 'bro'. Had a bunch of the appetizers, standouts were the fried chicken sliders and the Peruvian corn cakes - the sauces and salsas were also really good. Paired it with a cheap, delicious Argentinian Malbec and it was a very decent and surprisingly inexpensive meal.

Holy haircut! I just got the best haircut from Ashley (young brunette). I hate getting my hair cut because stylists tend to overrule what I ask for and go about cutting my hair how they think would be best. Ashley listened to what I wanted and gave me the perfect haircut. It is exactly what I wanted and it looks great. I will definitely go back.

$3 for a small popsicle is pricey, but it's definitely worth it! I love that this place has grown up flavors like goat cheese and cherry (which is super delicious) and is literally two blocks from my house. I know what my new "day off" treat will be all summer.

One of the ghettoest mall I have ever seen.  Very loud people and trouble makers throughout the mall.  The movie theater is very hard to tell if it is open or out of business.  A lot of empty and closed lots.  There are many cheap apparel & jewelry stores.  One of the worst malls in America.

I LOVE THIS PLACE. $10 bucks for quality food. Chicken is juicy and falafel is delicious. The sides always change. First time I enjoyed Hummus was from this truck. DELICIOUS!!! The falafel man is very upbeat, friendly, talkative, and nice. He brags about quality food and he has a reason to. Be prepared to wait about a half hour, but it's worth it!

Anthony, U r a bit tricky to find.
U should get a bigger sign where the tree doesn't cover it.
Thx for having an outside patio w the bar on both side. That's nice and convenient for ur patrons.  Ur eggplant app, ribs, meatballs wings r quite tasty, but I did have some wings that were overdone. I tasted it the next day. The toppings on ur pizza were gd and also some diff ones like Philly steak pizza and broccoli Rabi or cauliflower pizza. Ur pizza was tastier than most pizzas I've eaten in Florida. Pls note b careful of timing the pizza in oven. At times I did taste that overdone coal taste. Nonetheless I was quite happy w u

This place is a hidden gem.  It is a spacious restaurant where you can enjoy gorgeous wines and very decent food.  The wine shop is great, but find an employee to take you into their cooler for the good stuff.  They have 19th century Rothchild Lafite wines in their cellar!   Prices are similar to the wines at Bern's.  Pick out your bottle, then pay a $10 corkage to have it at the restaurant.

We had a lovely 1989 Grand Cru from Bordeaux's Margaux region.  To help wash it down, we went with their cheese and charcuterie platter.  They have an extensive list of cheese and meat.  I felt that the cheeses were better options than the meats.  We had an extra sharp Irish cheddar, a Spanish manchego, and an Italian truffled sheep and cow cheese.  We also tried the prosciutto and the Serrano ham.  They gave us cornichon, olives, dried fruit, grapes and nuts.  We probably could have stopped ordering then.

However, we wanted to try the entrees.  We both went with the special plates, so I can't comment on the regular menu, but I can say that the rabbit was mildly overcooked, but the potatoes cooked in duck fat were heavenly.  My husband finished his red snapper before I could think about stealing a bit.

Dessert looked fine, but who can eat after a bottle of wine, tons of cheese and cured meats, and generously portioned main courses? 

I don't think reservations are required since the place seemed half-empty, but it wouldn't hurt, especially if you have a large party.  There is free parking behind the restaurant.

This place had been on my list of brunch spots for a while so I was happy I finally got to try it!

Atmosphere: 5/5 Went around lunchtime on a Wednesday, so it wasn't very crowded and we were seated right away. Not too noisy.

Food: 5/5 Got the creme brulee french toast for myself and split a smoked lox benedict with my friend. The french toast was delicious and very filling (I couldn't finish it), especially if you have a sweet tooth like me... but the smoked lox benedict was amazinggg and I definitely liked it more than the french toast!

Service: 5/5 No complaints.

Price: 5/5 Prices are pretty comparable with other brunch spots, but for the quality of the food, definitely worth it.

Overall- HIGHLY RECOMMENDED

Was really excited to try this place and was expecting a meal that was to die for boy was I let down. I had the steak sandwich with a fried egg my steak was tough and cooked way to well done for me. My wife had the Cuban sandwich and said it was average at best our friend who was with us had the prime rib which he said had no taste to it. We also had the deviled eggs which were ok nothing special to them the only thing that everyone agreed on that was good was their homemade ranch dressing on the salads. It also takes along time for them to bring stuff back since the servers have to walk up a flight of stairs to get anything.

I was really looking forward to breakfast at the Green Eggs Cafe on 13th Street.  I had heard great things about the Northern Liberties location and the reviews on Yelp were good.  But sadly, I was disappointed by the food and the service.  I arrived a few minutes early to meet my friends and was told, that it was their "policy" not to seat people until the whole party was present.  (Policy?-WTF are they the State Department?).  Under most circumstances I would agree with that but not when there were 9 open tables.  I was force to stand in the door way and interfere with the wait staff trying to service the customers sitting outside.  Was I in their way, well sorry it is my policy not to stand out in the sun when it is hot.  

My friends arrived and we were seated at a table but 30 seconds later another hostess (is that politically correct?) asked us to move because they had a party of nine coming in and needed "that table".  So far they were not off to great start.  We ordered coffee and asked for a few minutes to look at the menu and the specials.  My friend ordered decaf and I order regular coffee and when it arrived I know I got the decaf (believe me I can tell).  We placed our orders and waited for 20 minutes to be served.  Meanwhile I noticed one table of two people who arrived several minutes after we arrived had gotten their food and the table of nine was already eating. (Maybe they phoned ahead?)

My friend order the peanut butter french toast which he said was great, but my other friend ordered the smoked salmon omelet which she said was dry and flavorless.  I ordered the three eggs my way and the vegan sausage.  Normally, the vegan sausage products I have tried at other places, most notably Honey-Sit-and-Eat, is great.  Green Eggs's  was hard as a rock, ice cold and terrible tasting.  We ordered multi-grain toast which arrived cold and barely toasted. No jam was served with the toast and the butter was apparently being rationed.   There was no salt or pepper on the table and no catsup or hot sauce either.  I asked for both but only got the hot sauce.  The rosemary potatoes were also disappointing, partially cooked and flavorless (hence the need for catsup and hot sauce). 

I was hoping for a breakfast place that rivals HS&E closer to Center City but I guess the trip to Northern Liberties is worth it.  HS&E is far better food and great, friendly service.  Wise up Green Eggs....

This is a hidden gem in Tampa - even long-time residents don't know about it.  From service to food everything was spot on.  One of us had the Get Blitzed Tasting Menu - of which each course was delicious and unique.  Everything is beautifully presented - so much so that we were inspired to take pictures (and we are not people who take pictures of our every meal).  Cocktails were fun and very tasty, and deserts were to die for - especially a "fancy" Snickers bar-ish desert and the panna cotta.  For the quality of the food, the prices are quite reasonable (we recently went to Charlie Trotter's in Chicago, and found this to be a MUCH better meal all around).  Highly, highly recommend it!

I came here with my bf for lunch last week. Although there are very few seats, the turnover is pretty fast, so we were able to get seated within minutes. It seems like most people pick up their order to go. While the menu is limited to tacos and burritos, everything is fresh and made to order. $6 for 2 tacos isn't cheap, but the tacos are huge and very filling. They come with salsa and guacamole unless you tell them otherwise. The taco fillings we got were chicken, pork, and steak & yucca. All were warm and delicious! The yucca added crispy texture to the tacos. The shells were soft. The fillings were moist and well-seasoned. The service was a bit insipid, but overall, it seems like a pretty solid establishment. The person sitting next to me had a very yummy-looking burrito, so I'll have to come back to try the burrito next time.

I've driven by this place so many times, but I never stop in because I rarely have a taste for seafood. I had a taste for seafood today, so I figured it was the perfect opportunity to try Shell's.

First, the atmosphere is very geriatric. VERY. Besides the ancient decor, I'm pretty sure the Golden Girls were dining directly behind me. When I walk in and see a bunch of old people, I do not have high hopes, as their taste buds are just as old and dusty as they are. 

On to the food- I got the fried everything platter, because I like variety, and I sure as hell like fried. The platter came with clams, scallops, fish, and shrimp, with new potatoes and cole slaw on the side. 

The clams were a little chewy & fishy, but I don't know jack shit about clams, so I'm not sure how they're supposed to taste. The scallops were good (ask for drawn butter- the other sauces overwhelm the scallops' delicate flavor) but tiny. I was hoping for larger scallops- quality over quantity. I got the opposite, but still, tasty enough. I was super bummed that the fish was tilapia- fried fish, IMO, should be a little more hearty- like cod. The shrimp, however, were perfect. 

The sides were passable- potatoes were nice and creamy, but needed a serious salting. The coleslaw had potential. I liked that it had bits of mandarin oranges- very different, but good different. I didn't like that it was too sweet. If they had shown a liiittle more restraint with the sugar, it would have been phenomenal.

Bottom line- I'll explore a few more options before coming back to Shell's, but not bad. Not bad at all.

The pineapple and basil was delicous. It was a bit small for the price, $3 or $4 I think, but it was good. The ice pop actually had chunks of pineapple in it. I got one at the Italian Market Night Market. Very good though.

Not bad but not great
Tastes good, healthy, clean, and bland
Good service.  Nice staff.
Pretty good. I just got a Hummas Platter. Good Price ($9.95 plus tax).  The Hummas was delicious!!! Salad was pretty normal. Falafel was a bit disappointing as it was dry and cold.

Fun bar to drink beer in. Nice selection of drafts and bottles. They have das boot if you want to channel your inner Beerfest moment. They have have a special on Jager Bombs. They have live music which to me makes a bar even better.

First time ever... came with a group. I was a little confused with the concept but after ordering and waiting for the food to arrive at our table. I was pleased with this concept. 

You walk in and order what you want. We decided with the group we had, we would go with the Family combo. At first we were thinking, hmm maybe we wont have enough to go around but in the end, we had plenty. Our combo came with tortillas(you can choose b/w corn or flour), pick meats b/w abodabo pork, chicken, carne asada... 4 drinks, 8 sides. The salsa bar they have has tomatillo sauces, pico de gallo, avocado sauce?, guacamole sauce etc.. so good. The sides that i enjoyed was the chiarro bean soup.. hard to explain but it was really tasty. I know some reviews for this place are not good but my experience was good So i cant complain. We don't have this kind of place back in Florida. 

Highlight was the soft drink dispenser...very awesome. WOuld be great to have one of those at home.

There is plenty of parking here. Any time of the day during operating hours, place is never too packed to not come to this place. We came on a Saturday evening.

Awesome place to get your sweet fix - the creme brulee french toast is AMAZING, and the red velvet pancakes are fun, delicious, overly indulgent and unnecessarily huge. I'm the type of person who can eat sweets unendingly and for once I couldn't finish. You should go with people who will share a mix of savory and sweet dishes. The eggs benedict was executed really well. I still need to go back to try the "kitchen sink". 

In terms of ambiance, it's not a particularly relaxing place to go for weekend brunch...it was packed, too loud for conversation, and the hostess made us wait outside in the 97 degree weather...The food makes it worth it, but I'd like to go back on a weekday when it's not so busy.

Visiting friends in Tampa and they wanted to bring me here to satisfy my Thai addiction.  The place is super cute with nice Thai decorations and a fabulous fish tank.  

Curry Puffs are something I must have at any Thai restaurant that has them on their menu.  Royal Palace has this tasty treat, so I tried them.  They are fried in wonton wrappers, I prefer spring roll wrappers, but they were mighty good!  The vegetarian spring rolls were average, tasting like any other spring roll.  

Their Pad Thai was a small portion, priced right and tasted really good too.  The order comes with chicken and shrimp for only $10.95, not bad at all.  My friend ordered the Super Roll and said it was really fresh, she would order it again for sure.  

We ordered only water for beverages and the staff was always on top of keeping them full, which is a good thing when enjoying Thai with hot sauce!  The prices are decent and the food is really good, try it if you're in the area!

Why 4 stars? Because who knew this place was so great??

   ---   ---   ---

Bar Louie, why didn't I stop by sooner? I had no idea you were such a good time. I had a very poor and false assumption of the Louie, that was proven wrong during a recent visit. I don't hang out too often at International Plaza, and a couple of experience at the nearby Blue Martini were the primary reason. There's nothing wrong with Blue Martini, but they obviously do not appeal to my demo, being that I am not a 40-something. Maybe one day, hanging out with cougars will be my thing, or maybe when that day comes I will be too old to garner their attention. Whatever the case may be, let's chat about my interests in 2012 - Bar Louie!

They have a decent beer selection and with the amount of TV's in the place, it seems like it might be my home during a future NFL Sunday. All of the food that I tried was good from the pretzels to the burgers, I will definitely be back. The cocktails, which are usually not my thing, really opened my eyes. I specifically enjoyed the Kiss My Aztec.

Lou, that was not a fun game of hide and seek. Don't play so hard next time.

Let's face it - making popsicles is hard. You've got to go shopping for the ingredients, blend them, create a big mess in the kitchen, put them in that little popsicle former, sacrifice a goat to Baal to bless your popsicles, clean up the goat blood, and(the absolute worst part) wait for the popsicles to solidify in the freezer.

This last step can take a lifetime. If I start making a popsicle now, maybe my grandkids will get to try them before they go on vacation to the new colony on LV-981.

Whooooooo has time for this?

Not me!

So when I want a popsicle, I head on over to the popsicle professionals at Lil' Pop shop. Sure, its a conservative 300 percent markup on the cost of ingredients, but it is damn good. $3 is a steal for how great these things are and their ability to satisfy your dessert craving.

The Vietnamese Coffee popsicle was the best one that I tried. The pineapple basil pop was pretty good. I intend to return for the salted caramel brownie fudge pop because that is brilliant.

Did the teppan thing, service ok, neither great or sloppy, had the chicken and veggies, portions large, yet flavor bland, nothing stood out, so u go for the friends and the experience, not necessarily for the food, would not go back any time soon.

What a great bar if your a serious beer drinker. My wife was drinking a Hefeweizen and I was drinking a Left Hand Milk Stout that was coming out of the nitro tap. The bartender Jeni was well versed in the beer selection and if you were not sure what you wanted tell what you like and she will find you something. Now beware don't go looking to get Bud Light and such this is a serious beer drinker bar with others who enjoy beer. Oh and one more thing they have no tv's so if your wanting to catch a game you won't be able to.

I'm going 4.5 star on this one, folks!  I've eaten here a lot and nothing was off center; not the food, the service or atmosphere. 

Items I recommend: VOLCANO ROLL - best in town!  Serious.  I eat this roll everywhere I go.  COCONUT CURRY BEEF STEW - traditional beef stew in a beef stock gravy and a hint of coconut curry?  Are you serious?  This is one of the best fusion ideas I've seen to date.  It's served with some very fresh French bread, but order a side of rice and throw it in your bowl.  You won't be sad about this decision.  HONEY WALNUT ROCK SHRIMP - some of the meatiest shrimp I've ever seen.  Very tasty and cooked to perfection.  Then it's glazed with a wonderful honey sauce and topped with walnuts.  Look, I don't even LIKE walnut, and I found this dish to be nothing but amazing.  Never mind the fact that it's served with melon scoops and rice.  MISO SOUP - bangin'!  Better than I've had at most Japanese restaurants.  True story.  CURRIED CRAB DUMPLING - curried crab, cream cheese, minced raisins and cilantro wrapped in gyoza skins and fried until crispy served with a sweet chili dipping sauce.  I mean, how much stuff can they shove into these things?  Just straight up DELICIOUS!

Ask for Marcella, she's the bee's knees.  I always look forward to my next visit for some House music and Asian-influenced grub!

KGUN 9 on your side reported three health violations last month. If you want unsanitary food with terrible service, this is the place for you.

This final location will be out of business soon and they have management and the rude waiters to blame

We returned to Mise En Place last week, and enjoyed our experience there as much as we did the first time.

The two best things about Mise En Place: 1. The place is quiet -- you can actually have a conversation without straining your voice, and 2. The service is impeccable.  Waiters are polite, efficient and unobtrusive.

We skipped the prix fixe menu this time (I was very disappointed by the wine pairings on our first visit) and went with a ton of small plates this time and a lovely bottle of 2003 Bordeaux.  THAT is the way to go, especially since my husband wasn't excited by the entrees but was fascinated by multiple appetizers.  We had the serrano ham, a cheese plate (try the gouda, it's divine), a small pizza with crab and mushrooms, the calamari, and the heirloom tomato salad.  We finished off the meal with the cheesecake.  

Everything was very tasty.  Nothing was earth shattering, but that's okay if the rest of the setting is lovely and relaxing.

This place is a hole in the wall, but worth more than what you think at first glance.
There are two rooms.  The bar is right as you come in, and it is TINY, covered in stickers and graffiti, and generally dirtily- charming.  To get to the stage, you go to the adjacent room, which is also tiny, but has a surprisingly great sound set up.  Lots of local bands and just starting acts.  There's a ton of street parking right around it, for free.  Definitely worth going once.

My biggest deprivation as a gluten free eater is getting a good juicy hamburger.  I've always been a fan of Burger 21, and simply ordered my burger choice sans bun.   I was delighted, though a bit skeptical, when I heard they now had gluten free buns- any buns I had previously tried at home had always crumbled around the meat.  

Boy was I surprised!  The bun was buttery and fluffy and delicious! Finally.... a good old fashion burger- that I could eat with my hands not a knife and fork!!  

Even better- when I ordered the GF hamburger- they asked if I also wanted my fries gluten free (which I assume has them made in a separate fryer).  When my meal came out- it had a bright green gluten free sticker on it- as in "be careful don't let this touch other things".   I thought this was a great idea- and helps those with gluten allergies and sensitives feel more confident about eating safely.

Thank you Burger 21!!

I was not picturing a full service restaurant with a wait to be seated sign.  I'm not sure why....you still serve yourself at the salsa bar and grilled accouterments (which is actually on the counter near the register and I completely missed it).  Service is kinda slow around here.

Here the bacon wrapped dogs are cooked in butter and mustard.  The bun is not as sweet as El Guero (did you know El Guerno gets their buns from a private bakery in Mexico), but for some reason it did not hold together either...maybe it's because I asked for no tomato and it came with tomato.  I did enjoy it though.  My second go around the bun stayed in tact and they did not add tomato!

I really enjoyed the carne asada...they cook it over a mesquite and as their sign says it makes a difference....nice smokey meat.  I had it in a taco and in a caramello.  As I type this, I am wanting some right now.

The cabeza is also good, my first time eating head meat.  It was very tender and if you have never tried it, go ahead...it just tastes like beef.

I REALLY liked the guac here...and I do not like guac.  It is avocado mixed with cottage cheese (this is not a typo) and diced jalapeno...it packed a heated punch that was GREAT!  It can be found in the salsa bar...which is clean and stocked with many options ie. lime, fresh peeled cucumber, fire roasted salsa, tomatillo salsa, and mexican cheese to boot...all is great!  Often times kids will hold up the line as they gather 30 limes, kinda crazy.

The queso fundido was really just melted cheese in a bowl, not a cheese sauce or dip...you can add chorizo, mushrooms or green chilis.

Funny signs are all over the place, most referencing the partaking in adult beverages, which,yes, you can get here.  I opted for the pina agua fresca, which I am currently having a serious love affair with.  I like that Mexican language cards are embedded in the tables, so I was entertained and educated while I dined.

So, El Guero and BK, former friends, bitter rivals...I am still torn.  I like you both....but I must admit BK's carne may have been better due to the mesquite...how every I adore El Guero's pina frothy drink much better.

I love this place. I stopped in for dinner with a friend last night. The first place we were going to try was closed at 8:30! Why is it so hard to find places that stay open a little later? On Saturday night please do not close early. Now back to Char's. We had the pineapple fried rice and Pad Thai with tofu. Both dishes were really good. We sat at the Elvis table under the mural. The décor is so creative it is uplifting. The woman serving our dinner was nice. However at three minutes before ten o'clock she came by and told us it was three minutes until closing time. Everyone in the restaurant (about five tables total) promptly left right at ten. Before we could get in our cars the lights were down. Does anyone know of cool places that stay open a little longer?

Friendly staff, great burritos, and speedy service, what more could you want?  Granted I was getting my food to go and not sitting in, the length of the line didn't deter me for a second--the efficiency made up for it.  Seems like it gets quite busy during the lunch hour, but that's to be expected.  I'm sure I'll be back!

The food here is colorful, vibrant and fresh - but it doesn't just "look' good, it tastes delicious too. I'm always impressed with how creative they get with their specials (used to be bi-weekly,  now monthly) Love the sushi here...two favorites are the Spicy Diablo and the Yellowtrails, and they have really good seaweed salad.

Their marsala sauce is awesome, you can just tell the recipe took years to perfect.  It's featured in a pasta, some of their wraps, and as a dipping sauce you can order on the side. As a condiment connaisseru, I love that they have a long list od homemade dipping sauces you can order along with your stir fry or wrap.

Must-trys: the Slam-gria (a huge sangria with an overturned bottle of wine: red, white or champagne and the cucumber martini. I love that they feature photos of their specials on their Facebook page. It's like using Foodspotting - I know exactly what I want to order before I even show up. Super fun atmosphere and friendly service. 
This is a great spot for lunch, apps before going out, or Saturday brunch. A DJ spins here on Friday or Saturday nights, and on Thursday nights, the acoustic band Tone Grenade that plays is awesome. Definitely one of my favorite Tampa spots!

So it was my lovely ladies birthday and we always wanted to go here so we went.
I guess they actually listened to the yelpers when they said the decor was beat because theyre re-modeling yet still opened right across the way. they will be reopening in a few weeks at the old spot.
I was a little disappointed that we were in an old beer house because i really wanted to see the actual place. I had asked if she wanted to go somewhere else and she had her heart set on it so we went anyway and couldnt be happier that we did.
We started with a cosmo martini and a sapporo and continued too the extensive yet easy to understand menu.
I fancy myself a good judge of Japanese food because I have family in Yokohama and I go there quite a bit and have for years.
I think that this place has done a fantastic job of keeping old tradition with new exciting fusion.
I started as always with the edamame and miso soup. brilliant and perfect.
and there was so much awesome stuff on the menu I couldnt stop ordering everything! the tuna sashimi melted in my mouth,I could have stopped there but didnt. I had to try the blue crab rangoon and the tempura lobster rolls and the spicy tuna and yellowfin and on and on and on and everything,I mean when I say everything was fan-fkn- tastic!
I had just eaten way to much,someone on here said the servings were small and they lied! It was just to much food and that was my fault.
I had so much left over,I dropped it off at a friends place of business when I was done and looked like a hero.
We got the check and I almost shit myself. It was unbelievably reasonable for the amount of food I gorged myself on and gave away. so much so I tipped at 40 percent cause the service was impeccable.
I want to be there the second they move back into the new place,well old place with new digs.
I was so happy when we were on our way out I had went to the hostess desk where there was management standing and said 'could you pass this message on to the management' and they said sure and I said 'everything is awesome!" they laughed and thanked me and were super sweet. I highly recommend this eatery and cant wait to go back! thank you Samurai Blue!

Best place for all my needs.Convenient for parking,taking bus.Shanon Lantz is best employee/manager i had.She know a lot when asking a question on a product with exception of a employee,Samantha which she looks confused when speaking with,it a great shopping experience.

After eating here so many times, I should probably write a review on it.

Although I can't speak for their dinner time, Mise En Place's lunch is amazing! Classy but affordable lunches. AND they're tasty & filling! I could probably say something good about everything here. The staff are always so professional and friendly. I am always impressed at how they memorize and eloquently announce the specials for the day. I want to clap each time.

The pre-lunch bread comes out nice and warm. My favorite sandwich to order, believe it or not, is their fancy shmancy grilled cheese. I know no one wants to pay money at a restaurant to just get a simple grilled cheese. Well, this one is not simple. 3 cheeses: white cheddar, goat, and gorgonzola, with mushrooms and tomatoes.

Another of my favorite sandwiches is their roast turkey. Stacked enough where I can only eat half of it, so lucky me can have the other half for dinner. 

THEIR FRIES. I would never even think about opting for the salad to replace the fries. It's like seasoned perfection. AND it comes with Mise En Place special ketchup. No idea what makes it so special, but it really is a different tasting ketchup that matches the fries perfectly. I would probably come here JUST for the fries.

It's a good and bad thing that this restaurant is walking distance from my work. Good for my taste buds, bad for my wallet.

As much as we all despise the heat wave that strikes the city every summer, at least there's a silver lining--it's the perfect weather for popsicles; more specifically gourmet organic popsicles from Li'l Pop Shop!  Located amongst many other great shops and restaurants in this particular section of West Philly, the shop lives up to its name in that it is quite small, but before I entered, it had me wondering whether it was also a soda fountain.  I was greeted cheerfully by the friendly associate Katie who filled me in on the shop's offerings (their flavors are quite unique and are sold until they run out), and after some deliberation, I chose the chocolate salted caramel brownie.  It ran me a steep $3, but being that it was homemade and not mass produced, it was worth every penny!  They even have a mini popsicle stand for kids to play pretend with, so it's a great place for parents to bring kids of all ages as well.  I hope to return before the end of popsicle season!

When I'm in South Philly and in the mood for a hoagie, this is where I go. July 4th I had a Wawa hoagie in Center City, and yesterday I had a Ricci Brothers hoagie. In comparing them to each other, the Ricci Brothers hoagie is far superior in taste to what I got at Wawa.

Mmm... It was good. Parking here is not so easy. You will have to hunt for a spot or double park. And it seems they are only open for lunch.

It's a few minutes away by foot from the steak sandwich capital of the world, where Geno's and Pat's are, so if the lines are too long over there, head over here and get yourself a real Italian hoagie.
The food was pretty good,i've had better but the waiter and the owner were fantastic and really funny I enjoyed myself as did my mother & grandmother

I frequent the parts counter at desert toyota and i have always received the best service they have called other dealerships in town so that i get my part asap. they ever held a part at the front counter after they had closed A+++++

Finding a drink during prohibition may have been just as tricky as trying to find the discreetly marked entry to Ciro's Speakeasy & Supper Club.

Tucked into the lobby level of the Bayshore Royal Condominium overlooking Bayshore Boulevard and Hillsborough Bay, you must park behind the highrise, walk past the pool and look for the large wooden door to the left that is located at the top of a steps.

Hopefully you remembered to call ahead to make reservations in order to be entrusted with the secret password of the day.

You'll need to know what to say when you knock and a small section of the door opens just enough for the person on the inside to discreetly ask for the password.
Only then does the door open and allow you passage you step into the other worldly experience that has made Ciro's a widely popular dining and drinking destination.

The cocktails are quite tasty - according to those who sampled them during my visits - but I tend to stick with white wine. Fortunately there was one, and I literally mean one, white wine offered.

The booths can be a little cramped depending on how many are in your party and how much of the delicious food is ordered.  Small knee-high tables can be tricky when trying to park multiple drinks and dishes or retrieve them without sending them toppling over.

Be forewarned there is a $5 fee for valet, if the valet is on duty, and the dress code prohibits baseball caps, baggy shorts and flip flops. And on busy nights there is an enforced two-hour time limit for occupying one of the booths but that only requires you to relocate to the bar.

Four words: Paul and Young Ron! This pizza is delicious... the meatballs, peppers, and ricotta make it original and it has been perfect every time. So happy there is finally one of these in South Tampa. I happen to love the thinner crust and charring that happens with the fire. Yes, it's different from your typical pizza but I am very okay with that. Definitely worth a try.

I've come to Kona Grill once for dinner and then for Happy Hour; that's the best time to go now! I usually like to meet up girlfriends here for Happy hour, you have to sit in the bar area. It's a big bar area with enough room for larger than normal groups. There's sushi ranging from $4-8 or so and appetizers in the same price range. Once of my favorite appetizers is the tuna on fried won ton - melts in your mouth! The drinks at Kona are superb! They have skinny options, for the diet conscious, and tons of great cocktails. Favorites are the cucumber gimlet and the mango mojito - the mango mojito is made with a mango rum and not overly sweet. The simple syrup and fresh mint make it a refreshing after-work option!

Professional football players, college athletes and even country music greats access areas of Raymond James Stadium that most member of the public never get to see.
But a behind-the-scenes tour offered Tuesday through Thursday weekly lets you walk on the same field where Super Bowls have been played, peek into the locker rooms where players have been given game-changing pep talks, check out luxury suites with really comfortable seats and even visit holding cells where unruly fans cool their heels until the concert, sports competition or special event they attended has ended.
Plan to stand, walk and wait and be sure to allow time to wind your way into the stadium parking lot, head for the ticket window area and line up to pay - cash or check only but there are ATMs on site - $4 - $6 per person.
Tours start at 2 p.m. and take a bit more than an hour.  The leisurely pace allows a lot of time for photos and questions. It is best to call ahead to make sure the tour schedule has not changed (813-350-6500).
The tour is informal but informative.  Facts and figures include the stadium's opening date of September 1998 and construction cost of $168.5 million.  And that both the Tampa Bay Buccaneers and University of South Florida Bulls play football here. But there are also just-for-fun facts. For example, you learn that the NFL Players Association voted Raymond James players' locker rooms as the "Best Smelling." 
The tour typically takes groups onto the field, into the locker rooms and on board the pirate ship located near one end zone that cost some $3 million to build and can actually float (Note to self: Next big rain-induced flood conditions, head here).  However new paint prevented my group from accessing the pirate ship so we were routed into the press box area where credentialed media cover professional and college football and international soccer matches.

Stopped in here for an iced coffee today...a medium iced was $2.35. It was pretty good, but not great. They don't put out skim milk (or even 2%) so I had to ask for it and the barista/cashier did it for me behind the counter. She didn't add as much milk as I would've liked, but it was crowded and other people were waiting so I didn't want to bug her to ask for more. The coffee was pretty good, but not stellar.

Attended an UYE today and was impressed. 

This place is located in an obscure building near beefs on s. Dale Mabry.

Parking is tight but there was room in the back lot. 

They have good drafts...magic hat #9, shock top, Stella and BL.

They showered us with sliders which were tasty, wings (try the garlic Parmesan) and buffalo chips. 

They do offer NFL tix and I could see myself hanging out watching a game here and drinking some brewskis.

We ate here about a month ago based on the reviews and would have to say that this is the first time we have been led astray by Yelp. I had the tacos which were marginal at best, not just a small portion, but missing the jicama ingredient, as well. Definitely was very unimpressive and we won't be back. That being said, there were a bunch of families with young children there so perhaps that is the clientele they cater to.

I came here with my boyfriend with a $25 groupon in hand, for 2 Brazilian Steak buffets.

I must say that, with the groupon the deal was great and very much so worth the food, service and OMG (see below) the dessert. However, I wasn't amazed by anything.. (except the dessert) -- which wasn't included in the groupon.

The food was pretty good. The meat was seasoned well, but some pieces were dry. The service was great, they were attentive. However.. there was some sort of un-cleanliness of the place that I felt, but nothing too serious. Just kind of an old little restaurant. 

The buffet stands were not big at all, I believe there were two carts. It was enough though. Everything on the buffet carts was pretty standard, nothing disgusting but nothing delicious. Just plain old, standard.

We enjoyed our meal, had a pleasant dinner with our groupon. But we still wanted to have some dessert even though it wasn't included. I can't remember what my boyfriend got.. but I got what the lady recommended.. THEIR FLAN. And my gosh, am I happy she recommended it. Maybe I'm crazy.. but I really do think it is by far the BEST FLAN I'VE EVER HAD. I haven't had many.. but I've had enough to know this was the most delicious I've had! It was nice and firm, but creamy. The sugar was just enough.. it tasted so caramelized and.. carmely.. it was soooo delicious. My boyfriend went back to get it for me two separate times, because I loved it so much. No steak, no dinner, just the dessert to go. It's $4.00 which isn't the BEST price, but it's quite good and I'll pay for it once in a blue moon for the pure enjoyment!

Overall, I'd say about 3-3.5 stars. It would just be difficult to pay THAT much on a normal basis without groupon. It's also $24.95 for lunch price (same meats available) for lunch at Fogo.

Fantastic little shop in Hyde Park. It's definitely more upscale, but the prices aren't too bad. The toys are over priced because my dogs are just going to shred them anyway, but the treats and bakery case are actually pretty fairly priced for the quality you get. I've shopped at other dog bakeries and Downtown Dogs surpasses them all in terms of freshness and quality, by far! I should make it a point to come here more often.

I went to this Sabrina's location because of a google offer that was available. Service was just alright. They only had about two waitresses for the entire restaurant on a friday night.

The food:
Calamari appetizer: pretty good, I would recommend this to others
Spring rolls: meh, wasnt a huge fan of these

Eggs benedict: This was pretty gross for some reason, but the potatoes on the side were pretty good. They forgot the sausage i ordered with it but brought it out eventually after i told them.

Stuffed French Toast: Get this if you are a first time visitor. I had a bite of it and it was super delicious

Dessert. BREAD PUDDING. This was really good, and came with a scoop of ice cream on top. It might be a bit much if you also ordered the stuffed french toast
Cheesecake: meh. Im not sure how they messed this up but it wasnt that good

There are way too many good reviews for this place so i may have to come back and give them another chance, but i wasnt too happy with the food

Overall: 3.5 stars.. 3.7 If I'm allowed to do that, haha!

I've been here probably 3 times total.

My in-dining experiences were all good. Service was great, I have no complaints in that department. The stuffed french toast with vanilla bean syrup, DELICIOUS. My best friend enjoyed it very much. I also had a side of polenta fries -- which I have gotten on every subsequent return to Sabrina's. They're delicious. I think they only give you 4.. but they're very filling. Comes with marinara sauce, and... mmm thinking about it now I'm just drooling.

Value? Good. I think it's GOOD value. It's delicious, prices range from 8.99 to about $15 for lunch/brunch items, and a bit more for dinner. It used to be my favorite brunch place. However, I've found a few more that I am more fond of.. and are consistently $3-4 cheaper for dishes, comparatively.

My living social experience: This is why I gave them a little downgrade from a solid 4 stars (which I considered them before). I bought one of those deals: " $5 for $10 worth of food for takeout or delivery. I ordered the Cali Turkey sandwich with sweet potato fries with no powdered sugar ( i don't like the way they make their sweet potato fries here). Well I show up, my order is ready, great! However, the lady (who is very nice and was trying to be helpful..) said that " normally you have to place your orders online to use that". I know EXACTLY which deal she is talking about. I have that other deal on my phone it says " MUST place order online for voucher". However, my deal didn't have that. I know. I'm a constant and avid living social/ grouponer, so I know. I explained to her, and then she said " I have to get my manager. I don't have the password." Fair enough.. but really? I mean I don't understand, do they not have the technology? Most people know that groupon or living social to go, is for those of us, ON THE GO. Like, usually in a rush.. I don't have 15 minutes to sit around for you. Most places I've gone to, you come in, they scan the QR code, and you're all set! This whole process of waiting for her manager, and then another 3-4 minutes of them trying to figure it out on their computer system was just a little much for me. They then told me my total was $5 after the livingsocial deal. I asked them.. how much was the sandwich? They said 9.99.. so maybe 10 something. So... HOW IS MY THING$5??? I'm pretty sure that if you have 10 dollars worth of food, and the sandwich is $10.. my total comes to 0, or a little more for tax. I had to actually EXPLAIN the deal to them. They're like " well, you paid $5 to living social, so that means you owe another $5 here, right?". AHEM noobs, no. It means you pay $5 to living social, and you get $10 worth of food at the merchant. I actually had to EXPLAIN that to them. 

I apologize for the rant. I must emphasize that they were very NICE and trying to help during this whole thing. I was just taken aback by how ... much they didn't know. Livingsocial/Groupon is pretty modern.. easy to use.. even if your job didn't train you how to do it. IDK.. i thought that the quote " $5 for $10 worth of food" is self explanatory. But no, I had to explain. I just.. don't understand when.. people... are like.. DERRRR. But AGAIN. they were nice, trying to help, just very unknowledgable...

Overall food for it's value I give a 3.7. Service/friendliness 4 stars. The other my whole rant stuff.. solid 3. I feel bad demoting them to a 2.5

SOLID 4 stars. 

I've read reviews about people waiting a very long time to be seated. I'm not sure why that was not my experience. I believe my boyfriend and I waited about 5 minutes one time we went, and another time we had a table of 5 and we waited just about the same.

We always have the same nice lady as our waitress, we really need to catch her name. The service is attentive, swift and I have no complaints.

The first time I went I got eggs benedict, and my boyfriend got the Red Velvet Pancakes ( I was feeling a bit more savory this time around). My eggs benedict was very good, poached perfectly and everything else was a little above standard. Nothing crazy, but very good and worth the $9. 
Oh my sweet baby jesus, my boyfriend's red velvet pancakes were delicious! They tasted very good. Unlike places that use food coloring for chocolate cake and call it red velvet, this one actually tasted genuine. I truly enjoyed stealing some bites of his.
