Upon another return, I was the fatass yet again who ordered the kitchen sink. And my god, it truly IS THE KITCHEN SINK. My bf and I are pretty similar. And I must say, if you eat like a guy, or are a guy.. what the hell can go wrong with BISCUITS, GRAVY, CHEESE, EGGS.. etc all smothered in one and served in a iron skillet! The only thing it was missing imo, is bacon. Never fear, they have sides of them for $3, thick cut bacon hehe. 

I must mention this, because my bf is in love with the African Nectar Tea. Obviously they don't pick the tea leaves themselves, but it's a good selection they hold. African Nectar tea, with a little bit of honey = delicious.

This was our newest favorite brunch place (Sabrina's used to be our old favorite - sorry Sabrinas!). We enjoyed the food, atmostphere and the prices were a bit more friendly! I also like how they seem to try to be pro-green!

This was my third visit to Anthony's Coal Fired Pizza and it was wonderful as usual. I used my foursquare check in to get a sample of the wings which are ridiculously delicious! I also had the tomato and mozzarella salad which was a good sized portion and very filling. The tomatoes were plump, juicy and fresh. The mozzarella was creamy and just perfect. Service was stellar, especially sine we were a party of 13. I will be back again and again.

I was really excited to come here because I had heard they have a lot of vegan options on their menu.  We decided to go this morning instead of fighting the weekend crowds.  It wasn't packed, but I was surprised at how many people were there.  We started off with coffee and a cranberry juice, but the juice was literally water with red food coloring.  I felt bad sending it right back, but I really didn't want it.  It was difficult to get the attention of our waitress and this theme continued throughout our meal.  Our dishes came out in a relatively decent amount of time, though.  I went with the vegan French toast.  It was beautifully plated, but sadly, my meal looked better than it tasted.  The toast wasn't cooked all the way through in some spots and then it was dry in other spots.  It had an overpowering salty finish to it, too (I'm assuming they melted margarine on it).  I wanted to like it here, but I was disappointed.  Supposedly the one in South Philly is better.  I think I will try that one out and I'll avoid this location in the future.
Great Thai food. Panang curry the best! With just the right amount of heat, fish sauce, and peanut flavors.
Excellent coal fired pizza. Get the traditional pie. You won't regret it

Why 4 stars? Because this might be the best pizza in Tampa.

   ---   ---   ---

I had heard people going crazy about Anthony's and it was on my to-do list for a while. Then I found out that it was actually a chain, so I was a little less excited to check it out, however, I still did want to see what the fuss was about. 

Chain or not, I was not disappointed at all! There's generally not a lot of variation in the taste of thin-crust pizza, in my opinion...but this was a whole new level of cheesiness. We had half pepperoni and half white pizza. Both were not to be missed.

The service was extremely friendly and welcoming. He heard us talking about the wings and even brought us out a couple to sample. I will admit, these were not my favorite, but "everything tastes better when it's free" is probably the most accurate cliche ever invented.

Anthony's, you done good.

I absolutely love The Surf Shack and their sister store in Trinity, Zim Zari. 

We are pretty much regulars for $2 tacos on Thursdays unless we are on a diet. 

I noticed on their special board that they now offer Korean beef tacos-note to self, I definitely need to add these to my to do list. 

We are always greeted by a friendly server in a very quick manner. Then followed up by a bus boy serving us a warm basket of their homemade chips and salsa. Again, a nice touch that makes me continually come back again and again. 

Their Monday night special of $5 burritos is also a fan favorite. Many a times we have gone there for lunch or dinner and ordered the burrito as a bowl, without the tortilla. I figure I can eat enough chips while waiting for my food that ordering the burrito naked won't hurt the waistline. Actually I enjoy the burrito tortilla-less better anyway. I always order the baja chicken burrito. So good with the avocado and black beans. (I'll give you a little secret, ask for a side of the chinichuri sauce).

If you're in the westchase area on a Monday or Thursday (or for that matter any day of the week), give the SS a try, I'm sure you'll love it.

Straightforward, home-spun Americana served at a bargain. The starter salad and blue cheese dressing were delicious, the homemade potato chips were addictive, and touches like Cracker Jacks with kids' meals were fantastic.

The mac n cheese was interesting. It comes with a nice layer of melted cheese on top and some bacon crumbles. It's worth noting that while it tasted good -- particularly that top layer -- the cheese beneath the top layer looked a bit suspect. It looked off-color (tinge of salmon/pink) and was a bit curdled. I had a war between my eyes and my taste buds with this. I'll think twice before reordering it. 

I'd suggest resisting ordering the rib-eye unless you really don't want to splurge on it at a true steakhouse. Why I had a craving for a rib-eye at a place that was doing everything else right but *within their wheelhouse* I have no idea. It wasn't perfect but also wasn't terrific. For the price, though, I didn't complain.

And where I REALLY didn't complain? The BOGO wine special. My receipt showed just $3 for two nice pours of an acceptable Chardonnay.

The table layout is a little odd. There was a table or two that butted up against the bathroom -- that can't be pleasant.

I'd return, especially on a Tuesday, when I'm told they offer affordable Alaskan crab legs and Kids Eat Free.

SOLID 4.5 stars.

I have been here on numerous occasions, and it is DELICIOUS. I've lived in Philadelphia for 6 years to date. And a year ago, a foodie friend of mine asked me if I've ever had the pleasure of eating Nan Zhou.. and that's where the story begins.

First time eating there it was a rainy day, I got the hand drawn noodles. Oh my GOD so good. First of all, the broth is just perfect. It's not too hot, you can easily eat/drink it as you may be starving like I was. But my god the broth is SO good. Not going to lie, the day after that, I went back to get it AGAIN. My friends and I joked that there was crystal meth in it because I HAD to get it the day after. But anyway, back to the handdrawn noodles. First of all, they make them FRESH. As in, you order -- the chef either slices it for the knife cut one, or hand draws them into noodles after you place your order. You can see them do it. So yes, the noodles are great consistency, fresh, delicious broth.. $6 can't go wrong! Plus they give you SO much noodles, SO much beef and enough vegetables.

I went there a few more times with my mom and best friend and even boyfriend who doesn't like noodle soup. All of the times were GREAT, except the 2nd time I went with my mom. She even noticed that the broth wasn't AS flavorful as usual. That is why this review is a 4.5 instead of 5 stars! That one time was the only inconsistency. Other than that, DELICIOUS. The knife cut -- i swears.. has a different consistency/texture. It is more " q" aka, chewy. It also seems they give twice the amount. It's funny. Hand drawn - i eat all that shit on my own lol. Knife cut - I split with either my bf or best friend. 

Hands down - delicious food, now $6.50 but still worth it.. although I'm not a fan for the raise in price. Delicious broth, savory beef slices, home made FRESH noodles = I am SOLD

Tried honest tom's for the first time tonight. I ordered the steak and yucca tacos and they were really tasty. They were not stingy with the steak and the yucca was really crispy so it added a nice but of textural contrast to the tacos. I would come again, but honestly, 2 tacos for $8 seems a bit steep to me. If they had a 3 for $8 deal, I think it would be better.

After hearing about their mint lemonade from a friend, I was super pumped to try Gavin's cafe lunch. I am so disappointed...

I should have been suspicious when there were only 2 tables of patrons during lunch hour, but I figured it may have just been an off day. I started to ask about the menu and was quickly told that there were like no substitutions, no way to not have it "as is"...which was fine by me until I realized that the salads weren't premade! I don't understand if I don't want tomatoes...why I can't get a salad WITHOUT tomatoes or what have you?! My final salad had a nonexistent lemon dressing and was super overpriced at just under $9. To make it worse? It took the woman behind the counter over 15 minutes to *start* assembling my 4-ingredient salad. 

Even the mint lemonade was pretty bad (at least when they made it for me) as it was just ice + mint leave for the first 2/3 and then lemonade at the bottom. I saw their advertisement behind the counter of a nicely mixed mint lemonade, but mine was pretty gross. I finished most of it purely because it was late July and I was thirsty, but ugh. 

The ambience is really cute, I could definitely see getting coffee here, but never when I was in a rush and/or wanted food.

Excellent appetizers, who knew that guacamole went well with Asian fusion?

Service was excellent, very friendly staff!

A word of warning, my friend and I ordered the Three Pepper Chicken, it was very good, and very spicy, but one of the ingredients used made both my and my friend's mouth start to go numb.  This isn't a spicy thing, since I can eat peppers like a champ, but was some other spice (we think cloves?) that left an unpleasant numbness which spread around the tongue and lips.

All in all, great ambiance, and definitely an eatery that I would return to again!

As a Philadelphia "midtown" resident, I was pleased to see a new restaurant coming to the area, and not only to the area, but to Chestnut Street (rather than 13th between Chestnut and Walnut where we already have a glut of amazing restaurants and shops). Here's hoping that the good food (and good shops) keep moving down Chestnut!

Spice 28's waitstaff was superbly friendly (handshakes and name exchanges) and attentive without being irritating. The food was delicious across the board: tom yum soup (spicy, especially enjoyed the snap pea pods!), chili mini wontons (nice heat!), potstickers (just as they should be), wonton soup (nice broth), vegetarian drunken noodles (does not come spicy).

Must give an individualized nod to the special order of spicy tuna wonton atop guacamole: fabulous!

I had to review this place, it's in my hood.

My go to: cheesesteak with fried onions, cheese and a little bit of ketchup. 

Tip: make sure to give them ENOUGH TIME if you're ordering fries. They do a good job, but give them enough time to finish the job.. otherwise they won't be crispy and delicious enough! And that's totally not their fault.

Prices are decent for their value. Its about $6 for a cheesesteak combo with drink, fries and cheesesteak. I can eat those cheesesteaks for 2 meals. Although a hungry guy might be able to eat the whole thing.

They also have cheap drinks, 4 loco for $3, cheap-ish 6 packs. They're way cheaper than Best House -- but of course beer distributors are still the cheapest. 

They 2 asian guys working there are very nice, lovely and always remember their repeat customers.

3 stars, no more no less.

Their food is definitely not less than 3 stars, but I honestly can't give it as good as a 4. 

They have great happy hour specials and even late night happy hour specials -- check out their website for those. They have different specials rotating daily. Anything from $3 margaritas on Mondays to $3 coronas on a friday night.. half off appetizers during happy hour etc. They're always a default for me, when my friends and I don't know where to go. They're a decent bar with decent food and more than decent happy hour prices.

I've never had bad service either. It's at par for good service, and they have plenty of TVs that show big games going on in the area.

Like all the other Asians, I have probably only ever been here on a Tuesday. Yaki Tuesdays they call it. $1 skewers.

This place is OK. Their hostess(es) usually always seem friendly although like all asians, seems like you turn their back and they go gossiping about nonsense. When I walk in the first floor -- the guys working behind the sushi/bar counter always stare and give me an uncomfortable feeling, just sayin'. The servers I've had there have always been great. There is one lady, kind of short/quiet/asian who is always very nice and the other gentlemen I've come across are nice as well.

The $1 skewers are fine. They're good. But although they're only $1 each. Keep in mind. they are ONE skewer, for one dollar. You may need to order like.. 10 to feel full. But for me, it's nice to munch on with a beer. On Tuesdays they also have $3 sapporos.
I think they've gotten cheaper on us though! I swear there used to be 4 bacon and quail egg things on the skewers. Went last Monday.. there was 2. And the asparagus comes with ONE asparagus, not like the picture teases you to think. The pork belly is still good. They are all just generously salted with garlic, pepper, salt and grilled. So honestly you're better off making them at home for cents a piece. All in all though, tastey bites for cheap.

The karaoke, upstairs is 21+. Never rented a room. But this place can be fun with a bunch of people. You can sing in front of the whole room as drunkenly as you would like.

Koreana is fine. 
It's really the only option you have in University City for a relatively cheap eat if you're craving Korean food. There is that place next to Kiwi Yogurt on Chestnut... but that place is much more pricier. Dishes are about $15, whereas Koreana's dishes range from mostly $7.25 to $10ish.

The food is fine. It's just that imo, I consider $5 to be cheap food. 6ish.. 7ish.. plus tax gets to $8 which is gray area for me, haha. 

Anyway I've been here a number of times. I always get jampbong, but last time I got the noodles with seafood gravy sauce on it. Yesterday I ordered with living social, some $1 deal gets you $10 worth of food! I ordered online, arrived at 5pm and my order was ready. Most importantly, they separated the noodles from the soup so that my noodles didn't get soggy, great :) The noodles were cooked fine. However, the taste of the gravy I give a 3. I actually had to add a little salt, and it was just not the best thing I've ever had. But for $1, I can't complain. Full price, I'd second think going back there. 

The jampbong I've had there before is good. I think it's the same price, $7.25. But it lacks a little flavor as well too. The bubble tea is terrible imo.. no offense I'm sorry but I don't like any place that uses fake powder to make flavors.. that's not what I'd pay $3 for. 

Service has always been friendly though. 

Overall, it's not a bad choice if you're craving Korean food in Ucity. If you have a car and want to drive $15 mins, I'd rather go to HMart.

I was really thinking a 3.5 but I'll round up for a few reasons

I've had a taste of 4 of their popsicles before, and they're delicious. + rating. I have tried their lemon rose, mango something , vietnamese coffee, and a lick of something my boyfriend got.. I think cantaloupe! All their flavors taste REAL, not artificial. That's why I like them. The taste/flavor is always on point. The popsicles melt like they should. not too fast, and they're not too hard. Those are things they truly have on point, and that's why I round up for them.

Why ... do you ask, why would I rate them below a 4? Well that's why I rounded up. Because it might just be my broke ass... but it's $3 for a popsicle. Not a large popsicle, just a little.. popsicle. So for me it's like AH $3.. but I didn't want to demote their ranking for my brokeass. They do have a loyalty program though buy 10 get 1 free. But thats 30 dollars worth of popsicles my friend.. and my bf and i could probably eat 5 each 0_0

It's definitely worth a stop to try it out though. Very cool idea, flavors are always unique and awesome, not to mention delish.

This was the GF and my second time coming here - the first being relatively early on in our courtship. I ordered a smorgasbord the last time (no shame or pretense obviously - if the lady can't stand a guy who can eat, she's not for me). In any case - on to the food! 

We came for an early dinner rez. I was seated right away despite the GF being 10 minutes away - greatly appreciated! Hate the no seating policy when there are clearly available seats. 

We started off with drinks - I had the lemonade vodka which was served in a mason jar - nice touch. Was cloyingly sweet ... the vodka hit me later on - well mixed! 

For food, we started with the sheep's milk ricotta - a must order - reminds me of Locanda Verde's version in NY - a compliment no doubt. Also had the fried chickpeas ... those were well seasoned and were interesting but not necessarily something I'd order again. Roasted beet salad was very nice - left most of it for the GF - from the get go I told her that I would always offer to pay the check as a gentleman ... but would never pay if all she ordered was a salad.

Next came the Asparo pizza with egg on top - nice, artisanal, tasty. Wish the guiancale came out a bit more. But then again .. I'm greedy. Gnocchi was divine as everyone says. 

After, I still felt a hankering for hanger steak - so I ordered it! Was pretty good - nothing exceptional. 

Ended with what else - caramel budino - salty ... sweet .... what more could you ask for?

Service was excellent. Seating is always a bit tight.

This has to be the GF and my 4th or 5th time coming back to this establishment. 

There's one reason I come back .... the pork belly confit cheese burger with fried egg and avocado ... aka the Betty Burger ... how's that for a run-on sentence. Ask for it medium and it comes out perfect. It's a sloppy mess and it's what I ordered when I first met the GF's parents ... but again .. no shame. It comes with fries and a pickle ... and the price is insanely well priced for the consumer ... sub $15! 

My GF begged off of ordereing two apps ... so we settled on one app and a side - the veggie quesadilla and the onion rings. Veggie quesadilla was alright - but it was $3 for a HH deal so a gret deal. The onion rings were da bomb! I'd get two orders if I wasn't already planning on taking a few baby aspirin to self-medicate my subsequent arteriorsclerosis. 

The lady had fish tacos - they were pretty good .. came three to an order. El Vez is still our favorite for fish tacos albeit pricey - I know I know .. we need to get out to Italian market more. 

Service .... was a bit slow getting us seated with about three people walking by and staring at us blatantly while we waited to be seated. There were maybe 2 other tables occupied in the restaurant at the time (early dinner). But once we were seated, service was fine. 

Conclusion - stumbled home with the meat sweats. I stlll dream of the pork belly confit.

Mhmmm seafood. The GF and I had a wonderful day - it was the weekend of her bday so we had mani-pedis ... I got a pedi - she got both. Don't hate! 

Had gone to Shake Shack earlier and was impressed (NY represent!). 

So we came to Devon for dinner before a LMFAO concert. Was seated right away. Our server was nice and took care of us. She remembered that it was Melissa's bday so brought out a candle with dessert, which is always appreciated. 

But let me go back to the beginning of the meal. We started with the sparkling wine flight, which included Moet Imperial (not Nectar). The flights are a nice touch. Cheese plate was next - quite good - standard hard, semi, and soft with fruits, honeys, and bread.

Baked bread is always amazing - a tad sweet, but different and nice. Wish they offered more of it! Also had the lobster rolls (mini) - they were ok. 

For our entrees, she had the Halibut - clean, meaty, yummy. A first for her and she thinks its her new favorite fish - that's always good! 

I had the seafood platter - shrimp, scallops, salmon, crabcake - because that's just how I roll. Very good - decent price point. 

Dessert - we've come full circle now - was the trio of ice cream. Nice ending.

Only quibble I had was the flies that constantly encircled our meal ... for the price, you would hope the establishment could do something about that. But alas ... it's sometimes expected I suppose ...

Three dollars for an ice pop seems a little steep. But I guess when you compare that to, say, the desserts at Franklin Fountain or Capogiro, it's a bargain. Though I do enjoy those other two quite a bit more. Perhaps I'm comparing apples to oranges? 

Nonetheless, this shop is adorable, with their popsicle puzzle toys for kids, bright colors and mini trays for serving the pops. There is an extensive list of pops containing dairy or not, and all of the flavors are interesting combinations. I went with a pineapple basil which was very nice for a hot summer night.

The GF was actually meeting the parents for the first time - we decided to have lunch near the Kimmel Center where the Beauty and the Beast Musical was playing. 

Estia - I had been there previously for a RW lunch and it was great. Great venue - nice clean flavors.

Lunch this time around was no different. To start, we had pita/humus which they replenished and was delish! Ordered the spanakopita and the calimari - nice and as expected. 

For the entrees, we had fish, greek salad, and moussaka. All very good! 

Beautiful location! 

Will be back.

Over the past two years, the GF and I have gone to El Vez at least 15 times. In fact, it was the site of our 3rd date! 

Start off with the guac with pistchios and goat cheese .. aka Bazooka Limon. Declicious. If we're with friends, we get the Macho Nachos ... huge plate with short ribs, beans, and cheese ... yum!

It's hard to choose between tacos or enchiladas ... so why choose? Get both the taco tasting platter and the enchilada tasting platter to try everything. 

Deal of the moment? $15 for soda, app, and entree during lunch! What a steal.

It gets especially pricey for the pitches of margs .... but they are great! 

Can't wait to go back!

Went here for Valentine's Day - they had a 4 course tasting. 

Amazing food - good service. GF thought it was cute that they gave us toasted pumpin seeds as a take away.

Please keep in mind - cash only! That's the reason for 4 stars - a bit inconvenient as dinner can run three digits. Luckily for us I knew the policy ahead of time. 

Unlucky for the couple who was seated next to us. We felt so bad for the poor guy as he had to go out to use the ATM across the street -  considering he was on his Vday date.

Great service on top of a beautiful ambiance = great time! We opted for the 7-course tasting menu. They allowed my girlfriend to substitute sweetbreads for the stuffed pork, which we appreciated. I love sweetbreads but asked if I could try the porkbelly in place since I love pork belly even more than sweetbreads. I didn't get an answer back, but granted, I did say that I would take the sweetbreads if it wasn't possible. Guess it wasn't possible! Either way, all the courses were excellent and will definitely be back for more. Happy Hour cocktails packed a punch too!

This was our second time coming for the lunch prix fixe. First off, they would not honor our living social deal (which was fine). The more egregious error(s) had to do with the fact that there were lobster shells in the lobster risotto ... the stuffed chicken was well overdone ... and the breadsticks were stale. The salads were pretty good and the gelato was excellent! I will be willing to give Bliss another chance since the first time around, the dining experience was much better. But hopefully we won't run into this problem again. Service was very good.

Horrible food and service! Never again! Worst dining experience ever. 

Had a 70+ year old woman waiting on us, which is fine except for the fact that it took 10 minutes for us to be seen. Did not bring us water without us having to request it first. 

Food was horrendous (we're suffering through stomach cramps now). The only blessing was that she understood our request to leave by a certain time and accommodated us. 

Hovered over us when presented with the check. 

Stay away!!!!!!! Was hoodwinked when I saw this place hopping during happy hour - but that's the outside patio area. Who knows - drinks might be decent.

Very good food - drinks are something special. They have a separate tonic menu as well.

I hear they have an amazing brunch as well.

Look forward to returning!

3.5 

I wanted to love this place, I really did. With all the rave reviews from yelpers, reviews from friends that have gone.. I was really excited to try this place after SIX years of living here.. last month was my first venture.

maybe it was hyped up too much, maybe I eat sushi way too much and have high expectations? Idk.

My boyfriend and I both got the 3 roll special for $10.95. And this IS a great deal, especially in this location in the city. Each roll is 8 pieces of maki. I got spicy tuna, yellow tail scallion and spicy salmon. We also tried the tuna dumplings due to rave reviews.

The 3 roll special - to me was very mediocre. There was nothing WRONG with the rolls, the fish was relatively fresh -- but nothing special. but yes, for $10.95, it was a good deal. In my opinion, if you're craving sushi real bad, on a budget, Vics is not a bad choice. I am satisfied/full after eating all 3 rolls, but it's just nothing special to me..
Also to note.. places like Fresh Grocer and such have pretty decent rolls. I'm sorry to compare them to a grocery store. But I've had the sushi at fresh grocer before, they're 5.99 for I think 12 pieces.. so if you get 2 of them its about $12, the same you would end up paying at Vics..

On to the tuna dumplings. Very original thought. The tuna on the outside was fresh. THe overall taste-- why again was I not SUPER satisfied? 2 dumplings = $10. I don't mind paying money for good food, ever. But idk, why didn't this impress me? I honestly might try to go back bc of all the amazing reviews about them.. and I do want to give it another go. Maybe it was an off night? I'm not sure.. I do intend on trying them again though.

After that initial visit -- we have gone back 2 or 3 more times for the roll special, but only when we were craving sushi and would die without it.

It appears that I can't let a Thursday pass me by without taking advantage of taco Thursday. 

We arrived around 845 and still had to wait 15 min. Perfect timing though since at 9pm all alcoholic beverages are half off. Oh and did I mention they have magic hat #9 on tap!

We ventured off the regular menu and ordered a special-edamame hummus with freshly fried pitas.

The pitas were fresh and hot and the hummus was good although a bit salty. 

We chowed on the complimentary chips and salsa and then my belly was full so I just had one taco:  my go to Tiger Shrimp. FANTASTIC as usual. 

And to top it off, our server Tabitha, was one of the best. Attentive, pleasing and all around great. Ask for her, you won't be disappointed.
well, we locked in cole... let's hope the season picks up.

Why 4 stars? Because it's a cheaper place to grab a steak without having to empty your bank account.

   ---   ---   ---

It has everything you'd expect from a high-frills steakhouse. It's in a cool part of town (Ybor.) The interior is very hip and trendy. The servers treat you like you're ordering $40 steaks. The ratio of servers:patrons is very high to ensure that you're never waiting long...for anything. You're greeted by the owner or GM outside, who couldn't be more hospitable. And not to mention - the food is really great, all-around.

So why was my prime rib dinner only about $20 with tip?

I'm not sure, but I hope these things don't change. 

I LOVE it all. The location of Carne is in such a prime spot, the building simply deserves to be a popular. I can't remember what used to be there, but it was unoccupied for quite some time, until now.

I always tell people that Ybor is incredibly diverse - there's something kosher for EVERYONE. But...the one thing Ybor was missing was a good steakhouse. That problem is now solved, and now Ybor truly does have everything. Well...they don't have a zoo. They should work on getting a zoo.

Or, for something that would actually be useful, how bout the new Rays ballpark?

I came back to try a burrito and it was delicious! 

The filling was very juicy and tasty. Like their tacos, the burritos also come with fresh salsa, guacamole, and sour cream. Their tortilla shell tasted fresh, and they toast it to seal the burrito, which is about the size of a small child. 

I agree with other reviewers that the ratio of ingredients is well-balanced. I also agree with a reviewer who said the beef is quite greasy. It's possible that they added the yucca to help absorb the grease from the steak. However, I didn't have a problem with this because I was watching TV while eating my burrito. As I was holding it vertically between each bite, excess grease just flowed out from the bottom of the burrito, through the tortilla, and onto my plate. Then I just discarded the grease that was on my plate.

I had to try it twice before I entered a review.  It is a lively place with good service and atmosphere.  Both times the pizza was very salty (maybe they want you to drink more beer) and the pizza was oily.  I gave it a chance.

Terrible service, passable food.  Our appetizer and wine were excellent but our entrees were unremarkable.  The service was awful - from the moment we walked in to the moment we squared up the bill - it was a painful experience.  There are far better dining venues in Tucson.

This place is the cutest thing in Hyde Park. Love it. High end dog treats, toys, clothes etc. I went in there with a very specific problem that my pup has with thunder - they introduced me to the thunder shit and told me to bring it back if it didn't work, but she assured me it would. Love a place that isn't just about sales - they care about our pets too! Will return.

Adorable small storefront.  I love the clean and colorful decor.  

$3 for a Popsicle. We had goat cheese, honey and cherry and mango curry.  Not typical Popsicle  flavors, but really well done.

11:15 Saturday seated immediately. Ordered a la carte: cinnemon roll, 2 eggs scrambled, 2 sausage patties. Patties were burger-sized and those must be ostrich! No need for toast, hash browns, etc. Sausage was tasty and eggs perfectly done. Cinnemon roll was homemade with ample cinnemon. Texture is a bit 'thick' and wouldn't keep for a day, so must be fresh.

Worth a stop!

Ambience is 70s-80s diner (that's when I was last here!), Formica and leatherett. Lots of ages from high-school to young family to retiree.

I was amazed that Bartram's Garden is as close to Central Philly as it is.  It was just a short bike ride, albeit through some more desolate areas.  

Grab a friend and go visit the gardens.  Pretty place for a walk, but lots of insects.

Let me tell you, I can't think of when we've had a better meal at a restaurant.

 The food was delicious! The service was fantastic. (Charles and Roxanne were our very attentive servers.) And the atmosphere was welcoming and embracing.
If you haven't been there, you are truly missing out on a wonderful dining experience.
I would give it an A+.
They always have good movies and the employees are very nice.  The theaters and restrooms are always clean.  We like their refill cups of soda.

We had stumbled upon this restaurant while walking around the city. We love spicy food, especially Sichuan cuisine, and the prices were right so we decided to give it a try. The staff were very friendly and attentive and as someone who previously lived in China for over six years I can say that the Sichuan food was quite authentic. We did not try anything off the fusion menu except for a complementary Ahi Tuna appetizer which was very good, but as for the Sichuan food we felt everything was on point.

We ordered the dry pepper chicken, the mapo tofu, and the dan dan noodles. The dan dan was not the best we've had in Philly (we liked Han Dynasty's better) but was very good nonetheless. The mapo tofu was one of the best we've ever tasted, while the pepper chicken was good albiet having a different texture from what we are normally accustomed to.

Regarding the "unpleasant" numbing feeling from certain dishes another reviewer has described, this sensation comes from the Sichuan peppercorn which is an essential and defining component of many authentic Sichuanese dishes (known in China as "ma la", literally "numbing spicy") and we enjoyed it very much!

We will definitely be returning in the near future - although we came for dinner the lunch menu's prices were especially attractive, and we highly recommend it if you are in town looking for some authentic Chinese cuisine!

Okay, here's my take on Shake Shack.  First of all it's busy, very busy but I like places with a buzz so that's a plus.  The service is incredibly friendly.  An older man next to me got up to get some condiments and one of the workers told him to next time tell her and she'll get anything needed for him so he doesn't have to get up from his table while eating. Another plus. It's def cool looking for a fast food place.  Plus # 3.  The food was very good as well - a tasty Shack burger and fries hit the spot.  The 4th plus. 
 Any downside .... well yeah.  It's def a little bit pricey for fast food and the portion size.  The burger isn't that big nor is the portion of fries.  So one minus.  
Total:  4 plusses, 1 minus.  I'll def head back again.

This restaurant has a few dishes that we have loved.  However, our last experience here will be just that - our last.  We walked in with a party of 7.  The restaurant was empty except for 5 tables.  We were basically refused service because they were not allowed to push tables together to accomadate us.  Our choices were to either sit outside in the 100 degree heat or separate our party and sit at different tables.  This was astounding to us.  Because of their major lack of customer service, they lost the revenue from the 7 of us.

After a few hours at the pool my friends and I were craving a crisp, cool salad. Two of the girls had gone here and really liked it and wanted the other two of us to go. We went around 2pm on a Monday and got seated right away. The waitress was prompt with waters, which is just what we needed after a hot day at the pool! 

We ordered: I got the Duck Panini (YUM!!!!) with sweet potato fries (even yummier!!! - drizzled with brown sugar and honey). My friends got a caprese salad and the Cobb salad both HUGE!!! I definitely suggest getting the dressing on the side because you will need to take it home or share it with someone! Taking it home will cost you $.25 to get a box (earth friendly?) I also got an iced coffee which came in a pint glass and served with simple syrup and creme, this was a plus in my book as I love simple syrup rather than straight sugar. I took my meal home and had it as left overs, cooked in the toaster oven it tastes just as good as it did when I ordered it! The last time the two girls went they got the Italian Panini and Chicken cutlet and said they were really good as well! I happend to live with the Italian Panini orderer and got to have the left overs to which I conquer- very good! 

Why I didn't give 5 stars? All of the salads were done with mixed greens- I am not a mixed greens fan and would have gotten a salad except that there were no options with any other lettuce :-( Also, in this day and age I find it annoying to go to a place that do not take cards. 

Definitely want to try it out for breakfast as I hear their red velvet pancakes are amazing!!

This place is a lifesaver.

They offer fresh food (not frozen) in S, M, L portions for every meal of the day and snacks. For anyone who works, this is a perfect option for a couple healthy (but delicious!) breakfasts and lunches to make your week easier without any guilt. Have tried a couple dinner items as well and they were also good. 

Food is very tasty and prices are completely reasonable. We are very lucky to have this in Tampa - seems like a business concept that would catch on anywhere.

Walked up and down Ybar downtown and found this little gem. There were a couple of tables available but could not pass up a chance to sit at the counter. Looked over the menu and they had large selection of crepes to choose from, You can pick "sweet" for fruit, sugar filled and "savory" for all items with meat, vegetable's.  

I looked at the menu very carefully and decided on the "apricot" - several hearty slices of soft brie, light spread of apricot and a splash of lemon juice and whipped cream. One last thing, make sure you get the "caramel macchiato" - It is to die for, not my usual drink but glad I did with the apricot crepe. 

It was so good I wanted to order another crepe and macchiato and then a good nap after that. 

Average price for the crepes were around $6 - $10 - Worth the every penny!

Why 4 stars? Because this BBQ joint stands out in the midst of the Columbian Corridor. 

   ---   ---   ---

Holy Hog just looks cool. It also really stands out in an area completely dominated by Spanish restaurants. BBQ on North Armenia? Win.

I went with my standard order- a Ribs and Pulled Pork combo dinner. Between the meats and the sides, I felt like everything was a solid 4. Everything was good, nothing was great, but nothing was bad. My favorite part was probably the sauce. 

Random Fact #1 - I'll tell you this- I have a big appetite, but I could not finish that massive beast of a food plate. 

Random Fact #2 - Surprisingly, Holy Hog has a decent selection of beer as well. It's not World of Beer, but they have some things that might surprise you. 

Random Fact #3 - I am currently thirsty.

I have to be honest the empandias are very very good here but(I understand after if,and,or but usually comes something negative) for $2.69 for one empandia is a problem to me. Stop by for some quick eats and order six beef empandias and the total was $17 I had to ask again how much?? So I looked at the menu and I was surprised when I saw the price.....good eating but too expensive.

I'm sorry to say, my group was very disappointed. A ton of fried dishes without a lot of flavor. Some awkward portion sizes and tough-to-justify prices in some areas. 

The menu is overly complex, yet didn't include pertinent information -- i.e., that something was prepared fried or that the Salt n Pepper Cracker Jacks were savory and came with scallions. For the casual vibe, the fussy menu required us to spend upwards of 20 minutes with our server at different points (probably 10-12 minutes consecutively at the start) -- she essentially became our glossary. Not efficient for the server, who admitted she was still learning the menu, and not all that fun for us. 

Since the concept is about "inventive food" and overall "innovation," edison might benefit from iPad menus, with hyperlinks/rollovers to define unique terms/ingredients.

Wait time for food was very high, though I agree that is to be expected from a new kitchen.

The smallest serving of wine is micro. Shell out the bucks for the midsize at the least.

On the upside, the server had a great attitude and was very patient, and the industrial decor rocked.

I've followed Jeannie to all of her popups and have had mostly fabulous experiences -- particuarly KB1 and KB3. Alas, with regard to edison, it would take a menu redo for anyone in my group to return.

Beware that if you order a chicken Ceasar salad it's going to run you $19 and the amount of chicks you receive is minimal. For $6 you can add chicken to their $13 salad. Pretty steep if you ask me. 

You're better off ordering the chopped salad-lots of ingredients included. 

They do háve a great deal in certain traditional dishes. You purchase one to eat while you're dining in, and then they give you an uncooked one to take home. Their baked ziti is great as well as the lasagna. Plus it's always nice to have more maggianos for later.

Casa Video...what can I say?  An abundant inventory, good central location, helpful staff, and gonga deals.  I come here at least one time weekly for DVD rentals.  

Earlier this year I was looking for Survive Style 5+, a surreal Japanese film.  The copy that was in their system was damaged.  The friendly clerk immediately placed an order for a new copy and one week later it was available for checking out.   

Thanks again, Casa Video!

I was part of a large group dinner at Spice 28 on Wednesday, and we had a great time. It's hard not to compare a lot of the menu items to the other king of Sichuan in Philadelphia, but Spice 28 is a very close second. And if it's ambiance you're looking for, Spice has the edge. 

The great thing about this place is the variety of the menu. Creative selections like wontons with guacamole and tempura lobster add an extra layer to your main dish. 

Speaking of the main dishes, the two I tried (dry pepper chicken and hot pot chicken) were so spicy that they bordered on unenjoyable. I could taste no difference in the degree of heat between spice levels two and three; both were hard on my pansy palate. That said, both were executed well, so I'll just opt for the lowest spice level next time. And there will be a next time. 

The price was right and I've got a 10% off check-in offer that I plan on utilizing soon. Looking forward to my next round of tastebud ass-kicking.

Philly diners, I'm going to beat you. I'm going to beat every last one of you. Why? You should already know. You don't know? Fine, I'll explain it-
For some idiotic reason, you have not been dining at Spice 28. When Bobby Flay opens a mediocre burger place(with great shakes) in U. City, the line might as well extend out to King of Prussia. In contrast, when the best new Asian fusion restaurant in Philadelphia opens its doors to the public, you ignore it like an ex lover who won't stop sending messages when you come away from idle on gchat.

Anyhow, the biggest highlight of my meal here was the the ahi tuna dumplings. These are delicious dumplings served on a guacamole that's way too good to be found at a non-Mexican restaurant.

The Dan Dan noodles are good, but Han's are better.
The spicy cucumbers are very, very good.
The blue crab salad is excellent. It is a nice hearty serving of crab served over a half an avocado.

It is really strange to see how well Spice 28 wields the power of the mighty avocado.

Wonderful way to start off a new restaurant.  My first impression, very clean and staff was very attentive to the needs of all patrons.  Had this place for lunch which consists of all you can eat hibachi, sushi, rolls and some other asian hot food items.  They bring the food to you!! Wonderful concept.  Menu was a little hard to understand since it is all you can eat, but they can only bring two items per person to the table at a time, but the food turnaround is very fast.  Sushi was very fresh and tasty.  Hibachi was standard for any local japanese restaurant.  Over all nice atmosphere, good food and I will dine with them again.  Lunch "all you can eat" only 8.95 per person.  Everyone should try this place out.  One disgruntled person was at the restaurant when I ate there, but he was the type of person who would not have been satisfied with anything.  This is just my fair, unbiased review from a person who eats out daily.  Enjoy, it is worth the money!!

What great service! I am so sad I can't remember our waiters name but I think it's James.  He was so gracious and accommodating.  We had just gotten seated when my husband and I notice that there was a large table with a reserved sign then two people sat down at the table and were followed by maybe two more adults and the rest of the the table was filled up by eight kids under the age of 10, with a baby.  (Don't get me wrong I have nothing against kids)  

The table ended up being very loud and the kids started screaming and asking there parents to get them this and that.  The baby was crying, and frankly it was kind of annoying, plus on top of that the parents weren't doing anything to stop them.  So when James came with our appetizer we asked if it is possible to move outside.  He picked our waters and told us to follow him, and we got out of there! 

 Outside was hot, but at least I could hear my husband speak.  It didn't take long when the kids at the table started to run out on to the patio to get out to the court yard of the plaza.  SERIOUSLY? We left so we don't have to see or hear them, but what are you going to do.  

James came out and checked on us every now and then it was wonderful that he did that. The food was great as usual, but I wanted to write this review on great service.

We started some sauce early morning and left for the market. Fresh cut pasta to order, now really how can you top that, WAIT hand stretched mozzarella ok, HOLD ON I'm swimming in a mini sea of olives. I'm ready to set up a hot plate outside and offer boiling water and sauce.

Om nom nom. My boyfriend and I stopped in today to try two new popsicles. I got the Vanilla Bean Orange Blossom, and any of you that are fans of South Park.. my boyfriend got the Creme fraaiicchee with figs popsicle. Both were delicious as always. I loved my vanilla bean orange blossom. You could easily see all the vanilla beans throughout the popsicle, and the orange was distributed evenly throughout the popsicle as well. It was creamy, which was good for the vanilla-ness of the popsicle. But I appreciated that it was STILL a popsicle, not ice cream :)

Just when I thought I had enough reasons to love Davis Island, a couple of coworkers introduced me to Thai Island. Yum-tastic. I sampled some tuna skewers, the hawaiian curry specialty with shrimp, and tofu pad see ew. I want to live in a world where this Hawaiian curry (with coconut milk, lychees, grapes, and pineapple) is available at every Thai restaurant. It was absolutely delicious. I'm spreading the word, and I will definitely be back soon.

Empanada what can I sayI love them there the beat to my heart......

As a young latin lady I love to find a tasty empanada aside from my dads and my own. In Miami there was Vickies that couldn't be touched and then I went to Winwood Kitchen and had theres and Fell in love like head over heels.

Well now that I have moved and I have a new place to explore I have stumbled upon Mr. Empanada. Oh my how there tasty and oh so fresh. I do have one downfall and that would be the darn prices. THey have many different taste and they are extremelygood but $2.50 for 1 is just to much when I use to pay like .75-$1.50 for 1. 

Aside from the price and the intersection were its located if your looking for a deelish empanada its a good place to hit up. Until next time yelpers adios!!!!

I thinks its high time I write a review of Piazza Gavi. I'd say that after numerous visits over the last couple of years, I've found some personal favorites on their enormous menu. There could be some hidden gems on their menu that never get the spotlight, because it's so big! I'm a huge fan of fried calamari and Gavi has more than mastered the art of crisp. The calamari is light and flaky. Add some fresh squeezed lemon and the dish is perfection. The chicken marsala is not something I typically order anywhere ever, but since tasting Gavi's chicken marsala, I was somewhat enlightened. The chicken is tender as can be and the sauce, (you'll have to stop counting calories if you want to really enjoy it) is rich and flavorful. Buonissimo! For those with a sweet tooth, the tiramisu is a homemade delicacy. Seriously, it is. And it's delicious. As much as I love tiramisu, my true favorite is the Sorbetto (one of the hidden gems I was talking about). The sorbetto is vanilla bean ice cream with Limoncello poured over it. Who knew something so simple could be so good! I have a hard time just eating one. These are my "go-to" dishes at Gavi. Although the service can be hit or miss, each experience is always memorable in it's own way.

Why 3 stars? Because the sides were a 5, but the meat was a 1.

   ---   ---   ---

For review #300, I present to you -Polarizing Establishment of the Year:

Wow, what a contrast. I had been on a BBQ path of destruction lately, determined to eat every piece of 'Q the city had to offer. Jazzy's was one of the last on my list to hit, and it was quite the polarizing experience. 

Let's start with the bad. If it were just for the meat, Jazzy's would be an extremely rare 1-star review for me. The ribs were burned, every bite had bones in it, it was dry and it could not have been less flavorful. The same goes for the pulled pork, sans the bones issue.This was definitely the worst I've in had in the Bay Area.

But the sides! The mac-n-cheese, the coleslaw and the cornbread - all 5-stars and probably the best I've had not only in Tampa Bay, but anywhere. 

The meat is more important, so I wanted to rate this place lower, but the side dishes overwhelmed me to average it out. Hopefully I just got them on a bad day for the ribs/pork.

And I've returned, yet again.
I purchased yet another $5 living social deal to receive $10 worth of food.
This time, I went with the Tuscan Chicken sandwich with polenta fries (add $1.79 on top of my order) sandwich was $8.99. Total I had to pay was $3.80 on top of the 5 from LivingSocial, so 8.80 for everything.

Polenta fries - normally $4.79 and they give you four fries. But if you've never had them before, don't let that price deter you. First time I had it, and they brought it out.. i was seriously like "4 fries??? really??" But they're thick.. more than 1 inch thickness wise, and and maybe 4-5 inches long. Plus, they're made of polenta, so they're very very filling. It cmes with a nice marinara sauce too, to compliment the delicious fries. They're soft, creamy on the inside, with a spicy kick.. but fried on the outside for nice crispiness! Probably my 3rd time getting these, and I'm never disappointed

Tuscan Chicken Sandwich - a first time fr me. Served on a sesame roll with artichokes, chicken, tomatoes. I think this sandwich is A-OK, a 3 star for me. Nothing really "wrong with it", but absolutely not worth $8.99 on a regular day. IE: I'd much rather spend $9 on Dinic's Roast pork (see my other review) if you know what I mean!  It's a good sandwich, but nothing great to make it $9. 

Coleslaw - I'm just not a fan of coleslaw, never have been.. not sure if I will ever be.

therefore, GET THE POLENTA FRIES, with anything yu order. Brunch, lunch, dinner, TRY THEM. Let me know if you loved em like I did!

We were in a rush, there wasn't a line, so VOILA, we ended up at Tower Style. I'll be sure to plan ahead in the future.  It's not that the pizza is Bad, per se, but I also wouldn't call it good. My beau and I nabbed three slices and two drinks for $12 (CASH ONLY!0, his were pepperoni and sausage and mine was a buffalo chicken slice. When we first received our slices, I looked at mine and immediately knew it wasn't hot enough. After a quick nudge of the thumb, my suspicions were confirmed and I had to ask for it to be heated up more.  My dining partner was so ravenous that he had already started eating, but admitted that he wished he'd asked for them to be heated up as well.

The dining area is cramped and generally feels a bit stifling as you're munching on the pizza. The fellow who was working at the cash register had a lot of trouble understanding our order, and we had lots of trouble understanding him, so at least the feeling was mutual. There looked to be another guy working ("working") who DID understand us but, instead of trying to help us out or take charge, he merely tried to relay the message to the original cash register fellow while leaning on the wall.  

The flavor of the pie itself wasn't terrible, but alas, there wasn't much of it. Never knowing when to quit, I took my standard route of dousing it in hot sauce, and toughed it out. We were in and out in about 15-20 minutes, and probably won't be headed back in any time soon.

Promising but ultimately disappointed. So excited for meals, which sounded good and probably would have tasted good too, if they weren't so terribly overcooked. Good meat (lamb chop and duck confit) gone bad!

It doesn't take much to describe this place. DELICiOUS! Best Sonoran hotdogs, great garnishes, the BEST Micheladas hands down, and their Caramelos are one of a kind. Service can be slow, but it's totally worth it.

Oh god. Where do I even start! This place is beyond amazing. My boyfriend and I were looking for a good place to grab a quick lunch before getting ready for a wedding. We roamed around reading terminal but nothing caught our attention. We were contemplating on going to ihop (I really wanted pancakes) but then I remembere spice 28 was closer and that I wanted to try it. I'm SO happy we did. 

Although there were only 3 tables of people or so, the vibe was nice and relaxed. The interior of the restaurant looks amazing. The music was chill and our server was nice and sweet. She was very informative as well. My boyfriend an I ordered the Ahi Tuna dumplings, and let me tell you, it was the BEST thing I've ever put in my mouth. Our server told us to use our spoons to pick up some of the sauce and eat it together. I wish I could bathe in the sauce that it came with. It was sweet and a touch of spice. The dumplings themselves were wrapped up in a crispy exterior and coming from a non-fish lover, the tuna on the inside was so delicious. These people are geniuses. Dumplings with guacamole?! perfect!

For our entree we shared the tropical mango chicken. It was so flavorful and not too heavy. Everything inside the dish was perfectly paired together. 

The owner,(??? perhaps) came by and checked up on us to make sure everything was okay. He was very kind and funny. He introduced himself and chatted with us for a little

Once we asked for our bill, we were notified that we were receiving a complementary dessert. Which I was thrilled about. I love dessert.... But we got these cute donut like cakes that were warm and delicious!! They had a type of sticky filling that was so good that I would live off of it. 

In total, I wish I could set up camp inside of this restaurant and eat their tuna dumplings for the rest of my life. I am definitely coming back to try out the rest of their wonderful menu!

Nice Mexican restaurant.  Unfortunately service is a problem.  We were in there for 90 minutes total.  Took foreever to get beverages or refills.  We needed extra napkins,t hey never came.  The meal arrived over 40 minutes after ordering.

Food was four-star flavor. We tried manythings, rellenos, enchiladas, fajitas.  Great flavors. Chips  & salsa were both great.  They have abou 4 sauces that come with your dinner--and none were fantastic--salsa was sufficient. (Cheese was spicy, green sauce came "stove hot").  The fried ice cream was delicious and a highlight of our meal.  

So if you have lots of time, this could be your Mexican fare.

Federal Donuts is a total success and with their early sellouts they've kinda developed a cult rep. I've been there a few times so I guess you can def put me down as a fan too.  However, I can't give it quite 5 stars despite the hype and my repeat appearances.  The chicken is great quality but I think the seasonings need to be a little stronger.  Harissa is a spice I'm into but the harissa chicken seems too weak on this flavoring. And in a way I feel the same about the donuts.  Again great quality but today I got the vanilla lavendar hot donut and couldn't really taste much of any flavoring.  If I didn't know what is was, I couldn't have told you for a million dollars if it had any flavoring or not or what the flavoring was.  I'm pretty sure for both the chicken and the donuts the flavor/spices  is the last thing added after they're deep fried.  Up the flavoring a bit guys and I'll give you that 5th star.
Been a long time customer, and they deserve a review. Their sandwiches, salads and desserts are great! Friendly and quick service. Always fresh and a reliable go to for lunch!
woah some seriously awesome brunch grub!

I went back to the coventry deli because they had turkey chili and wow, was it good! Excellent! They put cheddar cheese in the middle of the cup it is served in so it melts. It was wonderful, a must try. The girl at the chili counter was also very nice. I will be back for the turkey chili again.

I've been to stadiums all over Florida, and nothing compares to Ray-J!  

Even when the product on the field & the people behind the food counters (don't get me started) aren't quite up to par, the stadium is a great experience.

I would prefer them to have much better access to water fountains, which are few & far between.  Their deal with Levy Restaurants should be seriously looked at as Levy has major issues & has more than once has led me to having phone calls & email complaints.

Anxious like a kid on Christmas morning waiting for their parents to fight the yawns and get up, I sauntered with my friends up to EATS! American Grill eager to sink my teeth into their food. (Shark week had just ended teehee) 

We were promptly seated and the waiter was super friendly. We were all pleasantly surprised with the complimentary homemade chips and ranch dressing. The chips were just the perfect amount of thickness and the ranch tasted like it was scratch made. 

This prompted me for my salivary glands to start the cadence drum roll for the main course... I chose the Philly Cheesesteak, while my dinner companions opted for the fried pork chop sandwich and standard burger, respectively. 

This is where my review starts to go a tad bit awry. My highly anticipated cheesesteak came to my table looking meak. The hoagie roll was thinner than I'd prefer, and the shaved steak could have been more plentiful. Same goes for the cheese. The cheesesteak itself was very greasy and my stomach definitely was upset with me later that evening for induldging in so much grease. My boyfriends fried pork chop sandwich was surprisingly tender, but could have used a bit more flavoring (maybe  more sauce?) And our friend said his burger was good. 

Overall, I will definitely give EATS! another try. The cheesesteak wasn't bad, it just wasn't great (or maybe all the hype on Yelp led my expectations a bit too high). A follow up review to follow next time we head here.

Hands down, one of the best sports bars in town. Wings are outstanding and you can find any game you want. Drink specials daily and friendly crowd. Service is good and several staff members have been around for a long time. Menu offers a nice variety including healthier options. I love the selection of wing sauces!
Love the empanadas here. Everything from beef to chicken to broccoli and cheese. Great for lunch or take-out!

My favorite Thai place in all of Florida. Fantastic Pad Thai and Curries. Great service and ambiance. 
Love to meet a girlfriend here for lunch or take the husband out for a quiet dinner without the kids.

Mel's Kitchen is a good place to have on your radar.  Though it's not in a residential area, if you're in a car and are in the Roxborough/Manayunk area, def consider it fot a breakfast/lunch.  It has a parking lot.  I've had two variations of the roast pork sandwich, both of which were very tasty.  The staff is friendly and they have a few tables both inside and outside.  It kinda reminds me of a few extablishments on or near Delaware Avenue .... but it's more laid back.  Put me down as a fan.

Best thai food in center city area.  No doubt about it.  The first time I went, it was so good I went back the next day too.  

Tried the tom yum soup and drunken noodles on night #1.  Drunken noodles were amazing, got them with shrimp.  Unfortunately, didn't realize the soup had pork in it so I didn't eat that.  

On night #2, I tried the coconut soup, spicy cucumbers and the pad thai.  Ben, the manager, asked me how everything was and I mentioned that the food was excellent but I couldn't eat my Tom Yum the previous night.  He was great and comped me a tasty shrimp curry dish!  I thought the coconut soup was just ok, spicy cucumbers were great, and the pad thai was very good as well.

Overall, the shrimp drunken noodles and the spicy cucumbers were my favorite but I can't wait to go back and try more things.  This place has some Szechuan dishes too.

Great food, great service, doesn't seem too busy inside and does takeout. 

Only downside is no delivery, but at this level of quality, I totally understand.

MAYBE I'm just a bit biased, but I think that the people who work there are the best in the industry.  They give it their all, from the start to the finish, you see people giving blood (Sometimes very literally) sweat and tears.  

From the humble beginnings to the zombie extravaganza of last year, each new house that is rolled out, each new show and every performer is geared up to get YOU with a scare that will send a shiver down your spine and maybe some urine down your legs if you're the susceptible type. 

Wicked sets, creepy music and eerie lighting all add to the ambeince that create this seasonal creature feature, every person who works the event from behind the scenes, to the ride operators to the scare actors themselves really do throw every thing they have into it to make it the best event it can be.  They truly do scare from the heart and deep into your soul, creating a lasting impression that makes you want to come back year after year.

I've been going to Casa Video since there were two locations: one on Speedway and one on Grant.  I think there was a time when every class I took at the U of A was required to show at least one film from Casa Video, which your Spanish professor would pronounce Cah-sah Vee-dei-oh.  Everyone else just calls it Casa Video...or...the only non-Bollywood video store left in Tucson.

For all of you youngsters, there was a time when you couldn't just order up a video on  your computer or TV or go to your local convenience store and pick up a video like you were picking up a can of soda from a vending machine.   Back in the 1990's, we had to go to the store, search the shelves, and find the actual DVD or VHS tape, rent it, and drive back to the store to take it back.  But it was awesome.

Casa Video has a special place in my heart, and not just because of the free unlimited popcorn and the red-curtained porn room (hehe).  If you're a cinephile, this is your Mecca.  They have EVERYTHING on video.  After you've been disappointed by the selection online and on demand and in that red vending thingie, walk into Casa Video and bask in the movie history.  And they have deals so you can keep movies longer and get more movies at a discounted price.

If you're looking for something specific, check out their website or call ahead.  Or show up and ask one of the green-vested customer service people.  They will direct you to the right section.  If you don't know what you want to watch, show up anyway.  There's bound to be a new discovery that you'll cherish, whether it be a new indie you've never heard of, that obscure 80's movie you saw half of on HBO and want to see the whole way through, a tried and true classic you've always wondered about, or a foreign flick that will catch your eye.  There's also an amazing children's section that has way more than Veggie Tales and the latest Madagascar cartoon.  The key word about Casa Video's inventory is depth.  It will also be your new descriptor when you go to your next party and bring up all the great films, yes films, to your friends who just watched The Girl with the Mockingjay Tattoo.

I work near this restaurant and I've considered eating here on numerous occasions. Yesterday, I decided to try it for lunch. My first experience will also be my last. I ordered the glazed salmon and my wife had the lobster risotto. My dish was okay. The salmon was overcooked. The lobster risotto should have been called risotto with a pinch of lobster. 

We ordered diver scallops as an appetizer and received three scallops that were a little bit bigger than the size of a 50 cent coin. I'm not even sure why our waiter brought out two appetizer plates. It was completely unnecessary. 

Given the quality of the food and the dishes, Bliss is overpriced. If you want to spend good money on a dish, go somewhere else. Bliss is not worth the price.

This place is great!  I've been twice now on taco Thursday (where all tacos are $2 each) and the service and food were always great.  The location really does an amazing job of making themselves stand out in a strip mall and making you feel like you're at a stand alone restaurant.  

I've had several of their tacos, my favorites were the bangin' shrimp and the filet mignon tacos.  They're both phenomenal!  Also, the free chips and salsa they bring at the beginning is as fresh as it gets.  You can even taste the fresh cilantro in the salse, wonderful!  I've also had their raspberry lemonade (which came with free refills) and it was amazing!  I let my friend try it and she immediately regret ordering her beer haha.

I would definitely come here again and again!!  Great local gem of a restaurant!

I love hiking this hill!  I consider myself to be in pretty good shape, and it totally kicks my butt at the top.  Don't bring dogs, they aren't allowed.  Do bring water and a head lamp if you start after 6:30.  Do bring your cellphone for that perfect instagram photo of the view of Tucson from the top!

I was craving two things and by golly, NYPD had both on their menu!  I wanted a buffalo chicken pizza and I wanted a pizza with meatballs.  Let me clarify, not prepackaged nasty chicken and not frozen meatballs....and I hit a home run and NYPD was fresh and homemade.

The crust was nice and thin.  Now if they had a wood burning oven it would be perfection...but for a non wood burning oven, this is your place!  You can trust me I am a pie perfectionist.

The buffalo chicken pizza had a ton of cheese, chicken and it was saucy!  Unfortunately, they put the ranch cups on top of the pizza and it messed the pizza up ....but the flavor was SPOT on.  Spicy, but a perfect medium buffalo spice....so flavorful...my mouth is watering just thinking of it.  Besides the ranch cup snafu, the only thing that could improve it would be blue cheese crumbles on the zaa.  It was a tad greasy, but it had a boat load of cheese...which is stellar in my book.

The lasagna pizza had ricotta(one of the best pizza toppings ever), meatballs and mozzerella...and it was GREAT!  Garlic lovers dream.

I would order both again in a heartbeat.  Also, on my radar is the Philly Cheesesteak Pizza.  Best named pizzas are the Situation 20 inch pizza  and if you are brave the .38 special (meatballs, mushroom, onions and jalapeno).   

They deliver and sell by the slice.  Subs, pasta, wings, bread, panini, stromboli, deserts and they cater.

This place is a brand new restaurant that opened about a week or two ago. It's a nice location w very cute modern interior. It is all you can eat hibachi, Asian, and sushi, but it is not buffet style. You choose what u want from the menu and the server gets it for you.
You can choose to sit at a regular table or sit hibachi style and still get everything on the menu, plus the typical hibachi show w the egg and onion volcano, etc.our server was funny tho so that made it even better. We were watching another server Tommy and he was very outgoing and really wanted to make the show even more extraordinary.
I tried several rolls. I took a bunch of pics that I posted so u can c all of then. I liked the one w the fish on top the best. The Alaska roll was the most bland, the other roll w crab and tempura chicken had a Lot of flavor so that was pretty gd.

You had a choice of salad or soup. Salad was fd bc of the dressing. It was just simple lettuce but it was fine. The soup was the typical miso and another type of soup. One additional soup that was different than at any type of hibachi restaurant was the chicken soup, it tasted like it had egg and corn and it was really good.

Hubby and I requested to also try mango chicken and general Tso chicken the general tso chicken was pretty gd w fresh broccoli on side. The mango chicken never came even tho I acknowledged if it were coming a few times. That is ok bc we did get full but the server shouldn't have ignored and disregarded it as if we forgot about it.

The hibachi we ordered was steak and shrimp & chicken and steak respectively. It came w veggies and fried rice, and two sauces Ginger and a creamy seafood sauce. There were only 4 medium shrimp and the rice was kind of blandish but ok.

All in all I liked my experience. I feel that for the lunch price of 8.95 it was worth it. For anything more than that, I would think it is quite much. They say there is a lot more at dinner, so I'm sure it could very well be worth it, I just have not tried it.  17.95 for dinner is still a lot less expensive than what you would find at any other type of restaurant like this so that works for me.  I'll be back again for sure, and hopefully during that time I can try the mango chicken, lol.
Steaks were, whats another word for lousy? Frozen meat, are u kidding me in this town. If you really need a cheesteak by all means find other options, or just walk another 50ft to DiNic's.

Have been here several times and find it acceptable Mexican style food. We live close and it is reasonably priced. Happy hour 2 for 1 Margaritas and beer. Margaritas are not top shelf but pretty good. Chimichanga is tasty, Fajita quesadilla is  one of our favorites too. The combo platters are filling and fairly standard fare. I would stay away from the rellenos, canned chile w/ wierd ground beef. " Chalupa" is what I have  always called a "tostada", but it is good.  Tried the "Sopa pillas" for dessert which was a  greasy fried flour tortilla with squeeze margarine and sugar on it,.a must avoid! I have had real sopa pillas in New Mexico and this is not it! Overall not bad but service is slow if busy.

This was a fantastic surprise today.  We went for lunch to check it out since none of us had been before.  We weren't really sure what to expect, but we thought it was a buffet.  It's not.  It's better!  

For $8.95 a person (adult price), one gets to choose from any of the items on the menu as often as one wants.  There are several hibachi-style meals to choose from as well as several different entrees including pork, chicken, and steak.  For an up charge, one can add higher grade meats and even lobster tail.  One can also choose from a variety of sushi rolls as well.  Again, this is all for $8.95 and the food won't stop until you want it to!

It does make me pause and wonder if that price is too good to last, after all, one can purchase a sushi roll at other restaurants that, alone, cost in upwards of $8.  For now, though, I'm going to take advantage of it.  Don't be skeptical of the quality of the food for the price either as it's extremely good!  I also hope this trend continues!

The decor around the restaurant is tasteful, simple, yet elegant, and there's a warm, cozy feel to the entire restaurant.  It's clean and should definitely continue to be so as the people working there seemed very keen on keeping up appearances.

One quick story about our visit today.  We got there at 11:15, and upon entering were told they don't open until 11:30.  That's cool.  No biggie, but we would have liked to have seen hours on the doors to make sure we would know that.  The owner (?) was quick to chase after us in the parking lot to ask us to come back inside and sit down offering to make sure we could get some food.  What great service!  He was very gracious and even checked on us a couple of times during our visit. 

We will definitely be back!

If this place were judged strictly by the pizza, I'd have given it 5 stars... Service pretty much sucked. The young guy working the counter treated us as if he were doing us a favor by letting us eat here. He was cleaning some tables next to us and slamming the hell out of the chairs which made for an awkward dinner. On the way out he laid on the " Hurry up and get the f** out" pretty thick. Anyway, love the food but the service sucks. I'll stick to Brooklyn's, at least you get a hello and a see ya later.

I was really excited to try out Spice 28, so my boyfriend and I made reservations on Open Table for just the two of us this past Wednesday.  We had such a blast at Center City Sips with friends, though, that we decided to invite everybody along to dinner.  They had no problem switching our reservation from two to TEN people (yes, ten)!  Needless to say, the staff was so accommodating to our large (and loud) group.  I was able to try their spicy cucumbers, which I thought were excellent.  The dan dan noodles ended up being my favorite dish of the night, but the dry pot chicken certainly did not disappoint (it was just a tad too spicy for my palette, but I enjoyed it anyway).  The menu was not as easy to decipher as Han Dynasty's (I really do like their number system), but it was still pretty easy to follow and it had a nice amount of items to choose from.  I can't wait to get back here to try more of their dishes!

Lunch at Hard Rock was a good experience.  We had the pulled pork sandwiches, one hickory BBQ saquce, and one with mustard style BBQ sauce.  Both sandwiches were very tasty.  The sides were good also.  The memorabila on the walls was interesting.  Service was just fine.

We had an amazing night at Edison. From the moment we walked in Jillian and John were really friendly and engaging. We had some excellent beer from Cigar City while we waited for our table. Now for the food.. We started off with the duck confit and fois gras tacos which were heavenly, the cold oysters with N2o kaffir lime which was bright and refreshing, then the potato crusted oysters. Wow.. seriously I could eat these all day long. For dinner I had the lobster gazpacho and an order of the fois gras with shrimp toast. The piece of fois was huge!! Never any compliant for that! My companion had the burger on brioche. The flavor of the burger was spot on but the bun proved to be too much and chewy. The fries were awesome and they make all their own condiments which is a great change from the normal. For dessert I had the hummingbird milkshake. OMG. That was the best milkshake I ever had. Cream cheese ice cream, pineapple, bananas and nuts. Wow!! We also had the cruellers with carrot ice cream which was very tasty. All in all had a wonderful time, our server was friendly and knowledgable, the atmosphere is funky and fun, and food is really out of this world. Great job folks! Eagerly await our next visit!
Love this little dive bar, the live music and cheap tecate keep me coming back for more!

Love Evos. It's nice to have an option for quick and healthy. The greens for their salads are high quality. It makes you feel like you're making a healthy choice... unlike when you buy a salad at McDonald's or the like. AND they have sun-dried tomatoes which makes them alright with me!
The customer service tonight was great. He helped my sister pick out her meal- the taco wrap with chicken instead of steak (tasty and healthy post-workout meal), helped me download the mobile app, and provided me with the nutritional info.
Don't go too crazy here. Just because it's cooked healthier doesn't mean it's not loaded with calories. Most of it's a safe bet, but the crispy buffalo chicken wrap is not exactly one of their lighter choices.

Why 4 stars? Because this is a great fit for Channelside.

   ---   ---   ---

Channelside itself may be quickly deteriorating to a non-existent shell of its former self, but Grand Central at Kennedy is headed in the opposite direction. Pour House is a great edition for this corridor - a bar with great beer selection, which contrasts well with the new Tex-Mex spot, coffee shop and BBQ joint.

Any bar that offers more than your standard, boring beer fare is a welcomed addition to the Bay Area, in my eyes. 

Wow, what a brew-snob I've become.

Today we tried Creamy Plum with lavender honey and Drunken Plum
Both were good! It really tasted like there was alcohol in the drunken plum! Nom nom

But also to note we saw an episode of Bobby Flay's throwdown and it looks exactly like the idea from Las Palentas in Tennessee $3 pops too, episode was from 2008. Interesting...

Most Sunday mornings will find us at The Egg Connection.  As a result, we know the wait staff and our usual friendly  waitress knows how we like things.

Yes, the decor is funky kitsch but we find it comfortable and we're there for the delicious breakfasts.  Service is fast and the food is plentiful and good.  I almost always take home leftovers.  Julie will typically get some sort of pancake creation.  She's finicky about how her bacon is cooked and they almost always do it right (very well done).  I've tried a number of different dishes but some of my favorites include Nile's Special,  Sicilian Omelet & Chicken fried steak & eggs.

We try to eat in off times to avoid waiting.  If you get there before 9:30 AM you can usually get seated immediately.  After that the crowd starts building...and what does that say about the restaurant's popularity? And do check the AZ Star's Thursday Caliente magazine.  You can usually find a 2-4-1 coupon which makes this a real bargain.

Meh...,not great but not bad either. I am suffering from a cold and thought a good bowl of pho was in order. 

I got the chicken pho and it came with the typical accompaniments. 

The chicken was sliced, not shredded. I prefer shredded. 

The broth was ok but lacking flavor. 

Like I said, not bad but just ok. 

I prefer Pho An Hoa any day.
WAY TOO SWEET!  Too much corn syrup in their food....try Karuna's....not so heavy on the sweet.

I have been excited about Pizza Brain for MONTHS!!! And alarmingly, the experience and the quality of the pizza FAR exceeded my expectations. 

Brian gave us two slices to try before we even ordered to keep the slices in rotation. He made awesome recommendations and was super friendly. We tried the Jane pizza, which had a delicious selection of flavorful cheeses, fresh basil and tangy tomato sauce. The crust was AMAZING??  BEST CRUST EVER?? Charred and crispy and had a fermenty dough-y taste. 

We also tried the seasonal pie with sweet potato, honey goat cheese, and pumpkin pie spice. I was afraid it would be too sweet but it was delicious and savory and complex and had pools of butter or something on top??? I have no idea. But it was alarmingly delicious. we sat on the floor and crammed pizza slices into our mouths like gluttons. 

Also the pizzas were very hot, and the wait didn't even seem excruciating because we could eat ice cream appetizers and look at pizza trinkets while our pizza was cooking. 

I was initially more excited about the quirky museum part than the pizza restaurant part of Pizza Brain... But now I can say the actual pizza is WAY worth the trip.
