I have to admit when I first heard about the "Museum of Pizza Culture" I thought to myself this place is gonna be all cheese and no meat if you know what I mean. And then I saw one of the owners; a towering red-headed goof named Brian "Brain" Dwyer (I bet you no one has ever actually called him Brain, either), I thought to myself that there is no way this joker has his slice together. Boy was I wrong. 

As it turns out what could've just been a pizzeria founded purely on the notion of ironic pizza paraphernalia and self-promoting parlor tricks with no palette for a great pie is actually deafening cacophony of sights and sounds and yes, folks... taste, GREAT TASTES! So many inventive flavors, infused with fresh ingredients and sense of humor and style. I've gone several times to the shop and I've tried the Jane (Cheese), Jobby Pines (Pesto, Pine Nuts, Chicken), Forbes Waggensense (Pepperoni), Felix Huppert (French Onion Soup), Lucy Waggle (Prosciutto, Dates, and Thyme), but the stand out might have been a new pie who's name I didn't catch. It was a white pie with sweet potatoes. Crust Almighty! Unique and complex. 

I think its safe to say that decor aside there is no pizza shop like this in the entire city, but even more importantly there is no Pizza like this in the entire city, there might not even be pizza like this on the entire eastern seaboard. Pizza Brain just might be the king of pies. Nay! The Wizard of Za's.

Pretty underwhelming experience. Restaurant tries to be trendy with decor, vibe and faux gourmet food but it the end it fails in most aspect. We arrived with no reservation and we promptly told that there was a wait even though two tables were open. Bartendar was fantastic even letting us try wines/beers. Note despite being a drink lab they have no hard alcohol--beer and wine only. Best part of the whole experience. We tried the goat cheese truffles to go with our drinks. Served within minutes they were not fresh and way to dense. Could not taste the foie. 

Finally got a table after some waiting.  Ordered the clam and mussel starter which was ok and the migas which were some latin version completely unrelated to the Spanish style so be forewarned.  Halfway through our appetizer, our entrees arrived. Perhaps I am irrational, but this is a pet peeve of mind. Server offered to take them back but who wants heat lamped food? We had told the server earlier we were in no rush, but apparently they were. Burger was overcooked (ordered it medium rare and it was well) and fried chicken dull. If you are oging for comfort classics do them well. The fried chicken batter had been applied when wet and immediately fell apart upon cutting.

The final insult was the $100+ tab.

There are better places to spend your cash.
No, those big puffs of fried dough with powdered sugar on them in the window don't taste any good. Keep walking...

I totally agree with many of the other reviewers.
The cashier (Senora Bling) has always been rude,
but she really showed how mean she can be.  I
had a drink from a Circle K and ordered some food.
She asked me if I wanted a drink.  I said NO.  She
went ahead and charged me an extra 2 bucks!
I used to be a regular there.  They won't get a chance
to rip me off any more.
Hasta La Vista baby!

This place offers both entertainment and good food simultaneously so what's not to love?  The classic Jane had a slightly tangy sauce, crispy and thin crust, a good blend of mazzarella, and basil; it's good, solid pizza and the slice was huge, although it was more expensive than your typical pizza parlor slice.  They have more eclectic varieties, some of which are seasonal, so I'll definitely be trying more slices.  The parlor itself is very hip and fun, with an unbelievable amount of paraphernalia and a mural.  You could spend hours just looking at everything.  The owners are fantastic and definitely make the ambiance very fun and relaxed, which is kinda what we all need after a day at work (or even a half-day).  Plus, they are right next to Little Baby's so you can get your dessert and meal in one fell swoop.  Not that's what I call delicious convenience.

Kenny D's 1 & 2 are both BAD for YOU!  Service is bad. Sandwiches are bad. Prices
are bad.  Atmosphere is way bad.  Why would Kenny open #2, when # 1 needs a total makeover?  I miss the Sonic that used to be at the #2.  There is never anybody at the # 2.  I wonder why?  Kenny, Chicago wants you back home.

Straight off the bat, the AC wasn't working when we came for lunch, and I almost passed out from the heat. Hopefully this was a temporary thing (otherwise do NOT come in the summer). Anyways......

The lady taking orders was great, and they had a pretty diverse menu. I opted for the reuben with onion rings. They had the BEST onion rings I've ever eaten. Huge, crispy, just enough seasoning...yummmm! The reuben was okay. A little overly salty. I had to take some of the innards out because of that. It was also super duper greasy, but I guess that comes with the diner attitude?

My coworker ordered the italian sandwich which was ENORMOUS. He loved it, but he had to eat it with a fork considering it disintegrated into a greasy pile of food.

They serve same dishes as every other Sonoran place in town, but there's something different about this place. I see people raving about the meat, but the vegetarian burro is one of the better ones in town.

A few months ago I was reading a gossip website and someone posted a video of a violent girl-fight at an undisclosed Florida mall... my first thought before even watching was "I SWEAR ON MY LIFE THIS WILL BE UNIVERSITY MALL" - and it was! Yay! I have great intuition I guess. But honestly, this mall is not that bad. I come here to walk around when I'm bored, shop for the occasional outfit (there are a few legit stores) and have watched several movies in the upstairs theater, which is open as far as I know. I was disappointed when Forever 21 folded a few months ago - sad to say that was University Mall's best store and it appeals to broke college kids so that was a letdown. Wah. Another note: previous reviewer was right about the astounding amount of men's sneaker stores- at least a dozen different ones! If there's a market for it.... why not?

Anyways, I have this dream that the city of Tampa will just bulldoze the whole thing down and create a new, pristine shopping center like they did with the stores in Temple Terrace at Busch and 56th and the whole area will be renewed and beautiful! Then again, that strip mall, nice looking as it is, is not doing too well (in my opinion) and still has a bunch of cheap stores. So til then, University Mall is.... University Mall. A good place to get Ybor clubbing clothes, people watch, and get hit on. Yay University Mall!

Why 4 stars? Because this was an incredibly pleasant surprise.

   ---   ---   ---

Nico's! I have driven right past this place about 100 times over the last couple of months, and I was so impressed with my experience that I am taking the rare opportunity to write a review the same day I was visit a place.

I am taking a trip to NYC soon and was told that I need to hit up a place that serves Venezuelan Arepas while I'm there. Since I had never even heard of Arepas before, I wanted to test them out at Nico's, which is only about half a mile from my house.

I'd never tried Venezuelan before...but because I love just about all Latin food, I assumed I would enjoy myself some Arepas. I was not wrong. Wow! 

It reminded me of Puerto Rican mofongo, in that, it was a whole bunch of stuff all jumbled into some breading. Like a burrito, but more exotic.

I went with the Nico's Beef Special - shredded beef, chicken salad, and white sauce. The girlfriend got the La Pelua - shredded beef & white queso. I was in heaven!!

The only minor adjustment we could have hoped for was that hers was...yes...too buttery. It almost hurts to type that, because, who doesn't love a hunk of butter?? Hers was pretty doused, but still really delicious overall. Mine was perfection. 

Just a warning - this treat is a messy one - bring the fork and napkins... and be ready to be wowed! This is definitely a 4.5 star for me!

So we wandered over to Bliss (Living Social Deal in Hand!) to sit along Broad Street on a beautiful Fall evening.  Despite being a Saturday Bliss wasn't crowded at all which always makes me somewhat uneasy.
To start off our server was impossible to understand.  I am very patient but this man's accent was way too thick and we ended up asking a different server to tell us the specials.
We started out with Shrimp Spring Rolls and a Steak & Mushroom Flatbread.  The Shrimp Rolls weren't very good and they hardly gave us 4 bites for and $9 appetizer. The sauce they were served with tasted way too acidic (like straight champagne or something). The Flatbread on the other hand, was very delicious. It had just the right amount of each ingredient.
For our entree we had the Seafood Pot with Thai seasoning. It was just OK. There wasn't nearly enough seafood or other substance to really fill us up but the flavors that were there were pretty good.
All in all I doubt I'd return to Bliss unless we have another Living Social Deal but it was far from a bad dining experience. With so many wonderful places nearby I doubt we'll return to Bliss again.
Great wings and ambiance. Love this place and great for the whole family.

When I walked into Pizza Brain's the energy was through the roof! The Beastie Boys were playing, the scent of pizza was in the air, and the staff was rocking out making pies. 

We looked over the menu, and decided that the honey-goat-cheese-arugula pizza would be our pick for the night. We ordered a whole pie, but while we waited Brian (Pizza Brain himself) offered up a few slices.  The margherita, and peperoni slices were delicious! 

Our pizza was ready in about 30 minutes, but that seems like an average wait time for a fresh pie. The wait didn't feel that long at all.  We checked out the museum area, and watched the guys behind the counter spin some dough! 

Our pizza was beautiful! We gobbled up a few delicious slices, and let me tell you- YUM! The dough was cooked perfectly, and the thin crust didn't sag when you folded it. The ingredients were fresh, and of the highest quality. MMM! We washed down the slices of pizza with Levis Champ Cherry sodas. 

I am a tough judge when it comes to pizza. I feel like Philly has FINALLY stepped up it's pizza game with Pizza Brain.

The look of the pie was awesome and the crust perfect, thin but not too crispy like Taconelli's. We ordered the Jane because we just like pizza to be pizza without all the chicken and funky toppings. But the pizza was totally overpowered by garlic -- I could feel the tomatoes and cheese but couldn't really taste them over the garlic. Don't get me wrong the garlic was fresh. Cheese nice mozz and some crunch of the grana padano. Take the garlic down several notches and I'd be in pizza heaven.

The Oxford Exchange is a new mixed retail space across from UT off of Kennedy. They have a tea bar by TeBella, a coffee bar by Buddy Brew, a bookstore, a home goods store, and a restaurant. They also will serve high tea! 
I went yesterday for the soft opening, and the space is amazing. I had checked it out in it's transformation stage because they will also host events, and we were considering it for our wedding. Unfortunately, the space is too small for our party and we didn't like the L shape of the space cutting the dance floor off of the dining area. However, I would love to rent it out one day for a smaller party because this place is amazing. They didn't miss one detail- even the bathrooms are gorgeous!
Yesterday, I had a club sandwich that tasted what I imagine it would taste like if Ina Garten had made it for me- soft bread, crunchy bacon, tart green tomatoes, creamy avocado, and tasty turkey. It was not plain jane at all!
I am just too excited for them to finally open, and I will definitely be one of their regulars! Go check it out!

Just like a NJ Diner!!  I was there late on my way home from The Tampa Bay Times Forum. I tried Three Coins on the advice from fellow Yelpers! I was not disappointed. I had pork chops that came with 2 sides and soup or salad and dessert. Everything was delish!! The rice pudding was the best I've had in FL. The entertainment was great. An old guy with his shirt rolled up humming and dancing while using the ATM LOL!!! Always a show late night!!!

What a great event thank you to Brett and the staff at Busch Gardens. So where to begin free up close parking nice. Once we got inside everyone was very nice to us and treated us like well VIP'S. The 2 hour cocktail party was great bacon wrapped scallops, steak and blue salad, caprice salad, beef wellingtons with horseradish sauce, deviled eggs and stuffed artichoke hearts all yummy throw in the pumpkin crumble oh my. The free beer was nice also. Toward the end of the cocktail party the skies opened up but the Busch Garden staff was prepared gave everyone ponchos so we could continue on into the night. First house we went into was Nevermore nice house with the sound of a beating heart throughout it. We then wondered around a bit came across a gang of zombie clowns so beware if your scared of clowns. The line for the Zombie Mortuary was huge thank goodness had a front of the line pass since it seemed like was in and out of the house in 5 minutes.

I cannot say enough good things about the teachers at PPY. I've probably had a class with each one of the teachers here, and I've enjoyed them all. Some classes are a bit calmer than others, some are more physically difficult, but in the end, they all challenge you to push yourself to your limit and to give your body what it needs. Need a break? Take it. Thirsty? Have some water. Gotta pee? Go ahead. They're not as serious as some other studios I've gone to, which is really refreshing. Not only that, but everyone here is so nice... Coming to class regularly really gives you a chance to get to know your teachers, fellow yogis and yoginis.

There have been a few changes to the studio this year - new teachers, new classes, different furniture layout. At the end of the day though, the teachers remind you that coming to class is really an effort that you make to take care of yourself.

Are you ready? Bring some water and a towel with your mat. It will get hot (about 90 degrees?) , and you will sweat (a little or a lot, it's up to you). Most importantly, don't forget to BREATHE!

Ladies and gentlemen, I am here today to tell you that salvation is here. 

I never thought I'd live to see the day when sophistication would finally arrive in Tampa Bay, but that day has come. Let us rejoice!

The Oxford Exchange is quite simply the most gorgeous and refined space in all of Tampa Bay. I went there today, opening day, and had breakfast, and did some shopping. I am not going to ruin the surprise that is this place by posting photos. You just have to go. You will feel like you are in London or Manhattan. The food was very good, although some of the pastries were not executed well. That's about the only negative I have to say about it. They have nailed every single detail, from the decor, to the exquisite customer service, to the menus, to the types of candles they sell (Cire Trudon, need I say more?).

This space, and specifically the restaurant, is what every Tampa restaurant should aspire to be. Magnificent.

I had a scarilicious opportunity to get to go to Howl-O-Scream at Busch Gardens in Tampa, FL this past weekend courtesy of Yelp.com and Busch Gardens.  I must say, this is a MUST DO, especially to get you in the mood for Halloween.

Howl O Scream has 7 haunted houses, a haunted dance club, and a haunted show called Fiend.  Their haunted houses will have you spinning and screaming like crazy with different themes that you may be all too familiar with if you ever read eerie criminal novels, watch scary shows, or watch scary movies.

The way each houses plays out their themes will make you really feel like you are right there in the moment. Some of these haunted houses include Nevermore, Vampires, Zombies, Killer Clowns, and a Blood Asylum.  The details that they put in each of these houses were outstanding, and it really was as if you were in a scary movie. That is incidentally, the whole point of the Haunted House called Alone. That is the only haunted house that I did not go to. There is an additional fee,  but they say that the haunted house will blow your mind and guts out of you.

There was always endless frightfulness at Howl-O-Scream including the random acts of costumed scary monsters roaming around the park. They would never touch you, but they would always make it a point to either be heard; Chainsaws were the worse to me, or make it a point to startle you.

Some of the things that made me smile during my experience at Howl-O-Scream were the Halloween themed show called Fiend which had different costumed dancers and singers like Frankenstein and dressed up monsters and zombies. The dance mob had everyone in the park dancing as if they were in a real show of "The Mobbed".  Lastly, the V.I.P Front of the Line tickets were a great bonus. I just waved nicely to the people who were standing oh so patiently in line.  As the Halloween season draws closer, the lines will be even longer.  It is worth the wait, I will tell you over and over again.

I was also able to take part in the V.I.P welcome party which was in a nice air-conditioned area where they had great appetizers and great drinks included. Busch Gardens serves delicious food and drinks so make sure to give them a try.

Howl-O-Scream was an amazing event.  They say that it will be an unforgettable night. It sure was, thanks to Yelp and Busch Gardens. Therefore, with all my might, and all my fear in my body, thanks to Howl O Scream, I highly recommend that everyone takes a trip to Howl O Scream Busch Gardens, and experience All that they offer.  You will be so astounded.

Oh yes, don't forget to check out my Howl O Scream album at facebook.com/go2tampa and Click on Howl O Scream album.

Let me qualify this review in that I have had two friends whose opinions on food I respect greatly both have raved about Edison. My take is that they change their menu way too often. Nothing my friends recommended was on the menu when I went.

We were greeted quite nicely by the staff at the door and the waiters were quite attentive and helpful

Now comes the food. The tuna appetizer was mediocre. It was essentially a tuna tartar gone bad. It had a jicama type fruit, a tomolive and kalamata olives with a mayonnaise type dressing. The flavors did not go together. My scallops were very salty as was my friend's steak. My wife's snapper was dry and coated in a corn flake breading that was too thick. The sweetbreads also had too much breading and a sweetness to the sauce that did not lend its self to the sweetbreads.

I think Jeannie is trying too hard. The last two pop ups before she opened Edison I had similar experiences. Again, she was changing the menu from weekend to weekend. She is obviously talented as I have had some great meals come from her kitchen. I don't think you can constantly be changing the menu without a good amount of misses.

We were very impressed with the food! Everything was just incredible, I had the scallops and my boyfriend had the lamb. Best scallops I have ever had, truly such a great experience and one of the best restaurants in Tampa. We also has the sweet and peppered cracker jacks-amazing and the conch and bacon fritters-to die for.

The menu looks very complex but do not be discouraged, everything tastes amazing. This actually beats The Refinery for me(another one of our favorites.)

So much more than a taxi service, Uber is the most convenient (and classy!) way to get around Philadelphia. All you need to do is download the app and upload your info (including credit card), and you can request a black town car or SUV (fits up to 6) at any time. It's a little pricier than typical cab fares, but so worth it. Your card is automatically charged and fees include gratuity, so no money exchanges hands. Not only are the cars clean and comfortable, but on hot days you get guaranteed a/c. No more sweating it out in the back of a hot and sticky cab in a party dress with Uber around! 

We use Uber mostly for special occasions or if we have a discount, which they hook up quite often for events around the city or on holiday weekends. There's nothing better than rolling up to a party like a P-I-M-P in one of those smooth black cars. 

My only gripe (and I've expressed this to their customer service reps, who are super responsive and amazing, BTW) is that some drivers don't seem to know their way around the city as well as most cab drivers. I'm talking taking Chestnut Street during rush hour and not knowing how to get to Fishtown from Center City type stuff. So provided you know where you're going, it's all good. But you may want to keep one eye on the road as you're chillaxin' in the back seat.

Had a BLAST!  I can't describe how much fun I had because I was easily under the influence of yardie size beers throughout the night.  It rained throughout the night, and actually made it an even more FRIGHTFUL experience!...because all gloomy with a slight chill to the air.  The haunted houses were each unique and had there own charm, easily my front runner was the Carnival(clownhouse) 3-D house.  LOVED IT!  The roller coasters were good as ever.  I recommend getting the front of line passes, because the lines looked BRUTAL!!  Not waiting in line made all the difference in my book. Not only was the park fun, but got to meet some Tampa elite yelpers that we ended up partying with the whole time!  Good times, good people, good food(VIP event tent. SCORE!), and good scares.  Get your booty to Howl-0-Scream before your time is up! (in a reallycheesy pathetic scary vampire voice)

Went back to Koizi for my birthday. The price of 8.95 is what is most inviting to me. Also when I have a taste for sushi rolls, I think of this place bc my husband can also eat here and enjoy the hibachi and other chicken options. We finally got to try the mango chicken. It was tasty with sweet chili sauce and chunks of mango. I liked that. The side chicken dishes you ask for are a good size, not too big not too small, but with all the food options, you will definitely be full for the price.
For my hibachi, I had the steak and shrimp, and tofu. I liked both the steak and tofu, but this time, the shrimp tasted off like right from the water, but not fresh tasting, it tasted kind of dirty or something. Turned me off, I wont get that shrimp again.
I had the dancing krab roll, koizi roll, and rainbow roll. All of those were pretty good.  
In regards to the service, our hibachi cook and our waitress were pretty mute. The waitress seemed kind of crabby but she ended up to be more efficient than she initially was.
I think I will be back on those days when I am free during the day to get lunch, it's not on top of the list of places, but it's not on the bottom. I do like the atmosphere. 
I think that if I sit anywhere next time it will be at a regular table instead of a hibachi table. When we went this time, there were people by themself and it was awkward like you were staring at each other and no one was saying a word.

I wish it was more actual pizza museum than fancy pizza restaurant, but overall it's not bad. I finally tried all three slices and as far as pizza goes it's pretty good. They have a tendency to burn the slices, which is kind of a bummer, but I think it's worth the $3.50 a slice to check the place out. I've only been here on off-hours and so far haven't had a long wait. The staff is always super friendly. Since the place is so new, they've been getting a lot of press and the last time I was there, they had a camera crew in there interviewing the owner, wich is totally fine, but kind of inconvenient since there isn't a ton of seating. I haven't tried one of their pies yet. They sound good, but honestly $22 is a bit steep. I'd definitely come back though.

My fiancee and stopped by Pizza Brain last night for the first time! We had driven past many times but never had the chance to try it out. I'm a pizza snob so it takes a lot to impress me. They accomplished it! The pizza was outstanding! The ingredients were super fresh and dough cooked to perfection. 

I was also overly impressed with the friendly staff. The owner greeted us at the front door and told us about the menu. I loved the guy...he was so cool and nice! Whenever someone came in, he was so happy to see them. 

Even the employees behind the counter were super friendly. The pizza was so delish we came back a few hours later for another slice. As I write this, I'm craving it.

Also having Little Baby's Ice Cream next door tops the charts!

We will certainly be repeat customers!

CB is always good.  I'm not sure anyone on Yelp HASN'T eaten here, so I'll skip the long-winded review.  It's a chain with chain country fixins, so there isn't too much to say.  I've never had a bad meal at any location, especially this one.  Everything's always hot and fresh and the service is great.  If you haven't had their turkey sausage, do it!  It's the best damn non-pork sausage on the planet.  Trust me.  Also, their meatloaf is to die for.  It's soooo close to mom's.

This place was surprisingly great.  Yeah, it's a chain, but at least it's in the same group as Ruth's Chris, so I hope that means the quality is better.

The staff is very attentive and friendly.  Seats are comfortable.  Decor is typical of a seafood place of this price point.

My husband really loved the Grape Crush and drank two.  I had a lovely Sauvignon Blanc from New Zealand and ultimately ended with a 20 year old Tawny Port.  

To start, my husband had the New England Clam Chowder.  He liked it so much he didn't share with me!  

For his entree, my husband had the fried cod and shrimp.  He ate it all up without complaint then started begging for food from my dish.  I had the red grouper done Shang Hai style.  It was very tender but still had a fishy taste that I don't usually expect from grouper.  The soy-based sauce was a little too salty for me, but it was still edible.

This place is solid, and the prices are fine.

I was anxious about edison after reading the mixed reviews, but I was happily not disappointed.

We had an early dinner, which meant that the restaurant wasn't too crowded, and we got plenty of attention from the waitress.  The place wasn't full until we finished our meal, so it wasn't too loud.  Everything about the place indicates that it'd be loud if it was at capacity, though.

Their wine list is very reasonable.  There aren't any very expensive bottles, which is nice, although I wonder if a bunch of their selections might be hangover precursors.  We went with the most expensive bottle on their list, and we both felt fine the next morning.

To start, we shared the conch fritters and the caesar salad.  The chef very kindly split the salad so that only I got the anchovies, since my husband hates seeing the little fish on his plate.  Both were good but not great.

For his entree, my husband got the Kobe culotte steak, which he thought was good but not great.  I thought the outside was a little too charred -- I tasted burnt fat, not carmelized meat.  I tried the monkfish (a daily special).  It was heavenly.  The fish was so mild and flaky.  The miso caramel was a perfect complement.  The garlicky mushrooms and salty sea beans were so addictive -- I would have happily eaten them for the whole meal).  The celeriac puree was unnecessary in my opinion but wasn't bad.

We didn't have room for dessert.  I wish we had, but we did order a lot of food.

We just love this place!  It's my number one Thai spot in Tampa!  Even my sister in law drives out from St Pete even just for take out!

We get thai about once a week and this is always at the top of our list - mostly take out these days, but whether we eat in or I meet a friend for lunch or get take out dinner, it's always consistent and authentic and hot!!!

They've redone their small front deck for nice outdoor seating and when it's not burning up outside, it's really nice.

Our typical order includes the thai island special (app), tum kha gai (soup), chicken and shrimp panang (best panang on the planet) and thai island fried rice (love the pineapples in it!).

Small quaint spot on Davis Islands, it's one of our top picks consistently!

Ok, when they first put in OCF coffeehouse 1.5 years ago, I said to myself "is that really a coffeehouse with the drab grey exterior paint and tinted windows?"  People want to see into a coffeehouse from the street, etc.

BUT, the OCF coffeehouse got pretty good reviews on Yelp so I thought I'd check it out since La Va was packed and I needed to do homework on Sunday.  

The Good:  Good working environment / pretty quiet / nice table areas / plugs, lots of coffee varieties and teas

The Bad:  The sandwiches are SUPER RIDICULOUSLY priced.  They served on a rectangle plate and more than half the plate is empty so the baristas make a fancy napkin take up the half!  $8.67 for a sandwich that is SMALLER than a blackboard eraser, and not served with chips nor pickle nor nothing!  What a rip-off!

I actually went up to the baristas after and was like "WTHeck, the sandwich was decent but for so much money to get nothing with it, that's a rip."  They said "oh, we totally agree....we just can't figure out how to get the sandwiches paired up with something and we've been through sooooo many sandwich makers, blah blah blah."

Really??  How about a bag of chips or SOMETHING!!?!?!?!?!  Ahhhhhhhh.  Not going back anytime soon!

Boo!!!!!!!!!!

Typically we've had pretty good meals here, but we had a few visits that were under par.

We had our rehearsal dinner here in the Normandie Room which has since been remodeled.  We had a reservation for 20 people and we had to wait.  We had this planned for quite a long time - you don't make a rehearsal dinner party wait.  They seated people in our area in the hopes of them leaving in time.

We had table decorations to put out and really didn't give us time to get our piece ready.

We've had hit or miss service, mostly good.
We've had pieces of plastic placed in one meal - I thought everything was fresh here but it seems that the one thing (they say) wasn't fresh was a crab sauce topping on top of a piece of fresh fish.  That was rather unappealing.

We got that dish free and a free shark fin which is a spectacular dessert!

When it's not what you expect when you pay their prices, it's disappointing.  But it's not off the list totally, but we're not here as often as we used to be.  When it's good we truly enjoy the ambiance and the food is generally very fresh and good.

Great made-from-scratch, home style food.   Friendly & attentive staff.  If you order an omelet, share it...they're huge!  I've only ever had breakfast here, but I'm sure I'll be trying lunch sometime soon.  It's a fairly quiet place, the exception being two servers singing "The Way You Do The Things You Do" (not loud, I was just close to them); I thought it was part of the floor show :)

Great food, great atmosphere, great service,  finally a place with great quality food in the Bay Area.
 i have seen a comment about the menu changing , well it is not like everything gets changed. there is also the seasons 52 which does well and change the menu 52 times a year. If you enjoy great cuisine and are not afraid to try new great dishes created by the chef then Try Edison ..you will not regret..make sure to get the coffee, it is a fun presentation.

Why 5 stars? Because you can't walk in here without saying "WOAH!"

   ---   ---   ---

What.... is this place? How did it get here? Does the British mob own it? Why is it the size of six restaurants? What's upstairs? How did they hire so many people, yet keep it a fairly unknown secret? Do the employees have their own Publix somewhere inside? Is there a state income tax here?

If you find yourself asking these questions, don't worry, these symptoms are normal. And if you're asking if the food matches the ambiance... the answer is YESSIRorma'am!

I had no idea what I was getting into, so my first visit found me arriving around 4:00. I was very much unaware that from 3-5pm, they have an abbreviated "Afternoon Tea Menu" which consists of small bites and tea only. My chicken curry sandwich was really tasty, but it was $8 and consisted of only four small bites. 

I quickly made a return trip for the regular lunch, and I had very high expectations for the food. I convinced myself that I would definitely be disappointed because the entire existence of this place seemed too good to be true.

I went with the steak sandwich and Ms. Shanna D grabbed the Club. This was the perfect storm, these two sandwiches were the top two of each of our wish-lists. That steak sandwich was serious business. I would go as far as to say it's the best I've ever had. The Club was phenomenal as well. 

On the side, the roasted sweet potato rounds were the very obvious choice...and WOW. Wow. I have nothing else to say -just order these and be thankful for Yelp. What a lunch!

So a strange moment was that when we walked in, we were told that it would be a 5-10 minute wait... which didn't make a lot of sense because about 60% of the tables were unoccupied. I chalk it up to the staff still working out the kinks of a restaurant that opened a week before my two visits. This minor inconvenience was completely forgotten when our food came no more than five minutes after we put our order in. It was shockingly quick.

Now one more thing: tea. I have never been a tea drinker, but Oxford may be converting me. I have had the coconut, the cherry coconut kiwi, and the banana. They were so perfect, they have me craving tea for the first time in my life!

To summarize, you absolutely must visit Oxford Exchange. Even if you are underwhelmed with the food/tea (but you won't be if you get the steak sammie), the ambiance is like NOTHING in Tampa Bay... so it's worth a trip for that alone. Megan W said it best, "we're not in Kansas anymore."

Yes, believe the hype.  This place is really cool.  As soon as I walked in, I was sold.  It's a book store, coffee shop, tea bar, restaurant, and shop with unique ... stuff.  I could spend all day there and be happy.  

Food was unique.  They do everything a little differently. The tea had a different flavor, not your typical coleslaw, arugula salad had truffle oil and shaved parmesan.  Only area for improvement was slow staff and/or kitchen thus the four vs. five stars.

The food was Great and the prices are so reasonable. Definitely eating from Pepos again. The pork was cooked well and had lots of flavor not like other places that don't use enough seasoning or too much. The empanadas are one of the best I have ever tasted.

3.5! Great meat selection. Servers go around with different cuts of meat and you have a red and green card that you flip as a meat signal for yes or no. Fresh salad bar with lots of choices. Great service, usually.
Chocolate German caramel cupcake I had was extremely swee, sunken, and not worth the price.
Mussels and fries are amazing! Duck salad is great! Excellent beer selection. I love monks.

For its price, Koizi is pretty good.  We got there about 6 p.m. on a Sunday and it was halfway crowded.  I started off by ordering a shrimp tempura roll and salmon sashimi.  Both were good.  I'm happy that they give you only 4 pieces of the roll so you can try more of them.  I also got a Alaskan roll and a Salmon Skin roll.  I didn't like the Salmon Skin roll.  However, I've never tried it before.  I was spurred to try it, since it was all you can eat.  That's what I like the most about this place, the ability to try so many different types of rolls.  A lot of their rolls have either spicy tuna or spicy mayonnaise, but there is still a good amount of variety

My boyfriend tried the hibachi and the show was fun to watch.  The fried rice was ok.  I've had better at other hibachi places, but it was still tasty.  

Later, at around 7:30 it took a long time to get a Philadelphia roll and another salmon sashimi.  When it finally arrived, the Philadelphia roll was warm and the salmon sashimi was partially frozen.  I think it was because they were getting really busy.

I would suggest going at lunch.  The price at dinner is about $17 on most nights, and on Sunday night, our bill came out to about $40.  At lunch it is $8.95 and although the menu is more limited, I think it is more worth it then.  I am less likely to come back at dinner.
My stylist Jenn couldn't be better. I honestly wouldn't go to anyone else for a cut and/or blow dry.

First of all, I love Vietnamese food.  I'm from Houston, Texas where there is a rather large Vietnamese population.  If this place was in Houston, I would probably give it 3 stars.  However, being in Tampa where there is not an abundance of Vietnamese restaurants, I was pretty happy with my meal.

I got the Broken Rice with Pork.  (Sorry this is not a Pho review).  It was a good portion size.  However, if you want a meal that will fill you up and leave you with more to take home, get something with Fried Rice.  My friend ended up taking home over half of her dish!  

The food was fairly priced (around $11 for the entire meal plus tip) and you get a good meal for your money.  They bring soup to the table that is pretty tasty.  I also got Thai Tea, which was a tad diluted with the milk for my taste, but still very sweet and I definitely drank it down quickly.

You know a Asian food place is pretty legitimate when it is filled with Asian Americans and this one was teeming with them :)

I'm excited to go back, since I haven't had a chance to try the Pho yet!

I was disappointed by this museum.  I went specifically for its One Hundred Years One Hundred Chairs exhibit.  Although I absolutely love the concept behind this exhibit, I was disappointed by the execution.  The display of chairs seemed to be bunched together all in one room.  There really isn't the space in this museum to spread out exhibits.  I would have appreciated more information on each of the chairs as well.  However, this exhibit was the highlight of the entire museum to me.  It still had some interesting information, I just wish there was more of it.

As for the rest of the museum, I found it lacking substance.  It is very small, much smaller than I thought it would be.  I didn't find it that memorable.  After the chair exhibit, nothing else really caught my attention.  I agree with other reviews, I would not pay general admission to go to this museum.

I always enjoy Cheesecake Factories.  You get a lot of food for the price.  I like to joke that the Cheesecake Factory is the epitome of American food, big portions!  Like other reviewers say, it gets crazy crowded at night.  I was there on a Friday night with a group of about 10 and our first person got there an hour early to make sure we could get a table.  I don't think it is worth that wait, so I would suggest going at a time that is less crowded.

I love the bread that comes to the table.  I'm one of those diners that always manages to ruin his/her appetite by snacking on the bread.  Although I'm not a big fan of sour dough, I like the other bread that they serve.

I've gone to the Cheesecake Factory in Sugar Land, Texas a lot, so I can say with confidence that the cheesecake here is probably just as amazing as in Texas.  It is the variety that is so amazing.  At this Cheesecake Factory, I had a spinach and cheese omelet (yes I know it was dinner, but I'm a huge fan of breakfast for dinner).  It was delicious, fluffy, and of course, huge.  I'm sure I'll be back.

As you can see from my profile picture, my boyfriend and I are wearing Buccs gear so there is no way I would rate this less than five stars.  I went to University of Michigan, which means the Big House, which means I have seen great stadiums.  However, this one ranks up there with the best of them.

This stadium is amazing.  I have friends in Michigan who know nothing about Tampa, but know everything about how awesome the stadium is.  The huge pirate ship is great and it scares the bejeezus out of me every time it fires the cannons.  

The bathrooms are surprisingly clean for a football stadium.  My only complaint would be the lack of food options.  It has the standard hotdogs, hamburgers, popcorn, ice cream, etc.  It even has corn on the cob, but I would still like more variety.  However, honestly, what stadium cares that much about the food.

This is a great place to enjoy a football game.  This is a mustsee for people visiting the Tampa area during football season.

I went on a Tuesday night and was happily surprised to learn that there are $2 hand rolls on Tuesdays!  I got a salmon hand roll and a snow crab hand roll.  Honestly, when I ordered the snow crab I thought it would be lightly fried.  It was shredded snow crab, which was still good, just not what I expected.  In the salmon hand roll there was a good size chunk salmon.  It was delicious.  They also had half off wine during happy hour (which I think is before 6:30 p.m.).  I got there at about 6:45 p.m. and still got half off the house wine, so I might be wrong.  All in all, it was a great, light meal for less than $10!  I would suggest this place for a nice date, or a classy night out with friends.

Like the other reviewer says, this is a great starter apartment.  It is pretty cheap for being pretty safe.  The gate is broken, but there is a security guard who stands watch starting at about 10 p.m.  

Pros: Cost, they have studios, which not many apartment complex have.  Their 1bd/bth is nicely priced too. Nice pool.  There are 3 pools.  However, I would only classify one of them as actually nice.  One of the pools is by the leasing office and looks out onto the water.  It is a great place to relax and hang out.  The other two pools are ok, but not as great.  One of them is easily accessed by anyone and at times I think people who do not even live in the complex swim there.  I have not swam at the pool by the gym, but just by glancing inside it looks like a perfectly fine pool, but nothing special.  At the nice pool by the water there is a small bar b q that works well as long as you don't have too high expectations.

Cons: Gym.  It is pretty small with basically no free weights.  There are only two treadmills and two ellipticals (although one of the ellipticals is broken).  There are only a couple weight machine.  (On a side note, I once left an ipod touch in the gym and it was never turned in).  Pets.  There are a decent number of pets in this apartment and if you get unlucky you could end up in an apartment next to a barking dog.  Neighbors, although for the most part you will never talk/see your neighbors, some of the residents you do see seem kind of trashy.  This is expected for the price and most people are perfectly fine.  Kitchen, the kitchen is small.  Do not expect to be a great chef in this apartment.  There is basically no counter space.

Overall, I would suggest renting here if you are starting out your life or need a reasonably priced apartment for a period of time.
My favorite is the tacos and sopes!! Definitely a southern mexico style!!!

International Plaza then Citrus Park for my top two mall choices. Crowd wise, Citrus Park is much better! I can wander this mall for hours and amuse myself. Some great stores (as noted by my Yelp colleagues) and eateries where I can eat healthy make for a fun weekday afternoon! It's also in an area where I can hit some strip malls close by as well.

I always have a great time here just a good ole fashioned pub. Nice selections of beer on draught cheap shots on Jaeger Bombs. Have a fun karaoke when it's going. The bartenders are great especially Simone.
Went for lunch. Great lunch specials and prices. Volcano roll is fantastic. Attentive service. Nice decor. One of the best sushi places in the city.
Been here ton of times. Chicken saag is amazing!!! Order online and get 10% off. Chicken tandoori flavors are good though a bit dry. Love this place

Had a special noodle dish, I think it was called late night noodles. Chicken shrimp carrots shallot sauce. Red Curry is very good as well, but not much chicken. LOTS of bamboo shoots. Delicious! A bit pricy, but Would come back to try some other dishes.

Went for restaurant week. Amazing filet mignon. Great corn chowder. Rich chocolate mousse. Delicious crab gnocchi and lobster Mac and cheese. Overall very delicious, great great service, and amazing decor.
Pack classes so it doesn't offer the best attention. Enthusiastic teachers though. Good Yoga studio

Went here a few weeks after it opened, just getting to review now. Although then they were working out a few kinks, overall this is a very solid addition to the Tampa restaurant scene. 

Standouts were the potato-crusted oysters, sous vide chicken, and the side of brussels sprouts (really! try them!!). We did order the foie gras tacos but I found them disappointing, too many competing flavors. Waiter was annoying but knowledgable. 

Definitely worth a try, and a cool, urban space. Could turn into a nice bar scene.

No no no no. This is one of those times when Yelp has failed me. Either the reviews below are planted by people who know the owner of this place, or these reviewers don't know good Thai or good service. 

I went here on a weeknight. The waiter looked to be bothered by the fact that he had to take our order. He also wasn't interested in explaining menu items. This is despite the fact the place was empty. We ordered two soups and two entrees based on Yelp recommendations. The soups were tiny, much smaller portions than any other Thai place in Tampa Bay does for the same soups. The soups themselves were super salty and unremarkable. The entrees were also small portions and the quality overall was ok. One was a stir fry, the other panang curry. The stir fry was better than the panang. The panang was horrible. Sickly sweet and barely had any chicken. It was a gloppy sweet mess. If you have ever had a Panang Curry in Thailand, this is NOT what a Panang curry is supposed to taste like. It's never thick, super sweet, full of nuts, etc. It's supposed to be a thin, light-red curry sauce. Not brown (like at Thai Island), not white (like at many other Tampa Thai places), but light-red. 

In addition, the mark of a good Thai place is whether their Tom Yum Goong (soup) is good. The tom yum at this place is horrible.

Then we had to ask for our bill three times. When that didn't work, I had to get up and ask for it in person. I couldn't wait to get out of there. 

I would rather buy a frozen pad Thai at publix than eat here again.

Looking in from the outside you'd never know what to expect when you walk into the Oxford Exchange. It reminds me of a library mixed with a fancy Barnes and Noble, but to the nth power. When you walk inside you first enter an area with books and beautiful decor that makes you feel like you are in Hemingway's house.
 As you venture inside of the space, you see that they have a Buddy Brew Coffee stand along with Te Bella, if tea is more your thing (like me.) 
The prices there are very decent, in the $ range depending on what you order. Instead of just having a beverage when we came in we decided to stay for brunch when we noticed there was a large restaurant space in the back. We sat down to a table with a very classy-industrial look and I knew we were in for a treat. The brunch menu was very pliable with prices in the $-$$ range: I have their ham/spinach/swiss omelet. My favorite part when it came out was the side of grapefruit and orange with these large coffee cake crumbles on tip - super unique. If there was a 'down side' to the experience, it was the server that we had who said "Well, I'm at work" when we asked how he was doing - he seemed off for the day so I didn't want to base my experience off of one person's demeanor. When the food came I noticed that there was white bread with the omelet and when we asked the server he said they did have wheat, but that we had to ask for it - in my opinion we should have been offered the choice. 
On the way out I noticed the outside patio area that had beautiful open lighting in the ceiling and orchids and fountains galore. In the bathrooms there was even a glass ceiling that impressed me, opening up the space unlike most public restrooms.
On the way out I stopped for a chai latte that was delightful - instead of being from a mix, Te Bella steeps real chai and the sweetness is by no means too much like other chais I've had.
I definitely want to come back to the Oxford Exchange for other meals and to just sit and enjoy the space. There was a large staircase in the middle area that had old photos leading up to another area...not sure what's up there, but I might explore next time ;)

The food was horrible the service was bad the manager does not respect her customers and the food quality was horrific. Worst place ever.we will never ever waste our money there again DoNot go you will be so dissapointed.way better places in town.

Here is my review in short:

Pretty friendly employees.  Nice, helpful.  Can't complain about them (unlike their Little Baby neighboor).

Meh, on the pizza.  I think I had "the Jane" The crust was nice - crispy and chewy at the same time, the cheese ratio (to sauce) was a bit cheese heavy for my personal liking.  It was really just a decent slice with a few small basil leaves tossed on top.  While I wont go out of my way to go back, I wont decline if someone else whats to go.  

While the pizza decor is cute, there is not as much of it as I thought there would be.  Dont get me wrong, there is a good amount of it, but I just expected it to be overwhelming.  Also, there was not a single story attached to the items - something that would be cool to add.  

Go and try it for yourself, I was not wowed but you might be. . .

Do you measure a pizza's greatness by the number of bubbles and the quality of the crust and sauce? Because if you do, you will love Pizza Brain. We took a road-trip out to Fishtown this weekend with a couple of friends who wanted to try Little Babies Ice Cream. A co-worker had told me that if we were getting the veg-friendly ice cream, we absolutely had to try the pizza, so we turned our outing into a lunch and dessert combo. It's very easy to do this at the two combined storefronts.

First, we walked into Pizza Brain and asked for a suggestion about how many pies we should order because my husband and I are vegans and our friends are not. We ended up ordering two pies, one vegan one with cheese. My husband and I split the the veggie delight which was recommended by one of the pizza cooks. They start with their delightfully thin crust, add on just the right amount of their tasty tomato sauce, then add mushrooms, artichokes and sun-dried tomatoes. When it comes out of the oven, they pile on arugula and a delightful balsamic dressing. The meal became one of my favorite food hybrids: salad-pizza. The flavor of the pie was fabulous and the crust had a bubble at almost every slice. The pie was only marred by the fact that they burnt the heck out the crust. The bottom of our veg-friendly pie was basically black. Our friends' pie was not so over-done looking. Next time I go, I'll be sure to ask them to not to make it so well done. We will definitely be back.

Been back to my favorite place and the ribs were raw? I was shocked! They try to prepare them half way and then let the heat lamp do the rest according to the waitress there. I was shocked =-) I hope they go back to finishing the ribs the old way. I won't hold my breath though =-(

I live not too far from Bliss and walk by it often.  It is a simple place with a nice interior in a neighborhood that has a lot of variety.  It doesn't stand out but seems to have a pleasant atmosphere in the nicer weather when the windows/doors can be open to Broad St.

I took advantage of the LivingSocial voucher as well given that it was nearby.  I thought their menu looked great and had a tough time deciding exactly what I wanted.  I ended up with the poached pear salad which was very nice.  My mother had their soup of the day which was a ridiculously good, and yet seemingly healthy, cream of asparagus soup.  For my main meal I had a salmon sandwich and it was excellent.  The salmon was juicy and flavorful.  

I wish the atmosphere in this place was a bit more lively.  I think the food could definitely sustain this place but without much in the way of customers, I'm not sure how successful this restaurant will continue to be.

Michas is another Tucson oldie that has stood the test of time, 25 years to be exact! I walked in for an early lunch at 11 and the place was already filling up. Known for it's fast service it's definitely a convenient lunch spot for those who work in South Tucson. Full disclosure: Sonoran style Mexican food is not my favorite and I'm from here! And I prefer to eat it at home. So when I venture out, my go to dishes are usually fajitas, tacos, guacamole, albondiga soup and breakfast burrito. That being said, when I go out for Mexican food, my rating system weighs heavily on the quality of the salsa, the cut of the meat, what type of cheese is used, tortilla quality and salt content. This may seem unfair, but great quality ingredients for great Mexican food are basically at our fingertips in Tucson, so I expect Mexican restaurants to serve up the best! I ordered the Fajitas on this visit and overall they were tasty but not served as they traditionally are on smoking hot skillet. It was more like a chicken stir-fry with sauteed onions and bells, re-fried beans and rice. My side of corn tortillas was disappointing. They were dry and cracked. The salsa was underwhelming. Usually, I  have little to no self control when it comes to chips and salsa. In this case, one bite was plenty.

I have been eating their burgers weekly for months, they come to our office park weekly and the day they come has become my favorite day of the week. Burgers are cooked and seasoned perfectly,juicy and have a wide variety of toppings. Fries are crisp on the outside creamy inside and have just the right amount of their own seasoning mix. Love it

I've used Uber 3 times so far, and each was flawless. the first time my boyfriend and I were attending a wedding across town and I figured that would be the perfect time to give Uber a try. I clicked on the app, requested a car to my location and within 6 minutes I received a text saying that the car was downstairs. We head down and a black Lincoln Town Car is waiting and the driver has the door open for me. What luxury! 

I have to say...it's WAY easier than calling for a cab- which as many people know the wait time is usually "45-70 min. We'll get there when we can. Be patient." With Uber the most I have waited, 1:00 am on a Saturday, was 7 minutes! It's incredible. I couldn't recommend it more.

I went back for lunch on a Saturday and was disappointed.  It took forever to get the rolls and the hibachi servings were small (which was understandable, but they need to serve it on smaller plates so it does not look so sad).  We also tried the Mongolian Beef and we had about 4 pieces of beef in a bed of onions and green onions.  The beef was delicious, but there needed to be more pieces!  When the sushi did come out, a lot of times it was slightly warm.  I would also warn you to stay away from the tempura.  The outside reminded me of a breaded deep fried hush puppy, not a tempura sushi roll.  The price has been raised to 9.95.  It is still a good deal for the price, but I cannot see myself going back, unless I'm really craving a variety of sushi for a cheap price.

Absolutely delicious no frills down and dirty old skool goodness.
If your not into eating in the hood stay home cause you will just be worried and complain too much.

Called because they're open late after a night class and placed an order: was first told there were no discounts, then only students received 15% discount but was charged full price by the quick lipped phone attendant... When I asked for a refund and a manager - none were working and then she hung the phone up on me. If you want poor service for food that is not really worth the cost, this is the right place to call!

Uber is one of the well known secrets to the tech crowd for getting out and about in Philadelphia. (and many other cities as well)

As a center city resident sometimes walking or driving doesn't make sense ans we all have had horrible experiences with the local taxi cab companies:
1. Getting through via phone to order a cab
2. Waiting for the cab to actually arrive
3. The constant struggle to pay with a credit card
4. The smell and attitude of the cabbie

All 4 of those issues have been resolved with UBER.
Here is how it works.
1 Open the Android/iOS UBER app
2 click your spot on the map
3 order either a SUV or a CAR
4. The app notifies you immediately the driver name, cell number, and how long arrival will be
5 A notification comes through when the driver is 2 mins away and then again when the driver has arrived
6. A well dressed driver in a brand new lincoln, tahoe, cadillac or comparable vehicle  get out, greets you by name, and opens the door
7. The driver takes you to the location.
8 you hop out without opening your wallet
9 A receipt instantly comes via email to your phone with price, length of time and mileage from you ride and you can rate the driver

A private car service at 1.5x the price iof a cab. What a difference from the angry stinking, dirty old cab!

So I've used the service a few more times (I really do love the convenience) and I've learned a few things.

1.) If you order a town car and they send an SUV instead, you don't get charged for the larger vehicle (+1 for them)
2.) If you don't tell them EXACTLY how to get somewhere they will more than likely take a convoluted route or one that is gridlocked with traffic. For example, I took the car from the same pick up location to the same destination twice- the first time it was $18, the second was $28! The second trip the driver was talking a lot, and that was fine, but I felt bad interrupting him to give him better directions. So we drove and took the longest route ever. I contacted the customer service rep and they credited my account the difference because I complained about the huge difference. The next time I was picked up and took the same trip home (one that should cost me $18) and the driver missed his turn and didn't really drive like a regular cab driver- it was $23...so that slow driver and missed turn cost me another $5. So now I know I have to be explicit with my directions and not feel bad about telling the drivers exactly the route I would like them to take- or else I'm paying the difference. 

This is something to keep in mind. Uber is MUCH more convenient than a cab but WAY more pricey- especially if they're not sure of where they're going.
So nice to have this in my building in Channelside. I really love their downtown location and now just need to walk downstairs for my fix.

The restaurant has a nice ambiance. The sushi was no good though. The heart of the sushi is the rice. I has to have sticky, yet firm and dry. There should be a little bit of sweetness coming from the right balance of sugar and rice vinegar (or sake, if not both). The rice at Koizy was flavorless, had grains coming off the sushi/roll, and in some occassion it came dripping with some liquid (from the non-rice ingredients, I presume). For being a Friday night and having 3 sushi chefs on the floor, Koizi should have produced better sushi. I'm sorry, but it was simply terrible the night I went. The three people on my party got stomack sick later that night.

I'm very glad this place opened in channelside, the rolls and salads are fantastic still have a few things to try here. I hope they expand their menu in the future and add some of the dishes that the downtown restaurant offers...friendly staff and quiet atmosphere.

Went to Le Rendez-Vous to celebrate our 33rd Anniversary.  The chef answered the phone at the restaurant and took our reservation.  Very friendly and informative about the menu and specials.  We arrived 1/2 hour early, and although the place was packed, we were seated promptly (even though no one greeted us at the door upon our arrival... it took several minutes before our presence was acknowledged).  
  We sat for several minutes before someone brought menus to our table.  We tried to get a copy of the "small menu" attachment, but no luck.  The guests at the table next to us were very unhappy with the service, and waited a long time for their dessert, and later they were growing impatient when it took over a 1/2 hour for them to get their check (they actually had to get up to chase down a server to get their bill).  Initial table side greeting by our server was friendly, but during the evening she got lost or forgot us.  The gentleman who actually served our food was not friendly and actually threw the plates of food at us before running off to see if we needed any thing else or had a question.  Very unclassy service.  
  We ordered an order of Escargots au Chablis for our Hors D'Oeuvres (internet menu price $12.95, actual menu price was only $8.00), but VERY SMALL snails in a hot tasty melted garlic butter and chablis (I've had better)... but could not find our server or busser to bring us more French bread to dip in the garlic butter.  We sat such a long time waiting for our food to start arriving that we ran out of bread and butter (and water), and could not find anyone to provide service until I literally yelled across the crowded dining room to a busser to please refill out water glasses. 
  We also ordered a Soupe a l'Onion Gratinee, which came in a tiny cup (not a bowl).  The baked French onion soup was surprisingly the worst onion soup we ever had... all bread covered with cheese, but very little broth and onions... more mushy soaked bread with cheese rather than soup.
  For our main entree we ordered the Chateau Briand Bearnaise Sauce for 2 ($65.00).  The meat was delicious, cooked to our satisfaction, a generous portion, and excellent bearnaise sauce (we could of used more sauce, but our server never returned for us to ask for more).  Our server told us it was served with asparagus, which we never received.  Instead we got a skimpy helping of grilled zucchini chunks and a very skimpy serving of a few slices of potatoes.  For the price of entrees, you would think a small side salad would be included (not a la carte).  
  During dinner we had to chase down our server for an extra soup spoon (finally brought us a teaspoon when we were done with the "soup"), constantly looking to get our water glasses refilled (server never refilled out water, even after our request... said she would send one of the two bussers to the table with the water pitcher, and we waited and waited), never asked if we needed more bread or sauce or other drinks (wine or ice tea or other) once our meal was tossed at us by the server guy.  Dessert was the chocolate cake... my wife liked it more than me... served cold.  
  NEVER did anyone acknowledge our anniversary, although I mentioned it when I made the reservation for dinner and when the waitress first greeted us at our table... nothing, zip, nada.  Did not make my wife/us feel special. 
  For a Sunday night the restaurant was packed, extremely busy, but for a total tab of $109.00 for dinner (no alcoholic or other beverages included), the staff could of made the evening seem more special or romantic... overall we gave this restaurant 4 stars for the taste of the food, 2 stars for value, and 1 star for terrible service.

I stopped past here a few weeks ago to swap out the pedals on my bike.  The guys were VERY helpful assisting me with my purchase...Now not only did they change my pedals but the tuned my bike up for free...WOOOHOO....I would go back there for all my bike needs :)  Thanks again!!!

I have to agree with previous reviewers - the JCC is amazing! I love how much they have to offer:  from cardio equipment to weights, classes, etc. They have childcare available (both as "babysitter" and as full daycare) and offer so many activities:  lectures, rotating artwork collections, sculpture garden, etc etc etc. So much to do! I almost *almost* wanted to give 4 stars just because the price (a bit pricey), but considering all that you get, I had to give 'em the whole 5 stars

I had a bad experience with the service manager in service.  This was a few years ago.  He was so rude that I called the Toyota corporation.  He called me a liar and told me I had a lot of leaks when it was only one that was not fixed under warrenty and then they wanted 300 dollars to fix the same leak that was not fixed properly.  The service manager talked to me like I was his xwife or someone.  I have never had anyone be so rude.

Why 4 stars? Because this reminds me of NYC and SF.

    ---   ---   ---

You'd never know you weren't in a big-city China Town when stumbling in here. 

Everything costs virtually no money.

Everything is very basic, but oh so delicious.

And the bonus is the opportunity to feed bread to ducks and cats behind the restaurant. I was promised that they would fight each other over it, but the lack of this ended up being the only disappointment of my day.

I was a little wary to try a new sushi place because I am typically very loyal to another local sushi restaurant.  I was very pleasantly surprised by Matoi Sushi.  If you like spicy, this place is for you as I noticed that the spice level was higher than a lot of other sushi places.  The night that we were there was 30% off wine bottle night.  At $22 for a bottle of pinot grigio (before the discount), it ended up being a great value!  I will definitely be back.

Delicious cheese with a great variety. Friendly service as well! 

They had Brillat Savarin which I was stoked about. Delicious triple creme Brie at its best.
a good place for meeting new people great for the kids specially because of the water fountain the views are great
Great place with great assortment of shellac colors. Got a relaxing manicure and pedicure with a friend. Great extra massage and friendly ladies. Was a little pricey ($70) so only reason not 5 stars.

This place helped with my chronic sushi craving.

One problem that I face is that my husband does not eat sushi.  Solution: find a Korean restaurant, since he loves BBQed meats.  

So while my husband tucked into Bul Go Gi, I had salmon sushi, salmon roe sushi, scallop sushi and a Mexican roll.  Everything was good.  They could have been more generous with the amount of fish, but everything tasted fresh.  The scallop was particularly tasty.  I was a bit puzzled by the avocado in the salmon roe nigiri, but it certainly wasn't offensive. 

My husband thought the Korean food was good.  There wasn't a ton of banchan, but when the server noticed that we ate up all the green beans quickly, he brought us another small bowl.

The sake menu is limited.  I don't think anything was more than $15/bottle.  When you think about how much they charge for their sushi, you'd think they'd have a couple more upscale sake options, but whatever.

This place didn't serve me the best sushi or Korean food I've ever had, but it was solid, and it was excellent compared to most of the other sushi places I've tried in Tampa.

My review is more for the service here than anything. The server's are not attentive at all. Last time I was in my table was completely forgotten to the point we were about to walk out. When the food did come the orders were wrong and some food was cold.

One of my very favorite restaurant in Tampa.  I have only been here a handful of times but look forward to going back.  The family owned ambience is great in here.  The staff is nice and friendly.  Most importantly the food is DELICIOUS and affordable.

My personal favorite is the gorgonzola crusted steaks.  They also offer escargot as an appetizer, which is really good too.  If you are looking for a good date night spot, Iavarone's is your place.

Great spot for karaoke!  I have been here several times and every time I have gone in, it has been a different crowd.  I like this place because the prices are really good and it is stumblng distance from many other Ybor places.  This is a great place if you are looking to go be ridiculous with friends.

I love me some Ella's.  My biggest recommendation is to check it out on a Sunday.  During the week, Ella's still has some great things on the menu.  I love their pizzas and salads and they frequently have really good food specials.  Their sangria is really good and the beer menu frequently changes.

Sunday, though, is where its at.  The sides alone are enough to draw me in:  friend green tomatoes, okra, baked beans, sweet potato fries.  The bbq meats are really tasty and, although I haven't ordered it, the chicken and waffles looks pretty darn good.

The atmosphere is great here with a real Seminole Heights feel to it.  Great music a few times a week too!

One of the last times I was at the Mermaid Tavern, I looked around and thought to myself "How random.. I'm in a bar that is playing the movie Roller Boogie to disco music..and I'm playing Yahtzee."  And that pretty much sums up all of my experiences at "the Merms" as my friends and I call it.  Ever since the first time I stepped foot in there, I have had a blast every time I've gone.  I love that you have the option of sitting outside or in the nautical themed inside.  I love that they have Jenga and other old school board games.  I love how nice the staff has always been to me.  Honestly, if you haven't been - check it out.  It is sure to be a fun time!

I have tried and tried to like this place, and in the end, I just don't.  My fiance' LOVES Three Coins and always suggests it for breakfast.  I've just really never had anything that I have liked a whole lot.  And I've had hair in my food, more than once.  I don't usually consider myself a picky eater...but I'm just not into this place.

Reliable, good mexican food.  I really only like the location on 56th street but their food does not disappoint.  The salsa is really fresh and the margaritas pack a punch.  I got the Enchiladas Supremes last time I was there - the cheese enchilada was delish.  Never had anything that I didn't like here, though.

This is definitely a staple in Americanized Chinese cuisine.  I would be hard pressed, however, to recommend any of their specialty cocktails as they seem sloppily put together.  The main entrees I have ordered on my visits have always been good and tasty.  The Kung Pao Chicken and Chicken with Black Bean sauce are very good.

The ambiance and decor of this place is very authentic and always up beat.  It's a great place to meet up for happy hour or a drink with someone.  The specialty cocktails and beers are plentiful.  I have eaten here a few times and every time I look at the menu I wish they had more variety.  The service has been hit or miss depending on who's section you end up in.

I was quite intrigued the first time I came to Ciro's and had to use a password to get in.  My friends and I were just looking for a casual place to have a drink or two and we were not disappointed.  The bartenders have drinkmaking down to a science and put on a spectacular show conjuring up the innovative and tasty drinks they have here.  

My first experience led me to have a birthday get together here and although I was put off by their hesitation to accomodate my group of 13, they made it a great event once we got there.  Our server was very attentive and actually got us the private area to make sure everyone was comfortable and we would have an area to enjoy ourselves.

Wait.  Family style chinese food?  BYOB?  Lazy Susans?  SIGN ME UP!

This place is great.  When you walk in, you will see several large groups sharing food and drinks and just having an all around great time.  The food is to die for, too!  Get the calamari as an appetizer, it is award winning for a reason.  My favorite main dish is the kung pao chicken - really spicy due to the peppers and oh so good!  The servings are very generous too.  I've been to the newer location on Hillsborough, but its a bit too fancy for me.  Traditional Yummy House is how I roll.

Meh.  I feel like I SHOULD like this place.  I mean, it has all the elements of things I like - healthy food, atmosphere on the water, large drink menu, live music.  I just have never really been that impressed with it.  I've had to wait a really long time for tables and food before and I've never been blown away by the food.  Good place for afternoon drinks but not somewhere I go out of my way to have dinner at.

Great bar in the Channelside district. This is a very dog friendly place and since most people in the are have dogs it seems like a perfect fit. During the week it's a nice comfortable quiet place for happy hour as it comes alive during the weekends.....

I don't know why it took me so long to get over to the Oxford Exchange. I freaking work right across the street. But I am so glad I did! The entire place is just so ... cute. Quaint. Lovely. Makes you feel sophisticated. Bookstore, coffee shop, tea shop, boutique, and restaurant all in one location, plus meeting rooms upstairs. It's not your modern architecture, either. You're almost transported to a different area, like nothing else in Tampa.

I do have some qualms about it, though. My coworkers and I ate at the restaurant, where I found the prices to be a little over-the-top. Usually, I can look past that if the food matches up. Unfortunately, it fell a little short, in my opinion. I ordered the chicken salad sandwich with fries. Good sandwich, but nothing extraordinary about it. The fries weren't special either.

BUT they have an extensive list of various teas, which I adore. AND what gets me even more excited is that they have a 3pm afternoon tea time! Too bad I can't just take a 3 o'clock break from work to do tea time. But I WILL make it there some day.

I probably won't ever buy a book from their bookstore or a little trinket from their boutique just because of the price, but it really is a beautiful place and environment. Grab a book, buy some tea, and just snuggle up on one of the couches and enjoy the afternoon.

To say I'm a huge fan of Ciccio's would be an understatement. I could eat there everyday and never tire of it. They have a little bit of everything and you'd be hard pressed to not find something you like to eat. The wraps are divine and the portions are ample. The service is always fast and friendly and for some reason, everyone who works there is really, really, ridiculously good looking. Makes the food taste even better. Ha!

I finally got around to going to this place and it was no easy feat b/c I had to make a reservation for lunch. 

It's basically everything that everyone else has said in their reviews. Stunning interior with industrial fixtures and a book store and gift shop, you could definitely be in an Oxford library. 

The food is nothing extraordinary and the service was a little on the slow side. I'm willing to give them some leeway here because the place is just so popular right now. As for the prices, they are standard for the area and exactly what I would have expected for a place like this. I paid $10 for a club sandwich and fries. 

I would definitely go back for coffee or tea and just hang out in the coffee bar outside of the restaurant. Buddy Brew coffee is served there since it share the same owner as the tiny little coffee spot of the same name on Kennedy. 

Bottom line: Go for coffee, people watching, and pretending you're a college grad with a paper due in the morning.

Way to go Mise En Place.  You should know how to ruin an event!   

Cool backdrop -  Great theme (Beaujolais Nouveau-- bottomless glass) - 
Awesome exhibit
Well attended event-- 200.
Cost  -- 55 to 75 depending on the membership level.  All checks made out to the MUSEUM OF TAMPA.

Catering by Mise en Place -- Big fat ZERO.    
o yes, the cassoulet (beans to you ) was supposedly excellent.  Not really.  Bland and tasteless said many.  

The "passed hors d'oeuvres"  were ridiculously unimaginative.   Thin slices of quartered apples with a drop of something... and not caviar people. 
Tiny puffed pastries with salmon pate.   Pate on tiny crackers.  AND SO FEW OF THEM!  

While the servers were pleasant, I would never recommend another event to be serviced by this restaurant.    For  65 a person,  i would expect more, as did the majority of the attendees.   People left to go to dinner somewhere.  Couples were livid.   130 should buy more than 10 dollars' worth of chits.  

No worries Mise en Place, the word was out.  Friday  night you lost a lot of potential business   and your reputation took a dive.   
Two of the attendees were there to explore the site for a future wedding and were the only ones i encountered who considered themselves lucky--   thought they paid a very small price to avoid a very big mistake.

People, there are many caterers in this town who do not coast on a reputation....  They really try.  And once in a while, they mess up.... They might misjudge and run out.   This was not the case.    This event had everything to be a success.   Never ran out of wine.     The caterer sucked.  Seems everyone was  talking about it.

Solid BBQ with great sides.  Now if only they served beer.

The best thing about this place is the hush puppies.   They are perfectly crispy with flavored.  I could eat a ton, who needs the rest of the meal?

The ribs don't fall off the bone, but they are soft and perfectly seasoned.  I don't think they need the BBQ sauce (I do love that the BBQ is warmed before they bring it to the table in its bottle).    The pulled pork is fine but not that remarkable.  I found that it needed some hot sauce.

For sides, we tried the green beans (so good, flavorful and whole not the usual soggy mess of reconstituted frozen beans served by BBQ place), the fries (perfectly crisp) and the mac n cheese (the pasta was overcooked -- that was expected, but the sauce was cheesy and rich).

Jimbo's is pretty good for BBQ, but they really know their way around a deep fryer.

Beef O'brady's is not my favorite place in the world, but it definitely is a great game watching atmosphere.  If you like buffalo chicken anything - their buffalo sauce is really good.  Their curly fries are also delicioso.  The parking situation is a little dicey and you may have to park ont he street, but you will be able to find a spot.  Overall - a pretty good spot with cheap beer and decent food.

This is a great place to go if you're looking for a low-key place in Ybor to just hang out and people watch.  Be ready to experience a vast array of karaoker's if you go on a Friday night, which could be a good or bad thing depending on the talent.  It takes a while for bartenders to notice you at the bar and also for the lone server to make their way around the entire establishment to take drink orders. One last warning for all the non-smokers, be ready to reek of cigarette/cigar smoke once you leave this place.

I recently stayed at this hotel due to a work event that was being hosted here.  The block reservation allowed for a cheap rate and free valet upon our stay.
