The check in process could have run a bit smoother as they only had one clerk on duty helping customers on a Saturday afternoon when I was checking in.  The room was very clean and had a great view of downtown Tampa.  My first impression, however, of the room was that it was very small compared to other hotels I've stayed in before. Overall, our stay at this hotel was pretty standard and the speed of checkout and valet delivery of the car was great.  

 I'll include some information on the event I attended as well if anyone is looking for ratings of their banquet service.  Overall, the staff and location of our event was great.  The outdoor bars afforded a great view of the Hillsborough River and the changing lights of the Kennedy Blvd. bridge with the University of Tampa backdrop was amazing. The food was pretty standard but the desserts were definitely something to look forward to.

If you're looking for a quiet place to get away for a classic Mediterranean meal, this is not the place.  There's plenty of noise whether it be from the dancing wait staff, the throwing of napkins, or the breaking of plates.  To fill the rest of the time there is a 2 man band playing non-stop at blaring volume.  That makes it very hard to hold a conversation or hear what anyone at your table may be saying.

Moving onto the food.  If you're lookin for some authentic Greek cuisine, this is a very good place to find it.  The Hummus which is served with softy pita triangles is amazing and very fresh.  The Gyro Platter offers up a ton of food and will not leave you hungry.  On a different note, pass on the Sangria.  Either version they serve is not all they make it out to be.

I am at a point where I want to try new places.  Some chicas and I decided to give this place a try which I found on Yelp.  It is a small quaint place on W Davis Islands and the parking is limited so be prepared to park a bit down and walk but on a nice breezy evening, who cares?  Simple ambiance which to me seems more casual than trendy, upscale but you could definitely make it a date night where.  The price is about the same as any thai place and the portions are a good size.  Food was delicious and I must recommend the thai iced team and the thai calamari.  The owner and wait staff were very nice to my friends and I even when one was not happy with her food choice, they gave her something else no charge.  I would definitely come back to this place and it is cozy for just friends or your partners.  Must visit!
Love the wide selection of films.  Not a fan of the staff, they often seem unhappy :-(

I don't have a lot to say about this place the service was the best thing about it. 

Baby back ribs have more bone than meat. Sauces taste like they came from a can.

I liked this hotel a lot.  When it comes to hotels, I am pretty easy to please.  Have a comfortable bed and be clean and I am usually a happy girl.  What is so nice about this hotel is its location.  It is walking distance to everything in downtown Tampa.  My job had a work event in the ballroom, which was also very nice.  The service staff at the event were really nice and the food was great!

As for the room, we had two big beds and plenty of room.  There was an IPOD dock on the alarm clock, which was awesome.  All in all I really enjoyed my stay at this hotel and would stay here again!

Came here for lunch on a weekend and the place was very busy... The service was great but because of a very large bunch of students eating there as well, the food came out very slow... This place is different from other buffets since you order from a menu ahead of time and then re-order as needed.  It can sound weird to order 2-4 orders of one particular type of roll, but it only comes in 2-4 pieces per order, not like a standard order where there are 8-10 pieces per roll.  I thought the food was pretty good and I would have to come back another time to see if the service is better.  If the service is slow again, I'd drop the stars to 2 or 3, I'll give them the benefit of the doubt it was just a bad time....

Omfg well this movie theater is pretty cool its attached to the mall upstairs but, this review is more for the movie I went to see. My favorite movie of all time TWILIGHT BREAKING DAWN PART 2! I had a blast. We walk in and im like theres no one here to watch it until we hit the corner and see a line that crew out of control after a few mins. The movie theater is cool and probably the only one ill go to. Oh and TWILIGHT WAS GREAT! Until next time adios

We're not really fans of chain restaurants, but Mitchell's is definitely one of the better ones.  This was the first time we've had dinner here, but we would definitely go back.  Good food and good service.

I came here with two girl friends for brunch.  I loved the "maxmosas," huge margarita glasses full of fresh orange juice and a whole split of sparkling wine (they leave the upended bottle in for show).  Portion sizes were great.  I had the breakfast burrito with lots of egg and avocado.  No, the chorizo wasn't especially authentic, but it was tasty and not very greasy.

The staff was very patient with us (we stayed way longer than we deserved to hold up the table), and they even brought out a slice of tiramisu for us to share.

I love shopping here for anything I may need in my home.  It's always a plus that they make their $5 off and 20% off coupons readily available.  The variety of products and innovative items for the home are also another reason to shop here.  The service I have received from the clerks is always top notch, and every time I go in someone is always roaming the store to offer assistance.

This is the perfect spot for a quick meal that isn't technically fast food.  Their sandwiches and soups are always a go to for me and they even offer a wide variety of bakery products for a special treat.  I've also brought in bagels for work functions and the variety always amazes me and there are never any complaints!  For those that want to hang out here for a bit, the complimentary WiFi is always a plus.

This is my go to local mall.  It has virtually every store that anyone would need to frequent, with the exception of Old Navy.  It could use more dine in eating establishments, like other malls in the area, and I think with the introduction of a couple of new restaurants recently they are heading in this direction.  Parking and access is always easy and the variety of stores and activites going on here is always a plus.

I've been to this establishment a couple of times over the past few months.  I can say I was pleasantly surprised to find a restaurant of this caliber in the area it is located in.  Parking isn't always easy depending on when you go, but once you step inside you are sure to find food and beverages aplenty.  Their entrees and pizzas are something you can't go wrong with and their specialty cocktails are the perfect pairing to a great meal.  My personal favorite is the Cherry Mojito made with Bacardi Torched Cherry Rum.  They usually have live bands playing on Fridays and Sundays, which is always a plus to accompany a great meal.

I'd like to echo a previous reviewer's sentiment (I will come back later and properly cite him in MLA format) that I'm deeply sorrowed that it's taken me this long to try Mel's, even though I haven driven by it for a long time. I really think they have something special going here, at least for breakfast, which is what I ordered today. 

We called in a pickup order for a couple of breakfast sandwiches, and they were superb. I can recommend the JC Special enough: bacon, egg, and cheese on your choice of french toast or waffles, with a little syrup (get the french toast, trust me). The Mel's Big Breakfast is also amazing. Eggs, cheese, choice of meat, and home fries on a Le Bus seeded long roll (yes, the home fries are in the sandwich). I paired this with a side order of the housemade roasted long hot peppers to make a delicious coronary precursor. 

Everyone inside was super nice, food was ready when I walked in (so I was in and out in no time), and food was packed nicely so it was still hot when I got home.

Great work all around, and I will def be back to try lunch.

I would call this a great place for a third or more date, but not for the beginning stages or a large group.  

This place is beautiful.  The decorations give it a dark and glossy feel, which is lovely for a relaxing night of drinking.  Do not bring a lot with you, i.e. a heavy jacket or large purse, because most of the tables have stools, not chairs or booths.  You will end up sitting with your jacket on your lap.  Earlier I wrote that this place is not for early on dates or large groups, because it is loud.  Loud as in I can't hear my friend two chairs down unless she is yelling loud.  I'm not sure if it is the proximity of the tables, or the large screen televisions, but I was with a group of 6, including me, and I had a hard time hearing people at one end of the table, while I was at the other.  The wine and beer selection, along with the decor, make this a great date place as long as you know the other person well enough that you won't be self conscious to speak loudly or ask someone to repeat himself/herself.  

The drink selection is stellar.  I'm not much of a beer or wine drinker, when given another option, so I got the blackrazz cosmo.  It was just the right balance of tart and sweet.  Even the blueberries that were in the drink were plump and delicious.  There is only one page of mixed drinks, which is a good amount, but small compared to the beer and wine selection.  Mixed drinks will set you back about $10, which is exactly what I would expect at a place like this.

A friend at the table got the Brie and Honey appetizer, so of course I got to taste test.  It was delicious.  The perfect size for a nice appetizer.  

The night we went, they were showing football games (Notre Dame v.s. USC and Florida v.s. Florida State).  I was surprised that a place like this were showing football games, now that I think about it, I'm surprised there were so many t.v. screens at all, but a lot of people in the bar seemed to come just to watch.  I would suggest coming a day that a football game isn't showing. 

Again, this is a great place to take a date and you can always take a walk along the water afterwards.  It is a beautiful location at night and will set the right romantic mood.

My hubby and I have been fans since Eddie opened this pizzeria in the 1990s. We have been loyal customers since his "grand opening". We moved to Chicago for ten years and would come visit friends and family in Tampa and bring home a pie on the plane ride back. The quality and taste have remained fairly consistent. I do remember it tasting better. The side crust doesn't seem as light and airy as we recall. It's still the best "NY style" pizza in the bay area. We'll still frequent it (more so now since we're back in the bay area). However, it's a bit sad it's not as good as it used to be.

I love this mall! Especially during the holiday season.

The setup here is very cute, makes it look like a little inside village. They have a good selection of stores here as well.

It does get crowded, of course, so I try to venture here on a weekday while playing hooky (which I never do, dear boss who might read these reviews!)

WARNING! once you enter this place you will find it extremely difficult to leave. There's a well shelved bookstore and a curiously interesting gift shop to browse through before or after you get to the dining, coffee shop and tea shop areas. The coffee and tea shop areas have very comfortable lounging areas. The coffee and tea selections as well as the snacks are foodie worthy. The tea shop serves an afternoon tea with numerous savory and sweet tasties. The dining areas are huge but the noise levels were not too distracting. I've had breakfast, brunch and lunch here and it was all tasteful and well served. They eventually had to turn me out so that they could close. I WILL BE BACK!

I've only been here for breakfasts but that's enough. Breakfast prices and food are excellent as is the cafe con leche. Service is always friendly and efficient. A good place to start the day or finish the evening. 
Looking over the lunch menu I saw the usual Cuban fare. After talking with several people who dine here regularly I was given the impression that they have a kitchen staff that makes  the usual Cuban fare shine. I will return for lunch and report again.

Okay, so... it's a chain. But hot damn, if it isn't a good chain. And, screw it, I'm giving this chain 5 stars. Honestly, as far as chain restaurants to, Carrabba's really is "as good as it gets". 

The service is always awesome. Their bread is kick ass. Their food is super kick ass. And always consistent, might I add. 

The Italian Trio is my absolutely favorite go-to dish. It used to be a super-secret off the menu thing that a waiter told my husband and I about years ago, but I think they added it to the menu within the last year. It's a sampler trio of their three most popular chicken dishes. A half breast of the Chicken Bryan, Chicken Marsala, and Pollo Rosa Maria. And they will all blow your skirt up. Their sirloin marsala is also always perfect.

I've literally never been disappointed at Carrabba's. And for that, five stars!

So, we purposely chose to dine here based the outstanding ratings but much to our surprise it didn't come close to living up to the hype. Firstly, "Made with water trucked in from NYC", this is as close to theory and subjective opining as you can get. In fact, the crust itself needs work. It was gummy, too chewy and not proofed properly. The OO or the traditional All Trumps High Gluten flour is either not being used here or being improperly prepared however, the toppings, cheese(s) and fresh, simple sauce used were absolutely perfect. Suspect the usual, Stanislaus, and Grande product brands. If, they work on a dough that is consistent with the traditional, crispy on the outside and soft on the inside, they'll be sure to create a masterpiece.

2 girlfriends and I decided upon Iavarone's for our Christmas dinner.

Upon arrival, the parking lot was full, which I felt was a good sign of things to come.

We were told there was a 20 min wait, so we decided to have a drink at the bar.  The drinks were reasonably priced ($8 for a Cosmo, $7 for a glass of Pinot and $5 for a beer).  Not even 10 minutes later, our buzzer went off, signaling our table was ready.

Once sat at our table, we were greeted by a friendly waiter who described the specials for the evening.  

We decided upon an order of the Bada Bing shrimp ( their version of bang bang).  The appetizer arrived quickly and piping hot.  The shrimp were perfectly breaded and a decent portion for the $9 price tag.

All of our entrees came with the house salad which was served in a very chilled bowl, which was a nice touch.  The salad reminded me of the Columbia's 1905 salad.  Not much to it, but quite tasty and fresh.

We all ordered the petite filet, which came with a side. I had the fettucini Alfredo as my side and you could tell it was homemade sauce.  The filet was encrusted with Gorgonzola cheese and finished with a port wine reduction.  The filet, although tasty and tender, was not the best filet I've ever had, but for the $27 price it was great!  Now mind you the $27 included the salad and the fettucini....this price point is comparable to what you'd find at carrabba's but IMHO better quality and ambiance.  

I would definitely return to Iavarone's and would like to try more of their Italian dishes.

For $41 I received:  2 beers, part of an appetizer, side salad, Gorgonzola crusted filet and a side if fettucini Alfredo. Not too shabby!

I've been wanting to order from Evan's for a while now but finally got around to it last night.  I ordered online at Eat24 and was impressed by their wide menu.  We got a buffalo chicken pizza, cheese pizza and jalapeno poppers for around $25 with the delivery charge.  The food was delivered quickly and the driver was very friendly.  Everything tasted delicious!  I would definitely order from Evan's again.

I'll be the first to admit that visiting Busch Gardens at night harkens back to the darkness in my soul and the "Dark Side of the Gardens, where shovels scrape along the ground and bushes startle in the shadows, but none of that happened.  What did happen was nothing short of magical.

I kind of knew what to expect, I'd gotten a few hints from friends working the event: 80 miles of lights - yes MILES of lights. Carolers, snow men and snow drifts, light shows and ice skating, and who could forget Jimmy Osmond, but what I wasn't expecting was how lovely it would all turn out to be. 

Upon entering the park, your eyes are greeting with the most entrancing color scheme you'll ever see. Morocco at Christmas is simply stunning with it's purples and golds.  Each area you walk into is themed beautifully in all it's brightly lit glory for you to wonder at, and trust me, your inner 4 year old will gawp at it just like mine did, pointing at this and that with a smile -so- wide. 

There are surprises around corners, lighting effects that change, music that is coordinated with lights so spectacularly that you will not believe it. And of course, there are the Angels! 

Be sure that you check out the ice skating show that they've put on just for this event. It is spectacular. The little girl will steal your heart away.  

The sing along train is by far one of the most fun ways to get around the park, it will lift your spirits as you chug from Stanleyville to "North Pole Station" Right outside Santa's house. When you do go to see the Big Man himself, don't forget to get your cookie from Mrs. Claus!

Ride Cheetah Hunt in the dark for a more spectacular ride. I swear! It felt like it was torqued up a few notches. It felt faster, it felt more intense in the dark. My 72 year old Mother, who'd never ridden it, fell in love with the beast! 

All in all, this is going to become a New Holiday Tradition!   Well Done!

my husband and I were delighted with the quality and value of the meals during our visit... the morcilla in the chorizo/morcilla starter was tasty and moist... I had parihuela (in my parents' native Spain, zarzuela) -- and I was astounded at the amount of seafood and how flavourful the broth was for under $20!  dessert was lovely -- a nice spice cake made up for no flan on the dessert menu... The sangria was a bit too sweet for me, but my husband was in love with it... Definitely recommended -- we plan a return trip next time we're in Tampa!

Been here 1/2 a dozen times in the past few months. The different chocolates and other sweet items are decent. But every time I go in the girl behind the counter is not very pleasant. She's not rude, but a little smile and/or decent greeting would be nice. I hate giving my money to someone who makes me feel like their doing me a favor by selling me their goods. And there's been several times I didn't get a thank you. I would have given it 4 stars, but the unhappy service drops it's rating by two stars.

Holidays are when you eat what you shouldn't, what you normally wouldn't and just generally more than you might typically tackle. Evos can't keep you from too many trips to the candy dish or sneaking snacks in between meal. But it can give you a very healthy, very tasty and really satisfying way to eat by specializing in creating good-for-us food that you want to eat. 

I tend not to stray from the Spicy Thai Chicken Wrap (also known as the Crack Wrap for it addictive qualities) but after sampling their bacon cheeseburger, bacon chicken caesar wrap, strawberry, walnut and gorgonzola salad, their sublime meatless burger and quite delightful hot dogs, I now realize that Spicy Thai Chicken has some serious competition. One suggestion, which I will surely try, is to have the best of two great dishes and request the meatless burger be substituted for the chicken so I can still wrap but with a twist.

This is a place you can grab and go, pull up a seat and eat or even have the food catered for an office function or special event. They go to great lengths to get high quality ingredients that are not over-processed or injected with hormones. Both the buns and wraps are made from organic ingredients and there is not a fryer anywhere on the premises. Everywhere you look there is evidence of an eco-friendly philosophy- from the chaisr and carry out boxes to the clear plastic cups where are made from either sustainable or recycled materials.

Much as I want to praise Evos for their eco-friendly practices, the true test is in the taste and It. Is. All. Good. Shakes, smoothies (the motto is "Drink Irresponsibly), sauces, ketchups, airbaked chicken strips and Airfries will make you happy you ate here, help you maintain healthy eating habits and leave you feeling delightfully full without being bloated or stuffed.

It is one big festive party and feast for the senses Friday, Saturday and Sunday nights now through Dec. 23 as Tampa's Busch Gardens-by-day transforms into Christmas Town at night.
 
Lots of lights, sounds of singing and costumed characters add sparkle, joy and color to help get everyone into the holiday spirit. Warning: Spontaneous ho ho ho-ing has been known to break out among the park's visitors.
 
Of course, it's not a party without coasters. But this is Busch Gardens so these coasters don't cradle glasses. These are gravity-defying roller coasters with names like Cheetah Hunt and SheiKra that fly through the sky. Riding one of these may be the closest we will ever get to knowing what it is like to soar aboard Santa's sleigh.
 
This year marks the debut of Christmas Town, a magical transformation that makes this major attraction the place to be to have a photo taken with Santa Claus, admire an angelic figure that may just surprise you, marvel at an elegant ice skating performance and even enjoy some atypical Florida fun like playing in real snow in the themed section SnowWorld® Presented by Coca-Cola®.
 
Tour the park on foot or hop aboard a vintage-style steam locomotive for a sing-a-long of seasonal songs.  Enjoy seasonal snacks like roasted nuts and other sweet treats. Even the gift shops are decked out with holiday decorations and gifts like special ornaments and cold weather clothing.
 
Because a portion of the Christmas Town proceeds benefits All Children's Hospital, every visit is a gift that benefits youngsters undergoing medical treatment so while you treat yourself, you will also be spreading some holiday cheer to those who need a little extra this year.

Went here for a Yelp elite event but if I had paid to get in, I would have given it 1 star as it's just not worth it for me.

I've never been very impressed with Busch gardens itself and this visit really just solidified that. However, the event itself was fairly fun. It was a nice Christmasy event, lots of lights to look at, hot cocoa, fake snow (poorly done fake snow may I add) but it did have its highlights. 

Everyone we ran into that worked here was very pleasant and friendly. They have stepped up the beer selection and even offered seasonal choices. All the food we tried was horrid!! The lights were great! Some much better then others, like the blue zone with the lights seemingly dropping from the trees and the colony house interactive lights/music was neat to watch (and made us giggle when we walked out of the colony house to dozens of people staring at us...oh no...it was the lights..but for a brief moment...the fame!)

They "real" statue girl was fun to watch as she surprised the ones who didnt know she was real until she moved during their picture op.

The skating rink and the snow zone were fun, although crowded and very limited on how much time you can spend (3 mins and they herd you out and bring in the nex group) And oh, no throwing snow balls at each other or they toss you out. (WHat! I had a good target on the hubby)

All in all it was a nice Christmas thing to do, really put us in the holiday spirit and made us feel like we were up north in a small town but definitely not worth the priceif you pay full price that is.

Julie & I have birthdays one day apart and there was no question we'd celebrate with a grand meal at Vivace.

We arrived at St. Phillip's Plaza and were treated to the delights of Holiday lights glowing and twinkling all over the well manicured property. Entering the restaurant, we were led through a number of rooms decorated in a classy old world style and seated in the back room.  The "bus gal" almost immediately brought water and wonderful warm bread with a pesto dipping oil.  Resisting that bread was futile!

A few minutes later, our waiter took our drink order and left us to peruse the menu.  It's not a huge menu but the description of each item made it difficult to choose.  My eyes kept scanning a triangle as I tried to decide between three entrees.  When our waiter returned with our drinks (Julie's Margarita- light on the liqueur and my generous pour domestic Cabernet) I was forced to make a decision.

For appetizers Julie ordered the Caesar salad and I chose the stuffed mushrooms.  I found the 'shrooms to have a great texture of slight crunchy from the breadcrumbs and a bit of chewy from the mushrooms.  The lemony flavor made it a treat for my mouth. Julie found the Caesar salad to be just fine.

I settled on a new-to-me dish, Pork Sorrentino and Julie had the fish of the day, grilled Salmon with artichoke hearts and red peppers (substituting their garlic mashed potatoes for rice).  My mouth had a food orgasm with every bite of the pork fillets baked in cheese with a flavorful wine sauce.  My side of vegetable pasta was good but next time I'd also substitute for the garlic mashed.  Julie treated me to several dollops of her 'tatoes and mixed with my wine sauce it was a perfect combination.  The Salmon also received an enthusiastic two thumbs up from Julie.

For dessert we shared a creme brulee' which was a perfect size for two and came with a separate side cup of fruit.

If you want a fine dining experience, you could do a lot worse than Vivace.  A meal such as described above will run a little over $100 with tax & tip but well worth it.
The food is outstanding with service to match.  The pace of the meal was perfect for us (1 1/2 hours).   I would recommend reservations as even on a Wednesday night the place was packed, and for good reason.  Vivace...we'll see you again soon.

I am actually confused at some of the lesser ratings. If you don't get the pricey special rolls and just go for normal rolls or sushi, you can't go wrong. This is the best place i have had sushi in this area, period. Better than blue fin, better than Oishi and many others The fish is extremely fresh, which to me, in a place in a strip mall is all i care about. The quality of the fish is far superior to any place i have been in this area (philly and the burbs). I have no issues with the salads or the soups either. And if you order a $20 roll, please note they are huge and you really don't need much more than that anyway. I also enjoy the attention from the employees. I  live in Bucks County and this is the only sushi place I get excited to go to and have no issues driving past a dozen or so other places to go here.

I would give it a 4 1/2 if that was possible.  The only thing keeping it from 5 stars is the strength of the tea I had, but I will get to that.

My boyfriend and I went for Saturday brunch with 4 other couples.  For a table that large, the service was efficient and speedy.  Our waiter informed us that although they would take things out of dishes, they recommended making as few changes as possible to the dishes, because Saturday was their busiest time.  Actually, they said Thursday, Friday, and Saturday they do not make many changes to the dishes.  I appreciated this, because it meant that the food would come out in a timely manner, since they were not catering to picky eaters.

I ordered the smoked salmon on an everything bagel (with fennel salad on top as well).  I could have gone with a tad less fennel salad.  It had a strong pickled flavor to it and a little goes a long way.  However, on a whole, it was absolutely delicious.  For $8.50 I felt completely satisfied.  My boyfriend got pancakes without the fruit topping and a side of bacon.  He loved it.  He talked about how much he enjoyed the pancakes the entire ride home.

As for the tea, I got the iced pomegranate blueberry green tea with honey.  It lacked flavor to me.  The tea was not very strong or very sweet.  I could have added sugar or sweetener that was at the table, but I thought the honey as a sweetener would have been enough.  The tea came first and I was initially disappointed with it.  However, after eating my meal, I enjoyed the tea more.  I think it had to do with the juxtaposition of flavors between my food and the drink.  Still, I would not buy that tea again. There was a lot more of it than I was expecting.  It came in a rather large glass.  However, I would have appreciated more flavor even if it was less tea.

I don't want to give up on the tea, because I keep reading reviews about how spectacular it is.  I may have to go back to try another flavor.

I appreciate that they have parking, which they share with the First Church of Christ, Scientist.  I hate parking downtown and this makes it so much more convenient to visit.

Upstairs, they have a commerce club.  It is worth checking out the space, because it is pretty magnificent.  It actually reminded me slightly of the Reading Room at the University of Michigan (my Alma mater), so I felt nostalgic up there.  Apparently for a fee a month you can pay to use that space if you have a job that you can work from anywhere.  Also, as a member of the commerce club you can rent the conference rooms they have up there.

Another tidbit, apparently they will rent out space to do weddings.  They are closed on Sundays and after five on weekdays so there is plenty of time for a wedding to happen.  

I definitely think this is a place you have to visit at least once if you're in Tampa.  It is almost an homage to older times where things were made to be beautiful and last instead of convenient and cheap.

It's my favorite grocery store by far...spacious, clean, organic and non organic products, large parking space, ready to eat food, great wine selection, seafood, gelato and pizzas, friendly staff with all your regular Publix's offers!!!  My first pick in groceries stores.

I've been to this establishment a few times for a yearly pub crawl that I attend so that is my only experience with this place.  The bartenders and music are always good when I have been here so I can't complain.  Pretty run of the mill.

Good place for seafood. We ordered the artichoke dip, gumbo soup, salmon and crab crab cakes....everything was good but I was not impressed with the crab cake.  We visited during the week so the restaurant was not busy. Happy hour is ok depends on what you a looking for!!

I eat at Evo's quite a bit as my boyfriend is a huge fan. I've tried something different almost everytime I've eaten there and yet, I haven't found that one item that I can't live without. Evo's is perfect for a quick meal but again, there's just not any single item that stands out for me. I'll more than likely keep on eating there so hopefully I'll find my menu soulmate and keep ordering it and upgrade this review. Maybe I should get a milkshake?

I love the pizza here!   The service in the restaurant is great and I often use curb side too. I eat at Anthony's at least once a week. I think folks forget about this place at lunch. They are fast and prices are reasonable.

This place is located inside the Marriott on the east side of the hotel. We came here late night after a wedding and were the only ones in the whole place, and we were just having drinks, so this review does not pertain to the food here.

The bar tenders were on the older side but were fairly quick. We sat around the bar area and it was actually great having the place to ourselves. I;'m not sure if they stayed open for us or not, but if our group was not there, this place would have been completely empty. Good for a hotel bar, but I wouldn't make it my destination for the night.

You know what, I have no idea if I like this place. I feel weird writing a review about it, because it's been so all over the map for me. I've never walked inside, so maybe it's a completely different experience? But the drive thru... well...

So, in an apples-to-apples comparison, this review and rating should be considering in comparison/contrast to other drive thru Mexican places. And let me tell you, I've given 5 stars to some of the most shady, dodgy-looking drive thru places simply because not only is their food fantastic but as a surprise bonus I did not good food poisoning after.

Let's break it down:

Food: Not bad. I wish they'd use better cheese. It's that pretty cheap bright orange stuff. It doesn't even completely melt most of the time, giving an odd look of "sample display case example" plastic look. But edible. I like their beans. My husband reports that their carne asada burrito was fine, just average. That's the general vote I think, it's just ok.

Price: Er, about going rate if not a little higher than the average "drive thru Mexican" joint. 

Service: I read Richard's review and comments about their drive thru and was like, "yes! exactly!" It is a weird, strange drive thru etiquette. They are very abrupt, halting, and come across as annoyed, like you're putting them out in using their drive thru window. There's no summary of what you ordered or a price. I always feel like I'm fighting to get additional items in, they want to shut me down after the first item. They don't say "thank you" or "please pull around for your total" or anything like that. It just goes dead. It's just a very odd experience. They are usually friendly enough at the window... so, I don't know.

I haven't have the experience of a better drive thru in the immediate area, so despite this place's deficiencies... if you need Mexican food, and in a rush, this isn't bad. It just isn't good either. For convenience only, I'm sure we'll be back again.

I was really looking forward to trying out this place after all of the glowing Yelp reviews.  I have to admit, I was somewhat underwhelmed.  If this review were just based on atmosphere, I would give it 5 stars all day.  This place is BEAUTIFUL and huge.  We sat in an area that had a glass ceiling, so it was like dining outside without the temperature concerns.

We were told we'd have a 30 minute wait and were pleasantly surprised when we were called after 15 minutes.  When we walked up to the hostess stand, we were told they were clearing off our table and that it would be just a minute.  Another woman then walked up and asked if her table was ready and she was seated right away.  We were then told that our table wasn't ready and had to wait about 10 more minutes.  I'm pretty sure that our table was given away to this other person, and I'm not really sure why.

When we were seated, the service was quick and the food was good.  I ordered the shitake mushroom and roasted red pepper flatbread.  It was tasty.  My fiance' ordered the club sandwich.  The sandwich was supposed to come with avocado, but it was left off.  The waiter took it back and corrected the issue, however, the sandwich was somewhat falling apart once the avocado was added.  I was very surprised to hear that this place is not open for dinner or on Sundays.  I really think that Oxford Exchange would be a nice dinner spot and that they would be successful.  Overall, I had an okay experience at Oxford Exchange, but I'm not sure if it was a good enough experience for me to go back.

Beer sangria= the answer

Somewhat slow service at times, but with great drinks and tasty snacks...sitting on a patio shouldn't be rushed, anyhow :)
I would like to make this a bit more regular in my visits!

Real New York Pizza with New York Water

That is their motto -- and about sums it up.  The restaurant itself is nothing special, but the pizza is great.  They do lots of speciality pizza's "by the slice" so make sure you check out what's available before you order the same old boring stuff.

Great pizza -- a little greasy at times -- but good.  Very authentic to what you get in NYC.

Shiroi - how I've missed you.
The sushi chef who knows me "by face".
The older waitress who brings me genmaicha.

The incredibly fresh sashimi.

I miss it here so much!  I stop in whenever I can - which is about once a year now.

Daniel was Very good & the manager stopped by twice asking if we were OK, then he came by again to clean up our table! I was not expecting that... Wings & couch fries were very tasty & hot... I also took advantage of the $15.00 for $25 deal & paid 1.70 out of pocket plus extra tip... NICE Thanks Press Box & YELP!
The food looks and tastes good. Rolls are amazing, and better try the Green Tea ice-cream. Service was good and so was the ambiance.

Its a pretty scenic location where the former Curtis Hixon convention center was. You can look up and see downtown looming and over to the Minarets of the Plant Museum at University of Tampa. The Art Museam is right there, and its a short walk to a ton of downtown dining and fun!

I really want to write a 5-star review for Thai Island, but I. Just. Can't.

The food just isn't up to par, even Tampa par. While the service is fantastic (super-nice staff), the food has all sorts of consistency issues. I've been here twice, and on this latest visit, one dish was inedibly spicy and another was a curry with an overwhelming amount of thin "juice," for lack of a better word. Both dishes lacked flavor/seasoning.

On the upside, the presentation was nice, the veggies and meat were well-cooked and looked fresh. They just didn't deliver on taste.

I had to send my dish back, and my friend tolerated his.

One note for diners who prefer a sweet wine to complement their spicy Thai dish: The riesling on the menu is a DRY riesling, not a traditional sweet one. With plenty of chards, a tart sauvignon blanc and even a chenin blanc on the menu, this revelation was the last straw.

Not returning. Boo.

It's the best American-style pizza in Philadelphia; great staff and atmosphere too. It's better than Rustica and other artisan takeout places. And for the price it beats all of Philly's premiere pizza joints, too. All of the pies are great; they even have vegan options (but if your a vegan, pizza really shouldn't be your thing). I'd go out of my way to try their specials too--roast beef and jalapeno/fig jam pizza come to mind. And for the record, they can totally make huge orders (I heard they recently did a 44-pizza order!). When Pizza Brain offers more seating and delivery, it'll earn five stars.

My favorite atmosphere in Fishtown. It has a great local taplist, but with Brooklyn prices. Food is great, too. Apparently, it's a destination bar, so I'd stick to weekday hours. Oysters are great, so are burgers and fries.

Considering the limitations of a takeout place that doesn't have indoor seating, Johnny Hot's offers a great product, for an amazing price, with unbelievable fast service. Recommended for breakfast and lunch. It's great to take your sausage to the park across the street and enjoy.

Top notch atmosphere--one of the best in Philadelphia. Great, local tap list, even if it has Brooklyn prices. Great bar food, great comfort food, and occasionally adventurous. 

We had our engagement party here (impromptu) and it was the perfect venue. Great for big groups who want to hang and don't mind standing, too. The credit card minimum is annoying.

The Foodery is really an important take-out store in Philadelphia and it has so much to offer. In terms of variety, accessibility, and just the culture of the place--it is the ultimately beer destination in this part of town. The bars are cool, and sometimes even impressive, but the Foodery is just where it's at in Northern Liberties. You'd be hard-pressed to find some of the stuff they have here anywhere else in the city, and from what I understand the 2nd Street store is better than the one in Center City. It's and important store. And the I think the staff, though generally glum, has a good knowledge of beer that they are willing to share with you, too. Of course, I love going there to do some window-shopping. 

Best bottle shop in town, hands down. And the sandwiches from the deli aren't too bad, either! It is expensive, so only go here for bottles you are sure you can't get anywhere else. Employees are people; they can be helpful and sometimes a bit off-putting. I think that's normal.

O.K. taplist, interesting (if not too sweet) margaritas, stellar tequila list. This is a fun place for a crowd with good tacos and burritos (stay with the basics, the food doesn't get better as it gets more elegant and adventurous). Although the basic fare still isn't as good as a Mexican taqueria. Good for groups and happy hour, not ideal for a date. Service not noteworthy.

Solid pizza at a great price. Adventurous and standard options to boot. Great for take out. Fun and friendly staff. Not as good as Pizza Brain but still good.

My favorite pies are the white pies, and I think the plum tomato and eggplant takes the cake. 

Great for a late night slice, too, after a long evening.

Dock Street is one of our city's only brewpubs and it is a relatively shameful one at that. Nothing compared to Nodding Head (which, apparently, receives a great deal of criticism from some of my friends) or even Triumph, but nevertheless, Dock Street, up until recently, was really the only option. I don't think that Dock Street is particularly moving or intriguing. I like its Rye IPA and its Bohemian Pilsner all right, the problem I have though is that it's just an average brewpub. Furthermore, the environment, despite the excellent pizza there, is insane. It's far too loud and crowded--proving, again, that West Philly simply doesn't have a lot of options in terms of beer drinking.

By default, this is my favorite bar in West Philly. I don't mean to knock this bar, it's just frustrating when there is nothing else in West Philly and a bar becomes my favorite because of that. In general, I don't go to West Philly much, but when I'm there, it's always nice to stop by Local 44, whose most impressive feature remains the 20 beers they have on tap, and the two beers they are offering cask-conditioned. Brendan and Leigh, the geniuses behind Memphis Taproom, a bar that you've heard about time and again from this blog, did it right with Local 44. For beer-drinking, Local 44 is the place to go. I haven't frequented it very often (because it's like the MTR of the West), but my experiences there have been positive. West Philly really doesn't have much else.

The problem that Brendan and Leigh, though, have created for themselves is the presence of Memphis Taproom (a better bar, in a better neighborhood). And for a resident of Kensington, I don't have much reason to ever be in West Philly, so I will always compare their other creation to Memphis Taproom. And even though Local 44 has more beer options than Memphis (and it is totally awesome to only have Orval in bottles), Memphis beats it on all other fronts, in my mind. And that's just one problem, the other is that the clientele there is just more... bro-ish than in Fishtown. Sure, you'll find hipsters in Fishtown, but I can tolerate their four-square and electronica more than I can football fans and their pop rock music. 

But, Local 44 is still owned by the people that own the bar I frequent the most, so there is a large level of trust and reliability that I put in that bar. I know the beer will be good, even if the food menu and the atmosphere are not as good as what I'm used to. And you can blame that on college students.

But I visited Earth, Bread + Brewery in the Mt. Airy/Germantown section of the city recently. And I was quite happy. Though I will bring a wacky shirt next time (depending on what the special is) so I can get 20 oz. pours for the price of 13 oz. ones, overall I had a good time. Just remember, always carry plaid with you. There's really nothing wrong with being prepared.

So I fought the traffic and went to a neighborhood I often joke about being outside of the city--my friend Adam quickly reminds me that he pays city taxes, too. Sucker. Nothing against Mt. Airy, it's just a pain in the butt to get to. But I think my trip to Earth was worth it. And maybe it's just because the bar has a nice beer engine and is ready and able to pour 20 oz. glasses. More bars should take note. Sometimes I want 13 oz. and sometimes I want more. Offering two sizes makes sense! More and more places are getting this idea, and that's awesome for sure. Glad it's happening.

I got the Hotel Beacon, described as an English dark mild poured on cask--actually at a very good temperature. Though its advertised as room temperature (by our friendly, but knowledge-shallow server), it's certainly colder than that (but warmer than many other cask-conditioned beers in this town). It poured with a pillow head, similar to many beers like this, with a dark body. It has a medium mouthfeel with some chewiness. But it was delightful. It has a great earthiness to it and malt presence. Very well balanced and an indication that Earth has a classy sensibility (despite its anniversary beer being a DIPA). 

Along with the seed flatbread I ordered (the thing I've ordered every time I've been there), I thought the mild did its job. Mild's tend to be simplistic in their nature, pairing well with many other foods. Never getting in the way, or shouting like a Scotch ale or an IPA might--but just being present, used when necessary, and happy to do so. Delightfully humble, even. That's what I think of milds. They have good characters. They aren't annoying, but they are confident. How can you beat that?

Well maybe you can beat it with my second choice was the obscure and style-less Alehoof Grut, said to be brewed without hops, this is an ancient beer made with interesting herbs, maybe vegetables, and other items. It's interesting, to be sure. But not crazy. It still tastes like a beer, and despite its brown body, tasted particularly refreshing. No head retention on this one, with evidence of a thin body in its pour. Notes of sweetness, some malt, and root vegetables (turnips, beets) in its taste. It finished relatively dry and that's a good thing.

The reason to come here is to try the beer; the food is pretty stellar, too. Overall, a satisfying experience and i'm excited about the intelligence and ingenuity of the brewers.

Best "hill" in town....there's no question about it. The nonstop path to the top of Tumacoc Hill is 5-stars in my book and here's why:

1. It's Up Hill
2. It's Steep
3. It's only about 45 minutes-1hour round trip (depending on your pace)
4. The views are killer. 
5. It's paved
6. It's well trafficked making it feel safe. Not sure if I would go alone or at a time of day when no one else is around (I'm a lady!)
7. Peeps are friendly! Must be the endorphin high from reaching the top. So many smiling faces and hello's are exchanged.
8. It's close to my house
9. It's Free
10. It's not in the boonies.

LOVE TUMACOC HILL. This will for sure be included my regular cardio regimen.

I've been to this location a couple of times for Brunch and I was pleasantly surprised each time.  Both times we have been our servers have been spot on and very attentive.  My group always partakes in the Endless Bubbles Brunch which includes bottomless Mimosas and Bellinis.  The Bang Bang Shrimp appetizer is out of this world and has to be my most favorite item on the menu.  Each time I have gone, I have ordered something different whether it be from the Brunch Menu or off the Regular Menu and I have never been disappointed.

Awesome little store in the city.  I like to browse around and look for different items.  If you are looking for an interesting or funny gift for a friend or family member who loves the kitchen, this is your one stop shop!

Staff is friendly and they have a good idea of what they have in stock.  Very helpful, can't say enough good about the staff.

Parking is a hassle but it's the city so get over it.

Grey Lodge is a special place. The reason, for me, that it scores so highly is because of its remoteness. That isn't to say that the Northeast isn't filled with bars, but none with the level of beer program, nor such a definitively Philadelphian bar menu. Though the food could use some risk, it is certainly affordable. Recommended if you are in the area. One of the best pubs in town, if you ask me.

I love empanadas and the ones at Mr. Empanada are great.  They're made fresh while you wait in a variety of flavors from beef to crabmeat to guava & cream cheese.  I've never had anything there besides the empanadas since it never made sense to me to order a burger at a pizza shop, so to speak.

The only downside is the price for the amount of food.  I have to eat at least three to make a worthwhile lunch.  So, by the time I add a drink, I'm looking at about $12 bucks, which is a little high for a quick lunch of this type.  In all fairness though, a small chain like this doesn't have the economies of scale to survive without charging more than the huge corporations it competes against.

An added bonus for me is that I like supporting local businesses so I'll definitely keep going back to Mr. Empanada.

Food is basically the same as the Vallarta's on Andersen, which is to say it's delicious cheap Mexican food. I'm docking a star just because the interior of this location looks nasty & the service is just okay.

My wife and I ate here for a early dinner. The front of the house acted as if they did not care. The server was good I will give her that. My wife had the chicken Bryan which is always good. She ate every bit of it.
I had a salad and a pizza, the pizza was not done, like they took it out of the oven to early.
Most of the time we go to the Coral Springs, Fl location and it is great but this was a big letdown. Won't go back. It might be because they are so close to USF that they are full most nights and do not pay attention to the regular folks.
Even though it is a chain Carrabba's should do better. We normally are big fans but not this one.

Good breakfast place! One of my favorites. Usually packed on the weekends which can make parking and waiting a problem. There is almost always a wait on Sat/Sun but it usually moves pretty quickly. The servers are running their butts off waiting on everyone with no computer system. 

Frittatas are great! Veggie is the one I normally go for but there are plenty of meaty options too. The frittatas come with a small side of fruit, bread (get the cuban toast), and choice of side (get the potatoes! fresh and a nice hint of rosemary). The oatmeal pancakes are fabulous too (cinnamon in them, I think). One of the only places I've seen serving oatmeal pancakes. (They have regular pancakes too!)

Breakfast burrito, stuffed french toast, and breakfast sandwiches are also big hits. A great place for a breakfast out. Another plus? They are almost always open on holidays but they close early so don't sleep in too much. 

The are good about updating their Facebook page with daily specials that make your mouth water. Yum!

Koizi offers a ton of food for a good price. Lunch is the best deal with all-you-can-eat sushi, hibachi, and menu items for under $10. My favorite thing is dishes are "made-to-order" and do not sit around like the way a regular buffet would function. Of course this cuts into profits, therefore it is important that a business like this is supported.

The hibachi is just as good or better than Kobe Japanese Steakhouse or Arigato and far more affordable. I personally like their steak and chicken. The sushi isn't the best, but still not bad for the price. I wouldn't expect stellar sashimi - but they make some excellent rolls. I still brave some nigiri anyway because I can't resist raw fish.

The employees are friendly, but honestly can sometimes be a bit air-headed or hard to flag down, which is why I tend to try and tell them things I want upfront to delay the amount of time  it takes them to put in my order or bring it out. Koizi certainly is not a restaurant that someone who is in a rush should visit if you want to get value for your meal. Therefore if you're going for lunch I would arrive before noon with plenty of time to enjoy your meal.

The tables are huge and perfect for birthday parties or large get-togethers as you would expect at any Japanese steakhouse. They also have take-out at affordable prices and tend to offer plenty of specials if you check out their Facebook fan page.

Oh and they have "ramune" Japanese soda! My favorite :)

It's a CB and I'm pretty sure most know what to expect.  Where this one stands out to me is for its location. 

Most CB are located right off the interstate for travelers. This one is not and caters mostly to the locals and this is why I like it.

There never is a long wait here like the rest yet the quality is what you'd expect. 

Some of my favorites are:
Pecan pancakes
Turkey sausage 
Chicken and dumplings
Green beans

This Westchase establishment delivers quality that's matched perfectly to presentation.  Zen Bistro may not look much from the outside, but step inside and you are greeted with aroma of eclectic spices of the Asian cuisine.  The atmosphere is very inviting and during the evenings the interior is dimly lit evoking the cozy feeling.  The flavor of food served here has nothing dark or dim about it.  Much has been said about their Sushi and every bit of great taste mentioned is true.  Zen Bistro also excel in their presentation of each dish. 

My favorites here are too many to list.  However, if you are a Salmon lover, the Hawaiian Salmon ( fresh sushi grade salmon slow cooke with pineapple, soy, & chili peppers in a clay pot ) is to die for.  Just writing about it is making me hungry.  The description is indeed true to the actual dish that is served in front of you. The second - Kabayaki Beef is another superstar dish at this establishment. The beef is tender and enormously flavored with the right balance of sweet and heat.  One bite and you are hooked.

Don't ignore the many elixir Zen Bistro has to offer.  Each one is meticulously prepared with a slight twist - ex.  Jalapeño Pineapple Martini.   The staff are very attentive and demonstrate a true sense of pride of the food served.  

With great food, tasty drinks, and attentive service,  Zen Bistro has a winning combination truly deserving of a 4 1/2 star.

I love Rittenhouse nails.  I am picky as can be when it comes to waxing, having had a really bad experience outside of the city. Anyway, the women at Rittenhouse nails are good- never too thin and my sensitive skin never gets irritated after. I prefer Susan or Sofia, as they take their time with their clients. 

Mani and pedi from here lasts. The only downside is that their shellac manicures are far more expensive than competitors, but their regular manicure is priced very well given the drying massage. The pedi is on the expensive side but you get hot stone massage so again totally worthwhile. Occasionally I will get lucky with a great & lengthy back massage while drying, but even if its just a short one, just having it as an added bonus is completely worth every penny!

Great sushi, hit or miss service.  Atmosphere is very upscale and the bar area is accommodating and fun. Have had nearly everything on the menu without a complaint. Only downsides are lack of a huge assortment of rolls and occasional spotty service.

My husband and I love biscuits and as in a previous review, love the theme.  Did not know one needed to be right to enjoy the patriotism but do.  You are greeted like an old friend when you enter.  The food tastes good, which is why we keep going back.  We have lots of choices and the atmosphere and food are winners.  Our 7 year old grandson went with us last weekend and the waitress really embellished his hot chocolate - his eyes almost popped out of his head.  He ate almost all of his very generous two eggs (more like three), his bacon and two HUGE pancakes.  

The biscuits and gravy are what we normally get - it is very tasty. 

My only complaint would be the processed swiss cheese in the scrambles vs regular swiss but that is a small issue to me and I should not be eating any cheese anyway so I skip.  

BISCUITS HAS FREE WIRELESS. YEA!!!

Yes, we are fans.

We've definitely cut back our visits here recently after we saw how one waitress treated a table of customers.  One person at the table ordered an omelet that she didn't enjoy because she didn't realize it had onion in it.  She told the waitress, who then became slightly belligerent about the fact that "it clearly says it has onions in it on the menu."  The woman just didn't want it, so the waitress went ahead and made another for her, reluctantly and rudely.  And then at the end of the meal, SURPRISE!  They charged this woman for TWO omelets.  They refused to remove one from the bill.  When people do not enjoy your food, you re-make it for them.  Period.  A good restaurant would re-make it *and* comp the entree off the meal.  They weren't even willing to charge for one omelet and comp the other in good faith.  The most disturbing part is that the waitress didn't even inform the customer that they were intending on charging her for the second omelet.  Not comping or re-making meals free-of-charge is, as far as I understand it, the restaurant policy, not just the waitress being a jerk.  The entire event was absolutely atrocious for a restaurant to do.  You are supposed to want people to enjoy their experience at your restaurant.  Biscuits clearly proved that they don't care.  We were spending our money there once a week.  We won't be doing that anymore.  What if they make something badly one day?  I have to eat a bad meal, or pay for two of them?  That's completely unacceptable, and they really lost loyal customers in us because of that.

Furthermore, the decor has gotten even more out of hand.  It didn't really bother me, but now with the Christian music and magazines all over, I'm sorry, but it's not really appropriate.  It's a business at the end of the day, not a place to force feed your religion to people.  I go to restaurants to eat meals, not to be indoctrinated.

Sorry Biscuits.  Treat your customers with some respect if you want to stay in business.
The service was really good here! The pho was not the best I've ever had, but there were many items on the menu and I'd be willing to go back and try something else!

When it comes to my hair I am very fussy, as I think everyone should be; after all you have to see it every day. I have had a slew of bad and so-so experiences over the past five or so years. I am BLOWN AWAY by how AWESOME my hair looks after an appointment with Reno today! He spent about 10 minutes talking with me about what I wanted and looked at a picture of Natalie Portman (that probably cost her about $500) before he did anything to make sure he knew exactly what I wanted. Then he shared the options with me including the pricing so I wouldn't be surprised at the check-out counter (very considerate, and more than I can say for other salons I've been to). 

He took his time foil highlighting and low-lighting my insane amount of hair and asked Caroline, the woman who washed my hair to tone it after the foils came out, creating a totally natural and gorgeous color! (Not to mention the shampoo felt great :)) Then he gave me the best cut I have had in...ever... keeping the length I wanted, but adding beautiful layers. During the cut Caroline offered me a delicious cappuccino, which was an added bonus :) The staff are friendly and the atmosphere is delightful with fun music playing. I can not sing Reno and Oggi's praises enough! 

If you're sick of trying out new salons, only to be disappointed and perhaps traumatized by less-than-attentive stylists, then breathe easy, your search is over! I am so thrilled with the way my hair looks! I feel great! Thanks Reno and Caroline!!!

I have always been slightly biased against P.F. Changs.  I use to refer to it as very Americanized Chinese food.  The very first time I had it many years ago, I felt like they oversauced everything.  I decided to give this place another try, because my fiance loves this place.  I'm glad I did, because I was pleasantly surprised.

This place is dark.  It is beautifully decorated and the tables are a good space apart from each other, making it not too noisy.

We started out with the lettuce wraps.  They were delicious.  There were about six leaves of lettuce (or seven if my fiance snuck an extra one when I wasn't paying attention) and it was the perfect amount for the amount of chicken mix we got.  I felt like there was too many water chestnuts in the mix, but the fried pieces were a nice touch.  I would definitely suggest starting off with this.

I got the Seared Tuna Ahi salad.  The wasabi sauce was absolutely delicious.  I tried this once at Cheesecake factory, and I have to say I enjoyed this dish more and it was about $4 less!  The salad was more of a slaw mix than a salad, but I'm glad for this.  Usually salads are not interesting, but I would call this salad interesting complete with cilantro and radishes.  The tuna was perfectly seared as well.

We also got the Chicken Lo Mein and Mongolian Beef.  The Lo Mein had a good taste but the noodles were not firm enough.  They were too soft for my taste.  The Mongolian Beef also had a great taste, but it was really chewy.  

All in all, I want to give this place 4 stars, but every dish, except the Seared Tuna Ahi Salad, had something a little wrong with it, which prevents me from doing so.  Still, a very enjoyable lunch and I'm sure I'll be back.

The cuban bread here is amazing and its obviously made on site. It wont keep for more then a day and 1 roll is actually 2 in one so you can definitely be good with a single pack. 

Its cheap and good cuban bread not much more to say!

The seafood and the shrimp pasta dishes still rock! I liked coming here back in the day. I got a meal at a good price, beer and wine are reasonably priced. The menu looked a little expanded from what they served before. 

Looking around there wasn't anyone complaining about food, everything looked great. Portions and portions on other tables were almost excessive. Well, that's not a ding, I like having a decent meal being a growing boy. 

The new owner is the son of the former chain owners and he has a fire in his belly to make Shells what it was before. His staff is motivated, I know he is going to do well.

The people are helpful, I always say there could be more options for the snack bar healthy and unhealthy. 

Place is clean, movies are great and they have the stubs rewards which are helpful to a movie addict like myself. 

Its in a mall so all you have to do is go down the escalators to get to Starbucks, Maggiano's, and PinkBerry.

Living in the Tampa area for so long,  I had always passed this place many times and was always curious as to why it was always so busy.  I got to discover the reason why when one of my recent work lunches was catered from here.  The food is absolutely delicious and the portions are more than generous.  

I've since used this place to cater a couple of other work functions I've had and each time I was not disappointed.  My only grip with placing orders for a large group are that it is hard to reach them by phone at times to place your order and also when you go in to pick it up, the packaging isn't always the best as I did end up with a bit of a mess in my car.  The taste and quality of the food, however, more than made up for the minor inconveniences.

There are many reasons why I am giving this place five stars. The first reason is easy to say: it is not a national chain. This location is their only location, so they know how vital it is to make good food. And they exceed this expectations. There are other local breakfast and brunch places throughout Tucson, but The Egg Connection is the best of them.

I never really liked grits until I ate here. Now, I am a fan. Whether you order an omelette, breakfast plate, or a pancake/waffle dish, they go above and beyond to make their food delicious. For example, they put more cheese on their omelette than any place in Tucson, but they know not to put too much to take away from the egg. Their gave you plenty on the side dishes to help the main courses; the bacon is tasty and crispy and their English muffins are amazing! The prices definitely match the quality and quantity of the food.

The last time I ate here I ordered a California Turkey Sandwich with coleslaw. I don't know where they get their turkey, but it is absolutely delicious! I never had their soups but I've been told it is really good. While I haven't tried everything on their menu, I don't think there is one thing that is not good.

As s huge fan of mobster and gangster movies, I eat with satisfaction being surrounded by movie posters that include many of The Godfather. In their bathroom, there is a 3-D poster of the movie; how cool is that! Finally, the service here is very nice and friendly. They always will take a minute or two to have a conversation with you. That to me is excellent customer service. 

If you have yet to try The Egg Connection, I highly suggest to make it out here for a breakfast or brunch. This place is amazing!

Let me start this out  by saying that I love meat. I eat meat all day, every day. I dare to say no gluten or soy substitute in the world comes close to having that "hearty" taste. Yet, it was a recommendation from my co-worker that kept Magic Carpet in the back of my mind. After getting out of class early, I headed over to the cart, which had its legendary long line. It didn't take long though, because there were two guys manning the windows. When I was prompted to order, I blabbed the first thing I saw, the Dervish Delight. It came in a round foil tin with a lot of stuff. A lot of stuff indeed.

There was a salad, pita bread, hummus, taboulli and falafel. The pita bread was separate, but everything else was smushed together. Despite the fact that the lemon I had was a tad on the sour side, I was satisfied!The hummus tasted fairly fresh and the falafel had a little flavor kick to it. I didn't think much of itl, but something....(My stomach?) kept nagging me to go back. I caved, and this time tried the Bella Donna after reading some reviews on yelp. It was delicious to say the least. The veggie meatballs were warm and doused with cheese and hot sauce (I strongly recommend that combo!) At the bottom was some sort of rice with veggies. It was soooo good. I never have the heart to order anything else. The BD only cost $4.50, a la carte. Their menu is online, so get a good look on their other veg options.

In short, their food is awesome!

This place is run by a group of sisters.  Some of them are always present working hard to make sure you get a good meal.  This is a fantastic Thai restaurant.  I really like that before your lunch entree you are given soup.  It will either be vegetable pieces in broth (so-so), chicken and rice (yum!), or wonton soup (the best!).  I'm really partial to their noodle dishes (pad ba me or pad see ew).  They are delicious.  They're always good about asking about how spicy you want your food.  I love Thai food but hate spicy food (say what?) so that's something that I always have appreciated.

If you like Sushi they have a Sushi bar and there's also a large selection of alcohol and spirits you can choose from.  If the weather is decent it's nice to sit out on the porch and watch South Tampa in action.

I can't get enough of their food.  The parking situation is dreadful, but that's South Tampa for you and not their fault.

Went here and got a large chicken fried rice which was ehhh. Bland, mostly rice, unseasoned little strips of chicken, and a little bit of onions, carrots, and peas. Fast and friendly service. But $7 for bland fried rice...PASS.

This is a conflicted review.  I want to go 5 star because I've been eating here for about 15 years with nothing but good experiences in my memory bank, but I guess all good things come to an end, huh?  

We went to Ivaronne's this past Sunday for my sister-in-law's birthday, AND it was my chica's first time dinning at the establishment.  Everything started great.  Service was prompt and good, as always.   The aps looked and tasted great, especially the AHI TUNA.  That was one serious tuna steak!  Perfectly seared.  The salads came out in a timely fashion, and were delicious.  They really do have one of the best Caesar dressings around.  Everything was all was good until the main courses came out.  My sister-in-law ordered her steak medium, and it came out very much well done.  The damn thing was grey.  You can't UNCOOK a freakin' steak, so she had to sit there for 10 minutes watching us eat while they cooked another one for her.  And then her replacement steak arrived, it was medium RARE.  So back to the kitchen it went.  Finally, when it came out for the 3rd time, it was properly prepared.  But by this time, we were all done eating and the mental damage was done.  They did take her steak off of the bill, but at what actual cost???

My steak, ordered medium, was definitely leaning more towards medium well, but I wasn't going to let that stop me.  I ate and enjoyed my filet to the fullest.  My chica had a sirloin and some spinach casserole dish (can't remember the name), and she was enjoying it until she got sick at the table and had to excuse herself.  Maybe it's unfair to blame her sudden stomach pains on the Ivaronne's because I sampled everything on her plate and never got sick myself.  But one can't help wonder what it was.  It had been several hours since we last ate, and food poisoning doesn't take that long to catch up to you.  Either way, because we don't have any scientific evidence of what caused the illness, we have to assume it was probably Ivaronne's. 

For dessert, we ordered the BANANAS FOSTER, which has historically been a good choice.  It used to come out to the table and they'd set it on fire like an emergency flare right in your face.  The flambe experience was what dreams were made of.  What they brought us were some underripe bananas with crappy icecream.   And the sauce... It seemed like warm squeeze bottle caramel syrup from the grocery store that was pre-warmed in the kitchen and served on the side in some sort of gravy boat.  No dark rum or banana liqueur taste AT ALL.  For $8, it made me sad as hell.

So given the total experience, last Sunday will most likely go down in the record books as my final visit to what was once one of the best fine restaurants in town.  This would have been a 1 star review, but I'm going with a 3 because I have so many fond food memories here.  =(

I admit it... I'm a bit of a South Tampa snob.  I like nice things and I don't venture out of my South Tampa bubble as much as I should.

The Citrus Park mall is really, really nice!  (Turns out International Plaza is not the only nice mall in Tampa!) The mall is clean, beautiful, has tons of great high-end and low-end stores, has a great food court and restaurants, and they have a movie theater.  This is what Westshore Plaza should strive to be!

If you haven't been to Citrus Park Town Center check it out for a nice surprise!

Came here over the holiday season while my parents were in town.  I have to be honest this place may have just won me over.  I had been there before and was not a fan!
This time however my burger was delish! The fries I soaked in malt vinegar and yes it was DELICIOUS!

So I may be coming back to Five Guys I must say though I am still a fan of Lindy's, In N Out, and Whataburger before Five Guys just personal preference.

Oh and yes this place is pricey but it was good!

This was good a few years ago, but has gone completely downhill. It seems like they are just 'phoning it in' - making no effort. Navratan Korma with a bag of frozen mixed vegetables?  Seriously? I can buy a jar of curry sauce at the supermarket and do that myself.  The rest of the food seems like it was made with no care, effort or pride. Just thrown together to make money. Don't waste yours. Go to Tiffin in Mt. Airy (bit of a drive, but they make an effort ) or Saffron on Lancaster Avenue. Or if you want to stay in Manayunk, Han Dynasty Chinese is out of this world. Similar prices to Laxmi.

We (the boyfriend and I) are huge Ciccio's fans. There is something on the menu for everyone. We come here monthly and have never been disappointed. To start, the wait staff and host/hostess are always incredibly friendly, not to mention everyone that works there looks like models. The girls are adorned is scantily clad dresses and always have a smile on their faces. The guys are always dressed nice as well (nice jeans, button down shirt). 

Onward to the food. I've become partial to a few items on their menu, which is hard for me to stray from. The southwest chicken bowl with sticky rice (or farro, which is a high protein Italian grain) is HUGE. Not only are the portions generous, but the flavors are great. I always suggest ordering the southwest chicken bowl or one of their pizzas to first timers. Now to my absolute favorite: the Water Trio Bowl with mixed greens. This offers fresh julienne jicama, three different types of sushi grade sashimi tuna, a spicy mayo sauce, and sticky rice. I always order it with the $1 upcharged mixed greens, as it becomes more filling that way. 

Their wraps are all tasty and typical come with steamed broccoli and mashed potatoes. 

I'm not a big fan of their appetizers (thus why I knocked off a star), but their calamari is good. 

When you are looking for a good meal at a great price, Ciccio's is it, as their extensive menu will offer something for everyone :)

So the Tampa Museum of Art is much smaller than your typical NY or DC museums but I believe they do make an effort to bring interesting exhibits into town. Besides the exhibits the museum host the Art After Dark a quarterly night event based on a current exhibit with a cash bar and some of these events have special art work for sale made locally. There is the Sono Cafe (by Mise en Place) and the store offers interesting memorabilia and JEWELRY...... Fridays at 4p is Art on the House (free entry) sponsored by BOA (you may do a donation upon entry). Also every 2nd Friday of the month they host the Global Lens Screenings for the Gasparilla Film Festival (foreign films). There is so much more to this Museum that you may think and options to try so it's worth the visit!!

For a cheap yet quality and delicious breakfast....this place is it.  Cozy inside with counter seating too.  Best gravy I've ever had on country fried steak or biscuits. The neighborhood it's located in is possibly sketchy at 3 am but during the day you should have no worries.   The coffee is also delicious and the service was very good.  Also has a very cool outdoor seating area out front.  

I'll be back.

Write this place off!  Was there New Years (early reservation) and we were seated at a table that is usually an isle way.  The waiters served and removed over us rather that walk around the table.  Oh, and they need to learn that you do NOT remove plates while others at the table are still eating.  Any place above McDonald's knows that! The Salmon was bad but the Crab Cakes were garbage.  The New Years menus wasn't  very - can we say - French too boot.  The soups were served in bowls half the size that our first visit had, service awful and food ugly!  The price we paid was outrageous for the experience.  Never again

Their breakfasts include "double yolk eggs", which is to say, if you ask for one egg you get two.  Their thick, fluffy toast made from homemade bread should not be missed.  Bottom line - leave your healthful habits at the door.

Needless to say, the portions at breakfast are plentiful enough to fuel you for the day, but so are the prices. Our last visit rang up just over fifty bucks for four people. More than you are likely to pay for standard breakfast food elsewhere. I really don't mind the prices because at least you feel satisfied when you leave.

If you come during the lunch hour, just order breakfast. It's what Hungry Fox does well, period.

Fitlife Foods is perfect for people such as myself who don't have time to cook or aren't a fan of cooking in general. It's much healthier than any food I would prepare or pick up on the fly. And guess what? It's tasty and not frozen! The staff is so friendly and warm and they options for every meal and every lifestyle. It is a little pricey for pre-made meals but it's worth every penny in my opinion.

Being fairly new to Tucson, I was on a full hunt for my new favorite burrito and taco stop.   The burritos  are sooooooooo good, I haven't even ordered a taco plate yet.   I find the chicken burritos irresistible, with FRESH cilantro and I ask for green chile in it too.  The tortillas set those burritos off perfectly.  It's a treat to get to stop in Saturdays for burritos.

I recently tried their sandwich and I wish it had a bit more flavor so I will stick with the rolls, soups are great too and they are easily packed to go. They added beer, wine and sake to the menu, the outdoor seating is pet friendly and the staff is always friendly and helpful.

This is one of my "go to" places for wings and too many calories. I love being able to ADD my way across the many TVs and watch several sports at one time. 

I like the size and quality of the wings. They are not pigeon wings like so many places. Chili Lime wings are a great change up. Honey BBQ is a great option for those who think ketchup is spicy. I like the home made chips and I usually hate potato chips.

They have Yuengling on draft, the service is usually good, unless they are packed. Parking is troublesome, but welcome to South Tampa.

On a lazy weekend, the GF and I decided to order in from a new pizzeria. So we decided to try  Zio's Pizzeria on 13th Street, not the one on 9th Street, which I believe is different. So, we hopped on Seamless and decided to get it delivered, at no charge!

Unfortunately, the order came thirty minutes late, though granted - the weather was terrible. So we can't fault the driver for that. This resulted in our food being lukewarm, and frankly a bit soggy.

Grandma's Pizza Sicilian ($20)
Fresh mozzarella, sauce, basil, extra virgin olive oil, grated cheese

I was hoping for something along the lines of Coscarino's in NYC. That place knows how to do a proper Queens, NY grandma sicilian. Unfortunately, the quality of the ingredients was just not up to par. The soggy bottom crust and pasty marinara was also unfortunate. However, this was a change of pace from a regular pizza pie.

Panzarotti ($4.95)

Best described as a mini calzone, this was advertised as "deep fried, crispy, and golden brown." Unfortunately, only the latter description was accurate. This was a soggy mess that did not maintain its crispiness. In fact, I could barely make out that this was fried. The inner melted mozzarella and tomato sauce were pretty standard. Should've went with a calzone ...

Wing Zings ($5.95)
