Aching for Pandora's "wing dings," I chose these - particularly because I was interested in what exactly a wing zing was. Pretty similar to just a small juicy chicken wing, these were well seasoned. However, when compared with Pandora's dings - they zings were simply not crispy enough for my taste. Mainly because the batter was a bit limp.

Onion Rings ($2)

These onion rings were cheap, crispy, but most certainly frozen. Perfectly uniform, these were a bit bland, though well fried.

Overall, Zio's Pizza brought a big meh from these lips. I expected more from a $20 sicilian and the fact that I was thinking of other restaurants speaks volumes.

Tonight I got to meet a friend for the first time in a Decade of knowing them on line, I wanted a nice place to enjoy a meal and their companionship, so knowing the reputation of Bonefish, I opted for this to be our meeting place. I was not disappointed.

For a Wednesday night, the place was hoppin'! Parking was at a premium, but the parking fairy was with me and I got a spot right up front! Our wait time was quoted as 25 minutes, but we were seated in less than 15.   At our seat, our waiter, Joe was prompt with our drink orders and to our starters, we opted for the bacon wrapped scallops with the mango salsa.  These were delivered quite quickly and were exquisitely delicious. 

We were asked if we wanted bread and pesto, which we agreed to, and I have to admit the pesto was the best bread dip I've had in -ages- so flavourful and yummy! When our mains came, Lily's chicken with augratin potatoes and asparagus, my friend had the fillet with double garlic mashed, both were served quickly and absolutely perfect. The chicken was seasoned to perfection and cooked so that it was both juicy and tasty. The steak was asked for as medium rare and we could find no fault. 

As a treat, we chose dessert too, the Chocolate Creme Brulee. There was certainly enough in this dish for two people! It was so tasty! My friend had never had creme brulee before and now he's a fan!

I can guarantee you that I'll try another location of Bonefish in the future!

Bar Louie is a fun urban bar/restaurant in International Plaza that offers a large menu selection of appetizers, bar food, burgers, sandwiches and more.  The drink menu includes many tasty martinis (my favorite martini is the "Fizz" with St. Germain and Prosecco) and features 48 beers on tap as well as other bottled selections, and wines by the glass or bottle.  

Why only three stars?  I like the atmosphere and the food slightly more at The Pub in International Plaza.  Bar Louie is fun a fun spot to hear a live band, but the bar can get too loud at times so it's not a great spot if you want to have a conversation.

Check out their daily happy hour specials. Many of the drinks and appetizers are half off. They also have $1 burger nights every Tuesday (doesn't inlcude cheese or other additional condiments, but still a great deal!) and $1 draft nights every Wednesday.

I'm not happy with Dao's Tai Pan.  We were regulars here for years.  We come in often and ordered carry out often.  The last time I ordered carry out they charged us for an item, but didn't put it in the box.  We didn't realize it until we got home.  I called the restaurant as soon as we realized it and the person who answered at the restaurant couldn't believe that they had made a mistake.  She tried to convince me that they actually put the item in the box despite my telling them, no it was not there.  She kept trying to convince me they gave us the item, but finally she realized their mistake and said they would put my husband's name by the cashier so that the next time he makes an order they would take that off the bill.

So we called for another carry out order about 2 weeks later.  The person who answered had no idea what we were talking about.  I explained what happened the last time and they just refused to honor their word.  I know it would be hard for restaurants if customers are lying about not receiving an item and the restaurant has no way of knowing.  But they said they were going to reimburse us and they didn't.  

Sorry Dao's.  You just lost our patronage.  Your pho is too salty anyway.
I don't think I will go back to this place ever. Waitress was very rude and service was horrible.

Friends don't let friends spend fourbucks on Charbucks!
That said, I occaisionally splurge on a $3.00 iced latte served up by Brie (sp??) at the Passero's in Suburban station. Or a lovely Cafe au Lait for $2.45 in the afternoon.

The roasted-bitter espresso they use still has a good flavor and the service far supasses that of Charbucks.

Passero's is some of the best coffee in the city (that and La Colombe) - so I will be back.

Budget permitting. Love it here!

I have given Jazzy's multiple chances to redeem themselves, but no more. Absolutely the worst BBQ in town. In fact, it's not even BBQ. Extremely overcooked meats and fairly decent collards is about it. And by overcooked, I mean by at least an extra 30 to 60 minutes. The chicken and pork are consistently dry. The sauces are mediocre at best. I am truly clueless as to how anyone can think this is awesome BBQ. It may smell great from the outside, but it is absolutely terrible. Don't waste your money.

This is absolutely the WORST company I have ever dealt with in ANY capacity!  Had a main water line break at a rental property I have. It was close to 5:00pm so I called Al Coronado since I had seen them on TV. They sent someone out within the hour. The service tech (I.e. Scam Scout) called me and told me that the whole line needed to be replaced and that it would cost $4,100 + tax!! He said they could install a temporary line so the tenants would have water. I asked him if I could just pay them for the temp line and get some bids in the morning. He said "No... They don't do that". He said that I had to accept the entire job otherwise they would remove the temp line and my tenants would be left without water. Before I arrived to sign his "proposal", he dug a small hole (in mud, mind you), pulled out the section of pipe that had broken (with his hand) and that was it. After I signed the "proposal", he became very giddy. The next morning I called my regular plumber and he told me he could do the same job for $1600. I immediately called Al Coronado back and cancelled the entire job. About an hour later, the Scam Scout called me to verify that I cancelled. I told him yes I had. He said he would still have to charge me for the "labor" involved in digging the hole and attaching the temp line. (The temp line consisted of connecting a ball valve to the water main and attaching a garden hose from the main to the house). $485!! I actually didn't think they would have the balls to charge me that much for what they did, but a couple of days later there was a $515 charge on my credit card. I called to complain and after much BS, I was told that the tech was "written up" and I would receive $100 off the bill. I was not happy but he said that was the best he could do. I could go on and on, but suffice to say this company is a RIPOFF from top to bottom. Do yourself a favor and read the reviews on these guys wherever you can - including Angie's List. You won't want to use them either...

One of my favorite go to spots every time I have guests in from out of town. The hot sausage here is as good as it gets, and on the rare occasions I try something else on their menu, it's also been amazing. If you live in Philly, and don't know this place, you should question your whole existence.

I've been coming here since they opened. The food is great and they use all fresh ingredients. Their burgers and cheese steaks are incredible. They use LeBus rolls. They have great weekly specials. The portions are all very large. I can't say enough about this place. It's a little off the beaten path, but if you're on
Umbria st. you should check it out.

Oh, my absolute favorite part of the entire meal was the heavenly Grand Marnier souffle!  Adored the fact that they had several small plate options to choose from.  Being my first time there, I ordered several of them - - oh, how I love variety.  It's been several weeks since I tried this place, so truly don't remember which small plate was my favorite.  Can I go back JUST for the souffle!?  mmmm.

I cater in Liborio's for my team at work quite frequently.  I have also eaten here on a couple occasions.  Here is the thing about Liborio's:  COME HUNGRY.  Every "portion" will feed 3 people and it is INEXPENSIVE.

My recommendations:  The pork with black beans and rice and plantains.  Sounds like a pretty typical Cuban meal, but sometimes those are the best ones.  The pork is incredibly tender and topped with onions.  The beans and rice are delicious and the plantains are some of the best I've had.

If you go during the lunch rush, be prepared for a short wait in a cafeteria type line.  Although a bit off the beaten path, this place is delicious to the max!

Love this place!!!! The owner and Chef are friendly and amazing! Great Atmosphere, very intimate! Food tastes amazing and is perfectly made! Unreal wine selection! seriously its flipping amazing!!!!
This place is moire of what this town needs!!!

Really nice staff, accommodating.
Food is...acceptable, chain pub type food. 
Good place to go & watch a game, have a drink & a snack :)

Florida State Fair Review: Awful time getting in, but it was pretty cool once inside.

Parking is free, which is awesome, but you can end up parking pretty far from the entrance.  DO NOT BRING ANY FOOD OR DRINK.  They are really draconian about that.  I had a water bottle that I bring with me everywhere and they made me go back to my car to put it away (note: we had parked really far from the entrance).  I originally thought that they do not allow drinks, because they don't want any alcohol inside.  However, I offered to empty my water bottle, because I didn't want to walk the huge distance back and the lady told me that I wasn't even allowed an empty water bottle.  This was a huge pain.  It is obvious they want to milk every dollar out of you for food and beverages.

After making the trek back and forth from my car to dispose my water bottle, one of the two tickets would not scan.  We bought them online and printed them, but one of them wouldn't scan.  We stood there as the woman tried to enter the code and continued to mess up three times.  On the forth try, another woman worker at the entrance came up and told her that she should just let us go, since this wasn't our fault.  Thank god for that other woman, or else I'm sure we would have been standing there a lot longer.  All that being said, I entered the state fair being extremely irate.

This fair is huge.  There are a ton of food vendors, but many of them are repeated throughout the fair, so there is less variety than it appears.  By the end, I had a philly cheesesteak, steak on a stick, chicken on a stick, and kettle corn.  Obviously, you go here for the food.

I also got to see a circus performance.  I think that made the trip worth it.  The performance is free and it was a pretty professional show.  However, people line up almost an hour before to get a good seat.  We got there about twenty minutes before the performance and got in no problem and still got a pretty good seat.  

There were tons of other entertainment going on, but none of it seemed worth watching.

No fair beats the NYS Fair with it's big ol' wine court, state fair chicken (Baker's Chicken Coop y'all!), and dairy barn with the giant butter sculpture and desserts so good you'd kick your mother for one.  Still, the FL State Fair is pretty fun.  I was shocked that we were not charged for parking which was convenient, quick, and right off the highway.  There was a lot to see and we took every chance to see all the animals.  I saw breeds of cows I had never seen before (Guernseys, Jerseys, and Holsteins are prevalent in New York state which is #3 in the country for dairy production), adorable rabbits (we got to pet them too!), pigs, horses, and even dogs (we also got to pet them).  Especially cool was the "DiscOnnected K9s" which was a really cool show of rescued border collies do some amazing frisbee tricks.  

There was a nice variety of foods with all the typical fair "fare" as well as your obligatory swamp food (gator).  We got lobster rolls and crab quesadillas that were awesome.  The red velvet funnel cake is not all that special though.  There were plenty of places to sit and rest, grab a beer or wine, and also a lake in the middle that the kids could take a paddle boat for a spin.  All in all, quite fun for $12.00 at the gate.  The people watching is priceless and for the best views, stay after dark when the real characters come out from under their rocks.

Like some other people who have posted reviews, I am also from Texas.  This food, while not as good as the comida in Texas, is very tasty!  I have never had a problem with the service either.  I typically order the combo fajitas and add grilled jalapenos to it.  The amount of food, for the money, is a great deal!  I can eat on it for about 3 days.  If staff from Vallarta's reads this, maybe they should work on their customer service issues, as it sounds like many people have had poor experiences, based on service issues.

I tried out Jazzys for the first time tonight- the place had a real charm to it... Kinda a hole in the wall but still classy. Even later in the evening (9pm) it still had an ok crowd and the barbecue was still loaded with meat. I had the pulled pork dinner with baked beans and cole slaw- the slaw was out of this world, and the beans weren't bad either. My only suggestion would be for them to look at the ventilation system- when I left the place my clothes smelt like I had been at a campfire all night.

I used to live in Seminole Heights, and there are alot of great local restaurants in the area, and I unfortunately have to say that the Front Porch grill is my wife and I's LEAST favorite of the bunch.  

Being in an old house where you can eat outside on the porch (or indoors if you prefer), the atmopshere is pretty cool, but that's really about where my positive comments end.

The food, especially for the higher prices, is unfortunately very hit & miss (and I'd say more "miss" than "hit").  My wife ordered a steak that was extremely tough and gristly.  We usually grill steaks at home that are much better, and less expensive.  The fettucini alfredo that I had was extremely bland - almost like it was just noodles with no sauce on it.  The burger I had there once - bleh.  Just not good flavor at all.

My wife and I are serious beer connissseuers as well, and the beer selection here just is not much write home about, either.

Sorry to say, I can't really recommend the front porch grill, unless you just like to sit outside and enjoy the atmosphere.  Then again, you can do that at most Seminole Heights restaurants.  I'd suggest trying Ella's or the Refinery first.  Or drive down to Tampa Heights and give Lee's Grocery (really a pizza and beer place) a shot.

why have i not learned of this service before? loving this!!!! last night was valentine's day. uuuugggghhhh. this made up holiday brings everyone into the city. i was meeting my bestie in rittenhouse for cocktails. i called for a cab and was on hold because of all the dinner dates going on. thank god my son was here. he gave me a ride or i would probably still be waiting for a cab. my girlfriend got to rittenhouse the same time as me. both of us coming from deep in south philly. she said "oh i dont do cabs anymore. uber is how i roll". she explained it to me, i downloaded the app, put in all my info and forgot about it. at the end of our night, we utilized my new app. 6 mins later, a fancy towncar was outside with a nice gentleman driver holding our door open. luxury. i love how you can see on your phone where the car is en route. you can also see how many uber cars are near by. ive only used it once, so i still have more to learn about this service. my mind has been blown! no more waiting forever, for a smelly car and some times rude and/or non english speaking cabbies. you can rate your driver after the ride is over!!!! this thing is great. if you use taxis, switch now.

This is one of those times where I feel like I really don't have anything to say about Retrospect that hasn't been said before... so I'll keep it short and sweet.

The Good:
-Neat, amply-spaced, color coordinated collection of clothing, accessories and housewares organized by color
-Friendly staff that greets patrons and compliments your selections
-Fair prices, bought a set of 6 boyscout mugs for $12, a dress for $16 and a men's button up for $14

The Bad:
-Housewares and Jewelry selections are too small

The Ugly:
- Fuzzy dolman sweater with flower and gold bead applique. Keep it cute, not ironic, that's what's working for you, Retrospect.

In summary, Retrospect's 3 star average rating is unfair. I feel like many reviewers are overlooking what type of profit it takes to really sustain a business. You can't provide a carefully curated, color coordinated, on-trend selection with out selling merch at a somewhat substantial mark-up to cost.

I have to say I am on the fence with this...which means I definitly have to go back.  I ordered fried ravioli's as an appetizer which were OK.  I didn't particularly like the sauce you dip it with, I hate to to say this, but it tasted like it was from a can.  For an entree I ordered their salmon with lobster ravioli.  The salmon was AMAZING!  They glaze it in teriyaki then put some type of cream sauce over it, so it was salty and creamy which was a perfect combo.   The ravilo's wer EH (but I think I just wasn't in the mood for it).  I am usually not big on bread, but I probably finished a whole roll myself, it was THAT good!  They douse it in garlic butter and romano cheese!  I was hesitant to order their tiramisu because the only experiences I've had, the liquor over powered the dessert and it tasted horrible.  Well I am glad I did because I am in love!  The waitor we had could've been better, he wasnt very attentive and looked confused when I asked him to recommend me a red wine.  All in all, you should try it!

I still have not learned my lesson. I stopped in there to buy cookies for my son because they always had suck good cookies. I bought a box of them off the counter. Well, I get home, open them and they are steal! and there were xmas cookies hidden under the other cookies. Yes, xmas cookies hidden in bottom of box. Such sneaks! So sad what this place has become. Seriously? Its the middle of february, past the middle. Very disheartening!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

We go here about once a year, for special occasions. Yes, it is expensive, but the atmosphere and food are quality and it is worth the splurge. We split a "Chef's Choice" antipasto plate to start, and it was a nice assortment of meats and cheeses - the meatball was my favorite item on the platter. For my meal I had the Frutti di Mare, a mixed seafood dish in "spicy" tomato sauce.  Not spicy in the least bit, and the lobster was dreadfully overlooked, but the clams, shrimp, scallops, and mussels were delicious enough to make up for those shortcomings. Dessert was a moist chocolate espresso cake-yum! Dinner for 2, with a bottle of Moët Imperial, was $200+, but they will definitely see us again.

I have to say that I was underwhelmed with this place.  I knew the new building location for the museum had been constructed relatively recently, but I never got around to going to it.  When you're walking up to the building it looks enormous.  The architecture of the building itself is quite unusual and interesting to look at.  When you walk inside, the first floor is what I would describe as "barren".  There's a small cafe and a few tables in the far corner, but for the most part it's just a large section of emptiness.  I don't understand why they don't have something on display here.  Anything would be an improvement.  What about art from local children?  That would be perfect considering that there's a children's museum nearby.

After you've bought your ticket you head up some cool looking stairs to the exhibit area.  I was actually quite surprised at how little was on display.  We read every plaque for every piece and we were done easily in less than an hour.  There was not a single piece that had that "wow" factor for me either.  Honestly I wish we would have gone to the Salvador Dali Museum again instead of coming here.

I ADORE this local bookstore!  When I worked on the avenue I would try to stop in at least once a month and now I just try to get in there whenever  I can.  They have the best variety of books one can ask for and the best part is they even give away free books!  They have a section of free books you can choose from when you make a purchase of other books.  I try to shop for everyone in this little spot because they have so many trinkets, toys, notecards, journals and much more, basically something for everyone.  And if you refuse a bag they donate to local charities - win win!

I tried Char's for the first time this evening. I found it by accident (like so many of the best discoveries in life)... I was craving Thai food, and found the restaurant on yelp from my phone (thanks yelp)! I placed a takeout order over the phone when I was about 15 minutes from the restaurant, and it was ready to pick up when I got there. 

I'm a big pad Thai fan, and the pad Thai was some of the best I've ever had. I also had the chicken satay appetizer because I love peanut sauce. Well, let me just say that both the pad Thai and the chicken satay were excellent- perfectly prepared and melt in your mouth with flavor! I even have enough food left over for what seems like the next three days! :)  I will definitely go back to Char's soon, and maybe next time will try the sit down service because I love and crave a good Thai coffee too!

Prices are reasonable, and service seemed very friendly. I highly recommend giving this place a try!

Food is truly mediocre. Fish and chips were disappointing. I'd go to Four Green Fields any day over BOBs. 

A couple of interesting events included the Trivia night (Wed) with prizes to be won. And coin fed games for kids.

My family has been using Desert Toyota for years and never had a problem. My entire family has purchased vehicles, both new and used. We've always been treated with respect and walked out of there confident we were given a great deal.

The service area is top notch and I completely trust my service advisor Erin. They take care of my needs and are always very helpful. 

Wouldn't consider buying or servicing a car anywhere else.

They always have specials through Groupon, or other dining sites, so just keep that in mind. This place can get pretty pricey especially with all of their yummy tapas. I will say, all of their food was great. However, since this place is small, it is incredibly loud. It was a Friday night that I had gone. I was happy to get a corner seat where there were not as many people but it was quite loud. You will find yourself yelling over the table. There's a few tables outside, but it faces the parking lot. If I were to come again, I would just go there so I could actually have a conversation rather than just hear the background noise.
Otherwise the food is great and maybe I would try an off night if I were to go again.

This is the type of place that as soon as you walk in the bartender makes you feel at home and like you've lived around the corner for years. Came here just to get to know the area and have a drink after too much amish delights on the other side of town and fell in love.

I am one of those annoying touristy people that loves to try the funky bar menu drinks YET I don't like them to be too sweet or watered down. Needless to say, the bartender was extremely helpful in having me pick a drink that I would like (the spiked lemonade) AND made some modifications to it, so that by the end of my first Mason Jar's worth - I was feeling pretty nice.

Add to the friendly atmosphere and reasonable prices some R.Kelley that was playing and then the random guy that started belching it out  ("I beleive I can flyyyyyyyyy")- this made me a beleiver.

Finally, I had asked our very awesome (and cute) bartender for a different shot to celebrate my boo's PhD acceptance and not only did she make some awesome Midori concoctions.....she gave it to us on the house. Cheers to that!

I love Evos, and I love this location. The food is fantastic, and I have never had to wait more than 5 minutes (so maybe other reviewers came in on an off day?). I havent tried everything on the menu, but I have been satisfied with what I have gotten. The beef burger is good, not great. I mean, its not a 5 Guys by any means, but its tasty and healthier, the Champion burger, on the other hand, is fantastic. Its one of those meatless, but tastes like meat, type of burgers (like a Morningstar Griller type of thing). The veggie burger is good. Its more like a Garden burger. The chicken strips are fantastic, and I am really picky when it comes to chicken. The chicken is all white meat with none of the nasty gross parts (like the clear foul goop you find with most chicken strips). You wouldn't know it wasn't deep fried, honest. It tastes great on the Thai salad too (and the salads are quite large). I like the fries and always get them, but I rarely get to eating them because I am usually too full :).

Bacon Burger: Meh
Anchor Fries: AMAZING, Fat girl's dream. Yum, num.
Pretzel bites...not worth $6. 
Mozz Sticks: Crunchy Goodness

Great spot if you really enjoy diner food which I do. The omelets are thick and full of cheesy goodness. The place is really crowded but they turn tables really quick. The prices are really cheap perfect for a hangover or just a good greasy meal.

Very good Asian market specializing in Vietnamese food. Excellent produce selection including seasonal fruits and veggies. Pretty clean, fair prices and good selection. A banh mi, pho, and lunch counter Vietnamese restaurant is next door.

So I went to Aldi when it first opened and I hated it.  I was like, "Where in the HELL are the name brands?  This place is full of generic crap!"  True story.

I returned at the request of my chica a few months later on a challenge.  See, I was a Target shopper.  I have the Target debit card that rewards you with a 5% discount off your total bill, which can be quite helpful when buying a few hundred bucks worth of food and supplies.  So she said, you get what you need from Target, and let's go to Aldi and we shall see which is cheaper.  Challenge accepted.  I bought some items that totaled about $80.  We then went to Aldi, and she bought double the amount of items, some being the same types of items, and her bill was $75.  From that moment, I've been hooked.  

I think one of the things I like those most is their cheese selection.  They always tend to have really good cheeses.  What I don't like is their tomatoes.  Why in the hell would they have Canadian tomatoes when Ruskin is right down the street?  Those Canadian tomatoes are as flavorless as their culture.  Yeah, I said it.

I could really type up a bunch of words about my good experiences here, but I'll refrain.  Take a quarter, a debit card (no credit) and some grocery bags and get your shop on!

Downside to this location is that it's sort of ghetto compared to other locations in the area.  I find this store to be unorganized and not as well-stocked as others, but it's the closest to home, so I shop here a lot.

This is the best Thai I have found in Tampa. I have never eaten in, just ordered and picked up to go. Everything I've ordered has been solid, and better than the other Thai places I have tried in the area. So not the best ever, but I am a fan and if you are craving Thai it's a good choice.

So fun! This is my second year attending the MOSI Renaissance Festival, and I definitely see this becoming a yearly must!

Most of the shows are entertaining, but pretty cheesy- I usually watch the first 10 minutes then mosey on over to the shops! Lots and lots of cool things to be seen- shoes, clothes, crafts, jewelry, hats, they've got some thing for everyone, as long as you've got a little coin to spare! The shops are certainly not cheap! 

The food is good- a wee bit over priced, but not outrageously so, and the quality is surprisingly good for fair type food. The Bee Sting is a tasty must if you're looking to imbibe- 2/3 mead, 1/3 cider. Yum!  

I definitely recommend hitting the Ren. Fest if you're looking for something to do that's a little different! Oh, and the people watching can't be beat!

Brunch has always been one of my favorite meals and since I became a mother, my days of brunches are few and far between. When friend and fellow blogger Megan (I Run For Wine), invited me to be part of a monthly get together over brunch, I was ecstatic.  Megan, along with these fabulous ladies: Katie (Ruffles & Truffles), Leslie (Dash of Les) and Rachel (Runners Tales) formed this group as a way to see who has the best brunch in the bay area. I adore every one of these girls and am very excited to be a part of this special group. 

Our first stop of the brunch tour was at South Tampa hot spot, Ciccio's. This was my first time having brunch there and I was anxious to sample their house specialties. Brunch is not brunch without a cocktail and we started off with their Ciccio Bloody Mary. Made from scratch with a nice amount of spice, it was a great way to start to my day. The bacon was a delicious bonus.

Our first dish was their Tuscan Bowl. Ricotta blended scrambled eggs with fresh basil, vine ripe tomatoes and melted Parmesan, it was fantastic. This was the perfect dish for me in that it was incredibly tasty and high in protein. I would order this again in a heartbeat. Filled with flavor and packed with protein, this is a must-have at Ciccio's.

Next up was their Turkey Club Bowl and this was another highlight of my meal. Made with scrambled eggs, roasted house-made turkey, sausage and avocado, this one knocks it out of the park in the flavor department. 

As many of you know, I am a California Girl. I grew up eating tomatoes and avocados on a daily basis, so it was no surprise that I would enjoy our next selection, California Dreaming. Ciccio's did me proud with this dish that combines scrambled eggs, organic spinach, vine ripe tomatoes and fresh sliced avocado. Simply amazing, this also had farro at the bottom of the bowl.  All of their bowls can be served with your choice of farro or homefries. I love homefries but the farro really won me over. I think they should name this the "Tracy Bowl." 

The next dish we tried was my absolute favorite.  The Natural Chicken & Egg Sandwich Club is a heavenly creation of chicken, egg, avocado, bacon and garlic aioli on grilled sourdough bread. I seriously want to hug whoever created this masterpiece. The flavor combination of this sandwich is just sensational. I could have easily devoured the whole thing so thank goodness we were sharing. 

Next we tried the Lanza Burrito. A fantastic blend of scrambled eggs, homefries, bacon, black beans, cheddar and jack cheese sauce and then topped with avocado salsa, this breakfast burrito rocks.  I must say, Ciccio's won me over with this and I am happy that I found a place to cure my burrito craving in Tampa.

Another favorite of the day was the Big Chorizo Egg Wrap.  Eggs, chorizo, avocado and jack cheese all stuffed into your choice of a whole wheat or flour tortilla and paired with a Marsala or ketchup dipping sauce.  I really liked this and tried it with hot sauce. I love all things spicy and a nice hot sauce pairs well with the eggs and chorizo. Do not even get me started on the homefries. Like that famous brand of potato chips, nobody can eat just one.

Was I full at this point? Heck yeah. Was I going to surrender my fork? No way! We had two types of pancakes to taste and I have never met a pancake that I didn't like. The first was their Oatmeal Pancakes which is a generous portion of three oatmeal pancakes with butter and maple syrup. Fantastic! 

Our final dish made me squeal with delight......Oatmeal Chocolate Chip and Strawberry Pancakes. These were like three fluffy pieces of heaven and the fresh strawberries made them really stand out. I became enamored with the flavor that was sweet yet not too overbearing. Growing up, I used to look forward to going to church on Sundays and then breakfast immediately after. My brother and I were obsessed with pancakes and would order different types and share. His favorite were always chocolate chip, so these really reminded me of my childhood. I know my son would devour these so it looks like a brunch trip to Ciccio's for the kiddos and I will be in the very near future.

If you are looking for a fantastic brunch experience with tons of tasty options, be sure to check out Ciccio's!

Thus is a great place to start and spend happy hours.  Great deals! Good food! Nice accommodating staff!  Just an all around good happy hour bar to relax at and start off a night or just keep most of your money and have a few drinks with food.

Two words: Pancake Sandwich!!!!

The 45 minute wait was agonizing, but well worth it!  Our table of three ordered the Tuscan Frittata, Eggs Benedict, and a platter of eggs and bacon.  We also ordered the Pancake Sandwich for the table to split. (After smelling bacon for 45 minutes we were starving!!!)

When our food arrived at the table we realized ordered way too much food. The portions at Pinky's are huge!!!  After eating a third of the pancake sandwich I was stuffed.  I ended up barely touching my Tuscan Fritatta and had to bring it home.  Luckily the fritatta reheated nicely and was just as delicious as it was at brunch.  All of the food we ordered was fantastic, especially the Eggs Benedict. The highlight of the meal was the Pancake Sandwich with scrambled eggs, ham, and cheese sandwiched between two fluffy buttermilk pancakes.  Once I added the syrup it was a combination of sweet and savory goodness that I never wanted to end.  

I can see why this restaurant draws such a huge brunch crowd: the food really is amazing!

I can describe Vietnamese food as a mix between Chinese & Thai.  We ordered their beef co*m bò nu*ò*ng and chicken curry dish.  The beef was so tender and the curry type dish was bursting with flavor.  I didn't like their spring rolls, it was a clear jelly-type casing with veggies, shrimp & fried pork skin.  Overall This place is definitely delicious!

The best tacos in AZ and the US I am from Mexico City and is very hard to find a good taqueria in the United States that resembles Mexico City popular foods like "tacos de suadero" which some how Juanitos has being able to equal with their crane asada tacos :) 

If you want a know how real Mexican food taste like go here I repeat real Mexican food NO SONORAN Mexican food 

Great place very affordable and clean 
Ask anything in the menu and you will love it.

Personally the individual tacos is the way to go.  The sopes are very good as well and not to mention the soups for a cold rainy afternoon.

I do enjoy the food here.  I would skip the salad and the pita, and just order a side dish, a soup, or a meal.  Good flavors, feels healthy, and even though the lines can get long at lunch, they move fairly quickly.

This place is horrible! We went in and the Indian hostess told us that it'll be a 35 minute wait and we said okay we will wait. Turns out we had to put our names on there first, but she didnt even tell us. This is our first time here so we didnt know. She saw us waiting there for like the longest time and she didnt say anything!!! We were wondering why other people that came after us were getting tables. We went up there and asked her, and she told us to wait another 35 minutes. SCREW THAT! THANKS FOR WASTING OUR
TIME. WE BASICALLY WAITED FOR NOTHING! This place sucks and they need to hire a better hostess!

The best thing about their dishes is that there is not to much sauce.  the amount of sauce was just right.  Their rice was also very good - it had cardamom in it which made it especially flavorful.  The tandori roti was also delicious.  Don't get the rosewater lassi. It's just sweet lassi with no rosewater - so I was super disappointed.  I don't like how they add ice to the lassi.  When the ice starts to melt throughout dinner, you'll know what I mean.  The samosas were pretty good. 

They do need to fix that bathroom door of theirs.  I didn't like how I got locked in there and they had to bust the door open - making a spectacle.

I would say it's between a 3-4 star place

I couldn't find something to complain about here if I tried!

While staying onsite at the Hyatt I decided to come here for what would have been my 27 year anniversary (Hubby passed few weeks prior) It was odd at first to be eating alone but looking around I notice not only tables of couples but solo diners here on business so it made me feel at ease.

I dined on two seperate evenings the first evening with Joan as my server. Rumour has it she's a staple here and I can see why..charming..classing...a great server..just wonderful.

So on first trip I started with a bottle of Stags Leap Artemis ($125) not a bad price for the surroundings, I pay only slightly less at Bacoms. Had to try the Caprese salad with the house made mozzarella and a balsamic glaze ($12) wow, to die for.

Moving on I had the tagliatalle bolognese (appetizer  size $14-but you can get dinner size as well) Oh my, homemade pasta, a delicious thick ragu of a sauce. Just perfect

Ended the evening with the Grand Martini served by Joan at the bar. 

On my second visit two nights later with Alisa as the server (gorgeous gentle woman) I had the same wine (I'm hooked I admit it) and then the asparagus with duck egg and shaved truffle for appetizer ($16) so good,so fresh and you can't go wrong with truffles. Couldnt resist and had the same bolognese for dinner as it was that good.

I had met a woman their the night prior and she joined me for dinner and Alisa was kind enough to bring us out dessert sampling, gratis, how sweet (literally!!)

After dinner I went to the bar to have the same Martini for dessert, served by the handsome and charming Marco which was perfectly made and provided pleasant company before my turn in. (They really care about consistancy here as when it tasted just a tad different he promptly got with Joan and tweeked it so the second was perfect)

Would I come back here? Try to keep me away!! Dont feel bading dining alone and don't save it for special occassions, it's not that pricey and it is SO well worth it!!

This review is just for a quick round of drinks...

This place has all the old fashioned charm that I love in a bar. The old tiled floor, dark wood walls and dimly lit bar...all while the smell of fresh roast beef sandwiches filled the air. This was the perfect spot of a late afternoon Sunday drink.

I just love this place.. The coal fire oven provides a unique flavor to the pizza. All the pizzas are great along with the roasted  wings. Awesome service and great staff. I am pleased each time I come here.

Love the color arranged racks. Love how it's arranged up front with the belts, glasses, etc. It's pretty clean, well organized. But on my multiple trips in, I haven't found anything that I was interested in. I realize this is a risk with any thrift store - you get what they get. But even when I did see something I might consider, the prices were higher than I'm used to for thrift stores, so I skipped it. I'll still continue to stop in every once in a while, but I fear my results will be similar. If you're thrifting, it's worth stopping in, as you might just get lucky. But you're odds are much better at other thrift stores in the city.

You know what to expect here. 

Things that were different to me compared to other PFChangs was that the bar was much smaller (15 spots?) with 2 TVs. They had other tables in that section, but they always seemed to be full or not available.

They also have an outside eating area, but looks out to the parking lot.

Service was friendly at the bar.
Love this place! The food is delish! The Asian wings and cheese steak egg rolls are amazing!! The drinks are super cheap! And not to mention they have the best bartenders everr!!!

Great addition to the neighborhood. Great coffees and teas and an excellent place to study or work. Free and reliable wifi and comfortable atmosphere.

Tasty homemade tuna sandwich as well!

Food was delicious, atmosphere is amazing, service was poor. 

Came here with friends to catch up over dinner on a weeknight. Carne Chophouse is located in the Centro Espanol near Centro Ybor. The place is amazing with high ceilings and ornate details. 

We were promptly seated. Our server greeted us and did not mention any specials. We found out later they had $2 martinis. $2!! I was trying to save money so I opted not to drink that night. I would have totally sprung for a $2 martini though! Server fail. 

My friends ordered drinks and we were brought bread with garlic butter to start. My friend had a glass of wine and it was a very generous pour. For dinner, I ordered the Salmon with sun dried tomato beurre blanc. It came with a starter (salad or soup) and a side item. I chose the wedge salad with tomatoes, onion, bacon, and blue cheese dressing. I wished the dressing had a little bit more blue cheese flavor but other than that it was a good, fresh salad. For my side, I chose broccoli and ordered an additional side baked potato. The salmon was great. Cooked perfectly with a very tasty yet not overpowering sauce. 

I also tried my friend's Steak Au Poivre. It was amazing! A big sirloin, nicely flavored with peppercorns and a touch of mushroom. Service really started to go down hill once we had our entrees. We basically didn't see our server again. I couldn't get a water refill to save my life and all the tables around us were piled up with dirty dishes. Not exactly what you want to see. 

An FYI on the bathroom situation: there is a onesie for men and a onesie for women with a sign that more bathrooms are down the hall in the lobby. When my friend couldn't use the one in the restaurant she went into the lobby to find that bathroom doesn't have a lock on the door. Oopsies!

Overall, I really enjoyed the food and the location. I love the old buildings and architecture in Ybor City. I'll be back. I just hope the service improves.

Great place to see local and national talent.  Over the food is good but I feel partially over priced and I dislike they include automatic gratuity to each bill and the seating is really crowded.  I do like to come here now and again to have a laugh so it is great for that and the wait staff has always been courteous.

Citrus Park is one of my favorite malls in the area. My most favorite is Wiregrass because I live that way, and the other favorite is International Mall. This mall has so much to offer, so clean, and has great deals. I love getting the chance to go here.

Sandwiched in-between a sea of nondescript auto repair shops and Skate Country on east 22nd Street, is Kenny D's. It's about the only restaurant I noticed in this area. I discovered the place by accident today (on yelp- thanks Mark S) because of the need for an auto emissions test, and a quick sandwich. 

The sandwich was much better than I expected it would be, considering the hole-in-the wall that the place is. I enjoyed a turkey sub (I could only eat half of a half order). This huge and tasty sandwich was stuffed with turkey and cheese, served on a fresh, flavorful baguette (from La Baguette, according to the owner), and brimming with lettuce, tomatoes, pickles, and mayo.  Honestly, it was one of the best turkey sandwiches I've had in ages! I didn't get any of the grease mentioned in some of the reviews, but I'm sure- judging by the looks of the place, they know how to dish out greasy food too. The booths seem to be coated with a layer of it!

I would be giving this place 4 stars, except it seemed to me to be in need of a good cleaning and some general modernizing. It's Tucson though, so my expectations are not that high. To its credit, Kenny D's fits right in with its east 22nd Street location. Based on how great this sandwich was, I will definitely be back when I'm in the area. Great sandwiches, and a friendly owner. Very good local find...

My wife and I love this place we look forward to it every year. We get to be our full on Nerd selfs. From looking at all the costumes the drinking beers eating the Scotch eggs it's just a blast all day for me and my lady.

I've been coming here for years and I still maintain that Tia's has the best chips and salsa that I've ever had.  I am nuts about it.  Some places will only give you one bowl of chips and then charge you for a refill.  Not Tia's.  When your bowl is empty they will bring you another one if you ask.  Some places will bring out stale chips to you.  Not Tia's.  The chips are always warm and fresh.  If you get a to-go order they'll give you a gimongous (name trademarked by me) bag of chips.  The salsa tastes great and is not too chunky.

Their fajitas are delicious and come out on a sizzling platter.  They come with a side of rice and beans and assorted toppings.  You can actually substitute the rice and beans for other things such as vegetables (or even french fries!).  Wrapping all that up in a nice, warm tortilla (which the sign on the wall says that they make from scratch) = yum.  The amount of meat you get with each fajita serving is quite generous.  Monday is buy one get one free day for fajitas, which means that you can get a ridiculous amount of food for not much money.  They don't charge for soda refills either.  Their fried ice cream is a tasty, unusual desert.  Normally I have to have someone roll me out of here when I leave because I'm so stuffed.

If you're a sports fan the bar area has several large televisions all tuned to various sporting events.  You can also sit outside if the weather is nice.

I'll be honest.  With the people I know this place elicits either a "love it" or "hate it" reaction.  I am squarely in the "love it" category.  Is it Monday yet?
The shrimp pasta is incredible and is one of my all-time favorite dishes anywhere. Love, love, love it!!! Friendly staff, casual atmosphere and very reasonable prices.
This place is awful. It is hard to see how it stays in business serving food this bad.

This is a great place for Barbq. Grab some pickles and peppers when you walk in at the bar. I love the ribs and the beef. The sandwiches are generous, and the sweet tea keeps flowing. The sauce is theirs and is peppery and placed on your table hot, so it doesn't cool off your meat. 

This place has been here for years and years, and South Tampa locals love it.  

Don't overlook breakfast here. the Ham is great with eggs, and the strawberry jelly served in little creamer pots. I love the biscuits.

Ok so the museum is really beautiful and I enjoyed it.  I don't know if these other reviews are talking about the same location?  I felt there was a LOT of art and I didn't see anyone walking around.  I really enjoyed myself but I do think it should be free to the public like a library but only because of it's educational value that I feel would be a benefit to society especially those who wouldn't be able to pay the fees to enter.  


Please beware----

Stay out of the Poe Garage, they have  people running around writing out $30 parking violations.  If you do park there, look up at the ceiling, that's where they put the reserved signs.
The 'Flavor of the Day' is the best part of my morning, hands down. The service is awesome and FAST, and the people are really swell.  Passero's is definitely my go-to coffee place!

Been here a few times. This last trip was the Tampa Bay Pet Expo. It was smaller than the previous year, so it was just all right. If you are looking for something all about your pets, this is the place. It's nice that they always have some types of expos here for whatever tickles your fancy. Parking is easy, but there is always a parking fee, boo. That's okay.

Along with the Walmart in Norristown this is the worst store I have ever been to.  I am writing this review in the check out line which takes a minimum of 15 minutes per trip.  Avoid at all cost!  Completely inept management!  Long lines, under stocked.

I've been coming here for awhile. I belong to a group that has meetings here. This is a perfect dive bar- unassuming, unpretentious, a little rough around the edges but clean, good beer, bar food, no french fries though. They always have a nice selection on tap and more in bottles. Nothing too fancy, but Dogfishhead and Troegs are regualrly on tap. 

The food is really good. Again, bar food with no french fries, so nothing fancy here either, but delicious. I've had their hot roast beef and tuna fish sandwiches, and last night had a hot roast pork with sharp provolone. The pork was so juicy and tender. 

If you grew up aorund Philly, going to the corner bar for hot roast beefs with your parents when you were little, stop in for a bite. It's as good as the little neighborhood places I remember growing up.
The food was excellent, the horchata was perfectly flavored and the prices are hard to beat.

Went for some breaking-fast Burrr-eee-toes n did the drive through thing because A. I was being lazy and B. I had lots of adult beverages last night!  Anyway so ordered the egg, bacon and cheese, super loaded with great bacon, loved it.  

Now the sausage  burrito was like it came from a different planet, hardly any sausage  n no cheese.  It was like a second chef came on duty!

Red sauce is  weak, liquidity and flat.  Green sauce  was pretty lifeless...

Great drinks, great bartenders & deals!.. This place is a Tucson institution, strong drinks @ a good price , and the bar tenders have always been helpful & make great recommendations on their signature drinks. Must visit!..
I love their fresh rolls, especially the variety! A couple fresh rolls and a mug of hot tea (I like the Thai chai) makes a great light meal. If your enjoy tofu, try the savory tofu roll- yum!

I continue to frequent this restaurant and it's become one of my favorites.  I can indulge with a steak and a delicious dessert or I can go healthy with some amazing ceviche. Ether way, it's a treat. The service could be better, but the ambience and food keep me coming back!
The happy hour specials make this a fantastic spot for a drink and a yummy homemade tasting meal.

This location is super close to me & great for meat/poultry, produce. I actually always buy my ground beef here...the difference in the color from regular publix ground beef and the organic is sale and proof enough! What the heck are we feeding non organic animals? Jeez.

Lines are quick, great staff, super helpful, lots of cool speciality items(HAVE YOU SEEN THE CHEESE SECTION??!!)- everything is extremely pleasant. Never a bad run & I'm here a few times per week. 


YAYYYYYY.

Always a satisfying commerical pie. I typically order via the app (there are always bonuses! Specials! free pizza incentives!) on my phone. Pizza is done in 15 mins, the staff is always nice & happy to get me in and out with my food ASAP with extra sauces & seasonings. 

Kind of a no brainer.

I was taken here tonight by a friend for a belated birthday dinner. I've driven by here for months and finally took the chance to eat here. 

We placed our order for Black and Blu burger, Chicken Strips and the fries with cheese & Ale sauce. Everything came to the table quickly while we were catching up on my recent trip to Hawaii. The burger, even though I ordered the small one, was HUGE! The flavours were absolutely delicious and my friend's chicken strips were light, crispy and so juicy. They were probably the best chicken strips we've ever had. 

The sauce for the fries tasted -to me- like potato cheese soup, I would gladly eat it by the bowl full, although the fries left something to be desired. The ones that came with her strips were old, a bit cold when they came to the table. 

I'm only giving 4 stars because the bathroom was out of toilet paper and when we came in there was only one cashier behind the register at 6:30, still peak dinner time.

This is where the University of Michigan and West Virginia University (along with others) alumni officially meet to watch games.  Therefore, on days that certain teams are playing, this place will be completely packed!

I love this places nachos.  They are huge and fully loaded with everything you want on nachos.  Their wings are good also.  Honestly, all the food here is fine for bar food.  The real reason you come here is for the atmosphere.  

Usually they have two college alumni groups here at the same time.  One is in half of the bar and the other is on the other side.  This makes sure that on weekends this place is packed to almost standing room only on college football (and basketball) weekends.  

Parking here can be difficult.  The Beef O' Brady's parking lot fills up really fast and you end up having to park on the street.  Sometimes the Salvation Army opens up its parking lot to patrons.  I'm always wary of parking on the street, because I don't want to be towed, but it seems pretty safe here.

This is a great place to grab a beer and relive your college days with people of all ages.

Decent cheesesteak although they mixed up my steak w/American with my buddy's with provolone. Argh.
A little on the salty side. 
Really nice guy at the register. 
Line a tad on the long side but beats Dnics line which ran around the whole market.

The Bay Area Renaissance Festival has been one of my go-to events since I was a young thing. The first time I attended it I was but a tender 16 years old. Fast forward a half a lifetime and i still go every year. 

It's true, I used to work the event, but ever since Howl-O-Scream came along, I had to choose between them, October wins out.  

What can I say about an event that offers you multiple venues of entertainment including such favorites as the Demzarrah Gypsies, The Badwy boys, The Tortuga twins, Emrys Fleet; rat catcher, Washing Well Wenches, and many others.  There are rides offered to people of all ages, games of skill and shopping galore! It truly is something to behold. 

Each weekend is a themed weekend and holds something unique. This weekend was Fiesta weekend as well as Easter. To celebrate this, they held a Latino Market in Piper's Valley, which focused on the products and foods of the Latin Culture.  They held a taco and burrito eating contest which was sponsored by Chipolte. 

For Easter, there was an Easter egg hunt in the children's realm, where the Easter bunny was going to appear (I saw him!) and would pose for photos. And there was also King Henry's brunch. For an additional $15.00 in addition to your admission (if you think about individual food costs, this is a BARGAIN!) you got an all you can eat buffet of an amazing array of food specifically catered to this event. It included: Pasta salad, fruit salad, roasted root vegetables, ham, fried chicken, quiche, mac & cheese, scrambled eggs, biscuits & gravy, as well as things I can't remember.  There were desserts galore! Red Velvet cup cakes, eclairs, 7 layer bars.. the list goes on. All in all, it was well worth the price, as we didn't have to eat later in the day. Oh, and I forgot to mention. If you were over 21, you got 2 mimosas! They were AMAZING!

While I didn't wait for the pub sing, the end of the day came early for me, about 4pm after joining in with the International Wenches Guild 21 in a wenching. Nothing could have made my day better. 

This event is by far one of the highlights of my year!

This is, at its core, a bbq joint.  Don't let the throw-away cutlery and styrofoam flatware fool you.  The cheese grits with shrimp, brisket, Brunswick Stew (personal fave), etc... are 5-star-worthy.

Staff is lively and friendly AND it is owned/operated by a Tampa native.  You'll come in hungry amd leave with a smile from the total experience.

This is a bbq joint 1st, restaurant 2nd.  Rate it according to its genre when you try it......And you should definitely try it!

3 1/2 Stars - I have nothing bad to say about this place. I came here after a night of gambling at Hard Rock Tampa. It was the closest 24 hour diner from my downtown hotel according to Google but I still had to drive. Arrived around 2am (Fri) and the place was slow. I was just happy that it was open and can sit & eat fast. As I imagined, the place had old skool diner decor and as expected, the staff had old skool diner hospitality....courteous and called me "Sweetie". Love places like this but have to expect that the food will be mediocre but served in large portions for more or less than $10 bucks. My server was attentive and made my experience pleasurable enough for a repeat visit the following day :)

I know, Denny's. But I suddenly had a hankering for some pancakes because of a scene I had just watched in the cable TV series, "Breaking Bad." The two meth cookers were eating at a Denny's in the TV show. So, I had to, and this one was the closest.
The pancakes were pretty good. The sausage and bacon were fine, and the scrambled eggs were extremely yellow, which made me think there was something artificial that had been added.
Denny's food is neither great nor terrible. It will suffice for when you are in the mood, or there is nothing else around. My main complaint about this one is that it is seriously filthy and in desperate need of a facelift. There's dirt in every corner. The wallpaper is coming off the walls and hanging. The carpet is, well, let's just say nasty. I didn't dare go in the restroom. Please renovate this outpost, Mr. Denny;s.

I've visited the fairgrounds for the Tampa Bay Pet Expo (two years in a row now), the Florida State Fair, the Tampa Caribbean Carnival and probably a bunch of other things. Ultimately, what is great about the Florida State Fairgrounds is that this place is HUGE - so multiple events can go on at one time without hindering each other. 

Since this review is for the fairgrounds and not any event itself, I'll just say that overall they have a good system. Big expo halls, lots of land, covered picnic areas, friendly staff, and ample parking (though I would drop a pin on your phone when you park, I have lost my car several times since there is so much land and there are no real markers). 

It seems like they also have a wide array of events here; for example, on the same day of the Pet Expo, the adjacent expo hall was having a children's expo and a fossil expo. Not big on being around hundreds of kids, but if I had not had my dog with me I would have definitely checked out the fossils. Being able to bring my dog inside the venue was great, by the way. I like that the fairgrounds allows for this type of event.

I will definitely be back at the fairgrounds for the next random adventure that piques my interest!
Burger was overdone so dry.. Granted I know the other options are why this place exist but I was having a burger kinda mood, won't go back for a burger may try something else.

It's taken me awhile to warm up to the Tampa Museum of Art. I worried its small footprint might be a limitation and wondered whether exhibits would be well showcased here.

After multiple visits to see everything from works by Matisse and photos by Henri Cartier-Bresson to a well designed collection of chairs, I have learned to love this museum.

It feels open and airy, not at all stuffy or closed in, and the views afforded of the Hillsborough River, the University of Tampa and the downtown Curtis Hixon Waterfront Park are lovely.

After touring the current display of American Art from the Phillips Collection, I was impressed by the depth and diversity of art presented and in no way felt it was minimized or compromised.

This particular visit marked the first time I made a point to join the 1 p.m. Saturday docent tour that was lead by the delightful and quite knowledgeable Barbara. This exhibit ends April 28 and it offers an opportunity to see extraordinary works of art described as "100 important American paintings." 

Thanks to the Tampa Museum of Art for connecting the local art scene with world class art. That contribution is not small at all.

I've been to the outside of Houston, which is one of the largest in the nation, and this one is just as amazing!

This place was a lot bigger than I was expecting.  It seemed to be never ending, when I didn't have a map.  All different types of shops and shows.  It seemed like there was something for everyone.

I also have to say that more people dress up here than back home.  I don't know why, but I felt like people were more into it.  It really adds to the charm when people get so involved.  One day I'll muster up the courage and actually dress up :P

I thought the shows were pretty hit or miss.  A couple were just strange, some were meh, a couple seriously entertaining.  One thing you can say is that everyone who has a show loves to entertain!

The food is pretty good, but not great.  I got a Prime Rib Sandwich and my fiance got a Bar B Q Pork Sandwich.  My fiance wishes there was more meat in the Bar B Q Pork Sandwich.  

I love that there are tons of trees.  It helps shield you from the sun.

Usually the food is quite good and consistent.  However last night things were somewhat different. The salads were watery with little dressing. The side vegetable was so spicy & bitter, no one could eat it. The angel hair pasta, which usually is the best in Tampa tasted bland. The pasta dish that I had required more sauce and there was only one sausage where they used to serve two.
I know there is sequestration going on these days, and people, as a result, are going to restaurants less. But we are doing our part supporting restaurants, so we should not be punished.
I do hope this was just an off night.

I've always had a good time here.  Plenty of elbow room, unlike some comedy clubs, decent food and decent service.  I like the fact that you get to keep the glass your drink comes in.  I think I had 20 of them at one point.  I just recently let them go.  And who doesn't like the sweet potato fries?  Those things are righteous.  

The comedians that roll through are always funny as hell, no complaints there.  Jimmy Roulette was the best though.  They need to bring him back ASAP.  I think they need to work on the host comedians a little because they tend to tell the same jokes over and over.  

Overall, cool place to chill and catch some laughs.

I really enjoyed my first trip to The Front Porch Bar & Grill.  I love how they've fixed up an old house and made it into a neighborhood hangout.  According to their website the house is over a hundred years old.  Wow!  I did try to take a quick look inside but it was dark and there was a karaoke/dance event happening so I really couldn't see anything.  I was more than happy to avoid that!  It was a beautiful night so we decided to eat outside on the porch.  It's a large porch that can accommodate a good number of tables.  As you're sitting there you can't help but wonder what the history of this place was and who used to live there.

There's a wide variety of types of food on the menu and I thought that prices were quite reasonable for the amount of food that you get.  The southern pot roast was tender and juicy.  I got a flatbread pizza.  While I thought that it could have used more sauce it was still quite good and I would order it again.  For dessert we got "The Chocolate Porch", which was a warm slice of chocolate cake with a raspberry drizzle and a scoop of ice cream.  It was delicious.

As much as it pains me to say so if you don't like cigarette smoke I would avoid sitting outside on the front porch at all costs.  Several outside tables had smokers and people from the inside would occasionally come out onto the porch to smoke.  I despise cigarette smoke so this was extremely annoying.  

The character of the building, the friendliness of the servers, and the quality of the food impressed me.  I will absolutely be coming back here.

Pretty pricey (but delicious) coffee and tea offerings in a great location. This is what I was expecting from OCF.
When I wandered in last Saturday I could not have been more turned off by my whole experience.  The initial service, while below average, could have been overlooked since the coffee the staff made me was really good.  What followed this interaction with the barista could not be overlooked and needs to be addressed by management. When looking for a coffee shop you are probably looking for a good cup of coffee/tea, affordability, and a warm, inviting space to meet a friend for a date or get take advantage of some free wireless internet to get some work done. What your not looking for is 3 OCF staff members chatting loudly about crazy details of their personal lives that you never wanted or needed to know. This is what happened for well over an hour while I was trying (without success) to concentrate and get some work done. I am not incredibly sensitive to noise (grew up in a large Italian family) and actually prefer to work with some background chatter or music but this was beyond noise. Their behavior was not just bothering me. I could see clearly that other patrons were annoyed by their loud conversation and were having difficulty not saying something like "Please, be considerate".  
After a few hours of trying to ignore them, I gave up and went home.
What a disappointment this place was especially since I just moved around the corner and really wanted to find "My coffee shop" in this location. Something needs to be done with these staff members or they are going to lose business pretty quickly.

Pho, the magic elixir that cures all. And they do it right at Dao's. 

I've always been a big fan of pho and have tried numerous Vietnamese restaurants for that cure-all bowl of deliciousness but keep coming back to Dao's as I have found none better. The broth is clean and light and my absolute favorite is the 12C - thin slices of beef and fresh vegetables. And to be perfectly honest, my five star rating is for the pho because I really have no desire to eat anything else when I'm there. 

Dao's can get really busy during the lunch hour and all the tables fill up. The servers are pleasant and fairly quick. My perfect lunch hour is getting a table under one of the ceiling fans (I get hot when I eat my pho!), a big steaming bowl of 15C and a green apple boba afterwards.

I just love that they have a drive up window at Dao's and that I can take pho home on occasion. The broth comes in a nice styrofoam container separate from the ingredients of rice noodles, raw beef and veggies. The basic condiment plate of Asian basil, cilantro, bean sprouts, limes and jalapenos is included as well as a small container of sauce that is the perfect blend of Sriracha and hoisin sauce.

If you've never tried pho, you're missing out. And if you've never tried the pho at Dao's, you're TRULY missing out.

The pharmacist was helpful in finding specific types of vitamins. The store itself was quiet. We waited a few minutes for someone to show up at the cash register - potentially understaffed? The person who eventually showed up was quite nice and efficient though.

Called A1 Express Airport Parking located at 5317 Avion Park Drive in Tampa Florida, Phone number (813) 282-1480 on April 15, 2013 at 10:30 pm to find the information about my reservation. The lazy man answered the phone and he hesitant to give his name. What asked a question, he said he cannot access his computer and there are people standing near him. Never asked me about taking my contact phone number so he can return my call or put me on hold or gave my call to another person so I could be helped. FORGET ABOUT GIVING THESE FOOLS ANY TIPS!

A1 Express needs to improve the service or get rid of this lazy man.

Why 5 stars? Because this has become one of my favorites in Tampa Bay.

   ---   ---   ---

I officially love the existence of Nico's.

My Mom was recently in town to visit. As you could imagine, I took her on quite the food tour of Tampa Bay. Her favorite new spot was easily Nico's.

I couldn't stay away from my favorite Beef Special, and she went with the Shrimp Special. Someone in lined convinced us that we had to try the Cachapas Con Queso de Mano. It was ham and mozzarella cheese in a sweet corn tortilla. It was utterly delightful. I didn't notice the ham, but I didn't care. It was the perfect side dish to an arepa.

The only question I have to ask is, "why didn't I grow up Venezuelan?" Damn my luck.

Hey, if you want to pay double for the same Chinese food you can buy down the street at your local Chinese restaurant, hit this place up!  Slapping the word "bistro" on your sign doesn't give you the right to sell strip mall Chinese food for inflated prices.  Like my pops always said, "You can't pour syrup on a turd and call it a pancake."  Chinese food for two should never cost $40.  NEVER.

Why? Just out of convenience? You lack taste buds and like to overpay for lackluster Chinese food?  Save the money, head to any Chinese takeout and the food will be as good if not better.  Ok, no large horse statue...big whoop...

Head to China Wok, Yummy House or just spend a few minutes on Yelp looking for other Chinese restaurant options.

2.75 stars. Over-rated and way overpriced. Made reservations and then we got there right on time and the three jabber-jaw college girls made us wait in the lobby for a good 20 minutes past our reservation time. There was no where for us to sit while we waited and had to be subjected to the loud, bustling and smoking people outside the waiting area. No rhyme or reason was given for the hold-up. Once we got inside the seating area of the restaurant, there were plenty of empty tables; thus not sure why we had to wait? 

One of the big disappointments about this place is the dress code. We were dressed semi-formal (dress pants, shirt and tie for me and a nice dress for the lady). But there were people in the restaurant (and not just at the bar) dressed in shorts and baseball caps. Women were in jeans and t-shirts. If you're going to have a nice experience and spend a ton of money, they really need to do something about their dress code and spruce up the clientele.

Once seated, we were completely invisible to the wait staff. No one asked if we wanted water or even give us a menu (drink or food). We were eventually recognized and compensated but the damage was already done by that point. If you are hungry you might as well snack on something before arriving.

Ignoring the bitterness of the above rants; I want to be honest, truthful and unbiased about the food. We decided to go with the Steak Special for the night which was a 41-day salted aged steak. We ordered our steaks medium and they came close to it but the steak was quite chewy. Had to really dig and tear with the steak knife and chew a ton on it in order to eat the "Special" steak. It was somewhat flavorful and juicy but I've had better steak on my own grill or at a local steak place around the neighborhood corner. The asparagus side item was cold and tasteless. Completely bland with no butter or proper salt to flavor it. If you wanted to salt and pepper your side items, you couldn't unless you wanted to use the insanely large sea / garlic / bacon salt containers on your table. Not good for the mashed potatoes or other sides.

The worst part was that the steak was over $55.00! We knew it was going to be expensive but to have chewy sub-par steak and then pay over $55.00 for it?! This place needs to really step up its game if it wants to be considered a top steak restaurant in the Tampa area.

All in all, you're better going off to one of Tampa's other great steak places. The Council Oak has a lot of work to do in regards to its food, prices, customer service, atmosphere and dress code.

I have never received such terrible customer service. The driver calls me asking for directions and I tell him exactly how to get here. He was two blocks so I went outside to wait. When he arrives he goes on to shove the paper in my face yelling and screaming in my face because he could not find the address. The sauce for the calzone was ice cold, the dough was not fully cooked, and the salad was huge but came with an extremely tiny cup of dressing. Needless to say I will NEVER order from here again.

Picked up some take out from here tonight because I was craving Cuban food.  Let me mention that I was raised on Cuban food so I know what is good and not good!  

This is not one of the best I have had (like my mom's or mother-in-laws) but it was pretty good!  The Moro was very good, which is one of my favorites!  The yucca was also good....which is usually screwed up wherever you go.  I had a roasted chicken that was good but sort of dry. Of course I reheated it & it was a breast so that kind of explains that.  I got my husband the roast pork ( I tasted it & it was very good).  I also got him platanos which look authentic ( I don't eat these so I'd have to wait for my husband to taste).  The portions are big so you can definitely make 2 meals out of it, & the prices are very reasonable!

Every 2nd Saturday of the month, the Honey Pot is host to Fetish Circuit in their upper floors. It's an interesting evening that caters to some of the harder players in the scene. You will find riggers, people into violet wand play, sensory deprivation, and of course corporal punishment.  It is NOT for the faint of heart.

I've been going here for years of lunch breaks, and this place is consistently good.  The lunch specials are $7 for an entree (ranging from wings to shrimp to a few burgers) and a basket of fries.  Service is quick and friendly enough, and the wall of TVs lets anyone even slightly interested the sports catch one or two things.  

The place is, however, old and well-worn.  Plus, they took out the lobster claw-grab game.

I got the impression from reading the Yelp reviews that people either love this place or hate it, but the food sounded intriguing enough that I knew I had to try it for myself.  Knowing ahead of time that it is an expensive restaurant, we saved it for a special occasion- ta da - my birthday.  And, I have to be honest with you, our meal was AWESOME! Yes, capital-letter worthy even. I'm mad at myself for not taking a picture of the menu, because I'm sure I will not do the food justice with my simple descriptions.

My BF started with the gazpacho - perfectly chilled and refreshing (I can't describe more than that as I was only able to sneak one spoonful).  Next we shared the beef tartar.  A surprisingly generous portion, very fresh and moist, finely chopped, and topped w/2 quail eggs.  It even came served with thin cut French fries.  My entree, however, is what has me salivating still when I think about it, even a day later.  As a former Maryland girl, I automatically gravitate towards crab dishes. So, when I saw the soft shell crab dinner, I knew right away that's what I would have.  And it was spectacular!  Lightly coated with a cereal batter, not overlooked, hot and moist and juicy and crunchy.  Yum! Plus it was served with an edamame salad, which had fresh corn, Peruvian corn, jalapeños, and spices, which also was incredibly delicious.  It was one of the best entrees I have ever had.  My BF had one of their specialties - fried chicken with butternut squash waffles, served with a delicious corn & diced butternut squash side dish.  He said it was excellent, although he did have to request a side of salt for the chicken, in order to enhance the sweet/savory combo going on.  For dessert I had the lemon upside down cake served with blueberry crunch ice cream and lemon paper. Tasty and interesting, it was a nice light finish to my meal. My BF had the chocolate tort (?) and loved it.

Random details:  They only serve beer and wine, but they are in the process of getting a liquor license.  They do have cider on tap, so it was a treat to be able to get a snakebite. Our server Jess was friendly and efficient, and well versed in the menu. I like that she gave a brief overview, and answered questions we had, instead of boring us by going over the whole thing. The Mayor of Tampa came in with his family to eat while we were there which was kind of cool, I thought. One final note, although there is a parking lot behind the restaurant, only hair salon customers are allowed to park there. Restaurant guests have to pay a $1 fee to valet. Weird, right?  But, no harm, no foul, the meal was delightful, and we will definitely be back.

This is a pretty neat store. I love the concept of it and how it feels like you are taking a step back in time once you enter the doors. Thus far I found gray sperrys, really cool chambray and dooney and bourke crossover bag. It is worth the visit.

PROS:
-always has new stock
-clean
- nice and helpful staff

CONS:
- for consignment, its unusually overpriced

TIPS:
- look for the $5 rack. Sometimes you can find super cool things there. (This is where I found my light denim chambray
- walk around and look at different things, you will find treasure among the horrible 80s sweatshirts and old team tshirts

Somehow sitting outside on the patio makes for a good atmosphere- despite overlooking Florida Avenue.  

The cornbread they serve you in place of regular bread is DELICIOUS.  

I had the meatloaf and it lived up to it's expectations.   Also, the red sangria ranks up there in taste with my favorite restaurant sangria (Mellow Mushroom)- but doesn't quite have the high alcohol content.  

I didn't go inside at all while I was there- but overall the atmosphere is inviting and chill.  I'll be moving to Seminole Heights soon and I suspect I'll be hanging out here a lot!

Came here for a work lunch today.  Very cool decor & good choice of items on the menu.  This place has a really nice outside patio where you can sit to grab a bite or drink with your furry friends, which I love!  Will definitely come back in the evening to sample some of the beers on draft.  My only negative was that I wasn't that impressed with the beer choices on draft, but not a huge deal!!

My fourth visit while traveling to the Philly area.  Good food choices, nice venue if you sit in lower section.  Easy access to parking and departing and SAFE which is always a concern at some Ball Parks.  Congrats to Phillies on a Great Stadium from a MLB Fan and traveler.
This place is amazing.  It's super neat and the owner in increasingly nice.  She is very knowledgeable with an endless array of ways to help anyone create a gorgeous piece.  I'll be back!

The Tacu Tacu was a party in my mouth.  Everything else ceases to matter.

When we first arrived I was a little hesitant.  It is situated in a row of shops, which worried me.  The restaurant itself is small and dark.  It is nicely decorated, but on a sunny day outside I was thinking maybe this was not the best choice of places to eat.  There were a couple tables outside, but no one was sitting out there.  There is also a bar area.

As others have mentioned, service is slow, but I do not really fault the restaurant for this.  I think it is more of a cultural thing and adds to the charm.  Honestly, if you're eating, drinking, and laughing, the speed of the service fits right in.
