They give you bread to begin and it is adequate.  Nothing to write home about, but not terrible.

When I was looking at the menu, I realized I was extremely out of my comfort zone.  Everything seemed to be a novel idea.  This menu is filled with seafood, so it is a little difficult if you do not like seafood.  However, that isn't to say that there are not a couple options that might satisfy you.  I ended up choosing the Tacu Tacu, which is billed as crispy rice and white beans with a seafood topping, in a tomato/coconut milk sauce (my words, not theirs).  My fiance got the flat, fried chicken with fries (I'm sure there is a more specific name, but I do not remember it).

The Tacu Tacu was huge.  I actually just ate my left overs for dinner.  It was absolutely delicious.  All the different elements worked perfectly together.  The seafood consists mainly of shrimp and squid, which was fine by me.  I loved the generous helping of squid.  Honestly, I cannot remember a meal that I enjoyed as greatly as this one in terms of the actual food.  I know that any time I come back, I will always order that.  No branching out for me, I love it so much.  My fiance really enjoyed his chicken too.  The fries were ok, but I wouldn't keep ordering them.

This is one of those more upscale places that it is not out of place to wear jeans at.  Not a place to see and be seen, but definitely a place for exquisite food.  

I would suggest this place as a low key date night or a fun outing with friends.

Your lunch deal was great 4.95 and 5.95 for dark and light 1/4 chicken respectively with two sides.  The chicken was good. Even Mocha, my puppy liked it lol, especially when we were sitting outside. Thanks for being so considerate. he appreciated being able to eat lunch with me.
The sides were alright. I had yellow rice and yucca. Not too special for me though. There were a lot of other options but I did want to save my money. I would try it again especially since you are dog friendly.
It is the typical Cuban spread with the heated table with the food in front of you  like cafeteria style. It's across from Whole Foods so I kind of think it might be a bit off the beaten path.

My biggest like is the Chicken Burrito-  OMG, it's not a weekend unless I have a Juanitos burrito with fresh chicken, fresh cilantro,, fresh cebollos, mmmm.   They are SO good, I can't bring myself to order anything else!    The food is great, the kitchen is busy, the family is very accommodating, and will add/delete any ingredients.  The restaurant is..um...well, booths that are VERY well worn, with bumps and divots of wear.  The neighborhood is such that you should lock up your vehicle; common sense, right?   The homeless there are "regulars"   The food is well worth navigating the current construction.

The food and Pho was really pretty bland. Maybe we got it on an off night. No atmosphere, just a functional restaurant. I can't imagine wanting to go back. It was my 2nd time. The first time I went on a Living Social deal. Really, for me nothing special here.

Although it has great potential, I thought the mixture was a little odd.

I ordered the beef special (chicken salad, cheese, egg, pulled beef, and white garlic sauce all inside a crispy yet fluffy corn tortilla like sandwich bun. 

Would I go back? Yes but I'd create my own arepa. I'd get just the white cheese and the pulled beef with a side of garlic sauce to dip it in.

The pulled beef was really really good. It had lots of slow cooking flavor.  I could have eaten just a pound of the meat alone.

Disclaimer: Mermaid Tavern is popular and it does got loud and crowded at peak times. I like to go at slower times.

Stylish decor and fun ambience. This Seminole Heights hangout offers tasty food options. A great artsy and independent spot in a blossoming Seminole Heights.

This food truck in a tire lot on Hillsborough (at least when I went..) offers tasty Venezuelan arepa sandwiches. The cachapas is also good with a rich sweeter flavor and yummy melted cheese.

Fans of Nicos should also try the arepon at Pepitos After Hours on Waters.
A fun hipster gastropub in Seminole Heights. I especially like their grilled cheese sandwich with pear and honey.. Yum!
Enjoyed our meal and the drink specials were a good deal. The decor is lovely except for the tvs. Is this an upscale steakhouse or a sports bar? Maybe the tvs should be kept just to the bar area?

Very good Colombian (Antioquian) bakery and small restaurant. Delicious Paisa specialties like bandeja paisa (a big platter of food with steak, pork belly, beans, rice, egg and more) as well as yummy pan de bono and guava pastries. My favorite, the large twists with guava and cheese in them (trenzas) often sell out. 

Fresh fruit dishes and shakes are also offered. Yum!!! They are topped with condensed milk.

 Armenia is full of Colombian places to explore and there is a Colombian owned grocery store next door.

Two words.....P U !  Sorry, this place used to be great. Not any more! Something in the attitude of the staff has made it a less than enjoyable experience. It had the feeling of a family small town place at one time, but now it seems like they are only interested in the bottom line, both management and severs ( you know, the people who carry food around for a living. big difference from a real waiter). Food of course is comparable to a typical carrabbas, but why spend your money if you can't have a pleasant overall experience?

I'm not a fan of their burger, but I do love their cajun fries.  Would be wonderful if they came up with a no carb burger (bunless, with lettuce as bun instead) like In n Out Burger.  Then maybe, I'll go for their burger too.

Another restaurant to fall in love with. From the ambiance to the views, the wonderful food and the top notch service , you just can't go wrong here.

Since I'm a creature of habit (yes I admit it) i had a bottle of the Stags Leap artemis 2010 ($125) while watching the sun go down and listening to a wonderful piano player in the lounge.

Had the caprese salad for an appetizer served up a little different then last time but just as good with house made mozzarella, assorted tomatos and a wonderful balsmic glaze. THey also bring you a little loaf of fresh baked bread to help you along.

DInner was the tagliatalle bolognese which came out piping hot and just delicious.
Service was spot on.

From Joan and Alisa the waitresses (that title does them no justice!) to Karim at the bar, they're such welcoming friendly people it just makes you want to stay all evening, share some great food, wine and conversation , and really, isnt' that was dining out should be about?!

Is it the best cheesesteak in the city? Maybe. I can't tell because I don't think I ever had one in Philadelphia that I didn't like. 

Is it worth the line? Yes and no. Yes if it's moving smoothly like a machine and you're not too hungry (I came just after scarfing two Federal Donuts). No if there's someone in front of you that decides to order everything and the kitchen sink.

Price-wise: $7 basic, 75 cent extra ingredients. Since I'm from NY, this isn't egregious, but probably cheaper options.

Get one to go, grab a beer somewhere and enjoy.

I'm in no way a pho pro, but I liked Saigon Deli better than this one, but those who are looking for Pho, that place is not open in the evenings, at least I do not believe so.  The spring rolls here are smaller, there is definitely a lot less meat in the vermecilli noodles dishes, and the seasoning was not as tasty in my opinion.
I'm glad I also tried something different, the sweet and sour fish, it was pretty good but I think after a while the taste would be pretty strong.  I did really like the veggies in the dish though.
I couldn't stop thinking about the other place. The flavor, the sizes for the price, and the service at the other place was much better than my experience at Pho An Hoa.

Why 3 stars? Because it's Jekyll and Hyde.

   ---   ---   ---

On one hand, I'm quite fond of the fair. I really like coming here, and even though I've only lived here 6 years, it's sentimental to me. 

On the other hand, the parking situation is nothing short of a travesty here. I recently visited the Funshine Music Festival, and was basically sitting still in my car for 20 minutes after backing out of my spot. Why was the traffic so jammed up? You'd think there would be a team of parking attendants on-hand to prevent this, right?

Nope, just a single hillbilly yokel with a wand in his hand, attempting to (poorly) direct three different flows of cars. How does this happen?

Similar situations have occurred when leaving a concert at the Amphitheater. I don't know how it's never addressed, but it definitely lowers the quality of the experience.

It's that time of year again, time to celebrate the anniversary of when the Husbeast and I said our vows.  Bern's is where we choose to celebrate this momentous event because the food is beyond par and the service is absolutely incredible.

Though they suggest that you make your reservations at least 72 hours out, I tried a short order reservation on Monday and was able to get a 6pm time slot for Wednesday.  We actually arrived a bit early and were seated at about 5:45 pm in the first of the dining rooms.  

If you've never been to Bern's Steak House, the opening decor always reminds me of Disney's Haunted mansion, that kind of rich opulence, that one comes to expect from only the finest of establishments.  The tables are comfortably spaced and though each room can get crowded, they still maintain an air of intimacy.    

Our waiter, an older man who's name I didn't catch, was quick to the table with our water and tea. He gave us a few minutes to peruse the menu, but we were pretty well decided before we got there. We were set on getting the 18 ounce, 2 inch thick Del Monaco, cooked medium rare.  We also wanted to make sure that we got ourselves the charcoal grilled sea scallops because they are to die for.

The sea scallops were fresh and tasty and each course that was brought to the table, the french onion soup, the fresh greens salad and the main course of steak, vegetables and onion straws was gastronomical perfection!  The bill arrived, and it's something to note, they AUTOMATICALLY add a tip of 12% onto the bill for the waitstaff, be sure to add additional to qualify for their impeccable service. 

After dinner was settled up, we headed on up to the Henry Waugh dessert room where we each had a coffee, his Americano, mine was the Cafe con Leche ybor, which is a signature there, and is absolutely wonderful. We usually get the creme brule, but tonight we changed things up, we opted to get the sampler of the three molten cakes. They're small, cup cake sized, each served with a cannel of specially selected ice cream - though the cakes are small, with there being three of them, there's enough for two people.   They do NOT add the gratuity up in this room for the server, so please tip accordingly. 

And because they knew it was our anniversary,we were presented with a small anniversary dessert, a little desert shell filled with decadent whipped cream and topped with chocolate chips and a candle, It was a lovely touch for the night. 

You can not beat the service or the food.  The prices are high, but for two people, including dessert, but no wine, we didn't spend over $150.00, and for a special meal, that's a bargain in my book.

I am giving 5 stars to Fitlife Foods for many reasons. First, the fact that this is actually a way to lose weight!! I have lost 6 pounds in less than a week by taking the Fitlife Challenge! Second, because the food is genuinely good. I forget I am eating healthy because it taste like real food and I believe this will be a very easy diet to stay on. Third, because it is actually good for my body. Fourth, I love that I am saving time in TWO WAYS - by not cooking and not having to clean up after cooking. Fifth, the staff here is really friendly and super helpful. Sixth, I love the story behind how this company was created. It takes a motivated and strong person to take a wonderful idea and make it reality. This is a genius idea and I am very happy that theyre around to help me reach my weight goals in a very easy and convenient way!!!

Healthy (healthful?) fast food? Yes, please. 

The meat is great quality & is a nice option somewhere between a McDonald's burger for poor (yet delish) and inexpensive quality and a full fledged 10 dollar burger (sometimes worth it, sometimes not) at a restaurant that you have to wait around for....

Ketchup choices? That's worth the trip alone. Super fun, tasty & good for experimenting through a potentially mundane meal ( with me? never!).

Cute patio. Our dog even tries to go there when passing by. He loves Evos, apparently.

I am so not artistic. Nevertheless, I still ended up creating a beautiful piece with the awesome & well planned execution for the talentless such as myself, courtesy of The Potter's House Studio!

The shop itself is an adorable little gem, very comfortable, not overwhelming. Josie was amazing at explaining the process and was helpful throughout when we had questions (yes, even "dumb" ones).

The Studio features simple, yet aesthetically pleasing pieces to choose from with a wonderful array of paints to choose from.
THERE IS A DESIGN CENTER! This means you can choose from stencils of all sizes and patterns, stamp type devices to imprint designs, and best of all....a computer to search images that the studio will then image and re-size for you on the spot to have a custom stencil for your creation!

I've tried painting my own wine glass, blah blah...hideous, but really I was in it for the experience. This studio raised the bar so much for me! I can make my own pieces that I will use on a daily basis that I just love...

Headed back in a few weeks, I'm hooked!

Who knew that this artless girl could be so...artsy? Potters House most certainly had my number- 

ADORE, LOVE, REVISIT.

If you live in Tampa, I beg of you to visit & support the numerous non-chain Italian restaurants.  if you're visiting to Tampa, then I beg of you, go to ANY of the far better any kind of ethnic-focused restaurants.

If you've been to one Carrabba's you've been to them all.  I tend to think the wait time to food taste ratio is not worth it.  I will say I've eaten here on numerous occasions and I've liked the food but there are too many other options to consider first.

I don't understand why the earlier reviews were negative. Yes they are very patriotic and display an abundance of American-patriotic decor items, but so what? Over the top for me, but the food is great. . I like that the place has it's own personality. I'm a flaming liberal and I was not offended by the decor. The owners are allowed to decorate however they please and patrons can choose not to eat there if it's offensive. (BTW I'm a flaming liberal.)

 They make the best potato pancakes I've had in Tucson. I'm glad they give a military discount; the military don't make much money. The ownership has not changed and the food is as good as ever. Yeah sometimes service is a bit slow. Today they had one huge group in the back room and evidence that another large group had just left from the main room. They only had two or three wait persons that I could see, but they seemed to handle the job pretty well. I gave my waiter a 30% tip because he was polite and attentive and alerted me that since the potato pancakes are made from scratch they would take longer than other breakfast foods. I actually knew that, but he's a new waiter to me. The owner came to talk to a winter visitor who was leaving for the summer and wished him a safe trip. The owner was cordial when I paid and I made a point of complimenting the cook, who is his wife. I have no complaints about Biscuits; the food is well made American. The food is made at the time it's ordered and is not pre-made like chain restaurants

Dude, seriously... you'd think that the State Fair would hire people who know what they're doing. Obviously it's lowest bidder time.

I just had the pleasure of attending the Funshine Music Festival on opening day.  Getting parked, no big deal.  Getting in the gate? HOLY MOTHER OF MOSES! And worse? Getting OUT of the place.  I nearly got hit twice because of the doofus "directing" traffic, didn't have a clue what he was doing. He'd guided me, and then guided another car almost directly INTO me. 

It's not just for this event stuff like this happens, it's every time I come to the Fairgrounds. Even the vendors who were running the booths out on the midway couldn't decide what the price of water was. In one booth on center line it was $4.00 and the next one beside it it was $2.00, both run by the same company. *face Palm*

My recommendation, go with someone you love (or like a lot), order the 20 oz. NY strip and split it.  It's a great bargain at $55 and if you split it, you're looking at $27.50 for 10 oz. of high quality steak.  Could one person eat the whole thing?  Sure, if they're lacking testosterone and they're trying to come across extra masculine in front of people.  

The sides and steak toppings are all a la carte, so if you want mushrooms and blue cheese, that will be an extra $15, thank you.  Cha-ching.  Again, not so bad if you're splitting the steak.  

Caprese salad = One of the best out there.  $12 for 3 tomato slices and two pieces of mozzarella isn't bad, if (guess what?) you're SPLITTING IT.  Do you see a theme developing here?  =)

French onion soup = Face melting.  I long for the days of when The Colonnade was a great place to go and experience the best French onion soup in this quadrant of the galaxy, but they're long gone.  There is a new French onion soup in town, and it's right here at the Council Oak.  Refreshingly, they use Gruyère cheese instead of the standard issue Provolone.  This makes for more of a sweet and salty cheese topping as opposed to the sharp and smoked flavor of Provolone.  Hands down, best there is.  

Mojitos are very good as well, but at $12.75 a piece, not worth it.  Drink before you go and save the money because ain't no damn mojito worth almost $13 freaking bucks.

Volcano dessert, while it is impressive to watch the waiter set shit on fire next to your table, skip it.  Go grab an Oreo Blizzard from DQ on your way home, pour two shots of rum in it and light a match.  Boom!  You just saved $15.  

Service = exemplary.  Had a 6 o'clock reservation and we were seated immediately.  Waiter was on the ball.  No actual complaints other than the incessant sound of the saw buzzing in the butcher shop.  That got old real quick.

Better than Bern's?  Nope, but certainly more economical if you do it right.  Split, share and SPLIT!

West River drive, or should I now say Martin Luther King Drive? Nothing wrong with a name change if it still serves the same purpose.  Which is an escape from a hectic , mass confusion followed with a splash of disgruntled drives on the Schuylkill Expressway. Which by the way  I call it the Sure-kill Expressway with everybody under the sun racing to what? 

The M.L.K. Drive has a more relaxing ease to it , not as crazy like the Schuylkill , it even has great views to go with your commute. Great views of the river , boat house row , the park all leading towards the Art Museum. This with neighboring Kelly drive across the river people can actually stop at one of the parking lots located on here just to take a break and enjoy. You can also ride your bikes with the paths they have, to even fishing. 

Sometimes on holiday weekends in the summer (I believe certain holiday weekends)  they would close down this drive , where it would be open to the public to enjoy with bicycles , or just to jog on an open road.  Great for the Dadeville Regatta they do every year along the river, perfect spots to enjoy the race.

Great drive , with great views for all to enjoy. Myself I personally enjoy driving on here at night, where boat house row it lit up, the art museum is standing out , with the lights of downtown in the back-scape.

It's Cracker Barrel, a country staple. Sweet tea,rocking chairs on the porch and quick service.  While my Mother doesn't like them, I have no issue with this place and will eat at them when ever I go on a trip.  Now they've built them in town and I try to eat at a CB every so often.  The service is friendly and fast. The food is down home and the tea is always "just right".

This dealership not only has bad customer service, but also highly unethical practices. Particularly one of the managers is rude and condescending. Be careful with these guys, before showing your financing options they will make you wait while the financial "expert" plays with your credit, and let me tell you -not in a positive way-. They are known for selling overpriced cars to families that can't afford the monthly payments, or putting the families in debt for the rest of their lives. Take it from a fellow Tucsonian, before buying a car at this dealership,do yourself a favor visit the other Toyota dealership and decide for yourself.

Didn't know whether I should give this place 2 or 3 stars.  I've tried a few of their food off the menu and wasn't impressed.  There's really nothing special about it.

So... why did I go with 3 stars?

The crabs and wings special that they have on Mondays and Tuesdays!  Now, just because it's $1.75 per crab and $.20 per wing, they don't give you crap stuff.  My date and I try go every Tuesday after I get off work.  We also get the crab fries with cheese sauce on the side every once in a while, which are pretty good.

Adolf Biecker has been a great find! 

Erica is my colorist and is excellent - she listens, make suggestions and is an expert in her field! She is outstanding - I highly recommend her.

Ashley is my hair stylist and has made my thin hair look full of body and movement!  Ashley also listens and makes suggestions based on her expertise.  Ashley is just excellent!

One other nice piece about Adolf Biecker is that the receptionists are verynice.  They are accommodating and flexible.

Great salon, great people and Erica & Ashley are the best!
As authentic as it gets.  Great chopped pork, ribs, and chicken.  A little limited on the choice of sides.

Love love love this studio.  I prefer studios with lots of people and lots of energy and this one has both.  Two rooms for yoga, tons of classes and so far every instructor I've had has been awesome.  Conveniently located and reasonably priced.

I've been here plenty of time for dinner, but today was the first time for Sunday soul food brunch. Every item was amazing and the prices are reasonable.  Parking can be a bit tricky but it's worth it.  Was a long wait for food but we had 14 people.  Will def be back!

It was a disappointing experience. Almost all the food that we ordered are too salty. We had the mini Wellington, onion soup, roast duck and the scallop dish. The flavor is good but I expect more when it costs $30 for a dish. There are many other high end excellent restaurants in town with lower price tags.
Great neighborhood Italian. Friendly staff, great variety, big portions, and its small dining room that creates a very inviting atmosphere. Skip the chains and head to Gino's.

Red chile, to me, is usually the gauge of authenticity for a Mexican restaurant. 

You order the red chile burrito, toss away the knife and fork and eat it with your hands, which I did. The red chile soaks into the freshly-made-be-sure-you-take-home-a-dozen tortilla by the end, which it did. You walk around the rest of the day with your fingers smelling like red chile, which they did.

Their red chile and tortillas  - as authentic as it gets in Tucson.

Some of the better chicken wings in Tampa. The hot sauce is a vinger taste which I like. There are plenty of tv's to watch whatever game your interested in. Decent selection of beers meaning mostly the bigger name brands and a few Cigar City beers.

The Fire is exactly what people are describing a cool place to have a beer and to check out some local bands. The people are pretty chill there and friendly as well.
I'm not really sure about the parking though. I was there setting up for a band(Skank Week) so i needed to park close and it was tight.
Adorable doggy boutique. Located in the back of Hyde Park. We were greeted by Charlie {the owner or employees sweet lab} and enjoyed walking around the store.

This theater is a drive from our closest theater which is in Brandon.  Overall, it is nicer than Brandon AMC but still needs help.  The sound is much better than Brandon it is cleaner, but there is only one concession at the front that is not really convenient.  Also, the bathroom locations aren't great.  Definitely like the Veterans location better, but that's a longer drive for a movie.

We stumbled upon Burger Culture at the Tampa Margarita Fest and after scoping out the other trucks we decided to go with them and split a Cajun Burger ($8). There was almost no wait and we had our burger really fast. We found an area to sit down and we dug in. 

The burger was huge and it was pretty tasty. It had a lot of Gouda and sauteed mushrooms. The burger was really messy and I was glad that I grabbed extra napkins. My only issue with the burger is that the chipotle aioli they use is a little overpowering. It was spicy and we can handle spice, but the flavor dominated a little too much. I couldn't really taste the gouda or enjoy the mushrooms as much as I would have liked. If Burger Culture had put a little less aioli on this burger, it would have been phenomenal. I will definitely look for Burger Culture at future events. I want to try one of their regular burgers. For now, four stars because the burger was amazing the sauces are just a work in progress.

Do not judge this book by its cover.

Located in a strip center that has few occupants, Terra Sur did not disappoint. This restaurant has been on my "must try" list for awhile. 

We arrived around 6pm on a saturday and surprisingly we were NOT the only patrons here. 

We checked in on Yelp and took advantage of the $10 off coupon. 

I started with a glass of their red sangria and it was refreshing. The glass was quite large and tasty-it didn't have an alcohol taste to it yet I left a little loopy ;)

We shared the causa de pollo and it was Devine. It's hard to explain but it's a cold mashed potato with a fantastic chicken salad on top and garnished with avocado and a hard boiled egg.  Superb. 

For my main meal I ordered the mango and shrimp ceviche. That too was tasty and it had cubed avocado as well. 

My husband ordered the lomo Saltado and this is the meal that stole the show. I couldn't stop stealing bites of the flavorful steak. Soooo goood!!

I'd definitely return and would want to try more of their menu.

By far my favorite sushi restaurant in the Tampa/Westchase area. Every sushi roll I have tried has been awesome (my favorite being the Nirvana and Sixth Sense). Their drinks are creative and they even infuse their vodkas which is fun! Love the atmosphere and location.

Was not impressed.  The menu was huge and had A LOT to choose from so I was expecting a lot but the quality of my meal wasn't up to my expectations.  I came here with 6 other girls and it took almost two hours for them to bring us our food which was unacceptable whether we were with a large party or not.  They had a good band and the atmosphere was fun but we did not have a good experience.  I will definitely give it another chance because I would like to try more of what the menu has to offer!

It is not often that I crave French cuisine...  Honestly, I just can't think of a time where I suddenly had a huge urge for frogs legs, but over the years I have heard so much about Le Rendez-vous I figured it was about time to give it a try.

We ate there shortly after the new Executive Chef took over this spring.  My dinner date and I went all out.  We had the escargot and the mushrooms in puff pastry.  I am not an escargot fan but my dinner date really liked them.  However the mushroom appetizer I ordered was to die for.  I think I would have poked him with my fork if he wanted to try a bite!  I am tempted to go back once a week just for that.  

After hors d'Oeuvres I was already filling up but went for the scallops which were melt in your mouth delicious.  My date had the Veal with caramelized apples.  He was very pleased and said it was a much better version of his favorite pedestrian pork chops and applesauce.  

For dessert, both now bursting at the seams, we shared the Crapes Suzette prepared tableside.  They were not on the menu but someone had recommended them and the staff was happy to make them for us.

Next time I go back I want to try the Happy Hour in the bistro which features small plates so that I can sample more before getting completely stuffed!

A note before you go - this restaurant is designed for dining.  Be prepared to spend an hour and a half or so if you decide on multiple courses.  For us that was no problem!
It is now $2.75 per item. Still cheap. Still excellent service. They will not accept debit/credit card for transactions $10 and below.

Yesterday Jimbo's catered a baby shower for 34 people for me. I was able to feed everyone for just over $9 per person and we had so much food leftover. Jimbo's was great with the order letting me know I could tell them they day of if the number changed. We got chicken, pork, baked beans, coleslaw, hush puppies, 2 gallons of tea and all the silverware and plates plus an entire bucket of ice! Everyone was very excited to see the Jimbo's van in our driveway when they pulled up. I highly recommend Jimbo's for catering!!! They did an outstanding job and the food was delicious.
Food & Service was good for Lunch time... FRESH food ! I want to go back for dinner service next... Interior decor is modern, lounge feel but not tacky.

Rating for Hillsborough Avenue location ONLY.  Pollo Tropical is my all time favorite "fast" food chain restaurant.  I love that if offers tasty, healthy options.  I used to visit it years ago when I lived in Ft. Lauderdale, and was thrilled when they finally decided to give it (another) shot in the Tampa area. 

You'll notice that "fast" is in quotes, because unfortunately it depends on which location you visit.  Last night I walked in, waited in line without moving, then walked out.  It seems like this location on Hillsborough Avenue is always understaffed.  I usually try to go to the location on Waters Avenue - they have a better grasp on how to keep people moving along.

I have been coming to Royal Thai Palace for about 13 years.  The Shrimp or Chicken in Basil Sauce is fantastic!  Love the Phad Thai!  The parking is sometimes an issue and they are closed on Mondays, but the food is consistently very good.  If you are looking for very good quality Thai food, but understand that one serving is one serving, unlike a lot of local Chinese restaurants that give you mountain size portions of poor quality food.
Good service and oasis in the bustle of the Italian market. Sfogliatelle was good. Friends thought the coffee was nice and aromatic.

I like West Shore Mall. I have since I came to Tampa, it's always had a theatre. This is a pretty clean one. I wish they would enforce rules on phones in the movies, almost every time someone answers a call lately. It's part of why I often enjoy the 21+ only movies.

I really like this place.  The staff is very friendly and helpful. So far I've tried the miso salmon, chicken picatta, and turkey meatloaf in the medium size and all were delicious and I was very surprised with the amount of food in comparison to calories.  When it comes to price it honestly isn't much more than an organic microwave meal and it's fresher.  I bought two chicken breasts from Publix Greenwise for $6 tonight and couldn't help but think I could have almost had a whole meal at Fitlife.

The South Tampa location is where I first became aware of Fit Life foods. Their behind the scenes tour was pretty amazing.  It was interesting to see how things like Alfredo sauce is made - since it's not "cheesy".  I liked finding out how the meat was taken care of, and being able to taste a few of the entrees fresh. 

If you've never tried Fit Life foods, I suggest you do, you will NOT be disappointed!

Had them at The Brews with Attitudes event at the Pepin Hospitality Center, much better than any of the food they were serving up inside to the VIP guest.  Love to try different food trucks, this was one of 4/5 at the event and recommended, they were right it was very good.  This may have been my first Arepas.

First visit, went with Mom and husband.  Now, since you're already in the Hard Rock and spending an afternoon and night, you don't leave to eat.  This option was already in thought when we went, so we made a reservation and reserved a booth.

Reserve the booth if you haven't - it's a great view.  There's all of 3 of them.  Facing the all glassed in kitchen.  Very nice kitchen.  Clean, neat, watch your steak get grilled.

So my husband had the Cowboy ribeye.  WOW it's pricey.  Not his favorite cut because it's so fatty.  It was good, but he wouldn't do that steak again.
Nice touch with the huge bone and his initials carved into it.  But he'd go with a strip steak next time.

My porterhouse was nice.  A little too much fat on the strip side for me.
More leftovers for dinner 2 nights later....  It was good, but I wouldn't get it again for that price (everything is a la carte like a lot of steakhouses, but not all....)

My Mom got the pork chop milenesa.  Now.  This was FABULOUS.  I had some of it for leftovers 3 days later and OMG it was WONDERFUL and one of the less expensive entree choices on the menu.  Seriously spectacular.

We had a shrimp cocktail  It was 4 jumbo shrimp.  For $18.  Really?  They could have done 6 shrimp and at 3 bucks a piece would have still made a killer margin on them.  They were good, don't get me wrong, but not worth $18 for 4.  The cocktail sauce was awesome!

We had the lobster bisque.  I split it with my husband.  It was really good.  Top of the line.  One of the best, flavor-wise.  It was just a tiny bit loose.  Overall, very very good.  And we would get it again and split it!

We all 3 split one salad.  It was definitely enough.

We all split a side of garlic mashed potatoes.  They were good.

And we all split the tower of onion rings.  They were oily and don't reheat well (even broiled).

Overall, we'd go again but we wouldn't get those steaks and wouldn't make a special trip.
Now that we have experienced quite a bit on the menu.  We still have to try the seafood side and we will order much less food!  We didn't get to try dessert - will have to save room next time.

Added note: Forgot to mention that they have live music, sometimes a trio, sometimes acoustic, sometimes a solo artist.  Have been here since this last review and sat right in front of the stage to listen in on the great music!

I went for a celebratory lunch with my coworkers, and had a surprisingly delicious meal. Vizcaya has recently finished a big remodel, and the place now seems modern and hip.

Our meal started with 3 shared appetizers: a meat platter, calamari, and bacon wrapped dates, which were outstanding. I followed that with collard green and cannelini bean soup, then on to my entree of seafood paella. Yum!  Plenty of shrimp and the saffron rice was plump and moist.  Since I saved half my meal for dinner, I had plenty of room for dessert  - an excellent tiramisu.  There was a nice crowd at lunchtime, and I can't wait to bring friends here to check out their dinners.
Amazing service!   Staff were super helpful and friendly.  Food was good, little expensive.  Next time we will get a small fries for the three of us.

Best view in Tampa!  Antipasto bar is second to none!  You go select from a bounty of marinated veggies, meats and cheeses.  Food is wonderful.  Their signature veal scallopini is amazing, but can't lose with any selection.  Yes, it's expensive.  In my opinion, it's well worth it.  We like to go for date night and sit by the bar, listen to the pianist, have a couple drinks, get the antipasto bar and dessert.

This is the best vending food cart that I have ever eaten from. Let me preface this by saying that I am neither vegetarian nor vegan. The healthy food options here are superb! The style of food is more Middle Eastern and Mediterranean. 

Everything that I ordered from this food truck has been delicious. The line near UPenn's campus can get fairly long during the lunch hour. 

Here is what I tried so far:
Spinach Pie
Falafel
Taboulli salad
Greek Salad
Pita Sandwich on whole wheat pita with baba ganouj, w/ salad

They have two carts 11am-3pm Monday-Fri:
courtyard at 34th and Walnut streets
Spruce St. between 35th and 36th
Best program I have ever been on. Minus 15 pounds  in one month and still moving down on the scale!

There are so many wonderful aspects of joining the JCC.  Fortunately, it is good to get old and enjoy the few good benefits of aging.  No. 1 is having Silver Sneakers.  The "J" is like my second home. Stay well and make new friends.  Not to mention keeping your bodies in tip top shape.    The many choices of  classes they offer keep getting better and better.  The fun for me begins with Tuesday's dance party led by Rhina G. an amazing energized lady who is a joy to be around. .  Also the Zumba classes with Brenda are cardio as well as choreographed with finesse and class.  Can't get enough of them.  Thanks JCC for being a part of my life.

AMAZING. WONDERFUL. STRESS FREE. 
Vito is the best of the best. He was so reassuring and helpful throughout the process of selecting our flowers.  Planning a wedding can be stressful but Vito and Kathleen made everything easy. Looking through pictures to get ideas, being sent home with gorgeous bouquets. Getting prompt responses to every phone call and email. Looking at the wide array of hard wear. Everything was just easy. He didn't bat an eye when we went to look at a centerpiece with the flowers we had selected and didn't like the finished look. He took us to the fridge and started pulling things into the bunch, taking out and voila! The perfect centerpiece. 
The final product was breathtaking, it's been over a week since the wedding and guests are still commenting on how pretty and elegant everything was. I will post pictures as soon as I get some. I am truly sad that the planning period is over and I can't hang out anymore discussing wedding ideas.

$12 for a Ribeye sandwich that comes with potato chips.  The sandwich was about 4-5 inches long with a little bit of meat and onions on it.  It was bland and forgettable.  I think i enjoyed the potato chips more. The prices are too ambitious considering we're talking about pub food here.

Service was fine.

Yeah its ok, its a chain, its nothing special, its just average average.  I wouldn't eat here normally but its close by and I like breakfast in the middle of the day sometimes.   
Grab the country boy breakfast or mama's pancakes and you won't leave hungry.....or at least you shouldn't.

I've come here a few times for reliable and casual get-together's and never been disappointed. The sushi is tasty and reasonably creative. The rice bowls are huge enough to take half or more to go for your late night snacking pleasure. 

The atmosphere is classy enough while still remaining casual. The full bar always helps as nothing goes better with sushi than martini's, trust me :-)

It's a diner.  I came here for breakfast with my family last weekend and we all had a great meal.  Good eggs, potatoes, grits....even mickey mouse pancakes for the kids.   Nothing special, nothing over the top.  

The restaurant has mostly 4 person booths- just one or two tables for big parties- so just be aware if you're coming with a large group.  

The staff was friendly, prices were great, and they accept credit cards. :)

Of all the places in Philly why, oh why, would I ever come here?
Three words.

Fifty Dollar Gift-card.

Or is that 4 words (2 words and a compound word?)?

Yes, folks, I won a $50 gift card good at ANY Hard Rock location from a charity auction so a good-sport friend (I love her - and she put up with Hard Rock well) and I decided to play cheap-meal roulette.

I read the menu online before we went and luckily, they sat us is the back room (the Philadelphia room) which is much less loud and crowded.

Our self-effacing server, Stewart, was attentive and kind - especially since I can be such a pain in the a$$.  I ordered a pomegranate martini and ask for it NOT to contain Sierra Mist.  (I mean, really, Sierra Mist?)  The $8.50 martini was basically vodka, Monin Pomegranate Syrup and seltzer.  It was fine and I was able to cultivate a buzz in about 12 minutes.

My friend wisely chose the shrimp fajitas which were served on a sizzling platter and tasted pretty good.  I poorly chose a great steak which was so gristly I had to send it back.  Poor, Stewart took it back and the next one they brought was at least edible.

But flavorless.

I literally had to put salt and pepper on it and was eyeing the A-1 (I HATE A-1!).
A good steak should have flavor on its own.  Maybe that's why they put "Cabernet butter" aka Country Crock on all the grilled items?

Anyhow - dessert was a BOMB. Over sweet cheesecake on a cardboard and wood-shaving crust.  Do not even think about getting the syrup-ed-out creme brulee here.
At least it was cheap ($2.99 for the dessert "bites")

Believe it or not - I am giving them 3 stars (they really deserve 2) because they were very accomodating to all of our requests and we had a fun time.

Would I come here again?
Not unless I had another gift card - and even then - I think I would re-gift!

I have been here before right after they opened  a few years ago. I was not impressed enough to make a return visit but I had dropped my car off for service nearby and had time to kill while I waited. What better way than breakfast? 

At first, I was a little surprised to not see a chorizo burrito among the breakfast burrito choices. Then, I realized it's part of the regular burrito menu. I was told it included potatoes and asked them to leave those out. I pick up my breakfast burrito at the counter and, on the tray, are two squeeze bottles, each with a different salsa. I want to try both so I cut my burrito in half, Before I smother my burrito I cut off a piece of tortilla to taste each. The green is tart and spicy... tomatillo? The red one has an unexpected kick and actually made me cough at first. I liked the fact that the chorizo was not completely mixed into the eggs and you can still see pieces of it. Both salsas were good, but I liked the red one better.

Overall, it was a satisfying and tasty burrito. This after I have recently lamented the quality and price of breakfast burritos at other places in town. I go by this place all the time. I would go back for the breakfast burrito and to give the rest of the menu another chance.

As good as it gets! The pasta is fantastic. The owner is a real nice guy who gave us a free pound of pasta, after he realized we screwed up some pasta that we received in a gift basket. The rest of the staff are very nice too and very helpful.

Our visit was somewhat marred by the fact that the AC was struggling to keep up with the Florida summer that day. It was just too hot inside to be comfortable.

As for the tastes we experienced, the greens, jalapeno mac and cheese, and coleslaw side dishes were all excellent. Now, to the BBQ...

The meats were tender, well-prepared, and lukewarm. We generally grade Florida BBQ on a curve since the standards are low compared to a few other states (e.g. Texas, Tennessee). In this case, it was definitely well above average! The meaty ribs were very under-seasoned to my taste but to their pit master's credit, they did fall off the bone. The brisket burnt ends were incredibly moist and flavorful. The pulled pork was plentiful and hit the spot.

This might be my first Yelp review but this was not my first visit to Ralphs. My family has been going there for generations and I cannot even remember my first time there. Needless to say, it's an old favorite. 

I made a reservation for my husband and myself for Friday night for "date night". We ended up waiting at the upstairs bar for almost 30 minutes after the reservation time. We did get an upstairs table as we had requested. Unfortunately, we did not receive one of the private tables. We were seated at a table for 6- with another couple at the other end with an empty table in between. We eventually got used to other people at our table but we could not get used to the party behind us. There were several adults and a small child. You would think the child was the issue however one of the adults was causing a ruckus. He continuously made "fart" noises at the child- despite the pleas from his table to stop and my dirty looks. Luckily Ralphs was able to accommodate and move us to an open private table closer to the bar which was conveniently available as these issues occurred.  

The service was excellent. Despite a delay in being seated, our server was very attentive and apologetic when he thought he wasn't being. The food was also excellent. I had the veal francaise and my husband had the spaghetti with sausage and a side of meatball.  My veal was very tender and the sauce was apportioned perfectly. The extra lemons on top caused the dish to be slightly too lemony- but still enjoyable. Knowing how filling this meal could be, we skipped appetizers and dessert. We did sample the bread which was very fresh and flowing to our table. We also had a carafe of the house pinot grigio which was reasonably priced and delicious.

Looking forward to the next visit in July.

I had not been to this Beef O' Brady's in quite sometime, three years to be exact. I was looking for a place to take my kids and meet a friend and her kids for dinner and we decided on Beef O' Brady's. Needless to say, my kids had a blast playing games and I had fun chatting with my friends and eating wings. Our server was fantastic and I love how she was so attentive with all the kids. As a mom, it is nice to go to a kid-friendly place with great food and service. I will definitely be back.

This review is for the service department: I drove way East just to get my oil changed and I'm glad I did. I was a little skeptical considering the range of reviews on their business page, but how bad can a free oil change be? Appointments are strongly recommended but they'll make sure to squeeze you in if you show up sans appointment. Terry was my service representative and I can't recall his assistants name, but they were both fantastic and professional. My experience here was stellar from the moment I pulled up. The service center is quite large and they have plenty of people on staff to make things move quickly and efficiently. I checked in and about half an hour later I was out of there. The waiting area is super spacious with plenty of seating, the WiFi is free and fast and they offer complimentary coffee and water. For those who come with an appetite, they have a concession area. After an extremely poor experience with another Toyota dealer in town it's worth it to me to drive to this location because the customer service was excellent.

I facebooked The Press Box before going there,  and got a response saying it was 50 cent wing night, with $2.25 pints.   You'd think awesome right????  Until you get the bill.  Will never return, their customer service sucks!!!!!!!! When I showed them my facebook page on my phone, the manager told me he would take care of it... because it doesn't have domestic pints or anything specific.... just PINTS.  So he took my phone with him and while he was adjusting my bill, another manager comes by and tells him NO,  then comes to my table and says well it doesn't say anything specific just that we have them...... Are you kidding me????  You are under minding another manager AFTER he told me it would be taken care of?????  I was so pissed that I said I'd pay the damn bill but would never be back and would tell everyone I know not to go there. I can't believe this is the customer service this place wants to have representing them And for what... 6 bucks. And to under mind one manager who says they will take care of this for only another one to come over and say no we wont. Customer service means alot and says alot for a business,  this experience alone has completely ruined it for us.

I enjoyed my first visit to this restaurant. An ambitious menu and a fine choice of beverages (including beers from Gainesville's Swamphead and Dunedin's 7th Sun) in a fun modern atmosphere on Harbour Island. Not everything worked perfectly off the menu, but there was lots to try and many interesting and unusual tastes. I especially liked the dijon mashed potatoes (a special with the tenderloin for restaurant week). Restaurant is in a lovely area to walk around by the water inbetween Jasckson's and Channelside and across from the Times forum and HIstory Center all of which are in walking distance across bridges. The burger was excellent!

Great happy hour. For someone who usually enjoys only Coors Light I was impressed with their generous samples. I had the opportunity to realize what a beer whimp I have been and I finally found a great craft beer on tap. Can't wait to go back.

Recently, they've begun selling their pricey beer in 12-oz draft cups at the monthly Flicks & Food Trucks event, with no corresponding lower prices. I have noticed they are not as busy on these nights as they once were. I mentioned the cup size to a server and was told they had "run out" of 16-oz cups. But on subsequent visits, they were still using the 12-oz cups. Now, $6 and up for a 12-oz draft is a rip.

My husband and I love making the drive out to Fitlife foods to enjoy fresh, pre-made, and HEALTHY meals!

We only wish it were closer to us in Brandon ;)

The meals are of a wide variety from Greek meatballs and Ab tight steak (my favorites) to cashew chicken and BBQ beef and turkey meatloaf (hubby's favorite) - all of the meals are made in- house and taste awesome.

The facility is super clean and the staff uber friendly!

These meals are worth the price and come in handy for all kinds of meals and snacks without you having to think about measuring or looking at nutritional facts - they do it all for you!

DO NOT LET THE STEAKS ON THE FRONT DISPLAY FOOL YOU...Steaks are under seasoned and just tasteless!

Place is very classy and clean.  Inside the Hard Rock casino.  

Come here and grab a drink, grab a seat, and on watch the live band play. 

Service is attentive but very fake.  I know the waitresses are doing their jobs and they have to be nice to everyone, but you can just sometimes tell deep inside their friendliness is all a show and they're just plain bitchy...good thing they don't show it and try to give a smile.

Ordered a porterhouse, and it came out medium as we asked, but there was no flavor whatsoever.  The char and grill on the steaks seemed okay, but the flavor was just plain bland and nonexistent.  We ordered the steak around 12am midnight, maybe the head chef was already gone..?  Because this steak could not have been cooked by a pro.

The cocktail shrimp appetizer comes with 4 huge tiger shrimps with cocktail sauce.  This was so much better than the porterhouse.

Very friendly and helpful staff, large rooms, comfy enough, but terribly maintained; everything was either broken (thermostat balcony switch kept us non ac for a day), stained (headboard, shower curtain) or dirty (tub, toilet, balcony chairs, carpets...and ridiculously sticky breakfast tables...ewwww). Just some attention to maintenance would pick this place up a star or two! Great room rates, as well, and free wifi throughout, though $18 overnight parking is a bit of a rip. Ok to stay but they really need to systematically fix everything.

VIBE:  Neighborhood restaurant & bar, large bar area (about 1/3rd of entire restaurant)  is visited by "mature" regulars, big TVs in bar side, dim lighting.  
ORDER THE:  Filet tip salad.  Have steak and salad in one dish.  Comes with bottomless basket of hot bread with butter or olive oil.   A bargain at $14.00.  Ask for Terry for server. 

Secret for cigar smokers - have outdoor smoking patio in back on 2nd floor.  No food service and have to be nice to get server to bring drinks up.  Would rate 3.5 but have to go with either  3 or 4 rating
Their low fat mango smoothie was refreshing this time around. Their wifi is good as gold. Everyone is nice, and it's comfy here.  All is well.
I haven't had their cheesesteaks (apparently I should try them) but I've had their hoagies twice and they are mediocre.  Nothing wrong with them, but nothing that great either, for the price.  Meh.
Great, great Bun Ga and Pho...  I will return for sure.

We have been visiting this place for years, as another reviewer stated Robert's is closed on Sundays :)  Every meal we have gotten has been great from fried eggs and sausage or bacon to pancakes and french toast.  However just recently we ventured into their lunch section and were blown away!  We ordered a couple of different burgers and were very impressed.  They make their own bread, hand-shaped patties and hand cut fries - on appearance alone I was sold.  But the taste was delicious.  I ordered a burger with bleu cheese and it comes with the usual veggie toppings - juicy and SO good.  Cody's has been a long time favorite for burgers but since they are also closed on Sundays the Egg Connection is at the rescue!  Plus service has always been quick and efficient, it has a homey feel including bickering between the staff every now and then but its good entertainment while waiting for your food.

This is my go-to place for hair dye. They have the best selection in Philadelphia and even carry my favorite brand, which is 'Special Effects'.  Apart from that, Crash Bang Boom is a legendary shopping spot for Punk Rockers wanting to shop for interesting an unique goods.

Customer service is friendly and down to earth.

This place is really cool...remember those college days, they will come back when you walk in here.  Everyone is really nice, even the door man.  If you check in on four square you will get a free appetizer!.  

I ordered the cheesesteak and it definitely is done right...not one of these over glorified steaks that people seem to be trying to do now.  Cheap, good, bar food and a fun environment.  If you're in Manyayunk check it out.
Food was not terrible just not great. Great atmosphere. Sangria was lacking. Was hoping for more.

If you like waiting 20 minutes for a beer, getting your shoes stepped on because there is no room,  or waiting 10 minutes to use the small bathroom; this place is for you.

terrible..........

I finally got to try Nico's Arepas Grill at the July 4th Fireworks at Idlewild Church and Food Truck Rally. This was honestly the busiest truck there, and there were quite a bit of trucks. It was also quite rainy, but that did not stop anyone from fireworks and food.
I ordered shredded beef on an arepa and it was just so yummy.  There was a bit more salt than I am use to, but I felt that this was something different than I have had any where else.  These are the white arepas not the yellow corn arepas. I usually do not like the white arepas but with the filling it was great.
I would definitely go back, and I see why everyone likes it. Yum Yum.

I love Uber! 

You can actually see where your car is, and the estimated arrival time.

You feel fancy driving around in a lincoln town car.

The drivers open the door for you, making you feel even more fancy.

No strange smells.

No loud cell phone chatting by your driver.

You don't have to prepare yourself for an argument about not having cash and using your card.

Everything goes right through your phone, including tip, so there is no payment to deal with during your ride.

Yes it is expensive, but if you use the promo codes, you'll be surprised at how cheap it actually is.

Yay Uber!

Loved driving around in the black über car!  I loved the app and knowing exactly when the driver was going to be there. We had a nice pleasant ride and the driver was friendly, it's a bit pricier than cabs but you def get more for your money such as : convenience, pleasant ride, you can use your credit card in the app without the driver getting mad at you for not using cash! I would def reccomendt it
I am so happy that they make a vegetarian egg roll!

I always enjoy bonefish...yes it's a chain, but its a solid one at that. 

They've made some slight changes (bread on a plate not in a basket, less expensive drinks, and specialty drinks side of the menu) but all in all, pretty much how bonefish has been since its inception. 

We ordered the grilled scallops (on the regular menu) and the orange roughy off the specialty menu. Both were freshly prepared and tasty. And might I add well priced at $19 and $21.

My only complaint is the succotash that came with the entrees had meat in it and I didn't realize that until 2 bites in. If this is a garnish to everyone's plate, they should make it without meat for those who are vegetarians, or they should make sure that it suites people's dietary needs. Just a little tidbit of advice to management. 

We shared a flourless macadamia nut brownie topped with vanilla icecream and fresh whipped cream. Yum!

All in all, an enjoyable evening with the hubby.

I can't live without this place. I was initially surprised at their selection and low prices but now I've come to rely on them. As a Rite Aid wellness card holder and a fan of store brand products, I can really knock out a lot of my shopping here. The vitamins are often buy one get one, sometimes with surprise discounts on using the card. Don't even get me started on their buy 1 get 1 sales on Special K and other cereal! This is also my preferred pharmacy. The pharmacists are unbelievably friendly and helpful. Same goes for the front register staff. I can find myself just browsing the aisles sometime. It's a great Rite Aid location.

One of my favorite places on earth. Which is pretty ironic considering this is the road I take to commute to work. However on the weekends between April and October before 5pm it is all a joy ride. I never appreciated the beauty of the area until I started riding my bike as a hobby. There's nothing like having car-free road in scenic, picturesque setting where I can log some miles. If you get lucky, you can even share the road with a horse from the Belmont stables. I almost fell off my bike the first time I saw Shetland pony pulling a person on a little carriage. 

If only West River were open to bikers, skaters and joggers only after my drive home on the weekdays, it would truly be perfect. Unfortunately the trail on the sidewalk isn't that great and I feel the West River side is a little more buggy than Kelly. Although not as pretty in my opinion, and far more crowded, at least Kelly has a decent trail for some weekday riding!

I have to be up front at the start of the review- I have Target Syndrome. I have trouble walking into Target and only coming out with the items on my list. I have even more trouble going in to return something and coming out empty handed.

With that being said, I don't love this Target. Yea- I know where everything is and can call it my regular Target but its just never a good shopping experience. It is great that they have a produce section however I can never find a good bag of spring mix or spinach when I go in. Maybe I'm just going at the wrong time but they always seem to be out. 

Too often it can be home to some pretty sketchy characters. I was once approached by a panhandler when walking through the home goods section. Not cool. 

The proximity of the store to my office and the convenience of being able to buy almost everything I need will keep me begrudgingly going back.

Irish 31 seems to be either extremely crowded or basically empty as I drive by it all the time on my way to and from the LA Fitness in Hyde Park. I've stopped in twice and was very comfortable each time because it was basically empty on my visits. The constant drink specials make this more of a place to imbibe than dine, as I've found the menu to be average and perhaps a bit overpriced. The service is friendly and efficient, so I'll be back again for drinks...but probably not to eat anything more than an appetizer.

I've been a regular customer at EATS! for over a year now and I have many favorite items on the menu. The service at the bar is particularly good on Friday nights and most drinks, like the food, are affordably priced. I've met the owner and know he really cares about his business. Honestly, the only thing that keeps me from going to EATS! more often is when they have either Karaoke or live music at night. This place is too small for that as there is nowhere to hide for those of us who simply want to drink or dine without another bad version of Brown Eyed Girl or Don't Stop Believin'.

Evos offers healthy and vegetarian fast food options.  Would I come here if I was craving a great burger? No. Would I come here if I was in a time crunch and needed a relatively healthy meal?  Yes!

The burgers are pretty good and contain naturally raised beef with no hormones or antibiotics.  You can even add nitrate-free bacon to your burger.  Personally, I prefer the Honey Mesquite Chicken and the salads over the burgers.  The two most amazing items on the menu are the air fries and the milkshakes.  The air fries are not greasy like fast food fries and Evos offers four different dipping sauces.  The milkshakes are homemade and made with low-fat organic milk.  I have not tried the veggie burger or vegetarian corn dog since I am not much of a fan of meat substitute products.  

The food isn't exactly cheap at Evos, but very fairly priced if you consider the cost of grass-fed beef and organic produce.  If you are a vegetarian or just trying to eat healthier give Evos a try!

My friend was in town and staying near the convention center so we planned to meet up for dinner/happy hour. I wanted to select a place that was both close to the convention center and had an all around good happy hour with both food and drink specials. I had been to Smokin' Betty's before for Sips so they came to mind as a good choice. As with normal Sips fashion, it had been packed the last time I was there. Yesterday it had a nice crowd but nothing too overwhelming. 

We arrived a little before 6:00 and were told we can sit at a table and still get the happy hour specials. Score! It is nice sometimes not having to cram around the bar. I already had some idea that I wanted to try the pizza of the day. When I found out it was buffalo chicken, the deal was sealed. Both my husband and I had the craft beer of the day to drink. I honestly forget what i was, but it was $3 and pleasant. My friend had the grilled chicken salad and the cheesesteak egg rolls and my husband had the slider combo. We all enjoyed our meals. Nothing really to rave about, but it totally hit the spot at the time. In hind site, I probably should have ordered 2 appetizers because I was hungry later in the evening, but it was still a decent sized happy hour pizza. 

The service was so-so. Attentive and friendly enough. We did ask our server for two separate checks but he forgot about it. 

My only real complaint was the little flies circling the dining room. I believe they were just fruit flies- the typical, unavoidable summer type. I didn't see any in the food and nothing bit me but they did fly by every so often. I think that just happens on hot days when the doors are open.

Smokin' Betty's fit the bill for a happy hour spot with good food and drink specials.
This food truck make the best Venezuela arepas you can try the people work in there is very nice

McGillin's is one of my old stomping grounds. Especially on karaoke night. I made my epic karaoke return after a few year hiatus last night.

One of our initial concerns was grabbing a table for our group- we expected anywhere from 5-10 people. As luck would have it, a group was leaving as we were walking in. The place was already pretty crowded as its a good post-Sips location. I was actually blown away by the service. For a crowded place with lots going on, our server was unbelievable attentive. She anticipated our every liquid need and kept the water and pitchers flowing. A special shout out to the 2 for $1 tacos. I had eaten dinner prior to going out but I figured I'd give it a shot for $1. Given my expectations were very low to start for this cheap bar food, however I was pleasantly surprised. The tacos were hard shell and had a beef filling with cheese and lettuce and came with the option of salsa or sour cream. They even had slight kick to them. They had the spot for a snack, but if I wanted to make a meal out of them I would probably need to order 3-4 plates worth. Still a good deal.

Now as for the karaoke, this place is a blast!  The regulars were just as I had remembered. Talented and quirky. At least last night, everyone who participated picked crowd pleasers. My friend and I probably didn't get our moment of fame until after 12:30- way past my normal Wednesday bed time. It was worth it though. A great night out re-living some good times and creating some new memories.

I had a single-patty cheeseburger and my sister a single-patty bacon cheeseburger. The counter lady suggested we split an order of fries; it was a good choice, because the two of us could not finish them all. I thought my burger was pretty average. My sister liked hers a bit better. The music was way too loud- and I normally like my music loud. To be honest, I prefer In-n-Out. Their burgers are better, though I'm not crazy about their skinny fries. Their prices are much better. I may go to Five Guys again if someone else in a group wants to, but I probably wouldn't go on my own.
This place delivered my food hours later, in a size small instead of large. Needless to say, we were extremely hungry and disappointed when they finally arrived.

One of my favorite spots for lunch breaks in the city!  Serious variety of food choices, from salad bar, to deli, to baked potato bar, to soups, to off-the-grill menu items.  I always get the salmon burger, I'm so addicted to this, its delicious, with a chopped tomato bruchetta on top, only $5.50!  Wow!   Always get the HOMEMADE POTATO SALAD, its seriously the best potato salad you will ever eat.  I've heard their reubens here are bangin, but I don't eat those so can't tell by experience.  They have several different choices of bread, and a separate counter for desserts by the piece!  Grab a mini-cheesecake, fruit tart, or canoli!  

Best part besides the cheap prices and amazing food, is the FRIENDLY STAFF!  Everyone behind the counter making your food are so sweet, I love them all!   Also, they put out free samples of muffins, popcorn, cookies, whatever they have for the day in baskets along the counters so you can nibble while you're waiting for your food to be cooked.  Get a frequent-buyer card stamped, buy 12 lunches get your 13th free!

This is one of my favorite establishments in Philadelphia and probably one of the first I would recommend looking for a great meal or a date night. I've been coming here for years- the first being a restaurant week date night when I was dating my husband. Over the years I've visited for numerous special occasions including Valentines day, birthdays and anniversaries. It's hard not to come back for these occasions- if you sign up for the VIP club they send you anniversary and birthday offers each year.

Another notable thing about Bistro Romano is the happy hour. From 5-7 every Monday- Thursday they offer free appetizers and discounted drinks. It's not a poor quality buffet. Each customer sitting at the bar will receive a small plate of whatever the chef decides to make that day. Often I've seen calamari, one piece of lobster ravioli and bruschetta. Sometimes the chef uses it as a time to test new ideas. Top quality samples! Classics by Frank Sinatra can usually be heard in the background. The bartender has been working there for as long as I can remember and is always attentive and friendly. Take note of the gorgeous historic bar.

Downstairs is the main dining room and it lives up to the awards it's received of "most romantic". The ambiance is dark and candle lit and the tables are intimate.

On to the food. Try the Caesar salad. Just do it. It's made for two and made in front you. Sadly my husband is not a Caesar salad eater so I only get this great opportunity when we dine out with others. Very often they will give a small appetizer sample- similar to what is served during happy hour. My biggest problem at Bistro Romano is that there are two dishes that I love and I barely stray from my staples. The Veal Melanzane is "lightly breaded veal baked with fresh eggplant, roasted peppers, marinara sauce and topped with mozzarella cheese."  Then there is the lobster ravioli which is served in a succulent sherry cream sauce. On the rare occasion that there is room for dessert, might I recommend the tira misu.

They have some great week night specials like pasta Monday ($12.95 for a house salad and any pasta dish) and WOW Wednesday ($33.95 for any appetizer, entree and dessert). From time to time they will send the VIP club/fb fans special 3 course offers with a free drink and opportunity to save $20 on the next trip. They treat their VIPs well.

After writing this review I'm a little anxious for my next visit. Hope you can make it too!

I heard about Uber but decided to try it for the first time using the promo code for Yelp Summer Soirée at North Shore Beach Club. 

I had already downloaded the app but hadn't set up the account at that point. The entire process was super easy. Requesting a black town car took no time. I left a little wiggle room in case it was late but my ride was set to arrive in 4 minutes. I received an text confirmation and a phone call when the car was close by.

To be honest, I was a little nervous at first. I've only taken cabs and I was hoping this wasn't the beginning of an abduction movie. Well thankfully my imagination is over active and it was nothing like that. The driver was courteous and took a direct route. Having the payment handled a head of time was a huge convenience. 

I think Uber has a new regular!

Jack's is favorite brunch favorite for me and my husband. We thought it was a great spot to take our out of town friends to for brunch. 

We had a reservation and were seated right away. Our drink orders were taken promptly and I was able to start enjoying my mimosa. Feeling adventurous, I decided to stray from my two regular dishes and ordered the Southern Omelet.  Before our dishes arrived, we received our bread and jam basket. To be honest, I forgot that they did this for brunch and was pleasantly surprised by the tasty banana bread and biscuits. 

I was very happy with my omelet choice. The multi cheeses were perfectly melting with the proportional sausage and pepper. The breakfast potatoes were seasoned with a slight kick and mixed with pepper and onions. Added bonus in my mind that there was fresh fruit instead of bread. The entire party also seamed happy with their choices (another Southern omelet, firehouse French toast with apple butter and a cheese steak).


Perfectly closing out the meal was a round of tiny fresh baked chocolate chip cookies for the table. 

The service was friendly and attentive.

We will be back for many more brunches!

As a local to Philly, I'm pretty embarrassed to say this was only my second trip to the Reading Terminal Market. Wow. Just wow. I'm glad I didn't come hungry or I would have been quite overwhelmed. I was meeting my girlfriends for the start of a big day of eating. One of my friends suggested Reading terminal and based on what I've heard about the variety, it was the perfect pick,

We did a lap of the building before anyone purchased a thing. I wish I took notes to make my next trip easier. Things that stood out included the sushi, DiNic's, a stand with bacon, a fresh fish stand, a juice/smoothie place and the list goes on. 

We were able to grab a table with 5 seats and took turns getting our lunch. I love the variety at the table- sesame chicken, a turkey sand which, char grilled eggplant sandwich and a falafel. Since I was fresh off brunch, I decided to just have some ice cream. I was intrigued by the abundance of Amish vendors so picked Miller's Twist. After sample a few flavors, I decided on a small cup of banana ice cream. Perfect! Creamy and delicious.

If this wasn't the start of the day out, I'd probably go on a produce shopping spree. I can't wait to return hungry and ready to bring any purchases promptly to a fridge!

I'm Italian, so it's very hard for me to step into an Irish pub, let alone speak to an Irish person or even make eye contact with one of 'them' for that matter. I once found out a girlfriend was 1/64th Irish... I left her for her half sister who was 1/128th Irish (which doesn't count).

All joking aside. 

This place is pretty sweet. VERY comfortable, cozy and warm. I've seen it very busy driving by, so I can't comment what it's like packed, but we were there on a Monday night and it was slow (it was 930), so it was great. The menu has a lot to pick from as far as 'Irish pubs' are concerned and the food is priced dirt cheap. $22 bucks with a glass of wine and we both went home satisfied.

Waitress was super nice as well and the place was clean. I've only been there once but it's my new favorite Irish Bar out of the 2 I've ever been to. ;)

So, there's a reason why we hardly go OUT to the movies anymore and this last visit this past weekend just reconfirmed it.

The location is good, convenient.
The selection of movies is good, right on with new releases and options.
The theatres are decent, don't notice anything terrible, dirty, etc.
Haven't ever had a bad movie experience here.

But that's where the good ends.

The price of a 5:30 pm movie (which I consider twilight) is $10.50 per person.  That is RIDICULOUS!  I would rather wait until it's on RedBox for a buck fifty on BlueRay.

The price of a SMALL COKE is $4.75.  WHAT?!?!?!!!!!!!!!
And the price of a decent size bag of Reese's pieces is $4.25.  WHAT?!??!!??!???
There is no beer/wine sold at this theatre, a nice offering in most other theatres.

So, it cost us $30 for 2 tix for a 5:30 pm movie, one small coke to share and a bag of candy.  This is the biggest rip off!

I wish the $1 theatres would make a come back, I'd go every weekend, even during the week AND PAY THEIR CONCESSION PRICES!

When/if we go OUT to the movies again, we will avoid this theatre unless we're just absolutely set on seeing something showing only in this theatre.  We really kinda like the Muvico Theatre in Ybor, eventhough it's in Ybor.
Chicago. You think of the SNL sketch where the guys say "da bulls or da bears" great place

Vizcaya was a pleasant surprise!  Kim & I had tapas and the highlight was the bacon wrapped langoustines (really big shrimp or a really small lobster).  The salads were excellent and we were stuffed when we left.  We will be back!
I love mexican food and this new place its no to bad the place is nice I like the decoration all restaurant

Could it be my awesome charm? My sweet demeanor? Or are they just a really friendly and happy group of people who managed to smile even while I dragged my luggages zombie-like in the late PM hours? 

The low price and free shuttle to the airport lured me here. All times I've been here have gone smooth with no problem. I never had to wait longer than 15 minutes for the shuttle whether it was to the airport or from it. The driver even helped me with loading/ unloading my luggage each time. 

The one snag I hit came from them double charging me... I had pre-purchased the parking stay as part of a bundle with my plane tickets yet they still charged me upon retrieving my car (in my sleepy state I didn't think twice). Upon realizing the error, I emailed the company and they refunded me the next day... FAST and SWEET!

PYRA SAYS: This normal lady doesn't require much from a parking visit. I just want my car to be housed safe while not paying those same high prices as parking at the airport... this place delivers it.

There's something sort of clinical and uninviting about this place. Rigid lines, neon green, wood,  I don't know, I just don't get that fuzzy feeling of a friendly neighborhood independent coffee shop when I come here. 

Pros: Not usually too crowded. Convenient spot. Reliable internet. Great selection of teas. Music was fine, not overbearing or memorable, which is always good for studying.

Cons: Drinks and food are kind of on the pricey side.Tables are small, hard to spread out on. The barristas are fine, not overly friendly or attentive, and sometimes downright absent. Not too many seating options: tiny tables or weird back couch area.

I've studied here a few times over the course of the years and it's a convenient spot, but not some place I've made a habit of going back to.

I walk to this spot for lunch sometimes and its close to a mile away from my office. That being said, the walk is worth it. They have a great variety of slices. I've had a few different slices but I'm pretty basic. The wildest I've gone for is the mac n cheese w broccoli slice which was pretty good. The price is fair and the service is remotely efficient. I would recommend if you're in the area and in the mood for a quick slice.

Delicious!  Nice intimate atmosphere, fabulous sparkling sangria, and most of the food was SO good.

For starters our table of 4 shared yucca fries and octopus medallions. The fries were great-crunchy on the outside, soft on the inside. Served with a tasty dipping sauce.  I wouldn't bother again with the medallions, though - not much flavor in the tiny shaved pieces even though its dipping sauces were good, too.

My entree, the Bouillabaisse, was incredible. Flavorful tomato-based soup broth, stuffed to the gills (hah!) with seafood. Crab legs were piled in the top of the bowl, and mussels, clams, shrimp, squid, and huge chunks of fish were swimming below. 

One would think I'd be stuffed after such a treat, and yet I still managed to find room for the salted caramel cake. Yum! Cake was moist, and the combo of salty/sweet was just right.  

I think I might need to visit Terra Sur Cafe every month!

Waited for our friends at the bar on a busy Saturday night, and had a delicious gin specialty drink. Our table was ready when our friends arrived - TG for reservations, because this place filled up early and quickly.

For my meal I went with 2 starters: the crab cakes and the watermelon salad. The two plump crab cakes had a nice amount of crab in the mix, and were very tasty.  The watermelon salad, however, was awesome! Fresh chunky watermelon, feta, almonds, arugula, with a light dressing - I've never had anything like this, and I've been longing for it in the weeks since my visit.

For dessert I had one of their nightly specials - a red apple tart.  And it was good, but my BF got the summer cake, and it was better.

Nice ending to the night - we got two complimentary loaves of bread to take home - great touch!

Great location and, for the most part, helpful & courteous staff.  The rooms are "worn" and definitely in need of updating but clean and quite acceptable.
