I booked through Hotwire, so I was OK with price paid, but had I paid full price I would have felt overcharged. Our rooms were on the "city view" side which consists of a view of the street and the buildings across the street - the balcony went unused. I had requested a river view upon arrival and was told that other guests could request that, but I would have to take whatever was "left" since I booked through Hotwire. :(
We had 2 rooms and both had ongoing problems with the keys working. We had to get new keys almost every time we wanted to enter our rooms.  After we went to the desk a few times, we were told we could call from the phone by the elevator and they'd be brought to us - keys were delivered quickly when we had to do this.
Our location near the ice machine was unfortunate, as it is loud and we could hear guests using it at all hours.
We stayed Fri -Sun. The shower was slow to drain, resulting in water rising to mid-calf level while showering (unpleasant!).  We let the front desk know as we left for the day on Saturday, and it was working fine upon our return.  However, I also discovered a bag containing most of my cosmetics/brushes (nearly $200 worth) were missing from the bathroom. Staff was ultimately helpful but unapologetic and said I'd have to wait until the next morning to see if it had ended up at their laundry facility. Indeed, it had, and the bag was finally returned as we were checking out - after I completed a claim w/their ins. co. It would have been nice to have my makeup for our Saturday evening out, but I was relieved to finally get everything back and in good condition before we left.
Otherwise, our stay was enjoyable. The downtown location is convenient, the pool was clean and refreshing, and the patio well kept with a nice view of the UT and the river.  The rooms were a decent size and the beds comfortable. 
Charges you may not be prepared for/aware of include valet service & water.  Valet is not optional and is $18 per night. Also read the "fine print" on the water bottles! The small bottle is included but the large one is $4 - we were surprised by this charge at checkout, which is especially irritating because I just used it for the coffee maker and didn't drink the rest.

I have been loving this Chipotle for healthy, fast lunches. Yep, I said healthy. Because if you forgo the wrap, cheese and heavy meats, you really can have your Chipotle and eat it too. Ask for a burrito bowl, and then use a lettuce base with just a little bit of brown rice and black beans, plus onions and peppers. I've noticed that Chipotle workers across the board are super generous with toppings, which is awesome, but also dangerous if you are trying to watch what you're eating. 

I finish off my burrito bowl with pico and both of the tomato-based salsas, plus some guacamole for some rich flavor and healthy fat. Their guac is super fresh and tasty. No dressing needed! 

The employees are always fast and friendly. Once their diet coke dispenser shot out clear liquid which was a bummer, but besides that, this location rocks.
It is a pretty depressing mall! mold smell. needs major update to catch up with 21st century malls!

I love this place, and tonight reminded me I need to visit more frequently.

Usually we go on Soul Food Sundays (world's best fried catfish plus guava mimosas...mmm!) but it is normally mobbed then, and loud, and food and drinks can take awhile.

We went tonight around 6 pm, got sat right away, and enjoyed a very mellow and relaxed atmosphere.  Our server was on the ball, and the food came out so promptly even she commented on it.  Started with a cherry mojito and spiced edamame - both of which were very unusual, but incredibly tasty. For my entree I had the veggie burger, which was so thick and hearty, I actually forgot I wasn't eating meat. The burger was served with fried green tomatoes, which I love here and get every time I visit.  Then a good, but not great, rum cake for dessert. It was moist, but tasted like it had been heated in the microwave, because there were temperature differences throughout.   Per usual, I left happy and stuffed.
Their Meat Lovers pizza was very good, I would also recommend the boneless wings.

I had often heard good things about Jimbos so when we happened to be in the area and hadn't had dinner yet......kismet. This place reeks of old fashion inside with all knotty pine wood furniture. It actually has table service instead of counter service....a nice touch. Wife and I tried the ribs, BBQ beef and sausage. She opted for the 1/3 rack rib dinner, 4 bones instead of the 1/2 rack, 6 bones. I got the 3 meat combo, ribs, beef and sausage. Both came with 2 sides and hush puppies. We had the cole slaw, good, French fries, solid, and the BBQ beans, quite good. When you first arrive and the server gives you your table setup, silverware....etc....they also bring out a squeeze bottle of the only BBQ sauce they have. It is a Carolina type sauce, thin, tasty, and it is Warm. A very nice touch, but back to the BBQ. The ribs are just wonderful, fall off the bone tender but not gamey the way ribs can get sometimes. The beef was good, served sliced very thin. This is not brisket though. The sausage tasted like kielbasa, sliced in half and cooked on the flatiron.
Desert, blueberry crisp was more like blueberry soggy but it was edible with a very generous portion of soft serve vanilla. Wife's rib dinner - 9.99, mine, 13.99, not terribly expensive. The star here are the ribs. Worth a try. I'd give 3 and a half stars if I knew how to do halfs.

I've enjoyed the food I've sampled from El Perdigon. The prices aren't cheap but the flavors are vibrant and it's great to have authentic Venezuelan cuisine  available in Tampa. If you like El Perdigon try Pepito's After Hours (their Arepon is one of the best meals in Tampa) and Nico's Arepa Grill (a food truck serving Venezuelan arepa based cuisine) .

A delightful bakery amongst the many fine Latin American restaurantson Armenia  (across the street from Wholly Smoke Barbecue). Friendly service and lots of great baked good to try. Haven't had their sandwiches yet, but they are also known for their "Cubans" to-go. Very reasonable prices and friendly family atmosphere.

Not even a star! I have no idea how people buy here, but my experience is not good! The ham was like green color, the fruit is too ripe... And nothing is organic. So never again! I guess you pay for what you get?

It's been a long time coming, but I finally managed to find a bbq that meets my tastebuds.  The staff was extremely friendly and we had a nice experience joking with the staff, feeling right at home.  They're ribs are fall-off-the-bone and have just the right amount of sauce.  Many places roast their ribs in a dry rub with sauce on the side, these were slapped right on 'em!  It's a mild vinegary/sweet sauce that you can also order extremely hot (spicy).  I have experienced so many tough & fatty ribs since moving to Florida, and I must say this was a breath of fresh air.

I went here because I read they served Chicharones; and thanks to my best friends mom in Ocala, I fell in love with them.  But to my disappointment, I walked in to find dried and shriveled up food that they wouldn't make fresh.  Maybe this place got good reviews for it's bakery part (which looked fresh and exciting) but I don't have much of a sweet tooth - and most of everything else I wanted/needed grocery wise I could've gotten next door or across the street at the National Supermarket.

Why 4 stars? Because, my how you've changed.

   ---   ---   ---

When I lived in Ybor, I would come to Doppel Decker to escape the craziness, and just enjoy a quiet pitcher and a game of darts. I often wondered how Doppel Decker stayed in business, considering there were almost never more than 10 people inside when I would stop by.

Welp, last weekend... it was like I was in a new universe.

It was incredibly lively, but just under the level of being too crowded to where it was hot and uncomfortable. Some might say... perfect.

The karaoke was awesome. In between karaoke's, the DJ was hilarious and played a great mix of songs from alt rock to hip hop. 

I believe the owners are german, so they have plenty of good Hefeweizen in the house. Another key to my heart.

If I still lived in Ybor, this would be my favorite bar. Another stop or two in here, and I might be upgrading to 5 soon. It's nearly a full-on love affair.

I like Pollo Tropical.  I like Pollo Tropical, a lot.  Here is what I like about Pollo Tropical.
1.  Convenience - They are all over the place and it is a quick place for a delicious and healthy lunch.
2.  Consistency - I have never had a meal that I didn't enjoy.
3.  Food (obviously) - I have a strange addiction to their Tropichop bowls.  Seriously... a big bowl of rice, meat, beans, and veggies.  So delicious!
4.  Hot Sauce Bar - Need I say more!?

If you are looking for yummy fast food with a Latin flare, Pollo Tropical is your spot!

I've been using Uber for about a year now, and I love it.  I switched to Uber  because I would phone for a regular taxi so that could be picked up at my home, and they would never show up.   This is not the case with Uber. They always show up, apart from one driver that I have had three times, who gets lost coming to my house every time.  I think he is stoned. that same Uber driver also tends to douse his car with petroleum-based perfume.  If he picks me up again, I plan to decline the ride. He is also a terrible driver.

Anyhow, today I had the BEST driver. Her name is Teri, she opened the door for me, has a great personality, is excellent behind the wheel and I just wish I could request her again, but it's not possible to request a specific driver.

In short, Uber is FAR BETTER than a regular taxi, and much more affordable than a limo.  It's car service at a professional level.  The cars are climate-controlled, comfortable and clean.

I've driven by jazzy BBQ at least hundreds of times, and have enjoyed the smell of bbq emitted by this establishment . Unfortunately I never stopped. What I have been missing all these years. This is a hidden gem. Based on a Mark H's review I went today. No wonder he is elite, he knows good food and  
You can take his review to the bank. 

I did a carry out of pulled pork with mild sauce, Cole slaw, baked beans and corn bread. I made a mistake and only did one order. My wife went bananas with this BBQ and I had to share. It was soo good I licked the plate. This is now my favorite place for BBQ. Don't miss out go

The Salon Lofts are essentially a bunch of individually operated hair salons, all under one roof.  Each stylist has their own "loft", buys their own products, charges their own prices, and books their own clients. 

My stylist, Uliana, is in Loft 9, and she is incredible.  She worked for a major salon for 6+ years, and then recognized she was ready for greater challenges. You will never find a more talented, friendly, down to earth, skilled, hair-passionate stylist - I know, because I have tried many in S. Tampa.  She actually listens to what you want, and makes recommendations to help fit your needs.  Color, especially, is her forte.  

I have been going to her every other month for almost 2 years now, and I walk away happy *every* visit. If you can't say the same about your hair, you need to go visit Uli.

Outside of Old Nelson in 30th Street dishing out a satisfactory cup of La Colombe, Passero's hooks up the best train station cup of coffee hands down. Of course when it rivals the likes of Dunkin, Fourbucks, and Saxby's as competitors that does only say so much at times. But it does help that Passero's provides a good variety, tempting baked goods, service with a smile, and a friendly hipster vibe.

The best deal is by far their flavor and coffee of the day. But they do have a good share of lattes and other drinks to go with the change of the seasons. Really the only flaw to (at least this) Passero's by my book is that its severely limited by being a kiosk in a train station as random and disjointed as Suburban is. Because with the exception of the atmosphere of its environment, and as far as being able to chill and get some work done coffeehouse style from within, Passero's delivers every key thing that I look for when getting my cup of Joe better than most other coffeehouses do.

I'm out of my element when I arrived at this modest yet swanky Terra Sur Cafe. It's definitely intinate because I see many small tables seated with couples and their glasses of wines.

I met up with my friend and his group of close colleagues. Our server was prompt and friendly. My experienced was helped when everyone including the waiter were all outgoing. I was pleasantly surprised that we started with a pitcher of red sangria mix that could be shared among everyone (1 small glass for 6 people). 

The items were a bit pricey, which sometimes throws me off. But the menu looked appetizing. Before our meals, we got to try bread with their dipping sauces, whcih were yummy. I could just that. Since I had a late lunch, I wasn't very hungry so I opted for the shrimp and mango ceviche. It was light and had a very pungent citrus flavor. I had a good time and was fascinated by the different array of choices. One person tried the beef heart. That was interesting. It was really tender and flavorful like a steak would be. I never thought I'd try anything like that, but I'm glad I did. 

I think this place is great to have a nice gathering and definitely a date. Although, I liked the atmosphere, I'm not sure when the next time around I'd go again. I think I'd rather stick to going with a group again. Next time I'll have to try their desserts.

We ordered takeout, and decided on the crab cheese puffs, an order of the General Tso's Beef, and an order of the Cantonese Fried Noodles with chicken, beef, and shrimp, and was told that it would be ready for pickup in 10 minutes.  10 minutes??  That's impressively FAST!  When we arrived in the restaurant to pickup our dinner, we were greeted by a friendly and smiling woman.  Our food was piping hot and smelled delicious while we drove home.  The crab cheese puffs came with 10 pieces in our order, and they were delicious.  I had been craving crab cheese all week, and this definitely satisfied my craving.  The General Tso's beef came with peas and carrots, as well as rice.  This dish was very flavorful and went well with the white rice.  The fried noodles were authentic, crispy and yummy and covered in sauce.  We inhaled our food and smiled with full stomachs.  I definitely plan on ordering from Mei Mei again since it has great food and great prices.

Ok...im going to start out by saying the food was fine. I had a you pick two with a french onion soup(delish) and ceasar salad. The thing is, having worked in customer service forever, I cannot support a place that treats its employees so poorly. I witnessed some pretty disgusting behavior from a manager to a floor person, which included explicit cursing for the whole dining room to hear. From what I could tell it was over coming in missing a peice of the uniform. While I can understand the policy, I dont think anyone should be treated like that and disturbing all your guests at the time.

I will happily go to this shop to get my weekly Sunday morning bagel! They're always fresh and the cream cheese is pretty solid. 

The atmosphere is a tad off putting. It seems like all the people at the tables are glued to their laptops // ipads. It's one of those places that I feel weird carrying on a conversation in, but that usually doesn't stop me from doing so. 

The staff has always been super friendly and there are usually smoothie samples!

+ outdoor seating for the summertime!

Absolutely love it here.

The service is impeccable, from reservations, to the hostess, to the servers and the bartender (Hi!) just terrific professional people.

This time I tried the antipasto bar, WOW, I could live on that alone. For dinner I tried the gnocchi in a sauce described as "with a touch of cream" well, this dish was in a cream sauce, really too much cream for my sensitive tummy. Althoug it was quite good, I could not eat it . My bad for not asking questions.

WEnt back the next night and had the antipasto bar again this time with the tagliatalle bolognese, my personal fav. Appetizer size did come out smaller then I imagained and the server even appologized for it, but it was plenty for me!

Had a wonderful bottle of Chateneauf de pape, thanks to a great recommendation .
Top notch dining, gorgeous views, excellent service,..that's what keeps me coming back over and over.

MASSIVE portions.  The lasagna, meatloaf, many of the dishes that include a 'side' of pasta, the flatbread pizzas, etc. all come out in huge amounts; enough that I easily turn it into two or possibly three meals.  Great for lunch too!

The tomato caprese salad is a great appetizer they offer that is often overlooked; it's not like a typical Italian restaurant where you might get three tomato slices, here you get perhaps 10 to 12 smaller slices each with a big hunk of cheese topping it.  Very good value.

For desert, they have the best Tiramisu I've ever had, anywhere.

Only complaint about the place is no liquor; just beer and wine.  Would go a lot more if they offered the hard stuff.

Perfect late nite vittles.  Always hot, usually fresh.  usually because sometimes I get "re-fried" fries.  But the burgers are always spot on.  Hot, greasy and unhealthy as hell.  Thank you Rody Davenport Jr.

I've been going to Troy Jimenez for years.  I can't understand the post from a previous person on here about him.

When I moved to Tampa, I had to keep going back to Atlanta to get my hair cut and colored because I couldn't find anyone here who did decent hair...until I found Troy.  I'd follow him anywhere!  From the first moment you sit in his chair, you're relaxed and not worried or stressed about what he's going to do.  He has always been great helping me decide when and how much to highlight and lowlight as well as picking different hairstyles and lengths.

I've also gotten my hair straightened by Troy after years of flying to NY to get the Japanese straightening done.  He's does the Keratin straightening and its great as well.  I've been much happier with that than the Japanese technique.

Overall, Troy is a great hairdresser.  He works with all types of hair (I've been through curly and straight) and I've seen many of his other clients coming and going. Everyone looks great!

I highly recommend him!!

I have used Uber several times. I like that I don't have to call a cab company and wait on hold forever to get a cab. Just pull up the Uber app and request a car and there here to pick me up in minutes. I like that I don't have to worry about carrying cash. Uber charges your card and even adds tip. I love the fact that I can hop in and hop out ( and do it in such luxury style, I may add). The receipt is emailed to you and the app gives you the chance to rate your driver and service. So you see Uber cares about the level of service. Not to mention when you request a car you can see the drivers rating. All the drivers I have had in the past have received high ratings. So happy that Uber is in Philly. Keep up the good work.

I guess with the word "pho" in the name, you would think their pho would be pretty good.  Unfortunately, I didn't think so... My beef pho had quite a vinegar sour type taste to it.  Not sure if that made it more "authentic" than others, which may be true, but whenever any one flavor overwhelms the others then it can't be very good.  The atmosphere was very calm with a diverse group of patrons sort of lounging around, laughing, and having a good time.  The service was good, and I would consider coming back but I'd probably skip the pho.

I had some time to kill so I popped in for a quick beer.  The inside was smaller than I expected but was nicely set up.  I bellied up to the bar where the service was fast and friendly.  Nice atmosphere.  I didn't have time to try any of the food but the menu was diverse and I did over hear the customer next to me say how delicious her sandwich was.  I look forward to going back and trying some food.

We stopped by OCF for some tea - my friend got the iced green variety and I got the hot honey green. I'm a big fan of the glass pots that steep the tea right before your eyes. I somehow feel more a part of things than if they gave me a tea bag in a paper cup a la Starbucks or Saxby's. 

The interior was super cute - we sat in a deep, comfy corner couch around a wide, low table and enjoyed our tea as well as some hummus and pita chips they were offering up. The hummus was fantastic, though the portion could have been a bit bigger. 

The staff were helpful and we didn't feel rushed at all, sitting and loitering with our tea on a Sunday afternoon. If I'm in the area, I'd come back to OCF. Perhaps the perfect stop after walking South Street for the day!

The Good:
In my experiences, you tend to get more food for your money here than the Chipotles in Center City.
There's a considerable amount of seating, and if that somehow fills up, you're right next to Penn's quad, which is good for Taco picnics.
Compared to Qdoba, Chipotle's ingredients are fantastic. It's not necessarily authentic Mexican food, but it's tasty.

The Bad:
LONG LINES. At peak times especially, expect to wait a bit. This isn't a reflection on the staff, who are actually pretty efficient, but more a consequence of serving a college neighborhood.
The other patrons are genuine college students. Sometimes, it can get a little bro-ey. For someone like me who lives in Spruce Hill but isn't a student, that's a huge turn off.

Love this place! We go at least every couple of weeks for the ever changing sugar free flavors. The service is always friendly.  I've tried several other froyo places in the area, and Tanya & Matt's has the best quality by far. The recently revamped the toppings bar, and the new dispensers are a little difficult to use but not a big deal. Love that they let you sample the flavors before buying.

They need to stock their shelves faster and more during overnight.. Doesn't look good to have empty shelves on a Monday morning..

Usually target is good about having their shelves stocked but I guess they are slacking now.

The Target Pharmacy is pretty nice and they have an auto refill option which is nice.

I am a FAN FAN FAN! I love that this is pretty much in the middle of southwest Philly. Right next to the Bartrams projects. This is the shit I love about philly. You're in the middle of nowhere, and you find something so special, and so beautiful. Something to take you away from all the drugs, gunshots, violence and dirtyness everyone says about " Filthadelphia". 

It's a gorgeous walk. It's so confusing at first! We parked and we were like.. where do we go?? We just walked towards the back towards the trees, and walked through the woods. It was so peaceful, so much green.. so much nature happiness. If you're having a bad day, go. If you're having a good day, GO. 

Take a look at my photos for a taste, but seriously, you have to go to really experience it!!
This is some good BBQ! The sauce is very tasty and the pulled pork is one if the best I've had in the Tampa area, ribs are good too. Gets really crowded at lunch but worth it.

When a friend invited me to Gino's for his birthday, I wasn't really sure what to expect as I live pretty close to this establishment and have never heard anything about it.  When I popped on Yelp, I became very excited based on the high star rating and all of the glowing reviews.  It lived up to the hype!

First of all, $2.50 craft beers on Wednesdays.  Now, I went for the Chianti because the idea of craft beers and Italian seemed a bit overwhelming, but they do have quite the selection.  The table started with some Mussels, Calamari, and Eggplant.  My favorite was the Eggplant and I really think I'll order the Eggplant Parm for my meal next time I come here.  It was so cheesy and delicious.

For our main course, we had the Margarita Pizza.  How good can a Margarita Pizza be, you may ask.  This was the best one I've ever had.  Again, so freaking cheesy.  Our bill including 3 craft beers, 2 glasses of wine, a pizza, and an order of mussels was $35.

The atmosphere here is super laid back.  The service was really good and our drinks were never empty.  This joint got pretty crowded by the time we left, so I would recommend getting there early if you are coming on a Wednesday.  I can't wait to come back.

Had heard about this place so decided to try it.  Waited about a half hour for the escargot app which was great.  Waited 20 more minutes for dinner to arrive Both entrées were cold and not spectacular at all.  Both were dishes I could have made at home.  After tip, bill ended up at $93 for those three items.  Will never go there again.

This bar is so fun.  Been there twice in the last month.  Beer is amazing, bartenders are very knowledgeable and not cocky at all.  Very authentic beer garden style outdoor seating in addition to places to sit inside.  The attached cafe has amazing food.
Pretty much only go here for happy hour, but it's a great place for that.  Great prices, amazing beer selection and tasty food that's priced right during that time.

I came here for an impromtu solo lunch on a Saturday late afternoon with a CL deal. It was such a delicious lunch, I will return. I have to say this was the best gumbo I have had in Florida ( it was served with a small side of fresh made warm cornbread drizzled in a little honey butter. My entree was a nice thick piece of salmon, cooked perfectly and topped with blue crab and a buerre blanc sauce. My only request for next time would be to not drown the side of fresh zucchini in butter. Just not needed. I ordered a piece of chocolate rasberry cake to go, and was not impressed, so next time I will just drink my dessert since the cocktails served to others around me looked great! And cheers for warm, professional service Nicole!!

Ok I fell in love with this chain while on a cross country trip. I still love it just as much now as I did then. I crave the hash brown casserole, chicken fried chicken and blackberry topped pancakes. This is a southern style restaurant which serves breakfast all day. Although I have been to this establishment many times, I have never had anything but breakfast. 

This trip I actually tried the fried catfish which was fried well but a little under seasoned. Overall this was just what the doctor ordered and I enjoyed my meal as I always do. I should've ordered seconds on the hash brown casserole cause one serving is never enough.

Amazing food!  Love the story of how this place started (small and independent).  Best "food truck" in Tampa.  Prices have gone up a bit since they opened years ago, but the food's so awesome they can get away with it.
Love this park! There are always lots of great events going on.  Yoga in the park every Sunday night is the best.

This place is becoming A JOKE!  Now, if you've had one Chipotle, you've had them all.  It's pretty standard.... However, this particular location is on a MAJOR college campus, University of PA, which means it gets EXTREMELY busy.  So, I went there one night and right after I walked in at about 9:45PM, about 40 people literally walked in behind me and then even more came.  BUT, how about they did NOT have enough food already ready to feed the crowd.  When it was my turn, the server attempted to give me the scraps of peppers that were left.  I asked if there was anymore to which she replied they would be ready in 2 minutes.  However, I watched the guy cooking put a huge batch of peppers on the stove right at that moment.  WHY IS HE JUST PUTTING PEPPERS ON THE STOVE??? Those should have been on the stove waaaaaay before I even got to the counter! So, I told them I would step off the line and wait.  Then, I went there again, TONIGHT at around 9PM.  Before I walked in, there was already an extremely long line.  Once again, the same thing... not enough peppers... and not enough of many things... and they were completely out of lettuce!  However, they didn't discount anyone for their lack of product.  And the brown rice was a bit undercooked.  Again, they are on a college campus.  This is Mexican FAST FOOD!  And it's actually pretty good and healthy!  Maybe I need to write a letter to management because the cooking staff seems to be understaffed or unorganized.  Clearly there is a problem with them being able to keep up with the high flood of customers that enter the establishment.  It can be quite frustrating, especially if I see you intentionally giving me the bottom of the bin so you can keep the line moving! Get the kitchen in order!

I hadn't planned on getting carryout but since I had to make a stop at Safeway, opted to get some lunch to go.  The restaurant is small, and old looking inside.  A fresh coat of paint and some wax on the floor could do wonders.  Also a machine in the entrance where you put your thumb to discover your "sex power" kind of turned me off.  But I was already there, so went ahead and ordered. 

They have lunch specials for about $7.00, they include soup, eggroll, entree, and steamed or fried rice.  I ordered the Sweet and Sour Chicken, and she offered to give an extra eggroll instead of the soup.  I prefer fried rice and there was no extra charge (some places do charge extra for fried rice).  Anyway, my wait was about 10 minutes which I didn't think was too bad.  

Usually Sweet and Sour chicken comes with green peppers, onions and some pineapple chunks.  There was no green peppers, onion or pineapple.  Not sure if they just forgot or if this is how it normally comes.  Anyway, there were about 8-10 medium size pieces of chicken.  The flavor was a little different than what I usually experience but in a good way.  I really enjoyed the sweet and sour sauce which did not overwhelm the chicken.  The fried rice was equally as flavorful.  

She did give me 2 eggrolls which were really springrolls (I like these better anyway) and they were good too.  I wish they had been crunchier, not sure if they got soft because they were sitting in the rice or if they always come that way.  Overall, I was happy with the entire meal and I will plan on going back there for lunch really soon!

I love the family that owns this restaurant. They are always so nice and friendly to everyone! The food is good and consistent. It's not outstanding but remember it is basically a take-out restaurant. Certain things are better than others. Their Singapore Noodles are the best I've had in the city. They keep late hours and are open most holidays.

We enjoy Talluto's, particularly the cheese ravioli. But our latest experience was just bad. First of all, the employee was obviously very annoyed when I didn't tell her every type of fresh cut pasta at once. Sorry, I didn't know that's what the protocol was. No need to be snippy...I was the only person in the shop so it's not like I was holding up the huge line of people that frequent on weekends. Then, there was something very wrong with the whole wheat pasta...it tasted like straight up vinegar. I emailed to inquire...it was completely inedible so we had to throw it (and the meal we used it in) out. The responding woman ensured me she would look into the issue and get back to me ASAP. I never heard back. I followed up, never received a response. Very unfortunate that the customer service was subpar.

We went there for the 100 Food Truck event & it was not good... Too many lines & a lot of vendors were closed trying to cook more food... needs better organization & more trucks to accommodate the large crowds, each lines had an AVG of 25+ people in the Florida sun, one place had a 30+ min wait for cheese steak sandwich! Ive been to philly numerous times & the lines move very fast...

They did a nice job with the interior upgrades.though it was nice before as we'll. I asked for a dish that was not on the menu and they came through for me, which was nice, no question or objections The service was friendly and quick. I have no complaints what so ever.

I love this place and often when I want to switch it up stop here for some breakfast goodies...They seem to have everything you would ever want for when it comes to Columbian goodies.. All there breads and pasteles are awesome.... You cant go wrong with making a stop here to get some snacks if your in the mood for something Columbian

Well tried the Southern  appetizer: 3 pulled pork sliders, onion rings & fried pickles.  I was quite supirsed by the pork sliders, they were moist, flavorful and quite good.  Onion rings were not seasoned, not even salt and pepper.  The breaded pickles were lightly breaded and the breading was good, but bland, lacking seasoning and the pickles were sliced too thin.  You would have no idea what you were eating if you didn't know ahead of time.
Service was unremarkable, but restaurant was very clean!

This place is one of my favorites for breakfast and lunch sandwiches. They are quick and tasty. Breakfast usual for me is pepper and egg, but when I'm feeling "bad" it's the pork roll and egg. I love how thin the meat is sliced. My lunch favorites are the pork with long hots, or chicken cheese steak with fried onions. They make a very good cheese steak also. Once in a while, again when putting health aside, I'll indulge in a hot sausage with raw onions and mustard or a dog with kraut. 

Johnny Hots is a great place for a quickie in the tail end of Fishtown.

This is MY coffee shop. I'm here pretty much every weekend morning. 

Large Earth Cup (dark roast) with room for milk. A little sugar, a dash of cinnamon a dash of nutmeg. Yummy warm morning delight. 

Also, go early to get a bagel. Everything, toasted with plain cream cheese. I think they get their bagels from Four Worlds Bakery on Woodland Ave, but SRSLY... Best everything bagel ever. Get it toasted almost to the point of it being burnt. 

Love the deck/patio when the weather is nice. It's seriously one of the most relaxing places to sit and sip your coffee. 

The owner and *vast* majority of the baristas are INCREDIBLY sweet and make you feel like a member of their community. 

Coffee is better than Green Line. Service is better than Green Line AND Milk and Honey. It's a cute little hippie (not hipster) spot.

Tanya and Matt what's happening?  Your establishment is going down hill. What happened to the mature, responsible HS aged girls working there?  They were fast, efficient and kept the place clean. Lately it's been dirty, chaotic and lacking the basics like HOT fudge.  I know it sounds silly, but people like their hot fudge actually hot, not cold and a big glob like I found it today. 

3 stars because its close and I like to support local businesses, but clean it up, hire back the old staff and offer hot fudge please.

I love this place.  They have a vegan steak sandwich and it tastes good  (it was not on the menu last time I was there - you have to ask for it). 

Only complaint is the long line but it is worth it.

Attended a private event at Gavin's this weekend. Amazing empanadas, great catering, perfect for a 20-30 person party. Now I just need an excuse to throw a party...
***
Now that I am finally logging in to release this review, I must say that I eat breakfast/lunch at Gavin's at least a few times per week now. I am hooked on Jezebel's homemade empanadas (my favorite is the spinach) and tarta's (quiche - my favorite is the tomato basil). Fantastic food, every time.

I think if I rated anything less than a 5 I might have a few fans boo me.

I enjoyed my time at the game, easy to park, traffic was great, plenty of restrooms and good food.

Citrus Park mall is the only mall that does not give me anxiety as soon as I walk in.  I personally hate shopping and avoid malls at all costs, but if I have to go to one- I will try to get to this one.

Even at it's busiest times, it's never very loud in the mall, and everything is always very clean.  I also appreciate that the variety of stores- there are some more upscale ones, but it's not as snobby as International.

Still kicking myself for not buying the almost see through paper thin tortillas, and it has been weeks.  The lunch special was only 5.99.  One taco (hard shell), birria, beans and rice.  The birria had great flavor with a little kick of ole!  The tortilla that came with it was in deed as good as everyone says.  You place your order at the front, yes they take credit, and they call out your number when it is ready.  No a/c.  Just a few tables, so get there early or carry out.  The building used to be a bar wayyy back in the day, so there is a lot of history in the building.

Hi David

I believe you are the person who's attention it was brought to as the gentleman I work with comes in there every Friday for breakfast sandwiches and advised me he told you about it. Now if he really did, who knows. He said, she said, doesn't matter at this point.  But thank you for your response. I will be stopping in when you have cream of chicken soup or chicken barley again which is why I am raising you to 4 stars. Your soup was very good! Someone brought me some for lunch. Also, 4 stars for taking the time to respond as most places do not do that. Kudos!

I figure I'll get an Italian hoagie for lunch.  Since, I'm from Philly, I know a great hoagie.  I should've kept walking by Carmen's.  First, I asked how long to receive my order, I was told under 5 mins.  LIE
The 2 guys on the grill do more talking and gawking over the women then preparing your food.  Hopefully, no spit went into those cheesesteaks.  The guy who made my hoagie was better at talking to his buddies.  Then the girl who calls out your card number moves as slow as a slug.  While eating my hoagie I looked at my receipt, that's when I realize that they practically charge for everything.  I disputed one charge and the cashier said,"If u get all 3, my boss said to charge $.25".  Mind you the charge was for vinegar, oil and mayo.  The sandwich taste like crap.  I told the lady who was waiting to order that she should keep on walking, she did.

I revisited Carrabbas again last weekend and any slight disappointment I had in my last review was no longer present at all. In fact the food was excellent. 
Their angel hair pasta with pesto sauce is second to none other.
I ordered their pizza for the first time and was blown away how excellent it was. It is cooked in a brick oven and reminded me of the pizzas I had in Rome. The best pizza in Tampa! I strongly recommend it.

Pitchers is great for a casual night out to watch a game on several of the high-def screens. No matter where you sit, you will have a view.  It is definitely a little run-down, but it kind of goes with atmosphere.

Service is not the best. On a couple occasions, the bartenders had no idea what they have on draft and they take forever to get you what you ordered. They seems a little spacey. 

The food, I have to say, is delicious. A little on the pricey side for a dive bar, but worth it. Crabby fries are awesome and the cheesesteaks are fresh and high-quality. 

I am a fan.

I guess I wasn't a fan of cheesesteaks because I hadn't eaten a good one until now. To me, they tasted like over-cooked teriyaki style meat, with liquid cheese. Don't get me wrong, I like liquid cheese but not in a cheesesteak. 

ATMOSPHERE:
Typical city setting; long and narrow shop with simple decor and a TV on the wall. 

FOOD:
Cheesesteak - Got mine topped with grilled onions & mushrooms, lettuce, and real cheese (provolone.) It was really good and really big, but I ate the whole thing, being the pig that I am. The meat was tender and flavorful, and the roll was soft & fresh. Ordered a side of crinkle cut fries with salt & pepper. 

SERVICE:
You order & pay at your window and the food is supposed to be delivered to you, but my sister-in-law went up to get the food. The guy taking the orders, not sure if he's the owner, was great. At first glance I didn't think he'd be that patient & friendly, but he was. Don't judge a book by its cover!

OVERALL:
I know the rivalry is between Gino's and Pat's, but my partner who is a Philly native likes this place way more. And that I'll stand by!

I think Lauren C's opening sentence sums up my review of Earth Cup aka THE BEST CUP EVER. 

First off, it is a local coffee shop nestled in West Philly and I do believe, is West Philly's best kept secret. The coffee is super cheap and the homemade baked goods are amazing (try the zucchini chocolate chip bread, om nom nom). They also sell beans by the pound, which is awesome. Support local!

They have a cute arrangement. The interior has some seating but the outdoor seating is the best. Great for people watching, book reading, phone chatting, etc. 

Also, they now have an Earth Cup Card where when you get 10 punches, you get your next coffee free! 

The only con, because I rarely use wifi anywhere, is the ATM/Credit card issue. They only take cash but thankfully they have an ATM in the shop.
This is a beautiful place to walk around or to just sit and enjoy nature.  I love it!

They recently got the wine dispensers from the closed sister Wine Design at Channelside and I'm so glad for the mix when I don't want to do beer. They don't sell hard liquor so beer and wine it is. They offer complimentary popcorn, very pet friendly and staff is very friendly also. Almost everyone knows everyone at Grand Central it's definitely a cool place to hang out. During Flicks and Food trucks it can get a bit crazy and loud but still fun to hang out.

Amazing, unique food, great drinks, awesome eclectic, slightly hipster atmosphere.  You order at the bar, then servers bring you your food.  I had a great, grassed burger, portobello and feta app and glass of wine.  All were awesome.  Not too crowded or loud.  eclectic crowd.  Will definitely be back.

A wonderful cozy place to express your creative side! You pay based on what piece you select, and there is a flat sitting fee to cover paint, supplies and firing in the kiln. This is wonderful, because some pottery places charge you by the hour, the one fee covers you all day if you so choose. 

It's a relaxing activity, and a warm friendly shop. It's perfect for unwinding or throwing a birthday party. Love, love, love this place. :)

Sadly, I think the great Phillies run is over. Thanks to RAJ, the rebuild will be long and painful.  Outside a few weeks this season (when they were totally unwatchable), I am almost always taking a peak at the game or checking the score.  I love the passion of fellow fans and the park is lightyears ahead of the Vet.  I never turn down free tickets and am still willing to buy some too!

Once they fire RAJ, I'll be happy to push'em back up to 5 stars!

Went here for the $1 sub special they had. Due to having two assembly lines and quick workers and maybe other industry secrets, like maybe having pre-made packets for their most popular items, service was BLAZING FAST.

I've eaten JJ's before and have always liked the freshness of the flavor of their sandwiches. I like their bread, though I wish there were more choices, and I like their meats. On this occasion, I had the smoked ham and it was really good with an actual smokey flavor that I could taste. The meats seem to be more fresh than Subway's, which seem to be somewhat "factory" fresh rather than deli fresh to me. JJ's meats seem less "chemical" than Subway's to me. Just my impression.

I like how I can eat this sub easier because they are narrower than Subway.

JJ's are more pricey than Subway, but I don't mind more pricey if I feel the quality matches. They also serve 8" subs rather than 6" subs like Subway. I recommend JJ's for a change of pace.

I had the lunch buffet.  Lots of great items.  I went with my daughter and her friend and even picky teenagers loved it.  It gets busy on Sundays so get there early or expect a wait.  The Mariachis were amazing.  I will be back to try the breakfast buffet.
They offer the best miso soup you can find with delicious and unique sushi rolls. I also like their wok fried rice. Very trendy atmosphere.

Food is Good! Customer Service is Bad/Awful.

My friend and I placed a dinner order of chinese food earlier today via foodler to be delivered at 4:30pm.  Lo and behold it was delivered at 12pm.  We called the restaurant and told them their mistake and they told us no problem, keep the food we will deliver the food at the correct time.     Well they came at 4:30pm, but wanted us to PAY again.   After we explained the mistake the delivery guy left with our food.  

When we called the restaurant, all they did was hollar at us that we were trying to steal their food.   Never again.....................

Sam's Club - excellent place for large quantities - stock your TP or for your party, a great wholesale club, one of the best I've been to and continue to renew my membership!

Never had an issue with any products from here...

And WHAT?!??!!  The HUGEST pumpkins around for only $10 each.  Just bought 2 (my husband wouldn't let me get a 3rd) and they are tremendous!  Best pumpkins in Tampa!

So - here's the process at Zio's:  1) Tell one of the pizza making guys your order and they will put it in the oven to heat. 2) Step to the side and repeat your order to the checkout person 2 feet away from the pizza making guy. 3) Pay for your order. 4) Hope they don't screw it up. 

Here's what's so great about this process. You, as a customer work twice as hard to ensure you address Zio's staff communication deficits. You, as the customer pay double what the product is actually worth. For example, a plain slice worth about $1.50 will cost you well over 3 bucks. I guess because they throw it on hot bricks, convincing you it's "better". 

The "Soup Nazi" demanded a process for a stellar product, making it worth the wait and worth enduring the process. Zio's ain't worth either.

I LOVE their tortillas and burritos. The green salsa is my favorite. I have to say, however, other than one man that works there, the staff act like they're doing you a favor serving you-so rude. You'd swear I'd done this woman wrong at some point in my past. SHEESH!  I find that very irritating, but often go their for my breakfast burritos and choose to ignore it.

I will flesh this out for you, but let me sum it up quickly:  This is a good steakhouse, but very overpriced.

The good -- the restaurant itself is very nicely appointed and interesting to look at.  I love the modern style and the use of rich, contrasting woods.  The open kitchen is very appealing, as is the "butcher shop" up front.  The acoustics are fine, and the place is not overly loud or depressingly quiet.  The atmosphere is very good.

The service was also good.  At times we waited a bit too long for things, but overall our waiter was very pleasant and attentive.  He was not a big upseller, which I don't care for.  Good marks for service.

The food was very good -- but not so good as to be particularly memorable.  My steak was pretty tasty, although slightly undercooked.  The sides were good, but not great.  The onion soup and the salads were nice.  The volcano desert was good, and the whole flambe at the table thing was interesting.

However, the prices are quite high in my opinion.  An 8 oz. filet mignon was $38 a la carte.  The sides are large enought to share, but not for more than 2-3 people at most, and ran 9 to 12 for most.  It all adds up, and our bill for two people (with no alcohol) was over $140 before gratuity.

All in all, it was a good meal, but I wouldn't go back without a good reason.  I could have a better meal at Berns or Ruth's Chris -- and the Berns meal would be about 2/3 the price.

This Rite Aid was conveniently located on my walk to/from work for the past 2 years, making it my one-stop shop for just about everything. You could find me in here at least once a week. The pharmacy department is friendly and helpful. As a wellness+ gold member, shopping here ends up being cheaper for me than going to Target or the supermarket.
Very good gelato. But they had no ice. If that wasn't the weirdest thing. And she said the Owner would not buy a new machine. Ok, I get it. Na, I don't.
Hands down my favorite place to go in Tampa. A bit pricey but worth every penny. The atmosphere is unexpected by looking from the outside. Always amazing service and the menu changes daily.

So I have been to allot of the steak houses in the Bay Area.  I am not impressed about the steak here. First, they give you a salad and soup with every entree. As it is difficult to screw a salad up.. The salad was good... But the French onion soup is just not that appealing.  Although it comes with the meal, you would expect it to not look like a small tin cup with huge slab of cheese.. Then to finally break through and find only a couple of spoonfuls of broth and one crouton. I would of expected more from a nationally know restaurant.  To be honest I can cook a steak on a grill and it would be similar to Berns. We take clients there to eat, because it is a event. They give you a tour of the kitchen, show you a huge cellar of wine and wine collection. Take you to the dessert room... Which I cannot even think of one negative thing to say about the Harry Waugh room.. That would be the only reason I would sit through a dinner there.. Because it's so hard to get a reservation for only dessert in that wonderful room. But other than that... Berns is just an overrated steak house that doesn't make me excited to visit.

I normally come here exclusively for their drinks and to sit on the outdoors patio. After a long day shopping. This would be the perfect place for a cocktail. Everyone of the specialty drinks made by hand are perfect!  I even tried to replicate the cucumber gimlet at home several times. So if you want some great tasting drinks go here.. Ask them to put some dry ice in your cocktail as well while your at it;)

Now here goes the feedback on the food.  Maybe it was a bad night. But out of the 8 people in my party  eating dinner... No one was impressed.. Especially when your spending 40$-50pp. Almost 1k dollars later, I left feeling as though I could of spent my money on something more valuable.  Maybe even wasting on getting hammered at the bar.  But the food was not worth the money.  I don't mind spending that much on a plate of food.  We do it every time we go to Pane Rustica.  But I will spend the money when I know I will be happy.. I know it was worth it.. Because I am unable to make it myself and no one else can do it as good.

Best happy hour in town 7 days 4-7pm. Pretty much everyday during those times... 2$ martini... Ummm ... Not just any martini.. Finladian vodka, made how you wanted. This place also is the spawn of Bernini's down the street.

Came here for dinner since I didn't feel like driving to salt rock grill. A mistake... The king crab legs came out not cut or opened.. When I asked the server if they could cut it. He said they don't. Needless to say I had a huge,spiny crab leg in my bare hands.. Trying to get the meat out.. Very disappointing being that I paid 30$ bucks only to have my hand pricked, shirt and hand smelling fishy.

We come here all the time. I love their steaks! They are flavored by their aging process then cooked with this cedar wood.. This is where I come when I want a mammoth piece of meat.  I always feel like Fred and Wilma Flintstone when they bring my 42oz steak.. Ask to sign up for their emails.. You sometimes get a free steak or invites for one during special events

Eh... That's all I have to say about the dishes I had there. Just because it is big doesn't make it better. Everything is big there. The toast, the meatloaf, sandwiches. The size of everything is for the hand of giant.  But all that size doesn't make up for the taste.  Although the food is good... I would have to say it isn't a place I would crave or suggest on my own.

I wish I could take that 1 star away.... I have been a club member since having my son a year ago.. I heard diapers, wipes and other household items where better priced. Let me start by saying that I feel like my life is waiting in line when I go to sams.  Wait to walk in the door due to traffic, waiting to cash out at only a few or the many check out lines and then waiting in line to leave the store. It takes me a total of 5-10 mins to pick up my items.  But I will be at sams for a minimum of 40 mins.  Let me also let you know to never forget your card... You will wait in line for customer service to get a temp card. Which will be another 10-15 mins.... Oh and if you don't have a photo ID you won't even be able to get a temp card. I came the other day with my husband. Since he was driving and had his wallet.  I don't think to bring mine. So when we got to sams to pick up some formula. I waited in line for the customer service rep( who I swear walks and moves in slow motion) which only took about 10 mins since I was 2nd in line. But I was advised I couldn't get a temp card bc I didn't have a phot ID. I advised my husband had his which shows our address on the account. But the rep said no.. Because f security purposes!!!  It's buying groceries! Not hacking into a account. My membership doesn't have a credit line. I have a basic membership..  I couldn't believe that I was advised they couldn't give a temp card
To buy groceries. I do appreciate a company who cares about my privacy. But this takes
It to a whole bother level. This is a grocery store. It's
Like going to CVS and using my husband rewards card..

My friend loves their Eggplant Parmigiana so she recommended we eat here on a recent visit.  I can't stop thinking about it....

One person ordered the Italian Sampler, which consisted of lasagna, chicken parm and linguini alfredo.  Another ordered the eggplant parm, one ordered the baked ziti with ground beef and I ordered the lobster ravioli.

Before the meals, the server brought our salads and fresh warm bread.  I had the house salad and it was really tasty.  The bread?!  I could have eaten about 5 baskets of this bread.  It was hot, fresh and had just the right amount of butter and garlic.

I order lobster ravioli from different places.  Gino's has the BEST by far.  You can actually see and taste the lobster in each ravioli, no skimping here!  The tomato cream sauce that topped them was everyone's favorite...they kept scooping it up with pieces of bread!

Everyone cleaned their plates.....enough said.

Our server was attentive, pleasant and on top of his game.  

If you are in this area...RUN here!

This is a chain that we go to with family.  The food is typically good, the service is a little under par, but the manager at this particular store is accommodating.

We went here with a party of 4.  They had a special running and there were ABSOLUTELY NO SUBSTITUTIONS!  NONE whatsoever.  I wasn't trying to change anything but the type of pasta with the special.  The waitress, who was only doing what she had been told to do, would not make the change, but she diligently sent the manager over, we discussed it, they really aren't supposed to make any changes whatsoever (per Corporate) but since I was only asking for a pasta swapped with another pasta, he accommodated.
That was much appreciated!

This is an ok place, a generally varied menu and haven't had a bad meal.  We do enjoy the soup - my favorite is the lentil with sausage!  Mmmmm (good on a cold day!)

I've biked and walked past this cafe a bunch of times and always wondered who frequented here. The other day after a romp in the dog park I casually wandered over to see what's up. Nothing much, is the answer.
I got an alfajor (i think): it's basically a short bread cookie with dulce de leche sandwiched in between, with coconut rolled around it. Sounds delicious and sweet right? It was okay, nothing to be super wowed about, but definitely fancier than your average baked good which was fun. Grabbed a tea and coffee and noticed how small this cafe really is. It's basically like a hallway, but because it wasn't crowded I feel like it's a good place to sit and read the newspaper and chill out. Just like it's location it's a bit fancier than other cafes I've been to. Surprised by the Spanish touches. Never would have guessed!

Went to Bahama Breeze for lunch and was somewhat leary about their food. I was pleasantly suprised! Yucca Fries, cooked to perfection.  Fried crispy,  not greasy and light and fluffy inside. 
The combo of lobster & shrimp quesadilla was excellent.  Cooked perfectly with a crispy crust. The salsa tasted fresh and had a robust flavor. The accompanying Cesar salad was fresh, not overdressed and nothing more than ordinary. 
My problem is why doesn't this place have a Happy Hour? Too much competition on the Causeway to think you don't need cocktail specials if you want to attract the locals?
What's a girl to do?  Love to dine with a water view, but prefer not to have a highway in between.

Incredibly friendly staff in this little "diner."  The kid had the JCT which is bacon, cheese and scrambled eggs enrobed by two delish pieces of French Toast. I had the healthy alternative which was egg whites a mix of spinach, peppers, onions, shrooms on multigrain bread. Of course I opted for cheese but skipped the turkey sausage. Second time here, will definitely be back!!!

For fifty five a person you get wine, which we quickly titled welch's and then 5 courses. The appetizers were bland and poorly fried (turn up the heat on the cooking oil).  The soup was so bland we began adding parmesan cheese. The entrees were better, slightly more flavorful, as was some of the pasta in the pasta trio. Although the marinara was plain. The overall experience is like stepping back into time, with the plates from Niagara Falls and the endless pink (walls, bathrooms) and the accordion player with his straw hat. One of those places you go to once that makes you thankful for,good company.

I recently enjoyed a medium 500 calorie Cashew Chicken. The medium sized portion is fairly generous. It comes with chicken, rice, and an assortment of vegetables (approx. 1/3 of each). The flavor profile is tasty and the food is filling. This could probably best be described as healthy food, but the food actually tastes pretty decent.  It is amazing that they can pack all that food into a 500 calorie box!

Just trird the curled potatoes for Happy Hour. They had a decent taste,  were crispy and not really thst greasy, overall not bad. But, what is this cheese-salsa stuff. It is disquesting.  Get the potatoes just don't dip!
What's a girl to do? Keep trying new items.

It's a fine venue, as far as bars with venues attached go. I hear it's actually a great place to record your band play live too.

But the bar is unusual. Not enough bartenders to serve the masses--just one. 

And the prices are a bartenders' nightmare. $3.25 for lager and Kenzinger; $4.25 for better PBC and Yards Brawler, and $5.25 for the special (titled Weyerbacher IPA--no such beer exists, but I think they mean Last Chance). Those are reasonable prices as far as cost is concerned, but they are totally unreasonable in a cash-only bar. I suppose it should be cash-and-change only. If I pay for my $4.25 beer with a $5, which I did, I'm left in the awkward situation of asking for my $.75 back and giving the bartender a dollar, or just deciding to stiff him a quarter (one time I tipped $1.75, which is great, but not sustainable). 

Ultimately it's just a quarter! Not a big deal right? Exactly. So just be remotely considerate of your employees and customers and just make it a straight cash deal. I'd even pay $5 for a Brawler to circumvent the painfully unnecessary situation.

This place works if your in the mood for some crunchy spice in your life and want to avoid a possible blow out from eating Taco Bell. I do like the seasoning they put on their tortillas.  Kinda bbq, kinda salty=  satisfies multiple cravings at once. I almost gave it the place 4 stars.. But since the place is in a good location, good atmosphere and has a cool mural on the wall. Which makes me feel bad ass!  I am not going to give it just a 4..... I'm having a glass of wine right now and I'll give the 5 I would of gave, if I was drunk there now.
I hate to say this may be the best place in town... But.... It is! So make sure you make a reservation for dinner. There isn't much seating!

I have been to Pour House twice.  The first time was for Flicks and Food Trucks.  The second time was on a random Friday night for pre-dinner beers with the fiance'.

Flicks and Food Trucks:  This place is CLUTCH.  The fact that I can go in and promptly be served a beer or a glass of wine to walk around with is great.  For as crowded as it was the time I was here for Flicks and Food Trucks, the service was still good.

Random Friday night:  Really chill vibe.  We sat outside and enjoyed the nice weather.  It was refreshing to see so many regulars and dogs walking around.  Definitely a good spot to relax.

It looks as though you can order food from the restaurant across the court yard, Cena, and enjoy it at Pour House.  Definitely a spot I'll be back to.
Good BBQ. Been going religiously on Fridays for the solid Mac and cheese. Just go to jazzys, friendly people and awesome food!

The wife and I decided to meet our daughter here for Breakfast. I was surprised there was no wait so we were seated very quickly. From there it went to hell. We were there a good 10-15 minutes before a server stopped and asked if anyone has waited on us. We told her no and she said she would take our drink order and find our server. We gave her our order and she left. She came back and said she spoke to our server and she didn't know it was her table. So the server that acknowledged us took our order. Well by this time I was not a happy camper. We ordered our food but I also asked for apple butter with my biscuit. Well the food arrived in a timely manner but before I can tell the person who delivered our food that the apple butter was missing she was gone. Well it took a while before our server show back up to let her know it was missing I was almost done with breakfast. She apologized saying she was swamped with other tables that had just arrived that she couldn't get to us. WOW I can not believe she said that. 
Well I received the check and headed to the cashier. She asked me if everything was ok I explained the whole situation and she just said it happens. Oh again I could not believe my ears. So I am definitely not a fan of this place. 
Oh as for the food, it was good but nothing gave me that wow factor that I usually look for.

Was stood up for a date (after biking my head off to get downtown!) and found refuge here drinking my feelings. The gin & tonics were strong and the folks friendly. Will most certainly be back and under much neater circumstances!

My friend and I stopped in after an event at USF. It was late and pretty much anywhere serving food was closed, except for 3 coins and taco bus. So we opted to try something new. I was torn between a couple things on the menu, and the waitress suggested the French toast from my list. I went with it and while it was good, there was nothing that blew my mind. I also found myself wishing for real maple syrup as I always seem to do anywhere that only gives me "pancake syrup" as an option. Our waitress was fantastic, but the place is a total dive. I was reassured by the 3-4 cops that came in for their dinner break that night though.

Flowers were one of the last things on my list. They're expensive, they're troublesome, and they die. Point blank. I didn't start looking for a florist until about 4 months before my wedding, and honestly I really shouldn't have put it off because I was starting to panic (just a little bit)... Then I spoke with some florists who were out of my budget, some who were off-putting and didn't seem like they really cared about my vision, and some who didn't even respond to my calls and emails.

Cue: Vito, Carl, Kathleen and the team at Carl Alan. 

When I first met Vito, he was completely open to all of my ideas and he didn't judge me for being a flower-n00b. He didn't laugh or scoff at my tiny budget. I described what I wanted the arrangements to look like, along with some DIY ideas I was planning to do, and told him the color scheme and the feel of the wedding. Within that first meeting, he pretty much designed all of the bouquets, centerpieces, corsages, and boutonnieres. Vito showed me every single flower we talked about and made me feel completely comfortable that I was putting my wedding flowers all in the hands of a professional. At the end of the meeting, he sent me home with a mini bouquet of all the flowers we discussed and I knew that I had found my florist. 

A few weeks before the wedding, I came in for the centerpiece trial. Everything was just as I had envisioned. 

The day of the wedding, the flowers arrived on time and the Carl Alan staff did a great job arranging my centerpieces. I had provided all of the table decor as well as the vessel for the flowers (mason jars), and everything came together beautifully. My bridesmaids couldn't believe how big my bouquet was, and they all loved theirs. The flowers lasted a few days after the wedding and then I decided to dry my bouquet and the petals from the bridesmaids bouquets as a keepsake.

Throughout the entire process, the staff was courteous and professional. Kathleen is very responsive via email and phone, and I never felt like I was in limbo waiting to hear back from them.

THANK YOU, THANK YOU, THANK YOU to Vito, Carl, Kathleen, and everyone else at Carl Alan that helped to make my wedding day special!

What can I say about the original sliders.  They are so bad for you, but once in awhile they just call out to you. They have been around all my life & there is m ust nothing else like these soft bun tiny burgers. Ya gotta love them!

I am NOT the type of person that pays $13.00 or more per pound because something is labled organic or free range. I do read ingredients and the fewer the better. That being said, Fresh Mrket usually has ground chuck & chicken breasts for $2.99 a pound only on Tuesdays.
I am also not the type of consumer that will pay $5.00 for a dozen eggs because that's the going rate, I will wait a week or 2 and somebody will have them on sale.
I digress, Fresh Market, very clean, fairly expensive, has many shoppers and as much food that is full of bad fat and sugar as any other grocery store.
Tired, wanting to be at home with  my shoes off, I made that last stop and got a few packages of supposedly ground fresh several times a day ground chuck, that did not even have fat percentage labled. It was in butcher case so got exactally the sizes I wanted, butcher ( freezer) wrapped & tredged home.
Yes I checked out produce and other items ( bulk black beans $6.99 & about $1.29 packaged in regular grocery store. I took my ground chuck and limped home.
Last night I cooked the first package.  OMG! NO fat & I mean none, had to add some broth so it wouldn't stick to pan. Just browned it up nice & simple with onions, garlic,  fresh mushrooms & usual spices..oh and dash of Worcestershire.  WOW so delicious.  No fat, GREAT flavor & you could tell it had that fresh flavor.
Do yourself a favor, ALL grocery stores have loss leaders every week. When you see Fresh Market pay close attention to meat sales. They had prime rib roasts yesterday for $3.99 a pound,  can't wait to cook that bad boy, I guarantee it will be delish!
Fresh Market & meat sales... I think I found true love. You must check this out, you won't be dissatisfied or disappointed,  but your tummy & wallet will thank you.
Oh, and just on a closing note, love their checkout!  Wheel buggy up, end opens up and cashier removes items, scans & packages them. Several stores tried this a few years ago & it didn't last. WHY????  I am sorry I did not have a full cart!
Try them, check sales & be happy, it's a decision you won't regret. 
What's a girl to do?  Always look for items on sale that you need, so you can save to splurge on what you want!

.

Really overlooked this gem. Needed an air matress,  saw they had them on sale and set out to call them. 2 hours latter learned, no phones,  as I imited staff & don't want to waste their time, owned by a German Company & the same company that owns Trader Vics. Not many name brands, but some quality food items. Had some gourmet cheeses at great prices, low calorie line called fit& lively ( check labels not always so fit), canned pet food with few ingredients,  but good ingredients at super low price.
Frozen foods offered some great options from appetizers to desserts. Low fat yougat, flavored sugar and fat free coffee creamers, cottage cheese,  ricotta. Baffled at prices for quality items & only because they don't have a national brand name on them. 
Great prices on good selection of fruits and veggies as well as bread products. 
I checked out the meat & can't say their prices were anything special, but found a few items at a good price.
REMEMBER take your own bags or pay a few cents for them & no checks. Cash, credit or debit.
Unlike big box stores you only buy the amount you want. I got a jar of German sauerkraut and boy was it delish! 
First time shoppers go when you have enough time to investigate the store, items of interest & ingredients list, you & your wallet will thank you latter!
And don't make the mistake I did & be put off by having to put a quarter in to get a grocery cart. Think about the expense of a cart & how many times you see them all over town, where they shouldn't be. Ultimately we pay through grocery costs for the carts, so in the big scheme of things, put in the quarter, you get it back when you return the cart. 
One last observation & I am not profiling here, merely observing...many fairly high end customers have already discovered this hidden gem. Happy shopping!
What's a girl to do?  Love the great special cheeses, fresh veggies & produce, suprise utilitarian items & private brands & prices.
Bad service, burnt food 2x) and cold fries. Its a pub that shouldn't be serving food.

Beautiful restaurant with rich ambiance.  The martini was small and disappointing. The bread was merely fair, I expected a better quality. The olive oil with balsamic to dip bread in was tart, different, but I can't say that I appreciated the flavor.
The eggplant parmesan was a big serving size. The sauce was thick, rich and had the flavor of a sauce that simmered all day. The eggplant was somewhat firm, but not crispy. Overall the dish was delicious.  The noodles were not overcooked,  which often happens.  The amount of sauce was the appropriate amount for the dish.
Service was somewhat pompous and server tried to steer us to more expensive dishes and add ons. My reason for 4 stars instead of 3, is their purchase a pasta entee to eat there and get a free one to take home. I was quite amazed that a restaurant such as Maggios would have such a great promotion like this.
For their promotion for the working family, I will give them the 4**** stars.
The women's restroom was dirty and quite disappointing.  For such a beautiful restaurant,  you would think it would carry over to the restrooms, but they were merely ordinary.
Tampa has too many restaurant choices for ordinary to cut it, but I must admit for a Sunday night around 6PM, the place was packed.
I would return to try other menu options or the eggplant parmesan again, but it definitely is nowhere near the top of my list of places to go next time I dine out.
I loove loovvvee this place. Food is delicious and I love the staff, they are always so friendly!!

I like this place.  The ambiance is good, the food is good and it's better than eating deep fried egg rolls. You  have these great tasting fresh spring rolls to dip into wonderfully paired sauce.  They also have boba tea, loose leaf tea and smoothies.

Ate here for the first time tonight and we'll definitely be back. The mussels starter were delicious. The fried oysters and scallops were amazing. And the flounder tasted light, delicate and delicious. A great addition to the neighborhood.

Forget Home Depot and other huge retail hardware stores
 Ace has all of your necessities all organized compactly in thrir store. It is my experience that staff has longevity and can tell you the chanes in products from year to yesr. Staff is friendly, not pushy, well informed and very knowledgeable. 
Rather than go and get lost for hours looking for a hex screw, pop in and out of Ace in 10 minutes, with purchases in hand.
Please note they have some seriously great sales. So unless you are remodeling, do yourself a favor and save time, energy and emotional distress and just drop by Ace.

As a cinema, no problems.  Pictute quality great, seats ok, sound loud and overall a pleasant experience.  My problem is with staff, concession and women's bathroom. 
Staff is young and just plain rude. I attempted to inquire about a promotion for frequent movie attendees and they had no knowledge,  even though it was posted on the glass in front of them. Take the money, give the ticket and get out of my face (  they were not busy and there were other ticket takers).
A young man in a wheelchair took your ticket stubs. No smile, no greeting, just a shoulder gesture towards general vicinity of theater.
A coke starts at $4.75 for a small. Now having owned a restaurant the markup is unbelievable!  Including cup, top, straw & the 1 m inute it took minimum wage unfriendly concession worker to get it and take my money, the actual cost of that coke is no more than $.25. I understand profits and marking
Items up, but really, this is insanity!! No wonder people smuggle a can of coke or water into a movie theater along with a snack. My insanity stopped that night when I refused to pay those prices. 
And last, but certainly not least, the women's bathroom. There was a line 20 deep and finally make it inside to find no toilet paper or hand soap.  How disgusting.  Come on take a little bit of your concession profits and expand the women's restroom and keep it clean and properly supplied! And while you are at it add classes on etiquette and manners as mandetory for your business.
With the new Imax & Muvico theaters that cost the same to get into, are much more comfortable and offer a better, healthier & inexpensive food choices, why put my with mediocre?

What can I say, it is an institution.  Great burgers, seafood, salad & of course pasta anf itian dishes. Some may think it looks like a dive, but they would be sadly mistaken.
I always have trouble deciding what to eat, as it is all homemade and so yummy.
If food selection wasn't tough enough add the extensive wine and beer offerings. No matter what you select you will be happy, satisfied and full, with a people bag to go. Go for lunch or dinner. Enjoy!

Not impressed. Spicy orange beef had undetectable amounts of cow (although the citrus tasted fresh and real) Mongolian beef had similiar lack of protein, and excess onions. Chicken skewer appetizer was overly salty and dry. Egg rolls arrived hot & crunchy, but did not redeem order.

Gone downhill..

Today, we went to this place after a long time. The place was dirty.  Spoons were not clean. The yogurt was runny, and when I asked the person behind the counter did not seem to care.  She had bunch of teenagers hanging out by the counters and she seemed busy entertaining them rather than helping the customers.
Needless to say, I will not be going back, but it is disappointing to see how fast this place went from really good to just bad.  I would rather go to any of the other yogurt places now.

Stopped in here early one Saturday just as the door were opening for some lunch.  The the typical "lunch" spot but had $3 mugs and over thirty craft beers on tap.  Was fortunate enough to have the Founders Breakfast stout on tap.  For lunch ended up getting the buffalo chicken nachos and buffalo chicken egg rolls.  With expectation set low, arrived the best plate of buffalo nachos I've ever had.  Now what separates these from other pedestrian places is that the chips are deep fried tortillas.  There came with a wiz sauce.  This app was enough for me and another person to split so much so we didn't even get to the egg rolls.  The service was good considering it was one guy for the front of the house and the food came out relatively quick.  This place exceeded my expectations.

The name of this place is pretty generic and uncreative, but I guess it's par for the course in the neighborhood, with "Indian Restaurant" down the street?

It wasn't packed on a recent Sunday night, in fact, there was only one other table there for the first 5 minutes after we were seated and then they left.  After that, it was just us and the staff.  The service was really good, considering the chef came out from the kitchen to offer up various (off menu) vegetarian entrees that he could make.  I ordered the eggplant fried ravioli appetizer.  This was good, although I guess it's a personal preference, I usually prefer ravioli pan friend/sauteed, rather than deep fried.  I found it a bit dry.  Luckily there was some olive tapenade to help it out.

The entree I got was corn goat cheese risotto with asparagus and grilled beets.  This was delicious.  The risotto was filling but light at the same time.  The grilled beets were perfection, covered in a balsamic glaze.  I would totally order this again.

Since we were the only people in there and the chef kept coming out, it made it seem like we hired our own personal chef, which was kind of cool, but clearly not something that will happen for everyone who visits.  The atmosphere was ok, could use a bit more....coziness or something.

One of the best breakfast/lunch places in Manayunk. The portions are huge. They're all classics such as hoagies, burgers, sandwiches - and they're all delicious. 

Soup of the day is usually on point, but the daily crab corn chowder is a winner. Sweet from the corn, salty broth, and flavorful crab all blend together for a delicious warming experience. Hands down, this place is a solid hangover cure on the weekends with their stuffed breakfast sandwiches on long rolls.  

My friend's favorite is their JC Special that has eggs stuffed between slices of French toast with syrup over it, with bacon on the side. It's a great very-sweet and very-salty balance if that's your thing. I'm not a big sweets person so this is not my favorite. 

Their specials change all the time and you'll never know what you get. Some days it's burgers or meatballs or cutlet sandwiches, but they're always good. 

The space is small but the staff is always welcoming and friendly. It's a "our home is your home" type of feel. Usually people seat themselves and are welcome to grab their own menus and drinks from the fridge. In the summertime, the deck is great.

Casual observation: the food taste better on the days that Mel is actually there. Coincidence? I think not.

They have redone the bar area looks really classy.. Breadsticks were fab as usual, I ordered the Gorgonzola steak Alfredo with the wheat pasta.. Very delish, this is one if the only Italian places that the wheat pasta I taste yummy.  Our server was Brad. He was great! Very attentive and a manager came around twice.  Great service, great food will return.
