Lived in Manayunk for the past year so we would always go out on Main Street on the weekends. Only ventured into Pitchers a couple times on a Fri/Sat and it definitely isn't a top choice considering the surrounding bars have much more to offer in space and prices. 

I give Pitchers 4 stars because they have excellent Sunday specials (we would go for Eagles games). $4 10 piece wings that are very solid and $3 domestic drafts/Twisted Teas.

All in all I think Pitchers is a good bar but it depends on which day of the week you visit. Good food but some real douchers in this place.

Also: smoking hot bartender on Sundays, WOW

Mad River... where do I start? This bar/club is located at the end of Main Street in Manayunk. Always certain to have a cover charge of $5 or $10 on the weekend which does not make sense because they do very little to control the crowd inside. Expect to wait a good 5 min for a drink because of the over-crowding, so I recommend a solid pre-game at another cheaper bar down the street or at someone's house. Skip beerpong, go straight to boat racing to get you primed for Mad River.

My one positive experience from this place is that I always make out with a girl everytime I visit. Just a sloppy drunken makeout. You win Mad River.

I come to Denny's often and the food is always good as well as the service. I come late night right after the bars close. I have a Town Car Service so its fits in perfectly. My favorite server is Matia. She's the best. Matia always greets me with a smile and knows exactly want I want all the time. She is a TEAM player and a Five Star server. I also like to recognize two other individuals and they are Joe McFadden (manager) and David Buchanan (chef). Both of them are a great team together. When I walk in I enjoy having David prepare my meal for me. He has that special touch. Joe is also a good chef and a great manager. So when you come into Denny's ask for Matia and make sure that David or Joe is there to cook for you. You will not be disappointed. Tell him that DAN THE CAB MAN sent you.

I am all for my servers wearing pants and my hookahs having flavor without much effort.

Neither happened at Infusion last night. I didn't understand the servers' uniforms of provocative leotards and Hot Topicy stockings. I mean, it's not a strip club, right? Actually, would strippers even wear leotards?

I also didn't understand why they didn't have a drink menu. Our server was a "walking menu," and provided me with a Tom Collins-esque watered down cocktail when I asked to be surprised with a gin drink.

We ordered the Pirate Cove hookah ($25), which was relaxing and delicious when it could be tasted. There were issues with the bowl a couple times, and it didn't last as long as the hookahs at B-Side and Byblos.

When it came time to pay, I asked for a separate check from the couple I was with -- and the server put $13 of the hookah on mine, plus she gave herself a very generous mandatory $7 tip on my tab, which made no sense to me considering that my bill didn't warrant more than a $5 tip. Why would a 25% tip be mandatory, girl? I paid, but I wasn't happy about it.

Aside from the cost, the strange server costumes and the somewhat lacking products, the place was pretty neat. Lots of potential, hence the 3-star rating. It's dark and flashy -- located above Nick's Roast Beef in Old City -- and has comfy couches for groups, on which we sat. The bar area looked pretty nice, too.

My 4 star review has a lot to do with managed expectations.  I know what I'm getting when I go to Olive Garden- delicious breadsticks and unlimited salad.  Not 5 star Italian cuisine.  

It's sort of an embarrassment to my very authentic Italian family that I like Olive Garden, but I don't care.  Great for a quick lunch or a casual dinner.  I also appreciate that they now have gluten free pasta.  It's only offered in one or two versions (which i assume means its prepared ahead of time elsewhere and frozen and shipped), but when I need a place to eat pasta, it's a good option.

I should also note that when my friend and I were there for lunch the other day, he noticed our nametags and acknowledged that we were on our lunch break, and made sure the food came out quickly.  He also brought stickers for my friend's baby to help entertain her.  

I sometimes forget that this is so close to work from me, but I'll try to remember more! Sometimes you just need a bread stick to make your day better.

I love hookah. It's a relaxing experience. Infusion was only halfway relaxing. That's the only way I can describe it.

We got here around 10:00 on a Friday night and settled in one of the big, comfy couches. The scantily-clad waitress(?) came by to take our drink and hookah orders. We got a hookah menu but.. no drink menu. The waitress recommended a passionfruit martini, so I went for it. It was good, but ended up costing me $14. It wasn't THAT good. 

We got the pirates cove hookah, and while it tasted pretty good, we kept having trouble with our hookah. The coal kept sliding off of the top, I guess from the vibration of the bubbles. Then our first hookah just.. stopped working. No idea what happened to it, it just stopped working. They brought us another one and switched out the top, but the tobacco was pretty much done anyway. This is after about 45 minutes. Sad.

This place has potential if they would just stop being so odd. Did I mention our waitress was scantily clad? I'm talking bustier, fishnets, the works. Normally I wouldn't care if it went with the vibe of the place, but for here, I just didn't understand it.

This is a very pleasant Bone Fish, I have visited several times with friends and always received great service especially at the Bar, Russ was especially great. 

The bacon wrapped scallop was so delicious and had the yummy winter white Cosmo cocktail drink. I'm truly considering adding bone figs to my eat the entire menu list.

I have friends who swear Iavarone's is the best steak house in Tampa. In a city known for steak houses, I'm not sure Iavarone's is number 1, but it's darned close. With places like The Palm, Capital Grille, Ruth's Chris, Charley's, Malio's, etc. steak lovers have lots of choices in the area. However, if you don't mind driving a little bit north of downtown and searching off the main roads you will find one of Tampa's best places for steak and Italian food.

Unlike most steak houses in Tampa Bay, the steaks at Iavarone's are not a la carte! You actually get soup or salad with your entrée, vegetables or potato/pasta too! The steaks are mouth watering. Fine cuts of beef cooked and seasoned to perfection. 

I also love their house salads made with fresh parmesan cheese, olives and an awesome Italian dressing. I have had their pasta dishes too, Fantastic! 

The bartenders are all heavy pourers. You get a good drink for your dollar. AND they have a great happy hour. By the way, I suggest ordering at least one vodka martini with blue cheese stuffed olives. You probably won't be able to drink more than one!

If there is a downside to the restaurant. . . it's they don't accept reservations and are not open for lunch. You will not go wrong at Iavarone's.

What a wonderful place. Tucked away in an unassuming strip mall, Bazil's offers wonderful Italian fare, great service, and a surprisingly romantic ambience. There are no windows, meaning it's always a cool, dark retreat from the desert sun and heat. The booths and lighting are perfectly done, making for a really homey place.

When I lived in Tucson I used to order their exceptional pizza for delivery when I lived in apartments nearby. I also took my now-wife there for dinner on our first date, as well as the night I proposed to her. We were just back in Tucson this fall and had to return, and Bazil's again impressed.

If you want a cozy, romantic place for Italian, check out Bazil's, it's really a hidden treasure.
CB is consistently good food.  It is far from the best I have ever had but is also not the worse.  When you can not decide what you want it's a perfect filler.
Best baked pork buns reminds me of new York city...

I love everything about Laxmis. Having been to quite a few Indian restaurants around the Philadelphia area, Laxmis managed to beat them all. Our waitress was awesome, as was the food. Absolutely delicious. It's cute and charming inside with a very relaxed, comfortable vibe. I find myself coming here more often than my bank account likes.

Went to have dinner around 7.  Awkwardly stood at the hostess stand for a while while she took a to go order over the phone.  Seemed to take forever.  She did then tell us about the awesome happy hour if you sit in the bar area.  Was cool of her to tell us because we otherwise would have sat in the dining area and paid twice as much.  Food and bartenders were great!  I'd only go back for the happy hour prices though, as full price would have been too much for the price of the food.

This is the best theater in Tucson. It's our go to location for watching movies...even tho we live way across town. The facilities are newer than most, not to mention they keep it pretty well cleaned! Most of the staff I have encountered are very friendly. They serve White Castle Burgers here! Their loyalty cups are totally worth it!  Plus they are super cute (most cups have cartoon versions of all the popular movies from that year). Also....if you arrive too early and need to kill time, there are plenty of stores and a few restaurants within walking distance!

They have peanuts!  Just like Logan's.  

So I saw a post on Yelp Talk about this place. I had been here several times years ago, but for some reason we never came back.  Don't remember why.  I don't think it was anything bad.  It just didn't impress me enough to add it to my standard rotation. 

So, we came back.  The place looks nice. I mean, not in a Charley's kind of nice (meaning fancy) but clean and well organized.  Think a notch less fancy than Red Lobster with lighter colors.  They brought us free peanuts, but you can't throw the shells on the floor.  

We ordered the Jack Daniels Buffalo Shrimp appetizer but subbed chicken for the shrimp.  If you are expecting the dark, sweet Jack Daniels sauce from TGI Fridays, you will be disappointed.  It's more buffalo sauce than Jack Daniels, and a bit spicy.  I liked it, but remain puzzled by the Jack Daniels reference in the menu.  I took a picture and posted it.  The portion size was very generous for an appetizer. 

My wife ordered pasta primavera and I ordered the grilled seafood combo platter, which consisted of grilled tilapia and grilled shrimp.  I posted pics. Both plates were outstanding!  Very large portions, very good taste.  The grilled shrimp seem to have a very light glaze that is not overbearing, and the grilled taste comes through.  All in all, both the Mrs. and I were very happy with our dinners. 

Service was competent and the prices were good.  Key ingredients to a pleasant dining experience.  I'm a seafood fan, so I will definitely be coming back to try some of the other menu choices.  So for know I will declare myself a fan and shall return.

I've been trying to track down this truck for about a year now, but to no avail.  Well, last Saturday a bunch of us were at Yard of Ale watching the games when we saw the food trucks pouring into the MacDinton's parking lot.  There were 10 total, but I couldn't read the names of a few from across the street, so I ventured over with La Chica in hand.  We were really eyeing the Daily Grinders truck, but quickly changed our mind when we saw Nico's.

We ordered two different arepas for variety, and what I will say is that they're good.  Really good, in fact.  Had I not just recently eaten at Pepito's After Dark on Waters, then I'd give Nico's a 4 star, easily.  But as good as his arepas are, they just fall a little short of Pepito's.  Plus, Pepito's is stationary.  

Arepas are small, but Nico's stacks a LOT of meat on theirs (see La Catira pic), and they have a good variety of sauces to select.  I think that's their saving grace.  Fresh ingredients, and lots of them.

Would I return?  If I saw the truck, most definitely.  But I wouldn't purposely hit the road to find them.

Oh, Mad River.  It is what it is.  The floors are sticky.  The cover is outrageous for a place that does nothing for you once you're in there.  The drinks are too expensive for what they are.  And the people there... well, I can sum it up with one anecdote.  A complete stranger stumbled into stand among my group of friends.  He was lingering there for awhile, so I figured it was time to say something to this guy standing uncomfortably close to me.  He had a t-shirt with a slightly obscure reference on it, so, rather than rudely telling him to go away, I figured I'd ask him about the shirt.  He said "I have no idea what this shirt is.  I just know it gets me mad chicks.  Amarite?!?!  Amarite?!?!"

So, that's what you're dealing with there.  I wouldn't say that the place is awful, but unless that's your scene, or unless you want to dance with people you know and the sticky trash covered floors won't bother you, you might want to go somewhere else.

My wife and I had the good fortune of being chosen from the hundreds to attend the Yelp Elite Christmas Town Event on Friday December, 6.  We just want to say how grateful we are to Yelp, Brett, and Busch Gardens for this event! The service, staff and park were top notch for the event!  Thank a million Everyone!

I call this place douchey sushi, mostly for its outrageous decor and egotistic crowd. First the good; service is prompt and warm, meals come out in a reasonable amount of time. Plus this is now probably the cheapest Japanese joint in the area (8 bucks for a chicken hibachi platter ain't bad). Their sushi is decent, but others in the area have superior versions of the rolls I got. (One of my rolls had soggy, chewy seaweed which is the number one biggest sushi no-no for me). Plus the clientele on a Saturday was grating, there's nothing humble about this place, which is a trait I find endearing in food establishments. But to each their own. Not bad, just mediocre in the end.

My girlfriend and I were eating at The Refinery on the upstairs patio, which overlooks this bakery.  When we saw it was 24 hours, and saw that at midnight, they still had a fairly consistent stream of people stopping by, we walked over after our meal.  I thought we were just stopping to check it out, but next thing I know, my girlfriend is asking if they have any of the small cuban rolls (no, they weren't ready), and so she buys a full size cuban loaf.  Has to be 2-3 feet long. 

We were full, but we tore into that thing.  And then again during a 3 hour drive the next day to Palm Beach.  Holy hell is this bread delicious.  Flaky and crispy on the outside, soft and wonderful on the inside.  Fantastic.

Seriously, there should be one of these on every corner, or at least, on my corner.

This place deserves 5 stars because (1) they hand roll and bake breads on site daily (2) they are open almost 24/7,  I think they close for a few hours on Sunday (3) delicious Cuban bread (4) the largest loaf you will ever see; and (5) no preservatives or filler crap. 

The lightly crispy exterior crumbles and gives way to a soft airy center. Since there are no preservatives, this will go stale after a day. They offer these jumbo rolls that are about 3 feet long - no joke. They also offer smaller rolls.

CASH ONLY!

Polite service, gets the job done, and it's for a reasonable price!

All I needed was my Fossil watch battery replaced. I handed it over to him, and in minutes he came back out with it ready to go. I only paid $10.70. I don't know how much it costs at other places to get a new battery, but that seemed a-ok to me.

I love staling Uncle Hershels breakfast special. Nothing better than the country ham at this place. 

I like the apple butter with biscuits, and if I dont feel sweet the gravy is a great topper. 

Dont miss out on the candy sticks in the lobby.

Nice place on the Island. Great atmosphere. 

The beer selection was good, and the food came in plentiful heaps complimenting my beer consumption.

Cozy ambiance. small but nicely varied menu. Fried calamari was sweet and different but enjoyable. My friend had the NY strip which had a very nice pepper sauce, juicy, and was easily cut with a butter knife. i had the chicken and portabella mushroom. i feel like a side of mashed potatoes would have helped to balance out the heavy but very tasty goat cheese and sauce. Overall Food was 4 stars and appropriately priced. Negative waiter was 2 stars. Was unfamiliar with the menu, terrible at taking the order and didn't know how to pour the wine. Would go back!

THIS IS THE BEST. Who knew you could have so much fun outside in the winter in Phladelphia? Well you can have tons of fun when there is ice skating, firepits, shopping, beer, and food involved! And did I mention they usually have a DJ bumping killer jams? If I wasn't so freezing I would have spent the whole day here.

Getting to this place is a little difficult, but it is definitely worth it. The light show that they put on every hour is wonderful.

A co-worker and I lunched here. Their specialty is cooking half chickens on an open flame grill. How that make it tropical is beyond me. It just tastes like grilled chicken and although one gets the impression that you will somehow experience a Caribbean  type meal but nope. This  place is more Mexican than anything else. I had the fajitas, not bad, the guacamole really helped. Co-worker got the Pulled Pork, looked ok, he liked it.
Look, want a quick lunch that's better than burgers  mystery meat tacos and fried yard bird? This is a good choice. Lots of people agree. About noon you could hardly get in the place.

This place is INCREDIBLE! I knew going in that I was going to love it because it's by the same people that set up the Pop Up Garden on Broad. And this place didn't fail to deliver! The grounds are gorgeously landscaped (PHS wouldn't have it any other way) and the lights are stunning. 
The drinks are great (see the picture I posted) and my favorite is the spiked hot cider. The hot chocolate with vanilla vodka was awesome too, but a little too heavy for me to drink all night. But if you're a chocolate lover, it's a safe bet you'll never go without vanilla vodka in your cocoa ever again. 
The food was delicious! They had mashed potato fritters...yes balls of fluffy mashed potatoes and delicately deep fried served atop short rib. You read that right. I split one with the fiance and then went back up and got another order. The turkey leg with gravy looked insane, and by the speed that my friend ate it- I'm assuming it was great too. The vegetarian chili was perfectly spiced and made my meat loving fiance a convert (so much so, he stole it from me. lol). 
Head over here for the skating, the s'mores by the fire, the drinks and just to relax in something that looks like a classic Christmas movie.

This is a tough one, 4 stars if you know what to order, 3 stars if you are a newbie and not sure what to order.
First if there are 2 of you get a pitcher of margaritas. It is a mix, but it is tart and packs a punch. On my first visit I made the mistake of thinking the tequila was lacking, LOL it's there.
The house special is a great combination, usually cooked well and delivered very hot. Eat what you can and save what you think will reheat well the next day, because you will definitely have enough for the next day.
I keep meaning to try the buffet, but keep forgetting.  Soup and salad bar comes with your dinner, but it really is not very appetizing. 
One of their best dishes is not on the menu. A giant Mexican shrimp cocktail. It is served in an enormous margaritas glass, full of shrimp in a thick spicy salsa. This is truly a gem, but you must know to ask for it.
What's a girl to do? Just keep trying new and exciting things.

I started eating breakfast there about a month ago. Now I'm a fan. The food is very good and the bonus is that the people are HAPPY.  It's contagious. They make you feel good, so it's not just food. Price is right too. The place is tiny, so watch out on Sundays (but not always). Also, they punch a card each time you eat there. After 10 meals, the next one is free! (At least for breakfast - don't know about the other meals) Definitely a good place to eat.
Nice place to go for a simple inexpensive drink. Live music. Love the inside outside seating. Little neighborhood bar

This is a really great place for floridians especially those who have not been in the show before. Not the exact show experience but it comes in 2nd to the real deal. My son had a blast and when he has fun mommy is very happy. Very good place for family fun. Must see the Christmas light show that was super awesome and beautiful.

Not as good as La Segunda's, but when you're on this side of town, it doesn't get any better than this. 

Word to the wise-put your bread in the trunk. Otherwise half the loaf will be gone before you get home.

Love this theater. The loyalty cups and shirt are a must. I buy them the begining of every year then every time I come I get refills for a 1.50 and a free medium popcorn (you can upgrade sizes for a bit more). We come to the movies often so it saves us a lot. 18 theaters so they always have all the new movies. And for movie theater pizza this one is really good.

My friend a $25 coupon that expired on Sunday so we figured we'd head to Hard Rock before heading over to Field House to watch the Eagles game. 

Our experience matched our spend. Our server seemed straight out of a movie that mocks bad servers. He was flaky, fake and over us once he realized we were using a $25 coupon for an appetizer platter. The place wasn't too busy so it's not like we were taking up a valuable table. Maybe he thought we wouldn't tip on the pre-discounted price? Which of course we tipped on the full bill. He seemed angry when I asked for a straw with my beverage and extra napkins for our greasy food. He initially told us he'd be right back with napkins when he dropped off the food so I don't know why he was pissed when I asked for more napkins.  

So as I mentioned, we ordered the jumbo appetizer combo for our group of 4 plus a side salad and order of fries. We also each had an alcoholic beverage. Knowing I'd be moving on to beer, I went with a margarita. The margarita itself was fine, expect the salt from the rim had fallen into the drink. It was way too salty but I didn't want to bother our server and send it back. The appetizer combo had your basics: onion rings, chicken wings, potato skins, Mexican spring rolls and chicken tenders. Nothing was bad but nothing was good. 

It was also a bit odd that our server insisted we speak to the manager before we could settle up. He said that the manager likes to speak to everyone who uses this coupon. We had no problem speaking to the manager, except it took her about 10 minutes to come over. At that point, the game was on and we wanted to move to a bar with sound. Thankfully they were playing the game on all of the TVs, just on mute. When the manager finally came over she just asked us how our meal was, thanked us for coming and gave us a little coupon to come back. Seriously? We had to wait on our check for that conversation? 

Beggars can't be choosers, but I know I won't be choosing to go back.

Pretty crappy experience in this over priced tourist trap.  Went there for lunch before the end of the year.  Would have never entertained the notion of going to an HRC in the city I live in but had to use an expiring gift certificate.  Got the appetizer sampler for the table which is the best thing they offer and the fish and chips.  All was going well except that when it came to pay we were charged for a virgin drink.  It's understandable that my beer plus souvenir glass was not covered under the gift cert, but that waiter thought it was the biggest order to ask the manager to get the non-alcoholic drink taken off our bill.  Problems were compounded when trying get parking validated.  Apparently the voucher HRC gave us was only good on the weekend. So for less than two hours of parking it was $18 which is absurd and the whole ordeal for Lunch with a gift cert still was more expensive than choosing a far better restaurant in the city.

A tea room, is NOT just a tea room! Along with a restaurant calling itself a tea room comes the expectations of being transported in time to a kinder, quieter and more relaxed time.
I have been to this tea room, several times. It is usually fairly quiet, the decor is okay, but not overly inviting or comfortable.  The staff is usually knowledgeable,  efficient and polite.
The food quality varieties, as do some of the food offering.
I love scones and their scones are biscuits. The Devonshire cream is absolutely delicious.  The tea varieties are generally good.
They have completely commercialized their business. Murder Mystery dinners at night and interactive dinners.
I am all for small businesses growing and flourishing,  but not at the expense of the product. If you are going to do something, then devote all your energy to it and do it great. I think they need to get off the fence, make some positive changes and stick to their concept and business plan.
Really disappointed in this new twist of event, I really like the owners.
What's a girl to do? Be true to your scones, they are not biscuts.

I shop at Aldi's because it is a great way to save money. The cost here is at least %50 or more cheaper than its competitors. One of my favorite features about this store is that it not only sell grocery items but it also sells items such exercise equipment and toys and along with many other items. Although many of the items sold here are not name brand I can not tell the difference in taste with brand name items. I don't live near an Aldi so I have to carefully plan out each of my trips and buy items in bulk in order to last to my next trip. More Aldi's needs to be opened in order for more individuals to take advantages of the prices here. What I do is buy all that I can at Aldi's and the rest at publix to in order to keep my grocery bill at its cheapest. I love ALDI.

We just had the most pleasant meal. The mussels were tender and my husbands steak was fantastic. Kevin, the owner-chef came out to chat with us. The service was attentive, but not intrusive. This is a great neighborhood restaurant. We will be back.

Food here is just bar food. Nothing special. Big place but still manages to get packed. Turns into a club scene at night so be warned. 

I like watching games here. They have a bunch of TVs and even little ones in every booth. Very young crowd.

I loved my wedding bouquet from Carl Alan.  My parents were the ones who really worked with them, but I had some e-mail exchanges with Vito.  I liked that he was able to work with what I wanted, and was able to create a beautiful bouquet within our budget.  The flowers for the dinner were also very pretty.  Carl Alan was easy to work with and I recommend them.

Always a great place to host an event, I have been at many events here and have had quite a few events here.  The staff is always helpful and at the times I have coordinated events there Shelley Fitzgerald is the one that everything flow smoothly.
Centrally located a screaming deal for a shindig.

Great place to go on a Sunday for a quick farmers market trip. Just make sure to have plenty of cash on hand as most vendors do not accept cards.

Go early for the best selection of organic produce from Sweetwater and Little Pond.

Try a mushroom "burger"- portobello mushrooms, peppers, and onion sauteed in liquid aminos and served on a sandwich thin with mustard, ketchup, and greens. It's great with avocado on top.

You won't regret it if you grab a slice of pizza from the wood fired oven. The mushroom and truffle oil is ah-mah-zing, and I look forward to it every Sunday. They are cash only and $4 per slice.

Pop Craft is in the house from Sarasota with a great variety of frozen treats.

20 Shekels always brings a killer selection of fresh baked breads. They come from Clearwater and are often times a few minutes late setting up but totally worth the wait.

They also have soaps, farm fresh eggs, goat milk cheeses, salsa, olive salads, etc. Come check it out and always SUPPORT LOCAL!

Love watching sports here. Especially day time on Saturdays for college sports. Easy to get a seat at the bar. Lots of TVs. You can't beat cheap pitchers of beer. 

Good is just alright but wash it down with a pitcher and enjoy the game.

University Mall is anything but 'university'.... They should change the name to unaccredited diploma college mall... or 13th grade mall... or, well you get the point.

There are a lot of cheap stores... and by cheap, I mean really cheap quality and price wise. If you are looking for 15 inch heels and super short shorts for $4 this would probably be the best place to go.

A lot of the frequenters are um... interesting. People-watching at this mall is enjoyable and scary (scary because I actually feared for my life when a man with 4 teeth and 3 of those teeth being gold... got in my face showering me with compliments).

I say go to this mall if you enjoy people watching, have a high tolerance for screaming children running rampant, and like good deals on the cheapest of quality clothing.

I mean... It isn't the worst mall I have ever been to. :/

Beaunes, also known as West Palm Wines is located in a non- descript block building on the SEC on Palm Ave and 22nd St in Ybor City.  It is adjacent to Tony's Restaurant which is a good place of a totally different genius species. 

They specialize in French wines, we had a bottle of good white and red burgundy.  They have an extensive selection of cheeses to go with any wine you may order.  A bottle of wine, cheese tray and their great French onion soup makes a full meal.  The soup was the right combination of broth, cheese and bread - not too salty like you typically get.  

There were 4 entrees, salmon, duck, short rib and I can't remember the 4th.  Salmon was very good.  

To finish off, we had a very good flour less
Chocolate cake.   Only open on Thur, Fri and Sat nights. Also a very good venue for private parties - rehearsal diners, etc.

The Fresh Market is on a level all its own. There's nothing quite like it. They're somewhat like Whole Foods but smaller and somehow different. It's produce section is predominantly conventional with a  good amount of organic selections. It's prepared foods are delicious. No other "grocery bakery" can really compare to their selection of tarts, pies, cakes, breads, cookies, etc. They have a great selection of pre-seasoned/ ready-to-cook fish, chicken, meat, pizza, etc. What really sets them apart is their variety of nuts, trail mixes, candies, chocolate-covered fill-in-the-blanks, etc.
I just wish they offered more for vegans/vegetarians. That would bring them to 5 star level.
Bottom line, if you want to play Food Network star and host an impressive dinner party, The Fresh Market is where you ought do buy all you need.

They have many activities here at the fairgrounds. The parking is ample and access is fairly easy. There is plenty of space here for indoor and outdoor events. I have been to home shows, RV shows and a few other events here. They are always fun.

Great for what it is. Affordable in every way. Remember to bring your own shopping bag cause they don't have your typical plastic bag you get at other supermarket, you'll find empty boxes at times within the store that they also allow you to use, similar to Costco. Also, bring a quarter if you want a shopping card, you get your quarter back when you return your cart, it's another way they help you save money. Try their Colby cheese, it's very good. You'll find some items that you won't find anywhere else within their frozen section that's worth a try. Overall, great place if you are on a budget. It's far from Publix in comparison, but again, it's great for what it is.

Great meal from beginning to end.  My fiance and I ordered 2 appetizers, 2 entrees, and 2 desserts. All of it was great. This is likely the best restaurant in the G Ho area. I'll be back! The staff was very friendly as well.

Found stones in the bean and cheese burritos. About a half hour later, ended up with the sh**ts. Would go back to complain, but last time I was there the help was arguing with an elderly couple,and  I've read other unfavorable reviews regarding the "management". Stay away from this place.

Came here during the restaurant week period but we wanted to order off of the regular menu. I had my eyes on the duck but it turns out they only served restaurant week selections! Other than that, I was very much a fan of Entree (i give them the benefit of the doubt and bumped up the score). I had the salmon and my friend had the NY steak. Both were very tender and delicious. The calamari was good too---fried in a why that doesnt make it too doughy and definitely had a different taste to conventional calamari.

Fried oreo for dessert was delicious (with two scoops of ice cream and caramel on top). 

Also, our waitress looks like Olivia Wilde.....which made dinner amusing haha.

Good luck visiting here when they aren't out of at least one or more main ingredients for the bulk of the menu.

This has happened, NO JOKE, 5/6 times I have been to one of the locations.

One of the last times the person behind the counter actually said, ...' worst part is that the owner knows we are out of a bunch of stuff.'  

Keep that up and Xoom is destined to become a lot like Falora - no customers.

Have been going here for over 15 years.   The owner has lost their mind.  Breakfast sandwich, cup of coffee, Chicken friend steak and eggs. one orange juice ($4.00) for juice!   Total just under $27.00.  With tip over $30 for average breakfast at best.

Tucson avoid this place.  So many better places to eat for a much much better value.  

So long Hungry Fox.

Passed this location many, many times before finding it on Groupon as incentive to visit. Found the inside much more spacious than it appeared from outside and first glance at both beer and food menu was very appealing. While both draft and bottle beer selection is very diverse, I was more impressed by the food choices. It is definitely bar food, but there are lots of good offerings....including pizza on pretzel bread and multiple flavors of tacos and sliders. The food alone will bring me back, although I will be careful to avoid live music nights because there is no place to hide from the noise as The Brass Tap is really just one rectangular room minus walls or dividers. While service was very friendly on the night I visited, I had to be very patient because there was only one server working the busy floor and one bartender pouring drinks. I spotted a second bartender sitting at a nearby table drinking after her shift, which may also explain why my beer flight took way too long to arrive at my table. The Groupon being worth items (entrees and beer flights) and not store credit confused me on whether the value was worthwhile, but I'll definitely return and find out.

I don't know why people love this place.  Number 1, the place is not heated.  Dead of winter and no heat. Someone opens the door and you have an artic blast.  No. 2, they don't even provide water. You literally havr to help yourself to a bottled water out of the fridge. Same with all other beverages. I've gone their twice and ordered spanish omlette.  Both times I wad told they were out of refried beans and would use chili instead. How hard is it to keep refried beans in stock?

very professional. It is worth it to me when I go get a manicure and the staff doesnt speak to each other the entire time. I also like when there isnt blaring music or TV or GASP! both at the same time. to me, that's not very relaxing. ** First place I have ever been to that offers you little knit gloves with the fingertips cut off to protect your hands from UV rays if you are getting a gel or shellac manicure. pretty sweet. 
I got a chair massage and asked for it early on in my manicure, but she still gave me the complimentary one while my nails were drying under lamps. I thought that was pretty exceptional as well.

Oh Rigos, I always remember liking you a lot but whenever I eat your breakfast buffet I find it just A-Okay. I'll give you an extra thumbs up for the sopapillas as a selection on the buffet sometimes. The rest is pretty standard, nothing impressive. I like you guys and seem to come back so that's a good sign. Maybe simply because I find myself always working in South side every few years. You're dependable for hot breakfast food that's not a taquiera

I REALLY wanted to like this place. I checked out their menu before we went : tacos, fajitas, taquitos, flautas,etc....everything that is right up my alley. Ive heard good things from other people as well. When I go to a mexican place, I feel the salsa is a good indicator of how the food will be. So right away, I was disappointed. It was watery and thin, with not alot of flavor. Kind of on the sweet side...it was just not good. The chips were actually really good, which drove me crazy since the salsa kinda sucked. I ordered the taquitos mexicanos, which essentially is a rolled up fried tortilla stuffed with chicken and cheese. Now, I LOVE a good greasy meal. But this...was just too much. Literally covered my hands in grease.To make matters worse, I think there was more cheese and onions than chicken. VERY little meat inside. Anyway, The service was decent and its decorated pretty cool inside, but the food left me very underwhelmed and disappointed. Sorry Vallartas :(

Meat department and produce are absolutely stellar. Stay away from the deli department....overpriced salads that arent made in store. I love the wall section full of all sorts of unique drinks- teas, sodas, waters- all yummy and definitely not an ordinary publix find. They also get major points for having a huge variety of naked juices in the produce department. Yum!

Reading recent reviews of Press Box here influenced me to write my own, simply because I don't feel everything being said about this place is accurate. To be clear, Press Box within the last couple years updated every television in both the bar and dining areas to modern technology. There is one primary wall of TVs for the dining room and additional TV's sprinkled above other dining and bar areas. To call Press Box an "old-fashioned dive" would be as much of an understatement as saying "Greg Schiano was a terrible NFL coach," but that dive factor is also much of its appeal. The bathrooms are reason enough to not drink so much, the furniture looks like something from a neighborhood garage sale and the memorabilia on the walls features absolutely nobody currently on a professional roster. Press Box has built its reputation on inexpensive drinks and an above average menu that is available very late. There simply are not many places like this in the Tampa Bay area and that also works to its advantage. For a big sporting event, Press Box generally fills to capacity and the employees who have worked there for years balance good service with an attitude that reflects their particular customers. Unlike other sports bars, if you treat a bartender or server poorly here...you can expect to be treated that way in return. Therefore, if you have a bad experience at the Press Box, you either didn't know what kind of place it was in the first place or you brought it on yourself by acting like an asshole.

Worst experience in years!!

I recently went to Entree with a few friends for restaurant week. I read some good reviews so was pretty excited to try out this new restaurant. However, by the end of the meal I was left disappointed for a myriad of reasons. When we first arrived (on time) the server told our table was not yet ready and would be approx. 15 min wait. I saw an empty table, which was being reserved for a 14 top, so asked if we could sit there in the meantime. The server said OK and went to get us some wine glasses. We ended up waiting 45 minutes for our table to finally be ready!! To make matters worse, the servers only came check up on us a couple of times and never once offered bread or complimentary appetizer which I thought would have been the right thing to do in this situation. Once our table was finally ready, my friends and I were given no assistance by the staff to carry over our belongings, wine bottles, glasses, and even menus to the table. Once seated, the server quickly came and took our order. Our table was near the front of the restaurant which brought up another major problem. The 14 top had arrived and half of them stuffed themselves in the entrance, basically sitting with us at the table, while the other half held the door open. I politely asked them to close the door since it was letting the subzero night air into the restaurant. This happened 3 other times throughout the meal and it was clear we were not the only table that was freezing!! As for the food, I ordered the arugula salad and rib-eye. The salad was bland and lacked flavor. The steak was merely adequate and slightly overcooked. The other patrons enjoyed their salmon but felt that the sauce was a little overbearing and took away from the great flavor of the fish. For dessert I ordered the fried Oreos which I must say was a treat to the senses and a great way to end a not so pleasant evening. I know Entree is a new restaurant, but from the experience I had it has a lot of work ahead of them in the managerial and service department if they want to become a staple in our restaurant scene.

My boyfriend and I went to Entree for dinner recently. Reservations for the weekend are a good idea; we saw several people get turned away. Most of the restaurant was reserved for a larger party that fortunately came in at the tail end of our meal. We were seated up front by the window. There wasn't a whole lot of room between tables. Also, it was quite chilly with the front door constantly opening and closing - wear something warm just in case!

Our server was nice but maybe a little inexperienced. Silverware was not replaced between courses; it was taken from our plates and placed back on the table. One of our dishes was swept away with a few bites left though we weren't asked if we were finished. 

That said, the food was pretty good. We shared the wedge salad, mussels and calamari to start. The wedge was fine, the dressing was nice. The mussels were really good - I really loved the broth and the bread served under the pile of mussels sopped up that broth really nicely without getting too soggy, somehow. The calamari was a little overcooked but still tasty. 

My main dish was broccoli rabe ravioli served in marsala cream sauce. The ravioli were topped with a mix of artichokes, olives and other tasty bits that I can't remember. The flavors were unexpected and unique but I really liked the dish. My boyfriend had a bite and was not a fan. He seemed happy with his steak, though. 

We skipped dessert but there were a few options. I'd be willing to check out Entree again sometime.

I've been on a recent Tumamoc Hill kick and I'm loving it! Some days are harder than others, or even easy. The weather is perfect to hit the hill, the views are always gorgeous and calorie burning is guaranteed. Rush hour times it's super busy, like a sea of people busy, so prepare for lot's of foot traffic.

On the one hand, it's filthy, disorganized, and decrepit.  Let's start with the filth. the floors are dirty with black, have not been swept or mopped. What makes it worse is containers of product are laying right on it!  The frozen meat section smells of blood.  

Next let's talk about decrepit and disheveled: The very busy butcher aisle was cut in half by boxes piled up, creating a traffic jam.  Whole sections of product are blocked by piled boxes.  

The signs that mark each aisle from the ceiling hang crooked, like they've been shot in the foot. I noticed that in 1986 when I first visited, and it's 2014 and the same sign (the "spices" sign) is STILL crooked forty years later!!!  And it's missing the writing on the other side.   

Frankly, the store looks like what would happen to a Safeway if we had a nuclear war and were wandering around the ruins

So why four stars then? It comes down to this: They have an amazing selection of asian products.  I find stuff here you can't get elsewhere in town, like live crab, some good seafood, szechuan chile sauce from pixian. The prices are pretty decent too compared to some of the other asian stores.  
So, yes, you can go to Lee Lee and things are pretty much spotless.  I like Lee Lee. But there's something to the chutzpah of this place and their skill in evading the inspectors.
Great staff. Great food easy on the pocket. A place that makes you feel at home. It's convenient when your about to take the train or local to the area.

You have to be pretty confident in the prospect of your restaurant being great when you open a BYOB pretty much across the street from the beloved Pumpkin... and from what I can tell Entree is holding their own. 
Smaller restaurant, with a very warm cozy atmosphere. We didn't feel like we were sitting on top of other diners and it was not too loud either. 
The menu was limited of course as it was restaurant week but the food didn't disappoint. Wedge salad was definitely an A - fresh and dressed well. The calamari was some of the best I have ever had, not too heavily battered, served with peppers and spicy aioli - so crispy and flavorful. 
The steak was cooked perfectly, a little salty but very tasty and is a very large portion, and we couldn't get enough of the perfectly whipped mashed potatoes. Some liked the salmon, some were turned off by the raspberry glaze which was a little too sweet and heavy handed. We all agreed the butternut squash hash that comes with the salmon was the highlight of the meal - the consistency and the taste were out of this world. Oh and do NOT miss the fried Oreos WOW if its possible they are even better than they sound. 
Very good service as well! (The waiters are kind cute too!) 
Quick Summary: Cute little BYOB with good food, good service, get the steak and the oreos.

$1 for delicious Asian baked goods??? Okay!

We popped in here after a UYE this weekend.  I never knew that Fortune Star existed and was shocked with happiness at the prices.  I tried a few of the sweets (mad props to the almond cookie and peach cake) and they were really good.  The hot dogs wrapped in a massive amount of dough looked really delicious too.  I also enjoyed the back patio where we fed ducks and cats left overs.

My only gripe - a little 'tude behind the counter when questioned about whether they had tea or not.  Not enough 'tude to not make me come back for some cheap and lovely pastries, but enough to mention.

Bring your dog, they'll get a nice treat.  Very nice & friendly people.  Great selection of top quality food, snacks and clothing for your doggie.  Great to see many others come here and support this very positive business

We were in a pinch and the JCC saved us.

We had to plan a last minute work event that needed to take place off site, be nice enough for a high ranking public official and accommodate 100 people. JCC to the rescue! Seriously, they were able to set us up in the Heritage Room and cater the event as well with under two weeks notice. 

The Heritage Room is lovely and we were lucky enough that the walls were adorned with gorgeous western artwork on display for a month long fundraiser for Empire Ranch. The catering manager, Gwen suggested the menu after I told where the general direction we were taking and set a gorgeous spread, matching the overall colors of the paintings in the room by using fabric and serving plates.

This was my first time at the JCC and the place is incredible. I saw lots of families and children using the facility for childcare, fitness and other classes. What a great place!

Good Boars Head Products sold here, and sandwiches are made on fresh bread, however I would like to see more updated processes used, such as grilling hot sandwiches instead of microwaving them -- or at least give the customer that choice. Pretty much nothing here you couldn't make at home though. Poor sanitation practice observed behind the counter on my last two visits so will not be returning.

BB&B is an amalgam of too much stuff in one large SQUARE store.  I came in to find ONE item, and walked out with something completely different! It saddens me that I paid full retail just because I didn't have any of thei coupons, but I did sign up to get them on my phone!

We decided to try this place on a whim and so glad we did!  I ordered the mini skillet with bell peppers, onions, mushrooms, cheese, hashbrowns and one egg.  My love ordered eggs, corned beef hash, grits and pancakes.  Everything was delicious!  We mentioned to the waitress that it was our first time visting so the owner, Mike, came by to welcome us and tell us how grateful he was for us to try his establishment!  Truly shows that he cares about his customers which is great for customer service.  It was a nice personal touch to our experience.  We'll definately be back!
I've been coming here for years.  I order the 30 piece breakfast tray and take it into work.  I love this place and so does everyone at work!  Everything is absolutely delicious.

Though hard to find, the pop up beer garden in Penn's Landing was worth the schlep on foot. In Philadelphia for a weekend trip, my boyfriend and I were looking for something to do on our first night out in the city. I heard about the light show by the Blue Cross River Rink on the Visit Philly website, which also mentioned the Lodge. I was skeptical about a beer garden in the winter time, but was pleasantly surprised by the set-up and happily content with the brews.

I hope the Lodge is in Philly next December. I'll definitely be there!

MMMMM CHIPS
At the persuasion of my sister, I had the Seafood Nachos. Totally unappetizing in appearance because it's just a dripping blob of white sauce mixed with undeterminable  seafood plopped over tortilla chips- but delicious! The crunchy and slightly salted Chips were a great contrast to the creamy and softly firm Seafood mixture.  (True, I could only recognize the shrimp and the seafood taste was only in wisps) The Tamale was equally droopy looking (and feeling) but the flavor was pretty good. I wished the texture was less floppy. 

With low prices and the food seeming to have been created by families, don't expect poshy-poshy but simple, down-home Mexican dishes presented without fuss.

FIESTA ATMOSPHERE
And it wasn't just because of the nearby family celebrating something (You'll run into a lot of those here). Vallarta's is normally busy--period. Yet somehow in the craziness the servers expertly wound through the mass of bodies like an Olympic Skier on the slopes.

Located in one of the numerous, non descript strip centers along Waters Ave, you'll find Song Huong.  2 of my girlfriends and I had dinner plans for this evening and we had decided upon Vietnamese. Lucky for us, it was a VERY cold evening, making our decision that much more enticing. 

We entered to a clean, yet modest restaurant full of a table of Asian decent patrons-always a good sign. 

We all decided upon a bowl of the vegetable pho based on the wonderful, inviting pictures posted on yelp. We shared the fresh roll with shrimp. Although good, nothing great. Now onto the pho!  Wonderful broth (although I'm not sure the broth was vegetable broth....), crispy, al dente vegetables that consisted of broccoli, carrots, baby bok choy, mushrooms and cabbage. It really was a wonderful bowl and even better after adding all the extras. Soo good and I great choice for this chilly evening.

It's ok ... I have tasted  better sushi .  Service was slow and tight inside .   May come back but not necessarily a must.  Nevertheless, I enjoyed the company considering the company and being Valentines day!!  Not bad but just ok!

They need more wait staff on the weekend.  Went with a group of girls on a Saturday and service took forever.  Went with my family on a Tuesday after visiting the museum and the food and service were great but we were the only customers.  My bf and I went on a Sunday and ended up leaving.  All the tables outside were dirty and it was obvious they had been sitting Iike that for awhile.  Food was on the ground and beer bottles were knocked over. We never got service outside so moved in by the bar and that table hadn't been cleaned either.  We never got service there either so we left.  We left even though we paid for parking.  It's a shame about the weekend service because the food is good.  Too bad.

Being lunchtime, after walking in Al Lopez Park, I was in the mood for some comida Latina.

My wife suggested Viscaya. Looked them up on yelp, and was disappointed that they open for dinner at. 5:30 pm and consequently were now closed.

Since they were in the direction we were driving my wife suggested we drive by to see the restaurant . We have never been there .

What a mistake. They were open . They open for lunch between 11:30 am to 2:00 pm. Dinner starts at 5:30 pm.

We were so excited that they were open.

The place had a nice ambience, but that's where it ends.

I ordered mushroom soup and a grilled grouper sandwich. The wife collar collard green soup and fritas de bacalao.

Our soups had no consistency. Watered versions of the aforementioned.

Campbell's cream of mushroom soup would have been better and cheaper. At $6 each what a rip off.
 
Oh, I forgot for a restaurant that has been open for over 10 years, the service was slow. You would think that they have had plenty of time to work on it.

Our orders were brought out wrong, my sandwich had a tiny piece of grouper and my wife's fritas were hard like a baseball.

The only positive was the bread. Very good.

They apparently have live music on the weekends . Maybe that's what keeps them
Open.

You can get a better and cheaper meal at pollo Trop!

Carrollwood residents same on
You.

Note I literally got sick to my stomach!

Today I got an awesome haircut and style from Ashley, she is so sweet and friendly.  I love my new haircut!!  I am amazed how pretty it looks especially because she fixed a cut from a different salon.  Ashley did a beautiful job and I plan to come back for a trim in a couple of months.  The ladies at the front desk are also very kind and welcoming.  I enjoyed my complimentary water, and the shampoo that was used smelled so good.  I love my haircut!!!

To rate a dive bar accordingly, you must consider many things.  Obviously, this isn't going to be a classy joint.  It's a dive bar, and boy is it a dive bar.  But there's nothing wrong with it!  It's a cool place to hang out with a friend or two.

I came here to see my buddy perform, the crowd seemed to consist almost exclusively of people he knew or people there for the other acts on the bill.  Only about 20 or so people?  So it was subdued and intimate and no douchebags were present.

The wife and I decided to go for Mexican Friday night. We went to Vallartas because is near to home and in the past we had a great time there. Unfotunately, we were seated on a rear area of the restaurant that actually smelled sort of greasy. The ceiling tiles seem to have seen better days. The waiter was very friendly so I ask him if they had live Mariachi music. He said that live Mariachi music was available only on Sundays. The waiter also added that he did not care for Mariachi music. I ask him why there was no music playing on the ceiling speakers. He smiled politely and simply walked away. The food was good and plentiful; yet the place felt like a dead zone. The wife and I simply will not be returning to this place. All I wanted was to eat, drink and hear some Mexican music...I guess I was hoping for way too much.

Tackled the beast this morning!!! Rawr!!!

I was really excited to do Tumamoc this morning after my daughter A did it last weekend. A, K & I met on the street at 8AM where we parked and hit the trail. It starts off pretty easy with a slight incline but then you hit your first switchback and holy moly! It's quite the hike but so much fun and everyone on the trail is nice and encouraging. Being older and less fit....and a first timer....I did have to take a couple of quick breaks to catch my breath along the way but once you reach the top it is such a feeling of accomplishment. The views from the top are nothing short of gorgeous - one direction is the heart of our city, another is Starr Pass resort and surrounding homes, another is just beautiful desert and towering saguaros.

We had a couple of deer cross the road in front of us at one point on the way up and on our way down we were lucky enough to catch a buck and his harem of 5 does feeding in the grass and shrubbery. 

The shrine and prayer/meditation spot at the bottom is peaceful, cultural and beautiful. Tumamoc gets really crowded but don't let that hold you back, everyone is nice and there's room for us all.

I'm sold! Brass Tap was  another restaurant and I've been meaning to it out for months now. I'm glad I finally did. Very friendly service and an awesome platinum margarita. The best part for me about the Brass Tap is the location. It's in the same shopping plaza as the Publix I occasionally shop at, Plato's closet and my nail shop. I think this will be my new regular place.
I have not tried any of the food yet so I'm looking forward to trying out the menu. I'm also looking to keep the pitcher on Monday.

Tonight there was a live band playing some good tones. I really enjoyed my time here and the people.
Great food. Great service. Great ambiance. A true change from modern dinning. Took me back. Server, Jessica, was the best.

Been coming here for years and the quality never disappoints! Best fresh pasta around. Excellent "gravy". Marinated mozz is fantastic; as is the frozen asiago tortellini & raviolis.Nice selection of olives. Got some real nice stacked eggplant parm kinda thing at the counter. What's not to love about this place?

So.... The Arthur Fierman review was mine, logged in wrong. I love the good stuff coming out of this ugly orange truck! I don't eat burgers, but when I do....burger culture scratches my itch with low grease factor, thanks!

This place is pretty close to our house, so I'm happy that I had a good experience and the food was pretty delicious.  The server was polite & friendly, the food hot and fresh.  I love their pickle chips and the Reuben was pretty good.

Friendly staff, great selection and lots of delightful shiny things! I was very happy to see that they hae so many new 1940-50's inspired designs.  

I haven't had much luck here in the past, but today.. today I was blessed to find a new costume skirt for my corsets, a L/Xl from Burleska for under $100. The seaming and everything looked fantastic.  Pricey? somewhat, but it's good to know that I'm no longer too big to wear their fabulous fashions!

Great food.  A tad on the pricey side for a sandwich.  Having been going there for years and only order 2 things since I like them so much.  Sausage sandwich or Beef sandwich.    Don't even know what else they offer as I like both of them so much.  I ask for mine to be soaked in juice - which they do.

My favorite deli in Tucson. I normally get the Super Sub Italian Style, which is an Italian sub, delicious. I also like the croissant doffino and the meatball sub. The seating area is a crowded maze. The service is usually very good.

Before you visit: this is a Spanish restaurant, not Italian. Yelp snobs have ungraciously come here and bashed this place; so hang on. Cuba is not this super fancy place, restaurants are cozy hangouts with loud laughs. Cuban Americans are not pretentious, they are simply trying to show you a replica of the bar in the Ybor site that their parents build. Ya, the cheese on the Cubans looks  funny but it tastes great. Hung up on looks?, try the Mahi Mahi Cubana. It's perfectly cooked flakes melt in your mouth with peppers and onions to spice the situation up a notch. 

Keep in mind Kristy B., the Cuban sandwich here costs $8.95. That is cheaper than a meal from a food truck here. Maybe downtown next time?

The ambiance is great, but a fresher presentation of a few items wouldn't hurt!

This place is steeped in the old-school east-coast Italian ambience that I've never really experienced it Tucson. Sweet nostalgia.  Adding to that was the geriatric crowd of regulars - it was sweet that the servers and bartenders all knew them.  Dark and romantic, not for kids.

We opted to sit in the bar to order pizza, and it was delicious - definitely in my top five for pizzas in Tucson. Their sauce is boldy sweet and tangy - super good. We started out with an antipasto salad and wine.  It was a lovely evening - a great opportunity for my husband and I to unexpectedly slow down and spend some great time and conversation together.

Pricey? Yes. Modern and trendy? No. Inventive menu? Nope. Didn't stop me from loving it. Go with someone you love.

Real deal Viet food at this hole-in-the wall! We've become regulars here. You know this place is good when ALL you see inside is patrons talking in Vietnamese and ordering things you've never seen (not to mention that the menu makes it feel like you are in a restaurant in Vietnam). 

First, I want to comment that the Pho here is the best that i've had (coming from someone who's lived in NYC and HI). Flavorful, fresh veggies (a must with Pho), and doesn't leave you with that MSG thirst after eating. We order the everything or the rare beef and tripe Pho. It's very authentic.  The quail dinner is another favorite (try asking for a half order if it's just 2 of you). 

We also tried the family style hot pot (thai flavored), and it was DELICIOUS. Next time, we'll see if we can get just seafood and no beef. We hope to try more hot pots here soon (if we can resist not getting the Pho). The pork chop rice is average here. 

Love that they also give you a mild cold tea, instead of tap water. The on the house, Viet donuts for dessert make you wanting more if they give you ones that are fresh from the fryer.  Looking forward to trying more from here.

Heads up that the bathroom always smells funny, the decor is typical viet restaurant (but still clean), and the service is only average. The only waiter they have and the rest of the back restaurant staff are very nice, but they don't seem to speak much English. But be patient, that's why the place is so good, they are authentic. This is the type of place where you enjoy good cheap food, not the service or atmosphere.
Tried this place on Friday and loved it! Food was excellent, service was great, and the atmosphere is so nice. We will definitely come back for sushi!

I've used Firehouse a few times to shop for bike gear and fix things with my bike. Staff was super helpful -- Ricky was friendly and knowledgeable.

Great service.
Reasonable prices.

Great place for a tasty and cheap lunch. Place is usually busy at lunchtime but worth it. Portions are huge. I never eat there but take it on the go.
I suggest the chicken-the ropa vieja is OK but I have had much better...

Bought a used minivan from them.  All was good until it got hot out then you could smell cigarettes each time the AC was turned on.  They Ozone treated it twice at no charge.  Was pissed to have to deal with it, but they did try and take care of it.  Probably wouldn't have purchased the vehicle if I knew a smoker owned it, but I'm not sure they knew that either as you can only smell it when its hot and we bought it in March.

Im a regular customer here and have always been pleased with my experiences. See my review for Wolf Cycles as it is same staff. The added bonus about Firehouse is that you can go for a beer downstairs while you wait for your bike repairs. Ideal business for what they provide- new and used affordable bicycles.

The view makes the food taste better.
While I am not the biggest fan of the food at Columbia, this location is more about the atmosphere to me.  The outdoor seating is phenomenal.  For anyone that knows me, you will know that I hate outdoor seating due to hot weather and bugs.  But, the terrace here is perfect because the breeze from the water and the view is breathtaking.  There are plenty of umbrellas to hide under, and I like to arrive early before it gets too hot and crowded.

After eating here, just take a stroll down Tampa Riverwalk and admire all of the rich folks and their stuff.

There are restrooms downstairs that are easily accessible.

 Parking is free on the weekends before 5pm at the parking lot across the street, next to Oishii Sushi plaza (whatever that plaza is called).

+++++++++++++++Before you drive to the store READ THIS+++++++++++++++++

YES IT WILL BE MOBBED.
YES THEY MAY BE OUT OF ITEMS YOU WANT (CALL AHEAD TO CHECK):
YES THERE MAY BE A LACK OF PARKING.
NO YOU SHOULDN'T CARE, AND HERE IS WHY:

Trader Joe's likes to invest in its products and in its employees, not in parking lots. Lack of parking at Trader Joe's is COMMON all across America. Don't believe me? Just Google "Trader Joe's parking problems" and pick a city. They bitch about it Denver. They bitch about it in Burbank, Calif. They bitch about it in Miami. You can bitch about it in Tampa, but instead of bitching, why don't you be smart and shop there at OFF-PEAK HOURS?

This is just how it is; there is never enough parking for the mobs and mobs of people who love to shop at a grocery store that is essentially the size of a CVS with a CVS-size parking lot. If you REALLY hate parking hassles and crowds, then I guess skip it. But you'd be doing yourself a huge disservice. Because the stuff they sell is unique, cheap and besides, you are not the only person in Tampa, in Florida, or in the United States frantically looking for a parking space at a Trader Joe's store. You are one of hundreds of thousands. Get over it, move on and go get your Two Buck Chuck!

Now, the review:

Congrats to Tampa Bay for finally, after many years of cruel and inhumane deprivation, landing a Trader Joe's store. Finally we get to savor a little taste of California in Florida; people in California have been eating multi-ethnic, gourmet, healthy food like this for decades (OK, some of the stuff they sell, like salted caramel sauce, is NOT healthy, but I digress)! This store, No 766 in South Tampa, seems a little smaller than the other TJ's I've been to. But it may just have been all the boxes everywhere and employees (pre-opening day) and crowds making it feel cramped. Anyway:

Fantabulous, orgasmic, euphoric, to-die-for and astonishing. That's how I describe almost any Trader Joe's, and the Tampa location is no exception. How do I love Trader Joe's? Let us count the ways:

fresh Cubano wrap w/ cumin mustard sauce, frozen chicken tikka samosas, TO-DIE-FOR salted caramel dessert sauce, fresh individually packaged organic chicken breasts for a few bucks a pound, frozen chocolate croissants (when u bake these things u will feel like u are in Paris), frozen scones ready to bake, Chinese chicken salad cold and ready to eat, frozen sliced leeks, Spanish Serrano ham, the best chocolate chip cookie ice cream sandwich you will ever put in your mouth, peanut satay sauce, cilantro salad dressing, huge bottle of Italian EVOO for 7 bucks, fresh herbs cheaper than Publix or Fresh Market, chocolate covered potato chips, squash gnocchi, a bottle of wine for 3 bucks (Two Buck Chuck is an excellent cooking wine), fresh (not dried) rice noodles, 52,000 flavors of hummus, frozen seafood of every kind, vegan pad thai, cream scones fresh-baked, whole wheat water crackers, organic mango lemonade, high fiber trail mix granola bars, fresh pineapple juice, frozen pizza made in Italy that will make you never want to buy a frozen pizza at Publix ever again, frozen pie crust of superb quality, curried turkey burgers, double fire roasted salsa, organic + fresh butternut squash ravioli, caramelized onion and ham pizza, spinach and kale greek yogurt dip, freshly churned coconut juice, asparagus risotto and every kind of deliciously inventive salty snack, cookie or chocolate confection you can think of.

######Do you like Thai curry? If so, RUN to the refrigerated entree case and look for something called Chicken Medallions in Coconut Red Curry. That right there is basically the BEST red chicken curry you will ever eat from a grocery store and likely many restaurants. ****In fact, it is better than ANY red curry I have ever had in a Thai restaurant in Tampa Bay. Same goes for Mexican and other cuisines. You will find yourself going to a lot fewer restaurants. Craving Mexican? Much better to go to TJs and get their frozen chile relleno entree for a couple bucks than go spend 30 bucks in a mediocre Mexican restaurant.

Oh yeah, and their seasonal produce and foodstuffs are outrageous: pumpkin goodies in autumn, chocolatey goodies at Xmas, oh my!

Please tell me where else you can buy this kind of delectable stuff for a delectable price? Oh yeah, that's right: 

NOWHERE EXCEPT HERE.

Don't marinate on the idea of going to TJ's after it opens: JUST GO. 

The hype is true. Trader Joe's is freaking awesome. And it's not expensive (unless you are buying saffron or some crazy item like that). And the customer service rocks (hate their peanut butter pretzels? return them anytime, even if you ate half the bag, no questions asked). Also their employees are genuinely happy to work there and help you. And they even play The Smiths over the store speakers. 

I honestly don't know why anyone would NOT shop here.

I love that you can sit outside on nice days on their patio, relax in the sun, and enjoy the view.  I hate that the last three times I have visited, the service has been atrocious.  It's now apparent that this is the norm, not the exception.  This last visit I had a friend with me in town from Brooklyn, and even she was surprised at how bad the service was.

We were led to a table by a server, and sat.  Waited 10-15 minutes for someone to come over, when finally a server arrived with menus and to take a drink order.  Another 15-20 minutes go by, and the server shows with our sodas, and takes our order.  I order my favorite Spanish bean soup, and the grilled grouper sandwich, and my friend ordered gazpacho and the meat, cheese & olive appetizer for her meal.  Another half hour or so goes by, and our waiter finally shows with both our soups, and our entrees.  Really?  You didn't think the soups are supposed to come out first?  And you bring the fresh bread halfway through our meal?  

Spanish bean soup was delicious, but the gazpacho was watery and flavorless, and the paper-thin fish in my sandwich didn't have any taste either-maybe that's why they serve it with such a strong tartar sauce?  I left half behind because it wasn't good enough to take with.

Final thoughts - if you only get an hour for lunch, don't come here.  If you are very hungry and hoping for food in a 'normal' amount of time, don't come here.  If you have a few hours to hang out in the sun and people watch, and don't mind marginal food and service as the trade off, then *ding ding ding* this is your place.

I have been riding my Trek mountain bike for years and finally decided that I wanted to upgrade to a hybrid.  I went to a few local bike shops, but my last stop was the location on Lancaster Ave (Wolff Cycles) first.  I told the guys in the shop about my needs and how I wanted a decent used hybrid bike at a decent price.  I saw a great number of used high end bikes within the $200-$400 range.  I was informed that I should go to Baltimore Ave location because they have more used bikes at there.   I went to the Baltimore Ave location the next day.  The guys here were equally as friendly and showed me a few different models and was able to take them out on a test drive.  

The great thing about Firehouse Bicycles, I was able to trade-in my Trek bike and used it to lower my price on my new bike.  Also, with the purchase, you get a free tune-up in the first 30 days.  

I feel that bike knowledge and the good customer service should be enough to have you check out this place.  Great Bikes - Great Prices!!

Saw the Little River Band. I have a new found love for seeing bands from my childhood in a more intimate setting and this place did the trick.  Anyplace that can stick around for 50+ years has to be doing something right.  Stood at the bar until we made our way out on the dance floor. Staff was nice, good times! I will for sure be keeping an eye out on upcoming shows.

Loved it!! Went here with my wife's parents and had one if the skillet breakfasts with chorizo. Couldn't even finish it all, it was piled with chorizo, hash browns, eggs, salsa, and cheese...absolutely delicious!! The place was like a little mom and pop place out of the Midwest, felt right at home. The waitress was very friendly and the prices were great, will be back here very soon!!

Crazy is a word I would use to describe this place. Once you enter the gates its like your in a different time period. The workers here are almost all in character and it really sets the mood for the festival. There are lots of things to look at and buy here, especially if your into this kind of stuff. I have a sister who loves heavy metal and she would totally love this place. I would say that the cost for the things here are a bit high, I wouldn't come to a festival expecting to pay hundreds of dollars on cloths and crafts but I know that some people are really, really into that kind of thing. 
But even if your not here to spend more money, there are lots of shows and entertainment that are free to watch, but always nice if you give a donation at the end...seeing this is how they make a living. 

A word of warning, if you don't have cloths for the occasion, do come dressed ready to get a little dirty. Not only because of the mud show, but just walking around kicks up a lot of dirt and dust that will get your nice cloths dirty. But if your looking to buy some cloths of the period, be ready to spend a couple hundred dollars. Its good quality, just expensive.

Being a transplant , I was among the many just waiting for Trader Joes to finally come to Florida. But I'm definitely hoping they expand to Brandon or Riverview in the next few years. Against my better judgement I went on opening day. I happened to be driving by Dale Mabry anyway. And it actually wasn't so bad by 330. Tampa Police had done a good job in controlling traffic in the parking lot. The Trader Joes staff was well trained to answer questions, locate products, and keep things moving. I was in and out within about 30 minutes.

It's a real mad house on opening day. Have always love coming here for the variety, ambiance, service and reasonable prices. You may wanna wait till the tide goes down, people have been waiting with anticipation. Great sales on the wines and cheese, flowers are always fresh. Hope they continue expanding here in the Tampa area. If you've never been to a TJs before, now is the time.

This is a dream of a grocery store. The only way it can get better is for them to deliver it to your home, unpack and shelve it and maybe serve it to you. The produce was fresh, the products are of excellent quality and the prices are unbelievably better than other local grocery's sale prices. I had heard the hype but now it is true. Bought a few bagfuls of items and have been sampling ever since. Lots of exclusive items that are available at Trader Joe's only. No disappointments so far. Too good to be true? Let me stay in this dream forever.

No stranger to the food store phenom known as Trader Joe's, I previously battled for parking spaces, roamed aisles completely awestruck by the arrays of fresh, frozen and ready-to-eat treats and wonderful wines this shop stocks when I lived in Berkeley, CA.

South Tampa is now part of the Trader Joe family and it was a welcome homecoming to make my first - of what will be many - trips there. 

Grocery shopping is not high on my list of favorite things but I have to admit it's a chore no more. The store is spacious, the folks there are friendly and the goods are as good as it gets.

On a rainy Monday morning the store was already lively with folks loading up carts - not baskets - as they leisurely roamed through the displays of organic produce, past the frozen food cases and wound their way through the displays of wine.

All I can say is "Old friend, it is good to see you again."

Nice place to hang out at after work on the island! Pretty good choice of beers & the food was good bar food.  Cool atmosphere, & friendly staff.  A little small, but nice.  Heard there's a hospital employee night.... I'll have to check it out!

I have attended this festival probably about 5 or 6 times over the last 13 years, the first time at about 17.  It definitely does have perks!  There are a lot of cool shows and different types of entertainment.  My favorites are the Demzarrah Gypsies, Sirena, and the joust.  Also, one that I saw for the first time this year was Wheel of Death.  That is a must see!  There was also a good pipe and drum band whose name is escaping me unfortunately.  Something along the lines of Cu Dubh I believe.  

There are a lot of people in costume and in character.  I often cannot tell between patrons and employees!  It really does add to the atmosphere.  

As far as vendors go, there are many there and most have some really cool stuff!  You will find jewelry, weapons (like swords and decorative axes), clothing, decorations, art, and more.  There are tents where you can get henna, face painting, or even massages.  The stuff is cool to look at, but in my opinion most of it is overpriced and a lot of it you could probably find in a catalog for less.  There are some exceptions since some vendors hand make everything they sell, but just keep an eye out.  

For food, I recommend eating elsewhere before or after the fair.  The food is overpriced.  The scotch eggs are nice for a smaller meal/snack, but that along is $4.75.  I had a pepperoni and cheese calzone that barely had any filling in it which cost $6.00!  I'd rather save my money for beer!  Speaking of beer, there is a good selection.  They are pretty standard festival prices.  

One of the negatives I see is repetition.  If you have attended this festival before, it usually doesn't vary much from year to year.  The shows you saw last year will be very similar this year.  The chess and joust vary in outcome, but it's still kind of redundant in my opinion.  I guess this could be considered a perk to some people, but it bothers me a bit.  Also, all of the performers are shameless about getting tip.  I know this is probably a big part of their income, but I just found it slightly off putting.  

Overall, I would say if you appreciate Renaissance/goth/fantasy/nerd culture you should absolutely go to the Bay Area Renaissance Faire.  You will enjoy yourself.  The grounds are lovely and it's a pretty nice time of year in Florida generally to do something like this.  Huzzah!

This was the first place I ate at when I arrived to Tampa and I'm so happy it was. The food is definitely authentic, everything was cooked to perfection.
