It's a la carte style so all of the food is set out for you and you pick what you like. They give you really big portions for the price you pay. One plate for me will last about two meals.

Pretty cool. First timer. I like the private label and organic selection. Very crunched for space but they did the best they could per square inch. $2.99 wine screams my name. I will definitely be a repeat customer.

Funny, FUN, kinda romantic in a weird way, relatively (kinda sorta) affordable and just a downright great place for a date or a night out with friends!

We won tickets here, ironically at another event which we had won tickets to... through Yelp of course. Yelp is awesome!

I'll say it upfront, even with free tickets, the night wasn't that cheap. But not horrific. Tickets aside, two appetizers and two drinks cost about $55 with tip. Yikes, not even entrees. But it was worth it. 

The show was funny and entertaining and inappropriate all at once. It was called the "Are You Serious?" Comedy Magic show and consisted of maybe a half dozen comedians slash magicians. Half of which were great and the other half which were not. Oh well. 

Service was.... friendly.... but slow. I have pity for servers but on the other hand, when it's lacking it's lacking. We didn't have napkins and asked for some and never got any til the end of dinner. Waited for 20 mins after ordering drinks to receive them. I'd actually recommend eating and drinking a whole bunch beforehand and not trying here due to the volume of people (they're simply overwhelmed), but then again, it's part of the "experience" so that's up to you!

Another thing: arrive early. We got there right at 7 for the 8pm show and were behind a decent line, with perfect seats a few rows back. Don't be first because that's too close, but don't wait til the last minute to arrive or you will be too far back!

There's more I could say, but everyone should check it out for themselves instead. I assure you that you will have a good time.

I got food poisoning from the Mediterranean hummous yesterday. Maybe it was something they had sitting in the heat when they were stocking the store. I don't know. But it will be a long time before I can even look at hummous again.
Otherwise, Trader Joe's is a useful addition to the local grocery scene, but it's not meant to be for everything, even though you can actually buy Trader Joe's toilet paper, and some people really do. (I wished I had bought some after the food poisoning!)
They have a few specialty items that are okay, and some of the prices are even cheaper than Walmart, such as iceberg lettuce for $1.29 vs. $ 1.49 at the Wallyworld. The atmosphere inside is kind of grim, with very unflattering overhead fluorescent lighting and drab decor. I really don't get the reason why so many people adore this chain. I first encountered it in California 15 years ago. I'll be a regular, I suppose, but only for select things on sale, maybe about 10 percent of the stock.

I love Trader Joe's. They have really good wine besides the 2 dollar bottles. They also stock a pretty good assortment of organics and gluten free items, all at great prices. I am happy that there is finally one in Florida that is in my occasional stomping grounds. I can't wait until we have one in my town of Orlando.

Maybe great customer serice if you are buying a new car but they don't care about you if you are buying one of the used cars.
Was suppose to pick up a car but  they said they wanted to be out of building by 5pm for a game.  We were due there at 4.10 with check in hand.  Would other places of business turn away people 50 minutes before closing for a game!  Very disappointed.

You would think they would have noticed both window wipers on car needed replacing badly.  They claim to do a 100 + check on the car to be sure it's road worthy.
May only have been buying a used car this time but way to lose future business!

This review is for delivery. Ate in once - far from impressive restaurant. However definitely the tastiest pizza in the area. I started ordering from here for 2 reasons - they delivered to us faster than the pizza place right next door AND fell in love with the big square pie with bacon. 
Last night my husband ordered a little round 1/2 pep with wings & cheese fries. I wasn't even hungry but 1 look & I knew I wanted a slice. Seriously delicious! Idk much about the pizza biz but they somehow have better tasting crust than anywhere, except maybe Pizza Hut back in their hay day. We also like their cheese fries (whiz cup on the side). The wings were decent - no complaints. It's so hard to find really good pizza, unless of course we're at friends in South Philly.   I recently ordered a large Sicilian & cut it up to bring as a snack to a party - quite a hit!   Keep up the good work please Original Village!!

2 stars because just wasn't tasty. I held off writing this review because I really wanted to like this place. The concept is great and the meal descriptions sound amazing, but the two meals I tried - chicken parmesan and chocolate chip pancakes - fell short. They just weren't that flavorful. Felt like I was eating healthy. With the chicken parm they really scrimped on the sauce, which certainly didn't help. I will say the food was fresh and healthy.

Our service was awful! My girlfriend and I sat at the bar and ordered two drinks and two appetizers Sunday evening. The bartender was barely polite, un-attentive, and allowed us to wait an entire 45 minutes (the time at which we just asked for our check) before going back to the kitchen to ask where our food was. She returned to tell us there were four tickets in front of ours, although I watched several customers seat and receive food while we waited. In the end, we skipped the food and have no plans of returning.

I did it, I took the plunge to see what all the hubub was about. 

Make no mistake, I've been to a TJ's before, in LA, in GA & In Va, but here in FLA, I wasn't sure what to expect. I mean.. we've got Whole Paycheck, did we Really need a TJ's?  We did, and this one is very nice! The parking? It's a problem, it could have been better, but the produce, the fresh flowers, and the specialty items are WONDERFUL!  Those are the things that I like to have "nearish". 

I like that TJ's employes friendly people, that the offer some very interesting products that you're not going to find in Publix or Winn Dixie, that they're going to be worth the trip when I make it down to South Tampa. Over all, I'm glad there's one close by!

Pretty good, all things considered. In two visits, we had:

Filet (very lovely)
French Onion Soup (pretty good)
Caprese Salad (deelish)
Spinach Salad (super yum)
Wedge Salad (he liked it!)
Peppercorn & Bernaise Sauces (Skip the Au Poivre, blah. Bernaise, tasty)
Daphanoise Potatoes (Salty, but good)
Garlic Mashed Potatoes (very good)

The complimentary bread basket is noteworthy! 2 different breads, 3 different spreads. I love butter. 

Service was exceptional, the price tag comparable to other upscale steakhouses. Everything is a la carte, which tacks into the cost quite a bit. Presentation is great, ambiance pretty darn cool.

Shells serves up some pretty good casual seafood at a good price. There is no atmosphere, you just come for something to eat. Service is usually fast and friendly and the food is consistently good. The seafood pasta is a specialty here.

I like dining here. This is no frills cafeteria style food. They do a nice job with the chicken and the pork. I always like yellow rice, black beans and plantains as sides. The Cuban bread is always nice as well.
Good treats and coffee at the register with a nice and efficient staff, good to stop in while you are perusing the Italian market.
Pretty good chocolates in a tiny store in the Italian market that also carries some Italian candies and gelato. Price is kinda high in my opinion but I enjoy the chocolate covered strawberries.

This stay was A LOT different! My room was different, the staff was different, even the pool area seemed very different. Amazing what a year will do to a property. Upon check-in, there were several staff members hanging around the desk but it seemed only one was working and during a busy check in time. It took about fifteen to twenty minutes before I made it to the counter however, the girl at the desk was so sweet and friendly. When I got to my room on the fifth floor, it had an almost musky scent...as if the carpet had been wet and dried incorrectly but the odor was faint and I only smelled it in certain areas of the room. The room also looked a little run down and especially the walls, and the tub/shower (which is usually a dead giveaway of being upgrade time). The beds  were very very comfortable and warm. This hotel has the strangest room service menu I've ever seen and there are no on site dining options available late night or overnight. That's weird to me since the hotel is located downtown where lots of businesses close early. We dined in the restaurant downstairs and had very mediocre food but the bartender was also a super nice guy. Food service downstairs ends at 11pm. I was in town overnight to work an event at Curtis Hickson park and was starving when I returned at 2am. I ended up having to wait for valet to bring my car and drive 5 miles to a Denny's for awful food when I would have preferred room service in my robe. Valet is mandatory with the hotel and the fee is $20 per night  (I think). The pool area looked a little run down and unkempt. They should really consider updating and especially in the common areas, exterior building, and pool area first. This was an ok stay but not as nice as the last stay where I had a room with a balcony which was a bit nicer as well. I know I come across as a diva or a hotel snob at times so I will say that the room wasn't dirty just a tad old but overall, it wasn't a bad stay.

If it weren't for animal cruelty, the fair would get five stars. 

First of all, the festival itself. I don't even know what to say (it was that cool)... this is an experience that should be experienced. 

From the moment you walk in the gates, you feel like you've stepped into another, very strange, world. I used to be a fan of the Maryland Renaissance Festival when I was younger and this is smaller yet still comparable. Great performers, vendors and attendees alike. This is a place where you can really be yourself (assuming you think you are actually a wizard, a fairy, a king or a wench) and not be judged. Pretty fun!

My favorite part, however, is the location-the fairgrounds area is BEAUTIFUL with gorgeous trees setting the backdrop for a really magical experience. And it's close to USF and my hood. Yay!

A negative, and why they get one star: One "amusement" in particular: elephant rides. Anyone who cares a bit about animals can understand why this is so wrong. Do they REALLY need this? What does it even have to do with the medieval ages? The 58 year old asian elephant forced to carry people around for money all day is named Judy (I asked) and has been working fairs and circuses since she was taken from her home in the early 1960s. Let that marinate a little. When I was born in the 80s, Judy had already been working for over 20 years and is still working today. They poke her with a stick that has a sharp metal point on it and when she's not making them money, she stands there swaying back and forth in some sort of messed up trance (a behavior not exhibited in the wild). The handlers reason for keeping Judy on the clock? "Elephants were one of the first animals to be domesticated." But watch the behavior of elephants in the wild, where Judy is from, and tell me that they are still meant to be a solitary sideshow attraction at county fairs their whole long life. You can google Judy and see that people have been concerned about her well-being for years, but here she is at the Tampa Bay Renaissance Festival for our enjoyment and her owner's profit. What a shame! They need to do better. As much as I loved the whole festival, if they support practices like this again next year I won't return.

My first time here, and it was awesome.  As I walked thru the forest getting my feet dirtier than hell whilst eating BBQ and smoked turkey legs, I wondered why I had never before been.  I guess it's because I never had a nerd like La Chica in my life, and a nerd she is.  She bought me a kilt from the Got Kilt tent, and I wore it the rest of the day.  Booya!

Can't wait for this event to come around again next year.  So much food and beer!

After reading all of the positive reviews on here, I took a break from my usual nail salon to try out Rittenhouse Manicure & Spa one Saturday afternoon. I was extremely disappointed as it was the worst manicure/pedicure that I have ever had anywhere ever. 

1. Despite making an appointment and arriving 5 minutes prior to my appointment time, they did not have a proper station set up for me upon arrival.

2. The biggest complaint however was the quality of the mani/pedi: this was the most harsh and painful mani/pedi I have ever had. 

While trimming/shaping my toes the woman CUT my big toe because she was moving so quickly and was so careless. To make it worse, she then tried to cover up the fact that she had cut me - however, a) I could feel the pain of a cut on my toe, and b) my toe started gushing blood as a result of her work and she had to then put stuff on my toe to stop the bleeding. Not only is this unsanitary, but she offered no apology or acknowledgment at having cut my toe while working. After this she kept working and ended up cutting another toe - two toes were now bleeding and open to possible infection as a result of her carelessness.

It still got worse, for while doing my manicure afterwards she proceeded to cut both my thumb and index finder while doing the shaping/trimming of my nails. Again, my fingers started spurting blood, I was in a great deal of pain as a result, and this time she smiled in "apology" but come on - I did not come to the nail salon to end up bruised and exposed to infection.

Further, I did not see her disinfect the tools or take them out of an autoclave prior to starting on me, hence as soon as I ran home I had to apply Neosporin and band aids to all of my cut fingers and toes - so now my new mani/pedi was covered up by multiple band aids... lovely, thanks Rittenhouse Manicure & Spa.

3. On top of all this, she did a terrible job applying nail polish and both my toes and fingers looked terrible. The nail polish was unevenly applied, on several nails she failed to apply polish tot the entire nail, and the color chipped a few days later.

While I understand that I may have had a bad manicurist and an unseasonably bad experience, I have never before had such a bad experience at ANY nail salon before and will never again go back to this salon.

From the good people that brought us Lee Roy Selmon's comes their newest concept, which previously only existed in Maryland, Virginia and West Virginia. Because this particular location actually used to be a Lee Roy Selmon's, it still looks and feels like one. The difference, however, is that Glory Days has less BBQ influence in the menu and more traditional sports bar/restaurant fare. Hellfire chicken is a signature item (either as sandwich or entree) and offers a good kick without overwhelming the dish and the Buffalo Chicken Mac 'N Cheese speaks for itself. While I do not eat seafood, there was a lot of buzz about the crab and shrimp pretzels. Also, there is a really good late night Happy Hour offering plenty of attractive specials. The vibe is very comfortable and I was very impressed that on a Saturday night where they were not yet open to the public, a manager was outside informing new customers that they would be welcome back anytime in the future by offering each car a $10 gift certificate to encourage them to do so. Considering how much I've always enjoyed Lee Roy Selmon's with friends or to watch a big sporting event, one visit to Glory Days tells me that I will enjoy it just as much here.

This place is amazing!!! The Tapas are MASSIVE!! 
Please do not, DO NOT, listen to the bad reviews!!! This place has great atmosphere, great service, great food, HUGE portions, great pricing, & high quality foods!!!
Service was awesome very personable! 
Salads are worth way more than $8 for (non iceberg) the portion size!! 
Delicious!! Just awesome!
Music, dancing, & a nice bar! Outdoor seating too!
Quiet little gem on Dale Mabry!

Its definitely pretty sketch compared to Lee lees, it isn't as clean as you would want a grocery store to be but its definitely better than what it was before. In comparison to Lee lee's it does have some items that at cheaper, but its definitely smaller and a bit more cluttered than Lee lee's I guess if you need Asian groceries and you aren't willing to drive to Lee lee's or GNL than grantstone will do.

I like the atmosphere of this bar, It's a cool place to hang out and the staff is very friendly. Great for groups specially if you like karaoke. Prices are reasonable and as a known bar its usually part o the many pub crawls that happened in Ybor.

Used to live down the street & decided to give this place to try, glad we did! Having a friend from Colombia, we have enjoyed some authentic colombian dishes and this place did not disappoint our expectations! My husband and I have been back several times, but I always get the same thing- why change your order when it's just SO good! I always get the fried steak, forget what it is in Spanish- comes with lime and grilled onions on top, with rice on the side. Amazinggggg. My husband tends to try something new every time and hasn't been disappointed yet. The service is always good, I wouldn't say its the best (hence the 4 stars) and not too expensive. Also, love the fried plantains & yogurt dip they serve as an appetizer. Definitely worth a visit!

This is a hidden gem in Tampa... a great place to find good Latin cuisine. The menu offers a big variety from several Latin American regions.  it was a pleasant surprise to find out that they had some really good hot sauce. The server warned us that hot sauce was hot so we ask for several more dishes of it. 

Great food. Great service. Don't let the exterior keep you from going inside.

We were greeted at the door and quickly seated. It was a busy Saturday afternoon. The atmosphere is full of sports decor. Many televisions so you won't miss a moment of whatever game you're watching. Each table has a radio that can tune into those games. I like  this idea because the guests aren't shouting over the sound of the game, it's contained to your table. The bar is fully loaded and they had many beers on tap. I loved that they had Abita on tap. We tried the Crab & Shrimp dip for an appetizer. So delicious. It came with a side of tortilla chips, which were just ok. They were definitely just bar tortilla chips. I would have expected at least a better chip to go along with the hearty dip. Our waitress on the key with refilling our drinks. We waited a little bit longer than usual for our food, but that was due to how busy it was. We ordered the Bacon Cheddar Burger and the Tangy Buffalo Wings. The wing sauce was tangy and yum! I definitely would like to try that sauce on a chicken sandwich. The only thing that was missing from the wings was the sauciness. The saucy extra fingerlicking experience was missing. The sauce was fried/baked (however they prep it) into the wing and that was it. Still delicious. The burger was perfect, a well seasoned burger. Very juicy and the toppings weren't lagging. Each bite was filled with a burst full of flavored. They could have added more fries. I can't wait to return to try more stuff from the menu!

I was very glad I came here. My front wheel was recently stolen and I wanted a cheap replacement wheel and was having such a hard time finding one. They were very honest here and had a wheel + tire that they sold me for 20$ (they were originally going to charge me 30$ but they marked it down because the tire was a little old, which was very nice of them!!). They were very honest and I will definitely come back to this place for replacement parts again.

Customer service - So nice! Right when you walk into the door, they ask if they can help you with anything. I've gone here a few times already and there is always someone around when you need help. I came here recently to get some supplies for making macarons. Found almost everything I needed except for powdered food coloring. What I loved is that he did direct me to a place that might have it. Most places wouldn't direct business elsewhere, but he was so kind to point me to where I might be able to find such things.

They have a large selection of items ranging from bakeware to cookbooks. Price is ok. Sometimes I feel there are better deals elsewhere, but if i'm in a rush to find something that is hard to find elsewhere except online, this is my go to place

A steep hill, but well worth the 360 views of the city. While it was pretty busy on a Monday night in April, we never felt overcrowded. We had a park a little farther down the street, but it wasn't too bad as you are going to work out, right? Bring water!
What a surprising restaurant , where the food I very good and the staff is customer friendly and very attentive. The surroundings make you feel you are back in the islands!
Love love ninja!! The moment you walk In till the time you leave, everyone is so attentive, welcoming and the sushi and sashimi are yummy!!!

Ate here twice while we are on vacation, so not bad.  

What I liked about shells is that it was exactly what I expected.  The first night we ordered a Cesar salad with shrimp, fried calamari, and the crab cake sliders.  The kids got chicken fingers and fries.  The salad was good and came with a ton of shrimp which was marinaded with a teriyaki sauce and grilled to perfection.  The fried goodies were fried goodies, hard to go wrong.

The french fries were delicious!

Second night we went for Lobster Tuesday, sooo happy we went back. The lobsters were cooked to perfection and we ate every last bit.  The accompanying sides of red potatoes and cole slaw were surprisingly good.

We also tried the key lime pie which hit the spot.

The service overall was average, nothing good, nothing bad.  The decor was what you'd expect, wooden tables with wooden benches.  It had a very nice vibe.  If you're in the area on a Tuesday, definitely go for the Lobster!

Already a giant fan of the main Bamboozle location on Tampa Street, 
I come here often for 3 reasons-

1- Amazing fresh rolls & pho
2- Easy to get to
3- There is a patio where they will serve you and it's directly across from the Washington Street Dog Park. That's where I bring our baby, Stewie.

Armed with dog treats, the Tea Lounge employees come on out and visit, take my order, refill water bowls for Stewie while we wait. 
I've gotten in the habit of trying the tea of the day and have not been disappointed, not once! Get the White Blueberry if youhave the opportunity. So good.
The food is always consistent and fresh- satisfied customer every single time.

I would like to come one day to sit and hang out inside- welcoming couches with games for customers to entertain themselves and chill out...oh yeah!

My favorite sushi place as of yet!

Been here twice now after the gym, and the place is VERY clean, fast service and cheap prices. For those who enjoy sushi I highly recommend eating here. The gf gets the Pad Thai which comes in a huge bowl (where she eats the other half the next day). 

This kicks butt on Sakura / Akira. Once you go to Ninja, you won't go back to those.

Awesome place, steak and atmosphere. Love this place. Cowboy ribeye rocks my world. You will love this place. Bar is a great area to hang out next door. I dont gamble but i love this place. Great place to bring a hot date ;-)

WOW! Walked in to be greeted like a family member at a party! A sincere smile, and offer of help even though he was helping someone else and had a line.
 The coffee choices are stupendous, and there is a whip cream dispensing machine!
 A very long fountain drink counter with a ton of choices, including a cherry or vanilla add in syrup. Mmmmm.......
 There is a wide variety if slurpee type drinks, and not one had an out of order sign, which is rare at other stores.
 Very nice variety of good stuffs, and clean.
 Def worth going a little out the way for.
 Another sincere smile from the cashier, some witty banter, and he even asked if there was anything else he could help with.

Best spot in Philly hands down. 
Hookah, lounge, Music, alcohol, dancing, smoking, body painting. I mean where else can you go to get a experience like this. Plus the bartenders are so Kool if the atmosphere of just everything that's going on does not win it for you. Trust the bar tenders will just because how skilled they are. Especially Raphael. He will make you one of the best drinks. Not to say the other bar tenders won't cuz they will he's just one Kool guy. Hands Down Best spot in Philly oh and plus they have different theme events going on ever week. Like come on I was a Tiger last time.

I'd started coming to Bella Prana on a recommendation from a friend who comes here multiple times a week. Knowing she doesn't live close meant that she enjoyed it that much!

The instructors are all very pleasant and patient with new students! The had a great special for $39 for unlimited for 30 days for new students so that's what I'm trying out right now. I've been to some Power classes and Flow (love their Flow 2 class on Sundays!) and haven't been disappointed yet! The full classes show me that the are a studio in the area that many enjoy coming to. 

If you come during a busy time, there is parking down the street at the end of Edison, so not to worry.

So I tried this location when I first came to
Tampa, and was severely disappointed.  Since then, we usually order from the one by Davis island for lunch allot during work, which is always fresh, always delicious.  Thinking I should give this location another try, I am yet again disappointed.  I ordered the beach club w/ avocado, which I practically choked on the dry hard bread, mixed with a minuscule amount of avocado & mayo on there, I couldn't even see it, let alone taste it.  And my meat? Two thinly sliced pieces of turkey folded on an 8" sub.  WTF?  I mean, would it kill you to give a little more?  Because everyone loves a lettuce sandwich, you sure didn't skimp me on that.   This place is freaky fast and freaky crappy.  I'm over it.
Good place to go with freinds good for kids prices r a n Lil high but good place to go and have fun but they do not give law enforcement discount

An old, well established and reputable Tampa diner. It's your traditional no-frills breakfast menu serving moist and mouthwatering pancakes. Their cheese omelette is tasty as well. All menu items are reasonably priced. You'll leave full and satisfied!

We love this bonefish!!  We have been to many all over and this location seems to be consistently the best. The service is always great and the food is even better.  Chad is such a great manager and always makes sure things are perfect. 

Their blue cheese stuffed martini is delicious , along with their ahi tuna appetizer and of course the famous bang bang shrimp.  Everything we have ever ordered has been great!  My hubby loves the tilapia and I enjoy their house salad a lot. 

If your looking for a good date night restaurant this is it.

This is walking distance from my office and while I was not as enamored of the Downtown location, I found this location very enjoyable.  My co-workers and I sat outside during lunch and enjoyed our surroundings.  I order two rolls: the tofu and ahi tuna.  They were both delicious and the sauces were the perfect complement.  The portions were just right for lunch and left me full while not overly stuffed.

The servers were very nice as well, so overall, a very lovely lunch!

I've gone to BARF three or four different years and it's been the same sort of experience each time. Park, wander in, meander in and out of shows/performances, maybe check out some of the vendors, get a sunburn, and go home. 

There are some acts that are a staple at BARF, but frankly it's sort of a bummer that those shows don't get updated much. It's like certain acts have been there so long they have some kind of tenure and don't have to keep it fresh and interesting. Then there are some acts which are newer, like the mermaid tank that first came around in 2013 I believe. This year I really enjoyed the insultor show, where the audience pays to have their friends/complete strangers insulted. It was a great concept and a nice addition for adult audiences.

The vendors seem to have many repeats every year. Some of the merchandise makes sense (chainmaille, jewelry, leather boots/footwear, drums, etc.) and some of it doesn't (Victorian corsets, Chinese belly dance hipscarves, clip-on tails, etc.). 

Food at BARF was never the selling point for me. A lot of it is junk food and none of it is really meal-worthy in my opinion, so I try to avoid eating there and stop for lunch before or dinner after. I have stopped at the one stand that sells fresh fruit before though, and it's the only thing I would eat a second time.

One of the more entertaining things for me here is people-watching. There are lots of patrons in some... interesting outfits. I just know it's always warm out, couple that with uneven ground and dust, I would rather be comfortable.

Stopped in here real quick to grab some spring rolls and some PHO-to-GO!

Service was great, and the meal came out super speedy.  The soup is the best I've had thus far since I began eating the stuff a few years ago.  I got the small combo bowl with beef and tripe.  I don't even like tripe, but it was damn good.  I plan on going back in the near future and trying some other stuff to do a more thorough review.

The wait for the shuttle getting longer since I have use them for past 8 years. One thing that they have been doing is taking the nearby hotel passenger from airport and prolong the the drop off time to the parking lot since they have drop them off first. I am disappointed with their current business model.

I have make a comment to their employee. And they not willing to admit the customer is right for the delay pick up and drop off. They are getting the shouting match with you. Their attitude from my last parking has turn me away. I am recommending other parking area.

If the pickup include the nearby hotel guest fill up the van, you have to wait for second van. Longer the wait time where they did not tell you.

So in town with my friends and getting the whole Ybor experience.   We came for the drag show. But this is a place to people watch for sure.  There is a mix of everyone here and it's an open place.   The bartenders were great and super friendly.   

I was disappointed that the drag show was short in comparison to others that I have seen.   Also there is no where to sit even on the sides.   All in all a fun place.  Music was great, drink prices were decent and there was no cover.

Not bad at all.  The aisles are clean and uncluttered, and they have a good amount of clerks working the registers.  

I go whenever I think of the need to go.  I could complain that their prices aren't as low as Wal-Mart, but then that would be me spitting in the face of the good service and lack of horrible people tearing up the joint...

This has some very good pho and the by far best tasting and freshest rolls I've ever had. I think the sauce that comes with it helps make it so good.  I've only been here twice so far but both times were on my lunch break at work and used a large portion of my lunch hour placing my order then waiting for the food. I guess that's the price you pay for fresh food- will be back again!

This place was so yummy,that I so wanted to bring my friends that are totally not versed on Latin American food let alone Venezuelan food. Unfortunately, the last time we stopped in, the place was filled to the rim w people.
The first time we visited, we had an arepa and a cachupa. The cachupa is better to me bc of the sweet and salty taste. The marinated steak kabobs were so flavorful and were very tender. 
My passion fruit juice was so so good. I wish I can have it now. I love how they have a huge tv screen with pictures of the food. That is so helpful. The prices r really good and not too pricey. The service is good and will help u with the menu. Don't be shy just ask. I totally will be back here very soon.

I have had Camille as my stylist for at least a year, so when she called to tell me that she had moved to Bieker (where I had previously had great facials), I was thrilled. I brought some new potential pix of hairstyles and together, we selected what my look would be. Coloring and short cut were amazing as usual... I will be sticking with her at Bieker!

Great laid back bar that is hidden gem on Davis Island. Very chill vibe with many TVs so a definitely great place to watch sports. 

I have heard their wings are great so I will definitely be back to try their food. My friend ordered their cheese fries which I had a small bite of. Fries were soggy and not so tasty - but good food to soak up alcohol. 

Jen, our bartender was a sweetheart and a joy to deal with. 

The bar has limited outdoor seating and is quite small so not the best place to bring a large group of people.

I did not even make it to the back office. They had overscheduled appointments, let other patients in before me (who arrived after me) and told me they were not sure if/when they could get me in. Not only that but the blonde girl who looked perhaps 20 yrs old in the front lobby was very rude and condescending. I have never been to a Dr or salon where someone talked to me the way she did. They need to take some empathy classes and customer service too! Also overheard the way she spoke to the 2 new employees she was training, who she said were only there one week and she was rude to them as well. I walked out after waiting 45 minutes after my appt time and due to hearing her haughty condescension talking down to everyone. And because of the nauseating smell of the citronella/lavendar candles. Certainly wouldnt trust their service if thats the way their introduction to patients is.

I like the fact that it's a bit dim inside. Really makes for a cool and comfortable shopping experience. Staff is friendly too. It's a medium sized store, which to me, makes things less overwhelming in that I don't have to walk through countless aisles to find what I'm looking for.

The Fresh Market is pricey (especially when compared to the competition) so I typically won't buy more than a handful of items or so, but I always look forward to my next trip here. Still, there are customers that buy everything they can here and more power to them. 

There are a vast assortment of teas, nuts, chocolates and so on. Oh yea, stop by the bakery and check out the OMG selection of items they have. If you like sweets, you'll find yourself attached to the bakery and frustrated because you don't know what out of the several options to go for.

I recently helped host a bridal shower here this past Sunday. The bride told me it would be a party of 20, but unfortunately only 8 of us were able to make it. The restaurant informed us that we would have to pay for the difference because they had prepared enough food for 20 guests (which is why they called to confirm the amount).

Me and another bridesmaid PANICKED for a second but tried to keep our cool infront of the bride. When we approached the manager how each of us should pay, she was SO KIND, and told us that they would charge us the regular price, which was SO INCREDIBLY AMAZING of her!

The food was fantastic, tea delicious (especially the cute square sugar cubes). I would definitely recommend having a cute girls afternoon out here!

been here 50-100  times. Mondays are a great time to shop at store after work, it's calmer. They were stocking, but, that is okay, I was still able to shop and find my items. Ithink this location is relatively well stocked and clean. I like the Starbucks within the target as I was thirsty afterwards and got an Iced Americano. It's nice to use Target card and still get 5% off purchases. I think the household items are a good deal. Grocery items prices haven risen since my last visit a couple months ago. Still think it's a reliable choice and you can find most anything you need.

Consistently good with the solid Bonefish menu. It's a go-to regular for us. Tuna entree, house salad, after starting with Bang Bang. But we never go in Wednesday for the BB special because of the wait. So all good, except - servers tend to disappear, usually right when we want the check. I think they spread them to thin or have them doing lots of duties. It looks like they have plenty staff so I don't know why. So, if you are in a hurry, let them know ahead of time. That's what I do now. It's still a great place. Nice wine list too.

I had fun checking out the Sunday market (the last of the season) and the property. It's a neat place with some cool plants and areas to explore, for example pineapple, sapote tree, papaya tree, rope swing sippy hole, some nice Florida jungle plants and flowers, a herb garden in planters, and lovely stands of produce, soaps, cheese and yogurt, eggs, seeds, bread, pizza, veggie burgers n smoothies, ice pops and music. It was a fun experience. 

The address given is unhelpful because it drops you on the wrong side of the farm where you can't park. You have to enter off Hanley and turn onto Comanche. 

Sweetwater definitely has a hippie vibe and everyone was very friendly and nice. There were some very positive vibes and some beautiful people trying to live right. Worth supporting. Jah mon.

Even though I personally know the comedians who put the show together I still stood in line along with my girlfriend. Earlier in the day a couple of my friends (current Tampa Buccaneers players) wanted to attend and had interests in possibly bringing in more acts and getting more teammates involved. When I made it to the door, I advised the doorman (short black bald headed guy) that I had more people coming. This sorry POS started being loud and disrespectful to me infront of my girlfriend. My first instinct was to punch him in his mouth but I thought about how it would ruin the night for all involved. As I stood there advising him that my other party had their tickets already and would be there soon, he yelled out that he would not let them in. It seemed the more I tried to explain to this buffoon, the more ignorant he got. Finally he allowed me inside but not before hurling one last inappropiate comment to and about my girlfriend. About 10 minutes later my friends (Bucs players) showed up and guess what...???? The buffoon again put himself on display with his rude unprovoked and unwanted comments. Even going as far as allowing one of the guys girlfriend in and saying to them "Yall can't go in." It seems as if this guy was auditioning for standup himself, but my advice to him would be to get on stage and not try to get discovered at the front door. Again I was so furious, but I maintained my composure and didn't put this clown to sleep. After the show and at a local restaurant, my friends and I all agreed that we will not be returning to the Tampa Improv as long as their staff treats patrons in such an idioc manner. I hate the fact that I, as a healthcare professional at several of our bay area hospitals will have to share my unpleasant experience with my coworkers as well as my 20K twitter followers. 

Tim Hampton

No complaints here. Mom and Pops style diner with good traditional breakfast. Restaurant was small so was worried it would be busy but was pretty dead when we walked in. The service was quick and the food came out hot and fast. I orderd the farmers omlet which had ham, bacon, sausage, and potatoes. It also came with a large portion hash browns that were just as good! I would definitely recommend this late night breakfast joint!

Not a fan of the pho chicken or decor. This Restaurant is way too casual / bare bones for a date. They don't offer table service.  You have to walk up to a register to order. But for $10+ per plate they can't bring it to the table?  Parking is limited too. Avoid!

I went up to the old Carrollwood Publix area to eat some Mexican food. I noticed on the other side of that little complex a boat load of people cheering,  drinking and laughing, so I decided to investigate. Upon entering the establishment it was wall to wall people, tv's and a line of keg beers that any solid beer drinker would die and go to heaven for.
I am not much of a beer drinker, so that did not hold my interest, but there was a seat at the bar and soccer on the tv, put that with a lively crowd and I am a happy camper.  It is quite confusing as the largest menu is all of the beer and the regular menu was appetizers, flatbread and sliders...show me the beef! 
Next to me someone left a newspaper and under it was a copied sheet of paper with the specials typed on it.
This place was swamped and even though I had been sitting there for 20 minutes none of the 4 servers had even acknowledged me. I was starving and desperate times call for desperate measures.  I put myself in my NYC mode and did the proper moves of hailing a cab at rush hour, without the whistling and profanity.  It worked! I ordered a glass of wine and a French onion burger.
What is it with all these bars now: 3,6,9 or 5 & 7 ounce pours of wine and they are all short an ounce, just pour me a damn glass of wine!
The burger and fries arrived in only about 10 minutes. A half pound fresh Angus hand formed patty on a brioche bun, guda cheese, caramelized onions, pickles, lettuce and tomato. Yup you got it no condiments,  which meant flagging down another server before my food got cold. I some how accomplished this feat and recieved s&p, mayo, regular catsup ( the menu said they made their own) and a wonderful sauce unlike I have ever experienced before.
I believe that the mystery sauce was a combination of Worcestershire sauce, Saracha and dark grainy mustard... sounds odd, but it was delicious and the fact that it was so different made it especially delightful,  it lit up my mouth.
The burger was nothing short of phenomenally spectacular.  The meat and accompaniments were nothing short of perfection.  This may have been the best burger I have ever eaten that I did not prepare myself.  Look out Square One.
Few reasons I can't give it a 5* rating. Though the fries were well cooked they were meager and skimpy. Finding the real menu was a daunting task and one I found by accident. No codiments delivered with meal or asked if I needed anything. Bun was cold and pedestrian, a warm toasted gourmet bun to match the burger, would have definately put this burger in the winner's circle. This place is used to being slammed with customers,  but it is no reason to ignore a patron you have passed 30 times and take a couples order thst just walked up over the lone patient customer. There is never an excuse for service staff to be short, ignore you, give you less than gracious service and all in all make you fell like your patronage is insignificant.  You think maybe it was because I was a female by myself and the erroneous myth that we are poor tippers? I would drop it another star if the burger hadn't been so damn great.
Their staff definately needs a course in customer satisfaction and interaction.
What's a girl to do? If you love the food you may have to endure offensive staff or continue on in your quest to find a better burger.  The little things add up to gourmet or just great.

This is more like visiting someone's cabin than being in a Tampa Bay Area restaurant. There is plenty of comfort to go with the comfort food being served here. Employees talk to you like friends and seem to really enjoy their jobs, whether they are on the floor or at the drive-thru window. My food has always been very good and consistent in its flavor. Usually, I go inside to order take-out just because I'd rather get out of my car for a few minutes and it doesn't take very long for them to have it ready. While I don't eat them often, ribs here are always on my radar and not a messy dining experience at all. Sandwiches are always very filling also and do not ignore the entree salad options either. Oh, those hush puppies always hit the spot too.
Wonderful 19th Century estate garden, nestled in the middle of Southwest Philly.

Honestly. I wasn't that impressed. It was definitely a good time with the unlimited wine and the live accordion player but the food wasn't to die for. Also if you didn't drink a ton you definitely won't get your money's worth. Dessert left much to be desired.  I don't feel the need to rave about this place like everyone else does.

The restaurant is located at the plaza was plan to be the china town 20 years ago. However the china town was not happen in tampa. It came close to the planing. 

The restaurant sales Chinese BBQ food such as pork, duck, crispy pork. You can buy it by the pound. This restaurant was here before along the Ding Ho oriental grocery store. 

The best value is the beef noodle soup. The beef is stir fry and then add to the soup. The soup broth is chicken and bone base. The noodle is the Hong Kong egg noodle. The are thin and great to chew. Compare to Mc Donald. I will pick the value ($5.99) of this noodle soup anytime. It is more nutrition and fun to eat. 

They also serve traditional Chinese dishes. If you like fried rice try their dry scallop fried rice. It stir fried with dried scallop and slice asparagus. The other dish are the beef with Chinese broccoli with beef in hoisin source also a good dish. This dish is more to American style dish but still kept the Chinese cuisine technic.

The surprise dish is the Taro Crispy Duck. The duck is deboned and marinated. The duck meat then lay on the bottom and top paste with ground taro. They are deep fried and chop into pieces. The accommodate with sweet soy sauce and garlic and scallion. They are crispy on top and bottom. The top is a crispy taro create the net type crispy good. The bottom is duck create slightly burn crispy. This is a killer dish.

La Chica and I decided to have a Cuban bread battle... A "Batalla de Pan Cubano" if you will.  We hit up Casino, La Segunda and then Mauricio Faedo's and grabbed one loaf from each.  Then, we headed back to la casa, armed ourselves with nothing more than bread knife and some mantequilla.  La Chica's mom saw what was going on, and she couldn't resist; she wanted in on the action.  One slice each from all three bakeries, and the tasting began.  The results were stunning to me because I grew up on La Segunda's bread.  It's what The Green Store (Interbay Meat Market) and the Commissary (MacDill AFB) both carried, so I thought for sure, I'd like their's the best.  But this wouldn't be the case, because in the 90's I discovered Faedo's.  From the first time I walked into their massive, well-located bakery, I fell in love.  I SWORE they had the best Cuban bread.  For almost 20 years, I have been stopping by Faedo's whenever I had the chance, even taking loafs to other cities in the state to friends who aren't lucky enough to have Cuban bread in their back yard.  So mentally, I thought Mauricio Faedo was going to win the battle.  But this wasn't the case either.  It's really hard to put familiarity, nostalgia and personal bias aside, but when I did, I found that Casino came out of the battle untattered.  La Chica and her mother agreed with my findings, but for them it was easier with no emotions attached.

Location-wise, Faedo's makes it really easy for Tampanians to get their Cuban bread.  And just like Casino, they're a cash only business.  However, they do provide unprepared customers with a safe ATM located right inside the front door.

+ for Location
- for Taste
- for Cash only
+ for providing an ATM

Really, I don't know it's because I come from Cali where there is good Mexican food, but I am surprised this place can get to three stars.  The fajitas were terrible, very low quality beef.   The Chimichanga is supposed to be crispy, hence the different name.  My Chimichanga was soft, like it sat in sauce too long.  The cheese sauce was good, but it was like a cafeteria cheese sauce, which I happen to like.  The salsa was way too tomatoey and limey.  Sorry Vallartas, you need to step it up.

Last weekend we stopped in here as my friend knew my love of Latin food. I'm sorry , but it was new to me and you can never go wrong with Boliche.  I had the Boliche,  fried plantains,  black beans and rice. Wow another great Latin restaurant in Tampa and not on Latin row.
This little jewel is hidden away in the nieghborhood of Northdale, north of Carrollwood. 
My Boliche was fork tender, delicious flavors on everything and it was a great dinner for very little cash. I sure was a happy camper as I was right in my comfort zone.
My friend was not thst familiar with Latin cuisine and opted for the Cuban sandwich.  The sandwich was respectable, but by Tampa's high standards it was average.
We each nibbled on the others dishes to check them out. My friend was floored by the Boliche and wanted to know why I did not say that it was delicious pot roast. I explained I couldn't lump it in with pot roast, not sure he understood, but he loved the dish. With that being said, he was not ready to try the black beans or plantains,  LOL.
It was an enjoyable meal and evening at an off the beaten track eatery.
What's a girl to do? Eat what she enjoys and enjoy the company she is in.

I live right around the corner and just like the idea of going out in the neighbourhood on a weeknight and getting (gasp ) one drink , but the bartender gave me the stinkeye for closing my tab after one drink and told
Me "just for next time, the bar has a $10 minimum for credit cards" which I've never heard at ANY bar. Sorry I'm not an alcoholic and I don't want more than one drink on a weeknight...

Dissapointing.  Have visited four times in the past two months.  Their specials are a good deal early in rhe week.  Each time I have been the food has gotten worse.   My latest visit resulted in a hamburger way undercooked and sitting on a wet bun.  TERRIBLE for a $10 burger.  This is surprising for a franchise brought to Florida by the Outback founders. Service and the sports on TV are good, but this place will be a drink and nachos (excellent) stop for games only.
Advertise that you have boba drinks but don't have tapioca balls bc and I quote "you have to make them fresh" what the?? Never going here again!

Another great addition to Grand Central/ Channelside area. The restaurant opened on Monday and had their official family and friends opening on Thu night and the place could not any more crowded. They offered a selection of drinks for an hour for free but if you decided not to stand in line and ordered a drink the price was very reasonable. The interior gives you a feeling of being in a big wood cabin, there is an outside patio which adds to the pet friendly environment of the area. As for the food it has your typical sports bar menu flat breads, burgers, wraps," and salads. I had the chicken wrap and even though it was not what I ordered (it was extremely loud and the server could not hear me) it was still good. There was live music on Thursday which made carrying a conversation fairly difficult. So far all the comments have been positive, the staff is friendly and on e the "new" effect goes away I think this place will be great hangout and sports watching venue.
It's okay...  nearby the convention center.     If you have time, go to the orginal location in Yorba.   More food choices and more history.

Pricy restaurant but is worthies for it name. When you walk into the restaurant, you can see their aged beef display at the restaurant front. Impressive. Most of time you will see the worker prepare the lamb chop in front of the aged beef. They shave off the unwanted fat from the ribs bone and ready for grilling. 

Someone may ask me why steak predominate restaurant and eat the sea food. This restaurant may be the only one serve the African lobster. The price $65 provide two big tail and you can share one with your mate. This lobster tail is will preserve and fresh to deliver from Arica. The tail immediately came off from the shell and easy to eat. We also order the bearing sea king crab. The preparation is great. We enjoy our meal. Although it is expensive but is worth the money.  

The only thing downside is not about the restaurant. Due to the restaurant locate in the casino, the gambler usually smoke in the casino. You have to pass through this area to get to the restaurant. The service is good. We always like to see our neighbor what they have ordered when we are in the high class restaurant. 

The high roller or the winner is usually treat themselves a big steak. May be I should try them on my next visit.

This is the first and only yoga studio I've ever frequented. As an active exerciser with a broken ankle, my doctor told me I was allowed to do Pilates and yoga. So my relationship with Bella Prana began. There are a variety of different styles taught and info is available on their website and on MindBody.com about classes and instructors. I stuck to yoga 101/beginner's yoga, gentle yoga, hatha yoga, Shelley yoga, and stress relief yoga.

Bella Prana is a lovely, clean, calming place. There are lots of class options. I started with the new client special for 1 month unlimited class at $39. I tried as many classes and instructors as I could during that time. The studio has two classrooms. When you walk in, you sign in at the front desk on the class sheet that you are attending and then make your way into the classroom. The person at the front desk is always very helpful if you have questions, it's your first time, etc. They have water available for purchase and a water cooler. There are also two large, clean bathrooms with showers. Each classroom has yoga accessories that you may need during your class including yoga blocks, mats, yoga straps, eye pillows, bolster pillows,  and blankets. Each instructor varies what you may need but you always find out when you walk into class. Every instructor teaches a little differently. I didn't find any that I didn't like. One started getting a little "God-y"  for my taste but that's a personal preference. Carla sings in her classes and its amazing and soothing. Shelley remembers your name if you've been a couple of times and she likes to greet participants with a hug. Everyone who I met has great energy and is soothing, comforting, and relaxing in the best way possible. Nobody forces you into a position. There may be some light touch but it is never to injure. There is an atmosphere of "do what your body allows today" ;  "don't compare yourself to others" both of which I really dig. 

Overall, a great studio. I continued on when my month was over and intend to continue taking classes. I love the way I feel during and after.

Not bad as a renovation for Lee Roy Selmon's, but it's different in that Glory Days Grill is more of a upper class sports bar that goes outside the box and yet has better prices than Buffalo Wild Wings. 

It's also a very family friendly atmosphere, but a good place to watch any sporting event, from the Rays games to the World Cup. There are daily specials, including two Happy Hours and fun interactive games that include pass the ball game at the bar (go and try for yourself). 

Bottom line: it's a sport bar with class.

Earth Cup, now called Coffee Market is a great local coffee shop in the old Sam's Place building. On cooler summer days the deck next to the building is a great place to read, people watch, or ponder why they are stuffing square windows into the Dorian frames as they rehab the abandoned apartment building across the street. For brewed coffee, the "whoop ass" is great, a solid not too bitter dark roast. For take home, I usually go with the Ethiopian. The bagels are pretty good, the pesto cream cheese even better.

I had heard spotty things from friends about the salon but I do understand everyone's hair is different so I gave it a shot. I also fell for my old self delusional tendency to assume that if a salon is pricey it must be good. That having been said, I went to Forgione herself for blonde color and emerged with stripy orange hair with random brown uncolored areas about which I received far too many frankly negative comments from friends. The best comment was: 'Go back, make them fix it and don't even bring your wallet.' I called and went back in for round two. She (Forgione) gave it another shot that marginally improved things but not enough that I thought it looked 'good'; so I accepted that my hair must be just very difficult to get right and we'd keep at it over time. That was until I got to the register and was told I owed more than $100 (130 I think it was--and she told me she was giving me a break on that). I was so taken aback, and too embarrassed to haggle or complain in a small space where everyone could hear. So I stupidly paid and have resented it ever since.

I don't understand how any professional of any sort can fail to take the kind of pride in their work that drives them to produce work to a high standard. Yet this is what Forgione did not once but twice--and charged me for the privilege of trying and failing to get it right that second time. Just too bizarre to me. 

She also recommended and sold to me a shampoo she said was sulfate free and which I needed to use to protect my color. I recently looked at the bottle in the shower and noticed it does indeed have sulfate  and nowhere on the bottle does it claim not to. 

By the way, all in I paid nearly 500 between both visits, tip and the wrong products.
I'm still bitter, obviously.

Go elsewhere.
I will say the drive was worth it. The place is a little easy to miss, and I suppose at peak business hours, this place would be hard to get to, but I will say the pho is worth the trouble.

BBB with a 20% off coupon is hard to resist or beat. 

Best place to shop for your bedding and household needs. The place smells good and the staff is super helpful.

It was time to replace my tempur-pedic pillow and this was the best place to go to .

Tonite I rest my head on a new tempur-pedic   Pillow designed for a side sleeper. 

Can't wait to put my head down on my new queen size pillow.

Thanks BBB

Maybe I got lucky but after flying home from Washington, I had the fastest exit from the airport I ever have. 

The car was waiting for us when we arrived and we were on 275 just 20 min after landing. 

Thank you A-1!

Mexican buffet. Period. 

My friend and I have been frequenting this Mexican spot for many years. We do it for the buffet. Oh, the buffet. So much cheese. So much food. It never disappoints. 

If I had to narrow it down even further, I'd say I come here for the white, milky cheese sauce. I've been to many Mexican spots, and nothing comes quite close to this cholesterol-laden gem.

The lunch buffet is worth checking out. The menu food is, honestly, nothing special, and it's about on par with other "authentic Mexican" places in the Bay Area (La Hacienda, El Vallarta on Anderson, and Guadalajara off Hillsborough to name a few). But a Mexican restaurant with a lunch buffet? Uh, yes please. 

In summary, their food is of mediocre quality but definitely flavorful. Service has always been cordial, and all servers will definitely check on you (not just "yours"). At least, this had been my experience. Then again, I've been coming here for years... mostly for the cheese sauce, like I said.

(A more specific rating on a scale of 1-5 would be around a 3.7.)

I got a call from the owner of the Improv, he was very apologetic and assured me that my next visit will be better. The jerk I had a problem with worked for a subcontracted promoter, and as a result of my post, was fired. I am glad this was resolved because I do actually like attending shows at the Improv and their wait staff are great and food is delicious!

Their subs are great.  Especially love the #5 Vito. Bread is fresh and tasty, much better than subway.  They're quite speedy too (which is the whole motto -- "freaky fast").  However... for a place that advertises for freaky fast delivery, I was really annoyed that they refuse to deliver to my home which is 1.48 miles away from their shop location.  They deliver to my workplace which is 1.6 miles away from their shop location.  Doesn't make sense at all!  I live in a low traffic quiet residential area with tons of street parking (absolutely no cars parked in front of my house).  They need to standardize their delivery policy.

**EDIT, I felt bad taking off too many stars for the delivery.  I added a star to reflect more on their food rather than their delivery.

So, they have champagne sangria and it is on the water.  That alone will make me like this place.  That said - the food is delicious too!

Stopped in on a Saturday afternoon with some friends and had the champs sangria and some appys.  We went for the Calamari and the Queso Fundido.  Neither disappointed!  The server will actually make the sangria at your table, which is kind of a fun touch.  I like this place because you can get Columbia food in a very relaxed atmosphere.  Over all - I'm a fan and will definitely be back!

Very disappointed in the limited menu and the service.  

Went here for dinner last night and won't ever be coming back.  

The Bad:  The tofu cafe salad was tasteless.  Slow service and bringing out the wrong drink order.   I ordered jasmine hot tea, it took way too long to bring out (after I was long done with my meal) and instead of getting hot tea, I got iced tea instead,  I didn't mention anything at the time because i didn't want to wait another 20 minutes to get the get hot tea.   

My friend ordered a smoothie, it took 30 minutes to bring out, well after we were done with our meal and sitting around waiting on the smoothie so we can leave and never come back.  There was only 1 other table in the restaurant, I counted 3 employees.   but from what I can see only 1 guy was actually doing all the work at a slow, carefree pace  - the guy who took the order also answered phone calls, cleared the tables, made the drinks, not sure if he also made the food too

Also it was noticed that the customer who came after us to place a takeout order got her food and smoothie drink before us! 

The Good:  The Chicken banh mi (i.e chicken sub) was decent tasting, good portion size.  Another friend had the Veggie roll, she liked it but not enough to come back.     

Don't come here expecting a variety of Vietnamese food.  The menu consist of pho, spring rolls, banh mi (i.e subs) and salads.  This is also a very poor excuse for a "tea lounge", very limited selection of teas and the tea that I got just wasn't good.

This is a great bike shop.  Geared towards cyclists of all types.  I bought my bike there and have since gotten tune ups and flats fixed.  I've always been happy with their service.  Staff are very helpful.

First, the programs aren't cheap, but they work. They have several different options for weight loss depending on your body type and weight loss goals. When I first started going there a few months back the staff was really nice and accommodating and I had no issues. But recently some of the gals have been a bit snippy. Not bad enough to make stop going, but it's been noticeable. I feel that maybe they are under new management or something because the wait times have also increased a bit. Overall, it is a nice facility, the programs work very good and depending on when you go the customer service is decent.

Only two food trucks really get my attention in the Tampa Bay Area: Taco Bus and Burger Culture. Multiple visits to both and never a bad experience personally or bad review from anyone I take with me. Specific to Burger Culture, their menu is very creative and offers a real nice variety of options in both burgers and grilled cheese sandwiches. They also know how to promote their business as they seem active in getting the truck out somewhere at least once per day and regularly update those destinations on social media. Looking forward to their announcement of a permanent home as well (just like Taco Bus), so I don't have to chase them down at Cigar City every 2-3 weeks.

I've had my last meal from Fresh Market of Carrollwood.....

A long time ago they had good food albeit pricey. But today I got severe food poisoning within 30 minutes of eating their food. Still very ill and typing from my toilet. From Google...I suspect staphylococcal food poisoning because of my symptoms and the fact that it came on so fast. Comes from mishandling food (not wearing gloves) most likely. We were out today and didn't want to cook when we got home so stopped into Fresh Market and got a rotisserie chicken, olives and salad from the cold case. We only ate their food, so it is the culprit. I think it was the olives because I am the only one who likes olives. My husband and daughter only ate the chicken and salad. 

Last time we ate their food we got burgers that smelled like gasoline....come to find out sometimes hexane is used in ground meat to lower the fat content.  It's dirty pool.  

Also have gotten rancid almonds and expired chips in the past.  So they are using the nastiest and out of date food now and getting the highest prices. Won't be going back!

The bar at 26th and Pine had closed and gone dark. The old "Ladies Entrance" sign was finally consigned to storage. The lights came back on -can it be--four years ago now? Jezabel brought Gavin's Café (the name refers to a neighbor family who had links to the old establishment) to life with a gorgeous renovation. So my feelings for Gavin's start with gratitude for the transformation. The old bar is still there, magnificent with its original maker's plaque still on it. But now the light-filled, rejuvenated room welcomes all comers. The icy mint lemonade is a signature treat. You can order ice cream at the side window. I love the Argentine specialties--empanandas and maecenitas. Salads and sandwiches, assembled while you wait, are superb. Try the quiche! I enjoy how Jezabel keeps up with the latest technology, too. So the cafe has a lively online presence and I can keep track of doings there with Facebook, and even a Youtube video

Still amazes me they built this place out of a Boston Market. Maybe that's why I always sit outdoors at the bar because being inside seems cramped and gives me flashbacks to chicken, two sides and a piece of cornbread.

Never fails, I see Chris behind the bar all the time and he's an employee who really makes customers feel at home. This guy never recommends something bad from the menu, always gets the pizza order accurate and is quick to notice when a glass is running on empty. 

Their pizza certainly has a good taste to it, and I've always liked any brand of pie where the sauce's flavor is stronger than the cheese and the crust is well-done instead of soft and chewy. 

While ordering ANYTHING but pizza at a place like this is like ordering chicken at a good steakhouse, their roast beef sandwich is the most underrated item on the menu. I know they get attention for the way they cook and serve their wings, but this particular roast beef sandwich is like something you would expect at Wright's up the street.

Drink specials are available if you know which nights to go and they help offset the higher cost of pizza here. 

Honestly, the pizza will run you 2-3 times what it costs at the major delivery/takeout chains, but it is a restaurant (not a kitchen with a lobby) and the good taste and service from Chris is worth regular visits.

We went for lunch on a Monday and all opted for the buffet.   Red Chile is my standard test for Mexican restaurants and Rigo's did very well on that test.  Chilequiles were also good as was the rest of the buffet.  The staff were all friendly.  

Good food, good value.

I met a friend here for a quick drink tonight and I haven't been to a Beef's in years. We chose this bar more for convenience of location than anything else. 

I ordered a Apple Cider Martini, which I must admit was very good! They also introduce a brand new IPA every Monday which is priced at only $2! So, my friend was quite happy with that! 

Our server's name was Joy and bless her heart, it was her first night. Well, Joy was a "joy" to deal with (oh come on...you knew that it was coming haha) and did great for her first night. Sam, the bartender, would also check on us frequently, as well. So needless to say, the service was outstanding. 

This review is solely based on drinks because we did not eat. I will however be back because I have a $50 gift certificate I need to spend! I will definitely update my review then!

Simple atmosphere.  Clean looking and clean feel.  Nice simple menu which also offers a 3 course option.  Service was good.  The Sea Crab soup was unbelievably good.  The flank steak was fabulous and the cobbler desert was terrible.  Cobbler was all dough and very tough.  Really needed a steak knife to get through it.  As a trio the price was reasonable.  To order everything al a cart would be expensive.  The variety of alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks are nice.  Some refreshing options.  The coffee was fresh and good.

First of all, I credit the management of this location for their comments during the grand opening that parking would get easier as time progressed. 

While the lot is still small, I have had absolutely no issues finding a parking spot the last five to six times I've been here over the last few months. 

The only other Trader Joe's I have visited was Gainesville and this one seems smaller than that store, but I know that the concept stresses smaller being better as opposed to the major chains of super markets.

95% of the time, I find everything I am looking for (The infamous Two Buck Chuck wine, reduced fat guacamole, great cereals) and continue to find new items to try (Beef Pho, Wine Country Chicken Salad, Girasoli Ricotta & Lemon Zest pasta) each subsequent visit. 

The employees seem to always be happy and very ready to help you find something or get you out the door. 

One major red flag is the Trader Joe's brand beer. I've had my share of bad beer over the span of my adult years, but their Name Tag Lager just might be the worst in the history of mankind.

Purchased a truck from them this weekend.  We were in and out within 45 minutes, the gentlemen we worked with was great, helpful and kind.  We love our truck and so glad that we stopped in while we were searching.

In the past I would have given this place 3 stars. Everyone is friendly. The place is clean. The food ok!  This time around...the fish was over cooked and flavor less!  Did not help to watch the cooks drop food and try to save the food on the plate with the bare hand that was not clean and re-plate it. Not sure who that plate went to but it upset people at the table.

Quickly becoming one of my favorite places. Good flavored food. Enjoyed the prime rib steak. Great taste. Surprised me!  Did not like what it came with but they do substitute. Always good to know!  Steady service. But the real reason I am enjoying going there, is the ice cream.  Love the choice and taste. Mmm.  I will just walk in and grab an ice cream and walk around. Nice!

If I was rating my four-hour experience buying my Prius at Desert Toyota back in 2012, this would be a one-star review.  The highlight was when the finance guy was reviewing my credit report and told me I ought to buy an extended warranty because "you can afford it and you shouldn't let your husband tell you what to do."  

If I was rating the service department based on the time that Toyota's road-side assistance driver broke my hybrid battery and the service department tried to charge me for it, this would also be a one-star review.

Today must have been my lucky day!  I was dreading taking my car in to get the recalls fixed, but the whole process was totally uneventful.  My service representative was courteous and prompt.  It took the service department less than an hour to replace whatever it was that needed to be replaced.  And - to top it off - they didn't try to upsell me anything, even though I have "UPSELL" stamped on my forehead.  

My only gripe - and it's pretty minor - is the fact that you have to speak with your service representative, personally, to set up the appointment rather than making it with a receptionist.  That means you have to leave a message and get a call back later in the day.

I guess it's easy to impress when expectations are sufficiently low!
This is a great lunch spot. It's reasonable and they give you a lot of food. Don't get me wrong, there's better out there but this place is good.

I went to Rigo's many times for their dinner buffet, and I absolutely love the variety of foods that they have, and at a cheap price as well. I end up eating way too much if I go, so I try to limit the number of times I allow myself to go there ;) If buffets aren't your thing, there are individual plates that you can order from the menu.

OMG , I have been to bakeries and bakeries.  The cuban bread here is always fresh and literally to die for! I have always loved their pastries and bread and I am not a bread person per se.
I have a party to attend with an Italian theme and I gladly took the garlic bread! With my special toppings on their cuban bread...LOL I am a guaranteed winner in taste, texture and flavor.
I am confident in my toppings and in the bread product I purchased.  Winner, winner Italian dinner!
What's a girl to do? Purchase the best ingredients to make her culinary treats be standouts among the crowd.

It is simple. Great service and good food. Someone had sesame crusted salmon with fried spinach. Enjoyed. Aged prime rib steak with fully loaded potato...very good. Solid and simple. Creme brûlée cheese cake...good!  Had not been here in a few years. Enjoyed it it this time around!

What we ordered: red snapper and churrasco

The churrasco was TINY. Everything was overpriced. The service was awful. We waited a long time for our drinks, for our food, for our check, and for our change. Unfortunately I would not recommend this place to anyone.
Great food. Not so great turnaround times. Food doesn't come out in the order of the orders being placed. Took 20 min to get my food.

El Perdigon is a very small Venezuelan restaurant.  When I say small, I mean like 7 tables.  It's in a tiny little strip mall next to Hank's BBQ on Dale Mabry. 

But don't let the size fool you.  It is absolutely delicious.  Venezuelans are known for their "arepas", round corn cakes that are grilled then opened and stuffed full of goodness, like beef, sausage, and chicken.  They're about $7 and enough to feed two people.  

The "cachapas" are sweet corn cakes, shaped like pancakes, and they can fill those with ham and cheese.  The difference with arepas is that arepas are white and not sweet, and also crispier. Either way you'll be tasting authentic Venezuelan cuisine at it's very best. 

They sell the best cheese sticks on the planet.  Called "tequeños", these sticks are wrapped in bread.  They are delicious. 

They also have a plate called "patacon", which is essentially a huge industrial sized plantain with your choice of meat, cheese and/or beans.  You can get it either ripe (sweet) or less ripe (saltier).  It's an amazing dish for plantain lovers. 

This restaurant is a true gem.  No chain food here.  Everything has a fresh home cooked taste, and the food presentation is great.  But it's small so careful going for dinner on weekends.  It gets packed.  Well worth the wait though.  It's as good as it gets.
Great rolls for hoagies, one of outer "go to" places when we are visiting the area. Tomato pie is perfect snack for long drive home, requires no cooler.

Ok...updating my review. I lurve Jenna! Yes, I spelled that right...well...at least in my world. Had the wings tonight and they were good! 

Jenna is a sweetheart! She is ALWAYS so warm and cordial when we come to see her. 

Love this bar and adding it to my seasoned social drinker weekly rotation.

AMAMZING.
A small restaurant but yet a nice atmosphere. 
It's was busy so that says all.
The food is really good. The ceviche is delicious, even the guacamole burger was good. 
The server was nice. Very attentive.
The prices are good. I expected for it to be pricier.
We were really impressed. We stopped in this place because originally we were going to go to the BBQ place next door but decided to see what this place was all about. I am so glad we did! We fully intend on returning!
Overall all-2 thumbs up!
Currently here for dinner. Boyfriend ordered the filet mignon which was super fatty so he sent it back. Meanwhile my food gets cold while we're waiting for his meal to be corrected :(

There were construction near the front entrance when I was there and did not give me a good first impression (or curb side appeal).  

However, the friendly staff changed my mind - from negative to positive.   This place was about 10-15 minute walk to the convention center.  One of the mornings as I was walking out of the door, the front desk staff voluntarily offered me umbrella without me asking (as they saw my convention badge).  They saved me from being drenched by these sudden Florida showers    

I had a room facing the water and enjoyed the view.  The room was spacious and well kept - and the house-keep staff did their job well.   

Highly recommended if you have business in the convention center and want a place with good value.

Menudo. Need I say more. Excellent. Love going there for that. Service is always friendly. Atmosphere is quaint. For example out table was not level. Everything was clean etc. Somehow we always get lucky and find a parking spot

"Big Pizza" seems to have a bad reputation with some people, but I absolutely never have an issue when I order with this particular store. 
Whether my order is placed online or via phone, they always get it right, fast and it tasted good.
Since I almost always pick my pizza up at the store, I also notice that people working here are always in motion and getting things done. 
Besides, the half-price promo code for when the Rays score six runs the previous day is always worthwhile.

I have a love/hate affair with the Florida State Fairgrounds. The location is pretty central to most of the Tampa area. If you don't get stuck in I-4 / 275 traffic, then it is relatively easy to get to. The Fairgrounds are rather large and capable of hosting a variety of events including the State Fair, numerous concerts and special events like Food Truck Rally's. 

My biggest gripe with the Fairgrounds is the parking situation. They often make you drive past numerous gates all the way to the back of the fairgrounds to the main entrance to get in. I understand that they are trying to keep traffic from backing up onto I-4, but sometimes it is unnecessary and definitely a pain. Traffic could be so much easier getting in if they would use multiple entrances. There are TONS of spots, but they can be so hard to get to. Often we end up parking a mile away from wherever we are going and walking past tons of empty parking spots.
