Another downside is the "parking spots" are basically free form parking in fields. When it rains, it gets muddy and gross. You also get drenched in rain and covered in mud trying to get to the covered amp if you are there for a concert. 

I love the Fairgrounds and spend a lot of time there, but it is definitely in need of some improvements...mainly in the parking area.
I LOVE this place. Great breads, great tomato pie at great prices! The owner is fantastic and super friendly.

Went for lunch.  I had Breaded Prime Pork Tenderloin $14.95 With sauteed spinach and fontina cheese in a mushroom marsala sauce, served with mixed greens salad.  Excellent.  There was a slight buttery taste to the dish, which was nice.  Nice and tender, nice flavor.  The green salad had a nice light dressing with parmesan cheese on it.  Very refreshing.  The person I was with had the grilled salmon special.  It was cooked perfectly.  There was a slight bitter taste, not sure if it was all the "tomatoes compote" that was on top that gave it the slight bitterness, but other than that it was cooked perfectly.  The service was great.  Everyone was friendly and helpful.  The room was a little noisy, but bright and warming.  Lots of parking...always a nice thing.

although this place has value. The customer shopping experience lacks big time here.  The waiting in line everywhere makes this a day trip.  I find that when I come here I  am waiting in a line to get into the store, waiting in a line to checkout and then waiting in another line to leave.  These lines are long and time consuming. I wish there was a Costco closer.  Sams club needs to up the customer experience, possibly higher more staff when lines our ridiculously long with bulk items and try to make a effort of expediting.

The subs are pretty tasty for the most part. I usually get a tuna and hold the cheese. I wish they had pickles in their subs. The pickle on the side is too big and not very flavorful I don't think. Their multi-grain bread makes you feel like you are being healthier. 

Their thinny or skinny chips are great. 

Great service and super fast.

I went there with a friend because I love trying tea rooms. It is just a few minutes from my house. I didn't think it was good. The food is unremarkable such that I can't even remember eating it. That is huge for me. The tea was just one cup. No one even asked if I wanted a refill. We sat there for 45 mins waking for check with nothing to drink. 

Go to the Royal Tea room on macdill. Make the drive down there. Worth every penny.

This is a good bar. Service was efficient, beer selection good. Good sports tv going on. I loved sitting outside at the bar and being able to enjoy a cigar. Not every where cares for cigar smokers, but here they keep a few cigars stock for purchase. I had my own so no idea on quality of sticks they had available. For me  being able to go to a regular bar and enjoy a cigar is a nice change from a cigar bar. The pretzels with the queso dip is fabulous.

Food was ok, service was odd.  Caesar salad with salmon.  The salmon was cooked nicely.  Nice crust and tender inside.  As for the atmosphere...very noisy.  Not a place to talk softly.  The disturbing part was the service.  My chair was dusted with bread crumbs.  I has to brush off the crumbs myself.  Then wash up.  When served a cappuccino, it was sliding on a saucer, as the waiter bumped into me trying to avoid spilling it all over the place, I nearly jumped out of the chair to avoid it.  The next cup was served with a piece of paper cut out from some old menu, to avoid sliding.  When clearing the table next to me they were sweeping the floor "wildly", moving all around etc.  All pretty odd I thought,.  Seemed like the staff was untrained.
Excellent restaurant with prefect surroundings.  They brew their beer and Tim is the master brewer.  Excellent concept and food.  I love the decorations.

The Double Decker was the first bar of the FSU Pub Crawl from this past weekend we chose to patronize because it wasn't too cray cray from the madness yet. As I walked in, my memories of Ybor City from twenty plus years ago all came rushing back to me. 

I used to come to this bar religiously almost every night when it was the Irish Pub. So much so, that I would hang out with some of the bartenders outside of the bar, on their personal time. 

It hasn't changed too much except now it is a British Pub and there is karaoke...ok maybe two major changes haha. One thing has remained the same, it is still a good time. 

From the friendly bartenders to the stiff drinks, the more things change, the more they stay the same. Flacco made us this fabulous drink called the "Skinny Blue Bitch" and it was delicious because it wasn't too sweet or could you taste the alcohol in it! 

I don't generally bar hop in Ybor too much, but I will definitely be back. Great bar and fun times!

I've been trying to grow my curls out for YEARS so just hate having to go to a salon, but I knew I needed my ends trimmed and I love DevaCurl products so I did a bit of research and found Barbara's. I went for the first time in March of 2013 and was given a Devacut by Peggy. I felt like she took maybe just a little bit more off than I wanted but overall she was great and I was happy.

My second visit was a NIGHTMARE. I went back in October of 2013 and Maggie took those scissors and just completely butchered my curls. I was in tears when I left there that day. I actually ended up going back a few weeks later and Peggy tried to fix it but even she admitted that Maggie had cut off WAY too much and there was really no fix but to wait for it to grow out. 

I ended up going to a Devachan salon in NY in March of 2014 begging for them to help me. They did what they could but it's really just going to take time. 

I was basically Maggie's experiment. If I ever went back to this salon, I would go back to Peggy. Peggy is the only reason why I am giving this salon a 2 star and not 1.
best cuban bread in all of Tampa. If your looking for that true authentic taste of Tampa stop in early and get you a loaf or two of something extra special you can only find in Tampa.
Good food, fresh, decent service, nice soups, decent coffee. As advertised. Nothing more to say.

Love the oyster Rockefeller.  Really enjoyed their short ribs that was on the August menu. Really enjoyed the goat cheese soufflé cake. Well not much I do not enjoy there. Oyster selection is usually good. Served with nice condiments.  Service is usually friendly and prompt. The food is usually fabulous. Fresh and tasty. Nice atmosphere. Parking is not always easy but not bad at all. One of my favorite places for sure.

Usually one of my favorite places. Always have escargot. Love their beef Wellington. Their soufflé is fabulous. But this last time around the service was terrible. Had to ask three times for escargot forks. Twice for soufflé forks. Etc. I hope that the service is not the new norm. Was a out off for sure. But the food was great

Nice flavors. Would give 3 1/2 stars.  Traditional Indian selections. None of the super fancy Indian dishes one might have tried in many other cities.  This particular trip tho we tried the Mixed Indian Tandori grill. That dish I would not recommend. 3 pieces of meat were too tough and dried out to eat.  The Tandori chicken was very good. We had a lamb spinach dish that was very good.  I particular love their hot tea selections. Fabulous!

Have been coming here for years (since the $1.50 days). They recently ruined a skirt that I only wore once. When I picked it up, it had black, tar-like fingerprints all over the front and the back. Had I not examined it before putting it in my car, the hourly employee would not have said anything, and they get themselves off the hook with a generic tag that says, "Sorry, but we've tried to remove the stains on your garment." As it was, I took the skirt back and complained to the hourly employee. Her answer was to send it back through the dry cleaning process, to try and remove the black fingerprint stains. This (sending it back through the dry cleaning process) was done two separate times, unfortunately for me- to no avail. 

Be warned, the owner is never there, and if you call with a complaint- you just reach one of the hourly employees, who will tell you to email the owner at his gmail address, at which time he will argue with you about his many years of business experience. Bottom line, they have no customer service skills, other than collecting money and processing orders. The most unfortunate thing for their customers is that they have no remedies in place for when they make a mistake. As other reviewers have stated, you obviously can save some money here vs. a full service dry cleaners, but in the end- the old adage "you get what you pay for" rings true... After the experience I had, I will not be back.

They remodeled and it looks really great now. I was here a couple weeks ago and they had paper streamers hanging from the roof which I thought was really tacky, that has been since removed and it looks much better.
Have to try there hot sausage with just sauce its out of this world. No one does hot sausage like Johnny Hots. Even there sausage, egg and cheese on long roll are good to.

This restaurant definitely needs a facelift. We wanted to try the $25 prefix meal, but they were out of the ravioli which was one of the two dishes offered. So we tried the $50. Lots of food that didn't taste good. Antipasto was okay, pasta trio had an odd after taste, and the bread was hard as a rock. We both order filet mignon medium cooked, and was surprised that it was served in medallion pieces, cook well, with marina sauce. It was chewy and flavorless. The soup and the chocolate cake were the only two dishes that tasted really good. If you just want to get wasted on unlimited wine and fill your belly, this is the place.

If you are looking for a used bike I would check out Firehouse bikes.  The people who work here are not overly friendly but they are helpful enough. They have a nice selection of bikes in the store and it's well organized.  They have a lot of loyal neighborhood customers and it seems like they do good work on their bikes. I bought a gently used road bike here and I am happy with it so far. I will let you know how the 30 day warranty works when I try it...
Great place!  Lots of great pastas to choose from.  The fresh cheese raviolis are amazing.  Been a customer for years and still love it.

I thought this place was alright. I came in with the wife and the mother in law recently during the week for dinner. We enter and MIL doesn't have an easy time up stairs, so I ask if we can sit on the first floor, and apparently we can't. So we scale the 25 stairs or so up the stairs - which is no problem for an able bodied person. If you or someone you are dining with has a problem with stairs - be warned. 
We go upstairs and it is decorated very elegantly. It is surprising because it is much nicer than the exterior or even the first floor had led me to believe.
We ordered a party platter for the three of us and an additional special roll and it was all pretty good. Most of the rolls did not have fresh fish, so it was tough to judge the quality of it. From what we did have, the tuna and salmon seemed OK, and the albacore was good. The cooked fish and seafood was very good, particularly the soft shell crab. They did put lettuce in a couple of the rolls, which I strongly dislike as I see it as cheap filler that does nothing to enhance the experience. So if you're like me and detest lettuce in sushi, make sure to order rolls without it and to the follow up with a question. Did not try their other food, so can't comment on that.
The service was actually very good. They do a good job of using a main waiter for each table and then they have a busser type of person that helps out. It's a very good system and effective for creating a pleasant dining experience. 
The prices were very reasonable although do not be misled by their large 3.99 lunch special sign outside. I was surprised that they offered such a deal. While I visited, I was there for dinner, but curious about the lunch special deal as it seemed incredible. The lunch special is vegetable fried rice. That's not a lunch, that's a side. They had other lunch specials that were more than 3.99 and also more than a side dish. 
I would not be opposed to coming back here. They had a bunch of special rolls that looked very interesting that I did not try. However, with so many sushi-japanese restaurants in the area that have already won my heart and are wheelchair accessible, I doubt I'll be back anytime soon.

Had dinner here tonight with my husband, mother-in-law, and her boyfriend. When we arrived we were offered a table and asked for a booth, the manager himself cleaned one for us. But it actually started out pretty bad, the service was slow. It took a while just to get drinks and an appetizer, we had finished our appetizer by the time we finally got the bread. We had the calamari, which was great by the way.

We finished the bread and were offered another loaf, but no indication that our food was coming out at that point. Our waiter explained there had been a quick meeting which had caused a delay. Okay, no problem. Then we finished a second loaf, somewhat reluctantly. At that point we were just hungry and had been waiting too long to get actual food. Other tables around us that had been seated way after us were more than halfway through their meals.

Eventually the manager, Joe, came out and explained that one our meals had been dropped on the floor accidentally and was being remade, which was causing the extreme delay. It was nice to be given a reason, at least we knew what to expect. Some time later, two of our entrees came out. No indication of the fallen entrée or my meal though. It was about 15 minutes between the first two entrees coming out and the second two. By that point I had eaten calamari, half of one of my MIL's tacos, two glasses of diet soda, a bunch of bread, and honestly couldn't even eat my food once it came out.

The manager came by again and informed us that the delay and the time differential in getting our meals was totally unacceptable, no excuses, and that everything would be on the house, which we truly appreciated.

That said, the food was outstanding. Like I said, I sampled my MIL's fish tacos and they were just fantastic. My husband had the pecan parmesan-crusted rainbow trout which was hands down the best meal of the night in my opinion, the potatoes au gratin on the side just put it over the top. As for me, I had the shrimp cocktail (ordered the appetizer as my entrée) which was kind of like a shrimp ceviche. I did take a couple of bites before packing it up and I'm looking forward to enjoying it tomorrow as it was delicious. The presentation is also really nice, in a frozen bowl made totally out of ice. It was on the local temporary menu, so definitely get it while you can if you like shrimp. I had also ordered a side of crab fried rice, the bite of that I had was fantastic too. As I mentioned, the calamari was great, probably the best I've had in Tampa.

My husband had what I believe was an orchid pineapple martini, which he said was great. My MIL's boyfriend had margaritas which he also enjoyed.

To be honest, everything was so good that we almost felt bad having the meal comped because of the service issues. We left a tip, of course, but still felt a little guilty. The difference between a good restaurant and a great restaurant, apart from the food of course, is how they handle these types of situations. Joe handled the situation like a true professional, offering a remedy immediately without making excuses. We really appreciated that, and we will be back again and again(can't wait to have the pecan parm rainbow trout myself!).

Just got back from an evening at Ulele.  Overall experience was excellent, and the food was superb.  Some areas of improvement....our Server did not introduce himself which I thought was odd.  I ordered an ice tea; when it came to the table it was cloudy, actually muddy in appearance and it was awful.  Server came back by and I asked for it to be replaced with just a glass of ice tea, the house ice tea was offered.  When our server returned he explained it was hibiscus and coconut, ughhhhh, why wasn't that explained in advance?  All I wanted wasa regular glass of iced tea.  Had to ask several times for Splenda and Silverware, several times our Server just appeared lost.  But he was nice and did a good job when we engaged him, versus the other way around.  Now for the food.  We sampled a bit of everything.  The fried okra and crab Mac n Cheese were our apps.  Both were yummy and we finished them both.  My dinner partner had two dirty martinis, a Patron  Oyster Shooter, and then a Margarita.  All were made exceptionally.  Our entrees consisted of the Flank Steak with Jimmychurra, Mashed Potatoes and popcorn, Sisters Salad, deconstructed Sea Food Pot Pie, and the Squash Au Gratin.  All was excellent, could have used more than two pieces of popcorn in the potato. The sides for the flank steak were rice pilaf and asparagus.  The Rice Pilaf was very dry and I did not eat it because of this.  The three asparagus spears were "so so."  There was some confusion when ordering as I thought the "side items" on the Menu were for you to choose to have with your entrees.  I was wrong, those are for sharing, and not meant to be used as a part of the entree.  I didn't push the subject but mentioned to our Server that I really wanted the Mashed Potatos and the Squash Au Gratin as my two sides.  He obliged without hesitation.  While I was eating, I mentioned to my Dining Partner that although we were consuming a lot of food, I was not getting that full feeling, and he said the same.  We were able to finish all our food and still had room for dessert.  I ordered the Key Lime Ice Cream, and my dining partner ordered the Chocolate Torte with Vanilla Ice Cream shipped in from Uganda?????  Interesting.......Our coffee came in clear glass glasses and was exceptional.   Our Server said it is brewed in Ybor City.  The Ice Cream was very rich, half the portion would have been more than enough.  The Clientele was a nice Eclectic mix of people.  Ulele will definitely succeed and I am looking forward to my return visit.

The food here is amazing!  With a party of 10  plus a baby we walked into this decent sized restaurant and were greeted right away and asked to wait and just a few moments later we were seated with some glasses of iced green teas. 

Now I was going to deduct a star because frankly the service was pretty slow.  But the waitress was nice and they were pretty busy for lunch hour with a party of 20 plus people that had arrived before us so I guess it can be expected. The food was amazing.  I will admit I am a bit if a vietnamese food snob.  Frankly if my mom can cook it I won't order it because who could make it better then mom?  But the chefs here are defiantly making me second guess myself.  Our waitress recommended since we had a good amount if people we get the family dinner which came with approximately 5 dishes.


App: pork and shrimp goi (salad) delicious we finished this dish in a matter of minutes  

Main dishes: 

Fish sweet and sour soup with fish kho very tasty (but my dad's is still the best I have ever eaten)  

Beef loc lac: we had to order a second plate this dish was soo good it came with these amazing crispy fried potatoes that were covered in the loc lac sauce (I will seriously be dreaming of this dish)

Veggie: some green veggie sautéed with garlic that was just ok nothing 

We even had fresh vietnamese fried dough!  We asked for some to go but was told they ran out.  At 12 noon -.- by the owner.  I should have asked the waitress 

All in all the service was lacking a little umph but the food more then made up for it.

The Gonzmart family has done it again. They have created a phenomenal dining experience that is sure to become another Tampa icon, just as their Columbia Restaurants. 

I was so excited to be invited by my friend and fellow Yelper, Dorene, a couple of weeks ago to accompany her to dine at Ulele during opening week!  I am a huge fan of the Columbia Restaurant Group and was counting down the days until this past Wednesday. 

Unfortunately, my iPhone's GPS was telling me that Ulele was located in a residential area! After almost losing it, I pulled up Ulele's website and used Google Maps. I, unfortunately arrived 20 mins late and very frazzled. My anxiety left me after dealing with the friendly valets and staff who welcomed me with open arms into the restaurant. 

Ulele is located off of the Hillsborough River, in Tampa Waterworks Park. From the moment I walked onto the property, I was amazed. Everything about this area was simply breathtaking from the scenery to the building itself!

Our server Allison was such a joy to deal with, very friendly and always smiling. I started with the Fresh From Florida Mojito. It was very refreshing and not too bitter because they have substituted the limes with oranges! 

We decided to do several small appetizers and sides instead of an entree so that we could have a sampling of several of the dishes. The food took a little bit longer than expected and Christina, the manager on duty, came to our table to apologize. The truth be told, we didn't even notice or mind at all because we were just chatting away since Dorene and I hadn't seen each other in a couple of months. 

Dorene is not a seafood eater and I am an atypical Asian who is not a huge fan of spicy food. On this night, however; I decided to think out of the box and let Dorene do the ordering. 

We started with the Blistered Peppers, which are Shishito Peppers blistered and tossed in sea salt, garlic, and Olive oil. I was a little apprehensive, but these were not too spicy at all! Also, I am a salt-aholic so I quite thoroughly enjoyed them.

We then had the Alligator Hush Puppies which were made of alligator, country ham, duck bacon, corn, jalapeño on a honey datil pepper sauce and served with a horseradish aioli. I had not eaten alligator in a few years, so I was excited to try these. The hush puppies tasted very good, but because there are so many ingredients and prominent flavors in them, I did not seem to taste too much of the alligator. 

Next, we had the Jalapeño Cheddar Grit Cake which was made of cast-iron seared yellow cheddar grits with jalapeño and cheese. This cake was delicious but definitely had a kick to it. 

Ahh...but our favorite was the Mac n Cheese made of four cheese Ulele light beer sauce, blended with seashell pasta topped by a Parmesan Romana crust. There was an option to add crab, but even without the crab, it was simple pure goodness. I cannot wait to come back and try it with the crab!

For my second drink at dinner, I ordered the Waterworks Sunset made with Fontana Pisco, John DeKuyper Elderflower Liquer, Florida Ruby Red Grapefruit Juice, fresh lime juice, and house-made grenadine. I should have known it would be too bitter for me because of the grapefruit and lime, but I love Elderflower! Allison was so kind to bring out another shot of grenadine for me and it was so delicious that I drank it quickly and forgot to take picture!

We then finished up with the Candied Bacon Maple Fried Ice Cream served with a cinnamon corn flake candied duck bacon crust, made with a Knob Creek creme anglaise and caramel. I am not normally a dessert person. I am also a person who doesn't like to combine sweet and salty flavors. Surprisingly, the ice cream tasted all sweet and I think it was due to the crust. I probably wouldn't order this again, but I would highly recommend it to you dessert fiends!

We were then given these beautiful commemorative coasters for the guests who dined during opening week! It is so beautiful that I cannot bear to place anything on it. 

After dinner, we explored the gorgeous building and were stopped by a group of people in the beer garden. Ironically, they were the engineers from the company next door who help design the Ulele Building! They were even so kind to buy us a beer and show us some salsa moves! 

Dorene and I both had the Ulele Light Beer. I wasn't a huge fan and had planned to order the Wedding Beer. Tim, the brewmaster, explained to us, that he felt it wasn't ready. I am hoping it will be ready soon because it sounds very tasty! 

After exploring the rooftop bar, the brewery, and the beer garden we walked around the Riverwalk. Everything was just gorgeous! 

I highly recommend Ulele. I have become so much of a huge fan that I have reservations for this weekend and also plan to hold an unofficial Yelp event here. I cannot wait to return again and again. Thank you to the Gonzmart family for creating another phenomenal restaurant.

Wow, Ulele is fantastic.  Such attention to detail in every aspect of decor, service and cuisine.   There have no freezers - everything is fresh delivered or made on premises.

Other than two handicap spaces, all parking is valet.  They use the fenced lots of Stetson University and an adjacent law firm.   The valet asks your name and checks it against the reservation list before he takes your car away. 

Our group started with appetizers and the
Blistered Peppers won the taste test.  They are not jalapeño peppers but are Shishito Peppers.   The okra fries were the next most popular.  

I ordered Juan's Snapper which was served over garlic spinach rice.  Fantastic.  The deconstructed Seafood Pot Pie was a hit with our group.  

We wanted to try the house-made ice cream (coconut) but no room so will save for return trip.  

Our server, Aaron, did an excellent job.  The manager asked how we were doing and Richard Gonzmart did the same.  

Really good evening, can't wait to return and explore more of the menu.

We loved eating at the Vallarta's in Wesley Chapel when we lived out that way, but this place was absolutely filthy. I'd never go again. There's messy because it's been a busy day, and then there's, this shit has been on the wall for months.

I'm in love with this restaurant! Finally a restaurant in Tampa that opens up to a beautiful park and water without the hustle and bustle around. The food was excellent from start to finish and so was the service. I can't wait to go back and try new things on the menu!

An incredibly lively atmosphere, awesome food and great service! No doubt it will be a tremendous success. The beer cheese crab mac, lobster cake and filet are incredible, just to name a few. It's worth going with a larger group to be able to sample as much of the menu as possible; everything sounds so good (and is so good) it's hard to decide. I have been looking forward to a return visit since the moment I left.

This is a cool restaurant.

I had been waiting for this place to open, and I was so excited when it finally did.  Everything is fantastic.  The atmosphere, the building right on the river, the staff, the food, the drinks.  This is going to be our place to take our out of town guests (after they've experienced Bern's) to show off Tampa.

We started out with the calamari and the tomato and onion salad.  Everything was seasoned perfectly.

For our entrees, I had the pompano, and my husband had the skirt steak.  His was clearly excellent as he cleaned his plate and didn't offer me a bite.  My pompano was huge, and the meat was flaky.  I liked the creamy sundried tomato sauce.

Desserts were awesome.  My husband said the house-made ice cream was the best he's ever had.  I had the key lime ice cream stack, and the meringue was perfect.  The ice cream was frozen a little too hard, but after it thawed, it was delightfully tart.

To drink, we tried the Florida mojito, the Water Works Sunset and the Pisco.  All were really good.  The wine list looked promising, but I was dying for cocktails that night.

The restaurant can be loud, especially by the bar, but if you are seated by the horse, you can have a conversation.

I'm giving this place 4 stars because of its uniqueness and the need for this type of establishment in Tampa. 

We arrived at 630 on a Sunday night and immediately checked in for your reservation. We waited about 10 minutes to be sat, which was great considering this place was hopping and quite busy. 

The menu is "floridian" and local. There was menu items with gator, cornbread, grits, pompano etc. 

We opted to place an order for the jalepeno cheddar quick bread muffins. There was 4 in the order and they were quite tasty and went will with our side salad. 

For our entree we ordered the pompano, the grouper and the flank steak. The winner was the flank steak, sadly the pompano wasn't all that great and I wouldn't order it again. The grouper looked pretty tasty. 

After dinner, our server walked us outside. What a lovely set up!  It's right on the water with a huge field. Big enough actually that you could host a private party on the grounds. There is also a Biergarten and a hidden roof top bar. So cool!  We took a mini tour of the brewery and had samplings of the wedding beer (get this!) and the pale ale. 

This is definitely a place to take out of towners and locals alike to give them a true taste of what Tampa has to offer. Well
Done, ulele!

Beautiful renovation in a location that is key to Downtown Tampa's development.  Jazz on speakers throughout helped set a great ambience.  Tim's tour of the brewery was awesome.  Our waiter was friendly and efficient.  Manager made us feel welcome by bringing an oven-warmed towel to our son who was cold.  Food was very good.  This restaurant is definitely not a one-star experience. Tampa is fortunate to have it and you can be certain that it will only improve over time.

This place was awful! We went to watch a game and the wait staff was terrible. They took forever to bring us our food and drinks. They cut off several members of our party when they had only 3 drinks in as long of period. They were very rude and a server even cursed at a few of our group members. They brought out extra food that was never ordered then tried to sneak it on to peoples bills. This is so unprofessional and unacceptable I know our entire group will never go back. The food awe terrible and the customer service was dismal at best.
Never had a problem with the place like other people describe. I live close by and have been using the store for years. Good staff and nice selection of items for a little store.

I attended a baby shower here this afternoon.  The decor was lovely.  The owner was not.  
I arrived early with my friend to help setup.  My friend made the reservations several months ago and was under the impression that they would provide a table for the gifts and a table for the treats brought for the event.  The owner was incredibly rude and told her that they don't give more than one table for any party because the extra table is to be used for business and that she was already losing business by having parties.  Unreal!
The owner did decide to go ahead and allow our party to have an extra table to sit the gifts on so they weren't all over the floor after lecturing us on how it was taking business away from her.
There were 3 baby showers going on at the same time. 
As far as I could see, there were no other guests.  

Note to the owner:  Do you have any idea how many potential "future guests" were attending those baby showers?  With your attitude you have lost my business completely.  Lose the 'tude and accommodate the guests you have rather than worrying about people that aren't even there.
great fast service. Egg based dishes are good. Don't get the pork roll. Smells and tastes terrible.

Two thumbs down for this Applebee's location.  In fact, I'll even throw in a middle finger up as well.

Here's the deal.  Me and La Chica eat at Applebee's all the time.  Pretty much every location, whichever is closest to us when we're itching to take advantage of the half price apps.  90% of the time, it's the location on Sheldon and Linebaugh.  Not only is it close to home, but they know a thing or two about customer service.  The general manager at the Hillsborough location should be re-trained by the Sheldon staff and or simply demoted to a kitchen prep job.

This past Sunday, we decided to hit up this location before heading to the Bucs game to get or guts full, so as to avoid $700 nacho and half a million dollar hot dogs in the stadium.  We know that you can only take advantage of the half price apps at the bar, however there were no seats at the bar.  Not a big deal because our regular Applebee's on Sheldon will extend the offer if you're sitting at a high top in the bar area IF there are no seats available at the bar.  Enter our waitress.  We ask her if we can take advantage of the deal.  She's unsure, so asks the manager.  She came back with the manager, Steve, and he advised us that we could not take advantage of the deal.  He went on and on stating company policy, which apparently in non-negotiable and unwavering.  He wnt on to explain how the "computer" will not allow half price apps to be rang up for a table other than a bar seat.  So I said, well I can sit at the one seat at the bar, order and then bring the food over to our table, and he said, "No. I'm sorry that's not allowed either."  At this point, I'm pissed off because I don't understand how this deal is first come, first serve at this location.  "So because they got here before us, they can get the deal and I can't?" I asked while pointing over at the bar, just 10 feet away from me.  He replied with, "Unfortunately."  I explained that if we couldn't get the deal, we would go somewhere else, and he just didn't seem to care.  So we bounced.

All I can hope for is that this location quietly goes out of business or that they learn how to get staff that knows when to make exceptions.

This is the best comedy club in the central area.  And they get great acts.

They offer a bar area that opens about 1 1/2 hours before a show and doors to the club open an hour before the show if you want dinner and good seats.

To save time with our group and not eat too early, we opted for dinner in the club.
We ordered appetizers and entrees.  The dip trio was pretty good - the best dip in it was the black bean dip, followed by the queso, the spinach/artichoke dip wasn't great.  The tuna tacos were actually pretty darn good.

The drinks on the other hand, were not good.  When you order a martini, after 3 of them (Vampire), you should be pretty well hammered and a drink prior to that and I was still standing and talking without issue.....they really skimp on the alcohol.  Stick with beer or wine.  For the prices you pay for the drinks, you are much better off with something real.

The MC was funny, the opening act was even funnier and the headliner was funny as well, although I felt the opening act was the best during our show.

There is a balcony too, so there are lots of seats available.  It's a fun place and will definitely return.

This chain has been growing in the Tampa Bay area but this was the first one in town I think. Its the only one relatively close to my house so its my usual location to go when I want a TropiChop. This is fast food cuban food. Since we have such great Cuban food in my area, I usually do not make the trek here very often. 

The specialty at the restaurant is the TropiChop which features rice, beans, vegetables and a choice of meal. You can also order the meat with sides in an entree. Although chicken is the highlight, the preparations vary offering a wide variety of options ranging from salads to sandwiches and of course the bowls.

The restaurant serves beer and has dessert on the menu. They also have a sauce bar that lets you customize your entrees to fit your taste. The cilantro garlic sauce is MY FAVE!

Check your mail because there are always coupons for this place. They also have a rewards program now that offers specials and discounts via the email.

Newly renovated sports bar/restaurant filled with giant tv's and a great atmosphere (former LeeRoy Selmons). Visited recently and sat at the bar....the service was fair even though it was quite busy, it could have been better...we had to wait a while for our drinks, and then had to ask again before finally receiving them. I spilled a glass a of wine all of over myself, my date, the table, and the floor and the staff was kind enough to provide me with a fresh glass, yellow caution tape and safety cones around me to warn other patrons of my clumsiness...it was all very cute. We had the wings and they were pretty good. I am not a heavy sports fan, but I also won another free drink for having the baseball when a touchdown (or home run?) was scored. Free drinks are always nice.

After a wedding and reception downtown, I decided to treat myself by staying downtown. The Sheraton is a nice hotel. You gotta watch for where to pull in to park or you will miss it.
The rooms are a fair size and everything was in good repair. I paid the extra money to have a river view. If you stay on the downtown side it can get noisy with the street noise.
Overlooking the Hillsborough river is serene and quite tranquil. I enjoyed my short respite, the bed was very comfy, staff could have been friendlier and the room could use some upgrades for the price. Overall it was an enjoyable experience. Parking is a joke and way overpriced.
What's a girl to do? Take a quick break and don't look back.

Oh Biscuits...at one time you were up there with the best. Now, your glory certainly has faded.
I must admit I wasn't in a great mood to start with. 
Jethro's was packed...Baja Cafe was packed...at this point I was ready to go through the drive thru at Mickey Deez, but I was almost booed out of the car. 
So Biscuits was my Mom's suggestion. 
I've been a couple times before and hadn't been impressed. But I gave in...knuckled under. A decent meal could change my mood and my mind. I mean it's just breakfast (the most important meal of the day). How bad could it be? 
Hey- here are the positives: Biscuits got us in with no wait and service was friendly. 

That's all I got in the positives.
Here are the bads (one of which I can't really fault the restaurant for, but what the heck...I'm gonna anyway)
We got sat at a booth that had about ten inches of clearance from table to booth back. I need at least a good foot and a half for gut comfort. But somebody with a bigger gut was seated behind us and before us and was able to scootch the booth to accommodate their girth. I gained a couple inches by pushing our table into my daughter's chest. It's okay, she's seven. She doesn't need that much room, but it still was like wearing tight Levi's for me. Not flattering, and yeah...a dirty reminder that I need to lose some lbs.
I ordered eggs over easy and corned beef hash, home fries, wheat toast and coffee. My daughter had chocolate chip pancakes. My mom...I couldn't say. She always ends up making a bacon sandwich with her toast and is happy with that. Oh, and she likes to steal the individual jellies and take them home in her old lady purse that weighs in like a kettle bell. That's her thing- jelly thief.

The over easy eggs were good. Hash canned and acceptable. Toast was not over, yet mysteriously dry and horrible. Home fries? Uh, if your home is low budget industrial and frozen, then I guess home fries are what they were. Spongy cubes of something that might be potato is probably more accurate. 
Long thinning black hair hidden under the inedible toast and "home fries"? It was probably free, because I didn't see it on our bill. And really, I was just happy it wasn't short and curly.
My daughter's pancakes looked good. Butter for a nose...whipped cream smile and eyes...hot off the griddle. So much promise. She always drowns em in syrup. So how could it taste bad? They did. Salty weird. I don't know what was wrong with them, but something wasn't right. Something syrup and decent service can't hide.
Biscuits, you can only ride "the other places are packed" ride for so long.

Today was a special day.

For the first time since I learned about Burger Culture, I did not have to chase their truck. 

It was parked right in the courtyard of my condo starting around 5pm.

Gabe and company were busy for three hours making all the great burgers, grilled cheese sandwiches and fries that have made them one of the most popular food trucks in Tampa Bay.

As usual, my Cajun Burger was just perfect and even my property manager got a little nuts with the Belgium Waffle Burger. Another friend took one bite of a Grilled Cheese with Prosciutto and her knees almost gave out.

After running down that big orange truck at Cigar City, TGH and other locations around the area, it was a convenient treat to have Burger Culture bring all that good food right to my front door.

Stopped in for the fist time today after a recommendation. I got the roast pork with spinach and it was great. They told me i had to come back for the hot sausage. I figured why wait. I ordered one and they did not disappoint. So after 2 sandwiches I'm off for a nap, full and happy.
This place is awful. The beef put on the nachos is horrible. The beef tasted like grease. I will not go back for a second try.

If you are looking for a light dish my best two options here are the tuna salad and the Salmon which you can order with vegetables or as a platter with mash potatoes. I know its not typical for an Irish Pub but absolutely Delish!! I was told the flatbreads are good also but haven't tried them...still on the list. $5 Car bombs...does are dangerous.
Sorry it was closed, but after Hollow Scream, sounds like a good time to let my inner child out again.

I just woke up and I can't wait to get down to Mel's for breakfast. GREAT breakfast, very friendly people, fabulous sandwiches, decent prices. (One small down side - I don't like their bacon, but that's just me. Their pork roll makes up for it. Yum!).  Can't beat this place.

Yesterday I passed by the New Tampa area for a business meeting. I decided have a quick lunch at Glory Days and was welcomed by Katie at the bar. She really knows how to make someone feel welcome and have an enjoyable lunch. Next time I am in town I will be back to Glory Days to see Katie. She is a "home run".

We like the this restaurant and this is the third time we are here. The server is friendly, unfortunately they are not fluent in English. The food is very good. Their famous dish is the grill quail. Each order of the grill quail is coming with 10 pieces. They prepare with very tasty sweet blacken source. It is finger licking good. 

The dinner we have order is 7 courses of beef for two. The come with fresh beef slice wrap, beef cute, sesame beef, beef salad, beef congee, beef ball, beef desert. This is serious amount of food for two. The seven way to cook the beef in Vietnamese style open my eyes how it done differently. Of course, they afraid that you order this dis and share with more than three people. Be sure that you have order one more dish if you want to share. 

The restaurant is good with group  and couple. At the end the restaurant is in medium condition for the furniture. This is a place going for the taste not for look. The duck noodle soup is another dish that you may want to try if you don't mind the bone. I always like meat with bone because more tasty. If you are boneless food person this is not your dish.

The location of the restaurant as part of the city water park make the unique setting. The backdrop sunset can be better to tell the story of restaurant. 

We arrive at the restaurant at Sunday night 5:50 pm and the wait line is already require 50 minutes of wait. We decide to sit at the oyster bar and have our dinner. The menu is interesting. To start we have the pulpo carpaccio - shave octopus with spicy source. While we were sitting there, there are worker open the oyster. After we have finish the octopus while waiting for the entree, we decided to order the oyster. I ask the worker can we select the oyster for the charbroil. They allow me to select. I have select the bigger and nicer oyster and the send to the kitchen to cook. 

The oyster is definitely the best dish of the appetizer. For the entree we have order the Florida Pompano and the Chief red Eagle Duck. The duck is ok and they try to imitate the French cuisine Duck O' lean. The pompano is tasty however the fish is not salty enough. I believe they can be better. For our entree we have wait for 30 minutes after the appetizer was finish. The time to wait is too long. 

Our fellow elite yelp member is overrated this restaurant. The price range is expensive but the food is  mediocre. The only dish that impress me is the charbroil oyster. If you notice, half of the entree menu items are open grill cooking not stir fry or pan sear. This tell you the skill of the chef and their capability.

The only thing is South Philly parking is the pits I was riding around and around then I found one. After all of that the store was empty. Next time I will call and place my order and double park.  I took a menu and I will place a order next time phone when I want a hoagie.

This hike kicked my butt!! What a great hike. It is a short hike, but the incline is very steep so you get a good workout. We jogged down. And the hike was less than an hour. We loved it and will definitely be hiking Tumamoc hill again.

The outside sitting at this month is very pleasant. Blue sky no cloud and you not in hurry while you enjoy your lunch. Our office decided to celebrate a win of project at this restaurant. This restaurant is the seventh series of famous Columbia restaurant. The lunch menu is reasonable price. Since we have a big group, the outside sitting waiter have a lot to cover. He have a slow start to get our menu. Then the food come out faster then I have estimated. The speed of serving is good after the food is out.  

I have the Mahi Mahi Cayo Hueso.  It is grill Mahi Mahi with mojo marinated. It is good. The soup of chilled gazpacho is taste better than lunch entree. I also ask a dish to go for the coworker that cannot make it. The order is done in 5 minutes. This mean this is cafe style precook items and assemble on site immediately. The temperature of the food is warm and further evident precook item.  This may not be the cook to your order place.

Friday and Saturday night the y have live music at the river front. This may be the best time to take a sip and enjoy.

Interesting setting of the restaurant that show the picture of musician. They have live music on Thursday, Friday and Sunday. We were very lucky that we have a seat immediately. We were here when Genghis Flan playing here. Parking is challenge since the restaurant have a lot of traffic. If you come after 7:00 pm, you can park at the value pawn shop after they close. 

Tonight dinner we have begin with crab stuffed butternut squash. Butternut squash is a very difficult cooking material to be use, however, the chef did a very good job to prepare it with crab meat and citrus hollandaise source. The squash filled with crab meat area is extremely soft and sweet to eat. 

Our main course is chicken mole and beef tenderloin. The chicken is very juicy in curry source top over comb read arepa. Who can think about the combination of the dish. It is getting better with the preparation on the beef tenderloin. The dish come with 2 pieces of 4 oz tenderloin in port wine source. It grilled to perfect with beef juice seal inside. This dish also come with bacon wrap asparagus. Bacon is good with any food but who can think the combination of crispy bacon wrap with bacon juice drip into the asparagus. It also come with mash potato cake. The mash is well season and the top is bake to crispy. 

I am usually not given out 5 star. The cooking and the mixture of the material and taste is well balance. The food give me a wow and unique factor.

These guys are very consistent and actually it doesnt matter which location you go to.  The food almost always tastes and looks the same.  Gotta hand it to their franchise operations.

I always get the Gargantuan with NO ONIONS and I ask for peppers.  It is the largest sandwich you can get there and it is very delicious.  The lettuce is very crisp, sliced very thin and you get all sorts of meats.  

These guys will NOT let you down no matter what your diet is.

They better watch out, cause I am coming back for more.
Service is very friendly. Food is amazing. What else do you really wanna know? Tip: bring a friend that speaks spanish

So far everything I have eaten has been amazing. The thing is.... I only ate the burger so far. I just ordered the Italian beef. They make it amazingly fast. I'll add more to this review but it's 5 stars so far.

"Uncle" Carl is such a character and a blast to work with! He helped get the ball rolling and then, when my vision changed over the year, Pam took over and talked through my nebulous ideas until we knew what she would create.

In the end, Pam designed and executed the most perfect, award-winning boutonniere for my groom, a completely romantic-yet-funky bouquet for me (can you say succulents?), and perfectly balanced, unique centerpieces (carefully placed kale, dried lotus pods, and scabiosa!).

Thank you for sharing your collective knowledge of flowers and plants. And thank you for the amount of care you put into making our arrangements perfect!

Always have a GREAT meal. The girls here give AMAZING service.  EXTREMELY customer oriented establishment.  The establishment specializes in Columbian cuisine and offers several Puerto Rican dishes since the predecessor business at this location was Tainos, a Puerto Rican restaurant.  I've ordered both Columbian and Puerto Rican dishes. I am Puerto Rican myself and must say, their Mofongo is ON POINT. Taste just like made back in The Island. Very competitive pricing as well and prepared fairly quickly. MUST try the fresh made limeade. HIGHLY recommend for anyone craving good Columbian or Puerto Rican food.

loved it! the out side is pretty much getto and the in side is dated but the food was (with out sound like a teen age girl) OMG!. I DID NOT THINK it was going to be as good as it was but it was great will go back again

Why 5 stars? Because I can't believe I just discovered this!

   ---   ---   ---

This is a small, but very upscale Venezuelan eatery. The food takes a bit longer to come out of the kitchen, but it's 100% worth it, because the quality here is very high.

I ordered the churrasco, because I'm a predictable bore. (I just can't resist when I see it on a menu.)

This was different, as there was no chimichurri, but some different, equally delicious white sauce. There was also these things that looked like red peppers, but tasted like bacon on top. I feel dumb for not knowing what those were, but WOW, were they absolutely amazing. I wanted 10 more!

I also enjoyed some fruit/milk drinks - both guava and mango flavored. I loved both, but guava was definitely my favorite. These drinks go so well with the flavor profiles of most spanish dishes.

Bottom line: I'm mad I didn't come here sooner. Be aware that the parking lot is a bit claustrophobic, but beyond that... it's all wonderous.
We LOVE this place. They seriously have the BEST steak I have ever had at any high end steak restaurant. The service, the food, the ambience, everything. Hands down great!
Pork sandwich was perfect, rice and beans was pretty good too. Its a hole in the wall, and the folks behind the counter were a bit confused, but all in all it hit the spot.

I've been to the movie theater a few times most recently tired today I went and saw gone girl that I was pleasantly surprised because I had absolutely no idea what that movie was about going in, turns out it was well worth it not only was it intriguing it was thrilling; and you kind of didn't know exactly what the movie was going but it all tied together in the end, this movie theater in particular is very nice comfortable stadium seating and the chairs recline nicely so you're comfortable throughout the movie; what I particularly like is that the temperature is nicely climate controlled it's not too cold and it's not too warm I hate going into a movie theater and I'm freezing my entire time. Today I was looking up to see the new Denzel Washington movie although it came on in September I had forgot about it and then I saw it on the boards and I said let me see the equalizer oh I so enjoyed this movie, had myself some nice buttery popcorn and a drink to go along with it that's the only thing I don't like too much about movie theaters is it so expensive but the popcorn calls my name every time I walk in; Great thing at this theater is it's always fresh I've never had any problems with it not being freshly popped.

Opening night. A lot of excitement. Free food until 9 pm tonight 11-6-2014. They have a 40 feet long of buffet table as a feast for the open night. They have cuisine of Indian, Chinese, Thai, Arabian.  The opening night party include belly dancer, hoopla hoop dancing. If you are not here, you are missing out. This restaurant locate below the  Ramada hotel. 

They restaurant menu include Kosher Meat. Catering and banquet is available. The food is excellent. The goat curry is cook perfectly. There is a long line of people on the buffet opening night. I also have the tandoori chicken. It is serve at the hot platform. I have a lot food and entertainment and it is free at opening night. 

They have outside sitting and full bar. Cannot said enough. This is a party. Great party.

Located in Downtown Tampa's Channelside District in Kennedy Grand Central next to Powerhouse Fitness off Meridian and Kennedy, Maloney's Local Irish Pub is one of the best bars in the Tampa downtown area and one of the best Irish bars in Tampa Bay.  When I lived Downtown, I used to be a regular here. I highly recommend the Salmon, Bangers & Mash, Sheppard's Pie, Flatbread Pizza, and house specialty Maloney's Red Ale! Ambiance is a typical sports bar with high top tables, lots of tvs, 2 dart boards and a little bit of Irish flair.  Crowd gets busy on Thurs Fri Sat but it varies. There is 2 hour free parking available in Kennedy Grand Central's garage. After that it's $1 per hour with a Max of $10 charge. Service is nice and cordial and food comes out pretty quickly usually. Maloney's Local Irish Pub has a large craft beer selection including their house brewed Maloney's Irish Red Ale which is always only $3 and quite rich and nice in flavor. There is usually live music on the weekends, plenty of TVs to watch college and pro football, and there's even 2 dart boards. 

Specials:  $3 wells, $4 calls, $2 Bud Lite & Michelob Ultra drafts from 3pm till 7pm Monday - Friday, Monday - Chicken Sandwich 6.00, Tuesday - Sheppard's Pie 6.00, Wednesday - Burger Night 6.00, Thursday - Fish & Chips 6.00, Sunday - Chicken Finger Basket. For those in the WestChase area, Maloney's Local Irish Pub has a sister location there by the same name! Maloney's also has top notch Margaritas!

Inside of the building is much nicer than the outside. 

Bento box dinners are great. Tempura shrimp is light and crisp. It's made perfectly.

Try their hot tea. We asked about the very subtle, grainy flavor in the tea that enhanced the flavor and they told us it's rice added to enhance. It really has a great taste.

Wouldn't want to come on a crowded night, but it really is a very cool place to eat at during slower hours. It's a great place for a date night.

They need a traffic light at the fall bridge which intersection with Martin Luther King Dr is always a traffic jam in the morning but the sites are beautiful the trees are changing its just a wonderful sight the drive thru but when you get to a certain point is a traffic jam the city really need to look at it and revamp it and put a traffic light directing traffic

Ordered delivery from here the other day and was very pleased with our order. Not only did it come relatively quickly during dinner time, but it was pretty good. Ironically, the leasty tasty item that we ordered was the pizza. Granted it was simple plain cheese pizza, it tasted super generic. Kind of like a frozen pizza. It might very well have been frozen at some point.

The cheesesteaks were super delicious. Super basic, but great rolls and great meat. A perfect meat to cheese ratio. Pretty large as well. Also ordered soem buffalo wings which were small, but very very tasty. Even the buffalo wing afficianado in the family really liked them, and she's super picky about her buffalo wings. 

They also took extra care in preparing the food for delivery which I appreciated, by making sure everything was well wrapped and leak proof. One thing to watch for though: The price on our wings had "changed" since the menu we had was printed. Not significant, but it happened. I don't know if we just got screwed or what but it seemed pretty suspicious. So a heads up on that.

YUM!!!!! The BC food truck visited USF's campus for a food truck rally. After checking out the menu in advance I decided that's the truck I wanted to visit. When we arrived the line was maybe 20-25 people deep and it took a good 40 minutes to get through it. Those had to be two of the hardest working guys I've ever seen!! I was going to get a grilled cheese but then decided with BURGER in the name of the truck, I ought to try it. I was kind of confused as to why grilled cheese sandwiches were more expensive than burgers, though?? Anyway, I'm a bit of a burger snob - my dad makes incredible burgers for my mom's restaurant and I've only had 2 others (1 out of the country) that rival them. I decided to be adventurous and ordered the mango burger - burger, cream cheese, mango chutney, and grilled onions. Unfortunately they were out of onions but I decided what the heck let's try it anyway. Also ordered fries since I kept seeing them seasoning them and wanted to know what it was! Paid via credit card (I SO appreciate when food trucks have that capability!) and it took about 5 minutes for our food. When I walked up to take my box the guy running the place kind of looked at the burger in question as if it didn't look good enough and then said 'Want some bacon on it?' WHO IN THEIR RIGHT MIND SAYS NO TO THAT?! So yes I took advantage of that offer and he squished a massive handful on top! Bonus! I'll take that over grilled onions. 

We went to find seats and dug in! The burger was perfectly seasoned and cooked! Not chewy and overdone and it was a FAT burger, too. It's probably pretty tasty on its own but lemme just tell ya... those toppings. Oh my stars. I don't know WHY I was weirded out by the idea of cream cheese on a burger but that may be my new go-to cheese variety! The chutney added a great sweet flavor but wasn't overpowering (I don't even like mango, guys, but it sounded good). I wonder if there are other flavor options? I vote for that! I'm also very glad the bacon option was added because it added a salty crunchiness to it. Great suggestion, chef! Fries were tasty but I couldn't figure out what the seasoning was, kinda reminded me of the seasonings they have at Steak N Shake now? Very very tasty meal though! I hope they come more often :)

Just FYI this truck doesn't sell sodas. I think it's brilliant because that's gotta weigh them down and slow them down and keep them from stocking more delectable ingredients, but my friend was disappointed because she would have to go to another truck in another line to get drinks, but I say good call! I also visited the Facebook page and would like to see more information about where they'll be and when because I'll probably have a burger craving again soon! See y'all again!

Service was definitely an A+, food was typical sports bar food. I went with a few friends to watch the USF game. They arrived first and I was a little late. As soon as I sat down I was approached by the waitress who told me about beer specials (I don't do beer) so I just ordered a water. I glanced over the menu and thought a philly cheesesteak sounded tasty. It came out quickly and was wrapped up in paper to attempt to keep it together and less messy (didn't work much lol). It wasn't much like a philly cheesesteak at all - the meat was chewy, there were no grilled onions, there was mayonnaise and cheese... it was more like a hot roast beef sandwich. I ate half then focused on the fries (pretty standard). When the waitress came to check in she asked how it was and I just responded with a generic sort of 'eh it was okay' and she asked what was wrong. I was specific in saying it wasn't anything WRONG necessarily about the food, it just wasn't what I expected. She said she would send the manager over and I tried to argue that it was fine but she sent him anyway. He approached and asked what I thought and I just explained that it wasn't your average philly cheesesteak and a bit on the chewy side and just kinda disappointing but it wasn't anything they really did wrong on their part. He said he was going to comp my meal and offered to get me something else but after 1/2 the sandwich and fries I tried to tell him I was fine and still ate half and would pay but he insisted on covering it. When the checks came I didn't get one because I just had water to drink but wanted to make sure and tip the waitress (I only ever have cards... terrible cash person). I asked for a diet coke to go and when she brought it I said 'Okay now can I have a check please?' and she said 'no don't worry about it' and I said no I NEED a check to tip you! She laughed then printed one off and thanked me because I didn't have to do that. I'll try it out again for another game day, definitely had the sports bar feel and very friendly and accommodating staff, just will try some other food items.

Ummmmm NO! We just left MOSI and typed Foot Locker in the GPS and it directed us to this mall. As we pulled into the parking garage we noticed guys just hanging around congregated with sagging pants. Also, there were people casually walking around the parking lot (not being mindful of the cars driving). It appeared to be "urban" and geared towards a specific group of people. 

I didn't feel comfortable taking my baby out of the car or subjecting him to anyone or anything that's not conducive to his upbringing. Not to mention it would have been a waste of time because we wouldn't have found anything of our interest in this mall (other than sneakers at Foot Locker when is rather order online). 

If you value your safety, drive pass this place! I don't want to be one of those people who's at the wrong place at the wrong time...if you know what I mean?

The Saturday organic market is slow stroke relaxing location. Meeting the farmer, pickup a few organic items, listen to the live music, enjoy the vege product. 

I like their vege burger that cook with extreme spice mixing. You will not know this is a vege burger by the time you have finish. I have the rasta burger with avocado. We enjoy our burger and the live music from the local artists. 

It is country, gypsies, farmer mixed. A little corner of the metropolitan that set a side for down to earth place.

A favorite of my friend group, it's your typical Irish bar without the "dive". Inside is clean, smoke free, and quite spacious. They also offer a decent sized patio for outdoor seating.

Located in Channelside, this is a great stop for the locals that live in the high rises. The clientele ranges from young professionals to retirees.

The car bombs are reasonably priced and they usually have a live band on the weekends. More often than not, the band is fantastic! 

I am not a fan of the cheese fries (unless you are very drunk, then I highly recommend them). The flat breads are good, potato skins are classic and the service is great (and friendly). Most importantly, the bathrooms are almost always spotless!!!

After reading about the Sweetwater Sunday Market from a vendor on Facebook, I was excited to give this place a try.

Plenty of parking was free, and their webpage made it very clear which spots were available for use on Sunday.   The market had a small stage set up with live music, with many seats available for customers to purchase food and eat it there, or to simply sit and enjoy the performance.  I heard they did a farm tour from 1-2, but we missed that.

Unfortunately, the vendor selection itself was a disappointment - just not enough vendors to make it a worthwhile trip.  Two jewelry sellers (one with really cool felt rattles), a fermented tea seller, a fresh pizza seller, The Dancing Goat - with fresh dairy products and soap, a loose tea seller, some tie dyed clothing, a pet treat vendor, and the vegetable stand which sells farm tee shirts.  I'm sure I've forgotten some, but that was the gist of it.

I'm certain the amount of Vendors changes depending on the weekend, and it was very cool to see the farm's gardens all sprouting, but I probably won't be back.

When I saw this store I wondered why I never stopped in before. I was turned off after walking in. It looks dirty and unorganized. 

We were looking at the soy milk in the refrigerator and when we opened the door they all started tumbling out because they were stacked improperly. No one even came over to help even though the workers saw it happen. We had to put them all back in the refrigerator on our own. 

We didn't buy anything and won't be going back.

We stopped in while bar hopping for a friend's birthday. We never left. 

The karaoke was great, and it didn't take very long for out name to be called. An upscale dive bar. Drinks were reasonably price. (Especially for the Ybor strip). Service was very attentive for how busy it was. 

Everything was rather low key. However, we left at 1 am. I can image they have their crazy nights too.

No real "special" drinks, but they can make all the classics and offer beer as well. All in all a quality stop and we will be back again!

Cafe style. I have the take out all the time. This place have all kind Spanish food from fried whole fish to teriyaki chicken. They gave selection of small or big plate. Range from $4 to $6.50. 

They also have devil crab, chicken or ground beef empanadas, stuff potato. They also have freshly make Cuban sandwich. 

Compare to fast food change, cafeteria style is a better option. I always come here for for the guarapole ( sugar cane juice). They prepare the fresh squeeze on the site. It is good. 

Please be aware the lunch crowd build up after 11:30 am. Please come early. If you come after 11:30 am, the line is already to the door. Friday night, music and fun is happen here. People gather after work for music.
Best Indian food near south tampa.  Also they have a great creative loafing deal.  We had the butter chicken and lamb korma with naan bread.  Good service and food.  Highly recommend!

Small neighborhood bakery, family related to Alessi's if I am not mistaken.  Selection not as good but prices better and generally very good quality.  Despite the other reviews got an unsatisfactory carrot cake on a Friday afternoon.  Almost certainly had been frozen and put back on counter, dry and stale.  Had to give to the neighbor boys as no one in house would eat it (neighborhood boys eat everything, offerred or not).
   Still would recommend, but if you want good carrot.cake call ahead and have them make fresh.

I don't usually do grocery shopping here but if you are in the area and looking for a quick lunch this is a good option. They have a few pre made sandwiches and wraps and my favorite a Seafood Bar. Usually about 4 different options the shrimp cocktail and seaweed salad are very good. Prices are by weight. Good parking and usually not crowded.

I am very happy with the service I recieved from A-1. This was my first time using a service like this and I must say it was so worth doing so and will do it again the next time I have to travel.

 Friendly service and staff and quick shuttle service to and from the airport. I don't think I waited 10 mins for a shuttle both ways. In addition to parking sevice, they also offer car wash and car detailing at a very reasonable price. Some times their are coupons available for discounted parking. There was a little confusion for me on my parking situation but it was clearified to me by the service rep at the front desk. 

I am fan for sure and happy my first time experience was a great one.

We came here about with a group of about 10. I really love the setup that reminds me of the 1920's with leather sofas, cool decorations and really nice bar.   The hookah's were really good and I really appreciated the incredible service. A staff member came up to us every 10 minutes asking if we are ok and if we need anything else.  I enjoyed the loungy setup with very comfortable seating.  I would def come back here again when im looking for a chill evening.

Great location, great food, great drinks, great service... Did I mention the word great ?I have never had a bad meal here. I happen to live nearby so I can walk there, but if your not walking the street parking is a little hard to come by so look for the parking garage which is on the side of the building and has free indoor parking.
This place is AMAZING !! The yogurt selection is pretty small but the taste is wonderful. They also offer ice cream and smoothies !! The staff is very friendly and keep the place very clean.

My favorite studio by far. Every class I've taken has been great. The best thing about this studio is how many class are offered. There is always one at a convenient time and I've never experienced a class being cancelled. No one has ever been judge-y, and I've always felt comfortable going at my own pace. Marley is their best instructor. It's amazing how knowledgeable she is about the poses. I also like how every class has music, silence before class is not expected, and there are always plenty of props, no matter how crowded the class may be. It's clean, bright, and cheery.

Could be 2.5 stars.

Came here for lunch because I didn't want to go to a fast food place. Read the reviews and thought I'd give it a try.

Update: It is no longer a $5.99 lunch special, it is a $6.59 lunch special. And to answer Mark's review, yes, their egg rolls are always soft and not so crunchy. I ate mine fresh and the only crunchy parts were the ends. They are veggie egg rolls, by the way.

I ordered the kung pao chicken. It comes with diced carrots, water chestnuts, peanuts, bamboo shoots, and celery. The celery right away was a give away. To me, celery in Chinese food is a cheap filler choice by the chef and the flavor of celery does not complement any Chinese flavor at all. I do not like it when bamboo shoots are diced instead of sliced. And although it wasn't a tiny amount, the amount of water chestnuts and peanuts was not generous and a bit on the scant side.

The sauce was soupy, not saucy. The dish was literally swimming in soup. Kung pao chicken is also supposed to be spicy. It was a little bit spicy.

It came with the aforementioned egg rolls, and I opted for the lo mein instead of the fried rice. The lo mein hardly had any cabbage in it. The flavor was OK, nothing exciting, though.

My recommendation is if you want to try this place, go with one of the apparently successful entrees that are mentioned in the other reviews, just don't choose the kung pao chicken.

Service was very quick. I was the only one there at opening and I waited only five minutes. The chef's wife was very friendly. What astounds me is that she told me that Mei Mei has been there for 22 years. I thought that for a business to be there for that long they must have good food. Well, I was wishing that I had gone to Wendy's or What-A-Burger instead.

WHAT IS THIS PLACE?! It's delightful! It's adorable! It's boozy! It's a wintery wonderland! Boyfriend and I went ice skating the afternoon of my birthday after weighing options of things to do on short notice. A quick walk from South Street down to the waterfront on a warm afternoon gave way to what I'm sure will be an equally grand oasis when the weather turns cold for good. Wooden rocking chairs with plaid flannel blankets line the outdoor area of the warm lodges, fireplaces indoors keep you nice and toasty, while the fire pits outside allow you equal comfort for sipping hot beverages or the boozy kind that make you warm and tingly on the inside. I loved the huge trees and all of the festive lights, the observation deck above the outdoor bar for spotting friends out on the ice skating rink, and the entertainment! Two family friendly entertainers brought clown-like antics, tightrope walking and flame juggling to the area outside of the firepits while Dunkin Donuts (official sponsor) passed out peppermint mocha coffee. Ice skating was great, a nice way to spend the afternoon for sure. $13 gets you admission and a skate rental for an hour and a half long session on the ice, the $3 admission is apparently waived if you show your Independence Blue Cross insurance card (also official sponsor). Now knowing how great this spot is (I can't believe I never made it last year), l I keep thinking about how great it would be to grab some friends, have a few drinks and enjoy some late night ice skating under the holiday light show and believe that it will absolutely be happening this year. Great job PHS, you never fail to disappoint!!

Why 5 stars? Because this is now my favorite food truck and burger in Tampa Bay.

   ---   ---   ---

I have always known of Burger Culture, but for reasons unknown, I never actually tried them out.

That has changed as of today, and now I see the light. The light is nice.

We recently just hosted a Yelp event at Pair O' Dice Brewing Co, who doesn't have a kitchen, so we brought in Burger Culture to cater the event. There, I was able to sample about a half-size of the Mango Burger, which was heavenly. It had cream cheese and mango chutney, and I was borderline depressed when it was all gone.

So I knew I had to check them out for a legitimate meal ASAP, and found some time to head over that way today. I ordered the Street Burger, per the recommendation of Gabe (the owner) and I was not disappointed. Wow. It comes with cheddar cheese, pineapple, cilantro aioli, pink sauce, and those little mini fries. It's about the size of a baby elephant, and yes, I ate every single morsel. 

I absolutely love the fries as well. They come with giant shavings of sea salt, and I just can't get enough.

If I have to pick a favorite thing about Burger Culture, I think it might be that their menu is just insane. Usually I can narrow down my go-to order to 1-2 things at a restaurant, but I think I'd have to eat lunch at Burger Culture every day for 2 weeks to make a dent in the awesomeness of the menu. They also have an entire grilled cheese menu as well... my God.

You can find Burger Culture one of two ways - hit their stationary truck up at the Harley Davidson on Dale Mabry in Carrollwood, which is open every day for lunch... or check them out on social media to see their schedule for the roaming truck.

Seriously, one visit and one Yelp event is all it took. I'm a Burger Culture lifer.

Fortunato's was a huge surprise to me. I don't usually give 5 stars after just one great visit, but there it is. Nothing went wrong; everything was better than I could have expected.

I showed up at Fortunato's to eat lunch with a co-worker. He suggested it; I had never been there, although I have driven by it a few times. The inside is a bit crowded -- even cluttered -- but that gives it a sort of down-home charm. Especially if your home is an Italian deli, which mine is not.

I was all ready to order the #1 Italian Sub to see just how Italian this place is, but then I noticed a piece of paper taped to the bottom of the menu board with a "Tur-muen-ator" (Turkey, Muenster, &c) and since I love bad puns, I ordered that instead. The sandwiches come in two sizes; I was warned to stick with the "small". Heavy sarcasm marks on "small". I was still completely full afterwards.

At less than $8, I was surprised to learn the sandwich came with a large pickle spear and a side (potato salad or macaroni salad). When the sandwich arrived, the sesame-encrusted bread looked like it would be rock solid and I thought the insides would soon be spilling onto the plate as I tried to bite in. I was wrong. The bread was soft, and perhaps one of the best sandwich breads I've had in Tucson. And the creamy macaroni salad was so good that I finished it even though I was full at the end of the sandwich.

My co-worker bought a "large brownie" (which was about half the size of a full brownie pan) to take back to the office. I had a piece later, when I finally felt like there was room in my stomach. And you know what, it was pretty good.

I really had no idea how amazing this little place was. Friendly staff, surprisingly great sandwich, very filling, and a great price -- especially for this part of town, where things tend to cost more. I can't wait to try more of their sandwiches.

Yelp does it again! I was in the mood for Venezuelan near my office...  I quickly opened up Yelp and saw that there was a highly rated arepa location that was within 10 minutes of my office.

I ordered two arepas (way too much food) and a lulo (Colombian fruit) drink and was very happy.  The waitresses mainly speak Spanish so FYI.  I was able to maneuver my way through that.

This mall quite frankly scares me. It's old, It's out of date and the parking lot is just.. yeah. I came here for one particular store.. and found it, but had to drive all the way to the back, because I didn't want to park at the front of the mall. It's either time for a face lift, or time for this mall to be given last rights.

Big, overly salty and over all unimpressive.  Burger Culture boasts "gourmet" burgers, but I find them to be a little boring.  First off, when burgers are not flame grilled, I think the chefs owe it to the paying customers to do something special with the meat.  Make them unique by spicing up each individual burger patty based on what type of burger it's going to be.  You know, mix the spices into the ground beef.  When I was a kid, my parents used to take a packet of Lipton's French Onion Soup mix and mix that into the ground beef with some salt, pepper and Worchester sauce.  It made for an amazing tasting burger.  These guys just use standard beef patties.  They slap 'em on a flat top grill and sprinkle a day's worth of sodium and call it "gourmet."  Don't get me wrong, the burgers ain't bad, they just aren't worth ever buying again.  Head over to Maloney's or James Joyce to see what a "gourmet" burger tastes like, folks.

The Honey Pot is a lot of fun.  I have been here yearly for the Santa Pub Crawl.  This is always the start to the crawl and it is always a great spot to start.  I love the upstairs area and the stage is always fun for the costume contest.  The bartenders are attentive, the drinks are well priced, and the music is good.  I haven't been here on a regular night, but I hope to sometime soon.

Stumbled across this new cigar and hookah bar after a night out with some friends and thoroughly enjoyed ourselves. We got a mint and orange hookah and they set up an awesome table contraption with 4 pipes. The hookah was fresh and tasted great.. We went downstairs to a comfy living room with couches and a tv, hooked an iPhone up the speakers and had an awesome time relaxing and hanging out!
