It's byo so you can bring whatever alcohol you want and they will mix it with fresh fruit/herbs/whatever you want... I highly recommend the fresh daily!! The owner Andrew is super friendly and made us feel right at home. We had so much fun and I can't wait to come back! 

Bonus: it's next to gay pizza!

This place is awesome. Great selection, great service, well ventilated. Cigars, hookahs, bowls, ect... BYOB ontop of it with fresh fruit and mixers on hand. Andrew, made some cucumber drink for me where you could taste the freshness. Thank you! You guys really did a great job with this place...
Update: It's gotten much better, and many of the prices are much better than Lee Lee's.
One of the few places you can get chauource.  It's not the same as the real deal from France since it's pasteurized but still very good.
Nice place to dine at if you're in the tampa fl area i had the crab cake sandwich very good food

Pitchers is the the best bar on Main Street.  There, I said it!  Bar goers typically look for a few specific things (and here they are):

-Good selection: Pitchers has 30 beers on draft that are constantly changing.
-Great prices: They have the best drink specials on Main Street and often have all of their drafts for only $3.  The nice thing about their specials is that they often have good specials during peak hours and not just when they are trying to pull people into the door during odd hours.
-Friendly service: Pitchers has some of the friendliest bartenders on Main Street.  Their ability to serve one of the busiest bars on Main Street while maintaining their composure and friendly attitude is amazing.  .
-Good grub: Pitchers wings are FANTASTIC and are often 1/2 off ($4).  The other bar food is good as well but it is hard for me to get away from the wings.  For what they have to work with, they do an excellent job.
-Entertaining crowd: the crowd brings in a variation of customers from younger college kids to old townies.  The thing I like most about this scene is no matter who comes through the door they are met with friendly service and a welcoming and safe atmosphere.
Good place to watch the games: I think they have six large screen televisions in the bar with great quality pictures for a good view from anywhere in the room.  Bar is long and affords a good amount of bar seating with additional seating along the wall.

I empathize with the reviews regarding the ID's and can appreciate their frustration.  I think for some reason or another Pitchers once had the reputation for underage drinkers being able to get into (maybe because it was true from previous owners).  Quite simply, if they don't know you by face and name they ID nearly 100%.  This protects them and their patrons.  I imagine for the thousands of ID's the bouncers see and attempts of underage people trying to get it, it is a tough job.  I think if you ever come across that again, just be polite and if it escalates just give them the benefit they are trying to maintain the integrity of the bar.  If need be, call the cops to verify the ID is legit.  They put up with enough other nonsense so getting mouthy with them or giving them a hard time is a sure way not to get in.  I do think it would be beneficial for them to get an ID scanner.

All in all, this is by far my preferred bar on Main Street.

Royal Orchid is a very large restaurant with multiple seating areas and an open bar area in a Ramada Hotel in Westshore. They serve up some first rate Indian cuisine and also have other Asian and continental dishes in an extensive menu to meet all tastes. They are introducing a lunch buffet for 2015. They also have an extended happy hour and lunch specials.

The multiple seating areas at Royal Orchid can accommodate a very large gathering but the setup maintains intimacy and comfort and isn't overwhelming at all. The main space is a large open area with a bar area and dining space with high tables and comfy booths. There is also a patio area, more secluded side dining area and banquet rooms.

Royal Orchid serves various Asian dishes and offers a lunch buffet. The Tom Yam Goong, Thai shrimp coconut lemongrass soup, was tasty. The lamb korma was excellent with a rich subtle taste. The mango lassi was rich and delicious. The naan was excellent. They will spice to the heat desired and the food tends to have some zip.

Dishes are served in hammered silver bowls and rice (white, brown, jasmine) that are conducive yo family style and group dining. Have as much or as little of any one dish as you like! They also split a soup no problem so two of us could share it. Serving various dishes over a rice felt traditional and everything tasted very good. 

We met the chef who has more than two decades of experience internationally. Our server Kristina was super and sweet. It's a classy tasteful feeling space but also relaxed. There are even nooks with pool and Foosball for gamers.

Look forward to returning. Hope people find this great space with some very tasty dishes.

Christmas Town was included in the Season Pass package this year (2014). Last year you had to pay extra to walk through some of the light displays, etc. I am glad that it was included this year because it is such a great way to spend an evening during the Holidays. The lights are up through the end of the year. In addition to the lights there are also characters (snow man, etc.) that kids can take pictures with. There are also some great shows (i.e. Christmas on Ice).

Panera is good and consistent. Our service yesterday was fast and friendly. We both had the "You pick 2" with broccoli cheddar soup and the Sierra turkey sandwich  all was good. For dessert, we treated ourselves to a free birthday dessert for being a member of their club. We chose the coffee cake and liked it. 
Join the club. It's free and They email you benefits during the year.

Been a while since I have initially reviewed Downtown Dogs. We have been in a bunch of times. Everyone at Downtown Dogs is always welcoming to us and our dog Mocha Coconut. They always make sure he is comfy with treats and water.

Downtown dogs carries a great assortment of treats, dog bakery items, food, accessories, clothes, beds, and strollers. 

Everything you can imagine needed to pamper your pet is here, so you don't need to go anywhere else.

Premium pet food prices are fair and comparable to other places. Today, we purchased his food called Fromm. Our purchase and our service was perfect as can be.

This is a nice local pet store with all needed for a perfectly spoiled pet. They also have pet adoptions here and sponsor other pet events. All is perfect perfect here for your dog.

I have never been a fan of Indian food, until now. Indian food to me has always been too spicy or smelled too much of curry. 

My fellow Yelper and crazy friend, Nick S and I decided to try Royal Orchid out last week. It had just opened in early November of 2014. When I heard that Royal Orchid was located in the Ramada on Westshore, I was very intrigued. The Ramada has been in that location for years and is not in the best shape. 

When we walked in, we were amazed. The restaurant was huge and was decorated beautifully! The restaurant wasn't too busy, but we were also having a late lunch at 3 PM. There was one person working as a server/bartender, Raul.

There were many items to choose from on the menu. Too many. We asked Raul to recommend an appetizer and he suggested the Chicken 65. The Chicken 65 was a dish of boneless chicken cubes, cooked and tossed with an Indian spice and chili garlic sauce. I am wimp, so we ordered medium. There was a little kick to them, but I managed to enjoy the dish, which was very good. 

After we finished our appetizer, Khristina took over as our server. We decided to split the Naan, the Tom Yang Koong (Thai Shrimp Soup), and the Lamb Korma. All the dishes were very rich and flavorful. 

I recently went back this past weekend with a vegetarian friend and we ordered the Gobi Manchurian, Chicken Tikka Masala, and Navratan Korma. Once again, all dishes were very flavorful and delicious. My favorite dish was the Gobi Manchurian which are cauliflower florets, marinated and deep fried, then tossed with ginger soy sauce. So good that I couldn't get enough!

The Executive Chef, came out both times and said hello and checked on us. Raul and Khristina were both very friendly.

There are still some kinks to be ironed out. For example, both times I dined here there were only two of us, yet the other two settings were still left on the table. Other than a few minor kinks, I would recommend Royal Orchid. I will definitely be back.

I took my wife, son ( home from military ) and his GF here. In the past  I have always enjoyed my service and food.  This visit:   I had poor service, I was served appetizers with no silverware or napkins. Waitress was the worst. I even had to get up for my catchup. The fries with the fish and chips were cold. I went to the bar to see the manager and talked to a man (shaved head) ........ he point to bartender I told him  I had a complaint and wanted to talk to her. Either he didn't tell her or she didn't care. 
She never came to my table ...... Very disappointing night out!
Great food and beer! Trinity made our experience worth it! She was very enthusiastic and had great suggestions!

I've lived near this Food City for over two years and finally made a visit. Food City is known for their low prices produce, carnicerias and Mexican products. This location is particularly small, the produce selection was a little on the sad side and there's no carniceria, bummer! I'd return to this one in a bind, but this location doesn't offer any of the awesome things that Food City is known for.

Wonderful yoga classes, and instructors. You can borrow mats, props, etc. The only thing that bothers me is the rooms are tiny and it feels cramped when you are in class. Also they really try to squeeze in as many people as possible. I hate that.

I just had Krystal's for lunch.  It's almost like a southern White Castle. I had 2 cheeseburgers and 2 hamburgers with fries and 2 pup dogs. They are mini chili dogs. Krystal's puts mustard on their burgers instead of ketchup. It was good but I love The Castle more

No need ever again to have to go to North Tampa for great Indian cuisine.  No right herein South Tampa, close enough, you can have the most authentic and well prepared Indian cuisine in town! We had the Palak Paneer, Goan Shrimp and a Veggie curry that were mind blowing. Spiced to your individual liking. Each dish is prepared from scratch.  This is not fast food. Just as good as our meals when we were in India. Go try for yourself.  You will not be disappointed!

Small little bakery that according to their webpage has been family owned and operated since 1959.  Getting in and out of their parking lot can be tricky since it's at a busy intersection, but it is totally worth a visit.

First, they have excellent baked goods, pastries, cakes, and treats. I consider myself a dessert connoisseur, and I have loved every cake I have ever bought from there.  Cookies are fresh, never stale. The guava and cheese bread is outstanding.  Bear claw was flaky and delicious.  Their mixed Italian cookies have been a big hit whenever I bring them to a party.  They always have seasonal treats too - pumpkin cookies in fall, homentashin for Purim, Rudolph cookies for Christmas, etc.

They also have a nice selection of ready-made basic sandwiches like egg salad, tuna salad, turkey, and Cubans, etc. - that you can grab and go, or they will open it up and add lettuce/tomato mustard/mayonnaise to it, or press it, for you.

They do a big business in catering; they offer trays of sandwiches, vegetables, deli salads, and other options that will fit your every need, for very reasonable prices.  They even offer a Cuban platter shaped as an alligator, for lovers of a certain Florida team.

Insider tip - they close early on Sundays, and are closed Mondays, so they offer all in-stock pastries the last hour on Sundays for HALF OFF.  I haven't managed to take advantage of it yet, but oh yes, I will!

This is one of those places where you should stick to ordering their appetizers. By that I mean they had great appetizers but their main courses were "meh." I got the soup dumplings and the pan-fried Shanghai soup dumplings. Both were so good! Although I do prefer the soup dumplings from Sakura Mandarin more so. The service, unfortunately, wasn't so great. The waiters tend to ignore the customers. If I'm ever back, I'd probably stick just to the appetizers.

Why 3 stars? Because it is what we thought it was.

   ---   ---   ---

I simply come to Beefs for one reason: it's a Browns Backers bar during NFL season. If you're a huge fan of places like Chili's and Applebee's, then you'll probably find yourself content at Beef O' Brady's. 

Those types of businesses, however, are not my Cup O' Tea. I often would eat just before heading to the football game, just so that I didn't have to subject myself to eating at Beef's. 

Well... for the last game of the year I didn't eat before hand. I felt bad watching the game many times without ordering any food, so I got myself some loaded nachos. And the result was that the actual soggy plate of food did not even come close to resembling the majestic slice of culinary heaven from the menu picture. Unfortunately, it tasted like it looked.

So how does Beef's get 3-stars?

1) They are a Browns bar, and my favorite in the TB area.

2) Sam/Samantha was our server each week, and she is incredible. Serving for a bunch of hardcore football fans cannot be easy. You have to be a lot like a good referee - unnoticed, but doing your job flawlessly. You can't just jump in and ask for drink orders in the middle of a crucial play, but you also don't have the time to sit around and wait for a commercial to take orders... not when you have several tables of patrons watching other games that are timed up differently.

So kudos to them for hosting us, and kudos to Sam busting her ass. The food and the beer selection though? There's a lot left to be desired.

Great local chain.  The empanadas are made to order, so they are hot and crispy.  I tried the beef and cheese, and it was bubbly hot and filling.  I also had the Spanish bean soup, and it was very hearty.

For dessert, I had the guava and cream cheese empanada. That is such guava goodness.

The place is very clean, and the staff is super friendly.

I am always on the hunt for good Indian food, so when I saw that Royal Orchid had opened up near my office, I decided to head there for lunch with a coworker. We arrived and there was a line of people at the front, but no one to seat us. Eventually a gentleman came over and explained that if you wanted anything other than the Indian buffet (it looked like they also offered traditional bar food), you were going to wait a long while. Fine by us...we wanted the buffet anyway. He said to seat yourself and they'd come around with water. We went up to check out the buffet and joined 10 other people waiting for plates (I guess they had run out). On our first trip, they were completely out of rice, a chicken dish, and vegetable pakora. That's OK, plenty of other options. The food was very good. Authentic Indian flavors and well made. I can't remember everything I tried but there was a good variety from palak paneer to butter chicken to goat curry. After we finished our first plates, we still didn't have napkins or water. I went to the bar to request them and the lone bartender/server for the entire restaurant said he'd be over. Second trip to the buffet - no naan. When they finally brought naan out, they were out of plates again. We eventually got water and napkins, went up to the bar to pay our bill, and left.

The Indian food is very good here (I can't speak to the other cuisine). But they've gotta figure out the service. I don't blame the employees - they were pleasant. The restaurant was very busy, couldn't keep up with the demand, and didn't provide essentials like water and napkins. Hopefully with time they can figure out those kinks. If they do, I would definitely return.

I saw this place one day and dropped in, on my way back. Now, I come here ALL THE TIME!! I always order the same thing...Quarter chicken, yellow rice with veggies and yuca.  I tried Yuca here for the first time trying to be adventurous and it's delicious.  The food here is slappin!  The curry sauce and cilantro sauce, also.  The service is excellent and I can come in here at 11 am and be out in 5-6 minutes.  What more can you ask for? Fast, courteous and good!  Worth my money!!!

This was a cute spot. You wouldn't know it was here just by the outside. My boyfriend was unsure at first but when we walked in the inside was nice and my boyfriend said it reminded him of Columbia. The servers were nice and gave us plantain chips when we sat down. We ordered the fajitas and they came out in a timely manner. The waitress asked if I wanted hot sauce. I said yes and it was pretty hot but good :-) I came here because of groupon but would come back.
Owners were friendly and knowledgable. Great place to chill with a cigar. They have a very cost effective BYOB system that gives a cigar smoker a lot of list cost options to enjoy his cigar.

We were throwing a surprise "sprinkle" brunch for our friend. It was a party of 10. We wanted no lines and somewhere we can make reservations. That's a tall order on a Sunday in Philly. Talula's Garden was the place, a local farm to table. A STUNNING restaurant. A Steven Starr. From the valet guys chasing my hat that flew off and grabbing onto balloons we brought for her down Washington Square, and the hostess who was as sweet as could be to our adorable waiter that went above and beyond, the service itself was top notch all the way. The only thing that boggled my mind was that they were a farm to table that didn't have a salad on the menu. Hmm. That's crazy! My friends were ordering up their steak and eggs and pork belly with beet salads. Their gourmet cheese plates on classic slate trays (I asked our fantastic waiter where they were from...Brooklyn Slate. I bought a few for when we have company. I'm obsessed with charcuittere). Anyway,   I selected accompaniments from other dishes they serve and asked for them combine them on a bed a greens. Here's the custom salad they so graciously created for me. It was delish and I felt so special. Caramelized brussel sprout slaw with smoked honey crisp apples and roasted Kennet Square mushrooms in a sesame ginger vinaigrette. It was perfect! We dined at a big farmhouse table and laughed all afternoon. The drinks were flowing...I had a fantastic Rose', the Bloody Marys were perfectly spicy, fresh squeezed orange carrot juice and I ended my meal with the most delicious white peony tea in a perfect pop of neon tea pot while the girls enjoyed lemon cake, sundaes and dark chocolate salted caramels Eric, our handsome knight in shining armor waiter had specially made for the table. I cannot end this without taking about the decor. Clean and classic subway tiles, candles in over sized tins on the floor, live plants, books and pops of color. Completely rustic yet modern. The gardens is obviously what Talula's Garden is all about just check out the pics below. I'm excited to come back and sit outside at night. A five star, A plus experience all around. www.Talulasgarden.com

Soho Sushi is located in South Tampa off Kennedy across from Fox News doppler radar weather tower and 2 blocks from Dale Mabry. Soho Sushi has top notch sushi and is one of my favorite sushi bars in Tampa! I Highly recommend the volcano, caterpillar, mexican, soho, love, and Alaskan rolls! Ambiance is modern and authentic Japanese with Japanese music playing. Soho sushi does not have a full liquor bar. They only serve beer and wine. Soho sushi gets packed so make sure to call ahead with reservations or you could be faced with a 30-45min wait before you will be seated. There is very limited seating available for those waiting to be seating so a lot of people hang out outside.  The on-site private parking lot is tiny and they do tow in the back as there are some spots reserved for an apartment complex which is next door so you may have to park down the street on the curb. Service has always been quick and cordial and the food has arrived in a timely manner in no more than 10 minutes. Aside from the sushi, the ginger salad and Winton soup are very good here. Soho sushi also serves other delicious seafood dishes. Warning: Not open on Sunday's or Monday's!  Thanks guys! :)

I love Indian food and have been on a hunt for decent Indian food in the southern parts of Tampa for a while.  Royal Orchid is not a bad option.

I tried out their lunch buffet.  For $11.95 you have a decent selection of curries.  I found the vegetable curry way too salty, but the spinach paneer was quite good and so were the garbanzos.  The goat curry was tender and wonderful, but the butter chicken was utterly forgettable.

The location is clean and a good deal more comfortable (and roomy) than the nearby India's Grill.  Also, my waitress was very sweet and attentive.

I've been going to Barbara and her team for 7 years! They are always fantastic and accommodating! I've grown my hair out from a super short pixie- I can't even remember who did it- Peggy? Vicki? Any how- I love these ladies and Jai! I often go back and forth between darker and lighter shades of blonde and Barbara handles it with the certain panache only a true hair colorist with her experience could! The salon has a very approachable comfortable vibe- I have never felt pressured to buy any thing or to get a blow dry-  this place is like home to me!

Housewife Bakery is an institution in Tampa since it's inception in 1959. It is a small, nondescript stand alone building on the Southeast cornor at the intersection of Sligh and Armenia Ave.  Ingress & egress can be quite a feat, as this bakery is quite busy, has limited parking & is right at a busy intersection.  Please use caution entering and exiting this store.
Housewife Bakery is an independent,  fail owned and operated store. Once you enter this is so evident by their pride in workmanship & products. They do a large amount of catering of delicious sandwiches, pastries, and assorted baked goods.
Upon entering the store your senses are delightfully as sultry with all of the wonderful and varying smells that hit you so very nicely in a head on fashion. Now the decisions get even more difficult. You may have stopped in for bread or cupcakes, but once you see & smell the bountiful array of products, all sense of reasoning start to leave your head and you start operating purely on your sensory desires. 
They make many homemade sandwiches on a variety of breads and buns. Believe it or not, one of my favorites is their egg salad asndwich. It is simple, but complex at the same time and has just the perfect blend of seasoning and light mayo to make it perfect. Don't worry, their selection is large and all quality ingredients, you won't be dissatisfied. 
The Bakery carries almost anything you can imagine, with a heavy emphasis on Spanish specialty. Their flan is exceptionally creamy, rich & decadent.  Cookies, pizza, cakes, ...let you imagination run wild, or better yet, get over there and try it for yourself. After all these years in business, you know they have to be doing most everything right, which is also evident by their loyal fan follwing.
Birthday cakes ordered here are true works of art, both visually & in taste. Let them make your life a little easier, you won't regret the sweet rewards.
 What's a girl to do? Love independent stores that continue top quality for over 50 years.

Came here for lunch with my parents today and have been here several other times.  We really enjoy the sashimi specials here because they are fresh and and the rolls are very creative and large.   I would say this is my favorite sushi place in Northeast Philadelphia and its a byob. My favorites include:  sea food soup and 3 love roll for appetizer.  Felix roll, lexus roll, don roll, and spicy tuna are my favorite rolls.  The service is really good and the ticket times are fairly fast. I think for Bustleston ave  its a little pricy but it is really good and great quality. I would definitely keep coming here.

I am always wary of an Indian restaurant situated in a hotel as they usually are pretty bad. I am delighted to say that this is the exception. The place is pretty huge compared to most Indian restaurants, massive bar that is inside on one side and outside on the other and there is also outdoor seating.
The staff here isn't that great, slow, and always seem to be concerned about something other than the guests. I came for lunch so I was asked to seat myself where ever I wanted. They had the buffet in the back but you could also order off the menu which had bar food, thai food, indian food but it seems like they try to steer you away from the menu. No matter, I wanted the buffet anyway.
They had a rather good selection of items such as Tandori chicken, butter chicken, chicken curry, palak paneer, lentils, rice, pakora, naan, and some other vegetarian dishes. Here they actually had goat curry! It is hard pressed to find a lunch buffet with goat or lamb curry, usually I see it on weekends or on certain days of the week but they tell me they have it all the time.
The food tasted surprisingly good which was a shocker for me as I find most Indian food in Florida is third rate compared to what I'm used to in NYC. This is now my second favorite Indian place in the Tampa area, number one is in St. Pete, and this is after trying numerous places between Orlando and St. Pete.
Will I be back? Been back twice more already, mostly because my office is just down the street. Still, I would recommend this place, I know I'll be back again.

Lovely place to shop and get fleeced of all your hard earned money.  Coffee is good, produce is good, flowers used to be reasonably priced for a dozen roses but not any more.  A nice and expensive shopping experience.  Trader Joe's might be a good idea and Whole Foods also is much larger and again nosebleed high prices. You are paying for the lovely ambiance.  Someone has to pay the rent, right ?

Yes, when Yelpers agree--it means a lot! Antigone is a great book and gift store. They love books and they have a message too. A nice place to visit, get ideas, gifts and books for those who still read paper books and haven't yet discovered the wonderful joys of an e-reader. 
The people who work here are the very best. 
A true "Tucson Treasure."

The environment is awesome I really love the upstairs it gives in a city feel. The service was excellent and friendly. I liked the menus,  not only for what they had to offer but they were designed very well. I started off with something I've never had before, a seaweed salad. It was amazing!! The sushi was of high quality and inexpensive. I am excited to go there again.

Their xiao lung bao (soup dumplings) are definitely wayyy better than Dim Sum Garden. The skin wasn't tough or chewy, not skimpy on the meat and it was soupy! It's no Joe Shanghai, but won't complain.. This is by far one of the best places in Philly for soup dumplings. Service was great also. The waiter described some items to me in detail and also talked us into getting the beef pancake that I didn't even look at. The beef noodle soup was also very flavorful. Love that unique taste in it. Food came out in a reasonable time. Will definitely be back to try some of their other items.

My favorite pho place EVER. I come here too much and I probably will never stop. The NY Style is the best. Nowhere else does it and I hate that. Never fails to please me. Waiting staff is so great to be around and always attentive and nice. I would be devastated if this place ever closed

Sampan is probably my favorite restaurant in Philadelphia. I had Izakaya (in Borgata AC) and was informed Sampan had the same chef which was much closer to where I live. Edamame dumplings are a MUST thing to get when you are here. They're like angelic pierogies. The Kobe Beef Satay is melt in your mouth good. The chef's tasting is so worth it if you want a little bit of everything. Restaurant week here is superb, I've come almost 3 times now for it.

This was my first brazilian steak house i've ever been to and I loved it. I came during restaurant week too so it was only $35. Our waitress was an angel and I had a wonderful experience. I would recommend it for anyone that loves meat

I had a great time at winter fest. I brought my own smore materials since I was with kind of a larger group. The only issue was it was quite windy outside so the wind kept blowing smoke in our faces so our eyes were blood shot red by the end of the night. The drinks were great, I got baileys and hot chocolate. The only thing I didn't expect is that the skating goes by the hour, so I got there around 7 and skating was sold out until 10pm. Obviously I wasn't going to wait for 3 hours to ice skate. So definitely take note of that.

I came here for restaurant week and I had a pleasant time. The interior of this place is very romantic and intimate but the food quality was okay. I don't know if it's just like that on restaurant week because it was quite busy but I wasn't too impressed .Dessert was wonderful. I would like to come back here on a night that's not restaurant week to give it a permanent review

loved this place during restaurant week. we had to wait for a little bit at the bar because we were a little bit late but the wait staff were very friendly. the view is great so be sure to request a seat near the window when you make the reservation. i love the saffron risotto here and the desserts are to die for.

I came here for restaurant week because a couple of my friends are obsessed with this place. It was too small and dark for my liking and I was seated right near the kitchen which I didn't like. But who doesn't appreciate BYOB? The steak was too tough but the fried oreos are delicious

Who doesn't like handmade Italian pasta? I came here for a birthday dinner and I got to try the blue crab ravioli, escargot and the seafood risotto. The escargot was too smoky for my liking but the crab ravioli was STUFFED with crab, delicioso. The seafood risotto was a little soupy but packed with seafood which was wonderful. The chef's tasting here is so worth it. I will be coming back here to try the other handmade pastas.

Time is one of my favorite bar in Philadelphia. I never spend much time in the jazz bar but the whiskey bar is great. I always get a drink called "Green Tea." I think it's meant to be a shot but I love it as a drink. The whiskey bar is great when you get a table and you want to sit and drink with some friends but if you want to dance to some 2 Chainz or Beyonce go right on up stairs to the dance floor. It is quite dark up there but that's what DFMO friendly places are for. 

The bar upstairs gets quite crowded be sure to grab a drink downstairs. I've also had a valentines day dinner here at the jazz bar. it was quite loud and we had to yell to have a conversation. i wouldn't recommend that they continue dining seating in the jazz bar if they have loud music players

If you're someone that wants quantity over quality then this is the place. If you're on a budget this is a great place for a date. I got a hibachi dinner and sushi for under 20 bucks. You cant get there anywhere else. The steak was a little bit chewy but the hibachi rice was on point. I was a little sketched out by the sushi but hey don't ever be a brat about 6 bucks sushi yanno? I would come back here if I'm ever running low on cash and want a smorgasboard of food

Love Love Love this place.
Great atmosphere, Wings are the best I've had in Tampa.
Local Hang Out for those wanting something different in the South Tampa area, Sports Bar & Clean, Way better than average "bar food" Thumbs up!!!

This place didn't wow me. I went with two of my friends and our sole purpose was to try their soup dumplings. My two friends absolutely adore them so we were very excited about this place when it first opened up. We got two orders of the regular soup dumplings and two orders of the crab meat one to share among us. We had it and we all agree that is was good but nothing to swoon over. We all actually prefer it elsewhere so I doubt we'll be coming back for soup dumplings. We might come back to try other things on the menu but for the soup dumplings, no.

I do have to mention that the service was a bit chaotic but they all did try  to give us the best possible experience. It was a bit annoying being checked on by multiple servers but I did indeed see their sincerity in giving the best experience so I wasn't particularly annoyed. It was just a bit disruptive when trying to have a conversation with friends.

I'm so glad the Lodge has its own review page! I can't stand up straight on ice skates, but this pop-up lodge next to the rink? It's wonderful! You can come here, drink some beer and pretend winter isn't completely awful and gross for a couple hours.

I came here during the Brewfest event so my experience might have been different than on a regular night. The lodge was packed to the gills with people sampling away with neon green bracelets on. PS: What a great event idea? Maybe making it ticketed entry would have been better to control the crowd flow a little bit, but otherwise, thumbs up!

It's not heated inside, but it's not as cold as it is outside, of course. Plus, you can distract yourself by playing some fun games, like the tabletop hockey game! Fun, fun.

Beer selections and prices were great. Food? Not so much. My burger was so sad looking. It was the most pathetic burger I had seen in a long time. It was definitely not worth the price. I recommend eating ahead of time. I was still hungry after finishing it, which is saying quite a bit.

You can also head outside to a colorfully lit area where there are also perfect photo-ops with Mr. Ben.

Yep. Big fan.
Came here with a group of friends for lunch when they first opened. This spot, by far, has the best crab soup dumpling in Philly. Better than dim sum garden.  Their other dishes were just so-so.

EXTREMELY overpriced Spanish food! At first, I thought it was kind of neat and convenience having a Spanish restaurant down the street. Especially for the days where my fiancé and I just don't feel like cooking. Having just payed $32 for pork and fajita chicken, I think I can muster up the energy to cook a real Spanish meal for my family! Oh yeah, and for 1/4 of that price. Advice: Lower your prices(especially with Pollo Tropical around the corner)!

The service is great! The best is the beers! 50 oz. beer for 12.99! Moreover, there are about 50+ televisions, which if you request a channel, they will cater to you. Additionally, they have speakers for each table that give you the ability to choose whichever channel you want to watch/listen.

Hmmmm i was confused about what to eat tonight
I passed this place and i thought yeahhh we will try why not...the place looks new and clean

But the food is way beyond expectation
Compared to other chinese or asian resto like empress,penang,tai lake,sang kee or jade harbour even mai lay wah...thumbs down

Dissatisfied! Sorry from the chicken...soup or veggies...not a fan...i was about to peek who is the chef...
The portion is very small..and most importantly unflavour

I'm sorry to say...but i won't recommend this place :(

This was easily one of my favorite things to do this winter. Both times I went, I brought my children and over those visits, we did ice skating, smo'res by the fire pit, rocked in the rocking chairs, drank beer and cocktails and enjoyed food from the food truck and indoor restaurant, and played games in the arcade. 

I loved all of the variety of activity that ranged from things to do with the kids and adults. One of the weekend nights we went, there was free beer tastings and quizzo. So fun. Some of the stuff is really pricey though. I wish ice skating wasn't so much and the $8 we spent for a smo'res kit was ridiculous (4 marshmallows, 1 chocolate bar, and some graham crackers). Our marshmallows were super stale too, yuck. But other than that, this place is a blast!
seems nice a little on the pricy side for me, but it's a nice place. it doesn't  have that dive bar feeling that makes  feel at home but a nice place.

Saw signs for this place during construction a few months back. Haven't had the chance to try it out but forced my friend to walk here for lunch. 

We kept the order brief. One pork and one crab soup dumplings. The restaurant itself is nicely designed and clean. The lunch rush wasn't that busy so our service was good. Although I felt the waitress kept coming to our table too much to check if we finished our dumplings. I'm glad they give you a lid to keep the xlb to stay warm.... But the waitress kept opening it to check if we're fine so she could take it. That was a little annoying. 

Overall I thought the xlb were good, but the soup itself wasn't smack you in the face delicious as the other hot spot in ctown. Don't get me wrong... It's a good alternative. I don't like vinegar in general... So I opted to not use their sauce... The xlb still tasted good. 

We ordered the udon noodle stir fry dish. Came with bok choy and some meat. It was actually really good and enjoyed it a lot. And would order that again. 

The tea was strange tasting. I drink a variety of teas and this tasted a bit like brown rice tea but with another flavor I can't pin point. I'm cool with regular green tea please. Wasn't pleasant.

I think the hot sausage fish cake combo is still kicking around my stomach from a week ago.

While Philly is known for the cheese steak and roast pork sandwich, the wiser choice is hunting down Johnny Hots, where you can not only get the above, but get atleast the fish cake sandwich....adding the hot sausage will only make it better.

My life changed on Monday when i was taken to Johnny Hots.  Let it change yours too.

Their driver refused to get out of the car and knock on the door because of a Beware of Dog sign. Their excuse was that the driver didn't want to rile up a dog..... seriously?? SO is he afraid of the dog or the SIGN. because I could just as easily not have the sign up

THEN when I called an hour and forty five minutes later to have the delivery resent...They didn't even bother to apologize AND they sent the same (and by this time) COLD pizza. I have international guest and spent the better part of the evening raving about the ONLY pizza place we order from.... WON"T be making that mistake again. 

I asked them to remake the pizza and send it out. It is supposed to take 20 minutes now... I will change the rating if they manage to do something right this evening.... So far the level of disappointment is staggering!



UPDATE:   The delivery came with 30 minutes which was acceptable.  The new delivery person was crazy nice but unfortunately  missed on a tip because my guess were LESS than pleased with the overall treatment of the situation by the "night manager." I promised the delivery person that I would have her tip on the next delivery order, not thinking that I would not be ordering from them again.

So, in an attempt to make good on my promise, I ordered again tonight. I am really hoping that this goes well considering that I really do like their food. If it does go well I will my star rating.  Fingers crossed!

I pass this place almost every day and despite the friendly chalkboard, it's a little bit hard to see what's inside.

Finally came here with 8 friends after a cold and windy bike ride. Incredible pastries and empanadas. Delicious coffee. Warm and friendly service -- the cafe wasn't too full at the time but they handled the chaos of a large group very well. Will definitely be back!

Service has really gotten sloppy here. It's been a while since I have visited, and the usual wait staff that I dealt with are no longer there. Service has slowed down significantly and it takes forever to get anything. It's quite ridiculous how long it takes for a simple arepa. Before this latest visit, it would take 10 minutes max for the food to come out. It's been 30 minutes and I still don't have anything food. 

It's completely unacceptable for people who are trying to eat during their lunch hour.

Everything is casual here... The decor, the menu, the service, the ambience, the customers, and the vibe. This is not a bad thing.

Don't let the casual nature fool you. The food is excellent, and we were pleasantly surprised. My wife grew up eating here, and I'm so happy she took us on our last visit to Tampa.

I had, and recommend the classic seafood pasta and the fresh oysters on the half shell. I also had some local craft beers.

We're definitely coming here next time we're in town. It's a family tradition!

What a terrific new addition to Chinatown.  This new restaurant is nice on the eyes and great on the tongue.  The food is Shanghai and Taiwanese cuisines .... and a good idea is probably to order something from both of these Chinese culinary traditions.  The one appetizer you must try without a doubt are the incredible beef scallion pancakes from Taiwan.  With beef, scallions, and an oyster sauce inside - you will not want to share them with anyone else at your table.  You want to eat them all yourself! 
And to add to the great dining experience at this restaurant, the service was extremely attentive and friendly.

With the current Korean chicken craze in full flame, I'm planning on coming back to Shanghai 1 to try their Taiwanese lightly salted chicken.  Everyone tells me it is really tasty.  Judging by what I've already eaten at this restaurant, I believe it!

What a cool concept. Byob they will mix a cocktail. There is a corkage fee. But it's so low key, comfortable and friendly. Everyone was so accommodating. They also have hookah. A nice mix of folks. You can buy cigars or bring your own $8 charge. We came in and had a wonderful espresso. We will definitely be back.

Great new entrees. Loved the Pappardelle Pescatore $16.99. Pricey but worth it. 
At 5:30 on  Friday, Olive Garden wasn't prepared for such a crowd, but the manager seemed to rally the staff and we were fortunate to get Jay. He was probably overwhelmed with tables on two levels, but pulled it off with humor and timing. We never felt his pressure and it was probably considerable. He was informative and attentive.

Up until recently I've always had somewhat of a mental block running on West River Drive but that seems to have disappeared and I really enjoy this route.  It's nice that it is closed for recreational purpose April through October but with the amount of race held on this road there close more frequently that that one weekend's.  It can be a little bumpier than Kelly Drive especially if you are biking but it's less crowed which can be a plus during busy workout times.  I recently went running after one of the snow storms and the sidewalk was basically cleared which was great.  If you are looking for a little more sun and less of a crowd West River Drive is the route to take.

The staff were not very friendly, but neither were they rude or unhelpful. 
Although going to Brass Tap I was expecting something more. I went there 3 times and they all seemed not to be very interested in talking about beer and giving recommendations. 
I will probably come back in a future because they have a few good beers but lack of friendliness keeps me away from being a regular.
Manager popped over. Friendly service..pleasant surprise.. free drink( margarita) have a 4 minute time limit count down on it to order..having baby lobster pasta and fried scallops..

Biscuits is one of my favorite breakfast restaurants. The food is consistently good, prices are average and the staff is always very friendly. They have a breakfast rush, especially on Sunday's when the church's let out, but that's pretty normal and it's definitely worth the wait. I've been to most breakfast places in that area and when the rush is on they are all really busy. However, I have never had to wait very long to get my meal at Biscuits. And as the name implies the biscuits are awesome!

The Lodge is fun. This is where you can sit down and hang out , grab those beers, spiked hot chocolates or Cider & Whiskeys. They have jenga for you to play, a curling table.

I really like their couch set ups -- it's really homey. They even have blankets for you to completely lounge out and hang out with your friends.

The cons -- the hockey tables are broken, both of them. IMO, either fix them or remove them -- they're useless.
The menu is pretty delicious sounding -- but a bit harder for someone that has stopped eating beef and pork. They do have a black bean burger but I wish there were more options. 

Overall, cool place to hang out

It was a very cold night when my boyfriend and I decided to do tapas style and visited new restaurants in Philly including BonChon, Pho 20 and BingBing Dim Sum. So by the time we arrived at ShangHai 1, we were somewhat full. It was tough deciding what to order and sadly we ordered our second soup dumplings for the night. It was not good. For my taste, it was very salty and had more filling than soup. I had to use the dumpling soup vinaigrette to tame down the salt. I told my boyfriend that maybe we picked the wrong item and that they might specialized in something else. My boyfriend replied that if they choose "Shanghai" for a name, they should first master soup dumpling, which he had in Shanghai and said it was done perfectly. For me the best soup dumplings in Philly is still at Dim Sum Garden. Anyway, I would like to come back here and try other foods and will surely update my review.

I found this to be a very nice store with lots of things I wanted to eat. 

They have several "bar" areas for salads, seafood salads, olives as well as lots of prepared foods including sandwiches and whole roast chickens in various preparations. They also have some bulk foods to choose from. Definitely a.gourmet vibe and prices. It's not a Save-a-Lot, but we all deserve to live well. 

The fish and meat sections are impressive. And they have some good cheeses. Nice store and lovely flowers. Several unusual and decorative ones like plains pussy willow and other decorative options. A pleasurable and relaxed shopping experience.

This restaurant is similar to dimsum garden but without the crowd. The Soup dumplings were okay. Nothing crazy. Also I'm not the biggest fan of them anyway. 
The beef pancakes are good. I had the pan fried seafood noodle and that was a typical safe choice. The Dan Dan noodles were basically just in oil. There wasn't really a peanut sauce flavor to it. Service was fine. No complaints.

I was very happy going into this weight loss experience.  Until the receptionist wouldn't listen to me. I told her someone else there had told me it would much less expensive then she was quoiting.  She said oh no. I had to have a $90.00 refund because sure enough she charged me to much.   so far we have only gotten a $45.00 refund.  I plan to stay with the program.  Alice the NP and Becky the nurse were very nice and helpful.  I hope from now on it will be an enjoyable experience.  I want to lose this weight and know I can do it this way.

$2.10 per shirt to wash and iron with starch. $2.75 per shirt for dry cleaning. They will get your cleaning done in a day. I like the low prices. They have always done a good job, since I started doing business with them for about a year and a half now. The only negative I have is they charge you 50 cents if you use a debit card for charges under $10.

SERVICE: 6.25/10
FOOD: 8.9/10
AMBIANCE: 6.25/10

This place is small and it does get packed.
The menu will surprise your tastebuds. It will impress a foodie. I love taking new friends here.

If you're looking for something quick, inexpensive, convenient and delicious - hit this spot up next to the big bright red barbershop building on armenia near waters.  It has less variety for their Arepas than other places I've been too, but that doesn't mean it's not just as delicious.  And I was introduced to their sweet cachapas that is truly addicting (I want one now!!).  The guy that served us had allot of recommendations and I am glad I tried everyone because he was right on the mark and I loved all of it.  I hope this place doesn't move because I am super lazy with going anywhere to pick up dinner during the week.  I love it!

It's great to find a cafe that both makes really good coffee--AND has its own personality, with empanadas, and special desserts and so on.  It's not overwhelmed with laptop-staring Pennsters, which is a plus. Sometimes, in fact, it may have book-reading Pennsters, which is commendable, ha.

The location just off the park is a bonus, as are the tables outside in nice weather. Their service is great and friendly, and since it's a small place, sometimes there can be a wait--but contrary to some of the more self-centered Yelpers--that shouldn't be a problem when you're in a nice corner cafe.

Prices are great for handmade food, certainly cheaper than Starbucks or other prefab coffee chains.

I come here as often as I can, and am never disappointed. I love bringing people who live not far away, who say, "Wow, when did this open?"

This was the first tea house I have EVER been to and it was PHENOMENAL. I loved all the teas and the little sandwiches and desserts. I went with my cousin and we both ordered high tea. I would reccommend this to anyone who likes fancy upscale places and a really relaxed atmosphere. I can't wait to go back again.

I really did try to like this place, and at first I did, but at this point, I can not stand it here anymore and I just can't wait to get out. 

This is a terrible place for anyone who likes peace and quiet. It's no different than living in a college dorm with young people having parties and fights every night. I have personally witness the cops called to various neighbors and one was even arrested. They're so loud, so obnoxious, so rude, and always drunk. 

My neighbors smoke pot in their bathroom and it seeps into my home. After a long day of work I come home to a pot smelling house. It is really depressing. If you tell the front office, all they do is put letters in your doors, that's it! No one does inspections or anything! 

The parking is TERRIBLE! It is not uncommon to have to park at a space far from your building if you come back home at night. 

Everyone here has a dog and no one picks up. Sometimes, I see dog poop bigger than what a human can do and they leave it there to rot. Some people won't even walk their dogs on leashes and just let them out to roam around while they're still inside their apartments. How lazy can you be! I have had my dog bitten twice by larger dogs that the owners just refuse to control. 

So pretty much, if you like drunken college party type people, live here. It's perfect for you.  My lease ends this month and I can't wait to go. I'm dying to leave )':

First time here and got the fresh cheese ravioli and homemade marinara. It was reasonably priced, and tastes amazing!!! The filling of the ravioli is creamy and light. The marinara sauce is perfectly spiced. Can't wait to go back

We stopped in for lunch after the Flower Show and were promptly seated - and that was the fastest service we experienced at 13.

We were seated at a table for four and were provided with a small, but varied and serviceable lunch menu with starters, salads, sandwiches and some other options including a vegetarian Portobello mushroom and cheese 'burger'.

The waitress took our orders about 15 minutes after we were given menus and then the wait began. 

The four of us ordered two 'Philly Cheese Steak' sandwiches, a cheeseburger and the hangar steak and eggs.  Not a difficult or time consuming order but it took well over 40 minutes for our food to arrive.

Based on the service, there was not a high expectation that the food would be hot or good - and luckily, the food was served both hot and it was tasty.  The 'Philly Cheese Steak' was not a true cheesesteak (and since we are in the town famous for cheesesteaks, it was odd) but it was a good sandwich served with very good French fries.  The cheeseburger as also cooked perfectly and served hot as was the hangar steak (served rare as requested) with a loose, sunny side up egg.  

The food was well above most hotel restaurant fare and we all felt our dishes were very good.

If the service was better - faster - and the dining chairs less shabby looking; the rating for 13 would be much higher.  

As it was, the ambiance and the service really knocked the rating down to a 'meh' from a very good overall; but the food was definitely above average.

Great atmosphere,  Andrew was fantastic, great presence. Other bartender kaitie was just as great. Awesome hookah and cigar selection. Great cocktails as well. It's a BYOB so make sure you bring your alcohol but it was awesome.

I went here earlier today with a bridal shower party of about 12 or 15 women. Our server was Kathy and she was wonderful. The set up and table display was very nice and surprisingly it wasn't crowded at all considering we were there around 11:30ish. We had waffles, oatmeal, sausage, bacon, eggs, and many more breakfast/brunch items which were all very good. My favorite tea flavor was the green tea. The strawberry kiwi one was good too.

Pretty good little restaurant! I stopped in one day for lunch, since I was in that area... and starving! I ordered what I usually order for a first time in a new Cuban / Latin restaurant: Lechon Asado, Arroz Amarillo y Frijoles Negro, y Platanos Maduros. (Cuban Roast Pork, Yellow Rice, and Black Beans, with a side of fried Plantains) It was all good, nothing to to complain about! Portions were pretty healthy also... customer service was friendly, and the place was clean and neat. I will have to try something else on my next visit!

Voted best Thai restaurant for many years & there is no mystery why!  You will see the restaurants many award plaques when you walk in.  I highly recommend the pad Thai shrimp,  cashew nut chicken,  panang curry steak, and red curry chicken. You can request spiciness by number out of 5. I usually get 3/5 with extra spices on the side for me to make it a little spicier as I see fit with trial and error.  The portion sizes have been recently increased by a little as well as the prices for all those that have been complaining about the smaller than average portion sizes. I usually make mine extra spicy so I eat it slower and don't need as much of a large portion. However, Thai Thani in Channelside still has the largest serving sizes and probably the best bang for your buck.  The parking lot for the Royal Thai palace is tiny with tight spots for only 6 cars or so. You may have to find parking elsewhere if it gets busy here.  This is a small restaurant not suitable for large parties of more than 6 people. Decor is traditional Thai with an impressive classy gold deer, modern sushi bar, and a cool aquarium with a puffer fish which will keep your attention if you have to wait to be seated.  However, I rarely have to wait to be seated.  Service is always quick and courteous. I have never waited for more than 5 minutes to be seated and I have always received my meal in under 10 minutes here. I look forward to many more visits! :)

Oystercatchers is a top notch up-scale classy choice for Sunday brunch, Lunch, or Dinner with a beautiful view of the bay!  For $45, they have unlimited champagne, mimosas, bloodymaries, oysters, shrimp, scallops, omelettes, banana foster, custom made pastas, salads, sushi, waffles, cakes, a huge desert bar, and much much more! Service was excellent and everything was very tasty! However,  I was missing the usual crab legs that are included in other brunch buffets like the Rusty pelican that offers a brunch for $35 just down the road or at Jackson's Bistro that offers a brunch for $25 in downtown Tampa on the water.  There is plenty of free parking and valet. The ambiance for this place is minimalist modern and open with large windows giving beautiful bay views. This establish also has seating at an outside patio with a fire pit. Additional facilities in the immediate vicinity of the restaurant include a nearby boat dock, Sandy beach, Tiki bar,  koi fish pond, and pool, and pool cabanas. For Dinner, I had the amazing very flavorful Mahi Mahi Fish Tacos with freshly cut jalapenos that added quite a punch! They let me substitute a side salad based off their large salads from their menu. I got the pear salad with arugula, kale, goat cheese, cranberries, and walnuts and it was amazing as well.

Have top notch California New Age Mexican pulled pork tacos, grilled tuna tacos,  steak tacos, Fajitas, and chips and salsa here! Great for Taco Tuesday with $2 tacos and $2 coronas! Regular price on tacos ranges from $3-$4 and $12 for the taco trio. Free parking is available across the street. We had quick friendly service but at peak times like on Taco Tuesday's there can be a long 30-50 min wait for tables. For those peak times, you can reserve a table on Green Lemon's website or you call in your reservation at 813-868-5463. If you can't make it on Taco Tuesday's, Happy Hour is every day including on the weekends at 2PM-7PM featuring $8 taco trio combo which includes any 3  tacos. Ambiance is hipster artsy modern with cool art drawn on the walls, red led backlighting, and club music that gets louder as the night wears on. Don't bring grandma! Lol If you are used to the traditional Cuban Ropa Vieja or Habanero Beef, do not order it here.  The $12 Ropa Vieja is missing the tomatoe sauce, black Beans, plantains, and diced olives. They cut in tiny pieces of plantains instead of having whole plantains on the side making the whole dish too sweet and only included 6 black beans instead of a separate bowl like other restaurants. I don't understand how they can in good conscience call this Ropa Vieja when it is missing several key ingredients and tastes nothing like Ropa Vieja. Go to the Columbia or La Teresita for a Real traditional Ropa Vieja. The Habanero beef just has spicy aoili for the sauce which has way too much mayo in it instead of the typical spicy Habanero tomatoe sauce. How can they call this Habanero when it just has aoili?  If you are used to traditional Margaritas, do not order these here either. They put a weird citrus blend instead of the typical sour mix and it tastes nothing like a regular margarita. I couldn't even drink it.  I had the waitress bring me another drink instead.  This place has a separated party room that you can reserve which can seat 20-25 people, perfect for any birthday or large group gathering. Great place to start off your evening in Soho!

A great place to get a healthy well balanced lunch close to the office with healthy gourmet wraps, paninis, sandwiches,  protein bowl,  soups,  salads,  and smoothies! They have a lot of healthy Mexican and Thai options. They have quesadillas, burritos, salsa,  and Mexican rice bowls.  I highly recommend the carnitas bowl, sesame Thai rice bowl, Thai red curry bowl,  southwest taco salad, Mediterranean salad,  southwest chicken soup,  Italian wedding soup,  spicy buffalo chicken wrap, fish taco wrap, Hawaiian crush smoothie with pineapple coconut and banana, survival kit with strawberry banana immune booster and protein booster.  They also do different hot lunches every day.  Some of my favorite hot lunches are tacos, Shepard's pie, cuban sandwiches, and orange chicken. My only complaint is I wish they stuck to the top 5 most popular lunches and just kept those for every week bc the hot lunches can be hit or miss. Prices range from $5.99 for wraps to $8 for salads and rice bowls or half sandwich with a soup or salad. For those unfamiliar, there is no table service. You have to walk up and get your order. There is plenty of free parking available. The ambiance is basic / casual. Location is in an office park off the memorial exit. The line can run out the door sometimes but usually doesn't take more than 10min to get food. The line really depends on that time you hit it. I usually come back 10min later if the line is bad.

Bern's Steak House is by far the best steakhouse in Tampa Bay! We've been coming here for years! Bern's is perfect for any special occasion! Make sure to call ahead and get reservations at least 2 weeks ahead. Also, make sure to take the wine cellar and desert room tour!  The decor and atmosphere is definitely very royal and elegant with red carpets and brick walls.  The Filet Mignon steak,  French onion soup, and salad with macadamia nut dressing is delicious! Berns has several pages of wines available by the bottle or glass to appeal to any wine aficionado. Surprisingly, there is a large selection of wines by the glass available at very reasonable prices under $8 per glass. Their Margaritas are not too shabby either. Make sure to go to the Dessert Room upstairs. Bern's has the best desserts in Tampa! The waiters have some incredible stories of presidential and celebrity visits!  Service is legendary here. Berns Steak House has the largest private wine cellar in the world and their own farm for growing all their vegetables organically and locally!

Improv comedy center is located on ground floor of Centro ybor. There is $1 per hour parking available in the nearby ybor garage 2 blocks away. There is not a lot of free street parking in ybor unfortunately. I've seen some great comedians at the improv comedy center of Tampa!  This venue has a great setup with 2 stories of seating. There is No 2 drink minimum like side splitters and it is a much bigger and more modern venue than side splitters. I Highly recommend that you check out some shows! If you sign up for improvs mailing list you'll be notified of free shows via email or text. I got the chicken quesadillas and some miller light brew skies to watch the show. They were average but I didn't have high expectations considering this isn't a authentic Mexican restaurant but with the limited menu options, that looked the best.  

Cons: Improv comedy center of Tampa really could use a larger selection for the dinner and drink menu. There's only 4 items on the menu! Improv Needs some grilled chicken or other healthy options, including salads.  Specialty Drinks are not cheap here at $8-$12 per drink but beers go for $4 and at least there are no minimum drink purchase requirements.

I look forward to seeing many more great comedians here in the future!
Fantastic place to go skate with your partner.  I wish there were other options to drink and eat.  The lines where so long that I decided not to have a beer.

Seriously the best gel manicure I've had yet. Kinda pricey but lasts on average three weeks for me and I'm constantly doing something with my hands. The massage while your nails dry is the icing on the cake. Always friend and always has a spot for me!

I love this place. Always great food,  great prices. Friendly service. You can bring your boo for a casual drink or your can bring the whole family. The time is always hot and fresh. This is definitely a sports bar/ slash family restaurant. They serve breakfast on weekends now.  Yay!
Food is too salty most of the time. Wish to see some more variety on the menu. Service has been good. Not my most favorite place.

Whoever called this the last place to find an honest mechanic was right.

My check engine light came on, and the AutoZone people couldn't narrow down the differential diagnosis, so they suggested Prime Performance.

I brought my car there, and within a couple hours, they called and explained that none of the easy fixes for my car's warning light fixed the problem, so they felt that there was an extensive problem going on.  My mechanic suggested that I take my car into the dealership as it was likely still under the power train warranty, so they could fix it for less than he could.

He didn't charge me anything since he didn't fix my car.

Wow!  What a nice guy with an excellent work ethic.

My fork was dirty with jelly, the 2nd half of my food came when I was done with my 1st half, some type of substance was on the outside of my juice glass, and since you have to pay at the front counter, this took over 10 mins with a small line due to an incompetent person manning the register.

During this 10 min wait, I left my party to use the bathroom. Both stalls were unusable and had to use a nearby gas station bathroom that was way more decent than this restaurant's.
Always used them without a problem until last time when we returned a 1 dollar bill which was a memento from a good friend who passed away was missing from our center console
Easy reservation. Quick check in and transfer to airport gate. Seamless. The

I usually pick up a Cuban sandwich and café con leche and I am never disappointed. The staff is great too. I go pretty often since they are just a couple of blocks away in Carrollwood. The restaurant, tables, chairs, floor and counters are always clean and same goes for the bathroom.

Kurt, who works here is phenom!  Kurt kicks the sh**t out of customer service, carrying to a wonderful higher and very rare level; sooo much better than similar cook & gadget stores in the greater Phila area.
  I initially called to inquire about a Bodum tea maker, he answered all my queries (many), never making me feel as if I was wasting his time and made certain he had the 3 liter size that I wanted in stock.  

My daughter (Princess Persnickety at times) went in to pick it up later today, came out to the car telling me "Kurt is really good, while I looked around, I didn't feel pressured or rushed at all."  And she also bought another item there.

While these 2 items were less than $50.00, I am thinking about getting some All Clad or Mauviel cookware, when I decide on what I need, it will be Kurt @Kitchen Kapers that I go to for my purchases.  If he was so professional and personable about a tea maker, it's obvious how much help he'll' provide with higher end goods

I have truly just become so sick of this place and their horrific customer service.  I just placed an online order with them through Eat24 and there was an error on the menu,  
So, the woman called me and told me that I ordered wrong.  That I can't combine wings and fingers. Apparently the special I ordered is no longer available. Not really sure how I am supposed to know that but hey, whatevs! 

She asked me if I made  an error and tried to order two separate items....I explained that I cannot alter the order form the menu.. I just point and click... she says the item has never been on the menu. So the woman is not even familiar with the menu... obviously new fine.. gave her some leeway.  I let her know that the item is actually listed in my favorites, we have ordered it a ridiculous amount of times in the past year alone.  She again states she doesn't have the combo. 

Okay but you have wings....? -- "Yeah"
and you have fingers....? -- "Yeah" 
Then what is the problem?--- "You can't order them together."
Sure fine, no problem, but we still want the items...  it is only a 2.50 difference from your new menu (I checked their website by this time)  and her response was...

"You can't order them together so you can call Eat24 and they will put your money back on your card"

Really?  Wow  That escalated rather quickly... I asked to speak with the manager and am told that he is unavailable. (yes he was standing right there within earshot of the phone when he responded).  UGH!!

SERIOUSLY! ?  Why oh WHY did I get sucked back in again? The carrot cake...so sad..so so sad.... I did it to myself thinking they were fixing their issues.

 To quote the infamous, 'Fool me once, shame on ... shame on you. Fool me... You can't get fooled again!'

Anywho... I called Eat24.  They straightened it out.  But boy oh boy have I been burned for good this time.  Steer clear of this place.

This place needs a new manager and as far as the server Kate she was terrible. She didn't speak she never said thank u or your welcome... She never asked if we needed anything else or how we were doing... She acted bothered. Food was cold service was terrible I'll never be back.. Wait for this... I'm updating cause it's a couple days later and they took a five dollar tip unauthorized out of my credit card when I put a line through the tip area!!!

Arepas are my current food crush, and this place takes the cake! Corn cake, that is.

They have bizarre hours and travel around Tampa, so it can be hard to track them down. It's definitely worth it though! I tried the Nico's Beef Special, and it really is up there with one of the tastiest sandwiches I've ever eaten. It's absolutely loaded with toppings. As in: you will be covered in food if you're trying to eat this in your car. Just let it happen, and relish in it's deliciousness. The white cheese they use is nice and salty, which is a big thumbs up in my book. If you go for any sauce, you have got to try their green cilantro sauce. It's killer!!!

My ONLY complaint is that they don't really do lunch hours (like say, around noon). I can't wait 'till 4pm for a lunch arepa! Hopefully the stars will align again where I can eat another Nico arepa in the near future.

Love this theater and love the location! There are dinner spots around and who doesn't love dinner and a movie! Okay so love the large theaters the super duper great sound and the concession stand options! The usual popcorn (buy the loyalty shirt $25 for a whole year free medium popcorn or upgrade to large or extra lg for quarters on the $1) soda (loyalty cups like $6 and free refills for a year) and candy! Oh wait there's more- they have personal pizza s, cheese sticks, popcorn chicken and more! Oh and for those screaming babies they have a daycare!!! Please check in those screaming babies on an opening weekend- please!

This store is okay.  I usually end up shopping here if I'm looking for an item that might be 'fancier' than a typical grocery store would carry.

There are many positives to this store: ready-made foods, great specialty salad selection in the deli, large variety of cheeses, many different kinds of ground coffee, interesting selection of beers, and nice selection of organic produce.

However, they are a few negatives, too:  the parking lot is hard to get in and out of, costs for many common items are exorbitant, there are never enough cashiers open for check out, the cupcake I bought from the bakery was stale, and, affecting me directly, there is this one guy behind the deli counter who has been a jerk to me a couple of visits there.

So - between the jerk and the prices, I don't shop here very often.

The chips and salsa wear great for us and the salsa has a little kick which is nice compared to most places water downed stuff.  The carne asada had great flavor and the chimichanga dough or tortilla was finished perfectly.
A more scenic and often quicker alternative to the hell of 76.

Perdigon el Sabor is my first experience with Venezulan cuisine. 

First there are a few points of interest:

First of all, it is VERY easy to miss as it is wedged between Hank's catfish BBQ and a KFC. Honestly, the haristyling place draws your eye before you see Perdigon in the corner,trying to hide.

Second, the parking lot is miniscule. One of the few advantages Tampa has over places like Chicago should be ample parking for even the dinkiest of business. I came in around 2 pm, and by the grace of God I was able to park. Note: Dont try any Fast and Furious style antics in here

The restaurant is nicely decorated, modern yet classy. However it seems to suffer from Chicago popular restaurant syndrome- extremely tiny yet popular with patrons. 

I ordered a lulu drink, 1 cachapa, and an Arepa el sabor.
The server was nice and spoke mostly Spanish to me(maybe she thought Brown guy =Hispanic?)
The lulu , a freshly made drink made of a citrus (naranjilla?) was refreshing.

However, from the time I ordered 2:05 pm, I waited and waited. Despite the assurance from the server,

 I waited 46 minutes for my cachapa and arepa. 

The cachapa with guyanes cheese was nice. It is a corn based pancake with a nice melted cheese. It has sweet and savoury elements. I would consider it a breakfast or lunch item IMO.

The arepa was very good, It is a corn based sandwich esque item with chunks of chorizo, chicken, and avocado with the aforementioned cheese.
The chicken was grilled and juicy as was the chorizo. 

One  thing I give them major props for is the avocado. In most restaurants in the US, avocado is usually upcharged to any item and when it comes out, it is usually some processed shlock or stale. Thankfully, the avocado slices were tasty and fresh. Yay for aguacate!

FYI the prices on the website are outdated.
 The arepa el sabor is $7.00 The cachapas with guyanese is $6.75

Honestly the arepa was pretty big, so you may not even need anything else for lunch.

Despite the great food which is 4/5 IMO, the extreme wait time is unacceptable. 
I went on a weekday afternoon;I'm glad I didnt try and come on the weekend lol.
I didnt order a lot of items or anything out of the ordinary and I was by myself.

I would come if you have no time restrictions and on a weekday. 
Im a quick eater and it took me 1 hour 15 min just to get in an out.

Good food, but all these factors make it an occasional jaunt IMO

Panera is back in the game!  History:  Panera used to have great fresh food and plentiful servings and then went through a spell of a redesigned menu with tiny portions and reduced 'wow" ingredients so we stopped going. I have had the opportunity to meet for two business lunches at this restaurant over the last couple of weeks and have been super impressed that they are now serving great fresh food with normal size portions again!  Yeah!  This will return them to my staple list of great salad, soup and flatbreads once more.  If you haven't been in a while be sure to check them out - you will be pleasantly surprised - and very full!

This is my go-to spot for Viet food when I don't want a banh mi (since they don't offer that). I usually get the bun bo nuong-grilled beef with vermicelli. I think their beef is higher quality than other places. It's really good and the bowl fills me up. They don't skimp. I love that they serve free jasmine tea and a fried dessert, although sometimes they don't give me the fried dessert for whatever reason. Service good although the waiter's English is very limited, decor homey but clean, prices very good.

We went on a Sunday morning for breakfast. Our waiter was Mario, he was great and always had our tea filled. Carne Machaca burrito was delicious and my partner had Carne Asada burrito it too was very tasty. Their salsa is flavorful with a kick which I love. Would be nice if their burritos came with side of beans and rice (burrito by itself is not very presentable) also wouldn't hurt to put sauce and cheese on burrito (not just tortilla). Overall we were pleased so we will be back.

This is the second time I had been to this festival. The first time was that when we brought our students ts with us. I enjoyed it then but never had the chance to try everything due to the fact that  I was with bunch of my students.

This time I went with my friends and we had a blast. We had the chance to watch the bawdy boys. While it was raining  so bad! But that didn't stop us to have fun! When the rain stop for a lol bit we had the chance to work around and to search for the place for the wine tasting and ended up again at the same bar were we watch the bawdy boys. And again had the chance to watch them. And to have the most greatest toast ever! 

Looking forward for the next renaissance festival!
Its a good place , cheap beer and has natty on tap.  Lots of tvs. The food is good for a bar kinda Restaurant like. It's clean good place to meet up and drink.
THE best sushi in the northeast. Fresh and delicious. The atmosphere is pretty and it's very clean. Worth a visit!

Micha's catered a family party I had last week.  The food was delicious, hot and very appealing.  They deliver right to your home and help you set up the food.  The price couldn't be beat... I chose the Traditional Mexican Buffet to feed 20 adults for under $200 including tax and delivery.  All my guests loved the food and talked about doing this again for our next party.

Love a good neighborhood spot. Mike and Andre make you feel at home. They make great hooka flavors using real fruits to enhance the flavors. It's a byob so bring a bottle or some Beer.  They have great set ups for whatever you bring in to drink. Friendly and inviting crowd every time.  Weekends they even bring in live music and a Dj.

This location gets 5 stars from me because I just have no complaints. My order is always ready on time, my pizza and sides are always fresh and tasty, and the customer service is always great. I always order online so I cannot speak on the order process but when I come in to get my order everyone is always very friendly and I appreciate that.

So I'm sitting here enjoying my delicious #14 with cheese, and I pull up this location's Yelp page.  2.5 stars?  Really?  Did someone get freaking SPIT ON by an employee or find a live insect in their sammich?  I mean, what the deuce is going on here?  So I started reading reviews, and wow...  Really Yelpers?  

To the chic who was upset because the menu says "gourmet sandwiches," I think you need a lesson in satire.  You obviously didn't see all the signs hanging up around the building that read, "Customer is usually right" or "free smells" etc.  Everyone knows there ain't nothing "gourmet" about JJ's subs, just like everyone knows McDonald's doesn't sell real hamburgers.  The menu is facetious.  

Unbelievable.

I guess I've eaten at this particular location 5 or 6 times since it opened up 3 or so years ago, which is a lot considering I live no where close to it, and all I can say is that my bread has always been fresh, I've never had a salad's worth of lettuce on my sandwich, the service has always been friendly and "freaky fast."  15 minutes from order time to digestion time.  Additionally, the tables are always wiped down and ready for the next customer, so they've got cleanliness on lock down too.

Jimmy John's, I'm sorry some people just don't understand sarcasm or the concept behind your beautifully fast and simple service, but I do, so here's a freaking 5 star.  Keep pumping out them "gourmet" sandwiches!

I love Vietnamese food and have enjoyed everything I've tried. My favorite are the fresh rolls and the grilled beef over vermicelli... Yum!  
The waiter that I have been served by many times is very nice. 
The iced green tea is a nice touch.

I have visited this Olive Garden twice, both times when staying at the nearby Holiday Inn Express.

I cannot really recall last year so well, it obviously was at least par. However, my most recent visit was above average and prompted the review.

My son and I got dinner at the bar to expedite things since usually service it quicker. We got one of the two booths and the waiter came over after a short period of time. He was terrific through the whole service.
