Drinks were on the table quickly and orders taken immediately. I got the Lasagna Frita and my son the ravioli. We were already familiar with both dishes at Olive Garden. Bread and salad was brought out quickly and delish. The salad was fresh which has been an issue at other locations.

Within 20 min, or so the food was on the table. No issues and it was very good. The waiter stayed on top of refills. At one point the manager came over and checked on us too which is above average and appreciated. The check was brought promptly and checkout was a breeze. 

This review would be a four, but when walking out there was a lot of debris in the entry way stacked up in what looked like a flower pot, but could be something used to dispose of cigarettes. I surprisingly did not notice when walking in, but there is no way it was not there due to the amount. The restaurant was not busy and there were two hostesses in the front doing nothing. So what is the protocol for checking on the walking way, I don't know. But the amount of trash was significant enough for me to think they should be knocked down.

Great visit, great food, great service, clean up your act so you get the overall wonderful rating you would have got otherwise.

Love Xoom juice so much! The only downside is that now that the new owners started adding ice to the smoothies, which makes no sense because the fruit is already frozen. Really waters down the taste a lot. Other than that, the Grand Royal is phenomenal!

I can't believe this is my first review here. We love Pepo's. It's a casual, delicious and friendly restaurant. It is set up cafeteria style with a large menu board above you. I'm giving it 5 stars for the food and service. 
Wow- I've tried many items and they've all been great. Our favorites are the pork and the Cuban sandwich.  Lunches/dinners are very generous and takeout is great, too. 
The shredded pork is perfect!  We like it with the Moro rice, black beans, green beans and plantains. And, their flan is delicious. Also, an order of hot, buttered pan (bread).... Yum!

The coffee here is better than at Green Line and Milk & Honey Market. And if you buy 10, you get 1 free. I also like the tea selection. 

However, it's very small, there's no wi-fi, the pastries are so-so, and the service is not always top notch. I used to get morning coffee here before work, but I had to stop because the service was too slow. 

I would stand at the counter, trying to order coffee before catching the trolley to get to work, with no one in front of me, and the staff would be talking to someone (not in line) for a long time, without acknowledging me. 

I don't mind if people have different philosophical views on the concept of time, but maybe I'll buy coffee instead from a place that understands that people don't have all day to wait in line and can better adapt to different customers' needs.

Sat nearer to the front than I would prefer but it was the opening weekend of Avengers: Age of Ultron and I was with a group of 6 other people. I can't believe I'm saying this, but the volume was ear shatteringly high. As a child of a former rocker, I typically think the volume can't get high enough! But this was nuts. The manager came out to give the audience a bit of a pep talk - silence your phones, if you have little kids be prepared because this is a long, loud and action packed movie - nice thought on his part, but probably unnecessary. The seats were pretty comfy and the bathrooms were tidy.

If your looking for a great place to leave you vehicle for trips out of TIA then look no further. We recently used them and were very pleased. The staff was very friendly and helpful. They provided you with a safe and secure place to leave your vehicle while away and the shuttle to and from the airport was quick to arrive and take us as well as picking us up after our return flight. One tip I can add is to make sure and make a reservation prior to arriving at A1. If not you will not find a space in the garage and will have to valet your car. There was no extra charge for the valet but we did have to leave our vehicle in the front and trust that they would park and secure it safely.

Just went in last week. And several times before that. Been going here for four years since I moved here. It looks like from two one-star reviews that sometimes someone there botches it. I'm willing to bet it is a new employee because I've seen different people work there through the years. At any rate, I can only speak of my recent experiences which has been excellent and no complaints. Always get my garments back when they say. They are clean and pressed. Quick in and quick out.

So I've tried this place a couple times before I decided to write review. In a scale of 1-5 I give the food a 2, but I guess for the price I can't complain or expect more. Lunch plate is $6.50. The one small piece of baked chicken tasted stale and saturated with salt and garlic, the cassava (yuka) was over cooked and mushy. Second time I tried the pulled pork which was a much bigger serving than the chicken but dry and chewy. I wouldn't even attempt to try the fish. I work in the area and we kind of make this place a last option when ordering lunch. There are a couple Latin places near by that serve way better food for around the same price.
Best theater in town. Love the seats and love that I can put extra butter on my popcorn.

Came here for a meeting of 5 guys on a slow night.
We brought someone and ordered massive amount of sushi.  For the very generous price and the amount of sushi that we received, I really liked this place.  Its 2 floors and its a pretty good atmosphere.  I  dont know what a busy dinner night is like here but for less than $20/person you can have a feast.

We came here last Sunday, Mothers Day (of all days). The parking lot was full, walked through the door and were seated immediately (no waiting, right on!). I ordered their red chile chimichanga enchilada style and my partner ordered a carne asada combination plate. He wanted a small sample of their menudo and our waitress Lisa brought him a small bowl with lots of menudo (totally surprised by their generosity). He loved their menudo and thoroughly enjoyed his whole meal which he could not finish....leftovers. My chimi was huge, I could only eat half of it (took the other half home) and as always their chips and salsa were off the chain.....so good.
I have upgraded my star rating from three to five!

When Ari owned Xoom, it was amazing. The only downside was that there were ingredients out-of-stock every single time I went there. Now that there are new owners, they use ice to water down the smoothie which is really lame and a rip-off. They also have really bad employees..there is one guy that doesn't greet you when you walk in, or even look up at you for a few minutes until he is ready to take your order. He was incredibly rude. The smoothies taste so good, it's a shame that the experience going in there sucks.

This has been my preference of any BB&B location for as long as I can remember first shopping here.

Friendly employees are always around and I love how they seem to know when you need help finding something OR pull you over to the return/exchange desk to get you out the door faster.

For the most part, they never change the location of items. So, if you saw coffee makers one place on a previous visit, you'll find them there again. Same goes for kitchen stuff, bedding, towels, etc.

I hadn't bought a shower curtain in years, but I knew exactly where to find it based on my previous purchase and had plenty of options available at whatever I wanted to spend.

My new favorite item are the cutting boards shaped like states. Although effectively using the Florida one would seem to be a little difficult...unlike say Texas, Ohio or New York. 

Personally, I wonder if I can ever present them with a coupon past the expiration date that they actually would reject. 

20% off, $5 off or those rare $10 ones from many years ago...they never turn any of them down!
I'm not much of a cigar guy but every once in a while. Great looking space, very helpful staff and a huge selection.

We had a lunch at Micha's and although I wasn't impressed with the atmosphere or the standard chips and salsa, they were ok. When it came to the carne asada though it was some of the best I'd ever had. Very impressive and not at all that pricey. I had a chimi and the size was more than I could eat for a lunch meal and it was perfectly made.  The garnish was very good too with fresh peppers and not pickled. The rice and beans were average. Overall I would give the chimi a 5+ and the rest 3 stars. Worth the trouble to find for lunch.

i have come to terms with prices in philly, in comparison to socal where i can get a six pack of craft beer for $9...a six pack here will cost you at least $12 but it is the cheapest i've come across in this city. they are open late and have a decent selection of beers...lagunitas, stone, ballast point, etc. it's super convenient and service is always great. it's cash only but they have an atm inside.
Peggy gave me my 1st DevaCut. I had been growing out my chemically straightened hair for the last 1 year & 9 months. My hair was long but 1/2 straight/s

There are a lot of pizza options in all of Philadelphia, including West Philly / University City. And they all pretty much look the same: run down, corner store, same menu, same prices, geared towards college students or those on a tight budget. But this isn't one of those.. " they're all the same"

They have some pretty great creations here. Recently I tried their Sweet and Spicy Grilled chicken with bleu cheese which was great -- my palate tastes that it is bbq sauce mixed with buffalo sauce, and bleu cheese, great combo, great crust -- SOLID freaking pizza. 

Yesterday I tried their " wing zings" which were described by the owner as breaded, slightly spicy salted chicken wings. THEY WERE SO GOOD. I ordered 5. I could eat 20... alone. SO well crisped, so well seasoned , and so juicy on the inside. Holy crap, I am impressed by this " college pizza place"

I also tried their chicken cheesesteak which was solid. It's not the minced chicken that you may be used to, and also not chunks like at Lee's. It's somewhere in between, like shredded chicken with fried onions and cheese. I also added a bit of the sauce they gave us with the wings -- which to me tastes like marinara mixed with buffalo, which is yet again such a clever combo!

No, they probably don't make everything from scratch, but they are very creative with their ingredients. The owner was also really nice -- I didn't realize that they give  student discounts **** 15% off for USP/PENN/DREXEL students. That was really nice of him to suggest that for us when we didn't realize. THAT's how you get a repeat customer. Thank you for great food, and great service my friend!!
I enjoy this place a lot. The food and staff is good. It is a bit loud so be prepared, but it is a sports/Irish bar. The only thing I would say is, make sure you ask for your wings extra crispy!

Customer service is great! Staff is friendly including the owner E!! This my 2nd time coming.. So first time was on Friday night where there was a $10 cover charge. Parking is crazy after 8pm.. Friday night music was electric.. House music all night.. Initially not bad but I would've like to hear something else. There was a small with space at the bar the bar. The hookah lounge s of good quality, and reasonable. They have a nice selection of alcohol.. The hookah is the main attraction. This place is cozy and good for "boo loving "

I started TMWL back in March/April. Now it's June and, as of today, I've lost 40 pounds. 

I was at a point where I was eating whatever I wanted, whenever I wanted and I couldn't stop. Not only could I see the weight gain by looking in the mirror, but I could feel it in my clothes, skin, and joints. I made the decision to try RM3 and I haven't looked back. 

I was leery at first because, like so many others, I've tried all the diets. But I was at a point where exercise was painful or difficult (because of knee issues) and I needed a complete food-lifestyle change along with help. 

The location on Swan can be a little difficult to find at first (it's in the plaza where McMahon Steakhouse is but it's set on the far west side of the property behind the main complex so you have to drive back there and look for it). Once inside, though, it's really nice. Smells great, relaxing and they always offer you complimentary bottled water.

When you go in initally, they give you a list of the programs, how much the average weight loss is for each program and how much each program costs. RM3 is the most intense of these programs and the most expensive (just a heads up) but they go through it all with you and help you decide which program is best. 

Then you go in once a week for shots and a weigh-in if you want. They get you in and out quickly and the weekly shots are a snap. 

Once a month you meet with a doctor to decide if you're going to continue on the program, discuss any possible issues or side-effects you might be experiencing, and also to make sure you're doing the program right. 

The only reason i'm giving TMWL 4-stars versus 5-stars is because the first coupla times I went in to meet with the doctor, she was a little rude and dismissive but I discussed it with staff onsite and things have been great since. 

I feel amazing and will keep going until I get to where I need to be to have a healthy life! I would recommend this program and TMWL to everyone who needs help.
Potent menu selections, perfect portions, right pricing, and great chow!  The elderly owner still shows up and serves...js

I love Panera but the management at this location is terrible.   They are always short staffed at the register and the woman manager just stands and looks around confused.  I complained to corporate but they say its a franchise location.  :(

I Love this shop, wonderful vintage find in the middle of Ybor. I first went to La France to find an alternate wedding dress option, while they had a lot that would have worked I didn't find anything that suited my taste and as it is s vintage store, they can be a bit pricey. 
We also purchased bow ties for the men in the wedding party (pictured). They were wonderful and everyone loved the colors.

The employees at La France are helpful but it can get busy and therefore challenging to get assistance.

I love just getting lost in all of the interesting treasures in the store and if you go hopefully you will too!

Xoom juice is always my go to smoothie place, I've loved them for years, the staff are informed and friendly, and I enjoy the casual atmosphere while I wait for my smoothies.  The peanut butter smoother, and the almond soy infusionals are my favorites. Stopped in for one while running errands today.
Music is terrible on Saturday nights. A big thumbs down. No good lesbian clubs in Tampa.

Love the party atmosphere, food is good, typical bar food plus some better options. I was just happy they offered more than just fried food. They have great specials and always seem to have celebrations that offer free food or drinks.

We like to hit up Pour House and Maloneys usually in one night, nice to have another option in that area.

We will definitely continue going there!

As an employee here, I highly recommend the place. The owners are friendly and knowledgeable about every aspect that we provide. It's a fun laid back environment, and the only place where you can essentially get that living room / "homey" feel without the criticism like most places. We strive to provide the best service possible.

i don't know description of good Irish Pub, but i really have enjoyed my trips to Maloney's.  it's a fun place where it seems the locals have a great time.  i've enjoyed several items on the menu and the food and service are always above my expectation.

I'm a little embarrassed to say this-- but it was my first time checking this place out. I felt like I was cheating on bookmans. But now I realized they are very different stores. :-) We went shopping with our out of town friends here-- there was something for everyone! We all walked out with a bag full of goodies! Books (local authors and otherwise), local Tucson gifts, eclectic items and much more. We even found books in Spanish (which I'm sure isn't difficult in Tucson, but we didn't have to shop around, which was nice). This is a Tucson gem. It's great to shop local if you live here and if not I found it a great place to take out of towners too.

I can't say enough great things about Carl Alan floral! I worked with heather ( and tad bit with Vito)for my wedding, the level of service that she provided was amazing! I met with a few florists in the Philly area, as soon as I started talking with heather I knew this was the one! She was very knowledgable about all flowers and overall decor! She seemed like she really cared to get to know me and my vision for my wedding day!  I'm still obsessed with my bouquet ! It was gorgeous! When I walked into my reception I was blown away how amazing everything looked! There was so much detail- it was Better than I ever imagined! It was exactly what I envisioned x100.  I highly recommend working with Carl Alan for your wedding or event!!!!

Haven't been to any ALDI market before and I was surprise to see that this store is neat, clean, and organize! And they are cheap! They have stuff from wine to chips! Definitely will come back and shop kitchen, personal and bar needs!Z

Went in for some rolls, left with rolls and tomato pie and a rice ball (deep fried ball of rice, peas and gravy), all delicious and fresh. Super friendly service too. It's not on my normal route, but I'm glad to go out of my way to support a quality bakery like this.

We stumbled across this spot while walking through the neighborhood the other day for my friends birthday and I'm so excited for this find!

There are such a wide array of selections of cigars -- I know we got one chocolate mint, and another more " traditional" tasting one. I wish I got his name, but the employee was very nice and he helped us pick our cigar choices and directed us to the closest place to grab some booze as it is a BYOB cigar lounge.. yes you heard me right! They also have hookah, so I'm interested to check that out next time as well.

The basement/downstairs is the best! There's a TV, boombox to hook up to your phone for your personalized music, and a bunch of couches/chairs to chill at. It honestly feels like you're chilling in your own house/apartment.

All of the employees there were very friendly, continuously checking on us and making sure we were alright with our drinks and cigars. Definitely worth checking out!

This is probably the worst Aldi I've ever been to. It's really because of the ghettofied location. At most Aldis you can't expect good customer service, but the bad service at this one takes the cake because the employees lock the doors before closing time so they can get out early. Never been here when they first open, but something tells me they aren't opening a few minutes early either.

Overall I do like Aldi. You can't buy everything at Aldi, but the things you would want to buy are solid. In the frozen section I get wild caught seafood. In produce you can find organic Chiquita bananas occasionally as well as other produce items that might be worth buying. Just don't do all your produce shopping here. The dairy section is probably the best thing they have going. Organic milk, Almond milk, organic yogurt and an assortment of good cheeses. I've also tried some of the G Free gluten free brand and they're not bad...as not bad as gluten free can get. They have a health conscious brand called and fit n active. The fit n active cereals are good. I bought frozen berries and eggo style waffles in their organic brand called simply nature. I refuse to buy meat here. However if I ever see an organic brand of meat pop up in the store I'd try it.

Again, 2 stars is for this particular location. I give the organization a 4. Mainly because it beats out its comparable competitor, Sav a lot in cleanliness and its private label brands. Note that you'll find more name brands in Sav a lot, but they'll be dusty as hell.

Wayyyyy better than parking at the airport.  Everything was so easy here.  Dropped the car with the valet, dropped the keys off inside, jumped on the shuttle, and they drop you at the terminal.  Doesn't get easier than that.  Coming back was equally as easy.  Shuttle came every 2 min and brought us right back to A1.  

Our truck was very dirty when we picked it up, and they even offered to wash it!  We declined, but the offer was nice.  Very pleased and would highly suggest using this place!

This place is turning into my go to. Food is constant and for bar food its pretty good. The service is great and you cant bet the drink specials. My only complaint is that it can get pretty busy good for them not so great for me :o)

Feeling the new and improved Bees! lol

The totally gutted the bar area and redid it. I like the changes they made not only in appearance but in the food. They stepped up there game and added some cooler apps. Itried out the lunch combo special. You pick two items for lunch and I got chicken caesar salad and shrimp linguine. I base how I like a place but how much they give and how full i feel afterwards. They put the plate in front of me and I thought... ok decent amount of food for $9! The chicken caesar was so fresh and the chicken was cooked perfectly. They didn't put too an excess amount of sauce either. The shrimp linguine surprisingly had a lot of pieces of shrimp, that's a first in any restaurant. There were two sauces mixed together one was ok and then the other red sauce was a bit salty ... But ... I currently have enough left over to eat for my mid-day snack! #winning! 
There are also a ton of specials every day. 1/2 off apps from 10pm-2am! or at the bar from 11am-4pm Another special is Girls night out on Wednesday- 5pm to close $3 apps and $3 select drinks!

This bookstore has a particular niche with a wonderful selection heavy in feminist literature and LGBT related materials. Although I don't fit into either category, I still come here often to purchase gifts, find great notebooks, browse the book selection (excellent fiction and a bargain rack of books), and super knick knacks. Great for kids too. I often wind up with something for myself here. I'm so glad this bookstore is here and perfectly located on 4th. It's a mainstay for Tucson!

Create amazing one of a kind pottery pieces at this friendly South Tampa studio. Choose from a variety of plates, cups, bowls, vases, figurines, and more. Then, choose your paint colors, templates, and start designing your masterpiece. If you need ideas or help, the staff is ready with an encouraging word and helpful hints.  Pieces are fired in the kiln and ready for pickup in days. Worth the wait! Conveniently located on Westshore about a mile north of Gandy, this locally owned shop is a gem!

What a nice surprise!  I went there for lunch with a co-worker and found a robust menu with plenty of options for a Pescatarian.  Several fish options and a veggie burger too!

I ordered the Fish & Chips, small portion (1 piece) for $9.95.  The fish was crispy and tasty.  The one piece was rather large (for me) and I wound up taking half of it home.  It came with steak fries that were done just right.  Tartar sauce and malt vinegar packets rounded everything out.  

My co-worker ordered a burger and fries.  She said the burger was delicious.  We sat outside which was very nice, even this time of year, but were blasted by the train operator who laid on the horn the whole time he was passing in front of the restaurant.  Not Maloney's fault, but be prepared.

I'll be coming back to try out their happy hour and extensive draft beer menu.
The crepe was not bad but relatively expensive  and I was the first customers at the door and no discount on the first day of grand opening and they are cash only plus they charged tax...

Good smoothie place with a few locations across town. They've got a pretty good selection across the board and use actual (frozen) fruit in their smoothies not just purées or flavorings. They also have plenty of add ins including proteins, granola, and almonds which I really like since it gives the smoothie a good crunch. And who doesn't like granola and yogurt, I mean seriously. Seriously! You can easily sub any ingredient for another and now they even have kale and spinach to make you feel better about yourself. Cheers!

I passed by this place a couple times before it opened. To be honest, I was pretty excited for it to open and even considered applying to work here. Another trendy place to eat!

The sweet crepes are okay. There's a lot of toppings inside so it becomes a little difficult to taste everything in their description. In my opinion, they put too much cream cheese and too little ice cream. (One scoop is not enough!) Some of the flavors, especially the nutty chocolate, can become overwhelmingly sweet and it's hard to finish. Overall. the crepes are a filling snack and are decently priced! Just be sure to pick a flavor that you can enjoy. The drinks were a little overpriced. Coffees and teas had average prices, but the shakes were $5+ for one! I would much rather just head over to Tea Do next door. (The sweet crepes are already too much sweetness anyways, so a sweet drink is not necessary) I will be sure to try the savory ones next time. 

I visited T-Swirl on opening day with my boyfriend and a couple friends. Despite being overcrowded with customers, there was more than enough seating. The staff was friendly and speedy. The environment was pretty modern and they played great music to add to the hip atmosphere! (Not your average radio hits)

Finally this place opens!!!! I was waiting the longest! I LOVE crepes so when I heard about this place I went balls off the walls. Really cute inside, seems like another place for teens to hang out and chill. I thought the menu was nice and kept it at a minimum. My cousin looked at the size of the crepe and since we'd had just gotten there from lunch, decided to share one. We got the matcha azuki bean. It was pretty good. A little overwhelming with all the matcha. I didn't taste the red bean. The crepe was I believe over cooked to stay crunchier. Since ours had ice cream on it? Overall I think I'll come back to try every one they have lol. A little pricey though. And cash only!

Came here around 10pm on a weeknight. The establishment was fairly busy. Had to wait about 10-15mins for crepe to be ready. In the meantime, it was neat to watch them creatively place the toppings on the creams/custards and roll it into a cone. I got the Matcha Azuki Red Bean Crepe $7.95, scoop of matcha gelato included in price. For crepes that does not come with gelato, extra scoop is $1.75 I believe. The matcha flavor was great! I did not taste much of the red bean though. The poke sticks added a nice touch. The texture of crepe was crispy, but it did slowly become soft and soggy in the end. For a little more than $8, I do wish the crepe come with less cream/custard and more fruit. I would recommend sharing with a friend, because the sweetness can be overwhelming for one person. 

Pros: 
- Complementary lemon infused ice water 
- Plenty of seating area, go through the hallway and you'll find more seating in the back
- Lots of variety of sweet and savory crepes, great for dessert or lunch
- All the fruit toppings looked fresh, except the lychee, which looked questionable
- Indented lines in paper cone makes for easy peeling and clean eating
- Good way to step up your Instagram food pic game :) 

Cons:
- Cash only 
- Wait time, depending on crowd 
- Price 

Overall, I would give a 3.5 star rating if that was possible.

This place is a good place to get a snack. Although the price might be pretty high. But the staff work hard on keeping things organize. I have a friend who work there. The cashiers and some. Other workers are working. But training in a same time. Seem relly hard to work in such a fast speed. When just got to this environment. The fruits and ingredients where fresh.  ill give it a 5star.

This place was the bomb!! The crepe itself was sweet, light, moist and delicious!! I got a savory crepe (chicken teriyaki) wow, delicious!! Meat was fresh, veggies nice and crispy and I loved how there was an egg in it with that yummy sauce drizzled over. I don't give many places a 5 star review but this place deserves it! 2 thumbs up! Can't wait to go back and try something different. You may have to wait a bit for it to be made & some people may complain it's a little pricey, but it's made fresh so have a seat and be prepared to have bites of heaven and be sad when you ate it all. I think is worth every dollar! Enjoy!

I had the chocolate nut party but substitute the chocolate ice cream with vanilla seeing that there was so much chocolate already in it (highly suggested!!). Nevertheless, it was pretty good. The ice cream scoop (also a really small scoop) was only at the top of the crepe, so at the bottom it was just ALL chocolate which was a little overwhelming. I know it's called the chocolate nut party, but there was just too much.

There was one person in front of us in line (not as crowded as the pictures show) but it did take a while for us to get our crepes... Almost a 20 minute wait...

Heads up! They only took cash when I went (on 8/6)! Not sure if it's because they just opened and haven't gotten everything set up yet.

I tried the sweet matcha crepe and savory truffle mushroom crepe- both were good but nothing too special about them. Beware that it can become messy! Make sure you read the instruction on the side of the crepe box when you're eating. Also the prices are more costly than other crepe places around, although this it is a convenient location. 

Suggestion: For the matcha crepe (#8) on the menu, I asked to replace the red bean (because I'm not a fan) with mango instead and it complimented the taste really well! They add mango all around the crepe so it's a must try if you're not a red bean lover- don't worry there's still hope ;)

A very solid 3 star supermarket. Not stellar, but pretty decent when it comes to asian markets.  There is a pretty wide selection of goods. No, it is not very well organized. Yes, it has that funky asian market smell. But all the goods and produce I purchased were fresh and the prices are fair.

These are the best crepes I've ever had. 

The smoked salmon crepe was perfect. The strawberry banana crepe was even better. 

My only complaint is that they're extremely slow and very unorganized. I'll chalk that up to the fact that they've only recently opened. 

My suggestions for the owner(s) are to establish an actual system for the customer line and  make an actual crepe assembly line to get the crepes out faster. 

If I could get in and out fast for lunch I'd be here nearly every day!

Carl did the flowers for my parents wedding in the 80s and we had an instant connection! Working with Vito was great! I loved that they have a sample appointment with you so you can see the flowers you talked about so many months ago! They also send you pictures of your bouquets the week of your wedding, which was great! Other companies I met with didn't even show me flowers! Vito knew exactly what I wanted! I had mentioned that I really liked peonies, but they are usually out of season in August. However, he was able to put some in my bouquet! Everyone at Carl Alan was super enthusiastic about flowers, which made our appointments extra special!

Carl did the flowers for my parents wedding in the 80s and we had an instant connection! Working with Vito was great! I loved that they have a sample appointment with you so you can see the flowers you talked about so many months ago! They also send you pictures of your bouquets the week of your wedding, which was great! When you are discussing your flowers, they bring out examples of that flower so you know exactly what they are taking about.!Other companies I met with didn't even show me flowers! Vito knew exactly what I wanted! I had mentioned that I really liked peonies, but they are usually out of season in August. However, he was able to put some in my bouquet! Everyone at Carl Alan was super enthusiastic about flowers, which made our appointments extra special! Loved them!

This cute cafe is located in a building with a long history. I first visited it a couple weeks ago with friends. We were happy with the drinks we ordered, including the yerba mate. We weren't there for food, but it looks good. The tablecloths were rather stained and had food dried on them. If not for that, I'd have given this cafe 4 stars.

Was so happy with my service today at lunchtime...the food was awesome again....my server was so awesome....she needs a raise !!  I am glad that the menu is more understandable. The price went up for soup and salad and breadsticks...its understandable....but i sometimes have to wait for coupons in my e-mail.  Thank you ....

Got my car detailed while parked on a trip. So easy for me.  And my car even the trunk has a great clean scent. Perfect for me as I had taken it on a long car trip. Nothing like killing two birds with one stone. Tip: If you are taking the shuttle go to the taxi/rental car area. Call them to get your car pulled up so when you arrive it is available for you.

Lemme just say if I hadn't had a groupon for this place I woulda been preeetty peeved. The concept is cute and all, and I get that this is more of an old-school type of place, but for $55-a-head prix-fixe you can get yourself something better elsewhere. 

Since I'm a glass half full kinda gal, I'll start with the saving graces:

- Free wine and cordials with dinner (none of which blew my socks off but I can always get down with free booze).
-Pasta trio was actually pretty good. My favorite was the pesto. 
-Salad dressing had a little zing to it.
-Mamma herself popped out for a quick second to check in and she was adorable.
-We were the only two people in the restaurant for the latter half of our meal, and our server was attentive and friendly without hovering or making us feel awkward.

Now onto the meh:

-Rolls weren't fresh or warm (definitely seemed straight from Giant down the street).
-Antipasti course was on our table when we arrived- a little odd- and consisted of soggy veggies that had been breaded and maaaybe fried for like .2 seconds?? Turn up the heat on the oil, mamma! 
-Main course was okay, nothing memorable. I got the chicken in lemon sauce- it came with some potatoes and string beans. 
-Desserts were a lemon cream cake and a ricotta cake, neither of which were very good and this girl loves cake. 
-The restaurant itself was super dark and outdated. Felt like eating in old Aunt Sally's house deep in the heart of South Philly. Normally the appearance of a place doesn't matter much to me if I'm satisfied with the food, but the gloominess just kind of added to my meh feelings about the food.

Would give an extra half star if I could, since our waiter and mamma were so nice.

Krystal is a restaurant I see mostly / only in the south but not too far into southern Florida.  In north Florida, Georgia, Carolinas pretty much they are easy to find.

Well these Krystal mini burgers were like a staple for young kids who didn't have much money and loved to eat mini burgers. So... when I want to feel like a kid again just buy a dozen or so Krystals and it takes me back about 2 decades or so.

While this is not the best food it's cheap.  That's about all I can really say about Krystal that they're not great but they'll get you through a long drive if you have a bag full of them.  Driver safe?  Better than trying to eat a two handed burger while driving...

Great people! They were super friendly and gracious. Clean and new location. I got coffin shaped  nails for the first time and was weary if I was going to like them or not but LOVE them!  They took extra time to make sure they were perfect. I had my son with me and they were super accommodating to him. They gave me exactly what I wanted and this is the best experience I've had in a nail salon ever! I would recommend this place to everyone I know!

It is rare that I am amazed, but wow is this place good! I would compare this to the first time you have sex, smoke crack or win the lottery. I had the hot sausage/Fishcake with onions, hash and spicy mustard. It was instant euphoria. I almost wish I never came here because I will never be able to eat another hot sausage from anywhere else again. Like a dope fiend loves the needle, I fell in love today.
#JOHNNYHOTTSADDICT

OK so here's the deal...Indian food is definitely a 4 or 5 star. The buffet is expansive and the options change - recently I went and had an eggplant dish that I hadn't tried before and loved it! But the service is seriously 1 star. I don't know what the deal is....it's impossible to get drinks, a check, water, etc. Every time we go it's something else. I don't know if they are understaffed or just don't have good staff, but it shouldn't take 15 minutes to flag someone down for our bill after a lunch buffet. I love going here for the food, but leave frustrated by the service every time.

Great service, very friendly staff. Both employees working that night introduced themselves and were helpful. Love the lounge downstairs. Surprised there weren't more people here on a Saturday night. Got a double apple 'deluxe' hookah, ended up paying $30 with 2 people.

First time bowling here. It was a busy Saturday night, so they took my cellphone number and said it was going to be a 2.5 hour wait. We left to go smoke while we waited, and a lane opened up earlier than anticipated. I called to see whether we could be pushed back on the list, but they ended up canceling our queue. There was some miscommunication between the two individuals working the kiosk. We ended up still waiting 2.5 hours, but one of the employees was chose to give us free shoe rentals for the inconvenience. The lanes are wonderful, and the music is a lot of top 40. We had a lot of fun. Very friendly staff!

It really does live up to the hype. I am normally one of those people that are skeptical when it comes to things that are popular by word of mouth in Philly. I got the kitchen sink. It was well worth the money and super delicious. Our server was pretty great too, and very honest about what she liked and didn't like on the menu. Cash only :(

Used to come here a lot when I still lived on Baltimore. Great environment to get some studying or reading done. I typically would get an iced green tea, or a medium roast coffee. They are a bit overpriced when it comes to drinks, but I have always loved the friendliness of all the staff working there. It gets really crowded on Saturdays during the farmers market. All the baked goods are delicious. Highly recommend the peanut butter brownie (which can probably cause type 2 diabetes) and buttered croissants.

It really is not amazing, but will satisfy any spontaneous cravings for Korean food. First time I've ever had yukgaejiang with sliced beef instead of shredded, which was kind of strange. However, their kimchijigae is pretty decent. I tend to go back and forth between the two. galbitang is not on point. Not the best Korean food around, but the most decent you can get in University City without having to travel too far.

This store has great selection of veggies , fish and many noodles for all the Thai cooking
The store also has an aloe drink we all like that is natural from Vietnam , stocked up on that.
Huge selection of fish that comes in fresh on Friday.
I get all my vegetables here because they are fresher in the plastic bags and stay well in home fridge.
Bags of sweet rolled jackfruit with black sesame cookies area weakness , and here I find lots of other flavors.
The separate section has a good selection of cooking and serving bowls and spoons.
I got a beautiful floor mat for the lanai.

One of my favorite hookah joints in Old City. Good one-stop-shop. Tasty cocktails and hookah right at the bar. One side of the lounge is reserved for larger parties. There is also a smaller lounge area on the other side of the building with some nice leather couches. Really chill vibe, and not too loud. Good service, and very responsive staff that tends to the coals and any adjustments that need to be made to the pipe.

Came here for happy hour with a friend. Ordered a whole bunch of appetizers: chicken skewers, ahi tuna dumplings, indian pancake, and lettuce wraps. Each were about $4.00 a piece. Ahi tuna dumplings were pretty interesting. They were served over guacamole and some type of salsa. It worked. Everything was tasty and service was consistent. No complaints.

This was one of the first places that everybody said I needed to go to when I first got to Philadelphia. The ice cream is overpriced and lines are often pretty long. My palette is pretty crappy, but honestly to me it tastes like regular thrifty's ice cream that is served in Chinese take out boxes.

I own an Honest Tom's Taco Shop t-shirt. I love this place a lot. Tom is a great guy, he does philanthropy with Penn Alexander Middle School every year with the t-shirt sales. I've had everything on the menu except that Honduran fish soup, when they have it. I typically go for the chicken tacos or breakfast tacos (they only serve breakfast on Sundays). Love their halloween sauce. Everything tastes super fresh and is the closest thing I can get to my West Coast Mexican food. $6.50 for two tacos. Yum.

Their buffalo chicken fries are pretty epic. Service is hit or miss. Sometimes you get a pleasant person on the phone, other times it is a woman who sounds like she absolutely hates working there. It's a pretty great place to buy beer, and get your fried goodies. It's not the worst, and certainly not the best. Kind of reminds me of Best House Pizza when it was still around on Baltimore. I never really see people eat food, just go there to buy beer and hang out.

Looking for a green place to stroll and have a picnic?  Bartram's Garden fits the bill. You might even see some wildlife, such as:

Deer
Cardinal
Gull
Hummingbird
Wild turkey
Eastern kingbird
Brown creeper
White-breasted nuthatch

I have never had anything bad here. The Cubans are fantastic! So many people recommend the older restaurants in the area and this one is 10 times better for them than anywhere else I have gone. Also the pork and chicken dishes I have had are amazing. If someone has a bad experience with the food here it is their fault not Monseratte's. I do wish the staff was a little friendlier but that won't stop me from continuing to go.

Beautifully remodeled.  Very comfortable environment.  Excellent service and the fit slam was very good and quick.  I would have preferred the turkey bacon cooked longer but overall, a very good breakfast.  By the way, they have two TVs on the wall now.

My new fave store in Ybor. This reminds me of outfits you would find at a Speakeasy. The dresses are so detailed and vibrant. They have casual dresses, formal dresses, & even wedding dresses. Headpieces so intricate with feather pieces and jewels, top hats, and vests, and suits for men with fancy cuff links can all be found here.

All of the clothes have a plastic protector covering on them. It makes me nervous to touch them, but they are just so beautiful!!! This is a store to purchase the clothes, not rent.

Sweet Water Organic Community Farm hosts a farmer's market to the public every Sunday from October until May. The market is pretty tiny in comparison to other farmer's markets in Tampa, but it offers all of your basic market essentials. Organic and local produce, plants and flowers, fresh eggs, raw milk and cheese, freshly baked bread, organic coffee beans, raw local honey, hippie clothing, handmade jewelry, and soaps and lotions are available for purchase from noon until 4 p.m. at the Sunday market. 

My husband and I visited the Sweet Water Sunday market on opening day (October 18th 2015). Available parking was scarce during our visit; I'm not sure if the market is normally this busy on other Sundays. We brought our fur baby to the market (Note: pets must wear leashes!) and he enjoyed meeting the other dogs. During our visit we explored the gardens and visited Sweetie the Pig and the chicken coop. 

It didn't take us long to browse the vendors and the farm. Once I purchased my fruits and vegetables we ordered a Rasta burger with avocado and a green smoothie. The burger was very tasty and had a nice spicy kick. The green smoothie was good too, but I wish it was served colder. We enjoyed our burger while listening to some live music on stage.

TIP: Bring cash! Most of the vendors do not accept plastic.

This probably won't be my go-to Sunday farmer's market, but this tiny and charming farmer's market is worth a stop if you are looking to buy locally.
Best sushi place in the area. If you're looking for quality- go here. Been going here for 10+ years and it's better than any other new place in the area.

OUT OF BUSINESS!!!!! Need play B, as this no longer exists! Was it good????? It's  out of business, but doesn't mean the food wasn't good
What's a girl to do? I have one business closes another one opens, so you move on.
Fabulous.  I had an urgent problem and they sent someone same day who provided a short term fix and returned the next day with a permanent fix. Great company.  Mike is my hero.
These empanadas are amazing! Not too greasy, lots of filling, and great flavor! Plus, the place is very clean and the staff is friendly. I'll definitely be going back as a regular.

I ordered the Indian Pancake with curry sauce expecting it would be like roti canai at Penang, it turned out it was very greasy. When I complained about it, the waiter just said sorry an did not offer any substitute. Penang has the best roti canai. The blue crab salad was Ok, small portion with a very small dollop of crab meat with mayo in the middle and balsamic vinegar dressing. However, the drunken chow fun is very tasty and highly recommended.
They make best Italian bread in South Philly. I always get the seeded kind. Make sure you go there early on Saturdays because they sold out quickly. They also make pizza and cookies. Cash only.
The donuts are very light and fluufy, melts in your mouth. There is always a long line on Saturdays. The apple and blueberry fritters are very good. You can see how they make and fill the donuts.
The ba ba rum cake is to die for. The pesca ala creme (it looks like a peach) is a must

They make the best ice cream in Philadelphia. I love coffee flavored ice cream, their lightning rod is my favorite. It is coffee ice cream with espresso beans, dark chocolate brownies, espresso, and pretzel rod. It is insanely good. The southern sympathizer (rum raisin, praline, pistachio) is really good too.

Hidden gem in South Philly. The bread and rolls are very soft and light, perfect for dipping. The arancini (Italian rice ball) is very good. Super friendly guy. He said the raisin bread does not have preservatives so he recommended freezing it soon. Definitely come back for more rolls and bread.

You know it's a good yoga studio when you float out after a class. I'm literally in another state of mind when I leave. The front desk employee even said, "Looks like the class was good for you. I love seeing that." She must see it often. 

Bella Prana helped me take my yoga practice to new levels after years of classes around the country at different studios. The size of the studio and the instructor to studio ratio allows for some attention during challenging poses. I was always intimidated by inversions and I'm now doing some with ease. The power hour and flow 2 classes have excellent instructors, and advanced students are usually in attendance who will encourage you. 

My very favorite class and instructor is flow 1 with Tina. She is so gifted with a calming and uplifting aura. Although the class is for beginners and up, it's exactly what I need right now and allows me to start the day with a calm and present state. 

The studio offers a new student special for $39 that gives you access to all classes for a month. I suggest taking every class possible to find the right one for you. There are so many to choose from. The studio also hosts meditation sessions, book clubs, social hours, etc. What a great way to build a community of yogis.

What can I say! Josh Silverman is one rude unprofessional man. I went in to look at and purchase a truck. Josh did not greet me or introduce himself when I came in. I was ignored. I asked about a truck and was handed keys and left alone. I went back into discuss the truck were I was not greeted again nor did Josh ask my name or any other information after discussing the price of the truck. I only know his name from the business card he gave me when I left. I DO NOT recommend anyone going to or buying anything from Tucson Auto Sales.

Looking for Hong Kong style milk tea? This will be the best you find in Chinatown for less than $2, doesn't disappoint. 

Tip: Ask for less ice if you don't want your milk tea gone after several sips

Had egg white mushroom truffle and vegetarian lover. Both are very good. Highly recommended. The girl at the counter is very friendly and helpful. I took mine home and she gave me clear cone-shaped plastic bags that fit perfectly. Both were in good shape upon arriving home. Definetely will go back.

The shanghai crabmeat pork soup dumpling is very good. The shanghai steamed pork dumpling is good too. The crabmeat dumpling is one of their popular dishes. I had stir fried noodles (ho fun), it is ok, not recommended.

Their peanut-filled mochi is the best. The egg custard is pretty good too. Very friendly lady at the counter. They have steamed buns, I had regular pork bun and there's plenty meat filling. Next time I will try the bbq pork.

Mama's menu is ageless and the atmosphere of this place is intimately old school family style. You'll even get the treatment of the accordion player on the right night!! Spend a few hours eating with your loved ones here and enjoy the delicious homemade strawberrycello after dinner, exclusively created by Mama herself!

Nice, clean, friendly!!

Walk-ins welcome. 

It was a little hard to find, it's right next door to Coldstone by the Harkins Theater. 

Their prices are about average. 

The lady who did our nails was very nice and friendly. She did a great job on our nails. I asked her for black gel nails with glitter ombré. I think they look even better in person than they do in the picture. :)

The garbage disposal they put on about 2 years ago just fell off!  It was not clamped on properly.  Nor was the piping correct. On prior jobs they have missed appointments, and their staff is very spotty on their knowledge since Kevin moved on.  We are very happy with Racys Plumbing now.

This Publix isn't the newest but I've been coming here for so long it's my favorite. My hubby and i were just there Sunday and the staff was really attentive! A manager asked if we needed help and a short time later a staff member did too. The store is clean, has a nice wine section and there is an Aprons cooking station where a chef creates a dish for you to try. They then offer you a recipe card and have all of the ingredients in a case next to the cooking area. We've ordered custom cakes from
The bakery and they've always done a stellar job. Same goes for ordering breads for the various holidays throughout the year. Need wood for the winter? They always have logs outside ready to go.
Check out this store if you are in the neighborhood

Let me start off by saying ive been a customer for 9 years, 9 freaking years. 

I ordered through grubhub for wing zings and fries. One hour later I received whole wings and fries, incorrect order. I call back for them to correct it, and they say sure they'll exchange it, but give the whole wings back. Sure, no problem on my end but bad customer service and waste of food throwing that away. 

Another HOUR  later, yes TWO FULL HOURS after i ordered the food is still not here. I called back for a refund, which yes they said they'll honor, but I'm pretty sure that doesnt rectify terrible service and wasting two hours of my life. 

After being an unappreciated customer for 9 years that may be my last order.

This has been one of the most positive experiences I have had with a weight loss program. Everyone is professional, empathetic and knowledgeable. I have lost 21 pounds and feel much better than before the weight loss.

This review is LONG overdue! Some friends and I went into JJs fairly late on a Sunday evening a few weeks ago after having several cocktails. We were helped by Jamie, who was ALSO working the drive thru line, taking everyone's orders, and making sandwiches in light speed time. We were a bit on the loud side and surely obnoxious, but Jamie was so great, even joked around with us a bit, nailed all our orders and got them to us in what might have been less than a minute, honestly. The food here is always delicious- getting great customer service on top of that is what keeps us coming back.

I was thoroughly impressed with Jamies ability to be extremely good natured, despite being alone late at night and working the job of three people at once. As long as she's working there we'll be back often to say hi and get our late night sandwich fix. Thanks Jamie, you rocked!

Good place for lunch, I haven't had any problems here.  The staff are very friendly, service is quick.  Great for when you don't have time and just need something really quick but not too unhealthy for you.  I like the quesadilla salad here or the chicken sandwich with cilantro sauce.

The decor isn't new and updated, the food isn't the best you'll get on Passyunk, which isn't saying much considering the extremely high quality of offerings on Passyunk these days, but the whole experience together was quite nice and I will definitely go back!
My mom and I wandered in on a Saturday night and were immediately seated, it was explained that it's prix fix, we chose the smaller option of 3 courses for $35 pp. We were served red wine, bread, and antipasto. All of which were lovely. We chose salad (instead of soup), pasta (gnocchi in marinara), and chicken in vodka sauce. Pasta needed a touch of salt but it was tasty, chicken was incredibly moist and delicious. Then we were served a lemon cake with shots of homemade limoncello. 
The thing I noticed, and loved, was the pace of the meal. They did not rush us, the courses didn't come rapidly, the whole meal took 2 hours. Grabbing a meal out became an experience, an event, and it was wonderful. 

Long story short: if you're looking for haute cuisine then keep walking, there's plenty of that on Passyunk, if you're looking for an evening out with lovely food and time to spend with your dinner guests then it's the place for you!

I have been a guest here a few times in the last few weeks.  It has an awesome location and the balcony views are spectacular.  I especially like seeing the bridge colors and Riverwalk area.  You can always take a nice stroll or run down riverwalk.  I know the place was remodeled in the last 5-10 years, but I think it seems they might have rushed through a little in the details(shower head, etc...)  They could also use a deep cleaning of hotel rooms maybe on a yearly basis, replacing caulk, etc..  The food for breakfast is basic , and mostly unhealthy options and could be improved.  I have not ordered from breakfast menu though.  I just tried the buffet.  I have not tried dinner there yet.
For everyone who misses Nan in west Philly there is now a French Thai restaurant in Philly. Give it a try

Very nice and clean restaurant. The waitress is very friendly and helpful. Drunken noodle is very good and spicy. It is sauteed broad rice noodle, basil, onion, bell pepper, and chilli. Choice of chicken, beef, or shrimp. Definitely going back to try more.

I was a big fan of ACE hardware when I lived in California, but this particular location is very small and claustrophobic feeling.  They do not have Benjamin Moore paints at this location, and I feel like their paint color selections for the brands they do carry are small because of the small size of this store.

The location isn't great.  The minimall has a very confusing parking lot.

On a plus side, the staff was very friendly and attentive.

First of all, the smell is horrible!! and it's a big chaos, but the place is SO big and cheap and you find all kind of exotic stuff, I won't event take a single star for it's downside. 
I bought a GIGANTIC bag of rice noodles for 9 dlls.... about 3 months ago and I still have half of it... tea pots are cheaper than in ebay! and it's so full of stuff, I love it, too bad it's like 40 min away from my home, or else I'd go grocery shopping there weekly. The other Asian stores I've seen are small, like corner stores, this on is big, maybe not like a Walmart. but big!

I LOVED IT!

A great place for cool conversation with a kick ass sales staff, great t-shirts and groovy accessories.  You definitely can't walk in without walking out with something cool to wear.  They even have cool stuff for the toddling crowd.  So even your 3 year old can look cool. A must stop when on South Street in Philly.

I had heard allot about them but was a little intimidated to get the specialty toys and gadgets my wife uses in the kitchen. TodAy I went in for the first time and was greeted by an incredibly gracious staff who helped me with a purchAse of something completely trivial. They alstroemerias were very knowledgeable about a few things my wife had been wAnting, one of which I left with. I'll be going back allot more!!

Great food when you want healthy options and/or a quick bite. This last meal was really good. I ordered the Pick 2 so I chose the Garden Vegetable soup with Pesto. I was unsure if I would like it so the girl at the register asked if I wanted a sample first. Yes please!! Nice touch. I really liked the flavor and then fact that it was a lower calorie soup. For my 2nd item I chose the BBQ chicken salad. It was also very good. I liked that they gave me the dressing on ti side as I hate soggy salads and like to control how much dressing I put on for calorie purposes as well. They have more than one flavor of unsweet iced tea. I chose the hibiscus. It's really good. More of a sweet flavor than a flower flavor. This is a strip mall so you can shop at Hallmark and Steinmart then have lunch here and make an outing of it.

Excellent every time I've been there. Crusty and soft rolls, excellent garlic disks (for lack of a better term; butter and garlic on disk of bread), tomato pie that has the perfect crust and spice. Also have had their raisin bagels -- different from the typical "bagel," but like raisin bread in circular form and very good. Seems like all the bread stuffs from here have a distinct taste from their oven. Also, incredibly friendly owner/operator. Highly recommend.

What a great evening to get into the christmas spirit. The train ride singing carols was a treat, I like the displays. Even if you don't care for the thrill rides this is worth it. The shows are all there, and you can even catch some local schools playing carols in the bandshells. ALl good things for the Christmas season. 

I love the animal displays but they close at dark. Get there before dark.

Eat at the Crown colony right before the light show. Don't be late or you will be part of the show.

After a disappointing dinner at Bonefish we stopped in to Panera to get some dessert. We both decided on something pumpkin since it was that kind of season.

I don't quite know what the name of my pumpkin delight was but it was flaky and creamy and totally yummy. I could order it again and again and again.

My friends pumpkin dish was more like a pumpkin roll which was very moist and the filling was just super. It made for a delightful ending to a bad dinner at another location.

What's a girl to do? Gotta love the time of year when the pumpkin starts coming out.
It's good for what it is. Cheap food, decent beer selection, reliable ATM, friendly service.
Best red curry tofu I've had in awhile. Wait staff is pleasant. Entrees are somewhat pricy, but good.
Horribly organized. Here's why-- lines as long as Black Friday lines. Waited 45 minutes to get drinks and food. Don't come here to eat or drink. It's not worth the wait.

You could easily walk by Vallari Brothers in South Philly and not notice it, the corner building is pretty run down and there is one small sign indicating there is anything being sold at all there. That is usually an indicator of a product that speaks for itself. Inside, there were bags of bread and rolls strewn around and not much for decor. We waited for an older gentleman to realize we had entered and asked him about the tomato pie advertised casually in the window.

All out of tomato pie slices, I was instead offered  round mini pies for $1 each. The fluffy bread and sweet sauce satisfied.  My love for tomato pie makes me feel like a really Philly person. I feel like my family and friends from NY just don't get it. Have you experienced that?

For something sweeter- I also grabbed a gorgeously soft raisin bread that I enjoyed for breakfast later in the week.

A golfing partner and I won some nice gift certificates to this specific Beef's in a charity tournament and I'd openly like to praise the management at this store for their donation. 

That manager's business card was in each envelope with the gift certificates and he even came out to help me link to wi-fi while I was in the restaurant.

Had absolutely no issues using the certificate. That sounds like a given, but trust me that sometimes it is not as simple as it should be at certain places. 

Because the certificate was worth more than I would have spent had I dined in, I took a couple entrees home to get the full value and had a drink while I waited. 

This is a total "old-school" sports bar experience and I've honestly never seen TV's set up on either side of the front door. Very unique and maximizing the space.

My wait was not long at all and my order was completely accurate when I got home to enjoy it. 

I really enjoyed a turkey, bacon and guacamole sandwich that was new on the menu and the side salad I got with it was surprisingly fresh.

Nice job by the kitchen for the quality of my entire order and also to the wonderful Mariah, who was in charge of take-out orders and took very good care of me.

I look forward to coming back with another one of our certficates at some point to grab a beer and try more of the menu.
Fastest and best deli!!! They even cater to requests like spring mix while making sandwiches!! So cool!!!

I didn't pay for the "upgraded" experience of ChristmasTown and didn't get to meet Santa, but I was largely disappointed with the experience.  Maybe Disney has spoiled me as to what the "Awe" factor really is, but I've seen some homes decorated more thoroughly than this.  We came Saturday around 7PM and I was expecting the usual "order" that Busch Gardens has when it comes to parking.  I guess after a certain time of day, parking is free? and it was a free for all, people made 6-7 rows of cars only to shrink down to 2 so it was complete chaos.  Reminded me of trying to get into the city at the Lincoln Tunnel in NJ during rush hour.  Bumper to bumper with no where to go.  

After spending 30-40 minutes looking for a parking spot with parking staff just wandering around and not directing traffic, the trolley that brings guests to the park entrance had a huge line.  And I understand its popular and there's a lot of people at night wanting to see the lights, but to not anticipate that from a park management perspective is plain awful.  

We decided to walk to the entrance, except for the fact the sidewalk is tiny and there were a ton of people going in both directions, so some people stepped on to the street to walk, only to have cars and trolleys wiz right past them creating a dangerous situation.

After finally getting into the park, we weren't overly impressed with the lights and really to me looked like a fairly normal day.  I've never been in Busch Gardens after dark and if someone told me the lights are normally there, I would have totally believed it... just nothing that exciting or interesting.

This is our 2nd year in a row going to Christmas Town and we love it.  It's fun for all ages! I enjoyed that they have rides for toddlers the most. The Elmo Christmas show is a blast. Christmas town is a sight to see.  

I took away a star because in my opinion he food isn't that great. I have yet to experience food at Busch Gardens that has wow' me.

I think this is some of the best Chinese food I have had. Loved the hot and sour soup. The chicken fried rice had lots of chicken. The pot stickers were very good. General tso chicken was perfect spice and not over breaded.

Went there first time a week ago and my first impression was old, crowded, and not very clean. The front door location and restroom are very close and the avenue to get through the crowd is a tough job. 

The bar staff is bad I practically had to pop a flare to get them to see me waving my hands for service they don't work the whole bar.  

The bar needs to be opened up. The wall separating the dance floor with the bar needs to be removed.  

Positive note: the band playing that night was not bad they mixed it up a bit and vocals were good.  I also liked that there was no limitation on what you could or couldn't wear.  You got a mix of western wear, casual, or semi casual

Made an appointment and it took them over 40 minutes to start. Barely acknowledged me. No respect for customers making appointments. Very frustrating.

It was the day before nye so I couldn't afford to just walk out. But I highly recommend going elsewhere. Unprofessional and disrespectful.

I tried Mei Mei today after hearing good things about their lunch special here. So, maybe would be 4 1/2 but I am gonna say 5 for Tucson. My fault I was ordering for two people to go. So many choices, new place, no brown rice option.The steamed white rice that came with the shrimp lo mein was not great, for that person, my bad call, and only 3 shrimp. A lot of food, and what do you expect for a shrimp lunch at that price? The egg rolls were crisp and light. Almost so good I didn't miss a sweet and sour dipping sauce. Duck sauce? Maybe available if you ask for it. i asked and they did give me nice chopsticks. The other order was beef and broccoli with lo mein, and also 2 egg rolls. The person who got the shrimp wanted it. It was piping hot even after I transported it in a car for 10 minutes, delicious, large chunks of tender beef, large perfectly cooked broccoli flourettes and the egg rolls were delicious and not heavy. Even someone who normally does not like egg rolls, ate one and liked it. The Lo Mein was a nice thick noodle, not spaghetti. Could have more cabbage and bean sprouts, but tasty. Delicious sauce with the beef and broccollli. I'm a big guy and it filled me up. Got a menu, will be calling in for pickup in the future. So happy I found a place near downtown this good! Thank you Mei Mei! - Tribalinks

My co-workers and I come here often for lunch. They have a dining room where you can sit and eat comfortably in booths. They offer lunch specials such as pizza and sandwiches. All come with a side of fries and a coke.  The rest of the menu is reasonably priced as well and is consistantly good. The restaurant could use some updating. It's 1970s style in there... But it has some charm and is always kept clean. The waitress who is always there is super friendly, and she does a great job of making sure we are taken care of. Great for a casual lunch while on break.

So glad I decided to check this place out! The restaurant was almost empty when I stopped by on a chilly night with my boyfriend, but don't let this dissuade you. The menu is a mix of traditional Thai with classic French dishes mixed in which may be unusual but seems to work (I went the Thai route). The food was great (try one of the soups, huge portions!)  The service was excellent without being overwhelming. AND it's BYOB -- will be returning soon for sure!

Of the handful of Panera locations that I regularly visit, this one is definitely the largest.

Not only do they have the traditional tables outside, but the interior dining area is spacious and more comfortable than any of the other locations I've seen around town.

I like the friendly attitude of the employees at this and all Panera locations, and their bagels are a big reason that I frequent the stores.

Now, if only Panera would serve their breakfast items all day...like some other places do.

And while they do have a rewards program, the fact that you literally get fewer rewards the more you visit defeats the purpose. It should be the other way around if they really want regular customers.

Thai fusion with the standards as well as more innovative dishes like a duck ravioli and beef bourginon.  

We went with the traditional fare:
1. Pad See Yew: Ok but not very flavorful.  It's stir-fried noodles without much seasoning and very little egg.  I definitely prefer a more seasoned version.
2. Red Curry with Beef: Tiny portions and missing that creamy curry base.  The sauce was spicy but didn't have that body.  The beef was also slightly tough.
3. Stir-fried vegetables and tofu with garlic and duck fat: Okay but didn't stand out.

The service is good and the staff are friendly, but the traditional Thai food is just pretty meh.  Maybe the French dishes are better but the standard Thai fare is mediocre at best.

Usually decent food - reminds me of the corner Chinese store in the hood. Sadly the delivery driver was rude and inappropriate; he called to tell me he was outside and expected me to come out of my condo and downstairs to his car rather than deliver my food to my door. When I refused a tip, he went apeshit...poor soul.
Thai fusion here is alright. The food is as expected, they do a great job of dressing up the mood, the service, and the dishes.
Good, helpful service and a fantastic selection of vintage clothes. They even have a huge selection of actual hats in amazing quality as well
Decent place to hang out, kinda. Weak drinks, slow service, horrible lay out but TVs to watch the games if you want to stay local in manayunk.

Well, I find this interesting that THREE YEARS after the fact, there is a response to my review. All I have to say is, at the time that this was happening, there was no attempt by anyone from the company to reach out and make things right. Though I do appreciate the tone of his response, I really do not know what he is talking about in his reference to "procedures and protocols." Unless he is referring to his plumber calling the main office to figure out how to do the job and how to do the job in the most expensive way possible that is totally out of the realm of reasonable and honest cost. He also says, at least I think he was saying in his response, that he refunded above the service fee, which in this case would have been $100. He did not. He only refunded the $65.

Just read the other one star reviews and you will get an idea of what to expect from this company.
Always great prices and always well stocked. Only big issue is they get busy and most time only two registers open. Besides that awesome

We ordered two strombolis and they were pretty good. A regular size can definitely feed two people, they are pretty big! Only reason we gave it a four instead of five is that they messed our order up even though they repeated the order to us on the phone and it was correct. Other than that, everything was great!
We did take out and the food was ready in 15 minutes, which is pretty good as well!

This is a true old school punk clothing store where you could get some pretty cool things you cant find elsewhere. Their T-shirt selection is nice and these are shirts you wont find really anywhere else or on anyone else. Although not a huge store they do have a nice selection of skinny jeans, shirts, shoes, and lots of accessories.

The cashier who I assumed was the owner actually interacts throughout the sale and is definitely helpful. Music in the store isn't bad either.

Stores like this are hard to find and when you do see one support them!

Keep the scene alive!!

This is a perfect place to eat healthy.  Alot of salad options and healthy wraps.  It has plenty parking lot space since its in a corporate building.  They usually have daily specials.  They do home made cookies every morning.

This place is amazing! You have to stop in, no matter if you like the 20's or not.

Yelp peaked our curiosity and definitely came through on this one. The buyer/supplier for this shop must be very well versed in their eras. Scoring highly for in their attention to detail and definitely not lacking in the creative genius department. I wish we had one of these stores in Omaha. Actually, its probably better that they are so far away, we might spend a fortune for the right event! 

Steampunk, flappers, platform shoes, and quality in all sections! We didn't have room in our suitcase at the time but wish we did now! 

Go in to look, you'll be happy you did!

I live far away now from Crash Bang Boom but still order my hairdye and other items (like my fabulous new purse) from them online!  The best customer service around!  Wish I could walk in easily to see what they have but at least they are great about updating their website to offer to us out of towners!

I give 4 stars for the food..But the experience I had with the service gets one star..if that..

We arrived during a weeknight, we got there at exactly, 8:35 p.m...I sent a text to one of my sons that we had just arrived..We sat on the far right, behind the wall..The waitress "M"..I wont mention her full name, but I wrote it down..offered to take our drink order after no one came for more than 15 minutes..my son got up and asked the bartender to please send someone to us..

We got our drinks and before we had a chance to say " we know what we want" she scooted out of there..by the way, the place was EMPTY..waited and waited again..we just wanted to order anything from the table menu..the heck with waiting for the larger menu..

Finally my son gets up again, and asks the bartender( who couldn't be arsed) if we could get "M" back..

She came over with a look in her face like she was to bothered by us..we ordered the chicken wings for my son and the Cuban sandwich for me..IT TOOK TILL 9:55 to get our food! They were sweeping around us, cleaning around us..and we just couldn't believe that this so called quick meal and service had taken over one and a half hour..

Threw the tab at us, and I still left her a 20 percent tip...not my usual 100 percent like I am known in my neck of the woods to do..

"M", you're in the wrong business if you think waiting on a woman with her son, won't tip..I paid my way through college and grad school with tips...and I was dang good at my job...and my son is an owner of a local restaurant..

So impressed! I brought some vintage costume jewelry in to be repaired and they fixed it while I waited. Five minutes later I was out the door! I expected to have to drop it off and come back. Very friendly & quick, efficient service. They also have a beautiful selection of jewelry & watches. I'll definitely be back. 5 stars.
I constantly keep coming back.  Mrs. Josie is an awesome teacher  and a very gracious host.

Late last week I highly endorsed Desert Toyota however after today that quickly changed.  Upon delivery of the "new to me" car I was told that in order for DESERT TOYOTA to take care of the missing bracket I'd have to provide them with a referral!  
I'm appalled...this is NOT standard business practice.  DESERT TOYOTA YOU SHOULD BE ASHAMED!
Great dealership. Great service. I had a bad experience with the dealership I purchased my vehicle at & Royal Jaguar was understanding & listened to my needs aswell as taking care of my issue.

Went there for lunch on a Tuesday. Really nice ambience. Not a huge menu, which I actually prefer because otherwise I can't decide what to get  tried the Thai iced tea, green curry with veggies and drunken noodles. The drunken noodles were super duper spicy!! Paid around $30 I think for these items.

Christine is amazing.  I went to her one day when they first opened up & haven't ever been anywhere else.  She's skilled & has the patience to hand paint one of a kind designs that make my sausage-like fingers look beautiful. She's quick yet meticulous & she's AFFORDABLE.

Last week I bought a car.  I did have a bit of an issue but it was promptly & professionally handled by Patrick Betancourt, Sales General Manager. 

All in all, the vehicle I purchased is a beauty & they did work out a great deal for my trade-in. Other than the miscommunication, my salesman was not high pressuring & respectful to both my husband & I. Adam was the finance guy & he was great too.

After having issue with CVS messing up some prescription which one or two might of actually been the doctors fault I switch over to Rite Aid.  They stayed open while they remodeled which surprised me and was slightly annoying for a few months but the results were very nice.  It looks a lot cleaner not that it wasn't clean to begin with.  But the addition of the RediClinic has occasionally significantly slowed down service at the pharmacy window which wasn't always the quickest to begin with.  Give yourself a little extra time when picking up prescriptions here you never know if you're going to be in and out or waiting 15-30 minutes.
Best bakery in Tampa. Tamps is a sad, pathetic city in that it has little to no bakeries. But...at least we have this. A Tampa gem for generations. It's like stepping back in time in a good way.

We had some fun in this Vietnamese grocery stocking up on sweet soy sauce, thick soy sauce, chili paste, coconut milk, a few cold drinks, fresh veggies, fresh fruit, and of course some crazy varieties of ramen,

I think they were happy to have a non-oriental person in the store because they have us a huge bottle of fish sauce as a gift when we checked out. It will last a few years as I don't ever seem to use fish sauce when I'm cooking. Time to try some new recipes!
The food and service was outstanding. It is a must return restaurant! The rack of lamb was done just right.

I just tried to make an order for pickup, my line got disconnected and when I called back the person on the other line proceeded to accost me and ask if I was playing games with them???? Very rude. Will never give them any business ever again.

I have ordered numerous times and each time we end up talking about how true the commercials are. They are freaky fast... So fast, I put a stopwatch to it! 4 minutes and 12 seconds from when I ended my call for delivery. I think making the sandwiches in that time is fast, and to have it delivered in that time?! #speechless #fan #foodie

Tonight is just ridiculous and Applebees. It is ladies night but it is not that full. I'm sitting at the bar just ordered my second glass of wine and it took approximately 20 minutes which is unacceptable to me. Yes, tonight is ladies night but that should make no difference with anything.
