I had the Chicken Wonton Tacos which are excellent. They're crunchy, savory, juicy, and have coleslaw on top which is excellent. I also had the spinach and artichoke dip with chips which was pretty dismal. The chips were flat and not crispy. The dip was also flat and had no flavor even though I did have cheese on top of it. Somehow the flavor just did not come through on any way shape or form.

To get my second drink I just had to wait a good 15 minutes and I ordered water when I came in about 2 hours ago and I've yet to see it and I don't think I ever will. I guess it depends what staff you get on the night you come here and how will you get treated. It seems like they have their special customers and they get the special treatment.

What's a girl to do? Well, it's close to home and Wednesday nights are ladies night and a good investment.

Awful experience last night (03/31/2016). Cameron, A Cota and Joshua refused to add a line of service to my account and allow me to SWAP the devices because they said that "swapping was against Sprint policies." I demanded to see the policy and Cameron instructed me it could be obtained the Sprint.com website or by calling 800# and it would be sent to me in the mail. 

Upon my return home I logged on to Sprint.com and initiated a telephone call to Sprint customer service where both denied that swapping was against policy. Sprint customer service and their escalation department were very confused as to why the store had denied to fulfill my request.

Well, now it is $3.00 per item. So prices have doubled in four years.

Tip: To use plastic to pay, there is a $10 minimum.
Decent pad thai. Substantial portions. Clean place. Boring view.

I've been working with Al Coronado Plumbing for several years now. We have been in a few bad situations where we needed a plumber out FAST and all it ever took was a phone call to Al to get my plumbing issue fixed. There were several times that he DIDN'T EVEN CHARGE ME because we have been a long time customer! I will continue to do business with Al Coronado for years to come!

With Plumbers......Good service doesn't come cheap and  Cheap service is almost never good.

One more thing. If you give them a try please tell them that I sent you!

I wondered why I had never been to this store before  but as a reviewer from 2014  stated " the store was dirty and unorganized".  i was surprised to see cut vegetables floating in large bowls of liquid.  The sign stated fried tofu but it was not stored properly--I wondered how long it had been there.  I was really looking for something else--I will not be going back.

We started out with a few appetizers money purse, duck ravioli and the steamed muscles. For our main course we ordered a 1/2 duck with curry and pineapple along with a pork dish with peanut source,also the crispy duck salad and shared among us. 
We enjoyed all the food and our going to make this a regular spot, Very clean....

I always get a number 5 with the bread left in and lots of freebies and my sandwich is always done correctly and very quickly. I have never had any issues with this location, and I will continue to visit weekly.

Worst blow out ever should not have paid I am thinking of calling for a refund UGH
She use a flat iron at the end I mean really I could have done it oh and had to wait 30 min past my appt time
This is my go to Vietnamese grocery store I visit weekly! They have Asian fruits & fruits trees year round.  I like the speedy check out & friendly owners.

I have always gone to this location, and have never once had a single problem. I have ordered delivery and it gets to my house within 4-8 min every time(I live pretty close). When I do go in store the people are always super friendly and curtious. The price is a little high, but hey, I will gladly pay for quality. 

A side note: Jenny S, who goes to a sandwich shop for a greasy meal after the bars? Your review blew my mind with stupidity hahahahaha

Horrible place to eat. 

Dont even waste your time, I rather drive to Tennessee 10hrs for White castle.

I order Cheeseburgers 

Gave me no cheese

And no ketchup wen back they said they dont have ketchup... ????? Reallllyyy?????

The burger taste it like was frozen for weeks. Dry bread. Horrible place never ever again, Not even free i will eat there

This is about as good as gets for me (limited use), right around the corner, fast service, and cheap.
I would compare it to a Motel 6 if it's in an area without crack addicts and hookers, you can stay there, but it's not a vacation.
3 bucks (I always pay cash because of the 10 buck minimum for Credit) an item for dry cleaning is pretty darn good, I have 2 suits and I bring them both here, and have had no problems.  
So there you have it.

I have been coming to Barbara Forgione salon since 2011. Nobody does color better than Barbara Forgione! Barbara is not only amazing at color but also one of the nicest people, who really listens to her clients and goes the extra mile to achieve exactly what they want. Her team is great, friendly and professional. The salon also has convenient parking and sells good hair products. I would recommend you give Barbara Forgione salon a try. You will not leave her salon unhappy!
Called multiple times for information  and appointment. Could never get appointment or answers regarding program. Guess new customers are not a priority, if I could give zero stars I would.

Sadly CLOSED 2018.  

Absolutely the best tomato pie ever!  Go for the small round personal size.  They sell the full-sized variety in full, half, and quarter sheets.  You can buy a single slice too.  

Great fresh breads; also long, short, and round rolls too.  

Old world store with a friendly baker with an Italian accent. 

Located just below Snyder Avenue on 10th Street.

(Ignore hater reviews.)

This is my go to place for cheese when I go to the Reading Terminal Market.  The staff is very knowledgeable and they will tell you what will work best when you cook.  They have a fantastic collection of cheeses as well as some complimentary items such as meats, dried pasta, olive oil, and vinegars.  I also like the fact that they carry some unusual items.  For example, they had German Parmesan cheese.  I have never seen that before.  The staff was able to tell me the differences in flavor from the Italian Parmesan and offered a taste.  What I like best about Downtown Cheese is the breadth of their selections.  They really do stock cheeses from all over the world.  Great specialty shop.

I'm not complaining because their cheesecakes are amazing and this is the first time we have had a problem with anything from here. We come to get cookies and cheesecakes and everything time they been perfect , except this one time. Ordered three cheesecakes and the one with chocolate must've melted off when they made it. They were still delicious though. I should have checked them before I left. I was told by a woman working the counter a while ago that they bake everything in a room that's not air-conditioned at night , so maybe that's why there was a problem this time?
Their food is absolutely excellent and to pick up its super quick they never keep you waiting. The Lunch Special is a deal that cannot be beat! Very satisfied.

Close to work...David did an awesome job on pursuading me into trying a new full set by him.  I immediately noticed that THIS set felt more secure as he positioned the tip in a place that would stay. I've always been told that my nail beds are "too weak" or "too oily" and that's why they come off right at the tip. :-( I'm confident that won't happen with these...we shall see...feeling excited & pleased. :-)
They knew exactly what I wanted and even stayed open 45 mins after closing to help me! They charged me half of what I usually pay. This was my first time here and It was a perfect experience

A great little restaurant.  Took a lil while to get my food, but was extraordinary when it arrived. I dont speak spanish, and my waitress struggled to speak english, but it was all good...will definitely come back.

Great service, great half price appetizers.  The drinks are sooo much more expensive than any place ive ever been, even two for one.  The beer was more expensive than a food item, the mixed drink my friend got almost the price of two appetizers. If it wasn't for the wonderful service I would never go back

Royal Orchid is a refreshing change from the chains the dominate the dining scene in the Westshore area.

Located in the lobby level of a Ramada Hotel, the location is not very noteworthy but the interior of this restaurant is quite attractive. The layout is spacious eliminating any feeling of sitting too close to another party or feeling squeezed in surrounded by too many tables too close together.

We were immediately greeted and seated and service continued to be polite and professional throughout the meal.

I ordered an Indian dish and my guest went for the Grouper sandwich. Two very different dishes but we shared the same sentiment over each of our selections - total delight. I was especially thankful that the server returned to check on the level of spiciness I wanted for my order. I like it hot and even though I often request a high level of heat I am not always accommodated. In this case I was accommodated and enjoyed every spicy bite.

The Grouper sandwich and fries that were also delivered to our table included a generous portion of perfectly cooked fish and tasty, nicely seasoned fries.

The menu features many other Indian dishes, including some very tempting salads and soups, that I know I will try on future visits. Nice to find such a lovely dining spot featuring a wide variety of food so well prepared.

This is my to go to bakery. The location is a bit far from everything else in Chinatown but there egg tarts are amazing ! The shell is always crispy and the egg custard inside isn't too sweet just perfect. It's best when it's fresh and they have random time of freshness throughout the day. They open quite early in the morning I believe 7am or even earlier they start having pastries and even congee, noodle, and fresh soy milk. But those run out fast. The only thing I dislike about this place is in the morning there are lots of construction workers and other males that grab food from the bakery and becomes quite awkward every morning.

Expensive and they charge extra for a coffin nail because it's a different shape. I was given a price and the lady didn't ask if I wanted gel and instead did it and after let me know it was $5 more cause she did it. Did we forget that common courtesy is to ask for add ons especially if you already gave a price?!

We love that restaurant always go there being sure we won't be disappointed! It's a small place and usually quiet, but it's so cozy and relaxing, nobody talks loud you'll never here cursing here, we are lucky customers that we found that place and we've been coming here for 10 years. The owner of the restaurant mr. Wong knows his customers in face and always asks how are we doing never ignores! Sushi and rolls always good, always fresh, never saggy or mushy. Miso soup for some reasons is the best I've ever tried to my taste, edamame are Devine,just right not under or over cooked, with right amount of salt( sea salt by the way, not that chip one they use in other restaurants), rich sashimi salad to die for, must try it seriously, even the hot green tea tastes delicious(and i'm not really a big fan of green tea), and their green tea ice cream is so good. BYOB is a good option here as well as To-Yo dollars they offer which you can use on your next trip here which is great.recommend it a lot.

This is one of the best secret in S. Tampa. The staff are experts in their crafts and are dedicated to excellence. I always leave the salon with beautiful & healthy hair after my coloring, cut & style. Barb and her staff are super nice and provides a great atmosphere!

I love Colombian mix food!! Mix because I  believe they have a little bit of some South America countries. Churrasco is always good and most of their plates. The only plate that disappointed me was a rice and sea food soup that they have on their menu because the sea food was over cooked. Other than that is very good!

We had to try this place out after hearing about their all day happy hour event. I guess it's just the fact that we've had White Castle many times before that this place just could not cut it. We tried out four different types of sliders just so we wouldn't be short of variety. Ordered out 3 plain krystal burgers, 3 double Krystal with cheese, 3 chili cheese Krystal burgers, and 3 bacon with cheese burgers and an order of chili cheese fries. The regular krystal sliders did remind us of White Castle burgers but the other 3 kinds just tasted too salty. Their bacon was pretty big to be put in a slider but was also salty. The chili was also so salty on the burgers and fries that we didn't even get to finish the fries. The cheddar shredded on top of the fries looked like they just put it on top without even trying to melt it. We ordered 2 drinks and wanted the sweet tea they had at the drink counter but it ended up all out. Overall experience wasn't so bad but probably wouldn't be craving for these any time soon.

I've been here twice now and both times have been great! The service has always been excellent and I have never had to wait to be seated. The food has always come out surprisingly fast, I would have barely touched my appetizers when the entree came out. The food was good quality with portions that could serve 2. Also they were good at asking for refills on soup salad or breadsticks. Overall a great restaurant with good food and service
Everyone was welcoming and professional and really took the time to explain the options. I felt like they really care about my weight loss and are setting me up for success by all of their offerings.

We've been coming here for the last 15 months, every week! And we feel more than happy with the bartenders service, specially Christina and and the curly hair girl, they are amazing  
Why 4 stars?? Well the hostesses are not very welcoming, and management feels kinda stressed
Highly recommend! Best studio in Tampa. Josie is so nice, sweet and helpful!!!!!! Go see her!

I've placed an online order... An order out of convenience.... I don't want to to talk to anyone. Within minutes you call me to confirm?.... The luxury to me of an online order is it's simple, to the point, and efficient. I've declared exactly what I want. If there's any fault it's on me. #DontCallMe

By far, the dumbest people I have ever come across. These people have no business being in the food delivery service . I called these idiots for delivery of Chinese food. I ordered won ton soup, 2 spring rolls and steamed shrimp dumplings. The delivery person calls me 3 times on my cell phone telling me he is downstairs in the lobby and wants me to come down and pick up the food. And, all 3 times I told him to come upstairs and deliver it to my room. Finally, he shiws up after 10minures of these shenanigans. Then, to top it off, there was no spoon in the bag and instead of giving me spring rolls, they gave me egg rolls, instead, which were so loaded with salt, it was impossible to eat. This establishment is BAD News. Do yourself a huge favor and stay away. Theyre zero  stars.

This is a fantastic place! I started the new student unlimited month and have been trying as many different kinds of classes as possible! They recently moved to the new space on Platt which is beautiful with a third yoga room, more changing and shower space, even more classes on the schedule, and additional teachers and styles of yoga. Every class is so different and all of the instructors have been wonderful. I will definitely be continuing after my first month!

Update again.  There are two very good Asian markets in Tucson, Lee Lee and this. I like them both. Lee Lee is more international and this is an old fashioned Chinese market, jumbled with lots and lots of stuff.  Better prices than Lee Lee.  It's been here for a long time. The owner is a nice guy.

We joined the JCC based on recommendations from several different people and it does NOT disappoint!! I'm just mad we waited so long to check it out!

Not only is the facility large and beautiful but everyone is so welcoming and friendly. We use the gym facility often. It's got free weights, TRX, cardio machines and nautilus equipment along with a stretch area, spin class room, and several other private dance and yoga rooms. The locker room is spacious and everything is always really clean (which means a lot in a gym). It's got a ton of classes you can sign up for along with personal trainers - the litany of services you could expect to find at any other expensive, chain gym (sauna, jacuzz, steam room). 

While we haven't visited the pool area, it sure does look big from the gym. Oh, and they have tennis courts and a splash pad next to a large outdoor play area for kids. 

One of the big reasons we joined is because we need after school care for my 2nd grader. The J Care After School program picks him up from school and brings him to the facility, where they have a private room for J Care. They also have a computer room and a HUGE indoor playground.  They watch movies, listen to music, play sports - you know, typical kid fun stuff. My son has only been going for a few days but he really likes it. Plus the ratios are really good (of kids to non-kids). 

The facility also has a ballroom, a cafe, a library, and several small meeting rooms. Oh, and the gym has a babysitter! Highest recommendations for this place.

Super impressed with this place, came on my lunch break to get a fill done and Christine really took care of me. I made a last minute appointment and had to wait a bit but Christine did my fill so quickly, shaped my nails beautifully and painted them so quickly! She said she was short staffed so I understood the wait but was mainly impressed at how quickly she did my nails and how well she did them, I was nervous to get them done during my lunch break but she had me in and out in no time. I hate when I get my nails done and they take forever and I leave unhappy. Will definitely be coming back!

Great food- very nice people here. It's a small, primarily take-out establishment in the Rittenhouse area worth finding. I was referred to it and am very glad I made thIs stop.  I now have three favorites for chinese food in the city: Han, JinHouse, and New South.

Beef and broccoli was a nice-sized portion of delicious beef in a great brown sauce.  The meal was all served in plastic containers, presumably because nobody orders it to eat there.

The wontons- pork- were a delicious noodle.  Extremely reasonable.  I also tried the egg drop which I am a huge fan of.  Good find!  I'll be back soon.

After moving to Tampa from Los Angeles, my first attempt at finding a new salon was a massive failure. Barbara Forgione SAVED me!! I found her on Yelp, and the raving reviews were correct.  She studied my damaged, flat, annoyingly red-tinged hair, and then she returned it to a shiny, multi-tonal baby blonde. She keeps it looking completely natural by recognizing whatever it needs at the time...and most importantly it grows out SO well that there is never an awkward, unattractive phase between colorings. If you've had a disaster, Barbara can fix it. If you want to *avoid* a disaster, just come to Barbara in the first place!!  (And by the way, cuts here have been amazing as well.  Maggie and Peggy have both cut my stick-straight hair, and have each given me exactly what I wanted. Great to find hair artists who know their stuff, but also listen and work with you.)

Bella Prana is in a new and improved location (previous Yogani space on Platt) and I feel compelled to write an update. Still the best yoga studio in town. 

I love the instructors. Tina is still wonderful and I recently discovered the benefits of Ashley and Amee's teachings. Three out of three instructors whose classes I've tried are excellent yogis, so I'm sure the other instructors are just as rewarding. 

What I love even more about Bella Prana is the new location. There are now three nice sized rooms including a massive room with a ton of natural light coming through. I just wish it was a little cooler in the rooms or they had ceiling fans fans blowing. I'm not a fan of hot or warm yoga and I'm hot enough in Florida! 

Bella Prana now features a large retail space offering yoga gear, jewelry, candles, teas, etc. I highly recommend the Angel Mist; the aroma brings an instant calming. The jewelry is adorable and very reasonably priced. 

I love the owner's commitment to reducing plastic waste by installing a water filter. The staff is so pleasant and the energy of the studio is both calming and energizing. Very grateful for this studio. It's not just the best in Tampa, but I believe one of best in the country after visiting numerous yoga studios on both coasts.

Can't beat a live band with a full dance floor. 

$5 entry cover usually for some themed night they are consistently having. The drink specials are great and the band is perfectly situated in front of a large dance floor with plenty of room for half the bar to get out and dance. Which happens to be the case most of the time. 

They don't allow food or drinks out on the floor thankfully so there isn't sticky spots that put a damper on your date night dance partner. The seating is plentiful and the billiards table in the back has enough room to maneuver throughout your game. 

If you consider yourself country born and raised and own some good dancing boots, this is the spot for your Friday or Saturday night out.

(Skip to the end if you want it straight to the point )

Usually everyone I meet when I come in here is super nice and extremely understanding since my mom comes in regularly, but I came in one morning with my mom to buy some stuff and normally if it was part of policy I would understand why we couldn't do some of the things we do ( backstory: my mom uses a coupon ad and it limits one per customer and normally she will use it and then since I'm technically another customer I will also use it ) and so my mom has been going here for easily over a year and never had they ever given us the issue of using the same coupon for different people. Also know that my mom has gone there within the week (because we are working on our yard and need tools) and has never heard of this rule where you scratch out the coupons you use. 

Some backstory to myself I also work in some sort of retail so I understand how it may feel to be working a cashier or on the floor, and how we have policies to uphold, but the thing that irked me the most was that I'm sure she didn't even care about her customers not even that it just seemed as if she was making up rules or giving her customers a hard time. I think I would've definitely not been as offended as I was if it seemed like she even cared. Not because she didn't think it was appropriate to say sorry or anything after we were shocked about what had happened but more or so because she obviously just didn't give a f*ck. 

I'm sure working in retail and trying to make my customers happy and my first priority has shaped me to easily spot out bad customer service, or maybe just be super picky about it, but I hope anyone who comes in here next gets better service than I did.

I also hope in the future they will be more clear and consistent with their policies, but things I would like to ask would be if I had found this ad online used it successfully and simply handed my phone to the person behind me or even simply given him the link or even printed out multiples of this coupon/ad , how does this make it any different than reusing another in store ad ? If they made it that difficult could you technically just photocopy the ad you wanted to use again... (Hence my thoughts on why they have never made it a problem in the past to reuse ads or coupons they simply made it a limit to one a customer and had an expiration so it wasn't infinite)

Amazing hair salon for all hair types!!  Everyone works as a team at this salon, they help each other and all have various areas of expertise, so regardless of your hair type, or whether you need a cut, color, extensions, whatever, they will have the right person for you!  I love that the hair dressers openly ask each other for their input on how they would go about doing something or how they went about doing something that turned out really well, and what not.  It's completely refreshing!!  They encourage one another, validate when they're in agreement or assist if there is a better way.  I feel like most hair salons are the exact opposite & hair dressers don't want to ask each other, especially with hair color.  They seem like they know exactly what to do, then go in the back to mix the color & then try to figure it out on their own, in the 5 minutes they have back there.  Or they may ask the one colleague they're friends with, for their input (in a hushed whisper).  Meanwhile, you're sitting in your chair w/ your little robe on, praying to the high heavens that it's going to turn out right & already practicing your "I don't like it speech" just in case.  In that kind of setting = most hair dressers learn how to do good hair, by having to make mistakes first, i.e. jacking up you or someone else's hair.  But at Barbara Forgione, where there is an open forum of communication & support, the atmosphere enables fantastic work!  And if you really think about it, that's the best way to run a hair salon.  There's a lot of different hair types out there and everyone wants something different; lighter, darker, longer, shorter, bigger, flatter, etc.  It would take one hair dresser a long time to learn all that on their own!  But working as a team....well, 5 heads are smarter than one.  

I have very thin, kinky curly hair that's naturally dark brown.  Like most curly haired girls, it has taken me awhile to figure out what hair products work best for my hair, since not all curls are the same, but even longer to find a hair dresser that understands how to best cut & color my hair.  Before coming to Barbara Forgione, I was having serious breakage issues & was always struggling with my bleached blond hair turning brassy & gold.  I tried the blond shampoos, got a water softener, switched hair products, bought satin pillow cases, new hair ties, asked every blond & curly hair girl a million questions about their hair care routine... I basically became a hair researcher as a side job. While I do bleach my hair, I rarely blow dry it and have never straightened it (true story).  So I wasn't ready to accept that if I wanted my hair color lighter; I would have to accept that I was going to have to suffer the consequences of constant breakage & having to tone (or glaze) my hair every 2 weeks to keep it from turning yellow & brassy. Not to mention my curls had lost their va va voom & were truly lifeless. 
While doing my research I learned about a "deva cut" and was pleasantly surprised to find out that there are hair stylist out there who have a curly hair speciality!  Who knew!  Which now seems so obvious. The DevaCurl website (www.DevaCurl.com), even has a link on how to find a salon near you, which led me to Barbara Forgione, which I picked based on reviews.  So I made the painful break up with my then hair dresser (it's ok, we're still friends) and booked a consult appointment w/ my future BFF; Peggy.  That was a year ago (or maybe two? I'm not sure, time flies when you're having fun) Thanks to the expertise of Peggy, Barbara and the rest of the staff, I have never loved my curls so much!!  For starters, Peggy introduced my hair to Olaplex (if you don't know, stop reading this & look that up asap), I got a fantastic Deva Cut, did some hair treatments for the first few months and waited until my hair health improved before doing any coloring.  Today; only minor breakage issues (which I think we can tackle), my hair is bigger than ever (which is always my goal) AND for the first time ever, I'm a platinum blond!!  I didn't even think was possible?!  For thin, curly, dry, dark brown hair... it's really very impressive! Peggy always takes her time looking over my hair, comparing the new growth and then deciding on the best course of action.  It's not necessarily the same process, every time.  Which is also why I think I've fallen in a rut with hairdressers in the past.  After you've been going to someone for awhile, they just pull your color card & repeat the exact same thing, like clockwork.  But hair color = is chemistry and chemistry is hard & tricky, which is why I failed that subject in school.  And also why it's so challenging to find someone who does good hair color, consistently, b/c the variables are constantly changing thanks to hair products, diet, hormones & 20 other things that I'm sure I know nothing about. And that's just one more reason why I go see Peggy at Barbara Forgione Salon, b/c she's the bees knees.

Staff is friendly, appointments are always on time & facilities are nice however I saw only a 6 lb result over 6 weeks and over $400...that'd be almost $67/pound.

THE ABOVE WAS MY INITIAL REVIEW...my most recent review is below: 

As of 09/26/2016, I was surprised that someone from TMWL contacted me (within 24 hours of my Yelp! review) and truly listened to my feedback as well as accommodated my schedule to squeeze me into seeing one of their other practitioners. Each staff member I interfaced with, telephonically and in-person, were very thorough and knowledgeable. I was able to start their General Weight Loss program that has been working a lot better for my lifestyle and my budget. I highly recommend TMWL because they were able to truly take MY needs into consideration and help me figure out what was best for me.

I stopped by for happy hour.   Service was ok.  I did not eat so I can't comment on the food, however I saw 15-18 people at the bar and no one had food.  Looks like a local place for happy hour beers only.  While I was in there the bartender focused on the regulars but I never ran out of beer.  If you are thirsty give it a try.

I believe I gave the old studio location on Kennedy a much higher rating. The Pros: The Ambience. Bella purchased Yogani and did a great job with renovating the space it is stunning inside offering more spacious yoga rooms, located in S. Tampa, water station, and more bathrooms. The Cons: many of those who travel and use ClassPass are no longer receiving the welcoming feel or ability to choose a variety of classes. They are still listed on ClassPass and say 'No Spots' but when you call they have "plenty spots and welcome drop-ins" hm confusing.  If you were a regular at Yogani and Bella you would have probably noticed by now many of the beloved instructors are missing from the schedule and/or at another location off Henderson also strange. The class schedule is much different from the old location. It is too bad the overall energy here is not the same as the old Bella family. Unfortunately with all the changes I will be searching for an alternate location to continue practice.

It saddens me to only give them 3 stars. At their previous location it would have been a solid 5. Jenny's review below pretty much hits the nail on the head. 

I'll start with the positives: Easy parking, good location, & a beautiful lobby and shop. Also, I love Roni's classes where she also incorporates a message it's almost like "church yoga" although her messages aren't biblical but inspiring. 

Why only 3 stars? Since they moved locations, the vibe is different. It's hard to explain if you never went to the old studio. Yes, that studio was smaller but I think there was more of a sense of comradery amongst the students even though at times, we only had 2 finger widths apart from each others mats in the room - I didn't mind! I loved seeing a pack class and it pushed me to want to excel throughout the class.

Now for the negatives. The majority of their great instructors had left to go another studio. Not sure why the high turnover, but Mallory's class really took my level of practice to another level. Also, they went off class pass! Unsure what benefit they received from that but that's how I discovered them to begin with a year and half ago. As the previous reviewer mentioned below, they always have room in their class! Very frustrating that they aren't on class pass - I'll just have to find a new studio to love that honors that.  One other negative is that the yoga rooms themselves need updating. The floors need to be redone especially in the hot yoga room. They should use the Zebra flooring that another studio has. It's a lot cleaner and easier on your joints. There's also an obstructing pole in two of the rooms that just breaks the vibe of the class. It's not pleasant to look at if you're in the back. 

All in all, my love for Yoga began at Bella but I think until they make some changes I'll take my practice elsewhere.
I love that they have birthday cakes for my babies! Nice staff and very cute store! My dog was a happy camper.

I just had an early birthday celebration at the East over the weekend & I was made to feel like a true VIP. The waitresses were great & personable,  the security staff made my guests feel safe & escorted them to our table,  the bartender's made my drinks properly and finally,  the manager, Anna, was amazing in that she made sure my guests & I were all well taken care of throughout the night. I highly recommend Anna & her team if you want to feel like a true VIP.

Good to know there are still good 'ol fashioned men of integrity running around this town. Wouldn't call anyone else. Yes, Al is a real guy and for him, the customer is king. Al is a class act and trains his people to be classy, too. Wouldn't call a different plumber even if it was free.

Cafe Poca Cosa is amazing in that it is a one of a kind experience. From the moment you arrive to the moment you leave. The staff are knowledgeable and have great memories. The owner is wonderful for her food, her attitude and most of all- her hugs. The menu changes twice a day and there is always a vegetarian option; they even have vegan desserts. I'm neither vegan nor vegetarian but I can enjoy both types of dishes on the menu.

El Guero Canelo is a good place to eat for an affordable price. It is perfect for a stag moment or with the family. It does get pretty busy so there are times when it is better to avoid it (e.g. traditional lunch time/hour) nonetheless, the staff are moderately fast but you gotta check for accuracy.

Worth the wait....usually. 

I'm a bit biased with evans because I've been eating here the past 8 years since I live in the neighborhood. 
I always order the same items: burger, cheesesteak, pizza, rib sandwich, or chicken parm sandwich. 
Never let's me down. 
I will say that Evans has the best French fries and pizza anywhere but you have to judge the quality of the pizza by how many slices are left. 6 slices means the pie is fresh while 2? Eh you might decline til a fresh pie is available. It can take about 15min to get your food but at least there's a CVS across the street to waste time in or watch some sports in the pizza shop. 
They also always have a great assortment of beer. 
My favorite is the cheesy chicken parm sandwich because of my cheese addiction lol

Signs posted at this location indicate they're in the midst of remodeling, however, we saw no signs of any work in-progress. As the decor is very dated, I hope that a remodel really is imminent. Sunday evening was my first visit to this specific Beef's. I had low expectations based on lackluster experiences at other locations, yet I was hopeful because of the proximity to my son's apartment. Plus, we needed a big menu that would satisfy all our carnivores AND vegetarians. 

Somehow, I didn't get a photo of MY meal! But the California Turkey Melt was so good, with such a great flavor. And all of us raved about the fries. My daughter enjoyed her personal pizza, and hubs enjoyed his wings. My son ordered a burger medium-rare, & said it was perfect.

Service was mediocre... we had to order drinks at the bar because our waitress was in the back from the time she took our order until our food was ready. I almost made this a 3-star review because of the service, but the food quality & menu variety made the difference. This would be a great spot to watch football, or other sporting events. We'll certainly return soon.

Ben came to our office today to provide a quote for a new sink and a revamp of our bathroom. We're going to have a VERY unique Star Wars theme in our office bathroom soon, so he patiently listened and responded to all of our questions with different suggestions! He was also punctual, clean cut, friendly, professional, in uniform, and a well marked truck. He's going to do some additional research on fun options to help us to turn our bathroom into the Death Star, and to provide practical options to upgrade our team's kitchenette. There's no question, though, that this is the ONLY company we're EVER going to use for our plumbing needs going forward!

I am updating this review and upgrading it to 3 stars because of a pretty cool turn of events concerning this company. I would give 4 stars, but since this is not a review of an actual transaction or experience, backed it off a star.

So, you can read my previous reviews which gave a pretty blunt account of my experience with Al Coronado Plumbing. You can also read the other one star reviews. I did get a respectful response from the owner, albeit three years after the fact. This month, I received a message from an individual who said he and the consulting company he works for was hired to clean up their reputation and to make good on these bad experiences. For my landlord and me, that has been the case.

They sent my landlord a refund plus an additional amount for her troubles. This same company is also doing extensive sales training with the employees to make sure that they treat customers courteously, competently, and honestly. They have realized that 80% of their business is repeat business and they need to do better to maintain their base of current clients and to also grow the business with new, satisfied customers.

Another positive is that Al Coronado is no longer involved with the day to day operations. His sons are now managing the business and want to turn the company's reputation around. Mr. Coronado might have been the reason for the many negative reviews here, certainly mine. The sons are being proactive and are interested in a quality company, as manifest by their investment in employee training and contacting disgruntled customers from the past.

I must say, I'm impressed. I have never heard of a company doing this before and I think it is commendable. I met with the gentleman that contacted me and this is legit. I think it would be safe to say to give Al Coronado a try. If all is as this consultant company representative claims it is--and I have no reason to doubt him--things are improving for Al Coronado Plumbing.

Hilarious - "Also consider" lists Malio's Steakhouse.  Not eve in the same class 

I love Krystals. But, go through the drive-through. The dine-in area leaves something to be desired.

With Salumeria closing, this will be my new cheese shop at RTM. Very generous staff and knowledgeable too. Excellent selection, good prices. Very happy with this option. Glad this staple is still here.

The manager at this location is totally against gay people!  I couldn't even finish my meal cause I heard him say "disgusting" about a gay couple who was paying their bill!  I gave MY waitress $100 as a tip cause she is working in unacceptable work environment!  Second time I gave that tip....yet he finds it ok to say gay people are "disgusting". I feel bad for the servers who work under him!  He should be fired!!!!

Best smoothies in town!! Their menu is full of delicious options. From green, to classic fruit, to chocolate espresso smoothies, you'll definitely find a favorite. The struggle here is choosing which one to order because every smoothie sounds perfect. I really appreciate their punch card system too! The only thing that stops me from coming as much as I'd like is the fact that it takes forever to get your smoothie when it's busy. Avoid the busy times of the day if you're going to stop by (unless you don't mind waiting).

Immediate call back from my posting. They have changed phone carriers therefore causing confusion all the way around. I have made an appointment for next week. I will keep everyone posted on my journey to weight loss.

I have been going here for years and have loved it every time. I've always had a good time and never had a problem until tonight. To make a long story short there was an altercation between a couple and my sister, I was not present but when I walked out of the bathroom I saw she was on the floor. I explained that this was my sister and i just want to get her off the floor so we can leave, at this time a bouncer put his arm to my throat and pushed me. They continued to man handle us until we left, dragging my sister by her arm. When we exited (no resistance might I add) the same bouncer snatched my arm and ripped my wrist band off. This isn't he first time I've seen a male bouncer man handle a woman at this club but because it happened to me this time I am now speaking up. If you prefer to be disrespected and man handled please go here. If not this may not be the place for you. This is not out of spite, but to save other people from the mistreatment I have received here.  I understand these people are here to ensure safety and peace but this is not how this should have been handled. Most of the bouncers were treating us with respect with the exception of two who really abused the little power they had. Please understand no part of this story is fabricated or exaggerated but the whole truth. I am not one to ever write a review on anything but THIS one thing I feel strongly about.

Great market for Asian items. It's specialty is Vietnamese items, but they carry a good selection of other items also. Great selection of refrigerated and frozen items and a HUGE selections of soups, noodles and rice. 

Lots of fresh fruits and veggies also... (great dragonfruit! Yum!) And a really nice fresh seafood section, and the fish and crabs look fresh too!

If you like to cook Asian dishes, it is a great place to shop! They even have some of the basic ready to eat items, wrapped and ready to go for lunch and snacks!

If you are looking to live in a less than mediocre apartment complex and pay far too much for it, then this is the perfect location for you! Being a resident here be assured that you'll be able to listen to high volume music from a surrounding apartment all hours of the night, you'll hear every time a neighbor has an argument or when they leave there fan on in their bathroom including every toilet flush. You'll be accommodated with multi-layered paint encrusted, vintage cabinets and fixtures for free. With also a large capacity wash/dryer combo that allows you to wash four towels all at the same time!
   Have you ever smoked marijuana? If you should feel like you've missed out on that rite of passage or are looking to introduce your children to this illegal substance, look no further! Your apartment will wreak of it and you can guess which neighbor to turn to. 
   The office staff is wonderful. If you ever have a need for assistance you can ask Nicole, she will be able to thoroughly express  how much of a dollar sign you are and little your concern matters.

Antigone is a place that I'll go to every once in a while for small gifts or trinkets. Every time I've been there I've left happy with my experience. However today I found myself leaving quite upset and embarrassed. I purchased a small heart ornament and a single bell to decorate my grandma's grave for day of the dead, upon checking out I asked if I could keep the small wire display hook so I'd be able to hang the items on the tree next to her grave. Instead of a simple yes or no answer the clerk very awkwardly said, "I guess you can have it, we don't give them away with the items usually, they're not very pretty but i guess you can have it" and reluctantly handed me the hook. I told her never mind, it obviously seems like a big deal and placed the hook back on the counter. I turned to walk away and the entire time I was leaving she was shouting at me to just take the hook if I really wanted it. The hook was worth pennies at the most, and that isn't even the real issue. After working in customer service for years myself I can say this, you never know what kind of day someone is having. Small courtesies are always appreciated and go a long way. I still think this is a great local shop, but I am very displeased with the service I was given today.

I absolutely love Aldi's. I remember going when I was a young child with my mom in Connecticut and boy have they changed. They have become more health conscious. They offer a good variety of organic items. Their prices are almost unbeatable. Their own line, Fit and Active, has really amazing products. I especially like the low calorie desserts. The staff is nice enough. Remember to bring your own bags or you have to pay for theirs. They manage to keep costs down in many ways which is great for its employees ( I've heard they pay well).  Minimizing the amount of plastic bags in our landfills and oceans is something great and we can all agree on that. I didn't give it 5 stars because I cannot get everything there.

Not just a bakery.... this place has awesome Colombian breakfast! Probably the best Colombian breakfast in Tampa.
As a bakery, it's awesome! Huge variety of parba  (Colombian baked goods).
They also have a fruit salad section which I have not had anything from.
I recommend this place to everyone I know.

Great place to chill or chillax with your friends. 
Puff and just enjoy the drinks plus good music. 
Always popping, but Tuesday will be the day with the special events. You can check them out on facebook page too. Stay with the most current event. 
Great business/ marketing, trending business to just enjoy.

A cute little neighborhood bar it's sort of diving. They have a live band that plays Caribbean types of music well at least on the day that I was here,  it would be much better if they had a DJ that also played a variety of music to please the crowd.
Three stars because The drinks are a very strong so you don't have to spend a lot to feel tipsy I'd return

One of my favorite restaurants..hot wings all the way. They used to have this black n bleu burger back in the day which was absolutely ridiculous, but unfortunately they took it off the menu. One of the waitresses said that it was cause "bleu cheese crumbles were too expensive for the chain"...and I think that's such a terrible excuse but hey, what do I know. Honestly tho, the hot buffalo flavor for their wings is prob one of the best I've ever had.

We bought the Publix Thanksgiving dinner.  We ended up throwing out the gravy because it was excessively salty.  Also, the dressing was very watery and salty.  It was harder to do than advertised, not just heat and eat. The turkey had to bake for 2 hours.  The mashed potatoes tasted like instant and had a poor texture.  We still had to purchas sweet potatoes, dessert, etc, to beef up the meal.  We would not recommend purchasing holding day dinner from Publix.  We were disappointed.

Please don't review this business if you received a freebie for writing this review, or if you're connected in any way to the owner or employees.

I haven't been into Antigone Books in a while. Had called and was looking for a particular Calendar for 2017. They had it and more... pleasant staff guided me with purchasing children's books as well as a few quirky gifts for that "hard to buy for" person.. I spent an easy hour browsing and still hadn't seen everything. I have to come back for more.. who'd a thought half my Christmas shopping could be done at this quaint store on 4th avenue. And I stopped and supported local!!! I highly recommend shopping here. Pleasant, knowledgeable staff.No long lines and parking right in their lot next to the store. Thanks Antigone....

I was having issues with activating my iPhone 7 with the customer support over the phone sooo I decided to get to a sprint store. I initially called to make an appointment but they said that it was slow and an appointment wouldn't be necessary. Once I got in I was greeted by an employee and he was the best! There was apparently something wrong with the SIM card or IMEI, and he fixed it within 10 mins. I'm not entirely sure about the rest of the employees that work there but the person I interacted with gave this place 5 stars! I believe his name was Unno (also a very cool name).

I adore this store!

So many cute and vintage items to choose from. 
The staff is super helpful as well.

The only thing that makes it a challenge is nothing is sorted by size. It's all by style  or color.

Thankfully the staff is always there to help with that issue.

A very chic dog boutique in Hyde Park Village. They carry high end dog apparel and the finest dog food and snacks available. I saw a small kitty section, but they specialize in doggy couture and foods including a fresh bakery section that lookd good enough for human consumption. 

I especially love their dog beds and bowls. The beds and bowls are stylish enough to match your home decor. My favorite is the rustic wood with stainless steel bowls. They look perfect with stainless steel kitchen appliances and hardwoods. It's beautiful. Not your typical dog bowl. :)

Josh rocks!
Ignore those other reviews. We found him professional and helpful. We are very happy with our transaction.

I realllllly want Posners to be better than it actually is.  The staff has always been nothing but awesome and extremely helpful and friendly, which is kinda the only reason I keep going back.  

Their stock is really not that great.  They don't carry many paint colors or brushes, and the displays they do have are very empty much of the time.  I expect a local art store to have more unique paint colors and more specialty brushes (or even just standard brushes which they also have almost none of) than the chain store does, but it's just not happening at Posners no matter how many times I come back and check.  It's a real bummer.

They do carry a line of Princeton brushes that I like, but again, they were mostly out of stock.  I managed to find one that I could use, though.  And for a decent price, too.  Much of their supplies are a bit overpriced, however.  Especially the paint and medium.  Which would be ok if they carried colors or brands that I can't get elsewhere.

They do have an ok selection of pastels, pencils, and markers.  Their canvas selection is decent, too, but priced on the higher end of what I would consider acceptable.

Posners is not the best art store around, but their staff is the nicest.  I'll keep popping in to see what they have in stock, and to buy stuff every so often.

This place is a HOAX! I've never met an owner this rude in my life. My Friends threw me a surprise birthday party here past month, we had a reservation for 14 people, but only 7 people could make it. they gave us a space exactly for 7 people but, when time time came to pay our bills, they were charging us for 14 people, because "apparently" they held the spot for us. BULL SHIT. They had no written rules anywhere, no one explained this verbally when the reservation was initially made. The owner kept saying.. you should've known. Our server was really nice though, but the management team needs a lesson on how to run business to retain customers! NEVER AGAIN.. EVER!!

Usually we used to like Denny's. But, last few times the food has been such a disappointment. This location is less than 3 mins drive from my place. 
Today's experience - hash brown uncooked, egg white came out cold already & pancake wasn't done right either. Probably one of the worst breakfast experience as far as I can remember.

I stumbled in here on accident.  I have a love of Asian cooking.  I like to make Filipino food, Korean, Thai, and Vietnamese foods as well.  This has all things needed Vietnamese cooking.  I was able to get my fish sauce, tapioca pearls (so we can make our own boba), they even sell kimchi and coconut milk, there's quite a bit of fresh produce, fresh meat and fish.  You can actually grab your own fish from the case.  Everything is reasonable priced, well less than I've paid for it at Publix or Winn-Dixie, and not far from my house.

I have been at the Cove since August, and so far I am really enjoying living here. The location on the bay is so wonderful! It's so great to come home and watch the sunset after a long day. Maintenance has been very quick to respond to any issues I have, and everyone in the leasing office is amazing! 

Most of the residents on the property are fairly young, and very friendly. I've met so many nice people since moving here. There is also a dog park on the property, but during the warmer months little bugs that bite are an issue. It is also a little muddy since it is grass/dirt. Unfortunately because of this, I don't take my dog there as often. It is however a great place to socialize and meet other residents with dogs.

So far the gym has been really great. All of the residents take very good care of the equipment, and if you get into a good routine you can figure out when it is less crowded.

If you live by one of the pools, there are occasional louder gatherings on the weekends. It isn't excessive, but it does happen from time to time. I personally have quite a bit of patience and tolerance for these things, and they haven't been an issue for me.

The units themselves vary in terms of niceness. I live in an upgraded unit and it's quite nice. You can still see some signs of age and wear and tear, but it's an older complex. 

So far my only issues that can't really be resolved are the thinner walls. I've lived in two units since moving in, and my first building was so quiet! My neighbors were all very quiet and respectful of others. However, since moving into my new unit, I have definitely noticed A LOT more noise coming from both my upstairs and wall-sharing neighbors. This would be the one thing I'm not crazy about, but my neighbors are mostly respectful and aren't loud at weird times.

I will update my review after I've spent a bit more time in the complex, but so far so good!

The smell was too overwhelming and the organization of the store made it very confusing to find certain items. It has a selection of items but I think Kimpo has a better assortment, and they're about the same size. I don't think I'd be returning to this store; I would recommend driving to Kimpo or Lee Lees.

Looking for certain things will bring you into certain places, which is why I found myself in Bed Bath & Beyond for the first time recently.  

I was looking for a unique gift and when I typed it in on Google this was my best chance to find it. I had never been here before and was pretty overwhelmed with everything that they had in stock. I was impressed with their huge inventory of kitchen and cooking items and never realized how big their selection was. 

They actually had the item I was looking for, but they only had one type of model and it wasn't what I was looking to spend. The staff was friendly, the store was clean, and the variety of things they had was not like most stores in the area. 

This isn't a place I would normally come to, but knowing now how great their selection is I'll be back for sure.

Average.
I have been to this QT dozens of times and there is nothing exceptional nor anything to avoid.
The service is pretty fast but that can be said for every QT location.

Bed, Bath and Beyond... excellent place to buy kitchen stuff, towels, bed linens, and many other useful things. Quality and prices are decent... selection is usually very good. Wide variety of merchandise for all different tastes. This particular store was clean, and had friendly helpful staff. 

Since I love to cook, the whole kitchen area is a big toy store for me!

Been shopping here since they used to be called Zipperhead and were around the corner from here. I've become friends with Rob and Stephanie (the owners) just from stopping in every so often for a punk band t-shirt or pink hair dye. I remember I started shopping here when I was 22 - that's 13 years ago and I used to have a Chelsea cut and used to constantly dye it pink. Anyway - this shop has everything: band t-shirts, hair dye of every color, pins, belt buckles, punk clothes, belts, shoes etc. 

This place is a historic spot on South St and it's still my go-to for anything punk related.

Slow service! Burnt chicken & loud music ... dim lights. You can't even see what you're eating! 
I've been to this applebees 10 times. Out of which.. 7 times was poor service! 
Not coming ever again!

I came because of a friend who has been Raving about this nail salon.  I loved it. It was clean and everyone was professional and kind. I got a gel manicure and a pedicure. Prices are not excessive and definitely reasonable. Loved it. I will be coming back!

Not much there, even worse, a lady was selling organic facial said, "hey, I see you guys have greasy faces, you all need a facial." I walked by quickly with other people thinking who was she referred to..... 
But for the sake of organic produce, I will still be back.

This was my first visit to Posner's. This is a family business that could not have been more helpful, accommodating---and patient. And  being a beginner...this was most appreciated!!!!. Good old fashion service  and friendly atmosphere. 
I Will definitely go back.

Good times and good dancing always at the Chicago Bar. I've been here on and off over a number of years but came by recently on St. Patrick's day for a night cap. The patio is always nice on a pleasant evening to chill with a cold Red Stripe. The floor is set up nicely for dancing and there's always great dance music going on. Seems like a good thing going here and a great, pleasant experience. It's also nice to get my ID checked even though I look like I'm over 40!

Much better today! most people were friendly, enough variety of products to pick from. I enjoyed the authentic Mexican food as well. 
People were touring the farm which sounds like fun. 
They will be closed for Easter Sunday. Some of them(or all?) also attend first Sunday at Hyde Park farmer's market.
Never an issue getting my food on time, even the delivery is always under 25 minutes. They are quick and the bread is always super fresh! Staff is super friendly. Clean location

I was in dire need of a pedicure and yelp recommended them to me. Can I just say this salon is amazing and friendly. I'm so glad I took a chance. Everyone was friendly and professional, held conversation, and cuticle clean up (a huge thing for me) was excellent. I took my mom with me, who hates people touching her feet, enjoyed it very much as well. I would definitely recommend this salon to anyone and go back again.

Consistently excellent.  This is a Northeast gem that focuses on quality and quantity.  I recommend the Titanic as it is a combination of flavor that is perfected with the addition of the house Italian dressing.

I tried a cheese and avocado once, it was good, i was going to go, but I found the "free smells" sign kind of disrespectful to the customers who may want to try something and the answer is "you can smell it for free" it might just be me, but I would get offended, i mean if you don't give free samples, just say it, no need to be rude about it. 

So, today I had a coupon and I was like, what the heck, let's go and ignore that sign, and i came across an enlightening review stating that Jimmy John Liautaud, founder & CEO is a trophy hunter, and I found a picture of him with a dead rhino that @#$$#^^%& just killed for fun, i'm sorry but never EVER in my life will I contribute a single penny to his hunting trips.
Great bread, great tomato pies, and the best customer service.

This is a decent Asian Store.  It's not an Asian Supermarket like they have in the West, therefore if you're expecting a Walmart looking type of store then you're too narrow minded.  They have good selections on Asian things and have good parking area (unlike in Hawaii Downtown, you have to pay and fight for parking).  The cashiers are nice people, they're just trying to make a living in this world. They have great selections of ceramic things and melamine spoons and serving spoons.

Awesome professional service.  The are careful to keep the work area clean and safe while they work; they explain the work and pricing up front and are very through when answering your questions.  The employees seem happy with their profession and employer.  Would recommend them for ALL your plumbing needs.
Best scacciata ever! I've been coming to this bake shop for years and they have never disappointed me. I refuse to get scacciata anywhere else but here. The desserts are great also. Highly recommend.

They have the best pizza- reminds me of a pizza place I used to eat at as a child in my old neighborhood.was babysitting my nephew this night and ordered a cheese pizza for us to share with my daughter as well. Got it delivered very quickly and it was so delish. So much cheese and very fresh and still hot! 

When I go eat inside- the staff is very friendly and always helpful when it comes to making suggestions. The cheese steaks are also good and mozzarella fries.

Updated review: for nothing more than your convenience of a nail salon being five minutes from my work I decided to stop at a different place yesterday for a full set of nails. When I presented the design that I clearly stated, I wanted a simplified version of, I was immediately told it was "...too much a design..." and the answer was no.

Had I had more time to spare I would have definitely driven another 10 minutes and got my nails done here with Christine or any of her techs. No matter what kind of design I have presented to them they are always more than ready and willing, to at the very least try to duplicate it.

I won't be "cheating" anymore.  Amazing Nails has earned my longtime business.

So classic South Philly! This hole in the wall corner spot deep in the heart of South Philly is baking fresh breads and serving them up with a side of old school Italian charm.

 A trip into Viralli Brothers is such a welcomed step back into a simpler time. Even the prices haven't changed! I mean, .75 for a slice of tomato pie. $1.50 for a fresh loaf. You can leave here with a large haul for just $5. Cash only of course. 

Joe, the sweet older gentleman behind the counter who threw me an extra roll was full of smiles and laughter. He was such a memorable ray of sunshine on a dreary cold morning. He alone is worthy of a return visit. 

We tried the tomato pie which is dairy free. It was delicious! Even cold! 

The raisin bread was a smaller loaf which is really enough. Raisins in every bite. Not overly seasoned with cinnamon. Just right. And when warm and toasted....ooooh!

The large Italian loaf was gone in 60 seconds. So fresh and crisp. And cheap!! 

Joe reminded me often that there are no preservatives in his breads and he wasn't kidding. They won't last more than a day or two unless you freeze them. 

A must stop! Definitely had me wanting to make Sunday dinners with a big pot of homemade red "gravy" a regular thing. Man, South Philly is gooooood!

I luv Krystal's! I prefer them over White Castle due to where I was raised. This one is pretty old, outdated BIG TIME! restrooms need upgrades, from the door handles to the small of mildew. Good music is played here but the speaker keeps going in & out, holy hell! Ketchup packets are dirty from other ketchup packets. Staff is friendly & nice. Burgers were good, fries were fresh. Seems to stay busy though. I would come back.

First off, you get to view the apartment before signing a lease so anyone complaining of the look of the apartment or the size of the washing machine, that's on you.

The fact that The Cove has been able to keep the same name since they opened in the 80s, really speaks for itself.  If you're used to apartment living you know many places get out under a new name and "new management" in less than a few years easy.

I've lived in "luxury" apartments before where you pay too much to have these great amenities you barely use.  My husband and I decided to down size while I'm going back to school and we are already planning on staying here for another year.  We moved in around the holidays and everywhere everyone has been so welcoming.  It's nice when your neighbors talk to one another.  The appliances are older, but I never have any issues with them or the building itself.  My neighbors are all pretty quiet and normal, thank god.  I also really enjoy the set up of the apartment, I feel like the space is utilized well.

The people in. The leasing office are also super friendly and helpful.  Most of them live here too which, once again, really tells you something.

My only complaint would be the gym could use more regular maintenance, but that's been a problem everywhere I've lived.

All in all the place feels like home and the price of rent is really great for the area.

Stopped by to buy detergent and saw a lot of great deals. I saved $9 on detergent that will last me a year or more.   I also saw a lot of stuff I want to buy: bromeliads, planters, quinoa, vitamins.   This is a great bargain.  I don't buy as much food because I don't have space for it. But I will one day

If you want a cake with old school buttercream this is the place!  When the cake has been sitting for a little bit the icing has that delicious little crunch.  Oh so good!
They can make almost anything you can think of.  Give it a try!  You won't be disappointed.

Very nice country & western music venue. Friendly staff.  Live music and DJ keep the place jump'in.  Dance lessons offered.  But the food was very disappointing.  Website prices did not match actual menu prices.  Steak dinner terrible!  Server said nice thick delicious steaks, but they were overpriced lousy thin t-bones, overcooked, tough, nasty seasoning served with small baked potato (fake cheap margarine butter substitute spread - for the price could give real butter), side salad okay, no dinner roll or bread, and this was the second time we were very disappointed in the food. Recommend not arriving early for dinner and eating elsewhere.  And friend did not eat because he was not going to pay $10 for a bowl of chili, server couldn't accommodate him with a smaller cup of chili, said only served in a bread bowl (?), but said she would check with the kitchen (didn't), and when pressed her response was the computer wouldn't allow her to place a special order or offer a cup of chili.  Other menu items our party ordered were so-so, small portions, and OVER-PRICED cheap bar food.

In a tiny nestled away shop on the edge of Chinatown there's this hidden gem filled with delicious goodies.. These buns are by far my favorite in all of Chinatown Philly! The shop opens early on weekdays making it the perfect stop for a quick breakfast treat.

The buns are baked fresh daily, and when they're gone -- that's it for the day. They have a wide range of selection from your pork bun, hot dog bun, chicken pie, egg tarts, milk buns, AND my FAVORITE the pineapple bun (and pineapple custard bun). The best part?! THE BUNS ARE ONLY $1 each. I usually come in and pick out a few buns and a drink, and I leave more than satisfied. The drinks are also relative cheap compared to the bubble tea shops in town. The milk tea here is delicious (iced or hot) and only is a dollar or two! 

If you're planning on going to Chinatown for a quick bite -- I highly recommend checking out this small bake shop. Or maybe even as a quick dessert after your meal (TRY THE PINEAPPLE BUN-- NO IT DOES NOT TASTE LIKE PINEAPPLE). Think of a sweet soft fresh bun with crumbling flavors that melt in your mouth. The buns are fresh & delicious.. and now after writing this I want them.

Came here for a film festival.  The place is beautiful. I appreciate that they open their doors for events other than their own.  It was easy to navigate, the folks there were helpful and polite.  

I loved seeing all the artwork and items made by the kids.  

I felt super comfortable there.  I hope they do another film event there.

THIS REVIEW IS FOR THE ROUND PERSONAL TOMATO PIES AND THE PERSONAL GARLIC ROUNDS ONLY.


On the way out of town my buddy and I scooped up the last remaining pies (after we went to Sarcone's first and they'd already sold out).  The only thing left were the round personal pies and they LOOKED great, but they were really not very good TASTING.  The sauce on the tomato pie was excellent - even a little garlicky - but there wasn't enough of it and the whole pie was really really dry.  The garlic rounds tasted a little better, but they were also kind of dry and way too chewy to be enjoyable.  I will say that Carangi's square slices weren't any better.  Overall I'm shocked at how disappointed I was with the Philly tomato pie scene.  My family lives in Reading and at their market they have sheets trucked over from the Conshohocken Bakery and who knows how old those are by the time I buy them, but they are MOIST and sweet, and rich with a hint of parmesan and thicker/softer almost spongy in the middle crust.  I just assumed Philly would have tomato pies 10X better than those.  Sadly, I was wrong.

The shop was cool and the guy working there might have been the friendliest human I've ever encountered though!
Fast, friendly service. Had the battery replaced in two of my watches while I waited; I don't think I waited any more than 15 minutes.

Pretty good hardware place. The workers seem pretty knowledgeable the few times I've needed their help finding something. I generally come here to fill my propane tank. They fill it to the top and it's only $16. The blue rhino ones out there are about $18 to trade out and you only get 3/4th of a tankful. There is a big selection of things here and it's a good neighborhood hardware store that's closer than home depot or lowes.

I'm not in Tampa that much anymore, and I'm probably too grown for Ybor, but once in awhile it's nice to indulge in a ridiculous club. The Honey Pot has been around for awhile, but I spent most of my youth at the Castle and Czar, so I've actually never been. The d/l:

THE GOOD: No cover before 10:30PM, yay. They opened at 10:00PM (when we arrived), and the line was already getting pretty long. The gradually let people in, which is a good thing, because by the time we got in the bar wasn't super crowded. But it took like 20 minutes to get in, so if you arrive after 10PM, you might miss the no-cover cut-off.

Also (questionably) good: they had $1 wells (this was a Saturday). Nothing good has ever come out of $1 well drinks, but not all of us are moneybags, so, moving on...

I also liked the ambience. Not too loud (but still kinda loud), not too crowded, and it was nicely lit (just dark enough for my tastes). The space isn't HUGE, but the night I was there, it was full and accommodated everyone without being elbow-in-the-ribs crowded. Crowd seemed friendly and chill and a good amount of queer ppl were here. AND strangers complemented me on my moves, so maybe I'm not too grown after all.

THE BAD: Music was kinda awful. It's 2017, ain't nobody wanna hear a mash-up of Justin Bieber's "Baby" with Lil Jon's OKAYYYs thrown in. Like, I understand wanting to play Top 40 and crowd favorites, but all the "crowd favorites" they were playing were from like... 5-10 years ago? Seemed kinda "off".

Despite having a well vodka drink, I ended up getting a Tito's after (a standard for me, btw, so it's a flavor I'm pretty familiar with) that tasted suspiciously LIKE well, but ended up costing $7. I'm not saying they re-fill their bottles with acetone grain alcohol, but...

Also got charged $1 for tap water. Listen, I usually tip a dollar anyway no matter what I'm ordering, but isn't charging for tap water illegal in a bar/club? Maybe not in Tampa...

Also, I'm old, so I wish they had a few more places to sit and watch the crowd. The few seats seemed more like VIP/bottle service (lolol) areas.

Overall, it was a fine experience. Maybe would come back to stretch these ole knees.
Great service. Fast repair of two watches and it was very fairly priced. Recommend. Thanks!

Ok so Denny's is Denny's.  The food was average no issues.  We have eggs, bacon, quesadilla, pancakes, steak, chicken tenders, fries, hash browns and sausage biscuit and gravy.  The sausage biscuit and gravy would get 5 stars easy.  The biscuit was so soft.  I don't know if it is but it was like it was hand made.  

The service was good.  The wait however was not.  We waited about 20 minutes while others came in and got tables right away.  I think we were forgotten.  When we asked we were given a short response that we my party of 4 would be seated after the party of 12 that came in before us got their seats.  I'm thinking they needed to make sure they had enough seats in the large parties area until we were sat no where near them.  Then the same snappy manager apologized for the wait as I was paying.  It wasn't the wait it was not admitting they made a mistake.

This was my first and last time going to Aldi. With that said, it's in a plaza with lots of other unknown shops, plenty of parking available. There are no customer service reps working here, only cashiers. There's no deli, no meat department, no seafood; it's definitely worse than Walmart grocery stores. 

What brought me here was their local advertisements, $7.79/lb for salmon. Amazing deal but I knew there had to be a catch. It's a very dingy, dirty store. The produce were thrown into boxes, very messy. 

There was no customer service people!?! No one to answer my question. So i googled and found the number associated with this store. It was an automated service call to Aldi US and in order to cut costs for customers. In other words, to get an answer, I had to go on their website. What?!! 

No thanks! I'll stick to Publix and Sprouts for now

This hairdresser, Troy, is far less than professional. At times, you need to reschedule appointments. I had to go to the doctor because my eyes had swollen shut. I was told I had conjunctivitis and was contagious. He offered me a time during my work hours, which obviously I could not take.  I received an email stating that he, Troy, would no longer be my hairdresser and I should find another. So, I said gladly and told him to not contact me again.  He texted from another number and called me a fu***** cu**.
    Now... professional? Don't go there. Trust me. It is not worth the stress.

If I could think of a word that means less than not helpful AT ALL, I'd put that here. Couldn't print me a receipt. I'd gone to another store to get the receipt for an online payment & was told I have to go to a corporate store. Ha! All bullshit! Dude wouldn't even print a copy of my bill. Thanks for F-ing nothing!

I've always driven past this Xoom and barely noticed it until recently when I moved into the area. I frequent the Xoom further east on Wilmot, but was pleasantly surprised to find this almost exactly the same with identical menu's. It has a larger sitting area and there is iced water with cucumber available to sip on. I ordered up a "Max Daddy" which was chock full of good stuff and included a couple of boosters (things like vitamins, that I got to pick) and for .50 extra I added my favorite Almond Milk. The order was done quickly, it was delicious, and as much as I tried to pace myself drinking it, was gone way too fast. I like Xoom Juice's options a lot!

Moved to the area couple months ago and this is the nearest Publix.  Sad to say that I will be driving past this store to go to the bigger and nicer one by Fletcher for now on.  

It's not that this Publix does anything really bad, but the little nice things that I've come to expect are completely overlooked by management.

For instance, Publix is famous for their scales by their store entrance.  However, the scale to weigh yourself at this location is on the south side entrance.  The store only opens cashier lanes by the north side entrance.  If you happen to park your car by the south entrance so you can weigh yourself, due to shopping at other stores, or because of parking you have a LONG walk with your groceries.  

The medicine, first aid is separated from the rest of the personal hygiene product aisle.  Again, medicine, first aid, etc is by the north side entrance and the personal hygiene products are on the other side of the store in an aisle.  What?!  Talk about a long walk back and forth to get that Advil you needed!

They put the slowest cashier on the 10-Items or Less lane during the week, after work!  I mean seriously, I stopped in for a rotisserie chicken and potato salad at 6:10pm after work, the dang cashier was a senior woman who was s-l-o-w and chatted up every single customer about personal stuff.  It only took me 3 minutes to grab my two items, but 17 minutes to pay!  Speedy lane should have a speedy cashier. Period.

On the plus side, a shout out to the produce employee.  He always finds what you're looking for and/or walk you over to the area.  He is always upbeat and happy.

Sometimes, you just need a key copied, or a propane tank refill. A small hardware store can really be so much more convenient than a big box store! Parking is close, and there's usually no wait for friendly service. Hubs often comes here when he needs a specific tool or some hardware. I ordered my solid wood gardening bench from Ace's website & had it delivered to this location for free, maybe 10 years ago. It still looks fantastic... lasted longer than my fleeting green thumb. They really have a little of everything in stock, and will order anything that isn't. They also sell a variety of semi-related home items, such as citronella candles & mosquito coils, windchimes, yard decor & Yeti tumblers.
Agree with others, the honey turkey and mozzarella is where it's at. Busy at lunch, but the line moves fairly quickly.

Great value!! Big sandwich at great prices. Sliced to order! But... the bread never holds up. My buddy thinks this is the best hoagie and I respect his opinion, but he's wrong. So many hoagies so little time.....

Given the fact that I am a former New Yorker and a pizza snob, I still gotta give kudos to this Papa John's!!! I normally hate pizza chains with pan pizza, but this place really does a great job of making it work. While it's not traditional hand tossed NY style pizza, if you need to feed a party or are looking for a quick pizza fix it does the job. Anytime I've ordered online, it's been ready within 15 minutes. The staff is always pleasant and helpful. Plus, my orders are always accurate. The best thing about Papa John's overall: Order a specialty pizza and you can add unlimited toppings. My go-to is the Spinach Alfredo with bacon, onion, black olive and mushroom. Coupon codes are key as well...during baseball season, if the Rays score 6 or more home runs - Rays6. During hockey season, if the Lightning win - SLAPSHOT50.
