Which means that with this code, my large pizza comes to only $7. You can't beat that!!!! Put on top of that a convenient location, great employees and food ready in minutes :)

The service here is terrible. Me and my girlfriend are here currently on a Wednesday night as we got here around 9. We were quickly seated as we were told to sit wherever we'd like. Per our usual we chose to sit at the bar. There were two other parties in dinning and another party at the bar that was leaving while we were being seated. The first bartender gave us menus and read us the drink specials before darting off to clean the other half of the of the bar and failed to return. While waiting another couple came in and sat down next to us and was promptly served their drink order within minutes of sitting down. After about ten minutes or so flipping through a three paged menu, the second attendant finally noticed we had been waiting and came to take our order. 

We originally came for the .79 cent wing Wednesday special but was educated they are now 1.00 wings. I immediately sensed a red flag but figured it was better than the regular menu priced 8.69 for 6 wings. Me and my girlfriend both had burgers and split a 10pc wing. 
It all came hot and fresh within 10 minutes. This was probably about the height of the service and overall experience. After the food was brought out the service went from struggling to non existent. We weren't offered refills nor asked if everything was ok with our meal. 

Once tired of being neglected and starting to wish we had gone elsewhere the visit got even worse. We sat for another 10 minutes waiting to be acknowledged for the check and two to go boxes. When the check finally came back we were overcharged. The wings came out to $12 and we weren't given any of the beer specials listed. When asked about the wings price the server blamed tax. 

She got the other server to help out and they apologized since it was the first night for the new price wing special. Ultimately a $40 tab came out to 67+. The original server offered to comp us dessert but we were ready to leave already. Her other option was to give us a gift card but clearly after this incident we have no desire to return. 

Overall, decent food with horrendous service.

Moved to the area couple months ago and this is the nearest Publix.  Sad to say that I will be driving past this store to go to the bigger and nicer one by Fletcher for now on.  

It's not that this Publix does anything really bad, but the little nice things that I've come to expect are completely overlooked by management.

For instance, Publix is famous for their scales by their store entrance.  However, the scale to weigh yourself at this location is on the south side entrance.  The store only opens cashier lanes by the north side entrance.  If you happen to park your car by the south entrance so you can weigh yourself, due to shopping at other stores, or because of parking you have a LONG walk with your groceries.  

The medicine, first aid is separated from the rest of the personal hygiene product aisle.  Again, medicine, first aid, etc is by the north side entrance and the personal hygiene products are on the other side of the store in an aisle.  What?!  Talk about a long walk back and forth to get that Advil you needed!

They put the slowest cashier on the 10-Items or Less lane during the week, after work!  I mean seriously, I stopped in for a rotisserie chicken and potato salad at 6:10pm after work, the dang cashier was a senior woman who was s-l-o-w and chatted up every single customer about personal stuff.  It only took me 3 minutes to grab my two items, but 17 minutes to pay!  Speedy lane should have a speedy cashier. Period.

On the plus side, a shout out to the produce employee.  He always finds what you're looking for and/or walk you over to the area.  He is always upbeat and happy.

I came to Lien Hoa Oriental Market after trying Kotobucki and Kim Brothers market. The other two markets really do not have anything that caters towards chinese products and really focus on korean items.

Lien Hoa is to date the best market that I have been able to find that carries an actual variety of items. While they specialize in vietnamese items, they also carry some other tawainese and chinese brands and items as well, but you have to have the time to go around the store and really spend the time looking for them, and I will say this place like the others are still hit or miss. While this place did have the largest store and selection out of the three listed here as well as the best prices, I'm still having a hard time finding the best chinese super market within the tampa area.

While I will come back here, the parking is minimal and it's pretty far from where I live in tampa so it's not the most convenient.
Terrible customer service every time I come to this place. Would be nice if they could hire employees who understood what customer service meant.

This is my new favorite hoagie shop! I really dig the family owned vibe that has been around for almost 100 years. Everyone is super friendly there and they really know how to make a mean hoagie. There might be a long line but they know how to move it quickly. And while they're very focused on freshly sliced deli meats, they still have a very solid vegetarian friendly hoagie. The Veggie with Roasted Peppers and Eggplant has all the wonderful flavors you'd expect from an Italian hoagie.

It's also awesome that they won a Best of Philly award last year since they certainly deserved it!
This place seems to never get it right. Overcooked cheese and raw dough inside. Ive gotten sick a few times too many from eating at this location for it to be coincidence.
I really enjoyed the Honey Pot. Good atmosphere,  and fun times. They play cool music and the bartenders are great. They had a drag show that was so awesome! Definitely worth checking out
I have been a customer here for many years I love the work they do . Rose , Tom and Lee are great !! I recommend this place .

Very cute little dog store! 4 Stars because it was a little pricey for me, but I guess that comes with the area. I took lots of pics of the dog toys because they were absolutely adorable! Good quality and healthy dog treats among other dog paraphernalia.

Great place to shop. Never really crowded, the self check out saves a lot of time. They also sell cheap pizza, hot dogs, ice cream, etc. One thing is that they close kind of early at 8:30 - 9, and they don't have a gas station at this location. Overall it's a good department store.

Just had highlights and low lights(Blonde) done in my hair. Telsi did a awesome job! She also cut and put layers in my hair. I absolutely love my color and cut and would definitely recommend her! You cannot beat the price for great quality you get!

Terrible management, will not be returning to this location. Manager was very rude during our visit this afternoon. Server was friendly enough but you could tell this place is not properly ran, we will not be returning as there are plenty of other good options in the area. Very disappointed in the Dennys organization for making such a bad management choice at one of their flagship locations

Giving five stars for a "fast food chain" category. :) 
Jimmy John's is such a guilty pleasure. I love their super crisp and tightly wrapped lettuce "Unwiches" with "Xtra" whole grain Jimmy mustard. They create the sandwich in lettuce wrap form, then wrap it extremely tightly in paper that you peel back as you eat, then of course there is also the outer paper wrap. It's a salad, but tricks you into thinking you're eating a sandwich. 

This location is close to my home, so I can order delivery online and usually have it within 15 minutes. They live up to their "Freaky Fast" motto. 

Online ordering is simple. I usually get the Vito Unwich, swap out the Italian vinaigrette for Jimmy's mustard and Mayo. And I get "Xtra" of all the veggies and add cucumber and sprouts. All of this is easily customizable online, extra veggies do not come with an extra charge. Veggies have always been fresh on my sandwiches. 

All of my delivery drivers have been friendly!

Complete impulse visit and it goes something like this.

Traffic on 275 getting through downtown Tampa was bad mid-afternoon, so I exited Hillsborough Avenue and headed west that way.

As I was passing this Krystal, I saw a promotion on the sign and decided to stop in and see if it was true.

When I approached the counter and asked the friendly lady about the sign, she smiled and punched it in.

It was 10 burgers and 10 wings (bone-in or boneless) for $10. 

Forget all these other fast food 4 for $3's, 2 for $5's, etc.

This was 10 PLUS 10 for just $10?!?

Not quite the Deal of the Century, but we'll give it the "Deal of the Day." (Besides, Deal of the Century was a very underrated NBC game show a long time ago hosted by rock solid host Jim Perry.)

This deal alone was worth the impulse visit. Every lady working this particular afternoon was hustling and friendly. My order was hot and fresh. By the way, the wings are small but have more of a kick than you would expect.

So, I decided to feed the entire golf shop with all this food since the staff probably had not had anything from Crystal in a long time. Besides, Big Jake plays golf for Sickles HS and eats pretty much anything. He once combined two 4 for $4's at Wendy's in one sitting for what local golf professional Matt Mitchell still proclaims the 8 for $8!

I haven't been here in awhile but its TD's, great music, girls & staff what not to like?

Try this place and ten's

Been coming to this place for years. I don't live in the region, but I stop in for every visit and bring something to impress the family.

They carry several kinds of raclette, which was a hit at my family reunion. I like to scour the bin of little cheese "samples" for a fun snack or lunch.

At Christmas, I brought a fantastic cheese plate with cantal, emmenthaler, Beemster gouda, and manchego as well as some delicious crackers and membrillo from this place. It went over okay, but there was still enough for me to take some home for lunch the next two days.

Every cheese is great quality, and they have a wide variety so that there is always something new to try.

Solid hoagie spot that makes some design choices in their sandwiches that put it solidly among the pack, but nothing mind-blowingly good to exceed some of the others in the neighborhood.

Ordered the Italian hoagie -- meats and standard toppings were good, but wasn't a huge fan of the grated sharp provolone for me, found it mixed in too much with the other wet ingredients on the sandwich. Bread was good, but pretty consistent with other South Philly hoagie institutions in the area.

Friendly staff and they were good with keeping the line moving, so did not take long to place order, pay and be on my way. Limited outdoor seating available, but this place is really intended for sandwiches on the go.

If you're often in South Philly and looking for a new hoagie place to check out and add to your existing repertoire, would recommend you check out Ricci's.

Went to infusion lounge for drinks with friends. The lounge looks sumptuous and is comfortable. It was mid week during the holiday season and pretty quiet.

We got their promotional deal for flights of margaritas and mojitos. Each flight had three flavors: classic, strawberry, and blackberry. The drinks were delicious and made with real fresh fruit juice. My favorite was the blackberry margarita.

Service was excellent, friendly & chill.

Atmosphere, decor and lighting were great. 

I'd definitely recommend going there and getting those delicious cocktails with fresh juice.

Honestly don't know what I would do without this particular jj's it's very close to my house and last time I placed a order for delivery it took 5 minutes. Very friendly staff and even better food. Keep it up jj's!!

First time to this Tucson staple. $5 cover on a Friday, but $1 beers (domestic), so overall inexpensive night with fun dancing. The live music adds to the energized scene. Sometimes the dance floor gets crowded, but it was refreshing being at bar that's not on 4th ave or downtown. I would like to come back once I'm a better 2-stepper. One complaint - secondhand vape smoke indoors. Make sure you wear boots (even if they're not cowboy boots that's fine. You'll want your feet covered on the dance floor!).

-_- I'm salty af because I'm not Elite anymore. t-_-t. Anyways- Pollo Tropical. 

Let's discuss. I love their grilled chicken. I can easily eat here and make it fit my macros, and it's delicious. I've had salads, a wrap, and a chicken Caesar salad. It makes me feel a bit better than going through Wendy's and getting a four piece. I never end up feeling like a fatty mcfat. 

The service is alright. Nothing remarkable. I remember when they used to bring your food to you. Now not so much. I'm not sure what happened. Also, if you order an extra chicken breast, or anything ala carte, they look at you like you're an alien. 

Finally, the last time I was there I got a raw piece of chicken, and the lady acted like I was putting her out to replace it. Even going as far as telling me it's impossible for their chicken to come out raw. I wish I would have snapped a picture. It was definitely too under done for someone to eat safely. I would expect an apology. Instead I got an argument. I treat my food service professionals with more kindness and respect than most, because a) I love food, and they help my love of food by graciously serving me. B) you never want to piss the people who touch your food off. Ever. That's dangerous. 

I'll definitely be back. This location is just convenient, and their food is generally pretty dang good.
The velvet amazon acai bowl is delicious and decently priced for a huge size! Only wish it had fresh fruit/honey on top, but hits the spot

So I just had the worst experience here and I swear on my dead grandpa, I couldn't even make this up. So I called there on a Saturday night at 520pm to see if they would take anymore customers and they said yes. I arrived at 535 and was tasting and asking about the cheese so I would know which ones to buy. I asked the girl behind the counter if she had any imported camembert and she showed me the one that I tasted before and didn't like it and told her I wasn't a fan of that brand. Then someone else behind the counter said something that I wasn't able to hear and this girl laughed and I felt like it was about me but I ignored it because I wasn't sure. Then I asked her if she had something sweet, nutty, and hard and she suggested me a gouda. I tasted it and liked it and told her to give me a quarter pound. Then I asked for a stinky soft cheese. She was annoyed but I'm like idc cause I want and need my cheese and her job is to assist me. She suggested me a cheese and I tried it and didn't like it. She suggested me another cheese and I tried it and didn't like it. Then she said idk what kind of cheese you're looking for. I said soft and stinky and something similar to von trapp oma, if you've ever had that before. She said no but some man behind the counter would probably know. Then he said to me well aint no cheese in this world that is alike so dont come in here talking about a similar cheese to von trapp oma. I'm like this old white man is probably having a bad day and just nodded my head. Then he said we got 200 plus cheeses and you need to know what you like ok? Now it was 550 and they close at 6. He said to me you need to hurry up because we all trying to go home. I'm like flabbergasted. I mean I understand everyone hates a last minute customer but we all been there before and not only that, I called to make sure it wasn't a problem and whoever was on the phone said it wasn't a problem. One minute later, he proceeded to put the curtains down on me like I was nonexistent. I didn't realize he was trying to be a dickhead until afterwards when everything added up. He over here acting like his wife slept with his best friend or something but anyways I said can I try Ossau irraty? The girl behind the counter got me the cheese and he said look here, this is your last cheese, we all got stuff to do. Do it look like we want to fucking be here forever since you wanna ask a lot of questions, why don't you come in the after noon? He rolled his eyes at me and his face was getting so red. I responded well people fucking work 9-5 and people get off at 5 so what is you saying. This old white miserable man needs a better attitude. I will never come there again because the service was fucking shit and why would I wanna go there when there's Dibruno lmao. If I do come to the Downtown Cheese Shop then I'll come to TASTE the cheese lmao. I'll make sure I'll come every single time I'm craving cheese hehe. Then if I like the cheese, I'll head to Dibrunos to buy the cheese. Every cheese that the downtown cheese shop have, Dibruno has! Also, I had no choice to go to the downtown cheese shop because Dibruno was closed at 5pm. Dibruno has wayyyy better cheese.  More varieties. And they sell baguettes!! ANYONE THAT LOVES CHEESE NEEDS TO TRY DIBRUNO. BETTER SERVICE, CHEAPER CHEESE, AND BETTER CHEESE. THERE'S ONE ON 9TH AND WASHINGTON AND THERE'S ONE IN CENTER CITY! **IF I MADE ANY OF THIS UP THEN PLEASE GOD TAKE MY LIFE. DIBRUNO BROS, DIBRUNO BROS, DIBRUNO BROS, DIBRUNO BROS, DIBRUNO BROS, DIBRUNO BROS!!!!! I LOVE DIBRUNOS!!!!!

I have been here several times over the past 4-5 years. In fact, my partner held a domestic partnership commitment ceremony in the courtyard in 2012 before it was legal to marry same sex couples. I remember thinking back then what a lovely outside area for an outdoor wedding or even a tea party. We came last month for a recital and this past weekend for a conference. This facility is a lovely venue for conferences, weddings, receptions, bat/barmitzvah meetings, or any large get together. They offer many rooms some of which have the view of the sizable courtyard with real grass! And benches. 

If you were to walk to the back of the center, it leads to the outside where there are lap pools both inside and out. There's a well equipped gym facility upstairs, even a preschool that they offer, and there are tennis courts where I saw some girls taking lessons. I hope to come back for another conference or recital performance soon. Just let it be known, this is a kosher community. As a result, there is no mixing of meat and dairy if you bring or serve any meals.
They wanted 48 dollars for a pint of gesso. Amazon had it for 18. Things I needed that they said on the phone were in stock were not in stock after spending 45 minutes just to find parking. Sad.

Cozy apartments with a gorgeous community pool, Jeff is awesome and helpful, anytime something comes up including lease renewal. Gym is great for quick maitenance but the youfit around the corner is the place to go. Location is great also with SoHo, Hyde park, channel side, and Ybor close by and many unique restaurants you're never far from entertainment.

Worthwhile to rent an apartment here, nothing but good experiences, neighbors haven't been too loud, only had one person back into my rental car last year, thank god my car was in for service. Good luck!
Lame! Ugly girls and shitty ghetto music. Lame lame lame. Shitty tunes. $20 cover. Did I say ugly girls?

I have stopped in this place for a beer occasionally. However, my last experience left  a bad taste in my mouth so I will not let it go. I stopped at 8:41 PM on Weds April 4th. The bartender was waiting on someone else to my right. He completed that transaction and I know he saw me standing there. He then went back and brought out a case of cans and proceeded to load the cold box-right in front of me. By now I am here more than 5 minutes so i walked out. The lack of respect for not serving me is inexcusable. Further, I  am a senior citizen. Is that the reasoni=I am not in their obviously younger demographic and they don want "my kind" in there? Shame on these people. I will never return thats for sure.

The sandwhiches are decent. The sandwhiches are on the small size for the price your paying. They do not modify there sandwiches at all without paying more $. You get free smells! yay? lol. There are definitely better options for the same/better price.

After Anna, the previous manager left this place has definitely gone done. Granted, I hadn't been since she was last managing so it has been some time. Anyhow, it was $12 to get it, even for women before 10pm. Most other gentlemen clubs have cheaper entry charges before 10pm and are almost always free for women before said time. The doormen are friendlier than most of the waitresses the night that I went by. The decor is off, it looks rather plain and is not at all as welcoming as it once was. I requested to speak with Brandon (the former manager at Ten's) multiple times and never had the opportunity to speak to him.  No promotional drink specials or shots were announced during the nearly five hours I was there. The music is always decent and it's always popping when you have Cici Smallz there. Other than her and a few other ones, the dancers looked miserable and quite run down. Most girls reported they traveled from Phoenix and "heard this place was the spot." It has definitely gone down in entertainment. Apparently, to make up for the apparent loss of revenue they have now included hookahs which seems contradictory to their smoking outside policy. Also, another thing that was noted was the lack of cleanliness. The sitting areas were dirty, table dusty and women's bathroom needed desperate cleaning. Other than for Cici and a few awesome bartenders I won't be back anytime soon.

I love cooking on a Sunday Funday. Our barely used, almost brand new Martha Stewart's Navy 6 Qt. Dutch Oven was on a hot stovetop and the enamel cracked and hot pieces of it went flying all over the kitchen! 

Bed Bath came to rescue when this occurred!

First, we were greeted by someone that offered a sample of espresso with sugar and she even had a containerful of foamed milk! Oh my goodnesss, if everytime I was offered this kind of treat, I'd come often to buy out the whole store.

The customer service is great! There were multiple people helping us in the Kitchen Department (Good selection for brick & mortar store. William Sonoma may be able to beat them on variety though.), almost overbearing and offering their advice. We ended up with a Cast Iron Dutch Oven. It brought back sweet memories of my mom and I cooking lots of meals together at home! We followed the easiest recipe for a braised beef short rib where the meat cooked in the dutch oven slowly for a couple hours, making it nice and tender. 

I used a 20% coupon and their coupons have an expiration date, but they will still honor them, so I encourage you to save and collect them! 

I enjoy eating out and it's a luxury I'm grateful for, but it can't come near a homemade meal made with love while relaxing on a Sun. night to a glass of red wine and new episode of Westworld!

Great service! I walked-in and there was no wait time. The nail technician was so kind and my nails looked very nice. Just what I was looking for. Will go back for pedicure next. Highly recommend this place.

This place is special without pressure tactics.  Izeekio was our sales person and he was geniiunly friendly and sincere in all his dealing.   It was a two day process for us since we r not impulsive buyers.   But he called the next day to check on is just like he said he would.   We r happy with the car and with the service there.   Izzekio Santa fe is the reason we purchased the car from royal.  Thank u for an honest smooth car buying experience.

We have two range rovers. One we love the other is the sport. We have had nothing but computer issues with it from day 1! It's definitely not worth the money by any stretch. Second issue is after our check engine light came on my husband took the car in without an appointment knowing he would ride his bike back. (He's an Ironman so it's not a big deal for him) however, wouldn't you think after you spend over 120k at one dealership within 6 months you would hear from them the next day offering you a car? This is unbelievable actually. He is without a car and riding his bike to work when he spent 80k on a car 6 months ago. Save your money and buy a Mercedes! That's where I'm headed!!!

Amazing service by the two ladies in the front got  picture of one working hard in front while other lady was working hard at making the most amazing subs ever very clean suggestive selling very lucky to have such great employees working in your business Telicia and Ashley just rock
Papa John's has great pizza, bread sticks, and cheese sticks. Their delivery drivers have been kind and quick, and the carry out service is consistent. No complaints here!

This place is a go to on a Saturday night. Especially if you like live reggae music and a good time. The drinks are strong and the bartenders are fast despite having lots of thirsty patrons to take care of. My only gripe is paying a cover fee. I feel like you pay a cover for a club, not necessarily for a bar because it's not like it has great amenities or anything. It's essentially a dive bar. But it's a business thing I'm sure so I pay it in addition to paying for drinks and look forward to having a great time on a Saturday night!

I have been to Alma's three different times, and on this third trip today, I couldn't believe that I had not yet written a Yelp review about their business! Alma's Jewelry and Watch Repair definitely earns five stars from me. They are located on the south side of Kennedy and on the left side of a small business lot that also includes Soho Sushi (a South Tampa favorite) and a newish looking organic eatery.  

I rotate through about four different watches, and three of them have had batteries go out in the past year or so. Each time I need a new watch battery I stop in Alma's (grateful for their Saturday hours), and for about ten bucks, I get a new battery in my watch and it takes no time at all! The gentleman who runs the business is from Iran, which gives us some extra conversation opportunity, and I love that he is able to replace my watch battery right there, without having to order it and have me return a different day (an experience that I have had happen with other watch repair businesses that do not keep a wide enough variety of watch batteries in stock). 

While I cannot afford most of the jewelry for sale at Alma's I'll definitely continue returning to ensure that my wristwatches continue telling me the time!

This place is always dead. My boyfriend and I enjoy a good cigar and have been there about 6 times since they opened with no problem. In fact we have enjoyed our prior visits which is why I am giving them two stars. We went this past Saturday night and it was pouring rain outside, there were literally guys at the bar with tee shirts and tennis sneakers on. My boyfriend was refused entry because he was wearing designer sneakers. When I pointed out the guys at the bar with tees and non-branded sneakers on and asked why they were allowed entry, the ID checker/bouncer said they were part of a prepaid bachelor party and so they made an exception. So we left - no problem - and went to our favorite cigar spot. There is a chance we will be back in the future because we have enjoyed this place, but the experience tonight was a disappointment.

Fun club! 
If you are in the area def check them out. There is a 10 dollar cover so make sure you have cash with you. The drinks were strong and the atmosphere is great.

Amazing service by the two ladies in the front hot picture of one but other lady was making the most amazing subs ever very clean suggestive selling very lucky to have such great employees working in your business Telicia and Ashley just rock

As far as Chinatown bakeries go, International Bakery unfortunately falls a bit short compared to its competitors within closer range of Center City. Even though I came here early in the day for a fresh eggy-cake, the piece I got was dry and a bit tasteless. The shop seems a little smaller and I was overall unimpressed with the layout and the visit. I think there are other options if you're interested in cheap Chinese baked goods.

This pizza place has some of the best pizza in Northeast Philly. If you like a thick crust, give this place a shot. I've eaten in here often, as well as ordered take out from this place. I've never had any issues with their food. Their steaks are good, their burgers are great, their broccoli bites are the best I've ever had ! The reason why I gave us three stars as opposed to five, is due to the ignorance staff that works here. 

The last time I ordered delivery from this place, the delivery driver cannot find my apartment complex. On top of that he was extremely rude when I was trying to explain to him how to get to my complex. When he finally showed up and went to the right entrance, My friend went down to get the food and he had the nerve to put the food on the lobby couch and point to it while fiddling around on his phone. Very ignorant. You can't even hand our food to us because your mad you couldn't find the apartment? . That was the last time I ordered delivery. Regardless, I still order from this place once in awhile, but for pickup, just because there pizza is so good. Bottom line - - if you are looking for good pizza in Northeast Philly, which is actually very hard to find, this place is pretty good. Just don't expect good customer service

This place is constantly changing policy. First same day is no longer, than they return to same day except Friday and then new pricing and then now new hours... dry cleaning is supposed to make my life easier. With policies changing daily, it's so hard to keep up and what was once a convenience is now an obstacle.

So I've gone to this location twice looking for food. 1st time I went th whole kitchen was shut down... ok I understand... went again today ordered a pretzel. Well I was then informed they are out of their pretzels...
Just not good service in my book

We had been searching for a range rover for must wife for a long time. Went to range rover Scottsdale - had to basically donate an organ just to get a test drive.  Had a horrible experience trying to custom order a 120k car, i got irritated and we came home to tucson.

I ended up placing a call to jaguar/land rover of tucson, and spoke to a salesman named Zeke. He was very nice, assured me that he would make my transaction easy, and he did. 

He found a few cars in inventory and had them brought to tucson for me, we went in on a sat at 4pm- left with the car by 630. We went back on Monday to finalize some paperwork. 
 
This is how car buying should be - especially luxury vehicles.

I went back yesterday to get a tire service and zeke met me with a smile, remembered who I was and showed me some new cars :)

A+ and 5 stars to Zeke and Range Rover of Tucson. 

David

I'm a regular at this Nature's Table location. It's located in the building that I work in, and full disclosure, my company gives me $8 per day that I can use here for lunch. 

I've eaten at other Nature's Table locations in the past and this one is probably the more consistent of the locations that I've visited in terms of staff longevity and food quality. I get to see the same people every day and I know that the items that I order off of the menu (aside from the daily hot lunch special and weekly line special) are going to be prepared the same way every time.

The reason that I only give this location three stars is because a lot of times when I visit them for lunch at 12:00 PM, they've already run out of the hot lunch special. Sometimes they get a big rush of people (especially if it rains in the morning or right before the lunch hour) and they run out of other core ingredients to menu items (pork, sauces, etc) rather early in the day. I would love to see them cook more of the things that have been proven in the past to be popular so that the folks that don't eat lunch at 11:00 AM get a chance to have those specials too.

That being said, the staff here is wonderful. I don't think I've ever had a sub-par interaction with anyone that works at this location. The chef makes really tasty food and has done a lot to improve the quality of the food being offered. You can tell that he really cares about what he does. Additionally, Agnes is one of the sweetest people you'll ever meet and she has been really lovely to me ever since I started working here and making Nature's Table my daily lunch spot.

All in all, I like this location and I'm satisfied with my lunches there. I would just like to see more food cooked for the more popular specials.
Cute little garden. Dog friendly. Beautiful people. Smaller than I expected but still awesome. I look forward to it opening back up in the fall and enjoying more of the events here.

We had been shopping for  new car for a few months and went to Royal once we decided it would either be a Jaguar or a Range Rover. We met with Tommy Padula a few times. We liked him because he was knowledgeable,  polite and most of all not pushy. We had not decided on a model yet, but were narrowing it down to an suv crossover. We were leaning towards the Range Rover. Once we decided the Evoque convertible was what we wanted, Tommy was efficient finding up the right package.. .  And it was in the it to boot!  Tommy delivered the car to our home last weekend and showed us how to set up the tech features. We highly recommend him. He is a gem and made us feel comfortable right from the start!  
Thank you , Tommy and Royal for a job well done. 
Sharon and Steve 
Oro Valley

We came here to buy a steamer so that we could get the wrinkles out of my shirts easier. We knew which specific steamer that we wanted and we found the floor model in one of the aisles. However on digging around, we were unable to find a boxed model so we reached out to Patrick over in the cookware section and he began to try to track down an unopened steamer for us. He spent a good 10-15 minutes looking around the store because his inventory app told him there were two in inventory (including the floor model). After a while he said that he couldn't find it, but he'd be willing to order it for us and we'd get it in 3-6 business days (with free shipping).

We walked over to the cookware section where his computer was and he ordered the steamer for us. He was incredibly helpful, pleasant, and friendly throughout the whole process and I was really glad he was working that day. While I was checking out with Patrick, my wife wandered over to the Fiestaware section to browse, and I spent the next 10 minutes or so just chatting with Patrick about life in Florida (he's new here). He was so friendly and never once acted like I was imposing on his time.

Honestly, this is one of the best customer service experiences that I've ever had and that is worth its weight in gold. Thanks Patrick for your help! We'll be back!

Horrible chicken, bought a whole chicken from the Deli for my family everybody complained, it was white no seasoning no flavor still taste like it was alive, walmart has better season chicken and cheaper. I paid 8 dollar for a chicken that was nasty, no flavor, no color, it was jus a nightmare now i had yo go out and get chicken from pollo tropical. Horrible dont ever buy publix chicken

The Rite aid located at 2000 Hamilton street in Philadelphia Pa 19130 is the best you'll ever experience. Extremely clean, exceptional customer service. The location of this store is wonderful but don't take my word for it. See for yourself. You won't shop at any other rite aid again after experiencing this one.

Orders a pizza at 1134 pm with a delivery time that I as supposed to be 30 mins. I'm 5 mins from the store but was tired and hungry so decided to get it delivered. The "tracker" wouldn't work so I waited for a while and finally about 50 mins in called the store. They said that the driver had left already and they said that he had another stop and would be 15 mins more to get to my place. So over double the original quoted time. I asked how long the pie had left the store and that said 15 mins so it was 30-40 min in a car before it was to be delivered. No apology no sense of making it right. He best they offered was if the pie was cold they would remake it at that time (and have me wait for another delivery guy). You'd figure after the got rid of their ceo for being a racist they would want to be trying to retain customers. For those that don't know there is a Domino's a couple blocks away open late and with people that actually try. #PapaJohnsFail #DominosWinning

Tasty salad, quick service. Two of my favorite things when you have 30 minutes to find your mid day meal. My first time here and the staff was attentive and made my order fast as lightning. My Mediterranean salad was tasty and big. I feel like I got my money's worth. The only downside is lack of parking, rather convenient parking. I had to park quite a long distance from the restaurant. It is located in a corporate building with lots of employees. The walk in 95 degrees was no bueno but I am being an optimist that it will feel great in the Fall!

My kids love this place! They did a camp here over the summer and had a great time painting fun pieces. The stencils make it easier for them and the end product is something that's actually presentable and of use!

Camp times are from 10 to 2 pm, and they host regular paint sessions as well as birthday parties.

I love this place great customer service. So many things to look at . Very friendly staff , and helpful. 
Variety of choices for availability.

This is a store that is willing to sacrafice a little price to be a budget buster service. My shirts are nicely laundered, staff is nice and gets to know you and your return on investment is worth it. 

For an expensive outfit go get some consultation on the process and chemicals used. Seville is for a reasonable cost on a uniformed way to support a job with a collar.
No staff, I have caked st least 7 times, to either device and parts and no one ever picks up, always have to leave a message for a call back, who knows when. Tough to spend my money there.

I am a loyal customer to food city and shop there all the time but this is the last time I'm visiting this location. The customer service was beyond terrible. The cashier by the name of Nayeli was very rude to my friend. She gave her a lot of attitude for asking a simple question and then was refusing to sell her alcohol with a vertical ID when the law in AZ is that you can sell alcohol up to 30 days after their 21st birthday, then the ID has to be horizontal. As we proceeded to tell her that it was her 21st birthday she goes "happy birthday i guess". Excuse me? I was in absolute disbelief. I can not believe how they can allow someone so disrespectful and rude to work for them.

Just had an absolute 5 star experience here at the Harbor Freight on Ina Road! I had bought the 100 watt solar panel kit in April...it is now November and just now got around to opening up the box for the first time. Upon opening my heart sunk as the kit was missing several parts including the controller.....I thought oh no I am screwed as I bought it in April. 
Today I drove up to harbor freight and was loading in the front door of the store with my huge box in a cart. An employee by the name of Jeff jumped in to help me enter the store. I noticed Jeff's name tag said manager....oh sweet! Before I could even tell my story he said: " are you doing a swap? Because if you are you can stay right here and we will get you another one!" I told him it was missing the controller...within a minute another employee was wheeling one out to my car! The whole transaction took 4 minutes......I mean wow!  Both the employees were very pleasant. Thanks Jeff! You made me so happy today! Am doing my test run of the solar next week on a camp trip in the desert!
No staff, I have called st least 7 times, to either device and parts and no one ever picks up, always have to leave a message for a call back, who knows when. Tough to spend my mone

Be ware with this store.  My all time favorite cell phone company scammed me. Went  in to buy 2 new iPhones and was told I was getting free phone case with screen protector. Well that was a lie. I was being charge in my monthly statement for them. Not only did I go back to the store they refused to refund me my money Back. The only way I got half of the money back was by calling sprint customers support. Thanks unno- for scamming me. Please beware and check your monthly statement bill it's under accessories.

We really like coming here on Sundays with our dogs. The people are very sweet. We decided to get their CSA membership this year as well and love it so far. It's nice to know where your veggies are coming from. Very happy.

I needed new AA batteries and I had a coupon for free batteries with any purchase. So I got a Caulk Kwik-Seal Adhesive ($2.39 with a 20%-off coupon) and 24 AA batteries (free). These batteries don't last long in my computer keyboard and mouse, but the price is right.

They take expired coupons!

I get super nervous whenever I feel like I'm a rule breaker, and yes, using expired coupons falls under that category. I keep every single BBB coupon just in case, but I still brought my unexpired ones to use first. Alas, I had two unexpired coupons and three items so I was forced to hand over an expired coupon and see what would happen. The woman took it and scanned it like nothing was out of the ordinary! Success!

I heard this isn't the case at all BBB now. My dad is from Houston and the coupons there now say if expired they can be redeemed for 10% off (instead of 20%.) Also, with their new program where you can pay to have 20% off everything without having to track the coupons, I thought they might crack down on this expired coupon thing. I don't know if every cashier is as flexible as mine was, but this made me continue to hold on to my expired coupons!

Overall this is a nice BBB. It is clean and carries the usual array of things for the home. On my last visit I picked up two new mats for the bathroom and a dish brush. I am satisfied with my purchases and the 20% off just sweetens the deal.

I called and ordered pizza 1 hour and 40 minutes ago and was told it was 30-40 delivery time. Still no pizza and when I called 40 minutes ago they said it was out for delivery.  I told them to cancel my pizza because I don't want cold pizza. Epic failure.

I found this place by complete accident and I'm so glad I did. 

I had originally called ahead to another chain salon to just pop in and have my hair washed and straightened with a flat iron. Upon my arrival, the stylist asked me to take down my hair from my bun. When I did, she informed me that she didn't have time to straighten hair "like yours". I left, and in a rush, googled nearby hair salons and luckily found Insignia. 

I will never forget how that day they were able to accommodate me quickly and not only straighten my hair, but do an excellent job. And I have been using their services ever since. 

This salon is very welcoming and professional. All the staff are incredibly friendly. But what really sets them apart from a typical salon is how they accommodate such a large variety of different hair types. 

I have very curly but fine hair that is prone to frizz. Insignia has been one of the only salons in my life to properly straighten my hair in a way to truly get it flat and in a way that it stays straight (without washing or getting wet) for days! 

It's conveniently located and has ample parking.

I have never experienced any issues at this shop. The owners are very kind and always make sure I'm getting what I need done, they don't try to change my mind on services that I request nor do they try to sell me something I don't need. The woman who does my nails is by far the best I've had in as long as I can remember.. I don't loose nails because she makes sure she does them with extra TLC. I can't say anything negative and I continue to come in because they are my favorite people and they don't make me feel uncomfortable. Thank you

We visit here on occasion, it's always hot and miss. Last night was the last time we will ever get wings from here. They were not entirely cooked, and absolutely ZERO flavor. We ordered garlic parm, and Buffalo. The Buffalo was not the original beefs signature Buffalo sauce. The garlic parm wings had absolutely no garlic or parm which defeated the entire of purpose. The service is always good and fast but the food is not what it once used to be. You can taste the cheapness to it. Not that I'm expecting a superior dinner but I would atleast like my wings to taste like something, especially when they are two completely different flavors. Also the wings are "jumbo" they are standard sizing. Publix hot and spicy wings are by far way better then these horrible wings we had last night. On a good note, the cheesy bacon pub chips were really good except for they didn't give me the sour cream or ranch that was suppose to come with it. We are prettt much done with this place.

I arrived to pick up 13 items as I have been a weekly customer of dislocation for years and I found out that the sign said close for remodeling and I can pick up my items from the sparkle cleaners down the street on Campbell and Glenn. When I arrived at sparkle cleaners they told me that they are offering 50% off at this location to show appreciation for my business if I wanted to switch to them. I informed the attendant that it was only closed for remodeling and she said no, they are closed indefinitely and the owner didn't even let his team members know or his customers. I wonder if the other locations will close as this was the plant.

This is by far my favorite grocery store! Very clean. Can always count on great BOGO deals. Produce is always fresh. Staff is always super friendly and attentive. I don't think there's anything I can complain about with Publix. They definitely do it right! Even their subs and deli food is great.

Fellow yelpers, I will begin my review with this: Today, 3/9/19, my friends and I did "The Egg Tart Challenge". This was Bakery stop #4 of 7.

This bakery was very big with a lot of seats. Although there were no other patrons inside, the ambiance was complete with lively music! And several people came inside to pick up their custom cakes! Happy Birthday Sue (your cake looked awesome)!

Regarding the main event- the Egg Tart ($1.00). This egg tart was veryy veryy egg-y. You could taste hard boiled eggs over the sugar and vanilla. I personally didn't like this taste in a desert. But there was a big difference between this tart and all of the others on this challenge.

In am attempt to counteract the blood sugar high, we got a few protein dishes.

The Chicken Sticky rice was served warm and we loved it! Great flavors and a better ratio of chicken to sticky rice. Still alot of rice, but the chicken was tender and flavorful.

Also, we got a Roast Pork Pie. Again, the carb to protein ratio was off. But the barbecue pork was really really good! I had a few chewy pieces of meat but I really loved this pastry!!! Try this!!!!!!!

Thank you International Bakery!

So,

Here is an honest review of a car dealership - Tucson Auto Sales. I have purchased a number of vehicles from Tucson Auto Sales over the years. I keep going back, and I keep referring folks there. Why?

I am a customer. Not an employee nor am I a paid spokesperson. 

I continue to appreciate Shelby and Josh. They run a used-car lot, and their vehicles are decently priced for the year and mileage. Plus, every now and then, they put out good discounts / sales on their website! 

They are welcoming, always treat me with respect, and.... they listen and work with you - if you are honest. 

If you are looking for a vehicle, or you know someone looking for one, check-out Tucson Auto Sales. Go ask for Josh or Shelby. I'm not anyone special so I know that how I'm treated is exactly how you'd be. 

Huge appreciation for Josh and Shelby!!

This publix always confuses me... it's like.. HI I'm Publix but I don't have anything the other publix store has.. I'm a shell of publix... I feel like whenever I'm looking for something specific I ask myself.. 'WHY did I go to this one again?' Sure if you need bare essentials you're totally fine... but other than that I usually have to make another trip to Winne Dixie after I go to this one to get whatever else I needed to get.

Brought my 10 year old jag for wheel alignment service, treated like I owned a new one. Friendly, professional service, bent overboard to help. Free safety inspection a wash and vacuum, which my car appreciated. An excellent dealer experience. James gets my vote.
Ms. Josie is AMAZING!! My three year old an I had the best date together  great shop, great host! We will be back for sure!

This place opens and closes depending on the season. It is tucked away from sight of the road or event other houses and businesses. There are lots of plants growing in rows, not sure who tends to them but it looks like a nice community to get into. When the season is right there is an open market here where people sell foods, crafts and other goods. The majority if not all the vendors pride themselves in being organic in the things they make, raise or harvest. There are defiantly some yummy treats to be had here. Last season they had some stone oven pizza made on the spot that was to die for. Today they had a lady selling some hand made tamales. The presentation was awesome and the taste was so great. Be ready to pay a little more when you come here for the labor of love they put into there stuff. On some days they also have live music and even an open mic time slot for people to come up and do there thing.  The advertising for this pace could use some work...I never know when it's open or not. And there are not many vendors that come here so the selection is small. Parking is a little weird as well.
Brought a used Lexus form these guys, excellent experience! Low pressure and easy transaction. Would shop there again.

I love this place and have been going there for years. However, when they have the band play, the lead singer is the worst I've experienced ever in a bar. He sounds like a dying cat getting dragged through the streets with a semi. He kills every good country song out there. But, having Jack Bishop sing, exemplifies the band's greatness to a whole new level. I know a large amount of people who take their money elsewhere and never return here because of how terrible the band is. I know with certainty that if Jack Bishop sang more often, more money would be made at the whole location (bar, band, tips, etc) and the Maverick would succeed even more so. Please make this change in order to bring back more/better customers. Thank you.

Love this store.It's a little pricier but it's a doggie boutique. My dog's favorite toys are from this store. They carry brands that I don't see everywhere. They recognize my dog! I just bought a rainbow stuffed with thee small, unicorn toys. She is obsessed with this toy. The staff is always friendly and helpful!

Had a great experience, and I'm very happy with my purchase. The people there are very fair, and treated me right. I wouldn't say i am a savvy car shopper and I know when I go to Tucson auto sales I don't have to be. If there is something you like on their lot, they will treat you fair and make sure they do best they can for you. Can't hurt to look at their cars, before going somewhere else and getting taken advantage of.

The Chicago bar is awesome !
The bouncers are kind 
The bartenders are on point 
The dance floor is always popping . 
Chicago bar is a great spot with a diverse crowd of people . I'd highly recommend going on Saturday night when neon prophet performs love reggae music . don't be afraid to dance !

Harbor Freight fills an important need. They are niche tool suppliers and they sell tools that get the job done. My water heater recently went out and I was resigned on spending approximately $1500.00 to replace it. Since I had nothing to lose I decided to take a crack at repairing it. With a $15.00 dollar tool purchase at Harbor Freight I was able to resolve the issue, repair the water heater and save over $1400 dollars. I could have been a tool snob, insisted on Snap-On or some other expensive truck based tool company and spent hundreds of dollars just for the tool but I chose to just get the job done. The tool was well made and I didn't have any issues using it. If it failed tomorrow that would be fine because I've already saved big, but that won't happen because it was well made. Before starting a job check out Harbor Freight first, pay yourself and save money.

My co-workers and I deliver to this club quite frequently. The girl at the front desk never tips. Not only that but she's kind of rude as well. We all know that the girls there are making money hand-over-fist. Delivery drivers make less than minimum wage and rely on tips just like all of the girls at this (and any) club do. For her to tip not to tip is absolutely ridiculous. Someone who works in a profession that is regularly tipped should know better. It really makes the club look kind of well, ghetto. I think management should advise the girls that they either cannot order in outside food or that they must tip their drivers. The girls' behavior there reflects directly on the club. I suggest everyone visiting this club ask each girl for free lap dances and see what their reaction is. Because that is what they are making us do - work for them for free.

Out of all the bakery places I've tried in Chinatown, this was one of my favorites. I love getting my milk tea from here since it's only $2 and very creamy. The taste is rich and the tea to milk ratio is perfect.

I have never been to a club like this. Free line dancing lessons, $1.00 drink special on  Wednesday nights after 8:00 PM,  live music, old America  western ambiance, & oh, so friendly services. I was completely fascinated.
As if I were in a movie setting, I loved to dance there & watched others dancing, where old & new generations mingle soaking up everything they have to offer. I have traveled the world & am constantly traveling but this club has become one of my favorite spots. I have been there three times already every time I am in town, I love to hang out there with friends and family. Kudos to the management of the club offering such a neat & fun place.

In general, the country music nor line dancing is not my favorite but we can dance swing, push & pull, and other ballroom dances to the live music with no ones judgements. How charming some of the line dancers are! Thank you my private ballroom dance instructor, Benny, who introduced this bar to us! Highly recommend!
